Dawnguard.esm|0028AB: We're done with this when none of them are left standing. Let's go.
Dawnguard.esm|0028AD: Find their leader and kill him!
Dawnguard.esm|0028AE: Leave no vampire in one piece!
Dawnguard.esm|0028AF: Arkay and I will take care of the little ones! You find the leader!
Dawnguard.esm|0028B0: We'll secure the castle. You just get to their master!
Dawnguard.esm|0028B1: Kill their leader before it has a chance to escape!
Dawnguard.esm|0028B2: Find all of them! Don't let any of them get away!
Dawnguard.esm|0028B3: Destroy every last one of them!
Dawnguard.esm|0028CB: The trolls will serve us well, I think.
Dawnguard.esm|0028CC: Arkay says we're going to win this. Well, probably.
Dawnguard.esm|0028CD: I'm as ready as I can be.
Dawnguard.esm|0028CE: We have Auriel's Bow. We can't lose... right?
Dawnguard.esm|0028CF: We'll make sure there's nothing left of them.
Dawnguard.esm|002949: And now... I think I can rest. Farewell, my friend, wherever your travels take you.
Dawnguard.esm|00299A: So, the beast is destroyed. Not only that, but Auriel's Bow is in safe hands. The Dawnguard will now be dedicated to safeguarding it, making sure that prophecy will never come to pass. You've served Skyrim well. Even with these vampires gone, the fight isn't completely over. Once we're settled back in at the fort, there will be more work to do. We'd be honored to have you join us.
Dawnguard.esm|0029E8: Vampires need to be dealt with. Hunted, rooted out, destroyed. Once there was a group dedicated to a similar cause. They called themselves the Dawnguard. That was a long time ago, but the idea was, and is, a good one. That's why I'm bringing it back. This latest attack proves it's the right time.
Dawnguard.esm|0029E9: Well, maybe it really is time to resurrect the Dawnguard.
Dawnguard.esm|002AEE: Well it was... Thank the Vigilants for ruining that.
Dawnguard.esm|002AEF: Ugh. I thought this place was supposed to be secret?
Dawnguard.esm|002AF0: Hold it! He's the one Tolan mentioned.
Dawnguard.esm|002AF1: Hold it! She's the one Tolan mentioned.
Dawnguard.esm|002B08: Please, he's such a loyal creature, and he's been running for so long. You have to save him! A place like this will change you...
Dawnguard.esm|002B09: Arvak. My horse. We came to this horrible place together. We were attacked by monsters, so I told him to run.
Dawnguard.esm|002B0A: You must help me find my Arvak. He doesn't deserve to be in a place like this!
Dawnguard.esm|002B0B: Arvak! Arvak, where are you? Arvak, please come back! Come back!
Dawnguard.esm|002B3A: The only way to get to my brother is by following in the Initiates' footsteps and traveling from wayshrine to wayshrine just as they did. The first lay at the end of Darkfall Passage, a cavern that represents the absence of enlightenment.
Dawnguard.esm|002B3B: I'm afraid in their current form, they still believe the Chantry to be an active center of worship. They won't respond to you in any way other than believing that you're an Initiate and you're undertaking the journey to the Inner Sanctum.
Dawnguard.esm|002B3C: Welcome, Initiate. This is the Wayshrine of Sight. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment?
Dawnguard.esm|002B3D: They're ghosts of the snow elf priests that tended the wayshrines before being slaughtered by the Betrayed. Through the grace of Auri-El, they were restored to their spectral form to enable them to continue their duties.
Dawnguard.esm|002B3E: So, let's get this straight. We need to do all that nonsense to get into the temple, so we can kill your brother and claim Auriel's Bow?
Dawnguard.esm|002B40: Then behold Auri-El's gift, my child. May it speed your journey to the Inner Sanctum.
Dawnguard.esm|002B41: They swept into the Chantry without warning and began killing everyone without pause.
Dawnguard.esm|002B42: Of course. You're here for Auriel's Bow. Why else would you be here? I can help you get it, but first I must have your assistance.
Dawnguard.esm|002B55: May Auri-El's light guide you in your darkest hours.
Dawnguard.esm|002F17: We've been so terribly fortunate. I can't wait to see what's in these drawings.
Dawnguard.esm|002F18: I think even Gunmar's been impressed with what we've managed to do with the Dwemer drawings. Let's see what we can do with these ones!
Dawnguard.esm|002F19: Here. Let me show you how this works... I'll have some for sale soon.
Dawnguard.esm|002F1A: Sorine needs help with her project to improve our crossbows. And if that doesn't interest you...
Dawnguard.esm|002F1B: I want to add some punch to our crossbow capabilities, I've done all I can on my own. I need a little inspiration. I've always been fascinated with Dwemer artifacts. I take them apart, poke around in their guts, and try to figure things out - with mixed results. But as luck would have it, I've tracked down the location of some actual Dwemer diagram drawings! So, if you'd be a dear and collect those plans for me?
Dawnguard.esm|002F1C: Ready for more? I certainly am. Be a dear and fetch me another set of Dwemer diagrams?
Dawnguard.esm|002F1D: It's amazing what I'm able to learn from studying these Dwemer plans. Want to head out there and collect some more?
Dawnguard.esm|002F1E: I'm starting to think you like this as much as I do! Here's the location of the next set of Dwemer diagrams. Thanks!
Dawnguard.esm|002F1F: Alright! One more set of dwemer plans to go. Think I'll be sad when we're done collecting these. Who knows though, maybe more plans will turn up in the future. But I can't wait to see these drawings. Go on, get out of here!
Dawnguard.esm|002F20: I've located more dwemer plans. Be a dear and collect these diagrams for me?
Dawnguard.esm|002F5F: Because that means there's still hope that they might one day shed their hatred and learn to believe in Auri-El once again. It's been a long time since I felt that way and it's been long overdue. My thanks, to both of you.
Dawnguard.esm|002F60: A vampire? I see. That would explain much. Deep inside, it brings me joy that the Betrayed weren't to blame for what happened here.
Dawnguard.esm|00320A: He just became absorbed... obsessed. It was kind of sick, actually. The prophecy said that vampires would no longer need to fear the sun. For someone who fancied himself as vampire royalty, that's pretty seductive.
Dawnguard.esm|00320B: I wonder what these souls do all day. They look... bored.
Dawnguard.esm|00320C: Be careful with those crystals. They feel... hungry.
Dawnguard.esm|00320D: Ugh. The smell here... let's try to handle this quickly.
Dawnguard.esm|00320E: I did. But something about you makes me think I can trust you. I hope I'm not wrong.
Dawnguard.esm|00320F: Anyway, my mother and I didn't feel like inviting a war with all of Tamriel, so we tried to stop him. That's why I was sealed away with the Scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|003210: This better not be all the things you just can't be bothered with.
Dawnguard.esm|003213: All right, let's see what you have.
Dawnguard.esm|003214: Why don't you keep your things, and I'll keep mine.
Dawnguard.esm|003215: I think I'll hang on to my things, thank you.
Dawnguard.esm|003218: We'll talk more when we're there.
Dawnguard.esm|003247: My congratulations on defeating Harkon. Clearly, you are the superior vampire. You are the new Master; we bow to your power. A power which, I note, includes Auriel's Bow. The castle, of course, is yours. We shall follow you, and help spread your influence across Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|003248: It is an honor to serve Harkon, is it not?
Dawnguard.esm|003249: Let someone else rule. I just want to kill and feed.
Dawnguard.esm|00324A: Once saw Harkon rip a man in half. He's powerful like you wouldn't believe.
Dawnguard.esm|003339: I'll rend the flesh from your bones!
Dawnguard.esm|00333F: Run, coward! Run back to the filthy Dawnguard!
Dawnguard.esm|003340: Run, coward! You're a traitor to your race!
Dawnguard.esm|003341: When I find you, I'm going to gut you like a deer and feast upon you blood!
Dawnguard.esm|003342: May Auri-El keep you free from harm.
Dawnguard.esm|003347: I'll drain the life from you!
Dawnguard.esm|00334B: I should have killed you and your mother long ago!
Dawnguard.esm|00334C: I'll make you pay for this, daughter!
Dawnguard.esm|00334E: First you die, then Serana suffers!
Dawnguard.esm|003357: So, the deed has been done. My brother is dead.
Dawnguard.esm|0034B1: That's close enough, fool. Now you die!
Dawnguard.esm|0034B2: Forgive me, but I am on urgent business for the Dawnguard. I have no time to speak with you.
Dawnguard.esm|0034BA: Forgive me, but I am on urgent business for the Dawnguard. Stand aside!
Dawnguard.esm|0034BC: Well met, friend. We've been chasing that fiend for a long time. On behalf of the Vigil, you have my thanks.
Dawnguard.esm|0034BD: It's done, then. We've been chasing that fiend for a long time.
Dawnguard.esm|0034C5: Gods, Vampires! I... I can't take this anymore! I'm not cut out for this!
Dawnguard.esm|0034C8: Look, take whatever you want. I'm getting out of here!
Dawnguard.esm|0038F6: My name is Serana. Were you... sent to wake me? Who sent you?
Dawnguard.esm|003984: A basic one? Of course. That is, assuming you know anything about steel smithing. It's pretty simple. Talk to Gunmar if you need materials.
Dawnguard.esm|003985: Only what I've heard. Back in the Second Era, the Jarl of Riften had a son. Adventurous type, too much for his own good it seems. Wound up turning into a vampire. The Jarl, unable to kill his own son, spent a fortune building this keep and hiring men to guard it. Their job was to keep the Jarl's son contained within. They took it seriously, and served honorably. They were eventually forced to put him down. The Jarl banished them from the hold, but they stuck to their cause. Fought vampires wherever they found them. I aim to continue that mission.
Dawnguard.esm|003991: Of course, I wouldn't be telling you where to find it if I desired it for myself.
Dawnguard.esm|003993: I've located the last known resting place of a set of ancient Amulets of Night Power. They should be among our kind, not laying about unguarded for mortals to find.
Dawnguard.esm|003994: I've located one of the lost ancient Amulets of Night Power. It should be among our kind, not laying about unguarded for mortals to find.
Dawnguard.esm|003995: The amulets increase the effectiveness of your vampiric powers.
Dawnguard.esm|003996: They increase the abilities of your vampiric powers.
Dawnguard.esm|003998: I've discovered the whereabouts of a set of ancient Rings of Blood Magic. They belong among our kind, not gathering mold in some vagabond's treasure pile.
Dawnguard.esm|003999: I've discovered the whereabouts of one of the ancient Rings of Blood Magic. It belongs among our kind, not gathering mold in some vagabond's treasure pile.
Dawnguard.esm|00399A: Of course. I wouldn't be telling you where to find it if I desired it for myself.
Dawnguard.esm|003A1D: Arvak, please! You can stop running now!
Dawnguard.esm|003BA0: I am Harkon, lord of this court. By now, my daughter will have told you what we are.
Dawnguard.esm|003BA2: Then you will be prey, like all mortals. I will spare your life this once, but you will be banished from this hall.
Dawnguard.esm|003BA4: There is but one gift I can give that is equal in value to the Elder Scroll and my daughter. I offer you my blood. Take it, and you will walk as a lion among sheep. Men will tremble at your approach, and you will never fear death again.
Dawnguard.esm|003BA5: Not just vampires. We are among the oldest and most powerful vampires in Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|003C11: Not your first choice, I'd guess.
Dawnguard.esm|003C12: I knew she was deep into necromancy. I mean, she taught me everything I know. But I had no idea she had a setup like this.
Dawnguard.esm|003C13: They'd be even less welcoming to her than they've been to me. It would have been a bloodbath. And since the Dawnguard are still around, that must not have happened. Any other ideas?
Dawnguard.esm|003C14: There might be, but I don't think you're going to like it. Vampires aren't counted among the living. I could probably go through there without a problem.
Dawnguard.esm|003C15: Look at this place. This has to be it!
Dawnguard.esm|003C16: Besides, I can't imagine a single place my father would avoid looking. And he's had all this time, too. Any ideas?
Dawnguard.esm|003C17: Now that I think about it... I should have expected that. Sorry. It's hard to describe. The Soul Cairn is... well, hungry, for lack of a better word. It's trying to take your life essence as payment.
Dawnguard.esm|003C18: Maybe. What I can't figure out is why she said it that way.
Dawnguard.esm|003C19: Are you alright? That looked painful.
Dawnguard.esm|003C1A: The last time I saw her, she said that she'd go somewhere safe... somewhere that my father would never search. Other than that, she wouldn't tell me anything. But the way she said it... "someplace he would never search." It was cryptic, yet she called attention to it.
Dawnguard.esm|003C1B: Are you sure? I'm willing to turn you, but you need to think it through. You'd become the very thing you've sworn to destroy. I don't know how the Dawnguard will react.
Dawnguard.esm|003C1D: It's not a question of trust... he's just obsessed with the prophecy. And from what I could tell, a thousand extra years of obsession haven't made him any better. We should have found him a hobby.
Dawnguard.esm|003C1F: I'm sorry. I wish I knew a better way, something that would be easier for you. Just know that... whatever path you choose, I won't think any less of you. Sometimes things just have to be done. I know that better than anybody.
Dawnguard.esm|003C20: My mother was meticulous about her research. If we can find her notes, there might be some hints in there.
Dawnguard.esm|003C21: Didn't want that lot getting wind of it. Especially my father.
Dawnguard.esm|003C22: Maybe. We could just "pay the toll" another way. It wants a soul, so we give it a soul. Yours.
Dawnguard.esm|003C23: Let's take a look around. There has to be something here that tells us where she's gone.
Dawnguard.esm|003C24: Let's go. My mother must be waiting on the other side of that thing.
Dawnguard.esm|003C25: Get the ingredients in the vessel and let me know when you're ready.
Dawnguard.esm|003C26: We're getting close. I'm sure of it.
Dawnguard.esm|003C27: If we had the crests, we wouldn't have to keep hanging around this lovely place.
Dawnguard.esm|003C29: Do you have a moment to talk?
Dawnguard.esm|003C2A: I'm not sure about this circle, but it's obviously... something.
Dawnguard.esm|003C3E: True. But I know a way we can get to the courtyard without arousing suspicion.
Dawnguard.esm|003D10: Yes, I can feel your defenses crumbling. You want it to end. You want to give in to me. Now, acknowledge me as your master!
Dawnguard.esm|003D12: How much longer can you keep this up, Moth Priest? Your mind was strong, but you're exhausted from the struggle.
Dawnguard.esm|003D13: I will resist you, monster. I must!
Dawnguard.esm|003D14: The more you fight me, the more you will suffer, mortal.
Dawnguard.esm|003D1B: If Isran is really asking for me, it must be serious. I'll help. Where do you need me to go?
Dawnguard.esm|003D71: Those vampires put up a hell of a fight.
Dawnguard.esm|003D72: I'm working on it, now give me some silence so I can think.
Dawnguard.esm|003D74: I don't know, maybe. Won't know until we get him out of there.
Dawnguard.esm|003D76: I just hope we weren't too late. I'm not sure what they did to this Moth Priest, but he doesn't look right.
Dawnguard.esm|003D95: Yes, how may I serve you, milady?
Dawnguard.esm|003D96: I am but your humble servant, milady.
Dawnguard.esm|003D9F: But my master is dead, and his enemies will pay!
Dawnguard.esm|003DA9: Got a pretty full stock of potions and alchemy reagents.
Dawnguard.esm|003F3F: Thank you for breaking that foul vampire's hold over me.
Dawnguard.esm|003F40: That... that wasn't me you were fighting. I could see through my eyes, but I could not control my actions.
Dawnguard.esm|003F46: Are you alright? Come on, we can do this. I know we can.
Dawnguard.esm|003FB7: They're trolls. They smell like trolls. I can't help that either.
Dawnguard.esm|003FB9: And so I'm going to give you a chance to get yourself cured.
Dawnguard.esm|003FBA: I'd also like to not have to smell troll all the time.
Dawnguard.esm|003FBB: I've only heard stories. Not sure if any of them are true. Like that they were supposed to guard some vampire here, but wound up all becoming vampires themselves when they saw how powerful he was. And then preyed on the Rift until finally someone put them down. I guess it doesn't matter now. At least Isran is trying to do something noble with the name.
Dawnguard.esm|003FBC: Since you could've attacked me before now, I'm going to assume this was a little accident on your part.
Dawnguard.esm|003FBD: I can't help that space is cramped in here, Florentius.
Dawnguard.esm|003FBE: Oh, I've read a few things. Heard a few more things. Arkay's told me a bit as well. The story they'd like you to believe is that they were such a noble bunch, doing the honorable thing and saving lives. Only I hear that's not quite how it happened. Secret organization, you see. No one to blame when homes are broken into, things stolen. Heard they'd go on midnight raids, pillaging and looting, and then stash the spoils somewhere, guarded by their sigil. Arkay tells me the Sun Fire spell can break open those caches, but I've never found one. I think he might be lying to me.
Dawnguard.esm|003FBF: And it seems we have a problem.
Dawnguard.esm|003FC0: I'd like to be able to make a potion without tasting iron flakes, you know?
Dawnguard.esm|003FC1: Sorine and Gunmar are settling in.
Dawnguard.esm|003FC3: I don't know how you do it, getting them into that pen and putting armor on them. A very special relationship, clearly.
Dawnguard.esm|003FC4: Glad to see you've taken care of your little problem, and kept it from becoming my problem.
Dawnguard.esm|003FC7: Why the trolls, of course! You seem to have quite a way with them.
Dawnguard.esm|003FC8: If you can manage that, the Dawnguard still may have use for you.
Dawnguard.esm|003FCA: Yes, they smell like trolls. But do you have to? Consider washing up a bit more often.
Dawnguard.esm|003FCC: Go to Morthal and find Falion. He's the only one I know of in Skyrim that can cure vampirism.
Dawnguard.esm|003FCD: You'd better get out of here and get yourself cured.
Dawnguard.esm|003FCE: You're lucky Isran hasn't killed you outright. Get cured while you can.
Dawnguard.esm|003FD7: Well, I think it's time I got back to my work as an alchemist. The Soul Cairn will offer a unique opportunity to continue my studies, and I intend to complete my research. In the meantime, if there are any potions you might need, feel free to help yourself.
Dawnguard.esm|003FD8: I'm sorry, I don't feel comfortable giving them to you. I understand what you did for Serana and for me, but the prophecy never sat well for me, and I don't want to be a part of it. I'm sorry.
Dawnguard.esm|004211: That letter's the last I've heard of her and mentions where she is. Now please, leave me alone.
Dawnguard.esm|004215: I knew you had it in you! Arkay wasn't so sure. Between you and me, I think he didn't expect you to make it. But not me! I knew it all along!
Dawnguard.esm|004216: Congratulations, friend. You've won a well-deserved victory here. The people of Skyrim owe you a great deal, whether they know it or not. Shall we continue the fight?
Dawnguard.esm|00421C: Arkay be with you, my friend.
Dawnguard.esm|00421D: What can I do for you?
Dawnguard.esm|00421F: That many fewer vampires to worry about. Good work.
Dawnguard.esm|004220: Isran will never admit it, but I'm sure he's proud of you.
Dawnguard.esm|004221: Good thing we stopped that prophecy, huh?
Dawnguard.esm|004222: I really do enjoy killing vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|00422E: I don't want to either, but you're going to have to jump. I'll be... right behind you. Above you. Just go.
Dawnguard.esm|00422F: Is that... oh no. Are we going down there?
Dawnguard.esm|004231: If you were anybody else, I'd probably be taking some blood from you for asking that. You wouldn't like it. But I can see this having its uses. Do you have any elven arrows on you?
Dawnguard.esm|004232: Sure... do you have any arrows, though? They would have to be of elven quality, I'd guess.
Dawnguard.esm|004233: It sounds like... that's the last bit of the prophecy. The blood of a Daughter of Coldharbour can corrupt the bow.
Dawnguard.esm|004234: I think you'll need to find some arrows, first. Only elven ones will work for this, remember?
Dawnguard.esm|004235: Let's just get this over with.
Dawnguard.esm|004236: Just... try to make it quick.
Dawnguard.esm|004237: So I am. I guess we could use my blood. Do you have any arrows? Elven arrows. I'm not going to just bleed on any old thing.
Dawnguard.esm|004238: That I am. So I'm guessing you want to just use my blood, then? Let's just get this over with. Do you have any elven arrows?
Dawnguard.esm|004239: Is that what passes for security with your lot? I can't say that I'm feeling any more confident.
Dawnguard.esm|004A07: We've got enough to worry about right now. You stay focused, and I'll worry about me.
Dawnguard.esm|004A08: If? I've been assuming that's where all this is going. I've been trying to make my peace with it.
Dawnguard.esm|004A09: Don't tell me you're that naive. Really? Oh, wow. All right, well, what do you think my father's going to do once we bring the bow to him?
Dawnguard.esm|004A0B: Even before that, we weren't the most normal family. We did worship a daedra, remember?
Dawnguard.esm|004A0C: It's not like anything else in Skyrim, I can tell you that much. From now or... before. There's probably groves like this all over Tamriel. Most people just don't even know what to look for.
Dawnguard.esm|004A0E: Do you get lonely? Are there... people in your life?
Dawnguard.esm|004A0F: Not until we have the bow. But... you're right. This won't be easy. But we can handle it.
Dawnguard.esm|004A10: You've met both of my parents. They're both crazy in their own way.
Dawnguard.esm|004A11: You've already seen my father's obsession. My mother's not a whole lot better, but you'll see that soon enough.
Dawnguard.esm|004A12: And I thought the Soul Cairn was creepy.
Dawnguard.esm|004A13: That's too bad. You deserve to be happy.
Dawnguard.esm|004A15: I think you know. We both know what's going to have to happen once we find the bow. Keep strong, and eyes open. We're on a different path now.
Dawnguard.esm|004A16: I can't say it surprises me. I kind of figured we were heading for this some day. I just didn't know when.
Dawnguard.esm|004A17: I figured it would be more of an issue for you.
Dawnguard.esm|004A1A: There's not a whole lot to tell.
Dawnguard.esm|004A1B: I'm not talking about this with you any more.
Dawnguard.esm|004A1C: You brought that up before... I think I was a little short with you.
Dawnguard.esm|004A1E: I wonder how long they've been like this.
Dawnguard.esm|004A1F: I don't know that name. You say she's Jarl of Solitude? Who supports her?
Dawnguard.esm|004A20: That's what I wanted to hear.
Dawnguard.esm|004A21: If you need some help, I'm ready.
Dawnguard.esm|004A23: No, they didn't. But that's why I like being out here with you.
Dawnguard.esm|004A24: You'll have shown yourself to be strong, which means you can't be allowed to just stick around. And I'll be unnecessary once he has it.
Dawnguard.esm|004A26: I know how that feels. I mean... I know it isn't the same thing. But I was a pretty lonely child, myself.
Dawnguard.esm|004A27: Of course it matters to you, but you don't get to make this decision for me.
Dawnguard.esm|004A28: I can see that. This has been my life for a long time, though. Do you understand that much?
Dawnguard.esm|004A29: These Falmer are... they're frozen in the ice.
Dawnguard.esm|004A2B: I've... never heard of him. Is he well supported?
Dawnguard.esm|004A2C: Of course, if you've got any more adventures planned...
Dawnguard.esm|004A2D: Well, I guess now you have one more.
Dawnguard.esm|004A2E: A little bit. That's... one of the reasons I wanted to come with you.
Dawnguard.esm|004A2F: Cyrodiil is the seat of an empire? I must have been gone longer than I thought. Definitely longer than we planned. Please, let's hurry. I need to get home so I can figure out what's happened.
Dawnguard.esm|004A30: I know you didn't know your parents. But if they were anything like mine, you're better off.
Dawnguard.esm|004A31: I think both of our parents have failed us. Just you and me against the world, now.
Dawnguard.esm|004A32: We didn't all get to have parents like yours. Sometimes this is just... how it is.
Dawnguard.esm|004A34: No, you've proven your strength to him. Which just makes you a more dangerous rival.
Dawnguard.esm|004A36: That's too bad. Do you know how they're doing now?
Dawnguard.esm|004A39: Wait, is he talking about me?
Dawnguard.esm|004A3A: Another one of those things. Let's keep our distance.
Dawnguard.esm|004A3B: Does it bother you that I'm here?
Dawnguard.esm|004A3D: Well, growing up the way I did, you get used to it.
Dawnguard.esm|004A3E: He won't need either of us anymore.
Dawnguard.esm|004A40: Oh, I'm sorry. Didn't mean to bring up a bad memory.
Dawnguard.esm|004A42: I'll speak to Falion. I've heard he knows about these things. I'm going to go away for a while. I'll meet you back home when I'm done.
Dawnguard.esm|004A56: Oh, wonderful. A war of succession. Good to know the world didn't get boring while I was gone. Who are the contenders?
Dawnguard.esm|004A57: I'll probably stay here, for as long as they'll let me. I think we can rebuild here. Make my family's legacy something more respectable.
Dawnguard.esm|004A5B: I'll probably stay with the Dawnguard, for as long as they'll let me. They're respectable fighters, and I think they see the benefits of having a vampire on their side, now.
Dawnguard.esm|004A5C: Come on, the bow has to be in this valley somewhere.
Dawnguard.esm|004A5D: I don't think you can ever be ready to kill your parent. I'm doing my best to not think of him as my father anymore.
Dawnguard.esm|004A5E: Just... leave me alone for now. I'll be fine.
Dawnguard.esm|004A61: And I thought my father was the delusional one. At that point, you'll have given him everything he's ever wanted.
Dawnguard.esm|004A63: What about you? What were your parents like?
Dawnguard.esm|004A64: So you take a major decision for me, and make it all about you? Forget it.
Dawnguard.esm|004A66: I can tell this is important to you. Would you... do you want me to be just a normal human?
Dawnguard.esm|004A67: Oh wow. This is gorgeous. I'm glad you're here with me.
Dawnguard.esm|004A69: It's like from the stories I used to hear... the two adventurers seeing the world. I never knew it would be this beautiful.
Dawnguard.esm|004A6A: This is the kind of thing I've been wanting to see. Makes everything else worth it.
Dawnguard.esm|004A6B: Never saw anything like this back on the island.
Dawnguard.esm|004A6C: It... almost doesn't even look real. You know?
Dawnguard.esm|004A6F: I wonder if the horse's remains are around here... someplace.
Dawnguard.esm|004A72: Come on. We can talk about this another time.
Dawnguard.esm|004A73: Of course he will. But at that point, he'll have everything he's ever wanted.
Dawnguard.esm|004A75: It's not that simple. I guess it never is with families, is it?
Dawnguard.esm|004A76: It would be nice not to always be so thirsty.
Dawnguard.esm|004A7D: That's it. That's the last scroll. Come on, let's take it to Dexion.
Dawnguard.esm|004A7E: Anyway, this is all very touching, but don't we have some more important things to worry about right now?
Dawnguard.esm|004A7F: Oh. I didn't even know. What kind of woman is she?
Dawnguard.esm|004A80: Oh. I didn't even know. What kind of man is he?
Dawnguard.esm|004ABA: Oh. I didn't even know. Is it a happy home?
Dawnguard.esm|004ABB: You... always say what you think, don't you? I don't like him any more than you do. But he is my father. I'm getting there. Making my peace with it.
Dawnguard.esm|004ABC: No, it's probably not. I mean, what do you think my father will do when we bring him the bow?
Dawnguard.esm|004ABD: Let's... uh... let's just keep going.
Dawnguard.esm|004ABF: My father... no, not really. I did spend a lot of time with my mother, but she saw me more like a protege than a daughter.
Dawnguard.esm|004AC1: It's fine, it can just be kind of a sore subject.
Dawnguard.esm|004AF6: Don't say I didn't warn you.
Dawnguard.esm|004AF8: I don't think that's something you want. You're a little... I just don't think you're the type.
Dawnguard.esm|004AF9: Are you sure that's something you want? This isn't like picking out a new outfit. You'll be a creature of the night, like me.
Dawnguard.esm|004B12: I'm okay with the dark, but I've spent more than my share of time in caves already.
Dawnguard.esm|004B13: It's so bright out here. I don't know how you stand it.
Dawnguard.esm|004B2B: This must be that beautiful Skyrim weather I've always heard about.
Dawnguard.esm|004B32: I'm no fan of the sun, but it would be better than this.
Dawnguard.esm|004B41: Let's get indoors. Or in a cave. Anywhere, just out of this.
Dawnguard.esm|004B63: They used to call Windhelm, "the City of Kings." In my books, anyway.
Dawnguard.esm|004B64: I had expected Ysgramor's city to be... bigger.
Dawnguard.esm|004B65: From the castle, you used to just be able to see Solitude over the mountains. It's exactly what I imagined.
Dawnguard.esm|004B66: I'd read stories about the Solitude windmill, but I didn't expect it to be that big!
Dawnguard.esm|004B67: What a curious city. It's so... open.
Dawnguard.esm|004B6A: This place makes me a little uneasy.
Dawnguard.esm|004B6B: These people seem nervous, which makes me nervous.
Dawnguard.esm|004B6C: This land feels... twisted. I'm surprised my father didn't want to live out here.
Dawnguard.esm|004B72: Rocks and valley, crevices and hiding places. This is the kind of place where hermits and fanatics dwell.
Dawnguard.esm|004B76: I've never seen leaves this color.
Dawnguard.esm|004B7A: It'll be good to get out of the sun for a while.
Dawnguard.esm|004B7B: Good, let's get inside there. The sun is starting to get to me.
Dawnguard.esm|004B7C: So this is the grand tour of Skyrim's caves that you're showing me?
Dawnguard.esm|004B83: I always wondered what the dwarves actually looked like. I hear they're like elves, but with beards.
Dawnguard.esm|004B97: I don't like the look of this...
Dawnguard.esm|004B9F: You know where to find me if you change your mind.
Dawnguard.esm|004BA0: Well, I'll be out of your hair soon enough.
Dawnguard.esm|004BA1: Yeah. Is it just you, or do you have... people in your life?
Dawnguard.esm|004C1A: You're going to go kill a Dawnguard spy. But don't draw attention. Some kills are for fun, some for profit. And others are for sending messages. This one is a message. With any luck he'll bleed to death before anyone notices. A cold corpse will say what we want better than a warm one.
Dawnguard.esm|004C2B: Well done. Nothing like a good kill to get the blood pumping, is there?
Dawnguard.esm|004CF9: Hey! Grab my journal. If you survive, you're going to need it.
Dawnguard.esm|004CFA: Here's where I fell. Feels like ages ago.
Dawnguard.esm|004D02: Not bad. Not bad at all.
Dawnguard.esm|004D05: Look, there's a chest in there. Door's sealed tight, though...
Dawnguard.esm|004D0B: Watch yourself. Since the quake, this place has been pretty unstable.
Dawnguard.esm|004DCB: I don't want to hear about it. I want what I asked for.
Dawnguard.esm|004DCD: I didn't even think you knew what a book was, my brutish friend.
Dawnguard.esm|004DCE: Ah, yes. That. There were... well, complications.
Dawnguard.esm|004DCF: And you haven't forgotten what you need to do, have you?
Dawnguard.esm|004DD1: Ronthil, where is the last batch of alchemy ingredients I asked you for?
Dawnguard.esm|004DD3: Well, well. Where've you been, Vingalmo? Nose stuck in a book all day?
Dawnguard.esm|004DD4: Very good. I'll just... I'll be going now.
Dawnguard.esm|004DD6: My dear Salonia, how are you?
Dawnguard.esm|004DD7: In the unlikely event that I ever require you for some reason, I shall find you. Until then I see no reason for us to be speaking.
Dawnguard.esm|004DD8: No, I don't need anything right now. Please stop asking.
Dawnguard.esm|004DD9: Oh, I see. I just assumed he'd sent for both of us. How awkward for you.
Dawnguard.esm|004DDA: Arkay says it's good that we're here, but I don't know... Something about this place doesn't feel right. What? No, no. Yes, I see. All right. Right. I'll settle in and then Arkay and I will start to put things right. Don't you worry.
Dawnguard.esm|004DDB: No, I just... I thought perhaps...
Dawnguard.esm|004DDC: Can I assist you? You know I live to serve.
Dawnguard.esm|004DDD: What? What meeting? I wasn't informed of any such thing!
Dawnguard.esm|004DDE: Here we are. It begins again. I fear for what is to come.
Dawnguard.esm|004DDF: I don't recall addressing you, worm.
Dawnguard.esm|004DE1: Surprised you weren't at the last meeting with our Lord, Vingalmo. Too busy plotting to overthrow him to show up?
Dawnguard.esm|004DE2: Vingalmo, is there... can I do anything for you?
Dawnguard.esm|004DE3: Do you... do you need anything, Feran?
Dawnguard.esm|004DE4: And you'd do well to be more careful of whom you engage in witty banter.
Dawnguard.esm|004DE5: Well, this isn't exactly a palace, is it? Still, it's sturdy, secure, and seems defensible. I suppose I'd rather be here than most other places in Skyrim. If Isran needs me, it must be pretty bad. I wonder what we're up against here.
Dawnguard.esm|004DE6: You realize it's not really necessary to take on that form here.
Dawnguard.esm|004DE7: Life here is better than you could find anywhere in Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|004DE8: Remember your place, and you'll be taken care of.
Dawnguard.esm|004DE9: You realize that doesn't impress anyone here.
Dawnguard.esm|004DEA: You can't trust a word that comes out of Vingalmo's mouth. Remember that.
Dawnguard.esm|004DEB: We need strength, power. Not magic. Elves don't seem to understand that.
Dawnguard.esm|004DEC: I don't converse with my meals.
Dawnguard.esm|004DED: I wish Orthjolf and Vingalmo would just get it over with and kill each other. I'm tired of listening to them.
Dawnguard.esm|004DEE: Why worry about who has the bigger chair at the table when there's more than enough to go around?
Dawnguard.esm|004DEF: Present yourself to Lord Harkon at once.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF0: I saw more than enough political maneuvering in my time in House Dres. I was tired of it then, and I'm tired of it now.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF1: I have never understood the Nord fascination with weapons. Magic is so much more versatile, don't you think?
Dawnguard.esm|004DF2: You disrespect our Lord by speaking to me instead of him.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF3: No one will soon forget that you were the one to defeat Harkon.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF4: Never made it far in the Companions, but now... I'd like to see any of them try and call me "whelp" again.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF5: Yes, Feran. Of course. I'll see to it.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF7: You'd do well to watch that tongue, elf.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF8: There's only one thing worth remembering. When it comes to vampires, if you're sloppy or careless, you're dead. And good people will die because of you. I've lasted this long because I don't take chances, I cover my tracks, and I keep my eyes open. If you're smart, you'll do the same.
Dawnguard.esm|004DF9: I knew it would come to this one day. I knew, and no one believed.
Dawnguard.esm|004DFA: Be on your guard at all times. Avoid sleep if you can... Sleep is for the weak.
Dawnguard.esm|004DFB: Even with all my preparations, we can't be too careful. The monsters could strike at us at any moment.
Dawnguard.esm|004DFC: The vampires will show us no mercy. They've proven that.
Dawnguard.esm|004DFD: If we're going to survive this, we're going to have to be as ruthless as our enemies.
Dawnguard.esm|004DFE: Hope you're getting along with Isran. He takes some getting used to.
Dawnguard.esm|004DFF: I like what Isran has done with the place. It's good to see someone else who's really prepared.
Dawnguard.esm|004E00: Very few people appreciate all the horrible things that can happen to you if you're not being cautious.
Dawnguard.esm|004E01: There are at least thirty different ways of being attacked and killed in your own home at any given moment.
Dawnguard.esm|004E02: I think we have a better chance of surviving here, don't you?
Dawnguard.esm|004E04: This is fine for now, but we can't stay holed up here forever.
Dawnguard.esm|004E05: Our sense of what's right is what separates us from the vampires. Don't forget that.
Dawnguard.esm|004E06: We can't give up. There's no one else to stand against these monsters.
Dawnguard.esm|004E07: We'll stop these monsters. We have to; we're the only ones who can.
Dawnguard.esm|004E08: Arkay's been watching over me for years now. He says he's not too sure about you yet, though.
Dawnguard.esm|004E09: I once killed thirty vampires with my bare hands, you know. Ask Arkay, he'll tell you.
Dawnguard.esm|004E0A: Don't you worry about me. I've been through worse. I'll come out of this just fine.
Dawnguard.esm|004E0B: It is my sworn duty to see Arkay's wrath delivered to all the undead. It's usually a good bit of fun, too.
Dawnguard.esm|005039: Here I am. Human as ever. It's like the world is alive again. Just like me.
Dawnguard.esm|00503B: All clean. I feel like I can breathe again for the first time since I was turned. The world is alive, and so am I, for once.
Dawnguard.esm|00503E: There you are. I'm... I'm back.
Dawnguard.esm|00503F: I'll see you when I'm done. For now, I need to just do this.
Dawnguard.esm|005043: I know you do, but this is something I need to do alone. I'll see you again. Soon.
Dawnguard.esm|0050AE: And you carried out my instructions to kill the victim in view of others and while wearing the Dawnguard uniform. Very good.
Dawnguard.esm|0050AF: But you did not follow instructions to kill the victim in view of others while wearing the Dawnguard uniform. The letter left on the corpse shall have to suffice. It will no doubt be found.
Dawnguard.esm|0050B0: I see you chose a suitably high profile target. Well done.
Dawnguard.esm|0050B1: I had hoped you would have chosen a higher profile target. But the deed is done, and it's effect secured.
Dawnguard.esm|0050B2: Very good. That should, for a time, direct the people's anger towards the Dawnguard and away from us.
Dawnguard.esm|0050B3: The Dawnguard have increased their operations and have been aggressively recruiting. We need to put a damper on their efforts and give those trouble makers something to worry about other than harassing our interests. You are to make a public show of murdering someone. Do it in the streets for all to see. The higher the profile the better. Make it seem you are a member of the Dawnguard, thus sowing confusion among the herd. Wear this armor and plant this note on the victim.
Dawnguard.esm|0050B4: Good job. At first you may pity the thin-bloods. But after disposing of a few, you realize they aren't worth the worry.
Dawnguard.esm|0050B5: A bunch of feral vampires are running wild, upsetting the mortals. When the prey is riled, it makes things difficult for us. Go cull the brutish beasts before the problem gets out of hand.
Dawnguard.esm|0056F8: Wonderful. I'll make the necessary preparations for infusion into the Chalice presently.
Dawnguard.esm|0056F9: I've located the petrified remains of an elder vampire. Bring them to me, and I should be able to extend the effects granted by the Blood Chalice.
Dawnguard.esm|0056FA: Good work. It's always best to deal with a problem by ripping it out at the root.
Dawnguard.esm|0056FB: A feral-blooded has holed up with a group of bandits. Likely wants to create a nest of broodlings.
Dawnguard.esm|0056FC: A feral-blooded has holed up with a group of necromancers. Likely wants to create a nest of broodlings.
Dawnguard.esm|005735: You know what this is? It's a lock. A 'Tonal Lock'. Simple, and very, very deadly.
Dawnguard.esm|005736: Damn things are like flies. No matter how many you kill...
Dawnguard.esm|00573B: Take good care of it for me.
Dawnguard.esm|00573D: Hold up. We... need to talk.
Dawnguard.esm|00573E: None of the others ever made it this far.
Dawnguard.esm|005740: Hey, there's my bow! There, out on the log. Wondered what happened to it.
Dawnguard.esm|005741: Have to say, I'm pretty impressed.
Dawnguard.esm|005748: Hmm... well, you can pick up where I left off. My notes should still be in my journal, if you can read it.
Dawnguard.esm|00574D: ...Yeah. I thought I was prepared for anything. How can you prepare for a damned earthquake? And that was just one trap! Look around! Who knows what else this thing is capable of?
Dawnguard.esm|00574F: Quite a place, huh? Was even more impressive before it all fell to pieces.
Dawnguard.esm|005750: See the Resonators up there? Strike them in the right order, and the doors should open. Get it wrong, and... well. You've seen what happened when I tried it.
Dawnguard.esm|005855: And how exactly do you plan on completing the prophecy without the death of my daughter?
Dawnguard.esm|005856: He doesn't know we're here. I don't have time to explain.
Dawnguard.esm|005859: Safe? You call bringing her here safe? Has she explained nothing to you?
Dawnguard.esm|00585A: Serana? What are you doing here? Where's your father?
Dawnguard.esm|00585B: The soul of a dragon is as resilient as its owner's scaly hide. It's possible that your killing blow has merely displaced Durnehviir's physical form while he reconstitutes himself.
Dawnguard.esm|00585E: Is it really you? I can't believe it! How do we get inside? We have to talk.
Dawnguard.esm|00585F: Volumes written on Durnehviir allege that he can't be slain by normal means. It appears they were mistaken. Unless...
Dawnguard.esm|005861: You. Come forward. I would speak with you.
Dawnguard.esm|005864: Forgive my astonishment, but I never thought I'd witness the death of that dragon.
Dawnguard.esm|005865: Wait a moment... you've brought a stranger here? Have you lost your mind?
Dawnguard.esm|005866: So how has it come to pass that a vampire of mixed blood is in the company of my daughter?
Dawnguard.esm|005867: So how has it come to pass that a vampire hunter is in the company of my daughter? It pains me to think you'd travel with Serana under the guise of her protector in an effort to hunt me down.
Dawnguard.esm|00586C: No, you've got it all wrong. We're here to stop him... to make everything right.
Dawnguard.esm|00586E: If you believe that, then you're a bigger fool than I originally suspected. Don't you think I weighed that option before I enacted my plans?
Dawnguard.esm|005870: I must have failed. Harkon's found a way to decipher the prophecy, hasn't he.
Dawnguard.esm|0058BC: Marriage is a fine institution, however, one that does present certain difficulties for such as like ourselves.
Dawnguard.esm|0058BD: May I strongly suggest that you make a visit to this spouse of yours and "invite" her to the family by offering her Harkon's Gift. It is the greatest token we can bestow upon those we love and wish to see protected. Put her mind to rest, allay her fear through vampiric seduction and when she is in a pliant condition, embrace her and bite her on the neck. Nature will take care of the rest.
Dawnguard.esm|0058BE: May I strongly suggest that you make a visit to this spouse of yours. And "invite" him to the family by offering him Harkon's Gift. It is the greatest token we can bestow upon those we love and wish to see protected. Put his mind to rest, allay his fear through vampiric seduction and when he is in a pliant condition, embrace him and bite him on the neck. Nature will do the rest.
Dawnguard.esm|0058BF: Do I detect a hint of reproach? I am only looking out for your best interests. And perhaps, a small measure of my own.
Dawnguard.esm|0058C0: Good. I suspected you might possess some intellect.
Dawnguard.esm|0058C1: It's come to my inescapable attention that you, my dear friend, are married.
Dawnguard.esm|0058C2: My sincerest pardons, I but look after your own peace of mind.
Dawnguard.esm|0058C3: Excellent. I trust she'll find her situation more accommodating, now that she is a member of the family.
Dawnguard.esm|0058C4: Why, you would be distraught with emotion, perhaps even to the point of questioning your loyalty to this Court. And we ours to you.
Dawnguard.esm|0058C5: I see... I... I understand. Thank you
Dawnguard.esm|0058C7: I... I feel... different. What happened?
Dawnguard.esm|0058C8: An unattended attachment, such as found in your beloved wife creates... an opportunity for unnecessary agitation. Should your blushing bride go missing, perhaps unable to bear the thought of living with a... a monster...
Dawnguard.esm|0058C9: An unattended attachment, such as found in your beloved husband creates... an opportunity for unnecessary agitation. Should your husband go missing, perhaps unable to bear the thought of living with a... a monster...
Dawnguard.esm|00593D: Now, this Chalice needs to be filled directly from the bloodspring that is the source of Redwater Den. But that's not enough. Once that's done, the blood of a powerful vampire needs to be added to it. Keep in mind that Redwater Den has fallen into... well, let's just say less reputable members of society now dwell there. It's your choice whether to attempt to deal with them or force your way through. Hurry back. You don't want to keep Lord Harkon waiting.
Dawnguard.esm|005940: Excuse us. I need to fetch the Bloodstone Chalice.
Dawnguard.esm|005941: Well, then...  Best of luck to you.
Dawnguard.esm|005942: Lord Harkon has never cared to use the Chalice, relying on his more than adequate powers until now. This is an intriguing change.
Dawnguard.esm|005943: Lord Harkon. Remember your place. Now, what is our Lord's wish?
Dawnguard.esm|005944: Indeed. He's off to Redwater Den.
Dawnguard.esm|005945: Indeed. She's off to Redwater Den.
Dawnguard.esm|005946: And I suppose you're the one Tolan says is actually doing something about this mess. These Vigilants are nothing but talk.
Dawnguard.esm|005948: Our friend here has been ordered to fill the Chalice.
Dawnguard.esm|005949: Lord Harkon's orders, Orthjolf. Calm yourself.
Dawnguard.esm|005D91: No. It'll have to run its course, and there's always the chance I may never recover.
Dawnguard.esm|005D93: I'm afraid in my haste to read the first scroll, I neglected the time I needed to prepare. The failure is purely mine to bear. As you can see from my bandages, it's not that I'm unwilling, it's that I'm physically unable.
Dawnguard.esm|005D95: I'm afraid I won't be able to help you with the scrolls any longer, milord. Please, don't be angry with me.
Dawnguard.esm|005D97: It's my fault. In my haste to read the first scroll, I neglected the careful preparation required. I thought I'd be able to allay the after effects, but I was wrong. Now I'm paying for it.
Dawnguard.esm|005D98: Normally, the study of a scroll's meaning can take months or even years. Even then, there are no guarantees. This is generally performed at the Imperial Library, but as history has shown us, that isn't always possible.
Dawnguard.esm|005D99: Scattered across Tamriel are secluded locations known only as Ancestor Glades. There's one in Skyrim, in the Pine Forest. Performing the Ritual of the Ancestor Moth within the glade should provide the answers you seek.
Dawnguard.esm|005D9B: I'm sorry, my friend. I can no longer be of use in this matter.
Dawnguard.esm|005D9C: Oh thank you, milord. Thank you!
Dawnguard.esm|005D9E: Although I would gladly die by your hand, perhaps I could offer an alternative way to read the scrolls?
Dawnguard.esm|005D9F: I can't guarantee you'd be free from harm. Becoming blind could be the least of your worries.
Dawnguard.esm|005DA0: I trust your journey was successful?
Dawnguard.esm|005DA1: It's good to see you again.
Dawnguard.esm|005DA2: Yes, how may I serve you, milord?
Dawnguard.esm|005DA3: I am but your humble servant, milord.
Dawnguard.esm|005DA4: If only such a miracle existed, I'd overcome the impossible in order to secure it if it meant pleasing you.
Dawnguard.esm|005DA5: No, there's another way. The question is, how much are you willing to risk to find Auriel's Bow?
Dawnguard.esm|005DA7: Oh please, milord. Please... forgive me!
Dawnguard.esm|005E25: Yeah, too bad. Lord Harkon's new favorite, dead so soon after joining the family.
Dawnguard.esm|005E26: I see you have returned. Lord Harkon will be pleased. You met Stalf and Salonia, I assume? Lord Harkon and I suspected they might follow you there.
Dawnguard.esm|005E27: Vingalmo and Orthjolf are Harkon's primary advisors. I'm sure they're quite surprised to learn that you're taking the Chalice.
Dawnguard.esm|005E28: It's really too bad, you know. The little accident you had here, completely unexpected...
Dawnguard.esm|005E29: Well that's just fine. Orthjolf told me to finish off anyone who got in the way.
Dawnguard.esm|005E2B: Idiot. You didn't really think I'd let you walk out of here either, did you? Vingalmo wants you both dead.
Dawnguard.esm|005E2E: Welcome to the politics of Lord Harkon's court. Vingalmo and Orthjolf both long for our Lord's throne, but cannot make overt moves against him. Each sought to gain power by using his underling to kill you and keep the Chalice for himself. By ensuring the Chalice reaches our Lord, you have increased his power over them, and at the same time deprived them of their little pets. You've done Lord Harkon two great services. Take what you've learned to heart, and be careful who you trust. Now, I believe that Lord Harkon wishes to speak with you.
Dawnguard.esm|005E2F: Wait, what? That's not what we agreed. We take it back together.
Dawnguard.esm|005E31: We're just lucky I was here to return the Chalice to Vingalmo, so he could make sure Harkon gets it back.
Dawnguard.esm|005E41: I've located the whereabouts of a vampire masquerading as a common citizen. Track him down and dispose of him. Tread lightly. The law is unaware of the danger and will treat you as a criminal if you are caught.
Dawnguard.esm|005E42: Excellent. That's one less beast and nest of broodlings we needn't worry about any longer.
Dawnguard.esm|005E43: We need to destroy him before he turns his comrades into a brood of vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|005E44: He's holed up with a group of a bandits.
Dawnguard.esm|005E45: He's holed up with a group of a necromancers.
Dawnguard.esm|005E46: I've found another beast hiding from the light of day.
Dawnguard.esm|005E47: Very good, though I suspect there's more wherever he came from. We cannot afford to relax.
Dawnguard.esm|005E48: I've discovered the whereabouts of a vampire lair. Destroying the head vampire is usually sufficient to scatter its minions, though it's best to destroy every last one of them.
Dawnguard.esm|005E49: Very good. That's one less monster we need to worry about. Though there's plenty more where he came from. Be ever watchful.
Dawnguard.esm|006533: We usually feed on our visitors, but perhaps we'll make an exception in your case.
Dawnguard.esm|006534: Yes, I did. When I told you that I was grateful for my daughter's safe return, I told the truth. But I did not tell you everything.
Dawnguard.esm|006535: This is the power that I offer! Now, make your choice!
Dawnguard.esm|006536: Have you decided? I am not a patient man.
Dawnguard.esm|00669E: Well, I certainly can't help any less than the Vigilants of Stendarr, now can I?
Dawnguard.esm|00669F: It certainly seems the Vigilants of Stendarr can't help themselves. No surprise there.
Dawnguard.esm|006946: Well... we weren't the closest of friends... Here, take this.
Dawnguard.esm|006947: Afraid my memory can't be bought with what you've got there.
Dawnguard.esm|006948: All right, calm down... Here, take this.
Dawnguard.esm|006949: You don't scare me. Let's go.
Dawnguard.esm|00694C: Like I said. I don't know what you're talking about.
Dawnguard.esm|00694D: For those who cherish memories of loved ones, their compassion often conceals the beast. Our compassion compels us to destroy it.
Dawnguard.esm|00694E: I don't know what you're talking about.
Dawnguard.esm|00694F: I see you've already taken it upon yourself to gather the information you seek.
Dawnguard.esm|006950: I've learned of a possible monster. And while I do not know its exact whereabouts, I do have information suggesting someone who does. Pay a visit to the beast's last known contact. Do what you must to find where it's hiding now. Then root out the monster, and destroy it.
Dawnguard.esm|006951: That letter's the last I've heard of him and mentions where he is. Now please, leave me alone.
Dawnguard.esm|00696A: Change it? I can remake your face, if that's what you desire.
Dawnguard.esm|00696D: Indeed. It is no small thing to submit yourself to the flesh sculptor's knife.
Dawnguard.esm|00696E: I have not always been a ragged beggar. I learned my art from the masters of the art of flesh sculpture. I studied with the Faculty of Chirurgeons in Cloudrest. I walked with the Hollow-Faced Men of Nohotogrha for three years. I count the nobility of both the Empire and the Dominion among my clients. And yet, I have fallen so low that I must justify myself to a wanderer in the sewers of this backwater of the world.
Dawnguard.esm|00696F: Hmm. Very well. Just tell me what you desire - beauty or hideous deformity, nothing is beyond my skill.
Dawnguard.esm|006970: Despite my present low estate, I am no beggar. Begone until you have the coin to warrant my skills.
Dawnguard.esm|006972: Why, are you interested in my services? I warn you, my skills do not come cheaply.
Dawnguard.esm|006973: I do not practice my art for free, however. I doubt you can afford my services. You have the smell of the vagabond about you.
Dawnguard.esm|0069A8: As you know, vampires are powerful, but we have limits. Our great enemy is the sun, and until recently it's an enemy we've had no way to fight. For centuries I searched for an answer to this problem. I found an old prophecy written by a Moth Priest, those scholars who read the Elder Scrolls. The prophecy tells of a time in which vampires will gain power over the sun, and will no longer fear its tyranny. I believe the secret to unraveling that prophecy is written in Serana's Elder Scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|0069AA: Scions of the night! Hear my words! The prophesied time is at last upon us. Soon we will claim dominion over the sun itself, and forge a new realm of eternal darkness. Now that I have reclaimed one of my Elder Scrolls, we must find a Moth Priest to read it. I have spread false rumors about the discovery of an Elder Scroll in Skyrim to lure a Moth Priest here. Now it is time to see if those efforts have borne fruit. Go forth, and search the land for rumors of a Moth Priest within our borders. Look to the cities. Speak to innkeepers, carriage drivers, anyone who would meet a traveler. Go now, and carry out this task. This is my command!
Dawnguard.esm|0069AD: Any idea how you're going to find a Moth Priest? Skyrim's a pretty big place.
Dawnguard.esm|0069AE: Excuse me, but I must address the court.
Dawnguard.esm|0069AF: I have ordered the court to assemble. I have a new task for us all to carry out, and that includes you. Come now, and hear my proclamation.
Dawnguard.esm|0069B0: I don't know yet. The Moth Priest died before finishing the transcription, but he did name the Elder Scrolls that contained the prophecy. The scroll you found with Serana is one of those scrolls of prophecy. Now that it is back within my grasp, we can read the scroll and learn more.
Dawnguard.esm|0069E6: Come on, old man. You should get some rest.
Dawnguard.esm|0069E7: Very well. I'll hurry on my way there before more of those vampires turn up.
Dawnguard.esm|0069E9: I must rest now. The reading has made me weary.
Dawnguard.esm|0069EF: You have an Elder Scroll? Remarkable! If my knowledge of history serves me, I recall that the Dawnguard was an ancient order of vampire hunters. I will be happy to assist you with your Elder Scroll. Just tell me where I need to go.
Dawnguard.esm|0069F3: Now tell me, whom do you represent, and what do you want with me?
Dawnguard.esm|006A79: Oh, most certainly! Let's find out what secrets the scroll can tell.
Dawnguard.esm|006A9B: I'm quite alright, thanks to you. Dexion Evicus is my name. I'm a Moth Priest of the White Gold Tower. These vampires claimed they had some purpose in store for me, but they wouldn't say what. Probably hoping to ransom me, the fools.
Dawnguard.esm|006A9E: It's not exactly the hospitality I'm used to, but your man Isran has seen to my needs well enough. And might I add, this is a remarkable fortress. I have colleagues back home that would love to study this place in detail.
Dawnguard.esm|006AA2: The voice fades and the words begin to shimmer and distort. But wait, there is more here. The secret of the bow's power is written elsewhere. I think there is more to the prophecy, recorded in other scrolls. Yes, I see them now... One contains the ancient secrets of the dragons, and the other speaks of the potency of ancient blood.
Dawnguard.esm|006AE1: My vision darkens, and I see no more. To know the complete prophecy, we must have the other two scrolls.
Dawnguard.esm|006AE4: Ah, my rescuer! It's good to see you again.
Dawnguard.esm|006B2C: I'm impressed you could find a Moth Priest so quickly.
Dawnguard.esm|006B84: For the reading? Yeah. Just let the old man know when you're ready.
Dawnguard.esm|006BAA: You're back and alive to boot! Fantastic.
Dawnguard.esm|006BAB: I've got more leads on additional Dwemer plans. Those last ones helped me immensely. If you could collect this one for me, it's sure to result in extraordinary success!
Dawnguard.esm|006BC2: An impressive display, but a wasted effort. You delay nothing but your own deaths!
Dawnguard.esm|006BC3: Did you really come here expecting to claim Auriel's Bow? You've done exactly as I predicted and brought your fetching companion to me. Which, I'm sorry to say, means your usefulness is at an end!
Dawnguard.esm|006F9E: You think I'd have the audacity to place my own daughter in that tomb for the protection of her Elder Scroll alone? The scrolls are merely a means to an end. The key to the Tyranny of the Sun is Serana herself.
Dawnguard.esm|006FAC: This is, or was, the epicenter of our religion. Most of the snow elf people worshipped Auri-El. The Chantry was constructed near the beginning of the First Era to provide a retreat for those that wished to become enlightened.
Dawnguard.esm|006FFC: The Chantry is quite isolated, so it took some time for word of the dwarves' offer to reach us here. By the time the compact had been completed, it was too late for us to even attempt to intervene.
Dawnguard.esm|00701D: So are you here as a client? Shall I remake your face?
Dawnguard.esm|00701E: Yes. I once practiced my art in the salons and manors of Tamriel's great and good. Now, the scum of Skyrim are my only clients. But no matter. The greatest artists are never recognized in their own time.
Dawnguard.esm|00701F: I assumed you were here to see me about your face. To have it sculpted into something more... artful than Nature has bestowed upon you.
Dawnguard.esm|007020: Hmm, I suppose I can work with your face. After all, the sculptor cannot always choose the finest clay.
Dawnguard.esm|007021: Take my advice, don't listen to that talk of the "face sculptor" down in the Ragged Flagon. "Face butcher" is more like it from what I've heard.
Dawnguard.esm|007022: People say there's someone in Riften that can... change your face. Make you look completely different. Do you believe that?
Dawnguard.esm|007063: You risked everything to get Auri-El's Bow, and in turn, you've restored the Chantry. I can't think of a more deserving champion to carry it than you. If you wish to learn more about the bow, or obtain Sunhallowed Arrows for it, I'd be more than happy to help. You've but to ask.
Dawnguard.esm|007064: We were once a wealthy and prosperous society that occupied a portion of Skyrim. Unfortunately, we were constantly at war with the Nords who claimed the land as their ancestral home.
Dawnguard.esm|0071AB: What? What are you talking about?
Dawnguard.esm|0071B2: So, the deed has been done. The restoration of this wayshrine means that Vyrthur must be dead and the Betrayed no longer have control over him.
Dawnguard.esm|0071BB: And how exactly do you plan on stopping him?
Dawnguard.esm|0071BC: If Harkon obtained Auriel's Bow and Serana's blood was used to taint the weapon, the Tyranny of the Sun would be complete. In his eyes, she'd be dying for the good of all vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|0071C5: Even with Vyrthur gone and the Inner Sanctum destroyed, my duty as a Knight-Paladin of Auri-El remains. I've been sworn to protect this vale and everything it represents until I die.
Dawnguard.esm|0076CB: If they're bold enough to attack us here, then this may be bigger than I thought. I have good men here, but... There are people I've met and worked with over the years. We need their skills, their talents, if we're going to survive this. If you can find them, we might have a chance.
Dawnguard.esm|0076CC: Well of course we do. I'm old, not stupid. We're just going to need some help.
Dawnguard.esm|0076CD: When did I say that? We just need... we need help.
Dawnguard.esm|0076CE: You'll also want to find Gunmar. Big brute of a Nord, hates vampires almost as much as I do. Got it into his head years back that his experience with animals would help. Trolls in particular, from what I hear. Last I knew he was out scouring Skyrim for more beasts to tame. Bring the two of them back here, and we can get started on coming up with a plan.
Dawnguard.esm|0076CF: I warned them. I knew something like this would happen eventually. But they didn't listen, and look where it's gotten them. This attack... it's unlike the vampires. Do we at least know what they were after, or is this completely a lost cause?
Dawnguard.esm|0076D0: Might need a little convincing, but she should.
Dawnguard.esm|0076D1: Right to the point, aren't you. I like that. Not like those fools in the order. We should keep it small. Too many people, and we'll draw unwanted attention to ourselves. I think we'll want Sorine Jurard. Breton girl, whip-smart and good with tinkering. Fascination with the Dwemer. Weapons in particular. Last I knew, she was out in the Reach, convinced she was about to find the biggest dwarven ruins yet.
Dawnguard.esm|0076E3: How dare you! I gave up everything I cared about to protect you from that fanatic you call a father!
Dawnguard.esm|0076E5: This "stranger" has done more for me in the brief time I've known him than you've done in centuries!
Dawnguard.esm|0076E6: This stranger aligns himself with those that would hunt you down and slay you like an animal, yet I should entrust you to him?
Dawnguard.esm|0076EA: One more word of warning. There's a dragon that calls itself Durnehviir roaming the Cairn. Be wary of him. The Ideal Masters have charged him with overseeing the Keepers, and will undoubtedly intervene if you're perceived as a threat.
Dawnguard.esm|0076EF: You need to locate the tallest of the rocky spires that surround these ruins. At their bases, the barrier's energy is being drawn from unfortunate souls that have been exiled here. Destroy the Keepers that are tending them, and it should bring the barrier down.
Dawnguard.esm|0076F0: Oh, Serana. If you'd only open your eyes. The moment your father discovers your role in the prophecy, that he needs your blood, you'd be in terrible danger.
Dawnguard.esm|0076F1: Your intentions are still somewhat unclear to me. But for Serana's sake, I'll assist you in any way that I can.
Dawnguard.esm|0076F2: So to protect me you decided to shut me away from everything I cared about? You never asked me if hiding me in that tomb was the best course of action, you just expected me to follow you blindly. Both of you were obsessed with your own paths. Your motivations might have been different, but in the end, I'm still just a pawn to you, too.
Dawnguard.esm|007B80: Hey... we did pretty well for ourselves here. Good work.
Dawnguard.esm|007B81: I'll take this one back to the castle. This will put my father in a great mood. You should come enjoy it while it lasts.
Dawnguard.esm|007B82: I'll set out at once, then.
Dawnguard.esm|007B83: Certainly. Where can I find this castle?
Dawnguard.esm|007B84: By the divines! It's as if my eyes have been opened! I am blinded by the light of your majesty. I... I must obey you. What would you have of me, master?
Dawnguard.esm|007C2E: Arkay has some grave news, friend.
Dawnguard.esm|007C2F: I know! Arkay told me of your success!
Dawnguard.esm|007C30: Arkay's never clear on life and death. Is she still alive? There's always hope...
Dawnguard.esm|007C31: Arkay is so hard to understand when it comes to life and death. Is he still alive? There's always hope...
Dawnguard.esm|007C32: A vicious night stalker rules over a group of bandits - seeks to gain leverage over the righteous.
Dawnguard.esm|007C33: A vicious night stalker rules over a group of necromancers - seeks to gain leverage over the righteous.
Dawnguard.esm|007C35: My love! I knew you'd come for me! I just knew it!
Dawnguard.esm|007C36: You came for me? How did you know? I thought I was going to die in here. Thank you!
Dawnguard.esm|007C37: Don't worry about me, I'll be fine!
Dawnguard.esm|007EC1: Good. Maybe now they'll afford the vampire threat a tad bit more respect, eh?
Dawnguard.esm|007EC2: The Jarl's in danger. A vampire plays as a visiting "advisor" to the court. Destroy it before it's able to complete its foul business. But be warned. The Jarl's guards are unaware of the threat, and will treat violence against this "advisor" as a crime. Convince the Jarl of the threat and he'll let you destroy the creature. If not, then do your best not to get caught. Quickly now! The longer you delay, the more danger the Jarl is in.
Dawnguard.esm|007EC3: That's quite a bold claim. I'd need proof before I could possibly accept it.
Dawnguard.esm|007EC4: Well... I trust you'll take care of this unwanted visitor. My guards will not interfere with you carrying out my justice.
Dawnguard.esm|008386: Yes. Please, follow me. Keep watch for Durnehviir. With the prison's barrier down, he's almost certain to investigate.
Dawnguard.esm|008387: You managed to destroy all three Keepers? Very impressive.
Dawnguard.esm|008388: The sooner we get the scroll home, the sooner we deal with my father.
Dawnguard.esm|008389: I'd heard stories about the Soul Cairn, but never thought I'd see it myself. So far it's... about what I imagined.
Dawnguard.esm|00838A: Let's keep moving. I feel like we're being watched.
Dawnguard.esm|00838B: I feel terrible for the dead who end up here for eternity. Seems awful. Like they're still suffering.
Dawnguard.esm|00838C: You'd think a vampire would be right at home in this place. You'd be wrong. Let's just find my mother and get out of here, quickly.
Dawnguard.esm|00838D: Look at the sky. What kind of a place is this?
Dawnguard.esm|00838E: The air, the ground... this is all wrong.
Dawnguard.esm|0083FE: The only way I'd even consider returning to Tamriel was if Harkon were not only to fail, but to be destroyed.
Dawnguard.esm|0083FF: Like Serana, I am a Daughter of Coldharbour, an vampire offspring of Molag Bal. My presence in Tamriel would simply double the risk that Harkon could complete the prophecy.
Dawnguard.esm|008400: Even if I could, it would only serve to hinder your efforts. Harkon would likely sense my return to Tamriel. The last thing you need right now is a trail for him to follow.
Dawnguard.esm|008401: Did you have anything to ask before you depart?
Dawnguard.esm|008403: Idiot! I can't get to the scroll with Durnehviir attacking us!
Dawnguard.esm|008405: If you want that scroll, the barrier needs to come down.
Dawnguard.esm|008406: You must understand that he's no longer the man I married all that time ago. He's obsessed... insane. Everyone around him fears him as they would a rabid dog. For all of this to end, he must die.
Dawnguard.esm|00846E: In case you haven't noticed, Isran is always concerned about something. That's what got us working together in the first place. Still, if he sent you to find me, it may actually be pretty bad.
Dawnguard.esm|00846F: I... Well, that's actually something I never would've anticipated. Interesting. I'm not sure what they would do with one, but in this case Isran is probably correct in thinking it isn't good.
Dawnguard.esm|008470: Vampires? Really? Oh, and I suppose now he remembers that I proposed no less than three different scenarios that involved vampires overrunning the population. Well, what are they up to?
Dawnguard.esm|008471: By the Eight... All right, look. I'll consider it, but I can't just leave this bear to prey on more innocent people. Once it's dealt with, then perhaps I'll see what Isran expects of me.
Dawnguard.esm|008472: Yes, that was my point. I felt it was rather clear. Look, what is it you want from me?
Dawnguard.esm|008473: Vampires? That... well, that might change things. Tell me more about what's going on.
Dawnguard.esm|008474: Isran? Wants me? No, you must be mistaken. He made it exceedingly clear the last time we spoke that he had no interest in my help. I find it hard to believe he's changed his mind. He said some very hurtful things to me before I left. Anyway, I'm quite happy in my current pursuits. So if you'll excuse me...
Dawnguard.esm|008475: Isran? Needing someone else's help? Never thought I'd hear that. I'm afraid he's a few years too late. I've moved on. I have more important business to attend to. Besides, he can handle anything alone! He assured me so himself. What could he possibly need my help with?
Dawnguard.esm|008476: You haven't seen a sack full of dwarven gyros lying around, have you? I'd swear I left it right here. Do you think mudcrabs might've taken it? I saw one the other day... Wouldn't be surprised if it followed me here. Just look around, will you?
Dawnguard.esm|008477: You there, hold fast! I've tracked this damned bear for two weeks; I'll not let it have any more victims.
Dawnguard.esm|008479: Ah. Been working more on his secret hideout, has he? It'll be interesting to see how much progress he's made. I'll finish up here and head in that direction soon as I can. See you there.
Dawnguard.esm|00847A: All right. If nothing else, I suppose it wouldn't hurt to learn more about what's going on so I can better defend myself. But I'm not just going to abandon what I've been working on here. It's too useful. I need at least one intact dwarven gyro. So either I need to find the satchel those mudcrabs stole, or I need another gyro from someplace. You wouldn't happen to have one, would you?
Dawnguard.esm|008858: My traitor wife stole one of them away and then disappeared. As for the other, the last that I heard, it was lost in the bowels of a Dwemer ruin. It seems our work is not yet done. But I have waited this long, and we are so very close now. I can wait a bit longer.
Dawnguard.esm|008859: Master, I have done as you asked and traveled here.
Dawnguard.esm|00885F: Well, your thrall awaits and we have given him the Elder Scroll. Command him to read it, and let us hear the words of prophecy together.
Dawnguard.esm|008860: Now, if everyone will please be quiet, I must concentrate. I see a vision before me, an image of a great bow. I know this weapon! It is Auriel's Bow! Now a voice whispers, saying "Among the night's children, a dread lord will rise." In an age of strife, when dragons return to the realm of men, darkness will mingle with light and the night and day will be as one.
Dawnguard.esm|008861: Yes, I see that. I trust his capture was not too difficult a task?
Dawnguard.esm|008863: Ah, yes, the Elder Scroll! I admit, I'm looking forward to this.
Dawnguard.esm|008864: Of course. What is it that you need me to do?
Dawnguard.esm|008866: Well done! Somehow I knew it would be you and Serana who found our moth priest.
Dawnguard.esm|008868: That was... not as useful as I would have liked. Even so, you did well.
Dawnguard.esm|008869: What are you waiting for? Get out there and find a moth priest!
Dawnguard.esm|00886A: I think my father wanted to see you. Have you talked to him yet?
Dawnguard.esm|0088F4: He's been working on that place for years now. Never lets anyone in. His own little fortress. Well, I guess I'll get to see what he's been up to all this time. I'll meet you there.
Dawnguard.esm|0088F5: If Isran is anything, he's stubborn.
Dawnguard.esm|0088F7: Don't know how well I'd have managed by myself. You have my thanks. You've helped me, so I suppose the least I can do is find out what Isran wants. He's still at that fort near Stendarr's Beacon, I assume?
Dawnguard.esm|0088F8: Watch yourself. This bear is vicious.
Dawnguard.esm|0088F9: We need to stop this creature before it kills again.
Dawnguard.esm|008A7B: Look, I've just gotten myself out of quite a mess here, in case you haven't noticed, and while I appreciate your help, I... What's that? No, that's not what I...  Yes, but... Are you sure? Really? Fine. Arkay says it's a good idea for me to go. I don't agree, but he's not the sort of fellow you can just ignore. I'll see you at Fort Dawnguard, then. Don't worry, Arkay will show me the way.
Dawnguard.esm|008A7C: Isran? My help? Is this... some kind of a joke? Did Arkay put you up to this? Isran's done nothing but mock me. He's never given me the respect I deserve.
Dawnguard.esm|008A7D: I suppose I could... what, praytell, is there?
Dawnguard.esm|008A7E: I knew it! I knew Arkay would save me. I asked for help, and he sent you! You are a very welcome addition to this dreary place, my friend. I owe both you and Arkay a great deal. I'm sure I'll manage to repay him later, but you... What can I do to thank you?
Dawnguard.esm|008A7F: This is all well and good, but do we actually know anything about what they're doing? What do we do now?
Dawnguard.esm|008A81: All right Isran, you've got us all here. Now what do you want?
Dawnguard.esm|008A82: If anyone is going to stand in their way, it's going to be us.
Dawnguard.esm|008A83: And they have an Elder Scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|008A84: I'm sure you've heard a bit of what we're up against. Powerful vampires, unlike anything we've seen before.
Dawnguard.esm|008B23: Greetings, master. What do you need?
Dawnguard.esm|0098B3: Do you know where he's staying now?
Dawnguard.esm|0098B4: Why, because of that story about the prophecy? About some vampire trying to put the sun out? Do you actually believe any of that?
Dawnguard.esm|0098B5: Some Imperial scholar arrived in Skyrim a few days ago. I was staking out the road when I saw him pass by. Maybe that's your Moth Priest.
Dawnguard.esm|0098B6: Well, let's move then. I'm nothing if not persuasive.
Dawnguard.esm|0098B7: Well, the Moth Priests are the only ones I've heard of who can do it. They spend years preparing before they start reading, though. Not that it helps us anyway, because they're all half a continent away in Cyrodiil.
Dawnguard.esm|0098B8: So, in case you didn't notice the giant thing on my back, I have the Elder Scroll with me. Whatever it says, it will have something that can help us stop my father. But of course, neither of us can read it.
Dawnguard.esm|0098B9: It's pointless and vague, like all prophecies. The part he latched onto said that vampires would no longer need to fear the sun. That's what he's after. He wants to control the sun, have vampires control the world.
Dawnguard.esm|0098BA: It all comes back to my father. I'm guessing you figured this part out already, but my father's not exactly a good person. Even by vampire standards. He wasn't always like that, though. There was... a turn. He stumbled onto this obscure prophecy and just kind of lost himself in it.
Dawnguard.esm|0098BB: Thank you for your kindness. I'll remember it the next time I'm feeling hungry.
Dawnguard.esm|0098BD: You hear me? Don't feel like a guest, because you're not. You're a resource. You're an asset. In the meantime, don't make me regret my sudden outburst of tolerance and generosity, because if you do, your friend here is going to pay for it.
Dawnguard.esm|0098BE: I'd rather not be here either, but I needed to talk to you. It's important, so please just listen before your friend, here, loses his patience. It's... well, it's about me. And the Elder Scroll that was buried with me.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C0: A Moth Priest? What in Oblivion do you need a Moth Priest for?
Dawnguard.esm|0098C1: Fine, fine. No need to get your breeches in a twist. The obvious answer is to go to the Imperial City. The Moth Priests make their home in the White Gold Tower. Sometimes they go out looking for Elder Scrolls. Lucky for you, there's a Moth Priest in Skyrim right now, doing just that. He stopped in to do some research in the library, then left for Dragon Bridge. If you hurry, you might catch him there.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C2: Have I heard anything? It was the talk of the city for three days straight. Ain't too often we get a visitor like that.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C3: Oh, he just left a short while ago. He said he'd finished up his work and it was time to move on. That's all I can tell you. He asked me not to let anyone know where he was going. I suppose he didn't want the attention.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C4: Ah, well, in that case I'll tell you what I know. He took the road southwest out of town, and said he was going to be crossing over the Dragon Bridge. That's all that he told me. I hope it helps.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C5: Actually, now that I think of it... I'm going to come along with you. I've been really wanting to get out and explore a bit.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C7: No, and I'm not going to waste men looking. We're fighting a war against your kind, and I intend to win it. You want to find him, try talking to anyone who'd meet a traveler. Innkeepers and carriage drivers in the big cities maybe. But you're on your own.
Dawnguard.esm|0098C8: It can stay for now, but if it so much as lays a finger on anyone here, I'll hold you responsible. Got it?
Dawnguard.esm|0098C9: You probably weren't expecting to see me again.
Dawnguard.esm|0098D8: Hold it. If you're going downstairs, you keep your weapons to yourself or we'll be getting better acquainted.
Dawnguard.esm|0098D9: Head on in and behave yourself.
Dawnguard.esm|00A290: I have found such a soul and the stars appear cooperative.
Dawnguard.esm|00A294: While the blessed state of undeath can be "accidentally" conferred - consider the brutish creatures you find wailing in dank hovels in the wild... Only those who can trace their ancestry through Harkon's mortal blood line can faithfully accept the power conferred by his Vampiric blood. And among those, it seems, only when the heavens show the correct aspects.
Dawnguard.esm|00A295: Because he himself bestowed upon you a portion of his vitality when he infused you with his own blood the night you returned with Serana. A small measure of his blood now and forever flows through your veins.
Dawnguard.esm|00A296: Put his mind to rest, allay his fear through vampiric seduction and when he is in a pliant condition, embrace him and bite him on the neck.
Dawnguard.esm|00A297: Put her mind to rest, allay her fear through vampiric seduction and when she is in a pliant condition, embrace her and bite her on the neck.
