Dawnguard.esm|00A299: When you offer the embrace, do so away from prying eyes. Our new sibling will then likely require rest while his blood undergoes the change. Return to me when your part is done.
Dawnguard.esm|00A29A: When you offer the embrace, do so away from prying eyes. Our new sibling will then likely require rest while her blood undergoes the change. Return to me when your part is done.
Dawnguard.esm|00A29B: I've found a new candidate, ripe for Harkon's Gift. I'm sending you to welcome this soul into our family.
Dawnguard.esm|00A36F: Well, well. He wants the Chalice, then. You've heard of the Bloodstone Chalice?
Dawnguard.esm|00A85B: This structure is known as a wayshrine. They were used for meditation and for transport when the Chantry was a place of enlightenment.
Dawnguard.esm|00A85E: Leaving the wayshrines unguarded would be violating my sacred duty as a Knight-Paladin of Auriel. And an assault on the Betrayed guarding the Inner Sanctum would only end with my death.
Dawnguard.esm|00A85F: For the thousands of years I've served as the Chantry's sentinel, there hasn't been a single visitor here for any other reason. They request Auriel's Bow, and I request their assistance. It's been repeated so many times, I can't imagine it any other way.
Dawnguard.esm|00A861: You've nothing to fear from me. Please, we have much to discuss.
Dawnguard.esm|00A864: This may be the last time we're able to converse. If you have any questions before you leave, I suggest you ask them. Otherwise, all I can do now is grant you my hopes for a safe journey.
Dawnguard.esm|00A866: He's alive. I've seen him. But something's wrong. He never looks as though he's in pain or under duress. He just... stands there and watches, as though waiting.
Dawnguard.esm|00A868: Once you've located a wayshrine, there will be a spectral Prelate tending to it. They will allow you to draw the waters from the shrine's basin as if you've been enlightened.
Dawnguard.esm|00A869: I know how it all sounds, but if there was another way I'd have done it long ago.
Dawnguard.esm|00A86A: The Chantry was a place of peaceful worship. I led a small group of paladins, but we were no match for the Betrayed's sheer numbers. They slaughtered everyone and stormed the Inner Sanctum where I believe they corrupted Vyrthur.
Dawnguard.esm|00A86B: I prefer snow elf. The name "Falmer" usually holds a negative meaning to most travelers. Those twisted creatures you call Falmer, I call the Betrayed.
Dawnguard.esm|00A86C: Caves? Oh, no. The Chantry encompasses far more than a few caves, as you'll soon discover. But before I send you on your way, you'll need the Initiate's Ewer.
Dawnguard.esm|00A86D: Well, once the Initiate's enlightenment was complete, he'd bring the ewer to the Chantry's Inner Sanctum. Pouring the contents of the ewer into the sacred basin of the Sanctum would allow him to enter for an audience with the Arch-Curate himself.
Dawnguard.esm|00A86E: Absolutely. You could turn around and travel back from wherever you started empty handed, or you could assist me.
Dawnguard.esm|00A86F: There are five in total, spread far apart across the Chantry.
Dawnguard.esm|00A870: Once the Initiate completed his mantras, he'd dip a ceremonial ewer in the basin at the wayshrine's center and proceed to the next wayshrine.
Dawnguard.esm|00A871: I need you to kill Arch-Curate Vyrthur... my brother.
Dawnguard.esm|00A872: Auriel, Auri-El, Alkosh, Akatosh... so many different names for the sovereign of the snow elves.
Dawnguard.esm|00A874: Prelates of these shrines were charged with teaching the mantras of Auri-El to our Initiates.
Dawnguard.esm|00A875: The kinship between us is gone. I don't understand what he's become, but he's no longer the brother I once knew. It was the Betrayed... they did something to him, I just don't know why Auri-El would allow this to happen.
Dawnguard.esm|00A876: I am Knight-Paladin Gelebor. Welcome to the Great Chantry of Auri-El.
Dawnguard.esm|00B1B6: An ancient font, full of power. As much as it looks like blood, sadly it is not. Can you imagine if it were? We'd never need to hunt again. Still, its liquid is powerful, full of magic. Others have noticed, though they have never fully realized its potential.
Dawnguard.esm|00B4C4: All right, you've heard what it has to say. Now tell me, is there any reason I shouldn't kill this bloodsucking fiend right now?
Dawnguard.esm|00B53D: I never knew the darkness held such colors, or the night air smelled so sweet. You have given me a great gift. Please, make use of my coffin whenever the need arises.
Dawnguard.esm|00B667: Good question. Hard to say. I... I can't really tell. I feel like it was a long time. Who is Skyrim's High King?
Dawnguard.esm|00B668: Unh... where is... who sent you here?
Dawnguard.esm|00B66B: Your lack of planning fills me with confidence.
Dawnguard.esm|00B66C: It appears I have you to thank for turning my daughter against me. I knew it was only a matter of time before she'd return with hatred in her heart.
Dawnguard.esm|00B66D: I didn't want you to think I was one of those... you know, the women who just sit in their castle all day? I don't know. Coming from a place like this, well... it's not really me. I hope you can believe that.
Dawnguard.esm|00B66E: Figures I'd be woken by a couple of know-nothings. So where are we off to, now?
Dawnguard.esm|00B66F: My father and I don't really get along. Ugh, saying it out loud makes it sound so... common. "Little girl who doesn't get along with her father." Read that story a hundred times.
Dawnguard.esm|00B670: And instead you found me. So what's your next step, now that I'm here?
Dawnguard.esm|00B671: Let's just say that my mother and father had a bit of a falling out. Don't worry, I'm not in any danger or anything like that. It'll just be more unpleasant to run into my father.
Dawnguard.esm|00B672: You're lucky to be alive, then. I'm guessing you don't have a plan for where to go next, then?
Dawnguard.esm|00B673: I see. Where are you taking me, then? Is that much my business?
Dawnguard.esm|00B674: It's on an island near Solitude. Hopefully we can find a boat to take us there. It's my family home. Not the most welcoming place, but depending on who's around, I'll be safe there.
Dawnguard.esm|00B675: This is it. Home sweet... castle.
Dawnguard.esm|00B676: I... don't know who that is. Did he... mention my father at all?
Dawnguard.esm|00B677: I'd... rather not get into that with you. If that's all right. I'm sorry, it's not that... it's just that I don't know who I can trust just yet. Let's get to my home, and I'll have a better sense of where we all stand.
Dawnguard.esm|00B678: That something... was probably me. My name is Serana. Who sent you here?
Dawnguard.esm|00BDDB: That Moth Priest, Dexion. He said we needed two other Elder Scrolls. I think I know where we can start looking.
Dawnguard.esm|00BDDC: Well, you could waste a lot of time trying to figure out our next move, or you could let me help. Your choice.
Dawnguard.esm|00BDEC: Years of mocking, of dismissal... and now suddenly here you are, begging for help.
Dawnguard.esm|00BDED: Yes, you've explained yourself. I'm supposed to just throw myself in front of you all and offer protection, am I?
Dawnguard.esm|00BDEE: Isran, these vampires are different. I know you've disagreed with the Order in the past...
Dawnguard.esm|00BDEF: Why not just scream at the top of your lungs "Here we are, monsters! We're oh so tasty! Come and get us!"
Dawnguard.esm|00BDF0: Look at these fools, so concerned with tending their precious "beacon."
Dawnguard.esm|00BDF1: Well what did you expect, Tolan? Didn't I tell you? Didn't I warn you this would happen?
Dawnguard.esm|00C218: Half the people in your little crew would just as soon kill me as talk to me. That doesn't exactly make me want to open up. I got a warmer welcome from my father, and that's saying something.
Dawnguard.esm|00C616: There's an unused inlet on the northern side of the island that was used by the previous owners to bring supplies into the castle. An old escape tunnel from the castle exits there. I think that's our way in.
Dawnguard.esm|00C617: Wait... that almost makes sense! There's a courtyard in the castle. I used to help her tend a garden there. All of the ingredients for our potions came from there. She used to say that my father couldn't stand the place. Too... peaceful.
Dawnguard.esm|00C618: I don't think so. She said she wanted to stay awake in case the situation was resolved. It had to be one of us, and, well, she's so much more powerful than I am. It just made sense for her to be out here. Anything else?
Dawnguard.esm|00C619: We need to find my mother, Valerica. She'll definitely know where it is, and if we're lucky, she actually has it herself.
Dawnguard.esm|00C61A: Trust me. I lived here for a very long time and I know every nook and cranny.
Dawnguard.esm|00C61B: Oh, absolutely. But my mother's not a coward. That is... I don't think we'll actually trip over her there. But it's worth a look.
Dawnguard.esm|00C705: You have kept your side of the bargain, now let me fulfill mine.
Dawnguard.esm|00C706: I can feel my strength returning each time I am set free from that prison!
Dawnguard.esm|00C707: Ah! The free air of Vus at long last!
Dawnguard.esm|00C708: I rejoice in my freedom, if only for a moment.
Dawnguard.esm|00C709: The skies of Keizaal are mine again!
Dawnguard.esm|00C70A: You give me freedom from the enslavement of the Ideal Masters. Thank you.
Dawnguard.esm|00C70B: Feel the last word of Soul Tearing within yourself: "Zol" - the unlife that results when a soul is imprisoned in dead flesh.
Dawnguard.esm|00C70C: Hear now the second word of Soul Tearing: "Vaaz"- meaning to tear your enemy's soul from its living vessel.
Dawnguard.esm|00C70D: As promised, I now teach you the first word of Soul Tearing: "Rii" - the essence of your enemy's life force.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCC3: A handful of soul gem shards, some finely-ground bone meal, a good bit of purified void salts... Oh... damn it...
Dawnguard.esm|00CCC4: That circle in the center of the room is definitely some type of portal. If I'm reading this right, there's a formula here that should give us safe passage into the Soul Cairn.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCC5: The Soul Cairn is home to very powerful beings. Necromancers send them souls, and receive powers of their own in return. My mother spent a lot of time trying to contact them directly, to travel to the Soul Cairn itself.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCC6: I only know what she told me. She had a theory about soul gems. That the souls inside of them don't just vanish when they're used... they end up in the Soul Cairn.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCC7: You did? Let me see them.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCC9: Look at all this. She must have spent years collecting these components.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCCA: Hmmm. Not bad. We'd better hope that's good enough. Mistakes with these kinds of portals can be... gruesome. Anyway, enough of that. Let's get started.
Dawnguard.esm|00CCCB: We're also going to need a sample of her blood. Which... if we could get that, we wouldn't even be trying to do this in the first place.
Dawnguard.esm|00CD57: You are prey, like all mortals. I banish you!
Dawnguard.esm|00CDF8: Of course. I wouldn't be telling you where to find them if I desired them for myself.
Dawnguard.esm|00CDF9: Of course, I wouldn't be telling you where to find them if I desired them for myself.
Dawnguard.esm|00CDFA: Depends on who you ask. Some say Molag-Bal himself gave these amulets to ancient powerful vampire lords. Some say this is just a myth. Harkon disbelieved in their existence, though perhaps he felt slighted they were not already in his possession and only feigns indifference.
Dawnguard.esm|00CDFD: Well done! I trust you'll put those to good use then.
Dawnguard.esm|00CE00: Excellent! I'm sure you'll put those to good use.
Dawnguard.esm|00CE01: Depends on who you ask. Some say Molag-Bal himself gave these rings to ancient powerful vampire lords. Some say that is just a myth. Harkon himself disbelieved in their existence, though perhaps he felt slighted they were not already in his possession and only feigns indifference.
Dawnguard.esm|00CE02: Very good. Should you visit him again, I suspect you'll find our new sibling appreciates his transformation.
Dawnguard.esm|00CE03: Very good. Should you visit her again, I suspect you'll find our new sibling appreciates her transformation.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF41: ...this is where I ended up. It was... quite the fall.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF42: The name's Katria. I am - was - an adventurer. Raided ruins like this for nigh on twenty years. I was on the trail of something big. It led me here, and... I didn't make it.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF44: By forging something, of course! Go ahead. After everything you've done, you should do the honors.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF45: I had a map, in my journal. That's where we should start.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF46: See if you can find a pattern in the sequence. Most of these locks have a pattern of some kind.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF48: Oh. You read the book, didn't you? "The Aetherium Wars"? That was my theory, you know. My research. My life's work. All of it, lost! Stolen by my own damn apprentice! That's how I ended up here. I can't rest. Not until I find the Forge, until I can prove that it was my discovery. Mine, not his!
Dawnguard.esm|00CF4A: What are you still doing here? Let me guess. You're here for the treasure, aren't you? Just like all the others.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF4E: I... I almost can't believe it. We did it! We actually did it! There's only one thing left to do. We have to prove this actually works. That this is the real Aetherium Forge.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF4F: We have to find the other pieces, of course. There should be, hmm... three more. One for each of the four cities that worked on the Forge.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF50: You might look around a bit. Maybe one of our predecessors still has a clue.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF52: Now you decide to give up? Fine. I'll be here.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF58: I can hardly believe it. This is it. We've actually found the Forge! At last!
Dawnguard.esm|00CF5A: You could always go back and get my journal, you know.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF5E: My life's work. Unraveling one of the great mysteries of the dwarves.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF5F: Would you want me to? After what happened the last time I tried it? I'll fight anything that can be fought. Beyond that... I don't want your death on my conscience.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF60: Well, my notes were in my journal, but you didn't grab that, did you? Hmph.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF61: I'm not going to talk you out of it, am I? I know, I know. I was just like you, once. Well, if you want to reach the summit, you're going to need some help. You... want a hand?
Dawnguard.esm|00CF62: The air here... it feels different. Almost like...
Dawnguard.esm|00CF64: Let me see it. ...Huh... Look, on the edge here. This has been cut, precisely cut. If you had another piece, about the same size, it would... it would snap right in. I saw a drawing of this once. This shard... it's... it's part of a key. A key made of pure Aetherium! The key to the Forge!
Dawnguard.esm|00CF65: Beyond that... well, maybe one of our predecessors still has a clue.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF6B: To think... no one's been here in four thousand years...
Dawnguard.esm|00CF6C: Go ahead, give it a try.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF77: It's... it's true. It's all true! Come on, let's check it out!
Dawnguard.esm|00CF78: Look at the device here - the gear in the center is just about the right size. Try putting the shards in and... we'll see what happens.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF7B: You ever heard of Aetherium? A rare mineral used by the dwarves. Supposedly, they had to build a special forge, the Aetherium Forge, to even work with it. The items it made were so powerful they went to war over it, and the Forge was lost. Or so the story goes.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF7C: Well, there's the Falmer, to start with. Deadly rapids. Major earthquakes. Massive chasms. Unstable ground. Lethal falls. And then you get to the real danger. We can discuss that when the time comes. If it comes.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF7D: Hmph... maybe so. Then again, I thought I could, too.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF7E: Sure, that's what they all say.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF87: You made it! And you've brought the shards!
Dawnguard.esm|00CF88: If all else fails, just take a chance, see what happens. Wouldn't be surprised if half the traps had been sprung by now...
Dawnguard.esm|00CF89: Grab my journal, would you? We're going to need it.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF8A: It wasn't always like this. Best-preserved dwarven city I'd ever laid eyes on. And then... the earthquake hit. Now... you'd be lucky to find a single room intact.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF9B: Beautiful. That crown... it's everything I could have hoped for.
Dawnguard.esm|00CF9C: We still have to find the Forge itself. I had a lead on that, too. There's so much to do. I'm going to head out and start tracking things down. We'll meet again, I'm sure of it. For the first time in a long while, I think I - we - may actually be able to do this. And... and I owe it all to you. Thank you.
Dawnguard.esm|00D057: Heard they're reforming the Dawnguard. Vampire hunters or something, in the old fort near Riften. Might consider joining up myself.
Dawnguard.esm|00D058: They say that vampires attacked the Hall of the Vigilant. Burned it to the ground! Never heard of vampires doing anything like that before.
Dawnguard.esm|00D069: Brother Adalvald had been there not long ago. Said he found something of great importance. It must have been important to the vampires as well, for them to risk an open attack on the Vigilants.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3D9: She wasn't, always. But I think you're right. She was practically smirking as we left home. Almost like she was proud of herself. Like she didn't want to just stop my father... she wanted to stick it to him, too.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3DA: She actually did it... created a portal to the Soul Cairn. Incredible.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3DB: Neither do I. She always seemed happy, before we heard the prophecy. Then it all changed. She became a different person. They both did.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3DC: By the blood of my ancestors...
Dawnguard.esm|00D3DD: Possibly. I guess even a vampire mother is still a mother. She worried about me. About all of us. But she wanted to get me as far away from my father as possible before he really went over the edge.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3DF: I've been asking myself the same thing since we came back to the castle. She was so sure of what we did to my father, I couldn't help but go along with her. I never thought of the cost.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3E0: I promise to try and make this as painless as possible. Hold still.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3E8: Let's not waste too much time, then.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3EA: Turning someone is a very... personal thing for vampires. It's intimate. For us. I don't want you to feel like I'm forcing you into this.
Dawnguard.esm|00D3EB: Yes... yes, you're right. I'm sorry. I just didn't expect anyone to care how I felt about her. Thank you. Are we ready then?
Dawnguard.esm|00D3EC: Then the rest is up to me. Are you ready to go? I'm not entirely sure what this thing is going to do when I add my blood.
Dawnguard.esm|00D673: Auri-El himself may have been beyond my reach, but his influence on our world wasn't. All I needed was the blood of a vampire and his own weapon, Auriel's Bow.
Dawnguard.esm|00D674: You want to take revenge... on a god?
Dawnguard.esm|00D675: Enough, Vyrthur. Give us the bow!
Dawnguard.esm|00D676: The moment I was infected by one of my own Initiates, Auri-El turned his back on me. I swore I'd have my revenge, no matter what the cost.
Dawnguard.esm|00D677: You're... you're a vampire? But Auriel should have protected you...
Dawnguard.esm|00D678: Gelebor and his kind are easily manipulated fools. Look into my eyes, Serana. You tell me what I am.
Dawnguard.esm|00D679: You were waiting... all this time for someone with my blood to come along. Well, too bad for you... I intend on keeping it. Let's see if your blood has any power to it!
Dawnguard.esm|00D67A: Until the "Betrayed" corrupted you. Yes, yes. We've heard this sad story.
Dawnguard.esm|00D67B: A prophecy that lacked a single, final ingredient... the blood of a pure vampire. The blood of a Daughter of Coldharbour.
Dawnguard.esm|00D67C: How dare you. I was the Arch-Curate of Auri-El, girl. I had the ears of a god!
Dawnguard.esm|00D67D: The blood of a vampire... Auriel's Bow... It... it was you? You created that prophecy?
Dawnguard.esm|00D7D8: Everything's been torn down... the whole place looks... well, dead. It's like we're the first to set foot here in centuries.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7D9: She had to be up to something in that garden. I'm hoping it's a clue that will tell us where she went.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7DA: We've made it to the courtyard. Oh no...
Dawnguard.esm|00D7DB: It was very sudden. It was almost like one day we were a normal family, and then the next I didn't know who they were. I'd try to visit my mother in the garden, and she'd quickly shoo me away saying she was much too busy.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7DC: Well, at least we're getting closer. Let's go.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7DD: Like the best of friends. I would never hesitate to share anything with her.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7DE: I've never been in those tunnels before, but I'd bet they run right under the courtyard and into the tower ruins.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7DF: I used to walk through here after evening meals. It was beautiful, once.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7E0: Before my father became obsessed with the prophecy, mother and I spent quite a bit of time together. She was very fond of her alchemical garden in the castle courtyard. She taught me quite a bit about cultivating quality reagents.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7E3: Something's wrong with the moondial here. Some of the crests are missing and the dial is askew. I didn't even know the crests could be removed. Maybe my mother's trying to tell us something?
Dawnguard.esm|00D7E4: It appears that way. I suppose he wanted to put the past behind him. Perhaps if he had spent more time with us, he would have recognized the beauty for himself.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7E5: This was my mother's garden. It... do you know how beautiful something can be when it's tended by a master for hundreds of years? She would have hated to see it like this.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7E6: This used to lead into the castle's great hall. It looks like my father had it sealed up.
Dawnguard.esm|00D7E7: If I had to guess, I'd say the moment mother fled the castle, father went on a rampage. Knowing him, anything at all that reminded him of her was just destroyed.
Dawnguard.esm|00D87C: But now... we have a clue. Something made by the Forge. Something that can lead us to it.
Dawnguard.esm|00D886: I had no idea that her laboratory even existed. She had an alchemy setup in her drawing room, but nothing that even comes close to what's here.
Dawnguard.esm|00D887: Because most of the stories end with the Ideal Masters duping the necromancers, who end up dead or wishing they were dead.
Dawnguard.esm|00D888: I've read stories. Stories about fools that managed to... communicate with them. You give the Ideal Masters souls, they give you powers to summon the undead. It's all very business-like.
Dawnguard.esm|00D889: Honestly, I don't know. Necromancers are always interested in souls, though, so that probably has some kind of interest.
Dawnguard.esm|00D88A: The Soul Cairn is a tiny sliver of Oblivion, the realm of the daedra. It's ruled by unseen beings known as the Ideal Masters.
Dawnguard.esm|00D88B: Nobody really knows. As far as I've heard, no one's seen them and returned to Tamriel to tell about it.
Dawnguard.esm|00D88C: I don't know. Certainly not longevity. Kind of a waste of time for a vampire.
Dawnguard.esm|00D88D: Looking at the equipment and materials, it looks like she was trying to advance her necromancy.
Dawnguard.esm|00D892: Keep searching for her notes. She probably had a journal or something.
Dawnguard.esm|00D893: I can't even begin to imagine how much time she spent in this room. Doesn't look too cozy.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8D6: That's good enough for me. Go see what the vampires were looking for in this Dimhollow Crypt. With any luck, they'll still be there.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8D7: Yes, well... I never wanted any of this to happen. I tried to warn all of you... I am sorry, you know.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8D8: Oh, hey there! You here to join the Dawnguard, too?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8D9: I'm, uh... my name is Agmaer, sir.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8DA: Yes, that's it. Dimhollow Crypt. Brother Adalvald was sure it held some long-lost vampire artifact of some kind. We didn't listen to him any more than we did Isran. He was at the Hall when it was attacked...
Dawnguard.esm|00D8DB: Isran, Carcette is dead. The Hall of the Vigilants... everyone... they're all dead. You were right, we were wrong. Isn't that enough for you?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8DC: So who are you? What do you want?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8DD: Well? Ready to join the fight against the damn vampires?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8DE: If you change your mind and decide to join the fight before it's too late, talk to Isran at Fort Dawnguard, southeast of Riften.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8DF: Feel free to poke around the fort and take what you need. There isn't much yet, but you're welcome to anything you can use.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E0: Do I look like a "sir" to you, boy? I'm not a soldier, and you're not joining the army.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E1: And now that you've stirred up the vampires against you, you come begging for my protection?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E2: Ha. Isran's going to like you. Go talk to him at Fort Dawnguard, southeast of Riften. He'll decide if you're Dawnguard material.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E3: Yes, a crossbow. Best thing for killing vampires. Just take a few shots at those crates over there.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E4: While you're thinking about it, the vampires will be gathering strength in the shadows, waiting to strike.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E5: Tolan, tell him about, what was it, Dimhollow?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E6: Tolan, tell her about, what was it, Dimhollow?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E7: I remember Keeper Carcette telling me repeatedly that Fort Dawnguard is a crumbling ruin, not worth the expense and manpower to repair.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8E9: Here to join the Dawnguard? Good.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8EC: I'm going to Dimhollow Crypt. Perhaps I can be of some small assistance to you.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8EE: And now you want to come running to safety with the Dawnguard, is that it?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8EF: Where is everybody? This place looks almost deserted.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F0: You're not paying attention then. Like most everyone else around here. Haven't you heard that the Hall of the Vigilants was destroyed by vampires? They never took the threat seriously, and now they've paid the price.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F1: You there, boy. Stop skulking in the shadows and step up here. What's your name?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F2: Don't worry, I think we can make a Dawnguard out of you. Here, take this crossbow and let's see how you shoot.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F3: I know what you think of us. You think we're soft, that we're cowards. You think our deaths proved our weakness. Stendarr grant that you do not have to face the same test and be found wanting.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F4: Why do I even post guards? This is the headquarters of the Dawnguard. Well, it used to be, a long time ago. As you can see, we have some work to do to restore it to its former glory. But maybe you want to help me with that?
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F5: You know why I'm here. The Vigilants are under attack everywhere. The vampires are much more dangerous than we believed.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F6: You've probably killed lots of vampires, huh? I'm sure Isran will sign you right up. Not sure he'll take me. I hope so.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F7: We're vampire hunters. We search out and destroy those bloodsucking scum wherever we find them.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8F9: You Vigilants were never trained for...
Dawnguard.esm|00D8FA: You heard right. I'm glad word's finally starting to get around. But that means it won't be long before the vampires start to take notice as well.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8FB: That must be it. Fort Dawnguard... Wow. Bigger than I expected.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8FD: Talk to Isran. He'll decide if he wants you for the Dawnguard.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8FE: Isran's inside the fort. He's the one you need to talk to.
Dawnguard.esm|00D8FF: Uh, my weapon? I mostly just use my pa's axe, when wolves are attacking the goats or something.
Dawnguard.esm|00D900: Tolan, I don't think that's a good idea.
Dawnguard.esm|00D901: Got a fire in your belly to kill vampires, eh? Good for you. But look around - there's really not much to join yet. I've only just started rebuilding the order.
Dawnguard.esm|00D902: Why are you here, Tolan? The Vigilants and I were finished with each other a long time ago.
Dawnguard.esm|00D903: Hey, uh, don't tell Isran I was afraid to meet him by myself. Not the best first impression for a new vampire hunter, I guess.
Dawnguard.esm|00D904: Didn't I tell you to step forward? Hmm... Farm boy, eh? What's your weapon?
Dawnguard.esm|00D905: I'll meet you at Dimhollow. It's the least I can do to avenge my fallen comrades.
Dawnguard.esm|00D906: Isran will decide if you've got what it takes. Go on, he's right inside.
Dawnguard.esm|00D907: Truth is, I'm a little nervous. I've never done anything like this before. I hope you don't mind if I walk up with you.
Dawnguard.esm|00D908: You there. The Dawnguard is looking for anyone willing to fight against the growing vampire menace. What do you say?
Dawnguard.esm|00D909: Yeah? You change your mind? The Dawnguard could use somebody like you.
Dawnguard.esm|00D90A: Report to Isran at Fort Dawnguard. And get moving if you're serious about it. The vampires aren't going to wait around to make their next move.
Dawnguard.esm|00E7A8: I guess I'll see you at Fort Dawnguard.
Dawnguard.esm|00E7A9: Potions my friend, potions! Possibly some spells. Maybe some other things I've forgotten about. Actually I'm not sure. Let's see, shall we?
Dawnguard.esm|00E7AA: Oh, well... some... things, I suppose. Remedies, cures, a poison or two.
Dawnguard.esm|00E7AB: Look, I'll be honest with you. I have no idea. But I'm sure we can find something worth your time.
Dawnguard.esm|00E872: I know this is difficult for you. I hope you trust me. I'd never do anything that could hurt you.
Dawnguard.esm|00E873: My mother taught me a trick or two. I could partially soul trap you, and offer that gem to the Ideal Masters. It might be enough to satisfy them. It would make you a bit weaker when we travel through the Soul Cairn, but we might be able to fix that once we're inside. Maybe.
Dawnguard.esm|00E87C: Are you sure? I'm willing to do it, but you need to think it through. You'll remain mortal, but you'll find yourself weakened within the Soul Cairn.
Dawnguard.esm|00E87F: Thank you. Let's not waste any more time then.
Dawnguard.esm|00E880: I promise to make this as painless as possible. Hold still.
Dawnguard.esm|00E89B: To come in here alone... a fool like all the rest of them.
Dawnguard.esm|00E89C: I've heard the rumors around Skyrim. I know something's going on. And I've seen the vampires. I felt I had two choices. I could either live in fear of the night, or I could do something to help protect my people.
Dawnguard.esm|00E89D: You wouldn't dare. Now shut up and keep on watch.
Dawnguard.esm|00E89E: And I have half a mind to tell Lokil of your disloyalty.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A0: Glad you decided to give it a try. I hope Isran likes you.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A1: If you're here to join up, you need to see Isran. He's up at the fort talking to some Vigilant.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A2: I wish Lokil would hurry it up. I have half a mind to return to the castle and tell Harkon what a fool he's entrusted this mission to.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A3: All this talk is making me thirsty. Perhaps another Vigilant will wander in soon.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A4: I suspect now it was Brother Adalvald's journals they were after. He was obsessed with ancient vampire artifacts and burial sites.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A5: Ha. Those two deserved what they got. Their arrogance had become insufferable.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A6: Dimhollow Crypt. Brother Adalvald thought it contained some long-lost vampire artifact. It seems he was right.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A7: The Hall of the Vigilants is in ruins, my brothers and sisters dead or scattered.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8A8: The vampires are much more dangerous than we ever dreamed. The Dawnguard is the only hope now.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8AC: Isran was right all along. We should have listened to his warnings. It's too late for the Vigilants now.
Dawnguard.esm|00E8AD: He fought well though. Jeron and Bresoth were no match for him.
Dawnguard.esm|00E945: Which is where you come in. If killing vampires sounds good to you, go talk to Isran up in the fort. He decides who's Dawnguard material.
Dawnguard.esm|00E995: Which is where you come in. If killing vampires sounds good to you, come talk to Isran at Fort Dawnguard, southeast of Riften.
Dawnguard.esm|00E996: The Vigilants mostly hunt down daedra worshippers, which is why they got torn to pieces when they went up against vampires. That's why our leader Isran is reestablishing the Dawnguard. Real, serious vampire hunters.
Dawnguard.esm|00E997: Ha. Isran's going to like you. He's up the hill in the fort. He'll decide if you're Dawnguard material.
Dawnguard.esm|00E998: If you change your mind and decide to join the fight before it's too late, go talk to Isran up in the fort.
Dawnguard.esm|00E9DD: There's an offering altar not terribly far from here. I'm willing to bet that the gem you're looking for is there. Is there anything else? Could be your last chance.
Dawnguard.esm|00E9DE: Your soul essence was trapped inside a gem. When you and Serana entered the Soul Cairn, it was "given" to the Ideal Masters as payment. You simply need to retrieve the gem. The moment you touch it, your soul essence will be restored.
Dawnguard.esm|00E9E1: So my daughter applied some of the lessons I taught her about necromancy, did she? Don't worry, I think I can help you.
Dawnguard.esm|00E9E2: Now that you've retrieved the Elder Scroll, you should be on your way. If there's anything I can do before you depart, you must let me know.
Dawnguard.esm|00F356: I mean... as much as anyone. Not a lot. You'd figure a couple hundred years locked away with one would have given me some insight, but no.
Dawnguard.esm|00F35A: A small price to pay for the betterment of our kind.
Dawnguard.esm|00F35C: Well, before we spoke to my mother in the Soul Cairn, I would have said we bring the bow to my father. But after everything with her... I don't think we can trust him.
Dawnguard.esm|00F35D: I think we both know... it's time to face my father. If we don't, he'll keep chasing us for the rest of our lives.
Dawnguard.esm|00F35F: It's... not as shiny as I was expecting. Still, it's beautiful.
Dawnguard.esm|00F362: Yes, but when I was here it wasn't so... crumbly. This used to be a city. I wonder what happened here.
Dawnguard.esm|00F36D: This is true, but my techniques only work on the living. You've chosen a different path to remake yourself.
Dawnguard.esm|00F36E: What have you done to yourself? I can't work with you in this state, night-walker.
Dawnguard.esm|00F36F: But, my techniques only work on the living, not those that have crossed over into undeath.
Dawnguard.esm|00F4F0: Ha. Nothing can destroy an Elder Scroll. Worry more about protecting your hide, and let me worry about my things.
Dawnguard.esm|00F4F1: It's... complicated. I can't really talk about it. I'm sorry.
Dawnguard.esm|00F4F2: Yes, it is, and it's mine.
Dawnguard.esm|00F4F3: He's a... powerful man. Or at least used to be. Are you taking me to him?
Dawnguard.esm|00F70D: I've sensed the blood pulsing a little stronger, of late. I had assumed you had as well. I think our potential is on the rise. Hircine smiles on us. Let's not question too much.
Dawnguard.esm|00F70E: Something has shifted in the moons, brother.
Dawnguard.esm|00F70F: Something has shifted in the moons, sister.
Dawnguard.esm|00F710: The more we feed, the greater our prowess will grow. We could perhaps discover even more gifts of Hircine.
Dawnguard.esm|00F7C8: Fine, fine! I can't afford to forget about this work, though. Some very interesting applications. Now where is it I'm supposed to be going?
Dawnguard.esm|00F7C9: I'm sorry, I'm not about to do that without at least a single gyro to show for my efforts.
Dawnguard.esm|00F7FA: Here. Let me show you how it's made... Assuming you have the smithing skills, you should be able to make this yourself at Gunmar's forge. Either way, I'll have some for sale soon.
Dawnguard.esm|00F7FB: Ooh, good. I was just starting to get bored.
Dawnguard.esm|00F7FC: Let's see what you've got there.
Dawnguard.esm|00F7FE: You never disappoint. What've you got there?
Dawnguard.esm|00F7FF: Hmmm... I see. Clever dwarves. With a similar technique and a little tinkering I should be able to improve the capabilities of our crossbows. I often wonder where we'd be if the Dwemer were still around. Ah well. We'll have to manage with the scraps we find.
Dawnguard.esm|00F800: Would you look at that. An exploding crossbow bolt. This should be fun!
Dawnguard.esm|00F801: I see... similar to the exploding bolt, only this one will cause damage with splintering shards of ice sharp as glass. Charming!
Dawnguard.esm|00F802: Let's see... Ooh! This diagram explains how to fashion a bolt that will send a jolt of shock through whatever it hits. Testing this will require some extra precautions, I think.
Dawnguard.esm|00F803: Is it really... it is! I was hoping we'd find this. It's a diagram for a dwemer crossbow. Much more sophisticated, and powerful, than the ones we use.
Dawnguard.esm|00F804: Now this is a find! Plans for improving the Dwemer crossbow. This should add some extra punch to an already devastating weapon.
Dawnguard.esm|00F805: Hmmm... interesting... those dwarves were clever creatures. I'll tinker around with these techniques and see what I can come up with around here.
Dawnguard.esm|00F818: Thank you! It's not much, but this will help a great deal with some things I've been researching. Now where is it Isran expects me to go?
Dawnguard.esm|00F819: Well then I'm very sorry, but I just can't walk away from this project yet.
Dawnguard.esm|00F81B: You know where to find me if you need more.
Dawnguard.esm|00F81C: If you need anything else, I'll be here.
Dawnguard.esm|00F81E: Isran had a surprising stockpile of weapons and armor. Take a look.
Dawnguard.esm|00F81F: It's no Warmaiden's, but I've got a decent supply.
Dawnguard.esm|00F820: What do you need? Blades, armor, smithing materials?
Dawnguard.esm|00F821: This is everything I've got right now.
Dawnguard.esm|00F822: Keep yourself armed and protected as best you can.
Dawnguard.esm|00F823: I'll buy whatever you're looking to get rid of.
Dawnguard.esm|00F824: It's sort of a random assortment of things. See anything useful?
Dawnguard.esm|00F825: Here's everything I have. Sorry there isn't more.
Dawnguard.esm|00F826: It's not easy to get supplies way out here, but I'm managing. Take a look.
Dawnguard.esm|00F849: Time has very little meaning to me. Consequently, it has little meaning to the Ideal Masters as well. I suppose you could call this the ultimate waiting game, each watching the other to see which will give in.
Dawnguard.esm|00F84A: Have you been listening to me? Like Serana, I'm a pure-blooded vampire. My presence on Tamriel is as much of a danger as hers.
Dawnguard.esm|00F84B: Yes, he's a fanatic... he's changed. But he's still my father. Why can't you understand how that makes me feel?
Dawnguard.esm|00F84C: Unfortunately, yes. Since the Keepers weren't able to claim my soul, they had their minions construct a barrier that I'd never be able to breach.
Dawnguard.esm|00F84D: Now you're beginning to see why I wanted to protect Serana, and why I've kept the other Elder Scroll as far from her as possible.
Dawnguard.esm|00F84E: The Ideal Masters unleashed their Keepers and sent them to destroy me. Fortunately, I was able to hold them at bay and retreat into these ruins.
Dawnguard.esm|00F84F: Like myself, Serana was a human once. We were devout followers of Lord Molag Bal. Tradition dictates the females be offered to Molag Bal on his summoning day. Few survive the ordeal. Those that do emerge as a pure-blooded vampire. We call such confluences the "Daughters of Coldharbour."
Dawnguard.esm|00F851: When I fled Castle Volkihar, I fled with two Elder Scrolls. The scroll I presume you found with Serana speaks of Auriel and his arcane weapon, Auriel's Bow. The second scroll declares that "The Blood of Coldharbour's Daughter will blind the eye of the Dragon."
Dawnguard.esm|00F852: I requested refuge in the Soul Cairn, and in exchange, I would provide the Ideal Masters the souls that they craved. If I had foreseen the value they placed on my own soul, I would never have come here.
Dawnguard.esm|00F853: Glorious. Serana claims you're her guardian, yet your first instinct appears to be greed. I see that my daughter is still as naive as ever.
Dawnguard.esm|00F854: When I entered the Soul Cairn, I had intended to strike a bargain with the Ideal Masters, the custodians of this place.
Dawnguard.esm|00F855: Serana has sacrificed everything to prevent Harkon from completing the prophecy. I would have expected her to explain that to you.
Dawnguard.esm|00F856: I find your choice of words quite interesting considering that Serana is in far more danger now than she was following my "foolish plan."
Dawnguard.esm|00F857: You care nothing for Serana or our plight. You're here because we're abominations in your mind. Evil creatures that need to be destroyed.
Dawnguard.esm|00F858: You care nothing for Serana or our plight. You see the Tyranny of the Sun as your chance at deification, and like Harkon you won't hesitate to destroy anything that stands in your path.
Dawnguard.esm|00F859: No! I will not allow that happen. The Elder Scrolls only tell of events that might be, not events that will be.
Dawnguard.esm|00F85B: I had no idea you felt that way about me, Serana. It was never my intention to push you aside. All I wanted is for us to be a family again... for things to return to the way they were before.
Dawnguard.esm|00F896: Do you, now? Hircine doesn't usually bestow his favor on the fickle. But you've proven your worth. I'm willing to try. Are you ready now?
Dawnguard.esm|00F9BF: I believe you, Vigilant. And I don't think you even know what you've found here.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9C0: Yes, of course Lokil. Do not forget who brought you news of the Vigilants' discovery.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9C1: I'll never tell you anything, vampire. My oath to Stendarr is stronger than any suffering you can inflict on me.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9C2: Never seen a crossbow before, eh? Not surprised. Kind of a Dawnguard specialty. Nothing better for putting down vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9C4: And we will not return without it. Vingalmo and Orthjolf will make way for me after this.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9C5: Here, take this one and give it a try. You'll want to get to know how to use it if you really plan to join the Dawnguard.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9C7: He knew nothing. He served his purpose by leading us to this place. Now it is up to us to bring Harkon the prize.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9CA: We haven't gotten anywhere ourselves with...
Dawnguard.esm|00F9CB: I guess this is it. Wish me luck.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9CC: Are you sure that was wise, Lokil? He still might have told us something.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9CD: I never forget who my friends are. Or my enemies.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9CE: So go and meet your beloved Stendarr.
Dawnguard.esm|00F9E9: I will find you. Then you will beg me to let you die.
Dawnguard.esm|00FA2F: Harkon will reward us richly for this.
Dawnguard.esm|00FA6F: I've waited a long time for my chance to move up in the court.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAA1: I thought Lokil knew what he was doing. This is taking too long.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAA2: Jeron was furious to be left guarding the door.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAB5: Well, some necromancers believe they are the crystalline structures dotting the Soul Cairn. I believe there's more to it than that.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAB6: This stranger may call himself vampire, but he knows nothing of our struggle. Why should I entrust you to him?
Dawnguard.esm|00FAB7: Harkon's vision is a world plunged in eternal darkness where the vampire can flourish and never again fear the "tyranny of the sun." What he fails to realize is how much attention would be called to our kind if the prophecy came to fruition.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAB8: It was expected of her, just as it was expected of me. Being selected as an offering to Molag Bal is an honor. She wouldn't have dared turn her back on that.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAB9: I know very little about them. They're mystic entities that lord over the Soul Cairn, controlling every aspect from its fabric to its appearance.
Dawnguard.esm|00FABA: If eternal night fell, there are many who wouldn't stand for it. They would raise armies in attempts to return things to normal. The order of the day would be our destruction until every last vampire was hunted down and eliminated.
Dawnguard.esm|00FABB: It's what some call the domain of Molag Bal. His place in Oblivion.
Dawnguard.esm|00FABC: I do. It's how the vampire has survived for millennia, and the only way we can continue to survive in the future.
Dawnguard.esm|00FABD: The ability to summon powerful undead guardians as one would conjure an atronach or daedra. However, the majority of necromancers that are foolish enough to enter into a bargain with the Ideal Masters wind up here... as harvested souls.
Dawnguard.esm|00FABE: I think they transcend what we perceive as a physical form. Perhaps they were once corporeal beings, but they've obviously reached a point where they no longer require a tangible presence.
Dawnguard.esm|00FABF: The Ideal Masters's weakness is their insatiable hunger for pure souls. It's the reason for the Soul Cairn's existence, and the only leverage a necromancer has when bargaining with them.
Dawnguard.esm|00FAC0: Conduits through which the Ideal Masters speak to their underlings and feed on their victims.
Dawnguard.esm|00FD58: I heard a lot of vague nonsense. You could interpret that a hundred different ways. Only thing that stood out for me was Auriel's Bow. That's a powerful weapon, and I sure as Oblivion don't want the vampires to get hold of it. The Moth Priest can stay here for now. As for the vampire, I still don't trust it, so keep it on a leash.
Dawnguard.esm|00FD6F: I wish I could say that I did, but without the other two Scrolls, the picture will remain incomplete. I admit, I feel a bit frustrated that I can't be more helpful. Still, our enemy is likely just as much in the dark as we are, and that's something. Now it's a race to claim the remaining Scrolls and discover what part Auriel's Bow has to play in this prophecy.
Dawnguard.esm|00FE84: We hunt down and kill vampires. Haven't you been paying attention? In the old days, the Dawnguard kept all of Skyrim safe from those bloodsucking scum. Then people stopped taking the threat seriously, the Dawnguard was disbanded, and the vampires came creeping back. Now I'm trying to reestablish the Dawnguard. Take the fight to the vampires for a change. I hope it isn't too late.
Dawnguard.esm|00FFE1: That was the plan, yes. Assuming the rest of them can trust a vampire. Come on, I'll need you to help convince them.
Dawnguard.esm|00FFE3: I'm sorry, I had heard there were vampire hunters here. I thought they might want to know about a vampire plot to enslave the rest of the world. Was I wrong?
Dawnguard.esm|00FFE4: Well, back before I... you know. The College of Winterhold was the first place I'd think to go for any kind of magic or historical thing. The wizards know about all kinds of things that people probably shouldn't know about.
Dawnguard.esm|00FFE6: I guess you'll never get to find out.
Dawnguard.esm|01038D: I wasn't sure you'd come back for me. I'm still looking for a guide to get me home. What do you say?
Dawnguard.esm|01038E: You're back, good! So, what happened?
Dawnguard.esm|01038F: I suppose you're right. I should have a little more faith. Just be sure to keep your eyes open and your guard up.
Dawnguard.esm|010390: Home? Well, where is that? Some dusty crypt or frigid cave? No doubt she'd like you to lead her into such a place. Then, when you're alone and beyond help, the fangs will come out!
Dawnguard.esm|010391: She's... she's a vampire? Are you a complete fool? You can't trust them! She'll turn on you the first chance she gets!
Dawnguard.esm|010392: What do you mean? Why were the vampires after this woman?
Dawnguard.esm|010393: It's good to see you all in one piece. Who is this?
Dawnguard.esm|010395: I'll be here if you change your mind.
Dawnguard.esm|01056E: I'd be happy to, but you already have a creature under your command.
Dawnguard.esm|01056F: Alright. Now take note. He'll mind you and us Dawnguard, but there's no accounting for other folk. And heed also this. He's no pet. Mistreat the beast or leave him alone for too long, and he'll return to the wild forgetting you ever existed. Now, you be careful with the creature.
Dawnguard.esm|010570: I'd be happy to, but your purse is lighter than you think.
Dawnguard.esm|010663: Oh, I'm sure I have something you'll find useful.
Dawnguard.esm|010664: You'd be surprised what people leave behind when you burn their village to the ground.
Dawnguard.esm|010665: Many in the court don't bother with potions, but I keep things stocked just in case.
Dawnguard.esm|010666: I may not have potions to block the sun, but I do have nearly everything else.
Dawnguard.esm|010667: Always a pleasure to serve a fellow alchemist.
Dawnguard.esm|010668: If there's one thing I know, it's weapons and armor. Take a look.
Dawnguard.esm|010669: Always good to have a backup plan, in case the claws and teeth aren't enough.
Dawnguard.esm|01066A: I keep the forge running day and night in case Lord Harkon needs anything.
Dawnguard.esm|01066B: I keep the forge running day and night in case you need anything.
Dawnguard.esm|01066C: I'm not the most powerful of vampires, but I'm good with a hammer. See for yourself.
Dawnguard.esm|01066D: A little extra protection never hurts.
Dawnguard.esm|01066E: I am more than happy to buy or sell whatever you might need.
Dawnguard.esm|01066F: I'm sorry I don't have more to offer.
Dawnguard.esm|010670: I hope I have something that interests you. I always wish to be of service.
Dawnguard.esm|010671: You need something? I am happy to help, if I can.
Dawnguard.esm|010672: I've scrounged up a few things. Here, please, take a look.
Dawnguard.esm|010678: Yes. I've kept it safely secured here ever since I was imprisoned. Fortunately, you're in a position to breach the barrier that surrounds these ruins.
Dawnguard.esm|010679: I'm sorry, Serana. I didn't know... I didn't see. I've allowed my hatred of your father to estrange us for too long. Forgive me. If you want the Elder Scroll, it's yours.
Dawnguard.esm|01067A: I want us to be a family again. But I don't know if we can ever have that. Maybe we don't deserve that kind of happiness. Maybe it isn't for us. But we have to stop him. Before he goes too far. And to do that, we need the Elder Scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|010A53: Minutes? Hours? Years? I can't even begin to guess. I suggest we don't wait around to find out. Now, let's get you the Elder Scroll and you can be on your way.
Dawnguard.esm|010A6D: After all these years, that's the first thing you ask me? Yes, I have the scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|010A6F: My long-lost daughter returns at last. I trust you have my Elder Scroll?
Dawnguard.esm|010A73: For my daughter's safe return, you have my gratitude. Tell me, what is your name?
Dawnguard.esm|010A75: This is my savior, the one who freed me.
Dawnguard.esm|010A77: Now tell me, who is this stranger you have brought into our hall?
Dawnguard.esm|010A79: Of course I'm delighted to see you, my daughter. Must I really say the words aloud? Ah, if only your traitor mother were here, I would let her watch this reunion before putting her head on a spike.
Dawnguard.esm|010A82: Well, at least you're smart enough to want to learn more. I'll show you what I can.
Dawnguard.esm|010A83: You already know as much as I do. And that's a compliment.
Dawnguard.esm|010A85: You're already as good as I am. There's nothing you can learn from me.
Dawnguard.esm|010A86: Working metal isn't easy. Let me show you a few things.
Dawnguard.esm|010A87: You know as much as I do. I can't teach you anything new.
Dawnguard.esm|010A88: A noble cause. Arkay likes the idea very much, so I'm happy to help.
Dawnguard.esm|010A89: You know more than I do now. Arkay has us both beaten, but he's not giving up all his secrets.
Dawnguard.esm|010D84: Present yourself to Lord Harkon at once.
Dawnguard.esm|010DAD: As you've been traveling in the Soul Cairn, your body has become attuned to it. Let's just say a tiny part of you rubbed off on it, and in its place, a bit of the Soul Cairn filled the void. You should find no difficulty using the portal any longer.
Dawnguard.esm|010DB3: You're not getting the best of me!
Dawnguard.esm|010DB4: Here we go, let's get the blood flowing again.
Dawnguard.esm|010DB5: You're not going to keep me in here!
Dawnguard.esm|010DB9: Did you see where he went?
Dawnguard.esm|010DBA: Did you see where she went?
Dawnguard.esm|010DBB: Did you see where it went?
Dawnguard.esm|010DBF: I still remember how to fight!
Dawnguard.esm|010DC0: I'm not going to die like this!
Dawnguard.esm|010DC1: This won't end well for you!
Dawnguard.esm|010DC3: How many of you are there?!
Dawnguard.esm|010DC5: You fight worse than you smell!
Dawnguard.esm|010DCA: It's time for you to suffer!
Dawnguard.esm|010DCB: This is what your obsession brings you!
Dawnguard.esm|010DCE: I'm gonna have to kill you... again!
Dawnguard.esm|010DDA: Good. I assume you took care of that little infestation. Did you figure out what they were looking for in there?
Dawnguard.esm|010DDD: Well I don't know what that means. But we need to find out. She must either have something the vampires want, or be someone important to them.
Dawnguard.esm|010DDF: That would fit with the rest of it... I don't like it, but you did right to play along. We need to find out what this is all about.
Dawnguard.esm|010DE1: Well I don't know what that means. But we need to find out. She must either have something the vampires want, or be someone important to them.
Dawnguard.esm|010DE2: Damn fool. I told him not to go. The Vigilants always had more bravery than sense. Did you at least find out what the vampires were looking for in there?
Dawnguard.esm|010EB7: Tolan was telling me about some cave that the Vigilants were poking around in. Seemed to think it was related to these recent vampire attacks.
Dawnguard.esm|010EB8: First thing is to get this fort back into shape. Right now, a vampire could practically walk right in the front door. But I have something for you to do, since you're asking.
Dawnguard.esm|010EB9: I need someone out in the field, taking the fight to the damn vampires, while we're getting the fort back into shape...
Dawnguard.esm|010EBA: Got to keep alert. We don't want to get caught napping like the Vigilants did.
Dawnguard.esm|010EBB: I don't think the vampires would be stupid enough to attack us here, but you never know.
Dawnguard.esm|010EBC: I'm glad we're finally taking the fight to those monsters.
Dawnguard.esm|010EBD: I hope the vampires try to attack us here. A lot easier than hunting them down in their holes.
Dawnguard.esm|010EBE: Isran knows how to fight those monsters. He's been preparing for this day for a long time.
Dawnguard.esm|010EBF: Now that the Dawnguard are back, the vampires' reign of terror is about to end.
Dawnguard.esm|010F18: He was out of control, Garan. It had to be done. I'm not happy about this. He... he was still my father. But I suppose my father really died a long time ago. This was just the end of something else.
Dawnguard.esm|010F19: I suppose you're lucky you're not dead. Or worse, one of them.
Dawnguard.esm|010F1A: My lady, you have my deepest sympathies. I am sure this was not easy for you.
Dawnguard.esm|010F1B: So they have everything they wanted, and we're left with nothing.
Dawnguard.esm|010F1C: Lord Harkon, defeated. I never imagined I'd see the day.
Dawnguard.esm|010F1D: Right. So they have this woman, and an Elder Scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|010F1E: I think perhaps... I think you did more than that. You have my thanks.
Dawnguard.esm|010F1F: I think my father really died a long time ago. This was just... the end of something else. I did what needed to be done. Nothing more.
Dawnguard.esm|010F20: By the Divines, this couldn't get much worse. This is more than you and I can handle.
Dawnguard.esm|010F21: I... I suppose this is difficult for you.
Dawnguard.esm|010F22: I'm waiting to hear what any of this means.
Dawnguard.esm|010F23: It's over. He's dead, and the prophecy dies with him.
Dawnguard.esm|010F24: A woman? Trapped in there? That doesn't make any sense. Who is she? More importantly, where is she?
Dawnguard.esm|010F25: The two of them just never got along. That's why Isran didn't have you go looking for him when he sent you after us.
Dawnguard.esm|010F26: Of course, my dear. All will be well now.
Dawnguard.esm|010F27: And so you delivered her to them.
Dawnguard.esm|010F85: As a vampire, you will gain new powers, but also a weakness to sunlight. With each passing day that you do not feed, that weakness will become more deadly, but your powers will also become stronger. Feed upon those who are sleeping, and your vulnerability to sunlight will diminish along with your powers.
Dawnguard.esm|010F86: I have a task that will test your new powers, but first, do you have any questions?
Dawnguard.esm|010F87: There is much to learn, but if you master the powers of the Vampire Lord, few enemies will be able to stand against you.
Dawnguard.esm|010F88: Way I hear it, Lady Serana's been gone a real long time.
Dawnguard.esm|010F89: Good. Go and speak to Garan Marethi. Tell him it is time. He will understand.
Dawnguard.esm|010F8A: When you first transform into a Vampire Lord, you will hover in the air. While you do, you will command the powers of Blood and Night.
Dawnguard.esm|010FD1: I don't like the feel of this place. I'll wait for you back by the entrance.
Dawnguard.esm|011199: I can crush skulls with the best of them. Let me show you a few things.
Dawnguard.esm|01119A: You're pretty good. I don't think there's anything more you can learn from me.
Dawnguard.esm|01119B: Of course! It is my duty to serve, and I am happy to do it!
Dawnguard.esm|01119C: I am afraid you're beyond my meager abiliities. I am sorry I cannot serve you better.
Dawnguard.esm|01156B: My claws have rended the flesh of innumerable foes, but I have never once been felled on the field of battle. I therefore honor-name you "Qahnaarin," or Vanquisher in your tongue.
Dawnguard.esm|01156C: Cursed, not dead. Doomed to exist in this form for eternity. Trapped between laas and dinok, between life and death.
Dawnguard.esm|01156D: I fear that my time here has taken its toll upon me. I share a bond with this dreaded place. If I ventured far from the Soul Cairn, my strength would begin to wane until I was no more.
Dawnguard.esm|01156E: The hostility was necessary. I was bound to an oath.
Dawnguard.esm|01156F: Remember that Harkon isn't to be trusted. No matter what he promises, he'll deceive you in order to get what he wants. And promise me you'll keep my daughter safe. She's the only thing of value I have left.
Dawnguard.esm|011570: I believe in civility among seasoned warriors, and I find your ear worthy of my words.
Dawnguard.esm|011571: After what I've put Serana through, I would understand if she never wished to see me again. I leave that decision in your hands.
Dawnguard.esm|011572: For countless years I've roamed the Soul Cairn, in unintended service to the Ideal Masters. Before this, I roamed the skies above Tamriel. I desire to return there.
Dawnguard.esm|011573: Stay your weapons. I would speak with you, Qahnaarin.
Dawnguard.esm|011574: As much as it pains me to send you and Serana back alone, I can't take the risk.
Dawnguard.esm|011575: If my desire to end your life was still my purpose, we would not be speaking.
Dawnguard.esm|011576: Now that you've retrieved the Elder Scroll, you should be on your way.
Dawnguard.esm|011577: I appreciate your concern for me, but Serana is all that I care about. You must keep her safe at all costs.
Dawnguard.esm|011578: My desire to speak with you was born from the result of our battle, Qahnaarin. I merely wish to respectfully ask a favor of you.
Dawnguard.esm|011579: I have no choice. As I told you before, I'm a Daughter of Coldharbour. If I return to Tamriel, that increases Harkon's likelihood of bringing the Tyranny of the Sun to fruition.
Dawnguard.esm|01157A: The tone of your voice is understandable. Perhaps I can alleviate your suspicions.
Dawnguard.esm|01157B: Trivial in your mind, perhaps. For me, it would mean a great deal. I don't require an answer, Qahnaarin. Simply speak my name to the heavens when you feel the time is right.
Dawnguard.esm|01157C: Your words do me great honor.
Dawnguard.esm|01157D: I will place my name with you and grant you the right to call my name from Tamriel. Do me this simple honor and I will fight at your side as your Grah-Zeymahzin, your Ally, and teach you my Thu'um.
Dawnguard.esm|01157E: Forgive me, Qahnaarin. We will continue this when time releases you from its relentless grasp.
Dawnguard.esm|01158D: Farewell. Remember that Harkon's not to be trusted.
Dawnguard.esm|01158E: Take the scroll, then be on your way.
Dawnguard.esm|01158F: Now, let me get the scroll's case opened.
Dawnguard.esm|011590: Follow me and stay close. Keep your eyes open for Durnehviir.
Dawnguard.esm|011592: The Elder Scroll is just ahead.
Dawnguard.esm|011594: Be careful, and keep my daughter safe.
Dawnguard.esm|011595: The Elder Scroll is just a bit further.
Dawnguard.esm|011596: Only a single spire remains. Be careful, I'm almost certain the final Keeper will be prepared for your assault.
Dawnguard.esm|011597: You're doing well, but two of the spires remain intact.
Dawnguard.esm|0117DC: I understand that, but I really can't leave here without at least one usable piece of dwarven equipment. A single gyro would be enough, I think. Believe me, this is useful stuff.
Dawnguard.esm|0117F0: Aye. And you be careful with the creature.
Dawnguard.esm|01195A: I must head back to the Sanctuary before dawn. I will see you there, Listener.
Dawnguard.esm|01195B: I am sure Nazir's cooking is great and all, but my appetites are a little more... discerning.
Dawnguard.esm|01195C: You could call it that. Didn't put up much of a fight but made for a tasty snack. A rich bouquet of flavors, I would definitely recommend it.
Dawnguard.esm|01195D: Listener! Fancy seeing you out here.
Dawnguard.esm|011962: Can't you tell your own kind? I smelled you almost before my eyes were open.
Dawnguard.esm|011963: You think I can't tell my own kind? I smelled you almost before my eyes were open.
Dawnguard.esm|011965: Is he working for my father?
Dawnguard.esm|011966: That's not a name I know. And it doesn't sound like a name a group of vampires would choose.
Dawnguard.esm|011967: I... don't know who that is.
Dawnguard.esm|01196C: I was going to say the same thing to you.
Dawnguard.esm|01196E: He's a very powerful man. Or he was at one point. I'm surprised another vampire hasn't heard of him.
Dawnguard.esm|011970: I was expecting someone... like me, at least.
Dawnguard.esm|011972: I was expecting someone from my family. I don't recognize you. Are you one of my father's little acolytes?
Dawnguard.esm|011973: My family used to live on an island to the west of Solitude. I would guess they still do. By the way... my name is Serana. Good to meet you.
Dawnguard.esm|011977: A... can't you tell just from looking at me? A vampire.
Dawnguard.esm|01197A: That's... complicated. And I'm not totally sure if I can trust you. But if you want to know the whole story, help me get back to my family's home.
Dawnguard.esm|01197B: Why would someone who isn't... like me... come all the way down here?
Dawnguard.esm|01197C: Are you one of my father's little acolytes?
Dawnguard.esm|01197E: Don't think that'll keep them from turning on you eventually. They'll figure out what you are.
Dawnguard.esm|01197F: Not fond of vampires, are they? Well, look. Kill me, you've killed one vampire. But if people are after me, there's something bigger going on. I can help you find out what that is.
Dawnguard.esm|01198D: That is all I have to teach you. If you wish to be reminded of these lessons, you need only ask.
Dawnguard.esm|01198E: I was about to suggest that very thing. Yes, you most certainly deserve a reward.
Dawnguard.esm|01198F: You have done me a great service, and now you must be rewarded.
Dawnguard.esm|011990: For centuries we lived here, far from the cares of the world. All that ended when my wife betrayed me and stole away that which I valued most.
Dawnguard.esm|011991: Perhaps you should. I count some of the oldest and most powerful vampires in Skyrim among the members of my court.
Dawnguard.esm|011992: Very well. I am Harkon, lord of this court. By now, my daughter will have told you what we are.
Dawnguard.esm|011993: Not quite, though I can see how an outsider might arrive at that conclusion. No, we are vampires, among the oldest and most powerful in Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|011A23: In the meantime, we're going to get to the bottom of why a vampire showed up here looking for you.
Dawnguard.esm|011A24: We'll get to that. For now, get acquainted with the space. Sorine, you'll find room to start your tinkering on that crossbow design you've been working on. Gunmar, there's an area large enough for you to pen up some trolls, get them armored up and ready for use.
Dawnguard.esm|011A26: Making sure you're not vampires. Can't be too careful.
Dawnguard.esm|011A29: Let's go have a little chat with it, shall we?
Dawnguard.esm|011A92: I suppose she's right. I shouldn't let my personal feelings get in the way. Last I'd heard of him, he was aiding the Vigilants of Stendarr at Ruunvald. He may still be there. If he can maintain some appearance of normalcy, I'll allow him to stay.
Dawnguard.esm|011A93: Who said something, Sorine or Gunmar? I thought they'd have learned their lesson by now. I don't trust that man, and I don't want him here.
Dawnguard.esm|011DFF: We're going to a place I've never been... and we're goind to see my mother. After all these years. I just don't feel comfortable sharing that moment with a total stranger, I'm sorry.
Dawnguard.esm|011E00: Look, this whole blood ritual is a bit personal. I'd rather not have a bigger audience than I need to. Could you tell your companion to head home?
Dawnguard.esm|011E40: I'm not known for my patience.
Dawnguard.esm|011E41: I need to speak to you, stranger. Now.
Dawnguard.esm|011E43: It's only for Serana's sake that I have let you live.
Dawnguard.esm|011E44: I leave politics to others. I serve the lord of the castle.
Dawnguard.esm|011E45: I have everything I need right here.
Dawnguard.esm|011E46: Find your own thrall. This one's mine.
Dawnguard.esm|011E48: I'm a loyal servant of the lord of the castle, nothing more.
Dawnguard.esm|011E4A: The feast never ends here in Castle Volkihar.
Dawnguard.esm|011EC2: I just need to be alone. For a while. There's a lot of feelings wrapped up in this old place, and I don't think you're quite ready for them. So let me take the lead in there.
Dawnguard.esm|011EC3: Sorry it had to be this way!
Dawnguard.esm|011EC4: I won't fall for your tricks!
Dawnguard.esm|011EDA: With experience, your powers will grow and you will discover new ways to use them.
Dawnguard.esm|011EDB: Descend to the ground, and you can attack with your claws and feed upon your prey to regain your strength.
Dawnguard.esm|011EDD: A final warning... be wary, for you can only hold your Vampire Lord form for a brief time.
Dawnguard.esm|011EDE: Over time, your powers will grow in strength and you will find new ways to use your gifts.
Dawnguard.esm|011EE5: Here, you should take a crossbow. Good for taking out those fiends before they get close.
Dawnguard.esm|011F11: May Auri-El's brilliance illuminate your path.
Dawnguard.esm|011F12: May Auri-El's light guide you in your darkest hours.
Dawnguard.esm|011F13: May Auri-El's warmth imbue your body with strength.
Dawnguard.esm|011F14: May Auri-El's glow shield you from your enemy.
Dawnguard.esm|011F15: May Auri-El's radiance fill your heart with joy.
Dawnguard.esm|011F8F: Goodbye, hero! Take good care of Arvak for me! Such a good horse...
Dawnguard.esm|011F90: He'll help you get around this wretched place, but I'm sure he'd be much happier someplace sunnier.
Dawnguard.esm|011F91: He's such a loyal beast. Here, I'll teach you how to call him to you.
Dawnguard.esm|011F92: Arvak...You saved him! His soul is free, I can feel it!
Dawnguard.esm|012185: Trust me. I lived in that castle for a very long time and I know every nook and cranny.
Dawnguard.esm|012188: If you want me to come along, it has to just be me and you.
Dawnguard.esm|01218A: I'll be here. Don't take too long, now!
Dawnguard.esm|01218B: Don't let me stand in your way, then. I'll be at the dock whenever you're ready.
Dawnguard.esm|01218D: I don't want to step on anyone's toes. Come back to me when you're alone.
Dawnguard.esm|01218E: I'm not going to waste my time making sure your friend doesn't get killed. When it's just you and me, we can go.
Dawnguard.esm|01218F: Not until you get rid of whoever your other friend is.
Dawnguard.esm|012191: Well, I don't take just anyone into the secret parts of the place I grew up. Get rid of this interloper and you and I can be on our way. I'll wait for you at the dock.
Dawnguard.esm|012192: Come on. It's just around the side.
Dawnguard.esm|012196: If you want me to come along, it has to just be me and you.
Dawnguard.esm|012197: I'd... well, I'd come with you, but I don't know if I can trust your friend. Let me know if you want me along.
Dawnguard.esm|0124D1: I can't do everything for you.
Dawnguard.esm|0124D4: I'm not going to do that for you.
Dawnguard.esm|0126BC: Isran found something for you to do, eh? I thought he'd like you. Good luck!
Dawnguard.esm|0126BD: Well, well. You made it. Good. Isran's in the fort. He'll get you sorted out.
Dawnguard.esm|0126BE: Go on inside. Isran'll want to talk to you.
Dawnguard.esm|0126BF: You go ahead. I'll talk to Isran in a minute.
Dawnguard.esm|0126C0: That's what everybody says, right up until they find their throat being ripped out by a pack of hungry vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|0126C1: Changed your mind, huh? The Dawnguard could use somebody like you.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D1: Been a pleasure doing business with you.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D2: Tell you what, I'll sell it back to you for 4,000 coins.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D3: Finally came to your senses, eh? Realized what you gave up? Can't say I'm surprised.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D4: Back, huh? Well, price is still 4,000 coins.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D5: All right, I suppose I could do that. Here you are.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D6: No, sorry. It's 4,000 coins or no deal.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D7: Oh, very well. I don't know how you expect me to keep this place in order when I can't make a profit.
Dawnguard.esm|0126D8: Hey, if you don't like it, you could always shop for your Elder Scrolls elsewhere.
Dawnguard.esm|0126DE: And what do you plan to do with it? Do you even know what you're asking about?
Dawnguard.esm|0128A3: Seems you're the court favorite. At least, for the moment.
Dawnguard.esm|0128A4: I'm sure Lord Harkon is grateful you've returned his daughter.
Dawnguard.esm|0128A5: All this talk of Elder Scrolls and Auriel's Bow... Lord Harkon's ambition truly is great.
Dawnguard.esm|0128A6: Can we really trust the words of this Moth Priest? What if he's just lying to save his worthless skin?
Dawnguard.esm|0128A7: Lord Harkon will finally have his wish... to see the prophecy fulfilled. Then we will all benefit.
Dawnguard.esm|0128A8: First Serana, now you find a Moth Priest. I'm sure Lord Harkon is very pleased with you... for now.
Dawnguard.esm|0128FA: Watch the recoil. Takes some getting used to.
Dawnguard.esm|0128FB: You'll get the hang of it.
Dawnguard.esm|0128FC: Take a deep breath and let it out as you fire.
Dawnguard.esm|0128FD: You need to concentrate, boy. Try it again.
Dawnguard.esm|012DC3: Get it now while we can!
Dawnguard.esm|012E82: It's around the side of the castle. Let's move.
Dawnguard.esm|012EB0: Harkon's short-sightedness was his undoing. In his lust for power, he failed to consider the full consequences of his plans.
Dawnguard.esm|012EB1: Can you imagine it? A world without a sun... we would all have been doomed, vampires and men alike.
Dawnguard.esm|012EB2: Once again, the Elder Scrolls have proven their value.
Dawnguard.esm|012EB9: All right. I'll meet you at Darkfall Cave.
Dawnguard.esm|012EBA: All right. I'll meet you at the glade.
Dawnguard.esm|012EBC: Oh. All right then. You know where to find me.
Dawnguard.esm|012F4D: Of course they did. I never doubted for a moment. If I may ask, what will you do with them now?
Dawnguard.esm|012F4E: Ah, yes. I see. Well, if you do change your mind, please let me know.
Dawnguard.esm|012F50: Oh my, that's... Well, that's not exactly what I had in mind. Still, if it means their safe-keeping, I suppose it is worth it.
Dawnguard.esm|012F51: This has certainly been quite the adventure, hasn't it?
Dawnguard.esm|012F52: I'd very much like to have those scrolls. Someday I may be able to return them to their rightful place in the Imperial City. How would you feel about turning them over to me?
Dawnguard.esm|012F53: Yes, certainly. Are you willing to let me have them?
Dawnguard.esm|012F54: I did, in fact. Quite perceptive of you.
Dawnguard.esm|012F55: Oh dear. No, I'm afraid that's not going to work at all. I have an alternative for you, if you'd be interested.
Dawnguard.esm|012F56: No? Well, I've given it a bit of thought.
Dawnguard.esm|012F57: Ah, yes. Hmm. I'm afraid they don't quite work that way. Might I offer an alternative?
Dawnguard.esm|012F5C: Pitiful. Simply pitiful. I expected more from the defiant fool that beguiled my daughter. No matter. Serana will sacrifice her blood for the sake of the prophecy, and I will feast upon yours in Molag Bal's honor!
Dawnguard.esm|012F8A: My apologies. I shall not trouble you with it again.
Dawnguard.esm|012F8B: Then I think I shall see to the repairs. Thank you for your time.
Dawnguard.esm|012F8D: Now that the Castle is yours, I was wondering. Would you be interested in having the passage to the Courtyard restored? Harkon clearly never cared much for it.
Dawnguard.esm|012F8E: Mind yourself. There's a vicious bear on the loose.
Dawnguard.esm|012F8F: What would mudcrabs want with my satchel, anyway?
Dawnguard.esm|012F90: Just one gyro. One, and I can get back to work. Where are they?
Dawnguard.esm|012F91: I knew it was a matter of time before the vampires would come for us.
Dawnguard.esm|012FE6: I'll stick around this place if you need me.
Dawnguard.esm|012FE7: I'll be at Castle Volkihar if you need me.
Dawnguard.esm|012FE8: I'll be around here if you need me.
Dawnguard.esm|012FE9: All right. I'll meet you back at the castle.
Dawnguard.esm|01300D: Gods, you're one of them! Get yourself cured before you think about asking me to help you again.
Dawnguard.esm|01300E: Oh, no. No way. I'm not helping a vampire. Get out of here, get yourself cured.
Dawnguard.esm|01300F: You're infected? Get away from me. Get yourself cured, or don't come near Fort Dawnguard again.
Dawnguard.esm|013010: You've managed to turn into one of them. Wonderful. I won't kill you, but you'd better get yourself cured.
Dawnguard.esm|013011: I'll not aid a vampire. Not even you. Get yourself cured, or you're dead to me.
Dawnguard.esm|013284: It will be done, my lord!
Dawnguard.esm|01328C: In my language, the Qahnaarin is the Vanquisher, the one who has bested a fellow dovah in battle.
Dawnguard.esm|01328D: Free? No. I have been here too long, Qahnaarin. The Soul Cairn has become a part of what I am. I can never fully call Tamriel my home again, or I would surely perish. I only hope that you will allow me the precious moments of time there through your call.
Dawnguard.esm|01328E: There was a time when I called Tamriel my home, but those days have long since passed. The dovah roamed the skies, vying for their small slices of territory that resulted in immense and ultimately fatal battles.
Dawnguard.esm|01328F: I discovered too late that the Ideal Masters favor deception over honor and had no intention of releasing me from my binding. They had control of my mind, but fortunately they couldn't possess my soul.
Dawnguard.esm|013290: I was. But unlike some of my brethren, I sought solutions outside the norm in order to maintain my superiority. I began to explore what the dovah call "Alok-Dilon," the ancient forbidden art that you call necromancy.
Dawnguard.esm|013291: The Ideal Masters assured me that my powers would be unmatched, that I could raise legions of the undead. In return, I was to serve them as a Keeper until the death of the one who calls herself Valerica.
Dawnguard.esm|013292: Even in the Soul Cairn, the defeat of the World Eater has reached my ears, Dovahkiin. You may not be dovah, but the defeat of Alduin earns you the right of title.
Dawnguard.esm|013293: You are the Dovahkiin, the Dragonborn. You may not be one of us in body, but you have earned the right to bear this title.
Dawnguard.esm|013295: Forgive me, my instinct was to grant you this title. I am uncertain why. Perhaps one day it will become clear to both of us.
Dawnguard.esm|01329B: I will share that knowledge with you gladly, master. What do you wish to know?
Dawnguard.esm|01329D: Well, as far as I'm aware it's the only one in existence. The previous owners of the castle had a sundial in the courtyard, and obviously that didn't appeal to my mother. She persuaded an elven artisan to make some improvements. You can see the plates that show the phases of the moons, Masser and Secunda.
Dawnguard.esm|01329E: Hard to say. Maybe if we found the missing crests, we could figure it out.
Dawnguard.esm|01329F: That's the thing... what's the point of a moondial? I always wondered why she didn't just have the whole thing ripped out. But she loved it. I don't know. I guess it's like having a piece of art, if you're into that sort of thing.
Dawnguard.esm|0132AB: Dragons breathing fire in the sky. Vampires brazenly attacking people on the street. It's the end of the world I tell you.
Dawnguard.esm|0132AC: Where're these vampires coming from? We need to wipe them out before things get worse.
Dawnguard.esm|0132AD: They say if a vampire so much as scratches you, you'll turn into one. That better not be true.
Dawnguard.esm|0132B7: Ah, so that was a Moth Priest, then? He rode through town not long ago with an escort of soldiers. They didn't stop, just headed south across the Dragon Bridge.
Dawnguard.esm|0132B8: I don't know what a Moth Priest is, but I did see an old man in a robe not long ago. He was riding in a wagon with some Imperial guards. They didn't stop to visit, though. They rode through town heading south, and went across the big bridge. It was only just a little bit ago. I bet you can catch them if you hurry up.
Dawnguard.esm|0132B9: A Moth Priest? I'm sorry, but I don't know anything about that. Try asking one of the guards.
