Dawnguard.esm|0132C4: We need to do something about these vampire attacks.
Dawnguard.esm|0132CB: These vampires are becoming a real menace.
Dawnguard.esm|0133AD: All right, now pull your lever!
Dawnguard.esm|0133AE: Hang on... could you pull that again? It looks like there's another lever over on the other side. Stay here. I'll go check it out if you open the door back up.
Dawnguard.esm|0136BA: We both know that a band of fools with crossbows is no match for a scion of the ancient blood. I admit, I do not entirely understand your sense of humor.
Dawnguard.esm|0136DE: They are determined and well-armed, but foolish if they think they can defy us. We will deal with them in time.
Dawnguard.esm|013707: I wish I could have been there to hear the sounds of their screams.
Dawnguard.esm|013708: Do not presume to tell me who I can and cannot trust. I possess the wisdom of a dozen lifetimes, and I will make my own judgements. Now be silent, and hear what I have to say.
Dawnguard.esm|013721: Not as much as I would like. Even after spending decades of my life in study of the Elder Scrolls, they remain a great mystery to me. It's true for most of my brothers in the order as well. We don't really know where the scrolls came from or who wrote them. What we do know is that some are born with the special gift to read the Elder Scrolls. These chosen few can divine events of the past and future within the scrolls, though in time, it drives us to blindness. I'm afraid there's not much more to tell. Even today, the Elder Scrolls remain a great enigma.
Dawnguard.esm|013722: It is the great spire at the heart of the Imperial City, among the tallest towers in all of Tamriel. The lower floors are home to the Imperial Council, and the Order of Moth Priests occupies the middle floors. The tower suffered damage in the recent war with the Aldmeri Dominion, and is not what it once was. I hope one day to see it restored to its former glory.
Dawnguard.esm|013723: It is our sacred duty to study the Elder Scrolls. We read these ancient scrolls of prophecy in order to divine the future, and we use that knowledge to aid the Emperor. Sometimes we venture out into the world in search of more scrolls for our library at the White-Gold Tower. That is the very purpose that first brought me to Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|013763: What else would you like to know?
Dawnguard.esm|013764: Good. Strong instincts and a cunning mind will serve you as well as blade, spell or claw.
Dawnguard.esm|01376F: Who knows, maybe it has a death wish. Maybe it's just insane. I don't really care.
Dawnguard.esm|013770: Set my hatred aside? Not a chance. It's what keeps me strong.
Dawnguard.esm|013771: You'd better know what you're doing.
Dawnguard.esm|013788: I thought you said you were happy together. I'm not going to be the one to destroy a marriage. I'm sorry.
Dawnguard.esm|013789: If you're not happy with things... that's not my business. I'm not going to blamed for whatever happens.
Dawnguard.esm|01378A: You're sweet to say that, but you need to sort things out on your own. It's not something I'm going to get involved with.
Dawnguard.esm|01378B: You're sweet. And... I'm not stupid. I can see what you're getting at. But... that's just not something I'm going to be able to do. Ever. Given how I've lived... what I've done... I can't ask the gods for a blessing like that.
Dawnguard.esm|01378C: All right. If you say so.
Dawnguard.esm|01378D: Don't you already have someone at home waiting for you?
Dawnguard.esm|01378E: Look, you're great. Really. But I just don't think that's for us. Especially not me. I mean... with my history, I still kind of get a chill just walking by a temple. Can't imagine going in one.
Dawnguard.esm|01378F: Look, I care about you. A lot. And I'll come with you wherever you want to go. But... with everything in my past, I don't think I could bring myself to go into a temple and ask for that kind of blessing. It's just... it's not for me.
Dawnguard.esm|013790: Let's just pretend you didn't, all right?
Dawnguard.esm|013791: I think we've talked about that enough. Come on, now.
Dawnguard.esm|013792: Oh, I get it. Is that why you're wearing an Amulet of Mara?
Dawnguard.esm|0137BF: From what I saw in the vision, the Elder Scroll which foreshadows the defiance of the gods with the blood of mortals is the key to the prophecy.
Dawnguard.esm|0138C1: Is it really you? I can't believe it!
Dawnguard.esm|013C49: Be sure to enjoy some of our Redwater Skooma before you leave.
Dawnguard.esm|013C4A: Don't leave, you'd have better dreams here...
Dawnguard.esm|013C4C: Heh... wait, you're serious? This is the Redwater Den, purveyors of the best Skooma outside of Balmora.
Dawnguard.esm|013DF9: Leave it to my mother... always smarter than I gave her credit for.
Dawnguard.esm|013DFB: I don't think we've reached the top yet. I'd bet there's some kind of secret passage around here.
Dawnguard.esm|013E08: Well, you saved me from those vampires. I suppose the least that I can do is hear you out.
Dawnguard.esm|013E09: You do? Alright then, enough mysteries.
Dawnguard.esm|013E69: This "stranger" has done more for me in the brief time I've known her than you've done in centuries!
Dawnguard.esm|013E6F: Good thing the boss heard that old farmer running his mouth off in the tavern about finding this place.
Dawnguard.esm|013E70: The water from the spring makes the skooma so much more potent, we'll have all the thralls we'll ever need.
Dawnguard.esm|013E71: That's the last of them. Poor sods can't resist after they get a taste of your dosed Skooma.
Dawnguard.esm|013E72: The secret is our mystic Redwater Spring. The magic in the waters opens the mind to experience Skooma in a way like never before.
Dawnguard.esm|013E73: Hey there, friend. If you're looking for a fix, you're at the right place. Just head on down the trap door.
Dawnguard.esm|013E74: I got nothing on me. If you're looking for Skooma, head on downstairs.
Dawnguard.esm|013E75: If you're buying, go on in. Otherwise, shove off.
Dawnguard.esm|013FFE: Once I get it done, first I'll publish a regular edition, then the folio. I'll be rich!
Dawnguard.esm|013FFF: "Saviour of Morrowind." Hm, that's good but it sounds too narrow of scope...
Dawnguard.esm|014000: I wonder how many copies I'll sell? Hundreds? Thousands? I'll hire the finest bookbinder I can find.
Dawnguard.esm|014001: Now what was it I said to that prisoner? "Stand up, there you go. You were dreaming..." Yes, that was it.
Dawnguard.esm|014002: Was it "Between the forces of good and evil."? No, no... that's not it...
Dawnguard.esm|014003: Did I start with my ride on the prison ship, or was it before that?
Dawnguard.esm|014004: "And without any regard for my safety, I fearless charged the enemy!" No, much too dramatic...
Dawnguard.esm|014005: I can't believe I lost those pages.
Dawnguard.esm|014006: Hm. Did I call it "The Great Hunt" or was it just "The Hunt?" Bah, I can't remember!
Dawnguard.esm|014007: When I rewrite those pages... they'll see what they missed.
Dawnguard.esm|014008: Was it "Having the adoration of thousands..." No, wait. I could make it "the adoration of tens of thousands."
Dawnguard.esm|014009: Damn it. How could I be so foolish?
Dawnguard.esm|014039: Since it's your first time... sure. Find a booth and enjoy.
Dawnguard.esm|01403A: Well... so long as you don't tell anyone. Here take it and slip into a booth before someone notices.
Dawnguard.esm|01403B: How about you pay for your habit like everybody else. We're not running a charity here.
Dawnguard.esm|01403C: Ah, welcome, welcome! I don't believe we've seen you here before, have we? You can buy from me, then join any booth you'd like.
Dawnguard.esm|01403D: You seem tense. Join a booth and relax a bit. Or did you need another fix?
Dawnguard.esm|01403E: Go on downstairs. You're bothering me.
Dawnguard.esm|01414E: I had just moved from my ancestral home in Morrowind to the continent of Cyrodiil. I settled in the city of Kvatch to write my memoirs and to find some peace and quiet.
Dawnguard.esm|01414F: Soul what? What in the name of Vivec are you talking about? All I remember is that damn dremora and his minions bursting into my home and sending me to their prison.
Dawnguard.esm|014150: Indeed I was. I hunted down and killed every last one of them. Took years. The people of Morrowind were so grateful to have the roads safer again, they took to calling me "Saint Jiub" and then the other names followed.
Dawnguard.esm|014151: Exactly. He got to my target first and called the guard to cover his tracks. By the time I got there, the place was on alert. Walked right into a trap, and didn't even realize it until I was captured.
Dawnguard.esm|014152: And you think I do? Humph.
Dawnguard.esm|014153: Oh, this is simply getting infuriating!
Dawnguard.esm|014154: Well, I don't know. Everything seemed fine until that dremora attacked me. I only heard later about the gates from the other souls. I can only assume the gates have been closed by now.
Dawnguard.esm|014155: Terrible, simply terrible. I was in the midst of writing the second volume and they just threw me into this prison. It's unfair!
Dawnguard.esm|014156: All I heard is that without a Septim sitting on the throne of the Empire, the Dragonfires in a place called the Temple of the One were no longer lit. This meant that we were vulnerable to the forces of Oblivion.
Dawnguard.esm|014157: I can't say that I'm surprised by your answer. Until I get my second volume published, no one will ever know of my achievements.
Dawnguard.esm|014158: Yes. I'm speaking of the Cliff Racer of course. Wretched things. They used to criss-cross the skies over Morrowind like vermin. Nasty flying creatures with a habit of sneaking up on the unwary.
Dawnguard.esm|014159: Finally! I never thought I'd see these again. These pages meant the world to me. Thank you!
Dawnguard.esm|01415A: Well, that's a ridiculous question. Everyone traveling through here has said that the Oblivion Gates have been opening all over Tamriel.
Dawnguard.esm|01415B: After I was freed from prison, I decided to turn my life around. Give something back to the people of Morrowind. It became quickly apparent that the best way I could help would be by wiping out the cliff racers.
Dawnguard.esm|01415C: Well, I assume so. It cast a spell at me from some kind of strange black crystal. Next thing I knew, I arrived here.
Dawnguard.esm|01415D: Ah, yes. Saint Jiub, Jiub the Eradicator, Jiub the Magnificent... I've earned a lot of those titles thanks to my campaign against the Winged Menace.
Dawnguard.esm|01415E: Very well. I suppose a moment or two of my time couldn't hurt. I am Jiub. Some call me Saint Jiub. Others call me Jiub the Eradicator. Perhaps you've heard of me?
Dawnguard.esm|01415F: No... all these wasted months, or has it been years? I don't even remember how much time has passed. Now my work will never see the light of day. My name will be forgotten.
Dawnguard.esm|014160: Hmph. I tell you what's crazy. How about sitting in your home, minding your own business and then suddenly getting attacked by a dremora and his cronies.
Dawnguard.esm|014161: How does anyone expect me to write my opus with all of these rude interruptions?
Dawnguard.esm|014162: Oh, I see. You've decided to cease pursuing your busy schedule and wish to talk now. How kind.
Dawnguard.esm|014163: Maybe you should leave the writing to an expert and stop making foolish suggestions.
Dawnguard.esm|014164: Oh, wait a moment. Here, I want you to have these before you go. A pristine copy of my first volume. Only one in print, actually. And take this as well. Seeing as Saint Jiub has ascended to pure spiritual form, I won't need it any longer.
Dawnguard.esm|014165: That damn dremora and his minions. They didn't even give me a chance to explain who I was.
Dawnguard.esm|014166: You did it! You have all of my pages!
Dawnguard.esm|014167: That's over half of the pages found. Keep it up!
Dawnguard.esm|014168: Half the pages, eh? You're getting there.
Dawnguard.esm|014169: Still over half the pages are missing. Keep trying.
Dawnguard.esm|01416A: Any luck finding my pages yet?
Dawnguard.esm|01416B: You again? I thought it was clear I was busy.
Dawnguard.esm|01416D: That was a dark time in my life. I did a lot of things I ended up regretting. Maybe that's why I worked so hard to redeem myself once I was freed.
Dawnguard.esm|01416E: Perhaps. Do you have a way out of here?
Dawnguard.esm|01416F: A book? I'd hardly call a twenty-six volume epic simply a "book." Surely you've heard of "The Rise and Fall of Saint Jiub the Eradicator: Hero of Morrowind and Savior of the Dunmer."
Dawnguard.esm|014170: I murdered others for money. And before you think I belonged to some romantic or secretive guild, think again. I was simply an addict who needed money for skooma. The stuff wasn't cheap, and murder always seemed to be in demand.
Dawnguard.esm|014171: Maybe I can't leave, but my writings can. In order to write the second volume of my opus, I need the notes from my first volume. Otherwise I need to do all of this from memory.
Dawnguard.esm|014172: I finally bit off more than I could chew. I was paid to assassinate some high-ranking official from House Redoran. What I didn't know is someone had hired the Morag Tong to kill him as well.
Dawnguard.esm|014173: Hey, where are you going. I need those pages!
Dawnguard.esm|014174: At this rate, my first volume will be complete. Don't give up.
Dawnguard.esm|014175: Thanks again for searching. I don't know what I'd do without my pages.
Dawnguard.esm|014177: Leaving huh? Good. Maybe I can get some work done.
Dawnguard.esm|014179: Has it been that long? Oh my. Well, it started with the followers of Mehrunes Dagon having Emperor Uriel Septim VII assassinated. Without a Septim on the throne of the Empire, Dagon was able to leave Oblivion through the gates and attack.
Dawnguard.esm|01417A: Now, volume two begins! What else?
Dawnguard.esm|01417B: When I was tossed in here I felt myself falling... and I dropped the pages I had been holding. There were ten of them in all. Find them for me, please, I beg you!
Dawnguard.esm|01417C: They tried me for murder, threw me on a prison ship and sent me to the jail in Vivec City. Gave me a lot of time to think, that's for sure.
Dawnguard.esm|01417D: "What do I mean," indeed! If I hadn't lost all of the pages, I wouldn't be in this predicament and we wouldn't be having this conversation.
Dawnguard.esm|014202: Keep quiet! We're hunting vampires spotted in the area.
Dawnguard.esm|014204: Keep an eye out and let us know if you see any vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|014205: Well, if you do just let us know. We'll be in this area until we find them.
Dawnguard.esm|014206: We're looking for some Vampires that have been reported in the area. Seen any?
Dawnguard.esm|014207: Fellow hunter, we're looking for some Vampires that have been reported in the area. Have you seen any?
Dawnguard.esm|014208: Oh no! What have I done?
Dawnguard.esm|014298: This is the most peace and quiet I've had in a lifetime. There's no one around to disturb me and distract me from my writing. Present company excepted of course.
Dawnguard.esm|014299: Considering the state I'm in, I'd say that option vanished a long time ago. Besides this isn't so bad when you think about it.
Dawnguard.esm|01429A: Indeed! One moment I was writing diligently, and the next, my door bursts open. It was a cadre of dremora. The city was under attack. I took it upon myself to join the cause and fight the dremora, thinking this would simply be another feather in my cap.
Dawnguard.esm|01429B: Now that the first volume's complete, there's no much left to do but begin work on the second. There are plenty of other adventures I've had over the years, but you'll just have to read my next work to find out.
Dawnguard.esm|01429C: I'm still hard at work on the second volume of my epic.
Dawnguard.esm|01429D: Come to visit this lost soul again?
Dawnguard.esm|01429E: Back to visit Jiub the Magnificent, eh?
Dawnguard.esm|01429F: I hope you've read the first volume of my epic by now.
Dawnguard.esm|0142A0: I miss my homeland, but I can't say that I miss all the noise!
Dawnguard.esm|0142A1: The reason I'm standing in front of you as only a whisper of my former self should tell you that. I never knew what became of Kvatch. I wonder if they were triumphant without me.
Dawnguard.esm|0142A2: When you get back to Tamriel, let them know I still exist.
Dawnguard.esm|0142A4: Only thirty more pages and my next volume will be complete.
Dawnguard.esm|0142A5: If you think of any juicy details to add to my work, let me know!
Dawnguard.esm|0142D7: You test my patience with these questions. That is a personal matter, and one I will not discuss.
Dawnguard.esm|0142D8: Serana and her mother disappeared long ago. I commanded every vampire in the court to look for them, but after centuries of searching without success, I lost hope. In my heart, I know that it was my wife, Valerica, who took my beloved daughter away from me. If I ever see her again, she will pay most dearly for that betrayal.
Dawnguard.esm|0142D9: Her name was Valerica. She has been gone for so long now, I can only assume she has left this world, one way or another. I will say only that we did not part on good terms.
Dawnguard.esm|0142DA: I will banish you from this castle. I will spare your life this once, but after that, you are prey.
Dawnguard.esm|0142DC: Yes, I can smell it on you. The power of my blood will purge that filth and make you whole again.
Dawnguard.esm|0142DE: You contracted a disease, perhaps, but you are no true vampire. Accept my gift and I promise that you will learn the difference. Refuse me, and I will banish you from this castle. I will spare your life this once, but after that, you are prey.
Dawnguard.esm|0142DF: You dare walk away from me? In my own hall, before my own court, you dare insult me?
Dawnguard.esm|0142E0: We have not finished speaking yet. Come back at once.
Dawnguard.esm|01432E: That make you feel any better?
Dawnguard.esm|014330: I've waited so long for this moment. We found it. At last.
Dawnguard.esm|014332: But... it's hopeless. I died here, just like all the others. Turn back. Turn back before you become the next victim.
Dawnguard.esm|014335: I spent my life tracking down that legend... until my damn apprentice stole everything from me, and published it all under his own name. And now... now I can't rest. Not until I have proof. Something to show the world what I discovered, what I did. That my life... mattered.
Dawnguard.esm|014337: Careful now... this area's not stable.
Dawnguard.esm|014341: You've given me a great gift friend. The night air hums and the shadows are full of color. Make use of my coffin whenever you like. My home is your home.
Dawnguard.esm|014342: Great gift of blood you've given me. Blood that boils in my veins. Gives me strength. You can use my coffin whenever you like.
Dawnguard.esm|014343: My brother in darkness. While it's good to see you, we'll keep up appearances, yes?
Dawnguard.esm|014344: My sister in darkness. While it's good to see you, we'll keep up appearances, yes?
Dawnguard.esm|014345: We are blood bound, but we can never be too careful. Never know who might be watching, right?
Dawnguard.esm|014346: Blood of the sick. Blood of the weak. Blood of the unsuspecting noble lord. Savor each flavor. Unsuspecting, delicious prey.
Dawnguard.esm|014347: I keep an eye on their comings and goings. No one suspects poor Cairine. Poor, poor Cairine. Ha!
Dawnguard.esm|014348: The darkness hums and we sing its praises. Sweet melodies. Sweet like the water that flows through their veins.
Dawnguard.esm|014349: Haran manages the inn in Winterhold. A forsaken place under the shadow of the mages guild. She will help us keep an eye on the mages.
Dawnguard.esm|01434A: Valindor of Riften has his ears in places that we'd love to listening in. Joining our family should increase our possibilities in the south.
Dawnguard.esm|01434B: A pathetic creature in her present condition, but the gift will embolden her. And her lowly state suits her to being our eyes and ears in Markarth.
Dawnguard.esm|014360: Do you have a moment? Gunmar and I have been talking and, well, we're slightly worried. We both realized that if Isran's even allowed us in here, he must be really concerned. And if he's that concerned, the situation must be pretty bad. Make sense?
Dawnguard.esm|014361: Florentius? Really? I assumed Isran would want nothing to do with him.
Dawnguard.esm|014362: Well, that's the thing. We don't know where he is. Haven't seen him in years. I think he had regular contact with the Vigilants, and I know Isran kept track of them... So maybe you could ask Isran if he knows anything? Just keep in mind that he... well, he might not like the idea.
Dawnguard.esm|014363: Whatever is required! I am happy to do whatever is asked of me. I only wish to be useful. Often I take on tasks that Garan or Feran find distasteful, or... beneath them. If I can remain useful, those above me will see fit to keep me here.
Dawnguard.esm|014364: He's a priest of Arkay. Well, he was. It's... it's complicated. He's a little eccentric, but we can trust him and we could definitely use his skills.
Dawnguard.esm|014365: Yes. These vampires are a new threat, and a truly deadly one. Gunmar and I agree that we're going to need Florentius to help. Gunmar and I have a lot of work to do here, so we were hoping that maybe you could track him down.
Dawnguard.esm|0145DA: As you've been traveling in the Soul Cairn, your body has become attuned to it. Let's just say a tiny part of you rubbed off on it, and in its place, a bit of the Soul Cairn filled the void. You should find no difficulty using the portal any longer.
Dawnguard.esm|014731: Excellent. I trust he'll find his situation more accommodating, now that he is a member of the family.
Dawnguard.esm|014732: Your wife is such a soul, and the stars appear cooperative. Now is your chance.
Dawnguard.esm|014733: Your husband is such a soul, and the stars appear cooperative. Now is your chance.
Dawnguard.esm|014734: Return to me when your part is done. And may you enjoy your second nuptials.
Dawnguard.esm|014735: I don't feel... right... I'm going to lay down... for awhile...
Dawnguard.esm|014736: Something's happened, I can't quite remember...
Dawnguard.esm|014737: Our blood grants us power to calm and relax. If you currently lack the gift, forgo feeding for a day and that power will come naturally. Once your thrall is suitably sedated, you will be able to impart the gift without resistance.
Dawnguard.esm|014738: My best beloved! I never knew the night held such vibrant color, or that the air vibrated so. And at times, it's as if the very shadows whisper. It is a cherished gift you have given me. To see the world this way, through your eyes. It will only bring us closer.
Dawnguard.esm|01473B: I've been performing a little research on the Blood Chalice. I believe we can prolong its salutary effects if we infuse it with potent vampiric blood.
Dawnguard.esm|01473C: The potency of the Blood Chalice is beginning to wane. We must infuse it with more power if we are to keep it's fullest effects.
Dawnguard.esm|01473D: I've located additional petrified remains of an elder vampire. Bring them to me, and I'll extend the effects granted by the Blood Chalice.
Dawnguard.esm|014744: I see. You're simply here to stop the Tyranny of the Sun, despite the cost to Serana. Nothing else matters.
Dawnguard.esm|014747: Coming from one who murders vampires as a trade, I find it hard to believe your intentions are noble.
Dawnguard.esm|014771: The boss said it's some sort of profaned spring. It looks like blood, but it's not the real thing.
Dawnguard.esm|014772: So why don't you just drink the blood from the spring? I mean, that seems easier than running a skooma den...
Dawnguard.esm|014B44: He claims to serve loyally, but he really covets the throne. He sees himself in charge someday. His ambition will justify any actions. Put your faith in him, and you will regret it, I promise you.
Dawnguard.esm|014B45: Be careful who you trust. Not all of us have the court's best interests at heart. Orthjolf, for example. He'll tell you he means well, but believe me, he can't be trusted at all. Keep that in mind, will you?
Dawnguard.esm|014B46: You'll be a welcome addition to the court, I'm sure. If I may, let me offer you a piece of advice.
Dawnguard.esm|014B47: Simple. He says 'kill', I kill. Sometimes he doesn't even have to say it.
Dawnguard.esm|014B48: New blood, eh? Let's get something very clear. I don't care about politics. You want to fight over the throne with the rest of them, go right ahead. I feed when I'm hungry, I sleep when I'm tired, and I kill whenever I'm in the mood. Those are the things that are important to me.
Dawnguard.esm|014B51: This vampire showed up while you were away. I'm guessing it's the one you found in Dimhollow Crypt. Says it's got something really important to say to you.
Dawnguard.esm|014BA6: Katria? No, no that's not possible! You... you know too much. I can't afford to let you live.
Dawnguard.esm|014BA7: I'm on my way to Markarth to confer with my colleague, Calcelmo. I've no time to stop and chat.
Dawnguard.esm|014BA8: Then I'll take it from your corpse! Kill him!
Dawnguard.esm|014BA9: Then I'll take it from your corpse! Kill her!
Dawnguard.esm|014BAA: Taron Dreth, the world's foremost expert in Dwemer metallurgy. Not that you would know anything about that.
Dawnguard.esm|014BAB: What? You... you found the Forge? The Aetherium Forge?
Dawnguard.esm|014BAC: Your crown... gods, it's made of Aetherium! Pure Aetherium!
Dawnguard.esm|014BAD: Your staff... gods, it's made of Aetherium! Pure Aetherium!
Dawnguard.esm|014BAE: Your shield... gods, it's made of Aetherium! Pure Aetherium!
Dawnguard.esm|014BAF: Really? You had to take the armor? Come on, leave me with a little dignity...
Dawnguard.esm|014BB2: Taron Dreth. The world's foremost expert on Dwemer forging techniques. Now answer my question!
Dawnguard.esm|014BB3: No answer? Then I'll take it from your corpse!
Dawnguard.esm|014BB4: Where did you find it? Tell me!
Dawnguard.esm|014BB8: You need anything, talk to Master Dreth.
Dawnguard.esm|014C45: You're new here, so I'll explain this once. You feed from the thralls as much as you like. You kill them, and I'll split you in half.
Dawnguard.esm|014C46: No, and if you're smart you won't either. I know he's one of your kind and all, but he's only looking out for himself. You'll think you're best of friends, until you find his fangs in your neck.
Dawnguard.esm|014C47: Of course not. He's an elf, isn't he? He'll cozy up to you, use his honeyed words to get you to lower your guard, then use you and throw you away when he's done with you. You watch yourself around him.
Dawnguard.esm|014C48: Another elf, I see. I'm sure Vingalmo will be sizing you up as we speak, trying to figure if you're friend or foe. He'll tell you what a monster I am, that I can't be trusted... You mark my words, it's him you need to worry about. If it'll get him closer to being in charge, he'll slit your throat without a moment's thought.
Dawnguard.esm|014C49: Good to see a new face here. Especially one that isn't attached to an elf. Between you and me, there's too many of them. Can't trust the lot of them; never could. Want my advice? Avoid getting wrapped up in Vingalmo's schemes. He's after the throne, and he'll kill anyone that gets in his way.
Dawnguard.esm|014C4A: All over Skyrim. It's my job to collect them. I try and keep something for everyone. For every taste, I mean. Sometimes you get one who comes willingly, but most have to be convinced to join us here.
Dawnguard.esm|014CA2: Now that you've retrieved the Elder Scroll, you should be on your way.
Dawnguard.esm|014EA3: Aye, I was. Didn't progress very far. Never did care for their attitude, acting like they're better than everyone else. But none of that matters now.
Dawnguard.esm|014EA4: Can you feel it? You're now so much more than you were before. Time was I could barely hold my own in the Companions. Now I could slaughter them all without a second thought. And I'm not half the vampire Lord Harkon is. Truth is I'm better with a forge than I am with claws or fangs.
Dawnguard.esm|014ED3: Alas, the vampire can cloud the minds of those he preys upon. It won't be easy to convince anyone he's other than he appears. The law will require proof that we can't present. And Jarls disapprove of bloodshed they haven't called for themselves. Best not to damage our reputation. Let your actions be as discrete as possible.
Dawnguard.esm|014ED4: Vampires enjoy stalking their prey. Passing among us undetected emboldens them. Makes them feel powerful, untouchable. But we'll put a swift end to that.
Dawnguard.esm|014ED6: You must be clever. Find a way not to be caught or accused of murder. You'll figure something out. We must be vigilant and self sufficient if we are to defeat these monsters.
Dawnguard.esm|014EF0: Have to say, I'm glad that's over.
Dawnguard.esm|014EF1: There you are. Give me a hand with this door, would you?
Dawnguard.esm|014EF2: Come on, I think it's over here.
Dawnguard.esm|014EF3: I mentioned that there was one more danger. Here it is.
Dawnguard.esm|014EF5: Thanks again for all your help.
Dawnguard.esm|014EF6: We meet again. That was quite the trek.
Dawnguard.esm|014EFA: Could you get this for me? I don't exactly have a lockpick...
Dawnguard.esm|014EFF: Well, that's the second one - we're halfway there. I'll keep looking. See you soon!
Dawnguard.esm|014F00: That's, what, your third? All right, only one left to go. I'll meet you there.
Dawnguard.esm|014F01: That's the last one, isn't it? It's... it's almost time. I'll meet you at the Forge!
Dawnguard.esm|014F03: Don't just stand there! Come on!
Dawnguard.esm|014F06: Hmph. Beat me to it, this time.
Dawnguard.esm|014F07: Please, turn back... before it's too late...
Dawnguard.esm|014F08: Really? Well, now, that make things easier...
Dawnguard.esm|014F0A: Well, go on! What are you waiting for?
Dawnguard.esm|014F3A: Welcome, Initiate. This is the Wayshrine of Illumination. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment?
Dawnguard.esm|014F3B: You've reached the Wayshrine of Learning, Initiate. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment?
Dawnguard.esm|014F3C: You've arrived at the Wayshrine of Resolution. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment?
Dawnguard.esm|014F3D: You've found the Wayshrine of Radiance, Initiate. Are you prepared to honor the mantras of Auri-El and fill your vessel with His enlightenment?
Dawnguard.esm|014F3E: Come forward. You have nothing to fear here.
Dawnguard.esm|014F3F: Then behold Auri-El's gift, my child. May it light your path as you seek tranquility within the Inner Sanctum.
Dawnguard.esm|014F40: Auri-El bless you, child. For you are a step closer to the Inner Sanctum and everlasting wisdom.
Dawnguard.esm|014F41: Then go forth, child. May the enrichment of Auri-El strengthen your resolve as you undertake your journey to the Inner Sanctum.
Dawnguard.esm|014F42: May the blessings of Auri-El protect you as you climb the road to the Inner Sanctum and final enlightenment.
Dawnguard.esm|014F43: May Auri-El's brilliance illuminate your path.
Dawnguard.esm|014F44: May Auri-El's warmth imbue your body with strength.
Dawnguard.esm|014F45: May Auri-El's glow shield you from your enemy.
Dawnguard.esm|014F46: May Auri-El's radiance fill your heart with joy.
Dawnguard.esm|014F72: The moment we gave ourselves to Molag Bal, things got really icy between them. They were both drunk with power, and pulling in different directions. Then he found that prophecy, and... that was it.
Dawnguard.esm|014F73: Look around you. There are some extremely powerful undead here. Even a necromancer as seasoned as my mother would be willing to spend years trying to gain access to them.
Dawnguard.esm|014F74: No, but there's no way she would have left it in Tamriel. She wanted to get it as far away from my father as possible. I can't imagine a better place.
Dawnguard.esm|014F76: It's Durnehviir... he's here! Defend yourselves!
Dawnguard.esm|014F77: Probably to avoid whatever my father would do to her if he could get his hands on her. Or maybe her plan was to come back, but she was stuck here. We won't know until we find her.
Dawnguard.esm|014F78: I don't think anyone's ever met the Ideal Masters. I'm not even sure anyone knows what they look like. They could be underground, flying above us... They might be the ground. I have no idea.
Dawnguard.esm|014F79: Relieved... I think. All those things had been building for a while. You have no idea how long I wanted to say that to her.
Dawnguard.esm|014F7A: Well, I think it's specifically the black ones. I don't know if the Soul Cairn takes just any leftovers.
Dawnguard.esm|014F7B: Look at this place. Do you think anything would want to live here? The only things that can survive here are the Ideal Masters, the undead and the souls themselves. Well, if you want to call that "living."
Dawnguard.esm|014F7C: Just what my mother told me. I've also studied a little bit on my own, but there's not much. When something is trapped in a soul gem, and then the energy is used for powering an enchantment, the remnants are sent here.
Dawnguard.esm|014F7D: Lots of theories. Some say they feed on them like I feed on blood. Others think they use them as payment to an even higher power... almost like a currency. A very strange currency. Whatever they're doing with them, they've been harvesting for millennia. No telling how many souls are trapped here.
Dawnguard.esm|014F7E: I was. Honestly, it took me up until now to figure out that my mother was really just as bad as he was. He was obsessed with power. She was obsessed with seeing him fail. It was just so... toxic. Maybe I could have seen this coming. We could all be better off now.
Dawnguard.esm|014F7F: Exactly. It's a lost art. Most necromancers just raise up whatever bodies are nearby. A simple trick, really. Child's play. But bringing something from the Soul Cairn gives you something much more powerful.
Dawnguard.esm|014F80: Then we find out where she hid it. If she's still alive... well, as alive as she was before. Or is now. Or... you know what I mean.
Dawnguard.esm|014F82: Look, I loved my father, but when he found that prophecy... that became his life. Everything else, even me and my mother... we just became clutter. I was close with my mother, but she just kept feeding me her opinions of him, and eventually I started believing them.
Dawnguard.esm|014F83: Well, that's usually the trick. It's possible to do it through a simple portal. But to finalize the deal, you have to travel here yourself... and most of them never come back.
Dawnguard.esm|015009: Well done! I said you'd return victorious, Arkay wasn't so sure. Wield it bravely.
Dawnguard.esm|01500C: "A powerful warhammer imbued with magic that places warding runes on the ground to obliterate it's wielder's foes." Sounds useful.
Dawnguard.esm|01500D: A magical shield that when blocked with, surrounds it's wielder with a holy aura that harms the undead. Might come in handy.
Dawnguard.esm|01500E: An axe imbued with magic fueled by the destruction of the unholy. With every victorious blow against an undead creature, its power grows. Arkay warns it's baneful charge abates with each dawn. Something to keep in mind, I suppose.
Dawnguard.esm|01500F: I've been pouring over some of the tomes left here in the keep. Interesting stories, indeed. Seems the previous incarnation of the Dawnguard had, at one time, some interesting toys. Arkay and I think it could be a great advantage for us. You should look into retrieving it.
Dawnguard.esm|015010: What's that, Lord? Well, should I tell him?
Dawnguard.esm|015011: What's that, Lord? Well, should I tell her?
Dawnguard.esm|015012: Arkay says bandits. Or, wait... no, he says bandits.
Dawnguard.esm|015013: Falmer, if I understand Arkay correctly.
Dawnguard.esm|015014: Arkay says you'll be facing forsworn.
Dawnguard.esm|015016: Arkay says you'll be facing warlocks.
Dawnguard.esm|015062: Yes. They swarmed the Chantry in vast numbers until we were completely overrun. We never really stood a chance. I assume that the Arch-Curate was corrupted by them when they found a way to breach the Inner Sanctum.
Dawnguard.esm|015063: So, this is snow elf magic. Incredible.
Dawnguard.esm|015064: Correct. We only numbered perhaps a hundred at the time, so our presence remained a secret to the dwarves and the Nords. Ironically, our undoing came at the hands of our own people.
Dawnguard.esm|015066: I've often asked myself that very same question. The blinding of my race was supposedly accomplished with a toxin. Certainly not enough to devolve them into the sad and twisted beings they've become.
Dawnguard.esm|015067: There were splinter groups that resisted the agreement, and even some that sought alternate alliances. But when it was all said and done, those elves were either slaughtered, vanished or gave up and took the dwarves' bargain.
Dawnguard.esm|015068: In a manner of speaking. We had always maintained an uneasy alliance with the underground-dwelling dwarves, and when faced with extinction we turned to them for help. Surprisingly, they agreed to protect us but demanded a terrible price... the blinding of our race.
Dawnguard.esm|015069: It's symbolic. I don't expect you to understand.
Dawnguard.esm|01506A: All that just to end up dumping it out? Makes no sense to me.
Dawnguard.esm|01506B: Vyrthur and myself are the only two snow elves that remain.
Dawnguard.esm|01506C: So these Initiates had to lug around a heavy pitcher of water. Marvelous. How long would they have to do that?
Dawnguard.esm|01506E: What's that basin in the center signify?
Dawnguard.esm|01508F: I'll hang back for a bit, but you're not leaving me behind. This is too important.
Dawnguard.esm|015099: You know you can't win, so all you can do is hide...
Dawnguard.esm|0150A5: I was sure I heard something.
Dawnguard.esm|0150A9: Any moment now, I'll find you...
Dawnguard.esm|0150AB: One less monster in the world.
Dawnguard.esm|0150C1: Come now, you think you stand a chance against me?
Dawnguard.esm|0150C2: After 400 years, you think you'll be the one to take me down?
Dawnguard.esm|0150C3: I can't decide which parts of you I'd like to keep for a while...
Dawnguard.esm|0150C4: I'm going to rend you limb from limb!
Dawnguard.esm|0150C5: Every last drop of your blood is mine!
Dawnguard.esm|0150C6: You've no chance against a true vampire!
Dawnguard.esm|0150C7: Arkay and I will stand triumphant!
Dawnguard.esm|0150C8: You'll be meeting my friend Arkay very, very soon!
Dawnguard.esm|0150CA: No one is going to stand in my way!
Dawnguard.esm|0150CB: Not even the gods will show you mercy!
Dawnguard.esm|0150CC: Skyrim will be purged of your filth!
Dawnguard.esm|0150D9: Good. I knew I was sending the right man for the job.
Dawnguard.esm|0150DA: Good. I knew I was sending the right woman for the job.
Dawnguard.esm|0150DB: Precisely! The people will put pressure on the Dawnguard who are apparently murdering their fellows without provocation. Their focus will be shifted from us, giving us one less nuisance to deal with.
Dawnguard.esm|0150DC: You know, an important member of society. Someone attached to the Jarl's court, a merchant, a bard, a priest, someone with stature. That will make them all more upset to have one of their citizens slain, and under such flimsy pretense.
Dawnguard.esm|0150DF: What do you think I mean? Don't make a big scene. Don't get caught. The corpse is to be the message, not the attack.
Dawnguard.esm|0150E0: They're a splinter group of the "Vigilants of Stendarr." A bit more ambitious, it seems. The Dawnguard are apparently focused on exterminating vampires, noble and brute alike. Not a serious threat, but we can't leave them completely unchecked or they may become one.
Dawnguard.esm|0150E2: Apparently you had blood in your ears when I said not to draw attention to yourself. If I wanted a messy kill I would have said so. But a kill is a kill, and it'll send a message to the Dawnguard, if not exactly the one we wanted.
Dawnguard.esm|0150F9: For the time being, they will remain open. If remnants of our kind who escaped the betrayal at the hands of the dwarves exist out there, perhaps they will find this place one day.
Dawnguard.esm|015107: Oh yes. They were an important part of the process here. They represented the steps the Initiates took on the path to total enlightenment. Sadly, the magic used to construct these wonders were lost long before I arrived here.
Dawnguard.esm|015109: Our empire had temples to some of the other deities: Trinimac, Syrabane, Jephre and Phynaster rounded out the rest. But those temples paled in comparison to the glory of the Chantry and its wayshrines.
Dawnguard.esm|015112: No... I won't let you ruin centuries of preparations...
Dawnguard.esm|015113: This has gone on long enough.
Dawnguard.esm|0153B1: He's up there, on the balcony. Come on!
Dawnguard.esm|0153FF: The steam! Quick, shut it off!
Dawnguard.esm|015424: That part, I don't know. Once we have it, hopefully it'll be obvious.
Dawnguard.esm|015425: Auriel is one of the elven gods. He's with the rest of them in Aetherius. The way I've heard it, the sun represents the connection from our world to theirs. Supposedly, the bow draws its energy from the sun itself, which it why it shows up in that prophecy.
Dawnguard.esm|015426: Not much. If you read any history, it shows up from time to time, but it's a hard thing to track. As far as I know, though, it's never been held by a vampire. That would be a new one.
Dawnguard.esm|0156F3: Honestly, I don't think they care what a bunch of vampires would do with Auriel's Bow. They're pretty focused on the past. As long as we can get this "Vyrthur" out of the Sanctum, I think they'll be happy to just hand it over.
Dawnguard.esm|015737: Monster? What monster? Don't know what you're game is, but I'll humor for a moment. Lead the way. Take me to this "monster" of yours. But I'm warning you, do anything funny, and you'll regret it.
Dawnguard.esm|015738: What are you talking about? I don't know anything about any monsters.
Dawnguard.esm|015739: Fine. If it will get you to leave me alone. Lead the way.
Dawnguard.esm|01573A: You've mistaken me for someone who cares about you and your problems. Find someone else.
Dawnguard.esm|015744: Settle down. No need for threats. Lead the way. But I'm warning you, I'm not afraid to fight you if I have to.
Dawnguard.esm|015745: I'm no so easily coerced. I've got my eye on you. Try anything, and you will be the one who regrets it.
Dawnguard.esm|015747: I beg your pardon, ma'am. I don't know what you're getting at, but whatever it is, it's not appreciated.
Dawnguard.esm|015748: I beg your pardon, sir. I don't know what you're getting at, but whatever it is, it's not appreciated.
Dawnguard.esm|015749: A song? I, uh... I seem to have misplaced my lute. Another time perhaps?
Dawnguard.esm|01574A: My wares? I, uh... Oh so sorry, I'm all sold out.
Dawnguard.esm|01574B: Which shrine? Well, I... I'm going to them all. Yes. One at a time. Until I've visited all the shrines to all the gods in Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|01577C: With a sky like that, something bad is coming.
Dawnguard.esm|01577D: Watch yourself. The sky is a bad omen.
Dawnguard.esm|01577E: The sky is all wrong. What's happening?
Dawnguard.esm|015781: It's daytime... Why is it so dark outside?
Dawnguard.esm|015782: The sky is so dark. What does it mean?
Dawnguard.esm|015783: I can't see the sun. Where did it go?
Dawnguard.esm|015784: The sun's gone dark. What could be happening now?
Dawnguard.esm|015785: Dragons weren't enough. Now we've lost the sun too?
Dawnguard.esm|015788: The sky shouldn't look like that...
Dawnguard.esm|015789: Why does the sky look like that?
Dawnguard.esm|01578A: This weather seems all wrong to me.
Dawnguard.esm|01578B: By the Divines, what happened to the sky?
Dawnguard.esm|01578C: Look at the sky... the Divines must be angry with us!
Dawnguard.esm|01578D: What foul magic could put the sun out?
Dawnguard.esm|01578F: What's going on? Where did the sun go?
Dawnguard.esm|015790: The day is dark as night! What's happening?
Dawnguard.esm|015791: The sun is gone! We're all doomed!
Dawnguard.esm|015792: By the Eight... look at the sky!
Dawnguard.esm|015794: The tyranny of the sun is over!
Dawnguard.esm|0159FA: Hmmm, I'd say five of them would be enough.
Dawnguard.esm|0159FB: Absolutely. I've spent a great deal of time perfecting an extract that increases the potency of soul husks. If you wanted to bring some to me, I'd be happy to provide you some of the extract. It should help resist the draining aura of the crystals.
Dawnguard.esm|0159FC: I would gladly accept my fate at their hands if it robs Harkon of victory.
Dawnguard.esm|0159FD: Then I see nothing preventing my return to Tamriel. Allow me to gather some of my things and I'll head back to Castle Volkihar. And from the bottom of my heart, I thank you.
Dawnguard.esm|0159FE: Time is almost irrelevant to the Ideal Masters. I doubt very much that they'll hasten to exact revenge for freeing me from the ruins.
Dawnguard.esm|0159FF: If you think you can accomplish that feat, knowing hundreds before you have tried and failed, then I wish you the best of luck. But if this miracle were to somehow happen, I would gladly return to Tamriel.
Dawnguard.esm|015A01: As I've told you before I'm a Daughter of Coldharbour... offspring to Molag Bal. Knowing that Serana shares that blessing with me, I'm already terrified that Harkon will get his hands on her.
Dawnguard.esm|015A02: I'm putting a lot of faith in you. Don't let me down.
Dawnguard.esm|015A03: Remember, I'm not leaving until Harkon's no longer a threat.
Dawnguard.esm|015A04: Give Harkon my regards... and a dagger to his heart.
Dawnguard.esm|015A05: If anything happens to Serana, I'll have you answer for it.
Dawnguard.esm|015A06: Help yourself to anything you need in my laboratory.
Dawnguard.esm|015A07: I've planted some new herbs in the courtyard garden. I hope they'll grow.
Dawnguard.esm|015A08: It might take decades to restore the castle to its former glory... but I appear to have plenty of time.
Dawnguard.esm|015A09: Ah, it's good to be home again.
Dawnguard.esm|015A0A: I'll return to Castle Volkihar as soon as I'm finished gathering my things.
Dawnguard.esm|015A0B: You appear to have something on your mind. What is it?
Dawnguard.esm|015A0C: I trust you're keeping my daughter safe.
Dawnguard.esm|015A0D: Good to see you again. It's becoming quite lonely out here.
Dawnguard.esm|015A0E: No matter how long I've been here, I'll still never get used to this place.
Dawnguard.esm|015A0F: Slay any vampires lately have we?
Dawnguard.esm|015A11: I hope this is important, I have an experiment I'm working on.
Dawnguard.esm|015A55: That's actually only a fraction of its potential. With Sunhallowed Arrows, you would be able to produce a much more spectacular effect... causing bursts of sunlight to envelop your foes. The sunbursts would certainly hurt anything, but is especially devastating to the undead.
Dawnguard.esm|015A56: For the time being I will remain here, on the overlook and continue trying to keep the Sanctum free of the Betrayed. You're always welcome to return here at any time, of course.
Dawnguard.esm|015A57: The bow draws its power from Aetherius itself, channeling it through the sun. Therefore, when an arrow is loosed from the bow, it produces a magical effect very similar to being burned by fire.
Dawnguard.esm|015A58: There's no need for that kind of talk. I said it was possible. It's also quite possible that there are some other isolated conclaves of snow elves nestled elsewhere on Nirn. I'll also assume Vyrthur didn't exactly give you the option to stay your hand.
Dawnguard.esm|015A59: The bow was said to be carried by Auri-El himself into battle against the forces of Lorkhan in ancient and mythic times. Its craftsmanship has no equal anywhere within Tamriel and possibly beyond.
Dawnguard.esm|015A5A: If there's assistance I can provide, just ask. It's the least I can do.
Dawnguard.esm|015A5C: It's good to speak to you again.
Dawnguard.esm|015A5D: If there's anything at all I can help you with, please ask.
Dawnguard.esm|015A5E: Well, using an arrow with the bow that's been dipped in blood may cause it to function differently... corrupting its purpose. That's of course if you're foolish enough to try it.
Dawnguard.esm|015A5F: Farewell, friend. May the radiance of Auri-El fill your heart with joy.
Dawnguard.esm|015A60: Farewell, friend. May the glow of Auri-El shield you from your enemies.
Dawnguard.esm|015A61: Farewell, friend. May the warmth of Auri-El provide you strength.
Dawnguard.esm|015A62: Farewell, friend. May the light of Auri-El illuminate your darkest hours.
Dawnguard.esm|015A63: I can actually assist you in that regard. If you were to bring me some good quality elven arrows, I could imbue them with the proper incantations and rituals.
Dawnguard.esm|015A64: I'm afraid I'll need elven arrows if you want me to produce the Sunhallowed Arrows, and it seems you're all out of them at the moment.
Dawnguard.esm|015A65: Certainly. I can only do twenty at a time, but I see that won't be a problem in this case.
Dawnguard.esm|015A66: Absolutely. I can only do twenty at a time, so if you want the rest of your arrows blessed, let me know.
Dawnguard.esm|015A67: While it would fill me with joy to see the Chantry back to its former glory, that time has long since passed. Now that my brother's dead, it's quite possible I'm the last of our kind.
Dawnguard.esm|015A87: Hey, so... before we go in there...
Dawnguard.esm|015A88: Ah, it's so good to breathe again!
Dawnguard.esm|015A89: Umm... this is the part where we usually get in the boat. You with me?
Dawnguard.esm|015A8B: I think so. And thanks for asking.
Dawnguard.esm|015A8C: That's it, out there to the north. We used to keep a boat around here to get there and back.
Dawnguard.esm|015A8D: Something's not right here. Be careful. I think we may be in for some trouble.
Dawnguard.esm|015A8E: Another cave. I've spent enough time underground, myself, but if you want to go in, I'm right behind you.
Dawnguard.esm|015A8F: Nordic ruins. Even older than I am. I wonder if the draugr are as gullible as they were when I was a girl.
Dawnguard.esm|015A90: Is this a dwarven city? I can't believe they'd let it get so run down.
Dawnguard.esm|015A91: Glad you're finally listening. Come on, let's move.
Dawnguard.esm|015A92: Are you seeing this? It's fantastic!
Dawnguard.esm|015A93: I'll be with the Dawnguard if you need me.
Dawnguard.esm|015A94: Fine, don't worry about it. When we get in, you won't have to babysit about me any more.
Dawnguard.esm|015A95: Excuse me? I told you we should go to my family's home. Maybe you're finally ready to list to me.
Dawnguard.esm|015A96: This feels like the right way. I was starting to worry.
Dawnguard.esm|015A97: I know your friends would probably want to kill everything in here. I'm hoping you can show some more control than that. Once we're inside, just keep quiet for a bit. Let me take the lead.
Dawnguard.esm|015A98: What a wonderful place you've brought me.
Dawnguard.esm|015A99: So this is where your Vigilants came from? No wonder you look so lost all the time.
Dawnguard.esm|015A9A: So I'm thinking that wasn't the right way.
Dawnguard.esm|015A9B: All right, so obviously not this way, either.
Dawnguard.esm|015A9C: It's not about you! You've been so great in getting me back here.
Dawnguard.esm|015A9E: Well, we're definitely not going to find our way out from here. Do you... know what you're doing?
Dawnguard.esm|015A9F: I wanted to thank you for getting me this far. But after we get in there, I'm going to go my own way for a while.
Dawnguard.esm|015AA0: Even in this weather, it's better than the cave.
Dawnguard.esm|015AA2: Your guess is as good as mine. This place looks pretty different from when I was locked away.
Dawnguard.esm|015BF8: I hear you're doing a lot of the work in tracking down these vampires. If there's anything I can do to help, let me know.
Dawnguard.esm|015BFA: I heard what's going on. The vampires, the Dawnguard, all of it. I wanted to help, so here I am.
Dawnguard.esm|015C1B: I doubt my mother would waste her time with those fools. They probably would have tried to kill her, and that has a way of souring relationships. Any other ideas?
Dawnguard.esm|015C1E: Okay, let's head into the portal.
Dawnguard.esm|015C1F: I'm ready to go when you are.
Dawnguard.esm|015C22: Just get all of the ingredients into the vessel.
Dawnguard.esm|015C23: They shouldn't be that hard to find. Shelves, tables... should be easy to find. She wouldn't have hidden them.
Dawnguard.esm|015C24: I remember she use to keep a small journal. See if you can dig it up.
Dawnguard.esm|015C25: Turns out my mother was sneakier than I thought. I wonder what she's hiding?
Dawnguard.esm|015C26: Look around for the missing crests. Even in this mess, they should stick out.
Dawnguard.esm|015C27: I can't wait to see the courtyard again.
Dawnguard.esm|015C69: Find Sorine and Gunmar, and maybe we can do something about this mess.
Dawnguard.esm|015C6A: The Vigilants of Stendarr, in over their heads. What a surprise.
Dawnguard.esm|015C6E: May mortals quake at your approach.
Dawnguard.esm|015C75: I look forward to our next meeting.
Dawnguard.esm|015C7B: I try and meet the needs of everyone here. No reason for any of us to go hungry.
Dawnguard.esm|015C7C: No, no. You must speak with Lord Harkon, not me!
Dawnguard.esm|015C7D: I hope you find life in the castle to be to your liking.
Dawnguard.esm|015C7E: Feran is so good to me, allowing me to be his assistant.
Dawnguard.esm|015C7F: It is good to have powerful allies, is it not?
Dawnguard.esm|015C80: Look at you, wandering around causing trouble. Precious.
Dawnguard.esm|015C81: Politics bore me terribly, but they're a fact of life here.
Dawnguard.esm|015C82: Living here, I have access to magic I couldn't possibly imagine before.
Dawnguard.esm|015CD8: Woah! I think that might have been what we were waiting for. Let's head back up there and see if we can read the scrolls.
Dawnguard.esm|015CD9: Well, unless my vision's playing tricks, there seems to be some sort of magical effect around you. Seems like we're on the right track. Otherwise, I don't have the faintest idea.
Dawnguard.esm|015CDC: Ha ha. They seem quite taken to you now that you're carrying some of the tree bark. I guess we should have seen this coming... Moth Priest, moths... they're apparently related in some way. Couldn't hurt to gather more I suppose.
Dawnguard.esm|015CDD: Look at this place. No one's been here in centuries. I doubt there's any other place like it in Skyrim. It's beautiful.
Dawnguard.esm|015CDE: Well, if I were a betting woman, I'd say it's a scraper of some sort. Mother had something similar back home when she was tending the courtyard gardens to scrape moss and fungus off of the trees. That one's far nicer though... almost ceremonial.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF0: Are you... Did you get infected? Gods, don't let Isran see you like that. Look, I hear Falion in Morthal deals with this kind of thing. Go see him immediately. Otherwise, you're a threat to all of us.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF1: By the Divines... you're infected. What are you doing here? You get yourself to Morthal at once. Speak with Falion. He'll help you. But go do it now. Otherwise, you're a threat to all of us.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF2: Oh, look at you. What've you done? You've gone and gotten yourself turned into one of them. Well Arkay isn't going to intervene... not here, not now. Best point yourself towards Morthal, find a man named Falion. He can help you. Go now, please, before you lose control of yourself.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF3: How dare you set foot in here, after being infected by those foul beasts? I ought to cut you to pieces right where you stand... I'll give you a chance to redeem yourself. Go to Morthal and speak to Falion. He can help you. Otherwise you're dead to me.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF5: Get yourself cured immediately. You're a threat to all of us otherwise.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF6: You're not welcome here as long as you're one of those... monsters.
Dawnguard.esm|015CF7: You need to get cured, friend. You're a danger to the Dawnguard otherwise.
Dawnguard.esm|015E09: You have done well. It may seem cruel that we hold our thin-blooded kin in low esteem. But our needs must come before theirs.
Dawnguard.esm|015E0A: I hope you comprehend killing better than you comprehend speech. Don't. Get. Caught.
Dawnguard.esm|015E0C: I've learned the whereabouts of a troublesome Dawnguard member. You will track him down and dispose of him. But please, do be discreet. Our aim isn't to stir the nest, just to skin a rat, reminding them that they are the prey.
Dawnguard.esm|015E0D: A more "serious" group of the Vigilants of Stendarr. Sometimes when you skin a skeever, you find he has a more aggressive mate. The Dawnguard apparently like killing vampires. Almost as much as we like killing them.
Dawnguard.esm|015E0E: Good. I prefer to nip budding problems before they reach full bloom. I trust it wasn't too much of an effort on your part.
Dawnguard.esm|015E0F: Excellent. I knew I could count on you. That's one less trouble maker to keep an eye on.
Dawnguard.esm|015E10: While that's one less trouble maker to keep an eye on, you could have been more discreet in your approach to the problem. I had thought better of you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E11: A cadre of lesser beasts has become a nuisance, taking too bold an approach to their feeding. The herd is becoming distressed, which makes it more difficult for us to slip in and out undetected. It is time to cull the weak.
Dawnguard.esm|015E12: We don't need competition for blood, and we don't want the mortals getting up in arms making things difficult for us. Go destroy the feral beast before this becomes a problem.
Dawnguard.esm|015E13: Needless to say, we can't abide that, as it would raise general alarm amongst the herd, to say nothing of the increased feeding competition. You are to destroy the feral creature before this small problem becomes a larger one.
Dawnguard.esm|015E16: Speak with Florentius. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E1A: Speak with Sorine. She has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E1D: Speak with Gunmar. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E24: Speak with Orthjolf. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E25: Speak with Gunmar. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E2D: Speak with Vingalmo. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E31: Speak with Feran Sadri. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E3A: Speak with Garan Marethi. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E47: Speak with Fura Bloodmouth. She has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E51: Speak with Isran. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E55: Speak with Isran. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E56: Speak with Florentius. He has something for you.
Dawnguard.esm|015E58: One of the feral brood has joined a group of bandits, presumably with aims to grant the gift to the entire lot of them.
Dawnguard.esm|015E59: One of the feral brood has joined a group of nercromancers, presumably with aims to grant the gift to the entire lot of them.
Dawnguard.esm|015F92: The effect around you looks an awful lot like that huge column of light shining on the dais. Let's check that out.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA0: What about Auriel's Bow? Do you know where we can find it?
Dawnguard.esm|015FA1: After everything we've been through, it had damn well better work.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA2: I could see it in your eyes. You looked about a thousand leagues away.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA3: I never trusted those damn scrolls. Who knows what those things could have done to you... just look at Dexion.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA4: Are you okay? Almost thought I lost you there... you went white as the snow.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA5: Then let's get going. I want to get there before my father has a chance to track us down.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA7: You've got plenty of moths. What's our next move?
Dawnguard.esm|015FA8: Well, we've got that knife. There has to be a use for it.
Dawnguard.esm|015FA9: Dexion didn't really give us a lot to go on, did he?
Dawnguard.esm|015FAB: I hope we reach Ancestor Glade soon.
Dawnguard.esm|015FAC: Not sure what you're waiting for... step into the light.
Dawnguard.esm|015FAE: That bark's pretty fragrant, no wonder the moths are attracted to it.
Dawnguard.esm|015FB1: Don't worry, we'll find the glade.
Dawnguard.esm|015FB2: Then it's almost over. We can finally put an end to this ridiculous prophecy. Where is this "Darkfall Cave?"
Dawnguard.esm|015FB3: Then it's almost over. We can finally rewrite the prophecy as we see fit. Where is this "Darkfall Cave?"
Dawnguard.esm|016049: Want my advice? Never welch on a bargain with the Ideal Masters... they don't take it very well.
Dawnguard.esm|01604A: Did you know I visited Elswyr once? What a strange and wondrous place.
Dawnguard.esm|01604B: I yearn to see the great dunes of Hammerfell once again.
Dawnguard.esm|01604C: No one ever escapes. There is no escape. Why would you even try?
Dawnguard.esm|01604D: Death is but a door, time is but a window. I'll be back.
Dawnguard.esm|01604E: Shhh. They're watching us. Always watching.
Dawnguard.esm|01604F: A soul gem... that's what it was. Then... I must be... Oh, no.
Dawnguard.esm|016050: I can't remember how long I've been here. Has it been a day or a century?
Dawnguard.esm|016051: What day is it? What year? What era even?
Dawnguard.esm|016052: The screams... they pierce my mind. I can't stand it!
Dawnguard.esm|016053: All I said to Potema was I thought she looked a little portly in that gown. How was I supposed to know?
Dawnguard.esm|016054: Are you alive? I must be imagining this.
Dawnguard.esm|016056: Must stay away from the Keepers... must stay away from the Keepers.
Dawnguard.esm|016057: "Just step into the painting" he said. Yeah, sure... like it's that simple.
Dawnguard.esm|016058: I wonder if the fires are still burning in the Imperial City...
Dawnguard.esm|016059: I must return to the frontline Alessian headquarters at once... lives are hanging in the balance!
Dawnguard.esm|01605A: Damn Akaviri Shaman... how could I know it would trap my soul?
Dawnguard.esm|01605B: How could they trick me like this? I was one of Reman's court wizards!
Dawnguard.esm|01605C: I live in a dark world, where no light shines through. I carry this burden with every step I take.
Dawnguard.esm|01605D: Only those who have suffered long can find the light within the shadows.
Dawnguard.esm|01605F: This accursed place is nothing but a prison without walls.
Dawnguard.esm|016060: Stay away from the Oblivion Gate I told them. Did they listen? Of course not.
Dawnguard.esm|016061: How dare they cast me from the Battlespire! I will make them pay dearly.
Dawnguard.esm|016062: That sky... it feels like I'm being watched. Can't stand it anymore!
Dawnguard.esm|016063: My ship... what happened to my ship? Where am I?
Dawnguard.esm|016064: I miss the warm sunshine. Green grass. Blue skies. I miss being alive.
Dawnguard.esm|016065: I wonder if my family misses me? I wonder if they're still alive. How much time has passed?
Dawnguard.esm|016066: I no longer hunger, grow weary or feel anything but loneliness.
Dawnguard.esm|016067: One moment I'm raising a sword against that necromancer, the next moment I found myself here. What happened?
Dawnguard.esm|016068: At least the pain is gone...
Dawnguard.esm|016069: Stay away from the crystals or you may find yourself becoming one of us.
Dawnguard.esm|01606A: Beware the dragon Durnehviir, destroyer of souls and harbinger of the fallen.
Dawnguard.esm|01606B: The clouds, the sky... it's all wrong. Everything's wrong.
Dawnguard.esm|01606C: This is no place for you.
Dawnguard.esm|01606D: I thought death was a release, not an eternity of torment.
Dawnguard.esm|01606E: What am I doing here? I don't belong in a place like this!
Dawnguard.esm|01606F: So this is what it's like on the other side.
Dawnguard.esm|016070: One thousand steps and a thousand more...
Dawnguard.esm|0162E0: Isran is the only one trying to do something about these vampires. I don't much like him, but I can't allow the people of Skyrim to be preyed upon by those monsters.
Dawnguard.esm|0162E1: Not a lot of our kind here, I see. Suppose I shouldn't be surprised. Still, those of us here have as much of an obligation to protect Skyrim as any other, wouldn't you agree?
Dawnguard.esm|0162E2: Seems that I'm the only of my kind here. Suppose I shouldn't be surprised. Still, it's my duty to help protect Skyrim as much as any Nord's, don't you think?
Dawnguard.esm|0162E3: I lost two wives to vampires. I will avenge them. It is good to know that I will not have to do it alone. I am glad this Dawnguard exists.
Dawnguard.esm|0162E4: I'll tell you, the only thing more surprising than hearing from Isran after all these years was hearing that he wanted my help. I immediately realized things must be pretty bad. Looks like I was right.
Dawnguard.esm|0162E5: I have. There was a time, years ago, when we were both members of the Vigilants, and both equally dissatisfied with them. Their hearts are in the right place, of course. But Isran and I were never comfortable. We left together, but that partnership didn't last very long. I didn't agree with some of his methods.
Dawnguard.esm|016317: Hide if you wish, it matters little to me.
Dawnguard.esm|016319: Surrender and give us the bow!
Dawnguard.esm|01631B: Child, my life ended long before you were born!
Dawnguard.esm|01631C: This is a waste of time.
Dawnguard.esm|01631E: What... have I done... Auri-Eil forgive me...
Dawnguard.esm|016320: Run, coward! Run and never return!
Dawnguard.esm|016321: Pitiful sorcery. You'll not stay hidden long.
Dawnguard.esm|016323: Watch out! He's pulling down the ceiling!
Dawnguard.esm|016324: I'll feed your corpse to the Betrayed.
Dawnguard.esm|016328: Hide if you wish. It's only a matter of time.
Dawnguard.esm|016332: Your pathetic powers are no match for mine!
Dawnguard.esm|016335: I'll stain the ground with your blood!
Dawnguard.esm|016338: I'll darken the skies with her blood!
Dawnguard.esm|01633F: Enough of these games. Fight me!
Dawnguard.esm|016340: We end this here and now!
Dawnguard.esm|016342: Centuries of preparation will not fall to ruin because of you!
Dawnguard.esm|016343: I will herald the fall of eternal night!
Dawnguard.esm|016344: Let twilight fall and blood spill!
Dawnguard.esm|016350: Well, look what we have here... someone with flesh on their bones.
Dawnguard.esm|016351: Let's just say I sold something to a guy who turned out to work for a whole coven of necromancers. How in the name of Zenithar was I to know the ingredients were phony? Do I look like someone that would double-cross a necromancer?
Dawnguard.esm|016358: You here for the Skooma? Head around inside, there's a trap door that'll lead you in.
Dawnguard.esm|016359: Look, I'm just here to keep watch. What you're looking for is down inside.
Dawnguard.esm|01635A: If you're here for business, go ahead. Otherwise, get lost.
Dawnguard.esm|01635B: Wha- who... you're that one from Helgen. Barely made it out of there myself. I hurt my back and I- I just need something for the pain.
Dawnguard.esm|01635C: I ain't in the mood to talk. Ohhhh...
Dawnguard.esm|01635D: You there, bring me another bottle of skooma... wait, who are you? You look like fun. Pull up a seat friend.
Dawnguard.esm|01635E: Ohhh... that's good stuff. Believe me friend, I've tried it all and this is the best.
Dawnguard.esm|01635F: I once paid 3000 gold for this fungus that grows in giants armpits. Couldn't stand up for days, but no where as good as this stuff.
Dawnguard.esm|016360: If my friends could try this Redwater Skooma they might finally understand why I like slumming it with you commoners.
Dawnguard.esm|016392: I told you, I've prepared for this. It's going to work.
Dawnguard.esm|016393: I suppose we're all a little crazy to be here, right?
Dawnguard.esm|016395: Something bad happens, word spreads. More bad things happen, people worry. The more they worry, the more they talk about it. I've been hearing rumors for a bit now. I was glad to find out not only the bad rumors were true.
Dawnguard.esm|016397: We're not going to have a problem with your little... project, are we?
Dawnguard.esm|016398: I didn't say that. I wondered if perhaps he did it for the attention, but every now and then he'll say something that makes me reconsider.
Dawnguard.esm|016399: That's why I always try to be ready for anything.
Dawnguard.esm|01639A: Gunmar, do you think I could borrow a troll?
Dawnguard.esm|01639B: Well, that's certainly a line of thinking I can relate to.
Dawnguard.esm|01639C: So, you think he's just crazy?
Dawnguard.esm|01639D: Nothing. Well, not on purpose. But you never know. The strangest accidents can happen at any time.
Dawnguard.esm|01639E: And then realizing a troll with armor is even more intimidating.
Dawnguard.esm|01639F: What are you doing that's so dangerous?
Dawnguard.esm|0163A0: Florentius still worked with the Order from time to time. Last I knew, he was headed to Ruunvald. But then, we hadn't heard from anyone there in a few weeks. If he's moved on, perhaps the Vigilants there can tell you where he's gone.
Dawnguard.esm|0163A1: I'm... not sure. I've never heard of the Divines speaking directly to someone before.
Dawnguard.esm|0163A2: Fairly well, I'd say. I've managed to get this far without blowing myself up, I still have all my limbs...
Dawnguard.esm|0163A3: Isran has a problem with most people, if you haven't yet noticed. No, this was different. He never trusted Florentius, always thought him to be trying to get attention. I can't imagine that opinion has changed.
Dawnguard.esm|0163A4: Oh. Well, I'm not sure. I guess it came from fighting them, thinking "what could be worse?"
Dawnguard.esm|0163A5: Do you think Florentius really talks to Arkay?
Dawnguard.esm|0163A6: How is the work coming along?
Dawnguard.esm|0163A7: We don't have many other options, do we?
Dawnguard.esm|0163A8: I mean, why would that idea even cross your mind?
Dawnguard.esm|0163AB: If you're so worried about the trolls, why agree to let me go ahead with it?
Dawnguard.esm|0163AD: Why do you think I was asking?
Dawnguard.esm|0163AE: Are you mad, woman? After the trouble I've gone through?
Dawnguard.esm|0163AF: There's enough for us to fight out there. I don't need us having to defend ourselves within our own fort.
Dawnguard.esm|0163B0: Why in the world did you ever try putting armor on trolls?
Dawnguard.esm|0163B1: No. I try to avoid it. Always seems to end in an argument. You?
Dawnguard.esm|0163B2: Well I need to do some testing on this new weapon, and I was thinking a live target would be helpful.
Dawnguard.esm|0163C7: Turns out you don't learn much from just sleeping with something.
Dawnguard.esm|0163C9: The reason I had it... and why I was down there.
Dawnguard.esm|0163CA: The reason I was down there... and why I had the Elder Scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|0163EB: See that thing? Dwarves called them 'Kinetic Resonators'. Don't see them very often, least not among the clans in Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|0163F6: There you are. Looks like this place has been cleaned out. At least the shard is still here...
Dawnguard.esm|0163F8: There isn't any Aetherium here, is there? Damn it! Wait! ...Yes, yes there is. The shards we collected... they're pure Aetherium, remember? It's not much, but it'll do. With them, and the materials in this room, we should have everything we need.
Dawnguard.esm|0163FC: See those things in the alcoves up there? 'Kinetic Resonators', the dwarves called them. Strike them in the right order, and the doors should open. Get it wrong, and... well. You've seen what happened when I tried it.
Dawnguard.esm|0163FE: It feels... old, doesn't it? Even by dwarven standards. No one has been here in thousands of years.
Dawnguard.esm|016402: I... fell. When the earthquake hit. The ground just... opened up. Even so, I almost made it... another foot or two to the side, and the water would have broken my fall. But... nothing I could do.
Dawnguard.esm|01640F: Just hit them, and they'll... do whatever it is they're supposed to do.
Dawnguard.esm|01641A: Spiders? All right, we can handle this. Come on!
Dawnguard.esm|01641D: It's not working! Try something else!
Dawnguard.esm|01641E: Door's shut tight. I bet those Resonators would open it, though.
Dawnguard.esm|016667: Which, I'm sorry to say, means your usefulness is at an end!
Dawnguard.esm|016668: You've done exactly as I predicted and brought your fetching companion to me.
Dawnguard.esm|016669: Did you really come here expecting to claim Auriel's Bow?
Dawnguard.esm|01666E: Castle looks so big from down here. I mean, it is big, but, well, even bigger.
Dawnguard.esm|01666F: I used to hide down here and look out at the sea. Usually when my father was in a bad mood.
Dawnguard.esm|016670: An old jetty where boats would bring supplies to the castle. Once we moved in, it never got used, along with the rest of the undercroft. We'll need to watch our steps inside.
Dawnguard.esm|01667C: You've found her journal! Let me take a look at it.
Dawnguard.esm|0167E1: Perhaps it was nothing after all.
Dawnguard.esm|0167E2: Your time is at an end!
Dawnguard.esm|0167E3: I will erase you from existence!
Dawnguard.esm|0167EB: I won't let you defile this place, foul being!
Dawnguard.esm|0167EC: Auri-El, give me strength to smite my foe!
Dawnguard.esm|0167ED: I will send you back to the shadows!
Dawnguard.esm|0167F2: I absolve you of your sins!
Dawnguard.esm|0167F6: It's only a matter of time!
Dawnguard.esm|0167FC: Your deception is useless. I'll find you.
Dawnguard.esm|0167FD: Why hide? Face Auri-El. Face your end!
Dawnguard.esm|0167FF: May the light of Auri-El cleanse your soul!
Dawnguard.esm|016800: Your death will be my gift to Auri-El!
Dawnguard.esm|016801: You will not leave this valley alive!
Dawnguard.esm|016802: Death to those who defile the Chantry!
Dawnguard.esm|016803: A swift death is all you deserve!
Dawnguard.esm|016813: Let Auri-El smite you with his wrath!
Dawnguard.esm|016815: Back to the darkness with you!
Dawnguard.esm|016816: How dare you profane this place!
Dawnguard.esm|016837: I can take you to any port on the coast.
Dawnguard.esm|016838: May not look like much, but my boat can get you there faster than walking.
Dawnguard.esm|01683B: You just enjoy the fresh air and leave the rowing to me.
Dawnguard.esm|01683C: Yeah, all right. I'll take you as far as I can.
Dawnguard.esm|01683D: Climb on in the boat and get comfortable.
Dawnguard.esm|01683E: Climb on in and let's get this over with.
Dawnguard.esm|01683F: You think I do this for my health? Come back when you've got the coin.
Dawnguard.esm|016840: The one with the ruined castle? Don't you know that place is cursed? Even seasoned sailors steer far around that place.
Dawnguard.esm|016841: I'll take you as far as I can, but I'm not hanging around and it's gonna cost you extra.
Dawnguard.esm|016915: Oh sure, keep pestering me then, by all means.
Dawnguard.esm|016916: See you around... if you live long enough.
Dawnguard.esm|016918: This won't stop your soul from getting ripped out, but at least you'll look good when your corpse hits the ground.
Dawnguard.esm|016919: A wondrous blade... ahhh, never mind. Just take the darn thing.
Dawnguard.esm|016921: Very funny. And what exactly are you going to pay me with? Take a look around. Gold doesn't exactly win you any friends around here.
Dawnguard.esm|016922: You got it. Horse, wagon and all. Plopped here without so much as a word. I used to be one of the best traders in High Rock, you know. People would track me down just to see what I had for sale.
Dawnguard.esm|016924: Well, I hate to see it go, but what am I gonna do with it here anyway?
Dawnguard.esm|016925: Brought some soul husks or are you wasting my time?
Dawnguard.esm|016926: I can't believe I'm trading this for some dried up plants. Oh well, a sale's a sale.
Dawnguard.esm|016933: I can't believe it. You're one of them... Talk to Isran. Maybe he can help you. Please, I don't want to have to kill you.
Dawnguard.esm|016934: You really don't look well. Have you talked to Florentius? Maybe he can help you.
Dawnguard.esm|016935: I think perhaps you should speak to Isran. You don't look... well.
Dawnguard.esm|016936: Are you infected? Keep your distance from me.
Dawnguard.esm|016937: You seem to have lost the gift that Lord Harkon gave you. I suggest you tell him at once. Hopefully he'll be gracious enough to make you one of us again.
Dawnguard.esm|016938: You would dare show up here after becoming mortal, abandoning all that Lord Harkon has granted you? You'd best find him soon. Ask him to change you back.
Dawnguard.esm|016939: You're... you're mortal! What are you doing here like that? Go to Lord Harkon at once, beg him to turn you back. You're dead if you don't.
Dawnguard.esm|01693A: It's disrespectful and dangerous to walk around here mortal. What were you thinking? You'd best beg Lord Harkon to turn you at once. Although, you do smell delicious...
Dawnguard.esm|01693B: You've given up the powers granted to you? Get away from me. I don't want to be seen talking to you.
Dawnguard.esm|01693C: You're a fool to have become mortal again. I want nothing to do with you.
Dawnguard.esm|0175DB: Might be that I have, but I can't say for sure. I got enough troubles on my mind just tryin' to scrape by out here.
Dawnguard.esm|0175DC: That's one of them Imperial scholars, right? Old man with a gray robe?
Dawnguard.esm|017678: Oh, good. Well then... let's head over to the portal vessel and I'll do the rest.
Dawnguard.esm|017679: Oh, definitely. Mother would have plenty of those materials in her laboratory, you just need to find them.
Dawnguard.esm|01767D: You seem to have lost the gift that has been given to you. Perhaps Serana will grant it to you again.
Dawnguard.esm|01767E: Look at you. Mortal. Weak. I suggest you have Serana change you back. At once.
Dawnguard.esm|01767F: You can't walk around here like some common mortal! Ask Serana to make you one of us again, before someone makes a meal out of you.
Dawnguard.esm|017680: If you're not one of us, you're just another piece of meat. Talk to Serana, and decide which you want to be.
Dawnguard.esm|017681: Without the royal blood flowing through you, you're one step away from the rest of the thralls. You disrespect Lord Harkon by walking around here like that. Go see him at once, else you might find yourself in a cage.
Dawnguard.esm|017682: Without the royal blood flowing through you, you're one step away from the rest of the thralls. Serana can make you one of us again.
Dawnguard.esm|017683: Quite bold, to return here as a mortal. Some will take offense. You really ought to become one of us again. Bad for your health, otherwise.
Dawnguard.esm|017684: You're mortal? What are you doing here like that? No, no, you must get away! If I'm seen talking to you, they'll blame me!
Dawnguard.esm|017685: Mortals aren't allowed to freely roam the castle. Not even you. Find Lord Harkon at once and ask him to please... "correct" the situation.
Dawnguard.esm|017686: You are worth very little if you're not one of us. Serana can change that. I suggest you ask her to.
Dawnguard.esm|017687: We're all that stands between Skyrim and disaster.
Dawnguard.esm|017688: We'll stop them, whatever it is they're up to...
