Dawnguard.esm|017689: Have to put a stop to this menace...
Dawnguard.esm|01768A: Arkay, we're going to need your help more than ever.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A1: Who knows? It was in shambles when we first got here. My father's never been much for building things, so it just stayed like this. The poor thing living here probably just fell out of favor upstairs.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A2: Not the most solid construction here. About what I'd expect from... creatures like this.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A3: This leads out to the courtyard. Just head for the door.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A4: Like I said, we were never very close. Not a lot of father-daughter bonding if you know what I mean. But once we threw our lot in with Molag Bal... people just don't think about their families anymore. "Power takes precedence," he always said.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A5: I liked to explore. My parents almost never let me off the island, so yeah, I poked around down here a lot. It was a little... quieter... back then. Guess a little vampire girl was enough to scare off the rats.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A6: Not that I ever saw. My mother had a bit of a thing for magical constructs. Not... not what you're thinking. She just found them fascinating.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A7: Take a left up here. This is one of those weird double-barred security measures that my father put in when he got more paranoid. If we follow that path around, we can find the other switch.
Dawnguard.esm|0176A9: I've never even seen this part of the castle before. Be careful. I don't know what might be around.
Dawnguard.esm|0176AA: The old water cistern. On some days, this would smell just... be glad you weren't here then.
Dawnguard.esm|0176AB: Oh, they definitely were. I just wonder if that meant it was my job to be giving.
Dawnguard.esm|0176AC: Definitely. You must think my life has been so strange. It sounds like your family wasn't nearly as fractured as mine.
Dawnguard.esm|0176AD: I had always just assumed that the other tower was completely destroyed inside. My mother kept this a secret, even from me. She must have been up to something she thought was dangerous.
Dawnguard.esm|0176AF: You don't know what it's like, coming from a family like that. Everything eventually tumbles down to you.
Dawnguard.esm|0176B0: I know that in my head. But I just can't help feeling bad about... the way things are. Sorry, I know you're trying to help. Thanks.
Dawnguard.esm|0176B1: It was. But I got used to it.
Dawnguard.esm|0176B2: That's easy to say, but I definitely didn't help things.
Dawnguard.esm|0176B3: I was. But I think I turned out ok.
Dawnguard.esm|017892: I know this isn't an easy choice. Take your time.
Dawnguard.esm|017893: Have you made up your mind?
Dawnguard.esm|0179F6: My lord! Everyone! Serana has returned!
Dawnguard.esm|0179F8: Wait... Serana? Is that truly you? I cannot believe my eyes!
Dawnguard.esm|0179FC: Never much cared for folks who can't mind their own business.
Dawnguard.esm|0179FD: Sorry, but that Moth Priest's comings and goings are none of my business, and none of yours either.
Dawnguard.esm|0179FE: I can see that. He said he was heading for Dragon Bridge next. I hope that helps you.
Dawnguard.esm|0179FF: It's like I said, I'm not going to talk about it.
Dawnguard.esm|017A02: Ah yes, I remember your Moth Priest now. He wanted me to take him out to Dragon Bridge, but I told him it ain't one of my stops.
Dawnguard.esm|017A03: Can't help you, friend. I might remember better if my pockets weren't so light.
Dawnguard.esm|017A04: Now that I think on it, I did see your priest. He tried to hire me for a trip to Dragon Bridge, but I told him that ain't one of my stops.
Dawnguard.esm|017F6B: Well, if I were a betting woman, I'd say it's a scraper of some sort. Mother had something similar back home when she was tending the courtyard gardens to scrape moss and fungus off of the trees. That one's far nicer though... almost ceremonial.
Dawnguard.esm|017FE9: You know I'm more than capable. Why are you bringing this up?
Dawnguard.esm|017FEA: Well, it seems the Dawnguard really is needed again. While I ready things here, you find out what this woman means to the vampires. She must be important somehow. I'll admit, I don't know that I can trust you, but I know I can't rely on the Vigilants for anything. So we'll have to work together for now. If you can prove you're reliable, maybe you can join the Dawnguard. We have ways of dealing with vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|017FEB: Why her? What significance does she have? We don't have enough information.
Dawnguard.esm|017FEC: Can I cound on you to handle whatever smithing work we need done? Repairs, new armor, weapons...
Dawnguard.esm|017FED: None of this feels right. We need to know more.
Dawnguard.esm|017FEE: None of this makes any sense. What does she have to do with this?
Dawnguard.esm|017FEF: Do we at least know what the vampires are after?
Dawnguard.esm|017FF1: Right. Well, you can relax. I have it under control.
Dawnguard.esm|017FF2: I can't afford to make any assumptions. That's how people wind up dead.
Dawnguard.esm|018187: This is a statue of Auriel, but it's using the older signs of his power. This temple must be ancient. The bow has to be in here.
Dawnguard.esm|018188: Looks like they're better at making traps than they are at making bridges. A strange race, these Falmer.
Dawnguard.esm|018189: That... has to be the place. I've never seen a building like that before. It looks like some kind of temple.
Dawnguard.esm|01818A: Does this ice feel a little... thin to you?
Dawnguard.esm|01818B: Ugh. I told you! Next time I say to please not go over the waterfall, maybe you'll listen to me.
Dawnguard.esm|01818C: Careful, here. If you fall down that thing I'm going to have to drag you out of... wherever it leads.
Dawnguard.esm|01818D: More spiders? I liked the ones back home better.
Dawnguard.esm|01818E: I think that's another wayshrine up there. Here we go.
Dawnguard.esm|01818F: This is incredible. It's like a whole other world.
Dawnguard.esm|018190: Poor thing. I know it would have tried to kill us, but still...
Dawnguard.esm|0181BC: The Elder Scrolls vanished from the Imperial City around 175 of this era and have been scattered across Tamriel by forces unknown. If you want to find the remaining scroll, you'll have to consult with someone versed in magical knowledge. Perhaps at the College of Winterhold?
Dawnguard.esm|0181BD: I wish I could help. The scrolls have changed hands so many times, it's impossible to keep track. Normally, I'd send you to the Imperial City in Cyrodiil to seek answers at the Imperial Library, but that avenue no longer exists.
Dawnguard.esm|0181BE: Dexion said something about that scroll. What was it... something to do with dragons I think? Maybe we should try asking at the College of Winterhold. Sounds like something they'd know about.
Dawnguard.esm|0183AD: This place tears at me as though I'm still alive and being drawn and quartered. I... can't stand it anymore.
Dawnguard.esm|0183AE: That's the last time I argue with a dremora.
Dawnguard.esm|0183AF: Madness. It was madness to attack that coven of witches! Why didn't they listen to me?
Dawnguard.esm|0183B0: Why can't I just die? I fear I'm cursed to walk this desolate land forever.
Dawnguard.esm|0183B5: Is there no end to this nightmare?
Dawnguard.esm|0183B6: Oh, you're off on your own for a while? All right.
Dawnguard.esm|018603: This stranger aligns herself with those that would hunt you down and slay you like an animal, yet I should entrust you to her?
Dawnguard.esm|018604: This stranger may call herself vampire, but she knows nothing of our struggle. Why should I entrust you to her?
Dawnguard.esm|018806: These Vigilants never know when to give up. I thought we'd taught them enough of a lesson at their hall.
Dawnguard.esm|018B1C: If you're putting yourself in harm's way, you'd best be prepared. Take these.
Dawnguard.esm|018B1D: I am at your service, as always.
Dawnguard.esm|018B1E: I hope you find Lord Harkon's court to your liking.
Dawnguard.esm|018B1F: Here we are, in view of seeing the prophecy fulfilled. These are truly great times.
Dawnguard.esm|018B20: Don't get in my way, and I won't tear your arms off. Simple, isn't it?
Dawnguard.esm|018B21: So you found the Moth Priest. Let's see you find the rest of the Elder Scrolls all on your own.
Dawnguard.esm|018B22: You've gone from a between-meal snack to the darling of the court. Impressive.
Dawnguard.esm|018B23: None can deny your power now.
Dawnguard.esm|018B24: Parading around in that form won't do you any good here.
Dawnguard.esm|018B25: I am above the petty squabbles here. I have lived for far too long to be bothered with them.
Dawnguard.esm|018B26: Yes, yes. Aren't you lovely looking like that. Do you mind?
Dawnguard.esm|018B27: If you need anything, say the word. I can still forge a decent blade.
Dawnguard.esm|018B28: It's difficult to have a conversation with you like that. Would you mind changing back?
Dawnguard.esm|018B29: You're welcome here, as long as you don't overfeed on the thralls.
Dawnguard.esm|018B2A: Mind yourself in that form. Don't go damaging my thralls.
Dawnguard.esm|018B2B: If there's any way I can be of service, please let me know.
Dawnguard.esm|018B2C: I hope you're not trying to lord your abilities over me.
Dawnguard.esm|018B2D: Life here has many advantages, don't you agree?
Dawnguard.esm|018B2E: I find it tiring to converse with someone in that form. Too much effort.
Dawnguard.esm|018B63: No. Don't try and impress me. It only leaves you looking foolish.
Dawnguard.esm|018B64: It is a rather well-kept secret.
Dawnguard.esm|018B65: The Chalice has been in Lord Harkon's possession for quite some time now. It is, when used properly, able to increase the potency of our powers. He has neglected using it for ages. If he wants it filled now, then grander things are afoot. This is good. Follow me. I'll take you to it.
Dawnguard.esm|018C6B: Damn. Well what do you know?
Dawnguard.esm|018C6C: Look at this. I should've known it was only a matter of time before they found us. It's the price we pay for openly recruiting. We'll have to step up our defenses. I don't suppose you have some good news for me.
Dawnguard.esm|018C6D: Of course. Why did I suppose differently. Fine, tell me what you know.
Dawnguard.esm|018CDC: This is Fort Dawnguard. Originally owned by the Jarl of Riften back in the Second Era, it was a bit of a black mark on the Hold's history. I'm trying to put it to good use.
Dawnguard.esm|018CDD: They what? And you didn't stop them? You didn't secure the scroll?
Dawnguard.esm|018CDE: And so we've delivered her right to them. I should've kept her here until we were certain...
Dawnguard.esm|018DA1: I'm telling you, there's something strange with the moondial.
Dawnguard.esm|018DA2: The moondial definitely wasn't like this the last time I saw it.
Dawnguard.esm|018EDB: Is that another one of those wayshrines up there? I wonder where this one goes...
Dawnguard.esm|018EDC: These things are everywhere, aren't they?
Dawnguard.esm|018EDD: If we open that one, we'll be able to move around this valley a lot faster.
Dawnguard.esm|018EDE: Hmmm. Pull chains and traps. Be careful here. Whatever's on the other side of this, the Falmer wanted to keep there.
Dawnguard.esm|018EDF: Looks like these Falmer are here to stay. They must be who that priest was calling "the Betrayed."
Dawnguard.esm|018EE0: That... wasn't as unpleasant as I thought it would be. Kind of soothing, actually. I feel a little warmer, now.
Dawnguard.esm|018EE1: What's that? I can feel some kind of power from it...
Dawnguard.esm|018EE3: I wonder what happened to them.
Dawnguard.esm|018EE4: These people were... why would anyone want to set up camp here?
Dawnguard.esm|019073: A shrine to Molag Bal, the mighty Daedra Prince who is father to all our kind. Our power is a blessing from him. It is he who first bestowed the gift of the ancient blood upon me.
Dawnguard.esm|019074: My blood is potent. At first, the body is overwhelmed by it. After my bite, you collapsed and fell into a slumber. Now your flesh has acclimated to the new blood that courses through your veins. I assure you, no harm was done. In truth, your strength surprises me. Not all mortals can withstand my embrace.
Dawnguard.esm|019075: With my guidance you will become a deadly instrument, striking terror in the hearts of mortals wherever you tread. Now, listen to my words and do as I instruct.
Dawnguard.esm|019076: Awake at last, good. The power is growing within you, and now you must learn to wield it.
Dawnguard.esm|019077: After so many centuries, I feared the Elder Scroll and my daughter were lost to me.
Dawnguard.esm|019078: You have brought my daughter back safely, and for that I am grateful.
Dawnguard.esm|019079: You are a guest in this castle at my pleasure. I'll permit you to stay for now, but you're of no use to me like this.
Dawnguard.esm|01907A: Good. Do not offend me by losing it again.
Dawnguard.esm|01907B: You have forsaken my gift. The ancient blood no longer flows through your veins. Don't deny it! Count yourself fortunate that I'm a forgiving man. Now, be still while I give you the ancient blood once more.
Dawnguard.esm|01907C: You arrived just in time. I think those vampires were getting hungry.
Dawnguard.esm|01907D: I owe you a debt for the timely rescue.
Dawnguard.esm|01907F: What can I do for you?
Dawnguard.esm|019080: My trip to Skyrim has become quite the adventure!
Dawnguard.esm|019084: If I'd known Skyrim was so dangerous, I'd have brought more guards.
Dawnguard.esm|019085: Ambushed by vampires... I certainly wasn't expecting that.
Dawnguard.esm|019086: This is all more excitement than I'm used to.
Dawnguard.esm|019088: Divines keep you safe, my friend.
Dawnguard.esm|019089: If you find any more Elder Scrolls lying about, be sure to let me know!
Dawnguard.esm|01908D: I have nothing more to say to you.
Dawnguard.esm|01908E: The Empire will send someone to find me. You'll see!
Dawnguard.esm|01908F: Threaten me all you like, it won't matter.
Dawnguard.esm|019090: Find that Moth Priest! I will suffer no delays!
Dawnguard.esm|019091: Go now, and prove that my faith in you was not misplaced.
Dawnguard.esm|019092: If the Moth Priest has guards, show them no mercy.
Dawnguard.esm|019093: You've done well. I won't forget that.
Dawnguard.esm|019095: Go, then, and do not forget who is your master.
Dawnguard.esm|019096: In an age long forgotten to history, I ruled as a mighty king. My domain was vast, my riches endless and my power infinite. And yet, as my mortal life neared an end, I faced a seemingly invincible enemy -- my own mortality. I pledged myself to Molag Bal, and in his name I sacrificed a thousand innocents. In reward, he gave everlasting life to myself, my wife and my daughter. And so I have defeated mortality itself.
Dawnguard.esm|01909C: If you think I'll help you, you're mistaken.
Dawnguard.esm|01909D: Your kind are a blight upon Tamriel, monster!
Dawnguard.esm|0190A8: Shouldn't we keep moving? I really want to get back home.
Dawnguard.esm|0190A9: It's not too far, now. At least, I don't think it is. I thought you knew the way?
Dawnguard.esm|0190AC: Here we are. Ready to go in?
Dawnguard.esm|0190AD: This isn't really a good time.
Dawnguard.esm|0190AE: It's probably best for you if we're not seen together right now.
Dawnguard.esm|0190AF: So far this hasn't been too bad. Gets lonely exploring by myself, though.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B1: Nothing like a goose chase around the whole damned province.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B2: "The Tale of the Great Moth Priest Hunt." Not something I'd want to read.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B3: I'm a little nervous about all of this.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B4: So, this was my old stomping grounds. Not the most normal childhood.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B5: I'll bet my parents didn't know the half of this part of the castle.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B6: I promise you, it didn't always look like this.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B8: I'm wondering what my mother got up to, here. She definitely wasn't trying to keep the place clean.
Dawnguard.esm|0190B9: Well, looks like some other guests have taken over since my mother left.
Dawnguard.esm|0190BA: I was hoping it wouldn't smell this bad.
Dawnguard.esm|0190BB: I can't imagine living here. Or... being dead here. Or... whatever they'd call it. Ugh.
Dawnguard.esm|0190BC: Let's just find my mother and get out of here.
Dawnguard.esm|0190BD: The quicker we find the Keepers, the quicker we can leave.
Dawnguard.esm|0190CE: Gah... let's clear out this steam so we can take a look.
Dawnguard.esm|0190CF: That took longer than usual. How deep are we?
Dawnguard.esm|0191DF: There is one last thing you must know. Slaying mortal men with your life drain grants you new night powers and blood magic. I keep a stable of thralls in the castle should you need to feed like the baser vampires to stave off the sun.
Dawnguard.esm|0191E0: Good. You are weaker while on the ground, because you cannot access the blood magic. However, the night powers are still yours to command, and your claws are still formidable weapons.
Dawnguard.esm|0191E1: The true power of the ancient blood is found in the form of the Vampire Lord. Assume the mantle of the Vampire Lord, and we will continue.
Dawnguard.esm|0191E2: Should you run out of magicka, you can descend to the ground and fight as the beasts do. Do so now and we will continue.
Dawnguard.esm|0191E3: In this form, you can drain the life from your foes with your right hand. With your left hand, you can wield the power of blood magic to raise the dead. You can also call upon the powers of night to turn into a swarm of bats and reappear some distance away.
Dawnguard.esm|0191E4: Use the power of the ancient blood to transform into a Vampire Lord.
Dawnguard.esm|0191E5: Transform into the Vampire Lord, and I will teach you more.
Dawnguard.esm|0191F9: I don't think he even sees me as his daughter anymore. I'm just... a means to an end.
Dawnguard.esm|0191FA: That's always a possibility. She was almost as obsessed as my father by the time she shut me in. But I can't worry about that now. We need the scroll, and she's our only lead.
Dawnguard.esm|0191FB: If you have a better lead than this, I'm all ears.
Dawnguard.esm|0191FC: You know, I've asked myself the same thing. I thought... I hoped that if he saw me, he might feel something again. But I guess I don't really factor in at this point.
Dawnguard.esm|0191FD: Ever since he decided to make that prophecy his calling, we kind of drifted apart.
Dawnguard.esm|019202: No, you've got it all wrong. We're here to complete the prophecy our way, not his.
Dawnguard.esm|019305: The bow... you have Auriel's Bow! I've heard it described in tales, but I could never have imagined its beauty.
Dawnguard.esm|019309: Give him a crossbow bolt between the eyes for me!
Dawnguard.esm|0193F0: I'll see you at Fort Dawnguard.
Dawnguard.esm|0193F1: For two weeks I've tracked that bear, and now find that you've killed it on your own. I'm not sure whether that makes you brave or foolish. What brings you here?
Dawnguard.esm|0193F2: By the Eight... All right. I'll hear out Isran, see if there's anything I can do to help. Where do you expect me to go?
Dawnguard.esm|019507: I guess... we kind of have to go way back. To the very beginning. Do you know where vampirism came from?
Dawnguard.esm|019508: The first vampire came from Molag Bal. She... was not a willing subject. But she was still the first. Molag Bal is a powerful daedric lord, and his will is made reality. For those willing to subjugate themselves, he will still bestow the gift, but they must be powerful in their own right before earning his trust.
Dawnguard.esm|01950A: Not many people understand the appeal. You keep surprising me.
Dawnguard.esm|01950B: I will be. Just give me a little time.
Dawnguard.esm|01950C: Don't... Just leave me alone for a bit.
Dawnguard.esm|01950D: A cure? Why even think of it that way? I can't think of any reason I'd want to lose this gift. Especially after what I did to get it. It may have driven my family apart, but I'm still here, and I'm alive. I won't give this up.
Dawnguard.esm|01950E: I have to admit, I'm impressed. I didn't think you had the spirit for it.
Dawnguard.esm|01950F: I'm not sure it's really your place to judge me. To judge us. My family has its problems, but they're our problems. Let's just... move on from here, ok?
Dawnguard.esm|019510: This isn't that watered down child's power that you simply contract from another vampire. We're pureblood creations of original vampires. We earned this.
Dawnguard.esm|019511: Well, you've met most of us. My father's not exactly the most stable, and eventually he drove my mother crazy with him. And it all ended with me being locked underground for who knows how long. It's definitely been a bad thing, on the whole.
Dawnguard.esm|019512: Nobody's ever asked me that before. I... I don't know. I think... mostly I hate what it's done to my family.
Dawnguard.esm|019513: Now, you might already know that Molag Bal is the original source of vampirism. What you probably don't know is that he still grants it to his more impressive followers.
Dawnguard.esm|019514: The ceremony was... degrading. Let's not revisit that. But we all took part in it. Not really wholesome family activity, but I guess it's something you do when you give yourselves to a daedric lord.
Dawnguard.esm|019515: I know, I know, but just... anyway. Molag Bal is the original source of vampirism, and still grants it to his more worthy followers.
Dawnguard.esm|019516: Precisely. Looks like you know your daedra. What you might not know is that for the most devoted and impressive followers, Molag Bal will give the gift anew, in its pure form.
Dawnguard.esm|019517: The short version is that my parents and I... we were followers of Molag Bal.
Dawnguard.esm|019519: Wait... that almost makes sense! I used to help my mother tend a garden in the courtyard here. All of the ingredients for our potions came from there. She used to say that my father couldn't stand the place. Too... peaceful.
Dawnguard.esm|0195D5: Running will warm your blood nicely.
Dawnguard.esm|0195D6: You don't think you can escape me, do you?
Dawnguard.esm|0195E0: You dare to interfere with me?
Dawnguard.esm|0195E1: I knew I smelled mortal blood!
Dawnguard.esm|0195E4: Ha ha ha. Here you are.
Dawnguard.esm|0195E6: There's no use hiding from me.
Dawnguard.esm|0195EA: Running is your only chance to live.
Dawnguard.esm|0195EB: Soon... soon your blood will be mine...
Dawnguard.esm|0195EE: Stop struggling. I'll give you a swift and beautiful death.
Dawnguard.esm|0195EF: Why fight me? Your blood is already mine.
Dawnguard.esm|0195F1: Mortal fool, you can't stop us.
Dawnguard.esm|0195F3: You've seen your last dawn. I bring the night.
Dawnguard.esm|0195F6: The Tyranny of the Sun has been broken!
Dawnguard.esm|0195F7: The sun cannot protect you any more!
Dawnguard.esm|0195FA: You should not have come here, vampire.
Dawnguard.esm|0195FB: There's no blood here for you.
Dawnguard.esm|0195FC: You blood will not protect you from me.
Dawnguard.esm|0195FD: Let me show you how mortal you still are, vampire.
Dawnguard.esm|0195FE: You're no match for me, fledgling.
Dawnguard.esm|01961F: You had your chance to escape, vampire.
Dawnguard.esm|019621: Ha ha ha! I knew I'd find you.
Dawnguard.esm|019624: And so you learn the power of the night.
Dawnguard.esm|019628: Your blood will not protect you!
Dawnguard.esm|019629: It's the Dawnguard! Let none escape!
Dawnguard.esm|01962A: When will you fools learn? The night is ours!
Dawnguard.esm|01962B: I love it when dinner walks right into my arms.
Dawnguard.esm|019811: I've spent years cleaning the place up and making repairs. You can be damn sure the vampires won't be burning this place down.
Dawnguard.esm|019812: I won't lie - if this were any other situation, I'd put her down just on the chance she might be a vampire. But this is something different. I'd say do what she wants. Take her home, and see who else is in this castle.
Dawnguard.esm|019814: That's right. I used to be a Vigilant, but we had a difference of opinion. They didn't think Skyrim has a vampire problem, and I did. Kind of fitting that the Vigilants would be wiped out by vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|019815: That doesn't make much sense. Where is this woman? Who is she?
Dawnguard.esm|019816: So, any luck? Was Tolan right about the vampires being interested in Dimhollow Crypt?
Dawnguard.esm|019834: I don't converse with my prey.
Dawnguard.esm|019836: ...still always hungry... there's no prey down in this hole... need to hunt more... all this hiding and skulking around, pah...
Dawnguard.esm|019837: ...first kill is always the best... the blood has never tasted as sweet since...
Dawnguard.esm|019838: ...I won't hide down here forever... gather a few of the brethren, we'll strike out on our own...
Dawnguard.esm|019839: ...maybe the clans have it right... hunting's so chancy... why not keep a few thralls... blood whenever you want it...
Dawnguard.esm|01983A: ...blood... so hungry all the time now... need to hunt again soon...
Dawnguard.esm|01983B: ...she said I'd forget, and usually I do, but sometimes... still remember their faces, their pleading...
Dawnguard.esm|01983C: ...Dawnguard getting to be more than a nuisance... gather some of the hunters... teach them a lesson...
Dawnguard.esm|01983D: ...the clans think they're better than us... putting on airs... pah, they'll be the first to burn when the mortals get organized...
Dawnguard.esm|01983E: ...just fed but still hungry... blood... all I can think about these days... last kill was so good... just the right amount of struggle...
Dawnguard.esm|01983F: ...too much hiding... the mortals are weak... he's a coward to fear them, we should feed when we like... can't stand this hunger gnawing at me...
Dawnguard.esm|0198B0: Isn't it obvious? They're soft, the lot of them. Totally unprepared for any real threat. And yet I was the one ridiculed for trying to be ready for anything. "Too extreme", they called me. Well, that doesn't seem to have worked out in their favor, now has it?
Dawnguard.esm|0198FE: I'm glad we found the scroll, but I... I wish she could come with us.
Dawnguard.esm|019901: We have to keep my mother alive!
Dawnguard.esm|019902: Come on, she's leading us to the scroll.
Dawnguard.esm|019903: The barrier should be gone now. Let's head back.
Dawnguard.esm|019904: Let's kill those... Keeper things and get back to the prison.
Dawnguard.esm|019905: The sooner we get out of this place, the better.
Dawnguard.esm|019906: I'm glad you're here. I don't think I could be doing this alone.
Dawnguard.esm|019907: I can't believe we found my mother alive... well, you know what I mean.
Dawnguard.esm|019908: Look at this place. I can't imagine choosing to come here. My mother must have been terrified.
Dawnguard.esm|01990F: It's not safe out there. Horrible creatures attacking towns in the night... If they won't let me in, at least I can stay here.
Dawnguard.esm|019910: Look at the size of that fort. They must have room for a few more in there.
Dawnguard.esm|019911: I worry it'll never be safe to go home.
Dawnguard.esm|019912: If the Dawnguard can't stop these vampires, who can?
Dawnguard.esm|019913: I hope Alvide doesn't think less of me, but I can't protect her on my own. We're safer here.
Dawnguard.esm|019914: Why can't they just let us in? We won't get in the way!
Dawnguard.esm|019915: I've no love for living out of a tent, but I'll take that over being ripped apart.
Dawnguard.esm|019916: It looks like this is as close as we're going to get being safe from the vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|019917: Barknar insisted we come here, said we'd be safe. He also said we'd be inside the walls.
Dawnguard.esm|019918: What if the vampires come here, after the Dawnguard? We'll be stuck right in the middle.
Dawnguard.esm|019919: I hope we can go home soon. I don't like it here.
Dawnguard.esm|01991A: At least Barknar and I are here together.
Dawnguard.esm|01991B: Thought I'd come here to be safe. Now they won't so much as let us in.
Dawnguard.esm|01991C: How hard would it be to feed a few extra mouths? I'm sure they can manage, and not leave us sitting out here like cattle.
Dawnguard.esm|01991D: I won't beg for protection, but I'm not even sure that would work with this Isran.
Dawnguard.esm|01991E: Aren't the Dawnguard here to help, to make it safe for us? Can't see why they wouldn't let us in.
Dawnguard.esm|019922: I'm afraid I won't be able to help you with the scrolls any longer, milady. Please, don't be angry with me.
Dawnguard.esm|019923: Oh please, milady. Please... forgive me!
Dawnguard.esm|019925: Oh thank you, milady. Thank you!
Dawnguard.esm|019926: Let's move faster. The sun is... it's not great for my skin, if you know what I mean.
Dawnguard.esm|019927: I was always taught to avoid these types of ruins. I think I see why, now.
Dawnguard.esm|019928: Does the air feel... heavy down here? I'm a little woozy, but it might just be from waking up.
Dawnguard.esm|019929: It'll be good to get outside and breathe again. I haven't missed the sunlight, but I do miss fresh air.
Dawnguard.esm|01992F: But their time has come to an end. And you will be the one that ends it.
Dawnguard.esm|019932: The Dawnguard will, no doubt, soon become even more of a nuisance now that Auriel's Bow has been recovered. We need to strike a crippling blow against them before they muster more men against us.
Dawnguard.esm|019933: The Dawnguard will want the Bow, and they will fight us, hard, to get it. We should strike them first before they can muster a larger force.
Dawnguard.esm|019934: Excellent. That should prevent the problem from escalating. Though they are a rebellious and determined lot. While you've severed the snake's head, the body will likely writhe a while longer.
Dawnguard.esm|019935: Good. That should slow them down. But, we haven't gotten rid of them completely. A body can rattle around confused long after it's head's been severed.
Dawnguard.esm|019936: Good. They've holed up in an abandoned fort in the Reach. Killing their leaders should discourage them from an out and out war with us.
Dawnguard.esm|019937: Good. They've holed up in an abandoned fort in the Reach. Kill their leaders. That should put a stop to a full out war.
Dawnguard.esm|019941: Perhaps you still need convincing? Behold the power!
Dawnguard.esm|019A27: Wait, please! Don't leave me for the wolves to find!
Dawnguard.esm|019AAB: North, I think, then they turned west and that's the last I saw of them. They mentioned some place called Dimhollow Crypt, but I don't know where that is. While you're looking for the vampires, I'll try to get us some help. Come find me at Dead Drop Falls when you've learned more.
Dawnguard.esm|019CA1: Lord Harkon has searched for his daughter for so long. He must be very grateful.
Dawnguard.esm|019D8B: Land on the ground, and I will have more to teach you.
Dawnguard.esm|019D8C: Cease your flight and we will move on to the next lesson.
Dawnguard.esm|019F11: My family's home is west of Solitude. If you help me get back there, they'll be grateful to you.
Dawnguard.esm|019F13: I don't even know who that is... And frankly, I need to get home to figure out what's happening.
Dawnguard.esm|019F14: Yes, he definitely sounds like he knows what to do. If you want to waste your time, that's fine. But I've got my own destination in mind. Maybe you could help me get there when you're done?
Dawnguard.esm|019F15: Maybe he knows what's going on. Look, I'm happy to help you run any errands you need to, but I've got my own destination. Could you help me get there whenever we're done?
Dawnguard.esm|019F7A: I see. Interesting that you can set aside your morals when it suits you.
Dawnguard.esm|019F7B: No. He has to die. We have no choice.
Dawnguard.esm|019F7D: Indeed. The day hasn't been won while Harkon still walks Tamriel. But what of Serana? Can she be trusted to lift a blade against her own kind? Her own family?
Dawnguard.esm|019F7E: Provided for me? Are you insane? You've destroyed our family. You've killed other vampires. All over some prophecy that we barely understand.
Dawnguard.esm|019F7F: Ah, of course. The prophecy. You've come here to stop me from taking Auriel's Bow and shrouding the world in darkness.
Dawnguard.esm|019F82: The vampire is eternal, and with immortality comes the revelation that these bonds never endure... it's fleeting, momentary at best.
Dawnguard.esm|019F84: Have you been paying attention to everything that's happened? He'll betray us the second our backs are turned.
Dawnguard.esm|019F89: No more. I'm done with you.
Dawnguard.esm|019F8A: There's nowhere to run. And I'm certainly not moving in with my mother, if that's what your implying.
Dawnguard.esm|019F8E: Ah, I see you've been speaking to Valerica and she's convinced you that eternal night will plunge us into war with daytime dwellers. If only it were so. With the world in shadow, we would never tire, never weaken... more than a match for their pitiful armies.
Dawnguard.esm|019F8F: It's time we take the fight to their door. Gather your things and rest well, my friend. We meet outside Castle Volkihar.
Dawnguard.esm|019F90: So, I see this dragon has fangs. Your voice drips with the venom of your mother's influence. How alike you've become.
Dawnguard.esm|019F91: No. This has to end here and now.
Dawnguard.esm|019F94: I suppose that's as much as I can hope for. Let me address the Dawnguard, and then we'll be off. The men deserve to know that we've finally gained the upper hand.
Dawnguard.esm|019F95: No... Because unlike her, I'm not afraid of you. Not anymore.
Dawnguard.esm|019F96: Finally, a trace of honesty in our little conversation. How does it feel to hold the fate of the vampire in your grasp. Exhilarating, isn't it?
Dawnguard.esm|019F97: If we head back to the castle and kick the front door in, we're going to be knee-deep in his friends. Let's head back to Isran and let him see what we've got first. I'm betting he'll lend us a sword or two.
Dawnguard.esm|019F99: Come on then, we haven't got all day!
Dawnguard.esm|019F9A: Serana, my darling. I see you still favor keeping a pet.
Dawnguard.esm|019F9B: So, you've returned. Is your... pet keeping you entertained?
Dawnguard.esm|019F9C: It appears I have you to thank for turning my daughter against me. I knew it was only a matter of time before your ambition outgrew your loyalty.
Dawnguard.esm|019F9D: I keep thinking that there's got to be another way, but he'll turn on us both the moment he gets the chance.
Dawnguard.esm|019F9E: This is the time of the Dawnguard!
Dawnguard.esm|019F9F: For too long we've allowed these vampires to poison the night and kill our people! Now, we finally have the means to strike back! We now have Auriel's Bow. The gods themselves have favored us and we must answer with action! The time has come to finally put an end to Harkon and his unholy prophecy! We will march on their lair and destroy those wretched abominations so they can no longer corrupt our world! This is our fight and this is our fate!
Dawnguard.esm|019FA1: Then my daughter is truly lost. She died the moment she accepted a mortal into her life.
Dawnguard.esm|019FA2: A small price to pay for the betterment of our kind.
Dawnguard.esm|019FA3: Have you been paying attention? It's obvious my father's well beyond reason.
Dawnguard.esm|019FA5: I've been thinking about this for a long time. It's... it's not easy. But I don't think we have much of a choice.
Dawnguard.esm|019FA6: Very well then, you leave me no choice!
Dawnguard.esm|019FA7: Are you out of your mind? The moment you hand over the bow your usefulness ends. He'll kill you and then he'll take my blood for the damn prophecy.
Dawnguard.esm|019FA9: Thank you. Somehow I knew you'd understand.
Dawnguard.esm|019FAA: Yes, yes. Always the noble vampire hunter. And what happens when you've slain me? Is Valerica next? Is Serana?
Dawnguard.esm|019FAB: Oh, come now... spare me your notions of kinship. You've simply used Serana in an attempt to take away what should rightfully be mine.
Dawnguard.esm|019FAC: Yes, quite. I'm growing weary of speaking to you and my traitorous daughter. I'll give you a single chance to turn over the bow to me. There will not be a second.
Dawnguard.esm|019FAD: Of course I do. You disappoint me, Serana. You've taken everything I provided for you and thrown it all away for this... pathetic being.
Dawnguard.esm|019FAE: Of course I do. You disappoint me, Serana. You've taken everything I provided for you and thrown it all away for this... pathetic half-breed vampire.
Dawnguard.esm|019FC7: Little patience for talk? Good. Let's end this!
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B0: I'll meet you in Dimhollow Crypt. I owe these vampires a debt of vengeance.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B4: Yes. By destroying it, the vampires hoped to cripple our order's activities in Skyrim.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B5: Of course there are. Most of us spend our lives as nomads, traveling from place to place in our endless war against evil. I wish I could say how many we numbered, but only a privileged few knew that, and they're dead now.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B6: I wish I could say it was courage, or skill at arms that saved me. But that would be a lie. No, I am alive only because the vampires spared me. It seems they wanted to send a message to the other Vigilants still walking the roads of Skyrim. If was a foolish mistake, and I intend to make sure they learn that the hard way.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B7: The Hall was a place of respite, learning and comradeship, where a road-weary brother or sister could rest for a time in peace and comfort. Most of our learning could be found there. We always studied our enemies, so we might better learn how to defeat them. But of course, those days are gone now.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B8: Someday, we'll rebuild the Hall of the Vigilant. Mark my words, I'll see it done before they put me in the ground.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1B9: Vampires are worse than Daedra. I wish we Vigilants had taken them more seriously while we we had the chance.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1BA: One thing about living in Skyrim I could never stomach... all the damn vampires!
Dawnguard.esm|01A1BB: There will be time to honor the fallen later. For now, we've got to put a stop to these vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1BD: Thanks again for saving my life.
Dawnguard.esm|01A1BE: Something tells me the divines favor you, my friend.
Dawnguard.esm|01A256: All of Skyrim will tremble before us.
Dawnguard.esm|01A257: Now the whole world will fall at our feet.
Dawnguard.esm|01A25A: I knew you would be victorious.
Dawnguard.esm|01A25B: Who'll need thralls when the very sun is at our mercy?
Dawnguard.esm|01A25C: You've served us all well, friend.
Dawnguard.esm|01A310: I'm sure Lord Harkon will be most pleased with your efforts. Find him at once.
Dawnguard.esm|01A311: Don't keep our master waiting. Take him the bow immediately.
Dawnguard.esm|01A312: Find Lord Harkon. Let him know the time of the prophecy is at hand.
Dawnguard.esm|01A313: Present the bow to Lord Harkon at once!
Dawnguard.esm|01A314: I'm sure your reward will be great for ensuring our Lord's success.
Dawnguard.esm|01A315: Go find Lord Harkon. Tell him of your success.
Dawnguard.esm|01A316: Our Lord will be most pleased, I'm sure. I knew you could do it.
Dawnguard.esm|01A317: Auriel's Bow is ours. Deliver it to Lord Harkon at once, so we can reap the benefits of this prophecy.
Dawnguard.esm|01A335: You still got it, Morven... a salesman even beyond the end.
Dawnguard.esm|01A337: I'll tell you what. I appreciate what you're trying to do, so I'll play along. The only thing I see around here in abundance are soul husks. You know, those ugly fungus-looking things growing out of the ground. Bring me say... twenty-five of them, and you can rummage through what was left on my cart.
Dawnguard.esm|01A338: I might be just a soul now, but I'm still not going to give my stuff away. Bring me at least twenty-five husks and then we'll talk.
Dawnguard.esm|01A339: Sure, got plenty. What am I gonna' do with it?
Dawnguard.esm|01A33A: Well, I've been so busy, I dunno. What are you, kidding me?
Dawnguard.esm|01A33B: Of course I do. What do you want?
Dawnguard.esm|01A33E: Sure. Here you go. Can't understand a word of it, maybe it will do you some good.
Dawnguard.esm|01A342: Odds and ends I've gathered over the years. Well, used to gather before all of this nonsense happened to me. Tell me what you're looking for and maybe we can make a deal... if you have the husks.
Dawnguard.esm|01A343: No husks, no sale. Guess you have some scavenging to do.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3C5: We were hoping Isran would be of help. That now seems unlikely.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3C6: Even if he won't help the Vigilants, he could at least offer us shelter for the moment.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3C7: Never expected so blatant an attack by vampires, of all creatures.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3C8: Without the Hall, the Vigilants need a new rallying point.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3C9: Isran, of all people. We're reduced to asking him for help.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3CA: We knew Isran was working on something, but we had no idea he'd found this place.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3CB: If Isran hadn't left the Order, this could've been our home.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3CC: I know, and I'm sorry that happened. But now...
Dawnguard.esm|01A3D9: Well, well. I haven't seen one of these beauties in a long time. A rare find in the original falmer language. You're damn right I'm interested. Question is, are you selling?
Dawnguard.esm|01A3DA: If you're holding out for more gold, it's never going to happen.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3DC: Suit yourself. I'm not going anywhere.
Dawnguard.esm|01A3DD: My offer is still a thousand. Did you want to sell?
Dawnguard.esm|01A3DF: Well, considering no one else in Skyrim would buy something they couldn't possibly read, I'd say you don't have much of a choice. Tell you what, you bring me books like this, and I'll pay a thousand gold each. I'll even throw in a translated edition I have for free. Interested?
Dawnguard.esm|01A42D: For me? I've done what I set out to do. But you... take that out into the world. And if anyone asks, tell them what we discovered. Together.
Dawnguard.esm|01A431: Make it count. This may be the only chance we ever get to use the Forge.
Dawnguard.esm|01A432: And with that... it's done. No one could possibly deny what we've found now.
Dawnguard.esm|01A433: I don't know. I honestly don't know. After all these years, it's a miracle even this much survived.
Dawnguard.esm|01A434: Beautiful. That staff... it's everything I could have hoped for.
Dawnguard.esm|01A435: Beautiful. That shield... it's everything I could have hoped for.
Dawnguard.esm|01A673: You have earned my trust, so I will share what I can.
Dawnguard.esm|01A674: What else do you want to know?
Dawnguard.esm|01A676: Lady Serana's back! Open the gate!
Dawnguard.esm|01A770: Windhelm eh? City of kings, they say. Hope you packed warm though.
Dawnguard.esm|01A771: Ah, Solitude. Best port in Skyrim. You should try the Spiced Mead while you're there.
Dawnguard.esm|01A772: Dawnstar doesn't have much of a port, but the miners say its got rich ore veins.
Dawnguard.esm|01A773: They say anyone who steps foot on that island will meet an early death... hope you know what you're doing.
Dawnguard.esm|01A8B9: While I doubt we'll ever be truly rid of vampires in Skyrim, you've certainly made it a much safer place for now. I'm proud of what we've managed to accomplish. Let's get back to work.
Dawnguard.esm|01A905: Good luck out there. Hopefully I'll see you again soon.
Dawnguard.esm|01A906: I'm surprised you think you can manage without me, but it's your choice.
Dawnguard.esm|01A908: You can't get rid of me that easily.
Dawnguard.esm|01A909: And go where? You can't just leave me alone whenever you feel like it. I'm coming with you, like it or not.
Dawnguard.esm|01A90B: If you think I'm going to let you have all the fun, you're crazy. I'll let you lead ahead, but I'll be keeping an eye on you.
Dawnguard.esm|01A90C: Sorry, I'm not that easy to get rid of. If you want to run ahead, I won't stop you, but I'll be following.
Dawnguard.esm|01A90F: Three's a crowd. Get rid of your other friend, and I'm yours.
Dawnguard.esm|01A91C: I sense the power of the ancient blood in your veins. It has made you strong.
Dawnguard.esm|01A91D: I have kept my end of the bargain, now I expect you to keep yours.
Dawnguard.esm|01A91E: Do as I command, and you will reap the rewards. Defy me, and I can offer you only pain and suffering.
Dawnguard.esm|01A91F: Go on, get out of here!
Dawnguard.esm|01A921: Go away! We don't suffer trespassers!
Dawnguard.esm|01A922: Lord Harkon's a great man. Best show him the proper respect.
Dawnguard.esm|01A923: I watch the gate. Ain't much else to tell.
Dawnguard.esm|01A924: After all these years, Lady Serana's back. Now that's something.
Dawnguard.esm|01A925: Can you feel the power of the ancient blood coursing through your veins?
Dawnguard.esm|01A926: Remember, you are no mere vampire. You are something far greater.
Dawnguard.esm|01A927: Do not forget the price of your new power. I expect your complete obedience.
Dawnguard.esm|01A92B: Will you be a hunter, or will you be prey?
Dawnguard.esm|01A92C: It was no mere chance that brought you to me. It was destiny.
Dawnguard.esm|01A92D: You are welcome here... for now.
Dawnguard.esm|01A92E: You've been given a great gift. Do not squander it.
Dawnguard.esm|01A92F: As a king is to his subjects, so you are to other vampires.
Dawnguard.esm|01A930: Revel in your new powers, but do not forget who gave them to you.
Dawnguard.esm|01A942: Well, we got the knife... now all we need to do is track down one of those Canticle Trees.
Dawnguard.esm|01A943: Hope the moths like that bark as much as Dexion said they would.
Dawnguard.esm|01A944: Look at them... they've definitely taken a liking to you. And unless I'm seeing things, you're starting to... glimmer.
Dawnguard.esm|01A94F: It involves carefully removing the bark from a Canticle Tree which will in turn attract Ancestor Moths to you. Once enough of the moths are following, they'll provide you with the second sight needed to decipher the scrolls.
Dawnguard.esm|01A950: In keeping with tradition, you must use a specific tool in the Ancestor Glade, an implement known as a Draw Knife. Every Moth Priest is taught this ritual, but few ever get the chance to perform it... you should consider yourself fortunate if it works for you.
Dawnguard.esm|01A976: Sadly, yes. It's the inevitable fate we agree to pursue when reading the Elder Scrolls. It generally doesn't occur until a Moth Priest is in his latter years, but in my case I simply wasn't preparing myself properly.
Dawnguard.esm|01A977: You've come this far, and you've found several Elder Scrolls. Whether you believe it or not, the scrolls have a mind of their own. If they didn't want you to find them, they wouldn't allow it. Because of this, I strongly believe you were meant to hear the ancestral chorus. Only one way to find out.
Dawnguard.esm|01A978: By having the Ancestor Moths close to the Moth Priest, they can utilize the conduit and share the moth's augury. Only the most resilient of priests can do it this way... it takes years of practice to interpret the harmony.
Dawnguard.esm|01A979: Oh, the moths don't literally read the scrolls... but they maintain a connection to ancient magic that allows the Moth Priests to decipher them. If you listen closely when you find the glade, you should be able to hear their song... a soft, harmonious trilling. It's through this ancestral chorus that the moths tap into a form of primal augur and become a conduit for deciphering the scrolls.
Dawnguard.esm|01A97C: Good luck. I hope you find the answers you seek.
Dawnguard.esm|01A97D: Well, as I'm sure you've figured out by now, it's no mere coincidence that we're named "Moth Priests." The voice of the Ancestor Moth has always been an integral part of reading the Elder Scrolls.
Dawnguard.esm|01A97E: I've helped you all that I can. The rest is up to you.
Dawnguard.esm|01A97F: Yes, milady? What can I help you with?
Dawnguard.esm|01A980: Yes, milord? What can I help you with?
Dawnguard.esm|01A981: Deciphering one of the Elder Scrolls is nothing like reading a simple book. There's quite a bit of ritual and concentration required. It can take months or even years to complete a single scroll and even then, months to recover.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA05: Please, take the bow... it's yours.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA06: You care nothing for Serana or our plight. Whether or not you've become one of us in order to survive the Soul Cairn, you're still a vampire hunter at heart. You're here because we're abominations in your mind. Evil creatures that need to be destroyed.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA47: If this ends up being a wasted trip, your friend Dexion and I are going to have some words when we get back.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA49: Hmph... not very impressive, is it?
Dawnguard.esm|01AA5B: Looks like we're on the right track.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA66: That's a fine looking animal you've got there. I had a dog once. Kept getting fur in my mead.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA67: What kind of beast is that? No law against it, but if that thing starts attacking people, it's getting a sword in the gullet.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA81: Yes. Please, follow me. One more word of warning. There's a dragon that calls itself Durnehviir roaming the Cairn. The Ideal Masters have charged him with overseeing the Keepers, and will undoubtedly investigate the fact that the barrier has fallen.
Dawnguard.esm|01AA82: Yes. I've kept it safely secured here ever since I was imprisoned. Fortunately, you seem to have already killed the Keepers maintaining the barrier around these ruins.
Dawnguard.esm|01AACF: Drinking it gives you strength, but it also gets you pretty messed up... That's why we thought of putting it in the skooma.
Dawnguard.esm|01AAED: Perhaps they'll never return to their former appearance, but over the centuries, I've noticed a rise in their intellect. If a line of communication could be established with them, maybe they can find peace. It's the only way they'll discover that they weren't always malignant... they were once a proud and prosperous race.
Dawnguard.esm|01AAEE: The twisted forms you've seen didn't occur overnight. It isn't a plague or a disease that ravaged our species. The dwarves may have stolen their sight, but it took many generations for them to become what they are today.
Dawnguard.esm|01AAEF: I feel nothing but sympathy for the Betrayed, despite my actions against them. But I'm afraid that they're well beyond a cure at this point.
Dawnguard.esm|01C5FC: The ice entombs the heart. The bane of Kagrenac and Dagoth Ur. To harness it is to know. The fundaments. The Dwemer lockbox hides it from me. The Elder Scroll gives insight deeper than the deep ones, though. To bring about the opening.
Dawnguard.esm|01C677: I'll bring you everything we have on them, but it's not much. So don't get your hopes up. It's mostly lies, leavened with rumor and conjecture.
Dawnguard.esm|02725E: Somewhere up north, in the ice fields. Said he found some old Dwemer artifact, but... well, that was years ago. Haven't heard from him since.
Dawnguard.esm|027260: Oh no. I hope not. But even I haven't seen him in years, and we were close. Became obsessed with the Dwemer. Took off north saying he had found some old artifact. Haven't seen him since. Somewhere in the ice fields, if you want to try to find him.
Dawnguard.esm|0AE907: Nothing, until you choose to use it. Then, well... you got a taste. Stronger, faster. It doesn't last long, though. The blood of your foes can sustain you. If you're willing to feed.
Dawnguard.esm|0AF0E6: Do you? It is true there are some here who have spent years studying the accumulated knowledge of the scrolls. But what you seek does not come easily, and can destroy those without a strong will.
Dawnguard.esm|0B3101: I will see you dead, monster.
Dawnguard.esm|0E2FE3: And what do you plan to do with it? Do you even know what you're asking about, or are you just someone's errand girl?
Dawnguard.esm|0E3035: What about... wait. Are you? Were you the one the Greybeards were calling?
Dawnguard.esm|0E88F9: It's a werewolf! To arms! To arms!
Dragonborn.esm|017E6B: Have you seen Varona? I'm hungry.
Dragonborn.esm|017E6C: What! Who's going to make my tea? Why do these things always happen to me? Well, don't just stand there. Go find me another steward. I'm sure there's plenty of people in Raven Rock to choose from.
Dragonborn.esm|017E6D: Well find her. Tell her I want apple cabbage stew, with some canis root tea.
Dragonborn.esm|017E70: Hmm. I might have a few staffs, if you're interested in that sort of thing.
Dragonborn.esm|017E71: Not recently. Neloth keeps me very busy with spell research. Ask Elynea. She and Varona seem to be quite friendly.
Dragonborn.esm|017E72: Is it tea time already? Well, Neloth's just going to have to wait. Varona went to Raven Rock to arrange for supplies.
Dragonborn.esm|017E73: She left for Raven Rock hours ago. She promised to bring me back some fresh blisterwort.
Dragonborn.esm|017E74: Do you need all your toes? I could really use a fresh toe for...nevermind. You aren't moldy enough.
Dragonborn.esm|017E75: First it's ash spawn attacks. Then my home withers. It's almost like someone is out to get me.
Dragonborn.esm|017E76: Well, my pretties. What shall we do today? Maybe see if you can regenerate your fingers?
Dragonborn.esm|017E78: I don't think you belong here. Does Master Neloth know about you?
Dragonborn.esm|017E79: Why am I here? Did Master Neloth do one of his experiments on me again?
Dragonborn.esm|017E7A: It's nice to see a friendly face around here. Everyone else can be so grumpy.
Dragonborn.esm|017E7B: Sometimes being Master Neloth's apprentice is...painful.
Dragonborn.esm|017E7C: Hmmph. Another wanderer. I suppose you'll be wanting potions, just like the others.
Dragonborn.esm|017E7D: Mushrooms and molds of Vvardenfell have adapted to the ash far better than the green plants.
Dragonborn.esm|017E7E: I like it here at Tel Mithryn. Hardly anyone bothers me. People can be so irritating.
Dragonborn.esm|017E7F: We don't get many visitors. Is there something you need?
Dragonborn.esm|017E80: There are rumors of dragons on the mainland. I hope they don't show up here.
Dragonborn.esm|017E81: I'm going to have to go to Raven Rock soon. We're running low on some things.
Dragonborn.esm|017E82: I hope you're not expecting a meal. I've barely got enough for them that live here.
Dragonborn.esm|017E83: Oh dear. Why am I out here? I need to get back and make Master Neloth's tea.
Dragonborn.esm|017E84: I'd better tell Varona to bring apples and cabbage from Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|01842E: Well, I've never been one to pass up an opportunity. Sorry to run, but I'm off to my new job.
Dragonborn.esm|01842F: It would have to be someone pretty desperate. You might ask my apprentice Drovas. Lately he seems to be hard up for coin.
Dragonborn.esm|018430: Are you nuts? Neloth is crazy! I hear he talks to mud crabs.
Dragonborn.esm|018431: Uh... you mean leave Raven Rock? For good? To go work for a crazed Telvanni wizard? Sure. Just let me get my things. On second thought, nevermind. I can get new stuff.
Dragonborn.esm|018432: Have you found Varona yet? I could really use some tea.
Dragonborn.esm|018433: When you find my new steward, have him report to me. With a cup of canis root tea.
Dragonborn.esm|018435: Don't take too long finding me a steward.
Dragonborn.esm|018436: That woman is never around when you need her.
Dragonborn.esm|018437: Now where did I put...Oh, yeah.
Dragonborn.esm|018438: I don't care where she is. Go get her. Now. Any my tea better be hot.
Dragonborn.esm|018439: That Drovas man? I suppose he'll do. He makes dreadful canis root tea though. But, I should be grateful. Here, take this for your efforts. Oh, and you may use my staff enchanter if you want.
Dragonborn.esm|01843A: Dead? Well that's annoying. How did she die?
Dragonborn.esm|018AD0: Your contributions have earned you citizenship here, friend. I'll always consider you one of us.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD1: The mines are open and the ebony has begun to flow because of your discovery. My eternal gratitude and blessings go with you, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD2: I feel I owe you my life for thwarting the House Hlaalu plot to have me assassinated. My deepest thanks.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD3: Traveling beyond The Bulwark will be much safer as the result of your victory at Fort Frostmoth. Many thanks.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD4: Anything you can contribute to Raven Rock will be greatly appreciated... and rewarded appropriately, of course.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD5: Don't let the state of Raven Rock fool you. Our desire for Raven Rock's return to its former glory is as strong as The Bulwark itself.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD6: Whether you're simply visiting or staying here, I think you'll find our citizens will welcome you with open arms.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD7: Second Councilor Adrano can assist you with anything you might need.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD8: I can't thank you enough for reopening the ebony mines. You've brought light to the darkness, my friend.
Dragonborn.esm|018AD9: You've succeeded where I've failed by ending the threat to Councilor Morvayn's life. I shall not forget it.
Dragonborn.esm|018ADA: Assisting the Redoran Guard with the ash spawn attacks has earned you quite the reputation here. Keep it up.
Dragonborn.esm|018ADB: My apologies for doubting you, serjo.
Dragonborn.esm|018ADC: Your visit here will be tolerated as long as you abide by our laws, otherwise you'll answer to the Redoran Guard.
Dragonborn.esm|018ADD: I've sworn to keep Councilor Morvayn safe, and I intend to take that oath to the grave if need be.
Dragonborn.esm|018ADE: Most exiled dark elves call Vvardenfell their home. I call Raven Rock our home... may it last a thousand years.
Dragonborn.esm|018ADF: I can't believe Miraak made us build those shrines. To take our free will like that... just horrible.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE0: As the wife to the Second Councilor of Raven Rock, it would be my pleasure to help you any way that I can.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE1: Your efforts in Raven Rock have proven most invigorating for my husband. I haven't seen him this pleased in many years.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE2: You aren't here with bad news regarding my husband, are you? I couldn't bear it.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE3: It's been a long time since I walked the ashlands of Vvardenfell. I miss it terribly.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE4: Perhaps our town isn't as grand as Mournhold, but it's our home and we must make the most of it.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE5: With the ash spawn threat taken care of, I can give my men the rest they deserve. Well done, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE6: Modyn Veleth, Captain of the Redoran Guard at your service.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE7: The security of both Raven Rock and Councilor Morvayn is my primary concern.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE8: Looking for something to lend a hand with? We have problems aplenty in Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|018AE9: Don't let my posting here fool you. I was once in command of over a hundred troops... my luck simply changed when our homeland was destroyed.
Dragonborn.esm|018AEA: Some consider my methods harsh, but playing the coddling nursemaid has never won a battle.
Dragonborn.esm|018AEB: You honor the Reclamations with your actions. This will not be forgotten.
Dragonborn.esm|018AEC: I am Othreloth, Elder of the Reclamations and keeper of this Temple.
Dragonborn.esm|018AED: Though our spirits may be low, and danger lurks beyond the light, I am confident that They will never abandon us again.
Dragonborn.esm|018AEE: The Reclamations herald the dawn of a new age, and the downfall of the false Tribunal.
Dragonborn.esm|018AEF: If you're in need of anything material, Attendant Hlervu can assist you.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF0: You may have everyone fooled, but I see what you're doing here. The call of gold sends you scurrying like a rat.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF1: Excuse me, but the Temple is for followers of our faith alone. Outsiders aren't welcome here.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF2: Elder Othreloth is grooming me to be his eventual replacement. Soon, I'll be running the Temple my way.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF3: If they desire salvation, the citizens of Raven Rock need to open their eyes... and their pockets.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF4: The mine's open, people are buying drinks and I'm making money again... just like old times.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF5: I hear you're the reason Councilor Morvayn's still breathing today. Serves those damn Hlaalu traitors right!
Dragonborn.esm|018AF6: How about a drink for the hero who cleared Fort Frostmoth!
Dragonborn.esm|018AF7: Welcome to the Retching Netch, outlander. What can I get you?
Dragonborn.esm|018AF8: If you're looking for a room, or perhaps some libations, you've come to the right place.
Dragonborn.esm|018AF9: I know everyone in Raven Rock, and they know me. You want stories? I've got plenty.
Dragonborn.esm|018AFA: I've seen many visitors come and go, but you... there's something about you that I like.
Dragonborn.esm|018AFB: If I could only get my hands on Miraak. I'd make him pay for playing with my mind.
Dragonborn.esm|018AFC: Want to buy anything, or just laugh at our misfortunes?
Dragonborn.esm|018AFD: I'd be on the first ship off of this island if I had the coin. What a waste.
Dragonborn.esm|018AFE: Yeah, so. What can I get you? Got everything from potions to pitchforks.
Dragonborn.esm|018AFF: If you're looking to buy, then I'm looking to sell.
Dragonborn.esm|018B00: What if Miraak decides to make us do something else next... like kill someone we love. I can't bear the thought!
Dragonborn.esm|018B01: Miserable... just miserable. I hate this place.
Dragonborn.esm|018B02: This island's nothing but a pile of rubble. What's the point trying to make a life here?
Dragonborn.esm|018B03: If you're looking to purchase something, speak to my father, Fethis.
Dragonborn.esm|018B05: You're a coward, Miraak! Why don't you come out and face us yourself instead of controlling our minds!
Dragonborn.esm|018B06: Glover Mallory's the name. If you're looking for a smith, you've found one of the best.
Dragonborn.esm|018B07: Steel, dwarven, ebony... if it's broken, I can mend it.
Dragonborn.esm|018B08: Either you're here to get showered in sparks, or you're looking for something to buy. Out with it.
Dragonborn.esm|018B09: A lot of folks around here look to strangers for help. I hope you won't disappoint them.
Dragonborn.esm|018B0A: I can't believe you found a way to reopen the mines. I don't know how to thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|018B0B: There was a time when this town meant something. Now it's all gone.
Dragonborn.esm|018B0D: Maybe I won't live as long as the dark elves, but at least I didn't abandon my home.
Dragonborn.esm|018B0E: If it wasn't for my wife, Aphia, I'd have joined my ancestors long ago.
Dragonborn.esm|018EEA: I don't know what you did in those mines, but it put a smile on Crescius's face. I've never seen him so happy.
Dragonborn.esm|018EEB: Allow me to apologize for Crescius's behavior, I'm afraid he hasn't been himself lately.
Dragonborn.esm|018EEC: Don't be put off by my husband's mood... he enjoys having visitors.
Dragonborn.esm|018EED: It's been tough running the household alone, but Crescius isn't getting any younger.
Dragonborn.esm|018EEE: Those East Empire people should be ashamed of themselves for abandoning Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|018EEF: Your contribution to our community will never be forgotten. Azura protect you, serjo.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF0: Vendil Severin. A pleasure to meet you, serjo.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF1: Always glad to help, serjo. What can I do for you?
Dragonborn.esm|018EF2: We meet again, serjo. Speak your mind.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF3: You've brought so much hope to our people, we are forever in your debt.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF4: My husband Vendil feared visitors would shun Raven Rock. I'm glad to see he was mistaken.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF5: Always nice to speak to someone from the mainland.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF6: I hope you enjoy your stay in Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF7: Mmm. Always a pleasure to watch when a man like you walks by.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF8: It gets lonely out here you know, on this remote island.
Dragonborn.esm|018EF9: Oh, hello. You looking for a handout or something?
Dragonborn.esm|018EFA: My father and mother are the richest on the island... and that includes Councilor Morvayn.
Dragonborn.esm|018EFB: If you ask me, my father knows how to run things better than the Councilor.
Dragonborn.esm|018EFC: If I could just get my hands on Miraak for what he did to us...
Dragonborn.esm|018EFD: Welcome to Raven Rock, traveler. Could I interest you in any fine ash-grown foods?
Dragonborn.esm|018EFE: My wife and I are doing the best we can to keep Raven Rock fed.
Dragonborn.esm|018EFF: If you ever need apothecary supplies, talk to my wife Milore.
Dragonborn.esm|018F00: My wife's quite the alchemist. Thanks to her, my ash yams are growing quicker than ever before.
Dragonborn.esm|018F01: Just thinking that some madman was inside my mind. Gives me shivers just thinking about it.
Dragonborn.esm|018F02: Destroying the ash spawn has made it safer to work in the fields. Thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|018F03: Looking for any potions? Ingredients? Alchemical supplies?
Dragonborn.esm|018F04: If you're looking for any exotic ingredients, just ask. I might have what you need.
Dragonborn.esm|018F05: My husband Garyn maintains our farm. Without him, Raven Rock would likely go hungry.
Dragonborn.esm|018F06: I'm asking myself why Miraak was making us build these shrines... and I'm afraid the answer will be far worse than any of us can imagine.
Dragonborn.esm|018F07: Anything you can offer, please. I'm starving.
Dragonborn.esm|018F08: Head back to the mainland, wanderer. There's nothing here but misery.
Dragonborn.esm|018F09: I lost my house, my family, my life... and then I lost my mind. How could it have gotten any worse?
Dragonborn.esm|018F0C: Don't pity me. And don't look at me like that. I know what I am.
Dragonborn.esm|018F0D: I could swing a pickaxe better than any man in the mines. Made thousands of coins. Now it's all gone.
Dragonborn.esm|018F0E: How are you supposed to live when your only means of trade is yanked out from under you?
Dragonborn.esm|018FD6: Well... I guess nothing bad happened the last time. Fine, we'll sail as soon as I get paid.
Dragonborn.esm|018FDD: Load up, and we'll get underway.
Dragonborn.esm|01956B: What?! That's impossible. Wait... unless she didn't actually die! The heart stone could have kept her right at the edge of life and death. Could it be? Could Ildari still be alive? Could she be my nemesis? Wait a moment while I cast a more specific divination.
Dragonborn.esm|01956C: Just wear it. It will light up the source when you get close to it. I enchanted the ring with a bit of ash from the last ash spawn attack. It can follow the magical link back to its summoner.
Dragonborn.esm|01956D: Not yet. That may come later. First I need to identify the culprit. I've enchanted this ring to find the source of the attacks. Here, take it.
Dragonborn.esm|01956E: Patience is for fools. When you are tired of looking over your shoulder, you'll pay me.
Dragonborn.esm|01956F: Ildari? Then it's my own fault. She was my apprentice before Talvas. She volunteered for one of my experiments involving heart stones. It was quite annoying when she died.
Dragonborn.esm|019570: There are too many coincidences. There must be someone behind all of my recent troubles.
Dragonborn.esm|019571: Wow. Sorry about that. He's never going to stop you know. He'll keep sending thugs after you until either you pay him or kill him.
Dragonborn.esm|019572: Uh...Well, sort of. I owed him a thousand septims, but not any more. He doesn't dare try to collect now that I'm Neloth's steward.
Dragonborn.esm|019573: Hmm. I've known for decades that heart stones will animate the ash. But it would have to be very close by. Where exactly did you find this heart stone?
Dragonborn.esm|019574: Yes, yes. As I was saying, heart stones can animate nearby ash.
Dragonborn.esm|019575: Yes. I have enemies. Legions of them back in Morrowind. However, in this case I have reason to believe it is someone in Solstheim.
Dragonborn.esm|019576: Take your best shot, fetcher! But if you draw on me, the guards will put the bounty on your head, not mine.
Dragonborn.esm|019578: It seems you've inherited a situation.
Dragonborn.esm|019579: Are you ready to pay me yet?
Dragonborn.esm|01957A: You could try Drovas Relvi. He came in here asking my father if he needed any odd jobs done. Seemed kind of desperate.
Dragonborn.esm|01957B: As one Thieves Guild member to another? Try Drovas over in the Retching Netch. Not too long ago he tried to pawn some stuff off. Seemed kinda desperate for coin.
Dragonborn.esm|01957C: I don't have time to go traipsing all over Solstheim. If I don't hear back from you after a while, you're probably dead. Maybe I'll send Talvas out next.
Dragonborn.esm|01957E: Nothing for now. But watch your back. I'll be sending collectors to find you. They won't ask so nicely. One way or the other, I'll get my money.
Dragonborn.esm|01957F: The link is weak, so you'll have to be within about a hundred feet of the source.
Dragonborn.esm|019580: Pay him? Why? I mean, I don't have the money. Well, I might soon. Neloth does pay well. But the important thing is that I don't have it. In fact, it's not likely I'll ever have it. Expenses you know. Sorry about the thugs and Mogrul and everything.
Dragonborn.esm|019581: See, that wasn't so hard, now was it. Now get outta here.
Dragonborn.esm|019582: Maybe not. I hear they've been seen over Skyrim too. Even so, there is more than enough evidence that someone is out to get me.
Dragonborn.esm|019584: Drovas Revli owes me 1000 septims. At least he did until you set him up with that Telvanni wizard. Now he's untouchable. The way I figure it, it's your fault he can't pay me. So I guess I'm going to have to collect it from you.
Dragonborn.esm|019585: Tired of running? I knew you were weak. Now pay me.
Dragonborn.esm|019586: Don't be ridiculous. Who would run this place? You should ask around at The Retching Netch. Someone there might be crazy enough, or drunk enough, to do it.
Dragonborn.esm|019587: I'm perfectly content with my current position.
Dragonborn.esm|019588: I could never leave Adril behind, and he will never leave Lleril. Besides, I'd never work for Neloth! I heard he cut the heart out of his former apprentice.
Dragonborn.esm|019589: Ha ha ha! Gods, that's rich. Work for Neloth. Ha ha ha!
Dragonborn.esm|01958A: My father needs me. He can't run his market stand alone. Besides, Neloth is as loony as a skooma addict. He wanted my father to import a white mammoth. Alive!
Dragonborn.esm|01958B: Nah. I don't think so. Anyway, I don't think he'd take on anyone that isn't a dunmer.
Dragonborn.esm|01958C: Me? I hardly think so. That Telvanni wizard is unhinged. I heard he tries to drink the ash.
Dragonborn.esm|01958D: Now why would I want to work for House Telvanni? Besides, Neloth is a madman!
Dragonborn.esm|01958E: Ha, ha, ha! Not on your life! I'd have to be truly desperate to do that.
Dragonborn.esm|01958F: Not on your life! I'd sooner try to swim back to Windhelm.
Dragonborn.esm|019590: I'd saw off my own leg for a septim, but you couldn't pay me enough to do that.
Dragonborn.esm|019591: Would he give me skooma? I might do it for some skooma.
Dragonborn.esm|019592: My new steward is...acceptable. If only he made better tea.
Dragonborn.esm|019593: Who did this to us? It isn't another one of Master Neloth's games, is it?
Dragonborn.esm|019594: Ash spawn keep appearing. One night a part of my tower mysteriously withered. Then my steward was killed. You had to find me a replacement. I've even been attacked by dragons! Do I need to go on?
Dragonborn.esm|019595: Skooma! Lovely skooma. But I'd sooner give it up than go work for her. That dunmer is crazy!
Dragonborn.esm|0198B5: You're right, my friend. I just grow weary of the struggle.
Dragonborn.esm|0198B6: I should have another batch of vegetables ready for you soon, Geldis.
Dragonborn.esm|0198B7: She's not a little girl anymore, Fethis. When are you going to let her go?
Dragonborn.esm|0198B8: Dreyla, I have a message for you.
Dragonborn.esm|0198B9: We'll pull through, I promise. Councilor Morvayn would never let us down.
Dragonborn.esm|0198BA: Have you taken leave of your senses? We could be exposed... ruined. Ten years of planning all for nothing!
Dragonborn.esm|0198BB: I don't know what you're talking about. I'm here to do a job, and that's it.
Dragonborn.esm|0198BC: People laugh at things they don't believe. It helps them accept the unknown.
Dragonborn.esm|0198BD: Had we only left a day earlier... she'd still be with us. It's my fault she's dead.
Dragonborn.esm|0198BE: Of course I'm certain. I wished to bless the tombs with the monthly invocation, but I couldn't get the door to open.
Dragonborn.esm|0198BF: I'm sorry, my love. It's this island... it's never felt the same since we left Vvardenfell. It's as though a part of me is missing.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C0: Well of course, now that they're under House Redoran's protection, why would they want to go back to Mournhold?
Dragonborn.esm|0198C1: Very well, cover the difference from my personal treasury.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C2: My men have laid down their lives to keep Raven Rock safe. We've fought too hard to leave. We can't just let it all go.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C3: These hands were made for bending steel... not mending bonemold. But, it's certainly better than not working at all.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C4: There's no reason to bourdon you with my troubles. How about I just settle for some mazte and we'll forget I mentioned it.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C5: Things seem just as bleak here.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C6: She's jealous. Can't say that I blame her.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C7: What's wrong with you? Do you want to ruin everything?
Dragonborn.esm|0198C8: They've laughed at me, Aphia. They thought I was a crazy old fool. But I proved them wrong.
Dragonborn.esm|0198C9: You don't need to say anything, father. I miss her too.
Dragonborn.esm|0198CB: The Redoran Council tasked me with keeping Lleril alive, which I've done without question for over a hundred years. He's the closest thing I've had to a brother, and we all owe him our lives. Am I to suddenly turn my back?
Dragonborn.esm|0198CC: I'm hearing that the Council will be staying in Blacklight permanently.
Dragonborn.esm|0198CD: No, Councilor. I'm afraid we're going to fall short once again.
Dragonborn.esm|0198CE: Aren't you pleased to keeping busy?
Dragonborn.esm|0198CF: Geldis, I want you to let me know if you see Dreyla with Captain Veleth. I don't trust him.
Dragonborn.esm|0198D0: I'm afraid we're completely out of them at the moment. Sounds like you've had a rough day.
Dragonborn.esm|0198D1: We tried to start a new life on the mainland, remember? It was too dangerous. Vvardenfell isn't ready.
Dragonborn.esm|0198D2: I'm worried. I think she knows.
Dragonborn.esm|0198D3: Then I'll simply have to fall in love with some other senile, grousing old fool.
Dragonborn.esm|0198D4: Of course, Elder. Please forgive me.
Dragonborn.esm|0198D5: I never got a chance to thank you for everything you've done. Ever since your mother died...
Dragonborn.esm|0198D6: Attendant, why has the door to the lower level been locked?
Dragonborn.esm|0198D7: How much longer do you intend to serve as his shield, Adril?
Dragonborn.esm|0198D9: Did you look over next month's expenses? Will we have enough?
Dragonborn.esm|0198DA: I'm afraid not. If we don't do something soon, we may have to think about moving on.
Dragonborn.esm|0198DB: The usual. Piles of armor to repair for the Redoran Guard.
Dragonborn.esm|0198DD: How about a hundred Dunmer armed to the teeth?
Dragonborn.esm|0198DE: Garyn, did we make the right decision coming here?
Dragonborn.esm|0198DF: I don't know, look for someone else to hop into bed with perhaps!
Dragonborn.esm|0198E0: And what will you do when I'm gone?
Dragonborn.esm|0198E1: We no longer use that name for them. They have reclaimed their identity and no longer represent the patronage of the false Tribunal. You'd do well to remember that next time, Attendant.
Dragonborn.esm|0198E2: My primary concern is your safety, Councilor. I give you my word that no harm will befall you while I stand by your side.
Dragonborn.esm|0198E3: I don't know what we'd do without him. Send him my thanks.
Dragonborn.esm|0198E4: It's not looking good, is it?
Dragonborn.esm|0198E6: Captain Veleth! Good to see you again. What can I get you?
Dragonborn.esm|0198E7: Well, if we keep having harvests like this, you'll never have to eat one again.
Dragonborn.esm|0198E8: What am I supposed to do until then?
Dragonborn.esm|0198E9: It doesn't matter, my husband. Let's simply cherish the time we have for now.
Dragonborn.esm|0198EB: Your precious "contacts" have yet to conjure up a scrap of food or work for us, father. Don't make me choose, father... I beg you!
Dragonborn.esm|0198EC: Remember your lessons, Attendant. "The road to true belief is strewn with the incredulity of those that failed the journey."
Dragonborn.esm|0198ED: I'll speak with Garyn and see if we can't increase the crop production this season. Sorry, Cindiri, I fear we'll be feasting on ash yams a bit more often this year.
Dragonborn.esm|0198EE: I understand, Adril. I know you're both doing your best.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F0: How about you keep serving the drinks, and I'll decide what's right for her!
Dragonborn.esm|0198F1: He said he'll meet you where you met for the first time. He said you'd know what it means.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F2: You're doing your job, Adril. No one can fault you for that.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F3: Well, we had to do something. I'd rather starve than eat another one of those horrid turnips.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F4: If everything goes well, we won't have to stay here much longer. For now, we wait.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F5: I'll have food ready soon. You need to eat.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F6: I was. I wandered the wastes of Vvardenfell, carrying the message of the Reclamations from tribe to tribe.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F7: He's twice your age and only wants to marry into our family to use my contacts at the East Empire Company. No, I won't allow it.
Dragonborn.esm|0198F8: And the Reclamations, they did nothing? Why did they allow the Tribunal to gain our people's favor at all?
Dragonborn.esm|0198F9: I was concerned about our food stores. Gjalund can only carry so much on each journey, and I'm afraid we might run out.
Dragonborn.esm|0198FA: No, Councilor. He's spoken to everyone in town... twice. If House Hlaalu has a spy in Raven Rock, he's hidden beyond our means to find him.
Dragonborn.esm|0198FB: Fortunately, none. Captain Veleth was able to drive them off almost single-handedly.
Dragonborn.esm|0198FC: Oh my goodness, I'm so sorry, Geldis! Please, tell me... what did he say?
Dragonborn.esm|0198FD: I'm sorry. I shouldn't bark at you like that.
Dragonborn.esm|0198FE: They're incredible. I don't know how we ever got them to take in this soil, but I'm glad they did.
Dragonborn.esm|0198FF: This town bores me. How much longer must we stay in this wretched place?
Dragonborn.esm|019900: You're too good to me, Aphia. I only wish I could remain with you longer.
Dragonborn.esm|019901: You were one of the Ashlanders, Elder?
Dragonborn.esm|019902: Father, Captain Veleth and I are in love. He means everything to me. Am I to simply ignore my own heart?
Dragonborn.esm|019903: The Tribunal's destruction was wrought by their own hands. Their actions, or lack of action caused their downfall.
