Dragonborn.esm|019904: Of course, Cindiri. What troubles you?
Dragonborn.esm|019905: Has Captain Veleth discovered anything yet?
Dragonborn.esm|019906: As if we didn't have enough to contend with already. How many did we lose this time?
Dragonborn.esm|019907: Tell you what. If you use them to make your kwama egg stew, you can have them for free.
Dragonborn.esm|019908: Raven Rock doesn't offer much in the way of companionship. You should be happy for her.
Dragonborn.esm|019909: It's not from your father. It's from Captain Veleth.
Dragonborn.esm|01990A: Geldis, I need to know if you've heard anything about House Hlaalu setting foot on the island.
Dragonborn.esm|01990B: Are the ash yams growing like you expected this season?
Dragonborn.esm|01990C: Look, "dearest", what I do on my own time is my business. You stick to your business and we'll both be fine.
Dragonborn.esm|01990D: Well, I put up with you, don't I?
Dragonborn.esm|01990E: Dreyla! I told you I didn't want you seeing him anymore. Must I lock you into the house at night?
Dragonborn.esm|01990F: Elder, why have the Reclamations destroyed the Tribunal?
Dragonborn.esm|019910: May I have a word with you, Councilor?
Dragonborn.esm|019911: It doesn't matter, Adril. I'm responsible for those people and I'll make whatever sacrifices are necessary to keep them alive.
Dragonborn.esm|019912: Councilor, I'm afraid there's been another ash spawn attack at The Bulwark.
Dragonborn.esm|019913: How much do I owe you this time?
Dragonborn.esm|019914: He's twice her age and they barely know each other. You expect me to ignore them?
Dragonborn.esm|019915: Tell my father if he wants to speak to me, he should do it in person.
Dragonborn.esm|019916: I'm not holding back. If I knew something I wouldn't wait until you strolled in, I'd come find you.
Dragonborn.esm|019917: It's your choice, "father". Anytime you want to end it, you just give the word.
Dragonborn.esm|019918: I've seen the way you look at her. Do you take me for a fool?
Dragonborn.esm|019919: You're too kind to people, always giving them the benefit of the doubt. Don't know how you put up with it.
Dragonborn.esm|01991A: It was her time, father. None of us have any control over that. You mustn't blame yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|01991B: I'm certain it's only stuck, Elder. I'll speak to Glover Mallory and see if he can take a look at it.
Dragonborn.esm|01991C: Every Dunmer left a piece of themselves behind when they departed our homeland. All we can do is make the most of what we have.
Dragonborn.esm|01991E: Your coffers are running low, Councilor. At this rate, your treasury will be empty by next winter.
Dragonborn.esm|019AC4: Forbidden knowledge was somewhat of a specialty of the dwarves, eh? You don't think they would just leave it alone, do you?
Dragonborn.esm|019AC5: Yes, I do. I haven't been able to get it, though. But maybe together we can unlock the secrets the Dwemer left behind.
Dragonborn.esm|019AC6: Now that is a dangerous path indeed. Hermaeus Mora gives nothing away for free. You may end up like Miraak, of course. Two power-mad Dragonborn. It could be very interesting.
Dragonborn.esm|019AC7: Found one? Yes, and you read it, too, didn't you? Don't try to deny it, you've got the look. I can see it now. Dangerous knowledge is still knowledge and therefore useful. Usually turns out to be the most useful, in my experience.
Dragonborn.esm|019AC8: You refer to the tomes of esoteric knowledge that old Hermaeus Mora has scattered throughout the world?
Dragonborn.esm|019B1C: It's good that our people are now free. We can attempt to restore balance to our home.
Dragonborn.esm|019B1D: Mother worries when I walk too far from the village. She says a dragon might snatch me up, but I'm not afraid.
Dragonborn.esm|019B1E: I want to be a great hunter like Wulf. I practice stalking prey on the rabbits and the foxes in the woods.
Dragonborn.esm|019B1F: I fear to think what would have happened if you hadn't saved me from the elves.
Dragonborn.esm|019B20: There have been many strangers in our lands these past few moons.
Dragonborn.esm|019B21: We call you a friend, but you must still respect our ways.
Dragonborn.esm|019B22: The Dark Elves sometimes visit our village, though it's been awhile since I've seen one.
Dragonborn.esm|019B23: Whatever you and Tharstan found in the old tomb, he is very excited about it.
Dragonborn.esm|019B24: It is the will of the All-Maker that we cut firewood only from the dead trees.
Dragonborn.esm|019B25: As the beasts of the forest provide food and furs, so the trees of the forest provide fire.
Dragonborn.esm|019B26: All that the Skaal need can be found in the land and the water.
Dragonborn.esm|019B27: I have goods to offer, if you have need.
Dragonborn.esm|019B28: It brings me great comfort to know that Nikulas will stay. I thank you, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|019B29: It's been many winters since my husband returned to the All-Maker, but my heart still aches from the loss.
Dragonborn.esm|019B2A: As leader of the Skaal, I name you friend to our people. You are welcome here.
Dragonborn.esm|019B2B: The Skaal live in harmony with nature and take only what we need from the land.
Dragonborn.esm|019B2C: I've met a few Nords from Skyrim. They thought us strange, but in our hearts, I don't think we are so different.
Dragonborn.esm|019B2E: The historian has not stopped speaking your praises.
Dragonborn.esm|019B2F: There is strength and courage in your eyes. You have the spirit of a great hunter.
Dragonborn.esm|019B30: The dragons of old have returned! By the All-Maker, I hope they will leave our village alone.
Dragonborn.esm|019B32: You freed us from the dark dream, and for that the Skaal are grateful.
Dragonborn.esm|019B33: I've told young Nikulas that the world beyond our lands is no place for the Skaal, but he won't listen.
Dragonborn.esm|019B34: May the All-Maker protect us from whatever dark power took control of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|019B35: I've seen outsiders in our village before, but none were like you.
Dragonborn.esm|019B36: I heard that you and Tharstan explored an ancient crypt. That sounds like an exciting adventure.
Dragonborn.esm|019B37: Sometimes I wonder if we're the only Skaal. Could there be more villages like ours?
Dragonborn.esm|019B38: Fanari says the Nords of Skyrim have many gods, but none are as wise and powerful as the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|019B39: At his age, Tharstan should have known better than to fumble about in dark tombs.
Dragonborn.esm|019B3A: The Skaal value silence over needless talking.
Dragonborn.esm|019B3E: That was quite an adventure we had, wasn't it?
Dragonborn.esm|019B3F: I've tried to interest the villagers in reading and history, but I haven't had much luck.
Dragonborn.esm|019B40: I do enjoy sharing stories of my travels in Tamriel with young Nikulas.
Dragonborn.esm|019B41: I am called Wulf Wild-Blood. I am First Hunter of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|019B42: I know a warrior when I see one, friend of the Skaal. You have the heart of a mighty bear.
Dragonborn.esm|019B43: The blood of wolves runs through your veins, Skaal-friend.
Dragonborn.esm|019B44: If you would hunt a wolf, then you must know how the wolf thinks and moves. You must become a wolf yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|019B45: I fear what would have happened if you hadn't freed us.
Dragonborn.esm|019B46: The Skaal and the land are one, joined by the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|019B47: This Dark Elves of Raven Rock keep to their city, and we Skaal keep to our village.
Dragonborn.esm|019B48: Fanari speaks her mind, perhaps more than she should.
Dragonborn.esm|019B6F: So, Ildari does live after all. She thought she could hide from me, but she can't!
Dragonborn.esm|019B70: Well, well. You have proven to be a more valuable ally than I anticipated. I have a staff for you, but I would also like to make you a member of my household. A member of House Telvanni. No need to thank me. I know you are overwhelmed with gratitude.
Dragonborn.esm|019B71: My necropsies of Spriggans led me to believe that a heart stone could be implanted in a person's chest, granting them great power. I was considering replacing my heart with a heart stone. Fortunately Ildari offered to let me operate on her first. Of course this was before your briarheart expedition. Now I know that I did the surgery all wrong.
Dragonborn.esm|019B72: Me? No. Highpoint Tower will be filled with monsters and traps. It's much safer for me to risk your life. Don't worry. If you die, I'll find some other way to take care of Ildari.
Dragonborn.esm|019B74: Have you found the source of the attacks?
Dragonborn.esm|019B75: Don't lose that ring. It was difficult to make.
Dragonborn.esm|019B77: Are you prepared to deal with Ildari?
Dragonborn.esm|019B79: You're alive. I hope that bodes ill for Ildari.
Dragonborn.esm|019BCA: Sure, but then where would you go?
Dragonborn.esm|019BCB: If I had the resources at my disposal, I'd build you a wall myself.
Dragonborn.esm|019BCC: Is it because of your father? Or is it me?
Dragonborn.esm|019BCD: You must be using quite a bit of your raw materials repairing their weapons and armor. I'm sorry we can't compensate you.
Dragonborn.esm|019BCE: Perhaps that's reassuring enough for you, but it's something that still keeps me awake at night.
Dragonborn.esm|019BCF: I don't know. There are many who would've already expected us to leave this place.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD0: Excuse me, captain. Do you have any plans for the west end of town? We feel awfully exposed over there.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD1: I don't know if I can do this anymore, Modyn.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD2: Nah. He... finds things people are looking for. Makes a good bit of coin doing it, too.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD3: So what do you think, Vendil? You think this town will survive?
Dragonborn.esm|019BD4: I promise you, I have nothing but good intentions towards everyone in this town.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD5: Fethis, all of us mourn your wife's passing. Just don't let it affect how you treat your daughter.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD6: I'm going to be fine, my love. Captain Veleth would never allow anything to happen to us.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD7: That's quite a distance from here. What's his trade? He a blacksmith as well?
Dragonborn.esm|019BD8: Next time you feel like having a conversation, talk to someone else.
Dragonborn.esm|019BD9: I'm not talking about that. I have a knack for smelling trouble, and for some reason you reek of it.
Dragonborn.esm|019BDA: She has no idea what she's getting herself into. You may not always be there to take care of her.
Dragonborn.esm|019BDB: Good news, Adril. The next shipment from Windhelm should include the building supplies you requested.
Dragonborn.esm|019BDC: We'll finally be able to begin repairs on The Bulwark. Thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|019BDE: Just a brother. Lives in Riften.
Dragonborn.esm|019BDF: Nothing... I... I was just making conversation.
Dragonborn.esm|019BE0: Understandable considering the differences between us.
Dragonborn.esm|019BE1: What Dreyla chooses to do is her concern, not yours.
Dragonborn.esm|019BE2: Captain Veleth's mentioned how valuable you've become to the Redoran Guard. The Councilor and I appreciate your assistance.
Dragonborn.esm|019BE3: Is something amiss, Cindiri? I can sense your concern.
Dragonborn.esm|019BE5: What in the name of the Black Hands is that supposed to mean?!
Dragonborn.esm|019BE6: I want you to stay away from my daughter!
Dragonborn.esm|019BE7: You and I both know we can't spare anyone right now. I don't care if you still have friends at the East Empire Company or not. They abandoned us long ago, so your contacts are meaningless here. That's it.
Dragonborn.esm|019BE8: Apologies, Adril. In my experience it's usually the quietest before the storm.
Dragonborn.esm|019BEA: Fethis! How's that pretty young daughter of yours doing?
Dragonborn.esm|019BEB: I don't know what your playing at, Glover... but something about you just doesn't add up.
Dragonborn.esm|019BEC: I hesitate to ask for anything else. Your family's done so much for us already.
Dragonborn.esm|019BED: I'm glad to lend a hand.
Dragonborn.esm|019BEE: Not to worry, Adril. I'll recover my losses in due time.
Dragonborn.esm|019BEF: Why hasn't a Redoran Guard been posted at my shop like I've requested?
Dragonborn.esm|019BF0: Something troubling you? I'd think you'd welcome a little peace and quiet.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF1: So where do you hail from, Glover?
Dragonborn.esm|019BF2: Tell you what. I'll make sure we have a permanent foot patrol over there. It's the least I can do for you.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF3: At some point, you're going to have to choose. No matter the outcome, I'll always respect your decision.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF4: If there's anything else I can do, just let me know.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF5: I can't believe Raven Rock Mine is open again. Finally, something worth defending.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF6: If you're looking for work, the mine's been closed for a long time.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF7: Gods. Another mouth to feed? You've picked the wrong town to settle, outlander.
Dragonborn.esm|019BF8: Prospecting for ebony? You're wasting your time. The mine's as dry as a bone.
Dragonborn.esm|019BFC: We've got our eyes on you, scale-skin.
Dragonborn.esm|019BFD: Why you'd leave your homeland behind to venture here is beyond me, but suit yourself, Nord.
Dragonborn.esm|019BFF: If you intend to wander Solstheim, you do so at your own risk.
Dragonborn.esm|019C00: This may be the frontier, but we've still got rules. Break them and you'll wind up in the prisons.
Dragonborn.esm|019C02: Head over to the Retching Netch if the ash has your throat parched.
Dragonborn.esm|019C03: This is House Redoran territory. You'd do well to remember that.
Dragonborn.esm|019C04: You can go where you please, but I'd stay inside the Bulwark if I were you.
Dragonborn.esm|019C05: If you've got any gripes, check in at Morvayn Manor. Second Councilor Arano will sort you out.
Dragonborn.esm|019C06: If you need a ride back to the mainland, Gjalund's the only way off this rock.
Dragonborn.esm|019C07: There's nothing beyond the Bulwark except wild Nords and ash wastes.
Dragonborn.esm|019C08: Not much contact with others around here. Got the Skaal Village to the north, but the Nords there keep to themselves.
Dragonborn.esm|019C09: If you find yourself in the ash wastes, keep your eyes to the ground. Ash spawn like to spring up from nowhere.
Dragonborn.esm|019C0A: Last time I rode Gjalund's ship, I was heaving my guts for a week. Dunmer weren't meant to ride on water.
Dragonborn.esm|019C0B: You think you have it bad? Try walking the Bulwark at night.
Dragonborn.esm|019C0C: If you're looking for supplies, talk to Fethis. He'll set you up with whatever you need.
Dragonborn.esm|019C0D: If you find the time, couldn't hurt to pay your respects at the Temple.
Dragonborn.esm|019C0E: Glover Mallory is a wonder with the hammer and anvil. He mended my armor like an old pro.
Dragonborn.esm|019C10: If you're looking for the East Empire Company, they've been gone for over a hundred years. Try Windhelm.
Dragonborn.esm|019C11: If it wasn't for the Bulwark, we'd be digging our houses out from beneath the ash by now.
Dragonborn.esm|019C12: They serve the best sujamma I've ever tasted over at the Retching Netch. Worth every coin.
Dragonborn.esm|019C13: Headed east? May want to give Tel Mithryn a wide berth. I don't like the looks of that place one bit.
Dragonborn.esm|019C14: Councilor Morvayn is the only reason Raven Rock has survived all these years. You should pay your respects.
Dragonborn.esm|019C16: The entire island isn't covered in ash, you know. They have huge ice fields up north and it's freezing up there.
Dragonborn.esm|019C17: Misery? I'll give you misery. Try getting assigned to scrape netch scum off the front of the Bulwark.
Dragonborn.esm|019C18: Milore over at the Ienth Farm should be still mixing potions if you're seeking that sort of thing. Far west end of town.
Dragonborn.esm|019C19: If you witness suspicious activity, report it to Captain Veleth immediately.
Dragonborn.esm|019C1A: I can't believe I've been stuck here this long.
Dragonborn.esm|019C1B: Squatting isn't tolerated in the abandoned buildings. If you need a place to stay, head to the cornerclub.
Dragonborn.esm|019C1C: If you're looking for passage to Vvardenfell, you're out of luck. No ships are heading that way anymore.
Dragonborn.esm|019C1D: Fethis, I'm quite busy at the moment...
Dragonborn.esm|019C1F: You poor thing. Here you are.
Dragonborn.esm|019C20: Oh fine, fine. Here you go.
Dragonborn.esm|019C22: Don't let me catch you spending it at the Netch.
Dragonborn.esm|019C24: I'm sorry, I don't carry coin. Perhaps next time.
Dragonborn.esm|019C25: Bralsa, I can barely feed myself...
Dragonborn.esm|019C29: Help me out, milord. I haven't eaten in days.
Dragonborn.esm|019C2A: Help me out, milady. I haven't eaten in days.
Dragonborn.esm|019C2B: Food, coin, anything you can spare?
Dragonborn.esm|019C2C: Please, I'm starving. Any help will do...
Dragonborn.esm|019C2D: I know things are tough, but at least you have a roof over your head. Please, help me.
Dragonborn.esm|019C2E: Is a single coin too much to ask for?
Dragonborn.esm|019C2F: A fair question, my friend... a fair question.
Dragonborn.esm|019C30: I understand. I know you're doing everything you can.
Dragonborn.esm|019C31: He's never been the same since my mother died. I don't think he ever wants me to feel the pain he suffers.
Dragonborn.esm|019CB1: The book is housed inside that dome. I'll need to unlock the door for us. Let's get on with it.
Dragonborn.esm|019CB3: The Dwemer certainly knew how to build for the ages. These towers have outlasted their creators by millenia.
Dragonborn.esm|019CB5: Nchardak, an ancient Dwemer city, on the eastern side of Solstheim. It's largely flooded now, but luckily the main library is still above the water. It seems the dwarves were as interested in the Black Books as we are. They seem to have believed this one to be particularly valuable, as it is secured in a mechanism which I have been unable to open. Yet.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4DC: You don't have to apologize to me. Just pull yourself out of this and let's find a home. Either here or up there, it doesn't matter to me.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4DD: I don't want to get comfortable here. We need to be back up there.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4DE: I'm sorry if I was hard on you before.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4DF: Unless you're ready to go fight those things, just leave me.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E0: Once the rest of you are done licking your wounds, of course.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E1: You don't have to live like this now, though.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E2: That's not fair. We can still take it back.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E3: Don't you see, though? None of that matters. I'm going to go down as "the Unworthy" because of that rabble.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E4: Look around you, Kuvar. If any of us were strong, we wouldn't be on this beach.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E5: It's not your fault. There were too many of them. Nobody could have known...
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E6: Well, I hope you'll come back around. Looks like you might be just the thing we need to wake these idiots.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4E7: I wish you'd stop acting like I've grown weak.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4EA: Thirsk was respectable. And we let this happen. No, we'll get respect the way it should come.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4EB: I just wanted to make sure you were all right.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4EC: What's going on here is that we've been kicked out of our home. The mead hall, up there on the hill. We were... some Rieklings have taken it over.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4ED: No, you won't. But I'll love you anyway.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4EE: This is a decent spot. We're right on the water. This could grow into something respectable.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F1: We'll figure something out. And I'll get better, I promise.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F2: I wish you could just relax a little...
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F3: Come to mock our shame, like the others?
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F4: Unless you can help, leave us in peace.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F5: Yes, this camp is small, but it's all we have left.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F6: I don't really have time for this.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F7: Don't judge this place. We can make home wherever we are.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F8: I'd be better off with some mead in my belly, but the air in Solstheim is almost as sweet.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4F9: At night, the wind picks up from the ocean and... well, I'm just lucky to keep warm.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4FA: If you're a talker, go talk to my wife.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4FB: If you're here about the Rieklings just... tread quietly around my sister.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4FC: Bujold feels so guilty about what happened. Try not to provoke her.
Dragonborn.esm|01A4FD: They just came on so quickly...
Dragonborn.esm|01A4FF: Sorry, friend, I can't make anything for you down here. I'll need to get back to my forge.
Dragonborn.esm|01A500: I don't even want to think about what those... creatures are doing with my fires.
Dragonborn.esm|01A501: I've tried hitting against a stump by my tent but... iron needs iron, if you want it shaped right.
Dragonborn.esm|01A502: Need to get back to my anvil.
Dragonborn.esm|01A503: Have you been by Thirsk? Have you seen... is there any of the mead left?
Dragonborn.esm|01A504: I brought down as much as I could when we ran off, but it will only last so long.
Dragonborn.esm|01A505: A new batch of the Ashfire should be ready for bottling soon, unless the rieklings used it for bathwater.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA7A: As a scholar of history, I've always found Solstheim to be most intriguing. I've made many forays into the ancient ruins that cover the island. I've come to know those ruins so well, I could draw you a map from memory. That's why I noticed the new passage the moment I laid eyes on it.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA7B: I heard from Deor how you went in search of Baldor. That was very brave, and in fact, that's just the kind of courage I'm looking for.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA7C: An old tomb, I think. It must have been opened by an earthquake that accompanied one of the Red Mountain's eruptions. I'd love to have a closer look, but those old ruins can be dangerous and I'm no adventurer. I'll pay you to watch my back down there. I'll be heading back there soon. If you're interested, meet me at the ruins and we'll see what we can find. Now I'm off to pack for the expedition!
Dragonborn.esm|01AA91: That fetcher told me to fix his house. If I don't, he'll find a new mycologist who will. Decades of loyal service, and he'd just throw me out like yesterday's breakfast!
Dragonborn.esm|01AA92: What a relief. Master Neloth surely would have punished me had he found out. I can't pay you, but I could teach you the Ash Guardian spell... although maybe that doesn't sound like a good idea to you after this. I do have a staff I could give you instead, if you think the Ash Guardian spell is too risky.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA94: Damn that man! He is being totally unreasonable.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA95: I don't care if he's a Master Wizard. He can't treat me like this!
Dragonborn.esm|01AA97: Good. Now maybe Neloth will get off my back about it. I suppose you think you should get paid for helping an old woman. Here. Take these. I'll sell you more if you come back later.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA98: Of course I did. I tried to talk to you about it, but you just walked away. Well, at least it's handled now.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA99: Thank you! I was hoping you would. Hurry. I'll keep Master Neloth distracted.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA9A: You've got to help me! I conjured an Ash Guardian and now it's running amok.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA9B: Yes. Yes, I do. But they are for her to use to repair my withered tower. I'm too busy to deal with this right now. Go take them to her.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA9C: Truly? Well done. I'll just keep two of them for myself. No need for Neloth to know. Take that last taproot and plant it into the wall of the withered house.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA9D: It's special to the Spriggans. They seem to like to gather there. Something about the waters attracts them. There are bound to be some there, so be careful.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA9E: Fine! Let it be on your own head then.
Dragonborn.esm|01AA9F: No need to involve Master Neloth in this. It's just between the two of us.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA0: My apprentice has overstepped his bounds this time.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA1: Where do you think? You have to cut it out of a Spriggan. Although sometimes you can buy them from alchemists.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA2: What? That fool. His incompetence made this mess. Let's see how he decides to clean it up.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA3: Three taproots soaked in the headwaters of the Harstrad river. I'm an old woman. I can't possibly make that trip. It's way too dangerous. If you get some taproots, I'll pay you to dip them in the headwaters and bring them to me.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA4: Oh. It turns out that the spell requires a heart stone to cast properly. I didn't have one with me when I cast it. If you cast the spell without a heart stone, it attacks anything in sight.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA7: Is it done? Is the Ash Guardian gone?
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA8: Have you dealt with the Ash Guardian?
Dragonborn.esm|01AAA9: Someone should do something about that damned Ash Guardian.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAAA: I'm not leaving this kitchen until that thing is gone!
Dragonborn.esm|01AAAB: I need to tell Master Neloth about that Ash Guardian, but I don't dare leave my home.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAEE: And here you are, just as I asked. How very kind of you. I presume you've already seen some of what I've accomplished. There is so, so much more to be done. I'm glad you're here.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAEF: Miraak... Miraak... It sounds familiar, and yet I can't quite place... Oh. Wait, I recall. But that makes very little sense. Miraak's been dead for thousands of years.
Dragonborn.esm|01AAF0: You there... You don't seem to be in quite the same state as the others here. Very interesting. May I ask what it is you're doing here?
Dragonborn.esm|01AAF1: If only it could be so. In order to subdue this chaotic world, to set things right, I must return to this realm in full.
Dragonborn.esm|01AE1B: But still, this temple stands. The Tree Stone remains tainted. How can we be sure the Skaal will remain free? I can not return to the village until I have found the source of this evil. Will you come with me, further into this accursed temple?
Dragonborn.esm|01AE1D: I do not understand. Why would he send you, an outsider, to find me when he knows why I have come here? Our people are lost to the madness that infects this island. Perhaps Storn has been lost as well...
Dragonborn.esm|01B62E: Seems like nobody is willing to put their life on the line for research any more. I'm sure Talvas won't mind being my test subject again.
Dragonborn.esm|01B62F: Now stand still... it's my first time casting this spell, and I don't need any additional distractions.
Dragonborn.esm|01B630: Say, how would you like to help advance the art of magic?
Dragonborn.esm|01B631: Oh, nothing too dangerous. It should provide an additional spectral tether from your own lifespark to the aedric realm. You would experience it as a sort of... increased vigor. You would fight longer, feel stronger, that sort of thing. At least I think it will. Probably. Almost certainly. Of course, you never can tell.
Dragonborn.esm|01B632: Excellent! I'll just make a copy for myself. It's far too dangerous to carry the real book around. Of course, I'm sure you can handle yourself. Of course you can. Of course. Now, take this for your efforts, and we'll call the matter closed.
Dragonborn.esm|01B633: Well? What are you waiting for? Go get me that book!
Dragonborn.esm|01B634: I've pried open another of Hermaeus Mora's secrets.
Dragonborn.esm|01B635: Yes. Hermaeus Mora is devious, but then so am I. Now, it's only a rumor of a whisper of a conjecture. But I think this is the place. Now, hurry. That book might contain the final secrets of Xarxes, for all we know.
Dragonborn.esm|01B90A: Some of the Redoran Guard have taken to calling them "ash spawn." Me? I don't care what they're called... all I know is they're a danger to Raven Rock and they need to be stopped.
Dragonborn.esm|01B90B: Thanks... I wasn't sure I'd make it off this farm alive. I wish I could have said the same for my man here.
Dragonborn.esm|01BB9A: Or that the Dwemer's servitors continued to try to preserve the city after their creators' disappearance.
Dragonborn.esm|01BB9B: It won't be easy. The whole lower part of the city is flooded, which has shut down the boilers which supply steam to the reading room. If we can restore the steam supply, I'm certain I can open the book's protective case.
Dragonborn.esm|01BB9C: I sealed the door when I left to keep out ignorant meddlers. Let me unlock it.
Dragonborn.esm|01BB9D: Interesting. That would suggest that the city must have originally sunk during the first cataclysm of Red Mountain.
Dragonborn.esm|01BB9E: The Dwemer of Nchardak appear to have been fond of these control pedestals. Luckily I found a cube to operate it inside on my last visit.
Dragonborn.esm|01BB9F: It looks like most of the cubes were moved to the lower levels, perhaps to try to control the flooding before the city was abandoned.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA0: Yes... here we are. This device shows the location of four more cubes in this section of the city.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA1: Shall we get on with it?
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA2: Let's be on our way then.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA3: I'm not taking this trip for pleasure, you know. Let's get to Nchardak.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA4: But the pumps only operate when a cube is in the pedestal. And unfortunately, I only have one cube.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA5: As you can see, most of the lower levels of the city are flooded. But it isn't hopeless - the old Dwemer pumps still seem to work. Watch.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA6: So, if we can find four more cubes, we can turn these boilers back on and restore steam power to the room upstairs. Then I should be able to open the book's protective case.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA7: They're shut down, but they still respond to the control cubes.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA8: Here we are. Nchardak. The book we're after is right inside that dome.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBA9: Hmm. This place was deserted when I was here last.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBAB: I thought you were in a hurry to get to Nchardak.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBAC: The book is housed in the main dome. I sealed the door the last time I was here, as a precaution against thieves and other meddlers.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBAE: These four boilers provide steam to the room upstairs.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBD8: If they grow hungry enough, they will turn upon us.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBD9: I don't need so many, but thank you, Tharstan. You are as sweet as honey from the comb.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBDA: How much longer will you stay in the village, Tharstan?
Dragonborn.esm|01BBDB: The beasts are driven north in search of food. The ashfall in the south kills the plants upon which the small creatures feed. As the small creatures move north, the large ones that prey upon them must follow.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBDC: My dear chieftain, I will bring you back a whole bouquet if you wish.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBDD: Not if I can help it!
Dragonborn.esm|01BBDE: I have seen more wolves and bears among the trees since the last dark moon. Something is driving them north into our lands.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBDF: When you visit the ruins again, will you bring back some blue mountain flowers?
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE0: I thank you, Morwen. It means very much to me to hear you say so.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE1: Yes, I remember that you ate one of them all by yourself. Maybe this time, you will leave some for the rest of us!
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE2: I'm not setting foot out that door unless it's absolutely necessary. If I do that, I'll be even colder than I am now!
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE4: I understand. Know that you are welcome here for as long as you wish to be.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE5: Then take an axe and find a dead tree to cut. We keep only what we need in the village, you know that.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE6: I have a favor to ask of you, Tharstan.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE7: It's hard to explain, but as much as I have come to think of Skaal Village as a second home, I do still miss Skyrim.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBE8: Just make sure that when the berries ripen, you make more pies than you did last time.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBEA: The snowberries are not yet sweet enough. I tasted one just this morning.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBEB: And you want to put more logs on the fire?
Dragonborn.esm|01BBEC: Then you should stay. What does the world beyond our lands have to offer that we do not?
Dragonborn.esm|01BBED: When will you make another snowberry pie, Yrsa? It's been a long time.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBEE: Is it just me, or is it colder than usual today? By the Nine, I feel as if my bones have turned to ice.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBEF: To tell the truth, Morwen, I thought it would only be a year or two. However, I have come to be very fond of the village and of all of you.
Dragonborn.esm|01BBF0: That is the way of things. We must be prepared. At least the hunting will be easier with the beasts so close.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCEC: Good, good. As soon as I'm done here, I'll try it out.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCED: I've prepared the staff maker like you asked.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCEE: Idiot! Those are hard to find. Next time don't fill it at all if you can't do it properly.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF0: Yes, master. Although the grand soul gem only has a common soul in it.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF1: Don't think your false flattery will work on me, Talvas. You're still going to give me that skin sample.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF2: Did you fill those soul gems like I asked you?
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF3: Thank you, master. It's all because of your brilliant teaching.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF4: Wet, you say? I suppose... yes, I can see how that would have inverted the tether. Unfortunate. Couldn't you simply avoid water? No, no, that won't do. Can't have you forgo bathing... The ashlands can leave one a bit pungent and it's, eh, obvious you've been through them. Here, I'll just remove it. Here's a bit of coin. Should help mitigate your inconvenience.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF5: He was the original shadowmage. His advances in magic are legendary. Less well known is that he was also a pioneer in the field of enchanting staffs. Every staff of his that I have uncovered has led me to greater understanding. Today we would consider his techniques rudimentary, but the genius is still evident.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF6: Your enchanting has improved despite your best efforts.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF7: Perhaps later. For now I have a slightly more personal request. You see, I'm a collector. Of staffs. Magical staffs. In particular, those made by Azra Nightwielder. Through some divinations, I believe I've located one. If you retrieve it, I'll give you a staff of my own making. Nightwielder's creations don't really hold up in comparison to what can be made today, but they have a sentimental value for me. You understand.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF8: Yes, master. Thank you, master. I'll fix it right away.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCF9: We'll see. If the power levels aren't too high you may have it.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCFA: That was before you ruined that staff. Fix the staff and I'll teach you the spell.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCFB: Where is that ash sucking steward of mine? Varona!
Dragonborn.esm|01BCFC: Where is that lazy steward of mine? Varona! No, wait. She's dead. Drovas!
Dragonborn.esm|01BCFD: Things have been much calmer since you disposed of Ildari.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCFE: Working on those standing stones feels like a bad dream. I'm glad you woke us from it.
Dragonborn.esm|01BCFF: Who brought me here? They better not have touched my mushrooms. Neloth? Neloth! Is this your doing?
Dragonborn.esm|01BD00: That ash eating wizard is always on me about his tower. It's not my damn fault the thing withered overnight.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD01: May I keep this one, master?
Dragonborn.esm|01BD02: Master, you promised to teach me a new conjuration spell.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD34: I wonder sometimes if you are truly happy here, Nikulas.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD36: I'm in your debt, Wulf. That bow you gave me is twice as strong as the last one I had.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD37: I have given my answer, little cub. I know you are brave and have the heart of a great she-wolf, but all things have a time and place.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD38: Very well, mother. I will stay for now.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD39: I just want to see. I promise not to get in the way of the hunters, and I'm not afraid of the beasts in the forest.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD3A: My heart is still wounded from the loss of your father. I cannot lose my only son so soon after. When time has worn away my grief, then you can go with my blessing.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD3B: I've told you before, little cub, you're not old enough yet.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD3C: I don't fear hardship and danger. I am Skaal. The only thing that I fear is not to follow my heart, and to die an old man filled with regret.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD3D: Papa, when can I go on my first hunt?
Dragonborn.esm|01BD3E: I know that Tharstan has filled your head with stories, but those stories don't tell of the dangers and hardships of the world.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD3F: Then I will keep practicing my aim every day.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD40: I know why you're asking, mother. I am happy here, but I want to know more of the world outside our lands.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD41: It's not just the bow, Deor. Your arm has grown strong and your eye sharp. You've spent many days practicing your aim. The All-Maker favors those who work hard to hone their skills, as you have done.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD6D: By Malacath's toenails, where did that come from?
Dragonborn.esm|01BD6E: I had to clean out the riff-raff last time I was here, too. Where do they come from?
Dragonborn.esm|01BD6F: Besides, it could be very dangerous. These books are known to drive many people insane.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD70: At last. I hope it was worth it. Please... be my guest. You deserve the first look.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD71: Yes, it worked! The steam is flowing. Now it should be as simple as...
Dragonborn.esm|01BD73: What's the worst that could happen? Well, you could have your mind sucked dry by Hermaeus Mora... but that's supposed to be very rare.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD74: Please, go right ahead. I have plenty to occupy me for the moment.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD75: Aren't you going to read it? I thought that was the whole reason you dragged me out here.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD76: You should read it. I'd really like to see what happens.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD77: Don't worry, I'll take careful notes of what happens to you.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD78: I'm going to head back upstairs and see if the reading room has steam. If so, it should be a simple matter to release the book.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD79: Maybe now we can finally begin what we came here for.
Dragonborn.esm|01BD7A: That seems to have done it. Good. It took longer than I'd hoped, but at least it's finally done.
Dragonborn.esm|01BE13: This book... It seems wrong, somehow. Here, yet... not. It may be what we seek.
Dragonborn.esm|01BE14: There are dark magics at work here. Ready yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|01BE30: Well that gives me a lot to think about. I need to get back to Tel Mithryn. I have some ideas about how to locate more of these Black Books...
Dragonborn.esm|01BFC5: I don't. All I know is that the ash spawn are coming from this direction, so this is as good a place to start as anywhere else. Good thing the farm's been abandoned for a long time. I'd hate to see what they would do to an unarmed farmer and his family.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFC6: Hmm. I think I could talk the councilor into paying you, and I could use an extra pair of hands.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFC7: This is strange. The note says it's from General Falx Carius, but that's impossible.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFC8: Exactly. Something has to be directing them against us and we need to find out who, or what it is.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFC9: We were searching the farm when the ash spawn ambushed us. Have a look around and see if you can turn up anything useful.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFCA: Be careful, this General Carius sounds out of his mind.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFCB: Come find me if you decide to reconsider my request.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFCC: We've had two other assaults on the Bulwark at different spots on the wall. I know it sounds crazy, but it's almost like they're becoming more organized and probing it for weaknesses.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFCD: Besides, I don't feel comfortable leaving Raven Rock behind, and I'd hate to lose any more Redoran Guard.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFCE: No one knows really. Superstitious folk say they're the risen forms of those that died long ago, but I'm not so certain. They started attacking the Bulwark a few weeks ago. My men were able to kill them, but they keep coming back.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFCF: Good, I can use all the help I can get.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFD1: I was going to search for clues that might lead me to wherever they're coming from. I know it isn't the best place to start, but we know they've been coming from this direction.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFD3: There has to be a clue around here somewhere.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFD4: I hope you've reconsidered lending me a hand. Could really use it right now.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFD5: Finally. We were searching the farm when the ash spawn ambushed us. Have a look around and see if you can turn up anything useful.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFD6: If General Carius is still alive, there must be something keeping him that way. I need you to head out to Fort Frostmoth and check it out. I'm going to head back to Raven Rock and prepare the men for further assaults.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFD8: Well, Carius was the Imperial garrison commander at Fort Frostmoth, but he died over 200 years ago when the Red Mountain leveled the place. There's no way he could still be alive.
Dragonborn.esm|01BFDA: What news do you bring from Fort Frostmoth?
Dragonborn.esm|01BFDB: It's easy to criticize when you're just a visitor here. Since you're brimming with self-confidence, maybe you'd care to take over for me?
Dragonborn.esm|01C04B: Oh have you? Well, let me just extract those memories and I'll see if you found anything useful. I promise that any unrelated memories I run across will be kept in the strictest of confidences. Here, you deserve a bit of reward for your efforts... that was a lengthy trip for you, I'm sure!
Dragonborn.esm|01C04C: This is a Telvanni tower. It's my citadel, grown from the spores of the giant fungus trees found in Morrowind. Back in the homeland, there are other Telvanni wizards that have them as well.
Dragonborn.esm|01C04D: When Red Mountain erupted almost two hundred years ago, it flung ash and rocks for hundreds of miles. Among the debris were heart stones. Even two centuries later, they still smolder with the heat of the mountain. These are the molten rocks that lay next to the Heart of Lorkhan for millennia. Some vestige of its power must have seeped into them. I do know that heart stones sometimes raise ash spawn from the bits of bone buried in the nearby ash.
Dragonborn.esm|01C04E: Everything that can go wrong is going wrong. Ash spawn attacks, my house, and even my steward is assassinated.
Dragonborn.esm|01C04F: It's as if Tel Mithryn is cursed. Spoiled food, ash spawn attacks, my servants constantly getting sick. What's next?
Dragonborn.esm|01C050: I wonder if a dragon could be captured alive? It would make a fascinating test subject.
Dragonborn.esm|01C051: Excellent. I'll take it. Here. Have some septims.
Dragonborn.esm|01C052: Well, I'm hoping to do something similar... if I could connect a living being to the forces of the Red Mountain well... that would be formidable. I've done some experiments in this area, but, well, my test subjects are far too fragile. I'm hoping to learn some of the Briarheart techniques, their methods. I would venture out myself, but long voyages and dangerous risks are precisely what people like you are for.
Dragonborn.esm|01C053: I'm running low on heart stones. If you have any with you, I'll buy one. Otherwise, I can tell you where you might find one.
Dragonborn.esm|01C054: The Forsworn live in the hills on the western edge of Skyrim. From there, you're on your own. I'm given to understand that they are not difficult to find, if you gather my inference.
Dragonborn.esm|01C055: You didn't have to speak a word. I could sense it the moment you came in. You've proven your worth, and I'm as good as my word. Take this staff from my personal collection. I think you'll find it useful.
Dragonborn.esm|01C056: They are fierce warriors of the Forsworn tribes. They actually remove their own hearts, and replace them with large briar seeds. It puts their lifesparks in direct connection to the natural forces. I need you to examine one of them. Just investigate as much as you can. I'm going to put this memory trace spell on you, so you won't have to worry about taking notes. I doubt you could know what was important anyway.
Dragonborn.esm|01C057: The connecting soul thread is knotted three times, once for the heart, once for the spirit, and once for the body. The knot gives the connection a strength that may be the key that I've been looking for.
Dragonborn.esm|01C058: The man makes a dreadful cup of canis root tea. However, he seems to run the place well enough. Oh. Were you fishing for a compliment? A sign of gratitude? Don't waste your time. Those things are for the weak and foolish.
Dragonborn.esm|01C059: I've been having difficulties with my heart stone experiments. Have you heard of the Briarhearts?
Dragonborn.esm|01C05A: Oh yes. You rather rudely walked away. As I was saying then, have you heard of the Briarhearts?
Dragonborn.esm|01C077: Remarkable, isn't it? This tomb has been hidden for... well, who knows how long. Many centuries, I would think. There are some sealed doors up ahead. Perhaps you can figure out how to get them open. Lead the way, then! I'll follow along behind.
Dragonborn.esm|01C094: All that he knows he learned from me.
Dragonborn.esm|01C095: Yes, I know. And then Miraak's power will be yours as well.
Dragonborn.esm|01C096: I am Hermaeus Mora, the Gardener of Men, knower of the unknown, master of fates. You stand in my realm, mortal.
Dragonborn.esm|01C097: My servant Miraak would have found a way to bring me what I want. So will you, if you wish to surpass him.
Dragonborn.esm|01C098: Miraak served me well, and was rewarded. I can grant you the same power as he wields, but all knowledge has its price.
Dragonborn.esm|01C099: I know you will. And then I will give you the knowledge that you seek.
Dragonborn.esm|01C09A: Well done, my champion. Your journey towards enlightenment has finally led you here, to my realm, as I knew it would.
Dragonborn.esm|01C09B: All seekers of knowledge come to me, sooner or later.
Dragonborn.esm|01C09F: Your progress since obtaining my Oghma Infinium has been rapid. And now, I find you here, following in my servant Miraak's footsteps. You also are Dragonborn. You also seek to learn how to bend the world to your will.
Dragonborn.esm|01C0A1: Oh good. Be sure to say hello to Hermaeus Mora for me, if you see him.
Dragonborn.esm|01C0A2: You please me, champion. Your loyalty will be richly rewarded.
Dragonborn.esm|01C0A3: You have entered my realm. You have sought out the forbidden knowledge that only one other has obtained. You are Dragonborn, like Miraak before you. A seeker of knowledge and power.
Dragonborn.esm|01C0A4: No. Look around you. You have done nothing here on your own. You could spend a hundred lifetimes searching my library, and you will never find what you seek.
Dragonborn.esm|01C0A5: No. Not yet. I have watched your progress through my realm with growing interest. You are following in the footsteps of my servant Miraak. Like him, you are Dragonborn, and seek the power that is your birthright.
Dragonborn.esm|01C0A6: You will serve me, willing or not. All who seek after the secrets of the world are my servants.
Dragonborn.esm|01CAED: Indeed. I knew that the Dragon Priests were thought to have been powerful men of great will, but I had no idea. Well, I suppose I've seen everything I came for, and you kept up your end of the bargain. I promised payment, and here it is. Now I think I'll spend some more time studying this word wall. What a book this will make!
Dragonborn.esm|01CAEE: How amazing! A real Dragon Priest! In all my years as a historian, I never thought I'd see anything like that. I suppose that was Vahlok. He must have had this entire place built for him so that he could maintain his vigil, even beyond death.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB5A: This is a dangerous thing, then. We should return to my village, and show this to my father. Perhaps Storn can make sense of what is going on.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB5B: Where? Where is he? Can we reach him? Can we kill him?
Dragonborn.esm|01CB5C: What happened to you? You read the book and then... It seemed as though you were not really here. I could see you, but also see through you!
Dragonborn.esm|01CB5D: Then you and I both have reason to see what lies beneath us. Let us go. There is nothing more I can do here. The Tree Stone and my friends are beyond my help for now. We need to find a way into the temple below.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB5E: Send him back where he came from.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB5F: Send her back where she came from.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB60: And it is only a matter of time before Solstheim is also mine. I already control the minds of its people.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB61: I am unsure. Something has taken control of most of the people of Solstheim. It makes them forget themselves, and work on these horrible creations that corrupt the Stones, the very land itself. My father Storn, our shaman, says Miraak has returned to Solstheim, but that is impossible.
Dragonborn.esm|01CB62: Who are you to dare set foot here?
Dragonborn.esm|01CB63: I am Frea of the Skaal. I am here to either save my people, or avenge them.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDC8: Well done. Here are some septims for your troubles. If you come back in a day or two I should have the spell worked out. You can buy it from Talvas.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDC9: Master Neloth! What have you done to him? What are those things growing out of his eyes?
Dragonborn.esm|01CDCA: Master Neloth! What have you done to her? What are those things growing out of her eyes?
Dragonborn.esm|01CDCB: I'm still waiting for that ash sample from an ash spawn.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDCC: Well that's unexpected. I presume you are blind, given the state of your eyes.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDCD: Hmm. I do have a new spell I'm working on. I hope to be able to conjure ash spawn. If I could get a sample of ash from an ash spawn, that would be quite useful. I've made this special tool that will extract... well, you don't need to know the details. Just go harvest a sample. Of course, the creature will have to be dead first, but I trust that's something you can... bring about.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDCE: That's unfortunate. I'll have to use Talvas again, and he's a bit of a screamer. Although... maybe this could solve two problems...
Dragonborn.esm|01CDCF: Yes. That was a lifetime ago. I was just a young girl when Red Mountain erupted. Master Neloth was already old. My mother put me on a boat to Skyrim. The last time I ever saw her, she was standing on the dock as my ship sailed away.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD0: Excellent. If this works, I'll prepare a version that you can cast yourself, if you ever wish to inflict... er, study silence.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD2: Well, I believe I've discovered a new form of silence. Some philosophers postulate seven orders of silence, but I believe there's an eighth. If you allow me, I'd like to envelop you in the eighth form, to study the mental effects. What do you say?
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD3: The Telvanni are the master wizards of Morrowind. The greatest of us grow magical towers like this one from the mighty fungus trees. Together we form House Telvanni, one of the great houses of Morrowind. The great houses rule what is left of our once proud nation.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD4: I suppose I do. It's too bad it wore off so quickly though. Unfortunately you don't appear to be suffering from any side effects. Anyway, take this for your time.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD5: It's nothing that can't be fixed. The spell that is. I think I can fix the spell. It should only take a couple more years. But first, I need some data. Try wiggling them. No, don't shake your fist. Wiggle the tentacle things where your eyes used to be.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD6: Give me a moment. I still have flashback from when Master Neloth changed you.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD7: Thank you for keeping that matter of the Ash Guardian just between us. I shudder to think what would happen if Master Neloth had found out.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD8: Master Neloth was furious when the tower withered. It just seemed to happen overnight.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDD9: You'd think Neloth was still a clan leader in House Telvanni the way he carries on.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDDA: After what you did for me, you are always welcome here. Just try to stay out of Master Neloth's way.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDDB: I just can't seem to get the hang of making a decent cup of canis root tea.
Dragonborn.esm|01CDDC: I heard you fought a dragon. That must have been terrifying.
Dragonborn.esm|01D3AA: I have my doubts. But the city was a marvel of Dwemer engineering. Now... reduced to this.
Dragonborn.esm|01D3AB: In the old stories, when the Nords came to conquer it, it's said the Dwemer submerged the entire city beneath the sea until the invaders gave up.
Dragonborn.esm|01D3AC: Nchardak. The 'City of a Hundred Towers'. In its day, it was the largest of the great Dwemer Archives, and perhaps the most advanced.
Dragonborn.esm|01D8CC: So? Do you have it? Did you get my book?
Dragonborn.esm|01D8CD: Don't just stand there. Go get my book!
Dragonborn.esm|01D8CE: Perhaps this particular source was worth more than I thought he was. Pity. In any case, this is truly amazing. I think I can adapt this into a sort of whirlwind vortex energy. Yes, yes. I'll write it out, and you can pick up a copy from Talvas in a few days. In the mean time, take this for helping me.
Dragonborn.esm|01D8CF: Can you? Of course you can. What took you so long to ask? I've been trying to adapt Redguard magic to the ash. I think the key concept I'm missing is recorded in Afa-Saryat's book, Wind and Sand. I have some associates and correspondents who find things for me. One of them has a credible rumor on where this book might be. And I'd like you to find the truth of the matter.
Dragonborn.esm|01D928: I am in no mood to talk, outsider. One of the Skaal has gone missing.
Dragonborn.esm|01D929: South and west. If your road takes you that way, perhaps you could search for any sign of Baldor. The Skaal would be grateful.
Dragonborn.esm|01D92A: Hm, I do not remember Baldor acting strangely. Although, now that you ask, I did see something in the woods on the day he disappeared. I saw two elves in the far distance, dragging something behind them. Hunters often visit our lands, so I thought little of it.
Dragonborn.esm|01D92B: It is Baldor Iron-Shaper. As our only smith, Baldor is very important to the village.
Dragonborn.esm|01D95E: I don't want to have my spirit outstripped by some wanderer. So let's get up there and kill us some Rieklings!
Dragonborn.esm|01D95F: With tenacity and numbers. Didn't help that we'd grown a little too comfortable up there. Too much mead. Too many stories. Too few battles.
Dragonborn.esm|01D960: You seek my blessing for the leadership of Thirsk Hall?
Dragonborn.esm|01D961: I was afraid you might say that. Well, looks as though I'll have to prove my mettle against you now.
Dragonborn.esm|01D962: So I just need to take hold of it...
Dragonborn.esm|01D963: Good. See that you do. Now, if you'll excuse me, I have a hall to lead.
Dragonborn.esm|01D964: No. Nor is there any among you fit to serve. For a band in the wilderness, it is better to have no leader than a poor one.
Dragonborn.esm|01D965: Go forth and seek your fortunes. Inhabit the hall as you may. In time, a true leader worthy of the Thirsk name may arise.
Dragonborn.esm|01D966: But I see that new leader's coming is yet far off the distant horizon.
Dragonborn.esm|01D968: I'll need a witness and, well, you see the kinds of horker-brains I deal with around here. What do you say?
Dragonborn.esm|01D969: All I had to do was mention our little infestation, and he volunteered in a second!
Dragonborn.esm|01D96A: All I had to do was mention our little infestation, and she volunteered in a second!
Dragonborn.esm|01D96B: Look what's happened to us so far under his watchful eye. He gave his blessing to all the leaders who brought us to this point. Anyway, he's dead and we're alive. It's time to begin a new tradition for Thirsk. And I'm going to do that. All I need is for you to back me up. You don't even need to lie... just don't tell anyone else what you heard here.
Dragonborn.esm|01D96C: Then... the leadership is not mine?
Dragonborn.esm|01D96D: All right, then. I'll be waiting there whenever your honor wakes you.
Dragonborn.esm|01D96E: But look here. This outsider has more fire than any of you.
Dragonborn.esm|01D96F: You allowed your fellow warriors to grow weak while the dangers around you mounted.
Dragonborn.esm|01D970: Well let's not waste time talking about it, then!
Dragonborn.esm|01D971: To Hrothmund's Barrow! I need to get his blessing again before we take up residence here.
Dragonborn.esm|01D972: I know you're all starting to settle in here, and keep up the fat lives you got used to up in the hall.
Dragonborn.esm|01D973: I need to return to Thirsk. No matter what Hrothmund says, we need a leader. And I'm still the best person for that.
Dragonborn.esm|01D975: Ah! That was almost... exhilarating. How are you feeling?
Dragonborn.esm|01D976: My second! If we're going to take up residence here again, I need to get the blessing of Hrothmund again.
Dragonborn.esm|01D977: Hey, you lazy milk-drinkers, get over here!
Dragonborn.esm|01D978: The only thing I can do.
Dragonborn.esm|01D979: And well it is that this has happened. But I have always watched, and know that it was your softness that lead to your own exile.
Dragonborn.esm|01D97A: Well, look who's back. You ready to finally help us take out those Rieklings?
Dragonborn.esm|01D97B: Oh, come on now. I'm going to need you to perk up if you're going to come with me as my second.
Dragonborn.esm|01D97C: Are you... offering to help? Some new blood should be enough to rouse these layabouts to actually fight again.
Dragonborn.esm|01D97D: Ashamed. But I guess that was the point.
Dragonborn.esm|01D97E: You need to either help us or stop coming around here.
Dragonborn.esm|01D97F: Good to hear! In fact... that's just the spirit I'll need from my second. Do you want to come with me?
Dragonborn.esm|01D981: I do. It is I, Bujold. You blessed me in the past, and now I've rid the hall of Rieklings and returned it to its rightful owners.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9A4: Do not fear for me, my daughter. This is the destiny that the All-Maker has laid out for me.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9A6: You ducked out of the middle of a conversation with old Mora himself? Mighty impertinent of you! And, since you need secrets that only Hermaeus Mora knows, it seems like a rather foolish thing to do. But I'm sure you know best.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9A7: Hmph. What secrets could they have worth keeping from old Mora? Sounds like a bargain to me. Hermaeus Mora learns some fascinating new ways to skin a horker, and you become the second most powerful Dragonborn that ever lived.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9A9: No wonder the Dwemer were so interested in that book. It was indeed one that Miraak used to advance his power as Dragonborn. But I assume there's some bad news. It would be unlike Hermaeus Mora to allow anyone to gain such knowledge without exacting a price.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9AA: You will be either a worthy opponent or his successor, as the tides of fate decree.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9AB: That's a fairly common experience, at least for those who remain coherent enough to report what they've seen. You were in Apocrypha. Or at least your consciousness was - your body remained here of course. You should read the book again until you reach what appears to be the "end" - then you will have fully understood the knowledge it contains.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9AC: In return, I keep my promise, as befits a Prince of Oblivion: I give you the Word of Power that you need to challenge Miraak.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9AD: You're still acting surprisingly sane, too. What did he have to say? He must have wanted something from you.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9AE: Dragonborn, you have delivered me the gift I requested.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9AF: At last, the Skaal yield up their secrets to me.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9B0: What happened? What did you see? Different people have very different experiences when reading these books.
Dragonborn.esm|01D9B1: Did you actually learn what you needed to know by reading that Black Book?
Dragonborn.esm|01DEA0: He grows ever near to us.
Dragonborn.esm|01DEA2: Now through him do we see.
Dragonborn.esm|01DEA4: Now through them does he speak.
Dragonborn.esm|01DEA5: And when the world shall listen.
Dragonborn.esm|01DEA6: And when the world shall see.
Dragonborn.esm|01DEA8: That world will cease to be.
Dragonborn.esm|01DFB3: The Tree Stone is still corrupted... the land is still out of balance. But with the other five restored... it may be enough. It will have to be.
Dragonborn.esm|01DFB5: That is my hope as well. I am trusting that you will make this sacrifice worthwhile.
Dragonborn.esm|01DFB6: Give me the book. I will read it, and speak to old Herma-Mora myself. I will make sure he lives up to his part of the bargain.
Dragonborn.esm|01DFB7: Ancient lore, handed down from shaman to shaman since the All-Maker first gave Solstheim to the Skaal. How to talk to the wind, how to listen to the earth - these are our secrets. Nothing of power or mastery.
Dragonborn.esm|01DFB8: Yes. The Skaal also tell of the day when we must finally give up our secrets. When Herma-Mora finally wins. As shaman, it is my duty to guard these secrets, but also to decide when it is necessary to give them up. I believe that time is now. If I am wrong, may my ancestors forgive me.
Dragonborn.esm|01E15F: They have a ship. They took me there and showed me the map. You will find it on the northern coast of the island. Please, do not let Ancarion make his weapons. Kill him or let him live, but take the map from him. It belongs with the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|01E160: Enchanted ice as hard as iron and cold as death. Stalhrim can be forged into deadly weapons, but the art is known only to smiths of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|01E161: No, no, my wounds are not serious. I don't think the elves were trying to harm me. Perhaps they intended to frighten me. At worst, I have a few bruises to show for my ordeal.
Dragonborn.esm|01E162: Outsider! I remember you. You are the one who freed the Skaal from the dark spell. Thank the All-Maker that you have come. These accursed elves have taken me from my home.
Dragonborn.esm|01E163: It makes my heart heavy that you don't believe me, Fanari. Baldor could be in danger, and you will do nothing. If you won't help me, then I'll ask the All-Maker to send someone else who can.
Dragonborn.esm|01E164: Something isn't right, Fanari. I feel it in my bones and I smell it in the air. Baldor wouldn't leave without telling someone.
Dragonborn.esm|01E165: Don't worry yourself over nothing, Deor. Do you want a head full of gray hairs before you've seen forty winters?
Dragonborn.esm|01E166: Deor, you must be calm. I'm sure there's a good reason Baldor left us.
Dragonborn.esm|01E175: We brought ourselves out here, along with our shame.
Dragonborn.esm|01E8C2: Until our next meeting, Skaal-friend, may your hunts always bring you game and your crops grow tall and bountiful.
Dragonborn.esm|01E8C3: I know you faced great danger to bring this map to me. There are no words to tell how glad my heart is. Thank you, brave one. I name you friend of the Skaal, and I will trust you with the knowledge of forging Stalhrim. If you bring Stalhrim here to my forge, you can use my tools to make what you will from it.
Dragonborn.esm|01E8C4: It is good to see you again, my friend. Did you find the elves at their ship?
Dragonborn.esm|01E8C5: I will return to the village. When you have the map, please bring it to me there. And thank you. May the All-Maker bless your days.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA1: Ah, sweet Oblivion. Soon I shall return.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA2: I do so love dealing with mortals.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA4: Hmm. Excellent question. Once word gets out, there will be a lot of people to choose from. Someone with a brilliant mind and robust physique that is blindly obedient would be ideal. Oh, and they need to know how to brew tea. Ulves always waters it down.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA5: Well, somebody needs to get me a new steward. It might as well be you. But if you don't want to help a master wizard of House Telvanni, possibly earning his favor, you're a fool.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA6: Finally. Someone who recognizes my importance around here.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA7: Ah, a fresh mortal who seeks to bargain with me.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEA9: So soon? You must be hungry for a deal.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEAA: You have many things I wish to buy.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEAB: Obviously not, or you would have a cup of tea in your hands. I supposed you want to be asked nicely. Very well. Please, oh hero of Skyrim. Please find Varona. I shall be ever so grateful.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEAC: I'm sure we can strike a bargain.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEAD: Haggling with you can be so...entertaining.
Dragonborn.esm|01EEB0: What? Make my own tea? I am a master wizard of the House Telvanni. Other people make tea for me.
Dragonborn.esm|01F12C: It's a bit dried up, but it will do. Here.
Dragonborn.esm|01F12D: A bit ripe, isn't it? Here. Take this.
Dragonborn.esm|01F12E: Nasty bitches, aren't they? Here's your fee.
Dragonborn.esm|01F12F: Not bad for an amateur? Take this.
Dragonborn.esm|01F130: You could have cleaned it. It's still bloody. Here's your money.
Dragonborn.esm|01F131: Hmm. Better than average quality. I'll take it.
Dragonborn.esm|01F132: Well, I could use a daedra heart. Can't grow that in my garden.
Dragonborn.esm|01F133: Hmm. I'm running out of giant toes. I'll pay extra for one.
Dragonborn.esm|01F134: As a matter of fact, I need a hagraven claw. I'll pay extra if you find one.
Dragonborn.esm|01F135: How about some vampire dust? Bring me some of that and I'll pay handsomely.
Dragonborn.esm|01F136: You got a briarheart? That's what I really need. Neloth keeps taking all of mine.
Dragonborn.esm|01F137: You find some void salts and you bring them to me. I'll beat anyone else's price.
Dragonborn.esm|01F182: Bilgemuck run. Prize beast, run. Fool tribe-kin, chase away. Bilgemuck fear. You bring, he follow you. Yes?
Dragonborn.esm|01F183: Chief smart. Most Riekling dumb but chief smart.
Dragonborn.esm|01F184: We family. We strong, you stronger.
Dragonborn.esm|01F185: Bilgemuck prize beast. You find, he follow. Bilgemuck love meat. Give meat, he follow. You go.
Dragonborn.esm|01F186: Come on, let's get up there.
Dragonborn.esm|01F188: No strange. Strong. You strong, make us stronger. Help tribe-kin.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2BF: Oh, excuse me! I didn't realize there was anyone else here.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C0: What makes you think I haven't? Any time Adril and the Redoran Guard made a move, the Ulens must have been one step ahead and didn't show up.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C1: Without Councilor Morvayn, Raven Rock has no future. Keep that in mind next time you decide to help or not.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C2: Then... it's finally over. What of the Redoran Guard I sent to assist you?
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C3: Now, if you'll excuse me, I'd like to meditate a bit.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C4: There's an Ulen Ancestral Tomb near the Temple. Someone's been leaving ash yam offerings on the altar inside. If you were to wait inside for whomever that is, I'm betting they'll have some of the answers you're looking for.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C5: They had welcomed the Empire with open arms, and benefited from it. When everything was said and done, they were no longer recognized as a Great House and were dismissed from the Council.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C6: Traitors. The lot of them. Their collaboration with the Empire may have given them unrivaled political and economic strength, but their hearts weren't with the Dunmer people.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C7: Now that's just ridiculous! As second councilor, it's my responsibility to protect Councilor Morvayn from harm.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C8: If you have the evidence and it places your life in peril, I would expect you to do whatever you must to keep yourself alive. Here, this key should open the front door of the manor in case they've locked it. Good luck.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2C9: Oh, no bother. We all have a right to be here.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2CC: Most of my contacts live leagues away from Solstheim. Sending the occasional message back and forth is all we can manage, and sometimes takes months or more to arrive. However, any information we can glean in times like this I consider vital to House Redoran's survival.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2CD: I do. I've received some information from my sources that there could be elements of Ulen's house, House Hlaalu, already in Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2CE: People have mentioned it's a bit odd about how close in age Mirri appears to be to her father, but that's hardly a cause for alarm. Just make sure that you find solid evidence. If I accuse them without it, I'll be the laughingstock of the colony.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2CF: What do you mean? I thought I was clear. I'm simply honoring a fellow departed Dunmer.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D0: He underestimated the Redoran Guard. They were organized and brought a swift end to the coup. Vilur tried to run, but he was caught by Captain Veleth himself. Vilur Ulen was executed a short time later and his remains placed in the Ulen Ancestral Tomb.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D1: Actually, I do. If you want to catch those slippery slaughterfish, you need to let them come to you.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D3: I maintain a few contacts in other Dunmer settlements across Tamriel. Most hail from House Redoran, but there are a few from other houses.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D4: As second councilor, it's my responsibility to explore the possibility of any threat, no matter how paranoid it might seem.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D5: They're quite wealthy and they've been quite generous by sharing their fortune with the community. They've contributed coin, food, supplies... even helped repair a few of the buildings in town with their own hands.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D6: No, I just think he's going about this the wrong way.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D7: Before you dismiss this entirely, I was hoping you might be able to lend a hand by investigating on the councilor's behalf.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D8: It may be justified, but it certainly isn't appreciated. Councilor Morvayn simply feels that I'm being paranoid.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2D9: Tilisu is wife to the family patriarch, Vendil. They have a single child, a daughter named Mirri. They arrived here ten years ago, and have been nothing but a benefit to Raven Rock ever since.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2DA: What kind of nonsense is that!? I'm simply honoring a fellow departed Dunmer, and you hurl baseless accusations at me?
Dragonborn.esm|01F2DB: He arrived alone, but spread enough coin around to start changing people's minds. Half the bloody town joined his side. Can you believe that?
Dragonborn.esm|01F2DD: Summoning you here was part of my plan. I was hoping you'd be interested in investigating for me.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2DE: It's a private matter. Let's just say they seek revenge for the execution of one of their own... ordered by Councilor Morvayn himself. The problem is I don't have any solid proof that anyone from House Hlaalu is hidden among us... only my suspicions.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2DF: Since there aren't any Ulens left on this island, I've taken it upon myself to leave the traditional offering in their stead.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E0: Back in 95, Vilur Ulen came to Raven Rock with a plan to take over our town. The fetcher thought he could stir up the locals and use them against the Redoran Guard and then murder Councilor Morvayn when we weren't looking.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E1: Well, well. Adril's got himself a spy, does he? Azura knows he needs all the help he can get. He's been chasing the Ulens for years now.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E2: I had a feeling you'd be willing to listen to me.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E4: I'm not kicking down anyone's door until I have solid evidence in hand. If you're wrong about Tilisu, things would quickly go downhill around here and the real culprits might slip away. I can't afford for that to happen.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E5: When a Dunmer is laid to rest, the body is burned and returned to the ashen soil. Since the ash yam is born of the very same soil, it represents rebirth and renewal... the fact that life ends and begins anew.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E6: I need you to start out by being my eyes and ears... search Raven Rock and see if you can root out these traitors. The best place to start would be Geldis Sadri over at the Retching Netch Cornerclub. If he provides you any leads, check them out before bringing them to my attention. I can't afford to make any mistakes.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E8: Raven Rock is a small settlement, so it's hard to accomplish anything in secret. If any of them is from House Hlaalu, I wouldn't be able to make a move without them knowing.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2E9: The Ulen Family of House Hlaalu, a rival Great House, has placed a deathmark on the councilor's head.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2EA: Wonderful, simply wonderful! Councilor Morvayn will be quite pleased when he hears about this.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2EB: So Geldis's plan worked, eh? Everytime we tried it, no one would ever show up. If Tilisu's involved in any sort of plot to harm Councilor Morvayn, I'm going to need hard evidence to prove it.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2EC: I presume the same thing you're doing... leaving an offering for the departed. Nice to see a fellow Dunmer following the traditional customs.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2ED: I presume the same thing you're doing... leaving an offering for the departed. Forgive my surprise, it's just that I wouldn't expect that sort of behavior from someone who isn't Dunmer.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2EE: You can thank me if it works. Until then, I'd head to the tomb and wait until your visitor shows up.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2F0: Hlaalu deserved every bit of the hatred they received once the Empire released its grasp on Morrowind. Having past ties to the Empire immediately turned them into the scapegoat for the Dunmer people's suffering.
Dragonborn.esm|01F2F1: I'm afraid that one day I'll awaken and discover him dead, and I don't want it to come to that.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8BE: Master Neloth is going to be casting some rather loud spells in the next day or two. You should warn the others. Oh, and if the tower door suddenly gets icy cold, don't come in.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8BF: You have some tea, but I'll get you a fresh cup. Elynea is waiting for fresh taproot before she can regrow that chamber.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C0: I don't know how he drinks that stuff.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C2: Varona, get me some canis root tea. Oh, and remind Elynea that she needs to fix my tower. That chamber has been withered for too long.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C3: If you're here about the canis root tea, it's almost ready.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C4: I'm sick of all this ash. I know dunmer aren't supposed to mind the ash, but I hate it.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C5: That's what you said yesterday! I really can't put this trip off for much longer.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C6: Well you'll just have to wait. Last time I brought his meal up late he asked if I wanted to be a part of one of his experiments.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C7: Bah! Patience is for the young. An old dunmer like me can't afford it.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C8: Neloth is a master wizard of the House Telvanni. I've already learned more than I would have in twice the time at the College of Winterhold.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8C9: Maybe some daedra heart. Elynea never seems to stock it. Let me get you the rest of the list tomorrow. I'm just too busy to think about this right now.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8CA: Well I can't wait forever. Master Neloth keeps asking if I have his soul gems yet.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8CB: Soon. Be patient. Ulves is still making his list. And of course Master Neloth keeps adding to his. What with all the ash spawn on the road these days, I'm not leaving until the trip is worth it.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8CE: I would have had it ready, but Master Neloth wanted his meal early.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8CF: Are you going to Raven Rock soon? I need some thing.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D0: The pain has subsided. Thank you for asking. Hopefully the frost lessons are over.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D1: Hmm? Oh. Raven Rock. Right. I need soul gems...
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D2: Where is your list of things you need from Raven Rock?
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D3: Talvas is Master Neloth's apprentice. If you are interested in spells, he might be willing to sell you some. Elynea is our alchemist. She specializes in mushrooms and other types of fungus. Ulves runs the kitchens.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D4: I don't want to see any of you get hurt. Oh, could you get me an extra blanket. I think I might need it.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D5: How are your lessons coming, Talvas?
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D6: Master Neloth, I'm preparing for a trip to Raven Rock to pick up supplies. I have your list of soul gems, but nothing else.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D7: Maybe two hundred years ago they were. Now they say those green lands are ash covered too.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8D9: That man is going to kill himself one day, and take the rest of us with him. Thanks for the warning.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8DA: I already did, but it won't do any good. They haven't had any for months.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8DB: For now, not much. But in a few decades when I return to Vvardenfell, you would be seen as Morrowind nobility. In the mean time, we would keep a bed and chest here for you.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8DC: So you've said a hundred times. But didn't you also tell me the Telvanni lands were green and lush?
Dragonborn.esm|01F8DD: No. Talvas is his apprentice. I'm his steward. I manage the day to day affairs of Tel Mithryn.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8DE: Who brought me here? I demand to know who did this!
Dragonborn.esm|01F8DF: Talvas says Master Neloth is asking for me.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E0: I heard Mogrul is dead. That is a relief!
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E1: I heard you paid my debt to Mogrul. I don't know how I can every repay you.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E2: I hope Elynea has something to help me sleep at night.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E3: Taproot? That's all? Add it to the Raven Rock list.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E4: At least you don't have to taste every time to see if it's brewed right.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E5: It's a good thing you've always lived here on Solstheim. Vvardenfell is covered in ash. Red Mountain makes sure of that.
Dragonborn.esm|01F8E6: Tel Mithryn. It's the home of Master Neloth. You may have heard of him. He's a famous Telvanni wizard.
Dragonborn.esm|01FB8E: Hail, thuri. Your thu'um has the mastery. Climb aboard and I will carry you to Miraak.
Dragonborn.esm|01FB8F: Climb on and we will smite our enemies.
Dragonborn.esm|01FB91: How can I serve you, thuri?
Dragonborn.esm|01FB92: Hail, thuri. Shall we fly together?
