Dragonborn.esm|01FB93: Zu'u fent aam. I am at your command.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE07: Strong always fight strong. Old chief strong, me stronger. You stronger? Fight me!
Dragonborn.esm|01FE08: Bilgemuck follow you. Bilgemuck know strength. Tribe-kin stronger. You stay, help more.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE09: You strong! All tribe-kin stronger with you. But... maybe you too strong. Too strong, want be chief.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE0B: You know. We know. Come fight. We wait, but you, come fight.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE0C: Bad people. They come, fight us. We fight back now.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE0D: Bad Nords. Say Thirsk theirs. Live by water, bother tribe-kin. We fight. You strong. You help. No more bad Nords.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE0E: Good redgrass. Later, we burn. Now, final task. Then, you tribe-kin.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE0F: Here, like this. Go, bring two hands of redgrass. Be friend of tribe-kin.
Dragonborn.esm|01FE11: Tribe-kin missing redgrass. Need for godspeak dance. You bring redgrass, we dance.
Dragonborn.esm|01FEF3: A new master. It's been centuries.
Dragonborn.esm|01FEF5: More cups and plates for me to carry?
Dragonborn.esm|01FEF6: I don't suppose you'll carry some of this for a while.
Dragonborn.esm|01FEF9: A butler's work is never done.
Dragonborn.esm|01FEFD: I always dreamt of being a pack mule.
Dragonborn.esm|01FEFE: I was hoping you'd say that.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF4E: The legends speak of that place. Terrible battles fought at the temple. The dragons burning it to the ground in rage. They speak also of something worse than dragons buried within. Difficult to imagine, but if true...
Dragonborn.esm|01FF4F: So, you have seen things, yes? My magic grows weak, and so does the barrier around our village. Time is short. Tell me what you know.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF50: I feared that it would be so.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF51: I'm not sure, but it is fascinating, isn't it? Perhaps it has some relation to what's going on here. Quite unexpected. I'm afraid I can't give you any answers. But there are ruins of an ancient temple of Miraak's toward the center of the island. If I were you, I'd look there.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF52: I am unsure. It may mean that you could save us, or it may mean that you could bring about our destruction. But our time here is running out. The few of us left free of control cannot protect ourselves for much longer. You must go to Saering's Watch. Learn there the word that Miraak learned long ago, and use that knowledge on the Wind Stone. You may be able to break the hold on our people there, and free them from control.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF53: He has confirmed that Miraak is indeed behind the suffering of our people.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF54: She has confirmed that Miraak is the one behind the suffering of our people.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF55: No, but I have brought someone who has seen things...
Dragonborn.esm|01FF56: That is what the stories say. You are Dragonborn as well, then. Perhaps you are connected.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF57: Frea! What news do you bring? Is there a way to free our people?
Dragonborn.esm|01FF58: Then perhaps you are connected with him. The old tales say that he, too, was Dragonborn.
Dragonborn.esm|01FF59: Father! I have returned! There is yet hope!
Dragonborn.esm|01FF5A: It means what I feared has come to pass. Miraak was never truly gone, and now has returned. If you could go to this place and see him... Are you like Miraak? Are you Dragonborn?
Dragonborn.esm|0200A1: So Neloth is afraid to face me himself!
Dragonborn.esm|0200A3: Dead? Oh, merciful ancestors! That blasted wizard couldn't be bothered to give her some protection for those trips. This is all his fault. Best tell Neloth. I'm sure he'll mourn for all of two seconds.
Dragonborn.esm|0200A4: Dead? Truly? Oh, Varona. She didn't deserve this. This is a dark day for Tel Mithryn. Go tell Master Neloth. When I'm done here, I'll prepare her grave.
Dragonborn.esm|0200A5: She's dead? Oh my. Master Neloth is not going to be happy to hear that. Maybe you should be the one to tell him.
Dragonborn.esm|0200A7: I can't believe Varona is dead.
Dragonborn.esm|0200A8: Find a new steward for us quickly. Master Neloth is making me do all her work.
Dragonborn.esm|0200AA: Master Neloth's old apprentice? The one before me? I never met her. Master Neloth took me on because she died. I heard it was messy.
Dragonborn.esm|0200AB: She was a headstrong one. Always pushing Master Neloth to teach her more. Her lust for power was her undoing. She let Master Neloth do some ritual thing to her. We buried her out back. That was some twenty years ago now.
Dragonborn.esm|0200AC: Ildari? She was a beauty, she was. I tried to court her, but she had no interest in me. She was only interested in magic and power. She died in some experiment Master Neloth did on her. He claimed she wanted him to.
Dragonborn.esm|0200AE: Try walking around the grounds with the ring on.
Dragonborn.esm|0200B1: Mogrul isn't someone you can ignore.
Dragonborn.esm|0200B2: Sooner or later they all pay.
Dragonborn.esm|0200B3: Ugh! The heart stone protects me!
Dragonborn.esm|0200B4: There are more and more of those things showing up around here lately. I suppose you'll have to find me a new steward. Try Raven Rock. They are in awe of me there. I'm sure almost anyone would be willing to serve me.
Dragonborn.esm|0200B5: The Raven Rock road is dangerous. I hope nothing happened to Varona.
Dragonborn.esm|0200B6: Varona goes to Raven Rock all the time. I'm sure she's fine.
Dragonborn.esm|0200D1: It takes a strong will to command a dragon's soul. Perhaps you aren't as powerful as you think.
Dragonborn.esm|0200D2: Not this time, Dragonborn. This one's mine.
Dragonborn.esm|0200D3: This dragon's soul belongs to me.
Dragonborn.esm|0200D4: Do you ever wonder if it hurts? To have one's soul ripped out like that?
Dragonborn.esm|0200D5: Thank you for your help. We will meet again soon.
Dragonborn.esm|0200D6: One step closer to my return.
Dragonborn.esm|02066F: So it is. You have proven yourself an ally to the Skaal, and so the Skaal shall be allies to you.
Dragonborn.esm|020679: What a relief. Master Neloth surely would have punished me had he found out. I don't have any gold to pay you with, but I can give you this staff.
Dragonborn.esm|02067B: Yes, but don't tell Master Neloth. I'd have to give him your payment.
Dragonborn.esm|02067C: Not so much with, as for. I am Master Neloth's apprentice. He's teaching me the art of sorcery.
Dragonborn.esm|02067E: This is Master Neloth's tower. He's a Telvanni Wizard.
Dragonborn.esm|0206A0: Deer, bear and horker provide most of what we need for skins and meat. Sometimes, when the wolves grow too bold and threaten the village, we must hunt them to cull their numbers.
Dragonborn.esm|0206A1: When we stalk large game, I lead our hunters in tracking the beast. It is also a way of saying that I am the most skilled hunter in the village. I doubt that an outsider could truly understand, but I hope my words have helped to answer your question.
Dragonborn.esm|0206A2: Well, let me think... I suppose it's been nearly a year now. My goodness, how the time has flown!
Dragonborn.esm|0206A3: Oh no, most certainly not. I'm a historian. I'm here to learn about the history of Solstheim. Solstheim is a fascinating place, but we know so little about its past. There are many mysteries that remain unsolved.
Dragonborn.esm|0206A4: Many hunters pass through our village, and sometimes the Dark Elves of Raven Rock visit us. They bring us things from their homeland that we can't find on the island, such as spices, fine cloth and ores that we cannot mine here. It's true we Skaal prefer a simpler life than some, but we do still enjoy a luxury from time to time.
Dragonborn.esm|0206A5: Well, my mother used to say that I was born an angry child. It's true that my heart has always been restless, and fighting was the only way I could find peace. I was a soldier for a time, and then a mercenary, but I was never truly happy until I came here.
Dragonborn.esm|0206AA: You are an outsider, and I don't know if I can make you understand. I will try. The All-Maker is the maker of all things, and it is from the All-Maker that life flows like a great river. As all rivers must return to the sea, so all life returns in time to the All-Maker. I know our ways must seem strange to you, but the nine gods of the Empire are equally strange to us.
Dragonborn.esm|0206AB: The Skaal hunt not for sport, but to survive. We believe that all creatures have a right to live as they will, and when we take what we need from them, we thank the beast for its gifts. The less we disturb the land and the beasts within it, the more we respect the wishes of the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|0206AD: You risked your life to save Baldor. Your name will be honored in our village.
Dragonborn.esm|0206AE: I'm relieved that you were able to save Baldor. I admit, I wasn't ready to become smith of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|0206B1: I was chosen by the people of the village when our last leader, Skaf the Giant, departed the cold world to join the All-Maker. I suppose I've always spoken my mind and tried to do what's best for the Skaal. That's why the others call me Fanari Strong-Voice.
Dragonborn.esm|0206B2: The first piece of wisdom I teach to any hunter of the Skaal is to ask the simple question, should I truly kill this beast? We take only what we need, and so we preserve the oneness with the land.
Dragonborn.esm|0206B3: We are Nords, much like those you'll find in Skyrim. Long ago our path diverged from that of our cousins on the mainland. We live as one with the land, for the land provides all that we need. Also, like the Nords of old, we embrace the All-Maker. We do not worship the nine gods of the Empire.
Dragonborn.esm|020720: Three of the cubes are through here. This seems like a sensible place to start.
Dragonborn.esm|020721: Aren't you going to read the book? I didn't go to all this trouble just to stand around admiring it.
Dragonborn.esm|020722: These must be the "Great Workshops of Nchardak". Impressive even in ruins. In the city's days of glory, it was reputed to be able to assemble a complete automaton in a single day. Much of the Dwemer army at the Battle of Red Mountain must have come from here.
Dragonborn.esm|020723: Here are the cubes you left behind.
Dragonborn.esm|020724: Here, I picked up this cube for you.
Dragonborn.esm|020725: Wouldn't it be easier just to turn off the trap? Like so?
Dragonborn.esm|020726: I hope the rest of the cubes are this easy to find. Although knowing the Dwemer, I rather doubt it.
Dragonborn.esm|020727: Now that the boilers are working, it should be simple to release the book from it's protective case.
Dragonborn.esm|020728: All four boilers need to be working if you want to release the book. I thought I was clear about that.
Dragonborn.esm|020729: We have the cubes that we need to start the boilers. Let's not waste any more time.
Dragonborn.esm|02072A: As I said, we need to restore the steam supply to the reading room if we want to get the book.
Dragonborn.esm|02072B: You'll need a cube to unlock this door. I hope you remembered to pick up the cube I placed in the pedestal.
Dragonborn.esm|02072C: You should be able to unseal this door with the control cube I gave you.
Dragonborn.esm|0207B1: Miraak had this? This does not look like something of the Dragon cult. It is a dark thing, unnatural. I would have nothing to do with it. But the Dark Elf Wizard, Neloth... He came to us some time ago, asking about Black Books. I believe he knows a great deal about them. Perhaps too much. Seek him out to the south. Be cautious, Dragonborn. There is something else at work here.
Dragonborn.esm|0207B2: I cannot help with that. None here can. You will need the knowledge Miraak himself learned. You will need to learn more about this Black Book.
Dragonborn.esm|020947: Bandits sometimes come at night to steal our food. They think that stealing from us is easier than hunting for themselves. It will be dangerous, but if you'll deal with these thieves, we would be most grateful.
Dragonborn.esm|020948: Then may the All-Maker give you the strength of a great bear and make your will as firm as stone.
Dragonborn.esm|02094B: I was foolish to expect compassion from an outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|02094D: Your kindness warms my heart as sunlight warms the skin on a cold day. The Skaal are once again in your debt, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|02097D: Ah, good! Another cube. We're finally making some progress.
Dragonborn.esm|02097E: Look out for that Steam Centurion! Get to higher ground!
Dragonborn.esm|020980: With the water level this high, we can swim across the collapsed part of the passage and reach the other side.
Dragonborn.esm|020982: Ah, good. Now we can see if we can find a way to get that next cube.
Dragonborn.esm|020983: There's a passage up there. We could reach it if the water level was higher.
Dragonborn.esm|020984: You must have left a cube in one of the pump control pedestals. If you remove it, the pumps will stop and we'll be able to swim up there.
Dragonborn.esm|020985: Unfortunately, each cube we retrieve shuts down the corresponding pumps, raising the water level. We'll have to be careful.
Dragonborn.esm|020986: It looks like we'll need to lower the water level to make any more progress.
Dragonborn.esm|020987: I believe these pillars will activate the pumps for this room, assuming they still work.
Dragonborn.esm|020988: Very good. By my count, we've found a total of four cubes. I have one that you forgot to pick up. I hope you didn't leave any others lying around.
Dragonborn.esm|020989: Very good. By my count, we've found a total of four cubes. Assuming you haven't dropped any anywhere.
Dragonborn.esm|0209F1: Damn it, woman! I said to leave me be!
Dragonborn.esm|0209F3: It describes a discovery that was made in the mine by some of the diggers. They wanted Gratian to take a closer look. The East Empire Company must have felt it was of great value, as they locked that section of the mine off from everyone else.
Dragonborn.esm|0209F4: Who in the blazes are you?! Can't you see I'm busy?
Dragonborn.esm|0209F5: Look, my wife nags that I'm too old to go traipsing around these mines chasing my story. Time's my enemy... it's caught up with me before I could find the answers that I'm looking for.
Dragonborn.esm|0209F6: I don't think I've come across more than a chunk or two in my lifetime, and smithing's my trade. If you're looking to learn more, you should head over to the Skaal Village. Someone there's bound to know more about it than I do.
Dragonborn.esm|0209F7: Let me see that! Ha, I knew it! Gratian's death wasn't because of a rockfall, it was just a story to keep people away from the tombs. Now that I have his journal in hand, I can get some closure from the East Empire Company. Make them pay for lying to everyone.
Dragonborn.esm|0209F8: But mark my words, these mines hold a secret that could put Raven Rock back on the map.
Dragonborn.esm|0209F9: Crescius, you're an obstinate old fool and you're going to get yourself killed.
Dragonborn.esm|0209FA: Sigh. I don't care what you, or anyone else thinks about me.
Dragonborn.esm|0209FB: Hmph, maybe. Been difficult trusting people lately, they think I'm crazy.
Dragonborn.esm|0209FC: Gratian Caerellius. He spent his entire life exploring ancient ruins across Tamriel and he died in these very mines almost 200 years ago.
Dragonborn.esm|0209FE: Calling stalhrim an ore is like calling my forge a campfire. Some folks say it's "enchanted ice," but I think there's more to it than that.
Dragonborn.esm|0209FF: Now you're finally talking some sense. A mining tool belongs in a mining family. Just tell him I lost it or something.
Dragonborn.esm|020A00: Ask most of the folks around here and they'd agree with you.
Dragonborn.esm|020A02: I do. I want to know what happened to Gratian, and what the East Empire Company is keeping from all of us.
Dragonborn.esm|020A03: I'm telling you... there's something big down there, something that the East Empire Company wanted to hide from everyone.
Dragonborn.esm|020A04: It's the only tool tough enough to crack stalhrim, is what it is. Not many of those beauties left in the world... forging them is a lost art. The one Crescius "borrowed" came from the Skaal Village up north. I, uh, traded them some goods for it.
Dragonborn.esm|020A05: Good. Now let me get back to what I was doing.
Dragonborn.esm|020A06: Why should I tell you? I don't even know who you are.
Dragonborn.esm|020A07: That was almost two centuries ago. There may be nothing left to find.
Dragonborn.esm|020A08: You're back! Please, tell me what you found.
Dragonborn.esm|020A09: Exploring, huh? Most folks think this place isn't worth the effort.
Dragonborn.esm|020A0A: Um, I prefer to handle these things on my own. Look, if you see Crescius, tell him to give me that pickaxe back and I'll pay you for the trouble.
Dragonborn.esm|020A0B: Oh, very well. Here. Tell him I hope he drops the thing on his foot.
Dragonborn.esm|020A0C: And I'm telling you that I'll do whatever it takes to find my great-grandfather's remains. He's down here, I can feel it.
Dragonborn.esm|020A0D: Gratian kept a journal of notes about his discoveries. If you can find his... remains, I'm hoping it will help. Here's everything I have. The letter, the key. Please, do this for me so I can finally regain the respect that I've lost.
Dragonborn.esm|020A0E: A secret the East Empire Company swept under the rug two centuries ago. It killed my great-grandfather, and left Raven Rock with a worthless and tainted mine.
Dragonborn.esm|020A0F: No, no, no. This isn't just your run-of-the-mill pickaxe... I'm talking about an Ancient Nordic Pickaxe. They don't exactly grow on trees, you know.
Dragonborn.esm|020A10: The damn fool doesn't even deserve to have it! The pickaxe was made for mining, not selling. I'll bet he stole it from the Skaal in the first place.
Dragonborn.esm|020A11: My wife and I were cleaning out our home, and we came across some of my great-grandfather's things locked in an old chest. I found an unsent letter he'd written to the East Empire Company, and a key.
Dragonborn.esm|020A12: You haven't seen Crescius Caerellius have you? That foolish old man's taken my pickaxe again!
Dragonborn.esm|020A13: Thank you. Now, I believe I owe you a little more than a debt of gratitude for all that work you did. Here... it's not much, but it's the best an old, retired miner can do.
Dragonborn.esm|020A14: It is. Well, I assume it is. I haven't been able to find the entrance.
Dragonborn.esm|020A15: Crescius, last time you explored the mine you almost fell to your death. I'm not spending the rest of my days as a widow!
Dragonborn.esm|020A16: So you finally tracked down old Crescius, eh? Quite a character, isn't he. Tell you what. Since you went through all the trouble of finding it for me and all, you keep it.
Dragonborn.esm|020A17: You never heard of them? They're the largest trading company in Tamriel, originally chartered by the Emperor of Cyrodiil himself.
Dragonborn.esm|020A18: Now you're starting to sound like everyone else.
Dragonborn.esm|020A19: East Empire Company called it "a terrible accident," claiming that he was lost in a rockfall, but I know better.
Dragonborn.esm|020A1A: I just wanted to remind that codger you can't just go around taking things from other people. Now that you've delivered the message, I'm satisfied. Besides, that pickaxe hasn't done me any good in years. Maybe you can put it to good use.
Dragonborn.esm|020E90: Make them stop? Oh no, certainly not. At least not yet. Not until I've figured out what is going on. Imagine the power of being able to command entire villages to do your bidding. Imagine what I could do with that power.
Dragonborn.esm|020E91: They are off working on their pillar. Happens every night.
Dragonborn.esm|020EA8: Be careful down there, the mine can be treacherous.
Dragonborn.esm|020EA9: At least hear me out before you walk away.
Dragonborn.esm|020EAA: Sure, ignore the ravings of a "crazy old man." I get it.
Dragonborn.esm|020EAB: I'm sure he's down there somewhere, buried under a mountain of rubble like the East Empire Company claims. My greatest fear is that my stubborn mule of a husband will end up suffering the same fate.
Dragonborn.esm|020EAC: Poor Crescius is aging and I believe it's addled his mind. As the years have passed, it's gotten worse. He's become obsessed with his theories of conspiracy.
Dragonborn.esm|020EAE: Try the lower sections of the mine. I've never been able to get that far.
Dragonborn.esm|020EAF: Decided to give me a listen, eh? Good.
Dragonborn.esm|020EB1: Why you're leading Crescius on, I'll never understand.
Dragonborn.esm|020EFA: Really? When this is over, you'll have to tell me all about it.
Dragonborn.esm|020FB9: It's working, I'm flying! I'm really flying! Hahahaha!
Dragonborn.esm|020FBA: They said it couldn't be done. They said it. They said it. But they were wrong!
Dragonborn.esm|020FBB: There's nothing to worry about, nothing to worry about at all...
Dragonborn.esm|020FBC: The book in my dream showed me...
Dragonborn.esm|020FBD: To touch the sky like a dragon...
Dragonborn.esm|020FC8: I have an idea. I'll wait here and retrieve this cube once you find the last one we need. Good luck!
Dragonborn.esm|020FC9: What a mess. I'm getting tired of wading around in this muck.
Dragonborn.esm|020FCA: I'd guess that must be the pump activator up there. But how to reach it?
Dragonborn.esm|020FCB: You'll need to lower the water level before we can proceed any further.
Dragonborn.esm|020FCC: The last cube is somewhere through the northern door. We won't be able to open it until the water level is lower.
Dragonborn.esm|0214AC: Very well, I'll take a look at it later.
Dragonborn.esm|0214AD: There's no time for that now, I have work to do.
Dragonborn.esm|0214AE: Nikulas is restless. He's like an eagle, always seeking to fly somewhere new. I do not think he has the patience to work the forge.
Dragonborn.esm|0214AF: Oh? And what is her name, this secret wife of yours?
Dragonborn.esm|0214B0: One of our chairs is about to break. It will need to be mended with a new leg.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B1: Complaining to me won't make your pain go away, Oslaf. Go and see Edla, perhaps she has a remedy.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B2: Because I already have a wife, and I wouldn't want to make her jealous.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B4: There's a terrible ache in my back today.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B5: All-Maker willing, young Nikulas will take an interest. I would be happy to teach him.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B6: Why have you never taken a wife, Baldor?
Dragonborn.esm|0214B7: Make sure that you save some wood from the pile.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B8: I'm sure the All-Maker has more important concerns than your potatoes, Oslaf.
Dragonborn.esm|0214B9: It's tradition that the son of the smith take his father's place, but you have no son. Who will become smith of the Skaal after you?
Dragonborn.esm|0214BA: It makes my heart heavy, to think of killing a beast when we have no need of its hide or its meat. That is not our way. The bear might find food elsewhere. We will leave it alone.
Dragonborn.esm|0214BB: Hm, perhaps I'll make an offering to the All-Maker. He can make our potatoes ripen as surely as he makes the snow fall from the sky.
Dragonborn.esm|0214BC: You speak the truth, Baldor. It makes my heart glad to see her keeping busy after Heifnir's death. Very well, I'll ask Edla to trade for some coal.
Dragonborn.esm|0214BD: If I'd known that I was married to a fox who digs under beds to look for treats, then perhaps I would have.
Dragonborn.esm|0214BE: We had better do something about it, then.
Dragonborn.esm|0214BF: Be patient, my husband. The crop doesn't grow faster because it hears your growling stomach.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C0: True, but I think that Edla would be hurt if you didn't give her the chance to trade for it first. You know how happy it makes her to play the village peddler
Dragonborn.esm|0214C1: That is sweet of you, Deor, but you should find a better hiding place next time.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C2: Yes, I know the one you saw. It watches us each night, always moving closer. As the ashfall worsens, the land and water are poisoned. The animals have less to eat and become desperate.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C3: Are the potatoes ready for eating? I want to roast them with some onions for the night's meal.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C4: I could travel to Raven Rock and purchase some more.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C5: What?! That was supposed to be a gift! I was hoping to surprise you.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C6: There was a great brown bear on the slopes the other day. I could swear by the All-Maker that it was watching the village.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C7: We're nearly out of coal again.
Dragonborn.esm|0214C8: Husband, do you know why there's a pouch of spiced tea leaves under the bed?
Dragonborn.esm|0214C9: Then get back to it, you lazy horker! The day is wasting.
Dragonborn.esm|0214CA: Well, Deor and Yrsa are still young. In time, I'm sure that they will have many children. Perhaps one of them will want to learn the ways of iron-shaping.
Dragonborn.esm|0214CB: Her name is forge. Now be silent before she hears you and becomes angry.
Dragonborn.esm|0214E4: Watch out, the water's rising even higher with both pumps shut down.
Dragonborn.esm|0214E5: Do you have the cube? Good, good! Then I'll take this cube and we can be on our way.
Dragonborn.esm|0214E6: That was easier than I expected. I'm glad you were here to do all the hard work.
Dragonborn.esm|0214E7: That's it! Don't touch anything else. Quickly now.
Dragonborn.esm|0214E8: Well, that's progress of a sort. But we'll need all three down at once to get to the pump control.
Dragonborn.esm|0214E9: Now we can finally see about getting those boilers started.
Dragonborn.esm|0214EA: I have some cubes I'm sure you're going to need.
Dragonborn.esm|0214EB: You'll need the cube I picked up to start all the boilers.
Dragonborn.esm|0214EC: I'm quite sure the boilers will not work while they're under water.
Dragonborn.esm|0214ED: I thought you understood how this worked by now. The water level needs to be lower for the boilers to work at all.
Dragonborn.esm|0214EE: It should now be as simple as using the cubes to turn on the four boilers.
Dragonborn.esm|0214EF: As much as I enjoy this place, I'm ready to see if we can actually open the book's protective case. You do still remember why we're here?
Dragonborn.esm|0214F0: You need to start the four boilers. Then we should be able to open the book's protective case upstairs.
Dragonborn.esm|0214F2: With the cubes I retrieved, we should now have a total of five. I hope you haven't left more scattered about.
Dragonborn.esm|0214F3: With the cube I retrieved from the pumping pedestal, we should now have five cubes - exactly what we need.
Dragonborn.esm|02174C: How am I supposed to get anything done with part of the tower withered like this?
Dragonborn.esm|02174D: He never sleeps. I don't know how he does it, but he never rests for even a moment.
Dragonborn.esm|02174E: I hope Elynea has something for headaches.
Dragonborn.esm|023F60: Then this will be over soon for you.
Dragonborn.esm|023F61: Good. Then let us see who truly has the soul of the Dov.
Dragonborn.esm|023F62: The time for talking is long past.
Dragonborn.esm|023FC0: I assure you, if I could convince Adril to do that, I'd be on the next ship back to Blacklight. Unfortunately, Adril and Councilor Morvayn are very close friends, and they will never abandon one another... despite my feelings.
Dragonborn.esm|023FC1: I have to be. As Second Councilor, the security of Raven Rock is my primary concern.
Dragonborn.esm|023FC2: Well of course. With four other houses on the Council, someone has to keep the rabble organized. Our house leads the Council by virtue of our preeminence in battle, wisdom, and ancestral glory.
Dragonborn.esm|023FC4: This isn't Skyrim, outlander. A group of powerful and influential Dunmer families have been ruling Morrowind for millennia. They embody a council made up of five Great Houses: Telvanni, Dres, Indoril, Sadras and Redoran.
Dragonborn.esm|023FC7: He's in grave danger, and yet he continues to place himself in harm's way. When I fall asleep at night, all I can do is hope he'll still be there when I awaken. This island was never meant for us. There's too much death here.
Dragonborn.esm|023FC9: You've never heard of House Redoran? We're the mightiest of the Great Houses and we lead the Council, the ruling body of Morrowind.
Dragonborn.esm|023FCA: Ah, this is bonemold armor... quite impressive, wouldn't you say? It's fashioned from actual bone that's been reinforced with a resin-like material and then shaped to form the armored plating. It might appear brittle, but I'd wager it could stop the blow of a weapon better than iron or steel.
Dragonborn.esm|023FCC: Adril Arano, Second Councilor of Raven Rock. How I hate that title... damn fool's going to get himself killed over it someday.
Dragonborn.esm|023FCE: To protect Councilor Morvayn, of course. He has quite a few friends on the Council back in Morrowind and they wanted to ensure his safety while he was here. Other than that, I'm not at liberty to discuss it.
Dragonborn.esm|023FCF: Regardless, I hope to return there someday... which doesn't seem likely given my husband's commitment to Councilor Morvayn.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD0: We've fought quite a few battles together, and saved each other's lives on more than one occasion. Our bond is our strength. We've always been at each other's side and nothing will ever change that.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD1: Well, there's a small enclave of Nords to the north at Skaal Village. They pretty much keep to themselves, and we both like it that way. Then there's Tel Mithryn to the east. The Telvanni live over there... sort of a strange lot, but since we're both from Morrowind, we get along. Other than that, Solstheim is crawling with bandits and Reiklings, but that's to be expected living on the frontier like this.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD3: Hmph. Well, beauty's in the eye of the beholder, I suppose.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD4: I've known the councilor since we were both quite young. We rose the ranks in House Redoran together. When word arrived in Blacklight that the Councilor's father had passed, I was asked to sail to Raven Rock with him as his second. It meant passing on better opportunities at home, but I didn't want to leave his side.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD5: Solstheim is theoretically under my rule, but without revenue from the mine, I lack the resources to actually govern anything but Raven Rock itself. Not that there's much beyond the confines of our town anyway.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD7: I haven't seen the city in over fifty years. Even for a Dunmer, that seems like an eternity.
Dragonborn.esm|023FD9: If you're interested in pursuing the subject, help yourself to any of the historical volumes in my library.
Dragonborn.esm|023FDA: All of the soldiers in this town are Redoran Guard... some of the best warriors that House Redoran has to offer. I've spent quite a few years honing their skills and I can assure you they're not just your average city guard.
Dragonborn.esm|023FDB: Adril and I lived in Blacklight, the capital city of Morrowind. House Redoran has improved Blacklight substantially over the last thousand years... its grandeur rivals that of Mournhold itself. The Rootspire where the Council of Great Houses meet is something to behold.
Dragonborn.esm|023FDC: This isn't exactly the city of Blacklight. We're on the frontier out here, and we've had more than a fair share of troubles. After all we've been through together, I refuse to let Councilor Morvayn down.
Dragonborn.esm|023FDE: I'm the wife of a Second Councilor and certain things are expected of me. The most important is that I stand by Adril, no matter how I feel. And I intend to do that until we get off this miserable rock.
Dragonborn.esm|023FDF: Hmm. Pity that one of our own people doesn't fully grasp our ways.
Dragonborn.esm|023FE0: Yes, I imagine it would to someone not of our people.
Dragonborn.esm|023FFA: Don't tell me you believe that rot! Well, as soon as Jorn gets back, you'll see. You'll see.
Dragonborn.esm|023FFB: We don't even know what's inside. And besides, there's... the curse.
Dragonborn.esm|023FFC: See? I told you. Just where the map said it would be.
Dragonborn.esm|024032: Have you put those thieving bandits to the sword?
Dragonborn.esm|0240D6: Miraak? The name sounds familiar... Or does it? I can't remember.
Dragonborn.esm|0240D7: Why do I recognize that name? It sounds so familiar... Did I have a dream about someone named Miraak?
Dragonborn.esm|0240D8: I don't think so, though it sounds like a name I've heard before...
Dragonborn.esm|0240D9: I think so, but I can't remember how I know him. Or maybe I'm wrong. I'm not sure.
Dragonborn.esm|0240DA: That's not someone who lives in Raven Rock, I don't think... but it sounds so familiar.
Dragonborn.esm|0240DB: Miraak... I'm sure I've heard that name before. Haven't I? Was it in a dream?
Dragonborn.esm|0240DC: Do I? I was going to say no, but somehow I feel like I do. Does that make any sense?
Dragonborn.esm|0240DD: I think so, but from where? I can't recall.
Dragonborn.esm|0240DE: I recognize the name, but... I don't know how I know it. It sounds so familiar.
Dragonborn.esm|0240DF: No, I... Wait. Maybe. I don't know how I know that name...
Dragonborn.esm|0240E0: I think so, but I'm not sure where I heard the name.
Dragonborn.esm|0240E1: I think maybe I had a dream about someone with that name...
Dragonborn.esm|0240E2: Did I know someone by that name? Someone... Someone that wanted something...
Dragonborn.esm|0240E3: No. Wait... I recognize the name, but I'm not sure why.
Dragonborn.esm|0240E4: Miraak... It sounds very familiar. Not someone here, but... I'm not sure I can recall.
Dragonborn.esm|0240E5: Hmm. Sounds like something from a dream, and yet it might be more. Strange.
Dragonborn.esm|0240E6: I might know someone. Or maybe not. I can't remember...
Dragonborn.esm|0240E7: Why does that sound so familiar? There's no one here by that name, and yet I swear I've heard it.
Dragonborn.esm|0240E8: I... I'm unsure. I swear I know the name, but cannot place it.
Dragonborn.esm|0240E9: Do I? What a strange thing. It sounds so familiar, and yet I cannot place the name.
Dragonborn.esm|0240EB: I'm not sure. Perhaps I'm just remembering something wrong.
Dragonborn.esm|0240EC: I don't know. I keep thinking of the Earth Stone... Something to do with that.
Dragonborn.esm|0240ED: I hear that name, and I see a temple, here on Solstheim. What could that mean?
Dragonborn.esm|0240EE: I'm not sure. I think so, but the only thing that comes to mind is a shrine near the Earth Stone.
Dragonborn.esm|0240EF: Maybe... I can't help but think of some sort of temple here on the island. Why would that be?
Dragonborn.esm|0240F0: I just can't be sure. I... I don't think I like talking about this.
Dragonborn.esm|0240F1: No, I'm sure of that. And yet... I think I had a dream of a temple, and he was there.
Dragonborn.esm|0240F2: It's difficult... Something about it doesn't feel right. A... A temple, here on the island. That's all I can recall.
Dragonborn.esm|0240F3: It's so strange. I feel like it's from a dream. Something about the Earth Stone, and a shrine... That's it. That's all I can think of.
Dragonborn.esm|0240F4: I... I'm not sure. I don't want to talk about this.
Dragonborn.esm|0240F5: I don't know why, but I'm thinking about a temple, here on the island. Maybe it was just a bad dream.
Dragonborn.esm|02410D: You have no idea how good it is to be back here. Thanks for your help, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|02410E: The place still has some of the Riekling stink on it, but that'll wash out in time.
Dragonborn.esm|02410F: I definitely missed the forge. I wonder if it missed me.
Dragonborn.esm|024110: Finally, I can get some sleep at night.
Dragonborn.esm|024112: I hate most of the travelers that come through here, but you've shown yourself as one of the better ones.
Dragonborn.esm|024113: You planning on sticking around for a while? Think about ways to make yourself useful.
Dragonborn.esm|024115: Just warming up for a bit here, before I head out and take some revenge on those Rieklings.
Dragonborn.esm|024116: Just one more mug of mead, and then I'm off!
Dragonborn.esm|024117: Once Halbarn's done sharpening my blade, those Rieklings are going to pay...
Dragonborn.esm|024118: Make it quick. I won't be here much longer.
Dragonborn.esm|024119: I was able to salvage most of the latest batch of mead. The rest was... fouled.
Dragonborn.esm|02411A: Thanks for having my back, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|02411B: Can't talk too much, I'm afraid.
Dragonborn.esm|02455D: That's right, walk away while I'm still inclined to let you.
Dragonborn.esm|02455E: So you know my purpose here, then? I suppose you must have found that dullard of a blacksmith. Unfortunately for you, my mission here is also a secret. To protect it, I have no choice but to silence you. Now, give me one good reason that I should not kill you where you stand.
Dragonborn.esm|02455F: I might not trust you, but I will trust your greed. Very well, take the map. Return here with Stalhrim weapons and armor, and I will pay you a fair price for them. Tell the blacksmith whatever lie you will. We have no further need of him.
Dragonborn.esm|024560: You expect me to just hand you the map, so you can deliver it to the blacksmith and laugh at the foolish Thalmor you so easily outwitted? You have made a grave miscalculation, and now you will pay the price!
Dragonborn.esm|024561: And you will sell them to me. I suppose I'll have to give you the map first?
Dragonborn.esm|024562: You are interfering with official Thalmor business. You will leave immediately if you value your well-being.
Dragonborn.esm|024563: Get out of here, before I lose my patience.
Dragonborn.esm|024564: Leave at once! Do not test me, or I promise that you will regret it.
Dragonborn.esm|024565: Here to make me an offer, then? Very well, what do you have in mind?
Dragonborn.esm|024566: I admit, we are not making the progress I'd hoped. Perhaps you are right, this venture might be more trouble than it's worth. Very well, you have convinced me. We will depart the island shortly and seek our weapons elsewhere.
Dragonborn.esm|024567: You dare to question me? That insult will be your last!
Dragonborn.esm|024568: I can see that is no idle threat. Very well, we will depart the island once we have loaded the ship. No weapon is worth dying for, not even a weapon forged from Stalhrim. Here, give this map to the blacksmith. I have no further need of it.
Dragonborn.esm|024569: You dare threaten me? For that insolence, I will flay the flesh from your bones!
Dragonborn.esm|02477D: I've been sailing the Northern Maiden across the Sea of Ghosts for nearly twenty years now.
Dragonborn.esm|02477E: The Dunmer have been growing food in the ashlands for thousands of years. It was born out of the necessity to survive in the harsh climate of our homeland. And now, with the constant eruptions of the Red Mountain, it's more important than ever.
Dragonborn.esm|02477F: Solsthiem is still rich in ebony ore. I know there's more down there... I can smell it. I come from three generations of miners, and I'm telling you that the East Empire Company was wrong. They just didn't want to spend the coin trying to find it.
Dragonborn.esm|024781: There's actually a funny story behind the name, friend. A few years ago, I was walking down by the docks and I noticed a Dunmer staggering along with a bottle of sujamma in hand. He was one of my patrons, and liked to drink heavily, so I wasn't exactly surprised.
Dragonborn.esm|024782: In all honesty, it came down to a disagreement between what I believe and what Elder Othreloth believes. I'd prefer not to get into the details, but let's just say our opinions about the current status of the Tribunal vary widely.
Dragonborn.esm|024783: Those cowards left decades ago. Every single damned one of them. The moment the ebony mine dried up, they pulled up stakes. Ridiculous!
Dragonborn.esm|024784: Far from it. In fact, I'm no longer welcome there.
Dragonborn.esm|024785: I had a friend over there, a dark elf named Vanryth... a very talented armorsmith. Spent a lot of time with the guy swapping smithing techniques. Learned a heck of a lot, including how to repair bonemold. After he moved on to greener pastures, I decided to pack up, move out here and put those lessons to the test. Been here ever since.
Dragonborn.esm|024787: As you can imagine, the eruption of the Red Mountain took an awful toll on Solstheim. Hunting for game has been scarce, and the waters are apparently too tainted for fishing. According to Councilor Morvayn, my husband's been Raven Rock's most important food source for decades.
Dragonborn.esm|024788: It was. After refusing to allow anyone to bring him food, Councilor Morvayn ordered his door to be knocked down before Crescius starved to death. I was a Temple priest at the time, and they had me use my healing arts to restore strength to his ailing body. I stayed for a while and cared for him, then... I suppose we fell in love.
Dragonborn.esm|024789: A fine question, and the one that I hear most often from visitors to our town. I wish I had a more romantic tale to tell, but I was simply seeking my fortune and chose Raven Rock to ply my trade. Besides, knowing how to repair bonemold armor wasn't very useful in Riften.
Dragonborn.esm|02478A: Gjalund over at the docks brings me most of my supplies, and the rest comes from bartering with the people in town. Oh, Glover Mallory gets me a few things once in a while. He usually has the most amazing deals that I can't pass up.
Dragonborn.esm|02478F: It wasn't long before we became homesick... we were the only Dunmer in Morthal. When word reached us that a settlement of Dunmer was forming on Solstheim, we were thrilled. It's been difficult, but I'm not regretting it one bit. In fact, I'm glad we've been able to stay and help.
Dragonborn.esm|024790: Our home has been in his family for over two hundred years. Leaving would have been like abandoning precious memories. Instead, he stayed on Solstheim and spent most of his life prospecting for a new ebony source. Sadly, it never surfaced. A decade ago, this drove him into a deep depression. He shut himself inside his home and refused to come out.
Dragonborn.esm|024792: The question you should be asking is "what don't I sell here" because I have pretty much any supplies you might need. If it's not a weapon, potion or armor, then it's likely that I have it.
Dragonborn.esm|024794: The Dunmer race doesn't bury their dead, or leave them to rot inside a stone sarcophagus. When a Dunmer passes, his body is given to fire, so he might return to the ash from whence he came. The Dunmer don't believe that death is the end, we believe that it's the beginning.
Dragonborn.esm|024796: Bah, you're just like the rest of the folks around here. Mark my words... the East Empire Company will return to Raven Rock, and when they do, I'll be at the top of their list.
Dragonborn.esm|024797: Sotha Sil, Almalexia and Vivec represent what was... beings who won the Dunmer people's hearts with their actions, but blinded them just the same. They were aberrations, false prophets. They used their status as heroes to shield the True Tribunal from our minds. But now that they've been destroyed, the light can finally shine upon the House of Reclamations.
Dragonborn.esm|024798: After we left Morrowind, we found ourselves in Skyrim. We wandered around a bit and ended up in Morthal where we took temporary residence with Lami, the town's alchemist. I was fascinated by her work, and over a period of a few years she taught me quite a bit.
Dragonborn.esm|02479A: Well, the bottle broke on the netch, and it must have swallowed its contents because it started floating sort of tilted. Finally, it stopped and began to quiver. A few moments later, the foulest liquid to ever assail your nostrils spewed from its maw. I've seen plenty of drunks in my day, and I'll tell you right now that netch was sick.
Dragonborn.esm|02479B: The Reclamations are the True Tribunal... the daedra that consecrate the Dunmer people: Azura, Mephala and Boethia. We call them the True Tribunal because they have reclaimed their place in our hearts from the false Tribunal that came before.
Dragonborn.esm|02479C: No. Only the Telvanni have the ability to weave living growth from the ash. This simply takes the right ingredients, the right tools and a lot of patience.
Dragonborn.esm|02479D: To understand how we met one another, you have to appreciate what he's going through. Crescius is a third-generation miner... all his life, he's lived and breathed nothing else. By the time Crescius came of age, the ebony mine in Raven Rock was shut down. He took that quite hard.
Dragonborn.esm|02479E: We lived in a settlement perhaps a league from the border of Black Marsh, the homeland of the Argonians. Even though the Argonian Invasion ended a long time ago, there are still a few scale-skin clans that live within our borders. To put it simply, they attacked our settlement and slaughtered almost everyone. It was horrible.
Dragonborn.esm|02479F: Oh, absolutely. In fact, I've corresponded with Vittoria Vici herself more than once. You know, the lady who runs the East Empire Company Warehouse in Solitude... the biggest warehouse they have in Skyrim.
Dragonborn.esm|0247A0: Ah, but you see this particular Dunmer was as naked as the day he was born and singing badly at the top of his lungs. As I'm watching this spectacle, a netch floats over the Bulwark towards him. The man looks straight up at the netch... yells "have a drink" and tosses the bottle... and it never hit the ground.
Dragonborn.esm|0247A1: Not at all. I used to fish the waters around Solstheim. Made a damn fine living at it, too. But over the years, the ash from Red Mountain poisoned the sea up there and the fishing became difficult. If it weren't for my supply runs to Raven Rock, I'd have likely sold my ship by now and moved back to Riften.
Dragonborn.esm|0247A2: You stuck in Raven Rock too? Horrible isn't it.
Dragonborn.esm|0247A3: For all I know, I might be the last Imperial left on Solstheim.
Dragonborn.esm|0247A5: Bad? What if I told you that you're the first person that's even considered looking at my wares today. I've half a mind to contact my friends at the East Empire Company and see if they need an extra hand in Windhelm.
Dragonborn.esm|0248D4: Are you here to rescue me?
Dragonborn.esm|0248D5: By the All-Maker, I hope you are here to release me.
Dragonborn.esm|0248D6: I am a prisoner here. Please, you must help me.
Dragonborn.esm|0248D7: You will find the elf ship at anchor along the northern coast of the island. The one named Ancarion will have the map.
Dragonborn.esm|0248D8: I am truly in your debt, my friend. I did not think the elves would keep me alive for much longer.
Dragonborn.esm|0248D9: I am glad to be free of those elves. I have never seen such greed and treachery in a creature before.
Dragonborn.esm|0248DB: It was very strange. Was I... building something? A shrine, down on the beach near the Earth Stone? I'm not sure.
Dragonborn.esm|0248DC: Maybe I'm just confused. I can't be sure.
Dragonborn.esm|0248DD: I don't know... I feel strange just thinking about it. A temple, maybe? Here on the island? Something about a temple...
Dragonborn.esm|0248DE: I can't be sure. I know I've heard that name, but it... it doesn't feel real. Does that make any sense?
Dragonborn.esm|0248DF: I remember a temple on Solstheim, and working to build something. It... it was not a pleasant dream.
Dragonborn.esm|024AA1: Who do you think brought Septimus here? Who do you think protected you on your journey to open the box and loose my knowledge on this world? Your free will is an illusion. Whether you acknowledge me or not is your own business. But I will be in your mind.
Dragonborn.esm|024C34: Certainly not! Doing so would interfere with whatever is going on, and I would be unable to see how this all turns out.
Dragonborn.esm|024C35: Building something, clearly. And yet they don't seem to have much to say about it. I'm very interested to find out what happens when they finish.
Dragonborn.esm|024C36: What brings you to Raven Rock?
Dragonborn.esm|024C9F: Hmm. Still no steam. You didn't remove any of the cubes downstairs, did you?
Dragonborn.esm|024CA0: If you removed any cubes downstairs, you'd better go put them back.
Dragonborn.esm|024CA1: The boilers won't work if they're flooded, you know.
Dragonborn.esm|024CA2: All four boilers need to be working to open the casing. Are you sure you didn't remove a cube from one of the boilers?
Dragonborn.esm|024CA3: The last time I was here, I only explored a small part of the ruins.
Dragonborn.esm|024DB4: Thank you, wanderer. That would be most kind. Here, this key should open the door to the tomb.
Dragonborn.esm|024DB5: That would be splendid! I'd be more than happy to give you something for the effort.
Dragonborn.esm|024DB6: It can be. Even the most experienced warriors can fall prey to petty distractions. They usually didn't amount to much, but they make my job that much harder.
Dragonborn.esm|024DB7: Those foul vermin... the abominations we call ash spawn have risen from the ashes of our own ancestors. They're befouling the memories of our forefathers by defiling their remains.
Dragonborn.esm|024DB8: Yes, I'm proud to say that I grow or harvest most of it myself. I only wish I had a better source for netch jelly. Those creatures are difficult to track down since it's so dangerous beyond the Bulwark.
Dragonborn.esm|024DB9: Good, this will help me keep things running without having to worry about a bunch of soldiers falling asleep on duty. Here, take this for your trouble.
Dragonborn.esm|024DBA: They are where we inter our dead. They are sacred places, meant to honor their passing. Sadly, we've been unable to use the Ancestral Tomb beneath the Temple as of late, and have resorted to other locations to scatter the remains.
Dragonborn.esm|024DBB: They're too smart to have left it in the Bulwark. I'd search one of the abandoned houses on the outskirts of town.
Dragonborn.esm|024DBC: Yes, I suppose they might. Ah well.
Dragonborn.esm|024DBE: I was kind of hoping you'd say that. Here, take these samples of the sujamma and spread the word. If you get rid of all of them, there's something extra in it for you.
Dragonborn.esm|024DBF: Goodness, it must have been quite difficult tracking those creatures down out there. Here, take these. They should help you during your journeys.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC0: Perfect! All that's left now is to go ahead and wait for the patrons to come in. As promised, here's a little something for yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC1: The finest sujamma to cross your lips, friend. My own personal formula that I call "Sadri's Sujamma." In fact, I've just added some new ingredients to the mixture that I think makes it taste better than ever. All I need to do is get the word out that it's ready and they'll flock here for it.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC2: My goodness, you're quite resourceful aren't you? This is all I can offer in return for this. I hope it's enough.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC3: You would? That would be helpful. I just don't have time to do it myself right now.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC4: Here's a bit more. Hope you're not drinking this stuff yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC5: Spirits be praised! I'm hoping you didn't suffer much at the hands of those foul beings. Please, accept this as a token of my thanks.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC6: Well, I have these other ingredients for sale for now. It'll have to do.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC7: Good old Geldis is always managing to keep our spirits up... and our eyesight hazy. Thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC8: What? Oh. No thank you. I need to keep my head clear.
Dragonborn.esm|024DC9: Oh, how kind. Thank Geldis for me, won't you?
Dragonborn.esm|024DCA: Sorry, I'm always on duty and I don't wish to have my brain addled by Geldis's strange concoctions.
Dragonborn.esm|024DCB: Oh, wonderful. Anything to keep the spirits up.
Dragonborn.esm|024DCC: Get that vile liquid away from me!
Dragonborn.esm|024DCD: Sorry, Geldis won't allow me to drink while I'm working at the Netch.
Dragonborn.esm|024DCE: Geldis needs to bottle this up and send it to the mainland instead of wasting his time out here... his drinks are outstanding. Thanks.
Dragonborn.esm|024DCF: At least my father will let me drink whatever I want without complaining. Tell Geldis I appreciate it.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD0: Perfect! I was just thinking about heading over for a drink. Thanks for saving me the trip.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD1: Hmph, he's begging for patrons again, eh? Well, I suppose I could have just a bit.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD2: Well that sounds lovely. Thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD3: Not today, too many things on my mind.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD4: Sure, got nothing better to do.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD5: Well, tell Geldis that Mirri says she really appreciates it.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD6: Always a pleasure to taste anything Geldis comes up with.
Dragonborn.esm|024DD7: I should mix some of this into my next potion... who knows what would happen!
Dragonborn.esm|024DD8: No! I can't! It may be poison!
Dragonborn.esm|024DD9: The day I refuse sujamma from Geldis is the day I sprout wings and fly off of this rock.
Dragonborn.esm|024DDA: There's nothing finer than dark elf sujamma... like nectar it is.
Dragonborn.esm|024DDB: Take that swill and pour it down a well.
Dragonborn.esm|024DDD: Some of my own men have become quite accustomed to a spirit they called "Emberbrand Wine." Despise the stuff myself. It's powerful and tremendously addicting... I've seen men fail to finish even a single flask. If I knew where they were stashing the bottles, I'd put an end to it myself.
Dragonborn.esm|024F93: I suppose you're right. If she wants to spend her life at the bottom of an ale mug, it's her right. Tell her she'll welcome back anytime.
Dragonborn.esm|024F95: Well then, I suppose I'm off to do what I do best. Here. This was something I carried around in happier times. Maybe you can find a use for it.
Dragonborn.esm|024F96: Well, well. Seems you're proving fairly useful for an outlander. Here's your coin. Keep bringing me more, and I'll keep paying you for them.
Dragonborn.esm|024F97: They could be anywhere on the island. The mines might be a good place to start. If you find any while you're exploring Solstheim, bring them here and I'll pay you for them.
Dragonborn.esm|024F98: Have you seen the poor girl? She's a mess. She used to be richer than Councilor Morvayn and she's spending it all drowning her sorrow. As much as I love making coin, I can't watch her do that anymore. I won't.
Dragonborn.esm|024F99: It's an amulet that was made exclusively for the East Empire Company. They gave them to their workers as an incentive at one point... a kind of reward for extra effort. They're no longer being made, so they've become collectible. I'd love to get my hands on a few of them.
Dragonborn.esm|024F9A: Trust me, I've seen my share of hardcases, and she's doing this to herself for no reason. She could be someone again if she was given the chance.
Dragonborn.esm|024F9B: Sure, I bet you are. Everyone's always sorry.
Dragonborn.esm|024F9C: Mining gear? No. However, I used to sell East Empire Pendants until I ran out of them. Popular little item.
Dragonborn.esm|024F9D: You can try... If you can get that stubborn mule to let me into the Netch, I'll make sure you get what's coming to you.
Dragonborn.esm|024F9E: Well, there's morals, and then there's money. In this case, money wins. Tell Bralsa she's welcome here anytime.
Dragonborn.esm|024F9F: It's going to take more than that to change my mind.
Dragonborn.esm|024FA0: That fetcher Geldis won't even allow me inside. Said I was bad for business. If I want to spend my last bag of coins getting drunk, that's my business. What does he care?
Dragonborn.esm|024FA1: Okay, okay. There's no need to get violent. If Bralsa wants to come back, she's welcome here... I swear.
Dragonborn.esm|024FA2: I'm not afraid of you. She's not coming in here, and that's that.
Dragonborn.esm|024FA3: Doesn't everyone pity the hopeless? Look at me... I'm no better than a beggar. I used to be a miner, a good one too. I made more gold digging up ore in a month than some people earn in a lifetime. When the mine dried up, I was lost. I have no other trade, so I spend my time drinking my fortune away at the Retching Netch... or I used to anyway.
Dragonborn.esm|02506C: Hermaeus Mora has always tried to seduce mortals into his service with the lure of forbidden knowledge. Where the Black Books actually came from... no one really knows. Some appear to have been written far in the past, others might be from the future. Apparently time is more malleable if you're the Daedric Prince of fate and destiny.
Dragonborn.esm|02506E: It seems the ancient Dwemer discovered this book and took it to study. I found their "reading room" in the ruins of Nchardak. The book is there, but it's sealed in a protective case which I wasn't able to open. But perhaps the two of us together will be able to get at the book. To Nchardak, then. Follow me.
Dragonborn.esm|02506F: I'm afraid it isn't that simple. If it was, I would have the book already.
Dragonborn.esm|025071: You didn't know? Hmm, I thought it was obvious.
Dragonborn.esm|025073: If I remember correctly, there's another cube somewhere to the south of the main Workshop.
Dragonborn.esm|025074: I think we should focus on how to get up to the door on the north side of the Workshop.
Dragonborn.esm|025075: Don't you think we should focus on finding the rest of the control cubes?
Dragonborn.esm|025076: We need to find enough control cubes to keep the pumps running and to turn on all four boilers.
Dragonborn.esm|025077: Oh, it's you again. Still looking for Miraak? I haven't learned anything useful since we last spoke, I'm afraid.
Dragonborn.esm|025078: We still have cubes to find.
Dragonborn.esm|025079: Let's find these cubes and get out of here.
Dragonborn.esm|025120: Hmmm... No, that's probably not of any use.
Dragonborn.esm|025122: Maybe if we can find out...
Dragonborn.esm|0251D0: Why are you so hard on Elmus?
Dragonborn.esm|0251D2: Just trust me, you don't want to drink it.
Dragonborn.esm|0251D3: No. But you need to come on the hunt more than we need you on the hunt.
Dragonborn.esm|0251D4: I can handle it. Just wish there was more mead, is all.
Dragonborn.esm|0251D5: What did they... do to it?
Dragonborn.esm|0251D6: I... uh, I hadn't planned on it. Do you need my help?
Dragonborn.esm|0251D7: Not everyone was built for this kind of life. There's no shame in that.
Dragonborn.esm|0251D8: Well, you'd better say something. And soon.
Dragonborn.esm|0251D9: Were you able to salvage any of the mead?
Dragonborn.esm|0251DA: Are you going to join us on the next hunt?
Dragonborn.esm|0251DB: It's rough out here. But I'm getting by.
Dragonborn.esm|0251DC: Your stomach isn't my problem. We'll act when I say we do and not before.
Dragonborn.esm|0251DD: This is home, now, boy. Start living it.
Dragonborn.esm|0251DF: Then train them. What are we doing here? We should either get used to staying here, or be preparing to retake the hall. You're having us do neither, and I can't stomach it.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E0: Most of the old brew is still good, but I had to get rid of some of the newer batches.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E1: I... I hadn't really thought of it. I'm still hoping we can go home.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E2: I'm not going to lead them to their deaths if they aren't ready.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E3: Not everything is as happy as you do.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E4: We're not going to be lounging around in a hall anymore, so you'll have to be a little more useful. Can't make mead down here.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E5: I never said it would be. But either way, we have to try. Whether we succeed or not, it'll stir up some of the old blood in this crowd.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E6: Not everything is as dire as you make it out to be.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E8: It won't be that easy to get rid of the Rieklings, Kuvar.
Dragonborn.esm|0251E9: Because he's not hard on himself.
Dragonborn.esm|0251EA: So... what do you plan on doing?
Dragonborn.esm|0251EB: Come on, now, no time for chatter.
Dragonborn.esm|0251EC: I'd really just rather get to the barrow, if that's all right with you.
Dragonborn.esm|0251ED: We can talk after I've gotten the blessing.
Dragonborn.esm|025213: I thought I made myself clear the first time. The boilers in the Great Hall provide steam to the reading room upstairs, which we need in order to open the book's protective case. We need a total of five cubes - one to operate the pumps in the Great Hall, the other four to activate the boilers.
Dragonborn.esm|025738: Bit of a chill in the air down here.
Dragonborn.esm|025739: I wonder how long it took the ancient Nords to build this tomb.
Dragonborn.esm|02573A: Let's try not to disturb anything.
Dragonborn.esm|02573B: I'll have to come back to this place later and take detailed notes.
Dragonborn.esm|02573C: This is clearly in the same tradition as the draugr tombs of Skyrim.
Dragonborn.esm|02573D: We might well be the first to set foot in here since this tomb was sealed.
Dragonborn.esm|02573E: How remarkable to think these passages have gone undisturbed for so long.
Dragonborn.esm|02573F: This place was quite a find!
Dragonborn.esm|025740: Hm, I wonder if I should have brought more torches.
Dragonborn.esm|025741: I can't wait to get back to the village and start writing about this place.
Dragonborn.esm|025742: I was afraid the earthquakes might have done serious damage here, but the tomb seems to be in fine condition.
Dragonborn.esm|025744: This tomb is remarkably well-preserved. Fortune has truly smiled on us.
Dragonborn.esm|025745: This tomb is a discovery of great significance. We'll be remembered for this.
Dragonborn.esm|025746: I can't help but wonder if this place has some connection to the history of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|025747: If only these walls could speak, I would love to hear the stories they could tell.
Dragonborn.esm|02577C: Kuvar, I'm your wife. You can't...
Dragonborn.esm|02577D: Yes, I remember how I felt being there with her the first time, before we were wed. I hope to see you more around the hall, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|02577E: Thanks to you. You've taught me a lot.
Dragonborn.esm|02577F: That's what I'm trying to do...
Dragonborn.esm|025780: Just... leave her be. She'll find her way.
Dragonborn.esm|025781: Just... I can't even look at you right now. Get out of here. Run to the wilds.
Dragonborn.esm|025783: Let's not waste any time, then.
Dragonborn.esm|025784: I'm proud of you, Hilund. You've held up well.
Dragonborn.esm|025785: I don't want your apologies. I want Thirsk to live up to its name.
Dragonborn.esm|025787: Calm yourself, Hilund. There's plenty of time for that. Solstheim isn't going anywhere.
Dragonborn.esm|025789: No, no, nothing like that. He doesn't need to battle us to know us. He's always watched over Thirsk, and sees us from beyond. And in my case, since he's already deemed me worthy, it will just be a matter of him remembering why he blessed me before. Really, all you need to do is watch.
Dragonborn.esm|02578B: I'm trying to apologize. Do you want to listen?
Dragonborn.esm|02578C: And I don't think she needs any help from you to do that.
Dragonborn.esm|02578D: Are we off on the hunt again, soon?
Dragonborn.esm|02578E: Your words carry some wisdom. But if you truly believed in the spirit of Thirsk, you would have spoken them honestly. Instead you hid your shame.
Dragonborn.esm|02578F: Well, then. I'll do my best to keep the hall together until that day comes. Now, look... I understand you were just defending yourself. And because you helped us retake the hall, I'll look past it this once. But I wouldn't show my face in here again, if I were you. Do we understand each other?
Dragonborn.esm|025790: It's pretty simple. Hrothmund's axe is in there with him. Anyone who attempts to take hold of it has their spirit judged by Hrothmund himself.
Dragonborn.esm|025792: Do you know how tired I get of watching the softer ones here grow softer?
Dragonborn.esm|025793: Kuvar, listen to me. You said yourself that this place had lost sight of its past. We need a new tradition, to move us forward. Instead of listening to a ghost in a tomb, we should steer our own destinies.
Dragonborn.esm|025794: Ah. I see. I hope she... fought well. Was it... did Hrothmund recognize anyone else? Was it you? Is that why...
Dragonborn.esm|025795: The burial place of Hrothmund, who founded Thirsk. He brought a group of true warriors away from the soft life of the Skaal, to know the wilderness, to feel the sting of cold and of steel. I know that... sounds a little silly. It's just the way it's written in the books, and that phrase has always stuck with me.
Dragonborn.esm|025796: I'm sorry. The mead... it is good.
Dragonborn.esm|025797: I'm sorry about before. You're not wrong, it's just...
Dragonborn.esm|025798: I'm not harassing her. She just needs to decide what kind of person she wants to be.
Dragonborn.esm|02579A: Are you ready to go inside?
Dragonborn.esm|02579B: I know... I just... I wanted...
Dragonborn.esm|02579C: That's between me and the spirits, Kuvar. You know that.
Dragonborn.esm|02579D: I don't want you harassing Hilund anymore.
Dragonborn.esm|02579E: Don't want to talk to the likes of you.
Dragonborn.esm|02579F: We have nothing to say to each other.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A0: Glad to have a warm place to sleep, myself, but I would have left the rest of these idiots to toughen up a little longer.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A1: Well, let's hope she makes it back soon. This lot doesn't have much patience with me.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A2: We are if I have anything to say about it.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A3: There are more important things than mead, Elmus.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A4: What exactly happened at the barrow?
Dragonborn.esm|0257A5: My second would do well to keep his mouth shut.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A6: My second would do well to keep her mouth shut.
Dragonborn.esm|0257A7: Are you being serious? How... how did she take it?
Dragonborn.esm|0257A8: Bujold. Your second claims that Hrothmund did not yield a blessing to you. Is that true?
Dragonborn.esm|0257AA: Have you tried any of the mead I brought out of storage? It still tastes all right to me, but...
Dragonborn.esm|0257AB: I'm feeling motivated. That's enough for now.
Dragonborn.esm|0257AC: Don't worry about Kuvar. Just be patient, and enjoy the between times, like this one.
Dragonborn.esm|0257AD: And so I feel your shame all the more deeply. When it's time, we will seek you out. Until then, go and attempt to earn your honor in the old ways.
Dragonborn.esm|0257AE: I don't think you know how dangerous it is to say what you've just said. This is a serious accusation. I'm sure Bujold can straighten it out for us.
Dragonborn.esm|0257AF: How did things go out there? Where is Bujold?
Dragonborn.esm|0257B0: Bujold says you served well. Before we were married, I was her second on the first trip to the barrow. Hrothmund knows strength, in this world and the next.
Dragonborn.esm|0257B1: There's more, yet. Don't get too comfortable.
Dragonborn.esm|0257B2: Forget it. I'll make things better in my own way.
Dragonborn.esm|0257B5: Don't worry about me. You and Bujold should get up to the barrow.
Dragonborn.esm|0257B6: We'll clean up here. You go with Bujold.
Dragonborn.esm|0257B7: I'm going to stay here and check on the mead.
Dragonborn.esm|0257B8: A lot of work to do around this place...
Dragonborn.esm|0257DE: With all due respect, those "things" aren't the only problem we have right now. Be patient, keep the door locked, and we'll get there when we can.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E0: Haven't sold much. It's going to be a long time before things ever get back to the way they were... if ever.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E1: I appreciate your problems, Adril, but I can't be expected to run a temple with those... things running around in there.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E3: I'm sorry, Elder. I'm doing the best I can.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E4: Fethis, they doesn't care about us. If they did, we wouldn't be standing ankle-deep in ash trying to sell a few meager trinkets to survive. They just send you those promises to keep you off their backs... you must know this.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E5: Don't worry, my love. I'll find a way.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E6: I did indeed. When are you going to do something about our central Ancestral Tomb? I can't even go down there any longer. It's getting intolerable!
Dragonborn.esm|0257E8: I don't want you hunting them again. You almost got killed last time.
Dragonborn.esm|0257E9: You wanted to speak to me, Elder?
Dragonborn.esm|0257EB: Damn. I'm still low on netch jelly. He might have to wait until next month until I can gather some more.
Dragonborn.esm|0257EC: Faith's just going to have to wait, Elder. I'm sorry.
Dragonborn.esm|0257ED: Milore, I've finally gotten that letter back from the East Empire Company.
Dragonborn.esm|0257EE: Gjalund said our customer in Windhelm wants more of that balm.
Dragonborn.esm|0257EF: Our faith is being judged every day, captain. We can't ignore it.
Dragonborn.esm|0257F2: Elder, we barely have enough men to keep the town safe as it is. I can't pull them off watch.
Dragonborn.esm|0257F3: Have faith. Things will change for the better.
Dragonborn.esm|0257F4: Hmph. Very well. I'll just have to find someone else to assist us then.
Dragonborn.esm|0257F5: Spare some coin for a lost soul?
Dragonborn.esm|0257F6: On behalf of House Redoran and as Councilor of Raven Rock, I bid you welcome.
Dragonborn.esm|0257F8: Welcome to the Retching Netch Cornerclub, home of the finest sujamma that will ever grace your lips.
Dragonborn.esm|0257F9: Captain Veleth, I haven't seen you or your men visiting the Temple.
Dragonborn.esm|0257FC: May your path be free from danger.
Dragonborn.esm|0257FD: Don't stay away for too long.
Dragonborn.esm|0257FF: Next time you're hungry, stop by... I always have plenty of food for sale.
Dragonborn.esm|025800: Remember, I have plenty of potions and ingredients if you're ever in the market.
Dragonborn.esm|025804: Don't waste your life like I did.
Dragonborn.esm|025805: May the wind be at your back, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|025806: My doors are open if you wish to discuss matters involving Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|025807: I'm pleased I was mistaken about you, serjo.
Dragonborn.esm|025808: I appreciate the help you've been giving us.
Dragonborn.esm|02580A: Thank you for your assistance, it's raised my men's spirits quite a bit.
Dragonborn.esm|02580B: May Azura, Mephala and Boethia guide you.
Dragonborn.esm|02580D: Come back anytime, my doors are always open.
Dragonborn.esm|02580E: If you need any supplies, you know where to find me.
Dragonborn.esm|02580F: Keep yourself safe... Solstheim's still a dangerous place.
Dragonborn.esm|025810: Bring your weapons and armor to me. I'll fix it up right.
Dragonborn.esm|025811: Thank you, stranger. Thanks for everything.
Dragonborn.esm|025812: Mark my words. Raven Rock isn't finished yet.
Dragonborn.esm|025894: Here then is the knowledge you need, although you did not know you needed it.
Dragonborn.esm|025895: Send the Skaal shaman to me. He holds the secrets that will be mine.
Dragonborn.esm|025897: Lowering the water level is the next logical step here.
Dragonborn.esm|025898: Looks like we'll need those bridges down in order to get the pumps in here working.
Dragonborn.esm|025899: I know what you want: to use your power as Dragonborn to bend the world to your will.
Dragonborn.esm|02589A: But this is not enough. Miraak knows the final Word of Power. Without that, you cannot hope to surpass him.
Dragonborn.esm|02589B: The second Word of Power. Use it to bend the wills of mortals to your purpose.
Dragonborn.esm|025A5D: There appears to be a switch of some kind here.
Dragonborn.esm|025A5F: There you go! Now let's see what mysteries await us in the next room.
Dragonborn.esm|025A60: I wonder if this fire pit has anything to do with the riddle.
Dragonborn.esm|025A61: Here's another riddle. "Stay your course. To idle is to die." Well, at least it's straightforward.
Dragonborn.esm|025A64: Mind your step. There seem to be some corpses strewn about on the floor here.
Dragonborn.esm|025A65: Alright, lets see what we have next.
Dragonborn.esm|025A66: And two, I'm not going anywhere near them.
Dragonborn.esm|025A67: Perhaps a different form of attack will yield better results.
Dragonborn.esm|025A68: "A sacrifice will bring you closer to that which you seek." I wonder what it means.
Dragonborn.esm|025A69: Ah, splendid! I had no doubt you'd make it across. Let's see what awaits us ahead.
Dragonborn.esm|025A6A: One, the riddle is most likely referring to these flat square stones.
Dragonborn.esm|025A6B: Well, see what you can figure out. I'm going to inspect these pillars further to see if I've missed something.
Dragonborn.esm|025A6C: Now, I'm not sure if you can read the dragon language, but this seems to be a riddle of some sort.
Dragonborn.esm|025A6D: Keep moving, and don't look down! Well...other than to see where you're going, of course.
Dragonborn.esm|025A6E: Now, lets see if we can find the other half of that claw.
Dragonborn.esm|025A6F: Alright, now just unlock the other one.
Dragonborn.esm|025A70: Well, I'm pretty sure about two things.
Dragonborn.esm|025A71: They also have glowing stones of some kind at the top. They look vaguely similar to drawings I've seen of objects called Impact Stones.
Dragonborn.esm|025A72: I've had a look around and the only interesting thing I've found is this inscription here, and a switch below it.
Dragonborn.esm|025A73: If you can keep up with the platforms, you should be able to get across the chasm.
Dragonborn.esm|025A74: Ah! So, that's where that goes!
Dragonborn.esm|025A75: "Continue along the path, don't tread where you've been.." Interesting...
Dragonborn.esm|025A76: Three pillars surrounding a central one. Perhaps they are connected somehow?
Dragonborn.esm|025A77: By the nine! I've never seen anything like this. They appear to be platforms made entirely of magical force. Absolutely remarkable!
Dragonborn.esm|025A79: I expect there's another riddle in here, along with a puzzle.
Dragonborn.esm|025A7A: The inscription must be a reference to these pillars.
Dragonborn.esm|025A7B: I'm sure everything will be fine.
Dragonborn.esm|025A7D: Well done! It looks like we can head in either direction. Since you seem to know what you're doing, why don't you lead the way?
Dragonborn.esm|025A7E: "All men must die, often by their own means." Sounds rather grim, if you ask me.
Dragonborn.esm|025A7F: I'll just stand over here while you flip that switch.
Dragonborn.esm|025A80: Only five more to go! Now let's just hope something good happens when you hit the last one.
Dragonborn.esm|025A81: You go ahead and see what you can figure out.  I'll look around and see if I can find anything of use.
Dragonborn.esm|025A82: Hm... three pillars. Other than that plaque, there doesn't seem to be anything else worthy of note. Let's see what the inscription says.
Dragonborn.esm|025AC9: Galdrus, it's time for your lesson.
Dragonborn.esm|025ADA: The Reclaimations are Azura, Mephala and Boethia. They mean to reclaim what was lost from the Tribunal's falsehoods and lies.
Dragonborn.esm|025ADB: Why do we rejoice that The Tribunal has fallen and their false promises are but a memory?
Dragonborn.esm|025ADD: Sotha Sil, Almalexia and Vivec. Saints who were never meant to be the cornerstones of our faith.
Dragonborn.esm|025ADE: What consequences did we suffer from this fallacy?
Dragonborn.esm|025C80: More of the dragon-tongue. The runes have faded a bit, but I believe I can translate the inscription.
Dragonborn.esm|025C81: Let's find out what's written here.
Dragonborn.esm|025C82: Hmm, here's another mention of the guardian. I can only assume that this is the guardian's tomb we are standing in now. It says that he was loyal, and his reward was an honorable death. Quite fascinating.
Dragonborn.esm|025C83: The writing describes a guardian who defeated someone named Miraak. In fact, if I'm reading this correctly, it says that Miraak was a traitor. Most interesting.
Dragonborn.esm|025C84: Let's have a closer look at these runes.
Dragonborn.esm|025C85: Look! Here's another wall with dragon language inscriptions on it!
Dragonborn.esm|025C86: These runes have faded a bit, but I believe I can translate the inscription.
Dragonborn.esm|025C87: Let's find out what's written here.
Dragonborn.esm|025C88: Let's have a closer look at these runes.
Dragonborn.esm|025C89: Look! Here's another wall with dragon language inscriptions on it!
Dragonborn.esm|025C8A: Aha! Here's another of those dragon language inscriptions! I wonder what it says.
Dragonborn.esm|025C8B: This writing refers to a guardian who inspired both men and dragons. I wonder if the guardian was himself a man or a dragon?
Dragonborn.esm|025C8C: Just let me study these runes for a moment...
Dragonborn.esm|0261F3: Hmm. I'll have to get someone to bring this to my lab.
Dragonborn.esm|0261F4: What a waste of a good specimen.
Dragonborn.esm|0261F5: What...? Whoever did this is dead. You hear me? Dead!
Dragonborn.esm|0261F6: Hey! We got a deader over here!
Dragonborn.esm|0261F7: Ash and ice! Who did this? Come out and face me like a man!
Dragonborn.esm|0261F8: Do you have a death wish?
Dragonborn.esm|0261F9: I wouldn't if I were you. A wizard's pockets can be more dangerous than a dragon's mouth.
Dragonborn.esm|0261FB: They aren't very clever, are they?
Dragonborn.esm|0261FC: Keep your prying hands away from my secrets.
Dragonborn.esm|026203: Am I supposed to be frightened?
Dragonborn.esm|026205: Watch out! My equipment is fragile.
Dragonborn.esm|02620A: Finally. I can get back to work.
Dragonborn.esm|02620C: Are we done? I thought so.
Dragonborn.esm|026211: Don't fight back, and I promise I'll kill you quickly.
Dragonborn.esm|026212: Those boots'll look mighty fine on me.
Dragonborn.esm|026213: Your armor will look good on me.
Dragonborn.esm|026217: ...retire some day... get my self a place on the mainland...
Dragonborn.esm|026218: ...I've almost got enough... just need one good haul...
Dragonborn.esm|026219: ...might pay off my bounty this time... walk into Raven Rock a free man...
Dragonborn.esm|02621A: ...told him to just hand it over, but did he listen? Oh, no... he had to fight back...
Dragonborn.esm|02621B: ...ain't goin' back to prison... rather die in the ash...
Dragonborn.esm|02621C: ...he he he... callin' my bluff when he rolls dragon eyes... the fool...
Dragonborn.esm|02621D: ...just one more bottle... maybe a second one to get to sleep...
Dragonborn.esm|02621E: ...she was real surprised when I pulled that knife from my boot...
Dragonborn.esm|02621F: ...should have known she wouldn't wait for me... I'll gut that filthy farmer when I see him...
Dragonborn.esm|026220: ..."he's got your eyes," she says... lyin' tramp...
Dragonborn.esm|026221: ...captain promised me a life o' adventure on the high seas...instead I'm stuck on this cesspit of an island...
Dragonborn.esm|026222: ...damn wizards... not good enough for yer precious college, eh?
Dragonborn.esm|026223: ...I'd kill for a decent bottle of mead... not fair that they always take the good stuff...
Dragonborn.esm|026224: ...I should go back to pickin' pockets in Windhelm... easy money, less chance of an axe in your skull...
Dragonborn.esm|026225: ...knew a girl with hair of gold, I called her fair, she called me bold...
