Dragonborn.esm|026226: ...ale and mead, ale and mead, 'tis the only advice a sailor heeds...
Dragonborn.esm|026227: What do you think you're doing?
Dragonborn.esm|026229: Yes, yes. You're Dragonborn. We're all very impressed.
Dragonborn.esm|02623F: Even dragons submit to Miraak's Voice. Without that power, you cannot face him. So say I, Hermaeus Mora, master of the tides of Fate.
Dragonborn.esm|026240: The Skaal have withheld their secrets from me for many long years. The time has come for this knowledge to be added to my library.
Dragonborn.esm|026548: This is by his hand, for certain.
Dragonborn.esm|026549: He set off from here so long ago, it's hard to say. Could be in Hammerfell for all I know. If you happen to cross his path, be wary. He was a fierce warrior as a man. If he fell prey to his more bestial side, he could be deadly.
Dragonborn.esm|02654A: A long time ago, a man named Hrothmund left the Skaal to form his own tradition. Warriors in the wild, living like the Nords of old. They would hunt, they would fight, and the mightiest among them would rise up as leader. But those days are behind us, now.
Dragonborn.esm|02654B: Twisted beasts, a curse of Hircine. True bears are noble and great creatures of the wild. But the daedra have no skill for creation, so they befile the All-Maker's workings. I've heard tell of men who, by curse or by heart's desire, become transformed into one of those vile things. It is a pitiable fate. And one that I fear has fallen to my brother.
Dragonborn.esm|02654C: Do you know of the Skaal?
Dragonborn.esm|02654D: Thank you for setting my mind to rest. Now I can hope the All-Maker will show mercy to him in the beyond. Let me impart some of the wisdom of the Skaal to you. You've done me a service; I will do you one in kind.
Dragonborn.esm|02654E: Rieklings. Those filthy vermin... they came in numbers. Waves of the things. One at a time, they're pests, but with this many... And so here we are, out of our home.
Dragonborn.esm|02654F: I didn't take you for a coward. But until we know the limits of our courage, we don't really know ourselves.
Dragonborn.esm|026550: This... this is my brother's handwriting. This was his.
Dragonborn.esm|026551: I wish I could tell you. He had a wild gleam in his eye, more than most. In my darkest times, I fear he fell in among the werebears of the glacier.
Dragonborn.esm|026552: We used to be proud warriors of Thirsk Mead Hall. There, up on the hill. But we let that sweet life get the better of us. We grew soft, and...
Dragonborn.esm|026553: Well, the truth is my brother really died long ago.
Dragonborn.esm|026554: In days past, my brother Torkild and I would share the hunt, but that was... long ago.
Dragonborn.esm|026556: I wouldn't call it that. We've just got a bit of an infestation, is all. Down here to catch our breath, and then we'll be back in the hall once I get the rest of these up to move.
Dragonborn.esm|026557: I... I understand. That's not an existence I would wish on anyone. I hope you gave him a swift end.
Dragonborn.esm|026558: The Skaal is a group of Nords who live up to the north of here. They're always talking about their connection to the natural world. It's a lot of nonsense to me, but they seem to get by.
Dragonborn.esm|026559: It looks like I was right. He was taken in by that... Where did you find this?
Dragonborn.esm|02655A: A long time ago, a man named Hrothmund left the Skaal to form his own tradition. Warriors in the wild, living like the Nords of old. They would hunt, they would fight, and the mightiest among them would rise up as leader. But those days are behind us, now.
Dragonborn.esm|026598: Here is the foul book that killed Storn. Take it away from here. Throw it into the sea. It is evil.
Dragonborn.esm|026599: Good. Although I do not think he will help you. These secrets are sacred to us.
Dragonborn.esm|02659A: Only Storn can reveal the secrets you asked about.
Dragonborn.esm|02659B: You will not convince me to betray Storn's trust. Talk to him. He will decide the matter.
Dragonborn.esm|02659C: You must talk to Storn, then. As shaman, he is the keeper of such lore. I have learned some of what he knows, but it is not my place to divulge anything to an outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D0: Is it just me, or are these platforms moving a little faster?
Dragonborn.esm|0265D1: Ah, yes, here's something. The first one has to do with a breeze, or maybe it's wind.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D2: That looks like quite a drop! Is there something moving down there?
Dragonborn.esm|0265D3: Perhaps there's enough undamaged text in these etchings to tell us the combination.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D4: It seems these rings can be moved.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D5: Hmm...looks like it needs some sort of key. Maybe those half claws you found would fit in here if you put them together?
Dragonborn.esm|0265D6: There you go. I didn't doubt you for a second. Let's see what's up ahead.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D7: There must be hundreds of years of history etched in these walls. Too bad they seem to be too damaged to read.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D8: That's all I can make out from this etching. I'll check the rest and see if there's anything else. You keep trying out the combinations.
Dragonborn.esm|0265D9: Ah, the Hall of Stories. These are found in many ancient Nord tombs.
Dragonborn.esm|0265DA: And the third has something to do with fire. It also seems to mention scales.
Dragonborn.esm|0265DB: I didn't think you were going to survive that fall, let alone those things that were down there.
Dragonborn.esm|0265DC: The second one mentions the the night sky, and the moon.
Dragonborn.esm|0265DD: I'm guessing this one will be even faster. Just remember to stay focused, and most importantly, run!
Dragonborn.esm|0265DE: I'm glad these platforms appear once you finish them. I don't want to find out what's down in that water.
Dragonborn.esm|0265DF: These platforms are definitely moving more quickly. Can you keep up with them?
Dragonborn.esm|0266DE: So, you are smart enough to surrender to a master wizard of the House Telvanni.
Dragonborn.esm|0266DF: I should kill you anyway, but that would be boring.
Dragonborn.esm|0266E0: Get out of here before I toss you out.
Dragonborn.esm|0266E1: Leave, or I'll be forced to show you the true power of a Telvanni wizard.
Dragonborn.esm|0266E5: Does it bother you to be trespassing right now?
Dragonborn.esm|0266ED: You can't hide from a Telvanni wizard!
Dragonborn.esm|0266EE: So you want to play hide and seek, eh?
Dragonborn.esm|0266EF: Does stealing make you feel less impotent?
Dragonborn.esm|0266F0: I'll make you pay for that!
Dragonborn.esm|0266F2: I see you have little in the way of morals.
Dragonborn.esm|0266F3: You can only hide so long.
Dragonborn.esm|0266F4: Some sort of trickery is at work here.
Dragonborn.esm|0266F6: So you chose to day to die...
Dragonborn.esm|0266F8: By the fires of Red Mountain!
Dragonborn.esm|0266F9: You can't hide from a Telvanni wizard!
Dragonborn.esm|0266FB: Get your hand out of my pocket!
Dragonborn.esm|0266FC: Hmm. I expected you to die. Oh, well.
Dragonborn.esm|0266FD: I am a master wizard of House Telvanni!
Dragonborn.esm|0266FE: I command powers you can only dream of!
Dragonborn.esm|026700: The very elements are at my command!
Dragonborn.esm|026701: You are no match for me, dragon!
Dragonborn.esm|026705: I've just the spell for you!
Dragonborn.esm|02670A: Learn the power of the Telvanni!
Dragonborn.esm|02670D: Fascinating. I hope one of you dies.
Dragonborn.esm|026715: Should have known better than to challenge a master wizard of the House Telvanni.
Dragonborn.esm|026717: You are no match for my magic!
Dragonborn.esm|026726: By Malacath's hairy knuckles, you'll pay for that!
Dragonborn.esm|026729: So you think you can steal from me?
Dragonborn.esm|026AC8: I... I can't. I just can't be sure. I think maybe he had something to do with a temple. A temple here on the island. Does that help?
Dragonborn.esm|026AC9: I... I don't know. It almost feels like a dream. Something about a temple on the island...
Dragonborn.esm|026ACA: I don't think so. I'm not... The name has something to do with the Earth Stone, I think. But I'm not sure what.
Dragonborn.esm|026ACB: I'm not even sure... I can picture a temple, here on Solstheim. Must've been a bad dream.
Dragonborn.esm|026ACC: I have much to learn yet from Storn, if I am to one day become the shaman.
Dragonborn.esm|026ACD: For many years now I have attended to Storn, learning his ways.
Dragonborn.esm|026ACE: It's an honor to serve the Skaal as shaman, but I still miss my father.
Dragonborn.esm|026ACF: Fanari shows what it means to be a leader to the Skaal. In time, perhaps I could lead as well.
Dragonborn.esm|026AD0: Nature is out of balance. The very air feels wrong. Solstheim is in great danger.
Dragonborn.esm|026AE1: Then Raven Rock will no longer be attacked by those creatures. Councilor Morvayn was offering quite a bounty for assistance with this matter. I'd say you've more than earned it.
Dragonborn.esm|026AE2: Let me see that. Judging from these writings, General Carius was under the impression that the fort was still active as it was 200 years ago. What turned him into one of the undead appears to remain a mystery for now.
Dragonborn.esm|026AE3: You should bring this information to Captain Veleth as soon as possible.
Dragonborn.esm|026AE4: Excuse me? How can you be so certain?
Dragonborn.esm|026AE5: Councilor Morvayn told me to give this to you if you made it back in one piece... and you got rid of General Carius. Better than a soldier's pay, so you should be thankful.
Dragonborn.esm|026AE6: I had my suspicions that he was undead. How else could he have survived for over 200 years? It's a shame. There are quite a few tales of General Carius's exploits, including the founding of Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|026AF3: Have you tried to walk on the plates without stepping on the same one twice?
Dragonborn.esm|026AF4: Well, there's a warrior, an archer and a mage. Perhaps each statue is meant to represent a different method of combat.
Dragonborn.esm|026AF5: Hmm... Perhaps something is meant to go into the fire?
Dragonborn.esm|026AF6: If that fire has remained lit for all this time, there must be magic at work in it.
Dragonborn.esm|026AF7: Perhaps something is meant to go into the fire. But what could it be? Something that's already nearby, perhaps?
Dragonborn.esm|026AF8: This fire seems significant. I'm sure it's important for some reason.
Dragonborn.esm|026AF9: These statues are clearly the focus of this area. There must be some way to interact with them.
Dragonborn.esm|026AFA: I'd wager that the statues are the key to opening the chamber beyond.
Dragonborn.esm|026AFB: Interesting that these statues are different. If they represent archery, magic and swordsmanship, then what does that tell us?
Dragonborn.esm|026AFC: No doubt there's some connection between these pressure plates and opening the next chamber.
Dragonborn.esm|026B3E: Is that an Amulet of Mara? I'm surprised someone like you isn't spoken for.
Dragonborn.esm|026B5B: You should arrange the ceremony at the Temple of Mara right away. I can't wait.
Dragonborn.esm|026C6B: All my life. She's my big sister. Don't worry, most people can't tell we're related. But... she's always trying to be the one in charge, and feeling terrible when something goes wrong.
Dragonborn.esm|026C6C: Kuvar. He thinks we've all gotten a little too soft. I can see what he means looking at Elmus, but the rest of us still have our edge. At least, I think so. And... don't tell Elmus I said that.
Dragonborn.esm|026C6D: You don't know the half of it. Bujold's just embarrassed, I think. Doesn't help that her husband is one of the loudest complainers. She's always been quick to shame.
Dragonborn.esm|026C6E: Longer than I would have liked.
Dragonborn.esm|026C6F: Rieklings. You must have passed by the hall on your way down here? Well, it's been overrun. We're just out here temporarily, to regroup.
Dragonborn.esm|026C70: I wouldn't be at Thirsk if I wasn't. These days I focus a little more on the creature comforts of the hall, though. The food, the drink, the stories. It drives Kuvar mad, but someone has to do it or the place would be a little... cold.
Dragonborn.esm|026C71: I make it. I drink it. I share it with friends. Ha! Thirsk didn't always make its own mead. No, we used to get by on shipments from Skyrim. But those gold-grubbers at the East Empire Company started charging more than their share for bringing it over. We still buy what we have to, but I make as much as I can.
Dragonborn.esm|026C72: I make it. I drink it. I share it with friends. Ha! Thirsk didn't always make its own mead. No, we used to get by on shipments from Skyrim. But those gold-grubbers at the East Empire Company started charging more than their share for bringing it over. We still buy what we have to, but I make as much as I can.
Dragonborn.esm|026C73: That depends on who you ask. I fight. I protect the hall. I bring in meat. And I think I could take on most of the tree lovers up in the Skaal Village. But because I'd rather drink a mug of mead at night instead of training until I drop, Kuvar calls me a layabout. Wish he would just lighten up.
Dragonborn.esm|026C85: In Morrowind, the Telvanni grow buildings from special fungus spores. Master Neloth grew one here to be his home. Don't ask me how.
Dragonborn.esm|027101: This place was a remarkable find!
Dragonborn.esm|027102: This is most exciting, wouldn't you agree?
Dragonborn.esm|027104: Who knows what dangers await us in this place.
Dragonborn.esm|027105: At times like this, history truly comes alive for me.
Dragonborn.esm|027106: Walking in these chambers is like walking in the past.
Dragonborn.esm|0271D4: It is an ancient stone of power, a connection to the All-Maker. It represents one of the aspects of nature, a part of the All-Maker's creation. There are other stones around Solstheim. I fear for what has happened to them...
Dragonborn.esm|0271D5: There are few of us left unaffected by this curse. My father Storn, the shaman, protects them in the village. I fashioned an amulet to guard me against whatever has taken hold of the Skaal, but it is the only one of its kind. If I cannot find a way to save them, there is no hope for our people.
Dragonborn.esm|0271D7: I do my best to counsel my people, but I cannot allay their fears.
Dragonborn.esm|0271D8: So the dragons have returned. Well, the Skaal have survived worse.
Dragonborn.esm|0271D9: We must look to the All-Maker for guidance in these dark times.
Dragonborn.esm|0271DA: The Skaal will one day look to Frea. I hope she will be there for them.
Dragonborn.esm|0271DB: It's no secret to me that Frea hesitates. Her path is not clear.
Dragonborn.esm|0271DC: The elders talk of great cities much bigger than our village, but why would anyone need so many buildings?
Dragonborn.esm|0271E0: Words reached us that the dragons have returned. I wonder if we'll see them on Solstheim?
Dragonborn.esm|0271E1: With the mines shut down, there's no telling how much longer Raven Rock's going to last. Hopefully, Councilor Morvayn has a plan.
Dragonborn.esm|0271E2: Nothing specific, but I'm hearing rumblings of assassins living among us. I think they intend to target Councilor Morvayn, but I can't be certain.
Dragonborn.esm|0271E3: The ash spawn attacks on the Bulwark are getting worse. Captain Veleth is trying his best, but I think the Redoran Guard are overwhelmed.
Dragonborn.esm|0271E4: Haven't heard anything lately, but check back with me... you never know.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F1: Neloth is a real task master. And the tea. The thrice damned tea! I don't think I'll ever learn to make it properly.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F2: Well... I... Oh, fine. Here. This is all I have.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F3: I don't see why. He's your problem now.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F4: Uh... yeah. That sounds good. Half. I think that will work.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F5: How about I don't. You don't scare me.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F6: So gaining the favor of a Telvanni wizard isn't enough? I suppose I'll have to pay you. Heroes. They get greedier every century.
Dragonborn.esm|0271F8: I already told you, Mogrul can't touch me now. Whatever is between you and Mogrul is your problem.
Dragonborn.esm|027288: I stand beside you, father, as always.
Dragonborn.esm|02728A: I am ready for whatever the foul master of this book has in store for me.
Dragonborn.esm|02728C: Father, you must not do this. That book is... wrong. Evil. Against everything that you have taught me my whole life.
Dragonborn.esm|02753D: What's happening? You must stop it!
Dragonborn.esm|02753F: Go, then. Kill Miraak. Do not fail.
Dragonborn.esm|027540: Go. My father sacrificed himself so that you could destroy Miraak and lift his master's shadow from the land.
Dragonborn.esm|027541: I must, Frea. It is the only way to free Solstheim forever from Miraak's shadow.
Dragonborn.esm|027542: You... liar... gah! ... I won't... not... for you...
Dragonborn.esm|027543: I think he knew what would happen. He saw his death in that book.
Dragonborn.esm|027544: Do not let his death be for nothing. Go and stop Miraak.
Dragonborn.esm|027545: I do not blame you. I know he chose this. I just wish he had seen another way.
Dragonborn.esm|027546: Leave me to mourn my father. I have no more words for you now.
Dragonborn.esm|02757A: I hope your luck changes soon, friend. I'll do what I can to keep the ash from burying your investment, but we'll need more to keep going.
Dragonborn.esm|02757B: This isn't good. We only got a little ways in when something managed to wake up the draugr. Before I could blink, all the miners were dead and I was next in line. Don't think anyone else made it out alive. This is what I get for skimping on labor.
Dragonborn.esm|02757C: You think I've got that kind of money? No, I only get paid on delivery. Can't afford to go out of pocket for a thousand septims on this. Takes money to make money, all that rot. There's a lot of it at the other end, but I can't get there without a little kick to start it off.
Dragonborn.esm|02757D: Geldis put you up to this? Another adventurer without a coin to his name? Damnit, I told him not to waste any more of my time.
Dragonborn.esm|02757E: I never get tired of that sound. It'll take me a few days to dig up some more laborers, but swing back when you get a chance. See how we're doing.
Dragonborn.esm|027580: You ever hear of Ahzidal? He was the first great Nord enchanter, maybe even the first human to master elven methods. His best work was buried with him, though. A set that my patron calls "the Relics" are supposed to be down in his tomb. Now, they're old, and they're powerful. A combination like that makes them pretty valuable to certain people, and I happen to know certain people.
Dragonborn.esm|027581: I don't know. I... maybe? It's what... it's what he wanted. He just needed their energy. I don't know! I hope I didn't. But... maybe I... please, you have to believe me?
Dragonborn.esm|027585: Bah. Don't even mention that place to me. This whole trip has been a disaster.
Dragonborn.esm|027586: I... I don't remember. Ever since I got here... to Kolbjorn... I've heard whispers. Voices. Thoughts. Imaginings. They've only gotten louder since I've stayed. Pounding, driving. A couple of times I blacked out. It was... It was just before the draugr woke up.
Dragonborn.esm|027587: With me? What's the matter with you? Sorry, I've just had a lot of hork-faced cowards coming by to mock my efforts.
Dragonborn.esm|027588: Who told you that? Was it Geldis? I told that idiot not to send me any more...
Dragonborn.esm|027589: Well, don't just stand there! Go deal with the Draugr while I try to hold the survivors together.
Dragonborn.esm|02758A: Good. I'm glad I can count on you, at least. I think we're almost to the bottom of the Barrow, but... it's going to be everything I can do to find more miners. We've... developed quite the reputation. But for enough gold... I can do it. Just barely.
Dragonborn.esm|02758C: Look, this can be a slow business. Digging takes time. Come around later, and I'll have something more interesting to show.
Dragonborn.esm|02758D: We've got a crew on it. They're not sharp, but they're working hard. Come back soon and see how they're doing.
Dragonborn.esm|02758E: Aww, don't start holding out on me now. We're so close.
Dragonborn.esm|027590: Then why are you still talking to me?
Dragonborn.esm|027591: That's good. But until we can account for all the miners, I won't be able to recruit more. Keep searching. It should be safe now.
Dragonborn.esm|027592: That's a shame. I'll make sure to let their families know. In the meantime, finish off those draugr so it'll be safe when I bring in a fresh crew.
Dragonborn.esm|027593: I should have figured. Damn. Well, at least we can get back to work. But hazard pay keeps piling up, and this won't be getting any cheaper. I'm going to hire some mercenaries this time, too. Having some people around who know how to fight should... Well, it'll set the miners at ease. And hopefully keep them alive. But they won't be cheap either. It's going to hit three thousand this time, I'm afraid. Have you got that on you?
Dragonborn.esm|027594: "... but I can get it." Sure, sure. Listen, I've heard this song before and I'm already tired of it. Come back when you're not a waste of my time.
Dragonborn.esm|027595: Ah, so you aren't just another wandering vagrant with a pocket full of stories. Apologies. That changes things.
Dragonborn.esm|027596: (Failed) Don't insult me. You don't have nearly enough for this to be worth my time, or yours.
Dragonborn.esm|027597: Good, good. This time, I'll spring for some more experienced muscle. That should do the trick.
Dragonborn.esm|027598: Yes, well, the mercenaries did their jobs. Worth every septim. Of course, they might disagree... the dead ones, anyway. Gods, what a disaster. I feel like we've done this before, but that just means we're getting good at it. You go make the draugr dead again, and I'll keep the rest of these n'wahs from running off.
Dragonborn.esm|027599: Draugr again. I mean... we should have expected it, right? What else do you find in these damn crypts, but waves and waves of draugr? At least six of the diggers are dead. The rest of them ran off, and I don't blame them. You've had some luck with these things before. Do you think you could... go back in? See if anyone's still alive?
Dragonborn.esm|02759A: Oh... I was hoping I wouldn't see you quite so soon.
Dragonborn.esm|02759C: I don't suppose you have the gold yet?
Dragonborn.esm|02759D: Just finish the job. If you aren't back in a couple of hours, I'll start packing up.
Dragonborn.esm|02759E: Well, that's a start. Unless there's some way past that dead end, though, we're still stuck here.
Dragonborn.esm|02759F: Isn't that something. But I'm not heading down there, and there's no way anyone else will, until the draugr are gone. Keep at it.
Dragonborn.esm|0275A0: Well, perhaps there's some hope for us yet. Now, I'll have to go recruit some more miners... it won't be easy, seeing as none of their friends came back from the first attempt. We'll have to sweeten the pot a bit, this time. I think I'll need... say, two thousand septims to hire replacements.
Dragonborn.esm|0275A1: Hmm... it's all here. Well, then... looks like I have a good bit of work to start on. I'll head back to Raven Rock and round up some diggers. Once we've got something worth looking at, I'll send word to you. Pleasure doing business with you, partner. I'll see you soon.
Dragonborn.esm|0275A2: No, please! I don't want to die!
Dragonborn.esm|0275A3: ...I suppose you're right. What can it hurt?
Dragonborn.esm|0275A4: (Failed) You've wasted enough of my time already. Just... just let me be.
Dragonborn.esm|0275A5: Oh, gods... what happened? What have I done?
Dragonborn.esm|0275A6: Look, I appreciate your company, but that's not why you're here. You're the money in this operation. And I guess some of the muscle. But for now, I need you to be the money. So come back when you can do your job.
Dragonborn.esm|0275A8: Well, unless you want to deal with the draugr, this whole little venture of ours is over. Might be over either way. It looked like a dead end before the place started crawling. We could have missed something, though. I don't know.
Dragonborn.esm|0275AB: Damn it, what am I going to do?
Dragonborn.esm|0275AC: ... bah. It probably can't hurt to tell you. You look mostly honest. The name's Ralis. Ralis Sedarys, out of Mournhold. I've got a financier waiting for me back on the mainland. Sent me here in the interests of obtaining some rare antiquities. The "Relics of Ahzidal." But this whole thing became a disaster right quick, let me tell you.
Dragonborn.esm|0275AE: We're going to be making this back fifty-fold, partner. Trust me on this. I'll go hire a new crew, and some brawny types to keep them safe. Come back and check in on us. Nothing can stop us this time.
Dragonborn.esm|0275AF: And now you're back. Great, just great.
Dragonborn.esm|0275B1: What? Are you... are you serious? Well, I think I could make that work.. We can sort out the details of our little arrangement later, but for now, I'll manage the dig if you can manage the coin.
Dragonborn.esm|0275B2: Of course! I don't even want to hear the word "barrow" again. Ever. I'll go to Raven Rock and you... you won't mention this to anyone. If you see me there, just... give a nod and I'll lend a hand if you need it. It's... it's the least I can do. Just... keep this quiet.
Dragonborn.esm|0275B3: What? No! You can't... I have his will on my side! He will command all of us!
Dragonborn.esm|0275B4: You come out to the northern ashlands, you don't expect things to go easy, you know? But this was something outside my particular expertise.
Dragonborn.esm|027813: Those things had filled it up with slop for their beasts. Elmus helped me wash it out, but the fire still smells terrible. It's not as good as it used to be, but I still am, at least.
Dragonborn.esm|027814: Kuvar isn't enough to frighten me off. He makes a lot of noise, but at the end of the day, he wants what we all want. Peace, and a warm drink.
Dragonborn.esm|027815: Enough for now. But I'll either need to get some more barrels or start importing more if we're going to make it through next winter. Last thing you want is the mead to run out just as the fire dies!
Dragonborn.esm|027816: I don't think we need that old ghost anymore. Whoever leads us next can go plead with him if they want to, but I'd rather make a tradition of my own. Hrothmund was a great man, but he wasn't a god or a paragon. We're alive, he's not, and we'll look to ourselves for strength.
Dragonborn.esm|027817: We're going to rebuild. The hall, and ourselves. In some form or another.
Dragonborn.esm|027818: I'll lead those who will follow. And most of these are the following sort. So yes, I think we'll be all right.
Dragonborn.esm|027819: Now that we're back where we belong, I'm hoping we can get ourselves back to what Thirsk deserves. We're colder than the snow and harder than steel. We wrap ourselves in both to remind us of the world we face. At least, that's my hope. We've all learned lessons at the expense of this recent unpleasantness.
Dragonborn.esm|02781A: Sad to see her go, of course. But Bujold wouldn't have gotten as far as she did if she wasn't strong. She'll... she'll be ok.
Dragonborn.esm|02781B: I can't believe Kuvar would do that. To his own wife! I'll head out looking for her eventually. We'll wander the wilderness together. Just have to get some things in order here, first.
Dragonborn.esm|02781C: In the end, she was one of the better leaders we've had, but if she didn't deserve it anymore, I guess that's that. It's too dangerous out here. We can't waste time worrying about having a liar in our midst. Lying is the first step to cowardice, after all. I hope she finds what she needs out there.
Dragonborn.esm|02781D: Think we've got to get back to our ship now.
Dragonborn.esm|02781E: We're already late. Not sure why we decided to take such an extended shore time.
Dragonborn.esm|02781F: When the tide goes, we'll go with it.
Dragonborn.esm|027820: It's a long way back to Windhelm from here.
Dragonborn.esm|027821: Hope our shipments are still good. How long has it been?
Dragonborn.esm|027823: I have friends in Raven Rock, and a few among the Skaal. There's not much metal on the island itself, but we can find what we need, when we need it.
Dragonborn.esm|027894: Are you ready to help me now? We should not delay.
Dragonborn.esm|027895: I cannot leave this unfinished. I must remain.
Dragonborn.esm|027896: Where are you going? You would leave, now, when we have yet to find what is at the heart of this?
Dragonborn.esm|0279DF: She's holed up in Highpoint Tower. Go rip that thrice cursed heart stone from her chest.
Dragonborn.esm|0279E0: Don't get ahead of yourself. All you are going to do is wear this ring and walk around Tel Mithryn. If you find something, then we can talk about bounties and rewards.
Dragonborn.esm|0279E1: And leave my farm? No thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|0279E2: I call upon the powers of the sun, moons and stars. Ildari Sarothril, reveal yourself!
Dragonborn.esm|0279E3: Be sure to bring back an amusing story of how Ildari died.
Dragonborn.esm|0279E4: Why is it always about money? Yes, yes. I'll find something around here to pay you with. Now go.
Dragonborn.esm|027A00: Luck won't help. I need something to pry these things out of my head.
Dragonborn.esm|027A03: Good idea. They might leak out. Don't want them crawling into your head, too. Like me. They're in my head. Right now. Saying things.
Dragonborn.esm|027A04: No! That's what I'm trying to say. The secrets. They're in there. In there deep. Can't get them out. They're in my head!
Dragonborn.esm|027A05: Why won't anyone listen to me?! Listen to me! They're in my head!
Dragonborn.esm|027A06: You don't believe me. No one does. They don't want to. I don't want to either. But I can't help it. They're in my head!
Dragonborn.esm|027A0D: Oh, no. I didn't find it. It found me. Here. I'll show you. See. Right there.
Dragonborn.esm|027A0E: But don't go there. You don't want these things in your head. You don't. You don't!
Dragonborn.esm|027A0F: The Black Book. It shoved them in there. With black slick fingers. My fingers are too short. I can't get them out!
Dragonborn.esm|027A10: The secrets. They're in my head. Can't get them out.
Dragonborn.esm|027A12: I know things. Hidden things. Things you aren't supposed to know.
Dragonborn.esm|027A26: But I'm afraid this is not over. I shall return and take what is mine.
Dragonborn.esm|027DE6: Just make sure not to tell anyone what you saw here.
Dragonborn.esm|027DE8: There's a lot of work to do, still, getting this place back up and running.
Dragonborn.esm|027DE9: It's good to have trustworthy souls in this place.
Dragonborn.esm|027DF6: Can anyone hear me? I am a prisoner of the Thalmor. help!
Dragonborn.esm|027DF8: I have been taken against my will! I am in need of rescue!
Dragonborn.esm|027DF9: I'll be here when you have more Stalhrim weapons and armor.
Dragonborn.esm|027DFA: You have kept up your end of the bargain nicely. I admit, I wasn't sure that you would.
Dragonborn.esm|027DFC: Well, there is one reason that comes to mind. Baldor is the keeper of an ancient tradition, the forging of Stalhrim. It is an art we Skaal hold sacred. If someone wanted to make such weapons, they would have to get that knowledge from Baldor.
Dragonborn.esm|027DFD: Unless you have more Stalhrim weapons and armor for me, we have nothing to talk about.
Dragonborn.esm|027E3A: First I'm going to leave this place, as fast as I can. Then I'm going to get on the first boat to the mainland. Good luck to you.
Dragonborn.esm|027E3B: Horrible things. Experiments...and worse. I'm the last one. The others are all dead now. If you find her, don't make her end quick.
Dragonborn.esm|027E3C: Thank you for rescuing me. That witch was going to kill me soon, I just know it.
Dragonborn.esm|027E3D: We were digging in these mines when she found us. She was hurt so bad. We took pity on her and nursed her back to health. She repaid our kindness by attacking us in the middle of the night. Those of us that didn't die were made prisoners.
Dragonborn.esm|027E40: Please! You've got to get me out of here before she comes back.
Dragonborn.esm|027EA7: We must be careful in these ruins. Traps can be anywhere, and there will likely be many. Miraak was trying to take power here, and protect himself in the process.
Dragonborn.esm|027EA8: We should check these rooms for supplies before heading further into the temple.
Dragonborn.esm|027EA9: We should check these rooms for supplies before going any further.
Dragonborn.esm|027F92: By the All-Maker, yes! That is the very one! I can only assume you took this from Ancarion's corpse. I must take this back to Skaal Village right away. When you return to the village, come and meet me at my forge. I will reward you for this great deed you have done.
Dragonborn.esm|027F93: What do you have for me?
Dragonborn.esm|027F95: I was hoping you'd have more.
Dragonborn.esm|027F97: All you had to do was keep away. What a pity.
Dragonborn.esm|027F98: Did you really think our threats were idle?
Dragonborn.esm|027F99: You will die on this island, interloper.
Dragonborn.esm|027F9A: You should have heeded our warnings.
Dragonborn.esm|027F9B: You'll remember the name Ancarion after this.
Dragonborn.esm|027F9C: You cross the Thalmor, you pay the price.
Dragonborn.esm|027F9D: Did you really think you could interfere with us and not suffer the consequences?
Dragonborn.esm|027F9E: We will have that Stalhrim, and there's nothing you can do to stop us!
Dragonborn.esm|027F9F: Who sent you to stop us? It hardly matters, you're dead anyway.
Dragonborn.esm|027FA0: You will not stop my plans!
Dragonborn.esm|027FA1: I'll kill whoever I must, but I will see my plans fulfilled!
Dragonborn.esm|027FA2: Now you'll learn the folly of your mistake!
Dragonborn.esm|027FA3: Do you know how many men I've killed?
Dragonborn.esm|027FA4: I'll allow you to leave in peace... this time.
Dragonborn.esm|027FA5: Do not think we are now friends. I have tolerated you, nothing more.
Dragonborn.esm|027FA6: You are alive only at my pleasure. Do not forget that.
Dragonborn.esm|027FA7: I am done with you. Go on, get out of my sight.
Dragonborn.esm|027FA8: You would leave me here? As a prisoner?
Dragonborn.esm|027FA9: Do not abandon me to these elves, I beg you.
Dragonborn.esm|027FAA: You are a blessing from the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|027FAB: It took courage to rescue me from the elves, and I thank you for that.
Dragonborn.esm|027FAC: You have the heart of a great bear, as well as a wolf's cunning.
Dragonborn.esm|027FAD: Get away from here, before I get angry.
Dragonborn.esm|027FAE: You'll stay away from this cabin if you know what's good for you.
Dragonborn.esm|027FB0: This is Thalmor business and no concern of yours.
Dragonborn.esm|027FB1: Trifle with us at your peril.
Dragonborn.esm|027FB3: Meddle in our affairs and your end will be swift and painful.
Dragonborn.esm|027FB4: You'd best show us the proper respect, if that's even possible for someone like you.
Dragonborn.esm|027FB5: Do you not see that we are Thalmor, fool? Begone!
Dragonborn.esm|027FB6: Stay out of our way, or I promise you'll regret it.
Dragonborn.esm|027FB7: Do I look like the sort who suffers fools lightly?
Dragonborn.esm|02811F: I shall look around. We can proceed when you are ready.
Dragonborn.esm|028120: Impressive! Was it worth the effort?
Dragonborn.esm|028121: The stairs are knocked out. I have no doubt you can find a way up. You never know, you may find something of value up there.
Dragonborn.esm|028122: Look up there. I think I see something.
Dragonborn.esm|028123: Take your time. I shall follow when you are ready.
Dragonborn.esm|028124: I do not wish to imagine the kinds of things that happened in this chamber. Who were the poor souls trapped in these cages? What tortures did they suffer at Miraak's hands? Was it in service to the dragons, or for his own purposes?
Dragonborn.esm|02825C: The weapons of the Skaal will never be dull as long as Baldor Iron-Shaper tends the forge.
Dragonborn.esm|02825D: The ash from the Red Mountain has made the land sick.
Dragonborn.esm|02825E: I'm the leader of the Skaal, but it's the All-Maker who truly protects and provides.
Dragonborn.esm|02825F: For returning my mother's necklace, you have my gratitude. It means more to me than you can know.
Dragonborn.esm|028260: Every winter, the ash from the angry mountains creeps closer.
Dragonborn.esm|02851A: The last cube is somewhere in here. I hope it won't require more swimming around in this filth.
Dragonborn.esm|02851B: Try putting yourself back together now.
Dragonborn.esm|02851C: I could almost admire these Dwemer machines if they weren't so annoying.
Dragonborn.esm|02851D: Another one for the scrap heap of history.
Dragonborn.esm|02851F: There seems to be an inexhaustible supply of these Dwemer contraptions.
Dragonborn.esm|028520: I think they may be rebuilding themselves while we're not looking.
Dragonborn.esm|028521: Dagon's eyeballs, but you're an ugly one, aren't you?
Dragonborn.esm|028522: Dwemer mechanisms have no sense of self preservation.
Dragonborn.esm|028523: I admire the Dwemer skill but it can get tiresome.
Dragonborn.esm|028524: We'll need to get all three of these bridges down in order to reach the pump control.
Dragonborn.esm|028529: You are trespassing here. I strongly suggest you move along.
Dragonborn.esm|0285A2: Good, I could use a pair of extra hands. We were searching the farm when the ash spawn ambushed us. Have a look around and see if you can turn up anything useful.
Dragonborn.esm|0285A4: I'll see you hanged from the ramparts!
Dragonborn.esm|0285A5: First I'll deal with you, then the soldiers in Raven Rock!
Dragonborn.esm|0285A6: This will all be over soon!
Dragonborn.esm|0285A7: Enemies of the Empire will fall!
Dragonborn.esm|0285A8: I'll see you hanged for this!
Dragonborn.esm|0285AA: I'll have you drawn and quartered!
Dragonborn.esm|0285AF: I'll crush the life from you!
Dragonborn.esm|0285B0: How dare you invade my fort!
Dragonborn.esm|0285B4: There's an intruder in the fort! Repel him immediately!
Dragonborn.esm|0285B5: You're treading on Imperial soil! Prepare to defend yourself!
Dragonborn.esm|0285B6: Invaders have breached the fort! Attack!
Dragonborn.esm|0285B7: All troops, repel the invaders! Show no mercy!
Dragonborn.esm|0285BB: Dragons. Master Neloth says they are no match for a Telvanni wizard. But what about a mere apprentice?
Dragonborn.esm|0285BC: It's almost time to feed the mushrooms. They like a drop of blood every now and then you know.
Dragonborn.esm|0285BD: Damnable ash! No matter how much I wash the food, it still gets in it.
Dragonborn.esm|0285BE: I'd better check on Ulves. The kitchen supplies were getting low.
Dragonborn.esm|028661: I feel, different somehow. More myself.
Dragonborn.esm|0286FB: My thuri Miraak is the only true Dovahkiin.
Dragonborn.esm|0286FC: Krin mey. You are a fool to challenge Miraak. His thu'um is supreme.
Dragonborn.esm|0286FE: Aam uv dir. Those that will not serve Miraak must die.
Dragonborn.esm|0286FF: Soon Miraak will return to resume his rightful junaar over Solstheim.
Dragonborn.esm|028700: When Miraak returns, all will bow before him.
Dragonborn.esm|028701: Now we will see whose thu'um is the stronger!
Dragonborn.esm|028702: You are strong, Dovahkiin. Rinik mul. But not strong enough.
Dragonborn.esm|028703: Bein Rotmulaagge! What foul Words are these?
Dragonborn.esm|028704: No dovah would stoop to such vile tahrovin.
Dragonborn.esm|028705: Miraak has commanded your death. So it shall be.
Dragonborn.esm|028706: What does it mean? What was that? What happened to him?
Dragonborn.esm|028707: He was a good man. The strong center of the Skaal. If you know how to stop whatever that was, go and stop it.
Dragonborn.esm|028708: These are dark times. How will we get through them without Storn to guide us?
Dragonborn.esm|028709: You will stop... whatever that was - so that that evil does not swallow the Skaal like it did Storn?
Dragonborn.esm|02870A: Frea will grieve, but she will prove a good shaman. Storn taught her well. He knew she was ready to guide us in his place.
Dragonborn.esm|02870B: Storn was like a father to me. To all of us. I do not know what we will do without him.
Dragonborn.esm|02870C: Whatever that was... I trust you will make it pay for what it did to Storn.
Dragonborn.esm|02870D: I don't understand. I thought the village was safe. What are we going to do now?
Dragonborn.esm|02870E: You brought this thing into our village. I do not know why Storn trusted you, but he did. Make sure he did not die for nothing.
Dragonborn.esm|02870F: A manifestation of Hermaeus Mora... that book Storn was reading, I've read vague hints of them, but never thought to actually see...
Dragonborn.esm|028710: He has returned to the All-Maker. He is at peace now, no matter what that evil thing may have done to his body.
Dragonborn.esm|028711: Oh... so terrible. When will these dark times be over?
Dragonborn.esm|028712: It can't be... please, All-Maker, send him back to us...
Dragonborn.esm|028713: I... uh... you'll be missed, old man.
Dragonborn.esm|028715: We won't forget what you did for us, Storn Crag-Strider. Walk with the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|028716: Goodbye, old friend. We will miss you, but do not worry. Frea will guide us well, thanks to you.
Dragonborn.esm|028717: May the All-Maker guide your soul to the next life.
Dragonborn.esm|028718: I don't really understand what you did, but I trust you did it for the good of the village. So... thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|028719: Frea... what happened? What was that thing? I thought the village was safe now.
Dragonborn.esm|02871A: You always protected us. I hope you find peace in the next life. Goodbye, old one.
Dragonborn.esm|02871B: By the gods... I've never seen anything like it... I, uh... I don't think this will go in my book.
Dragonborn.esm|02871C: Return to the All-Maker, old one. The Skaal will never forget your sacrifice.
Dragonborn.esm|02871D: Oh... so terrible. When will these dark times be over?
Dragonborn.esm|028733: Dead end? Impossible. There must be something more! Look around. I will let you know if I find anything in the dining area.
Dragonborn.esm|028734: I have no doubt that lever turns these blades off. I shall wait here for now.
Dragonborn.esm|028735: How much deeper can this be? I had been told that Miraak's power was great, but to have built so large a temple... It cannot be much farther now. I feel it in my bones.
Dragonborn.esm|028736: You have a much better chance than I to make it through these traps.
Dragonborn.esm|028737: I suspected there would be something down here. Do you know those spells already?
Dragonborn.esm|028738: I am not going down there. It would be foolish to attempt it.
Dragonborn.esm|028739: Interesting. This may be worth exploring.
Dragonborn.esm|02873C: I wonder if we will find some answers to what happened so long ago.
Dragonborn.esm|02873E: I do not know what it is Miraak learned that gave him reason to turn on his masters. But his path seems to have been a cruel one.
Dragonborn.esm|028740: Thank you. Miraak took great pains to make it difficult to reach him, it seems. Let us hope that is the last of these traps.
Dragonborn.esm|028741: I knew it! A secret passage in the dining area just opened up. This must be it. Let us move quickly.
Dragonborn.esm|028B31: All of my people have left to work on that sacred stone. It's quite fascinating. Annoying, but fascinating.
Dragonborn.esm|028B32: Miraak was a formidable opponent. I wonder what he would have been like as my apprentice.
Dragonborn.esm|028EAB: Not so tough now, are you?
Dragonborn.esm|029266: Adril... I haven't seen a smile on your face like that in a long time.
Dragonborn.esm|029267: Good. I'm going to send two of the best Redoran Guard we have to take them down. I want you to assist them any way you can.
Dragonborn.esm|029268: Now. I'm certain Adril was prepared to reward you appropriately for everything you've done. However, since a bit of coin hardly seems like enough... I've decided to provide something more substantial.
Dragonborn.esm|029269: Excuse me, Councilor Morvayn? I have some wonderful news.
Dragonborn.esm|02926A: Then we finally have them! All that remains now is chasing them down, and bringing them to justice.
Dragonborn.esm|02926B: What you've done for me... for all of Raven Rock... goes far beyond what I would have expected from a traveler to our town. For this, you have my deepest gratitude.
Dragonborn.esm|02926C: Well, what happened? Were you able to track down Vendil? Out with it!
Dragonborn.esm|02926E: Please, I don't wish to be disturbed while I'm meditating.
Dragonborn.esm|02926F: Don't say that. It's not your fault, old friend.
Dragonborn.esm|029270: Damn it. I was hoping to protect our people by sending them, not end up with casualties. To think that Vendil would stoop so low as to hire those fetchers... I'm glad you killed him.
Dragonborn.esm|029271: They did it to gain our confidence, councilor. They had us all fooled. I should have been more vigilant. I'm sorry.
Dragonborn.esm|029272: Vendil? But he's done so much for Raven Rock... how could this be possible?
Dragonborn.esm|029273: If even one of these House Hlaalu s'wits escapes, they won't just be after Councilor Morvayn. You've proven devastating to their plans and they'd stop at nothing to hunt you down. It's time to take the fight to them, and finish this.
Dragonborn.esm|029274: Vendil, Tilisu and Mirri Severin weren't who they appeared to be. I'm afraid they were here to avenge Vilur Ulen's death.
Dragonborn.esm|029275: See that you do. We can't afford to let these traitors slip away.
Dragonborn.esm|029278: For Vilur Ulen! Long live House Hlaalu!
Dragonborn.esm|029279: Since the Severin family... or whoever they were...turned out to be criminals, their property is now forfeit. As Councilor, I hereby award you Severin Manor and everything contained within. You've earned your citizenship here, and I hope you'll consider staying with us as a member of our community.
Dragonborn.esm|02927A: This visitor has single-handedly dealt with a threat that could have ended your life.
Dragonborn.esm|02927F: I've been trained by the Tong, fool!
Dragonborn.esm|02938B: Oh, I doubt it. Deor has always been quick to worry. Go and speak to him if you like, but do not be surprised if you end up in a wild fox chase. It would not be the first time.
Dragonborn.esm|02938C: This is what I hired you for!
Dragonborn.esm|02938D: The wise man knows when to retreat!
Dragonborn.esm|029391: Steer clear of this cabin, or you'll regret it.
Dragonborn.esm|029392: This is Thalmor business, now get out of here!
Dragonborn.esm|029394: Move along. There's nothing for you here.
Dragonborn.esm|029396: Unless you're looking for a fight, you'd best walk away.
Dragonborn.esm|029454: Please, tell Storn what has happened.
Dragonborn.esm|029455: Do not speak. Focus is essential, or else the barrier may fall.
Dragonborn.esm|029456: Please, do not distract us. This barrier must be maintained at all costs.
Dragonborn.esm|029457: Do not distract me. I must dedicate myself to maintaining the barrier.
Dragonborn.esm|029458: Please, leave us. We must concentrate.
Dragonborn.esm|029459: Do not disturb us, I beg you. We must focus on Storn's magic.
Dragonborn.esm|02945B: Storn will protect us. We will be safe.
Dragonborn.esm|02945C: We are only safe so long as we concentrate...
Dragonborn.esm|0295D6: Ashfallow Citadel's been abandoned for a long time. Be ready for anything.
Dragonborn.esm|0295D7: Be careful, if the Severin's have betrayed us, then they'll be well-armed.
Dragonborn.esm|0295D8: Don't worry, you can trust Geldis. He's been with us for a long time.
Dragonborn.esm|0295D9: I hope Councilor Morvayn will still be alive when you decide to return.
Dragonborn.esm|0295DA: All he wanted me to do is track you down and send you to him. Look, this is important... even more important than wiping out the ash spawn. Please, go speak to the second councilor as soon as possible.
Dragonborn.esm|0295DB: I'm sorry to bother you, but Second Councilor Arano asked me to come find you. He said it's quite urgent.
Dragonborn.esm|0295DC: I'm sorry, I've already said too much. Talk to Second Councilor Arano if you're willing to help us.
Dragonborn.esm|0295DD: I believe Councilor Morvayn wants to speak to you.
Dragonborn.esm|0295DF: I'm glad to see you're well. I don't like sending people off to battle.
Dragonborn.esm|0295E0: Did you find any evidence at Severin Manor yet?
Dragonborn.esm|0295E1: I hope Geldis proved useful. Have you made any progress?
Dragonborn.esm|0295E2: If there's anything at all I can do to help, please let me know.
Dragonborn.esm|0295E3: I hope you've reconsidered helping us. Councilor Morvayn's life may be depending on it.
Dragonborn.esm|0295E4: Thank you for coming. After everything you've already done for Raven Rock, I hate to ask for more but I can't risk Councilor Morvayn being killed.
Dragonborn.esm|029687: You will die. And with the power of your soul, I will return to Solstheim and be master of my own fate once again.
Dragonborn.esm|029688: But now I will be free of him. My time in Apocrypha is over. You are here in your full power, and thus subject to my full power.
Dragonborn.esm|029689: And so the First Dragonborn meets the Last Dragonborn at the summit of Apocrypha.
Dragonborn.esm|02968A: No doubt just as Hermaeus Mora intended. He is a fickle master, you know.
Dragonborn.esm|0297CC: You can't have them! Stay away!
Dragonborn.esm|0297CD: Who are you? Have you come to take them away?
Dragonborn.esm|0297CE: Why did he have to call me insane?
Dragonborn.esm|0297CF: Their mine. The spiders are mine to control!
Dragonborn.esm|0297D0: Maybe I can find more of them. More spiders!
Dragonborn.esm|0297D1: No one can take them away. No one!
Dragonborn.esm|029A29: Off you go. If you discover any side effects, come back and tell me straight away.
Dragonborn.esm|029A2A: You won't feel a thing. But if you do, try not to scream too loudly. My ears are sensitive.
Dragonborn.esm|029A2F: Every time the Red Mountain belches, more of them land here. Look around for impact craters, exposed rock, that sort of thing. You may need to pick away at it to pry the heart stone itself loose, but you look... strong. Good luck.
Dragonborn.esm|029A34: Certainly. The translation was "All men must die, often by their own means."
Dragonborn.esm|029A35: Yes, of course. It said "A sacrifice will bring you closer to that which you seek."
Dragonborn.esm|029A36: Most certainly. The translation was "Continue along the path, don't tread where you've been."
Dragonborn.esm|029A37: Now where did I leave that book on dragon language translation?
Dragonborn.esm|029A38: It's got to be in here somewhere...
Dragonborn.esm|029A39: Curse my lack of organization... I know that book is here.
Dragonborn.esm|029A3A: Hm, I wonder if I should find something warmer to wear...
Dragonborn.esm|029A3B: Let's see... I ought to bring a few torches, and certainly my journal so that I can write down my observations.
Dragonborn.esm|029A3C: Perhaps I'll need some rope. Oh, who am I trying to fool? At my age, I'll be lucky if I can climb a steep set of stairs.
Dragonborn.esm|029A4C: Did you think to escape me, Miraak? You can hide nothing from me here.
Dragonborn.esm|029A4D: Beware. Miraak is strong. He knew you would come here.
Dragonborn.esm|029A4F: No. Not yet. We should greet our guest first.
Dragonborn.esm|029A50: Sahrotaar, are you so easily swayed?
Dragonborn.esm|029A51: Miraak harbored fantasies of rebellion against me. Learn from his example. Serve me faithfully, and you will continue to be richly rewarded.
Dragonborn.esm|029A52: May she be rewarded for her service as I am!
Dragonborn.esm|029A53: May he be rewarded for his service as I am!
Dragonborn.esm|029A54: No matter. I have found a new Dragonborn to serve me.
Dragonborn.esm|029D8F: There is more coin if you wish to claim it. You know what I want.
Dragonborn.esm|029D90: You have kept up your end of the bargain nicely. I admit, I wasn't sure that you would.
Dragonborn.esm|029D91: I'll be here when you have more Stalhrim weapons and armor.
Dragonborn.esm|029D92: Back with more Stalhrim items, I trust?
Dragonborn.esm|029D93: Unless you have more Stalhrim weapons and armor for me, we have nothing to talk about.
Dragonborn.esm|029D99: It's good to see you again, Skaal-friend. By saving Baldor, you helped to preserve our sacred traditions.
Dragonborn.esm|029D9A: Tharstan hasn't stopped talking about the crypt you explored with him.
Dragonborn.esm|029D9B: I welcome you, friend of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|029D9C: Perhaps I'll be the first Skaal to slay a dragon. That would be worthy of a tale!
Dragonborn.esm|029D9D: The others call you a friend, but I still see an outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|029D9E: I came here to study the history of Solstheim, but it's the Skaal who intrigue me the most.
Dragonborn.esm|029DAC: Crescius's great-grandfather worked for the East Empire Company. The letters mention some sort of discovery he was sent to investigate. There's really nothing remarkable in the letters. I don't know what he's so excited about.
Dragonborn.esm|029DAE: He's convinced himself that there's some sort of conspiracy involving the East Empire Company and the mine's closure. We came across some old letters that belonged to his great-grandfather in our house and he believes every word written in them.
Dragonborn.esm|029DB0: That foolish old man thinks he's thirty years younger than he is. He's going to get himself killed traipsing around the mine.
Dragonborn.esm|029DB1: Look, I know I don't have much to go on. I have that unsent letter, and that's really it. But I also have a miner's instinct that's run in my family from before Gratian was even born. I may sound like a crazy old man to you, but I'm telling you, the tunnels here are solid... they would never collapse like that.
Dragonborn.esm|029DB2: My beloved is almost eighty years old and I think the shock of finding one of his ancestor's things has left his mind a bit... confused. I love him dearly, and I support everything he does, but I can't let him chase this silly idea or he may end up joining Gratian in a rocky grave.
Dragonborn.esm|029DB3: There was a horrible accident, and he was killed when one of the tunnels collapsed. They were never even able to go down there and recover the body... the East Empire Company sealed that section off to prevent any more tragedies. Crescius is convinced it's a story cooked up by the company to cover up something else, but I don't know.
Dragonborn.esm|029DB4: Gratian Caerellius. He was an explorer for the East Empire Company. They'd send him all over Tamriel when they had difficulties in a mining operation. Poor man... can you imagine doing all that dangerous work just to end up dying at your own doorstep?
Dragonborn.esm|02A575: Much of what was known has been lost to the ages. He was Dragonborn, and yet he served the Dragons. A priest in their order, highly esteemed and very powerful. Then he turned against them, becoming something they feared. He was defeated long ago, but it seems he was never destroyed.
Dragonborn.esm|02A576: Some dark influence wields power over them, forces them to forget themselves and act against their nature. At first it was only during the night, but now every moment is spent building some strange shrine around the Wind Stone. I believe if the shrine can be destroyed, the Skaal will be free once more.
Dragonborn.esm|02A577: Miraak is behind what is happening to our village, and so the knowledge he has gained as Dragonborn is at the heart of it. You are Dragonborn as well. You too can wield this power, perhaps to a better end.
Dragonborn.esm|02A578: We already owe you much, but I must ask you for one more thing. Frea, my daughter, went to seek out the source of these dark magics. She traveled to the old temple at the center of the island. I did not wish her to risk herself, and I worry for her safety. If you could find her and see that she is safe, I would be grateful.
Dragonborn.esm|02A579: It is good to see you again.
Dragonborn.esm|02A57A: And there is the one who has freed us.
Dragonborn.esm|02A57B: A strange thing, to lose control of oneself. We are lucky to be free.
Dragonborn.esm|02A57C: It would seem so. And you are to thank for it.
Dragonborn.esm|02A57D: You have prevailed. I can feel it.
Dragonborn.esm|02A57E: The air is different. We are safe, which means you have succeeded.
Dragonborn.esm|02A57F: I cannot maintain the barrier for much longer.
Dragonborn.esm|02A580: My strength falters. We do not have much time.
Dragonborn.esm|02A581: Put an end to this evil magic before it consumes us all.
Dragonborn.esm|02A583: If anyone can help us now, it is you.
Dragonborn.esm|02A584: We must tell Storn what we have seen.
Dragonborn.esm|02A705: Then I'll settle down peacefully and never set foot in this mine again. I've been waiting for this moment for a long time... even before I discovered Gratian's old things. I just hope both of you don't mistake my enthusiasm for insanity.
Dragonborn.esm|02A706: She thinks that old age has addled my mind... that I'm seeing things when there's nothing to be seen. I realize she cares about me, but she has to understand that I can't rest until I find out what happened to Gratian.
Dragonborn.esm|02A707: Our home has stood in Raven Rock since the colony was founded. Gratian helped build it with his own two hands. After Gratian died, he left behind a wife and a son... my grandfather, Rellicus. Rellicus lived there for quite a long time, working in the other sections of the mine until he was slain by reavers.
Dragonborn.esm|02A708: Until I met my darling Aphia, yes. I suppose you could have called me a bitter old hermit... now I'm just old.
Dragonborn.esm|02A709: Unfortunately, yes. I only had enough coin saved to send one of us back to the Imperial City, so I sent my mother alone. I wanted her to see her homeland before she died.
Dragonborn.esm|02A70A: Well you know it as well as I do. Raven Rock Mine wasn't empty at all... it's still rich in ebony ore. I came from three generations of miners, and I couldn't get anyone to believe me. Well we showed them, didn't we!
Dragonborn.esm|02A70B: I never knew my father... my mother and I always lived alone. I asked her about him many times, but she seemed angry and refused to speak about him, so eventually I let it go. When I was old enough, I began working in the mine hoping to make my fortune and eventually move to the Imperial City.
Dragonborn.esm|02A809: You've gone far enough. Neloth is a fool to think he could send some low life to finish me off.
Dragonborn.esm|02A885: Where beast? See you, no see Bilgemuck. Go, go!
Dragonborn.esm|02A889: Stab and run! The tribe-kin way.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8ED: There comes a time when everything must change. Nothing that lives remains the same forever.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8EE: How can I serve you, thuri?
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F3: Your thu'um is stronger than Miraak's. Vobalaan in. I serve you now.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F4: Miraak has forced me to serve him for too long. Nahkriin saraan lingrah. Let us destroy him together.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F5: Hermaeus Mora is laughing at us, you know.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F6: Fate decreed that you had to die so that I could win my freedom.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F7: Felling Alduin was a mighty deed, and I thank you for it. He would have proved troublesome to me.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F8: They wanted to use me to deal with Alduin - Hakon and the rest. I chose otherwise.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8F9: You fight valiantly against fate, but I am stronger here.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8FA: I know things that the Greybeards will never teach you.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8FB: This cannot be. I am master of my own fate!
Dragonborn.esm|02A8FC: Beware. Hermaeus Mora will betray you as he has me.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8FD: I am done being Hermaeus Mora's pawn.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8FE: This is the only way, Dragonborn. The only way I can be free.
Dragonborn.esm|02A8FF: You could have been mighty, if fate had decreed otherwise.
Dragonborn.esm|02A900: So, you use my own Shout against me. You learn quickly.
Dragonborn.esm|02A901: You are strong. Stronger than I believed possible.
Dragonborn.esm|02A903: I always knew you were weak-minded, Sahrotaar.
Dragonborn.esm|02A904: Join the Last Dragonborn in his destruction.
Dragonborn.esm|02A905: Join the Last Dragonborn in her destruction.
Dragonborn.esm|02A906: I thought you were stronger than this, Kruziikrel.
Dragonborn.esm|02A907: So easily you betray me, after all these years.
Dragonborn.esm|02AA03: That whole thing with the standing stone was very odd. I'd just as soon forget about it.
Dragonborn.esm|02AA04: My back still hurts from all that stone work. Why were we at that standing stone anyway?
Dragonborn.esm|02AA05: Still can't figure out why I was working on that damned pillar.
Dragonborn.esm|02AA06: I can't thank you enough for setting me up as steward of Tel Mithryn.
Dragonborn.esm|02AAF8: I have gone to some pains to ensure that I am immune to many forms of control. I cannot say for certain which is currently protecting me, but it bears further investigation at some point.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD0F: Ahmik oblaan. My service to you is over.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD10: I have served you for long enough. Tiidi do ahmik oblaan.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD11: Zu'u ni zaamiil. You cannot command me forever.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD1C: I told him not to head up there. Sounds like the bloody fool got what he deserved. Here, I want you to take this key. Head inside my house and unlock the door in the basement. Help yourself to whatever you like inside. I won't be using it anymore.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD1E: You do that, and I'll make sure you're set up right. You won't be disappointed.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD1F: Well, before he ran off, he was talking some nonsense about trying to fence goods to the rieklings at Castle Karstaag. I told him he was crazy to even think about talking to those vicious little buggers, but he wouldn't listen. I'd bet a fat purse of coin that he's either dead or hiding out up there.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD20: A fellow Breton who went by the name Esmond Tyne showed up on my doorstep about a fortnight ago. He noticed the shadowmarks like you did, and I thought he was one of us.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD21: Don't look at me like that. I'm all alone out here, with no one to watch my back. And besides, it's really my problem anyway. I caused it.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD22: That he did. Right out from under my nose. Can you believe it? It's my own formula for improved bonemold. Took me years to perfect.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD23: Eager to make some coin, eh? That's the spirit. I'll tell you what. The Guild's got a problem out here that I've been trying to deal with for years, but haven't been able to handle.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD24: Good to hear. Damn layabout never even bothers to send his own flesh and blood a letter saying how he's doing. Can you imagine? Anyway, I suppose you're looking for work. Well, I've got bad news. This place is dryer than a damn bone.
Dragonborn.esm|02AD25: Well, well. It's been a long time since someone from the Guild's bothered to make their way out here. So tell me... how's my brother Delvin? Still spending his nights at the Ragged Flagon trying to win Vex's heart?
Dragonborn.esm|02ADCE: Suit yourself. I'll be here if you change your mind.
Dragonborn.esm|02ADCF: You've got yourself a ship. We'll cast off immediately.
Dragonborn.esm|02ADD0: They still pay me for supplies and passage and I can always use the coin. Still interested in heading over there?
Dragonborn.esm|02ADD2: I'll give you a fair price. Not many people headed over there these days. If you ask me, I can't blame them. Things aren't what they used to be out there.
Dragonborn.esm|02ADD3: Well, here we are. This is Raven Rock. Can't say I'm all that glad to see it again. Good luck. Maybe you can figure out what's going on around here.
Dragonborn.esm|02ADD4: If you're looking for passage to Solstheim, you won't find a finer vessel than the Northern Maiden.
Dragonborn.esm|02B00D: Powerful and malevolent entities who make their home on the plane of Oblivion. Some of the more powerful among them can exert their influence within our world, and when they do, men inevitably suffer.
Dragonborn.esm|02B00E: Yes, that was a most disturbing experience. But to answer your question, the architecture was strange, almost otherworldly. Given that, and the considerable power it must have taken to affect our minds so completely, it would suggest the work of a powerful mage. Either that, or perhaps a daedra. If so, then may the nine protect us.
Dragonborn.esm|02B00F: Well, not so far. They think I'm a bit strange, but they seem to tolerate me. In fact, they've been very accommodating. Hospitable, even. They seem happy to talk about their traditions and history.
Dragonborn.esm|02B011: All-Maker bless you, friend of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|02B012: I am grateful for your interest in my goods.
Dragonborn.esm|02B6FE: Surprised you've never heard of it. It's an island northeast of here. Has a small dark elf town called Raven Rock and a handful of Nords living in Skaal Village. People don't usually sail over there unless they have a particular reason. It can be a pretty rough place.
Dragonborn.esm|02B979: Mogrul, I thought I told you I didn't want you hanging around here.
Dragonborn.esm|02B97B: Well, then you'll just have to clean them twice a day. Whatever it takes.
Dragonborn.esm|02B97C: Sorry, boss. The ash comes in faster than I can clean it up.
Dragonborn.esm|02B97D: Drovas! I need you to clean the rooms again. I went through there, and there's ash everywhere.
Dragonborn.esm|02B97F: And I'm supposed to run my operation outside in a pile of ash? I don't think so. I'm not asking you if I can stay, I'm telling you. Now that's the end of it.
Dragonborn.esm|02B980: Damn it. I wish you would have told me sooner.
Dragonborn.esm|02B981: Look, we've been over this. I paid you off, I don't owe you a thing.
Dragonborn.esm|02B982: We ran out of jazbay berries almost two weeks ago.
Dragonborn.esm|02B983: Yeah, I heard you before. I just don't care. You won't kick me out of here, and we both know it.
Dragonborn.esm|02B984: Drovas, I thought I told you I needed the jazbay crushed for my Emberbrand Wine. Where is it?
Dragonborn.esm|02B985: Welcome to the Retching Netch Cornerclub, milord. If you need anything, Master Geldis will be happy to help you.
Dragonborn.esm|02B986: Welcome to the Retching Netch Cornerclub, milady. If you need anything, Master Geldis will be happy to help you.
Dragonborn.esm|02B987: We have all sorts of authentic dark elf food and drink here. Geldis spares no expense.
Dragonborn.esm|02B988: Geldis may be a tough boss, but I can put up with it. Keeps a roof over my head.
Dragonborn.esm|02B989: I don't mind working at the Retching Netch, but I was hoping for a bit more when I moved to Solstheim. Ah well.
Dragonborn.esm|02B98A: There's got to be more to life than sweeping and cleaning all day.
Dragonborn.esm|02B98B: I'll give you this warning only once. Stay out of my way, and we'll do just fine.
Dragonborn.esm|02B98C: If you're dealing with me, there's only one thing to remember. Pay your debts or end up hurt.
Dragonborn.esm|02B98D: I might lend coin to people that need it, but I never lend to strangers. So shove off.
Dragonborn.esm|02B98E: I don't know you, and the last thing I want is a stranger with his nose in my business. Go away.
Dragonborn.esm|02B98F: I don't know you, and the last thing I want is a stranger with her nose in my business. Go away.
Dragonborn.esm|02B990: I can tell you don't like me. Well guess what. I don't give a damn.
Dragonborn.esm|02BA0A: No need to be worried, I suppose.
Dragonborn.esm|02BA26: I return you to the ash.
Dragonborn.esm|02BA28: There was no other way it could end.
Dragonborn.esm|02BABB: You have? You have no idea what this means to me... to my family. Reclamations bless you! Well, you've certainly proven your worth around here to me. Please, accept this gift as a token of my appreciation!
Dragonborn.esm|02BABC: Mephala bless you! You found it! Well, you've certainly proven your worth around here to me. Please, accept this gift as a token of my appreciation!
Dragonborn.esm|02BABD: Offer to help and then change your mind because I mentioned the reavers? Hmph, some sellsword you are.
Dragonborn.esm|02BABE: Thank you, you have no idea how much it hurts losing such a precious heirloom!
Dragonborn.esm|02BABF: One of the sailors survived. Poor thing crawled all the way to Raven Rock on his hands and knees. Before he died, he told the Redoran Guard about the wreck and warned us that it was going to be crawling with reavers.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAC0: Yes, the "Strident Squall" or something like that. The damn thing sunk right off the coast of Solstheim. Can you imagine?
Dragonborn.esm|02BAC1: A few months ago, I had sent an important folio to the First Edition Bookshop in the Imperial City to be rebound. They sent it back to me recently, but it never arrived.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAC2: The Redoran Guard have spotted a pack of werewolves in the mountains. The hunters up at Frostmoon Crag might know more.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAC3: Coast has been dangerous lately... lot of Reavers about. I've heard they're searching for the treasure of Haknir Death-Brand, the pirate king.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAC4: The blacksmith up at Skaal Village seems to have gone missing and they've asked us to keep an eye out.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAC5: I heard crazy old Crescius has been sneaking into Raven Rock Mine again. At his age, he's going to get himself killed in there.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD1: Join you? You mean leave the village to journey with you and fight at your side? I'm flattered, but the village is my home. It's very rare for one of the Skaal to leave, and I have responsibilities here.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD3: As shaman of Skaal Village, I serve as both a guide and a healer, as well as a keeper of traditions. I also remind the Skaal to live as one with nature and to honor the will of the All-Maker, so that we'll be worthy to join him in death.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD4: I need to get back to the village. My father will have need of me.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD5: What would you ask of me?
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD7: Is there still something you need of me?
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD8: You're right. I suppose the others can get by without me for awhile. Very well, if you need my help, seek me out here in the village.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAD9: I am sorry, but I must refuse. I am grateful for your help, but my place is here, with my people. We Skaal are not like you. We do not seek adventure, we wish only to live in peace and to honor our traditions. I hope you understand.
Dragonborn.esm|02BADD: If that is what you wish.
Dragonborn.esm|02BADE: Mother used to get so worried when I walked too far from the village.
Dragonborn.esm|02BADF: My son's heart was restless. He wanted to see the world beyond our lands, and nothing I said could change his mind.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAE0: The land provides all that I need. I have no use for your coins.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAE1: As I've already told you, I have no need of money.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAE5: May the sun light your path.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAEC: Strength and courage be always with you.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAF1: I'm sure you have more important things to do. Farewell, then.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAF2: May your hunts always bring you game.
Dragonborn.esm|02BAF3: Remember to hunt only when you have need.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBB9: If you've got gold on your hands, you might talk to Ralis, up at Kolbjorn Barrow. I've heard he wants to excavate the place.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBBA: Your actions here have been a blessing to us all, serjo.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBBB: I don't know who sent you here, but you've given us something to build on here. I hope someday we can repay you for your kindness.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBBC: Because of you, my life has meaning again. Thank you!
Dragonborn.esm|02BBBD: Look at me, I'm a different person. And I have you to thank for it.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBBE: You've brought Raven Rock back from the brink, traveler. I don't know how to thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBBF: It's busier now that things are starting to look up. I guess I might stay a bit longer after all.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBC0: The mines are open and the shop is busy again. Thank you!
Dragonborn.esm|02BBC1: You've got a good thing going here now that you've reopened the mines. Don't let it go to waste.
Dragonborn.esm|02BBC9: Men, an invader has entered the fort! Prepare yourselves for an ambush!
Dragonborn.esm|02BBCA: Our defenses have been breached! I need reinforcements!
Dragonborn.esm|02BBCB: Fort Frostmoth will never fall! Long live the Empire!
Dragonborn.esm|02BC48: Zu'u nis. I cannot do that.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC49: Zu'u nis krif tol. It cannot be done.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC4C: We are here. Wundun oblaan. How else can I serve you?
Dragonborn.esm|02BC50: Zu'u ni zaamiil, Miraak! I am free from your service!
Dragonborn.esm|02BC51: The Dovahkiin has freed me! Now you will pay for my years of slavery!
Dragonborn.esm|02BC52: Vobalaam in! Dovahkiin los nu thuri!
Dragonborn.esm|02BC54: You will never enslave me again, Miraak.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC55: Thu'um se Dovahkiin inhus, Miraak. Hin suleyksejun oblaan.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC56: Drem. There is no prey here.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC58: Krosis, the land below us is empty of prey.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC59: I will look for a spot to land.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC5A: As you command. Mu daal wah golt.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC5B: Yes, thuri. Wah golt. I will land at once.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC60: Well, take a look at what I have and maybe we can make a deal.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC61: If you've got something to sell, I'm willing to buy. Why don't we see what kind of a deal we can make.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC62: If the quality's right, I'll take those off your hands. Let me take a look.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC63: Got the best merchandise on the island. Have a look for yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC64: Just got some of my merchandise off the latest supply ship. See if anything strikes your fancy.
Dragonborn.esm|02BC65: All sorts of useful things. Feel free to browse.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF03: I will follow you, Skaal-friend, but I must return to the village afterward.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF04: You have a companion already. You don't need my help.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF08: You there. What brings you to this place? Why are you here?
Dragonborn.esm|02BF09: We must find out what is at work here.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF0A: I must not fail my people.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF0B: The evil in this place must be rooted out.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF0C: That? It's... Well, it's a shrine for... something. I'm not sure what.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF0D: Yes, I... I'm not sure what to say. Did we build that? I really don't remember.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF0E: The Earth Stone is there, and the rest... It's a shrine, but I... Did we build that? I'm not sure.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF0F: I'm... I'm not really sure. I think it's important, but I can't say why...
Dragonborn.esm|02BF10: A shrine for... Well, it's... I think it's important.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF11: Did we build that, or did I imagine that?
Dragonborn.esm|02BF12: It's a shrine to... I don't remember. Isn't that strange?
Dragonborn.esm|02BF13: I'm not sure. Did we build it? I can't remember...
Dragonborn.esm|02BF30: Our traditions do not speak of them. But they and Miraak are connected. You read a Black Book, and saw him. And the book's power comes from the same dark source as that which corrupted the Wind Stone. But beware. You are now walking the same road as Miraak.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF32: I was here alone then, and I find an assistant is absolutely essential for this kind of dirty, dangerous work.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF34: He also is searching for them. In fact, he has already found one. He showed it to me when he came here. It was very like the one you found in Miraak's temple. A thing of dark magic, not of the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF35: I can feel it. The balance of the living land has nearly been restored. But while the Tree Stone remains under Miraak's influence, the natural order remains out of balance. And all can be undone if Miraak exerts his will again. You must continue on your path, and gather the knowledge you need to defeat him.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF36: Yes. There are six sacred Stones - Wind, Water, Earth, Beast, Sun, and Tree. Through them the Oneness of the land is maintained. Frea saw that the Tree Stone now stands imprisoned in Miraak's new temple. I doubt you can free it until his power is broken. But you may be able to cleanse the other Stones. Even if this does not stop Miraak, it surely will delay his return.
Dragonborn.esm|02BF37: The power of the Stone had been corrupted. It was the source of the influence that had taken control of the Skaal. Your Shout broke whatever evil will controlled the Stone, and restored its true nature as a conduit of the All-Maker's gifts. Freeing the other Stones will diminish whatever dark influence is spreading across Solstheim.
Dragonborn.esm|02C021: Come, there looks to be a way out through here.
Dragonborn.esm|02C034: It's the capital of Morrowind... been that way ever since Mournhold was sacked by the Argonians. I don't visit there often anymore, but we still get the occasional supply ship and visitors from there.
Dragonborn.esm|02C035: How can you have Dunmer blood in your veins and have no knowledge of Blacklight?
Dragonborn.esm|02C036: Your ignorance of our homeland doesn't surprise me.
Dragonborn.esm|02C037: Very good, Galdrus. Your lessons are going well. You may carry on.
Dragonborn.esm|02C038: The destruction of our homeland, the exile of our people and the rebuilding of our civilization.
Dragonborn.esm|02C039: Who were the perpetrators of those falsehoods?
Dragonborn.esm|02C03A: We rejoice as the New Temple rises. We rejoice because we embrace the Reclamations as our saviors.
Dragonborn.esm|02C03E: I have to get back home.
Dragonborn.esm|02C03F: I can't talk now. I must get back to the village.
Dragonborn.esm|02C040: I... I don't know how I got here.
Dragonborn.esm|02C041: Is this a dream? No, it is a nightmare, surely.
Dragonborn.esm|02C042: I can't speak now, friend. I must return home.
Dragonborn.esm|02C043: Thank you, my friend. You have set us free.
Dragonborn.esm|02C044: A fever must have taken me. What else could explain this?
Dragonborn.esm|02C045: I don't mean to be rude outsider, but I must get back to the village.
Dragonborn.esm|02C046: Who are you? What am I doing here?
Dragonborn.esm|02C047: This... this is some dark magic. It has to be.
Dragonborn.esm|02C048: I have no time to speak now, outsider. I'm on my way home.
Dragonborn.esm|02C049: All-Maker bless you! You have saved us!
Dragonborn.esm|02C04A: The whole village is here! What sorcery is this?
Dragonborn.esm|02C04B: I must get back to the village. As leader of the Skaal, I'm needed there.
Dragonborn.esm|02C04C: Why was I building a temple?
Dragonborn.esm|02C04D: I must be going mad. But then, so must everyone else.
Dragonborn.esm|02C04E: Sorry friend, but I'm on the road home. Find me there and perhaps we can talk.
Dragonborn.esm|02C04F: You are a true hero. I thank you, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|02C050: We're the victims of some evil magic, but why?
Dragonborn.esm|02C051: I'm on my way to Skaal Village. Perhaps I'll see you there.
Dragonborn.esm|02C052: Finna? Aeta? Where are my wife and daughter?!
