Dragonborn.esm|02C053: By the All-Maker, please let my wife and daughter be safe.
Dragonborn.esm|02C055: What in Mundus am I doing out here at this hour?
Dragonborn.esm|02C056: This is both fascinating and terrifying.
Dragonborn.esm|02C057: I don't know what spell you freed us from, outsider, but I thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|02C058: I pray to the All-Maker that whatever this was, it doesn't happen again.
Dragonborn.esm|02C059: My road takes me home, outsider. Hunt well.
Dragonborn.esm|02C05B: You saved us! But from what? Is it truly over?
Dragonborn.esm|02C05C: Forgive my impatience, but I must get back to the village right away.
Dragonborn.esm|02C081: Wise choice. Now come along with us. After we confiscate any stolen goods, you'll be free to go. After you pay the fine, of course.
Dragonborn.esm|02C082: Good enough. I'll just remove any stolen goods on you, and then you're free to go.
Dragonborn.esm|02C083: Very well, you're free to go. Next time you might not be so lucky.
Dragonborn.esm|02C086: Stop right there... where have I seen you before?
Dragonborn.esm|02C087: Well, I suppose I could forget that I saw you this time.
Dragonborn.esm|02C088: All right, just keep it quiet. I warn you though, there's still a price on your head. Next guard you run into might not be so... agreeable.
Dragonborn.esm|02C089: Good. I've been itching for a fight.
Dragonborn.esm|02C08A: If death is what you seek... I'll be happy to oblige!
Dragonborn.esm|02C08B: Then in House Redoran's name, you'll get your wish!
Dragonborn.esm|02C08C: Solstheim has no use for your kind!
Dragonborn.esm|02C08D: What in the name of Azura...?
Dragonborn.esm|02C08E: In House Redoran's name, I order you to stop!
Dragonborn.esm|02C08F: You've committed crimes against the Dunmer people of Solstheim. What say you in your defense?
Dragonborn.esm|02C090: Right then! Off to the Bulwark with you.
Dragonborn.esm|02C091: I suppose I can overlook it for now, but you better keep your hands clean or I'll come after you again... you got that?
Dragonborn.esm|02C092: You're lucky the bounty on you is so low, otherwise I'd be hauling you to the Bulwark myself. Get out of my sight.
Dragonborn.esm|02C093: The only mistake here belongs to you. You're a wanted man and there's no way I'm letting you walk away.
Dragonborn.esm|02C094: The only mistake here belongs to you. You're a wanted woman and there's no way I'm letting you walk away.
Dragonborn.esm|02C095: There's no mistake. You've committed crimes against the Dunmer people of Solstheim, and in the name of House Redoran it's time to face justice.
Dragonborn.esm|02C1E6: This is Dusty. I personally trained her from a larva. Feels like a lifetime ago now. She hasn't been in traveling condition for quite a while now. I've tried to let her go off on her own but she insists on staying here.
Dragonborn.esm|02C1E7: From that look on your face I'm guessing you've never seen a Silt Strider before?
Dragonborn.esm|02C1E8: We get by trading odds and ends with the locals. I've decided to stay here and make sure she passes in peace. It's the least I can do for her. Every once in a while a local makes it out here. Although, they usually just want to catch a glimpse of Dusty. Silt Striders are pretty rare now.
Dragonborn.esm|02C3F3: Mostly for transporting goods and people. They were pretty commonplace in Morrowind, but that was ages ago. A Silt Strider carapace is so strong it can withstand the harshest of weather. Even the legendary sandstorms of Vvardenfell's Molag Amur Region were said to barely make a dent in them. Of course, that's all ash now.
Dragonborn.esm|02C3F4: The blast from the volcano wiped most of them out. Found Dusty here in a small cave on Vvardenfell. Silt Strider cocoons are pretty tough.
Dragonborn.esm|02C3F5: What would you like to know?
Dragonborn.esm|02C429: Not sure. I was told it's an old Imperial farm that was wiped out by the eruption of the Red Mountain. Not certain who lived there... it was already a pile of rubble when I arrived in Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|02C42A: You out here for a reason?
Dragonborn.esm|02C42B: Abide by our laws, and we'll get along well.
Dragonborn.esm|02C42C: Report back to me if you discover anything unusual.
Dragonborn.esm|02C435: Show me you are the hero they say you are!
Dragonborn.esm|02C436: I have done all that can be done. There is nothing left. No quests to be undertaken. No villains to be slain. No challenges to face. Except for you. You are my last challenge. Only you can send me to Sovngarde with honor. Make your preparations. When you are ready, come find me at my last vigil.
Dragonborn.esm|02C438: I suppose I could dig up a staff or two for you.
Dragonborn.esm|02C43A: Don't be ridiculous. Of course I can. The question is, should I? I suppose if it's going to be done right, I'll have to do it.
Dragonborn.esm|02C43B: I could, but I won't. It wouldn't do to have you become better than me after all.
Dragonborn.esm|02C59D: Come on. Let's see what you're made of.
Dragonborn.esm|02C8A4: You've brought warmth to my heart, outsider, and I'm in your debt. Please accept this as a token of gratitude. Once again, you've proven yourself an ally to the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|02C8A5: Ah, well, I suppose she'll always be that fiesty little girl I used to know. Of course, when you reach my age, you'll find that the word "young" is a very relative term.
Dragonborn.esm|02C8A6: Ah yes, Bera's silver necklace. It was a wedding gift and she rarely took it off. I'll be glad to oblige. What a touching gesture, to display the symbol of her devotion in such a way. Tell Morwen I'll be happy to grant her request. I am sure it will bring joy to her mother and father as they walk in Aetherius.
Dragonborn.esm|02C8A7: Morwen, you say? Now there's a name I haven't heard in a long time. Left to join her mother's people on Solstheim, as I recall. I knew her parents quite well, you know. Bera and Ulfarr were good people, very much in love. Now, what can I do for young Morwen?
Dragonborn.esm|02C8A8: I came across a few of my mother's old things not long ago. Among them was a necklace that my father gave to her when they were married. I believe she would want it placed upon her tombstone in Falkreath, as a symbol of her undying love and the great sacrifice she made for it. If you find yourself in Falkreath, please give this amulet to the priest Runil and tell him that I sent you. He will understand. I don't have much to give in reward, but return to me when the task is done and I'll do what I can. Thank you, Skaal-friend.
Dragonborn.esm|02C8A9: A trade ship from Skyrim struck ice off the coast near the village, and the Skaal took in the surviving sailors. My mother fell in love with one of the sailors - my father - and eventually returned with him to Skyrim.
Dragonborn.esm|030CC7: When next we meet, it will be at my last vigil.
Dragonborn.esm|031783: You're a fool to test me, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|031784: If I must die to protect my people, so be it!
Dragonborn.esm|031833: I grieve that you must meet your end this way, but necessity demands it.
Dragonborn.esm|03184E: This realm is beyond you. You have no power here.
Dragonborn.esm|031C82: The Ulen family being a part of House Hlaalu didn't matter. It's Dunmer tradition for the ashes of the deceased to be placed in an ancestral tomb. Valin Ulen and his son's remains were both laid to rest there because that's what he'd requested.
Dragonborn.esm|031C85: Vilur Ulen wasn't the first Ulen to live in Raven Rock. His father, Valin Ulen, was one of the first Dunmer to settle here. Unlike his son Vilur, Valin was a good man. He contributed to the community and was quite respected. When Valin died, his family provided the funds to construct the tomb.
Dragonborn.esm|031C86: House Redoran took charge and was slowly able to build an army. It took years, but it became a force to be reckoned with. When the Argonians invaded years later, House Redoran's army was able to prevent them from sweeping across all of Morrowind. It would be fair to say that House Redoran literally saved the Dunmer people from destruction.
Dragonborn.esm|031C87: I won't allow you to ruin years of planning!
Dragonborn.esm|031C88: For House Hlaalu! For Vilur Ulen!
Dragonborn.esm|031C89: During the Oblivion Crisis, gates to the plane of Oblivion opened all over Morrowind. The Empire pulled most of its forces out of Morrowind to deal with the gates in their own homeland, leaving us virtually defenseless. We had no standing army at the time, just uncoordinated pockets of resistance.
Dragonborn.esm|031C8A: Follow me and I'll take you to the councilor so you can speak to him yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|031C8B: House Hlaalu will... have its revenge...
Dragonborn.esm|031C8D: I join you in death, father...
Dragonborn.esm|031C8E: Careful Mirri... someone's in here with us!
Dragonborn.esm|031C8F: Come on, I know you're in here.
Dragonborn.esm|031C90: Watch it Tilisu, I think we might have a visitor!
Dragonborn.esm|031C91: Stop hiding, coward. I'll make it quick, I promise.
Dragonborn.esm|031D64: What... never seen a Redoran Guard before?
Dragonborn.esm|031D67: What are you gawking at, outlander?
Dragonborn.esm|031D68: We've got our eyes on you.
Dragonborn.esm|031D6A: Obey our laws, or suffer the consequences.
Dragonborn.esm|031D6B: Just remember who's in charge around here.
Dragonborn.esm|031D6E: Serve House Redoran, or serve no one.
Dragonborn.esm|031DE4: Blast! That careless old fool. Well, thanks for trying anyway. Here you go... for your trouble.
Dragonborn.esm|031DE5: You tell Crescius he better give that pickaxe back to me, or else.
Dragonborn.esm|031DE7: Plenty of work in the mine, if you're looking.
Dragonborn.esm|031DE8: I hate to break it to you, but there isn't a scrap of ebony left in the mines. You might want to just move on.
Dragonborn.esm|03208E: You would steal from those who have so little?
Dragonborn.esm|03208F: You're no better than those thieving reavers.
Dragonborn.esm|032091: You skulk and sneak like a riekling.
Dragonborn.esm|032092: Come out and face justice, coward.
Dragonborn.esm|032093: The All-Maker sees you, even if I cannot.
Dragonborn.esm|032094: A coward hides. A true warrior does not.
Dragonborn.esm|032095: Another soul returns to the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|032096: Life should never be taken without reason.
Dragonborn.esm|032097: Your heart is as cold as winter.
Dragonborn.esm|03209C: By the All-Maker, no! It cannot be!
Dragonborn.esm|03209F: Why do you steal from us?
Dragonborn.esm|0320A1: What gives you the right to do that?
Dragonborn.esm|0320A2: So that is how you treat others?
Dragonborn.esm|0320A5: It is a shame that was necessary.
Dragonborn.esm|0320A6: I thank you for your protection, All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|0320A7: I am sorry, but you left me with no choice.
Dragonborn.esm|0320AC: The wind plays a trick on my ears, surely.
Dragonborn.esm|0320AD: I thought I saw... no, surely not.
Dragonborn.esm|0320B1: I'm sorry, but you must leave now.
Dragonborn.esm|0320B2: It is time for you to go, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|0320B3: You cannot stay here any longer.
Dragonborn.esm|0320BC: All-Maker, I am prepared to return...
Dragonborn.esm|0320BD: Soon I will be one... with nature...
Dragonborn.esm|0320BE: Violence is not our way, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|0320BF: Your actions show a lack of respect.
Dragonborn.esm|0320C0: The All-Maker frowns upon such needless violence.
Dragonborn.esm|0320C1: I have slain more dangerous creatures than you!
Dragonborn.esm|0320C2: You will not break me, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|0320C4: Did you think I would run?
Dragonborn.esm|0320C5: I don't want to kill you, but I will.
Dragonborn.esm|0320C6: You anger one, you anger us all!
Dragonborn.esm|0320C7: Are you truly ready to die?
Dragonborn.esm|0320C8: My steel will be your death!
Dragonborn.esm|0320CE: Remember that you chose this path.
Dragonborn.esm|0320D3: If death is what you want, you'll have it!
Dragonborn.esm|0320DC: Oh, believe me... I'd be on the first ship off of this miserable island if I could. But I'm not sure I could do that to my father. I think it would break his heart. We've already lost my mother, I don't want to leave him alone.
Dragonborn.esm|0320DD: Don't like it? I hate it! Every day it's the same thing. We open the shop, we hardly sell a thing, we close the shop and go to sleep. What kind of a life is that?
Dragonborn.esm|032146: Gjalund. I was starting to wonder what happened to you.
Dragonborn.esm|032147: I don't recognize you, so I'll assume this is your first visit to Raven Rock, outlander. State your intentions.
Dragonborn.esm|032148: All right, Adril. Don't worry about rushing it. Just pay me when you can.
Dragonborn.esm|032149: We, uh... we were delayed by bad weather.
Dragonborn.esm|03214A: After all these years, they're gouging us for every last drake we have. Let me talk to Lleril. I'll see what we can do.
Dragonborn.esm|03214B: Look, the East Empire Company didn't give me a choice. They've raised their prices again, and there's nothing I can do about it.
Dragonborn.esm|03214C: Damn it, Gjalund. You know we don't have that much coin.
Dragonborn.esm|03214D: This load cost me double what we had agreed on. Nothing I can do about it.
Dragonborn.esm|03214F: Before you even ask... yes, I have the supplies you requested. But...
Dragonborn.esm|032164: If you tire of the search, read your book again to return to your mortal life. For a time. The lure of Apocrypha will call you back. It is your fate.
Dragonborn.esm|032165: Perhaps you are a fool or a coward. If so, you are in peril. Read your book again and escape before Apocrypha claims you forever.
Dragonborn.esm|032166: Sate your thirst for knowledge in the endless stacks of my library.
Dragonborn.esm|032167: Perhaps you will prove clever enough to uncover the secrets hidden here. If so, welcome.
Dragonborn.esm|032168: I know you, champion. The Oghma Infinium was only the beginning.
Dragonborn.esm|032169: So, another seeker after knowledge enters my realm.
Dragonborn.esm|03216A: This is Apocrypha, where all knowledge is hoarded.
Dragonborn.esm|03216B: I am Hermaeus Mora, Prince of Fate and Lord of Secrets. This is Apocrypha, where all knowledge is hoarded.
Dragonborn.esm|032287: I work for Mogrul. You got a problem, you talk to him.
Dragonborn.esm|032288: Mogrul doesn't lend coin to outlanders. Look somewhere else.
Dragonborn.esm|032289: You looking for trouble? Stay out of my way.
Dragonborn.esm|03228A: Haven't you got something better to do?
Dragonborn.esm|0323E1: There's probably an exit through one of these sarcophagi. Let's get moving.
Dragonborn.esm|0323E2: It looks like a secret entrance opened up along the wall. You lead the way.
Dragonborn.esm|03277D: I am Hermaeus Mora. I am the guardian of the unseen, and knower of the unknown. I have been watching you, mortal. Most impressive.
Dragonborn.esm|032795: It's a shrine, I think. But I don't remember where it came from.
Dragonborn.esm|032796: I think it's important, but... I couldn't really say.
Dragonborn.esm|032797: The Earth Stone, and... It's a shrine that... Hmm. I can't recall.
Dragonborn.esm|032798: I don't know, it's hard to remember...
Dragonborn.esm|032799: There's a shrine at the Earth Stone, but... well, I can't seem to recall what it's for.
Dragonborn.esm|03279A: I don't know. I feel like I knew something about it, but now I can't remember.
Dragonborn.esm|03279B: There's a shrine at the Earth Stone, I think.
Dragonborn.esm|03279C: I can't really remember... I think I knew at one time, but now I'm not sure.
Dragonborn.esm|03279D: The Earth Stone is there. There's also a shrine...
Dragonborn.esm|03279E: Are we building it? I had a dream about that...
Dragonborn.esm|03279F: Do you mean by the Earth Stone? There's a shrine there, but I don't remember what it's for...
Dragonborn.esm|0327A0: Well, I... You know, I can't remember. It's important, I think, but it's hard for me to think about.
Dragonborn.esm|0327A1: There's a shrine at the Earth Stone. It's... I'm not really sure what it's for. I thought I knew at one point.
Dragonborn.esm|0327A2: I don't know... Did we build that?
Dragonborn.esm|0327A3: There's a shrine there, but I'm not sure what it means...
Dragonborn.esm|0327A4: Why can't I remember? I was sure it was important.
Dragonborn.esm|0327A5: It's a shrine, for... Err, I don't remember.
Dragonborn.esm|0327A6: I thought I remembered, but now I'm not sure. Is it important, or did I imagine that?
Dragonborn.esm|032C90: That's right. I want to go out into the world and earn great glory. I want to hunt the deadliest creatures and claim vast riches. I am weary of living in this village. I want to do something with my life other than farm, fish and hunt deer. I know it must be hard for an adventurer such as you to understand this, but we Skaal live very boring lives.
Dragonborn.esm|032C91: You would do that? I know he would listen to you. You've been out there in the wide world. You know its perils. Yes, perhaps you can convince him. I would be grateful if you would do this thing, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|032C92: His name is Nikulas. He's a good lad, strong and loyal. I only wish he wasn't so eager to leave the village. I've tried to tell him that the world beyond our lands is dangerous and cruel, but he won't listen.
Dragonborn.esm|032C93: You are? I admit, I was not expecting to hear that.
Dragonborn.esm|032C94: I can see that in my eagerness for adventure, I have forgotten my duties as a son and as a Skaal. You have helped me to see wisdom, and I thank you for that, Skaal-friend.
Dragonborn.esm|032C95: Well, I know there is some danger, of course, but I didn't think it would be that bad. If the risk is truly so great, I will train with Wulf Wild-Blood and hone my skills. Perhaps in three or four winters, I will truly be ready.
Dragonborn.esm|032C96: There is no fear in my heart. My father's blood flows in my veins, and he was a mighty warrior. I will face any danger.
Dragonborn.esm|032C97: Yes, you're right. She still mourns for my father, and having me near brings her comfort. You have my oath that I will not leave the village until my mother is ready to let me go.
Dragonborn.esm|032C98: I am grateful for your offer, outsider, but I am Skaal. All that I need, I was born with.
Dragonborn.esm|032C99: There is truth in your words. She has not been herself since my father's death.
Dragonborn.esm|032C9A: I know that it would be difficult for her, at least for a time, but I am a man now. I have the right to choose what life I will.
Dragonborn.esm|03314D: Thalmor, to arms! Kill the intruder!
Dragonborn.esm|03314E: You will find him on the deck. Any hint of treachery, and your life is forfeit.
Dragonborn.esm|03314F: I do not know this word Thalmor, but if you mean the elves, they were trying to learn the secrets of forging Stalhrim. Their leader, an elf named Ancarion, has a map. He says that it shows the location of a hidden source of Stalhrim.
Dragonborn.esm|033150: I hope that you can make my son see wisdom.
Dragonborn.esm|033151: If Nikulas leaves me now, I will be truly alone.
Dragonborn.esm|033152: I hope that Nikulas will hear your words. He no longer seems to hear mine.
Dragonborn.esm|033153: If Nikulas leaves, I fear he will never return.
Dragonborn.esm|03370D: Modest as well as brave. Most admirable! I can see why the Skaal think highly of you. Yes, I think you're just the adventurous sort I need. I have a job offer for you.
Dragonborn.esm|03370F: Remarkable, isn't it? This tomb has been hidden for... well, who knows how long. Many centuries, I would think. There are some sealed doors up ahead. Perhaps you can figure out how to get them open.
Dragonborn.esm|033710: Quite right! Quite right! This, however, is paying work. Allow me to explain.
Dragonborn.esm|0337E9: I've been thinking about that, and I believe it does. I believe it was the ancient ancestors of the Skaal who built this tomb for their master, the Dragon Priest they called the guardian. In all likelihood, these people were ancient Nords who came with the Dragon Priest to live here on Solstheim. Separated from their people on the mainland, they developed a distinct culture that became the Skaal we know today. Of course this is all just theory and conjecture. It is likely we will never know the truth.
Dragonborn.esm|0337EA: This is clearly a tomb, if a rather elaborate one. I would guess it was built for a Dragon Priest who achieved great fame in his time. It seems he was held in high regard by both men and dragons, because he defeated a traitor named Miraak. Perhaps Miraak was also a Dragon Priest, probably a contemporary of this guardian. He must have turned against his own order. After his defeat, Miraak was banished to Solstheim and the guardian was made his jailer.
Dragonborn.esm|0337EC: Very well then, back to work.
Dragonborn.esm|0337EE: A good question. Partly to keep unwanted intruders away, I suppose. They might also have been meant as tests, to ensure that only the truly worthy can reach the main burial chamber. Who can say, really? The motives of the Dragon Priests are as inscrutable as those of the Dragons they worshipped.
Dragonborn.esm|0337EF: This place is all I'd hoped, and much more.
Dragonborn.esm|0337F0: History truly comes alive here, don't you think?
Dragonborn.esm|0337F1: You can leave if you like. I think I'll be safe now.
Dragonborn.esm|0337F2: Now that was a proper adventure!
Dragonborn.esm|0337F3: I admit, it was quite a thrill to watch you vanquish that Dragon Priest.
Dragonborn.esm|0337F4: You've quite a keen mind to go with those steel nerves.
Dragonborn.esm|033A74: I know you can hear me!
Dragonborn.esm|033A89: You better... kill me... while you have the chance.
Dragonborn.esm|033A8D: Comfort is given, justice is taken
Dragonborn.esm|033A90: Better to suffer a wrong than to do one.
Dragonborn.esm|033A95: If you value your hands, keep them off that.
Dragonborn.esm|033A9E: I'll let you stay. This time.
Dragonborn.esm|033A9F: You can stay, but you're not supposed to be here.
Dragonborn.esm|033AA5: There are so many delightful ways I've pictured you dead.
Dragonborn.esm|033AA6: We're one of the same kind, you and I. I'm glad to have met you.
Dragonborn.esm|033AA8: Filth. Run to the horizon before I hunt you down.
Dragonborn.esm|033AB4: Boethiah devour you. Get out of here!
Dragonborn.esm|033AB6: I should cut off your hands for that!
Dragonborn.esm|033ADF: I'm not going to warn you again.
Dragonborn.esm|033AF2: What are you doing that for?
Dragonborn.esm|033AF4: I saw you do that, you know.
Dragonborn.esm|033AF6: What do you think you're doing?
Dragonborn.esm|033AF7: You're not supposed to be in here.
Dragonborn.esm|033AF9: You'll give that back if you know what's good for you.
Dragonborn.esm|033B0D: You'll have to do better than that!
Dragonborn.esm|033B0E: By Dagon's eyes, you'll pay for that!
Dragonborn.esm|033B22: Let the ghosts consider their new brethren.
Dragonborn.esm|033B23: Well, worry not over what can't be undone.
Dragonborn.esm|033B25: I can see Boethiah smiles in your heart.
Dragonborn.esm|033B28: Sheogorath take fools like you, put that down.
Dragonborn.esm|033B31: You'll need to be luckier than that!
Dragonborn.esm|033B64: That's certainly one way to get attention.
Dragonborn.esm|033B77: You should have listened. Guards! Help! Trespasser!
Dragonborn.esm|033B79: I'm not going to warn you again. Get out, or I'll call the guards!
Dragonborn.esm|033B7B: This is the last time I'll say it. Get out.
Dragonborn.esm|033B7C: It's time for you to leave, my friend.
Dragonborn.esm|033B7D: You, of all people. Get out!
Dragonborn.esm|033BA1: I'll have your hands for that!
Dragonborn.esm|033BAE: I was sure I heard something.
Dragonborn.esm|033C5F: Hmm. From the way they are waving about, I'm assuming they aren't under your control. And I see now that your tongue is also...well, it's probably best if I don't say. Don't bother trying to scream though.
Dragonborn.esm|033C60: I...I think I'm going to pretend that never happened.
Dragonborn.esm|033C61: Is it over yet? Does he have eyes again?
Dragonborn.esm|033C62: Is it over yet? Does she have eyes again?
Dragonborn.esm|033C63: It looks like it's beginning to wear off.
Dragonborn.esm|033C65: I think I'm going to be sick.
Dragonborn.esm|033CE2: Ahh... You are Dragonborn. I can feel it. And yet...
Dragonborn.esm|033CE3: You have no idea of the true power a Dragonborn can wield!
Dragonborn.esm|033CE4: He can await my arrival with the rest of Tamriel.
Dragonborn.esm|033CE5: She can await my arrival with the rest of Tamriel.
Dragonborn.esm|033CE6: You have done little beyond killing a few dragons.
Dragonborn.esm|033CE7: You have defeated dragons, yes, but still...
Dragonborn.esm|033CE8: You have slain a great many dragons, I see. And yet...
Dragonborn.esm|033CF5: Find me that stash of wine... I'm itching to be rid of it.
Dragonborn.esm|033CF6: Just get them to taste my sujamma... once they take a sip, it'll sell itself.
Dragonborn.esm|033CF7: My husband is second councilor, so I certainly have the means to reward you if you find my folio.
Dragonborn.esm|033CF8: Be cautious, friend. The ash spawn are soulless beings with no regard for compassion.
Dragonborn.esm|033CF9: You look able enough to venture outside of town. Perhaps you could help me recover something precious that was lost?
Dragonborn.esm|033CFA: Are you here to pray, or perhaps pay your respects at our Ancestral Tomb?
Dragonborn.esm|033CFB: Quite a while ago, yes. They cut a bloody swath across most of the southern region of Morrowind. Nobody seems certain why it happened... an attempt at expansion, revenge for the enslavement of their people or perhaps both. Whatever the case may be, thousands of Dunmer needlessly lost their lives and many more were exiled from their homeland.
Dragonborn.esm|033CFC: It might take more than a simple request to change Geldis's mind. He can be quite stubborn.
Dragonborn.esm|033CFD: Bralsa's may be welcome here again, but I still expect her to pay her way like everyone else.
Dragonborn.esm|033CFE: The East Empire Pendants are scattered all over, so leave no stone unturned.
Dragonborn.esm|033CFF: Councilor Morvayn is House Redoran's representative on Solstheim. As second councilor, it's my job to support him and make certain his rule is enforced.
Dragonborn.esm|033D00: Careful with those netches... they may look tame, but they can seriously hurt you when threatened.
Dragonborn.esm|033D02: You're making my buyer happy. Keep them coming.
Dragonborn.esm|033D03: As long as you keep bringing them, I'll keep buying them.
Dragonborn.esm|033D04: I'm beginning to like this partnership. Here you go.
Dragonborn.esm|03401F: They can't talk the wave signs!
Dragonborn.esm|034020: Who... thirst for sky... All-Maker craves the night of sun.
Dragonborn.esm|034021: He hears the underslake of blood dust!
Dragonborn.esm|03409E: When I first got to Kolbjorn, you couldn't even see it. Buried in the ash, like most other things on this blasted island. Dug out a pile of the stuff taller than me just to find the barrow. Who knows how much to reach the door. Excavating isn't exactly my specialty. Even if it was, the ash storms fill it in faster than you can dig.
Dragonborn.esm|0347EC: Hold a moment, Mogrul. I've gotten a report that you've been harassing some of the townsfolk. Is this true?
Dragonborn.esm|0347ED: Oh... Mogrul. Care for anything from my stand today?
Dragonborn.esm|0347EE: I said to put it on my account and that's the end of it. Understand?
Dragonborn.esm|0347EF: I'll see what I can do...
Dragonborn.esm|0347F0: About your account... it's been a few months...
Dragonborn.esm|0347F1: That isn't my problem. I was clear when I loaned Garyn the money that I wanted it back by the first harvest.
Dragonborn.esm|0347F2: Why don't you say that louder so the entire town can hear you, idiot. Just give them to me and put it on my account.
Dragonborn.esm|0347F3: You can't get blood from a stone, Mogrul. As it is, we can barely make ends meet.
Dragonborn.esm|0347F4: Mogrul... I got those special poisons you asked for.
Dragonborn.esm|0347F5: Milore, you need to talk some sense into your husband.
Dragonborn.esm|0347F7: If you want that beautiful wife of yours to stay that way, you'd better.
Dragonborn.esm|0347F8: See that they stay that way, or else I'll send you back to Skyrim in a crate. You understand me?
Dragonborn.esm|0347F9: Look, I know why you're here. I'll get the coin, I promise.
Dragonborn.esm|0347FA: Oh, come on. You know me, Captain Veleth. My hands are clean.
Dragonborn.esm|0347FB: Ash yams? Disgusting. I'd rather eat cheese that's been sitting in the sun for a week.
Dragonborn.esm|034BFB: You won't help me!? Master Neloth is going to kill me. Assuming he doesn't make me into a test subject again.
Dragonborn.esm|034BFC: I need to keep Master Neloth distracted so he doesn't find out what I did. There is no telling what he would do to me. While you are taking care of the creature, I'll try to figure out what went wrong.
Dragonborn.esm|034BFD: I am embarrassed to admit that it was my fault. Master Neloth refused to teach me the Ash Guardian spell. So I read his spell book on my own and learned it. Something went wrong when I cast it. The creature is attacking everything in sight.
Dragonborn.esm|034E43: The unworthy flee before the Master's power.
Dragonborn.esm|034E46: You cannot escape the Dragonborn's justice!
Dragonborn.esm|034E4E: The true Dragonborn returns to this world soon, and all will be judged!
Dragonborn.esm|034E51: We shall clear the way for the coming of the true Dragonborn!
Dragonborn.esm|034E52: Your blood will flow in Miraak's name!
Dragonborn.esm|034E54: You would dare call yourself the true Dragonborn?
Dragonborn.esm|034E55: I will purify the world for Miraak's return!
Dragonborn.esm|034E5D: The true Dragonborn will judge you!
Dragonborn.esm|034E5E: You will be an offering to the Master!
Dragonborn.esm|034F78: Sometimes things fall off the back of a cart. I find them, clean them up and resell them.
Dragonborn.esm|034F79: Who am I? Who are you? Buy something or keep moving.
Dragonborn.esm|034F83: I was one of the wealthiest miners in town... I could haul up almost three or four times what the average digger could. I never saw the end coming like it did... the mine running out of ebony. Unlike most of my fellow miners, I was too foolish to save enough coin for that day. The rest I spent on sujamma to drown my sorrows. Councilor Morvayn was kind enough to allow Rirns and I to live in one of the abandoned buildings. At least it keeps the ash out of our eyes.
Dragonborn.esm|034F84: I was one of the Redoran Guard under Captain Veleth's command. A few years ago, I was out on patrol with two other men when we were attacked by some ash spawn. They sprung from the ground with no warning and killed one of my companions instantly. I was so frightened, I ran all the way back to Raven Rock. Unfortunately, I'd left the other guardsman behind and he never made it. Captain Veleth discharged me from service. There isn't a single night that passes where I don't regret what I did, but it's too late for that now.
Dragonborn.esm|034F85: Couldn't be better. The sections that are being opened up have revealed some huge deposits of ebony. Old Crescius has a nose for exactly where to dig. If they'd only listened to him years ago, the town wouldn't have been in such dire straits. At this rate, I'll be able to put a roof over my head in just a few months.
Dragonborn.esm|034F86: Bralsa's been nice enough to show me how to swing a pickaxe. She's an old hat when it comes to digging up ebony. I may not be an expert at it yet, but it's good to be back to work and have some coin in my pockets.
Dragonborn.esm|034F87: With the mines open, Raven Rock has become a significant colony of the Dunmer people again. I've been able to convince my superiors at House Redoran to send me the resources they've been denying now that we're deemed more important. Soon, we'll have a steady supply of workers and materials to keep things going for years to come.
Dragonborn.esm|034F88: Things are picking up... for now. All I'm wondering is how long it'll take for House Redoran to begin ignoring us again if the mine runs out of ore for a second time.
Dragonborn.esm|034F89: I've been made the foreman of the mine thanks to everything you did down there. It's good to get the old nose working again. I missed sniffing out the ebony deposits and getting my hands dirty. Best of all, Aphia isn't so cross when I go down there any more!
Dragonborn.esm|034F8A: You might think it's all perfume and roses now that the mine's open, but all I see is a new security problem on our hands. Making sure those shipments of ebony make it safely from the mine to the shipping docks is my new order of the day. And with Solstheim crawling with reavers and Azura-knows what else, I can't risk any attacks on Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|034F8B: How do you think? It's a blessing. I've got regular paying customers again, thirsty workers at the end of the day, and more coin than I can count.
Dragonborn.esm|034F8C: I've always had a steady stream of work thanks to the Redoran Guard, so it hasn't affected me much. However, I've seen the smile on people's faces and there's hope around here once again... something we've been lacking for a long time.
Dragonborn.esm|034F8D: I've had to muster some more men to protect the mine, but I don't really have a problem with that. Second Councilor Arano is concerned that once word reaches the reavers that the mine has reopened, they might think about paying us a visit. I say let them try. My men are ready for them.
Dragonborn.esm|034F8F: Watch yourself out there... looks like an ash storm might be brewing.
Dragonborn.esm|034F90: Come back if you're feeling thirsty. I'll set you up right.
Dragonborn.esm|0353FE: As you'll see, that's easier said than done. This way to the boilers.
Dragonborn.esm|0353FF: You should not have come here!
Dragonborn.esm|035403: No, we'll have to do this the hard way. If we can restore the steam supply to this room, I'm certain I can open it.
Dragonborn.esm|035404: So tantalizingly close... But trust me, no magic will open that. I'd have had the book already if it could.
Dragonborn.esm|035409: Ah, now we can finish this.
Dragonborn.esm|03540A: Did you think to escape me?
Dragonborn.esm|03540B: It's too late for regrets, Dragonborn.
Dragonborn.esm|03540C: You can see the book right here.
Dragonborn.esm|035425: There's no escape from this place.
Dragonborn.esm|035426: You are in my power here.
Dragonborn.esm|035430: I grow ever nearer to you.
Dragonborn.esm|035432: Now through me do you see.
Dragonborn.esm|035434: Now through them do I speak.
Dragonborn.esm|035435: And when the world shall listen.
Dragonborn.esm|035436: And when the world shall see.
Dragonborn.esm|035438: That world will cease to be.
Dragonborn.esm|03543B: Miraak has you, just like the others!
Dragonborn.esm|03543C: Why did you touch the Stone?
Dragonborn.esm|03545B: Oh, how the Deceiver wags his clever tongue. But you shall not evade us with guile and deceit. No. You shall pay for your blasphemy!
Dragonborn.esm|03545D: When Lord Miraak appears all shall bear witness. None will there be who oppose him.
Dragonborn.esm|035460: Then it is too late. The lie has already taken root in the hearts of men. So we shall rip the falsehood from their breasts by tearing the false heart from yours!
Dragonborn.esm|035465: You there! You're the one they call Dragonborn?
Dragonborn.esm|035466: Your lies fall on deaf ears, Deceiver. The True Dragonborn comes... You are but his shadow.
Dragonborn.esm|035467: Then it is too late. The lie has already taken root in the hearts of men. So we shall expose to them the falseness in their hearts by tearing out yours, Deceiver!
Dragonborn.esm|035468: Your lies fall on deaf ears, Deceiver! We know you are the False Dragonborn! You shall not stand in the way of the true Dragonborn's return. He comes soon, and we shall offer him your heart!
Dragonborn.esm|035469: You cannot escape the truth. You cannot escape the True Dragonborn! When Lord Miraak appears all shall bear witness. None will stand to oppose him!
Dragonborn.esm|03546A: When Lord Miraak appears all shall bear witness. None shall stand to oppose him!
Dragonborn.esm|035479: No! Do not touch the Stone while Miraak controls it!
Dragonborn.esm|035533: It's on your head if something goes wrong. Just let me get back inside first.
Dragonborn.esm|035534: The, uh... the lab is too small. Also, I need the ashy soil. Master Neloth knows what I'm doing. No need to bother him about it though. He's quite busy at the moment.
Dragonborn.esm|035535: Master Neloth has tasked Elynea with repairing the tower. Why aren't you doing this in the lab, where it would be safer for all of us?
Dragonborn.esm|035536: If you must know, I'm trying to get this Ash Guardian spell to work. Now let me concentrate. Besides, shouldn't you be worrying about the damage to the tower?
Dragonborn.esm|035537: Why are you out here? Aren't you supposed to be in the tower assisting Master Neloth?
Dragonborn.esm|0355A3: Felgir paw tuwani barj. Goora! Goora! Goora!
Dragonborn.esm|0355B4: He never felt the call of the All-Maker as I do. As we all should. We seek to live in peace with the land, but he had an eye for dominance and strength. For unnatural strength, you need look no further than the beasts of this island, mangled by the daedra. It's not a fate I would wish on anyone. And not what I wanted for my brother.
Dragonborn.esm|0355B7: I hope you will give me word if you find anything about my brother.
Dragonborn.esm|035648: Yes. I'm trying to summon an Ash Guardian. I'm just having trouble deciphering Master Neloth's handwriting. I don't mean to be rude, but I need to concentrate.
Dragonborn.esm|035649: Another border...no, aetheric order. I think that's right.
Dragonborn.esm|03564A: Two fingers pronated...what's pronated? Oh. Rotated. That might work.
Dragonborn.esm|03564B: Tart bones? That doesn't even make sense. I'll just ignore that part.
Dragonborn.esm|03566C: That was you? You're the Dragonborn? Please, you have to believe me. I did everything against my will, I had no choice!
Dragonborn.esm|03566D: Not... exactly. Look, it's difficult to explain. I heard... a voice in my head. It told me to bring the cultists to Windhelm. I didn't want to do it, but I did it anyway. I couldn't control myself.
Dragonborn.esm|03566E: I don't know. I can't remember everything clearly.
Dragonborn.esm|035673: I fear there is too much we do not yet know.
Dragonborn.esm|035674: But how is that possible? After all this time...
Dragonborn.esm|035A06: It is no wonder the dragons razed his temple to the ground. Seeing the remains hung up like trophies must have enraged them to no end.
Dragonborn.esm|035A07: I had heard Miraak had turned against the Dragon Cult, but to display the remains in such a manner as this...
Dragonborn.esm|035A08: It is eerily quiet. I do not suspect that will be the case the further we go. Be on your guard.
Dragonborn.esm|035A09: It looks like there is only one way to proceed. I leave the honor of pulling that handle to you. I do not want to put my hand anywhere near the mouth of that statue.
Dragonborn.esm|035A0A: I do not recognize this statuary. We passed by a few of them earlier, but they are becoming more frequent as we get further in. I do not like this place. It almost looks as if these statues will come to life at any moment.
Dragonborn.esm|035A0C: I wonder if there is something here that tells the story of Miraak...
Dragonborn.esm|035AED: Meat. Furs. Bone. Trophies of the hunt. But those are of little use to you. There is one more thing... Since the days of the Great Hunt, our pack has kept a set of rings, blessed by Lord Hircine himself. I sense his favor on you. For you... I will part with them, if you can pay my price.
Dragonborn.esm|035AEE: Since I was born. My parents were members of the pack, back when Majni's father was Alpha. After they died, Majni and Akar took care of me, taught me to hunt. I'm not as good as they are, but... I do my best.
Dragonborn.esm|035AEF: Hold, traveler. You have no business here.
Dragonborn.esm|035AF0: You might find a few others. Strays. Lone hunters. But ours is the last true pack that remains.
Dragonborn.esm|035AF1: Deer. Sabers. Rieks. Boar. Bear. I hunt soon. Go bother Majni.
Dragonborn.esm|035AF2: Hold, traveler. You have no business here. Be on your way. ...Wait. You... you're one of us, aren't you? A werewolf?
Dragonborn.esm|035AF4: Aye. The blood still runs true among some on Solstheim, as it has for centuries. We have chosen to embrace Lord Hircine's blessing. You know it well. The speed and strength of the beast. The thrill of the hunt. The triumph of the kill. This is our purpose, our way of life. So it was for my father, and his father before him. And I welcome any who walk that path.
Dragonborn.esm|035AF6: I am Majni, Alpha of the Frostmoon Pack. You are welcome here, so long as you respect our law.
Dragonborn.esm|035AF8: Welcome, brother. It's been too long since we met another who shares our blood.
Dragonborn.esm|035AF9: Welcome, sister. It's been too long since we met another who shares our blood.
Dragonborn.esm|035AFA: Akar. Majni's brother. I hunt. He talks. Go talk to him.
Dragonborn.esm|035AFC: For now. My pack lived in the mountains northeast of here, 'till they were wiped out. Damn werebears. Majni offered to help me reclaim my territory. I hunt with him until then. After that... we'll see.
Dragonborn.esm|035AFD: Why? Have you seen how they live? They cower behind their walls, afraid of every ash hopper, every netch! No, I'd rather be here. Free to hunt, free to live my life as I choose.
Dragonborn.esm|035B11: I don't trust you, whelp. Keep that in mind.
Dragonborn.esm|035B13: Then perhaps you do have business here... Forgive me. I'm Rakel, of the Frostmoon Pack. Welcome, brother.
Dragonborn.esm|035B14: Then perhaps you do have business here... Forgive me. I'm Rakel, of the Frostmoon Pack. Welcome, sister.
Dragonborn.esm|035BC8: That is our name for him - the Demon of Knowledge. He has always been our enemy.
Dragonborn.esm|035BC9: We know him as Herma-Mora, the Demon of Knowledge. It is in his nature to hoard secrets to himself. Their value to him is of no consequence. The very fact that the Skaal have kept knowledge from him has merely increased his desire to have it.
Dragonborn.esm|035BCA: It may. It should. And I will think on what you ask of me, to decide if it is necessary, or just another of Herma-Mora's tricks.
Dragonborn.esm|035BCB: The land is out of balance. Miraak's power still corrupts the sacred Stones.
Dragonborn.esm|035BCC: Have you? The actual Oghma Infinium? That's... I've searched for it myself for many years without success... Well then, you should know better than anyone that Hermaeus Mora is not to be trifled with. But he is subtler than most of the other Daedric Princes, as you would expect of the prince of knowledge and fate. You seem to have escaped the fate of many who find themselves ensnared forever by the lure of his secrets. Or... perhaps not.
Dragonborn.esm|035BCE: So it falls to me to be the one to give up the secrets to our ancient enemy. I do not know if I have the strength to face him. With Miraak's corruption of the Stones... the Oneness of the land is disrupted, and our connection to the All-Maker is broken.
Dragonborn.esm|035BCF: So it falls to me to be the one to give up the secrets to our ancient enemy. I do not know if I have the strength to face him. The Tree Stone is still corrupted... the land is still out of balance. But with the other five restored... it may be enough. It will have to be.
Dragonborn.esm|035BD0: If you have to ask that, you'd probably be best leaving his books to wiser heads than yours. Like all Daedric Princes, he's not to be trifled with. But he is subtler than most of his ilk, appropriate for the prince of knowledge and fate. Many scholars and loremasters have been ensnared by the lure of learning the secrets that only Hermaeus Mora possesses. But don't worry. I have no intention of joining them in their endless search through the infinite halls of Apocrypha.
Dragonborn.esm|035BD1: No... I am still too weak to face him. It will take all my strength if I am to force him to hold to his bargain. You must cleanse the other Stones so that I can draw power from the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|035BD2: Hermaeus Mora... old Herma-Mora himself. So he is the source of Miraak's power. Of course. We have many tales of Herma-Mora trying to trick us into giving up our secrets to him. And now he comes again for what we have long kept from him.
Dragonborn.esm|035BD4: There is nothing else to say. I am in the All-Maker's hands now.
Dragonborn.esm|035BD9: What next? When will it stop?
Dragonborn.esm|035BDA: Go and do what you must.
Dragonborn.esm|035BDC: Go. Let us mourn him in our own way.
Dragonborn.esm|035BDD: Poor Storn. I can't believe it.
Dragonborn.esm|035BDF: This is all too much for me.
Dragonborn.esm|035BE8: What have you brought into our village?
Dragonborn.esm|035BEB: By the gods... I can't believe...
Dragonborn.esm|035BFE: Why would you do such a thing?
Dragonborn.esm|035BFF: I doubt that was truly necessary.
Dragonborn.esm|035C00: The All-Maker frowns upon such violence.
Dragonborn.esm|035C01: I will strike like a hawk!
Dragonborn.esm|035C06: I come home to you, All-Maker...
Dragonborn.esm|035C07: How long do you think you can hide?
Dragonborn.esm|035C08: There is no honor in this.
Dragonborn.esm|035C09: All-Maker, guide my eyes and ears.
Dragonborn.esm|035C0A: Come now, there is no need to hide.
Dragonborn.esm|035C0E: I am not so old that I cannot fight!
Dragonborn.esm|035C0F: Back to where you came from!
Dragonborn.esm|035C10: You have left me no choice.
Dragonborn.esm|035C11: I was a fool to trust you!
Dragonborn.esm|035C1E: Come back when you actually have the gold.
Dragonborn.esm|035C1F: I don't sail for free. Come back when you can actually pay me.
Dragonborn.esm|035E1C: You have no business with our pack. Begone.
Dragonborn.esm|035E1F: We mean you no harm, but... be on your way.
Dragonborn.esm|03611C: I am his mycologist. He may have grown Tel Mithryn by himself, but I nurture it. Mushrooms and fungi are my life's work. When I saw Tel Mithryn I knew I had to be here. I've learned so much since then.
Dragonborn.esm|03611D: Me? No. That's Master Neloth's doing. I wasn't even born then. It's Telvanni magic at its best. He brought the cuttings with him from Morrowind and used sorcery to grow them.
Dragonborn.esm|03611E: Winterhold? That small college that is falling into the Sea of Ghosts? You're the one that recovered the Staff of Magnus. Impressive. Talvas should finish his apprenticeship in a couple of decades. Come see me then. I think I could teach you a trick or two.
Dragonborn.esm|03611F: Savros? Ha! He wouldn't be fit to be my apprentice. Of course he does have a knack for wards that would be handy to learn. If you see him, let him know that I might be willing to take him on as my apprentice. For a price of course.
Dragonborn.esm|036120: Of course you have. Everyone has heard of the master wizards of Morrowind. We are one of the great houses that rule Morrowind.
Dragonborn.esm|036121: I am likely the greatest wizard you will ever meet. Who else do you think could create a staff enchanter? If you make yourself useful around here, I might just let you use it.
Dragonborn.esm|036122: I don't recall inviting you into my tower. I do hope you make this worth my while.
Dragonborn.esm|036123: You again. Didn't I see you in Raven Rock?
Dragonborn.esm|0362C4: If you have released the Wind Stone and broken the hold on my people, perhaps you can do the same for the rest of Solstheim. I doubt it will fully stop whatever Miraak is doing, but it may slow his progress.
Dragonborn.esm|0365DE: My word is as true as fate, as inevitable as destiny. Bring me what I want, and I will give you what you seek.
Dragonborn.esm|0365E2: Now we will see if he knows what he is talking about.
Dragonborn.esm|0365E3: It would be madness to go through there.
Dragonborn.esm|0365E5: Amazing to think that they built this so long ago, and it still survives.
Dragonborn.esm|0365E6: Finally. We will not have to listen to his complaining.
Dragonborn.esm|0365E7: The dwarves must have feared this book to lock it away like this.
Dragonborn.esm|0365E9: The Skaal have always avoided ruins such as these. Nothing of the All-Maker is to be found within.
Dragonborn.esm|0365EA: You should leave slogging around in the muck to us youngsters.
Dragonborn.esm|0365EB: That's correct. I'm glad to see someone else has been paying attention.
Dragonborn.esm|0365EC: So, we only need one more of these... cubes?
Dragonborn.esm|036603: Got this from a pretty excited looking dark elf around Raven Rock. He said it was important I got it to you as soon as I could.
Dragonborn.esm|036604: Another note from your friend Ralis. I hope everything is all right.
Dragonborn.esm|036605: Another letter from Ralis. He's starting to scare me a little.
Dragonborn.esm|036606: I've got another letter from Ralis, but this is the last thing I'm delivering for him. That place is just too much for me.
Dragonborn.esm|036614: Well, what are you waiting for? Go ahead and open it.
Dragonborn.esm|036615: Maybe... we should think about this some more.
Dragonborn.esm|036616: No, no, I couldn't. ...You open it.
Dragonborn.esm|036617: I can't believe it. We... we actually found it!
Dragonborn.esm|03668A: I'd been waking every morning with my hands scrapped and raw and now I know why. I appreciate what you've done for me.
Dragonborn.esm|03668B: Just thinking that some madman was inside my mind. Gives me shivers just thinking about it.
Dragonborn.esm|03668C: That fetcher... making us build his shrines. If I could only get my hands on him...
Dragonborn.esm|03668D: The voices in my head are gone now thanks to you. Now I can finally get some rest.
Dragonborn.esm|03668E: Ugh, I can't believe I was forced to do manual labor at those shrines. It was horrifying...
Dragonborn.esm|03668F: Being freed from building the shrine... I don't even know how to begin to thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|036690: I can't believe that I was building that shrine at night... I never knew.
Dragonborn.esm|036691: I've heard of stealing things from people, but never thought you could steal someone's mind.
Dragonborn.esm|036897: May the All-Maker bless you with many sons and daughters, outsider. Once again you have shown that you are a true friend of the Skaal. I wish that I could give you a reward that is equal to the joy in my heart, but I don't have much to offer one who has so much already. Perhaps these will prove useful to you. These herbs are unique to the island, and their like cannot be found elsewhere.
Dragonborn.esm|036898: Perhaps someday I will have to, but for now I need Nikulas here. You see, his father, my husband, was recently lost to us. Nikulas is all that I have left in this world. I know it is selfish of me to say so, but I cannot lose him. Not while I still grieve. Also, I know his heart. Nikulas is reckless and foolish, like most young men. He cannot understand what awaits him in the wide world.
Dragonborn.esm|036899: My son and I gather items to trade with travelers who visit the village.
Dragonborn.esm|03689B: I gathered some plants that are useful for making potions, but I have other goods as well.
Dragonborn.esm|03689C: Among other things, I have some fresh bread I baked this morning and vegetables from Finna's garden.
Dragonborn.esm|03689D: Mostly things from the village or the land around it. Have a look.
Dragonborn.esm|03689F: Have you taken leave of your senses?
Dragonborn.esm|0368A1: Not very sporting of you to do that.
Dragonborn.esm|0368A3: You'll pay dearly for what you've done.
Dragonborn.esm|0368A4: You'll regret what you've done, murderer!
Dragonborn.esm|0368A7: I join you in death, father...
Dragonborn.esm|0368A8: I'll cut you in two, fetcher!
Dragonborn.esm|0368A9: I'll scatter your ashes to the wind!
Dragonborn.esm|0368AB: How dare you invade our territory!
Dragonborn.esm|0368AC: Your remains will be hung from the Bulwark!
Dragonborn.esm|0368AE: I bring the might of House Redoran upon your head!
Dragonborn.esm|0368AF: You'll regret raising a weapon against me!
Dragonborn.esm|0368B1: I may not be able to see you, but I can hear you.
Dragonborn.esm|0368B2: Enough hiding. Come out and face me.
Dragonborn.esm|0368B3: You can play this game as long as you like... I will find you.
Dragonborn.esm|0368B6: That noise... I'm not alone here.
Dragonborn.esm|0368CE: Another victory for the Redoran Guard.
Dragonborn.esm|0368E0: I'll stain the ash with your blood!
Dragonborn.esm|0368E1: I'll not fall to the likes of you!
Dragonborn.esm|0368E4: Time to teach this whelp a lesson!
Dragonborn.esm|0368E8: I'll stay my weapon... for now.
Dragonborn.esm|036A59: No Ash Guardian. Maybe the book has something.
Dragonborn.esm|036A5C: I've got the best selection of mushroom and fungi.
Dragonborn.esm|036A5D: I probably have some potions around here that might interest you.
Dragonborn.esm|036A5E: Every type of fungus in Solstheim or Skyrim. Oh, and a few other ingredients and potions.
Dragonborn.esm|036A5F: Defeating Miraak was an impressive feat. I'm curious to see if Hermaeus Mora seeks you out because of it.
Dragonborn.esm|036A60: It's good to have everyone not running off to that damn sacred stone every night. I suppose I have you to thank for that.
Dragonborn.esm|036A61: One day I'll inherit his power. It will all be mine. Mark my words.
Dragonborn.esm|036A6B: Well, then, I'm going to head back and get myself a drink. Maybe I'll see you soon.
Dragonborn.esm|036A6C: Just let me know what needs doing.
Dragonborn.esm|036A6F: What was it you needed, again?
Dragonborn.esm|037249: Mogrul says pay up. We figure we can just take it off your corpse.
Dragonborn.esm|0373C8: You are welcome among us, so long as you mean no harm.
Dragonborn.esm|0373C9: Hail, brother! I am Rakel, of the Frostmoon Pack.
Dragonborn.esm|0373CA: Hail, sister! I am Rakel, of the Frostmoon Pack.
Dragonborn.esm|0373FA: Unless of course you'd like to contribute to my investigation. It could be very enlightening to observe you.
Dragonborn.esm|0373FC: I would not advise touching the stone again. The effects of repeated contact could be...
Dragonborn.esm|0373FD: Ah, so you appear able to resist the effect by exerting your will. Fascinating!
Dragonborn.esm|0373FE: Fascinating. By touching the stone, you appear to have fallen under whatever influence is affecting the others.
Dragonborn.esm|0373FF: I wonder how long this effect will last. Perhaps it is permanent as with the others.
Dragonborn.esm|037400: That would be a shame. You were an interesting exception to the otherwise universal phenomenon.
Dragonborn.esm|037401: Can you hear me? Do you have any free will left, or are you completely under this outside influence like the others?
Dragonborn.esm|037402: Hmm. It appears that you were not as unusual after all.
Dragonborn.esm|0374CC: Now I just have to figure how to get this thing started...
Dragonborn.esm|0374CD: If you say so. That old crackpot made the trials sound a lot harder than that.
Dragonborn.esm|0374CE: Nonsense. A few Dwemer Spiders will pop out, we kill them, and the Treasure of Kagrumez will be ours!
Dragonborn.esm|0374CF: Maybe we should come back with more men. We don't know how many of those things will come out.
Dragonborn.esm|0374DE: I suppose that depends. Are you willing to be an experimental subject?
Dragonborn.esm|0374DF: You've never heard of the great houses of Morrowind? House Telvanni is the house of the master wizard, one of the ruling houses of Morrowind. Master Neloth is one of the oldest and most respected members of House Telvanni.
Dragonborn.esm|03755A: Well, at least if the other Nord returns, they'll take care of him.
Dragonborn.esm|03755B: Ha! Wait... Did you hear that? Sounds like those Ash Spawn are back.
Dragonborn.esm|03755C: Who cares? Nords all look the same to me.
Dragonborn.esm|03755D: Do you think he's the one we left in the tunnel?
Dragonborn.esm|03755E: Ha ha ha! No more! What a fool.
Dragonborn.esm|03755F: Listen to this one! "My nights have been impossible to bear without you." "I beg of you: Please depart that blasted isle and return to Solitude at once!"
Dragonborn.esm|03784D: Well, I'll be. That's the one. What happened to Esmond?
Dragonborn.esm|037852: We have lived on Solstheim for many generations. Our people are tied to the land itself. We try to serve the All-Maker, to live in balance with nature, instead of exploiting it as others would.
Dragonborn.esm|037853: His story is as old as Solstheim itself. He served the dragons before their fall from power, as most did. A priest in their order. But unlike most, he turned against them. He made his own path, and his actions cost him dearly. The stories say he sought to claim Solstheim for himself, and the dragons destroyed him for it.
Dragonborn.esm|037857: If you're looking for passage, talk to Captain Gjalund.
Dragonborn.esm|037858: Talk to Captain Gjalund if you're looking to book passage.
Dragonborn.esm|037859: The Northern Maiden is ship-shape and ready to go.
Dragonborn.esm|03785A: Captain Gjalund does the business around here... I'm just a ship's mate.
Dragonborn.esm|037B94: Miraak? The one all the townsfolk are always chanting about?
Dragonborn.esm|037B95: Bring that cube. We'll need it.
Dragonborn.esm|037B96: Well, I knew something connected with Hermaeus Mora was spreading its influence across the island. I wasn't sure that it was in fact the same entity as this legendary namesake of the central temple. Although the villagers seem quite convinced.
Dragonborn.esm|037B97: Is this somehow connected to your search for Miraak?
Dragonborn.esm|037B98: What could you know of them?
Dragonborn.esm|0383AF: I'd rather be stuck out here than floating in there.
Dragonborn.esm|0383B0: And now we're stuck out here listening to it all day and night.
Dragonborn.esm|0383B1: I saw the Fadrin brothers bobbing around in there this morning, so that makes three.
Dragonborn.esm|038419: My mother was Skaal, but she left the village and I was born in Skyrim.
Dragonborn.esm|03841A: My son's heart is restless. He wants to see the world beyond our lands, and nothing I say will change his mind.
Dragonborn.esm|03841D: I cannot deny it. You remind me of all the things I miss about Skyrim, and my heart beats a little quicker when you are near. Do you feel the same?
Dragonborn.esm|03841E: An Amulet of Mara? I haven't seen such a thing in a very long time.
Dragonborn.esm|038444: You can't make me go back in there.
Dragonborn.esm|038446: Talk to Ralis about this mess.
Dragonborn.esm|038447: Bradyn and Mireli... I hope they're all right.
Dragonborn.esm|038448: I think you may have wasted your money, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|038449: Don't waste your time with me.
Dragonborn.esm|03844B: If they couldn't hold off the draugr, who can?
Dragonborn.esm|03844D: I can't take this much longer.
Dragonborn.esm|03844E: It's a death pit, I tell you.
Dragonborn.esm|038530: Frea! I had worried I would not see you again.
Dragonborn.esm|038540: Then... it was the All-Maker's will, as he said. I know I should not doubt it. But it is good to hear, all the same. Thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|038541: Thank you. Whatever your other reasons for acting, you have done the Skaal a great service. We will not forget what you've done.
Dragonborn.esm|038542: Then my father's sacrifice... it was not in vain. He died to free us. Tell me... was it the only way? Did he need to die?
Dragonborn.esm|038543: I can feel it. The Tree Stone is free again. The Oneness of the land is restored. Does that mean... is it over? Is Miraak defeated?
Dragonborn.esm|038544: Oh, it clearly is not associated with the same power that has overtaken the island. And I'm not talking about Hermaeus Mora. These Black Books are all his, of course. No, what you're looking for is a specific book. Presumably because Miraak's power derives from it.
Dragonborn.esm|038545: Herma-Mora forced you to serve him in order to defeat Miraak. Do not let him lure you further down that path. The All-Maker made you Dragonborn for a higher purpose. Do not forget that. Walk with the All-Maker, Skaal-friend.
Dragonborn.esm|038546: Yes. I haven't been idle while this fascinating madness engulfed Solstheim. But my book isn't what you're looking for. I'm quite sure it is unconnected with this Miraak. But I do know where to find a Black Book that can help you.
Dragonborn.esm|038547: Yes, his "help" which did not require him to kill my father. Such is the way of Herma-Mora, the eternal enemy of the Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|038548: He has served me long and well. But he grows restless under my guidance. His desire to return to your world will spread my influence more widely. But it will also set him free from my direct control. It may be time to replace him with a more loyal servant. One who still appreciates the gifts that I have to offer.
Dragonborn.esm|038549: It was Miraak who threatened Solstheim. With him gone, Herma-Mora has been foiled once again. Tell me, though... my father's death... was it necessary? Was there no other way to destroy Miraak?
Dragonborn.esm|03854A: Oh yes. They're not hard to locate once you know how to look for them. I have one here that I've been using to locate more.
Dragonborn.esm|03854B: I know. The Skaal will not forget this. Storn's death will become another of the many tales of Herma-Mora's treachery.
Dragonborn.esm|038555: Looks like you've already have someone with you. If you want me to come along, you'll have to ask them to leave.
Dragonborn.esm|038557: Unless you have enough coin on you, I'm not going anywhere.
Dragonborn.esm|038558: It looks like you already have someone with you. If you want me to join, you'll have to part ways with them first.
Dragonborn.esm|03855A: I was enjoying our travels together before we parted company. I'd be happy to rejoin you for no charge... if you're interested.
Dragonborn.esm|03855B: It's been a while, outlander. Looking to unleash some havoc across Tamriel again?
Dragonborn.esm|03855C: Teldryn Sero, blade for hire. If you have the coin, I'm at your service.
Dragonborn.esm|03855D: The best swordsman in all Morrowind is at your service... for the right price.
Dragonborn.esm|03855E: Don't pass up on this opportunity, outlander. I'm worth every coin.
Dragonborn.esm|03855F: I've got swords, spells and a few other tricks up my sleeve. You'll find I'm full of surprises.
Dragonborn.esm|0386B9: Soon they will finish building my temple, and I can return home.
Dragonborn.esm|0386BA: You see that green light? That comes from the Wind Stone, where my people work against their will. They must be freed soon.
Dragonborn.esm|0386BB: The village is just ahead. Storn has used his magic to raise a barrier around it, protecting the few of us left. That the barrier is still there is a good sign.
Dragonborn.esm|038883: You're going to pay for this.
Dragonborn.esm|038884: Come on. Let's see what you've got.
Dragonborn.esm|038885: I'm going to put you down.
Dragonborn.esm|038886: I'll send your soul as a gift to Boethiah.
Dragonborn.esm|038887: Sheogorath's beard, you're an ugly one.
Dragonborn.esm|038888: You thought to reject me, and yet here you are. Your journey towards enlightenment has led you to my realm, as I knew it would.
Dragonborn.esm|038889: As I told you when I gave you the Oghma Infinium, your free will is an illusion. Why else would you be here?
Dragonborn.esm|038962: We have enough problems around here without having to build that blasted thing outside. Thanks for helping us wake up and move off it.
Dragonborn.esm|038963: You ever tried hunting with no sleep under you? It wasn't pretty. Now I can rest properly, it should all be going better.
Dragonborn.esm|038964: Good to finally be getting some sleep at night. Things are bad enough without everyone around here looking death-eyed, too.
Dragonborn.esm|038965: My hands weren't made for building things. Thanks for getting us off that project up there.
Dragonborn.esm|038966: If there's one thing I can't stand, it's shoddy construction. Glad to not be working on that... thing any more.
Dragonborn.esm|038A66: As shaman of the Skaal, I am charged with the spiritual well-being of my people. While you are not of the Skaal, you are Skaal-friend, and so I give you this warning.
Dragonborn.esm|038A67: I don't recall asking you to kill Miraak. That was your own project. And I'm not even mentioning the fact that now we'll never know what would have happened when Miraak returned. Although... from all indications he could have proved a serious nuisance. So, yes, I am grateful that you dealt with him.
Dragonborn.esm|038A68: I wasn't worried. Just interested. I don't get to observe first-hand many people who've spoken to Hermaeus Mora.
Dragonborn.esm|038A69: One more thing, Dragonborn, if you will.
Dragonborn.esm|038A6A: One more thing, Skaal-friend, if you will.
Dragonborn.esm|038A6B: Well, that is what you hero types do, isn't it? I wasn't expecting anything less.
Dragonborn.esm|038A6C: Who? Oh him. Well, I assume you killed him. Or Hermaeus Mora turned on him when you looked like the winning bet. Or a bit of both. Miraak's influence has vanished from Solstheim. So I assumed you had handled things. Why, did something interesting happen?
Dragonborn.esm|038A6D: Incipient madness. Loss of self-awareness. Black spots in the whites of the eyes. Any of the documented indications of Hermaeus Mora's permanent influence. Hmm, no, you look fine. Well, at least no different than when I first saw you.
Dragonborn.esm|038A6E: I know it is not my place, but... may I offer a word of advice... of warning?
Dragonborn.esm|038A6F: Hold still. Let me get a good look at you.
Dragonborn.esm|038A70: I am Hermaeus Mora. I have been watching you since you defeated Miraak. Your progress is most impressive.
Dragonborn.esm|038A71: I am Hermaeus Mora. I have been watching you since we last spoke, and I am pleased with your progress.
Dragonborn.esm|038A72: I understand. Walk with the All-Maker, Dragonborn.
Dragonborn.esm|038A73: Now all that's left are these Black Books, waiting for another ambitious fool to be lured into Hermaeus Mora's clutches. Speaking of which... I think I've located another book, if you're interested. Here, let me show you.
Dragonborn.esm|038A74: Now all that's left are these Black Books. It's a shame I haven't located any more. So much more to be learned.
Dragonborn.esm|038ABC: Your master cannot help you now!
Dragonborn.esm|038ABD: You've no right to be here, monster!
Dragonborn.esm|038ABE: This was a foolish mistake, outsider.
Dragonborn.esm|038AC6: I still cannot believe that Storn is gone.
Dragonborn.esm|038B43: Did you still want me to help you or not?
Dragonborn.esm|038B44: If it'll help us get moving quicker, sure.
Dragonborn.esm|038B46: You're going ahead without me? Suit yourself...
Dragonborn.esm|038B47: Oh fine, go on... leave me here. When you decide to return to your senses, you know where to find me.
Dragonborn.esm|038B4A: What do you have in mind?
Dragonborn.esm|038B4D: You better not be gone long.
Dragonborn.esm|038B4E: Make it quick, I don't like this waiting game.
Dragonborn.esm|038B4F: So this is the fabled College of Winterhold. Amazing.
Dragonborn.esm|038B50: I hope we don't bump into any Forsworn around here. I heard they're as wild as a pack of beasts.
Dragonborn.esm|038B51: The Pale reminds me of the northern part of Solstheim... rife with freezing cold weather and full of unfriendly creatures.
Dragonborn.esm|038B52: I can't believe how carelessly the dead are buried here. I can't imagine my body just rotting away like that.
Dragonborn.esm|038B53: Whiterun... the pride of Skyrim. Doesn't look like much to me. Now Blacklight on the other hand, that's a proper city.
Dragonborn.esm|038B54: I lived in the Gray Quarter of Windhelm for years... it's a wretched place full of bitter Dunmer. Let's be done here quickly.
Dragonborn.esm|038B55: Can you imagine what Markarth must have looked like when the dwarves were living here? I can't even begin to imagine.
Dragonborn.esm|038B56: Solitude... home to the East Empire Company, the heartless bastards.
Dragonborn.esm|038B57: So this is Riften. Glover Mallory told me a good deal about the place. It looks exactly as I'd pictured.
Dragonborn.esm|038B58: You may want to be careful down here... the Thieves Guild makes the Ratway it's home.
Dragonborn.esm|038B59: Dawnstar is full of struggling miners. Sounds a lot like a certain town in Solstheim, eh?
Dragonborn.esm|038B5A: Ugh, what a wretched place. Who would build a town in the middle of a swamp?
Dragonborn.esm|038B5B: Riverwood looks peaceful. If I had to settle in Skyrim, this might be the place I'd choose.
Dragonborn.esm|038B5C: Vampires are wretched beings. Feeding off of others the way they do... it's disgusting.
Dragonborn.esm|038B5D: Warlocks practice all sorts of foul sorcery. We should proceed with caution.
Dragonborn.esm|038B5E: Careful, it looks like something's lived here recently.
Dragonborn.esm|038B5F: Damn bandits are worse than the vermin that infest the ash wastes back home.
Dragonborn.esm|038B60: Dragons, eh? Good, I was looking for a challenge.
Dragonborn.esm|038B61: There are restless dead about... I suggest we proceed with caution.
Dragonborn.esm|038B62: Despite the foul beings that infest this place, I'm certain we'll find plenty of wealth to make it worth our time.
Dragonborn.esm|038B63: The dwarves were a clever race. I wonder what happened to them?
Dragonborn.esm|038B64: To think the Falmer are loosely related to the Dunmer sends shivers down my spine.
Dragonborn.esm|038B65: Not much of a farm. How do they expect to grow anything in this ash-free soil?
Dragonborn.esm|038B66: The Forsworn are little more than beasts. They won't listen to reason, so prepare for the worst.
Dragonborn.esm|038B67: Ugh, the stench. We must be nearing a giant's home.
Dragonborn.esm|038B68: Becoming a hagraven requires a sacrifice to their foul deity. Show them no mercy before we become one of the victims.
Dragonborn.esm|038B69: I never fancied becoming a miner... didn't want to spend the rest of my days cracking rocks.
Dragonborn.esm|038B6A: I was briefly in the employ of an orc warrior once. I had to quit because he refused to bathe. Disgusting.
Dragonborn.esm|038B6B: Nice place you have here. I never knew you were so wealthy.
Dragonborn.esm|038B6C: I wonder how many souls were lost when this ship met its fate.
Dragonborn.esm|038B6D: Spriggan are loathsome creatures. They summon some of the most benign beings of the forest and bend them to their will.
Dragonborn.esm|038B6E: What would possess someone to give their allegiance to Hircine, only to see their free will taken from them like this?
Dragonborn.esm|038B6F: I'm glad that I'm finally traveling with someone who seems competent. My last patron was killed trying to tackle an entire fort of bandits alone.
Dragonborn.esm|038B70: I grew up in the city of Blacklight in Morrowind. If you ever get the chance, you should visit there... it's spectacular.
Dragonborn.esm|038B71: I don't know why I decided to make my way to Solstheim to seek my fortune. I should have stayed home.
Dragonborn.esm|038B72: You like my chitin armor? It was specially crafted for me by Grendis Rolovo... one of the finest master armorsmiths in Morrowind.
Dragonborn.esm|038B73: All this walking... I wish there was a silt strider around here.
Dragonborn.esm|038B74: I wonder what became of Saint Jiub? I met him once, you know.
Dragonborn.esm|038B75: Our journey's been quite rewarding so far. Hopefully you won't mind sharing the spoils.
Dragonborn.esm|038E7F: The food is better up here, too.
Dragonborn.esm|038E90: It's colder down here. I don't like the cold.
Dragonborn.esm|038E91: We just keep waiting for Bujold to say the word...
Dragonborn.esm|038EBB: Oh, that's the stuff all right. You're a good friend to Thirsk, and to me. I thank you.
Dragonborn.esm|038EBC: Well, the easy answer, of course. Mead! Actually, now that I think of it, I have been missing the Ashfire. It's a special brew that we make at Thirsk. If you could... somehow... get up there and grab a bottle, I'd be a long way towards forgetting how miserable it is down here.
Dragonborn.esm|038EBD: Oh, perfect. Just the right ripeness, too. Good work, friend.
Dragonborn.esm|038EBE: We lost most of the batches we had brewing when... before those things were here. One is just ready to take its turn, though, and it's about time to add some of the flavorings. You can't find them on Solstheim, but if you find yourself back in Skyrim, I wouldn't mind you bringing some juniper berries. They add a different kind of sweetness. Tart. Makes the flavor more round.
Dragonborn.esm|038FB7: To tell you the truth, I've been a little distracted. I've got this... idea. I want to make a reminder. So we never forget what happened the last time we got soft. I want to ring the whole hall with their little spears. Mocking us, from the past. Making us work harder. Fight better. The only problem is, I'm going to need a lot of spears. A lot. So, if you could find a bunch of them for me, that would make it easier on me.
Dragonborn.esm|038FB8: Just in time, too. Kuvar was starting to give me some grief about his new armor. This will do the trick nicely. You come back if you ever want some fancier armor yourself, now.
Dragonborn.esm|038FB9: Cleaning out the forge was the easy part. But those blasted rieklings made off with most of my materials. And I can't forge air. You tend to wander around, don't you? If you come across any stalhrim or ebony in your travels, bring them back to old Halbarn. Help each other out.
Dragonborn.esm|038FBA: Oh, I'm sure it will be. Give them here, and I'll get to work soon!
Dragonborn.esm|03910C: I don't care how much you're paying me, there's no way I'm doing that.
Dragonborn.esm|039117: Raven Rock started out as an Imperial settlement, but the Empire decided to abandon it just as my people were first setting foot here. They left the town a shambles... but when House Redoran took over, we turned it around and it became ours. And even if it's been a struggle to make this island livable, we're proud to call it home.
Dragonborn.esm|039202: Hah! Not if Fanari has anything to say about it. When it comes to being chieftain, she is like a wolf with her jaws clamped tight upon a fresh kill. But that's alright. The Skaal need a shaman and I know that is my place.
Dragonborn.esm|039203: It is not right that I should say this, because I must honor my father's wishes, but this is a path I never chose. I always thought that I would be a great hunter, or even the village chieftain.
Dragonborn.esm|039204: It was Skaf the Giant, our former chieftan, who taught me the ways of battle. He was a great bear of a man, and a fearsome warrior. I used to be terrified of him when I was a little girl. I was a restless and angry child, and Skaf taught me swordplay so that I could, as he used to say, "put that fire to good use." He was a great man and a good leader. I miss him dearly.
Dragonborn.esm|039205: I have been preparing for it all my life. There is no question that I'm ready, but...
Dragonborn.esm|039206: We Skaal do not fear death. We know we will return to the All-Maker and be born anew. Life never truly ends. Death is just a passage from one form to the next.
Dragonborn.esm|039207: We are bound to this land, like a great pine with roots that run as deep as the tree is tall. I know it must seem strange to you, but for us, the thought of leaving our village is equally strange. The land and the Skaal are one. There is no other way to say it.
Dragonborn.esm|039208: My heart is still heavy with the loss of my father.
Dragonborn.esm|039209: My father was a noble man, and a true Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|03920A: My thoughts are always with my people.
Dragonborn.esm|03920B: Even now, the All-Maker watches over us.
Dragonborn.esm|03920D: No matter where the road takes us, my thoughts are of home.
Dragonborn.esm|03920E: Is it good to see more of the world, for wisdom is the child of experience.
Dragonborn.esm|03920F: Your fearlessness never ceases to impress me.
Dragonborn.esm|039211: She died when I was very young. She was caught in a terrible snowstorm while returning home from gathering firewood. You must understand that, for the Skaal, life is a constant hardship. We take nothing for granted. We cannot afford to. Between the wolves, the weather and the threats in the south, even a simple task like gathering wood for the fire can turn deadly in an instant.
Dragonborn.esm|03921D: That's definitely safer. I'm not sure I'll risk summoning an Ash Guardian again myself. Too easy to forget about the heart stone!
Dragonborn.esm|03921F: All right. But remember, don't cast this without a heart stone! Otherwise the guardian will attack everyone, including you.
Dragonborn.esm|039220: Please, you have to help me. Master Neloth is not patient or understanding. At all.
Dragonborn.esm|039222: Have you destroyed the Ash Guardian yet? Master Neloth will kill me... or worse... if he finds out!
Dragonborn.esm|039223: That's right. Don't be like me and have to learn the hard way. Here's the spell tome, and a heart stone so you can try it safely.
Dragonborn.esm|039225: Um. Well, he is a very great wizard. It's a great honor for him to have taken me as his apprentice. To be honest, it's horrible. He's very unpleasant and hardly teaches me anything. Sometimes he uses me in his experiments. Even so, I pick up a lot from just watching him.
Dragonborn.esm|039226: Heart stones are only found on Solstheim, as far as I know. Master Neloth believes they may be fragments from inside Red Mountain that were flung here during the huge eruption two hundred years ago.
Dragonborn.esm|039235: They attacked us. Those bastards at Frossel. Stole our treasure - wait! Forget I said that. That's my treasure. You can't have it!
Dragonborn.esm|039237: Must go back and get it.
Dragonborn.esm|039251: You've picked one of the worst places in Morrowind to do that, but suit yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|039252: Miraak... I... I'm not sure that I do.
Dragonborn.esm|039253: Until we ended up tracking him to one of the largest bandit encampments I'd ever seen. My patron took one look at me, and made the most menacing grin I'd ever seen. At that moment, I knew I'd never see him again. No amount of money would ever be worth that kind of death.
Dragonborn.esm|039254: Just remember, Raven Rock is sovereign territory of House Redoran. This is Morrowind, not Skyrim. While you're here you will be expected to abide by our laws. Any questions?
Dragonborn.esm|039255: Simple... he paid well. Very well. Anyway, we'd just cleared a ruined fort somewhere outside of Whiterun and when we got outside, we saw one of them galloping away on horseback. Well, my boss wasn't about to let him get away, so we pursued... on foot, for three days.
Dragonborn.esm|039256: Long story, but I don't see the harm in sharing it with you. My last patron was a true Nord. He was dressed in animal skins, had tattoos on his face...  a real traditional type, if you know what I mean. He had an insatiable bloodlust and was extremely stubborn... one of the toughest employers I've ever had.
Dragonborn.esm|039257: That's the kind of attitude that's going to land you in prison, but I'll let it go for now.
Dragonborn.esm|03925B: You sure showed Miraak who's in charge around here!
Dragonborn.esm|03925C: I can't tell you how relieved I am that Miraak's gone from my mind. I appreciate what you've done.
Dragonborn.esm|03925D: I know you risked a lot freeing me from Miraak's control. Thank you!
