Dragonborn.esm|03925E: Stealing a man's mind... it just isn't right. Thanks for saving me from that madman.
Dragonborn.esm|039717: Is that so? Well, you'll never get that treasure now!
Dragonborn.esm|039718: Boss said to bring you back or bring back your corpse.
Dragonborn.esm|039719: I'm not chasing that map. It's a fool's errand. I'm done.  Leave me alone.
Dragonborn.esm|03971C: Boss says you're to come back with me.
Dragonborn.esm|0397C3: It used to be one of the richest ebony mines in Morrowind, but it dried out years ago. Now, we're barely holding on out here. Without the mine, Raven Rock doesn't feel like it can last much longer.
Dragonborn.esm|0397DE: I can call you back to my tower as often as I like.
Dragonborn.esm|0397DF: Your death will not be that painless.
Dragonborn.esm|0397E0: Did you truly think to be rid of me so easily?
Dragonborn.esm|0397E2: You cannot hope to defeat me here.
Dragonborn.esm|0398A1: Well, now... a fellow beast-blood. Well met, friend. Not many of our kind still hunt these lands.
Dragonborn.esm|0398A2: Remember Majni's pack, at Frostmoon Crag. Now, I must be off. The blood calls.
Dragonborn.esm|0398D5: Hmm. Your eyes appear to be completely back to normal. Interesting.
Dragonborn.esm|0398D6: I do not think that is wise.
Dragonborn.esm|0398D8: You should leave those books alone.
Dragonborn.esm|0398DA: Do not let Herma-Mora tempt you further.
Dragonborn.esm|0398DC: Are you sure this is a good idea?
Dragonborn.esm|0398DD: I wish you would stop doing that.
Dragonborn.esm|0398DE: You won't catch me doing that.
Dragonborn.esm|0398DF: I'm never going to get used to that.
Dragonborn.esm|0398E1: I can't believe you do that on purpose.
Dragonborn.esm|0398E3: That can't be good for you.
Dragonborn.esm|039A66: The giant air-floaters from the dark elf lands. Only the brave dare to hunt them, only the strong survive them. Come. Test yourself against the beast with us. We all will share in the glory of the hunt!
Dragonborn.esm|039A6A: Luck? Luck will play no part today. We will earn our victory or return to the All-Maker.
Dragonborn.esm|039A6B: Well met, friend. May we all fight or die well.
Dragonborn.esm|039A6D: A warrior expects nothing, and therefore is ready for anything. If our aim and our hearts are true, the All-Maker will grant us strength. If not, today is as good a day to die as any other. Either way, they will sing songs of our bravery around the campfires for many years.
Dragonborn.esm|039A6E: We have come south to test our worth against the mighty Netch. Will you join us?
Dragonborn.esm|039A6F: The mighty beast will fall and we will rise.
Dragonborn.esm|039A70: We will test ourselves. May the All-Maker not find us wanting.
Dragonborn.esm|039A71: They will sing stories of this day.
Dragonborn.esm|039A72: We will honor and sing praises for the fallen.
Dragonborn.esm|039A73: The beast was not the only one to fall this day. May the All-Maker welcome our brethren with open arms.
Dragonborn.esm|039A74: Our victory will bring meat and bones to our people and glory to our families.
Dragonborn.esm|039A75: The beast was strong, but we were stronger.
Dragonborn.esm|039B31: Say hello to Hermaeus Mora for me.
Dragonborn.esm|039B32: Ah, yes, the lure of the Black Book.
Dragonborn.esm|039CF9: Of course! In fact, Councilor Morvayn has given you the full run of the place. Help yourself to whatever you like. We're all in agreement that you're entitled to as much as you can carry.
Dragonborn.esm|039CFA: It's good to get back to mining after all these years.
Dragonborn.esm|039CFB: This mine's amazing! Have you ever seen so much ebony in your life?
Dragonborn.esm|039CFC: I've been out of the business so long, I almost forgot how to swing a pickaxe.
Dragonborn.esm|039CFD: Good to know that the mine's safe again. I heard a lot of strange things happened here.
Dragonborn.esm|039CFF: Help yourself to as much ore as you like... you've earned it.
Dragonborn.esm|039D00: I haven't set foot in these mines for ages. It's strange being back after so long.
Dragonborn.esm|039D01: There's nothing quite like the sound of metal striking rock.
Dragonborn.esm|039D02: Nice to be making some coin again.
Dragonborn.esm|039D03: Thanks to House Redoran getting these mines reopened, I can provide for my family again.
Dragonborn.esm|039D04: Welcome to Raven Rock Mine, outlander.
Dragonborn.esm|039DC9: Now hold on! That wasn't my fault... I didn't know they were going to attack anybody. I don't even know how I got here.
Dragonborn.esm|039DCA: I already told you, I'm not going back to Solstheim.
Dragonborn.esm|039DCB: If you're looking for passage to Solstheim, too bad. I'm not going back there anymore.
Dragonborn.esm|039DCD: Alright, you have a point. Taking you back to find out who sent them is the least I can do. Besides, maybe you can put a stop to whatever's going on over there. I owe them a bit of payback myself.
Dragonborn.esm|039DCE: Look, I'm sorry about your troubles, but I've got to look out for myself. I'm not going to risk it.
Dragonborn.esm|039DCF: Surprised you've never heard of it. It's an island northeast of here. Has a small dark elf town called Raven Rock and a handful of Nords living in Skaal Village. But I'm not going back there. There's something wrong about that place.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD0: Well, here we are. Welcome to Raven Rock. Just... be careful here. Things aren't what they used to be.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD1: Sure. Yeah. That's me. Why? Who sent you?
Dragonborn.esm|039DD2: Well... a man's got to make a living, after all. Fine. We'll cast off immediately.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD3: That's a tempting offer... when you actually have the gold, I might be willing to consider it.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD4: Whatever you want. The answer isn't going to be any different later though.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD5: There's been something strange happening on Solstheim for a while, but this... this is something else. I hope you get to the bottom of it.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD7: It's hard to explain... I remember those people with the masks coming on board, then... Next thing I remember, I was here and they were gone. That's not right, losing whole days like that.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD8: There's been something strange going on there for a while, but after this... I'm done. I'm not going back to Solstheim.
Dragonborn.esm|039DD9: Hold on, now. I'm just a simple sailor. I never wanted to get mixed up in any of this. Alright, I'll take you. But we leave immediately.
Dragonborn.esm|039DDA: Yeah, well, and I don't want them trying to kill me. So the answer is still no.
Dragonborn.esm|039DDB: Have you been listening to me? I'm not going back there.
Dragonborn.esm|039DE4: We hunt not only to eat, but also to test our skill.
Dragonborn.esm|039DE5: The wise hunter is cautious, always.
Dragonborn.esm|039DE6: If you've come here to hunt, there is plenty of game to be found.
Dragonborn.esm|039DE7: If the All-Maker favors us, we will eat well for many days.
Dragonborn.esm|039DE8: Be wary! The reavers have camps all over the island.
Dragonborn.esm|039DE9: Most of the game has been driven north by the ashfall.
Dragonborn.esm|039DEB: May your eyes be sharp and your aim true.
Dragonborn.esm|039DEC: Be careful out there. Solstheim is full of dangers.
Dragonborn.esm|039DED: Peace and bountiful harvests to you.
Dragonborn.esm|039E4C: I... I can't believe it. All those years on the farm, and my mother never told me about any of this. Words can't even begin to describe how much I appreciate you bringing this to me. Here, take this. I used to carry it for good luck, but I think you deserve it more than I do.
Dragonborn.esm|039FBD: I don't know. But I mean to find out. I still have some research to do before I'll know anything for certain.
Dragonborn.esm|039FBE: Red Mountain dominates the island. Every now and then ash and debris from an eruption land on our shores.
Dragonborn.esm|039FBF: You are too young to remember the Nerevarine. He defeated Dagoth Ur and saved us all from the blight.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC0: When the city of Vivec was destroyed, it took decades for us to rebuild. We are still but a shadow of our glorious past, but some day we will rise again.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC1: The ignorant think it is only a place of ash and lava. It had rolling green plains and swamps and verdant hills too.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC2: There is the big island of Vvardenfell, and the coastal mainland. House Telvanni has it's properties on Vvardenfell. I've never been to the mainland myself.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC3: It is the only place where silt striders can be found. Although I managed to transplant a few here when I arrived.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC4: It's the homeland of the dunmer. The lesser races call us dark elves. We built great works there, like Vivec and Ald'ruhn. Many of them are destroyed now, either by Red Mountain or the argonian invasion.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC5: It was fascinating to watch those tentacles grow out of your eyes.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC6: Now that my tower is healed, I can finish some of my more delicate experiments.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC7: How do you like my new laboratory? I use it to dissect spriggans. I've learned so much from them.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC8: Talvas, I think I'll start with the burnt spriggan. They don't squirm as much.
Dragonborn.esm|039FC9: The ash from Red Mountain holds secrets. Secrets I mean to uncover.
Dragonborn.esm|039FCA: I hope Master Neloth can explain this.
Dragonborn.esm|039FCB: Those poor spriggans. Ever since you healed the tower, Master Neloth has been experimenting on them.
Dragonborn.esm|039FCC: Somehow Master Neloth found out about the Ash Guardian. I'm terrified of what he's going to do.
Dragonborn.esm|039FCD: This just isn't right. Why was I working on that stone?
Dragonborn.esm|039FCE: I've got to get back. Tel Mithryn is probably falling apart.
Dragonborn.esm|039FCF: Thank you for rescuing us from that awful stone pillar.
Dragonborn.esm|039FD1: I... I must have been drugged. That's it. Someone drugged me. Maybe one of Mogrul's cronies.
Dragonborn.esm|039FD2: There must be a way. I'll find it if I have to vivisect every spriggan on this island.
Dragonborn.esm|03A12A: He's off doing research in Raven Rock. I think. He told me all about it, but I can't seem to recall the details right now.
Dragonborn.esm|03A12B: The, uh... the lab is too small. Also, I need the ashy soil. Master Neloth told me to practice this spell while he's away. No need to tell him about it when he gets back. He knows all about this.
Dragonborn.esm|03A12C: I, uh... I'm going to be reading the spell book. Maybe it can tell me how to banish the Ash Guardian.
Dragonborn.esm|03A12D: I'm off to take up my new position with Master Neloth.
Dragonborn.esm|03A12E: Neloth may be crazy, but it's better than staying in Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|03A130: Shh! Master Neloth is concentrating. At least I think he is. He might be sleeping with his eyes open again.
Dragonborn.esm|03A131: Tel Mithryn. It's the home of Master Neloth. You may have heard of him. He's a famous Telvanni wizard. He's not here right now. I think he's in Raven Rock.
Dragonborn.esm|03A136: She fights well enough, for a Skaal.
Dragonborn.esm|03A139: Can't say I'd mind heading out of here. I still need to get paid, of course.
Dragonborn.esm|03A140: So you have slain Alduin... Well done. I could have slain him myself, back when I walked the earth, but I chose a different path.
Dragonborn.esm|03A141: So this is Raven Rock, eh? Not all that much to look at.
Dragonborn.esm|03A142: I've heard tales of the mushroom towers of Morrowind. Never thought I'd see one.
Dragonborn.esm|03A144: Hmm. Reminds me of the standing stones back home.
Dragonborn.esm|03A146: Do you have enough coin for the journey?
Dragonborn.esm|03A147: The Maiden doesn't move until I've been paid.
Dragonborn.esm|03A148: We sail as soon as I'm paid.
Dragonborn.esm|03A149: If you've got the gold, I'm your captain.
Dragonborn.esm|03A14A: My ship's ready to sail if you've brought enough gold.
Dragonborn.esm|03A14B: I came a long way to kill you.
Dragonborn.esm|03A14C: I don't plan on being killed by the likes of you.
Dragonborn.esm|03A14D: I don't have to know what you are to kill you.
Dragonborn.esm|03A14F: I don't know whether to kill it or pet it.
Dragonborn.esm|03A150: That's a nice trick, but it won't save your skin.
Dragonborn.esm|03A260: If you ever need aid, seek out Majni's pack, up at Frostmoon Crag. They've long welcomed outsiders. Now... my prey awaits. Good hunting to you.
Dragonborn.esm|03A29B: Heard that Reiklings got hold of Thirsk Mead Hall. Forced the Nords who lived there to move to a camp nearby.
Dragonborn.esm|03A29C: So the stories are true... the dragons have returned. But why?
Dragonborn.esm|03A29D: Dragons... even on Solstheim. I can't believe it.
Dragonborn.esm|03A29E: They're saying that the dragons have returned. As if we didn't have enough to worry about already.
Dragonborn.esm|03A29F: I've heard that dragons have been seen in the skies over Morrowind. I'll believe it when I see it for myself.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2A8: I know, you could almost buy a house for that much, but like I said... we've got quite the reputation out here. And coin is the only counterweight for a bad reputation.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2D9: Sadly, no. She died in Falkreath, where I was born.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2DA: Well, the dragons are a constant danger, but so far they have taken little interest in our village. Thank the All-Maker for that! But have they forced us to change the way we live our lives? No, not at all. They are but one more hardship added to the many we already endure.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F3: It's not your fault, Drovas. You do other things well. Varona was never able to do the repair work that you do.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F4: Unearthly screams? What have I gotten myself into.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F5: I can't help it if I have a black thumb. It's like the mushrooms hate me.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F6: Master Neloth is preparing to... well, it's better if you don't know. However, if you hear unearthly screams, I would advise you to not come up.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F7: I miss Varona. She used to help me with the gardening.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F8: Ah! The wood is for the staffs. Put them away when you get a chance. But don't get in my way.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2F9: She lives. Seek her in Highpoint Tower.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2FA: Master Neloth is the most powerful wizard of House Telvanni. Maybe the most powerful in all of Morrowind. Someday I will inherit all of this. It's worth a little pain.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2FB: Mostly books. There were a couple of soul gems, some ingredients for Elynea, and some strange wood.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2FC: I suppose you're right. At least here I'm safe from Mogrul.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2FD: I don't understand why you let him do these things to you.
Dragonborn.esm|03A2FE: What did they bring? Books? Soul gems?
Dragonborn.esm|03A2FF: Why are you out here? Master Neloth is away. Aren't you supposed to stay in the tower?
Dragonborn.esm|03A300: They're back to regular size. It didn't hurt nearly as much this time.
Dragonborn.esm|03A301: Master Neloth, the silt strider traders just dropped off some supplies.
Dragonborn.esm|03A302: You complain too much. Tel Mithryn is the safest place in Solstheim. Only a fool would challenge Master Neloth.
Dragonborn.esm|03A304: Yes, yes. Canis root tea. And don't let Ulves water it down.
Dragonborn.esm|03A305: Do you ever get lonely out here in the middle of nowhere?
Dragonborn.esm|03A306: I know, I know. So, unearthly screams, don't come up. Got it.
Dragonborn.esm|03A307: I'll go fetch some, Master Neloth. Canis root, right?
Dragonborn.esm|03A308: Not now. Wait until we are back at Tel Mithryn.
Dragonborn.esm|03A309: Thank you. I didn't mean to be so defensive. I know Varona was your friend. It's okay to miss her.
Dragonborn.esm|03A30A: You better get used to this, Drovas. This isn't that unusual for Tel Mithryn.
Dragonborn.esm|03A30C: If you're going to be up here, stay out of my way.
Dragonborn.esm|03A30D: Sometimes I feel sorry for them. Master Neloth's experiments tend to be fatal.
Dragonborn.esm|03A30E: I hate this ash. It gets into everything.
Dragonborn.esm|03A30F: Master Neloth certainly keeps me busy. Taking care of this estate is a lot of work.
Dragonborn.esm|03A310: Thank you for rescuing me from High Point tower.
Dragonborn.esm|03A311: I still have nightmares about what Ildari did to my friends.
Dragonborn.esm|03A33E: Anytime you want to head for Skyrim, I'm right with you. I've spent enough time on Solstheim to last a lifetime.
Dragonborn.esm|03A33F: Hope we don't run into too many ash spawn out here.
Dragonborn.esm|03A340: I'm not sure why House Redoran is so insistent on maintaining Solstheim. Seems like more trouble than it's worth.
Dragonborn.esm|03A341: With the ash from Red Mountain covering half the island, it's astonishing how much Solstheim reminds me of home.
Dragonborn.esm|03A342: How can the Skaal stand it up here? It's freezing!
Dragonborn.esm|03A343: The Telvanni are amazing. Look what they've grown from the ash... have you ever seen anything like it?
Dragonborn.esm|03A344: I've called Raven Rock my home for a number of years. Azura knows why... it's a mess.
Dragonborn.esm|03A345: Seems like things are looking up in Raven Rock now that the mine's open. It's a nice change.
Dragonborn.esm|03A3D8: I've been sailing the Northern Maiden across the Sea of Ghosts for nearly twenty years now. Might be time to find a new home port, though, the way things are going around here.
Dragonborn.esm|03A3DA: I... I understand. That's not an existence I would wish on anyone. I hope you gave him a swift end.
Dragonborn.esm|03A43B: It's true that I normally prefer to stay home and conduct my research in peace. Everything's so much more convenient there. But, given that something seems to be taking control of the minds of all the inhabitants, I thought it worth investigating.
Dragonborn.esm|03A461: Hot fire. Cold dizzydrink. Happy tribe.
Dragonborn.esm|03A465: Tribe-kin live all places. All of world within great water.
Dragonborn.esm|03A46B: Were we... did you help us?
Dragonborn.esm|03A46C: Why are we up here? The smell is just... ugh.
Dragonborn.esm|03A46D: Thanks for taking that thing down. We're supposed to be warriors, not crafters.
Dragonborn.esm|03A46E: Glad to not be working on that ugly thing anymore. Finally getting some sleep.
Dragonborn.esm|03A46F: I'd rather make mead than ugly shrines. You did good getting us away from there.
Dragonborn.esm|03A475: I am sorry, but my duty is to my people right now.
Dragonborn.esm|03A47E: I suppose Master Neloth won't mind. He's always telling me to get out of his way.
Dragonborn.esm|03A496: You must fight against what is controlling you!
Dragonborn.esm|03A498: Ysra, I'm here to help you!
Dragonborn.esm|03A499: It is not safe here! We must go back to the village!
Dragonborn.esm|03A4A6: Well, first you'll need to obtain some Stalhrim, of course, and then you'll need to work it.
Dragonborn.esm|03A4A7: With skill and patience. It's very much like working ebony. Yes... I'd say if you know how to forge ebony, then you know everything you need to know in order to forge Stalhrim.
Dragonborn.esm|03A4A8: You can find some in Raven Rock Mine, as well as that new source of Stalhrim on the map you recovered from the elves. Just be sure to bring an ancient Nord pickaxe with you. It's the only pickaxe that can mine the Stalhrim properly without breaking it.
Dragonborn.esm|03C0DA: It has been an honor, Skaal-friend.
Dragonborn.esm|047AD6: Fine! I'll take care of it, just please leave me alone!
Dragonborn.esm|068FE7: I used to be an adventurer in my day, then I settled down. Got married.
Dragonborn.esm|075CA3: You've embarrassed the temple and spat in the face of love. What do you want?
Dragonborn.esm|07A50B: I'd best get back to Master Neloth then. No telling what's happened since I've been gone.
Dragonborn.esm|085506: I think I can teach you a thing or two.
Dragonborn.esm|094052: Want me to prove it? I bet a hundred gold I can take you, bare-handed.
Dragonborn.esm|09408A: I'm the best warrior in Morthal, and that's no boast.
Dragonborn.esm|0940C7: Come back when you have the coin.
Dragonborn.esm|0AB81D: Whenever a group of marauders attack a Nord village, Ulfric is the first to sound the horn and send the men. But a group of Dark Elf refugees gets ambushed? A group of Argonians, or a Khajiit caravan? No troops. No investigation. Nothing. There's a group of cutthroats out there right now that Ulfric doesn't lift a finger to bring to justice, as long as they don't threaten Nord land.
Dragonborn.esm|0AB822: That's a brave offer. I'd be happy to throw in my coin behind that. You deal with them, I'll make sure you get paid. Maybe this old soldier will throw in some lessons as well.
Dragonborn.esm|0AB859: Now that's good work. Everyone will be a lot safer with them gone, Dark Elf and Nord alike. Here, some gold as promised, and let me show you a bit about wearing heavy armor. Save you some aches on a long march.
Dragonborn.esm|0AD08B: Show those marauders what Windhelm justice tastes like.
Dragonborn.esm|0C278A: I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD86F: An Amulet of Mara? You're not married? Surprising.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD870: That amulet. I've seen the Nords wear these when they're looking to marry. With your strength, you should have no trouble.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD871: Is that Lady Mara's amulet? I'm shocked a fine person like you isn't married already.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD872: You're wearing Mara's amulet? You? I'd marry you. I'd marry you twice.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD873: Look at you. Wearing an Amulet of Mara. Are you saying you're available?
Dragonborn.esm|0CD874: An Amulet of Mara, you're looking for marriage, then?
Dragonborn.esm|0CD875: You go ahead and arrange the wedding ceremony when you're ready.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD876: You should speak to the priest in the Temple of Mara to arrange the ceremony. No sense waiting.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD877: Have you spoken to the Temple of Mara, yet? I'm so excited.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD878: Hey, shouldn't you tell the Temple of Mara that we're getting married? Be hard to have a wedding without a priest.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD879: Now shouldn't you be off arranging our wedding day? Don't keep me waiting.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD87A: You should tell the priest we're getting married. They'll take things from there.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD87B: I'll see you at our wedding!
Dragonborn.esm|0CD87C: Our wedding is soon. I'll see you then!
Dragonborn.esm|0CD87D: Never thought I'd get married under a Nord god. But with you, it feels right.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD87E: Our wedding is soon, under Mara's loving eyes. It'll be perfect.
Dragonborn.esm|0CD87F: We have our wedding soon, don't we? That's right, we do!
Dragonborn.esm|0CD880: I'm looking forward to our wedding. You and me....
Dragonborn.esm|0CD881: The wedding is soon. It'll be a good day.
Dragonborn.esm|0CDA1A: Won't lie, I am. And you?
Dragonborn.esm|0D8DC1: What do you need to take?
Dragonborn.esm|0D8DD4: I'm heading home if you need me.
Dragonborn.esm|0D8E1C: Go on ahead. I'll stand guard.
Dragonborn.esm|0E3A3C: I'll show you what I can about protecting yourself.
Dragonborn.esm|0E3A47: It's all in the swing. Let me show you.
Dragonborn.esm|0F07B9: Ugh. You mangy piece of pit-bait.
Dragonborn.esm|0FE6F5: I'm sorry, but I'm not going to do something like that. Even for you.
Dragonborn.esm|0FE6F7: You already appear to have someone with you.
Dragonborn.esm|0FE6F8: I'm sorry, that can't be done.
HearthFires.esm|000888: Maybe you want to read this first.
HearthFires.esm|000889: Hold on. We didn't know it was you we were messing with. Look, we'll let her go and call it even, all right? No need for any more trouble.
HearthFires.esm|00088A: Hold on. We didn't know it was you we were messing with. Look, we'll let him go and call it even, all right? No need for any more trouble.
HearthFires.esm|00088B: Is that how you want to play it, huh? That's fine with me.
HearthFires.esm|00088C: Well if you don't have any money, what are we standing here talking about?
HearthFires.esm|00088D: Relax, hero. She's fine. At least she was. The chief liked her so much, she took her back home. Here's a letter for you.
HearthFires.esm|00088E: Relax, hero... he's fine. At least he was. The chief liked him so much, she took him back home. Here's a letter for you.
HearthFires.esm|000890: I'll try to hold them off!
HearthFires.esm|000891: You won't enter this house while I live!
HearthFires.esm|000892: You're stupid. All we wanted was money! They're gonna cut his throat now!
HearthFires.esm|000893: You're stupid. All we wanted was money! They're gonna cut her throat now!
HearthFires.esm|000895: Well if you don't have any money, what are we standing here talking about?
HearthFires.esm|000896: Well if you don't have any money, what are we standing here talking about?
HearthFires.esm|000897: Could you take a look in the basement? I've been hearing strange noises down there lately.
HearthFires.esm|000898: You look like the type who'd rather let your girlfriend die than fork over some gold. And people think we're heartless monsters...
HearthFires.esm|000899: You look like the type who'd rather let your boyfriend die than fork over some gold. And people think we're heartless monsters...
HearthFires.esm|00089A: Don't worry, we're not monsters. She's fine. We get the money, you get the girl. Everybody wins, right?
HearthFires.esm|00089B: Don't worry, we're not monsters. He's fine. We get the money, you get the guy. Everybody wins, right?
HearthFires.esm|00089C: Don't worry, she's on her way. Just be patient for a minute.
HearthFires.esm|00089D: Don't worry, he's on his way. Just be patient for a minute.
HearthFires.esm|00089E: Well, well. You're a lot smarter than I expected. I hope you brought the gold.
HearthFires.esm|00089F: The chief wanted me to tell you... your girlfriend was real nice to us while you were gone.
HearthFires.esm|0008A0: The chief wanted me to tell you... your boyfriend was real nice to us while you were gone.
HearthFires.esm|0008A1: You gotta talk to the chief. Rochelle.
HearthFires.esm|0008A2: I just want to get out of here!
HearthFires.esm|0008A3: I knew you'd come for me!
HearthFires.esm|0008A5: I'm glad to see you! Let's get out of here!
HearthFires.esm|0008A6: I think there's something in the basement. I'm afraid to go down there.
HearthFires.esm|0008BC: Have it your way. It isn't worth it. There are easier ways to make money.
HearthFires.esm|0008BE: You don't want to be bothering me.
HearthFires.esm|0008BF: I think you're looking for the chief.
HearthFires.esm|0008C0: You'd better be on your way now.
HearthFires.esm|0008C2: I can't wait to get back home.
HearthFires.esm|0008C8: You don't get a second chance with me.
HearthFires.esm|000973: I knew you'd come for me!
HearthFires.esm|000997: Really? You mean it? Wow, thanks, Ma! I promise, I won't be any trouble at all!
HearthFires.esm|000998: Really? You mean it? Wow, thanks, Pa! I promise, I won't be any trouble at all!
HearthFires.esm|000C2E: Then by my right as Jarl, I grant you a title to the land.
HearthFires.esm|000C2F: Hm. Well, if you change your mind, come see me again.
HearthFires.esm|000C31: No, I'm sorry. But there is land, available to you for purchase.
HearthFires.esm|000C37: Oh yes, of course. It's available to you for purchase.
HearthFires.esm|000C50: Let me know if you change your mind.
HearthFires.esm|000C51: Of course. It's available, if you want to buy it.
HearthFires.esm|000C53: No, I'm afraid not. But there is land if you want to buy it.
HearthFires.esm|000C55: Excellent. Then I grant you a title to the land.
HearthFires.esm|002DA3: I found something for you. It... sounds like things have been bad lately, so... I hope this cheers you up.
HearthFires.esm|002F4F: I was just getting settled in. Where are we going?
HearthFires.esm|002F50: Oh really? And where were you thinking?
HearthFires.esm|002FF7: A fine steading like this needs a good steward. I'd be happy to take on that position, if you'd like.
HearthFires.esm|002FFB: Ah, I see. Well, it's possible that the property will still be available when you amass the necessary funds.
HearthFires.esm|003000: You won't regret it. Here's the title to your steading. Follow the road east from Falkreath, then north at the crossroads. Turn left just past Pinewatch.
HearthFires.esm|003006: Of course. I didn't mean to meddle in your private affairs.
HearthFires.esm|003007: Ah, excellent. The Jarl was hoping you would be interested. It's a fine property. Well-watered, plentiful stands of timber, beautiful view.
HearthFires.esm|003008: There's no house available, but I could sell you a fine piece of land where you could build your own house.
HearthFires.esm|003009: Yes. It's a fine property. Well-watered, plentiful stands of timber, beautiful view.
HearthFires.esm|00300A: Thank you! Let me know if you have anything you need from me. I'm at your service.
HearthFires.esm|003D81: Congratulations! If you ever want to adopt another child, you'll always be welcome here.
HearthFires.esm|003D86: I'm running food and supplies to the miners. Don't pay much, but they let me sleep by the fire in the inn most nights.
HearthFires.esm|003D89: What do you do for a living?
HearthFires.esm|003D98: They're... dead. My pa was a sailor. Took me with him on his last trip. After he got sick, they... put us off here and left without us. He... died a few days later. I've tried to make do since then, but... it's been hard. Real hard.
HearthFires.esm|003DA0: Congratulations. I hope you have a wonderful life together.
HearthFires.esm|003DA3: First, I'll need to ask you a few questions.
HearthFires.esm|003DB6: Go on, speak to the children! I'm sure they're eager to meet you.
HearthFires.esm|003DBA: That's all I need from you. Congratulations! I think you'll make an excellent parent. Go meet the children and decide who you'd like to adopt. Once you've made a decision, I'll collect their things and be sure they make it over to your house.
HearthFires.esm|003DBE: Not much. But I hope you like them!
HearthFires.esm|003DBF: They... they're dead. My mama died when I was little. I... I don't remember her very well. My father was a Stormcloak soldier. One day, he left and... didn't come back. I'm all alone. I... I try to sell flowers so I can buy food. It's not much but... what else can I do?
HearthFires.esm|003DC0: Both my parents were in the Legion. There was... an ambush. Katla said she'd feed me if I could make myself useful. I take care of the animals, run errands, that kind of thing. I guess it could be worse. But... I'm sick of sleeping outside with the horses. I want a real home, real parents. Not... this.
HearthFires.esm|003DC3: What... what am I going to do?
HearthFires.esm|003DCA: Yes, of course! That's wonderful news!
HearthFires.esm|003DD3: Okay! You stand right there and count to 10... no peeking! Ready? ...Go!
HearthFires.esm|003DD4: I'm sorry, but until you own a home with a room for your children, I can't allow you to adopt anyone.
HearthFires.esm|003DD7: Where will your child be living?
HearthFires.esm|003DD9: I'm sorry, no. Grelod is the headmistress here, and she's decided not to allow any adoptions right now. I... I wish things were otherwise. All of these children deserve a better home. But there's nothing I can do.
HearthFires.esm|003DDA: Why yes, yes of course! What a wonderful surprise!
HearthFires.esm|003DDB: Very well, let's try this again...
HearthFires.esm|003DDC: You already have children, don't you? I'm sorry, but I can't let you adopt anyone else.
HearthFires.esm|003DDF: Thanks... thanks for talking to me.
HearthFires.esm|003DE5: Here's a letter from the Jarl's steward. Looks like official business.
HearthFires.esm|003DE6: There's a new lady in charge of the Orphanage in Riften. She asked me to hand out these notices to everyone I met.
HearthFires.esm|003E09: Really? Are you... are you sure?
HearthFires.esm|003E0B: Hi Mister! Would you like to buy some flowers? Please?
HearthFires.esm|003E0C: Hi Lady! Would you like to buy some flowers? Please?
HearthFires.esm|003E16: I hate it. Everyone feels sorry for me, so they don't treat me too bad, but...
HearthFires.esm|003E19: What? If you aren't going to be... ...wait. Shor, you're serious! Well, I... all right then.
HearthFires.esm|003E1A: What? What kind of a joke is this? Look, don't waste my time. Come back when you're ready to discuss this for real.
HearthFires.esm|003E1D: ...Look, let's be realistic. You're an adventurer. It's not exactly a safe or reliable profession. I need to be sure your child will have a safe, stable home to grow up in. Show me that you can support a child. Buy a house. Furnish it. Then come back, and we'll talk.
HearthFires.esm|003E1E: My mother and father brought me here a year ago. They said they'd be back soon, but... they're awfully late. I... I guess it would be all right if you want to adopt me. At least until my real parents get back.
HearthFires.esm|003E1F: Name's Samuel. Never met my parents. Grelod said my mother worked here at the Orphanage, but she died when I was born. Constance and the others are nice enough, but I... I want to have a real family.
HearthFires.esm|003E20: I'm Hroar. You know, like the sound the lion makes? That's what my mother used to say. I want to be big and strong like a lion, too. Someday.
HearthFires.esm|003E22: I've been the only girl here since forever. The boys pick on me, and they make me do all the chores. You want to adopt one of us, right? I'm strong, I'm fast, I'm smart, I can do anything better than them. Come on, pick me? Please?
HearthFires.esm|003E31: We're married. I guess we'll need to decide where we're living, won't we? You can always stay with me.
HearthFires.esm|003E32: I guess we have our entire lives together, now. Where should we live? You could stay with me....
HearthFires.esm|003E34: Please, won't you buy a flower?
HearthFires.esm|003E36: No one bought any flowers again today. I... I don't know what to do.
HearthFires.esm|003E3C: Mister, could you spare a coin?
HearthFires.esm|003E3D: Lady, could you spare a coin?
HearthFires.esm|003E40: Talk to Geimund if you want a horse, or Katla if you're looking for work. I can't help you.
HearthFires.esm|003E41: I take care of the horses. And the goats. And the chickens. And the garden.
HearthFires.esm|003E42: You probably want someone else. I'm just the stable boy.
HearthFires.esm|003E43: My... my parents died, and... and now I'm all alone...
HearthFires.esm|003E44: Mama died, and now I'm... I'm all alone...
HearthFires.esm|003E45: My papa died and... and I'm all alone...
HearthFires.esm|003E46: My... my parents died, not too long ago. I... I don't know what to do...
HearthFires.esm|003E47: My pa died, and... and now I'm all alone.
HearthFires.esm|003E48: My ma died, and... and I don't know what to do...
HearthFires.esm|003E49: Really? Do you... do you have a place I could live?
HearthFires.esm|003E4A: Oh, wow! Are you... are you sure? You really wouldn't mind?
HearthFires.esm|003E4D: Yea! Thanks, Mama! I promise, I'll be the best daughter ever!
HearthFires.esm|003E4E: Yea! Thanks, mister- um, Papa! I promise, I'll be the best daughter ever!
HearthFires.esm|003E5F: We face the world together now. Where should we live? My home is yours.
HearthFires.esm|003E62: This is the happiest day of my life. Where should we live? My home is your home now, of course.
HearthFires.esm|003E64: Some pretty flowers. I picked them all by myself!
HearthFires.esm|003E65: Could you give me an allowance?
HearthFires.esm|003E66: Could I get a few gold to spend? Please?
HearthFires.esm|003E67: I got you a present, Mama! I hope you like it!
HearthFires.esm|003E68: I got you a present, Papa! I hope you like it!
HearthFires.esm|003E69: Hey, Pa, I got you something! I hope you like it!
HearthFires.esm|003E6A: I got you a present, Ma! I hope you like it!
HearthFires.esm|003E6B: Hey, I got you something. Hope you like it!
HearthFires.esm|003E6C: Look what I found! Here, take it!
HearthFires.esm|003E6D: I know we don't have much money, but I... I wanted to get you something. It's not much, but...
HearthFires.esm|003E6F: Hey, Pa! You want to play a game with us?
HearthFires.esm|003E70: Hey, Ma! Want to play a game with us?
HearthFires.esm|003E71: Hey, Pa! You want to play a game with us?
HearthFires.esm|003E72: Hey, Ma! Want to play a game with us?
HearthFires.esm|003E73: Papa! We're playing a game. Do you want to play?
HearthFires.esm|003E74: Mama! We're playing a game. Do you want to play?
HearthFires.esm|003EA6: It's... it's what Brenuin said I should do. He's the only one that's been nice to me since... since mama... ...since she died. My aunt and uncle took over our farm and threw me out. Said I wasn't good for anything. I wound up here, but... I... I don't know what to do. I miss her so much...
HearthFires.esm|003EBA: Oh, but... you have children, don't you? We'll have to find somewhere with room for them.
HearthFires.esm|003EBB: You have children, though, don't you? We'll have to find somewhere with room for them, too.
HearthFires.esm|003EBC: But you have kids, right? We should find a home where we can live together.
HearthFires.esm|003EBF: We should find a place with room for your kids, though.
HearthFires.esm|003ECE: All right, I'll see you there!
HearthFires.esm|003ED6: Aww... come on. Just a few more minutes? Please?
HearthFires.esm|003ED7: But it's not even dark yet!
HearthFires.esm|003EDA: What do you want to play?
HearthFires.esm|003EDB: Sure! What do you want to play?
HearthFires.esm|003EDE: Wha... seriously? No way... With this, I... I could buy everything in the market! Wow! Thanks!
HearthFires.esm|003EDF: Wha? But.... but it's dark out. Is it... really okay?
HearthFires.esm|003EE0: Pick one of the others. I'll be fine.
HearthFires.esm|003EE1: Are you really gonna adopt one of us?
HearthFires.esm|003EE2: You're here to adopt someone? Really? Oh, wow!
HearthFires.esm|003EE5: This war has created so many orphans, and so few are willing to open their hearts to them...
HearthFires.esm|003EEF: Ma took me to the market. We saw this huge fish! I bet even you couldn't lift it!
HearthFires.esm|003EF1: Pa said he'd take me hunting soon. I can't wait! I bet I'll bring down a mammoth!
HearthFires.esm|003EF4: Mama took me to the market! There were lot of pies and cakes there. She said she'd show me how to bake one!
HearthFires.esm|003EFE: I need to get my things and say goodbye to everyone. I'll meet you at home. Thanks! Thanks so much!
HearthFires.esm|003EFF: Just... just let me get my things and say goodbye to everyone. I'll meet you at home. Thanks! Thanks again!
HearthFires.esm|003F01: Wow! I can't wait to see my new home!
HearthFires.esm|003F02: A new home! Wow! I can't wait to see it!
HearthFires.esm|0042C1: You came to visit! It's so much better here with Grelod gone. Thanks to you.
HearthFires.esm|007023: I'd be honored to be your steward. You've built a fine steading here.
HearthFires.esm|007024: I'll see to it at once.
HearthFires.esm|007025: I'll keep an eye out for a good cow to purchase.
HearthFires.esm|007026: Very good. I'll see about hiring a carriage driver at once.
HearthFires.esm|007A8B: Ah, good. There's one I've had my eye on. I'll see if it's still for sale.
HearthFires.esm|007B3B: Did you get me a present?
HearthFires.esm|007B66: You got me a present? Wow, thanks, Ma!
HearthFires.esm|007B67: You got me a present? Wow, thanks, Pa!
HearthFires.esm|007B6A: What is it? What is it?
HearthFires.esm|007B6B: You got me a present? Really?
HearthFires.esm|007B6C: Wow, really? What'd you get me?
HearthFires.esm|00823C: Certainly. What did you have in mind?
HearthFires.esm|008773: Grimvar said a boy used to live in that abandoned house, all alone, 'til the Dark Brotherhood came 'n got him. Scary, huh?
HearthFires.esm|008774: I'm gonna join the Stormcloaks. They're the best!
HearthFires.esm|008775: The Argonians at the docks say they can breathe underwater. I wish I could do that...
HearthFires.esm|008776: Banning said I could come play with his dogs. I want a dog.
HearthFires.esm|008777: I climbed all the way up to the temple, but they said I wasn't old enough to get in. They're just mean.
HearthFires.esm|008778: I hate Markarth. There's nowhere to play! It's all just rocks. Rocks rocks rocks.
HearthFires.esm|008779: Aicantar let me see his museum. It's really nice.
HearthFires.esm|00877B: Mister Brynjolf gave me an apple, Papa! He seems nice.
HearthFires.esm|00877C: There are lots of scary people here, Mama. Promise you'll keep me safe?
HearthFires.esm|00877D: Maramal said I could visit the temple whenever I wanted.
HearthFires.esm|00877E: Mister Brynjolf said he could teach me a lot of neat things when I'm older.
HearthFires.esm|00877F: I wanna go swimming, but the guards keep yelling at me. The canal's not that dirty...
HearthFires.esm|008781: I like it here. The tree in the park is so pretty!
HearthFires.esm|008782: That big tree in the park looks so sad... I feel sorry for it.
HearthFires.esm|008783: Braith is a big meanie. She keeps telling Lars 'n me what to do. I don't wanna play with her anymore.
HearthFires.esm|008784: I played with Lucia in the park today. I feel sorry for her.
HearthFires.esm|008785: Lars is such a milk-drinker. He can't even stand up to a girl.
HearthFires.esm|008786: Dragonsreach is so cool! All the kids there are mean, though.
HearthFires.esm|008909: Make it stop! Please, make it stop!
HearthFires.esm|00890B: I... I'll be all right. Please, just... just make it stop!
HearthFires.esm|00890D: Papa said we could go fishing! With my help, I'm sure he'll catch something this time!
HearthFires.esm|00890E: When I grow up, I wanna join the Legion!
HearthFires.esm|00890F: The Palace looks so pretty. I wish they'd let me inside...
HearthFires.esm|008911: Do you think I could be a Bard? I really like singing.
HearthFires.esm|008912: I saw a Thalmor the other day. He was really scary!
HearthFires.esm|008913: Rolff says I shouldn't go to the Grey Quarter anymore. He said it's not safe there.
HearthFires.esm|008915: Did you really build this house all by yourself? Wow!
HearthFires.esm|008916: I went fishing at the lake. I almost caught something!
HearthFires.esm|008917: I like living here. The lake is so pretty.
HearthFires.esm|008919: This place is scary. There are monsters in the swamp. I just know it!
HearthFires.esm|00891A: Look at all the snow! I want to go sledding! Or skating!
HearthFires.esm|00891B: It's so c-c-cold here! If I play outside for too long, I'm going to freeze solid!
HearthFires.esm|00891C: Oh, fine. So many guards on the street here I hardly have to worry.
HearthFires.esm|00891D: Hmph. This city's crawling with filth. It's everything I can do keep the children safe.
HearthFires.esm|00891E: You tell me. Between the Forsworn and the mines, Markarth is no place to raise a child.
HearthFires.esm|00891F: Oh, fine, if you want to bring up little pickpockets. This place is a terrible influence on a child.
HearthFires.esm|008920: As well as can be expected. Dear, this house would work for the two of us, but with children? We need to find a bigger place.
HearthFires.esm|008921: Fine, when we're not fending off attacks by wolves. Or bears. Or giant spiders. Honestly, what were you thinking, sending us out here?
HearthFires.esm|008922: Very well, love. This is a beautiful house, and Solitude is so safe, so diverse. It's a wonderful place to raise a child.
HearthFires.esm|008923: Well, love, very well. There's so much history in this city, so many things we can do here.
HearthFires.esm|008924: All right. I do worry, though, no matter how many times the guards say Markarth is safe.
HearthFires.esm|008925: Just fine, love. The weather here is wonderful, perfect for playing outdoors.
HearthFires.esm|008926: Just fine. Whiterun is a lovely city, and there are so many other children here- they all love playing together.
HearthFires.esm|008927: Fine, love. I think the fresh air does wonders for all of us, and this house... it's just perfect.
HearthFires.esm|008928: Fine, just fine. This house is perfect for a growing family.
HearthFires.esm|008929: Great. There's so much history here in Windhelm, so much to learn about our ancestors and our land.
HearthFires.esm|00892A: Honestly, I'm a little worried. Markarth is nice enough, but... I wonder if it's really safe for children.
HearthFires.esm|00892B: Well enough. Riften's a tough city, but... it's a tough world. Kid has to learn to stand up for themselves sometime.
HearthFires.esm|00892C: Couldn't be better. Whiterun is perfect for a child- great weather, plenty of space, friends to play with. What more could you want?
HearthFires.esm|00892D: Fine, fine. Living out here on our own will take some getting used to, but I think it'll be good for us.
HearthFires.esm|00892E: Meh. Children get soft when they're coddled in a place like this.
HearthFires.esm|00892F: Good. This is a city that respects strength. Honors it. A good lesson for children to learn.
HearthFires.esm|008930: Fine, fine. This city's not safe, but... that's good. Keeps a kid on edge, alert. Could keep 'em alive when they grow up.
HearthFires.esm|008931: Hmph. This city's crawling with thieves. Cowards. Kid could learn some bad habits here.
HearthFires.esm|008932: Fine, fine. Plenty of kids here to play and spar with. Plenty of trades to learn. It's a good place to raise a child.
HearthFires.esm|008933: Better than ever. Nothing like living off the land to teach you how to fight, how to survive. We'll have a little warrior on our hands in no time.
HearthFires.esm|0094F9: Of course. What are we running short of?
HearthFires.esm|0094FA: Is there anything else you need?
HearthFires.esm|0094FD: Ah. Unfortunately, I'm afraid you're a bit short of funds at the moment.
HearthFires.esm|009B81: Doing some building, eh? We can deliver it wherever you need.
HearthFires.esm|009B82: When you've actually got the coin, we can do business.
HearthFires.esm|009B83: I'll have them hauled over right away.
HearthFires.esm|009B85: Sure thing, friend. I can use a break anyway.
HearthFires.esm|009B86: Sure thing, friend. I'll even have it delivered to your building site, no charge.
HearthFires.esm|009B87: I can't just give away good timber.
HearthFires.esm|00B0B0: Of course. What room would you like me to furnish?
HearthFires.esm|00B0B1: Very good, I'll make all the arrangements.
HearthFires.esm|00B0B2: I'm sorry, but you can't afford that right now.
HearthFires.esm|00BE02: Well, that's your business. Was there anything else you needed?
HearthFires.esm|00BE03: Excellent. We're glad to have you as a landowner in Hjaalmarch. Here's the title to your steading. If you don't mind getting your feet wet, your best bet is to head north past the mill. The odd tomb or two along the way won't bother you. The land is on the coast, across the Karth delta from Solitude. An inspiring location, as you'll see.
HearthFires.esm|00BE06: Yes, it is. The Jarl thought of you when it came up for sale. It's on the north coast of Hjaalmarch, with a magnificent view of Solitude on its crag. Just the place for a new manor house.
HearthFires.esm|00BE07: There aren't any houses for sale in Morthal. But if you're interested, I could sell you land in the hold where you could build your own house.
HearthFires.esm|00BE08: Yes. It's on the north coast of Hjaalmarch, with a magnificent view of Solitude on its crag. Just the place for a new manor house.
HearthFires.esm|00CD2E: Certainly. I'll have it delivered to the chest near your workbenches.
HearthFires.esm|00CD2F: Hmm. You might try Gray Pine Goods in Falkreath. They carry all sorts of things like that.
HearthFires.esm|00CD30: Bits and Pieces in Solitude is probably the place to start. They sell all sorts of things like that.
HearthFires.esm|00CD31: You know Belethor's shop in Whiterun? That'd be a good place to start. He carries all sorts of things like that.
HearthFires.esm|00F9A5: You'll need to build a pen for it first.
HearthFires.esm|00F9A6: I think you'll want a stable before we spend good coin on a horse.
HearthFires.esm|010A2E: I think we may have a problem in the basement. Been hearing a lot of scurrying down there lately.
HearthFires.esm|011CB4: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Hjaalmarch. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl, and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
HearthFires.esm|011CB5: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of the Pale. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl, and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
HearthFires.esm|011CB6: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Falkreath. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl, and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
HearthFires.esm|013ED9: Look, Papa! I made a new friend!
HearthFires.esm|013EDA: Look, Mama! I have a new friend!
HearthFires.esm|013EDB: Pa! Look what followed me home!
HearthFires.esm|013EDC: Ma! Look what followed me home!
HearthFires.esm|013EDE: I'll never speak to you again!
HearthFires.esm|013EDF: Why... why did you do that?
HearthFires.esm|013EE5: Do you... do you think I could keep him for you?
HearthFires.esm|013EE6: Do you... do you think I could keep him?
HearthFires.esm|013EE7: Could I... could I keep him? Please?
HearthFires.esm|013EF1: A doggy! Papa, I didn't know you had a doggy! He's so cute...
HearthFires.esm|013EF2: A doggy! Mama, I didn't know you had a doggy! He's so cute...
HearthFires.esm|013EF3: A dog! Wow, I didn't know you had a dog!
HearthFires.esm|013EF5: A pet! Wow, I didn't know you had a pet!
HearthFires.esm|0140FD: I'd... I'd really like a dolly. Or maybe a pretty new dress?
HearthFires.esm|0140FE: Well, sweets are always good. Or a new outfit. Or... or maybe a wooden sword? All my friends have swords.
HearthFires.esm|014100: Constance takes good care of us. But... I still want a real home. A real family.
HearthFires.esm|014101: It's not too bad here. But... I still miss my parents.
HearthFires.esm|014103: I hate it here. I... I wanna go home.
HearthFires.esm|014105: Are you... are you going to adopt one of us?
HearthFires.esm|014106: Well... I heard there's a really nice tailor's in Solitude. And... and maybe the Khajiit sell dolls? They always have the best toys.
HearthFires.esm|014107: I'm sure the Khajiit would have one- their toys are the best! For clothes... there's a tailor's shop in Solitude. I'm sure they'd have lots to choose from.
HearthFires.esm|014108: This is... this is great! It's everything I could have asked for!
HearthFires.esm|014109: It's... it's everything I could have hoped for.
HearthFires.esm|01410A: A real home. A real family. I almost can't believe it.
HearthFires.esm|01410B: I... I have a home again. A family. I almost can't believe it.
HearthFires.esm|01410F: I have a family again. I... I can't thank you enough.
HearthFires.esm|014110: This is more than I ever could have hoped for. Thanks. Thanks so much.
HearthFires.esm|014113: I... I know it's not much, but... I've found a few little things. I've put them in the chest in my room. If you see anything you want, take it. You've done so much for me... it's the least I can do.
HearthFires.esm|014114: I... I know it's not much, but... I've found a few pretty things. They're in the chest in my room. If you see anything you like, please, take it! You've done so much for me. It's... it's the least I can do.
HearthFires.esm|015D0E: Lead on, Thane. My blade is always yours to command.
HearthFires.esm|015D10: Good. My place is by your side, Thane.
HearthFires.esm|015D1F: Is it time to move out?
HearthFires.esm|015D21: Yes, my Thane? Do you need something?
HearthFires.esm|015D38: My blade is yours to command, Thane.
HearthFires.esm|015D45: I'm yours to command, Thane. I'll wait here until you need me.
HearthFires.esm|015D46: Don't be long. My place is by your side.
HearthFires.esm|015D47: I will remain on guard here.
HearthFires.esm|015D4E: Of course, Thane. How can I be of service?
HearthFires.esm|015D4F: Do you need something from me, Thane? Or can I carry something for you?
HearthFires.esm|015D52: How can I serve you, my Thane?
HearthFires.esm|015D53: I'll keep watch at Lakeview Manor until you return, Thane. Good hunting.
HearthFires.esm|015D54: I'll return to Windstad Manor and remain on guard, my Thane.
HearthFires.esm|015D55: As you command, Thane. I'll guard Heljarchen House until you return.
HearthFires.esm|015D56: I'll wait for you at the Jarl's Longhouse in Falkreath until you need me again, Thane.
HearthFires.esm|015D57: I'll return to Highmoon Hall in Morthal until you need me again, Thane.
HearthFires.esm|015D58: As you command, Thane. I'll wait at the White Hall in Dawnstar until you need me again.
HearthFires.esm|01612C: You know what? I like you. You're not afraid to get your hands dirty. I hereby grant you permission to purchase property in Falkreath hold. Talk to my steward if you're interested.
HearthFires.esm|01612D: Oh. For some reason I didn't realize you were the same person all the stories were about... Well, in any case, you still need to kill those bandits, remember? Then we can discuss you buying property in Falkreath.
HearthFires.esm|01612E: Ah, it's you. Yes, well, now we'll see if the stories about you are true. There's a group of bandits in my Hold that I... may have had a few discreet dealings with. The cut they were giving me was good at first, but now it's time to clean things up. Go and take care of it.
HearthFires.esm|01612F: There's that group of bandits that need to be taught a lesson, remember? The cut they were giving me was good at first, but now it's time to clean things up. Go and take care of it.
HearthFires.esm|0163ED: Grr... I'm gonna get you for that!
HearthFires.esm|0163FD: Oh yeah? Well, I've seen mudcrabs that're smarter than you!
HearthFires.esm|016412: Is... is that your pet, Mama? He looks really scary!
HearthFires.esm|016413: Is... is that your pet, Papa? He looks really scary!
HearthFires.esm|016414: Is... is that your pet? Really? Wow, he looks tough!
HearthFires.esm|016425: Well, you smell like a Hagraven. And you look like one, too!
HearthFires.esm|016CA2: I do hope mummy and father come to get me soon. Ever since that Aventus boy left, this place has gone mad.
HearthFires.esm|016CA3: When Aventus escaped, Grelod was so mad. I got double the beatings that day. But that won't happen ever again.
HearthFires.esm|016CA4: When Aventus took off, when he said those things, about killing Grelod... I never imagined he was serious...
HearthFires.esm|01770A: That's unfortunate. Maybe I misjudged you.
HearthFires.esm|01770B: Wonderful. I'll sleep better knowing you're watching the southern marches. Here's the title to your steading. Just follow the road south from Dawnstar, past Fort Dunstad, then head south. The land is near Giants' Gap, right on the border with Whiterun Hold. You'll almost be in sight of the Loreius Farm, in fact.
HearthFires.esm|01770C: Land? Ah, yes. The Heljarchen estate. I thought of you immediately when it became available. It's in the south of the Pale, near Giants' Gap. Just the spot for someone like you to establish a steading.
HearthFires.esm|01770D: Sorry, no houses for sale in Dawnstar right now. But... I could sell you land in the hold where you could build your own house.
HearthFires.esm|01770E: Yes, it is. It's in the southern part of the Pale, right on the border with Whiterun Hold. All the room in the world to build your own house.
HearthFires.esm|01770F: Ah, a letter from the Jarl! Moving up in the world, eh?
HearthFires.esm|017710: Hmph. Suit yourself. I can't promise someone else won't buy it in the meantime, though.
HearthFires.esm|017EAC: Whoa! They killed Grelod! Ha ha ha ha ha!
HearthFires.esm|01819F: Married. It doesn't feel like I thought it would, but I'm happy. Where should we live? You could stay with me....
HearthFires.esm|018DC8: Your wish is my command, as always.
HearthFires.esm|018DCB: Allow me to introduce myself. I am the bard Llewellyn, known as "The Nightingale". Your steward has arranged for me to take service with you.
HearthFires.esm|018DCC: The most elegant of instruments. The pinnacle of the bardic art.
HearthFires.esm|018DCF: It would be pleasant to have some music in the house. I'll make inquiries about retaining the services of a bard.
HearthFires.esm|018DD2: You're a connoisseur, indeed. Not many appreciate the subtle beauty of the flute.
HearthFires.esm|018DD5: Certainly. I am trained in the three traditional bardic instruments: lute, drum, and flute. Which would you prefer?
HearthFires.esm|018DDC: Sit back and enjoy some classical drumming.
HearthFires.esm|018DDF: I'm sorry to hear that. It was my honor to have you as a patron. Please let your steward know if you want to hire me again.
HearthFires.esm|0195C2: Yes? Did you change your mind?
HearthFires.esm|0195C6: Oh. Well then, I'd best be on my way.
HearthFires.esm|0195D7: Ah, excellent! We'll just call this a misunderstanding between friends. I've already forgotten it, myself.
HearthFires.esm|0195D8: As you wish. We can consider this incident nothing more than an unfortunate misunderstanding.
HearthFires.esm|0195D9: Good! I knew you couldn't have been serious when you dismissed me. Life without music is hardly worth living, is it?
HearthFires.esm|0195DA: Good day to you. I am Sonir, the bard that your steward has hired. Please let me know if there's anything specific you want to hear.
HearthFires.esm|0195DB: Hello, my name is Oriella, the bard that you asked your steward to hire. I hope my music will be pleasing to you.
HearthFires.esm|021271: Are you a soldier? One day, I'm going to be a soldier!
HearthFires.esm|021272: Pa says I'm too young to fight in the war, but I can still help by patrolling the town.
HearthFires.esm|021273: I wish I was a soldier! They get to travel and stay up late and have fun!
HearthFires.esm|021274: Have you met Lucky? He's my pet goat, and he helps me patrol the town.
HearthFires.esm|02A7C8: What're you lookin' at? I'm not afraid of you, ya know. Even if you are my elder.
HearthFires.esm|02A7C9: Boys, girls, dogs, elders - there's nobody I won't fight!
HearthFires.esm|02A7CA: Stupid Lars Battle-Born. I fight him almost every day, but it's not much of a fight, 'cause he don't fight back.
HearthFires.esm|02C6D5: Assur and I are playing a game. Don't tell him where I am!
HearthFires.esm|02C6D6: Assur had to move away because of the Empire. But we get to stay.
HearthFires.esm|02C6D7: Assur says his pa says the College made lots of bad things happen, but the mages I've seen look nice.
HearthFires.esm|02C6D8: There's not many people left in Winterhold, so I only have Assur to play with, and sometimes he's mean.
HearthFires.esm|02C6D9: Assur always wants to play "Hunt the Elf" and he makes me be the Elf.
HearthFires.esm|02E11A: We're married. I guess we'll need to decide where we're living, won't we? You can always stay with me.
HearthFires.esm|02E176: Papa says I won't see Roggvir any more, but he wouldn't tell me why not.
HearthFires.esm|02E17A: I don't have anything to say.
HearthFires.esm|02E1A9: Mama said I could help with the goats!
HearthFires.esm|02E49F: This is the Old Hroldan Inn. Clean beds and strong drink.
HearthFires.esm|02E4A0: My mother's dead, but I won't close the inn. I've hired Leontius to run things.
HearthFires.esm|02E4A1: If you drink too much, you're out. No drunks.
HearthFires.esm|02E4A2: Changed the linens myself. They're fresh.
HearthFires.esm|02EC49: Why is everyone so upset? If there's Forsworn, the Jarl will take care of them, right?
HearthFires.esm|02EC4A: Why do they call it Left Hand Mine? Can the miners use their right hands too?
HearthFires.esm|02EC4B: Have you met Torom? He's the best dog in the Reach.
HearthFires.esm|02F7E8: My papa says I'm greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him! He says it means "smart," but my brother says he's wrong.
HearthFires.esm|02F7ED: I... I don't want to talk.
HearthFires.esm|0300F5: Hi! I've never seen you around here before. Are you a miner too?
HearthFires.esm|0300F6: Sondas takes care of me when mom's not around.
HearthFires.esm|0300F8: Mom says the Empire is mean, but I don't think they're that bad.
HearthFires.esm|0300F9: The Stormcloaks are named after Ulfric. He's a bad man, but don't tell the soldiers that.
HearthFires.esm|0302A8: I hope you're not here to start some trouble. I'll fight you if I have to.
HearthFires.esm|0302A9: It's just me and my mother at the mill here. My father is gone. We don't know what happened to him.
HearthFires.esm|0302AA: My mother taught me the millwork trade... you know, which trees to cut and which ones to pass up.
HearthFires.esm|0302AB: I'm not afraid of anyone. Put a sword in my hand and I could defend this place if I had to!
HearthFires.esm|0302AC: Millwork sure is boring, but at least I don't have to work in a nasty mine.
HearthFires.esm|0302ED: You're a guest. And father says we have to be polite to guests.
HearthFires.esm|0302EE: Father says I don't have the knack for reading, but mama says I won't need it to work the farm.
HearthFires.esm|035042: Derkeethus used to take me diving way down deep in the river. I miss him.
HearthFires.esm|038735: If you beat up my sister Sissel, I won't tell.
HearthFires.esm|038736: I'm the older sister, by nearly five minutes. Sissel's barely worthy to walk in my shadow.
HearthFires.esm|038739: I wish I could be an adventurer like you, and go wherever I want.
HearthFires.esm|03873A: Some day soon I'll stop being afraid. Jouane is teaching me magic. He says I'm real good.
HearthFires.esm|04542A: You're new around here, so I'll go easy on ya. But don't get on my bad side.
HearthFires.esm|04542B: Get me mad, and I'll prank ya. And not a little. A lot.
HearthFires.esm|04542C: Ah, everybody around here's so serious.
HearthFires.esm|04542D: My friend Dorthe doesn't like my pranking. "You're being mean, Frodnar!" So what if I am?
HearthFires.esm|04542E: Mother wants me to work at the mill. But I ain't nobody's slave.
HearthFires.esm|04542F: You gotta have better things to do than talk to me all day...
HearthFires.esm|045473: Papa says I'm too friendly with strangers, but you seem all right.
HearthFires.esm|045474: My father is Alvor, the blacksmith. I'm his assistant. I mean, apprentice.
HearthFires.esm|045475: Some day I'm going to forge my own sword!
HearthFires.esm|045476: Papa says I'm not ready for weapons. So I make lots of horseshoes and hobnails.
HearthFires.esm|045477: Mama doesn't think I'm girlish enough. But that's just stupid girl talk.
HearthFires.esm|045478: Papa may have wanted a son, but he got me. He's a lucky man.
HearthFires.esm|045479: Ain't nothin' a boy can do that I can't do better.
HearthFires.esm|0458BA: So I like to have a little fun, so what?
HearthFires.esm|0479F5: It's a wonderful house, my love. If you don't mind, I'm going to start a small store. Something to do whenever you're on the road.
HearthFires.esm|04BCEB: Hey. You wanna play a game?
HearthFires.esm|04BCEC: Figures. You elders are always so boring.
HearthFires.esm|04BCEF: I know all the good spots! You don't stand a chance! You stand right there and count to 10. No peeking. Ready? Okay go!
HearthFires.esm|05C826: I will be back at your house if you need me, Thane.
HearthFires.esm|05C838: As you will, my Thane. I will protect you with my life.
HearthFires.esm|060023: I am sworn to carry your burdens.
HearthFires.esm|0649A9: Don't have to tell me twice. But then, I'm not the one that buried iron under these mountains.
HearthFires.esm|0653FA: Are you new around here? You should go to the inn if you need a place to stay.
HearthFires.esm|0653FD: Sissel thinks she's so special. No way she's more special than me.
HearthFires.esm|06541A: I had a dream that there was a good dragon. He was old and gray, but he wasn't scary.
HearthFires.esm|065494: Most days, I do all I can to stay away from my sister and my father. The beating's the same from either one.
HearthFires.esm|06625F: Hope Pavo will be able to go back to his mine soon. Everyone needs a home.
HearthFires.esm|066260: What'cha doing in Left Hand? Do you dig up rocks too, like my mom?
HearthFires.esm|06BBA6: I can't believe my mother's gone. Now what'll I do?
HearthFires.esm|079B19: Watch your tongue, stranger. We don't take backtalk in this inn.
HearthFires.esm|08BBCE: Please, mister. You got to get me out of here. Ever since Aventus left, Grelod's been even meaner than usual. I swear, she's going to kill one of us.
HearthFires.esm|08BBCF: Please, lady. You got to get me out of here. Ever since Aventus left, Grelod's been even meaner than usual. I swear, she's going to kill one of us.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD0: I used to hate it here, but it's not so bad anymore. Constance is real good to us.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD1: It's too bad the orphanage ain't letting us kids get adopted. I'd be the best son ever.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD2: I was named after the sound a lion makes. You know: rrroooaaaarr! Really.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD3: I can't believe it! Grelod is dead! She's really dead!
HearthFires.esm|08BBD4: My parents dropped me off here a year ago. But they're coming back to get me. They said so.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD5: You look kind of scary. But nobody is scarier than old Grelod.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD6: Constance Michel says we have to be careful of people we don't know.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD7: I'm really afraid of Grelod. When Aventus escaped, she was so mad. I got double the beatings that day.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD8: I hate it here. Grelod the Kind is the meanest person in all of Skyrim.
HearthFires.esm|08BBD9: Kill one person, and you can solve so many problems. I wonder at the possibilities.
HearthFires.esm|08BBDA: I'm strong, and quick, and can do any chore you want. Come on, adopt me. Please?
HearthFires.esm|08BBDB: Come to get yourself a kid? You're out of luck. No way Grelod will let any of us get adopted. Especially not since Aventus took off.
HearthFires.esm|08BBDD: Constance don't care much for strangers in the orphanage. Afraid someone might hurt one of us kids. Which, I gotta admit, is kind of nice.
HearthFires.esm|08BBDE: Why's someone like you want to hang around a place like this?
HearthFires.esm|08BBE0: Grelod ain't going to like some stranger just hangin' around. Not that I care.
HearthFires.esm|08D2BE: You came to visit! It's so much better here with Grelod gone. Thanks to you.
HearthFires.esm|08D2C0: When I grow up, I'm going to be an assassin. That way I can help lots of children, just like you.
HearthFires.esm|08EC3B: Magic? That's dangerous. You want to learn that, go to that College in Winterhold. They teach that stuff there. Hey! I know. The Jarl's wizard could teach ya. He's lots closer.
HearthFires.esm|0904EE: Finally. I got stuff to do, ya know.
HearthFires.esm|090DF7: You've proven reliable. There's a group of bandits in my Hold that I... may have had a few discreet dealings with. The cut they were giving me was good at first, but now it's time to clean things up. Go and take care of it.
HearthFires.esm|090DF8: Teach them to stop paying me. Here. You deserve a reward for your service.
HearthFires.esm|092A5E: You'd better give me five septims right now, or I'm going to bloody your nose...
HearthFires.esm|092A5F: I... I don't have any money.
HearthFires.esm|092A60: Ten septims tomorrow, baby Battle-Born. Or I'm gonna bloody your nose and your lip.
HearthFires.esm|092A61: No! Please! I really don't have any money, Braith! I'll give you ten septims tomorrow. Just don't hit me.
HearthFires.esm|0934E2: Yes. Braith bullies me all the time. All the elders think I'm a milk drinker 'cause I can't fight. Nobody understands. If you can get Braith to leave me alone, I'll be your best friend... forever! I'll pay you, too! My whole life's savings - two septims!
HearthFires.esm|0934F7: I don't fight Lars no more, if that's what you're worried about.
HearthFires.esm|094D80: There's a new museum opening up in Dawnstar. The owner is asking me to hand out invitations to travelers.
HearthFires.esm|0A349E: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Falkreath. Congratulations. I grant you this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
HearthFires.esm|0A34A3: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Hjaalmarch. Congratulations. I grant you this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
HearthFires.esm|0A34A8: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of the Pale. Congratulations. I grant you this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
HearthFires.esm|0A4029: If there's nothing else, I really must ask you to leave.
HearthFires.esm|0A402C: Grelod... murdered. Here. I... I need... air... You... you... Get out of here! Get out!
HearthFires.esm|0A402D: You really shouldn't be in here.
HearthFires.esm|0A402E: I'm sorry, but the children aren't up for adoption right now. You should go.
HearthFires.esm|0A402F: My only concern is for the children. The poor darlings have no one else.
HearthFires.esm|0B2D88: Hiya! What's your name? Are you visiting? What do you do?
HearthFires.esm|0B2FCD: Five, four, three, two, one... go!
HearthFires.esm|0B2FCE: Five, four, three, two, one... go!
HearthFires.esm|0BE97F: Come back if you want to play tag.
HearthFires.esm|0BE9B5: Papa doesn't like when I talk to customers, anyway.
HearthFires.esm|0BEE0A: For a stranger, you're not so bad.
HearthFires.esm|0BFBBD: I'm eleven years old, and I could take you.
HearthFires.esm|0BFBBF: Someday I'm going to kill a dragon all by myself.
HearthFires.esm|0C0FB0: Mother says I shouldn't talk to people...
HearthFires.esm|0C1D48: Papa used to say I was greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him. I miss him...
HearthFires.esm|0C57F7: I'll get everything ready and meet you there.
HearthFires.esm|0C57F8: Our new home. I'll see you there!
HearthFires.esm|0C6E2E: Very well, I'll make the arrangements. The next time you visit your house, your new furnishings will be in place.
HearthFires.esm|0C7B08: Aren't you a sight for sore eyes.
HearthFires.esm|0C7B09: Hello friend. Make yourself at home.
HearthFires.esm|0C7B0B: I trust you're not planning any trouble. What can I do for you, friend?
HearthFires.esm|0CB264: Good to see you. Come on in.
HearthFires.esm|0CCCD8: Very well. I'll meet you there.
HearthFires.esm|0CCCDF: All right. I'll see you there.
HearthFires.esm|0CCD3E: If that's what you want. I'll see you there, soon.
HearthFires.esm|0CCD40: Then I'll see you at home!
HearthFires.esm|0CCD5A: You have quite a house. I'm thinking of setting up a small store here. Sell odds and ends.
HearthFires.esm|0CCD5B: Strange house, but strong. Solid. I think I'll open a store. A task to tend to whenever you're away.
HearthFires.esm|0CCD5C: This house of yours is wondrous, at least to these eyes. I've thought about starting a store. Something to do here when you're out.
HearthFires.esm|0CCD5D: This house is huge. I'll be tripping my feet all over this place. Hey, I think I'll start a little business here. Something to do, you know?
HearthFires.esm|0CCD5E: I've seen bigger houses, but it's yours, so I like it. Now, I think I need something to do if you're ever away, so I'll be opening a store.
HearthFires.esm|0CCD5F: A good house. You chose well. I don't like sitting still, so I've decided to open a store. Buy and sell whatever is needed.
HearthFires.esm|0CD58D: A home for the two of us. I'll meet you there.
HearthFires.esm|0CD590: It will be our little nest. I'll be waiting for you there.
HearthFires.esm|0CD591: The two of us, living together. I'll see you at home!
HearthFires.esm|0CD593: I'll see you at home, then. Don't keep me waiting!
HearthFires.esm|0CD594: I'll be waiting for you at home, then.
HearthFires.esm|0CD60C: I guess we have our entire lives together, now. Where should we live? You could stay with me....
HearthFires.esm|0CD60D: We face the world together now. Where should we live? My home is yours.
HearthFires.esm|0CD60E: We're united, but where should we live? My home is yours, of course.
HearthFires.esm|0CD60F: I'm a married man! Hey, where should we live? I guess you could always stay with me.
HearthFires.esm|0CD610: This is the happiest day of my life. Where should we live? My home is your home now, of course.
HearthFires.esm|0CD611: Married. It doesn't feel like I thought it would, but I'm happy. Where should we live? You could stay with me....
HearthFires.esm|0D0598: I do hope mummy and father come to get me soon. Ever since that Aventus boy left, Grelod has been so angry.
HearthFires.esm|0D0599: Things are much better now that Grelod is dead, but still. I'm sick of this place.
HearthFires.esm|0D059A: I can't believe Grelod is dead. I had no love for the woman, but to be murdered, in cold blood...
HearthFires.esm|0DA661: By the gods, a dragon's been sighted just south of the city, flying around Lost Tongue Overlook.
HearthFires.esm|0E0CFE: The mages don't let us go in the College. They said it's not safe in there.
HearthFires.esm|0E3E8E: I knew it! I knew my father was a hero and he didn't just run away.
HearthFires.esm|0E3E8F: Someday I'll take over the mill... when I'm old enough.
HearthFires.esm|0F11B0: I'll be right here. Be careful now!
Skyrim.esm|0130DA: You survived! You have it, then? Let's hope it's as powerful as the Psijics believe it to be.
Skyrim.esm|013113: That's done it! Look, it's coming to life!
Skyrim.esm|013114: You did it. There's the entrance!
Skyrim.esm|013115: There's no telling what we might find inside!
Skyrim.esm|013116: Ah... here's the "blood seal." Another of the lost Akaviri arts. No doubt triggered by... well, blood.
Skyrim.esm|013119: After you, Dragonborn. You should have the honor of being the first to set foot in Sky Haven Temple.
Skyrim.esm|01311A: Esbern's probably right. Try using your blood on the carved seal on the floor.
Skyrim.esm|01311B: If you need help drawing some blood, let me know.
Skyrim.esm|01311C: Try dripping blood onto the carving on the floor.
Skyrim.esm|01311D: Look here! You see how the ancient Blades revered Reman Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|01311E: This whole place appears to be a shrine to Reman. He ended the Akaviri invasion under mysterious circumstances, you recall.
Skyrim.esm|01311F: After the so-called "battle" of Pale Pass, the Akaviri went into his service. This was the foundation stone of the Second Empire.
Skyrim.esm|013123: Gods above, what are you waiting for?
Skyrim.esm|013626: A flame atronach's body might provide fire salt. They're dangerous creatures that can be summoned by wizards. Of course, it would be much easier to check with an alchemist. They occasionally have them for sale.
Skyrim.esm|013628: Shutting this place down would dishonor my family's heritage, but what other choice will I have?
Skyrim.esm|01362A: Amazing! These must have been very hard to come by. You've saved my business and warmed an old smith's heart. For that, I thank you.
Skyrim.esm|01362B: You will? Thank you! Ten pinches of fire salts should give me all I need to bring this forge back to life.
Skyrim.esm|01362C: Return anytime. You're quite welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|01362D: Remember, nothing but genuine fire salts will do... the forge knows the difference.
Skyrim.esm|01362E: If you need any more smithing work, come see me again.
Skyrim.esm|01362F: I can't believe you did that. You people are monsters! You demand payment for protection and you can't even protect yourselves. Take your coin and tell Brynjolf to leave us alone.
Skyrim.esm|013631: What brings you to Balimund today? Repair? Purchase?
Skyrim.esm|0136F1: What's going on, Madanach? You wouldn't have old Grisvar killed unless you weren't planning on needing him.
Skyrim.esm|0136F8: The Reach belongs to the Forsworn!
Skyrim.esm|013709: The Reach belongs to the Forsworn!
Skyrim.esm|01370A: The Reach belongs to the Forsworn!
Skyrim.esm|013733: The Reach belongs to the Forsworn!
Skyrim.esm|01374B: The Reach belongs to the Forsworn!
Skyrim.esm|013A70: By the gods, is that the Voice?
Skyrim.esm|013A91: That sound could bring mountains down!
Skyrim.esm|013A93: Louder than the roar of the Mane, that is!
Skyrim.esm|013AA2: In the absence of the Moot, it would further legitimize his claim.
Skyrim.esm|013EF5: That the best you can do?
Skyrim.esm|0142B6: I'm not allowed to stand on furniture.
Skyrim.esm|0142B8: You mind swinging that somewhere else?
Skyrim.esm|0142BF: What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|014318: Amazing, eh? To be standing at the very entrance to Sky Haven Temple itself!
Skyrim.esm|014319: Are we close? Thalmor spies could be anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|0153B8: There have been whispers. A boy, up in Windhelm, name of Aventus Aretino. Been trying to contact... the Dark Brotherhood.
Skyrim.esm|0153B9: Lucan at the Riverwood Trader had his store broken into the other night. Oddly enough, barely anything's been stolen.
Skyrim.esm|0153BA: Nobody trusts those mages, way up north in their College. Gods only know what they do up there.
Skyrim.esm|01544A: Maro is dead, I know. But we've got bigger problems right now!
Skyrim.esm|015464: The fool went absolutely berserk! He wounded Veezara, tried to kill me, and then he fled. I knew that lunatic couldn't be trusted.
Skyrim.esm|015465: Oh, but I will speak. I will speak to you. For you are the one.
Skyrim.esm|015466: Is that a threat? Back off, or by the Eight I'll cleave that arrogant head from its shoulders.
Skyrim.esm|015468: I'm sorry, but I really don't have time for this. Move along.
Skyrim.esm|015469: Keep back, citizen. I have important business I must attend to.
Skyrim.esm|01546B: Agallon is an efficient man, and will certainly already have begun his tour of Skyrim. Unfortunately, we don't have his schedule. So, you've got two options. Try to find Agallon yourself, somewhere on his route between the major cities. Or... find and steal his schedule. You'll find that in his office, in the Legion stronghold of Dragon Bridge. It's your choice. Now go, and may Sithis speed you.
Skyrim.esm|01546C: With the Emperor's arrival in Skyrim now a certainty, the Imperial Legion will need to begin its preparations immediately. Security is being handled by a man named Voltus Agallon. His job is to travel throughout Skyrim, and ensure each city is prepared for Mede's visit. You must find and kill Voltus Agallon. Doing so will disrupt security sufficiently that we will be able to, eventually, get to the Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|01546D: What? Filthy assassin! We'll see who lies dead!
Skyrim.esm|015490: First of all, I need to sew confusion and doubt through the rebel troops. I want to assassinate their commanding officer.
Skyrim.esm|015494: And we have an opportunity to slay the rebel field commander. Without a clear command structure, they should fall into confusion and disarray.
Skyrim.esm|015496: This won't be easy, but that's why I'm sending you. Think you can handle it?
Skyrim.esm|0154AB: Your objective is the enemy held fort.
