Skyrim.esm|0154AC: The rebels are keeping some of our men prisoner there. I aim to turn that into an advantage. I've sent men to scout out the area. You are to meet them, find a way to slip in, free our men, and take over the fort. They won't be expecting an attack from the inside.
Skyrim.esm|0154AD: Go in quiet, break our man out, then get out of there. Don't risk combat if you can avoid it. If you can't, then give 'em hell.
Skyrim.esm|015B47: Why? I want to watch the soldiers.
Skyrim.esm|015C5E: Your fealty is precious to me. I will make good use of it. You bear my ring.
Skyrim.esm|015C5F: And skillfully too. I've been watching you for ages, it seems. You have the makings of a fine hunter. You may even be my champion. Perhaps.
Skyrim.esm|015C60: I am the spirit of the hunt, just one glimpse of the glorious stalker that your kind calls Hircine.
Skyrim.esm|015C61: And found my favor. That skin will serve you well, child. Look more closely at it. My glories shall protect you from all this world's grievances. Good hunting.
Skyrim.esm|015C63: There is no retribution in the hunt. It is not vengeance I seek, but the blood course of a living hunt. There are others who would gladly accept my favor. They will hunt him while you delay. Your choice.
Skyrim.esm|015C64: Fly, my hunter. There are others who vie for my favor. A bit of competition. Don't dally while the prey flees.
Skyrim.esm|015C65: The one who stole it has fled to what he thinks is his sanctuary. Just as a bear climbs a tree to escape the hunt, but only ends up trapping himself. Seek out this rogue shifter. Tear the skin from his body, and make it an offering to me.
Skyrim.esm|015C66: I may consider it. But you must first do a service for my glory.
Skyrim.esm|015C74: Thanks! I thought I was going to rot in here. So what's the plan?
Skyrim.esm|015C76: Good! I've got a few scores to settle.
Skyrim.esm|015C89: Amazing that such beauty could exist in a place as desolate as the volcanic tundra.
Skyrim.esm|015C8A: Praise Kynareth for allowing such beauty in the world.
Skyrim.esm|015C8B: A truly remarkable place, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|015C8C: Hello there, friend. Have you come to enjoy the sights and sounds of this beautiful sanctuary, as I have? Truly remarkable, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|015CC7: I wish I had more contracts to dole out. You handled those jobs like a born killer.
Skyrim.esm|015F51: Raise the alarm! We've been infiltrated!
Skyrim.esm|016758: You're gonna get me out of here?
Skyrim.esm|016759: You made it! I'm impressed. It was a gamble, but it turned out well.
Skyrim.esm|0167BB: Good. Glad we straightened that out.
Skyrim.esm|0167BC: I need to ask you to stop. That... shouting... is making people nervous.
Skyrim.esm|0167C8: You shouldn't leave weapons laying around. Someone could get hurt. And if I see you do it again, that someone will be you. Have I made myself clear?
Skyrim.esm|0167C9: I've got my eye on you.
Skyrim.esm|0167CA: Can't say I've heard of any laws against... whatever that is you're doing. But I'll lock you up if I have to.
Skyrim.esm|0167DF: Tell me, Husband. Is it true what I've heard?
Skyrim.esm|0167E1: Come now. What everyone else is saying. It can't possibly be true...
Skyrim.esm|0167E2: But really. A horker, some twine, three Wood Elves, and a hatchet? Points for creativity, if nothing else.
Skyrim.esm|0167E3: It's the part with the severed head I find most impressive. My compliments.
Skyrim.esm|016810: I'm just trying to imagine you hiding inside the body of a mammoth. Bloody brilliant idea.
Skyrim.esm|016811: Ah, you must mean my little adventure on the ship...
Skyrim.esm|016813: Oh, you mean in that manor house? During the thunderstorm? Heh. What can I say? It happened, just like you heard.
Skyrim.esm|01681D: It's all thanks to a special technique I developed. I call it the "Razor-Winged Butterfly." I'd teach it to you... but then I'd have to kill you.
Skyrim.esm|01681E: Romantic, isn't? Like that time we first met. Remember that? Never seen so much blood. Ah, to be young again...
Skyrim.esm|016952: I'd rather take a moment and look around than walk blindly into an ambush.
Skyrim.esm|016953: Auxiliary, see if you can find another way through. We'll charge in to help as soon as we hear fighting.
Skyrim.esm|016954: But there isn't any other way through, Legate.
Skyrim.esm|016955: Let's not jump to conclusions, soldier. The Legion always finds a way.
Skyrim.esm|016956: There may be some way to get through on the level above. Hurry!
Skyrim.esm|016957: Maybe you can find a way through somewhere above us.
Skyrim.esm|016958: I don't like the look of this. Perfect spot for an ambush. Ten to one they're just waiting for us on the other side.
Skyrim.esm|01695E: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|016AD2: You did? Maybe that's why I'm so sleepy now.
Skyrim.esm|016AD3: By the Eight, I didn't think you could do it! Now maybe we can put all this behind us. Take this as a token of our gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|016AD4: By Ysgramor's axe! It's true. Morthal owes you a debt. Here is your reward for solving the murder, but I have another request. Unfortunately Morthal is still in danger. The journal mentions Movarth, a powerful master vampire that I had assumed was destroyed. I'll gather together some able-bodied warriors to clean out Movarth's lair. They'll be waiting outside for you to lead them.
Skyrim.esm|016AD5: So Alva did it to get Hroggar for herself? I should have suspected her.
Skyrim.esm|016AD6: By the gods! I assume you have proof.
Skyrim.esm|016CA1: When Aventus escaped, Grelod got meaner than ever. I really thought she was going to kill one of us. Guess not!
Skyrim.esm|016CA2: I do hope mummy and father come to get me soon. Ever since that Aventus boy left, this place has gone mad.
Skyrim.esm|016CA3: When Aventus escaped, Grelod was so mad. I got double the beatings that day. But that won't happen ever again.
Skyrim.esm|016CA4: When Aventus took off, when he said those things, about killing Grelod... I never imagined he was serious...
Skyrim.esm|016CA5: Aventus Aretino. He was sent to live here after his mother died. He escaped. And... Before he left, he said he was going to do the Black Sacrament. You know, to call the Dark Brotherhood. So they'd come and kill old Grelod! Word out on the street is that he went home to Windhelm, and people have heard strange chanting coming from his house. Looks like he really did it!
Skyrim.esm|016CA6: A little boy who lived here. Aventus Aretino. He was nice, but really quiet, and sad, because his mother just died. He ran away. Back home to Windhelm. Samuel told me he was trying to get some murderer people to come here and kill Grelod. Did he really do it?
Skyrim.esm|016CA7: Oh, Aventus Aretino. He was sent here when his mother died, but he ran away. I heard he went back to his house in Windhelm, to try and get the Dark Brotherhood to come and murder Grelod. And... he actually pulled it off!
Skyrim.esm|016CA8: Aventus Aretino? He only lived here for awhile. I think his mother got sick and died or something. Anyway, he's gone now. He ran away. But Samuel told me he's back home in Windhelm, and did some kind of dark ritual. He summoned killers to murder old Grelod. He's saved us all.
Skyrim.esm|016CF8: Here for work? Get an axe and bring me all the wood you can chop.
Skyrim.esm|016F88: You're probably right. I was hoping to avoid having to involve them in this, but it seems we have no choice.
Skyrim.esm|016F8A: Shor's bones! Here it is! Alduin's Wall... so well preserved... I've never seen a finer example of early second era Akaviri sculptural relief...
Skyrim.esm|016F8B: Alduin's defeat is the centerpiece of the Wall. You see, here he is falling from the sky. The Nord Tongues -- masters of the Voice -- are arrayed against him.
Skyrim.esm|016F8D: I was afraid you were going to say that. I guess there's nothing for it. We'll have to ask the Greybeards for help. I hoped to avoid involving them in this, but we have no other choice.
Skyrim.esm|016F8E: Yes, yes. Let's see what we have...
Skyrim.esm|016F8F: Here, the humans rebel against their dragon overlords -- the legendary Dragon War.
Skyrim.esm|016F90: So we're looking for a Shout, then. Damn it.
Skyrim.esm|016F91: Are you paying attention, Delphine? You might learn something of our own history.
Skyrim.esm|016F92: Yes, yes. This here, coming from the mouths of the Nord heroes -- this is the Akaviri symbol for "Shout." But... there's no way to know what Shout is meant.
Skyrim.esm|016F96: Look, here. In the third panel. The prophecy which brought the Akaviri to Tamriel in the first place, in search of the Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|016F97: Ah. Thank you! Excellent. Now... where were we?
Skyrim.esm|016F9A: Esbern. We need information, not a lecture on art history.
Skyrim.esm|016F9B: Ah. Of course. Not everyone has an appreciation for the artistic wonders of the world. Let me see if I can find the right panel...
Skyrim.esm|016F9C: "When misrule takes its place at the eight corners of the world" "When the Brass Tower walks and Time is reshaped" "When the thrice-blessed fail and the Red Tower trembles" "When the Dragonborn Ruler loses his throne, and the White Tower falls" "When the Snow Tower lies sundered, kingless, bleeding" "The World-Eater wakes, and the Wheel turns upon the Last Dragonborn."
Skyrim.esm|016F9D: Look, here is Alduin! This panel goes back to the beginning of time, when Alduin and the Dragon Cult ruled over Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|016F9E: Have you ever heard of such a thing? A Shout that can knock a dragon out of the sky?
Skyrim.esm|016FA0: Bah. What's so good about it, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|016FA2: Patience, my dear. The Akaviri were not a straightforward people. Everything is couched in allegory and mythic symbolism.
Skyrim.esm|016FA3: So, does it show how they defeated him? Isn't that why we're here?
Skyrim.esm|017224: Well... maybe not. Dragons haven't been seen in Skyrim for an age or more. But wherever that dragon came from, and whatever it wants, Ulfric will get to the bottom of it. You can count on that. Besides, you have your own score to settle with the Empire now. And with that dragon.
Skyrim.esm|017227: No. Not yet. After all, a dragon... something out of old tales and legends... no one could have expected that. But you can bet he'll be trying to figure it out. This could shift the whole balance of the war. If you want to help stop that dragon, your best bet is to go to Solitude and join up with the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|017228: I don't blame you for being angry about it. I would be, too, in your shoes. But it was all a mistake. You weren't supposed to be on that cart with those Stormcloak traitors. And the Imperial Legion needs your help, especially now, with that dragon out there somewhere, and Ulfric back on the loose.
Skyrim.esm|01725B: Kerah, do you have anything... exotic? Something no other woman in Markarth would be wearing.
Skyrim.esm|01725C: You there, pretty lady. You need some fresh meat for your family? Just look at the blood coming from this cut....
Skyrim.esm|01725D: Thongvor, I have the latest reports from Cidhna Mine and the smelter. We should go over them immediately.
Skyrim.esm|01725E: I said no! No adventures, no theatrics, no thief-chasing!
Skyrim.esm|01725F: I'm afraid the necklaces aren't selling well. Customers are not interested in medallions engraved with the symbols of other provinces.
Skyrim.esm|017261: We don't have travelers from Elsweyr or Hammerfell anymore, Endon. Only the natives and the Nords stay in Markarth now.
Skyrim.esm|017262: I do have one set of bracelets in a style that is popular with the nobility in Sentinel.
Skyrim.esm|017264: Did I hear correctly, Thonar? Is your brother really asking you to settle another dispute between Mulush and Nepos?
Skyrim.esm|017265: Yes, I have. Adara made the rings herself. She is learning to bend the metal well.
Skyrim.esm|017266: I need to get back to the customers. We'll talk more of this when I come home tonight.
Skyrim.esm|017267: That's disgusting, get that rotting carcass away from me!
Skyrim.esm|017268: Reburrus, there is a war being fought across all of Skyrim, and you bother me with the usual mundane reports about silver?
Skyrim.esm|01726A: Someone has to keep the silver production running, Betrid, and that means keeping Mulush and Nepos in line once in a while.
Skyrim.esm|01726C: Ugh. You're the most disgusting merchant I have ever seen. The guards should take you away.
Skyrim.esm|01726D: Sentinel? That's in Hammerfell, correct? And you say these bracelets are only worn by the nobility?
Skyrim.esm|017270: Look at these figures, Thongvor! Ore yield is down by half, and the amount of silver bars being smelted is slipping even more than that.
Skyrim.esm|017272: Humph. Who's running the Silver-Bloods? You or your brother? You need to tell Thongvor that he should settle this himself or step down.
Skyrim.esm|017273: How are the necklaces selling, my wife? We could make more.
Skyrim.esm|017274: The only people I offend are cheats who don't pay me enough gold coin, or idiots who don't know good meat when I throw it at their faces.
Skyrim.esm|017275: Humph. Used to be everyone in Markarth wore a necklace showing his home province, from Elsweyr to High Rock to Hammerfell and Cyrodiil...
Skyrim.esm|017276: What's wrong? You have to look at the blood closely to see the quality of the meat. How else will you know it's fresh?
Skyrim.esm|017277: Hello, dear husband. Have you brought more silver jewelry for me to sell?
Skyrim.esm|017279: They are popular with the nobility there, yes. This particular bracelet is a copy of one worn by a Crown Princess.
Skyrim.esm|01727A: They are perfect. Thank you both.
Skyrim.esm|01727B: As they always have, along with those crazed Forsworn. They've driven off the color that used to make this city great.
Skyrim.esm|01727C: Oh, very well. I'll send my brother. He'll straighten this out.
Skyrim.esm|01727D: The only thing I need to do, dear wife, is make sure profits are up, no matter what. If Thongvor won't do the dirty work, I will.
Skyrim.esm|01727F: If you ever think of throwing meat at me, I will have you dragged off to Cidhna Mine so fast the guards will yank those filthy arms of yours off.
Skyrim.esm|017280: I don't need to know if it's fresh from this distance.
Skyrim.esm|017281: Care to try out a new ring, or perhaps my daughter could make you a necklace...
Skyrim.esm|017283: Hmm. Maybe in a few seasons I could afford such a treasure.
Skyrim.esm|017284: You'll never be in charge of this family with that attitude, loving husband.
Skyrim.esm|017286: What do I do to deserve such hatred? You give people good food and good prices, and all they do is insult you.
Skyrim.esm|017287: Very good, Thongvor. It relieves me to hear you say that.
Skyrim.esm|0176A6: Maybe you could talk some sense into my pig-headed brother.
Skyrim.esm|01770B: No more! I yield! I yield!
Skyrim.esm|01770F: Ha! Like the bite of a flea!
Skyrim.esm|017805: Lad, I know what you've been up to.
Skyrim.esm|017809: Your hearts are full of grief, and my own weeps at the loss of Skjor. But his death was avenged long ago. You have taken more lives than honor demanded. The cycle of retaliation may continue for some time.
Skyrim.esm|017C2A: I am busy keeping this family in coin and your pretty little head adorned with jewels, so I'll thank you not to question where I go at night, wife.
Skyrim.esm|017C2C: You're a very pretty young woman, Rhiada.
Skyrim.esm|017C2D: Ah, Thonar, don't you worry. Nana Ildene isn't going anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|017C2E: Rhiada, did you clean the tables today? They're filthy!
Skyrim.esm|017C30: Well maybe if you would finally convince Thongvor to put you in charge of the family, I wouldn't need to question you anymore.
Skyrim.esm|017C4F: You're married, yes? To that young metal worker?
Skyrim.esm|017C50: Oh, I know, Thonar. Only a mad woman would ever betray the Silver-Blood family.
Skyrim.esm|017C51: No, don't bother. You're useless, as always. I'll get Ildene to do it.
Skyrim.esm|017C69: You have worked here in the Treasury House for a long time, haven't you Ildene?
Skyrim.esm|017C72: It's just... aren't you worried about the Forsworn? Maybe it's not safe here.
Skyrim.esm|017C73: He's a lucky man to have such a beautiful young wife.
Skyrim.esm|017C74: Oh, stop apologizing. It makes you look like an idiot. Learn to talk back a bit, girl! Why, when I was in the temple....
Skyrim.esm|017C76: Thanks Nana. I feel better now.
Skyrim.esm|017C77: I misspoke. Never mind what I said. Now get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|017C78: Ah, Nana Ildene, whatever would my family do without you?
Skyrim.esm|017C7B: Oh dear, whatever is a girl to do with such fearful brutes about her?
Skyrim.esm|017C7C: Where were you last night, Thonar? Busy with work or have you found some other woman who's willing to tolerate your lack of ambition?
Skyrim.esm|017C7F: Why, thank you Thonar. I'm flattered.
Skyrim.esm|017C82: Um, good. We reward loyalty, you know.
Skyrim.esm|017C83: I'm sorry, Betrid. I'll clean them right away.
Skyrim.esm|017C86: I'll take control of the family on my own time, Betrid. Until then, my brother remains in charge and you'll keep your mouth shut.
Skyrim.esm|017C88: Eltrys, yes, just last season we were wed.
Skyrim.esm|017C8A: I'm sorry, Betrid. I thought I had cleaned the tables properly.
Skyrim.esm|017C8C: Don't you worry Rhiada. If any Forsworn coming knocking on our door, Nana will let you know....
Skyrim.esm|017C8D: Thank you, Thonar. That's generous of you to say.
Skyrim.esm|017C8E: The temple, Betrid? You were one of Dibella's faithful?
Skyrim.esm|017C91: Yes, child. Nana Ildene has taken care of the Silver-Bloods for many years....
Skyrim.esm|017DC0: Secondly, we plan on liberating a settlement from Imperial hands.
Skyrim.esm|017DC1: Next problem - Imperial thieves have, yet again, stolen an important resource.  So we're gonna take it from them permanently - by destroying it.
Skyrim.esm|017DC2: And then, it seems an Imperial worm wiggles it's way back home, carrying information stolen from us. We need to steal it back.
Skyrim.esm|017DC3: And perhaps more importantly, we have a brother rotting in an Imperial prison. He won't languish in there long, will he now?
Skyrim.esm|017DC4: We also have the opportunity to slay an Imperial Legate. That'll put fear and confusion into those faithless dogs.
Skyrim.esm|017DD0: First off, there's a band of Imperials hiding in a nearby village. We're going to flush them out and put them to the sword.
Skyrim.esm|017DD1: First problem here is a pack of Imperial dogs drooling over resources stolen from the people. A little sabotage will sour the taste of that victory.
Skyrim.esm|017DD2: First, we've learnt the whereabouts of an Imperial cur fetching documents for his master in Solitude. We need to put a muzzle on that, don't we?
Skyrim.esm|017DD3: First of all, a son of Skyrim rots in an Imperial prison. We'll be getting him out of there soon.
Skyrim.esm|017DD4: First off, we hear a group of Imperial legionnaires are coming around to our way of thinking. With a little encouragement they'll join our side.
Skyrim.esm|017DD5: I see. Well, make it quick, or do you have better things to do than fight for the people of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|017DD6: What do you say? Can you do this?
Skyrim.esm|017DD7: This won't be easy. But that's why I'm sending you. You got the grit to get this done?
Skyrim.esm|017DD8: Good. You've got real fire in you. I like that... Talos guide you.
Skyrim.esm|017DE1: They have some of our boys locked up there. But with a little luck, we can turn that to our advantage. I've sent some men to scout out the area. Meet them, see if you can't find a way to slip in, free our men, and take over the fort. They'll never suspect an attack from the inside.
Skyrim.esm|017DE2: Go quiet, break him out, then run. This isn't a suicide mission. Don't risk combat if you can avoid it. If you can't, then give 'em hell.
Skyrim.esm|017DE3: You're alive! It was a gamble - but as Talos keeps us, we keep each other - I wasn't going to just let them rot in there.
Skyrim.esm|017DF8: Where's Cosnach, is that lazy idiot out drinking at the Silver-Blood Inn again? On my coin?
Skyrim.esm|017DF9: There are no shipments scheduled today. Stop covering for him!
Skyrim.esm|017DFA: Worse. The loss of shipments from Forsworn attacks are really hitting us hard.
Skyrim.esm|017DFB: Fine, I won't fire him, but I'm doing this for your sake. Not his.
Skyrim.esm|017DFE: He's just waiting for the shipments, Lisbet. He'll be back as soon as they come in.
Skyrim.esm|017E00: Bleak Falls Barrow is just outside of town. I can show you.
Skyrim.esm|017E01: Mara bless you for agreeing to help us.
Skyrim.esm|017E02: He's my cousin. Just give him another chance. Please.
Skyrim.esm|017E03: Imedhnain, I was just comparing the ledgers from last season.
Skyrim.esm|017E04: Oh, really? You're just like my brother. Fine, but if you change your mind, I'll be right here.
Skyrim.esm|017E06: If you're going after the claw, you'll need to know the way.
Skyrim.esm|017E07: So, you need a guide out of town after all?
Skyrim.esm|017E9E: So I hear. You've brought honor to the Companions and yourself.
Skyrim.esm|017EA7: Can't a man drink in peace?
Skyrim.esm|017EAC: Whoa! They killed Grelod! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|017EF6: Going straight through or did you want to stop somewhere along the way?
Skyrim.esm|017EF7: Just sit back. This won't take long.
Skyrim.esm|017EF8: I don't do charity. No gold, no ride.
Skyrim.esm|017F2C: You trying to scare off my customers, son? Lively! Keep it lively!
Skyrim.esm|017F2E: Well, I was thinking maybe something mournful. A High Elf dirge, perhaps? These are rather trying times, after all.
Skyrim.esm|017F2F: So Mikael, what'll it be tonight, hmm? Something upbeat? A Breton jig maybe?
Skyrim.esm|017F30: Hmm... Yes, you do raise a good point. What about the "Sweet Maidenfair from Hadleyshire"?
Skyrim.esm|017F31: I think "Seven Septims for Sigurd" will do nicely. Especially when they're in their cups.
Skyrim.esm|01846A: It's an old Nord Tradition, a test to prove your worth in the eyes of Kyne. Show that you're a hunter and no simple butcher. Kyne teaches us to respect the beasts and blesses the hunter who will face their champions. True Nord hunters are those who survive the Trials.
Skyrim.esm|018479: Well, look at this. Another city rat crawled outside the walls. I don't suppose you've come up here to tell old Froki about your supposed Divines?
Skyrim.esm|01847A: Good. This is Skyrim, and we should honor the old ways. These foreigners don't even respect their own gods, much less Kyne and her Sacred Trials.
Skyrim.esm|01847B: They're stolen idols! Imposter gods, sold by silver tongues. No thank you. Froki will not forget Kyne, or her Sacred Trials.
Skyrim.esm|01847C: Those sycophants in the Temple would call her Kynareth. Just a pale shadow of the truth, like all the Temple Divines. Kyne! Blessed Warrior-Wife. Shor's widow, sacred to any true Nord hunter. She's the mother of men and beasts, and her veil is the storm.
Skyrim.esm|01847D: Do you think you're strong enough for Kyne's blessing? I would be glad to pass this tradition down.
Skyrim.esm|01847E: I'll anoint you with the symbol of the wolf, the crab and the skeever. This will compel the guardian to appear when you reach his lair.
Skyrim.esm|01847F: That's what an old man gets for thinking the younger generation might be interested in learning some honor.
Skyrim.esm|018480: You must defeat guardian beasts, blessed by Kyne.
Skyrim.esm|01893D: Mikael is quite the songbird. Maybe he's the reason you're here all the time.
Skyrim.esm|01893E: Well, that's not bad. But I was thinking "Mogo's Mead" really gets the place moving.
Skyrim.esm|01893F: You know, Hulda, there's this lovely Argonian ballad I've been studying...
Skyrim.esm|018940: Oh Jon, darling. You know you're the only stone-biting, knuckle-dragging Nord for me.
Skyrim.esm|018941: Sing you a special song tonight, beautiful lass?
Skyrim.esm|018944: Mikael, son. How about something with a bit o' kick?
Skyrim.esm|018947: Bah. You're always working. Why else would I spend so much time here?
Skyrim.esm|018948: Alright, alright... Um... How about "The Guttersnipe"?
Skyrim.esm|018949: Don't forget to polish those glasses later.
Skyrim.esm|01894A: You still need to remove that blood stain. From the... incident.
Skyrim.esm|01894B: Just a reminder, sweetie - the blankets still need to be washed.
Skyrim.esm|01894C: We're running low on the Black-Briar mead. In a couple days I'll have you make a run, fetch some more.
Skyrim.esm|01894D: I'll be needing you to mop the place down extra tonight. Lots of spills!
Skyrim.esm|018951: Oh yes, mum. I'll make sure it gets done.
Skyrim.esm|018952: Aye, the bard's got talent, I'll give him that. You just make sure his music is the only thing you're admiring.
Skyrim.esm|018953: Ah! An excellent suggestion. I'll do that one, then.
Skyrim.esm|018954: We've been down this road before, Mikael. Save the sap and sadness for your lady friends. My patrons need life! Cheeriness!
Skyrim.esm|018962: Hmmm, you again. What is it this time?
Skyrim.esm|018964: Hey! You dropped this back there.
Skyrim.esm|018978: You again? We have nothing else to discuss. Now move.
Skyrim.esm|01897B: I don't deal in land or property. Now, if you're looking for goods, you've come to the right person.
Skyrim.esm|0189E2: Urag can be very helpful. Just don't antagonize him.
Skyrim.esm|0189F7: I understand you're the one that found this... Orb in Saarthal.
Skyrim.esm|018A78: I'm so sorry. I hope the Eight give you more peace in the future.
Skyrim.esm|019459: I see... Well, the Legion will still be here when you change your mind. Rikke believes in you. And I'm starting to see why. Consider this. What greater glory than to serve the Emperor and his citizens here in Skyrim, in these days of greatest need?
Skyrim.esm|01945B: Well, then. Repeat after me. "Upon my honor I do swear undying loyalty to the Emperor, Titus Mede II..."
Skyrim.esm|01946E: Changed your mind? Decided you want to soldier for the Empire after all?
Skyrim.esm|01951D: Expecting free information, eh? Help me deal with business first, then we'll see how I can help you. Besides, you look like your pockets are a little light on coin, am I right?
Skyrim.esm|01951E: And I'm busy. You help me out, and I'll help you out. That's just how it is.
Skyrim.esm|019706: Well, I'd better get to work. I have to fix another saw blade for Gerdur's mill.
Skyrim.esm|019707: Hello, husband. You're rising late. You spent too long at the Sleeping Giant last night, didn't you?
Skyrim.esm|019709: Ah. Uh. Of course I'm not just rising. You must have been out when I woke up.
Skyrim.esm|019F57: Balgruuf, my Jarl. A word, if I may.
Skyrim.esm|019F58: I need but a moment of your time, my lord.
Skyrim.esm|019F59: If you don't mind me saying so, Ahlam, your husband is a damn fool. Any moment spent thinking about him is a moment wasted.
Skyrim.esm|019F5A: Ahlam, let's be honest. Your husband is an arrogant, domineering buffoon. These are the words you used to describe him to me... remember?
Skyrim.esm|019F5B: Oh dearest, who cares? So your husband is a lout. The work you do here, with me, is invaluable. The people of Whiterun know your true worth.
Skyrim.esm|019F5D: Are you alright, Ahlam? You haven't seemed yourself today.
Skyrim.esm|019F5E: What's wrong, dear friend? You seem... distracted today.
Skyrim.esm|019F5F: Now, now, my friend. You must look on the bright side. If soldiers storm the city, there's a good chance your husband may get killed.
Skyrim.esm|019F60: Let those soldiers come here! You've never seen me throw incense. I could fell a giant.
Skyrim.esm|019F61: Ha! Well, when you put it like that, it certainly puts things into perspective. But enough wallowing. And thank you, Danica.
Skyrim.esm|019F62: Oh, you are terrible. You do know that, right? Ha ha ha ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|019F6C: Oh, it's Nazeem. He's been spending more and more time fawning over the Jarl. I swear, we're hardly ever home at the same time anymore.
Skyrim.esm|019F6D: It's nothing, I just... It's Nazeem. How did we get to this point, Danica? Is there no love left between us?
Skyrim.esm|019F6E: Hmm? Oh, well... To be honest, I was just thinking of Nazeem. The gap between us seems to be widening. He is just so... self-absorbed.
Skyrim.esm|019F6F: But it's more than that. Nazeem is obsessed with this civil war, as is all of Skyrim. I swear, it will consume us all.
Skyrim.esm|019F70: Maybe Nazeem's obsession has merit. This civil war seems so big, so unceasing... I'm afraid, Danica. Afraid for us all.
Skyrim.esm|019F77: Damn it, Proventus. I've got war lurking just outside my gates, and this is what you offer me? This? Begone.
Skyrim.esm|019F78: Yes, yes. Take care of it, Proventus. Whatever it is you're talking about...
Skyrim.esm|019F79: Proventus, you're my steward. Your job is to offer me counsel. This? This is not counsel.
Skyrim.esm|019F7A: Hmm... All right, yes, I see your point. Make the necessary arrangements.
Skyrim.esm|019F91: Well... It's your son. Nelkir. The boy has been acting increasingly peculiar. Moreso than normal, I mean. Something really should be done...
Skyrim.esm|019F92: We really must discuss the security here in Dragonsreach. Perhaps we could limit patrols in the city, and retain more guards here?
Skyrim.esm|019F93: There's been talk again in the streets. A general fear that the giants may move south, and then... Well, you understand.
Skyrim.esm|019F94: One of the servants had a wonderful idea. Fireproofing Dragonsreach. In case of attack by, um... air.
Skyrim.esm|019F95: My daughter Adrianne wants to forge a new sword for you. Now, it wouldn't be a Skyforge blade, I know, but she really is quite gifted...
Skyrim.esm|019F96: I think the time might be right for another public appearance. Maybe address the patrons at the Bannered Mare? Or visit Jorrvaskr?
Skyrim.esm|01A063: Well, I was only trying to help. Fine, I'll just save myself then. Thank you for letting me out of that cage.
Skyrim.esm|01A071: Well, I... If you're sure... I'll just be on my way then. Please do be careful, and thank you.
Skyrim.esm|01A0B3: Fine. Take them and be gone. Never return here, or else you'll face my full wrath.
Skyrim.esm|01A0B8: Here's where you need to go.
Skyrim.esm|01A0BA: Don't mock it, pup. Even the lowliest beasts can kill the weak and arrogant. We'll try more powerful enemies next, if you survive this.
Skyrim.esm|01A0BC: Return when you've defeated the guardian wolf, crab and skeever.
Skyrim.esm|01A0E6: Ah, hello my good friend. Would you do me a kindness? I seem to be down to my last drop of spirits. Let me tell you, sobriety is highly overrated.
Skyrim.esm|01A0E8: So you do. Thank you, this will help quite a bit.
Skyrim.esm|01A0ED: If it's work you need, how about chopping up some wood for the fires?
Skyrim.esm|01A0F2: Well? What's it going to be?
Skyrim.esm|01A189: Walk always in shadows, so that you will see your foes before they see you.
Skyrim.esm|01A18A: The blade can be as lethal as the spell, but deadlier still is the one who wields both!
Skyrim.esm|01A18B: Sure, you can hire a swordsman or a spellcaster to fight at your side, but why not get both for the price of one?
Skyrim.esm|01A18C: I fight with both blade and spell. You might say I'm equipped to handle nearly any threat. So, shall we talk price?
Skyrim.esm|01A18D: Versatility is the key to survival. Hire me, and you'll be well -prepared to face threats both magical and mundane.
Skyrim.esm|01A18E: You seem to be a little short on coin. Come back and see me when you've got the gold.
Skyrim.esm|01A1CD: You have chosen wisely. Lead on, and I shall follow.
Skyrim.esm|01A1CF: What dangers and wonders shall we discover in the frozen wastes? I can hardly wait to find out!
Skyrim.esm|01A29E: I wish I could help you, but I've been a prisoner in this very chamber for the last quarter century. The only possible help I've come across are the remains of some poor fellow who was trying to follow in your footsteps. Perhaps his journal can help?
Skyrim.esm|01A2A4: Yes, my friend? How may I help?
Skyrim.esm|01A2A5: Welcome to the Legion, auxiliary. Listen up.
Skyrim.esm|01A2AE: My, my. What do we have here?
Skyrim.esm|01A2B0: It's been a number of years since I've set foot on your world. Or perhaps it's been moments. One tends to lose track.
Skyrim.esm|01A2B2: Then... it's over and my death wasn't in vain. I owe you a great deal, Nightingale.
Skyrim.esm|01A2BB: I bid you to drink deeply from the Ebonmere, mortal. For this is where the Agent of Nocturnal is born. The Oath has been struck, the die has been cast and your fate awaits you in the Evergloam.
Skyrim.esm|01A2C5: The Key! You have the Skeleton Key! I never thought I'd see it again. And Mercer Frey?
Skyrim.esm|01A2C8: So... once again the Key has been stolen and a "champion" returns it to the Sepulcher. Now that Ebonmere has been restored, you stand before me awaiting your accolades; a pat on your head... a kiss on your cheek. What you fail to realize is your actions were expected and represent nothing more than the fulfillment of your agreement.
Skyrim.esm|01A2CA: Don't mistake my tone for displeasure, after all, you've obediently performed your duties to the letter. But we both know this has little to do with honor and oaths and loyalty. It's about the reward; the prize. Fear not. You'll have your trinkets, your desire for power, your hunger for wealth.
Skyrim.esm|01A2CC: That's quite a noble sentiment for someone in our... well, in your line of work. My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
Skyrim.esm|01A2DE: I'll be damned if I let this rabble take my city without raising my own sword.
Skyrim.esm|01A2DF: It's a little late for that, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|01A2E7: Protect the Jarl with your lives!
Skyrim.esm|01A2F5: Halt in the name of the Jarl Balgruuf the Greater!
Skyrim.esm|01A302: Elder Scrolls. Indeed. The Empire. They absconded with them. Or so they think. The ones they saw. The ones they thought they saw. I know of one. Forgotten. Sequestered. But I cannot go to it, not poor Septimus, for I... I have arisen beyond its grasp.
Skyrim.esm|01A304: I can almost... hear them. I feel their life energy. Come, I will make the mixture.
Skyrim.esm|01A305: I was only trying to help you, you know! I hope the Caller turns you inside out!
Skyrim.esm|01A3C1: Well, soldier? Can you do this?
Skyrim.esm|01A4DD: May your will carve itself upon the world.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E0: Mind my words or I'll mind them for you: I'm slave to no man, no god, and no Daedra. Boethiah talks about leaving your mark, a sign of your passing.... Well, you can make a mark on the world without treachery and murder. Whenever a man's life is saved by armor made with these hands, these hands have changed that man's destiny and his family's. So you can tell that heartless Daedra bitch I'm done doing her dirty work.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E1: A man who feels no fear has no heart. I know fear. But it does not control me. If you desire to fight me, then let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E2: The Prince of Plots usually gets what she wants. I'm surprised I'm not dead already. She must have had some reason to keep me alive so long.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E3: That was a long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E4: I told you. I'm done with all that. There's enough blood on these hands. I'm interested in creating things now.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E5: I desired stealth and secrecy, but you executed the task with as much subtlety as a enrage mammoth. But my purpose was ultimately gained. My previous champion was erased, and a new one born. If you can learn to be subtle in rendering death, perhaps I will share more of my power with you.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E6: You have done well, my Champion. You have earned my respect, a feat few manage and live to tell about. I shall write your name upon the Tablet of Absolute Darkness.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E7: Ahhh. Quite fine my friend. Quite fine. So sad what happened to young Elisif. Tragic really. Such a young and pretty girl. Was in over her head, I'm afraid. But we should be done with murders in the Blue Palace. I've stepped up security around here.
Skyrim.esm|01A4E8: Hmm... Yes, I've heard about all that fuss. I myself know it's all a terrible, terrible misunderstanding. I'm quite sure you're innocent in all this. This one time only, I'll grant you special immunity for any past transgressions, real or perceived. But going forward, I'm sure you realize that your fate is in your own hands. And the law can not be bent for personal friendships.
Skyrim.esm|01AACA: Now, if you're going to get those thieves, you should head to Bleak Falls Barrow, northeast of town.
Skyrim.esm|01AE50: So the disgusting pig sent you to bother me? One day, I'll tear his face apart so he can leave me alone. My father doesn't know anything about me. But I know about him. And about the war. More than he might think.
Skyrim.esm|01AE51: Yes, my youngest son. He's a dark child. I don't know what to do with him. He was always a quiet lad, but lately... something has changed. He's become brooding. Violent. He won't say a word to me, but I don't know how I upset him. If you could speak to him. Draw out the truth. I would be immensely grateful.
Skyrim.esm|01B222: Oh, hi, I'm glad I ran into you. I was wondering if...
Skyrim.esm|01B224: I don't know. If you see any big strong men around, let us know, will you?
Skyrim.esm|01B226: Oh, come now. Don't go away mad.
Skyrim.esm|01B227: Hello, ladies! Is there anything a big strong man can do for you this fine day?
Skyrim.esm|01B24B: Wait here. I'll be right back with your reward.
Skyrim.esm|01B24C: My camp is nearby in the ruins of Nilheim. Get me there safely and you'll be rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|01B25C: Bandits attacked and ransacked my cart. Can you help me?
Skyrim.esm|01B31E: You must help us appease Malacath.
Skyrim.esm|01B9AA: Sadly, yes. Even the townsfolk have taken to calling her "Grelod the Kind." Her very existence has become something of a running joke. Grelod runs this orphanage because she's old, and set in her ways, and doesn't know any other life. These children need love, and comfort. I try... But... I'm sorry, you should go. The children aren't up for adoption, and it's cruel to get their hopes up. Besides, Grelod hates... visitors.
Skyrim.esm|01B9AB: Well? Grelod the Kind. Is she... you know?
Skyrim.esm|01BACA: Off to Solitude with you. Time to give the Emperor some real motivation to visit Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|01BB42: Easy, easy. Don't get up so quickly. How are you feeling?
Skyrim.esm|01BB45: No, I saved your life. My arrow was tipped with a unique paralytic poison. It slowed your heart and kept you from bleeding out. Had I intended to kill you, we wouldn't be having this conversation.
Skyrim.esm|01BB53: And when the elves marched into your beloved Cyrodiil, and everyone took up arms against them, wasn't that treason? Against the Dominion? Huh?
Skyrim.esm|01BB54: Oh, I know enough. I know those trouble-making Stormcloaks refuse to submit to Imperial authority. Such seditious behavior. Why it's treason!
Skyrim.esm|01BB56: Why that's not the same thing at all. The Dominion were invaders. Conquerors. We had to fight them, to preserve our own way of life.
Skyrim.esm|01BB57: Typical Imperial rot. You speak of Skyrim like you know the land, know its people. You're from Cyrodiil! You know nothing about us! Nothing!
Skyrim.esm|01BB60: You and I have business to discuss. Ah, but you have not yet reported back to Astrid, have you? Do that, and then we shall talk.
Skyrim.esm|01BB64: Dear brother. I've been waiting for you. Your next contract awaits, as I'm sure Astrid indicated.
Skyrim.esm|01BB65: Dear sister. I've been waiting for you. Your next contract awaits, as I'm sure Astrid indicated.
Skyrim.esm|01BB66: As I said, go speak with Gabriella. She's been assisting me with your next contract. This Emperor business, well... it does involve everyone.
Skyrim.esm|01BB99: Emperor Pelagius III. Now surely even you know about Pelagius' decree? On his deathbed - oh, and this was inspired - he forbade... death! That's right! Death! Outlawed!
Skyrim.esm|01BBE6: Oh. Well, um, actually... you're not. Wearing a chef's hat, I mean. There's one right over there on the shelf. You can't very well cook without it...
Skyrim.esm|01BBE7: What's this now? ...order of his eminence... ...possessor of these papers... the "Gourmet"... By Azura. The Gourmet! I... I'm sorry! I didn't realize! We... we had no idea who to expect, you understand. You're not dressed as I would have suspected... But please, don't let me keep you. Proceed to the kitchens straight away. Gianna, the castle chef, has been eagerly awaiting your arrival.
Skyrim.esm|01BBE8: Oh... Oh how marvelous. Just delicious. It is everything I had hoped it would be. It.... I....
Skyrim.esm|01BBE9: The Gourmet. This is such an honor.
Skyrim.esm|01BBEA: The misses and I love your strawberry tarts.
Skyrim.esm|01BBEB: Hmmm, the Potage le Magnifique. Smells delicious.
Skyrim.esm|01BC09: Oh... Oh how marvelous. Just delicious. It is everything I had hoped it would be. Please, everyone. Enjoy.
Skyrim.esm|01BC11: We'll begin as soon as you're wearing the hat. Check the shelf over there. Wouldn't want the Emperor mistaking you for someone else, now would we.
Skyrim.esm|01BC16: Oh, just look at you. Absolutely brilliant. Now... now you're just as I imagined.
Skyrim.esm|01BC31: The Gourmet. Leave it to Titus to spare no expense.
Skyrim.esm|01BC40: I prefer a good roast duck. But your soup will do.
Skyrim.esm|01BC42: I've been waiting for this opportunity for a long time.
Skyrim.esm|01BC59: So glad we kept you in Skyrim. Serving your Emperor was worth the wait, of course.
Skyrim.esm|01BC5A: You're not at all what I expected. I imagined you'd be fatter, honestly.
Skyrim.esm|01BC5D: You've done your duty and we're all very grateful. Now... I'd like to eat.
Skyrim.esm|01BC8B: This isn't a damn summer picnic! Hurry up. We need to hit them before they know we're here.
Skyrim.esm|01BC8E: Looks like the damned rebels got here first. No matter, we have the element of surprise. Prepare to move out.
Skyrim.esm|01BC8F: Ulfric's right-hand man, Galmar Stonefist, has located what he believes is the final resting place of the Jagged Crown. We're going to make sure he doesn't get his hands on it. The rest of my men are already assembling outside Korvanjund. I'll meet you there as soon as I finish up here.
Skyrim.esm|01BD31: Send another one in here! Send two! I don't care!
Skyrim.esm|01BD32: One of you get in here! I could put all of you down!
Skyrim.esm|01BD33: What are you waiting for? I know you want to kill me. Now's your chance!
Skyrim.esm|01BDF9: They're fighting over that thing, I think.
Skyrim.esm|01BE1D: Don't touch that dagger! It's mine.
Skyrim.esm|01BE29: I don't think so. That axe is mine.
Skyrim.esm|01BE46: Keep away from that bow. It's mine.
Skyrim.esm|01BE48: Hands off. That's my armor now!
Skyrim.esm|01BE49: You're too slow. I saw it first.
Skyrim.esm|01BE4B: You can take it over my dead body!
Skyrim.esm|01BFC0: Skyrim is now host to giant, flying lizards and two-legged cat-men... and you're surprised by me? Yes. I just talked. And am continuing to do so.
Skyrim.esm|01BFC2: I know, I know... Wars to fight, dragons to confront, guild business to conduct.
Skyrim.esm|01BFC3: My master and I had a bit of a falling out. We got into an argument and it got rather... heated. He's kicked me out until I find someone who can settle our disagreement. That's where you come in.
Skyrim.esm|01C034: That's done it! Let's get in there.
Skyrim.esm|01C036: You can't shake us off that easily. Let's see what's here!
Skyrim.esm|01C03B: This looks like the way out! I was starting to wonder if we'd ever make it.
Skyrim.esm|01C03E: Better to stick together down here.
Skyrim.esm|01C054: This is it. We've found Gathrik's tomb.
Skyrim.esm|01C055: Well? There must be a way through. Look around.
Skyrim.esm|01C0AA: Keep it together, Gianna. Keep it together...
Skyrim.esm|01C0AB: Maybe when this is all over, you could sign one of your cookbooks for me?
Skyrim.esm|01C0E6: And no doubt you're one of them. Leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|01C0E7: Oh, how reassuring! Most likely you're with the Thalmor and this is just a trick to get me to open the door!
Skyrim.esm|01C0E8: Come in, come in! Make yourself at home!
Skyrim.esm|01C0EA: Ah. Indeed, indeed. I do remember. Delphine really is alive, then? You'd better come in then and tell me how you found me and what you want.
Skyrim.esm|01C0EB: Oh, and when you find Esbern... if you think I'm paranoid... you may have some trouble getting him to trust you. Just ask him where he was on the 30th of Frostfall. He'll know what it means.
Skyrim.esm|01C0ED: This one always sticks... there we go.
Skyrim.esm|01C0EE: I told you to go away. I'm not opening this door for anybody!
Skyrim.esm|01C0EF: It was a cold day. The end of Frostfall is nearly winter in the Jerall Mountains. We heard the news at Cloud Ruler by courier, riding hard from the Imperial City. 30th of Frostfall, 171. Thirty years ago. The Great War started that day. The Thalmor ambassador delivered his ultimatum to the Emperor Titus Mede: the heads of every Blades agent within the Aldmeri Dominion. I knew, that day, that it was truly the beginning of the end.
Skyrim.esm|01C1CF: Teach you to mess with me.
Skyrim.esm|01C1E1: You can't hide from me forever.
Skyrim.esm|01C1E5: Oh well. Must have run off.
Skyrim.esm|01C3B5: Hey! Somebody get down here! Help!
Skyrim.esm|01C3B6: Boss was looking for you - said he'd be up at the summit. Better not keep him waiting.
Skyrim.esm|01C3B7: Eh? Who's there? Rodulf, that you?
Skyrim.esm|01C487: Oh. I... well, I honestly didn't expect you to come back. Of course, I'm glad you did! Now... I don't really want to touch that thing, though. Do you think you could handle the next steps?
Skyrim.esm|01C488: Wonderful. I'll use it to repair the tree. Thank you so much for all your help.
Skyrim.esm|01C48C: So, have you gotten Nettlebane back from those filthy Hagravens yet?
Skyrim.esm|01C48D: Do you have the Eldergleam sap?
Skyrim.esm|01C4D2: Hrmph. You're no fun at all. Guess I'll have to make my own fun elsewhere. And with the pup back, I'll be restored to my full power. There's a whole world just waiting for me!
Skyrim.esm|01C4D3: And I could absorb the spirit of poor, dead Barbas. He'd still be reunited with me. And I'm sure Barbas doesn't want me to have that axe. He'd want it this way. The choice is yours, friend. We're all counting on you to make the right decision. Put him out of our misery!
Skyrim.esm|01C4D5: Well, well. Hello there, big man.
Skyrim.esm|01C4D8: You have business with Clavicus Vile?
Skyrim.esm|01C4D9: Yes, I think you'll be just what I need.
Skyrim.esm|01C4DA: By all means, let's hear it. It's the least I could do, since you already helped me grant one final wish for my last worshippers... They were suffering so from vampirism, and begged me for a cure. Then you came and ended their misery! I couldn't have planned it better myself. So, what's your heart's desire? What kind of deal can we strike?
Skyrim.esm|01C4DB: Thank you. Now, since he banished me, Vile's been rather weak. He can't manifest very far from one of his shrines. I know there's a cult that worships him at Haemar's Shame. We should be able to talk to him there. If this works out, I'll make sure you're rewarded. Just don't trust any offer he makes you... okay?
Skyrim.esm|01C4DC: Very funny. My master is Clavicus Vile, Daedric prince of wishes. As you can imagine, he's quite the important person.
Skyrim.esm|01C4DE: Really? Power? You're a dragonborn; you already have more power than most people who aren't immense, fire-breathing monsters.
Skyrim.esm|01C4E0: All right, then. We'll get together later.
Skyrim.esm|01C4E1: Excellent work. A hero and his faithful companion, retrieving the ancient artifact for the prince. It's almost... storybook. Ah, but it almost seems a shame to give a weapon like that away, doesn't it? I suppose I could be persuaded to let you keep it... But only if you use the axe to kill Barbas. Simple as that.
Skyrim.esm|01C4E3: Absolutely! Now I can reabsorb his body, and return to full power. All without having to listen to his whining. Oh, sure, he'll be back in a century or two, but think of the fun I can have 'til then. Enjoy that axe! And don't worry about poor old Barbas. After all, he really should have picked a better friend...
Skyrim.esm|01C5C3: That's what I like to hear. I'll get the full report from Legate Rikke. Now then...
Skyrim.esm|01C5C4: That's not for you to decide. Soldier. I wouldn't have sent you in the first place if it wasn't going to be worth it. Now then...
Skyrim.esm|01C5C6: Excellent work, soldier. I have to admit, I had my doubts it even existed. Did you run into any trouble?
Skyrim.esm|01C5C9: Yes. Well, I didn't respond as quickly to the Legate's suggestion as perhaps I should have. But at least we ended up with the damn crown. Now then...
Skyrim.esm|01C5CE: We've got nothing to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|01C5EA: Never thought I'd see you again.
Skyrim.esm|01C5EB: We were told you may be coming.
Skyrim.esm|01C5EC: Return when you have completed the Great Hunt.
Skyrim.esm|01C5ED: I've been looking for a way to appease Hircine. There is a certain beast in these lands. Large, majestic. It's said that Hircine will commune with whoever slays it. I tracked it into these woods, but then had my... accident with the child. I want to beg his forgiveness. Give him back the ring. But while I'm stuck in here, the beast wanders free.
Skyrim.esm|01C5FB: Extraordinary. I see it now. The sealing structure interlocks in the tiniest fractals. Dwemer blood can loose the hooks, but none alive remain to bear it. A panoply of their brethren could gather to form a facsimile. A trick. Something they didn't anticipate, no, not even them. The blood of Altmer, Bosmer, Dunmer, Falmer, and Orsimer. The elves still living provide the key. Bear you hence this extractor. It will drink the fresh blood of elves. Come when its set is complete.
Skyrim.esm|01C5FC: The ice entombs the heart. The bane of Kagrenac and Dagoth Ur. To harness it is to know. The fundaments. The Dwemer lockbox hides it from me. The Elder Scroll gives insight deeper than the deep ones, though. To bring about the opening.
Skyrim.esm|01C602: Please, don't just let it stay like this. It's disgraceful.
Skyrim.esm|01C603: It was taken by a lightning strike. Wish I had time to deal with it, but it's hard enough with all these wounded from the war.
Skyrim.esm|01C604: What is it... what has happened to the Gildergreen? I have traveled long here to worship beneath its branches.
Skyrim.esm|01C607: Here. Well, here as in this plane. Mundus. Tamriel. Nearby, relatively speaking. On the cosmological scale, it's all nearby.
Skyrim.esm|01C662: Dig, Dwemer, in the beyond. I'll know your lost unknown and rise to your depths.
Skyrim.esm|01C677: I'll bring you everything we have on them, but it's not much. So don't get your hopes up. It's mostly lies, leavened with rumor and conjecture.
Skyrim.esm|01C682: The box contains the heart. The essence of a god. I have devoted my life to the Elder Scrolls, but their knowledge is a passing awareness when compared to the encompassing mind of divinity. The Dwemer were the last to touch it. It was thought to have been destroyed by the Nerevarine, but my lord told me otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|01C6AD: Under deep. Below the dark. The hidden keep. Tower Mzark. Alftand. The point of puncture, of first entry, of the tapping. Delve to its limits, and Blackreach lies just beyond. But not all can enter there. Only Septimus knows the hidden key to loose the lock to jump beneath the deathly rock.
Skyrim.esm|01CB2A: You're here! I was beginning to think you might not make it.
Skyrim.esm|01CB7B: But... I can't run the Temple without the support of people who are inspired by the Gildergreen. How can this little tree bring new worshippers?
Skyrim.esm|01CD16: I... you're right of course. It can be hard to hear the winds of Kynareth when all you hear are the rabble in the temple. Death feeds new life. I'm sure that, in time, this little sapling will grow into a new Gildergreen that will tower over Whiterun. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|01CDA9: Don't worry. I'll make sure he sees the light. I trusted you, now you trust me.
Skyrim.esm|01CDAA: Well... I guess you could say I got on his nerves. I tend to be the voice of reason and he finds that... irksome. He couldn't just kill me, you see. We're technically part of each other. But he was able to banish me from his domain. Of course, because of our separation, Vile is now much weaker. I guess he figured it was a small price to pay for not having to listen to me anymore.
Skyrim.esm|01CDAB: There's an axe. An incredibly powerful axe. An axe powerful enough for me to have quite a bit of fun, indeed. If you bring it to me, I'll grant you my boon. No strings attached. No messy surprises. At least, not for you. As I recall, it's resting in Rimerock Burrow. Barbas can lead you right to it. The little mutt might even earn his place back at my side.
Skyrim.esm|01CDAC: As much as I hate to say it, you're almost as powerful as I am right now. But that's just because half of my power resides in that mutt, Barbas. Come to think of it, I know of a win-win situation for both of us.
Skyrim.esm|01CDAE: I knew I could trust you!
Skyrim.esm|01CDB0: Ugh. That insufferable pup? Forget it. Request denied. No deal. I'm glad to be rid of him. Even if it does mean I'm stuck in this pitiful shrine, in the back end of... nowhere. Well... perhaps there is a way he could earn his place back at my side. Maybe. But no promises.
Skyrim.esm|01CDB1: Yeah, rub it in. Just imagine what I could get done with my full power and none of these pesky "morals." Still, I've got this axe, and even if I can't use it, I'm sure someone would make a deal for it. Maybe I'll have a word with Molag Bal...
Skyrim.esm|01CDB3: Oh, if I had my full power, granting that would be trivial. I'd simply snap my fingers, and everyone in Skyrim would die. War resolved.
Skyrim.esm|01CDB4: Yeah, yeah, dog gets master, master gets cosmic axe, everyone's happy. Just get over here, mutt.
Skyrim.esm|01CDB7: Ah, there you are. I've been waiting for you to arrive.
Skyrim.esm|01CDB8: Collect the Rueful Axe from Rimerock Burrow, and I believe we'll have a deal.
Skyrim.esm|01CDB9: Ah, you've got the axe! And my dog. Splendid.
Skyrim.esm|01CDBA: It's nice to be rid of that detestable dog. Things are so much more fun without his incessant preaching.
Skyrim.esm|01CEDF: One of the quickest ways to make some coin in Skyrim is to clear out some of the wealthier homes of their valuables. If you're interested, I could point out a good place that could use a little housecleaning. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|01CEE0: I think we have a nice wealthy home for you to break into. Think you can clear the place of valuables and keep it quiet?
Skyrim.esm|01CEE1: These people just inherited some heirlooms from a relative. Good timing for us, bad timing for them, eh?
Skyrim.esm|01CEE5: Just got a tip on this house... should be stuffed with loot. Care to head over there and find out?
Skyrim.esm|01CEE7: Got a message from one of my people that this place just came into some coin. Should be a good target. Want to take it?
Skyrim.esm|01CEE8: Someone with connections to our Guild wants us to hit this place and send a message by clearing it of valuables. You want to handle it?
Skyrim.esm|01CEEB: Good timing. The people in this home recently got one of your fellow Guild mates sent to jail. How about you make them pay for it?
Skyrim.esm|01CEEC: You can give this house a once over. I hear they're fairly well-off and probably have lots of good stuff ripe for the picking. Sound good?
Skyrim.esm|01CEED: The owner of this place just got an inheritance from a relative lost in the war. Sound like something you can sink your teeth into?
Skyrim.esm|01D252: You've been here for a while, right? What was it like back when we could still make runs out to the coast?
Skyrim.esm|01D253: The Captain has it locked away in that drowned ship by his quarters. He says it's "to keep it safe" but I know he's just keeping it for himself.
Skyrim.esm|01D254: Maybe. For now just lay low and do your job. I have a feeling we'll be rid of the Captain soon...
Skyrim.esm|01D255: Well, before that cave-in sealed our ships in here we could loot any size ship we wanted.
Skyrim.esm|01D256: If only we could get our hands on that treasure. We could get out of here and never look back.
Skyrim.esm|01D257: Where did you keep all that loot?  I've been here almost a year and haven't seen any of it.
Skyrim.esm|01D4D8: I thank you for your kindness.
Skyrim.esm|01D4D9: I am a traveler. A pilgrim. I follow the voice of Kynareth wherever it can be heard. I've dreamed of seeing Eldergleam for years. Might I travel alongside you? I promise not to get in the way.
Skyrim.esm|01D4DA: I understand. Some journeys must be taken alone. I'll be here if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|01D63D: Follow me. I think I can convince the tree to help us.
Skyrim.esm|01D63E: Well... yes. There is something. It won't repair the tree back at the temple, but we could bring them a new one.
Skyrim.esm|01D63F: You would violate this marvel of Kynareth's glory to fix that half-breed stump in Whiterun? That's abominable. Barbaric. I'll have no part of this. Why didn't you tell me what you intended?
Skyrim.esm|01D640: I had no idea you were a man of violence. What exactly are your intentions in this blessed place?
Skyrim.esm|01D641: With pleasure. Nature has a way of finding vengeance, though. You walk a bleak path, friend. One that will lead to suffering.
Skyrim.esm|01D642: Very well. I can't stop you. But I can't bear to watch it, either.
Skyrim.esm|01DA31: Ah, well, I recently approached Sibbi Black-Briar about buying Frost, one of the finest breeding horses in Skyrim. He agreed to sell. I paid Sibbi half the cost of Frost up front, but before he could deliver, Maven had him locked up. Sibbi believes this exempts him from our deal. It does not. I want you to talk to Sibbi for me. Then, I want you to bring me Frost and the lineage papers that prove the horse's worth.
Skyrim.esm|01DA33: Ah, that's a real shame. You would've fit the bill nicely.
Skyrim.esm|01DA35: Good man. You won't regret it.
Skyrim.esm|01DA36: Don't tell me - Sibbi and Louis Letrush? Well, Sibbi is in jail and Letrush is certainly welcome to try. My hired help is more than a match for him.
Skyrim.esm|01DA37: Looking for work? I need someone to deliver a message to Sibbi Black-Briar.
Skyrim.esm|01DA38: Go on, then. Deliver my message to Sibbi.
Skyrim.esm|01DCF2: Am I going to have to beat it out of you? Is that what you want?
Skyrim.esm|01DCF4: You start flapping that jaw of yours or I'll start flapping it for you. You understand me?
Skyrim.esm|01DCF7: (Sigh) Let's just kill them both, eh?
Skyrim.esm|01DCFD: Look at me when I'm talking to you, eh?
Skyrim.esm|01DD00: I... I don't know, it wasn't me. I swear!
Skyrim.esm|01DD40: The Twilight watches us all, guardian.
Skyrim.esm|01DDF6: Sad, isn't it? We can't afford to keep the place running since these attacks started. Pirates, you see. Raiding all along the coast, from Hammerfell to Vvardenfell. Only the Shatter-Shields appear safe.
Skyrim.esm|01E1AA: Just in time. This is Adelaisa, one of my superiors from the Company. She's been dispatched to... eh... see to it that we get back on schedule. You should probably speak to her.
Skyrim.esm|01E1AB: Ha! Heard of 'em? I've been captaining a crew of 'em for well on nine years now. What's it to you?
Skyrim.esm|01E1AC: I can't express how much easier you've made my job. Maybe Adelaisa will finally ease up, but I expect she won't. Ah, yes, your pay. The Company thanks you for your services. Should we ever be able to help you, stop by.
Skyrim.esm|01E1B0: How did you get it? No, never mind, I probably shouldn't know. I see... it looks like Suvaris has been traveling to Dawnstar to meet with a pirate crew there. Get to Dawnstar and see what you can find out about these rogues. I'd wager they won't be too far from the tavern. Try to find out where their fortress is. The captain, Stig Salt-Plank, has to know something.
Skyrim.esm|01E3A9: Ha, I knew you'd join up! Great news. We'll take back Skyrim together, eh? I'm glad I'll have you beside me in there. This place chills my blood, and I don't care who knows it.
Skyrim.esm|01E3C0: Well done, indeed. This will be of great service. Understanding the enemy's position makes us all the more equipped to usurp it. And with the rebels thinking us occupied elsewhere for the time being, we'll be able to take them by surprise.
Skyrim.esm|01E497: Well, now that you mention it, there is something I've been trying to get my hands on.
Skyrim.esm|01E4E1: Great Malacath, we beseech you, aid us in our time of need...
Skyrim.esm|01E62D: How went your meeting with Ennodius Papius? Did you prove his paranoia legitimate?
Skyrim.esm|01E62E: I see you're back from Dawnstar. Beitild lies dead, I hope?
Skyrim.esm|01E62F: You're alive. Then I guess you haven't paid a visit to the vampire yet, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|01E630: Please tell me you flayed that horrible bard alive and made a drum from his skin...
Skyrim.esm|01E631: Taken care of that layabout Deekus, yet? Or does the idea of killing a fellow Argonian make your scales crawl?
Skyrim.esm|01E632: No new boots? Does this mean the Argonian is still alive?
Skyrim.esm|01E633: You smell like dead cat. Can I assume Ma'randru-jo has been taken care of?
Skyrim.esm|01E634: How went your encounter with Ma'randru-jo? Back in Hammerfell, I always loved watching two cats rip each other to shreds...
Skyrim.esm|01E635: Hmph. No arrows protruding from your skull... Can I assume Anoriath is dead, then?
Skyrim.esm|01E636: Ah, back from Fort Greymoor. The old servant has been put out of her misery, I hope?
Skyrim.esm|01E637: So, you've returned from Mzinchaleft! A dwarven ruin... what an exotic place for a kill. Assuming Maluril is actually... dead?
Skyrim.esm|01E638: Back from your little vacation in Falkreath. So, did you enjoy the sights, sample the food... kill the housecarl?
Skyrim.esm|01E639: You haven't faced that pirate captain yet, have you? Safia? I only ask because you're still alive...
Skyrim.esm|01E63A: So many contracts, so little time...
Skyrim.esm|01E63B: I don't have anything for you just yet. Please, talk to Nazir, and see what he has available.
Skyrim.esm|01E63C: I'm still trying to find a suitable contract for you. Continue to work with Nazir on the, um, supplemental assignments.
Skyrim.esm|01E63D: The Keeper will be arriving soon, with the body of the Night Mother. So many preparations to be made...
Skyrim.esm|01E66C: Whatever it is those Psijics are up to. They cannot be trusted. For all we know, he's here to scout out the College. I'm sure they want to undermine our efforts.
Skyrim.esm|01E66D: This! This... Orb. The Eye of Magnus, whatever you want to call it. It's clearly powerful, and no doubt they want it for themselves! I expect to be informed if they make any future attempts to contact you.
Skyrim.esm|01E66E: Well? What is the meaning of this?
Skyrim.esm|01E66F: I'm not sure what just happened, but I assure you I will get to the bottom of it.
Skyrim.esm|01E670: What? What trickery is this? You're not going anywhere until I find out what you're up to!
Skyrim.esm|01E671: Don't play coy. You asked to see a specific member of the College. Here he is. Now what is it that you want?
Skyrim.esm|01E672: Don't play coy. You asked to see a specific member of the College. Here she is. Now what is it that you want?
Skyrim.esm|01E674: There's been a misunderstanding. Clearly I should not be here. I shall simply take my leave.
Skyrim.esm|01E675: I'm sorry, I'm afraid I don't understand.
Skyrim.esm|01E678: I am not "up to" anything. I apologize if I have offended you in any way.
Skyrim.esm|01E999: I can see to that. We'll need plenty of fresh corpses to rebuild her army, you see.
Skyrim.esm|01E99A: None can stand against the Wolf Queen!
Skyrim.esm|01E99C: You've come far, mortal. No doubt you seek to enter Potema's Sanctum.
Skyrim.esm|01EA16: 'Accidents' like this don't just happen! Someone is trying to sabotage my uncle's research!
Skyrim.esm|01EA4E: Did she, now? Well, as it turns out, there are a few lingering contracts we haven't had the chance to complete just yet. And more, dribbling in from time to time. I'll assign them to you as they become available. To be completed at your leisure.
Skyrim.esm|01EB6C: Ah, you're a smart woman. That's exactly what I wanted to hear.
Skyrim.esm|01EB6E: Head to the Riften jail and talk to Sibbi. Get him to tell you where Frost and his lineage papers are and bring them to me outside Riften. You'll be well paid for your time.
Skyrim.esm|01EC48: But this is supposed to be your work.
Skyrim.esm|01EC49: I really don't have time to deal with you right now. Please just let me get back to my work.
Skyrim.esm|01F338: Of course you are! I prayed, and you came, and now you'll accept my contract!
Skyrim.esm|01F339: My mother, she... she died. I... I'm all alone now. So they sent me to that terrible orphanage in Riften. Honorhall. The headmistress is an evil, cruel woman. They call her Grelod the Kind. But she's not kind. She's terrible. To all of us. So I ran away, and came home. And performed the Black Sacrament. Now you're here! And you can kill Grelod the Kind!
Skyrim.esm|01F33A: Aretino? Why that little bastard! You tell him I'm coming for him! And when I find him, it'll be the beating of his miserable life!
Skyrim.esm|01F33B: You don't have to say anything. There's no need. You're here, so I know you'll accept my contract.
Skyrim.esm|01F33C: It took so long. So very long. But now that you're here, you can accept my contract.
Skyrim.esm|01F33D: The what? Why how dare you! I will not be intimidated in my own orphanage! Get out! Get out this instant!
Skyrim.esm|01F33F: What are you staring at, you worthless piece of gutter trash? I simply must start locking the doors again...
Skyrim.esm|01F340: I have a family heirloom you can have. Supposed to be sort of valuable. I hope that's all right.
Skyrim.esm|01F342: It worked! I knew you'd come, I just knew it! I did the Black Sacrament, over and over. With the body, and the... things. And then you came! An assassin from the Dark Brotherhood!
Skyrim.esm|01F343: Finally! My prayers have been answered!
Skyrim.esm|01F344: You've come at last! I knew you would!
Skyrim.esm|01F345: Grelod is the headmistress of Honorhall Orphanage. But not for long.
Skyrim.esm|01F347: Please don't kill Constance Michel. She really is kind.
Skyrim.esm|01F349: What do you want? You have no business being in here!
Skyrim.esm|01F34A: Riff-raff! That's all you Riften people are!
Skyrim.esm|01F34B: This is an orphanage, not an inn. Begone from here.
Skyrim.esm|01F34E: You're trying to frighten me, hmm? Do your worst!
Skyrim.esm|01F376: You're damned right it does. I'll never breed another prize-winning goat like Gleda! And don't you think of coming back to Rorikstead until you get her back from that Giant.
Skyrim.esm|01F37C: Just a prank? Just a prank? The Daedric Lord of Debauchery does not deal in mere "pranks." This may have begun as a minor amusement, but it wasn't long before I realized you'd make a more interesting bearer of my not-quite-holy staff.
Skyrim.esm|01F37D: You really don't remember stealing a goat and selling her to a Giant? Are you thick? Go get her! At least she's bound to follow you back - you smell just like the fermented feed she likes.
Skyrim.esm|01F37E: Let's be honest, here. I don't always think my decisions through.  But you... you're going places. Maybe a little influence from your old uncle Sanguine could help adjust your course a bit...
Skyrim.esm|01F384: My pleasure. But I think it's time for you to go. No fun keeping you locked up in here with the staff.
Skyrim.esm|01F385: So, you're finally back. Look, I've been patient, but you still owe me.
Skyrim.esm|01F386: Details, details. The Sanguine Rose breaks all the time and is fixed with but a thought. In fact, here it is now. Think of it - a never-ending stream of minions. And all of the prestige of being Sanguine's chosen.
Skyrim.esm|01F387: Of course! You don't remember getting here.
Skyrim.esm|01F389: Gleda! And not a scratch on her! Happy day! I still can't figure out why you stole her. You left a note explaining it, but half of it was gibberish and the rest had mead spilled on it. Only bit I could make out was "repaying Ysolda in Whiterun," and even that's mostly scribbles. Guess you could try there.
Skyrim.esm|01F38A: You! You've got a lot of nerve showing yourself in this town again. What do you have to say for yourself?
Skyrim.esm|01F391: You went right out to give it to your fiancee! Don't you even remember where you left her? And after you told me that sweet story of how you met in Witchmist Grove! I can see why she left you.
Skyrim.esm|01F392: Sorry's not good enough! Not while my Gleda is still out there, alone and afraid. You kidnapped her and sold her to that Giant.
Skyrim.esm|01F393: Dibella teaches love and compassion, but that doesn't mean we're just going to tell you what you want to know and let you walk away from this. Pick up your mess, then apologize, and if we think you're sincere we'll consider lending you aid.
Skyrim.esm|01F396: Aw, what's wrong? Did the engagement fall through? Look, how about we call it even, as long as you bring back the wedding ring? That's really a shame - I was so looking forward to the wedding. You said you'd have all the most interesting guests.
Skyrim.esm|01F397: I really just needed something to encourage you to go out into the world and spread merriment. And you did just that! I haven't been so entertained in at least a hundred years.
Skyrim.esm|01F399: Is that so? Does the name Gleda ring a bell? The star beauty of my farm? Kidnapped by a drunk lout and sold to a Giant? You'd better remember her right fast, before I call the guards and have you hauled away.
Skyrim.esm|01F39B: It's not about the money, really. I wouldn't have given you the wedding ring on credit if you weren't so obviously in love. But if there isn't going to be a wedding, the least you can do is give the ring back. That was one of my best pieces.
Skyrim.esm|01F39D: Wake up! That's right, it's time to wake up, you drunken blasphemer!
Skyrim.esm|01F3A3: Excellent! Sorry it didn't work out with you and your lady. I know how excited you were for the wedding. You kept saying it would be a huge ceremony at Morvunskar. You said you even had some magic staff there that would handle all the guests.
Skyrim.esm|01F3A4: How could you forget? It was the sweetest story I'd ever heard. You met at the full moon, under the biggest tree in Witchmist Grove, surrounded by fireflies. It was straight out of a storybook.
Skyrim.esm|01F3A5: I suppose that'll do. Dibella teaches us forgiveness, after all. Even for a drunk like you.
Skyrim.esm|01F501: What did I do? Please, whatever it is, I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|01F50D: I don't have time for this nonsense. I've got a home to keep and children to feed. Now let me out of here!
Skyrim.esm|01F5C0: Tell you what. You release me, and I promise my associates won't hunt you down like an animal and butcher you in the street. It's a win-win.
Skyrim.esm|01F5E2: My name is Fultheim. I'm a soldier. Well, mercenary, really. You know, a... a sellsword. I've lived in Skyrim all my life. That's all! I'm a nobody, really. So can't you just let me go?
Skyrim.esm|01F5E7: None of your damned business who I am! If you're going to kill me, just do it already! As Mara is my witness, if I didn't have this hood on right now I would spit right in your face...
Skyrim.esm|01F652: Ahhh... Vasha, at your service. Obtainer of goods, taker of lives, and defiler of daughters.
Skyrim.esm|01F653: Excuse me? What kind of question is that?
Skyrim.esm|01F656: What? Oh gods, I don't want to die...
Skyrim.esm|01F6CB: Aha! I knew you could do it! I just knew it! I knew the Dark Brotherhood would save me! Here, just like I promised. This should fetch you a nice price. And thank you. Thank you again.
Skyrim.esm|01F6CD: Grelod the Kind. Is she... you know?
Skyrim.esm|01F7A1: Our own security forces are limited, and all the mercenaries are tied up in this ridiculous war. We don't even know their base of operations, and attacking single ships won't really solve the problem.
Skyrim.esm|01F7A2: Well I can't just tell that to any skeever that washes up. From the look of you, I'm guessing you're more looking to cause some trouble for the Horkers.
Skyrim.esm|01F810: Magic? Got no good use for that here. Mages keep to their College in Winterhold. Go bother them. Wish the Jarl's wizard would up and leave with ya.
Skyrim.esm|01F819: No? Very well, it's your choice.
Skyrim.esm|01F81A: A standard spell for one skilled in Destruction magic is the Firebolt. Casting one at the seal on the ground here would be sufficient.
Skyrim.esm|01F81B: Those invested in Restoration magic find Healing Hands to be essential. Can you cast it on me? That would prove your skill.
Skyrim.esm|01F81C: The Fear spell has saved the life of many an Illusion mage. If you could cast it on the seal here on the ground, that would prove your skill.
Skyrim.esm|01F81D: The Flame Atronach is a vital companion for anyone relying on Conjuration. Summoning one here would certainly show your skill.
Skyrim.esm|01F81E: The Mage Light spell is useful to any mage, not just those specializing in Alteration. Can you cast one on the seal on the ground?
Skyrim.esm|01F81F: Unfortunately, one needs to be able to cast at least Apprentice level spells in any school of magic before being allowed to join the College. And thus, you're not skilled enough to be a candidate. I'm afraid we're not interested.
Skyrim.esm|01F976: Yes, my friend. Though no one has been able to get close to her for as long as I can remember.
Skyrim.esm|01FA4F: Join the forces mustering at the [CAPITAL] (infos conditions on location go here)
Skyrim.esm|01FA50: The enemy is mustering for a final attack.
Skyrim.esm|01FA51: Infos conditioned on Attack Delta go here.
Skyrim.esm|01FAD3: The rumors were true! You carry the one weapon Eldergleam fears - Nettlebane. I beg of you, don't harm the tree! Nothing good can come of it.
Skyrim.esm|01FAD7: Oh, I don't believe those rumors, my friend. Besides, who would ever want to harm such... Wait, it can't be.
Skyrim.esm|01FB22: Welcome to Eldergleam Sanctuary, my friend. If you have any questions, speak to Asta.
Skyrim.esm|01FB23: You don't know what you're dealing with. There will be consequences if you harm that tree. Leave this place - you're not welcome here anymore.
Skyrim.esm|01FB28: Kynareth humbles us with her beauty.
Skyrim.esm|01FB29: Don't come any closer. I don't want Kynareth to think I'm on your side.
Skyrim.esm|01FB2F: You are in Eldergleam Sanctuary - just a small glimpse of what beauty Kynareth has to offer. Wonderful place, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|01FB79: Oh, I don't believe those rumors, my friend.  Besides, who would ever want to harm such beauty?
Skyrim.esm|01FB7F: I doubt the Forsworn would risk holding this mine a second time. They lost many of their warriors.
Skyrim.esm|01FB80: If you had to kill your own brother to be allowed back home, you'd think twice as well.
Skyrim.esm|01FB81: Of course, sweetie. What'd you like to know?
Skyrim.esm|01FB82: Oh, I don't believe those rumors, my friend. Besides, who would ever want to harm such beauty? Now, if you'll excuse me, I have nothing more to discuss. I encourage you to take your time and enjoy yourself. You look like you could use the rest.
Skyrim.esm|01FB83: Do you remember the Legion? Fighting the Thalmor in the Great War?
Skyrim.esm|01FB89: The rumors were true... I beg of you, don't harm the tree! Nothing good can come of it.
Skyrim.esm|01FB8A: I don't know. What'd he look like?
Skyrim.esm|01FB8B: There were many good battles, friend. I miss those days.
Skyrim.esm|01FB8D: As you can see, Eldergleam's roots are large and stretch far, blocking any path to her trunk. Though, believe it or not, there are rumors of a weapon that even Eldergleam herself would lift her roots for, more out of fear than respect.
Skyrim.esm|01FB8E: He had your soft face, and that silly grin you get when you've done something wrong. That's his, too.
Skyrim.esm|01FB9D: You miss them? I don't know how many times I feared for my life.
Skyrim.esm|01FBC4: You didn't die. A battle you live through is a great honor.
Skyrim.esm|01FC34: I can't agree to any peace council until I know Tullius will be there. The other Jarls could take that as a sign of weakness.
Skyrim.esm|01FC35: Yes. I'll give Tullius one more chance to quit Skyrim with his tail between his legs.
Skyrim.esm|01FC36: It's about time they turned their gaze from the heavens, back to our bleeding homeland. What do they want?
Skyrim.esm|01FC37: Good. Maybe he's realized he's beaten. The way things are going, we'll be knocking at the gates of Solitude itself before too long.
Skyrim.esm|01FC38: Good. We still hold half of Skyrim despite everything the Empire could throw at us. I doubt the Empire has the stomach for much more bloodletting.
Skyrim.esm|01FC39: Good. They've had their victories, but Tullius is no fool. The Empire can't afford to keep this up much longer.
Skyrim.esm|01FC3B: Hmm. We could use the time to bring in new recruits. Our losses have been heavy recently...
Skyrim.esm|01FC3C: You're right. Let Tullius be the one to insult the Greybeards by refusing their summons. Show up these Imperials for the foreigners they really are.
Skyrim.esm|01FC3D: He is weak. And no doubt knows it. He's no fool, he'll come, if only to give his troops a chance to refit. But you're right. Victory is within our grasp. We can afford to give the Empire one more chance to withdraw.
Skyrim.esm|01FC3F: I feel like we're mining even more ore than before.
Skyrim.esm|01FC41: Good to see you again, friend! We've opened the mine back up. If you're here for work, I'll buy any ore you have.
Skyrim.esm|01FC9F: The mine is just how I remember it.
Skyrim.esm|01FD1B: It's good to be mining again.
Skyrim.esm|01FD3F: *Sigh* I was part of the gang in the ruins here. Friend of mine went crazy and stole the boss' sword. They blamed me for it. Look, I'm done here. Mop the floor with these idiots for all I care. Just get out of my way.
Skyrim.esm|01FD53: Who in Dagon's name are you? Speak, or I'll cut you down where you stand.
Skyrim.esm|01FD5C: I've never seen Pavo so happy. It's good to be back in Kolskeggr.
Skyrim.esm|01FD5D: Did you ever want to return to your stronghold? Go back to your family?
Skyrim.esm|01FD5F: It's the rush of good fortune, my friend. It makes you work harder.
Skyrim.esm|01FD60: I would need to challenge the chief, and take his head, to return home. I'd rather stay here.
Skyrim.esm|01FD61: Are you worried the Forsworn might return?
Skyrim.esm|01FD63: Mama, can we talk. You know, about papa?
Skyrim.esm|01FD65: Most women would rather die than dig, but I have a daughter to raise.
Skyrim.esm|01FD66: Don't tell me you, of all people, are afraid of a battle.
Skyrim.esm|01FE8C: I need at least five more, if you can find them.
Skyrim.esm|01FE8D: Bring me another seven of them, if you can.
Skyrim.esm|01FE8E: Ten more would keep me stocked for a bit.
Skyrim.esm|01FE93: I'm running low on Petty Soul Gems.
Skyrim.esm|01FE94: I'm running low on Lesser Soul Gems.
Skyrim.esm|01FE95: I'm running low on Common Soul Gems.
Skyrim.esm|01FE96: I'm running low on Greater Soul Gems.
