Skyrim.esm|01FED6: You go speak with Muiri. I've got to deal with the Night Mother and her little... clown.
Skyrim.esm|01FED7: Go, take care of the Muiri contract. There will be plenty more, I promise you.
Skyrim.esm|01FED8: Just do whatever the contact wishes. Be professional, represent us well, and get the job done. Since it's your first contract, I'll let you keep whatever Muiri pays. She'll be generous, I'm sure. They always are.
Skyrim.esm|01FED9: I do indeed. You must go to the city of Markarth, and speak with the apothecary's assistant. You'll probably find her in The Hag's Cure, when the shop is open. The girl's been running her mouth, wants an ex-lover killed. She's apparently performed the Black Sacrament. Her name is Muiri. I need you to talk to her, set up the contract, and carry it out.
Skyrim.esm|01FEDA: Ah, there you are. Good, I was done speaking with that muttering fool anyway. We've got some business to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|020012: You see, that little Aretino boy was looking for the Dark Brotherhood. For me, and my associates. Grelod the Kind was, by all rights, a Dark Brotherhood contract. A kill... that you stole. A kill you must repay.
Skyrim.esm|020013: Who I am really isn't as important as what I am. And what I am is an admirer. Of sorts.
Skyrim.esm|020015: It is. These aren't particularly glamorous assassinations, I'll be honest. Don't pay much, either. But they'll keep you busy. Just do them as you're able. There's no real time limit - the targets aren't going anywhere. You can turn each one in as it's completed, or wait and turn in the whole group when all the targets have been eliminated. Whichever works for you.
Skyrim.esm|020016: Half of Skyrim knows. Old hag gets butchered in her own orphanage? Things like that tend to get around. Oh, but don't misunderstand. I'm not criticizing. It was a good kill. Old crone had it coming. And you saved a group of urchins, to boot. Ah, but there is a slight... problem.
Skyrim.esm|020017: Well then, let's get started. I've got three available right now. Your targets are the beggar Narfi, an ex-miller named Ennodius Papius, and Beitild, a mine boss. When you've completed all those, we'll see if I might have some more.
Skyrim.esm|020019: Well now. Funny you should ask. If you turn around, you'll notice my guests. I've "collected" them from... well, that's not really important. The here and now. That's what matters. You see, there's a contract out on one of them, and that person can't leave this room alive. But... which one? Go on, see if you can figure it out. Make your choice. Make your kill. I just want to observe... and admire.
Skyrim.esm|020027: I see. Well, please let me know when you are.
Skyrim.esm|020028: The prominent feature here is the Hall of the Elements. It's our primary location for lectures, practice sessions, and general meetings.
Skyrim.esm|020029: Another new student... I'm surprised at how many of you there are lately. Well, first you'll need these. While you're not required to wear them, you may find them more to your liking than your current clothes. I'll give you a brief tour, and then we'll get you to your first class. Are you ready to begin?
Skyrim.esm|02002A: Yes, just a brief overview of our facilities. Are you ready?
Skyrim.esm|02002B: The College of Winterhold has been a fixture in Skyrim for thousands of years.
Skyrim.esm|02002C: Wonderful. Please follow me, and don't wander off.
Skyrim.esm|020121: There you go. Take a look around, there should be plenty of gear to choose from. I'm going to see if I can find something for these burns.
Skyrim.esm|02012E: Let me see if I can get that door open.
Skyrim.esm|020130: We should keep moving. Come here. Let me see if I can get those bindings off.
Skyrim.esm|020132: Looks like we're the only ones who made it. Was that really a dragon? The bringers of the End Times?
Skyrim.esm|020133: Let's keep moving. That thing is still out there.
Skyrim.esm|0202D6: Well would you look at that! This appears to lead somewhere. Let's see where it goes.
Skyrim.esm|0202DA: I'm not sure. I certainly wasn't expecting this. This appears to lead somewhere. Let's see where it goes.
Skyrim.esm|020303: Unfortunately, we've had to implement more stringent entry procedures, due to some problems with the local Nords. We don't anticipate any real violence, but it never hurts to be prepared.
Skyrim.esm|020304: Now I'll show you to your quarters. You're going to be sharing space with your fellow Apprentices, who you'll meet shortly.
Skyrim.esm|020305: This is where you'll be staying. This bed and desk are yours. Please try and be considerate of others. Now, let's go back to the Hall of the Elements, where most of the members gather for lectures and study sessions.
Skyrim.esm|020306: Initially you'll be learning from Tolfdir, one of our most esteemed Wizards.
Skyrim.esm|020307: Our newest members are housed here in the Hall of Attainment. I'll ask that you please keep your voice down while inside, as others may be working on research or... delicate experiments.
Skyrim.esm|020308: Tolfdir is likely already addressing the new Apprentices. Go on in, and if you have any problems, let one of our senior members know.
Skyrim.esm|020428: Ah, so the hunter came to know the fear of the hunted, hmm? Your payment, my friend. Well done.
Skyrim.esm|020429: I hope the elf didn't give you too much trouble. I once got set on fire by a sorcerer. Nasty business, that. But I digress. Here's your payment.
Skyrim.esm|02042A: No sarcasm this time. You faced a vampire, and lived to tell the tale. Well done. And if you contracted Sanguinare Vampiris, be sure to get that taken care of. Else you'll end up like our own Babette. Unless, of course, that was your goal all along. Here's your payment.
Skyrim.esm|02042C: By Sithis, now I am truly impressed! To kill a pirate captain, on her own ship no less? Simply masterful! You've not only earned your payment, but my respect as well. And believe me, friend - that does not come easily. I'm afraid that concludes our business. You have exhausted all available additional contracts. But I'm sure you've got plenty to keep you busy right about now, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|02042D: Congratulations. You slaughtered an emaciated beggar in cold blood. You are truly an opponent to be feared.
Skyrim.esm|02042E: Wonderful. The only good bard is a dead one, as far as I'm concerned. All that singing and... mirth. Your payment, as per the usual.
Skyrim.esm|02042F: Personally, I've always found Argonians to be incredibly unpredictable opponents. Especially near water. In any event, nice work. Here's your payment.
Skyrim.esm|020430: Ah! Well done! Well done! Those caravaners can be an elusive bunch. Takes a fair bit of stalking to take one down. But then, that's half the fun, isn't it? Here's your payment. Well earned, indeed.
Skyrim.esm|020432: Good. And I hope you were careful in that lumber mill. Those splinters and rusty nails can be quite nasty. Your payment, for a job well done.
Skyrim.esm|020433: Hmph. Well, that's sure to throw a branch into Falkreath's well-oiled political machine. Here's your payment. Well earned, as always.
Skyrim.esm|020434: Don't worry, my friend, I'll spare you the facetious quip about killing a defenseless old woman. Infiltrating an occupied fort and making it out in one piece is nothing to joke about. Well done. Your payment for services rendered.
Skyrim.esm|020435: Well, what happens now is you start your new life in the Dark Brotherhood. You're part of the Family, after all. This, as you can see, is our Sanctuary. You won't find a safer place in all of Skyrim. So get comfortable.
Skyrim.esm|020436: Of course she is. I hear the mining business is extremely cutthroat. And those hours... they're murder. I could do this all day. Here's your payment.
Skyrim.esm|020535: Keerava, Bersi Honey-Hand and Haelga. Do this right, and I can promise you a permanent place in our organization.
Skyrim.esm|020536: Honestly, the debt is secondary here. What's more important is that you get the message across that we aren't to be ignored. A word of warning though... I don't want any of them killed. Bad for business.
Skyrim.esm|020537: Well, well... color me impressed, lad. I wasn't certain I'd ever see you again!
Skyrim.esm|02053B: Reliable and headstrong? You're turning out to be quite the prize! So... now that I've whetted your appetite with our little scheme at the market, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
Skyrim.esm|020540: I'll be here when you're done.
Skyrim.esm|020541: Come back when you grow a backbone.
Skyrim.esm|020542: Keerava's stubborn, but she's got a soft spot for family. Talk to Talen-Jei at the Bee and Barb and see if you can get something out of him. They're... well acquainted if you catch my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|0205BD: Hmph. Yes. Yes, I believe you are. Very well, then. You need to find and kill Safia, the captain of the pirate ship Red Wave. The ship travels the northern coast, and sometimes isn't even in Skyrim at all. So you'll have to be patient. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0205BE: Well then, your timing couldn't be better. As it turns out, I've got two new contracts. One rather easy assignment, and another that should prove quite a challenge. Your first target is an Orc bard named Lurbuk. The other is a vampire by the name of Hern. Happy hunting.
Skyrim.esm|0205BF: Well aren't we the eager one? Sorry to disappoint, but there's nothing. Try me again later.
Skyrim.esm|0205C0: Well, yes. Two of them. But you're reaching the end of what I have available. Your targets are a dark elf wizard named Maluril, in the dwarven ruin of Mzinchaleft. And Helvard, the housecarl of Falkreath. Kill these two, and I'll have one last contract to give you. I'm just finalizing some details now.
Skyrim.esm|0205C1: Just one right now, actually. I need you to eliminate an old woman named Agnis, a servant in Fort Greymoor.
Skyrim.esm|02064E: Enjoy this while you can. Soon you will all be slaves.
Skyrim.esm|020651: Someone get that thing out of here and burn it.
Skyrim.esm|020655: And you. A Stormcloak? I'd thought better of you. You'll all come to regret this day.
Skyrim.esm|02065A: I presume you're talking to me?
Skyrim.esm|020660: Yield. I yield, Stormcloak! The city of Markarth is yours.
Skyrim.esm|020666: I said, on your knees, Elf!
Skyrim.esm|020674: Don't worry. We'll take good care of your city when you're gone. When Ulfric Stormcloak becomes High King, Skyrim will finally be the way my ancestors intended. It's a new day in Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|02068D: A bit melodramatic, even for you Laila. Pack your things and go.
Skyrim.esm|020695: I will not surrender Markarth without raising my own sword in her defense.
Skyrim.esm|02069B: The Empire will return. Count on it.
Skyrim.esm|0206A2: He wants the city this badly, he can fight me for it.
Skyrim.esm|0206A7: He's as pig-headed a man as you'll ever find. The key is that ugly dwarven urn in his shop. Smash that thing to bits and he'll change his attitude.
Skyrim.esm|0206A8: Haelga is a devout follower of Dibella and dotes over the statue to the Divine that she keeps at the Bunkhouse. Use it as leverage and she'll cave.
Skyrim.esm|0206B5: She wants the city this badly, she can fight me for it.
Skyrim.esm|0206BD: Please, please! This is exactly what I'm talking about. Eagerness must be tempered with caution, or else disaster is inevitable.
Skyrim.esm|0206BE: Sir, I think we all understand that fairly well. We wouldn't be here if we couldn't control magic!
Skyrim.esm|0206BF: Kill them all. Protect the Jarl at all costs!
Skyrim.esm|0206C0: So, as I was saying, the first thing to understand is that magic is, by its very nature, volatile and dangerous. Unless you can control it, it can and will destroy you.
Skyrim.esm|0206C1: Then what are we waiting for? Let's get started!
Skyrim.esm|0206C2: Of course, my dear. Of course. You all certainly possess some inherent natural ability. That much is not being questioned. What I'm talking about is true control, mastery of magic. It takes years, if not decades, of practice and study.
Skyrim.esm|0206C3: But we've only just arrived here -- you've no idea what any of us are capable of. Why not give us a chance to show you what we can do?
Skyrim.esm|0206C4: Welcome, welcome! We were just beginning. Please, stay and listen.
Skyrim.esm|02078B: Ready to listen to the Silver-Blood family now, Igmund?
Skyrim.esm|020799: Halt in the name of the Jarl Hrongar.
Skyrim.esm|020816: Thongvor. I should have executed your whole treacherous clan when I had the chance.
Skyrim.esm|020826: Come Galmar, we have much to do.
Skyrim.esm|020880: Are you two quite done? You, go pack your things. And you, go sit on that damn chair and start acting like a Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|020881: Get over to Windhelm. Tell Ulfric of our victory here.
Skyrim.esm|02088E: They will sing stories of this day.
Skyrim.esm|020948: I'd be a lot warmer and a lot happier with a bellyful of mead...
Skyrim.esm|02094A: Just a few more hours and I can crawl under some furs...
Skyrim.esm|02094D: They say Ulfric Stormcloak murdered the High King... with his voice! Shouted him apart!
Skyrim.esm|02094E: Stay alert. Never know when those Stormcloak dogs might charge.
Skyrim.esm|02094F: Keep an eye out for Stormcloak spies.
Skyrim.esm|020950: Gotta keep my eyes open. Damned dragons could swoop down at any time.
Skyrim.esm|020953: Can you do it? Can you devour a dragon's soul?
Skyrim.esm|020954: I used to be an adventurer like you. Then I took an arrow in the knee...
Skyrim.esm|020955: The Imperials think we need their laws. Pfft.
Skyrim.esm|020956: I long to be out there, with my brothers, waging war against the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|020957: The Imperials think us all lawless beasts. I'm proof of their ignorance.
Skyrim.esm|020958: Legion soldiers gleam like fresh-fallen snow and clank like a kitchen. They head this way, we'll know it.
Skyrim.esm|02095B: I'll fight the entire Legion myself if they dare show their faces here.
Skyrim.esm|02095C: 'Tis a wicked axe you wield there, friend. That blade looks sharp enough to cut through a god.
Skyrim.esm|0209BB: Vile! None of that - you made a deal, and the mortal stood by it honestly. Now it's your turn to come through.
Skyrim.esm|0209BD: Oh, fine. Have my boon and be done with it. I've got more interesting deals to make, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0209BE: A lack of ambition like that really ought to be punished. Perhaps by turning you into a worm, or maybe a few decades of...
Skyrim.esm|0209BF: I'll make sure this war ends, all right. Just as soon as the dragons swarm across Skyrim and wipe out all of you puny...
Skyrim.esm|0209C0: You can be a part of a tremendous power - all you have to do is let your soul be absorbed by this axe. I never said it would be your power...
Skyrim.esm|0209C1: In Rimerock Burrow, there's an artifact called the Rueful Axe. Bring it to me, and I'll take Barbas back. Simple as that.
Skyrim.esm|0209C2: Ah, that feels so much better! You forget how nice supreme power feels until you've been stuck in a cave for a few years.
Skyrim.esm|0209C3: It's a shame you wished for something so dull as me taking back the mutt. Quite the lack of imagination on your part.
Skyrim.esm|0209C4: Now, as for your wish... An end to the war, was it?
Skyrim.esm|0209C5: Now, as for your wish... Power, was it?
Skyrim.esm|020A23: Well, your classmates certainly seem to disagree with you.
Skyrim.esm|020A25: Not going to weigh in either way? I admire your restraint, but there are times when you must make decisions, even without all the facts. Rely on instinct.
Skyrim.esm|020A26: See? He agrees with us too! Why don't you actually show us something?
Skyrim.esm|020A27: See? She agrees with us too! Why don't you actually show us something?
Skyrim.esm|020A28: Oh, don't listen to him. We can do it, just give us a chance!
Skyrim.esm|020A29: Oh, don't listen to her. We can do it, just give us a chance!
Skyrim.esm|020A2A: You've been quiet so far. What do you think we should do?
Skyrim.esm|020A2B: We're all pretty new at this, right? Let's just give it a chance. What's the worst that could happen?
Skyrim.esm|020A2C: You'll need to speak with Mirabelle Ervine. Her quarters are in the Hall of Countenance, but she could be anywhere on the grounds.
Skyrim.esm|020ACC: Well, I see that my time is up. Come back tomorrow if you wish to buy.
Skyrim.esm|020ACD: Do you want me to hold your hand as well? You have the ring. All you have to do is pick Brand-Shei's pocket and plant it there.
Skyrim.esm|020ACF: How did you get that, then? No one's seen them in years!
Skyrim.esm|020AD0: That's what you said about the Wisp Essence and it turned out to be crushed nirnroot mixed with water!
Skyrim.esm|020AD3: Oh come on, are you talking about the Snow Elves?
Skyrim.esm|020AD4: We've been contracted to make sure Brand-Shei remembers not to meddle in affairs that aren't his own. Now, since we're not the Dark Brotherhood, we're not going to kill him, we're just going to make sure he sits in the prisons for a few days.
Skyrim.esm|020AD6: That was a simple misunderstanding, but this item is the real thing. Lads and lasses I give you, Falmerblood Elixir!
Skyrim.esm|020AD7: Patience, Brand-Shei. This is a rare opportunity, and I wouldn't want you to get left out.
Skyrim.esm|020ADA: It's all about sizing up your mark, lass. The way they walk, what they're wearing. It's a dead giveaway.
Skyrim.esm|020ADC: Sorry... I usually have a nose for this kind of thing. Never mind then, lass. If you change your mind, come find me.
Skyrim.esm|020ADE: No pushing, no shoving. Plenty of room!
Skyrim.esm|020AE2: Everyone! Everyone! Gather 'round! I have something amazing to show you that demands your attention!
Skyrim.esm|020B85: Learn a library's worth of knowledge in moments!
Skyrim.esm|020B89: Who knows what powers the elixir will unlock!
Skyrim.esm|020B8A: Please hurry, I have only a few bottles left!
Skyrim.esm|020B8D: Twenty coins? No one has that much.
Skyrim.esm|020B8E: Don't listen to him, he's making this up.
Skyrim.esm|020B8F: Why would you even listen to him?
Skyrim.esm|020B90: He gives other merchants a bad name.
Skyrim.esm|020B93: My sources must remain a secret for their own protection but I can promise that the contents are genuine. One sip of the elixir and your wishes will be granted. Great wealth, everlasting life or perhaps limitless power could be yours!
Skyrim.esm|020B94: Come on, Brynjolf... what is it this time?
Skyrim.esm|020B95: Bah. My organization's been having a run of bad luck, but I suppose that's just how it goes. But never mind that, even though you fouled up the job I still think you've got that spark I'm looking for. There's plenty of gold out there for the taking... if you're up to the challenge.
Skyrim.esm|020B9C: You want to talk to me? After everything you've done?
Skyrim.esm|020BA0: I hope the wilds claim you, one of these days.
Skyrim.esm|020BAD: What do you need, my friend?
Skyrim.esm|020BBA: I've had just about all I'm willing to take from you. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|020BC2: You and me, we're the only people around who aren't complete fools.
Skyrim.esm|020BCE: The one and only. Mystical beings who live in legends and were masters of great magic. Imagine the power that coursed through their veins!
Skyrim.esm|020BD0: I dunno, sounds like I should try it.
Skyrim.esm|020BD1: What if he's right? Maybe I'll buy one.
Skyrim.esm|020BD4: I could get rich and stop selling these trinkets.
Skyrim.esm|020BD5: This is just what I need right now.
Skyrim.esm|020BD6: I can't afford to get one, but can I afford not to?
Skyrim.esm|020BD7: That Wisp Essence didn't help, but maybe this will.
Skyrim.esm|020BD8: Only twenty gold septims! Hurry before my supply is gone.
Skyrim.esm|020BD9: There's someone that wants to see him put out of business permanently. That's all you need to know. I'll be out in the market all day, from eight in the morning until eight in the evening. Meet me then if you've still got the stomach for it.
Skyrim.esm|020BDA: Damn. I knew I shouldn't have waited.
Skyrim.esm|020BDD: Our Family, my dearest. Our Family. Together, united as one, the Dark Brotherhood can accomplish anything. But you must be anxious to get to work. I'm arranging a job, but need a bit more time. For now, go see Nazir. He's got several smaller contracts. Soon, the Night Mother will arrive. And things around here are sure to get even more interesting. Ah, but one last thing. A welcome home present. The armor of the Dark Brotherhood. May it serve you well in all your... endeavors.
Skyrim.esm|020BDE: Soon, my dearest. Soon. I'm arranging a job for you, but I need some more time. For now, go talk to Nazir. He may have some smaller contracts to tide you over. Soon, the Night Mother will arrive. And things around here are sure to get even more interesting. Ah, but one last thing. A welcome home present. The armor of the Dark Brotherhood. May it serve you well in all your... endeavors.
Skyrim.esm|020BDF: Hmm... Yes, the silence suits you. Gives you an air of mystery. Now, down to business. I'm arranging a job for you. But in the meantime, go talk to Nazir. He may have some smaller contracts to tide you over. Soon, the Night Mother will arrive. And things around here are sure to get even more interesting. Ah, but one last thing. A welcome home present. The armor of the Dark Brotherhood. May it serve you well in all your... endeavors.
Skyrim.esm|020BE0: Am I to take your silence as acceptance? Then you know where we stand. Make your kill, and we're square. Repayment of your debt is but a discreet knife thrust away.
Skyrim.esm|020BE1: See, I knew we could resolve this civilly. A debt owed must be repaid. You understand that. Well, get to it then. Pick your guest, and send the poor fool to the Void.  I'll give you the key to this shack, and you'll be on your way.
Skyrim.esm|020BE2: Now that is a shame. But what you fail to realize is that you involved yourself in this "insanity" when you took Grelod's life. You made your choice. Now it's time to face the consequences of your actions. You don't leave this shack until someone dies.
Skyrim.esm|020F44: This isn't over. You hear me you old fool! This isn't over!
Skyrim.esm|020F45: Welcome back. I've left a few surprises for you to discover.
Skyrim.esm|020F46: Ahhh... It's good to be home.
Skyrim.esm|020F48: Enough! Both of you! There is a burning city out there that needs a government.
Skyrim.esm|020F6E: He's right. Galmar, come, let us restore order.
Skyrim.esm|020F93: Time to cleanse the Empire of its filth.
Skyrim.esm|020F9A: The only mistake was you showing your face. You've committed crimes against Skyrim and her people, and it's time to face the Jarl's justice.
Skyrim.esm|020FA5: Halt. You're under arrest for murder and conspiracy against the city of Markarth!
Skyrim.esm|020FA9: You aren't fooling me. Trusted witnesses have seen you commit several murders. And there are accounts of your suspicious behavior throughout the city. Now come quietly.
Skyrim.esm|021109: That's her business. Not mine. Probably not yours either.
Skyrim.esm|02110B: Yes. A map of ancient dragon burial sites. I've looked at which ones are now empty. The pattern is pretty clear. It seems to be spreading from the southeast, starting in the Jeralls near Riften. The one near Kynesgrove is next if the pattern holds.
Skyrim.esm|02110D: You're wrong. It is my business. You may be the only one that can stop these dragons. But you'll understand that soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|021110: You're not afraid of me, are you?
Skyrim.esm|021111: Just close the door, will you?
Skyrim.esm|021115: The attack will be launched imminently. Gather your weaponry and get to the front lines... Now, soldier!
Skyrim.esm|0211D9: You should know. You got the map for me from Bleak Falls Barrow.
Skyrim.esm|0211DC: This is no time to play the reluctant hero. You either are or aren't Dragonborn. But I'll see for myself soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0211DF: Close the door, then we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|0211E2: Good. And you'll have a chance to prove it to me soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0211E9: I didn't go to all this trouble on a whim. I needed to make sure it wasn't a Thalmor trap.
Skyrim.esm|0211F2: You should know. You got the map for me. The dragonstone you got for Farengar, remember?
Skyrim.esm|0211F8: If I were you, I'd keep away from the barrow on the east side of town... it's haunted.
Skyrim.esm|0211F9: Need a room? Maybe a drink? You name it.
Skyrim.esm|0211FA: Let me know if I can get you anything.
Skyrim.esm|021200: Anything I can help you with? A drink or a room perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|021202: If you fancy a bit of music, let me know.
Skyrim.esm|021209: If you ever settle down to have children, think twice before you do it.
Skyrim.esm|02120A: I can't believe Fastred ran off to Riften with that... that bastard.
Skyrim.esm|02120C: I can't believe Fastred's gone. I... I should have been nicer to her.
Skyrim.esm|021211: You must be another pilgrim on the way up to High Hrothgar. No other reason to pass through here.
Skyrim.esm|021212: And to think that we've been supplying the Greybeards with our own food supplies. What have they ever done for us?
Skyrim.esm|021214: I hope Fastred's doing well in Riften. I miss her.
Skyrim.esm|021219: Oh! You're a traveler! You must have so much to tell about the world outside this boring town!
Skyrim.esm|02121A: I can't imagine how exciting it must be like to journey from place to place. You're so lucky!
Skyrim.esm|02121C: Have anything interesting to tell me?
Skyrim.esm|021221: I can't see you, Reyda! I can't find you! Why are you hiding? Hiding, hide, hide, hide! Don't make me sad!
Skyrim.esm|021222: Oh Reyda! Redya! You live among the clouds now, dear Reyda!
Skyrim.esm|021223: I miss Reyda. She was so nice to Narfi. Narfi's sad he can't be with Reyda.
Skyrim.esm|021225: Is it absolutely necessary for you to bother me right now?
Skyrim.esm|021229: You don't look like a pilgrim. Why bother visiting Ivarstead?
Skyrim.esm|02122F: Thanks again for delivering those supplies to the Greybeards.
Skyrim.esm|021231: 7,000 Steps indeed... next time they need to build it closer to the ground...
Skyrim.esm|021258: Oh, I'd love to help you figure it out, but I'm so busy cleaning up the mess you made of our temple... Now if you were to help tidy up and perhaps apologize afterwards... I might be able to help you.
Skyrim.esm|021260: You went right out to give it to your fiancee! Don't you even remember where you left him? And after you told me that sweet story of how you met in Witchmist Grove! I can see why he left you.
Skyrim.esm|021261: If you're looking for a room, you want the Four Shields.
Skyrim.esm|021262: It's no easy thing to make a living in the wilds of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|021263: Don't see many travelers out this way.
Skyrim.esm|021264: This land's tough and unforgiving, but beautiful, too.
Skyrim.esm|021265: You hear all this talk about a war? I hope it never comes here.
Skyrim.esm|021266: You look like you've seen your share of adventure.
Skyrim.esm|021269: Welcome, traveler. Mind yourself in our town.
Skyrim.esm|02126A: We aren't a big city like Solitude or Markarth, but we like it this way.
Skyrim.esm|02126B: Seen some mean-spirited folk pass through lately. Thought I was rid of those sorts when I left Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|02126C: If it weren't for Michel, I'd probably be dead in a Markarth gutter by now.
Skyrim.esm|02126D: Got a loving wife, a healthy son and a patch of land to call my own. What more could a man ask for?
Skyrim.esm|021271: Are you a soldier? One day, I'm going to be a soldier!
Skyrim.esm|021272: Pa says I'm too young to fight in the war, but I can still help by patrolling the town.
Skyrim.esm|021273: I wish I was a soldier! They get to travel and stay up late and have fun!
Skyrim.esm|021274: Have you met Lucky? He's my pet goat, and he helps me patrol the town.
Skyrim.esm|021277: You're a well-traveled sort, I can tell just looking at you.
Skyrim.esm|021278: Rest a spell! Can't work every minute of your life.
Skyrim.esm|021279: Working hard gives a man reason for drinking hard! Well, that and putting up with a wife like mine.
Skyrim.esm|02127A: Money's been tight since the mill burned down. My wife won't let me forget it.
Skyrim.esm|02127B: You been to Markarth? They've got a tavern called the Silver-Blood Inn. Best ale this side of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|021280: You going to try and sell me something? Well, I ain't interested.
Skyrim.esm|021282: Did my worthless husband send you?
Skyrim.esm|021286: Got nothing to spare for travelers nor refugees, here.
Skyrim.esm|021287: If you're looking for handouts, look somewhere else.
Skyrim.esm|021288: You want anything in this life, you gotta take it. Just not from me.
Skyrim.esm|02128B: Faida's the name. I keep the inn.
Skyrim.esm|02128C: Seen a lot of travelers lately. Mostly soldier, nowadays.
Skyrim.esm|02128D: The meals I serve ain't what I'd call a Jarl's feast, but they'll fill your belly.
Skyrim.esm|02128E: You met the Lylvieves? Quiet family, but they're the heart of Dragon Bridge.
Skyrim.esm|02128F: Something different about you, I can tell. Hope you ain't here to cause trouble.
Skyrim.esm|021290: Poor Horgeir. Everyone in town knows about his wife's affair but him. Probably better if he doesn't know.
Skyrim.esm|021291: I think the dragons are here to test us, and make sure we're still true Nords.
Skyrim.esm|021292: I was a barmaid at the Winking Skeever when I met my husband, rest his soul. Wasn't for him, I'd still be working there!
Skyrim.esm|02129B: Respect the law, and you'll have no trouble in Dragon Bridge.
Skyrim.esm|02129C: Mind you don't cross Tassius. He's in charge around here.
Skyrim.esm|02129D: Don't start any trouble and I think we'll get along just fine.
Skyrim.esm|02129E: At least, if you're gonna cause trouble, go somewhere else, okay? Please?
Skyrim.esm|0212A3: Heard there were wolves on the road up north.
Skyrim.esm|0212A4: Those rebels better not try coming to Haafingar.
Skyrim.esm|0212CD: Oh, very good. Very good indeed. You, my dear, are going to fit in here quite nicely... Now, I need your assistance with a matter of a more... personal nature.
Skyrim.esm|0212CE: Isn't there some business you should be tending to?
Skyrim.esm|0212D1: That bastard got exactly what he deserved. But last I heard, Nilsine is still alive. So I guess you decided not to kill her as well. I have to admit... I'm disappointed. Here's the payment for killing Alain. And nothing for letting Nilsine live. Just remember, that was your decision.
Skyrim.esm|0212D2: Thank you. That bastard got exactly what he deserved. And... I heard about Nilsine. You have more than fulfilled your part of the bargain. Please, take this - as payment, and a symbol of my affection. I'll never forget you.
Skyrim.esm|0212D3: Ah, you're back. So, how went your first real contract? A bit more exciting than what Nazir's been offering, I'd wager.
Skyrim.esm|02144D: I went to Windhelm to see the Shatter-Shields. They were old and dear friends, and... in mourning. Friga was killed recently. Murdered... I met Alain in the tavern, while I was... drinking my sadness away. He was handsome, and charming. He said I was the "beautiful lily" of his dreams. Alain made all the pain just... go away. But it was all lies! Alain used me. He ruined my name, destroyed my friendship with the Shatter-Shields... Do you know why Alain was in Windhelm? He heard about Friga's murder. He wanted to befriend the family, in their grief... and rob them blind. Alain used me to get close to my friends. And now they all think I'm some kind of... monster. Alain Dufont took my life. And now I'm taking his.
Skyrim.esm|02144E: Why are you looking at me like that?
Skyrim.esm|021451: Of course, dear. Of course. And, from what my little ravens tell me, you handled yourself quite well. Now, I need your assistance with a matter of a more... personal nature.
Skyrim.esm|021452: Excellent. Once Alain is dead, I'll pay you. In gold. I've saved up a bit. I hope that'll do. But, well... There is one more thing. If you're interested?
Skyrim.esm|021453: Very well. I respect your discretion. Now, I need your assistance with a matter of a more... personal nature.
Skyrim.esm|021454: What I need? What I need is for Alain Dufont to die! I want him hunted down and murdered like the dog he is!
Skyrim.esm|021455: The Dark Brotherh... Oh. Oh! I... my goodness, you're really here! The Black Sacrament. It actually worked?
Skyrim.esm|0214D2: Aventus really did it! He got the Dark Brotherhood to kill old Grelod! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0214D3: Ha ha ha ha ha! Hooray!
Skyrim.esm|0214D4: Grelod is dead! Aventus did it! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0214D5: Hooray! Grelod the Kind is dead at last! We're saved!
Skyrim.esm|0214D6: Someone has killed Grelod! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0214D8: We love you, Dark Brotherhood! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0214DF: Something you need, you miserable wretch?
Skyrim.esm|0214E4: Now why would I want to talk to you?
Skyrim.esm|0214E5: Good to see you. Finally someone useful is around.
Skyrim.esm|0214E6: Friends like you are hard to find and very valuable to me.
Skyrim.esm|0214F2: I don't like talking to someone who holds their honor so cheaply.
Skyrim.esm|0214F6: You have something to say, dog? Something on your mind?
Skyrim.esm|0214F9: Good to have you by my side, friend. I need reliable people around.
Skyrim.esm|0214FB: You're someone who can get things done. I like that.
Skyrim.esm|021502: I should bash your face in after all you've done.
Skyrim.esm|02150C: You've been a good friend to me. That means something.
Skyrim.esm|021518: In continuing with our theme of safety, we'll start with Wards. Wards are protective spells that block magic. I'll teach you all a ward, and we'll see if you can successfully use it to block spells, all right?
Skyrim.esm|021519: All right, let's settle down. I suppose we can try something practical...
Skyrim.esm|021524: Looks like you're still in one piece. And more importantly, so is the book. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|021532: Look, I'm only telling you this because I care for her. Don't mistake this as acceptance for what you do. Keerava has some family at a farm just inside of Morrowind. If you mention you know about it, she might just listen to you. Just please, don't harm anyone. I couldn't bear the thought.
Skyrim.esm|021533: Look around you, I'm barely keeping this rathole together. The war's seen to that.
Skyrim.esm|021534: With the rumors going around about how poorly your Guild's been doing, she's become much too bold. I'm not that foolish. The last thing I want is a war with your people.
Skyrim.esm|021537: I wasn't sure that he'd see the light, but I'm sure you "explained" it to him. Well done!
Skyrim.esm|021538: Good, and take this little message back to your bosses. I'm not paying any of you a single septim... ever! Now get out of my inn!
Skyrim.esm|02153A: Now if there are no more questions, how about following me and I'll show you what we're all about.
Skyrim.esm|02153B: She's as stubborn as she is beautiful, isn't she? Glad you found a way to get her to pay.
Skyrim.esm|02153C: She was the toughest of the bunch. Honestly, I'm amazed she didn't kick you out of the Bee and Barb herself.
Skyrim.esm|021541: Now what did you want? I already paid you.
Skyrim.esm|021542: I'm not paying you or Brynjolf a single coin! Now get out of my tavern!
Skyrim.esm|021543: I said get out! I'm not paying a single coin!
Skyrim.esm|021545: Here for a room or something to drink? Show me some coin first.
Skyrim.esm|021546: We got the message... now just take your business elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|02157A: I heard a scream and came running, but she was already... like this... when I got here.
Skyrim.esm|02157B: Another girl killed. This is Susanna, from Candlehearth Hall. Served me a drink just a few nights ago... but I can't say I knew her.
Skyrim.esm|02157C: Eehhh... no. Sorry. But I did notice that her coinpurse was still intact, so whoever did this wasn't after gold. I'm going to keep preparing the body, if you'll excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|02157D: Just like always -- nobody saw anything useful. The bastard's escaped again.
Skyrim.esm|02157E: To the east of here is a hidden grove where the Eldergleam resides. It's the oldest living thing in Skyrim. Maybe all of Tamriel. Our tree here in the city was grown from a cutting of that tree. You can still feel the glory of the mother tree through it. Even its name is an echo.
Skyrim.esm|021580: Was I correct in hearing that you were traveling to the grove of the Eldergleam?
Skyrim.esm|021581: Sorry. I thought I saw a fellow running away, but didn't get a good look at him.
Skyrim.esm|0215AF: You can't tell them! Please, I need your help and there isn't anyone I can trust here in the city.
Skyrim.esm|0215B0: I am not the person that the people of Whiterun think I am. My real name is Iman. I am a noble of House Suda in Hammerfell. The men who are looking for me, the Alik'r, they are assassins in the employ of the Aldmeri Dominion. They wish to exchange my blood for gold. I need you to root them out and drive them away before they find me and drag me back to Hammerfell for an execution.
Skyrim.esm|0215B1: Are you sure? Oh no! They've found me? I need your help! Please, come with me. I need to speak to you privately.
Skyrim.esm|0215B2: There will be. Of that, you can be assured.
Skyrim.esm|0215B4: We're looking for a fugitive who comes from Hammerfell. A Redguard woman. She may be somewhere in this city.
Skyrim.esm|0215B5: Then I need your help and there isn't anyone I can trust here in the city.
Skyrim.esm|0215B6: Of course, sent by... what is it that she's calling herself these days? Shazra? Saadia? One of those, correct? Did she appeal to your sense of honor? Your greed? A more... base need, perhaps? It doesn't matter. No doubt she's convinced you that she's the victim. But, do you know why we pursue her?
Skyrim.esm|0215B8: No. There are too many people around. Come with me.
Skyrim.esm|0215B9: So the Alik'r know where I am? What did they offer you? Gold? How many more of them are coming? Tell me!
Skyrim.esm|0215BD: A woman -- a foreigner in these lands. Redguard, like us. She is likely not using her true name. We will pay for any information regarding her location. We are not welcome here in Whiterun, so we will be in Rorikstead if you learn anything.
Skyrim.esm|0215BE: You have a death wish, then? If you know that name, you must know to meet him would be to meet your end. But it seems we both have needs, friend. Perhaps we can help each other out.
Skyrim.esm|0215BF: You buy my way out of here, and I'll tell you what you want to know.
Skyrim.esm|0215C1: 100 gold will secure my release. You can afford that, can't you? I suppose you'd better hope you can, if you want the information. Get the money into the hands of one of these guards, and I'll tell you what you want to know.
Skyrim.esm|0215C2: Very well. Kematu is west of Whiterun. It's an unassuming little cave called Swindler's Den. You realize if you set foot in there, you're never coming back out. They'll kill you. But that's your problem, not mine.
Skyrim.esm|0215C3: Then we have nothing more to say to one another.
Skyrim.esm|0215C5: Because I was clumsy enough to be captured. Kematu always says we're supposed to be the best of the best. I wasn't.
Skyrim.esm|0215C6: I have dishonored my brothers by being captured, and so they have left me here. My life with the Alik'r is over now, but I have no wish to die in this gods-forsaken land. If I can be released from prison, I may start over. See to that, and I'll tell you what you want to know.
Skyrim.esm|0215C9: Can you help me find someone? There's gold in it for you.
Skyrim.esm|0215CA: We're looking for someone. Maybe you can help.
Skyrim.esm|0215CB: I have some questions for you. I'm trying to find someone.
Skyrim.esm|0215CC: The Alik'r are not permitted within the city walls.
Skyrim.esm|0215CD: You're not one of those Alik'r thugs, are you?
Skyrim.esm|0215CE: By decree of the Jarl, the Alik'r are banned from Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|021607: Sweet Mother, sweet Mother, send your child unto me, for the sins of the unworthy must be baptized in blood and fear.
Skyrim.esm|021608: Please... How long must I do this? I keep praying, Night Mother. Why won't you answer me?
Skyrim.esm|021609: Sweet Mother, sweet Mother, send your child unto me, for the sins of the unworthy must be baptized in blood and fear.
Skyrim.esm|02160B: Grelod, you old crone. You'll get what you deserve. The Dark Brotherhood will see to that...
Skyrim.esm|021618: What do you want? You've caused enough trouble.
Skyrim.esm|021621: Good to see you again, friend.
Skyrim.esm|021623: Now here's a man I'm glad to see.
Skyrim.esm|021624: Now here's a woman I'm glad to see.
Skyrim.esm|02162B: You! You're going to hurt me again aren't you? Just stay away.
Skyrim.esm|021630: I don't mean you any trouble. Just leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|021631: It's so good to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|021637: Divines bless you. May the ground you walk quake as you pass.
Skyrim.esm|02164A: My favorite drinking buddy. Let's get some mead.
Skyrim.esm|02164E: Just looking at you makes me upset.
Skyrim.esm|021659: Good to see Skyrim still has such fine people. You give an old man hope.
Skyrim.esm|02166B: All right, then. I noticed a trail of blood leading away from here. Helgird's taken the body into the Hall of the Dead to prepare for burial. She's a little crazy, but if she knows anything, it's dead bodies.
Skyrim.esm|02166D: Uf. Have you been talking to Viola Giordano? She posts those all over the city, and someone keeps taking them down. Ask her about it if you want an earful.
Skyrim.esm|02166E: These are difficult times indeed, when men stalk their brethren like beasts. My men are stretched thin as it is. If you offer your aid, I gladly accept. The guards will be told to assist you as necessary. I'm happy to lend a hand as much as I can, as well.
Skyrim.esm|02166F: I've been following him for months now. Well, not actually following. Trying to find him. The guards won't help. The people won't help. I'm the only one who thinks he can be caught.
Skyrim.esm|021671: Friga Shatter-Shield's old place? It's been abandoned ever since she was killed.
Skyrim.esm|0216A0: Look here. There's something strange about this cabinet.
Skyrim.esm|02170A: Now you're speaking my language. Tell you what. If you need any stolen goods fenced, you bring them to me and I'll pay you good money for them. Consider me your new friend in the north.
Skyrim.esm|02170B: I asked her the same thing and she wouldn't come out and tell me. But now that I know who she is, I'd say she's trying to hit the Thieves Guild where it hurts.
Skyrim.esm|0217DF: Yes, I suppose there is. There is a letter, one I have had for some time now. I think it is time it gets delivered. I cannot leave Idgrod's side, so I must stay here. Could you see that it reaches Captain Aldis in Solitude?
Skyrim.esm|0217E0: Oh, no. I hadn't heard from Gorm for a while, so I'd hoped he'd given up on his little scheme. The last time we'd discussed this issue was before this damnable war broke out. Things were different then. Now, what's he's asking, it's just impossible. Even if it worked, the questions and accusations it would cause... Well, I'll take it under advisement. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0217E1: Well now, that is something. Hand it over, please.
Skyrim.esm|0217E2: Is that so? I should have expected as much. Fine, I shall give you 200 gold, and you shall leave me in peace. Now go, leave me to my work.
Skyrim.esm|0217E3: Ah, so it is not just dreams then. I have seen this. I will see that it is dealt with. Thank you for bringing clarity.
Skyrim.esm|0217E4: Is that so? Well, I'll make sure that's taken care of immediately. It's good that you brought this to my attention.
Skyrim.esm|0217E6: I see. If something does happen, you know where I am.
Skyrim.esm|0217E7: Either this is some kind of a joke, or you're keeping something from me. Whichever it is, I don't like it.
Skyrim.esm|0217E8: Thank you. I will not forget this.
Skyrim.esm|0217EA: I'm told that you're up to something out in the marsh, Falion.
Skyrim.esm|0217EB: What is the meaning of this?
Skyrim.esm|0217EC: There's a... A problem, yes. I'm worried about Idgrod, and I don't know what to do.
Skyrim.esm|0217F5: Lone travelers can find these woods a little treacherous.
Skyrim.esm|0217F6: We've been looking for someone to join us in a hunt.
Skyrim.esm|0217FA: Hern and I do so adore guests! These guards are such a bore.
Skyrim.esm|0217FB: The patrols never seem to give us time to be alone, the nosy pests.
Skyrim.esm|0217FD: Why don't you stay a while?
Skyrim.esm|021808: Is Hert his sister or his wife? Or both?
Skyrim.esm|02180C: Hert keeps giving me this look. I don't know if she wants me or if she wants to eat me.
Skyrim.esm|02180F: Just my luck, stuck in the sticks with this crazy old woman.
Skyrim.esm|021811: I grew up hearing songs about Eyldi the Bear! She wrestled a storm out of the sky!
Skyrim.esm|021813: It is none of your concern, I assure you. Just... Forget that you ever saw me here.
Skyrim.esm|021863: She's so lost in her visions, and it's only getting worse. I've looked after her safety for so long... If she can't tell what's real and what's not, how will she continue to lead Hjaalmarch?
Skyrim.esm|021889: What I am doing is none of your business! Why are you here? Did you follow me? I demand that you leave at once and never speak of this again.
Skyrim.esm|02188A: Ah, Falion. Always looking where he shouldn't for things he doesn't need. What has he done now?
Skyrim.esm|02188B: I've never trusted him... what has he done?
Skyrim.esm|02188C: You've been exploring places you shouldn't, and you know it.
Skyrim.esm|02188D: I don't even want to know what it is you've been doing.
Skyrim.esm|02188E: It stops at once, or else there will be consequences of an unpleasant nature. Do you understand?
Skyrim.esm|02188F: It doesn't happen again, or else I'll have you thrown in jail. Clear?
Skyrim.esm|021890: I am under no obligation to tell you anything. Now, leave me at once and never mention that you saw me.
Skyrim.esm|021A6A: Now, if you'll just stand right over there, I'll cast a spell at you, and you block it with the ward. Here we go.
Skyrim.esm|021A6B: Well, then this should be no problem at all for you.
Skyrim.esm|021A6C: That's all right. I can teach you a very basic ward, one that's enough for the purposes of this lesson.
Skyrim.esm|021A6D: That's what this lesson is for. Perfect time to try it out.
Skyrim.esm|021A6E: Would you mind helping me with the demonstration? Are you at all familiar with ward spells?
Skyrim.esm|021CDF: I hope your parents are proud of you. They have a lot of reason to be.
Skyrim.esm|021CE1: Leave me alone. Can't an old man have some peace?
Skyrim.esm|021CE2: I was apprenticed to Balimund before he died. He taught me everything I know about forging armor and weapons.
Skyrim.esm|021CE5: Catchy, isn't it? In my youth I was a fisherman... I had a beautiful ship named "The Brawny Prawn." But the years have a way of creeping up on you. I ended up selling that ship to open this place. Seemed only fitting to name it after her. Well, changed it a bit I suppose. If I had been smarter, I would have kept my boat. Coming to this city was a big mistake.
Skyrim.esm|021CEA: You're not as dumb as you look.
Skyrim.esm|021CF1: Well, look at you. If only everyone acted like you did.
Skyrim.esm|021CF2: It was nice to meet you.
Skyrim.esm|021CF4: Be careful out there... Riften can be dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|021CF6: Door's over there. Why don't you use it?
Skyrim.esm|021CF8: How can I help you today?
Skyrim.esm|021CF9: You. Every time I look at you my blood boils.
Skyrim.esm|021CFA: Have you met Balimund? I'm his apprentice. He's teaching me all about being a blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|021D00: It's a fine day with you around.
Skyrim.esm|021D07: You Nords and your bloody sense of honor.
Skyrim.esm|021D08: If Ulfric's making a move for Whiterun, then we need to be there to stop him. Draft another letter with the usual platitudes, but this time share some of your intelligence regarding Ulfric's plans. Embellish if you have to. We'll let it seem like it's his idea.
Skyrim.esm|021D0F: Worthless. Everyone like you is completely worthless.
Skyrim.esm|021D12: Good to see you. At least you know how to treat someone properly.
Skyrim.esm|021D28: Eldergleam's sanctuary grove is to the east of here. You can use Nettlebane to retrieve some of its sap.
Skyrim.esm|021D2C: The hags are hateful of anything which grows from the earth. They made the weapon strong against the natural world. It's the only thing I've even heard of that could cut Eldergleam. Stick it in, give it a twist, and the stuff will just flow out.
Skyrim.esm|021D2D: The hags are hateful of anything which grows from the earth. They made the weapon strong against the natural world. It's the only thing I've even heard of that could cut Eldergleam.
Skyrim.esm|021D2F: I understand. Some journeys must be taken alone. I'll be here if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|021D31: I had no idea you were a woman of violence. What exactly are you intending to do here?
Skyrim.esm|021E62: After I've finished showing you the grounds, we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|021E63: How do you think we should spend the remainder of our classroom time?
Skyrim.esm|021E64: If you have any further questions, can you please hold them until I've finished?
Skyrim.esm|021E7A: Likewise. Now, I have a lot to do, so I'm afraid you'll just have to leave!
Skyrim.esm|021E7B: We've run into a rough patch lately, but it's nothing to be concerned about. Tell you what. You keep making us coin and I'll worry about everything else. Fair enough?
Skyrim.esm|021E7F: Petty threats and fist waving are not going to sway me. You people are all talk, and everyone knows it! You demand payment for protection and you can't even protect yourselves!
Skyrim.esm|021E80: Look, I can't make the coin appear out of thin air. Please, be reasonable. I'll... I'll pay next month.
Skyrim.esm|021E82: Despite your mistakes, I see more potential in you than half the snot-nosed footpads that stumble their way into the Flagon. I'm going to put my neck on the line. We need people like you in our outfit.
Skyrim.esm|021E85: You... you have my statue! What are you going to do with it?
Skyrim.esm|021E86: And so have I. What's the point of paying anyway? Your outfit can't even fend for itself. I could do better tossing the gold into the sewer. You can't scare me with your tough talk. I'm not paying you people a single coin.
Skyrim.esm|021E88: I hope you're here for friendlier reasons now... after all, I'm all paid up.
Skyrim.esm|021E8A: Please, reconsider what you're doing. Maybe I can pay later...
Skyrim.esm|021E8B: I... I don't care! I'm not paying!
Skyrim.esm|021E8C: I'm sorry, you're going to have to leave. Tell Brynjolf he'll just have to make do without my coin.
Skyrim.esm|021E8E: So, can I interest you in anything today?
Skyrim.esm|021E8F: Look, I got the message. What else did you want?
Skyrim.esm|021E90: Please! Don't take the statue! It's the only thing of value I have left!
Skyrim.esm|021E91: Ask all you want, you can't squeeze blood from a stone. Not in this part of the world anyway.
Skyrim.esm|021E92: It's you! Please, don't hurt me!
Skyrim.esm|021E95: Then I thank you for showing me mercy. Here, take this coin to him. If it keeps us together, it's worth its weight.
Skyrim.esm|021E96: Don't fool yourself. It's only a matter of time before you people are run out of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|021F77: Every time I think of you, I imagine a saber cat closing its claws around your face.
Skyrim.esm|021F86: It's so good to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|021F87: You're mean. I hate you. I hate you forever and ever and ever.
Skyrim.esm|021F88: I hope monsters come and eat you.
Skyrim.esm|021F93: I bet you could slay one of those mean old dragons. I bet you could do anything.
Skyrim.esm|021F94: You're the best! Can you be my father?
Skyrim.esm|021F95: You're the best! Can you be my mother?
Skyrim.esm|0221E4: First off, they've got some rebels holed up in a nearby settlement. Need to clean them out so our boys can move in.
Skyrim.esm|0221E5: First, we need to limit Stormcloak capacity and send them a not so subtle message by sabotaging a resource they claim to control.
Skyrim.esm|0221E6: First, we have information regarding the movements of a rebel courier. We need to intercept him and confiscate his package.
Skyrim.esm|0221E7: And perhaps more importantly, I need to break one of our boys out of a rebel controlled fort.
Skyrim.esm|0221E8: Secondly, we got more of 'em hiding in a village, using the local populace as shields. Not to worry. We can dispatch them without harming the locals.
Skyrim.esm|0221E9: Secondly, I need to cripple rebel production capabilities with a little sabotage.
Skyrim.esm|0221EA: Soldiers win wars, but where to put them is the key. We have word of an enemy courier in the area. We aim to confiscate his documents.
Skyrim.esm|02238F: Very well. You, outsider, come here! I want a word.
Skyrim.esm|022395: So it will be. Malacath has spoken, Yamarz. Your path is clear.
Skyrim.esm|02239F: This is all your fault, you know. I'm stuck fighting a giant now, thanks to you. So you're going to help me. You're going with me, and you're going to make sure I don't have any trouble reaching that giant. Don't worry, I'll make it worth your while.
Skyrim.esm|0223BD: If there's a chance this will save me, then I'm taking it.
Skyrim.esm|0223C8: Excellent. Now, you must come with me. You've become a part of this. You must be present for the ritual.
Skyrim.esm|0223C9: You don't deserve to call yourself an Orc! You're weak, you're small, and you're an embarrassment!
Skyrim.esm|0223CF: Why are we bothering with this?
Skyrim.esm|0223D0: I'll lead the way, but you'd better back me up.
Skyrim.esm|0223D2: This giant... It's not the only thing in Fallowstone Cave. Getting to it isn't going to be easy. You're going to clear a path to the giant, so I can conserve my strength. I'll make sure you're paid for it. So you meet me at Fallowstone Cave, and I'll get this over with. I can have my tribe back, and you can... You can leave us alone.
Skyrim.esm|0223D4: Thanks to you, I've just been issued a challenge in front of my whole tribe, by a daedric prince, no less. I can't ignore that. I didn't say I wouldn't fight the giant. I just said you're going to help me get to it. Meet me outside Fallowstone Cave. You clear the way to the giant for me, and I'll make sure you get paid for it.
Skyrim.esm|0223D5: You let giants... Giants!... overrun my shrine. Bring me their leader's club as an offering, and I might release you from this curse!
Skyrim.esm|022437: Yes, you. You, who shares my iron tomb, who warms my ancient bones. I give you this task - journey to Volunruud. Speak with Amaund Motierre.
Skyrim.esm|022438: What? What treachery! Defiler! Debaser and defiler! You have violated the sanctity of the Night Mother's coffin! Explain yourself! Speak, worm!
Skyrim.esm|02243A: She... she said that? She said those words... to you? "Darkness rises when silence dies"? But those are the words. The Binding Words. Written in the Keeping Tomes. The signal so I would know. Mother's only way of talking to sweet Cicero... Then... it's true! She's back! Our lady is back! She has chosen a Listener! She has chosen you! Ha ha ha! All hail the Listener!
Skyrim.esm|02243C: Oh, but how can I defend you? How can I exert your will if you will not speak? To anyone!
Skyrim.esm|02243D: She... spoke to you? More treachery! More trickery and deceit! You lie! The Night Mother speaks only to the Listener! And there is... no... Listener!
Skyrim.esm|02252C: What do you want, milk drinker?
Skyrim.esm|02252D: The gods know what you've done.
Skyrim.esm|022530: A fine day to you, friend. May you die with a sword in your hands.
Skyrim.esm|022531: May the gods watch over your battles, friend.
Skyrim.esm|02253A: Oh, it's you. I was wondering why I was smelling something unpleasant.
Skyrim.esm|02253E: Absolutely no time to deal with lowlifes these days. Go away.
Skyrim.esm|022540: So nice to have pleasant company for a change.
Skyrim.esm|022546: There are so few pleasures in life as fine as your company.
Skyrim.esm|022549: Filth. Run to the horizon before I hunt you down.
Skyrim.esm|02254A: There are so many delightful ways I've pictured you dead.
Skyrim.esm|022554: We're one of the same kind, you and I. I'm glad to have met you.
Skyrim.esm|02255A: For your crimes, may Malacath boil your blood and grind your bones.
Skyrim.esm|02255F: What do you want? Aren't your hands coated with enough blood?
Skyrim.esm|0225B6: I ain't afraid of the old hag. Still, I wish Constance was in charge. She treats us real good. Constance, sometimes she gives me little snacks. Which is good, 'cause we only get but one meal. Late, too, in the afternoon. I'm a growing boy. I need as much food as I can get.
Skyrim.esm|0225B7: Miss Grelod is... well she's a terrible old crone. No person could be that cruel. I think she's part hagraven. She only lets us out in the yard once. In the morning. And she just stands there watching us. Like I said. Hagraven.
Skyrim.esm|0225B8: I hate her. Everyone hates her. She's the meanest person I ever met. Sometimes she even locks us in the room. Constance tries to stop her, and is real good to us, but it's never enough.
Skyrim.esm|0225BB: Sometimes, when she goes to her room to work or sleep, I slip out for a bit. A boy's gotta make a livin', right?
Skyrim.esm|0225BC: I will do nothing of the sort. You have no business here. I'm sorry, but you really must leave.
Skyrim.esm|0225CD: May your next fight bring you victory, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0225CE: May your weapons be sharp and your prey fresh.
Skyrim.esm|0225D2: Its scent is foul. Unclean. Hurtful.
Skyrim.esm|0225D6: Khajiit wishes for you to go away. You have done us too much harm.
Skyrim.esm|0225DD: These sands are cold, but Khajiit feels warmness from your presence.
Skyrim.esm|0225E0: Speak. Khajiit will listen until all the sugars in the world turn sour.
Skyrim.esm|0225E3: It fills us with rage. What harm does it bring to us now?
Skyrim.esm|0225E5: It annoys us. What does it want to take from us now?
Skyrim.esm|0225EB: Ours is to smile at your passing, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0225F0: It is our most favored company.
Skyrim.esm|02287B: Haven't I always been a good father? A good chief?
Skyrim.esm|02287C: Yes, wouldn't want to miss the lush summer we have ahead.
Skyrim.esm|02287D: There you go, Son. Be your own man!
Skyrim.esm|02287E: You just need to remember that there's a war on, and firewood isn't cheap these days.
Skyrim.esm|02287F: Do you ever get a weird feeling about those sisters?
Skyrim.esm|022883: That's just it... if she's not happy, there are plenty of other women who will be.
Skyrim.esm|022886: Come on, we just need a little elbow grease.
Skyrim.esm|022888: Why else have they cursed me? Killed all my wives?
Skyrim.esm|022889: Oh, good point. I wish my head were made of rocks.
Skyrim.esm|02288A: It's good to toughen her up. Not an easy world out here. Keep at it.
Skyrim.esm|02288B: But we choose our own path now.
Skyrim.esm|02288F: How will I know when I'm ready?
Skyrim.esm|022893: Oh, like the sleepy rebel supporters in this town are going to tell anyone.
Skyrim.esm|02289B: But I thought there was only one chief.
Skyrim.esm|0228A6: I've just never heard of a midwife losing all her mothers like that.
Skyrim.esm|0228B0: Mama, when can we go play in the river again?
Skyrim.esm|0228BF: What are you on about? Let's just get the treasure here and go.
Skyrim.esm|0228C0: Don't rush ahead. This is a trap if I ever saw one.
Skyrim.esm|0228C1: This is it! Gathrik awaits us!
Skyrim.esm|0228C7: Well, we have to cut down something!
Skyrim.esm|0228C8: Oh, if you're worried about me trying to meddle with you...
Skyrim.esm|0228C9: What about my father? Was he a good hunter?
Skyrim.esm|0228D1: So, Roggi, when are you going to get yourself a woman?
Skyrim.esm|022910: Did I tell you I got a letter from General Tullius? They've conscripted some more archers and will need...
Skyrim.esm|022911: They squander their father's love. We can't dally this time.
Skyrim.esm|022914: Need to get going. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|022919: I'm proud of you for helping provide for us.
Skyrim.esm|02291A: No matter. You'll take care of things like you always have.
Skyrim.esm|02291D: I don't think we're going to get those potatoes to come in.
Skyrim.esm|02291F: Let me know as soon as you've found anything.
Skyrim.esm|022920: I want to make money just as much as you do, but we don't want to make the Nords angry.
Skyrim.esm|022925: There are places in the world that aren't wrapped in ice, you know.
Skyrim.esm|022929: Keep your eyes and ears open.
Skyrim.esm|02292A: She said you threw her into a goat pen yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|02295F: If he does show up with a bride, we'll have to move quickly.
Skyrim.esm|022963: I'll provide you wood and timber to make whatever riggings you'll need.
Skyrim.esm|022968: I'm not sure that's a good joke to tell with women present...
Skyrim.esm|022969: Be careful. The spirits can speak to the living if there's a strong connection.
Skyrim.esm|02296A: If you like mead so much, why not go live in Riften?
Skyrim.esm|02296B: Well that's just it. Some of them might not be sacred.
Skyrim.esm|02297A: Make sure the back fence is repaired. I swear I saw some skeevers in nibbling the leaves yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|02297B: But papa was going to take me hunting tomorrow!
Skyrim.esm|02297C: Bolar, I must ask you something.
Skyrim.esm|02299E: Nothing! I... it's ill fortune. That's all.
Skyrim.esm|022E79: What? How? I thought they weren't allowed within the city!
Skyrim.esm|022E7C: At last. I can rest safely. You have done me a great service, warrior. I cannot thank you enough. Take this reward. I managed to sneak some of my wealth out of Hammerfell when I left. It's the least I can do. For now, I will maintain my ruse here. You are always welcome in the Bannered Mare.
Skyrim.esm|022E7E: Assassins? No, nothing so crass. "Saadia", as you know her, is wanted by the noble houses of Taneth for treason. We were hired to see her returned to Hammerfell for her crimes. You can help us with that, and make sure no one else gets hurt.
Skyrim.esm|022E7F: She trusts you, at least to some extent. She sent you after us, and has no reason to think that you'd do anything other than that. Convince her that we'll be coming for her, and she needs to leave. Lead her to the stables outside Whiterun. We'll be waiting to take her into custody. I'll gladly share a portion of the bounty in return for your efforts in seeing proper justice done.
Skyrim.esm|022E83: I'd hoped we could work something out, that you would see reason. That we could avoid having to kill you. Very well, then. If that's the way you want to play it, we will.
Skyrim.esm|022E84: Good. We'll be waiting for you at the stables. It will be good to finally be able to return home.
Skyrim.esm|022E87: Good. Wipe them out. So long as one of them is left alive, they will come for me!
Skyrim.esm|022EE3: Molag Bal. You think you can best Boethiah's faithful? I have won this contest before!
Skyrim.esm|022EE8: Ah. But I have my own champion this time, Logrolf.
Skyrim.esm|022EEB: No more.... No more.... I submit, Molag Bal. I submit!
Skyrim.esm|022EED: Mortal. I give you my mace, in all its rusted spitefulness. Crush the spirit from Logrolf's bones. Make him bend to me.
Skyrim.esm|022F30: Very little. You have my word on that. The Night Mother represents a chapter in the Dark Brotherhood's history that has long since been closed. Today we live by our own rules. We're the last Sanctuary in all of Tamriel, and only by forgoing the old ways have we survived for so long. My only worry is her "Keeper." I'm not sure what Cicero expects to gain by bringing the Night Mother here, but he'll soon learn this is my Sanctuary.
Skyrim.esm|022F31: Used to be the Dark Brotherhood was bound by Five Tenets, but we stopped following those years ago. All those rules, all that discipline, and look where the Dark Brotherhood ended up. We're the last of our kind, and we live the way we see fit. Bottom line - respect your Family. Do that, and everything else will fall into place.
Skyrim.esm|022F32: The Unholy Matron, the Shrouded Lady, the Mistress of the Void. She goes by many names. Ages past, Sithis gave a woman five children. She killed them to win his favor, thus becoming the Night Mother. If you believe that sort of thing. Today, she's... well, she's a skeleton. An ancient corpse. But, more importantly, a corpse that's being brought to this Sanctuary by her Keeper.
Skyrim.esm|022F33: They used to come from the Night Mother. Potential clients would perform the Black Sacrament, and she would hear their prayers. The Night Mother would communicate this to the Listener, who would then dispatch a Speaker to arrange the contract with the client. But that was a long time ago. There hasn't been a Listener in years, not since Cyrodiil was overrun in the war with the Thalmor. But people don't know that. So they still perform the ritual... and we eventually hear about it. When someone wants us, we find out.
Skyrim.esm|022F34: Of course. And you've repaid your debt, in full.  Here's the key to the shack. But why stop here? I say we take our relationship to the next level. I would like to officially extend to you an invitation to join my Family. The Dark Brotherhood. In the southwest reaches of Skyrim, in the Pine Forest, you'll find the entrance to our Sanctuary. It's just beneath the road, hidden from view. When questioned by the Black Door, answer with the correct passphrase: "Silence, my brother." Then you're in. And your new life begins. I'll see you at home.
Skyrim.esm|022F35: Yes. A few months ago I received word from the Night Mother's Keeper that he had arrived in Skyrim from Cyrodiil. The Night Mother's crypt, in Bravil, was destroyed. A result of the chaos caused by the war with the Thalmor. That forced a relocation. I don't know where the Keeper has been these past few months, but I recently received another letter. He's bringing the Night Mother here. Soon.
Skyrim.esm|022F40: The Sanctuary is in the southern Pine Forest. Close to the road, but away from prying eyes.
Skyrim.esm|022F41: Have you talked to Nazir? He's sure to have some additional contracts for you.
Skyrim.esm|022F42: That twisted jester will pay for what's he's done...
Skyrim.esm|022F92: No! Roggi and Kjeld need some more support timbers for the mine.
Skyrim.esm|022F93: Papa, mama's making me do kitchen work again.
Skyrim.esm|022F94: What about Ulfric? Did you ever hear back from him?
Skyrim.esm|022F97: How have things been with you?
Skyrim.esm|022F98: Oh dear, I hadn't thought of that.
Skyrim.esm|022F99: I don't know what your aunts told you, but...
Skyrim.esm|022F9B: No, I don't need to mark it. I remember it.
Skyrim.esm|022F9C: And what about the dogs? How are those precious little angels?
Skyrim.esm|022F9D: So have you ever actually spoken to Captain Lonely-Gale?
Skyrim.esm|022F9F: Bolar said you had been looking for a new wife?
Skyrim.esm|022FA1: Yatul and I do all the hunting. Mul and Gadba do all the mining. It seems like all you do is find more wives to get killed.
Skyrim.esm|022FA3: And then I told her about the vampires and how much they would pay me for her.
Skyrim.esm|022FA4: When do I get to go hunting?
Skyrim.esm|022FA6: Let's just not take the chance. We can talk about this later.
Skyrim.esm|022FA7: Now, I've been reading about the best ways to grow corn in permafrost...
Skyrim.esm|022FCF: Perhaps it's actually a sign of their love for you. They don't want to see you with unworthy mates.
Skyrim.esm|022FD1: Come, child, I will tell you how to track a snow cat.
Skyrim.esm|022FD4: They are, but your mind has to be sharp, too.
Skyrim.esm|022FD5: Father, I'm worried about Gadba and Mul.
Skyrim.esm|022FD6: Bolar and I will tell you.
Skyrim.esm|022FD9: And a whole lot of good that's gotten us.
Skyrim.esm|022FDB: Well, right. One of us will be a chief.
Skyrim.esm|022FDC: I've just learned not to trust luck around here.
Skyrim.esm|022FDD: It's not you I'm worried about.
Skyrim.esm|022FE1: Not with all those soldiers all over the hold. Their horses can't see you, you know.
Skyrim.esm|023020: Maybe we should just admit that this is a horrible place for a mill.
Skyrim.esm|023021: Oh, you've seen your father just trying to chase a skeever from his house. He's a bit soft.
Skyrim.esm|023022: Oh, I'm not sure I could ever settle down like you have, friend.
Skyrim.esm|023025: Have you scried anything new in the entrails?
Skyrim.esm|023026: They are indeed. Nothing like your mother, though!
Skyrim.esm|02309A: Oh, no skeevers out here, sir. They can't stand the cold.
Skyrim.esm|02309C: Please, let's not have it come to that.
Skyrim.esm|02309F: I was wondering if you had ordered the shipment of that frostwheat, sir.
Skyrim.esm|0230CD: Well why don't you be quick about finding my book that you lost?
Skyrim.esm|0230D0: Bah. He never had a connection with any of them beyond what his loins told him.
Skyrim.esm|0230D5: Well someone has to provide around here.
Skyrim.esm|0230D8: I'm not building a ship, sir. I'm trying to make things grow.
Skyrim.esm|0230E1: Once she's with child, I will not harm her.
Skyrim.esm|0230F1: But don't you get it? She thought he was a troll!
Skyrim.esm|0230F4: Sir, I'm going to need some better equipment if you want me plowing the frost.
Skyrim.esm|0230F5: Oh, I wouldn't be able to help them. Their skills were given by the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0230F6: There has to be more to it than that, though. I wish I knew where the bees got it from.
Skyrim.esm|0230FB: I'll let you explain that one to the Nords.
Skyrim.esm|0230FC: All the apiaries... it will take years to get those bees happy again.
Skyrim.esm|023100: Oh, you know. Little Kjeld is a handful, and Froa is just so quiet.
Skyrim.esm|023103: I suppose Kjeld asked for the usual discount?
Skyrim.esm|023107: Oh, I don't think you're old enough for that yet. You can help me salt the cheese curd.
Skyrim.esm|023108: How much do you know about the brewing of mead?
Skyrim.esm|023109: No, but his housecarl sent us a letter.
Skyrim.esm|02310E: Come back to me when you have something useful.
Skyrim.esm|02310F: Well, your ill fortune means I have no mother.
Skyrim.esm|023111: And what did you say to that?
Skyrim.esm|023113: Healthy and strong. Ysgramor's coat's gotten so thick, he looks fat!
Skyrim.esm|023116: By the gods, he's just a retired ship's captain.
Skyrim.esm|023117: They're not my aunts. They're your aunts. And they told me enough.
Skyrim.esm|023118: You see? That's why I didn't tell you!
Skyrim.esm|02311B: I'll bet this is better than trying to grow something in Morrowind, though, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|02311C: Are the gods angry with me?
Skyrim.esm|023133: I got another harangue from Torsten yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|023147: Then they should send one they consider worthy.
Skyrim.esm|02314A: Well of course, not with an attitude like that!
Skyrim.esm|02318C: That is rich. What did she do?
Skyrim.esm|02319F: It's filthy in there! Dirt everywhere, and it looks like the dogs have the run of the place.
Skyrim.esm|0231A0: I keep telling you, without a warming enchantment, it will never grow past your ankles.
Skyrim.esm|0231B4: Since Galka was taken from us. She's been... sad, I guess.
Skyrim.esm|0231C7: That last brigade of soldiers said there's a tall forest on the eastern edge of the White River.
Skyrim.esm|0231C8: We don't just take in any milk drinker who stumbles in. Talk to Kodlak if you think you have what it takes to be a Companion.
Skyrim.esm|0231CA: But you said that was impossible.
Skyrim.esm|0231CB: Mauhulakh's aunts. You know... the way they're all around him all the time.
Skyrim.esm|0231CC: When you're able to draw a bow all the way to your shoulder.
Skyrim.esm|0231D3: What's the most dangerous beast you've ever killed?
Skyrim.esm|0231D4: Sometimes I think your head is made of rocks.
Skyrim.esm|0231D7: Sometimes I'm surprised he's still the chief.
Skyrim.esm|0231DA: Someday, you can have your own stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|0231EE: How much longer do we have to work this backwoods mine?
Skyrim.esm|0231F4: I'll never understand why you're so bitter about everything.
Skyrim.esm|0231F5: But your aunts don't even seem to care.
Skyrim.esm|0231FF: How is your hunting with Yatul?
Skyrim.esm|023202: Yes, but that fool Endario can't get it in. I'll thrash him when I get back to the city, don't you worry.
Skyrim.esm|023203: That's what everyone keeps saying. But you didn't kill her. That's what I was asking.
Skyrim.esm|023205: You need to take better care of your sister.
Skyrim.esm|02320A: Very little. It's an uncertain time.
Skyrim.esm|02320C: Maybe, but the way you burn through all that wood to keep the cabin toasty, they might mistake this place for Elsweyr.
Skyrim.esm|023211: I hear Mauhulakh is wandering again. He's after a new woman.
Skyrim.esm|023214: We've got a new order in.
Skyrim.esm|023216: Maybe you could be a taster or something.
Skyrim.esm|023217: True... we could simply make sure she never reaches Narzulbur.
Skyrim.esm|023218: What is that supposed to mean?
Skyrim.esm|023219: Do the bees need to be happy?
Skyrim.esm|02321A: I get it. It's funny. It just might upset your wife.
Skyrim.esm|02321B: Yes, you've made that quite clear in the past. And now we have Urog and Dushnamub because of your weakness.
Skyrim.esm|02321D: He takes after his father, and she takes after you.
Skyrim.esm|023223: They have to be reasonable about it at some point.
Skyrim.esm|02323D: Don't make me bring your father into this.
Skyrim.esm|02323E: Have the spirits told you where I may find a wife? One who will be safe from this curse?
Skyrim.esm|02324D: Firstly, I've never even seen Morrowind. Secondly, in volcanic soil the plants practically grow themselves.
Skyrim.esm|02324F: I found a big load of ebony at the bottom pocket in the mine.
Skyrim.esm|023251: I know all there is to know about the drinking of it, and that suits me just fine.
Skyrim.esm|023252: Sssssh! That's not something we want everyone to be hearing.
Skyrim.esm|023253: Just... try and keep out of our way.
Skyrim.esm|023254: Why didn't you tell me? What did he say?
Skyrim.esm|023255: He's been spending a lot of time at the graves again.
Skyrim.esm|023257: Well, sir, it's a farmhouse. It's not going to be as clean as your house in the city.
Skyrim.esm|023258: Yes, but that's too close to Mixwater Mill. We don't want to get Gilfre angry at us by stealing her trees.
Skyrim.esm|023259: Maybe to you, but he's just so... his story is...
Skyrim.esm|02325A: Right, but if you plow the soil with fire salts...
Skyrim.esm|02327C: She can't tell me what to do!
Skyrim.esm|02327E: There is some good news though. I think the borders of the shrine may be smaller than we think.
Skyrim.esm|02327F: Don't worry about him. I know you work hard, and I'm the one who gets you your money.
Skyrim.esm|023280: She's just worried about you. We all are. We just want you to be happy. We want to make you happy.
Skyrim.esm|023281: Why do you ask me that?
Skyrim.esm|023283: You can tell it because it has lots of ebony.
Skyrim.esm|023285: That's just how it is with families. When we're chiefs, you'll see how it is.
Skyrim.esm|023286: I don't think you're quite ready yet.
Skyrim.esm|023287: The ground is practically frozen. We can't dig down far enough.
Skyrim.esm|023288: I can do that! I did it yesterday!
Skyrim.esm|023289: I think this is yet another conversation that wouldn't make your wife happy.
Skyrim.esm|02328B: No, just very difficult. You have to look for shift in various shades of white.
Skyrim.esm|02328D: I wasn't wanting to get anyone in trouble, sir. Just wanting to get the new planting started, is all.
Skyrim.esm|02328E: Sorry, sir. These old bones do like a bit of warmth in the evening. There's no need to make fun.
Skyrim.esm|02328F: That would be good! I could use it to smash other rocks. Easier to mine, then.
Skyrim.esm|023292: We all are, my dear, but sometimes the Divines reward those who don't seem to deserve it. Call it dumb luck.
Skyrim.esm|023293: I'm bitter because we had lives in Cyrodiil. And we came out here to be paupers.
Skyrim.esm|023295: A bit longer, yet. We need more money if we want to be chiefs of our own.
Skyrim.esm|023296: I think it may have to be far away from here.
Skyrim.esm|023297: They are good women. They love me. You will speak no more ill of them.
Skyrim.esm|023298: And you'll be trampled to death!
Skyrim.esm|023299: You don't worry about two Orc men coming into your stronghold? That they might challenge you?
Skyrim.esm|02330A: We are strong. We get the job done.
Skyrim.esm|02333C: He's lonely. What do you expect?
Skyrim.esm|0233CA: All right, please stay close to me while we're inside. It should be safe, but it's always better to be cautious.
Skyrim.esm|0235BC: Feel free to grab a pick and help them out. I prefer not to sully myself with manual labor.
Skyrim.esm|0235BD: They seem less intelligent each time you raise them, if that's even possible.
Skyrim.esm|0235BE: You hear that? The others must have found something!
Skyrim.esm|0235BF: These thralls of yours are slower than Argonians in a blizzard.
Skyrim.esm|0235C0: Bah. Weak-willed rabble. Even dead they're almost useless.
Skyrim.esm|0235C1: As long as they can swing a pickaxe where I tell them, they're as smart as we need them to be.
Skyrim.esm|0235C3: We'd better go see. These can tend to themselves for a few minutes.
Skyrim.esm|023608: Let's listen to Enthir's translation before we decide what we do.
Skyrim.esm|02369B: Oh, my, I hadn't thought of that.
Skyrim.esm|02369C: Oh, all things in time, my boy. All things in time. What more do you need that Yatul and I cannot provide?
Skyrim.esm|0236AC: Have you heard about Goldenglow Meadery?
Skyrim.esm|0236AD: Ah, but I can do that right here. Right now!
Skyrim.esm|0236BE: Eh, let her get upset. She'll be angry no matter what I say, might as well enjoy myself.
Skyrim.esm|023718: Well we have to harvest somewhere! This is getting absurd.
Skyrim.esm|02371D: Wonderful! My lady Mara will be quite pleased! Nightcaller Temple is only a short walk from Dawnstar. Come, we must hurry.
Skyrim.esm|02371E: Amusing. Now if you'll excuse me, I need to continue my research. I have no time for idle chatter with any simpleton who strikes up a conversation.
Skyrim.esm|023720: Well, that's the good and the bad of it.
Skyrim.esm|023727: Your concerns are understandable; trust is a very difficult commodity to come by these days. I can only give you my word as a priest of Mara that my intentions are honorable.
Skyrim.esm|023728: These dreams are manifestations created by the Daedric Lord Vaermina. She has an awful hunger for our memories. In return, she leaves behind nightmares not unlike a cough marks a serious illness. I must end her terrible influence over these people before the damage becomes permanent.
Skyrim.esm|023729: I still have my key to the library. Whenever you're ready, let's move on.
Skyrim.esm|02372B: Apologies accepted. I understand how this entire story can be mistaken for fiction since the circumstances are so abstract. However, I can assure you that I will do everything in my power to keep you safe if you're willing to help me.
Skyrim.esm|02372C: Lore holds that the Skull of Corruption holds a constant hunger for the memories of others. The Skull has been out of touch for so long, I fear it's gained the ability to reach out on its own and try to feed.
