What it does with these memories is just conjecture and an argument for scholars and historians to this very day.
Skyrim.esm|02372D: I need to return to the source of the problem, to Nightcaller Temple. Perhaps you'd be willing to assist me in that regard?
Skyrim.esm|023730: Wish to converse with the crazy old elf, eh? Very well. Go on.
Skyrim.esm|023732: Let me take a look... Mara be praised! There is a way past the barrier to the inner sanctum. It involves a recipe for a liquid known as Vaermina's Torpor.
Skyrim.esm|023735: The entire town is being plagued by horrible nightmares. They're in serious danger but I'm afraid there's little I can do about it.
Skyrim.esm|023738: Impossible actually. Hmm, I wonder... There may be a way to bypass the barrier, but I must check their library and confirm it can be done.
Skyrim.esm|02380A: I've been trying to figure out what makes Riften mead better than the stuff from Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|023843: Gemma. This town is called "Kynesgrove." That stand of trees is Kyne's Grove. You don't honestly think you can just cut some down.
Skyrim.esm|023844: Well, some tools that aren't rusted through would be a start.
Skyrim.esm|023848: Of course! You can't make good mead without happy honey!
Skyrim.esm|02384C: We're the ones he loves. Me, and you.
Skyrim.esm|023859: I just know that our sailors are able to keep their ship quarters clean, and I expect the same of you.
Skyrim.esm|02385A: I'm going to need your help in the kitchen tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|02385B: I'm disappointed in your failure. But the Empire still needs you. I'll have to over look it for now.
Skyrim.esm|02385C: Turns out you're a screw up like the rest. But I still need you. So I'll overlook it for now.
Skyrim.esm|02386F: Good. I can use someone I can trust.
Skyrim.esm|023870: We're taking the city. Meet the soldiers gathering for the attack. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|023871: Got my hands full as usual.
Skyrim.esm|023880: With the Emperor's arrival in Skyrim now a certainty, his security service, the Penitus Oculatus, will need to begin its preparations immediately. Security is being handled by a Commander Maro. Astrid and I have devised a plan to break the man, and in doing so, cripple the Emperor's protection. You are to slay the commander's son, Gaius Maro, and once he is dead, plant false evidence on his body implicating him in a plot to kill the Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|02392E: Wait, so you think we'll be able to harvest some of the sacred trees up there?
Skyrim.esm|02392F: To earn your bonus, do not kill Gaius Maro in Dragon Bridge, or on the road. Kill him in one of the other major cities he'll be visiting. There, the body will be discovered quickly, as will the letter implicating Gaius Maro in the plot to assassinate the Emperor. Do that, and Astrid has authorized me to grant you a rather unique bonus. It is a special token, to be given to Olava the Feeble, in Whiterun. Olava is an old and dear friend, and a powerful seer. The token entitles you to a reading of your future. It's an opportunity one should not pass up.
Skyrim.esm|023931: Thank you, miss, I appreciate that.
Skyrim.esm|023937: Good. The more time he pines for the dead wives, the less time he spends finding a new one.
Skyrim.esm|023939: He... thanked us for contacting him, but said they didn't need any more lumber at the moment.
Skyrim.esm|02393A: Tulvur! I've just completed inspection of the farmhouse.
Skyrim.esm|02393B: He's just a guard captain. And not a very good one, at that.
Skyrim.esm|023961: Then you've salted it and nothing will grow at all. Genius.
Skyrim.esm|023962: No, it's fine. I don't mind being poor.
Skyrim.esm|023963: Bolar has been in a bad mood for weeks, now.
Skyrim.esm|023964: About how the Divines cursed you. What horrible thing did you do to deserve it?
Skyrim.esm|023965: A stranger comes to our hall.
Skyrim.esm|023975: Greetings outsider. If you have some business here, speak it.
Skyrim.esm|023978: When you get to be my age, you'll come to miss the smell of blood. Strange, I know.
Skyrim.esm|02397E: Some nights I dream about the mists of Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|02398E: Let's not waste too much time talking when there is glory to be had.
Skyrim.esm|023994: Haven't seen your face before. I'm watching you.
Skyrim.esm|023995: This is a place for warriors, new blood. What brings you here?
Skyrim.esm|023996: You still need to prove yourself, whelp.
Skyrim.esm|023997: Are you looking for help with something? This is a busy time.
Skyrim.esm|0239A2: I can't keep helping you with everything.
Skyrim.esm|0239A7: If you wish to hunt with me, your feet need to be quick, and your eyes quicker.
Skyrim.esm|0239A8: Ysgramor himself wouldn't have the patience to deal with all the rabble around here.
Skyrim.esm|0239A9: I've heard you may actually be stronger than you look. Perhaps we can hunt together someday.
Skyrim.esm|0239AA: Don't let them intimidate you, sister. We both know how to keep our heads while the men let their hearts rule.
Skyrim.esm|0239AB: It's good to have another quick-eyed Shield-Sister in the hall. Come to me with any troubles.
Skyrim.esm|023A03: You are new here. I don't trust the new.
Skyrim.esm|023A04: My brother Vilkas is a better talker than me. He should be around someplace.
Skyrim.esm|023A13: Skjor says that I have the strength of Ysgramor, and my brother has his smarts.
Skyrim.esm|023A14: Some people don't think I'm smart. Those people get my fist. But you, I like.
Skyrim.esm|023A2F: Thinking I need to train some more.
Skyrim.esm|023A3A: Getting tired of hanging around talking all day.
Skyrim.esm|023A42: No time to talk. Out of my way.
Skyrim.esm|023A46: Did you just wander in out of the white?
Skyrim.esm|023A47: Great, another ambitious visitor. You think you can just wander in here and join us?
Skyrim.esm|023A6E: Come to me with questions. I know our history almost as well as Vignar by now. Except I can remember it.
Skyrim.esm|023A7A: What brings you to me? Find some strange creature in your travels?
Skyrim.esm|023A7B: I wouldn't have expected someone like you to be the Harbinger, but Kodlak trusted your judgment. And so will I.
Skyrim.esm|023A7C: I suppose you have another question?
Skyrim.esm|023B6A: That's quite an accusation. I assume you have proof?
Skyrim.esm|023BC5: You're a talkative one, aren't you.
Skyrim.esm|023BFC: So you don't know anything about this house?
Skyrim.esm|023C0A: Anyone seen entering or leaving? Any strange lights or unusual noises?
Skyrim.esm|023C13: I'm not going to warn you again.
Skyrim.esm|023C14: Once more and I'm going to have to kill you.
Skyrim.esm|023C15: Don't do that again. Last warning.
Skyrim.esm|023D9C: Ah, fresh blood. Hope you know how to handle yourself.
Skyrim.esm|023E3B: I always thought the new Harbinger would be taller, myself.
Skyrim.esm|023E3C: I avoid going to Eastmarch if I can. Something in the air... I think it's just too close to the border.
Skyrim.esm|023E3D: Are you thinking you're better than me? I crawled west out of the wasteland, so show some respect.
Skyrim.esm|023E3E: We are all Ashlanders now, brother. Good to find another family that cares where they came from, eh?
Skyrim.esm|023E3F: To what do I owe the honor?
Skyrim.esm|023E40: Look, I've got training to get to.
Skyrim.esm|023E41: You don't belong here. What do you think you have to offer?
Skyrim.esm|023E42: I'm still trying to figure out why Skjor let you in in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|023E43: If the Circle vouches for you, I'm sworn to respect their judgment. But that doesn't mean I like it.
Skyrim.esm|023E46: Here to be a Companion? Well, they just let me in, so there's probably no more room.
Skyrim.esm|023E47: I was the newest Companion, until you came along. I guess that's ok. Just means I can show you the ropes!
Skyrim.esm|023E48: Oh, hey there. I killed a bear yesterday. Did you kill anything?
Skyrim.esm|023E4B: I haven't seen you before. Have I? Have we met? I'm sorry if I've forgotten.
Skyrim.esm|023E4C: The latest recruit! Right? Wait, is that right?
Skyrim.esm|023E4D: Might head down to the meadery later. See what they're brewing up. You can smell the honey on the wind.
Skyrim.esm|023E4E: What do you want from me, now?
Skyrim.esm|023EE1: A letter... not sure who from. He wouldn't say. Just that he was a friend of yours.
Skyrim.esm|0240AE: The one who calls himself Ancano. He seeks information about the Eye, but what he will find shall be quite different. His path will cross yours in time, but first you must find that which you need.
Skyrim.esm|0240B0: No, though you may be the last. The one who calls himself Ancano has sought my knowledge as well, through very different questions. Your path differs from most. You are being guided, pushed towards something. It is a good path, one untraveled by many. It is a path that can save your College. I will tell you what you need to know to follow it further.
Skyrim.esm|0240B1: You, and those aiding you, wish to know more about the Eye of Magnus. You wish to avoid the disaster of which you are not yet aware. To see through Magnus' Eye without being blinded, you require his staff. Events now spiral quickly towards the inevitable center, so you must act with haste. Take this knowledge to your Arch-Mage.
Skyrim.esm|0240CD: If the Forsworn aren't attacking my town, the Nords are trying to force me off it.
Skyrim.esm|024124: Are you... threatening me? Walk away fool. It's not worth it.
Skyrim.esm|024125: Ah, so the Brotherhood has come for old Hern, has it? Well then, let us meet as equals.
Skyrim.esm|024126: The wood elf may run a stall in the city, but he retreats to the plains outside Whiterun to hunt. Kill him there, and no one will bear witness...
Skyrim.esm|024127: She separated from her husband several months ago. Now the two run competing mining operations. Beitild's angry and desperate. A fighter.
Skyrim.esm|024128: I've a mine to run, so if you'll excuse me...
Skyrim.esm|024129: Well then, you won't be leaving here alive!
Skyrim.esm|02412A: He's an Argonian layabout, likes to scavenge shipwrecks. My guess is he'll run if threatened. Or swim, if given the chance.
Skyrim.esm|02412B: Ah, yes. The pirate captain. A ruthless she-devil, by all accounts. Deadly with a blade. And let's not forget her crew - loyal and bloodthirsty, the lot of them. This is my final contract, and certainly the most challenging. Be careful with this one, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|02412C: He's the housecarl of Falkreath. You know what that means? He's the bodyguard to the Jarl himself. So be careful.
Skyrim.esm|02412D: What? Well, we'll see about that now, won't we?
Skyrim.esm|02412E: He's a hapless beggar living in some ruins just outside the village of Ivarstead. Easy even for you.
Skyrim.esm|02412F: Hmm... All right... How about this? Shadows creep, and... and phantoms leap! A man got... he got scared. And the demons dared! To um... visit upon him all which they feared! Brilliant, I know. It's a gift.
Skyrim.esm|024130: And let me guess, that someone is my so-called husband Leigelf? Well the feeling's mutual.
Skyrim.esm|024131: I swear, you people are an odd bunch. Please, just step aside, that I may return to my duties!
Skyrim.esm|024132: Who are you? You... you're with the Dark Brotherhood, ain'tcha? I knew it. I knew you'd come for me!
Skyrim.esm|024134: He's dangerous. Very dangerous. A rogue Dark Elf mage doing research into Dwarven artifacts should be both respected... and feared. Just not by you.
Skyrim.esm|024135: Sleep? I can't sleep now! There's too much to be done. And what's this Void you're rambling on about?
Skyrim.esm|024136: Ha! Oh, that's a good one, friend. Killing Lurbuk... I mean, can you even imagine something so ludicrous? Depriving the world of my unique talents?
Skyrim.esm|024137: But I have many more rooms to clean. Fort Greymoor is quite large, and rather filthy if I do say.
Skyrim.esm|024138: What? What do you mean? Oh! Oh... no... No!
Skyrim.esm|024139: Do... do you want something? Look, if you ain't gonna talk, or spare no coin, just go away...
Skyrim.esm|02413A: No! Oh, by the gods, please no!
Skyrim.esm|02413B: Well now, you are a strange one. You remind me a bit of myself, when I'm stalking a deer. I'm not quite sure how I feel about that...
Skyrim.esm|02413C: He's a vampire, and has blended into human society for years. He's never far from his female companion, Hert. Also a vampire. The contract is for the male, Hern. But you'll probably have to contend with the female, too. So for Sithis' sake, watch yourself.
Skyrim.esm|02413D: Oh... Oh no... Please, please... I ain't never harmed no one... Old Narfi just wants to be left alone...
Skyrim.esm|02413E: Hmph. Don't say much, but there's a strange aura about you. You walk in the shadow of death... Leave here. Leave here and never return.
Skyrim.esm|02413F: He lives just outside Anga's Mill, in the woods. He was once the miller. Now Papius is just a paranoid recluse who thinks someone is out to get him. And he's right.
Skyrim.esm|024140: She's a maid. Old, and feeble. But with this contract it's much more a matter of "where" than "who." Your political affairs are your own, but any allegiance you may have in the ongoing war could very well affect your access to Fort Greymoor.
Skyrim.esm|024141: He is, by all accounts, the worst bard in all of Skyrim. Apparently so many people sought his death, Astrid had to hold a lottery to determine the client.
Skyrim.esm|024142: Is that so? We shall see, villain. We shall see!
Skyrim.esm|024143: What's the matter, friend? Khajiit got your tongue? Maybe you need a little ditty to loosen your gob, hmm? There once was a stranger, with eyes full of danger, he spoke not a word, but his meaning was heard... Sing on, sweet Lurbuk, sing on!
Skyrim.esm|024144: He's an accomplished wizard. And, locating him may be challenging, as he travels with the caravans.
Skyrim.esm|0241FA: Not all of us, just the members of the Circle. We shouldn't be talking about this anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0241FB: It was the blade hefted by Ysgramor when he returned to drive the elves out of the old Skyrim. Without that axe, this whole land would be overrun still.
Skyrim.esm|0241FD: A group of werewolf hunters who have gotten wise to our little secret. They've made it their mission to hunt us down, but we are hunters ourselves.
Skyrim.esm|02420C: Oh, isn't that a shame. And here I am in the Bloodworks. Looks like you aren't such a sharp investigator after all.
Skyrim.esm|02420D: I'm glad you were able to correct your mistake. I'll release Wuunferth immediately.
Skyrim.esm|02420E: Indeed. I... I thought you captured the Butcher. You said the city was safe, now. How could this have happened?
Skyrim.esm|02420F: Obviously. You should visit the Bloodworks to talk to Wuunferth -- we need to figure out where the evidence went wrong.
Skyrim.esm|024237: I'm one of the few native sons of the Reach that owns land. It doesn't make me very popular.
Skyrim.esm|02423B: Yes, my son, what is it I can help with?
Skyrim.esm|02423C: Yes, my daughter, what is it I can help with?
Skyrim.esm|02423F: Ainethach is dead. What will happen to Karthwasten now?
Skyrim.esm|024246: Not many Nords would work for a Reachman, but Ainethach is a good man.
Skyrim.esm|024247: Damn it. The priests must have activated this barrier when the Miasma was released.
Skyrim.esm|02424C: Go now! The orcs could breach the inner sanctum at any moment!
Skyrim.esm|02424E: The good mine is closed and the other mine has no ore. Gods have cursed me.
Skyrim.esm|024254: Brother Thorek, we must remain here and guard this Skull with our lives if necessary.
Skyrim.esm|024255: And what about you, Brother Casimir? Are you prepared to serve the will of Vaermina?
Skyrim.esm|024256: Then it's decided. Brother Casimir, you must activate the barrier and release the Miasma. Let nothing stop you.
Skyrim.esm|024258: You're not from here, and we don't need to be talking.
Skyrim.esm|02425A: Then let it be done. Farewell, my brothers!
Skyrim.esm|02425D: Ainethach's in charge of Karthwasten. You should bother him.
Skyrim.esm|024262: We have no alternative. It's the will of Vaermina.
Skyrim.esm|024264: Then we have no choice. The Miasma must be released.
Skyrim.esm|024265: These Reach folk are so... nosy.
Skyrim.esm|024266: But... no more than a handful of us remain, brother.
Skyrim.esm|024267: Ragnar has a strong arm. Pity he isn't an Orc.
Skyrim.esm|024269: We must hold. We can't allow the Skull to fall into their hands.
Skyrim.esm|02426A: The orcs have breached the inner sanctum, Brother Veren.
Skyrim.esm|02426B: I will watch over you as you slumber to ensure your safety. If I deduce anything is amiss, I will use my arts to bring you back. Otherwise, I am uncertain what will end your Dreamstride. Perhaps when Vaermina's curious appetite has been filled.
Skyrim.esm|02426E: Vaermina resides in a strange realm known as Quagmire... a nightmarish land where reality shifts upon itself in seemingly impossible ways. From her citadel at the center, she reaches forth to collect our memories, leaving nothing in return apart from visions of horror and despair.
Skyrim.esm|024271: Thank Mara. I was afraid I'd never be able to convince you. Allow me to gather the materials I need for my ritual, and then I will meet you outside the ruins.
Skyrim.esm|024274: I believe there is a laboratory in the east wing. If we proceed there, we should be able to locate a sample.
Skyrim.esm|024275: You'll be viewing the memory of another through your own eyes and with your own body. Those around you will perceive you as normal and you will find the words you utter may not be your own. Thanks to all of these odd principles, there is quite a lot of debate as to whether this is really a dream or just the machinations of Vaermina.
Skyrim.esm|024359: The Great War... only thirty years past and already ancient history to most people. Seems to me like it just ended... it's never really ended for me. But to answer your question, the White-Gold Concordat was the fancy name they put on the peace treaty between the Empire and the Thalmor. It ended the war and saved the Empire to fight another day. Also trampled on the sacred name of Talos, and gave the Thalmor free reign to stamp out Talos worship throughout the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|02435B: Dragons are coming back, and you're a born dragonslayer. That's all you need to know right now.
Skyrim.esm|024399: Soul gems are the subject, friend. They're used in enchanting, then they break. Except one.
Skyrim.esm|0243BC: A few coins for my soul? If only you understood the irony.
Skyrim.esm|0243BD: I don't think you understand. I'm not answering questions.
Skyrim.esm|024463: Perhaps that is what you are meant to believe. To better serve your role as Azura's champion. Do not fight it. Azura's prophecies always come to pass. To deny them is to go headlong into the darkness with no light to guide you.
Skyrim.esm|02450D: Azura's Star. A Daedric artifact that allows any number of souls to pass through it. Some of us wanted to find out how. I was working under Malyn Varen, then. If only we knew what he was really planning.
Skyrim.esm|02450E: Azura? Gods, it's all finally coming back to haunt me.
Skyrim.esm|02451E: Yes. There were others at first, but Azura's visions tested everyone's faith. One by one, they left. Afraid to know their own future. But I refuse to abandon the shrine. The visions are a gift. Azura warns me of tragedy, war, death before it happens. I won't leave her guidance.
Skyrim.esm|024520: That's what caused all the trouble. Soul gems are used in enchanting, but they break afterwards. Except one.
Skyrim.esm|024521: My people, the Dunmer, built it. We fled from Morrowind after Vvardenfell erupted almost 200 years ago. Those of us who were faithful to Azura were given a vision that led us away from the island before the worst came. This shrine is our thanks to her. That none will forget that she watches over us all.
Skyrim.esm|024522: They are. Except the gem is always consumed. They're frail. Except for one.
Skyrim.esm|024523: What do you know about soul gems?
Skyrim.esm|024525: We are children of the Twilight. Beings who are to be guided from the darkness into the light. And from the light into darkness.
Skyrim.esm|024526: Azura. Who led her children away from the disaster of Vvardenfell. You are the prophet that reveals our true future.
Skyrim.esm|024527: No harm shall come to me, for I live under Azura's wisdom. Her foresight protects me. Her insight sustains me.
Skyrim.esm|024528: Have you seen that Shrine of Azura? They say the dark elves built it after they fled from Morrowind. Sight to see.
Skyrim.esm|02452C: Did you say an "elf" and "stars"? I hope you don't know what you're asking.
Skyrim.esm|024578: "An elf that studies stars"? Where did you hear about that?
Skyrim.esm|02457A: Studies stars you say? Why, there hasn't been a mage doing anything related to stars since.... Oh....
Skyrim.esm|02457D: Why specifically stars? Wait, you aren't referring to... No, there's no way you would know about that.
Skyrim.esm|02458E: I know plenty of spells that produce light, but stars? Hmm. There was that problem a few years ago, but that couldn't be related.
Skyrim.esm|024591: Stars? You wouldn't be referring to that incident a few years ago? I still say that exile was far too harsh.
Skyrim.esm|024592: Why would anyone bother to study stars? Well, unless you're talking about that artifact that got all those... dimwits thrown out.
Skyrim.esm|024594: Studies stars? I remember the last time the word "stars" was flown through the Hall of the Elements. Bad things had happened.
Skyrim.esm|024598: Well, I've certainly heard worse things. I think the man you want to talk to is Nelacar. He was exiled a few years ago, bad research. Still hangs around the Frozen Hearth.
Skyrim.esm|0245A2: You were given a vision? Are you serious? Whatever. The person you probably want is Nelacar. He's in the Frozen Hearth. Be careful, he was exiled from the College for a reason.
Skyrim.esm|0245A3: No. I wouldn't. Why don't you head into town and get a drink at the Frozen Hearth? I hear old Nelacar still has a room there. He knows a lot of stories, that one.
Skyrim.esm|0245A5: Vision? What vision are you talking about? Oh, no matter. You should head to the Frozen Hearth and speak with Nelacar. It's his story to tell, and I wouldn't want to discredit him by talking behind his back.
Skyrim.esm|0245A7: You should go into town and talk to an elf named Nelacar. He was involved in the whole mess.
Skyrim.esm|0245A8: Yes, I did. I shouldn't be the one to tell you about this. There's an Altmer named Nelacar in the Frozen Hearth. Believe me, he'll know.
Skyrim.esm|02460E: Just a man's life work, is all. I've finally derived the location of the White Phial, but this doting busybody won't let me get it.
Skyrim.esm|02461F: Oh no, I'm not talking about that. I'm not being blamed for saying something out of place. Again. If you want to know, ask Nelacar. He's staying at the Frozen Hearth. Knows the whole sad story.
Skyrim.esm|024620: Of course there's a collapse in trade, Raerek. The Stormcloaks stalk every road and harass every one of our patrols.
Skyrim.esm|024622: Almost the opposite. The College got rid of something... someone, that was too much trouble. There's an Altmer named Nelacar staying at the inn outside the College. You should ask him about it.
Skyrim.esm|024623: The Forsworn aren't our real concern, defeating the Imperials and making sure we keep control of this city is.
Skyrim.esm|024625: Raerek, how is The Reach faring against this Forsworn menace?
Skyrim.esm|024626: Officially, nothing. Why don't you head into town and get a drink at the Frozen Hearth? I hear old Nelacar still has a room there. He knows a lot of stories, that one.
Skyrim.esm|024627: You want to know a secret of mine, young girl? The most effective poison to kill a man? A potion to make others fall in love, perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|024628: Mama, when is Cade coming home?
Skyrim.esm|024629: Will I need to talk so loudly with my wife when I get married?
Skyrim.esm|02462A: I have one. A nephew. He's in prison for being a member of the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|02462C: Aren't you wonderful! Why can't your father be more like you and your sister?
Skyrim.esm|02462F: When will I learn how to forge weapons and armor? Isn't that my job?
Skyrim.esm|024630: Always such a model for decency in these troubled times, Yngvar.
Skyrim.esm|024631: I just wanted to know if you needed anything.
Skyrim.esm|024633: You're right, dear. I'm sorry to worry you.
Skyrim.esm|02463B: Well, it's a... minor stain on the College's reputation. One of many, unfortunately. You should head to the Frozen Hearth and speak with Nelacar. It's his story to tell, and I wouldn't want to discredit him by talking behind his back.
Skyrim.esm|02463C: Cowards, the both of you. My father would not sit idly back and wait while evil men take over his lands. We're done discussing this for now.
Skyrim.esm|024640: Uh, right. Maybe just one of the popular songs, like "The Slaying of the Falmer Princes"?
Skyrim.esm|024641: Don't worry, my love. Just show the bugs your adoring face, and they'll scurry away in complete fear in no time.
Skyrim.esm|024647: Thongvor, do you really think that's wise? I mean, King Ulfric is such a busy man, and there are so many areas of Skyrim to protect.
Skyrim.esm|024649: I'm a cripple. I have a reason to be drinking.
Skyrim.esm|02464A: It was a few years ago. A few of the mages got overconfident and were exiled. They had no talent, anyway. I hear one of them, Nelacar, still hangs around the Frozen Hearth. Guess he has no where else to go, poor thing.
Skyrim.esm|02464B: I second that, Igmund. Who knows what that family will do for power? They could be working with the Forsworn, for all we know.
Skyrim.esm|02464E: There was some bother about a group of mages being exiled. I can't remember what the details were. I think one of them came back. Nelacar. Stays at the Frozen Hearth, but I don't know why. He's not welcome in the College.
Skyrim.esm|02464F: Clean the rooms yourself Kleppr. It's not like you do anything important around here.
Skyrim.esm|024653: That's just terrible. I'm so sorry to hear that.
Skyrim.esm|024655: You shouldn't ask me. There's an Altmer named Nelacar in the Frozen Hearth. He's the one to talk to.
Skyrim.esm|024657: Oh, no, I'm not falling for that one again. Next thing I know, I'm getting yelled at for saying something inappropriate. If you're so curious, talk to Nelacar, in the Frozen Hearth. He can tell you the whole sad story.
Skyrim.esm|024658: The kind of things that even the College won't tolerate. Things that can get a wizard exiled for life. There's an Altmer who's staying at the Frozen Hearth. Nelacar. You should speak with him, if you're so curious.
Skyrim.esm|024659: Malyn wanted to alter the Star. He was dying. Disease. He thought he could store his own soul inside. Become immortal. It drove him mad. Students started dying. Eventually, the College exiled him. He took a few loyal disciples to Ilinalta's Deep and vanished. Look, I don't care who asked you to find the Star, but don't take it back to Azura. The Daedra are evil. They're the reason Malyn went insane.
Skyrim.esm|02465A: What does that mean Reberrus? Are we winning or aren't we?
Skyrim.esm|024660: I want word on our campaign against the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|024663: Just calm down. I'll tell you everything.
Skyrim.esm|024664: You're lying. You're not quick enough to be a pickpocket. What's the real reason?
Skyrim.esm|024666: Adara bends the silver so naturally. Grandmother would have cried if she saw how deft our daughter has become.
Skyrim.esm|024667: Do you think muscling me is going to work? I'm a wizard. An old. Elven. Wizard. Think about it.
Skyrim.esm|024669: Nonsense, you're a vital part of my research team. You'll be there, have no fear! Now, I don't want to hear another word of protest.
Skyrim.esm|02466A: Not to mention the rebels completely control Windhelm and all of Eastmarch hold.
Skyrim.esm|02466B: How are your chores coming, my son?
Skyrim.esm|02466D: Can I join the Legion when I grow up?
Skyrim.esm|024672: What? What did I do this time? I was just standing here!
Skyrim.esm|024674: No, just check in the guests and manage the coin and keep a roof over your head. No, nothing important.
Skyrim.esm|024675: Next season, nephew. Next season for sure....
Skyrim.esm|02467A: I was actually just wondering if you had any family....
Skyrim.esm|02467F: Are you going to walk over to father and start hitting him again?
Skyrim.esm|024681: You're an idiot, Kleppr. Why did I ever marry you?
Skyrim.esm|024683: Very well, Thongvor. With the Stormcloaks here to help, the Silver-Blood family control nearly every route in and out of the city.
Skyrim.esm|024684: Move it. Get to work. Get to work!
Skyrim.esm|024687: Igmund, we need to talk about the Silver-Blood family.
Skyrim.esm|02468A: Dibella's Heart, son, I hope not.
Skyrim.esm|02468C: Your job is to do what I say. And I say you have many nails and fittings to make before you even touch a blade. Now get to work.
Skyrim.esm|024692: Things are bad, Igmund. Our forces are barely able to keep the Forsworn in check. Merchants are reporting the loss of more of their shipments.
Skyrim.esm|024693: Enough! We can't imprison the very people that own the jail we would be throwing them into. The Silver-Blood family are to be left alone, am I clear?
Skyrim.esm|024696: Another drink, Cosnach? A porter with Arnleif and Sons deserves nothing but the finest here.
Skyrim.esm|024698: That's a sensible lad. Now fetch me drink.
Skyrim.esm|02469E: Whose side are you on, Reburrus? I'm not worried about Skyrim, I'm worried about the Reach. Specifically, the part of the Reach we're standing on.
Skyrim.esm|0246A0: Now now, brother. Arnoit is just being his usual gloomy self, he didn't mean it, did you Arnoit? You're just being silly again.
Skyrim.esm|0246A6: Never mind your mother, Hreinn. You listen to me, and you'll be a great man one day.
Skyrim.esm|0246A7: Um, no. Nothing. Nothing at all.
Skyrim.esm|0246AC: He's off fighting, Adara, with the Stormcloaks. He'll return when the war is over.
Skyrim.esm|0246AE: "Mmmhmm"? I've been working with you for over 10 years, and all you have to say is "mmmhmm"?
Skyrim.esm|0246B3: Filthy natives. Get to work. We have meals to prepare and no time to prepare them!
Skyrim.esm|0246B4: Well, I'm proud of both of you. I haven't been so busy since those nobles from Solitude come into the city three years ago.
Skyrim.esm|0246B5: Ah, my son, you'll be a fine innkeeper someday, like your father.
Skyrim.esm|0246B7: And I'm a porter with no shipments coming in. You're not getting any coin from me, Degaine.
Skyrim.esm|0246B8: Is that what you call it? In the Legion we would have called it "useless." Look how brittle the metal is!
Skyrim.esm|0246BA: I, uh, I can't tell Thongvor. It's anyone's guess.
Skyrim.esm|0246BE: We need to get back to Jorrvaskr.
Skyrim.esm|0246C6: Fell off the top of the cliff after a drunken party with the priestesses of Dibella.
Skyrim.esm|0246C8: Why do you pester me with battles we can't win? I need my steward to plan for our victory, not nag me with every ill account.
Skyrim.esm|0246CD: Except the ones attacked by Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|0246D8: I watch you to make sure you are honorable. If you are honorable and strong, then I can call you sister.
Skyrim.esm|0246E1: I would hope that's obvious. Do well here, and you'll be a full member of the Companions. Fail, and it won't matter anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0246E2: One of those icebrains who wanders the land getting people's hopes up. From my view, I don't think we'll find anything here. You'd better hope there's still a chance for you to prove yourself, though, or this Trial might last longer than you'd like.
Skyrim.esm|0246E3: A smart man came and told us about a blade piece. Skjor thinks you should find it, and I'm supposed to watch you.
Skyrim.esm|02471D: I have a few very detailed missives from border patrols, beginning with Fifth Irregulars' reports on the movements of....
Skyrim.esm|02471E: Today I've cleaned the linens, washed the floors, and checked in on all our guests.
Skyrim.esm|024725: You were just standing there when you were supposed to be serving drinks to our customers!
Skyrim.esm|02472F: If there's still a Legion left, Adara. I'm sure you'd make a great soldier. Just like your brother.
Skyrim.esm|024734: This is why I say we take the fight to them. Gather the Legion and drive those blasted beasts out of the Reach for good.
Skyrim.esm|024735: No, not right now. Maybe later, if I'm still upset.
Skyrim.esm|024739: "Next season for sure..." What? Uncle Calcelmo, I don't understand what you mean.
Skyrim.esm|024844: Yes, Thongvor. I'll draft the letter immediately.
Skyrim.esm|024845: How are your chores coming, Son?
Skyrim.esm|024846: Whatever. Just get back to work. Now.
Skyrim.esm|024848: All your drinks are watered down, Kleppr. You aren't fooling me, and you know Lisbet can't afford to pay me enough for the finest drinks anyhow.
Skyrim.esm|02484A: Our son joining the Stormcloaks. If this war had never happened, Cade would have joined the Imperial Legion, like Grandfather.
Skyrim.esm|02484B: Father, why do you and Mother fight so much?
Skyrim.esm|0248EE: I have some thoughts. There are three crucial elements. Some may be easier to find than others. On the top of the Throat of the World is a patch of unmelting snow. No heat can touch it. Then we need the tusk of a mammoth, ground to a fine powder as only the giants know how. The final step is tricky. It requires the briar heart from a Forsworn of The Reach. If you can bring me these materials, the Phial can again be made whole.
Skyrim.esm|024982: You've shown yourself useful so far. All these lunks are too afraid of Haldyn to head in before the fog's cleared. So if you're willing to perform one more service for the Company, we could put this matter behind us for good. Take out Haldyn, and we'll handle the rest.
Skyrim.esm|024985: Good to hear. You can be a difficult man to find. Thankfully those couriers are tenacious.
Skyrim.esm|02498C: Good to hear. You can be a difficult woman to find. Thankfully those couriers are tenacious.
Skyrim.esm|0249E6: Voada, you spilled laundry water in the soup pot again! Do I need to cut off your fingers to teach you a lesson?
Skyrim.esm|024A3D: Why are you still standing around here? Go get me the Phial, you fool.
Skyrim.esm|024AA1: Who do you think brought Septimus here? Who do you think protected you on your journey to open the box and loose my knowledge on this world? Your free will is an illusion. Whether you acknowledge me or not is your own business. But I will be in your mind.
Skyrim.esm|024B3F: Ooooh, an observant one. How clever to ask of Septimus. This Dwemer lockbox. Look upon it and wonder. Inside is the heart. The heart of a god! The heart of you. And me. But it was hidden away. Not by the dwarves, you see. They were already gone. Someone else. Unseen. Unknown. Found the heart, and with a flair for the ironical, used dwarven trickery to lock it away. The scroll will give the deep vision needed to open it. For not even the strongest machinations of the Dwemer can hold off the all-sight given by an Elder Scroll.
Skyrim.esm|024B40: When the top level was built, no more could be placed. It was and is the maximal apex.
Skyrim.esm|024B41: How long will it be sung? My feet were set upon the rock but it turned to mud and drew me down.
Skyrim.esm|024B43: It lick the panes and smokes the glass...
Skyrim.esm|024CE5: Yes, you're innocent. As innocent as anyone can be in this city. I guess we also owe you something for locking you up. Here, my family's ring. And everything the guards confiscated from you. Now if you'll excuse me, I have a mine that needs new workers to fill it.
Skyrim.esm|024CE9: My eyes inside Cidhna Mine tell me that Madanach is dead. You've done a great service to the Silver-Blood family. I've had the jarl officially pardon you, and take care of a few other loose ends.
Skyrim.esm|024E56: The Dark Brotherhood? Yeah, yeah, yeah. You think this is the first contract I've had on my life? I'm a pirate, sweetie. Assassination attempts are an occupational hazard. Now, shall we?
Skyrim.esm|024E58: And why would you be looking for me, hmm? I am not sure I appreciate your presence here. Friend.
Skyrim.esm|024E59: Death? Oh, there's death to be found here, to be sure. But not my own!
Skyrim.esm|024E5A: Ah, I see. The old Dark Brotherhood "silent intimidation" treatment. Oh, I know who you are. Don't be so surprised. You ain't the first assassin sent to kill me. Won't be the last. Now let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|024E5B: Go away! I don't know who you are, but this is my spot! Whatever's on that ship is mine!
Skyrim.esm|024E5C: Who are you? Why are you here? Leave this place. I have much work to conduct...
Skyrim.esm|024E5D: Yes... quite. But if that power could be harnessed. Controlled. The possibilities would be... But... who exactly are you? And why are you in here? I think it best you leave Mzinchaleft. These ruins can be quite... dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|024E5E: You'll have to catch me first!
Skyrim.esm|024E5F: Sithis? Let me guess: "In the Void!" By the gods, you Dark Brotherhood are a predictable lot, aren't you? Okay, here's how this works. You try to assassinate me, and I kill you. Horribly. I have done this dance before, you know.
Skyrim.esm|024E61: What? Why? I don't understand... I've done nothing to wrong you. Please, mercy!
Skyrim.esm|024E62: What? You dare challenge Ma'randru-jo? You will suffer for your insolence!
Skyrim.esm|024E63: Fiend! You will not leave here alive! Guards! Guards!
Skyrim.esm|024E66: Now aren't you a strange one. Well, if you're here to shop, shop. If not, find some other caravan to stalk.
Skyrim.esm|024E68: Should have figured the little lunatic would completely snap.
Skyrim.esm|024E69: Still here? What's the matter, can't handle what I'm throwing your way?
Skyrim.esm|024E6A: And why would I... Oh. Oh! Guards! Guards, I need assistance!
Skyrim.esm|024E6B: Right. Well. If you have some kind of official business with the jarl, I'm afraid now is not the time. Go on then. Off with you.
Skyrim.esm|02513A: Onmund, please search that area over there. See if you notice any... residual energies. Alive or undead.
Skyrim.esm|02513C: Now, let's see. What shall we have the rest of you do?
Skyrim.esm|02513D: J'zargo... what shall we have you do... Ah! Why don't you verify that we're the first ones here? Look for any amount of tampering with the tombs.
Skyrim.esm|02513E: Brelyna, my dear, why don't you search for warding magics. Anything designed to keep people out. Don't interact with them, just identify them.
Skyrim.esm|02513F: Well, are there any questions before we begin?
Skyrim.esm|025140: And here we all are. Shall we step inside?
Skyrim.esm|025141: All right, everyone. Let's be careful, but have fun!
Skyrim.esm|025142: Ah, yes. Hmm. Well, why don't you see if you can assist Arniel Gane? He's one of our scholars, here working on cataloging our finds. I expect he'd appreciate some help in locating any additional magical artifacts here in the ruins. Any enchanted items will do; the usefulness of the enchantment is irrelevant. If you find anything, the class can look it over.
Skyrim.esm|025143: Find Arniel Gane. He's somewhere in the northern section of the ruins. He'll show you what to do.
Skyrim.esm|025145: No? I daresay we don't have all day.
Skyrim.esm|025146: Still? If I have to wait much longer, you may need to just bury me here.
Skyrim.esm|02515D: Help! Get that beast out of my house!
Skyrim.esm|02515E: It's still here! Get it out!
Skyrim.esm|02515F: Get that thing out of here!
Skyrim.esm|025160: Thank you! I thought that beast would be the end of me!
Skyrim.esm|0251DA: What about them? They seem loyal enough.
Skyrim.esm|0251E0: No, no, girl. It's the pickled spider tongue and then the decayed taproot. Do it the other way, and your cure turns into a poison.
Skyrim.esm|02523E: Me? I like to talk to my targets before the kill. Taunt them, scare them... even challenge them. That can have interesting results. Some victims may flee, others may cower in fear. If you can goad a target into attacking you first, you may even find any local guards coming to your aid.
Skyrim.esm|02523F: Just be yourself. Eliminate the targets any way you see fit. Quiet, loud, quick, slow - whatever you feel is best. Occasionally I'll give you a contract with certain parameters. Stay within them, and you'll earn yourself a bonus.
Skyrim.esm|02525C: The emissary? What's this about? Wait... who are you? Intruder!
Skyrim.esm|02525E: Ha. I'd like to see those arrogant bastards taken down a notch. Always looking down their noses at us lowly footsloggers.
Skyrim.esm|0252BA: If a dragon does show up, maybe we'll get lucky and it will eat the mages first. Might give us enough time to kill it.
Skyrim.esm|025321: You gave those mongrels a real whipping, eh? Good work. We'll be sure to send a garrison to reinforce the area and keep the peace.
Skyrim.esm|025322: We hit 'em where it hurts. As soon as we can, we'll take over - make the needed repairs. In the meantime, the people no longer slave for the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|025344: Good work. Knowing more about the enemy's position makes it that much easier to take it from him. And of course, they'll be caught off guard thinking we're off doing something altogether different.
Skyrim.esm|025364: Ah, good. I've been wondering how we were supposed to defend this place from a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|025365: No. They're high mages, just in from Alinor. I guess herself is finally getting worried about all the dragon attacks.
Skyrim.esm|025366: Did you see those robes march in this morning? Who are they with? More of the emissary's treaty enforcers?
Skyrim.esm|0253A8: Not a day goes by I don't ask that question myself, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|0253AA: Don't be. We all make mistakes.
Skyrim.esm|0253AB: Give us a coin, Cosnach. You'll just spend it on cheap ale, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0253AC: Ah, Yngvar, here to sing? You know an employee of the Silver-Blood family is always a guest of honor here.
Skyrim.esm|0253AD: Reburrus, I need you to draft a letter to Ulfric requesting more men.
Skyrim.esm|0253AF: I miss those days, before the Forsworn took up arms again, when you could walk the roads in peace.
Skyrim.esm|0253B1: Forgive me for worrying about the future of Markarth. The lifeblood of a city is gold, Igmund, and it doesn't stop bleeding because there's a war on.
Skyrim.esm|0253B5: I... I'm sorry. I just, well, the forge gets so hot and I thought that...
Skyrim.esm|0253B7: How did you manage to cripple yourself, Degaine?
Skyrim.esm|0253B8: Oh, Talos' breath, Reburrus. Skip ahead to the good part.
Skyrim.esm|025CF6: What can I get for you, sir?
Skyrim.esm|025CF7: What can I get for you, ma'am?
Skyrim.esm|025D20: I don't know, my darling wife. I assume you're going to tell me?
Skyrim.esm|025D21: I don't buy that story, either.
Skyrim.esm|025D25: And what do Legion soldiers know about the hills of The Reach and the caves that make up those hills? The Forsworn can hide and ambush every assault.
Skyrim.esm|025D27: Shouldn't you be helping me then, wife of mine? Sharing my problems? Being my better half?
Skyrim.esm|025D29: We sold all those lovely little necklaces you brought me today. Did you make them yourself?
Skyrim.esm|025D2B: I see. Well, let's continue this discussion later, then.
Skyrim.esm|025D33: Endon, we talked about this... Are you still disappointed in Cade's choice?
Skyrim.esm|025D50: Ready to pay the toll? One shiv.
Skyrim.esm|025D5E: I'll let that comment go because you're new here. Ask things out of turn again, and we have a problem. Now, are we clear on all of this?
Skyrim.esm|025D60: Then find one. That dung heap Grisvar's been known to make a few.
Skyrim.esm|025D62: This better not be another waste of the Guild's resources, Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|025D64: Good. Then I think it's time we put your expertise to the test.
Skyrim.esm|025D65: If you're not sure maybe you don't belong here. I'll ask again, are we clear on all of this?
Skyrim.esm|025D66: Goldenglow Estate brought in a mountain of gold for the Guild. You could almost call it our sweetest deal. Then out of the clear blue, Aringoth stops sending us our cut. Mercer was... well, angry to put it kindly. So we send in Vex and find out he's hired a bunch of mercenaries to guard the place.
Skyrim.esm|025D67: The catch is that you can't burn the whole place to the ground. That important client Mercer mentioned would be furious if you did.
Skyrim.esm|025D68: Mercer? This is the one I was talking about... our new recruit.
Skyrim.esm|025D6B: You've made a mess of things and Maven's furious. I told you not to burn more than three of the hives! I've smoothed things over with her for now, but you can forget your cut.
Skyrim.esm|025D6D: Ah, now that's a good idea. Head on in. But don't try anything in there. Madanach is smarter than you think.
Skyrim.esm|025D6E: They're built like small fortresses to resist the weather, but their one weakness is flame. Besides, nothing tells the people of Riften we mean business better than a huge column of smoke.
Skyrim.esm|025D6F: Maven prefers that Aringoth remains alive, but if he tries to stop you from getting the job done, kill him. The Guild has a lot riding on this. Don't make me look foolish by mucking it up.
Skyrim.esm|025D70: That's not how things work around here. The Guild depends on an arrangement of influential people to keep things running smoothly.
Skyrim.esm|025D71: Goldenglow Estate is a bee farm; they raise the wretched little things for honey. It's owned by some smart-mouth Wood Elf named Aringoth. We need you to teach him a lesson by burning down three of the estate's hives and clearing out the safe in the main house.
Skyrim.esm|025D72: Ah, Borkul muscle one out of you on your way in? Fine. Take this one.
Skyrim.esm|025D76: Maven wants to see you right away. I suggest you head right over.
Skyrim.esm|025D77: Before we continue, I want to make one thing perfectly clear. If you play by the rules, you walk away rich. You break the rules and you lose your share. No debates, no discussions... you do what we say, when we say.
Skyrim.esm|025D7A: So tell me, did Aringoth enjoy your visit?
Skyrim.esm|025D7D: All eyes are on you, lad. Don't disappoint us.
Skyrim.esm|025D7E: I suggest you listen to what Mercer has to say... if you want in.
Skyrim.esm|025D7F: It's just this way. Keep following me.
Skyrim.esm|025DA3: I wasn't aware that Lisbet was having trouble, Cosnach. Is Arnleif and Sons not doing a good turn of business these days?
Skyrim.esm|025DA5: Well yes, of course you had to go and mention that.
Skyrim.esm|025DE1: Jarl Black-Briar. I must admit, I do like the sound of that. And don't worry about any rioting, Legate. I have it under control. We will soon begin publicly executing captured men. That should send a clear message to the people.
Skyrim.esm|025E46: Same as the others. Looks like she was stabbed, and then cut apart. It's like an animal got to her, but these are the wounds of a blade.
Skyrim.esm|025E48: Look, friend, if you think you can do better than the legion of guards, be my guest. You'll need to talk to Jorleif, though. We can't just let anyone go around claiming to be on official business. If he's willing, then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|025E49: Look, there's a war going on, if you hadn't heard. We barely have enough soldiers to walk the streets, much less patrol every corner.
Skyrim.esm|025E4A: I don't want to believe it. Wuunferth has been a trusted friend to Ulfric for many years. It pains me to see that the whispers had truth to them. Wuunferth shall be apprehended. I thank you for your diligence in getting to the bottom of this matter. The streets of Windhelm are now safe.
Skyrim.esm|025E4B: Well... all right. What exactly do you need to know?
Skyrim.esm|025E4C: Well... all right. What exactly do you need to know?
Skyrim.esm|025E4E: I've never seen anything like it. I would take this to Calixto at the House of Curiosities. He has a good eye for strange trinkets. Might even give you a bit of gold for it.
Skyrim.esm|025E4F: Eehh.. Calixto and his books are often confused about such matters. It happens to the best of us.
Skyrim.esm|025E50: Suit yourself. It's only of value to collectors, though. Good luck finding anyone else who will appraise it that highly!
Skyrim.esm|025E51: They say they're too busy with the war -- I say what good is winning a war if we're still terrorized by one of our own?
Skyrim.esm|025E52: I've had enough troubles recently. What is it that you're after?
Skyrim.esm|025E53: Susanna's the third. It's always the same: young girl, killed at night, body torn up.
Skyrim.esm|025E54: Wuunferth. There have been rumors swirling about him for years. As long as I can remember. But he's a dangerous man. It's why they call him "the Unliving." I wouldn't approach him directly. This information needs to go straight to the steward. He'll listen to you.
Skyrim.esm|025E55: Wuunferth? Bah. It's purely ceremonial, and he has no use for it. Besides, I wouldn't want to be the one to give it to him. Gives me the creeps. They say he dabbles in necromancy.
Skyrim.esm|025E56: Necromancy? I am a member of the College of Winterhold, in good standing! They haven't allowed necromancy for hundreds of years!
Skyrim.esm|025E57: If you want to help, ask some of these gawkers if they saw anything useful. I'm going to examine the body before the rats can get to it.
Skyrim.esm|025E58: I've been noting a pattern to when the killings happen. Now that we know they're tied in to some sort of necromantic ritual, I think I know when the next might occur. Let's see. From a Loredas of Last Seed until a Middas of Heartfire... it will happen soon. Very soon. Keep watch in the Stone Quarter tomorrow night. That's almost certainly where the killer will strike next.
Skyrim.esm|025E59: What made you think I was to blame in the first place? I've actually been after the killer myself.
Skyrim.esm|025E5A: My what, now? I've never kept a journal, I can assure you. What exactly did this amulet look like?
Skyrim.esm|025E5B: I've been trying to put those up around the city, to warn people. Have them keep their eyes open. But someone keeps taking them down. You say you found it in Hjerim? Friga's old place. Let's go have a look around there. I've got a feeling about this one.
Skyrim.esm|025E5F: Oh, they care all right. Just none of them thinks to do anything about it. They say I'm just snooping around bothering people, but I'm trying to save lives!
Skyrim.esm|025E60: Wonderful. This will be a splendid addition to my private collection.
Skyrim.esm|025E61: I know it well. Or at least, I've heard of it. I would wager that carving once depicted a skull. That is the Necromancer's Amulet, of legend. It appears you were at least half-right. There is necromancy at the heart of this.
Skyrim.esm|025E62: Let's go. I'd rather not stay out here alone any longer than I have to.
Skyrim.esm|025E63: I'm sorry -- she was very dear to me, and it's rather painful to think about.
Skyrim.esm|025E64: I'd rather not talk about my daughter, if you'll excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|025E65: Let me see... ah, yes. This is the Wheelstone. It's an heirloom symbol of the power of Windhelm. Traditionally it's carried by the court mage. I would... eh... be interested in acquiring it. If you're willing to part with it, that is. For a piece like that, I could pay... 500 gold?
Skyrim.esm|025E66: The Wheelstone? Have you decided to sell it, yet?
Skyrim.esm|025E67: Hjerim? Well, I'm not sure what you think you'll find there, but you're welcome to have a look.
Skyrim.esm|025EB2: There's just so much to memorize. How am I supposed to remember what goes where for every single herb?
Skyrim.esm|025EB4: But mother says you're a terrible innkeeper!
Skyrim.esm|025EB8: You touch her, and I swear it'll be your fingers that go missing, you stupid Breton!
Skyrim.esm|025EB9: The excavation, Aicantar! Right after I finish my book. We'll start excavating Nchuand-Zel again.
Skyrim.esm|025EBA: Playing tonight, Ogmund? Everyone here is anxious to hear the old ballads performed by the True Skald of the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|025EBC: My Jarl, we need to discuss the state of Markarth's collapsing trade with the other holds.
Skyrim.esm|025EBE: Sometimes I think we deserve these miserable lives of ours.
Skyrim.esm|025EC0: I'm sorry. You don't have to tell me if you don't....
Skyrim.esm|025EC1: We don't fight, son. We, um, talk. Loudly.
Skyrim.esm|025EC3: What is it nephew? Can't you see I'm trying to think here?
Skyrim.esm|025EC4: Very well. I'll send a request for additional Legionnaires to protect trade to the other Imperial holds. Hopefully Solitude can spare more men.
Skyrim.esm|025EC5: Endon, dear, please don't.... Save your frustrations for work.
Skyrim.esm|025EC7: Shut it, Kleppr. Thongvor isn't here, so save your kissing up for a Nord who cares.
Skyrim.esm|025ECB: I've been cleaning up spills and there is fresh linen for the rooms.
Skyrim.esm|025ECC: You thought what? You would skip a step? Not heat the metal all the way through? Useless! Now do it again.
Skyrim.esm|025ECD: Frabbi, my dear loving wife, could you please clean out our guest rooms?
Skyrim.esm|025ECE: Ha! About time we got some more muscle in this city.
Skyrim.esm|025ECF: Another foul day at the smelter.
Skyrim.esm|025ED0: That's right! Worst innkeeper in all of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|025ED1: Loyal? Thongvor supports Ulfric and his Stormcloaks! We should imprison the entire family as a precaution.
Skyrim.esm|025ED3: What are you, blind? That store is empty. Forsworn get almost every shipment that comes in.
Skyrim.esm|025ED4: The safety of the city and our settlements must be our only concern, Igmund. The rest of the Reach is on its own.
Skyrim.esm|025ED6: Kleppr, you idiot! Can't you do anything right?
Skyrim.esm|025ED7: It's rotten because the wood is cheap and its soaked with ale! Now we'll have to replace all the furniture before bugs set in.
Skyrim.esm|025ED8: Oh, right. Oh, well, things are going well enough. At least, as well as we can hope.
Skyrim.esm|025ED9: Mining accident, back when I was in prison for being a pickpocket.
Skyrim.esm|025EDD: No. I think if I was a young man, I would have done the same.
Skyrim.esm|025EDE: You will. Until then, you'll taste every potion you make before you sell it.
Skyrim.esm|025EDF: All the wood furniture in this inn is rotting to the core. Do you know why that is, Kleppr?
Skyrim.esm|025EE0: I am your better half. And your better half is telling you to stop being an idiot and serve the customers their drinks!
Skyrim.esm|025EE1: That's right! Papa helped me with the clasps, but I did all the links on my own.
Skyrim.esm|025EE4: Son, I know thousands of the old ballads. If I played all of them, everyone in this inn would be as grey as I am before I finished.
Skyrim.esm|025EE5: How goes the trade in and out of the city?
Skyrim.esm|025EE7: Always another big meal to cook. Figures.
Skyrim.esm|025EE8: Too bad. That one's my favorite.
Skyrim.esm|025EEA: Could I maybe, skip the excavation, Uncle Calcelmo? I, uh, I just think you won't need me.
Skyrim.esm|025F06: If you ladies are finished bickering, there's much to be done. Jarl Black-Briar put your government together. Do it now, or there will be rioting in the streets. We must move quickly to prevent further violence.
Skyrim.esm|025F3A: Hroggar's house? It burned down not too long ago. It's a real pity about his wife and kid. The screams woke half the town. Most folk won't go near it now for fear it's cursed.
Skyrim.esm|025F3B: That's what they say. See, he's living with Alva now. That started the day after the fire. It ain't right, movin' in with a new love the day after your kin die like that.
Skyrim.esm|025F3C: Aye. Our Jarl would sure like to know if he did though. Might even pay to find out.
Skyrim.esm|025F3D: Hroggar claims it was a hearth fire. Some folks say Hroggar started it himself.
Skyrim.esm|025F44: So enters Maven Black-Briar, the glint of Imperial coin in her eyes. So tell me, what's the price for a woman's integrity these days?
Skyrim.esm|025F45: One day when you can see past your own interests, you will come to see that we were right. And this... This is all wrong.
Skyrim.esm|025F52: You never were able to see the forest for the trees, were you?
Skyrim.esm|025F5A: Oh? I don't expect anyone to miss my meaning. Come, Legate. There's much to do.
Skyrim.esm|025F5B: I knew you looked like a clever one...
Skyrim.esm|025F5D: Of course not! Thieves take what is precious. Thieves steal. We just take what will not be missed. What no one needs anymore. Maybe we take some weapons and stop a few deaths. Who would call that thievery?
Skyrim.esm|025F5E: Don't worry about them. We'll make sure they wash up safe. Maybe they'll even pay us for the heroic rescue from their sad crash.
Skyrim.esm|025F60: Turn me in? For what? What crime's been committed? I'm just making idle talk about what would most undoubtedly be a tragic event... A tragic, and very, very profitable event.
Skyrim.esm|025F62: Yes, but not likely the one you intend.
Skyrim.esm|025F63: With the war, many more ships come through these docks. Loaded with weapons and pay, but few people. And they pass through dangerous waters. We have an interest in one of those boats, the Icerunner. The Solitude Lighthouse will be guiding it in... but if its fire were to go out, the Icerunner would run aground.
Skyrim.esm|025F6B: The effects have already been felt. The Icerunner has failed to dock on time. Without the Lighthouse, the Icerunner will have swung wide of the western shoal and run aground on the eastern coast. My sister Deeja will be at the wreck with our Blackblood Marauders. She knows to expect you.
Skyrim.esm|025F6C: We Nords were proud warriors once. And we still could be. Evidenced by the men and women who fought and died bravely today. You could see that if you had any faith left in that black heart of yours. But no, you're content to snatch scraps falling off the Emperor's table. Fine. Take my home. Take my city. May it burn down around you.
Skyrim.esm|025F6D: Hey! What're you doing in here?
Skyrim.esm|025F6E: Get out of here or I'll call the guards!
Skyrim.esm|025F6F: That's it! You're leaving, right now.
Skyrim.esm|025F70: You look new. I'm new, too. I think we could be friends.
Skyrim.esm|025F74: Stop right there! Were I can see you.
Skyrim.esm|025F7C: I think I'll like living here.
Skyrim.esm|025F86: As you wish. Protect the Jarl!
Skyrim.esm|025F8D: Riften denies the Emperor. You are here illegally. Stand down and you will be arrested and tried in the morning. Resist and you will die.
Skyrim.esm|025F9C: She's watching over the others. Look alive now.
Skyrim.esm|02617D: This warning is passed to you because the Psijic Order believes in you.
Skyrim.esm|02617E: Can you tell me more about what you saw?
Skyrim.esm|026180: I... I swear I felt something rather strange just then. What just happened?
Skyrim.esm|026182: Judgment will be passed on your actions to come, and how you deal with the dangers ahead of you.
Skyrim.esm|026183: You, mage, and you alone, have the potential to prevent disaster.
Skyrim.esm|026185: How in the world did that happen?
Skyrim.esm|026186: Judgment has not been passed, as you had no way of knowing.
Skyrim.esm|026187: Know that you have set in motion a chain of events that cannot be stopped.
Skyrim.esm|026188: I'm afraid I didn't see anything...
Skyrim.esm|026189: Really? Perhaps the amulet is important somehow. Is there some way you can use it?
Skyrim.esm|02618A: Take great care, and know that the Order is watching.
Skyrim.esm|026197: I have no idea! This is amazing. Absolutely amazing. The Arch-Mage needs to be informed immediately. He needs to see this for himself. I don't dare leave this unattended. Can you return to the College and inform Savos Aren of this discovery? Please, hurry.
Skyrim.esm|026199: The Psijics have no connection to these ruins. And no one's seen any of their order in a long time. Perhaps we should take a look inside these coffins... Now please do be careful. Who knows what we're going to find.
Skyrim.esm|02619A: The Psijic Order? Are you quite sure about that? That's very odd. And danger ahead? Why that doesn't make any sense at all.
Skyrim.esm|0261A4: Well let's not put it like that. Wouldn't want the Thieves Guild catching wind of us, eh? Nah, just creep in and have a go at taking it out. I'll be here, and pay you half of what my man in Riften promised for it. You'll be helping out an old beggar, and the Divines smile on that sort of thing.
Skyrim.esm|0261A5: Aye, a whole heap of it. You see that Temple? Way up there? All them women, keeping to themselves. Won't let poor Degaine in, no. Not even for a look. But they've got a treasure in there. Gold statue, size of a child. Who knows what they use it for, but I know what I'll use it for.
Skyrim.esm|02632D: Didn't you hear me? I said the keep is under attack!
Skyrim.esm|02632F: Don't you even know what's going on? A dragon is attacking Helgen!
Skyrim.esm|026330: Come with us. We need to get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|026331: There's no way out that way, you know...
Skyrim.esm|026333: Forget the old man. I'll come with you.
Skyrim.esm|02633F: An old storeroom. See if you can find some potions. Might come in handy.
Skyrim.esm|02634B: A dragon? Please. Don't make up nonsense.
Skyrim.esm|02634D: Hold on, now, we only want to...
Skyrim.esm|02634E: Damn, that dragon doesn't give up easy.
Skyrim.esm|02634F: You have no authority over me, boy.
Skyrim.esm|026351: You fellows happened along just in time. These boys seemed a bit upset at how I've been entertaining their comrades.
Skyrim.esm|026352: Grab what you can, and let's go.
Skyrim.esm|026353: Although, come to think of it, I did hear some odd noises coming from over there.
Skyrim.esm|0263B7: Then it is true what they said? I am touched by the gods?
Skyrim.esm|0263B8: You... you found her? I can't believe it. Let the girl come with me and we can begin her preparation. I will send word to her family that she is in good hands. You've truly earned the Blessing of Dibella, child. Prostrate yourself before her altar, and she will bestow her gift.
Skyrim.esm|0263B9: Damn it, man, are you trying to taunt us?
Skyrim.esm|0263D7: Thank you for bringing this to my attention. Tolfdir normally looks after your little group, yes? Since he's apparently occupied, and I will need to see this discovery for myself, I think perhaps you should begin researching the subject. Speak with Urag in the Arcanaeum. See if he is aware of anything that matches your discovery. And... good work. The next time you find yourself exploring Nordic ruins, perhaps this will be helpful.
Skyrim.esm|0263D8: Please don't tell me that another one of the apprentices has been incinerated. I have enough to deal with right now.
Skyrim.esm|0263D9: I... see. I trust that Tolfdir will provide a more... specific explanation.
Skyrim.esm|0263DA: Very well, I trust that you wouldn't be here were it not significant.
Skyrim.esm|0264CB: Clan Battle-Born's got a proud lineage in Whiterun, but unlike the Gray-Manes, we've also got wealth.
Skyrim.esm|0264CC: My bloodin' days might be over, but my son Idolaf is out there fightin' for Skyrim! Fightin' for the Empire! Ha!
Skyrim.esm|0266EB: My Alembic. I need it for some research, and can't seem to find it.
Skyrim.esm|0266EC: Well, yes. This is rather embarrassing, but it seems I've misplaced something.
Skyrim.esm|0266ED: Ah, yes! There it is! Thank you so much.
Skyrim.esm|0266EE: I can't find my Alembic. I suppose I could use another one, but... well, it sounds silly, but mine has sentimental value. If you happen to see it, would you mind bringing it to me?
Skyrim.esm|026732: Paarthurnax has made the decision to help you. This is the road we have to walk. Even the Greybeards must bend to the winds of change, it seems. So be it. Tell Ulfric and General Tullius that the Greybeards wish to speak to them. We will see if they still remember us.
Skyrim.esm|026733: Yes, of course! But how to force a dragon to talk to you... There is one possibility. But... no, the Jarl would never agree.
Skyrim.esm|026734: I see. The dragon will lead you to Alduin, but without the Jarl's help...
Skyrim.esm|026735: Do you know Dragonsreach? The Jarl of Whiterun's palace? Its name is no accident. The palace was originally built to hold a captive dragon.
Skyrim.esm|026737: The old tales say that he is able to travel into Sovngarde to devour the souls of the dead. But they don't say how he does this.
Skyrim.esm|026738: You misunderstand our authority. The Greybeards have never involved themselves in political affairs.
Skyrim.esm|02673B: You might be able to trap him there, exactly. You'd require the Jarl's cooperation, of course. That might not be easy to come by. You must trust in the rightness of your cause, and the strength of your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|02673C: Oh, this was back in the days of Olaf One-Eye, thousands of years ago. In those days, dragons could still be found among the remote mountain peaks. Before the Blades wiped them all out.
Skyrim.esm|02673E: This is High Hrothgar, our temple, sanctuary, and home. We ask that you respect our solitude while you are here.
Skyrim.esm|02673F: We are not warriors. What is overlooked in the Dragonborn is not permitted to any other followers of the Way of the Voice.
Skyrim.esm|026740: Ah! Dragonsreach! There are many difficulties, but I cannot think of a better plan. Although I fear the Jarl may not be easy to persuade. Remember, the Way of the Voice trains you for more than battle. Indeed, we believe that battle is the least of its uses. May the strength and truth of your Voice serve you well. Breath and focus.
Skyrim.esm|026741: Alduin... we heard the Dragonrend Shout from here... you defeated him?
Skyrim.esm|026743: I feared as much. I thought it was him we saw flying east after your battle.
Skyrim.esm|0267B7: Ah, an excellent question. Mehrunes Dagon is the Daedric Lord of Change, Destruction, and Ambition. Dagon's Mythic Dawn cult killed the Septim Dynasty and opened the Oblivion Gates into Tamriel. They called it the Oblivion Crisis.
Skyrim.esm|0267BF: The Razor is Mehrunes Dagon's personal artifact. It has always heralded bloody change and carnage. It's held many names: Dagger of the Final Wounds, Bane of the Righteous, the Kingslayer. The Mythic Dawn worshipped Dagon as a god. Having his Razor would be invaluable to my collection.
Skyrim.esm|0267D8: Get moving. I'll see if I can't delay any patrols out there.
Skyrim.esm|0267DF: The "problem" is your disrespect for the laws of this land. It's time to face the Jarl's justice.
Skyrim.esm|0267E0: Smart man. Now come along with us. We'll take any stolen goods, and you'll be free to go. After you pay the fine, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0267E1: I guess you're smarter than you look.
Skyrim.esm|0268DA: We got everything in hand here. You aren't needed 'round here.
Skyrim.esm|0268DB: We chop trees, we mill the wood. What more would you want to know?
Skyrim.esm|0268DC: It's a hard way to make a living, but we find ways to make ends meet.
Skyrim.esm|0268DD: We don't take kindly to people snooping around our business.
Skyrim.esm|0268DF: These axes can chop a lot more than wood, you know.
Skyrim.esm|0268E1: My brother and me bought this place when we left the army. Boy, did we lose a bundle, but at least we're not fighting the war!
Skyrim.esm|0268E2: We're making our money back, bit by bit. Every extra bit of work helps...
Skyrim.esm|0268E3: We served our time in the Legion. Now we're just trying to make it day to day.
Skyrim.esm|0268E5: I used to serve with Trilf and Steirod. Good guys, but don't get on their bad side, you know?
Skyrim.esm|0268E6: I once saw Steirod tear a whole tree out the ground with his bare hands. Glad he's on our side!
Skyrim.esm|0268E7: These boys do good work for the Empire!
Skyrim.esm|0268E8: Nice to be here on the home-front. Wouldn't mind being down in Cyrodiil, though.
Skyrim.esm|026A5F: It's considered a great honor for the family. Their daughter will never know hardship, and the goddess blesses them with great bounty.
Skyrim.esm|026A61: We prefer not to announce that the Sybil has died until we can introduce the new one. People like to know that we're connected to the goddess.
Skyrim.esm|026A62: The Sybil of Dibella spends her entire life in direct communion with the goddess. From the time she's a little girl to the moment she expires, she is a direct connection with the heavenly mother. From her reverie, we know the mind of our god. We have had a vision of the next Sybil, living in a village to the north. She must be brought to the temple for her reverie to begin.
Skyrim.esm|026A69: Damn it, woman, are you trying to taunt us?
Skyrim.esm|026A6A: Our daughter, Fjotra, was taken from here. The Forsworn descended and... they didn't touch anything else. Just our little girl.
Skyrim.esm|026A6C: It was the Forsworn from Broken Tower. Never been much trouble before. What's it to you?
Skyrim.esm|026A6D: You, my dear, can earn the Blessing of Dibella. You'll find men to be more pliable in the future... or more vulnerable.
Skyrim.esm|026A6E: Have you heard of the Blessing of Dibella? It can help you with the wooing of women, if that's your aim. Or give you strength if you happen to offend one. Typically we avoid bestowing it upon men, but for the Sybil's escort, we could make an exception.
Skyrim.esm|026A71: She... really? That's... I wouldn't have dreamed. Of course, we need her in Markarth, then. I'll come with you.
Skyrim.esm|026A72: That's... that's good of you. If you were to save her, I would be very grateful. I don't think there's anything I could give you, though...
Skyrim.esm|026B3E: Is that an Amulet of Mara? I'm surprised someone like you isn't spoken for.
Skyrim.esm|026B5B: You should arrange the ceremony at the Temple of Mara right away. I can't wait.
Skyrim.esm|026B5C: Here you are. Now remember, people in Skyrim respond to deeds. You will find more people interested in your love if you help them. Good luck. Mara bless you.
Skyrim.esm|026B75: Lost the last one I gave you? Very well, here you are. Mara keep you.
Skyrim.esm|026B7F: I'm sorry, but even Mara's Temple needs coin. We will have a symbol ready for you when you come back with enough gold.
Skyrim.esm|026BAA: You wish to be married? It's good to see love blossoming, even during these difficult times. Your wedding will be held tomorrow, from dawn until dusk. Don't be late, you don't want to miss your own wedding!
Skyrim.esm|026C28: Use the gloves to clear the focal points. Return to me when you have finished. I thought I made this rather clear.
Skyrim.esm|026C2A: Very well. These specially enchanted gloves will allow you to delve directly into the focal points for these energies. Go to each focal point and remove whatever impurities you may find there. Return the gloves to me when you have finished. Please exercise caution. This is not a procedure without possible... consequences.
Skyrim.esm|026C2B: As you may be aware, the College has gone to great lengths to purify and enhance the natural energies of the world. This both enhances our connection to the realm of magic, and eliminates possible interference from outside sources. These energies, on occasion, become... polluted. Effort must be made to see that they are kept pure, clean. Do you wish to aid in this cleansing?
Skyrim.esm|026C2C: There may be a matter in which your... skills... could be useful. The various points of focus for magic energies around the College have become polluted, and need cleansing.
Skyrim.esm|026C2D: The magical energies underneath the College still require purification. Your assistance would be most useful.
Skyrim.esm|026C2E: A book of mine. I was at least halfway through, and wanted to finish it, but apparently I put it down somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|026C2F: Yes, they have. I can feel the difference already. But of course, I'm far more in tune with these sorts of things than most. Thank you. I appreciate your assistance.
Skyrim.esm|026C30: I seem to have misplaced the book I was reading.
Skyrim.esm|026C49: There have been reports of weird happenings near Wolfskull Cave. Travelers disappearing, odd lights. I suspect wild animals or perhaps bandits. I don't think it's worth our time with the war going on, but if you want to clear out the cave, I'll make sure you're repaid for your work.
Skyrim.esm|026C4A: You mean the Dragon Bridge issue? I'll be honest with you, I was planning to let that go. Varnius is a bit jumpy at the best of times.
Skyrim.esm|026DEB: Ho, now... Keep me out of this...
Skyrim.esm|026DEC: And... you'll take care of it?
Skyrim.esm|026DED: Wait, let me guess. You'll get to it later.
Skyrim.esm|026DF2: The corn stores are getting low again, Orgnar.
Skyrim.esm|026DF3: You promised me you'd sweep the entranceway.
Skyrim.esm|026DF4: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! That's my boy!
Skyrim.esm|026DF5: Ha! I knew it! Had your name written all over it, that one did!
Skyrim.esm|026DF6: See, now that's what I'm talking about. Working hard, making your mark on this world. Good job, son.
Skyrim.esm|026DFB: I don't suppose I could convince you to take care of it now, could I?
Skyrim.esm|026DFE: You do realize these simpletons wouldn't know real music if it bit them in the britches, hmm? Very well. Your wish is my command.
Skyrim.esm|026E00: Well. There's something to be said for honesty, I suppose.
Skyrim.esm|026E03: It's just that papa promised I could make something at the forge. So maybe we could do something together, hmmm... next week? Is... that okay?
Skyrim.esm|026E04: Just stop trying to change me, mother! I'm not a baby anymore! I'm almost a woman! I... I can't be who I want to be!
Skyrim.esm|026E05: I'm sorry, mother. I can't help it. All that stuff you like to do, it's just so... boring.
Skyrim.esm|026E07: Wow. Yeah. That sounds great, mother. Only, um... it's just that...
Skyrim.esm|026E08: Oooh. Ahhh... Well... See, the thing is...
Skyrim.esm|026E09: Dorthe, dear, I've been growin' some really lovely vegetables. Maybe tomorrow you could help me plant a new patch? What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|026E0A: Dorthe... I was thinkin' I'd knit a couple of blankets, sell them to Lucan. Would you like to make one? Might be fun, workin' together.
Skyrim.esm|026E0B: You know, Dorthe, with all those vegetables I've been growing lately, I was thinkin' we could make a right lovely stew. Together. How about it?
Skyrim.esm|026E0C: It's about your singing. Well, not your singing, your songs. But you know what I'm talking about.
Skyrim.esm|026E0F: Shor's crown! There are things ladies like us are supposed to do. We've talked about this...
Skyrim.esm|026E10: Let me guess. You'd rather play "Daedra and Battlemages" with Ordnar. Or go fishing, or catch frogs...
Skyrim.esm|026E11: Dorthe, listen to me. Your father and your friend may treat you like a boy, but you're not. Do you understand? So please. Be a girl. For me.
Skyrim.esm|026E13: You just wait. My new pranks are going to the best ever! You'll see! He he he he.
Skyrim.esm|026E14: Ha! That ain't nothing. Wait till you see what I got planned for next week.
Skyrim.esm|026E15: Hmm. All right. Do you think... maybe we should?
Skyrim.esm|026E16: Well, I was thinking... we should. We could stock all kinds of specialty items. You know, to cater to some of our more.... "eclectic" customers.
Skyrim.esm|026E17: Hmmm... Well, maybe we could go on a sort of trip. Take the wares on the road. Get some new customers.
Skyrim.esm|026E19: All right. But I am getting a bit tired of waiting.
Skyrim.esm|026E22: That's really not at the top of my list, no. Please, Camilla, I need you to focus.
Skyrim.esm|026E23: Dear sister, we talked about this sort of thing before. We sell dry goods, nothing more, nothing less.
Skyrim.esm|026E24: Camilla, really? You don't have better things to do? Because I could certainly find quite a few tasks...
Skyrim.esm|026E25: Of course, Lucan. Of course. I was just, well... Never mind.
Skyrim.esm|026E26: Okay, okay. Just trying make this place a little less... boring.
Skyrim.esm|026E27: I know, I know. My head's in the clouds again. Just... forget I said anything.
Skyrim.esm|026E2B: Fine. You play in the mud, or play with your father's forge, or get into sword fights with your little friend. But this is not over.
Skyrim.esm|026E2C: Oh, Dorthe. You're breaking your mother's heart, you know that?
Skyrim.esm|026E2D: Well. I can see your father and I are going to have to talk about this. And when we do, things are going to change.
Skyrim.esm|026E2E: Sure I did. And I'd do it again.
Skyrim.esm|026E30: Ha ha ha ha! Yeah, I sure did. It was one of my best pranks yet, father.
Skyrim.esm|026E31: Hey boy, I heard you nailed a Septim into Lucan's doorstep. Damn near drove him crazy. That true?
Skyrim.esm|026E32: Tell me, son. Any truth to these stories I been hearing. About you gluing a cow's udders shut? You really do something like that?
Skyrim.esm|026E33: Heard me a rumor, boy. Something about you putting bugs in the stew over at the Sleeping Giant. You do this?
Skyrim.esm|026E36: Wonderful. And let's not have this chat again next week. Agreed?
Skyrim.esm|026E37: Later, little one. I need to finish what I'm working on. But keep watching. Watch, and learn.
Skyrim.esm|026E38: He he. Not just yet, Dorthe. I need to keep forging, and you need to keep watching.
Skyrim.esm|026E39: Patience, Dorthe. You'll get some time at the forge, I promise you. But not right now. You need to keep watching me. That's how you'll learn.
Skyrim.esm|026E3B: Lucan, we don't sell silver buckles, do we? One of the customers was asking.
Skyrim.esm|026E3C: We don't sell any imports from Summerset Isle, do we, Lucan?
Skyrim.esm|026E3D: Lucan, I was thinking we should have a grand sale. Lower the prices by half, maybe get some jugglers. We could attract lots of new customers.
Skyrim.esm|026E3E: Can I make something now, papa?
Skyrim.esm|026E3F: Is it time yet? Time for me to make my sword? Or maybe... an axe?
Skyrim.esm|026E40: I'm bored standing around, papa. I could take over for you...
Skyrim.esm|026E42: No, dear sister. We do not.
Skyrim.esm|026E44: A sale? To attract new customers? Camilla, everyone in Riverwood already shops here. There are no more customers.
Skyrim.esm|026EB5: The dawn surely opens upon you, child. You must bear its light that all may see. Mara has reflected an image to me. At the foot of the throat and young woman... almost a girl... her fickle love must resolve itself. The village of Ivarstead. The woman, Fastred. This is the prayer heard by the goddess and relayed to her servants. Return when she has seen her path. I will entreat Mara on your behalf.
Skyrim.esm|026EB6: So I hear. Come here, you.
Skyrim.esm|026EB9: I don't want you to leave Ivarstead.
Skyrim.esm|026EBA: Not all can hear the broad echoes of deepest dawn. To receive the touch of Mara, you must first act as her hands in the world. Explore the facets of the infinite jewel.
Skyrim.esm|026EBC: The goddess smiles at your efforts, child. The dawn shines bright upon you. You have seen the young, fickle love of youth, and helped a more seasoned love find its way. There is one final aspect we wish for you to explore. For a strong love can withstand storms and even survive death. Take this symbol of Mara. She will guide you to the wandering souls of two whose love was so great that their entwinement binds them to this world.
Skyrim.esm|026EBD: Oh my. Probably something about the men. We all wish we had her problems. Now, don't tell my husband, but I don't have any problem with Bassianus.
