Even if it means her leaving Ivarstead, I want Fastred to be happy. If they just snuck out of town together, I could manage my husband.
Skyrim.esm|026EBE: I'm not sure what this is all about.
Skyrim.esm|026EC0: We are devoted to the Goddess Mara, who gave mortals the gift of love that they might have a hint of eternity. Her blessings are many. To love is to know the true nature of the gods.
Skyrim.esm|026EC1: Oh, and to be with you!
Skyrim.esm|026EC5: Your efforts have helped illuminate the entire province. I trust by now you've achieved a higher comprehension of love. Carry the blessings of Mara with you, so the rest of the province may shine with her glories.
Skyrim.esm|026EC6: What good are fish if you have to eat them alone?
Skyrim.esm|026EC7: This is... lovely. Calcelmo wrote this... about me? I never knew he had such feeling in him.
Skyrim.esm|026EC8: The trouble is that I can't seem to speak around her. My mouth goes dry, and I start to shake. I could never hope to approach her.
Skyrim.esm|026ECA: Ysmir's beard, you're right! No fish was ever caught by staying away from the shore. I'm off to see her!
Skyrim.esm|026ECB: We must leave. Your father will never approve of us, and I grow weary of this place.
Skyrim.esm|026ECD: I always had a feeling she liked me. More than that brute Jofthor, at least. Jofthor... what about him? He'll come after us and... it will be unpleasant.
Skyrim.esm|026ECE: Come back when you've got the gold, then.
Skyrim.esm|026ECF: Clearly. I've never even thought of him in that manner. I wish that I was as skilled with words as he. Here, take him this letter. I may not be as eloquent as he is, but I hope he understands.
Skyrim.esm|026ED1: Are you prepared to receive my golden words?
Skyrim.esm|026ED2: Wonderful. I'll write it out so you won't forget it.
Skyrim.esm|026ED3: Fastred, I have something to say to you.
Skyrim.esm|026ED4: Sssh. It's all right. You don't need to say anymore. I had no idea you were so... nuanced.
Skyrim.esm|026ED6: Look around here. There's not much left. Used to be a good-sized town, but folks have been moving to Riften for a while now. If all the young people leave, what happens to Ivarstead?
Skyrim.esm|026ED7: This is... more than I could have hoped for. Have you really done this for me? I have to see her. Please, excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|026EDB: Oh, my love. How I long to take her away from all of this. Why did Boti send you to me?
Skyrim.esm|026EDC: May you carry the warmth of Mara to all corners of the frozen wastes.
Skyrim.esm|026EDE: Are you the one sent by Mara?
Skyrim.esm|026EE0: Oh, thank you so much! It's wonderful to have someone around who understands.
Skyrim.esm|026EE3: You know how children are. Her fancies change with the moon. And like always, the boy lacks any kind of spine. He'll need some convincing. A little push and this would all be simpler.
Skyrim.esm|026EE5: Oh, Fastred. I'm not sure what happened. One moment she's smiling at me, the next I can't even meet her eyes. I'll bet Bassianus would know what's going on. He understands women.
Skyrim.esm|026EE7: I prayed to Mara and she spoke to me in a dream. She said a stranger would appear to help me. Is that you?
Skyrim.esm|026EE8: My parents are being impossible. Bassianus wants to marry me. And I think I love him. But he wants to move to Riften afterwards, and my father won't allow it. He only cares about this stupid town, but Bassianus makes me happier than anyone ever has. My mother's not any help, either.
Skyrim.esm|026EEB: Then you know what a beauty she is.
Skyrim.esm|026EED: I was beginning to lose faith that any help would come. Eh... You see... I've been thinking about Faleen quite a bit. You know Faleen?
Skyrim.esm|026EEE: To Riften, my pet. How you'll adore the city.
Skyrim.esm|026EF0: Well, suffice to say she is resplendent, but not without resolve. Striking, in all senses.
Skyrim.esm|026EF1: I'm sure I know what this is about. She thinks she's in love with Bassianus and wants to leave town. With him. Let me tell you how I see it. Until a few months ago, the girl was head over heels for Klimmek. Wouldn't stop talking about him. Now if I thought she was really in love with Bassianus, that would be one thing. But she's a child. It will pass.
Skyrim.esm|026EF2: But I thought you only cared about your fishing!
Skyrim.esm|026F4C: You a fighting man? How about a little bet?
Skyrim.esm|026F54: The final tempering of the Phial was performed with a type of old magic that's been lost since the Dragon War. The nearest modern incarnation of it is the strange rituals practiced by the Forsworn. With the heart of one of their Briar Warriors, I can lock the Phial's magic into its physical form. It's difficult to explain to the layman -- forgive me if I begin waxing alchemic.
Skyrim.esm|026F55: On top of the Throat of the World is a patch of snow that's never melted. They say the Greybeards taught it to ignore the sun, and heat simply washes over it. The original Phial was made from this snow. We'll only need a small bit for the repairs.
Skyrim.esm|026F56: The ivory produced by those beasts is as hard as iron. But the giants have found ways to make it yield. They're able to grind it down so fine that it can be infused into the lattice of packed snow. The finished material has the delicate nature of fresh powder snow, but the strength of the hardest steel.
Skyrim.esm|026F57: Figures -- I doubt you have sufficient knowledge to harm the Phial even if you wanted to. Either way, this is the end of it. Now if you'll excuse me, I'm not quite in the mood to entertain guests. I trust you can show yourself out. Here's for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|026FE8: Take my advice. Never gamble. Damn Irnskar has me in debt up to the eyes.
Skyrim.esm|02707C: I might just be a servant among lords, but don't think for a moment I don't take my duties as seriously as the jarl himself takes his!
Skyrim.esm|02707D: Got of lot of steel to shape.
Skyrim.esm|027080: Why you still starin' at me?
Skyrim.esm|027085: I'm... Well, who I am isn't important. Let's leave it at that, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|027087: Forget Solitude or Windhelm. Whiterun was the crown jewel of Skyrim before... Well, before... Never mind.
Skyrim.esm|027088: This was such a glorious city. But then everything had to change. The Empire came... and my brother was lost...
Skyrim.esm|027089: Ah, but Skyrim! Skyrim is supreme among all nations! Our people, our native people, are unmatched in all Tamriel!
Skyrim.esm|02708A: One day the war will end and the true heirs of Skyrim will inherit this great realm.
Skyrim.esm|027090: Tired of bread and stew? Had enough of fish and fowl? Treat yourself and your kin to a choice cut of steak or a tender rack of ribs!
Skyrim.esm|027092: I can't stand those braggart Battle-Borns. They think they're the gods' gift to Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|027093: Eorlund and I... we both miss our son terribly. Thorald... he went missing, fighting the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|027094: Everyone thinks Thorald is dead. But not me. I know my boy is alive out there. Somewhere...
Skyrim.esm|02709B: What's the matter? You can't stand the sight of a strong Nord woman?
Skyrim.esm|02709C: When the Stormcloaks took control of Whiterun, it was a victory for all true Nords.
Skyrim.esm|02709D: There's nothing a man can do that I can't do better, whether it's serving drinks or slaying trolls.
Skyrim.esm|02709E: I miss the days when the Gray-Manes and Battle-Borns got along. Ever since the war started, we've been at each others' throats.
Skyrim.esm|02709F: The daughters of this land are just as strong and loyal as its men.
Skyrim.esm|0270A0: You wouldn't know it now, but the Gray-Manes and Battle-Borns were once fast friends.
Skyrim.esm|0270A5: Halt in the name of the Jarl!
Skyrim.esm|0270A6: How kind of you. And I've brought an army to give you a going away parade.
Skyrim.esm|0270A7: If you two are finished with the touching homecoming, there's a city in chaos out there.
Skyrim.esm|0270AA: Vignar Gray-Mane! Your family was noticeably absent from the walls. Now I know why. Wouldn't a dagger in the back have sufficed?
Skyrim.esm|0270CD: Protect the Jarl with your lives!
Skyrim.esm|02718B: Indeed. Come, there is much to be done.
Skyrim.esm|0271DA: If they want my city, they're going to have to take it from me personally.
Skyrim.esm|0271F4: Stop! Enough! Stand down! The city is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0271FD: It's over! Everyone stop! I surrender... I surrender...
Skyrim.esm|0271FE: Somehow I doubt that will persuade them.
Skyrim.esm|02725E: Somewhere up north, in the ice fields. Said he found some old Dwemer artifact, but... well, that was years ago. Haven't heard from him since.
Skyrim.esm|027260: Oh no. I hope not. But even I haven't seen him in years, and we were close. Became obsessed with the Dwemer. Took off north saying he had found some old artifact. Haven't seen him since. Somewhere in the ice fields, if you want to try to find him.
Skyrim.esm|027262: Here you go. Try not to spill anything on them.
Skyrim.esm|027486: Know about them? Lad, you're looking at them.
Skyrim.esm|027487: They call themselves the "Blood Horkers." They've been around as long as I can remember, but only recently focused on our ships. A dastardly group, but they're more than simple brigands. Shrewd. Clever. Not to be trifled with.
Skyrim.esm|02748F: You know him? He made my favorite dolly, but I can't find her. Are you sure you aren't a stranger?
Skyrim.esm|027490: Helgi. But father says I'm not supposed to talk to strangers. Are you a stranger?
Skyrim.esm|027491: She's mine! She's my reward. You can't have her!
Skyrim.esm|027492: The smoke woke me up. It was hot and I was scared, so I hid. Then it got cold and dark. I'm not scared anymore. But I'm lonely. Will you play with me?
Skyrim.esm|027493: Okay! Let's play hide and seek. You find me and I'll tell you. We have to wait for nighttime though. The other one is playing too, and she can't come out until then.
Skyrim.esm|027566: I certainly do. Since you went so far as seek out the Augur for advice, I thought you'd be more enthusiastic.
Skyrim.esm|027567: No one here does. The Synod seemed convinced it was somewhere in Skyrim. They inquired about the ruins of Mzulft, but that's all I remember. It sounded like they were heading there, though they were rather secretive about why. I suppose if you're intent on looking for the staff, there's a chance they might be in Mzulft yet. Just don't expect them to be cooperative.
Skyrim.esm|02756E: A most impressive attitude. Keep this up, and you'll do quite well for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|02756F: Really? And what might that be?
Skyrim.esm|027570: Something as specific and ancient as the Staff of Magnus... I'm not sure we'd ever find something like that... I seem to recall Mirabelle mentioning the staff somewhat recently. Why don't you see if she can tell you anything? I'm quite pleased with your progress, you know. You've certainly proven yourself to be more than a mere Apprentice. Well done. This circlet once proved invaluable to me. I hope it can be of use to you now.
Skyrim.esm|027571: The Augur? Just what have you gotten yourself involved in? Whatever is going on, whatever you're up to... Be very careful.
Skyrim.esm|027574: I see. Well yes, I suppose I did mention it, though I'm not sure what he expects me to tell you. I only brought it to his attention a few months back when the Synod showed up here looking for it. They were apparently under the impression we were keeping it in a closet somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|027575: And how do you know this?
Skyrim.esm|027576: The "Eye of Magnus"? I can appreciate that this... thing, this Orb... It's very impressive. Very unique, and definitely worth studying. But let's not jump to any conclusions, or assign it importance beyond what we're certain of.
Skyrim.esm|027577: Well now that's an odd question. Why in the world would you be asking?
Skyrim.esm|027578: Mages based out of Cyrodiil. They fancy themselves the Imperial Authority on magic these last few hundred years. My understanding is that all they really do is make noise in an attempt to curry favor from the Emperor. Lots of politics, little magic. I was quite surprised to find them on our doorstep. They seemed amiable enough, but their line of questioning made me... uneasy. It became clear they're trying to hoard powerful artifacts, looking to consolidate power.
Skyrim.esm|027579: Did you really? And he specifically mentioned the Staff of Magnus? I... I'm impressed with your initiative. Of course, someone will need to follow up on this.
Skyrim.esm|02757A: I'm sorry, what? Well... I'd certainly love to have such a powerful staff, but I'm not really sure that any of us need it.
Skyrim.esm|02757B: Well, it's said to be very powerful. Has the capacity to store an incredible amount of magical power, as the story goes. But it's more myth than anything at this point. I've no doubt that it actually exists, but no one has seen it in what, decades? Longer? I'm not sure. The only time I've heard it mentioned was when those Synod characters showed up some months ago looking for it.
Skyrim.esm|027923: Here you go. This should cover it.
Skyrim.esm|027926: I think you'll find this payment to be more than fair.
Skyrim.esm|027928: Well, well. I was looking for this little beauty. If you happen across any other unusual trinkets like this, be sure and bring them to me. I promise it'll be worth the effort.
Skyrim.esm|027F49: Okay, okay! All I know is that Idolaf made some effort to find out what happened to him, but was told to stop asking questions. He got some letter from the Legion and has left it alone since then. I'll prove it to you, all right? I'll get you the letter. Just... meet me at the statue of Talos, and don't tell anyone about this.
Skyrim.esm|027F4A: I'll round up what men I can, and meet you outside Northwatch Keep. We'll get in and out as fast as possible. I'll see you there.
Skyrim.esm|027F4B: No? So you don't know anything about my brother? Then what are you doing here?
Skyrim.esm|027F4E: What makes you think I know anything? Everyone knows he's dead and gone!
Skyrim.esm|027F50: Thorald... Thorald Gray-Mane? Fighting for the Stormcloaks, disappeared and believed dead? You really don't know anything about this, do you?
Skyrim.esm|027F51: I used some contacts in the Legion, asked some questions. Was told I should mind my own business.
Skyrim.esm|027F52: You what? Oh no. No, no, no. This can't be happening. Please, you can't tell anyone. It must remain a secret! I'll do whatever you want.
Skyrim.esm|027F53: That's privileged information. Now move along.
Skyrim.esm|027F56: Last I'd heard, he'd been carted off to Northwatch Keep. Ever heard of it? If you go in there, you don't come out. Ever. Here, see for yourself. This is the last word I received. I'm telling you, he's gone for good.
Skyrim.esm|027F57: A likely story. They won't even do their own dirty work now, eh? Well, you won't get the better of me! Now tell me where my brother is!
Skyrim.esm|027F58: Who are you? What are you doing here? Are you working with the Battle-Borns? Speak now before I put you in the ground!
Skyrim.esm|027F59: I'm here! I knew I'd find you!
Skyrim.esm|027F5A: Because they've got Thorald locked away somewhere, and if they find me here I'll disappear too! I am not going to let that happen!
Skyrim.esm|027F5B: There's no time. We've got to get you out of here! Come on, let's go!
Skyrim.esm|027F5E: I can't do this all on my own. Are you... are you offering to help me?
Skyrim.esm|027F5F: Do you have news? Did you find something?
Skyrim.esm|027F60: Please, anything you can do to help find my Thorald...
Skyrim.esm|027F61: What's there to say? You go against the Empire, you wind up dead. Thorald got what was coming to him. Best not to ask too many questions about it. People might get the wrong idea about you.
Skyrim.esm|027F62: Between the two of us, his family is better off thinking he's dead. Thorald and I have history, all right? We used to be friends. So I looked into it when he disappeared. I found out the Thalmor have him. And they don't let anyone go. So I've kept my mouth shut. Like I said, his family is better off believing he's gone, because he's never coming back.
Skyrim.esm|027F63: He fought on the wrong side, and he paid for it. That's all I have to say.
Skyrim.esm|027F64: Oh come on, leave it alone. He's gone. There's no use in talking about it anymore. I wish everyone could just get past that and get on with their lives.
Skyrim.esm|027F65: It's been a while now. I miss him, but it's clear he's not coming back. He knew what he was getting into. I don't... I don't blame anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0283A4: Tell me, Vignar. Was all this worth it? How many of those corpses lining our streets wear the faces of men who once called you friend? What about their families?
Skyrim.esm|0283A5: Enough! That's enough. I surrender... I surrender. Peace! Everyone stand down. That's an order. Stand down!
Skyrim.esm|0283A8: A convenient position to hold now. But mark my words, old man, in the days to come, Ulfric will spread his rebellion thin. And what then? We need the Empire, as much as it needs us. We Nords are the Empire! Our blood built it. Our blood sustains it! You of all people should know that.
Skyrim.esm|0283A9: If this was my Empire, I'd be able to worship whoever I damned well pleased. You wish to see an Empire without Talos? Without its soul? We should be fighting those witch-elves, not bending knee to them. The Emperor is nothing more than a puppet of the Thalmor. Skyrim needs a High King who will fight for her, and Whiterun needs a Jarl who will do the same.
Skyrim.esm|0283AA: You think this is personal? The Empire has no place in Skyrim... not any more. And you? You have no place in Whiterun anymore.
Skyrim.esm|028471: These Stormcloaks are ridiculous. They won't shop here because they think I'll sell them poisoned tonics. I should just go back to Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|028472: You look rather pale. Could be Ataxia. It's quite a problem back home in Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|028473: Maybe a hardy Nord like you doesn't need a cure-all. Hmm? What about an invisibility potion?
Skyrim.esm|028475: I have several elixirs for sale.
Skyrim.esm|02847D: I enjoy this work well enough, but I'm ready to retire. I've been thinking of selling the inn to Ysolda. Anyway, what do you need?
Skyrim.esm|02847F: Hungry, tired, or just plain thirsty?
Skyrim.esm|028480: I'm just out and about, love. The Bannered Mare can do without me for a bit, I suspect.
Skyrim.esm|028481: I don't get much time time off from the tavern, but I enjoy it when I do.
Skyrim.esm|028483: I'll get back to the Bannered Mare soon enough. Just need to stretch my legs a bit...
Skyrim.esm|028484: If it's work you're looking for, Hulda's the one to talk to.
Skyrim.esm|028485: If you need more food or drink, speak to Hulda.
Skyrim.esm|028486: Mind your manners and pay your bills, and you'll always be welcome.
Skyrim.esm|028487: There was a great battle here in Whiterun. It was quite thrilling! I must compose a song about it someday.
Skyrim.esm|02848A: I'm a bard by trade. Perhaps you've heard of me?
Skyrim.esm|02848B: I don't have much of a reputation in Skyrim yet, but I'm working on that, one sweet lady at a time. Haha!
Skyrim.esm|02848C: No doubt they still tell tales of Mikael and his sweet songs across Cyrodiil, where last I traveled.
Skyrim.esm|02848D: I enjoy steady patronage in the Bannered Mare. I do hope you'll watch me perform.
Skyrim.esm|02848F: Welcome to Belethor's General Goods. Welcome, indeed.
Skyrim.esm|028491: I know what you're thinking: "This Belethor, how could he part with such magnificent treasures?"
Skyrim.esm|028492: Imperials, Stormcloaks... I don't care who's in charge around here. I just want to make money!
Skyrim.esm|028493: I'd even buy one of your relatives, if you're looking to sell. Ha ha ha ha. That's a little joke.
Skyrim.esm|028494: You just let me know what you need. I am at your beck and call.
Skyrim.esm|028497: Want to hear a little Nord wisdom? You don't really know a woman 'til you've had a strong drink and a fistfight with her.
Skyrim.esm|028499: You look like maybe you need a new weapon. Something big, perhaps? An axe maybe? Or a greatsword?
Skyrim.esm|02849A: We've got small weapons as well as big. Maces, daggers, that sort of thing.
Skyrim.esm|02849B: Most of our weapons were crafted by Adrianne Avenicci. My woman's an artist, is what she is.
Skyrim.esm|02849C: Crush, clobber, slash or slice. We've got a weapon for any occasion.
Skyrim.esm|0284A0: Need something? Talk to my brother, Elrindir.
Skyrim.esm|0284A3: Do you hunt? The plains outside Whiterun are ripe with game.
Skyrim.esm|0284A8: You should try the venison. It's delicious.
Skyrim.esm|0284AA: Oh! You startled me. I wasn't expecting company out here.
Skyrim.esm|0284AB: Ah, hello my Nord friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Skyrim.esm|0284E0: Potema herself? Please tell me you stopped them.
Skyrim.esm|0284E4: Ah Potema. Former queen of Solitude and one of the most dangerous necromancers in recorded history. She was responsible for the Empire's near collapse almost five hundred years ago. I believe I have a book about her...
Skyrim.esm|0284EF: Any resident of Solitude can tell you, she was the deadliest person ever to come out of Skyrim. Queen of Solitude, granddaughter of Emperor Uriel Septim II, five hundred years ago. Her fight for succession nearly tore the empire apart. After twenty years of fighting, she had raised armies of the dead, led by vampire generals. If she returns to power, nobody could stop her.
Skyrim.esm|0284F0: A curious figure. Unrepentantly evil and nasty, of course, but also astonishingly brilliant, and obviously quite a necromancer at her end. She was always so close to being Empress, but despite her machinations, it was never to be hers. Ironically, if she were alive today, she'd be the only living member of the Septim bloodline. By all rights, she would now be Empress.
Skyrim.esm|0284F7: The College of Winterhold is an amazing sight. I've never set foot on the grounds, but always wanted to.
Skyrim.esm|0284F8: There is an unnatural chill in the air. Some evil power is at work in this place.
Skyrim.esm|0284FE: I do not envy those creatures that make a home in such cold, dark places as this.
Skyrim.esm|028504: Careful. Our footsteps will echo in every chamber and tunnel.
Skyrim.esm|028505: By the Nine, I'd pay good money for a pint of ale to warm my bones!
Skyrim.esm|028506: Curse this chill! I should have dressed more warmly.
Skyrim.esm|028507: If this cold numbs my fingers any worse, I'll scarcely be able to hold a weapon.
Skyrim.esm|028509: Gods, I'd hate to lose my footing in a place like this. A fall on this stony floor will break bones.
Skyrim.esm|02850D: Take extra care you don't hit your head on a low-hanging bit of stone.
Skyrim.esm|02850F: Mind your step. I think I spied a patch of ice on the floor.
Skyrim.esm|028545: Don't go stopping for a nap. If you fall asleep in this place, you'll never wake up.
Skyrim.esm|028547: Are you sure you can find your way? It would be easy to get lost in here.
Skyrim.esm|028549: This cold is unbearable. It's all I can do not to shiver until my teeth rattle.
Skyrim.esm|028552: Would it be foolish to stop for a while and light a fire? I suppose it would.
Skyrim.esm|028555: Gods willing, we'll find a chest full of gold in one of these chambers.
Skyrim.esm|02856E: At times like this, I truly appreciate the joys of a warm fire and a stiff drink.
Skyrim.esm|028570: There are strange echoes in these caves. Keep you eyes open.
Skyrim.esm|028573: If a man can't feel at home in a freezing cave, then he isn't truly a Nord.
Skyrim.esm|028579: It seems warmer down here than it was outside.
Skyrim.esm|02857B: I have to admit, I admire your courage. Most folk in Skyrim wouldn't go near a place like this.
Skyrim.esm|02857E: When I was a boy, my friends and I used to play "seek the wumpus" in caves much like this.
Skyrim.esm|02857F: In a mine, the poor man labors to find precious stones that only the rich man can afford.
Skyrim.esm|028580: I wonder how many poor souls have lost their lives working these tunnels.
Skyrim.esm|028581: These earthen passages do not agree with me. I already miss the wind and sky.
Skyrim.esm|028582: The fewer of these mines we venture into, the happier I'll be.
Skyrim.esm|028583: The cloying air in this place is almost unbearable.
Skyrim.esm|028585: It is strange to think that these chambers were once home to the ancient Nords.
Skyrim.esm|028586: My nose and mouth are so full of dust, I can hardly breath.
Skyrim.esm|028587: Wouldn't it be something if we found a vein of gold?
Skyrim.esm|028588: It would be easy for someone -- or something -- to sneak up on us down here.
Skyrim.esm|028589: Are you sure you wouldn't rather explore, say, a nice tavern?
Skyrim.esm|02858A: Let's try not to make too much noise. I've heard that cave-ins can start that way.
Skyrim.esm|02858B: I wonder what they were mining down here.
Skyrim.esm|02858C: Let's hope the miners didn't unearth anything besides precious stones, if you take my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|02858D: Not for all the gold in Skyrim would I spend my life working in these tunnels.
Skyrim.esm|02858E: I've heard that miners sometimes die from poisonous gases trapped beneath the ground.
Skyrim.esm|02858F: I'm already looking forward to getting back above ground.
Skyrim.esm|028590: You know what I miss the most right now? Fresh air.
Skyrim.esm|028591: It takes uncommon strength to work the mines in Skyrim. The earth here is cold and hard, like the heart of a Nord.
Skyrim.esm|028592: There's an old maid's tale that says our ancestors dug these mines with their bare hands.
Skyrim.esm|028593: I've half a mind to grab a pickaxe and start digging. Never know what you might find.
Skyrim.esm|028707: You've done a great thing, today. It doesn't matter who you support in the war; Potema would have been a blight on the land for both sides. Without you this would have been a disaster. I should have paid more heed to Varnius' warnings.  I won't make that mistake again.
Skyrim.esm|0288DB: Your not winnin' points with me with that attitude.
Skyrim.esm|0288DC: When you find your nerves, then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0288DD: Fine, but you're not gettin' any wealthier by weaslin' out of everythin' I offer you.
Skyrim.esm|0288DE: You're not going to get richer with that attitude.
Skyrim.esm|0288DF: You need to bloody well get your priorities straight if you want to make it around here.
Skyrim.esm|0288E0: Too bad, looks like I need to find someone else to take care of business.
Skyrim.esm|0288E1: If you think you'll make your fortune kickin' around the Flagon all day, that's where your wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0288E2: And here I thought you were a master criminal. Guess I was wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0288E4: Finally, someone with backbone. Here you go.
Skyrim.esm|0288E5: Now that's what I like to hear. Off you go.
Skyrim.esm|0288E6: Nice. Here, this is what you'll need to know.
Skyrim.esm|0288E7: Keep this up and you'll be rich enough retire before you know it. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0288E8: Just remember the rules... keep it clean and keep it quiet. Here's the details.
Skyrim.esm|0288E9: Here's everythin' you need. You get caught, and we've never heard of you.
Skyrim.esm|0288EA: Just keep your eyes open and your mouth shut and you'll do fine. Let me give you the details.
Skyrim.esm|0288EB: Good job. Now here's your part of the deal.
Skyrim.esm|0288EE: And here's the spoils. Plenty more where this came from!
Skyrim.esm|0288EF: I can pat you on the back all day, but coin's coin, right?
Skyrim.esm|0288F0: This kind of work suits you... and is going to end up makin' you rich.
Skyrim.esm|0288F1: Couldn't have done it better myself. Here's your coin.
Skyrim.esm|0288F2: If you don't watch it, I may actually start to respect you. Job well done.
Skyrim.esm|0288F3: What's the matter with you? We said keep it clean! Well, you can just forget about your payment.
Skyrim.esm|0288F4: Sure you finished, but you killed someone to do it. Next time listen to the rules if you want to get paid.
Skyrim.esm|0288F5: You needed to do this clean and quiet, and you failed. Now we need to use your payment to cover your mistake.
Skyrim.esm|0288F6: I hope you weren't expectin' to get paid for this blundered job. Next time, the mark stays alive... you hear me?
Skyrim.esm|0288F7: This ain't the Dark Brotherhood. Next time, you keep the job clean and you'll get your coin.
Skyrim.esm|0288F8: Hey, you approached me. You want to play coward, that's your problem.
Skyrim.esm|0288F9: Look, I don't like wasting my time. Come back when you decide to get serious.
Skyrim.esm|0288FA: Not interested? Fine. Then let me offer it to someone with a little more backbone.
Skyrim.esm|0288FB: You need to start pulling your own weight around here, otherwise I might "forget" to offer you any more jobs.
Skyrim.esm|0288FC: Fine. I'll just keep all this coin for myself then.
Skyrim.esm|0288FD: Look, if you just want to sit around the Flagon all day getting drunk, that's your business. Just quit wasting my time.
Skyrim.esm|0288FE: The last time I checked, gold coins aren't falling from the heavens. You better take some jobs if you want to earn your share.
Skyrim.esm|0288FF: Keep turning down jobs like this and the Guild will begin to lose respect for you.
Skyrim.esm|028900: Good. Here's what you need to do.
Skyrim.esm|028901: Fine. Here's everything you need to know.
Skyrim.esm|028902: All right. Take this and get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|028903: Fine. Just keep it clean. Here you go.
Skyrim.esm|028904: Good, just remember the order: do job first, get drunk second. Now take this and go.
Skyrim.esm|028905: Here you go. Hope you don't muck it up.
Skyrim.esm|028906: Here. Just don't make me look like an ass in front of the Guild and mess it up.
Skyrim.esm|028907: Whatever, just get it done. Here's the job.
Skyrim.esm|028908: Oh, you're back? I suppose you'll be wanting your pay then.
Skyrim.esm|028909: You did the job and you did it clean; that's all we ever ask. Enjoy the gold.
Skyrim.esm|02890A: Didn't muck it up I hear. Good. Here's your share.
Skyrim.esm|02890B: I suppose you want your share now? Here you go.
Skyrim.esm|02890C: And with no strings attached. Not bad. Here, got some coin for you.
Skyrim.esm|02890D: Amazing what people leave laying around for us to take, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|02890E: Fine. I guess you'll need your coin then.
Skyrim.esm|02890F: Here's your gold. Try not to spend it all in one place.
Skyrim.esm|028910: Are you out of your mind? We said no bloodshed! Well, guess what. You can just forget about your gold.
Skyrim.esm|028911: I thought it was crystal clear... no one dies on these jobs. Remember that next time you want to get paid.
Skyrim.esm|028912: You an assassin or a thief? Next time, keep your blade sheathed if you want to make any gold.
Skyrim.esm|028913: You drew blood, so you aren't getting paid. That's the rules. You don't like it, take it up with Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|028914: You idiot! We have to cover your mess up and that costs us gold... which means you don't get any.
Skyrim.esm|028A01: I need some more supplies. Can you help out with that?
Skyrim.esm|028A02: Some other time? That's not going to help me much, now is it?
Skyrim.esm|028A04: If you're feeling adventurous, I'm always looking for someone to procure valuable books from some more... dangerous locations.
Skyrim.esm|028A05: If you can bring me three more, that would be helpful.
Skyrim.esm|028A06: Well all right. Here, I scribbled down the last place I'd heard about. Check it out, but be careful.
Skyrim.esm|028A07: Oh, yes. Thank you. I'll get to work on this right away.
Skyrim.esm|028A09: Sure is. I've got another client -- wants me to do some enchanting. You can do the pick-up for me, if you want.
Skyrim.esm|028A0A: In fact, there is. I need a soul gem filled, if you're up to it.
Skyrim.esm|028A0C: Excellent. Thank you, I'll see that this gets put to good use.
Skyrim.esm|028A0D: Yes, a Grand one. Soon, please.
Skyrim.esm|028A0E: Here, take this Grand Soul Gem, fill it for me, and bring it right back. I'll be waiting.
Skyrim.esm|028A10: Yes, yes I did. I've already sent instructions, you just need to make sure this arrives in one piece.
Skyrim.esm|028A11: I'm running low on Grand Soul Gems.
Skyrim.esm|028A12: All right. Be quick about it, will you? This is a good way for the College to bring in some money. Here's the name and location of the client.
Skyrim.esm|028A58: No! Not again! I shall not be denied again!
Skyrim.esm|028A5A: You must be the one Falk spoke so highly of. Welcome.
Skyrim.esm|028C5F: I'll admit, even I'm having a hard time disagreeing with you...
Skyrim.esm|028C60: What are you waiting for? My husband is out there somewhere with that lunatic. Find them! Do what you've been tasked to do!
Skyrim.esm|028C62: Hush, Veezara. You were very brave. Astrid may well be dead if not for you.
Skyrim.esm|028C65: Look, we've got to deal with this situation. You've got to deal with this situation.
Skyrim.esm|028C6B: Achh... Thank you, dear. You are most kind. The jester's cut feels as bad as it looks, I'm afraid.
Skyrim.esm|028C7D: It's all up to you now... Listener.
Skyrim.esm|028C7E: Bested by a fool. Who's the fool now, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|028C7F: She's right. I'll be forever in your debt, dearest brother. Now be quiet. Just... just rest.
Skyrim.esm|028C80: I want you to find that miserable little fool and end his life! But first... find my husband. Make sure he's all right. After the attack, Arnbjorn flew into a rage. When Cicero left... Arnbjorn went after him. They disappeared into the wild. Search Cicero's room. Maybe there's something in there that sheds some light on where he might have gone. Let me know the minute you find something. I've got to see to Veezara, and calm everyone down.
Skyrim.esm|028C81: Dammit, this never should have happened! We knew better. We knew better, and still we let our guards down. Agh!
Skyrim.esm|028C82: Just try to relax, Veezara. Let the elixir do its work. You'll feel better, shortly.
Skyrim.esm|028D4F: I know that he still worships Talos. That he hates the Thalmor almost as much as the Stormcloaks do. That he worries about being chased from Whiterun. That he... that I'm...  that I don't have the same mother as my brother and sister.
Skyrim.esm|028D50: Regrettably, I cannot reach your plane so directly. But I forgive you for not knowing who I am. Few hear my whispers anymore. I am Mephala, the Lady of Whispers. I tug at the web of connections between mortals. Love, hatred, loyalty, betrayal. The boy was good at sussing out secrets. You, I expect to take a more active role.
Skyrim.esm|028D79: The castle is old. Lots of places nobody's been in a long while. Places where you can overhear things. See things. And the Whispering Lady.
Skyrim.esm|028D7D: She won't tell me her name. I've gotten good at listening to keyholes. At the door in the basement, I hear her talking to me. I thought I was caught, but she started telling me even more secrets. But I can't open the door.
Skyrim.esm|028D7E: In the basement. Trust me, you'll see it. I bet she'll talk to you too.
Skyrim.esm|028D7F: First, you must open this door. A piece of my power has been locked away behind it, and even my eyes cannot see past the seals. I'd much rather it be in the hands of an ambitious and talented person such as yourself.
Skyrim.esm|028DA3: What do you think? Are we going to let Ulfric dictate terms to us before the negotiations even start?
Skyrim.esm|028DA4: By Ysmir's beard, the nerve of those Imperial bastards, eh? To think that we would sit down with that... Thalmor bitch. I say she walks or we walk.
Skyrim.esm|028DD0: Hagraven or horker, it matters not. We've got the arrows to fell any game.
Skyrim.esm|028DD2: So what can I get you?
Skyrim.esm|028DD3: Look at you! Betcha think you're somethin', huh?
Skyrim.esm|028E27: All right, you convinced me. Doubt I'd be much good to you, anyway. The little fop cut me pretty deep. But I slashed him good. Pretty sure I severed an artery. Don't know what you're going to find in there... but you can probably just follow the blood.
Skyrim.esm|028E29: Oh, it's ancient. Apparently one of the first Sanctuaries in Skyrim. It hasn't been used by the Brotherhood in close to a hundred years. If you say Cicero has gone there, it stands to reason that he's familiar with the place. That means you'll be fighting him on his own ground. Tread carefully.
Skyrim.esm|028E2A: In there! Through the door. Some old Sanctuary, by the looks of it. I would have followed him, but I don't know the phrase.
Skyrim.esm|028E2B: Good hunting. I'll see you back at the Sanctuary.
Skyrim.esm|028E2C: Should have figured Astrid would send you.
Skyrim.esm|028E2D: Look, the Dark Brotherhood is a family. This Sanctuary is a family. And, we've always welcomed those... shunned by society. Werewolves, wizards, eternal ten-year-old vampires... what does it matter? In truth, I've rarely met a lunatic I haven't liked. Cicero's problem isn't his madness. It's an adherence to an ancient, outmoded way of life. The Night Mother's ways... simply are not our ways. He just couldn't accept that. And now he'll have to pay the price.
Skyrim.esm|028E2E: What gave it away? Ha ha ha ha ha. Yeah, got to admit that little jester is good with that butter knife. But don't worry, I gave as good as I got.
Skyrim.esm|028E71: Good. I'm spread thin. I could use the help.
Skyrim.esm|028E72: We're taking the city. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Skyrim.esm|028EDE: Good. If you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Now get going.
Skyrim.esm|02953B: She began to spend a lot of time with Alva. Yet just a week before, she despised her. In fact, the night she disappeared, she was supposed to meet Alva. Alva told me later that she never showed up. I never got to tell her goodbye.
Skyrim.esm|02953E: You found me! Laelette was trying to find me, too, but I'm glad you found me first. Laelette was told to burn mommy and me, but she didn't want to. She wanted to play with me forever and ever. She kissed me on the neck, and I got so cold that the fire didn't even hurt. Laelette thought she could take me and keep me, but she can't. I'm all burned up. I'm tired. I'm going to sleep for a while now.
Skyrim.esm|029540: Now there's a mystery. She ran away when the war started. Her husband, Thonnir, was devastated. No note or nothin'. I'm pretty sure she up and joined the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|029541: Laelette? I thought she left to join the Stormcloaks. Ah! My poor Laelette!
Skyrim.esm|029726: Very well. Here. Take this back to Brynjolf and tell him he'll have no more trouble from me.
Skyrim.esm|02972C: My wife died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|02972D: My husband died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|02972E: My father died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|02972F: My mother died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|029730: My daughter died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|029731: My son died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|029732: My sister died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|029733: My brother died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|029734: My aunt died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|029735: My uncle died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|029736: My niece died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|029737: My nephew died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|029738: My cousin died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|029739: My cousin died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|02973A: My kinsman died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|02973B: My kinswoman died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|02973C: My friend died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|02973D: My friend died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|02973E: My girlfriend died. Things won't be the same without her.
Skyrim.esm|02973F: My boyfriend died. Things won't be the same without him.
Skyrim.esm|029923: All right! I get it. I'll pay on time from now on. Just don't smash anything else. Here, take your gold and leave me in peace.
Skyrim.esm|029924: Not Lady Dibella? No, please! I can't lose her! I get the message. Here, take your gold. I hope you choke on it!
Skyrim.esm|029946: How I envy you. I can only imagine the excitement of seeing history through the eyes of another! Sadly, I am resigned to just reading of its wonders through my research of the Skull.
Skyrim.esm|02994E: It... it worked. Mara be praised! You vanished after drinking the Torpor and materialized on the other side. I have never seen anything quite like it.
Skyrim.esm|029952: I thought I was clear regarding the dangers that awaited you within the Dreamstride. However, this hardly matters any longer as you appear to have exited unscathed.
Skyrim.esm|029954: After what you just experienced, you are not amazed? I am at a loss for words.
Skyrim.esm|029A91: We used to think the pirates had an army of battlemages at their side. As it turns out, it was just one. Haldyn. I've since learned more about him. The most dangerous sort of man -- quick to anger, but patient in exacting vengeance. He also has a fearsome control of Destruction magic. I wish you the best of luck.
Skyrim.esm|029A92: A very powerful family in Windhelm. They run a private shipping operation, and would love to have the docks to themselves. Wouldn't surprise me if they were in league with the pirates, somehow.
Skyrim.esm|029A94: Not yet, no. But perhaps an intrepid person such as yourself could help with that? They have a Dark Elf who oversees their operations. Suvaris Atheron. She's meticulous to a fault, and keeps a logbook with every transaction she makes. If it were to somehow end up in my hands, I wouldn't argue.
Skyrim.esm|029A96: I know this place looks horrible -- please don't hold it against the Company.
Skyrim.esm|029A98: If you get me the logbook, I'll have proof of Shatter-Shield's treachery.
Skyrim.esm|029A99: Let me know when you've learned more about the pirates.
Skyrim.esm|029A9A: She's probably the one you ought to talk to.
Skyrim.esm|029A9B: We're so grateful for your help.
Skyrim.esm|029AA2: You think Alva... but that means... Ye gods! You think Alva is a vampire?
Skyrim.esm|029B01: I don't know much. Only the old tales about the ancient heroes who would use the Voice to defeat the enemies of Skyrim. They say the emperors used to be Dragonborn, but that all ended when Martin died during the Oblivion Crisis. The Greybeards can tell you much more than I can. They're the masters of the Way of the Voice and taught Tiber Septim himself.
Skyrim.esm|029B77: The only thing I remember is the old story of Tiber Septim being called Dragonborn by the Nords, before they switched sides and joined the Empire. No doubt those Greybeards up at High Hrothgar will talk your ear off about it.
Skyrim.esm|029C0B: They live in seclusion on the slopes of the Throat of the World, the great mountain of Skyrim. They are masters of the Way of the Voice. If you really are Dragonborn, they're the ones who can teach you how to use your power.
Skyrim.esm|029C0D: Near as I can tell, a bunch of ancient Nord hermits who live on top of a mountain. Never heard of them talking to anybody before. If they really do want to talk to you, you'd better go find out what they want. You never know.
Skyrim.esm|029C17: In the old tales, Nord heroes would use the power of their Voice to Shout down the gates of cities and strike down their enemies. But only the Greybeards study the Way of the Voice any more. If you can Shout... you need to talk to them.
Skyrim.esm|029C24: Old Nord folk tales always talk about Shouting their enemies to pieces. I always thought it was just a myth. If you think you might be able to do that kind of thing, the Legion could certainly use your help. Go talk to the Greybeards, see what they can tell you.
Skyrim.esm|029CE5: I'm just the housecarl. If you need something, you should speak to Kraldar.
Skyrim.esm|029CE6: We may not have as much to offer as Whiterun or Solitude, but we'll do what we can to make your stay a pleasant one.
Skyrim.esm|029D04: Winterhold doesn't offer much, but we like living here. Been here all our lives.
Skyrim.esm|029D05: If you're here for the College, don't let the folks in town bother you. Just try and keep to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|029D06: The mages at the College keep to themselves for the most part, so we don't see much of them.
Skyrim.esm|029D07: Turns out who runs Winterhold doesn't change much. Can't say I'm surprised.
Skyrim.esm|029D08: If you've business with the College, you're welcome to stay here. It's where most of our business comes from, in fact.
Skyrim.esm|029D09: Unlike some folks here in town, we have nothing against the College or the people that come and go.
Skyrim.esm|029D0A: Don't worry about Nelacar. He has a permanent room here at the inn, but he keeps to himself and he doesn't cause any trouble.
Skyrim.esm|029D0B: Let me know if you need anything. We're here to provide.
Skyrim.esm|029D0C: Without us looking out for Winterhold, it's doomed. Simply doomed.
Skyrim.esm|029D0D: Korir will be the first to tell you that if it weren't for that college, we'd all be better off. I agree with him.
Skyrim.esm|029DCC: Winterhold might yet thrive if that college had never been built.
Skyrim.esm|029DDD: We do the best we can, even though we have next to nothing. But we Nords don't give up, not ever.
Skyrim.esm|029E0F: Gaius Maro still lives. I am surprised. I had assumed you were more... competent.
Skyrim.esm|029EF9: Assur is going to grow up to be a great man, because he'll have lived through such hardship.
Skyrim.esm|029EFF: Driven out of my own home. After all this, I can't believe it.
Skyrim.esm|029F05: You know Winterhold used to be the seat of power in Skyrim? Now it's a shell.
Skyrim.esm|029F08: Few will admit it, but we know the truth about the Great Collapse.
Skyrim.esm|029F09: Those cursed mages... It's their fault Winterhold is gone.
Skyrim.esm|029F0B: Azura has seen your coming, traveler. It was not curiosity, but fate, that has led you here.
Skyrim.esm|029F12: Azura has given me the gift of foresight. I had a vision of you walking up the steps to this altar long before you were born. You have been chosen to be her champion. I know it is unexpected, but do not worry. It will all unfold as she has predicted.
Skyrim.esm|029F17: The College is the worst thing that's ever happened to Winterhold, maybe to Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|029F40: Empire's in charge now, huh? Only a matter of time before we start getting bothered by them Thalmoricars... err, Justimors... Y'know, them elves.
Skyrim.esm|029F52: I'm just trying to have a quiet drink here, all right?
Skyrim.esm|029F53: It's not like there's anything to do here but drink anyway.
Skyrim.esm|029F55: You ask me, not enough of Winterhold fell off into the sea.
Skyrim.esm|029F57: Nothing to do here but drink till you forget you got nothing to do.
Skyrim.esm|029FE9: If you're looking for Ranmir, he's either passed out in bed, or working on winding up that way.
Skyrim.esm|029FFE: My brother's a good-for-nothing drunk. We'd have lost the house by now if it were up to him.
Skyrim.esm|029FFF: Just because we live in the middle of nowhere doesn't give Ranmir the right to drink himself into oblivion.
Skyrim.esm|02A000: Ranmir has no problem drinking himself under the table, so I have to be the one to make ends meet.
Skyrim.esm|02A001: I'll sell just about anything, if it's worth my time. Keep that in mind, will you?
Skyrim.esm|02A005: If you're here for sight-seeing, well then you've seen the sights. Might as well head somewhere warmer.
Skyrim.esm|02A006: Don't worry about those Stormcloaks. You're safe from them here. As if they'd want the place anyway.
Skyrim.esm|02A167: Keep clear of the College, and keep yourself out of trouble.
Skyrim.esm|02A185: Hard to believe I ever complained about Riften being cold...
Skyrim.esm|02A2EC: Take care if you're venturing too far from town. That's a bitter wind coming off the Sea of Ghosts.
Skyrim.esm|02A31B: Winterhold belongs to Skyrim, not the Imperials, or the Mages.
Skyrim.esm|02A37C: By order of the Jarl, stop right there!
Skyrim.esm|02A37D: You have committed crimes against Skyrim and her people. What say you in your defense?
Skyrim.esm|02A380: You're the one who conspired with the Forsworn against Markarth! You're coming with me, murderer!
Skyrim.esm|02A3EE: Don't take too long figuring it out. I've got a war to run here.
Skyrim.esm|02A3EF: Well, soldier? Are you up to the task?
Skyrim.esm|02A3F0: I knew I could count on you, soldier.
Skyrim.esm|02A46B: That's it. Don't say I didn't warn you.
Skyrim.esm|02A46C: This is your last warning. Get away from there, or else.
Skyrim.esm|02A46D: You're trespassing here. You'd better clear out.
Skyrim.esm|02A46E: You should have listened. Guards! Help! Trespasser!
Skyrim.esm|02A470: I'm not going to warn you again. Get out, or I'll call the guards!
Skyrim.esm|02A472: It's time for you to leave, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|02A473: You, of all people. Get out!
Skyrim.esm|02A475: Can't a man have a bit of privacy? Get out of here!
Skyrim.esm|02A476: Can't a woman get a moment to herself? Leave me!
Skyrim.esm|02A5A8: Just stay out of my way.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E2: Good. I'll be waiting right here.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E3: I'm tired, I haven't slept well in weeks. You kill the giant, bring me the hammer. We go back to Largashbur, I tell everyone I did it. You keep your mouth shut, and I pay you. Handsomely.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E4: Excellent work. You have a true fighting spirit. Did that better than I could have. Of course, I can't let you leave here alive. Wouldn't want anyone finding out.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E5: Yamarz has fallen. It is now up to you to return the giant's hammer to Atub. The tribe may yet survive, but only if you are willing to do what is right.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E6: Yamarz has revealed himself as a coward and a schemer, and has earned the fate of all who follow that path. The tribe may yet survive, but only if you are willing to do what is right. Take the giant's hammer to Atub, and tell her what has happened here.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E7: All right, I'll go kill this giant. Unless, of course, you'd like to make some extra gold.
Skyrim.esm|02A5E8: Fine, then wait here. This should only take a second.
Skyrim.esm|02A732: Stop, outlander. You must pay for your crimes in blood or gold.
Skyrim.esm|02A733: You've broken the Code of Malacath. Your choice - pay with your blood or your gold.
Skyrim.esm|02A734: Today is a good day to die.
Skyrim.esm|02A735: Fight well, and I'll make sure your corpse is not dishonored.
Skyrim.esm|02A736: We haven't forgotten your crimes, outlander.
Skyrim.esm|02A737: Huh. Okay. We'll test your mettle another day.
Skyrim.esm|02A738: Typical outlander weakling. But your gold will strengthen our clan.
Skyrim.esm|02A739: Then your poverty is my reward. Kill or be killed!
Skyrim.esm|02A785: Whiterun once again belongs to its rightful heirs. It's a glorious day for Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|02A786: For thirty years, I served the Legion as a commander. Fought in the Great War.
Skyrim.esm|02A787: We Gray-Manes have been feuding with the Battle-Borns for years. There's no more bitter enemy than an old friend.
Skyrim.esm|02A78D: Vignar and I have our hands full. It's no small task, running an entire hold.
Skyrim.esm|02A78E: I had a bad time a few years back, an' I turned to drink. But Vignar, he wouldn't let me stay weak.
Skyrim.esm|02A791: Olfrid, patron of the great Clan Battle-Born, a name I'm sure you know well.
Skyrim.esm|02A792: I'm as much a Nord as the next man, but I'm no fool. I don't live in the past, like those Gray-Manes.
Skyrim.esm|02A793: Eorlund Gray-Mane's a stubborn fool. Our friendship ended when he turned his back on the Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|02A794: It's a miracle these Stormcloaks haven't robbed us blind yet. I'm sure it's only a matter of time.
Skyrim.esm|02A795: Evgir Unslaad has come at last. The "Season Unending." War... without end.
Skyrim.esm|02A796: Can you believe those pathetic Gray-Manes still show their face in public?
Skyrim.esm|02A797: There was love between our families. For generations. Now, there is only enmity.
Skyrim.esm|02A798: Has the Empire forsaken us? How could Whiterun fall into the hands of these stinking half-wit Nords?
Skyrim.esm|02A799: Avulstein Gray-Mane and I were best friends as children. If I see that traitor again, I'll throw him in jail myself!
Skyrim.esm|02A79A: The cold, frightening truth is that the Empire represents progress. Anyone who fears progress is a coward.
Skyrim.esm|02A79B: The Stormcloaks are content to live in the past forever. No wonder the Provincials think we're all baseless savages.
Skyrim.esm|02A79D: You know what's wrong with Skyrim these days? Everyone is obsessed with death.
Skyrim.esm|02A79E: People have forgotten humor, poetry and romance. I love a good sword fight as much as the next man, but there's more to life.
Skyrim.esm|02A79F: It's my desire to enter the Bards College. My parents are hesitant, but I pray they let me attend.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A0: All I want it is to escape from this madness. And if Olfina can come with me, all the better. But I've probably said too much.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A1: Need something? Unlike the rest of my family, I've got work to do.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A2: This war's as stupid as our feud with Clan Gray-Mane.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A3: My brother Jon's got the right of it - join the Bards College and avoid this pointless war.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A4: My boy Lars has been getting beat senseless, and by a girl, no less!
Skyrim.esm|02A7A5: It's no fun getting pushed around all the time...
Skyrim.esm|02A7A7: My friend Olava? The seer? She says things will get better for me, once I start sticking up for myself.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A8: I can't fight. Not even a little bit. My family thinks I'm a little milk drinker.
Skyrim.esm|02A7A9: Come to Olava for a reading, did you? Sorry, pup, but I'm not quite in the mood.
Skyrim.esm|02A7AA: Oh, I tell the future, all right. When I feel up to the task, that is. But I'm afraid my energies haven't quite peaked yet today.
Skyrim.esm|02A7AB: Maybe some other time I'll reveal your destiny, hmm? Tea leaves, palm reading, crystal scrying... Oooh! Maybe trepanning? Ha ha ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|02A7AD: I hope you're looking at me like that because you want to buy something?
Skyrim.esm|02A7AF: I've got a hungry daughter to feed, so selling my produce is really the only thing I care about.
Skyrim.esm|02A7B1: I work with my mother, to sell fruits and vegetables. It's fun most days, but hard work.
Skyrim.esm|02A7B2: You'd be surprised how much work it is selling things at a stall. It's not all standing around and chatting.
Skyrim.esm|02A7B5: Did you know there are some fruits that actually grow better in the cold weather?
Skyrim.esm|02A7B6: You have come! You have come to hear the word of Talos!
Skyrim.esm|02A7B7: Praise Talos and all the Divines! Whiterun has been liberated by the true sons of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|02A7B8: There are those who would silence the Dragon's truth! But not I! Not Heimskr! His word will be known!
Skyrim.esm|02A7B9: The Imperials silence us because they fear us! They are cowards! Cowards and fools who have forgotten the truth!
Skyrim.esm|02A7BA: The truth, child of Talos, is that the Dragon's children have come! To purge the world in fire and righteousness!
Skyrim.esm|02A7BD: He has returned... Oh, how Talos has returned. Helgen has been purged in his light. Will Whiterun be next?
Skyrim.esm|02A7BF: I spend a lot of time at the market stalls so I can learn the merchant's trade.  I need more experience if I'm going to run an inn someday.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C0: Once I've made enough money trading with the Khajiit caravans, I'm going to buy the Bannered Mare from Hulda.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C1: We Nords are strong, and we're as tough as stone. But I'll bet you didn't know there's some of us with a good head for business, too.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C2: They say there's nothing a Nord woman can't do if she puts her mind to it.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C3: There's some company I certainly prefer over others. Have you met Mikael? The most amazing bard I've ever heard.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C6: You tried mercenary work? It might suit you.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C7: Sometimes I miss the soldier's life, but when I hold my daughter in my arms, I know I made the right choice.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C8: What're you lookin' at? I'm not afraid of you, ya know. Even if you are my elder.
Skyrim.esm|02A7C9: Boys, girls, dogs, elders - there's nobody I won't fight!
Skyrim.esm|02A7CA: Stupid Lars Battle-Born. I fight him almost every day, but it's not much of a fight, 'cause he don't fight back.
Skyrim.esm|02A7CE: If you'll excuse me, I really just want to get home.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D0: Nothing like a good adventure or romance to lose yourself in.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D1: My daughter Braith says I read too much. "Play with me, mother!" Girls need to learn how to take care of themselves.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D2: Really, I wish my girl would read more. It would certainly keep her out of all those fights.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D3: Oh, thank Arkay. Please, take this gold for your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D4: I spend so much time among the dead, I sometimes forget how much I miss the company of the living.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D5: As Whiterun's priest of Arkay, I spread his word whenever I'm able. In the meantime, most of my hours are spent with the city's dearly departed.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D6: With this terrible war, I'm a bit overwhelmed. As are the catacombs.
Skyrim.esm|02A7D8: Well, would you look at that! Argonian Ale! My thanks, friend. Here, take this here healing potion. I, er, "found" it in the temple.
Skyrim.esm|02A7DA: That Heimskr's crazy. Him and his talk o' Talos. Wish he'd just shut up.
Skyrim.esm|02A7DB: What? Why you lookin' at me?
Skyrim.esm|02A7DC: Oho! And what do you want, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|02A7DD: You're in my home. That's right. This here place, Breezehome, it belongs to old Terek.
Skyrim.esm|02A7DE: Now I don't care about no deeds, or laws, or other nonsense like that. This house was empty, so I settled in. And now it's mine.
Skyrim.esm|02A7DF: You can't kick me out of here. You want me gone, you're gonna have to kill me.
Skyrim.esm|02A7E0: I'm a generous man, to those who treat me right. Might be I'll even let you live here with me, if you mind your manners.
Skyrim.esm|02A7E2: These are trying times for us all, I know. Many come to the Temple of Kynareth to pray, and I assist them as best I'm able.
Skyrim.esm|02A7E3: Lately it seems I am more nurse than priestess, tending to those who have suffered, both in mind and body, from this terrible war.
Skyrim.esm|02A7E4: Come to chat with an old woman, hmm? Do your good deed for the day?
Skyrim.esm|02A7E5: Looking for a horse? I own these stables, but Skulvar can help you with a purchase.
Skyrim.esm|02A7E6: My friend Maven and I - have you met Maven, in Riften? - we know our place in this world. Know the place of others, too.
Skyrim.esm|02A7E7: Do you like horses? You  should visit the stables. I own them, you see. And our stock is excellent.
Skyrim.esm|02A9A1: Azura's Star! I knew the Lady of Twilight had sent you for a reason. Hand it over to me. I will ask Azura to restore the Star to its original purity.
Skyrim.esm|02AB8F: Do you get to the Cloud District very often? Oh, what am I saying - of course you don't.
Skyrim.esm|02AB91: Oh, it took years, but I earned my way to the top. I own Chillfurrow Farm, you see. Very successful business. Obviously.
Skyrim.esm|02AB92: I actually advise the Jarl on political matters. My input is invaluable, of course. But this is all probably a bit over your head.
Skyrim.esm|02AB93: Looking for my husband Nazeem? Check the Jarl's backside. That's usually where he stuffs himself these days.
Skyrim.esm|02AB94: Are you married? You look the type. Make sure you treat your wife with the respect she deserves.
Skyrim.esm|02AB95: Here you stand, talking to a married woman. Why? Am I that interesting? Or perhaps there are other things on your mind.
Skyrim.esm|02AB96: Men are all alike, from Skyrim to Hammerfell. They care only for war and politics, and treat their women like cattle.
Skyrim.esm|02AB97: Come to Dragonsreach to discuss the ongoing hostilities, like the rest of the "great warriors"?
Skyrim.esm|02AB98: It seems this damnable conflict has claimed everyone's passions of late. Me, I prefer my books, and my spells.
Skyrim.esm|02AB99: There's a tranquility in magic, don't you think? It is simply energy, to be harnessed by those with the will and knowledge to do so.
Skyrim.esm|02AB9A: Got some good pieces out here, if you're looking to buy. More inside.
Skyrim.esm|02AB9B: I don't claim to be the best blacksmith in Whiterun. Eorlund Gray-Mane's got that honor. Man's steel is legendary. All I ask is a fair chance.
Skyrim.esm|02AB9C: Have you met my father? He's the steward, up at Dragonsreach.
Skyrim.esm|02AB9D: I help my father in more ways than people realize.
Skyrim.esm|02AB9E: You think all the council my father provides the Jarl is his alone? Hmph. Let's just say I advise the advisor, and leave it at that.
Skyrim.esm|02AB9F: Exile or no, my duty to Balgruuf remains. Keep that in mind when you approach him, for I will be watching.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA0: It seems our enemies have no end of spies, saboteurs and assassins to send against us.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA1: Make a move toward Balgruuf, and you're dead.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA2: Got the fleetest steeds in all o' Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA3: Man like you needs a warhorse, I reckon.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA4: Lookin' to shatter records or bones? My beasts can do both.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA5: Only thing better than ridin' a swift horse is ridin' a swift horse over the enemy.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA6: Need a horse? Talk to my father, Skulvar.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA7: Old lady Maiden-Loom owns the stables. But my father and me, we run things.
Skyrim.esm|02ABA8: So I mostly sweep and shovel. You got a problem with that?
Skyrim.esm|02ABA9: Father says I ain't smart enough to be stablemaster. Some day.
Skyrim.esm|02ABB7: Yes, from what I've heard I expect Tolfdir will be occupied for some time. Hmm, that is a problem, isn't it? I suppose someone else will need to look after the Apprentices for now.
Skyrim.esm|02ABB9: I'm not sure what to expect here. Please be on your guard.
Skyrim.esm|02ABBA: Well, perhaps it's time for some independent study. Check with Urag gro-Shub in the Arcanaeum. See if he's aware of any references to this... thing that you and Tolfdir found. I'd like to know as much as we can about it.
Skyrim.esm|02ABC9: Come to gawk at the dark elf?
Skyrim.esm|02ABCA: Well look at us. Like two gray specks in the snow.
Skyrim.esm|02ABCB: I mean no offense. I am but a humble farmer, not a great wanderer, like you.
Skyrim.esm|02AC67: You! You'll help me! You help people, right? That's what you do?
Skyrim.esm|02ACC2: So he has been punished? And what of us? What fate shall we suffer?
Skyrim.esm|02ACC3: What? Why would he do such a thing?
Skyrim.esm|02ACC4: You have done admirably, little one. You have helped rid the Orcs of Yamarz in a way that avoids the destruction of their tribe. They will become stronger for it. And you... You have proven your strength. You have earned my favor for now, mortal, and so I give you a little gift. Use it well.
Skyrim.esm|02ACC5: You, place that hammer on the shrine. You're the only one who's proven worthy in all of this...
Skyrim.esm|02ACC6: Ah, that is good to hear, then.
Skyrim.esm|02ACC7: But... What of Yamarz? I see. He did not survive. He... He died well?
Skyrim.esm|02ACC8: You'll have to prove yourselves, but I'm willing to give you a chance. Gularzob's in charge now. Let's hope he's a better chief.
Skyrim.esm|02ACC9: Yamarz was a coward and a weakling. His deceitful ways have cost you all greatly.
Skyrim.esm|02ACF8: Are you all right? I heard the commotion. Who was Cicero talking to? Where's the accomplice? Reveal yourself, traitor!
Skyrim.esm|02ACF9: Volunruud? Well, that is interesting. I know this place...
Skyrim.esm|02ACFA: Then what in Sithis' name is going on? Cicero spoke to the Night Mother, but she spoke to you? Is this just more of the fool's rambling?
Skyrim.esm|02ACFB: By Sithis. And... what did she say?
Skyrim.esm|02ACFD: What? The Listener? What are you going on about? What is this lunacy?
Skyrim.esm|02ACFE: It's true, it's true! The Night Mother has spoken! The silence has been broken! The Listener has been chosen!
Skyrim.esm|02ACFF: By the almighty Divines. You've come. You've actually come. This dreadful Black Sacrament thing... it worked.
Skyrim.esm|02AD00: There are indeed. Three, to be exact. There's Deekus, a shipwreck scavenger. Ma'randru-jo, a traveling caravaner. And Anoriath, a hunter.
Skyrim.esm|02AD02: Hmph. How about, instead of giving you another contract, I simply deride you for not finishing the one you still have?
Skyrim.esm|02AD03: So it would appear. Though I'd suspect she'd want you to complete your other contracts first. Do those, then we'll talk about something new.
Skyrim.esm|02AD04: Hmm? No. No! Listen, I don't know what's going on here, but you take your orders from me. Are we clear on that? The Night Mother may have spoken to you, but I am still the leader of this Family. I will not have my authority so easily dismissed. I... I need time to think about all this. Go see Nazir - do some work for him. I'll find you when I'm ready to discuss the matter further.
Skyrim.esm|02AD08: By Sithis, this ends now! Back away, fool! Whatever you've been planning is over!
Skyrim.esm|02AD0A: Look. Something is happening here. I'm not sure entirely what that something is, but... Well, we need to find out. If the Night Mother really did give you an order to talk to a contact, we'd be mad to ignore it. And I think we'd both agree, Cicero's brought quite enough madness to this Sanctuary. So go. Go to Volunruud. It's a crypt, pretty far to the northeast. Talk to this Amaund Motierre. And let's see where all this leads. Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|02AD0C: I spoke only to the Night Mother! I spoke to the Night Mother, but she didn't speak to me. Oh no. She spoke only to him! To the Listener!
Skyrim.esm|02AD0D: I spoke only to the Night Mother! I spoke to the Night Mother, but she didn't speak to me. Oh no. She spoke only to her! To the Listener!
Skyrim.esm|02AD0E: Amaund Motierre? I have no idea who that is. But Volunruud... that I have heard of. And I know where it is.
Skyrim.esm|02AD12: As a matter of fact, I do. Two of them. One rather easy assignment, and another that should prove quite a challenge. Your first target is an Orc bard named Lurbuk. The other is a vampire by the name of Hern. Happy hunting.
Skyrim.esm|02AD13: I'm sorry, I don't. Come see me again later.
Skyrim.esm|02AD14: More work? My friend, you still haven't finished the last set of contracts I gave you. Why don't you concentrate on eliminating that final target.
Skyrim.esm|02AD15: More work? My friend, you still haven't finished the last two contracts I gave you. Let's get those done, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|02B36C: He was an Apprentice here at the College. Not very skilled, but got involved with a group of mages who took a liking to him. When they left, he took off after them. Stole supplies and books from the College, I suppose as a way to ingratiate himself.
Skyrim.esm|02B36D: I know what you want. Word travels fast around here. Discovered some big mystery, huh? Well you don't even need to ask. No, I don't have anything for you. Not anymore, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|02B36E: Let's just call it a "difference of opinion" with the College. They were interested in research that goes outside the bounds of what the College allows, so they were... persuaded to leave.
Skyrim.esm|02B36F: I said not anymore. Orthorn stole a number of books when he ran off to Fellglow Keep to join those Summoners. Some kind of peace offering. I think one of those volumes may have had some relevant information. If you want them, you'll have to talk to Orthorn.
Skyrim.esm|02B386: And I would deserve it, wouldn't I? I can't stop you if that's what you want to do. Hircine is too powerful. But if you spare me, I can be a powerful ally to you. And I would promise to never return to civilized life. I know now that I can't live among people.
Skyrim.esm|02B389: Thank you for your help. I will make my home here, away from anyone I might hurt.
Skyrim.esm|02B38D: A little girl is dead because of me. Believe me, it wasn't anything I ever intended to do. I just... lost control. I tried to tell them, but none of them believe me. It's all on account of this blasted ring.
Skyrim.esm|02B38E: Thank the gods. Now let's deal with these other hunters. We hunt together!
Skyrim.esm|02B832: What is the music of life?
Skyrim.esm|02B834: Oh ho. The whimpering Nord, eh? Yes, yes, I can see how you'd come to that conclusion. Interesting choice.
Skyrim.esm|02B83F: Ah, at last! I hope you found the place all right.
Skyrim.esm|02B8AB: Oh, I couldn't. Goes right through me. Besides, I have so many things to do...
Skyrim.esm|02B8AC: Reeaaaallllyyyy? Ooh, ooh, what kind of message? A song? A summons? Wait, I know! A death threat written on the back of an Argonian concubine! Those are my favorite. Well? Spit it out, mortal. I haven't got an eternity! Actually... I do. Little joke. But seriously. What's the message?
Skyrim.esm|02B8AE: You are the best Septim that's ever ruled. Well, except for that Martin fellow, but he turned into a dragon god, and that's hardly sporting...
Skyrim.esm|02B8AF: You are far too hard on yourself, my dear, sweet, homicidally insane Pelagius. What would the people do without you? Dance? Sing? Smile? Grow old?
Skyrim.esm|02B8B1: Yes, yes, as you've said, countless times before...
Skyrim.esm|02B8B2: Last I saw him, he was visiting a friend in the Blue Palace. But no one as mundane as the Jarl. No, no... such people are below him. No, he went into the forbidden wing of the palace, to speak with an old friend. Said it had been ages since they had last had tea. Oh and you'll need the hip bone... it's very important. No entering Pelagius' Wing without that.
Skyrim.esm|02B8B3: So many undesirables to contend with. Naysayers. Buffoons. Detractors. Why, my headsman hasn't slept in three days!
Skyrim.esm|02B8B4: Hafrumph! Well then, if you're going to be like that... Perhaps it's best I take my leave. A good day to you sir. I said good day!
Skyrim.esm|02B8B8: I have a saying - I'm not going to feel what you have to like. Or something like that.
Skyrim.esm|02B8BB: He is a great man, but one rarely praised! He rules twin empires that span the length and breadth of our minds! All know him, but few can name him! But... he has forbidden me from saying his name. He says it distracts him, and woe to those who draw his ire. But you will know him when you see him. He's the one who made me like this!
Skyrim.esm|02B8BD: I'll make an exception for you, but do not bring anything out when you leave. Too many dark deeds transpired in those halls...
Skyrim.esm|02B8BE: If you really want to... Just be careful of the ghost! He snuck up on me once and scared me sick. It took a week for me to feel better...
Skyrim.esm|02B8BF: Well... I suppose in that case, it's okay to let you in. But only briefly. Be careful, and come right back!
Skyrim.esm|02B8C1: My master has abandoned me! Abandoned his people. And nothing I say can change his mind. Now he refuses to even see me. He says I interrupt his vacation! It's been so many years... Won't you please help?
Skyrim.esm|02B8C2: Absolutely not. That wing has been sealed for hundreds of years, and for good reason. They say the ghost of Pelagius the Mad still haunts it. Ghost or not, there are reminders of his dark rule that are best left buried away.
Skyrim.esm|02B8C3: Not on your life. It's dangerous in there, and Falk doesn't even like me and Erdi going in every year to clean out the spiders.
Skyrim.esm|02B8C4: Oh, I'd get in trouble for that. It's not allowed. Besides, it's scary in there!
Skyrim.esm|02B967: Well, well. And you seem to be in one piece! Thank you. I'll look these over, and inform Mirabelle if I find anything relevant. "Night of Tears", eh? I remember this one. Well, isn't that interesting. Did you read it yourself? If I recall it correctly, that has some interesting implications. You should mention that to Tolfdir. And... here. I suppose you've earned these.
Skyrim.esm|02B99E: You. You must take the Lexicon. Free me of my burden.
Skyrim.esm|02BA6B: Head to the Bannered Mare in Whiterun and look for Mallus Maccius. He'll fill you in on all the details.
Skyrim.esm|02BA6D: No time for idle chatter. Mercer is awaiting your presence.
Skyrim.esm|02BA70: Don't come back until every one of those things are dead.
Skyrim.esm|02BA71: You're a firebrand, aren't you? It's about time Brynjolf sent me someone with business sense. I was beginning to think he was running some sort of beggar's guild over there.
Skyrim.esm|02BA74: Mercer thinks he knows a way to identify this new thorn in our side. He wants to meet with you right away. And if I were you I'd hurry. I've never seen him this angry before.
Skyrim.esm|02BA7B: I'm going to keep this short 'cause we've got a lot to do. Honningbrew's owner, Sabjorn, is about to hold a tasting for Whiterun's Captain of the Guard and we're going to poison the mead.
Skyrim.esm|02BA7D: So you're the one that burned down Goldenglow Estate. Do you have any idea what that little stunt you pulled is going to cost me? I'm amazed you even bothered to show your face here.
Skyrim.esm|02BA83: What are you gawking at? Can't you see I have problems here?
Skyrim.esm|02BA89: You did this didn't you? I'll get you for this!
Skyrim.esm|02BA8B: I believe Brynjolf wishes to speak with you. We're done here.
Skyrim.esm|02BCFA: Yaaawwwwnn.... Oh, pardon me. Were you saying something? I do apologize, it's just that I find myself suddenly and irrevocably... Bored! I mean, really. Here you stand, before Sheogorath himself, Daedric Prince of Madness, and all you deem fit to do is... deliver a message? How sad.
Skyrim.esm|02BCFB: Yes, yes, go. Leave me to my ceaseless responsibilities and burdens...
Skyrim.esm|02BCFC: Were you now? By whom? Wait! Don't tell me! I want to guess! Was it Molag? No, no... Little Tim, the toymaker's son? The ghost of King Lysandus? Or was it... Yes! Stanley, that talking grapefruit from Passwall. Wrong on all accounts, aren't I? Ha! No matter! Honestly, I don't want to know. Why ruin the surprise? But more to the point. Do you - tiny, puny, expendable little mortal - actually think you can convince me to leave? Because that's... crazy. You do realize who you're dealing with here?
Skyrim.esm|02BCFD: Oh, you just don't understand. Without him, I am not free! Without him, I am doomed! All of his empire shall fall into chaos...
Skyrim.esm|02BCFE: Jolly good guess! But only half right. I'm a mad god. The Mad God, actually. It's a family title. Gets passed down from me to myself every few thousand years. Now you. You can call me Ann Marie. But only if you're partial to being flayed alive and having an angry immortal skip rope with your entrails. If not... Then call me Sheogorath, Daedric Prince of Madness. Charmed.
Skyrim.esm|02BCFF: Oh, no no no! No mistake at all. What you made was a choice. Granted, not a very wise choice, but these things happen. Ah, the folly of youth... You know, you remind me of myself at a young age. All I cared about was riding narwhales and sleeping in honeycombs and drinking babies' tears... Word of advice if you ride a Narwhale. Mind the pointy end. Ah, but there I go, waxing poetic about my misspent youth. Now where were we? Ah yes. You're the mortal messenger. An I am...? Honestly. Have you any idea?
Skyrim.esm|02BD00: Wrong! Actually, you do. Sort of. I am a part of you, little mortal. I am a shadow in your subconscious, a blemish on your fragile little psyche. You know me. You just don't know it. Sheogorath, Daedric Prince of Madness. At your service.
Skyrim.esm|02BD01: He stays in the Pelagius Wing of the Blue Palace. The doors are locked, and only Falk Firebeard can grant entry. But I hear the maids, Una and Erdi, know how to enter it for cleaning. Surely they can help for such an important matter?
Skyrim.esm|02BD02: Yes, but don't try to use force! He's far too mighty. He'd crush you like a clay doll, or drop you from the sky, or simply eat your brain right up! Oh, but he probably won't do those things to you. Probably. Just remind him to come home, and... there'll be no fighting or unpleasantness. Probably. He's just a little confused at the moment, is all. Knowing my master, he may very well reward you for bringing him to his senses!
Skyrim.esm|02BD03: How rude! Can't be bothered to host an old friend for a decade or two.
Skyrim.esm|02BD04: Please, take pity on an old madman!
Skyrim.esm|02BD05: Who among you can help me?
Skyrim.esm|02BD06: Without his guidance, our homeland is falling apart. North wages war against south! The holy flame flickers and dies! We need his return. Oh, but... I'm sure he'll repay you when he comes to his senses! His favor is a powerful, powerful thing. And so very worth any... inconveniences.
Skyrim.esm|02BD2C: Now that's the real question, isn't it? Because honestly, how much time off could a demented Daedra really need? So here's what I'm going to do. I'm going to leave. That's right. I'm done. Holiday... complete. Time to return to the hum drum day-to-day. On one condition. You have to find the way out first. Good luck with that.
Skyrim.esm|02BDE0: The Companions? In Jorrvaskr? They're recruiting new members, from what I hear.
Skyrim.esm|02BDE3: Be glad you're here in the city, and not out in Riverwood. Those poor sods don't even have a wall.
Skyrim.esm|02BDE5: You should talk to the Jarl up in Dragonsreach, lend any aid you can.
Skyrim.esm|02BDE9: Fear not. Come dragon or giant, we'll be ready.
Skyrim.esm|02BDEA: Need a blade? You should talk to Adrianne, at the forge.
Skyrim.esm|02BDEB: Need supplies? See Belethor at his store. Sleazy little man, but he has good stock.
Skyrim.esm|02BDEC: If you need potions, you should try Arcadia's Cauldron.
Skyrim.esm|02BDED: I worry about the other settlements in the hold. Unwalled, lightly manned - prime targets for those damned dragons.
Skyrim.esm|02BDEF: Rorikstead's a nice enough little hamlet, but a boring posting for a guard. If a dragon attacks, well... then it'll be a different story.
Skyrim.esm|02BDF1: You look tired, friend. The Bannered Mare has beds for rent.
Skyrim.esm|02BE10: Let me guess - someone stole your sweetroll...
Skyrim.esm|02BE11: Could sure use a warm bed right about now...
Skyrim.esm|02BE12: Disrespect the law, and you disrespect me.
Skyrim.esm|02BE47: At least you remembered one of the things I asked. Let me see that.
Skyrim.esm|02BE54: Oh my. You would do this for me? Here, take it. I don't want anything to do with this wretched thing anymore. Seek out the beast. He wanders these woods. Bring him down and... well, the Lord of the Hunt should smile on you. I wish you luck, but should leave here while I still have my skin. Should our paths cross again, I will remember your kindness. Farewell.
Skyrim.esm|02BE55: This is the Ring of Hircine. I was told it could let me control my transformations. Perhaps it used to. But I'll never know.
