Hircine didn't care for my taking it, and threw a curse on it. I put it on... and the changes just came to me. I could never guess when. It would be at the worst times. Like... with the little girl.
Skyrim.esm|02BE56: Our daughter. Our little girl. She hadn't seen her tenth winter.
Skyrim.esm|02BE57: I don't suppose there's a point in keeping the secret if I'm going to die in here anyway. I'm sure you've heard of men who shift to beasts under the influence of the moons. I am one of them. A werewolf. It's my secret, and my shame. That's why I wanted the ring... it was said to give men like me control. Now I may look like a man, but I still feel the animal inside of me, as strong as ever.
Skyrim.esm|02BE58: I had just come into Falkreath. They needed some help working the mill, and I thought that would be something safe. Something I could do. When I saw the little girl, I was just... I could feel it coming on. I could taste the... I needed to hunt. But this pitiful, limited body wasn't meant for hunting. Slow. No claws. Weak, mashing teeth for chewing cud. I held in my rage as long as I could. But it boiled inside of me. She looked so fragile. Helpless prey. And then... I... I feel terrible about what happened. About what I did. It would probably be best for everyone if I just went away.
Skyrim.esm|02BE59: Sinding. Came through as a laborer. Seemed like a decent man. He's stewing in the pit while we figure out what to do with him, if you've got the stomach to look at him. What could drive a man to do something like this?
Skyrim.esm|02BE5B: She was... he ripped her apart. Like a sabre cat tears a deer. We barely found enough of her to bury.
Skyrim.esm|02BE77: You take care of yourself, now. And if you ever find yourself in New Sheoth, do look me up. We can share a strawberry torte. Ta ta!
Skyrim.esm|02BE78: Luggage! Now where did I leave my luggage?
Skyrim.esm|02BE7A: Ha! I do love it when the mortals know they're being manipulated. Makes things infinitely more interesting.
Skyrim.esm|02BE7B: And as for you, my little mortal minion... Feel free to keep the Wabbajack. As a symbol of my... Oh, just take the damn thing.
Skyrim.esm|02BE7E: Pelagius Septim the Third, once the Mad Emperor of Tamriel, now so boringly sane. I always knew he had it in him!
Skyrim.esm|02BE7F: Let's make sure I'm not forgetting anything. Clothes? Check. Beard? Check! Luggage?
Skyrim.esm|02BE80: Yes, yes, that's quite enough celebration. Let's send you ahead, shall we?
Skyrim.esm|02BE81: Care to take a look around? This is not, I dare say, the Solitude botanical gardens. Have you any idea where you are? Where you truly are? Welcome to the deceptively verdant mind of the Emperor Pelagius III. That's right! You're in the head of a dead, homicidally insane monarch. Now, I know what you're thinking. Can I still rely on my swords and spells and sneaking and all that nonsense? Sure, sure. Or... you could use... The Wabbajack! Huh? Huh? Didn't see that coming, did you?
Skyrim.esm|02BE83: Master! You've taken me back! Does this mean we're going home? Oh, happy times! I can't wait to...
Skyrim.esm|02BE84: Well, I suppose it's back to the Shivering Isles. The trouble Haskill can get into while I'm gone simply boggles the mind...
Skyrim.esm|02BF93: If I make it back to Solitude, I'll put in a good word for you with General Tullius. The Legion could sure use someone like you. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|02BF95: Stick close. Damn rebels could be anywhere. Don't worry, though. General Tullius will have them on the run soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|02BF97: The Stormcloaks pay blood for blood!
Skyrim.esm|02BFAD: I killed a lot of High Elves in the Great War, and I didn't die. I guess that makes me a war hero. The "Great War"... there was nothing great about it. Thousands died on both sides, and where did it get us? Did we really save the Empire? Or did we just plant the seeds for Ulfric's uprising, and another war?
Skyrim.esm|02BFAF: You one of those "Skyrim for the Nords" types?
Skyrim.esm|02BFB6: And you're right, at least as far as I'm concerned. Don't let Ulfric or some of these other short-sighted Nords bother you. Most of us are happy to welcome newcomers.
Skyrim.esm|02BFB8: That's just what Ulfric wants you to think. If that's what you really believe, then you're a fool. Never had much patience for fools, myself.
Skyrim.esm|02BFB9: Good. Too many Nords in this town have been listening to Ulfric's narrow-minded words. He's tough, loyal to his men and a good leader, but if you're not a Nord, Ulfric will never trust you.
Skyrim.esm|02BFBA: Is that so? You ought to join the Stormcloaks, if you haven't already. Seems to me they're a bunch of narrow-minded bigots, just like you. Meantime, you best steer clear of me.
Skyrim.esm|02C001: I'm sorry to be such a bother. We'll talk another time.
Skyrim.esm|02C011: Not a day goes by that I don't think about returning to the ancestral home of my people. Still, even with the rough treatment we've had at the hands of Ulfric and his lot, I've got ties here. It's probably more trouble to pack up and leave than it is to stay. Besides, Ambarys can't run the cornerclub on his own.
Skyrim.esm|02C0A9: Fresh fruit and vegetables for sale!
Skyrim.esm|02C0AA: Fruits and vegetables, all ripe and ready for your table!
Skyrim.esm|02C0AB: Take home Windhelm's finest and freshest produce today!
Skyrim.esm|02C0AF: I always have bedlam jobs. Just remember to hit them fast and don't let them see you. Feel like going for it?
Skyrim.esm|02C0B0: Assorted fruits and vegetables, all delicious and affordable!
Skyrim.esm|02C0B1: Come and browse my fine selection! Everything's fresh and delicious!
Skyrim.esm|02C0B2: I've a little of everything for sale, come and see for yourself!
Skyrim.esm|02C0B4: Cookware, knives and other goods for the home, all at reasonable prices!
Skyrim.esm|02C0B5: Of course. Don't forget to keep out of sight, it doesn't count if they catch you red-handed. You ready?
Skyrim.esm|02C0B6: Taking an easy job, eh? Well, go and do some damage. Just stick to the shadows and keep out of sight. Want to go for it?
Skyrim.esm|02C0BE: Assorted wares and tools you just can't live without! Come and see for yourself!
Skyrim.esm|02C0BF: Buying and selling a wide range of assorted goods! The best selection in Windhelm!
Skyrim.esm|02C0C0: Windhelm is divided into four quarters. The Gray Quarter is the one that's home to all the city's Dark Elves. If it looks to you like an impoverished slum, that's because it is. Ulfric prefers that we live in squalor. He has nothing but disdain for anyone who isn't a Nord. He tolerates us, but that's the extent of his hospitality.
Skyrim.esm|02C0C1: Help the Dark Elves? Oh, you must have heard me talking to Malthyr. The Dark Elves live in a run-down slum called the Gray Quarter. Ulfric's content to keep it that way. I guess they think I can open Ulfric's eyes to their plight, and get him to lift a finger on their behalf. I'm trying, but Ulfric is set in his ways. For him, there's two kinds of people in this world -- Nords, and the folk beneath them.
Skyrim.esm|02C0C2: I'd be out there fighting the Imperials right now if it wasn't for my war-wound.
Skyrim.esm|02C0C3: I'll find some way to finish my book.
Skyrim.esm|02C0C4: Come slumming to the Gray Quarter, have you?
Skyrim.esm|02C0C7: There's two kinds of Pit fighters -- good ones and dead ones.
Skyrim.esm|02C0C8: I told you, I've got no patience for fools. Keep walking.
Skyrim.esm|02C0C9: There's no glory in war. It's just something they tell soldiers so they'll risk their lives.
Skyrim.esm|02C0CA: It bothers me that fewer people come to Windhelm these days.
Skyrim.esm|02C0CB: I'll never have Master Nurelion's skill with potions.
Skyrim.esm|02C14E: Infos conditioned on Attack Delta go here.
Skyrim.esm|02C14F: We are ready to mount our final attack.
Skyrim.esm|02C150: Join the forces mustering outside of [CAPITAL]. (Infos conditioned on Capital location go here)
Skyrim.esm|02C151: The attack will be launched imminently. Gather your weaponry and get to the front lines... Now, soldier!
Skyrim.esm|02C168: Skyrim has no use for your kind.
Skyrim.esm|02C170: Stop, in the name of the Jarl!
Skyrim.esm|02C171: Come quietly or taste the Jarl's justice!
Skyrim.esm|02C172: By order of the Jarl, I command you to halt!
Skyrim.esm|02C173: Sheathe your weapons and come quietly!
Skyrim.esm|02C186: Very well. Empty the vessels into the sconce near the throne, then sit in the throne. When you have done so, I will unbind Hevnoraak. He will awaken in a weakened state, but the lich is still to be reckoned with. Be ready.
Skyrim.esm|02C187: Find the three vessels within Valthume, then return to me. With these we can defeat him.
Skyrim.esm|02C188: In life, Hevnoraak drained his own blood from his body. His goal was to transfer his power back into himself after death, becoming a powerful lich. The vessels contain that very blood. Empty them and you remove any chance he has of regaining his former powers.
Skyrim.esm|02C189: The cave has a bad history. Long ago, Potema the Wolf Queen used it for necromantic rituals. That's where it got the name. That was over 500 years ago. Nothing much down there now - but everyone's always convinced the cave is haunted.
Skyrim.esm|02C18A: More dangerous than you know. Left alone, however, he will awaken when he has grown too powerful to be stopped. This is the only way to be sure.
Skyrim.esm|02C18C: Yes, old friend, I'm afraid it's not good news. When you broke up the binding Potema escaped. We've encountered some of her minions. Styrr says she's still in spirit form or we'd all be dead already. You've already done us a service in stopping the binding, but I need you to go talk to him, to see if Styrr can tell us what to do next.
Skyrim.esm|02C250: This... it matches every description of the Phial that I've found in lore. But if it can't hold liquid, there's no way of knowing. How did you manage to damage it, then? This is what I get for not retrieving it myself.
Skyrim.esm|02C255: Thank the gods. I'll get to work on the Phial straight away.
Skyrim.esm|02C256: Even though the Phial was damaged, I still think your efforts deserve reward. Now, if you'll pardon me, I'm going to tend to Nurelion. I can make his final days a bit less painful.
Skyrim.esm|02C2C6: Trouble? He's one of the most stubborn lizards I've ever met! You have your work cut out for you.
Skyrim.esm|02C2C7: Oh, wait... did you say Goldenglow Estate? My apologies. I'm sorry to say I know very little about that... bee farm, was it?
Skyrim.esm|02C2C8: So, what do we have here? Hmm. Let me guess. By your scent, I'd say you were from the Guild. But that can't be true, because I told Mercer I wouldn't deal with them anymore.
Skyrim.esm|02C2C9: Gulum-Ei works in the East Empire Company Warehouse. He helps maintain all of the shipments of goods that goes in and out of Solitude. That means he has the pick of the litter from some of the finest goods to grace Skyrim's shores. He isn't exactly in the Guild, but he pays us a cut of all the stuff he lifts from the warehouse.
Skyrim.esm|02C2CA: I can't believe Gulum-Ei's mixed up in all this; that Argonian couldn't find his tail with both hands. Don't get me wrong, he could scam a beggar out of his last septim... but he's no mastermind.
Skyrim.esm|02C2CC: Gulum-Ei is our inside man at the East Empire Company in Solitude. I'm betting he acted as a go-between for the sale of Goldenglow Estate and that he can finger our buyer. Get out there, shake him down and see what you come up with. Talk to Brynjolf before you leave if you have any questions.
Skyrim.esm|02C2CE: There are thieves and then there is Gulum-Ei. No honor, no code at all. He'd shake your hand and stab you in the back at the same time. The cut he's supposed to provide the Guild has dwindled as of late. He says pickings in the warehouse are slim, but I'm certain he's lying. Keep your eyes on him, he's quite crafty.
Skyrim.esm|02C2D2: No, no. Mercer wants him alive, for now. Stubborn or not, he's the only contact we've got at the East Empire Company. Just keep on his tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
Skyrim.esm|02C2D3: A mercantile group that has established ports all over Tamriel. They pretty much dominate the whole shipping industry. The Emperor himself supposedly backs them, which means they have fairly unlimited resources... so don't get their feathers in a ruffle.
Skyrim.esm|02C2D6: Ah, there you are. I've consulted my contacts regarding the information you recovered from Goldenglow Estate, but no one can identify that symbol.
Skyrim.esm|02C2DB: Aringoth was a fool to think he could get away with this.
Skyrim.esm|02C2DC: Maybe I did, maybe I didn't. I can't be expected to remember every deal I handle.
Skyrim.esm|02C2DF: Just head right back to the Guild and get the information to Mercer. Nothing else is more important. If you discover Gulum-Ei's holding out on us and has more loot stashed away than he claims, we'd find that information quite valuable as well.
Skyrim.esm|02C2F8: Shor's bones, what happened to you, boy?
Skyrim.esm|02C2F9: Okay, okay. Come inside, then. Sigrid will get you something to eat and you can tell me all about it.
Skyrim.esm|02C2FA: What's going on? And who's this?
Skyrim.esm|02C2FB: Changed your mind? Come with me. I'm heading to Riverwood. It's just up the road and my uncle Alvor is the blacksmith there.
Skyrim.esm|02C2FD: Are you in some kind of trouble?
Skyrim.esm|02C2FE: He's a friend. Saved my life in fact. Come on, I'll explain everything, but we need to go inside.
Skyrim.esm|02C2FF: She's a friend. Saved my life in fact. Come on, I'll explain everything, but we need to go inside.
Skyrim.esm|02C300: Come on. I'll feel better once we're under cover in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|02C301: I hope that dragon isn't still flying around here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|02C302: I still almost can't believe it... a real, live dragon...
Skyrim.esm|02C303: Hadvar? What are you doing here? Are you on leave from...
Skyrim.esm|02C304: Shh.. Uncle, please. Keep your voice down. I'm fine. But we should go inside to talk.
Skyrim.esm|02C32A: She is the Goddess of Dawn and Dusk. Azura sees into the Twilight of the future, and guides her followers through it.
Skyrim.esm|02C332: Who... who are you? What's going on?
Skyrim.esm|02C338: Priest of Arkay. I'm your friend.
Skyrim.esm|02C339: Namira grind your bones for my bread!
Skyrim.esm|02C33A: I will drink your blood and feast on your flesh!
Skyrim.esm|02C34C: No, I found her horse. Don't worry, I've taken care of it... she won't be using it to escape. Let's get moving, I want to catch her inside while she's distracted. Take the lead.
Skyrim.esm|02C34D: I'm sorry, I was under the impression I was in charge. You're leading and I'm following. Does that seem clear to you?
Skyrim.esm|02C34E: The last thing I saw was Karliah dumping his body into a opening atop the ruins; an unceremonious end for a remarkable man. To this day I've regretted allowing her to escape, even if it meant I had died trying. I owed Gallus that much.
Skyrim.esm|02C34F: She was a stubborn Dunmer... always had to do everything her way. But she was also the best... bringing in more coin a month than some thieves heist in a year. Gallus trusted her too much and let her get too close.
Skyrim.esm|02C350: Karliah was a master marksman and her greatest weapon was the element of surprise. I was lucky... she missed my heart by mere inches. I staggered away from the ruins and my vision began to blur. It's then that I realized the bitch had poisoned her arrows.
Skyrim.esm|02C351: If you want to call it that, yes. Me? I think she was softening him for the kill. Gallus would call her his "little nightingale." He was absolutely smitten by her.
Skyrim.esm|02C352: Just make certain you keep your eyes open. Karliah is as sharp as a blade. The last thing I need is you blundering into a trap and warning her that we're here.
Skyrim.esm|02C353: Good, you're finally here. I've scouted the ruins and I'm certain Karliah is still inside.
Skyrim.esm|02C355: Twenty-five years ago I was standing outside these very same ruins. Gallus told me to meet here but he wouldn't say why. When I arrived, Gallus stepped from the shadows. Before he uttered a sound an arrow pierced his throat. Before I could even draw my blade, her second arrow found its mark in my chest.
Skyrim.esm|02C362: Greed? Jealousy? Spite? Who can say what drove her to such an iniquitous act. One thing's certain. I intend to find out before she draws her last breath.
Skyrim.esm|02C436: That was just lying around? Good find.
Skyrim.esm|02C449: A dragon? That's... ridiculous. You aren't drunk, are you boy?
Skyrim.esm|02C44A: Hadvar, did you really see a dragon? What did it look like? Did it have big teeth?
Skyrim.esm|02C44D: Not much more to tell. This dragon flew over and just wrecked the whole place. Mass confusion. I don't know if anyone else got out alive. I doubt I'd have made it out myself if not for my friend here. I need to get back to Solitude and let them know what's happened. I thought you could help us out. Food, supplies, a place to stay.
Skyrim.esm|02C44E: I don't know where to start. You know I was assigned to General Tullius's guard. We were stopped in Helgen when we were attacked... by a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|02C44F: Of course. Take what you need. But what's all this about? What are you two doing here?
Skyrim.esm|02C450: Hadvar! We've been so worried about you! Come, you two must be hungry. Sit down and I'll get you something to eat.
Skyrim.esm|02C451: Of course! Any friend of Hadvar's is a friend of mine. I'm glad to help however I can.
Skyrim.esm|02C453: Hush, child. Don't pester your cousin.
Skyrim.esm|02C454: Husband. Let him tell his story.
Skyrim.esm|02C455: Now, then, boy. What's the big mystery? What are you doing here, looking like you lost an argument with a cave bear?
Skyrim.esm|02C48E: You assaulted one of my family members. I've come to accept your apology.
Skyrim.esm|02C490: I am prepared to deal with your insolence...
Skyrim.esm|02C491: Fine. Then it will be an honor to kill you for your offenses against my kin.
Skyrim.esm|02C492: I trust this is the last I'll hear of your violent outbursts.
Skyrim.esm|02C4A0: He is gone. But thanks to you, he died knowing his life's work was not in vain.
Skyrim.esm|02C4A1: It doesn't matter. Look, it's refilling with your tonic as we speak.
Skyrim.esm|02C4A3: As am I. You acted very nobly in helping my master realize his life's work. Thank you for that. As for myself, I've always been content to simply be an alchemist. I fear keeping the Phial would just remind me of Nurelion's obsession, and how it consumed him. Here, keep it. You are as much a part of its legend now as Curalmil was. I hope it brings you the happiness that my master desired.
Skyrim.esm|02C59D: Come on. Let's see what you're made of.
Skyrim.esm|02C59E: You want a real fight, eh?
Skyrim.esm|02C59F: Twelve septims on the big one!
Skyrim.esm|02C5A0: Drive that snowback to the ground. Come on!
Skyrim.esm|02C5AE: Don't let him get away with this!
Skyrim.esm|02C5B0: Come on, he's not so tough.
Skyrim.esm|02C615: By Sithis, but you are a persistent one. Haven't I told you enough?
Skyrim.esm|02C619: Please, leave me to recover my... mental energies.
Skyrim.esm|02C654: It's nice to be back in a friendly spot, huh? Listen, I'm going to lay up here for a while. You can make your own way to Solitude from here. I'd recommend heading to Whiterun, just down the road from here. From there you can take a carriage to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|02C655: I hope I'll see you in Solitude. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|02C656: Did you really see a dragon?
Skyrim.esm|02C658: Take what you need, my friend. Within reason, of course.
Skyrim.esm|02C6A6: See that it doesn't happen again.
Skyrim.esm|02C6A7: See, that wasn't so difficult, was it.
Skyrim.esm|02C6A8: No? Do you care about rotting away for a month in a cell? Ah, but nobody wants that, right? Painful for you, annoying for me. So, you pay my fine... and you walk away.
Skyrim.esm|02C6AA: Is that so? Well then, I'll have to charge you with disturbing the Jarl's peace.
Skyrim.esm|02C6D3: I still need you to make a batch of those fried potatoes. We can serve them with the ale.
Skyrim.esm|02C6D4: You are going to brew a new batch of ale, right? Like we talked about? What we have's been sitting in that barrel for over a month.
Skyrim.esm|02C6D5: Assur and I are playing a game. Don't tell him where I am!
Skyrim.esm|02C6D6: Assur had to move away because of the Empire. But we get to stay.
Skyrim.esm|02C6D7: Assur says his pa says the College made lots of bad things happen, but the mages I've seen look nice.
Skyrim.esm|02C6D8: There's not many people left in Winterhold, so I only have Assur to play with, and sometimes he's mean.
Skyrim.esm|02C6D9: Assur always wants to play "Hunt the Elf" and he makes me be the Elf.
Skyrim.esm|02C6DA: Pa says the Empire made us leave, but the Empire just does whatever the elves tell them to.
Skyrim.esm|02C6DB: We don't get many people like you visiting Winterhold. Usually it's elves.
Skyrim.esm|02C6DC: Did you know that most of Winterhold fell into the sea? I heard it's because of that College.
Skyrim.esm|02C6DD: Someday I'm gonna grow up and be the Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|02C6DE: I heard the College teaches you how to kill people with magic. Is that true?
Skyrim.esm|02C6DF: I'm very busy, you know. I'm the only one Kraldar can rely on to assist him.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E0: Kraldar has big plans for Winterhold. We'd be better off if he was in charge.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E1: I'm not one to complain, but I'm not sure why Kraldar insists on keeping Malur Seloth around.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E2: My job is to serve the Jarl, not waste time talking with travelers.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E3: Thonjolf is convinced I've got some connections at the College, so he goes easy on me.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E4: I don't really know anyone at the College. I guess everyone just assumes I do because I'm a Dunmer.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E5: People won't say it, but they're afraid of me, so they leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E6: It turns out that if people think you're some sort of secret wizard, they'll let you get away with a lot.
Skyrim.esm|02C6E8: I'm trying to do as little as possible. You're getting in the way of that.
Skyrim.esm|02C6EA: Winterhold may not look impressive, but it has a very rich history.
Skyrim.esm|02C6EB: Winterhold was once the capital of Skyrim, you know. Quite an honor.
Skyrim.esm|02C6EC: I'd like nothing more than to see Winterhold regain its former glory.
Skyrim.esm|02C6ED: It's hard to recover from something like the Great Collapse, but I believe Winterhold can do it.
Skyrim.esm|02C6EE: The College of Winterhold is truly an asset to our city... err, town.
Skyrim.esm|02C6EF: Just say the word if you need a drink or something to eat.
Skyrim.esm|02C6F0: If you need a drink, just say the word.
Skyrim.esm|02C6F1: Something I can get for you?
Skyrim.esm|02C8CE: Now, I'm not sure that you're quite as attuned as I am, given my extensive years of experience, but can you feel that?
Skyrim.esm|02C8D1: Find out why he's here, and what he wants.
Skyrim.esm|02C8D2: Really? Well, allow me to clarify the situation. I'd like to know why there's someone claiming to be from the Psijic Order here in the College. More importantly, I'd like to know why he's asking for you specifically. So we're going to go have a little chat with him, and find out exactly what it is he wants.
Skyrim.esm|02C8D3: I need you to come with me immediately. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|02C8D5: I suppose we'll continue this at some later time, when we can avoid interruptions.
Skyrim.esm|02C8D6: I'm sure you've already noticed the markings. They're quite unlike anything we've seen before.
Skyrim.esm|02C8D7: I'd simply like to talk to you.
Skyrim.esm|02C8DA: I am that which you have been seeking. Your efforts are in vain. It has already begun. But those who have sent you have not told you what they seek. What you seek.
Skyrim.esm|02C8DC: Well, I'm quite sure I've never been interrupted like this before... the audacity!...
Skyrim.esm|02C8E0: Now, you are going to speak to this... Monk... and find out why he is here, and then he will be removed from College grounds.
Skyrim.esm|02C8E1: I'm afraid I must intrude. It is urgent that I speak with your associate immediately.
Skyrim.esm|02C8E2: The Augur? Really? Well, it's certainly not my place to say anything about it. I think you'd better go see Mirabelle Ervine.
Skyrim.esm|02C8E3: The what? No. No, I'm quite sure I have no idea what you're talking about. I suggest you mind your own business and return to whatever it is you do here.
Skyrim.esm|02C8E4: Well now, there's a name I haven't heard in some time. My goodness, it's been years since I've spoken with him. I suppose he's still down in the Midden, but I haven't checked. Are you going to see him? Do tell him "hello" for me, won't you?
Skyrim.esm|02C8E5: Yes, I've no doubt of its gravity. This, however, is a matter that cannot wait.
Skyrim.esm|02C8E6: Arch-Mage Aren is already hard at work, and hopefully we'll have more information soon.
Skyrim.esm|02C8E7: This is most inappropriate! We are involved in serious research here!
Skyrim.esm|02CC3D: I don't know. When I asked her where she was going she just muttered "Where the end began." Here, take the Goldenglow Estate Deed as proof. And when you speak to Mercer, tell him I'm worth more to him alive.
Skyrim.esm|02CC3F: Consider it a gift to ensure your silence. That thing seems to be bringing me nothing but trouble anyway. Karliah didn't even want it, she wanted to keep the sale a secret. I can see how well that went.
Skyrim.esm|02CC40: Now, there's no need to do anything rash... This isn't as bad as it seems. I was going to tell Mercer about everything, honestly! Please... he'll have me killed!
Skyrim.esm|02CC41: Did Gulum-Ei give up any information on our buyer?
Skyrim.esm|02CC42: No, it.. it can't be. I haven't heard that name in decades. This is grave news indeed, she's someone I hoped to never cross paths with again.
Skyrim.esm|02CC43: Karliah and I were like partners. I went with her on every heist. We watched each other's backs. I know her techniques, her skills. If she kills me, there'll be no one left that could possibly catch her. If only we knew where she was...
Skyrim.esm|02CC45: All right, all right. It's Karliah... her name is Karliah.
Skyrim.esm|02CC46: Yes, I'm going with you and together we're going to kill her. Prepare yourself and meet me at the ruins as soon as you can. We can't let her slip through our fingers.
Skyrim.esm|02CC47: Help... ? No, no! Look I didn't even know it was her until after she contacted me. Please, you have to believe me!
Skyrim.esm|02CC48: I see you wish to be reasonable, perhaps I misjudged you. The name of the person you want is Karliah.
Skyrim.esm|02CC49: Mercer never told you about her? Karliah is the thief responsible for murdering the previous Guild Master, Gallus. Now she's after Mercer.
Skyrim.esm|02CC4A: I have a long-standing arrangement with the Dark Brotherhood. If I need someone in the Guild taken care of, we do it ourselves. We both agree it's best to keep these matters in house.
Skyrim.esm|02CC4B: Karliah destroyed everything this Guild stood for. She murdered my predecessor in cold blood and betrayed the Guild. After we discovered what she'd done, we spent months trying to track her down, but she just vanished.
Skyrim.esm|02CC4D: There's only one place that could be. The place where she murdered Gallus... a ruin called Snow Veil Sanctum. We have to go out there before she disappears again.
Skyrim.esm|02CC4E: They were a secret cabal of the three top rogues in the Thieves Guild. No one knew their identities. Some people say they watched over the group, others say they were reserved for the most elite jobs. I don't really know. It wasn't until recently that I heard Mercer Frey and this Dark Elf named Karliah were a part of that group.
Skyrim.esm|02CC76: I've heard stories about the wonders of the great Temple in Markarth. But I never dreamed that I would even get to see it. I am honored to be called for this duty. Please, lead on.
Skyrim.esm|02CC83: I hope we reach Markarth soon.
Skyrim.esm|02CC84: Please, open the door. I need to get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|02D4E9: You see, you're not well! Have a seat and I'll fetch you some tonic.
Skyrim.esm|02D4EB: Master, you're far too old for this sort of journey. We don't know what's inside...
Skyrim.esm|02D4EC: I'm so close to the answer...
Skyrim.esm|02D55F: You really think I can be a blacksmith someday, papa? As good as Eorlund Gray-Mane? Or maybe... even as good as you?
Skyrim.esm|02D5C2: Oh, I know it! You keep working hard, like you been doing, and you could be the greatest blacksmith Skyrim has ever seen.
Skyrim.esm|02D751: Like I said, I'm glad to help in any way I can. Help yourself to whatever you need, within reason.
Skyrim.esm|02D752: In joining the Legion, you'll be taking an oath binding you to the service of the Emperor and thus to every citizen of the Empire. Are you prepared to make that commitment?
Skyrim.esm|02D755: Just head north - you can't miss it. It's the capital of Whiterun Hold, the biggest and the best of the nine holds of Skyrim. But don't take my word for it.
Skyrim.esm|02D756: Cross the river and then head north. You'll see it, just past the falls. When you get to Whiterun just keep going up. When you get to the top of the hill, you're at Dragonsreach, the Jarl's palace.
Skyrim.esm|02D89A: An excellent question. Vittoria Vici is the first cousin to our dear Emperor, Titus Mede II. Vici has obvious Imperial connections. Her husband has ties to the Stormcloaks. Their union is a step toward reconciliation. So if there's a murder at the wedding... Not only will it stall the peace process - it will send shockwaves throughout the entire Empire. The Emperor's hand will be forced. He'll have to travel to Skyrim to deal with the aftermath... and he'll find the Dark Brotherhood waiting.
Skyrim.esm|02D89C: Soon. I'm not exactly sure when. But not to worry - the ceremony itself is being followed up by a public banquet that will last for several days. Both Vittoria and her husband will be there for the duration, and everyone in Solitude is invited to attend. You'll have plenty of opportunity.
Skyrim.esm|02D8A1: Best put your other tasks aside. The real challenge is about to begin.
Skyrim.esm|02D8A2: Splendid. Then we're ready to begin. Or, more specifically, you're ready to begin. After all, you're the one the Night Mother spoke to. But by Sithis, I hope I'm making the right choice here. You may be an effective killer, but your command of guile leaves much to be desired. The sealed letter you delivered to me was mysteriously unsealed upon its arrival. I can only assume you've read its contents. Sloppy... But moving on to business. As you probably already know from reading the letter... you're going to a wedding.
Skyrim.esm|02D8A3: Splendid. Then we're ready to begin. Or, more specifically, you're ready to begin. After all, you're the one the Night Mother spoke to. Now, then. I hope you have something nice to wear. Because you're going to a wedding.
Skyrim.esm|02D8A4: This is a public kill. How you do it is entirely up to you. Arrow to the throat? Knife in the belly? Your choice, so long as it's loud and messy. Because of the current political climate, people are going to assume the murder is related to the bad blood between the Legion and Stormcloaks. In any event, when Vici dies, it's going to be complete pandemonium. Best have your escape route planned out in advance.
Skyrim.esm|02D8A5: Well, more like the public reception. It should be a lovely affair. You'll mingle with the guests, eat some cake... stab the bride. Oh yes. You've got to kill the bride. At her wedding. And they say romance is dead.
Skyrim.esm|02D8A6: Hmph. He had a trip to Skyrim planned for months, and canceled at the last minute. Much to his cousin's dismay. Seems the Emperor realized his presence would necessitate a more direct role in the ongoing hostilities. A role he was, obviously, unwilling to take. All that will change with his cousin's murder. Everyone will think it was motivated by the conflict between the Imperial Legion and Stormcloaks. And the Emperor will be forced to come clean up the mess.
Skyrim.esm|02D8CA: If you need any help with Destruction spells, please let me know.
Skyrim.esm|02D8CB: I'm sure when the time for promotions comes, they'll go to the most deserving mage.
Skyrim.esm|02D8CC: Don't you think I'd make a wonderful master wizard? Maybe even Arch-Mage someday?
Skyrim.esm|02D8CD: It's a small group of us here at the College. Favors don't go unremembered. Neither do affronts.
Skyrim.esm|02D8CE: Another day, another opportunity to learn!
Skyrim.esm|02D8CF: Someday the College may be fully accepted by the Nords. I hope that my work as Arch-Mage may help.
Skyrim.esm|02D8D0: Like it or not, this college is a direct influence on the public's impressions of magic and those that use it.
Skyrim.esm|02D8D1: It is up to each of us to present ourselves publicly as maturely as possible, and to remain above petty local squabbles.
Skyrim.esm|02DC9F: A legendary bottle, forged in the days when Skyrim was just starting its turn to ice. A small container, made of the magically infused snow that first fell on the Throat of the World. It is said that the Phial will replenish whatever fluid is placed inside of it. For an alchemist like myself, it is the most pure expression of my art. To create from nothingness is... poetry.
Skyrim.esm|02DCA0: Bah. If there was a tonic that could help me, I would have found it by now...
Skyrim.esm|02DCA1: I have spent my entire life searching for it. It brought me all the way to these frozen reaches from the warm embrace of Summerset Isle. Entire years spent in libraries. Seeking out tiny villages with local legends that contain but a whisper of a hint of the Phial. I even named my shop after it, hoping that it might attract anyone who had heard of it. And now it's within my grasp... but the Eight, it seems, have chosen me for their amusement. ... for in my current condition, I'll never be able to lay my hands on it.
Skyrim.esm|02DCA2: You would do that? It's good to know there are some people out there who are willing to help an old man. It's buried with its maker, Curalmil, in a long forsaken cave to the west of here. Curalmil was a crafty one, even in death. You would need the skills of a master alchemist to reach its resting place. Luckily for you, I've already made the mixture. Here, take it. Please... don't dally. I've wasted enough time arguing with my useless assistant here.
Skyrim.esm|02DCA3: May the Eight guide you to the Phial...
Skyrim.esm|02DCA4: Please hurry -- I fear my master doesn't have much time left.
Skyrim.esm|02DD26: I assume you have proof? Can't go making accusations like that without proof.
Skyrim.esm|02DD27: Alva? Huh. Didn't think she had it in her.
Skyrim.esm|02DD28: So it's true. The traitorous bitch! Morthal owes you a debt. Here. You were promised a reward for solving the crime, but I need one more favor from you. Morthal is still in danger. The journal mentions Movarth, a master vampire I thought was destroyed a century ago. I'll gather together some able-bodied warriors to clean out Movarth's Lair. They'll be waiting outside for you to lead them.
Skyrim.esm|02DDE4: What? Were you hoping to claim this house for yourself? Well, the Aretinos always liked me, so I think I'm family enough to take up here.
Skyrim.esm|02DDE5: The milk drinkers in this town wouldn't last five minutes in a real battle.
Skyrim.esm|02DDE6: Rumor holds that that Aretino boy brought an assassin here. Sounds like nonsense to me.
Skyrim.esm|02DE36: I'm going with you whether you like it or not.
Skyrim.esm|02DE37: You want in there? Here's my spare key. Take whatever you want.
Skyrim.esm|02DE38: We were supposed to find a sacrifice. Human sacrifice. I told her I couldn't do it and she attacked. I've done horrible things living here, but never taken an innocent life. I couldn't just find some person for them to kill.
Skyrim.esm|02DE39: The Initiation. My mother is going to become one of those things. I can't be a part of that. Gods, I can't run away from this. I have to stop her.
Skyrim.esm|02DE3A: Let's get up there before Mother finds out what we're doing.
Skyrim.esm|02E0F1: The mine will be running better than ever thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|02E0F2: Sorry, the mine's dry right now.
Skyrim.esm|02E0F3: I hate seeing a good mine go to waste like that, you know?
Skyrim.esm|02E0F4: If you're looking for supplies, I'd advise heading to Riften.
Skyrim.esm|02E0F5: You think you're tough, huh? Wandering Skyrim is a picnic compared to tunnel crawling!
Skyrim.esm|02E0F6: The real work happens in places like this. Blood and sweat is what makes the world turn.
Skyrim.esm|02E0F8: Sure, I used to be a soldier... but that was child's play compared to cracking rock.
Skyrim.esm|02E0FA: Came all the way out here to see us, did you?
Skyrim.esm|02E0FD: Making that trip to Darkwater Crossing was a huge help. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|02E0FE: I trust my parents are well?
Skyrim.esm|02E0FF: Aye, I see the look you're giving me. Don't worry, I won't bite.
Skyrim.esm|02E100: You're looking at the best miner in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|02E101: I'd wager I could crack rock faster than anyone. Even you.
Skyrim.esm|02E102: Ha! You showed those spiders a thing or two!
Skyrim.esm|02E11A: We're married. I guess we'll need to decide where we're living, won't we? You can always stay with me.
Skyrim.esm|02E142: I'm hoping to run my own inn someday.
Skyrim.esm|02E143: Please stop looking at me like that.
Skyrim.esm|02E144: First the war and now there's talk of dragons. What's next?
Skyrim.esm|02E147: You mentioned something like that... when you were running off with my goat! Tell you what - you bring back my goat, and maybe I'll give a damn about your staff.
Skyrim.esm|02E148: You said a whole lot of nonsense about selling Gleda to hire a priestess for some big event. There was a bit of talk about some staff you had in safe keeping. Never said where, but you sure didn't have it with you!
Skyrim.esm|02E149: It's good to see a fresh face. You wouldn't have happen to come from Whiterun would you?
Skyrim.esm|02E14E: If you're looking for potions you should talk to my aunt, Angeline Morrard.
Skyrim.esm|02E154: Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything.
Skyrim.esm|02E15A: I bet my papa could beat you up.
Skyrim.esm|02E15E: I've got weapons for givin', and armor for gettin'!
Skyrim.esm|02E160: Seems there's no end to the needs of the Imperials. But what can I help you with?
Skyrim.esm|02E163: Why, hello! Here to buy? If you have to ask the price, you might be in the wrong store.
Skyrim.esm|02E164: We provide clothing for all of Solitude's most... influential residents. But for you, I'll make an exception.
Skyrim.esm|02E168: Oh, what delight. Another charming customer...
Skyrim.esm|02E169: Oh, let me just drop everything to help you. I don't mind. Really.
Skyrim.esm|02E16D: Welcome to the Winking Skeever, friend.
Skyrim.esm|02E16E: If you need a good rumor or two, I think I can oblige...
Skyrim.esm|02E170: Traitors, civil wars, dragons. Bah. Those troubles don't cross my doorway. So sit, relax.
Skyrim.esm|02E175: Don't mind me. I'm just running errands for the old man.
Skyrim.esm|02E176: Papa says I won't see Roggvir anymore, but he wouldn't tell me why not.
Skyrim.esm|02E177: Spare some gold for a veteran?
Skyrim.esm|02E178: Plenty of fresh fish for ya.
Skyrim.esm|02E17A: I don't have anything to say.
Skyrim.esm|02E17B: Welcome to Solitude. Home of the arts here in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|02E17C: I'm Pantea Ateia, the master vocalist.
Skyrim.esm|02E17E: Hello, yes? I'm the Dean of History. And scribe work, when I can find some quiet time.
Skyrim.esm|02E17F: I wish only to graduate and enroll with the Legion. My drums will lead our troops to victory.
Skyrim.esm|02E180: Lady Ateia says my voice is second only to hers. But she's selling me short.
Skyrim.esm|02E181: Dean Inge says a bard can never have enough practice. So, would you like a tune?
Skyrim.esm|02E183: Unless we have specific business, I'm not interested.
Skyrim.esm|02E184: The Imperials are good for business, and business is good for Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|02E185: Welcome to the center of the world. Or at least my brother Erikur's world...
Skyrim.esm|02E186: Speak if you must, but mind your tongue. I do not suffer fools gladly.
Skyrim.esm|02E187: Mmm... Spiced Wine for ya? It's a family recipe.
Skyrim.esm|02E188: I'm too old to work, but that don't make me too old to drink.
Skyrim.esm|02E189: Hmm? Sorry, I was lost in thought. I'm planning my wedding, you see. So many details to work out.
Skyrim.esm|02E18A: You look like you could do with a ripe apple. Or a tomato, cabbage, or leek?
Skyrim.esm|02E18B: Not much work being a jailor. Long as you can ignore the pleading and all that.
Skyrim.esm|02E18C: With the passing of my dear husband Torygg, the weighty position of jarl has fallen to me. Please direct questions to my steward.
Skyrim.esm|02E18D: I am the jarl's steward. If you have court business to tend to, you talk to me.
Skyrim.esm|02E18E: Please address all questions to the steward, Falk Firebeard.
Skyrim.esm|02E18F: Just arrive in the city? Is it true what they say, then? There are dragons about?
Skyrim.esm|02E190: Be quick. I have little patience for mundane concerns.
Skyrim.esm|02E191: Jarls come and jarls go, but a good cook stays for a lifetime. And I'm a very, very good cook.
Skyrim.esm|02E193: Watch your feet. We just cleaned there!
Skyrim.esm|02E194: Hello, my child. Arkay watch over you.
Skyrim.esm|02E196: Blessings of the Eight Divines upon you. How may I help?
Skyrim.esm|02E197: Blessings to you. The Divines are listening.
Skyrim.esm|02E198: I have no business with you. Leave me.
Skyrim.esm|02E199: You, talk to egg-brothers. Deeja has no words for you.
Skyrim.esm|02E19A: Dock work is not good work. Better than Black Marsh, though. Much better.
Skyrim.esm|02E19B: Oh my, old Ma'zaka has a visitor!
Skyrim.esm|02E19D: Ah, yet another new face. I'm from Hammerfell, you see. I can hardly keep track of all the people I've been meeting.
Skyrim.esm|02E19E: Make it quick. I get enough delays from politicians.
Skyrim.esm|02E1A0: You here for my next interrogation? I got nothing but time...
Skyrim.esm|02E1A1: You got business at the stables?
Skyrim.esm|02E1A2: Is this about the stables? My husband, Geimund, handles the business.
Skyrim.esm|02E1A3: Looking for a horse? You'll want to talk to my father, Geimund.
Skyrim.esm|02E1A6: My duty is simple - oversee the Thalmor Justiciars, and make sure they perform their duties admirably.
Skyrim.esm|02E1A8: Oh, hello. Have a seat, will you?
Skyrim.esm|02E1A9: Mama said I could help with the goats!
Skyrim.esm|02E1AA: The flame of my master burns low. Without him, we are all lost and forever gray... Please, help us!
Skyrim.esm|02E2C8: We have to go through town and across the bridge to get to Bleak Falls Barrow. You can see it from here, though. The mountain just over the buildings.
Skyrim.esm|02E2D5: I handle the fishin', numbers and bedlam jobs... the ones with the more personal touch. If break-ins are more your thing, go talk to Vex.
Skyrim.esm|02E2D6: Let me make this plain and simple. No more jobs until you pay Vex. No get to it.
Skyrim.esm|02E2D7: You're not getting anything until you pay the Guild back.
Skyrim.esm|02E2DA: I handle the burglary, shill, sweep and heist jobs. Most of them involve breaking and entering. If you don't like that kind of work, talk to Delvin. He runs the more up-close and personal jobs.
Skyrim.esm|02E2DB: I think you should finish that special assignment Delvin gave you, then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|02E34A: I guess we'd better try this way.
Skyrim.esm|02E34B: Go ahead. I'll follow your lead and watch your back.
Skyrim.esm|02E34C: You see what talking to them gets us?
Skyrim.esm|02E3CB: I don't know who you are, or what's going on here, but you ought to be ashamed of yourself.
Skyrim.esm|02E3CC: What's happening? Damn it, what are you doing?
Skyrim.esm|02E3CD: Get these things off of me!
Skyrim.esm|02E3CF: Cowards! Stealing a woman from her home! For shame!
Skyrim.esm|02E3D0: By the gods, when I get out of here you're dead! You hear me? Dead!
Skyrim.esm|02E3D2: Thank you! Thank you! I won't tell anyone what happened here, I swear it!
Skyrim.esm|02E3D3: Oh, gods, please! Let me live! I'll do anything you want!
Skyrim.esm|02E3D4: I... I can hear you talking out there. Please, let me go. I've done nothing to you.
Skyrim.esm|02E3D6: Is this about that raid last week? I told Holgrim there was no honor in killing sleeping men, but he wouldn't listen! It wasn't my fault, I swear!
Skyrim.esm|02E3D7: I'm just a soldier. Killing is my job! Surely you can't fault a man for doing his job?
Skyrim.esm|02E3D8: Please, oh please, I don't want to die...
Skyrim.esm|02E3D9: Ah, sweet freedom. Now that's more like it. You have my thanks, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|02E3DA: Whoever this is, clearly we got off on the wrong foot. Ah, but no worries. This is not the first time I have been bagged and dragged.
Skyrim.esm|02E3DB: Hello? Hello? Ah, I know you're listening.
Skyrim.esm|02E3DC: Come now. Whatever the problem, we can talk about it like civilized folk. Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|02E3DD: A man in my position makes lots of enemies. But what is a life-threatening situation if not an opportunity for mutual benefit? Hmmm?
Skyrim.esm|02E3E6: You'll let me go. I know you will. This is all just part of the game we play...
Skyrim.esm|02E3EC: Come on, show an old man what you got!
Skyrim.esm|02E493: The Old Hroldan Inn is still open, bless poor Eydis's memory.
Skyrim.esm|02E498: You'll be looking to rent Tiber Septim's room I take it?
Skyrim.esm|02E499: Old Hroldan is the stone that the Empire's history is built on.
Skyrim.esm|02E49B: Don't underestimate my son, Skuli. He may look young, but he can handle any drunks we get.
Skyrim.esm|02E49D: Skuli's father is off fighting for the Empire. I'm sure he's just fine.
Skyrim.esm|02E49F: This is the Old Hroldan Inn. Clean beds and strong drink.
Skyrim.esm|02E4A0: My mother's dead, but I won't close the inn. I've hired Leontius to run things.
Skyrim.esm|02E4A1: If you drink too much, you're out. No drunks.
Skyrim.esm|02E4A2: Changed the linens myself. They're fresh.
Skyrim.esm|02E51E: Yamarz was a fool. Always trying to scheme his way out of responsibility.
Skyrim.esm|02E523: Now, take Shagrol's hammer back to Largashbur, and we'll see about whipping the rest of them into shape.
Skyrim.esm|02E524: Well, the giants took care of him, and you took care of the giants. Two problems solved at once.
Skyrim.esm|02E525: But, you took care of him and the giants. Two problems solved at once.
Skyrim.esm|02E6AF: Got a shop owner with a very choice item a client wants to get his hands on if you do the dirty work.
Skyrim.esm|02E6D7: If you bring mead, you're welcome here! Even if you don't, you're still welcome!
Skyrim.esm|02E6D8: All this talk of dragons is making people antsy. Nonsense!
Skyrim.esm|02E6D9: I visited the meaderies of Riften once, as a lad. Wish I had their skill.
Skyrim.esm|02E6DA: Working our mine is dangerous work, but Dravynea keeps it going. Well, Dravynea and the promise of more mead.
Skyrim.esm|02E6DB: You've got a fine look about you. Come have a visit at the Braidwood Inn. Better than home!
Skyrim.esm|02E6DC: Welcome to Kynesgrove. If you want to do business here, you'll have to deal with me.
Skyrim.esm|02E6DD: If it's malachite you're after, you've come to the right place. I'll pay for any you dig up out of Steamscorch Mine.
Skyrim.esm|02E6DE: Kjeld speaks for Kynesgrove. Anyone who says otherwise is aching for a fight.
Skyrim.esm|02E6DF: Roggi and I do the work around here. My wife and brats just sit around on their brains wasting time.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E0: My wife is a good woman, but every man needs a little variety, you know?
Skyrim.esm|02E6E1: An outsider? No reason to stop in Kynesgrove. Keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E2: The mine's always on the verge of collapse. Good thing I'm around to keep it up.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E3: Morrowind used to be a lovely place, but you couldn't pay me to live there these days.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E4: Most people in Skyrim don't care for Dunmer. Roggi's the only one around here even half-decent.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E5: I should get over to Windhelm more. See how the other Morrowind refugees are getting along.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E6: Seen the remnants of the grove? That... well, that was me. Me and my sister.
Skyrim.esm|02E6E7: Came here to start a lumber mill. Who knew the Nords cared about a bunch of old trees?
Skyrim.esm|02E6E8: But... well, they did. Sacred, they said. So we work the mines until we've worked it off. Fair enough, I guess.
Skyrim.esm|02E6EB: Oh, great, another wanderer. Slumming it on your way to Windhelm?
Skyrim.esm|02E6EC: Wouldn't even be here if it weren't for my idiot sister.
Skyrim.esm|02E6EE: I can't believe she dragged me all the way to this snowball. I'd rather be broke in Cyrodiil than broke here.
Skyrim.esm|02E6EF: If these Nords don't let up soon, I might just make a break for it. At least Cyrodiil is warmer than this.
Skyrim.esm|02E6F0: Did you see my husband already? I'm sorry... he can come off a bit rough at times.
Skyrim.esm|02E6F1: I hope you'll stay for a bit. We could certainly use the business.
Skyrim.esm|02E6F2: This is really quite a nice town when you get down to it. Just look at the view!
Skyrim.esm|02E6F4: Got some nice comfy beds at the Braidwood Inn, if you're looking to flop down somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|02E6F5: I've never seen you before. Leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|02E6F6: My pa runs this town, and you're going to be in big trouble if you mess with me.
Skyrim.esm|02E6F8: You don't look so tough. I'll bet I could take you.
Skyrim.esm|02E7B2: Got a good one here involving a shop, a waylaid shipment of goods and an angry client. You fill in the blanks. You want in?
Skyrim.esm|02E8E1: Thank you, hero. Now I may finally rest. Take Hevnoraak's iron mask. It may be of use to you, and serve as some reward for your feat here.
Skyrim.esm|02E8E2: With those three vessels, Hevnoraak can restore himself. Find them before he can.
Skyrim.esm|02E8E3: Kill him now, while we still can!
Skyrim.esm|02E9B2: I have just the place. The owner likes to stash some lovely trinkets in his strongbox like this beauty. Can you get it for us?
Skyrim.esm|02E9D6: The owner of this place just inherited an heirloom from a relative. You want in?
Skyrim.esm|02E9FD: Sure. A client's relative lost this item in a gambling match and they're paying us to get it back. So much for fair play, huh?
Skyrim.esm|02EA00: The owner of this shop stiffed us on their last protection payment, but we're taking it anyway. You want to help us on this one?
Skyrim.esm|02EA02: 'Jiit caravan brought this shop a nice piece from Elsweyr. Think you can break into their strongbox and get it for us?
Skyrim.esm|02EA04: Got a client in desperate need for this item. He won't say what it's for, and we don't care. Can you take this one?
Skyrim.esm|02EA38: ... and may we one day rejoin them in eternity.
Skyrim.esm|02EA39: May the spirit of Lavinia and all those who have left this world and its suffering know the beloved serenity of Aetherius...
Skyrim.esm|02EA3A: But he willingly gave up this existence that we might better understand the vagaries of life and death.
Skyrim.esm|02EA3B: Do you not know the Daedric Lord of the Hunt? He revels in the chase, and also gave the "gift" of lycanthropy to mortals. A powerful force, not to be crossed. As I learned too late.
Skyrim.esm|02EA3C: The god Arkay was once like us, bound to winding mortality.
Skyrim.esm|02EA3D: It is through the ebb and flow of this cosmic tide that we find renewal and, in the end, peace.
Skyrim.esm|02EA57: You like creepin' around in the shadows? Magic'll work just as good there as anywhere else.
Skyrim.esm|02EA58: Talk to Babette about potions. She's the creepy little girl - can't miss her.
Skyrim.esm|02EA59: Yes, yes. I'm your brother and I'm supposed to greet you respectfully. So "hello." There, satisfied?
Skyrim.esm|02EA5B: Don't let my appearance throw you off. I'm older than you. A lot older. Getting bitten by a vampire when you're ten will do that to a girl.
Skyrim.esm|02EA5C: It's been quite a boon, looking like a child. No one ever suspects a thing.
Skyrim.esm|02EA5D: I could brew some potions for you. Or train you in Alchemy.
Skyrim.esm|02EA5E: You looking to buy some potions? Or maybe I could help train you to make your own?
Skyrim.esm|02EA60: Let me guess - Astrid failed to mention I'm her husband? She has a tendency to do that. Doesn't want the others thinking she plays favorites.
Skyrim.esm|02EA61: You're part of a family now, hamshank. I trust you... you trust me. Got it?
Skyrim.esm|02EA62: Don't take offense to the nicknames, morsel. I'm a werewolf. Hard not to think of you as a snack.
Skyrim.esm|02EA66: In the Dark Brotherhood, I found acceptance, love, and my life's true purpose.
Skyrim.esm|02EA67: Does death not come to us all? Is it not a certainty? I am, therefore, honored to be doom's herald. To play some part in a being's inevitable end.
Skyrim.esm|02EA68: Death is but the time to sleep forever, in the Void.
Skyrim.esm|02EA69: Sithis' whisper be upon you, brother.
Skyrim.esm|02EA6A: Sithis' whisper be upon you, sister.
Skyrim.esm|02EA6D: Me, I have been part of the Dark Brotherhood from the day I first hatched. I am a Shadowscale, you see. Born under the sign of the Shadow.
Skyrim.esm|02EA6E: I serve Astrid, and this Sanctuary. Life is simple, and good.
Skyrim.esm|02EA6F: The Shadowscales are all but forgotten. An ancient tradition, from another time.
Skyrim.esm|02EA74: We don't get many visitors through here, unless their headed up to High Hrothgar of course.
Skyrim.esm|02EBE1: By the Eight, I didn't think you could do it! Now maybe we can put all this behind us. Take this as a token of our gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|02EBEB: They say a strong drink, a sharp axe and a good woman are all a Nord needs to be happy. Two out of three's not bad.
Skyrim.esm|02EC2A: You want me to smile? What's there to smile about? Soldiers and dragons are going to be trampling all over the crops this year. I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|02EC2C: Oh, stop being such a grouch! Not every visitor to the farm wants to burn it down! Maybe they just want to have a nice supper with us, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|02EC2E: Damn Forsworn. Only a matter of time before they hit here, too.
Skyrim.esm|02EC2F: Left Hand Mine deals with iron, the perfect metal to dig with a war going on.
Skyrim.esm|02EC33: Kolskeggr, overrun with Forsworn, what next?
Skyrim.esm|02EC3C: Kolskeggr Mine is lost, friend. Forsworn killed everyone.
Skyrim.esm|02EC3D: Don't go to Kolskeggr. It's not safe.
Skyrim.esm|02EC3F: Gat and I are the only survivors.
Skyrim.esm|02EC42: Pavo and I had to fight our way out of Kolskeggr. Forsworn killed everyone else.
Skyrim.esm|02EC45: Poor Pavo and Gat. To have lived through a Forsworn attack.
Skyrim.esm|02EC46: My Erith has her father's eyes. She's all I have left of him.
Skyrim.esm|02EC49: Why is everyone so upset? If there's Forsworn, the Jarl will take care of them, right?
Skyrim.esm|02EC4A: Why do they call it Left Hand Mine? Can the miners use their right hands too?
Skyrim.esm|02EC4B: Have you met Torom? He's the best dog in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|02EC50: Oh, wipe that frown off your face Rogatus. You've been wearing that frown for over 50 years now.
Skyrim.esm|02EC52: You don't invite monsters into your home to have supper! I won't have them here, and I'm certainly not smiling for them!
Skyrim.esm|02EC54: I'm been farming this bit of land all my life.
Skyrim.esm|02EC55: My boy Leontius lives in Old Hroldan. Damn drunk.
Skyrim.esm|02EC56: Next thing you know there will be some kind of damn blight and undead will walk the land.
Skyrim.esm|02EC57: You ever have to farm land while there was a battle going on? I have.
Skyrim.esm|02EC58: Oh, what is this? A visitor? To our farm? Oh, do stay for a while, dear.
Skyrim.esm|02EC59: Now, you don't let Rogatus cause you any bother.
Skyrim.esm|02EC5A: Rogatus and I have been married over 50 years. He's always been a bit of a grouch.
Skyrim.esm|02EC5B: Our son Leontius moved to Old Hroldan nearly 30 years ago. I still miss him.
Skyrim.esm|02EC67: Shout for us, Dragonborn. Then we may have time for questions.
Skyrim.esm|02ED61: I just don't understand what kind of a man does that.
Skyrim.esm|02ED67: Show us the path to the prey.
Skyrim.esm|02ED68: Hircine chose you to lead the Great Hunt. So lead.
Skyrim.esm|02F186: All right. Keep up the good work.
Skyrim.esm|02F187: Let's make sure we're just as productive this month as we were last. If Bryling's happy, we're happy.
Skyrim.esm|02F189: We've got all the miners we need, so I hope you're not here looking for work.
Skyrim.esm|02F18A: Nothing here but the mine. Probably best you keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|02F18B: Pactur and I, we make sure the miners aren't stealing, and that the ore makes it to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|02F18D: Sorli and I are just here to make sure the mine is run proper.
Skyrim.esm|02F18E: Wouldn't ever have expected to wind up living in a place like this, but the money's good.
Skyrim.esm|02F18F: Don't bother the miners. They've got work to do.
Skyrim.esm|02F190: I'm in charge now that my parents are in Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|02F191: My parents keep a close eye on the miners. They can't be trusted too much.
Skyrim.esm|02F192: I hear there's been fighting in Skyrim. Rebellion, maybe. Wonder if our iron is being used for weapons.
Skyrim.esm|02F193: If you're here about the mine -- it's already got an owner, and she's not selling. If not, then please stay out of my workers' way.
Skyrim.esm|02F194: You get used to the cold after a while. The harder you work, the warmer you stay.
Skyrim.esm|02F195: Bryling owns this mine. I wouldn't have much to live for if she hadn't offered me a job.
Skyrim.esm|02F196: My job is my life. Whatever happened before doesn't matter.
Skyrim.esm|02F1A4: I'm just here to work the mine. Nothing else matters anymore.
Skyrim.esm|02F1A5: The outside world means little to us, and we'd like it to stay that way.
Skyrim.esm|02F1A6: When you work hard enough, really get into a rhythm, everything else just fades away.
Skyrim.esm|02F1A7: I'm just trying to make a living. I've no interest in trouble.
Skyrim.esm|02F1A8: My business is my own. Why can't others see that?
Skyrim.esm|02F1A9: The mine gives us purpose. Something many of us have wished for.
Skyrim.esm|02F1AA: Stonehills' warmth is a welcome change in this cold, bleak land.
Skyrim.esm|02F1AB: Teeba-Ei the hatchling never imagined working a mine in this cold place. Greater things were dreamt of.
Skyrim.esm|02F1EB: Cowards! We must kill the vampires. We have to make them pay!
Skyrim.esm|02F1EE: I want vengeance for my wife! Vengeance for Laelette! We're marching on Movarth's Lair whether you come with us or not.
Skyrim.esm|02F210: Is everything well with you, my scaly friend?
Skyrim.esm|02F211: Yes, well... That's all we ask of you. Carry on.
Skyrim.esm|02F212: Of course not. I never thought otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|02F213: I'm fine, father. Work is fine.
Skyrim.esm|02F216: Good, good. Keep up the hard work. It'll all pay off.
Skyrim.esm|02F217: Teeba-Ei is cold, but Teeba-Ei works hard as always.
Skyrim.esm|02F218: Good, good. It's honest work, son. No shame in that.
Skyrim.esm|02F219: Right. Well, you let me know if you need anything.
Skyrim.esm|02F2C5: We will see if you truly have the gift. Show us, Dragonborn. Let us taste of your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|02F38D: But the Night Mother is mother to all! It is her voice we follow! Her will! Would you dare risk disobedience? And surely... punishment?
Skyrim.esm|02F38E: But make no mistake. I am the leader of this Sanctuary. My word is law. Are we clear on that point?
Skyrim.esm|02F391: Oh, be quiet you great lumbering lapdog. The man has had a long journey. You can at least be civil.
Skyrim.esm|02F392: Oh yes, mistress. You're the "boss." For now.
Skyrim.esm|02F393: Oh, what a kind and wise wizard you are. Sure to earn our Lady's favor.
Skyrim.esm|02F395: You and the Night Mother are of course welcome here, Cicero. And you will be afforded the respect deserving of your position as Keeper.
Skyrim.esm|02F396: Oh yes, mistress. Perfectly! You're the boss.
Skyrim.esm|02F397: Oh, yes yes yes! Thank you, thank you, thank you!
Skyrim.esm|02F399: Mister Cicero, I for one am delighted you and the Night Mother have arrived. Your presence here signals a welcome return to tradition.
Skyrim.esm|02F57F: First of all, one of our men was captured by rebels who refuse to surrender him. So we're going to break him out.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A1: Impressive. Your Thu'um is precise. You show great promise, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A2: Master Einarth will now teach you "Ro," the second Word in Unrelenting Force.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A3: Without training, you have already taken the first steps towards projecting your Voice into a Thu'um, a Shout. Now let us see if you are willing and able to learn.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A4: We will perform your next trial in the courtyard. Follow Master Borri.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A5: So... a Dragonborn appears, at this moment in the turning of the age.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A6: Try it again. Breath and focus.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A7: Is this your best effort? Again.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A8: Come, Dragonborn. Attend to Master Borri.
Skyrim.esm|02F7A9: Now we will see how quickly you can master a new Shout.
Skyrim.esm|02F7AA: First, let us see if you truly are Dragonborn. Let us taste of your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|02F7AB: Stand next to me. Master Borri will open the gate. Use your Whirlwind Sprint to pass through before it closes.
Skyrim.esm|02F7D8: As high priest of the Eight Divines, I bid you welcome.
Skyrim.esm|02F7DA: You look troubled. Have you come seeking guidance?
Skyrim.esm|02F7DC: The Divines saved me from the streets, and they can save you from your trials, as well.
Skyrim.esm|02F7DE: Go away. I have more important things on my mind right now.
Skyrim.esm|02F7E0: You have no knowledge of who you speak to. Go away.
Skyrim.esm|02F7E1: You want knives? You want them in your belly? No? Then you stop talking to Deeja.
Skyrim.esm|02F7E4: What brings you all the way out here?
Skyrim.esm|02F7E6: Oh, for the salt spray, the creek of the mast, and the clash of sabers. Those were the days...
Skyrim.esm|02F7E8: My papa says I'm greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him! He says it means "smart," but my brother says he's wrong.
Skyrim.esm|02F7E9: Can you spare a septim? Lost an eye during the Great War, or I'd earn it myself...
Skyrim.esm|02F7EA: Them Imperials left me to die in Anvil, and I had to fight my way back home. Then they followed me back here!
Skyrim.esm|02F7EB: I've got some salmon to sell.
Skyrim.esm|02F7ED: I... I don't want to talk.
Skyrim.esm|02F7F0: Are you here to talk about my brother? Do you think his execution was justified?
Skyrim.esm|02F7F4: Seems there's always more to be done...
Skyrim.esm|02F7F5: I generally don't involve myself in politics. Good tales deal with the issues of the day, but a wise bard remains a neutral observer.
Skyrim.esm|02F7F6: Never forget, the most important job of any bard is to entertain. The day you lose your audience is the day you cut your lute strings.
Skyrim.esm|02F7F7: It's been my honor to perform at the Blue Palace on many occasions. His late lordship was quite the admirer.
Skyrim.esm|02F7F8: If you ask me, this damned rebellion can't end soon enough. I had to end my tour of Skyrim's courts. Too dangerous too travel.
Skyrim.esm|02F7F9: To be honest, I simply can't stand some of my students. Only Aia shows true potential for greatness. The rest are just wasting my time.
Skyrim.esm|02F7FA: I'm Dean of Lute at the Bards College, and no, I don't give private lessons.
Skyrim.esm|02F7FB: Only reason I'm so good with a lute is because I'm old. It takes a lifetime to master.
Skyrim.esm|02F7FD: My position, "Dean of History," actually covers a wide range of subjects. I teach poetry, writing, history, and the finer points of court life.
Skyrim.esm|02F800: It's an honor to have Viarmo for instruction on drum, but he's far too reserved for my tastes.
Skyrim.esm|02F801: Lisette's a fine lass, but when I join the Legion, she's not the type to wait for my return.
Skyrim.esm|02F802: Few can resist my charms. Even fewer are worthy of them.
Skyrim.esm|02F804: You're new here, right? Welcome to the Bards College. It's like a... musical museum.
Skyrim.esm|02F805: You know, I don't get the impression that I'm very well liked at the College...
Skyrim.esm|02F80A: When I was a young rascal, I broke a man's arm with a single punch. Course, I had a horseshoe hidden in my glove...
Skyrim.esm|02F80B: Bryling's obsession with honor and tradition is quaint, but politically irrelevant.
Skyrim.esm|02F80C: Half of the businesses in town owe me money, and I own most of the rest. I practically run Solitude already.
Skyrim.esm|02F80D: My duty is to keep the Jarl safe. Not play nursemaid to you sycophants.
Skyrim.esm|02F80E: Keep your weapon out of your hands, and we'll get along just fine.
Skyrim.esm|02F80F: Hello. I'm Gisli. But you can just call me Erikur's sister. Everyone does.
Skyrim.esm|02F810: Have you met my dear brother Erikur? The man's as trustworthy as a Khajiit goldmonger.
Skyrim.esm|02F811: Working for Erikur may be below my stature, but the compensation is... acceptable.
Skyrim.esm|02F812: Sybille Stentor has a grasp of magical theory that I would never have expected from a human. Even a Breton.
Skyrim.esm|02F813: Wanderer like you needs a little fire in the veins. Some spiced wine?
Skyrim.esm|02F814: If you see my father Octieve around, stay clear. He'll only ask you for money or mead.
Skyrim.esm|02F815: Being old's not so bad. Daughter keeps me fed, and my working days are done.
Skyrim.esm|02F816: You got nothin' better to do than bother an old man?
Skyrim.esm|02F817: Some say I got my position because my cousin is the Emperor. Nonsense. The man's given me nothing. He can't even be bothered to attend my wedding.
Skyrim.esm|02F818: I don't have time for anyone too lazy or cowardly to make their own way in this world.
Skyrim.esm|02F81A: An apple to keep you healthy? A tomato to keep you strong? Surely you need one or the other...
Skyrim.esm|02F81C: You stay out of trouble, now. The Castle Dour dungeon can break even the strongest will.
Skyrim.esm|02F81D: If you have business, please see Falk Firebeard, my steward.
Skyrim.esm|02F820: Do you have business with the court?
Skyrim.esm|02F826: I have so very many ways to deal with people. Very few of them pleasant.
Skyrim.esm|02F828: Everyone knows General Tullius wields the real power Solitude. Elisif? Ah, she's a figurehead. A puppet.
Skyrim.esm|02F829: Folks at court won't admit it, but Sybille Stentor scares us all to death.
Skyrim.esm|02F82B: Are you my gallant hero? Have you come to whisk me away, to a life of adventure and romance?
Skyrim.esm|02F82C: Got lots of things to take care of. What do you need?
Skyrim.esm|02F82E: May Arkay bless you with a long life, a proud death, and a restful grave.
Skyrim.esm|02FD3F: Wander no more, for you are among friends here, and safe.
Skyrim.esm|02FD42: Hello and welcome, my friend. How may I help you on this fine day?
Skyrim.esm|030094: Did you see the fires from Goldenglow Estate? I was afraid the whole place was going to burn to the ground!
Skyrim.esm|030095: If you're looking to purchase goods, I have none to sell. You should head into Riften instead.
Skyrim.esm|030096: Pretty quiet out here on the farm. My wife and I kind of like the solitude.
Skyrim.esm|030097: Vulwulf has been quite generous to let me take care of their farm considering I am not part of their family.
Skyrim.esm|030098: The Snow-Shod's are very trusting to let me run this place without their supervision.
Skyrim.esm|030099: I'm glad there are guards outside the farm. I don't think I could defend the place alone.
Skyrim.esm|0300E8: I try to think of it as coaxing the ore from the rock.
Skyrim.esm|0300E9: I keep hearing stories about Morrowind. Sounds like a wretched place.
Skyrim.esm|0300EA: I hear there's baked slaughterfish tonight.
Skyrim.esm|0300F0: Is there something I can help you with?
Skyrim.esm|0300F1: What's your trouble? Dasturn telling tales again?
Skyrim.esm|0300F2: We keep hearing that the war could come through here. I try not to worry about it.
Skyrim.esm|0300F3: It's tough to keep track of Hrefna. If her father wasn't such a snowback, he'd be around to help out.
Skyrim.esm|0300F5: Hi! I've never seen you around here before. Are you a miner too?
Skyrim.esm|0300F6: Sondas takes care of me when mom's not around.
Skyrim.esm|0300F8: Mom says the Empire is mean, but I don't think they're that bad.
Skyrim.esm|0300F9: The Stormcloaks are named after Ulfric. He's a bad man, but don't tell the soldiers that.
Skyrim.esm|0300FF: A traveler? I'll be you've seen some sights. We should swap tales sometime.
Skyrim.esm|030100: There's a gorgeous tree that grows right up through a cave to the northeast of here.
Skyrim.esm|030101: If you're heading to Kynesgrove, stick to the road. Some pretty dangerous caves between here and there.
Skyrim.esm|030102: Verner used to love exploring before we got married. Now he's become a bit of a milk drinker. Likes to stay home.
Skyrim.esm|030103: If you head straight west from where the White River splits towards Whiterun, you can get a look at the Cradlecrush giants.
Skyrim.esm|030104: You looking for work? We could use an extra hand in the mine. Just bring me any Corundum you dig up.
Skyrim.esm|030105: Annekke used to help more with the mining, but nowadays she's a bit of a bounder.
Skyrim.esm|030106: I do love my wife. Just wish she'd get her head out of the clouds.
Skyrim.esm|030107: If you want some information about the area, Annekke knows it better than anyone.
Skyrim.esm|030108: Sometimes I feel like Annekke cares more about finding new rocks than being with me.
Skyrim.esm|030127: That's right. College of Winterhold, just north of town. Hard to miss.
Skyrim.esm|030143: How do you think the crops are coming, dear?
Skyrim.esm|030144: Terrible. Only a matter of time before soldiers come and burn all my hard work into dust!
Skyrim.esm|030145: Look at the sky. Be a terrible day tomorrow. I know it.
Skyrim.esm|030148: No. I have enough trouble without more mouths to feed! They're the Jarl's men, they can eat supper at the keep.
Skyrim.esm|030149: Still shaken from all the fighting, my friend. It's been too long since I had to kill someone.
Skyrim.esm|03014A: Yes it can. I haven't been wrong in 50 years, and I won't be wrong tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|03014E: Maybe we could invite some of those nice guards over. You know, for some supper?
Skyrim.esm|03014F: Oh, Rogatus! Maybe tomorrow will be pleasant for once, hmm? It can't be terrible every day!
Skyrim.esm|030258: Dravin! One of the soldiers I spoke to on the way home said you threatened them. What's come over you?
Skyrim.esm|030259: That doesn't matter. All it would take is a stray wolf or a bandit crossing your path... I can't bare to lose you after everything we've done.
Skyrim.esm|03025A: All you'll accomplish by threatening them is ending up in the Riften dungeon. You're lucky they didn't take you seriously.
Skyrim.esm|03025B: I don't care! Just stay away from the soldiers and keep to your business. It's the only way we'll make it through this.
Skyrim.esm|03025C: I can't stand them, they represent everything I hate about Skyrim. They're tearing the place apart!
Skyrim.esm|03025D: If it puts your mind at ease, I'll take it with me. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|030262: Now that you've brought Dravin's bow back, he's back to his crabby old self. For better or for worse, I still thank you.
Skyrim.esm|030264: You'll have to forgive my husband's manners. He's not used to pleasantries.
Skyrim.esm|030265: The war's been hard on the farm and hard on Dravin. We don't know what to expect from one day to the next.
Skyrim.esm|030266: Someday, I hope we can get away from here... perhaps move back to Morrowind. This isn't at all what we expected.
Skyrim.esm|030267: Husband, you worry far too much. We are within an hour's walk of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|030268: Synda... you left your dagger behind again yesterday. I told you, the road isn't safe.
Skyrim.esm|030269: Sorry, Marise. I just can't sit back and watch them treat us like a resource. This is our home!
Skyrim.esm|03029E: I wish you didn't hate him so much... I wish the whole family could be together again.
Skyrim.esm|03029F: There is a copse of elms to the east. We could really use that wood.
Skyrim.esm|0302A0: Just be careful, Gralnach. That copse doesn't look safe. If you see any trouble, you run right home.
Skyrim.esm|0302A1: That's because there's nothing to tell. He left us to fend for ourselves and that's all you need to know.
Skyrim.esm|0302A2: Mother, I finished cutting at the northern stand. Where do I move on to now?
Skyrim.esm|0302A3: Despite what you discovered it's still comforting to know my husband is at peace.
Skyrim.esm|0302A4: Men are utter pigs... they only care about one thing.
Skyrim.esm|0302A5: If I ever catch my husband in bed with another woman, he's going to find himself homeless.
Skyrim.esm|0302A8: I hope you're not here to start some trouble. I'll fight you if I have to.
Skyrim.esm|0302A9: It's just me and my mother at the mill here. My father is gone. We don't know what happened to him.
Skyrim.esm|0302AA: My mother taught me the millwork trade... you know, which trees to cut and which ones to pass up.
Skyrim.esm|0302AB: I'm not afraid of anyone. Put a sword in my hand and I could defend this place if I had to!
Skyrim.esm|0302AC: Millwork sure is boring, but at least I don't have to work in a nasty mine.
Skyrim.esm|0302AD: I was hoping to head into Riften for supplies, but I can do that after I'm done with the elms.
Skyrim.esm|0302AE: Gralnach! I told you not to go through your father's things!
Skyrim.esm|0302AF: I'm sorry mother, I just want to know more about him. You never tell me anything!
Skyrim.esm|0302CE: True, I may look the part of dashing swordsman, but I prefer the art of wit to that of warfare.
Skyrim.esm|0302CF: Your typical Solitude wench is like the city itself. Stormy exterior, but ah the charms that lie within...
Skyrim.esm|0302D0: If you're looking for any archery or fletching supplies, you should see Fihada. I'm just the apprentice.
Skyrim.esm|0302D1: Fletching suits me. It's difficult, and sometimes tedious. But a well made arrow strikes forth like the fury of a god.
Skyrim.esm|0302D2: I trust this is something important?
Skyrim.esm|0302D6: The General and I have our disagreements, but he's the best hope for both the Empire and Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0302D7: Many of my countrymen joined the rebels. They can't see the big picture. Skyrim needs the Empire as much as it needs us.
Skyrim.esm|0302D8: I stand with the Stormcloaks. You Imperial dogs will never break my will. Never!
Skyrim.esm|0302D9: Don't think I don't know what's coming.
Skyrim.esm|0302DA: Work hard for the future and leave the past be. That's my motto.
Skyrim.esm|0302DB: With a wife like Fridrika and a son like Horm, I'm the luckiest man in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0302DC: I really don't mean to be rude, but I don't think there's much I can help you with.
Skyrim.esm|0302DD: My family's weathered its share of problems, but we don't dwell on the past.
Skyrim.esm|0302DE: I grew up tending some of these horses. They're almost family.
Skyrim.esm|0302DF: When I was a boy, there was a year we couldn't afford enough feed. So I snuck my dinner out to the stables, and gave it to the horse.
Skyrim.esm|0302E0: Come in, come in! I was just about to take a break.
Skyrim.esm|0302E1: Living outside the walls is a risk, aye. But at least the guards don't come nosin' around.
Skyrim.esm|0302E2: We had some trouble, while back. Stormcloak tried to set the mill on fire. But we sorted her out.
Skyrim.esm|0302E3: Out here at work, Hjorunn treats me like an equal. Better than most in town.
Skyrim.esm|0302E4: Any day is a good one, long as I don't have to go into town. But some days, Hjorunn's too drunk...
Skyrim.esm|0302E5: The Empire, in its wisdom, has come to recognize Talos worship as a barbaric remnant of a bygone age.
Skyrim.esm|0302E6: Talos was a heroic man, but not a god. It pains the Altmer that we must remind our younger cousins of the difference.
Skyrim.esm|0302E7: We only want what is best for Tamriel. Surely, the rebels will accept peace when they realize this.
Skyrim.esm|0302E8: I could swear I've seen you somewhere before...
Skyrim.esm|0302E9: My husband handles the indoor work. Just as well - he'd only hurt himself out here.
Skyrim.esm|0302EA: It's hard work keeping Solitude fed, but I wouldn't trust any other family to do it.
Skyrim.esm|0302EB: I was a much younger man when I married Katla. Now I can hardly keep up with her.
Skyrim.esm|0302EC: My brothers would have been appalled to see a man outdone by his wife, but not me. Without Katla, this farm would fall apart.
Skyrim.esm|0302ED: You're a guest. And father says we have to be polite to guests.
Skyrim.esm|0302EE: Father says I don't have the knack for reading, but mama says I won't need it to work the farm.
Skyrim.esm|0302EF: Wait, hear my plea! My master, he is lost between worlds and I cannot bring him back!
Skyrim.esm|0302F0: Why does everyone ignore me? Why do you turn your heads? Why will no one help me?
Skyrim.esm|0302F1: Do you have business with the East Empire Company?
Skyrim.esm|0302F2: I will thank you not to interrupt me in my tasks.
Skyrim.esm|030309: Stand next to me, and prepare your Whirlwind Sprint. Master Borri will give the signal.
Skyrim.esm|03030A: Follow me. Master Borri will test your Whirlwind Sprint.
Skyrim.esm|03030E: You must feel the Word within yourself.
Skyrim.esm|03030F: You learn quickly, as only a Dragonborn can.
Skyrim.esm|030311: Your quick mastery of a new Thu'um is... astonishing. I'd heard the stories of the abilities of Dragonborn, but to see it for myself...
Skyrim.esm|030314: No. Indeed not. But beware that your skill does not outstrip your wisdom.
Skyrim.esm|030315: You are now ready for your last trial. Retrieve the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller, our founder, from his tomb in the ancient fane of Ustengrav. Remain true to the Way of the Voice, and you will return.
Skyrim.esm|030316: Masters Einarth, Borri, and Wulfgar will each demonstrate a Shout. Make your choice. Don't worry, though. You will find all three Words in Ustengrav.
Skyrim.esm|030317: Dragons have the inborn ability to learn and project their Voice. Dragons also are able to absorb the power of their slain brethren. A few mortals are born with similar abilities -- whether a gift or a curse has been a matter of debate down through the centuries. What you have already learned in a few days took even the most gifted of us years to achieve. Some believe that Dragonborn are sent into the world by the gods, at times of great need. We will speak more of that later, when you are ready.
Skyrim.esm|03031E: Thanks for the help. Not many willing to take on the Forsworn these days.
Skyrim.esm|0303CF: Boring? That food is paying for everything. If we lose even a single crop, we could starve or be forced to beg. I won't have it!
Skyrim.esm|0303D0: All right, I understand. I'll do it tomorrow, okay? Just get off my back.
