Skyrim.esm|0303D1: Painting won't put food on the table, but our crops will. Be patient Aduri... it will happen someday, I promise.
Skyrim.esm|0303D2: I'm sorry, Aduri, not this year. Perhaps next year after the harvest, I don't know. There's so much to do.
Skyrim.esm|0303D3: It's not going to be next year, nor the year after that! You just care about this farm more than you care about me!
Skyrim.esm|0303D6: I don't suppose you could come back another time? I'm very busy.
Skyrim.esm|0303D8: I have a lot to do around the farm, so make it quick please. What is it you wanted?
Skyrim.esm|0303D9: This place is boring. Nothing but the same thing every day. Sigh.
Skyrim.esm|0303DA: I wish I could head to one of the capital cities... maybe even back to Mournhold. Anywhere but here.
Skyrim.esm|0303DB: One day I hope to become a painter, but at this rate it looks like it's never going to happen.
Skyrim.esm|0303DC: Can you imagine how wonderful it would be to set what you see on a canvas so you'd have that memory forever?
Skyrim.esm|0303DD: My sister keeps telling me that the farm keeps us alive, but I don't really call this living.
Skyrim.esm|0303DE: Have you decided if I can head up to Solitude yet? I hear it's the best place to meet someone who may be willing to teach me to paint.
Skyrim.esm|0303DF: Aduri, how come the fields haven't been tilled like I asked?
Skyrim.esm|0303E0: Because it's boring. The fields are going to do fine... you don't have to dote over them so much.
Skyrim.esm|0304E5: I'll just prepare the subject now...
Skyrim.esm|0304E8: I can't let you do this, mother. You have to die now!
Skyrim.esm|0304E9: Daughter. Who is this you have brought me?
Skyrim.esm|0304EB: I found a willing subject for you. Payment has already been discussed, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0304EC: Splendid! Please take a seat on the chair behind me, would you?
Skyrim.esm|0304ED: This is the last hagraven between us and my mother!
Skyrim.esm|030560: I'm grateful, Skaggi. I'm sorry my daughter gives you so much trouble.
Skyrim.esm|030563: But mom, it's so boring with just me and Torom. I was just asking Skaggi some questions.
Skyrim.esm|03056E: Skaggi tells me that you were bothering him today. Can't you and Torom just play by yourselves?
Skyrim.esm|03056F: You work hard, Daighre. Glad you have stayed with us.
Skyrim.esm|030572: You leave him alone. I don't want to hear Skaggi telling me you've been bothering him again!
Skyrim.esm|030588: Mining out this old rock is a lot quieter, now. Thank the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|030589: Hard to believe there'd be an old crypt underneath the mine.
Skyrim.esm|03058E: It's crazy to still be working here. What if there's more draugr down there?
Skyrim.esm|03058F: This mine will be our grave yet. I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|031277: So you're the newest member of our dwindling, dysfunctional little Family. I've heard quite a bit about you.
Skyrim.esm|031296: Stop. Turn around. And go back the way you came. Astrid is waiting for you. She's down near the entrance. You must have passed right by her.
Skyrim.esm|0316F8: Divines sake, Skald. Who do think we're threatening with our old war wounds? We're not soldiers, anymore.
Skyrim.esm|03172D: Alduin's Wall was created by the ancient Akaviri Dragonguard, the forerunners of the Blades. One of the lost secrets of the Blades. Where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy. Its location was lost for centuries, but I know where it is: Sky Haven Temple, an ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden within the crags of the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|03172E: You... Dragonborn? Is it really true?
Skyrim.esm|03172F: So Delphine keeps up the fight, after all these years. I thought she'd have realized it's hopeless by now. I tried to tell her, years ago...
Skyrim.esm|0318B1: Why do I always have to be the Elf? I don't want to!
Skyrim.esm|0318B2: No. Even Ranmir can only drink so much.
Skyrim.esm|0318B3: I suppose that's a benefit to having so few customers.
Skyrim.esm|0318B4: How long are we going to let Ranmir drink himself into a stupor? When will enough be enough?
Skyrim.esm|0318B5: As long as he keeps paying, I suppose. He keeps to himself, and isn't hurting anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0318B6: It's not my place to get involved in their family's business, remember? You're the one that told me to stay out of it.
Skyrim.esm|0318B9: Are we running low on mead?
Skyrim.esm|0318BA: We're playing Hunt the Elf. You're the Elf.
Skyrim.esm|0318BC: Let's just keep an eye on the supply. It's such a chore to restock.
Skyrim.esm|0318BD: Good morning, Assur. What do you want to do today?
Skyrim.esm|0318BE: Too bad. That's what we're playing, and you're the Elf. Now go hide so I can hunt you!
Skyrim.esm|0318BF: Are you so sure of that? I think Birna might disagree with you.
Skyrim.esm|0318F3: Yes, yes, so you said. But so what? The end is upon us. I may as well die here as anywhere else. I'm tired of running.
Skyrim.esm|0318F4: But, give me... just a moment... I must gather a few things...
Skyrim.esm|0318F5: Yes! Yes! You see, you know but you refuse to understand!
Skyrim.esm|0318F6: Oh, yes. It's all been foretold. The end has begun. Alduin has returned. Only a Dragonborn can stop him. But no Dragonborn has been known for centuries. It seems that the gods have grown tired of us. They've left us to our fate, as the plaything of Alduin the World-Eater.
Skyrim.esm|0318F8: I know, I know. I'm hurrying.
Skyrim.esm|0318F9: Come in, come in. Mustn't speak of these things in the Ratway.
Skyrim.esm|0318FD: Haven't you figured it out yet? What more needs to happen before you all wake up and see what's going on?
Skyrim.esm|0318FE: Alduin has returned, just like the prophecy said! The Dragon from the dawn of time, who devours the souls of the dead! No one can escape his hunger, here or in the afterlife! Alduin will devour all things and the world will end. Nothing can stop him!
Skyrim.esm|031925: No, certainly not. A small experiment may have gone awry, but clearly with no discernable consequences.
Skyrim.esm|031926: It's just children playing, Haran. I wouldn't fret over it.
Skyrim.esm|031928: I say, you didn't hear any loud noises last night, did you? No, ahh... explosion sounds?
Skyrim.esm|031929: I'm not "fretting." I don't want Eirid playing those sorts of games!
Skyrim.esm|03192A: I... see. And is this something I'll need to worry about in the future?
Skyrim.esm|03192B: I understand Korir's been complaining about our customers again.
Skyrim.esm|03192C: I believe he was suggesting their presence causes him harm.
Skyrim.esm|03192E: And there haven't been any... problems?
Skyrim.esm|03192F: If I wanted to get yelled at, I'd be home. Can't you leave me be?
Skyrim.esm|031930: Please, come home. Just... Just come home.
Skyrim.esm|031931: Why don't you go... sell something.
Skyrim.esm|031932: Ha! Drink ain't gonna kill me. Not today, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|031934: Oh, certainly I hope not! That would be quite unfortunate for my research.
Skyrim.esm|031935: Oh yes, quite fine. My accommodations here continue to be just what I need.
Skyrim.esm|031936: Are you referring to your other customers? No, everything is quite all right. I expect a bit of animosity now and then.
Skyrim.esm|031937: Is there a reason you're asking me?
Skyrim.esm|031938: All right, all right. I'll speak to her.
Skyrim.esm|031939: What of it? It's our Inn, and they cause him no harm.
Skyrim.esm|03193A: Well, then he's welcome to eat and drink at home, isn't he?
Skyrim.esm|03193B: I see. Well, let me know if things get out of hand.
Skyrim.esm|03193C: How much of our money have you spent today, Ranmir? Anything left, or is it all gone?
Skyrim.esm|03193D: Oh, I'm sorry. Am I bothering you? Am I making your life more difficult?
Skyrim.esm|03193E: Can't. Got at least two rounds to go.
Skyrim.esm|03193F: I understand Eirid's been playing "Hunt the Elf" again.
Skyrim.esm|031940: Why? Because I'd rather be here trying to keep my only brother from drinking himself to death.
Skyrim.esm|031952: That's because you always lose... Elf.
Skyrim.esm|031960: Fine, Elf-Lover. I'll find her myself.
Skyrim.esm|031961: Eirid and I are playing Hunt the Elf. It's my job to find the Elf, and kill it dead. Have you seen it?
Skyrim.esm|031962: I'll find that Elf. I always do.
Skyrim.esm|03277B: Indeed. Speak with me when the box has been opened, and all shall be revealed.
Skyrim.esm|03277C: Your continuing aid to Septimus renders him increasingly obsolete. He has served me well, but his time is nearing its end. Once that infernal lockbox is opened, he will have exhausted his usefulness to me. When that time comes, you shall take his place as my emissary. What say you?
Skyrim.esm|03277D: I am Hermaeus Mora. I am the guardian of the unseen, and knower of the unknown. I have been watching you, mortal. Most impressive.
Skyrim.esm|03277E: Come closer. Bask in my presence.
Skyrim.esm|03277F: Aye, that's the work of Septimus Signus. He's the world's master of the nature of Elder Scrolls, but... well. He's been gone for a long while. Too long.
Skyrim.esm|032781: What is this... it's... it's just a book?! I can see. The world beyond burns in my mind. It's marvelous....
Skyrim.esm|032782: Go forth. Do the biddings of Septimus, for he is working my will in your plane.
Skyrim.esm|032783: Now you have my Oghma Infinium. It contains the knowledge of the ages as revealed to Xarses, my loyal servant. For hundreds of years it's been shut away from the world. Septimus was a useful tool for unleashing it. Now it is in your hands. Let us work wonders together...
Skyrim.esm|032784: Be warned. Many have thought as you do. I have broken them all. You shall not evade me forever.
Skyrim.esm|032816: Keep an eye out for Imperials. They still hold this entire region. As long as we don't do anything stupid... we should be all right.
Skyrim.esm|032883: There's a pack of werewolf hunters camped nearby, at Gallows Rock. The Silver Hand. I think you've met them before. We're going to slaughter them. All of them. Lead on. Skjor's already scouting ahead.
Skyrim.esm|032885: To reach the heights of the Companions, you must join with us in the shared blood of the wolf.
Skyrim.esm|032886: He was one of the strongest we had, but numbers can overwhelm. He should not have come without a Shield-Brother. Get out of here. I'm going to make sure we got the last of them, and see if there's any information to be gotten from the bodies. You and I have work to do. The Silver Hand will tremble at our sight.
Skyrim.esm|032887: I'm glad you came. It's been a long time since we had a heart like yours among our numbers. That pitiful ceremony behind the hall does not befit warriors like us. You are due more honor than some calls and feasting.
Skyrim.esm|032888: We do this in secret because Kodlak is too busy trying to throw away this great gift we've been granted. He thinks we've been cursed. But we've been blessed. How can something that gives this kind of prowess be a curse? So we take matters into our own hands.
Skyrim.esm|0328F9: Like I said in my note, I've heard that you might be Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0328FB: I'll explain what I want when I want, got it? You'd already be dead if I didn't like the look of you when you walked in here. But I had to know if the rumors about you were true...
Skyrim.esm|0328FE: Dragons aren't just coming back, they're coming back to life. They weren't gone somewhere for all these years. They were dead, killed off centuries ago by my predecessors. Now something's happening to bring them back to life. And I need you to help me stop it.
Skyrim.esm|032901: If you don't trust me, you were a fool to walk in here in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|032902: So you're the Dragonborn I've been hearing so much about.
Skyrim.esm|032905: You'll be back. If you're really Dragonborn, this is your destiny.
Skyrim.esm|032907: Surprised? I guess I'm getting pretty good at my harmless innkeeper act.
Skyrim.esm|032909: You can't be too careful. Thalmor spies are everywhere.
Skyrim.esm|03290C: You ready to hear me out?
Skyrim.esm|03290D: Ha. A few years ago, I said almost the same thing to a colleague of mine. Well, it turned out he was right and I was wrong.
Skyrim.esm|03290E: I'm not your enemy. I already gave you the horn. I'm actually trying to help you. I just need you to hear me out.
Skyrim.esm|03290F: I'm part of a group that's been looking for you... well, someone like you, for a very long time. If you really are Dragonborn, that is. Before I tell you any more, I need to make sure I can trust you.
Skyrim.esm|032910: Kynesgrove. There's an ancient dragon burial near there. If we can get there before it happens, maybe we'll learn how to stop it.
Skyrim.esm|032911: That mound you remembered sounds like what we're looking for. East of Kynesgrove, right?
Skyrim.esm|032913: It was the only way I could make sure it wasn't a Thalmor trap.
Skyrim.esm|032938: Of course. But why not let him go in first?
Skyrim.esm|032939: Of course. But why not let her go in first?
Skyrim.esm|03293A: They may be cowards, but I'm not. I'll go with you.
Skyrim.esm|03293B: I suppose you're right. I'm not a fighting man. Go, and avenge my Laelette for me.
Skyrim.esm|032A47: All right. If I make it back to Windhelm, I'll make sure Ulfric knows how you helped me. Skyrim needs people like you, if we ever hope to regain our freedom from the damned Empire. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|032B91: I was hoping you'd change your mind. Come on. I'm heading to Riverwood, just up the road. My sister Gerdur will help us out.
Skyrim.esm|032D55: Karliah? Then she's finally found it. Do you have Gallus's Journal?
Skyrim.esm|032D58: Then you were well informed. I am at this very moment on the cusp of completing my magnum opus on the subject. I'm calling it "Calcelmo's Guide to the Falmer Tongue." It will revolutionize the way we understand those ancient beings.
Skyrim.esm|032D5A: No. However, I know someone who might. The court wizard of Markarth, Calcelmo, may have the materials you need to get this journal translated. A word of warning. Calcelmo is a fierce guardian of his research. Getting the information won't be easy.
Skyrim.esm|032D5B: A problem? Let me see it. This is just like Gallus. A dear friend, but always too clever for his own good. He's written all of the text in the Falmer language.
Skyrim.esm|032D5D: I'm not certain how many scholars you're accustomed to dealing with, but I can assure you that personal wealth is our lowest priority.
Skyrim.esm|032DCF: Gallus had his wealth and he had you. All he had to do was look the other way.
Skyrim.esm|032DD0: You're a clever girl, Karliah. Buying Goldenglow Estate and funding Honningbrew Meadery was inspired.
Skyrim.esm|032DD5: I'm no fool, Mercer. Crossing blades with you would be a death sentence. But I can promise the next time we meet, it will be your undoing.
Skyrim.esm|032DDD: Did you forget the Oath we took as Nightingales? Did you expect him to simply ignore your methods?
Skyrim.esm|032DF3: How interesting. It appears Gallus's history has repeated itself. Karliah has provided me with the means to be rid of you, and this ancient tomb becomes your final resting place. But do you know what intrigues me the most? The fact that this was all possible because of you.
Skyrim.esm|032DF4: You always were a quick study.
Skyrim.esm|032DF6: Give me a reason to try.
Skyrim.esm|032DF7: Not quick enough, otherwise Gallus would still be alive.
Skyrim.esm|032DF8: Do you honestly think your arrow will reach me before my blade finds your heart?
Skyrim.esm|032DF9: "To ensure an enemy's defeat, you must first undermine his allies." It was the first lesson Gallus taught us.
Skyrim.esm|0330AE: I'm going to find whoever did this.
Skyrim.esm|0330E4: The way you and your mother forge weapons is impressive.
Skyrim.esm|0330E8: Your muscles ripple when you work the forge. It's pleasing.
Skyrim.esm|0330F2: Malacath is pleased with your strength, Larak.
Skyrim.esm|0330F9: It's simple Orc smithing practice, my chief. Nothing that hasn't been passed down for years.
Skyrim.esm|0330FB: You are speaking to Larak, chief of Mor Khazgur.
Skyrim.esm|033100: I have poisons for your weapons, and cures for your ills.
Skyrim.esm|033101: Our chief is strong. That is all that matters.
Skyrim.esm|033105: Larak is strong, but he has much to prove as chief.
Skyrim.esm|033109: My son, Olur, learns the forge well. His work brings me pride.
Skyrim.esm|03310C: I envy you, outlander. You can choose to leave if you wish.
Skyrim.esm|03310D: Soon, I will be of age. The taste is bitter.
Skyrim.esm|03310E: Perhaps I'll join the Legion one day, as my father had done.
Skyrim.esm|033110: I work in the forge and the mines. Mother says it pleases her and our people.
Skyrim.esm|033111: Even good hammers warp metal if struck without care.
Skyrim.esm|033610: Patience, my son. The hunter days not welcome the blood of his prey, he waits for it.
Skyrim.esm|033611: He is pleased. Ours is the strongest tribe in all the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|033612: Tell me, mother, does Malacath favor our stronghold?
Skyrim.esm|033613: Do they, my chief? Am I now favored enough to replace Shel by your side at the feast? Back your words with action!
Skyrim.esm|033614: You could challenge Burguk, my brother. Yours is the stronger swordarm.
Skyrim.esm|033615: My chief, Gharol threatens to have me work in the mines. You won't allow my pretty hands to dirty, will you?
Skyrim.esm|033616: The air has the smell of beasts. The hunt is good today.
Skyrim.esm|033618: Ah, the air is good today. I welcome the smell of blood from all the kills I will make.
Skyrim.esm|033619: I saw you working the forge today, wife. Your muscles have grown strong from the metal shaping!
Skyrim.esm|03361A: This metal will serve the forge well, my brother.
Skyrim.esm|03361C: No more talk of Shel. As chief, I will do what I please, so point your fiery gaze to our enemies.
Skyrim.esm|03361D: Serving in the Legion taught me to respect leadership. The stronghold needs Burguk.
Skyrim.esm|03361E: No, my wife. You have been given other gifts to please your chief than mine work. I will talk to Gharol and all will be well.
Skyrim.esm|03361F: Yes, mother. I'm eager to prove my skills today.
Skyrim.esm|033627: Malacath rewards the strong and punishes the weak.
Skyrim.esm|03362A: Murbul knows many poisons and cures if you need them.
Skyrim.esm|03362E: You wish to trade in Orc steel?
Skyrim.esm|03362F: As forge-wife, I'm also in charge of our mine.
Skyrim.esm|033632: I am Burguk's favored wife. He knows my beauty should be praised.
Skyrim.esm|033633: Arob and Gharol eye me with envy. They don't know how to please their chief.
Skyrim.esm|033634: Umurn and Nagrub will be held up as weak next to the son I will one day bear for my chief.
Skyrim.esm|033636: I work the forge, with my mother Gharol. We mine our own ore and shape our own metal.
Skyrim.esm|033637: One day I will shape a weapon fit for a chief. Maybe that chief will be me.
Skyrim.esm|033638: I help in the mines as well as the forge.
Skyrim.esm|03363A: I help my mother Arob with the hunt. We bring much meat and skins to the stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|03363B: Brother Umurn is a strong warrior. I look forward to fighting him for chiefdom one day.
Skyrim.esm|03363C: One day, I intend to be chief. I will fight my father and my brother for it, as is tradition.
Skyrim.esm|03363E: Sorry, outlander, but this old Orc cannot help you. Burguk is the chief here.
Skyrim.esm|03363F: I don't know how Ghorbash has so much spirit. He should act his age.
Skyrim.esm|033640: I serve my chief and my brothers by working in the mines. It's the only place fit for an aging Orc.
Skyrim.esm|033642: I used to wander the corners of Skyrim like you. I miss those days.
Skyrim.esm|033643: We honor the bravest fighters, but I have seen many types of bravery in my travels.
Skyrim.esm|033645: It is a fine day to work the mines.
Skyrim.esm|033DC1: You have what you came for. Now leave.
Skyrim.esm|033DC2: Take your books and go, before I change my mind.
Skyrim.esm|034037: There's no mistake. You're a wanted man... and it's time to pay for your crimes.
Skyrim.esm|03403A: In the name of the Jarl, I'm ordering you to stand fast!
Skyrim.esm|03403B: Well, your bounty is low... All right, get out of my sight. But you're known to me now, scum. Remember that.
Skyrim.esm|03403C: You know what? You're not worth the hassle. Go... be some other guard's problem.
Skyrim.esm|0340B6: Sure I am. We get some furs and tree branches, paint them white, and then tie them to Stump. Instant Frostbite Spider!
Skyrim.esm|0340B7: Frodnar... nobody is going to believe that your dog is a... Frostbite Spider. And if they do... they'll kill him. That's not much of a prank.
Skyrim.esm|0340B8: Wait, so... a costume? You're not serious...
Skyrim.esm|0340B9: How about... we play tag! You're it!
Skyrim.esm|0340BA: Hey, no fair! Get her, Stump!
Skyrim.esm|0340BB: Oh yeah, and what would you rather do?
Skyrim.esm|0341B9: Ah! You've retrieved the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller. Well done. You have now passed all the trials. Come with me. It is time for us to recognize you formally as Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0346F6: I feel the eyes of angry spirits upon us. We are not welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|0346F7: Few would dare to trespass in these ruins. What you lack in wisdom, you make up for in courage.
Skyrim.esm|0346F8: Do not ask me to take treasures from this place. I will not risk the anger of the dead.
Skyrim.esm|0346F9: They say ruins like these are filled with treasure. Of course, they're filled with traps, too.
Skyrim.esm|0346FA: I could have sworn I heard something right behind us.
Skyrim.esm|0346FB: I'll bet some of these artifacts would fetch a good price. I hope they're not cursed.
Skyrim.esm|0346FC: I wonder what the ancient Nords did in these ruins. Unspeakable sacrifices, maybe?
Skyrim.esm|0346FD: If these ruins frighten you, take comfort in the knowledge that I am here.
Skyrim.esm|0346FE: Do try not to set off any traps, will you?
Skyrim.esm|0346FF: I suppose you intend to carry off every item of value in these ruins.
Skyrim.esm|034700: These ruins might be interesting if I wasn't already an expert on Nordic history.
Skyrim.esm|034701: Remember to check behind you every once in a while. Who knows what might try to sneak up on us.
Skyrim.esm|034702: Keep your eyes open for traps. Old ruins like this are usually filled with them.
Skyrim.esm|034703: The ancient Nords were a savage people, but they built some remarkable crypts and temples.
Skyrim.esm|034704: There's a lot of history in a place like that. I'll bet these walls could tell some amazing stories.
Skyrim.esm|034705: Look at this craftsmanship! These old halls fill some folk with dread, but as a Nord, they fill me with pride.
Skyrim.esm|034706: We're bound to find some gold in these ruins, but out of respect, let's leave a few coins for the dead.
Skyrim.esm|034707: It's strange to think that some of my own ancestors might be buried in here.
Skyrim.esm|034708: Plunder these ruins at your peril, friend. The spirits of the dead won't look kindly upon it.
Skyrim.esm|034717: The Dwemer who built this place were sometimes called Dwarves, but history records that they were no shorter than humans.
Skyrim.esm|034719: There are scholars in Cyrodiil who have dedicated their lives to solving the mystery of the Dwemers' disappearance.
Skyrim.esm|03471F: It is remarkable that so many of these ancient Dwemer creations still function.
Skyrim.esm|034721: There are those who believe that the entire Dwemer race will one day return just as suddenly as it vanished.
Skyrim.esm|034723: What good is a sword or an axe against an enemy made of brass and steel?
Skyrim.esm|034725: It's unnatural, building all these machines. If you ask me, the Dwemer got what they deserved.
Skyrim.esm|03473C: I don't like fighting these Dwemer creations. Where's the fun in killing something that doesn't even bleed?
Skyrim.esm|03473D: Watch out for the hot steam vents down here. They'll burn you worse than fire.
Skyrim.esm|034744: Had they placed their faith in magic instead of machinery, the Dwemer might still be around.
Skyrim.esm|034785: A living Dwemer was once discovered in Morrowind. Can you imagine it, being the last surviving member of your race?
Skyrim.esm|034798: I should bring some of these Dwemer artifacts to the Arcane University in Cyrodiil for study.
Skyrim.esm|03479B: I wonder what keeps these machines working so long after their masters disappeared.
Skyrim.esm|03479E: The Dwemer created so many wondrous machines, and yet nobody ever followed in their footsteps. Why is that, I wonder?
Skyrim.esm|03479F: Among other things, the Dwemer were expert trap-makers. Watch the floor for tripwires and false plates.
Skyrim.esm|0347B1: I've half a mind to dismantle one of these Dwemer machines. I'd like to see how it works.
Skyrim.esm|0347B4: I suppose the warmth in this place comes from all the hot steam vents.
Skyrim.esm|0347B7: Scholars say the Dwemer were an advanced culture. Ha! The Nords outlasted their entire race. So much for being advanced.
Skyrim.esm|0347B8: I wonder why the Dwemer came to Skyrim. Why did they build this place? I suppose we'll never know.
Skyrim.esm|0347CB: Do these steel walls and strange Dwemer devices make you uneasy? Perhaps it's just me.
Skyrim.esm|0347E5: I've heard that you can sell Dwemer artifacts on the black market down in Cyrodiil. They fetch a handsome price, too.
Skyrim.esm|0347E6: So many shadowy corners to strike from! I could learn to like this place.
Skyrim.esm|0347EA: Silence will serve us well in this place. Let us take our foes by surprise.
Skyrim.esm|0347EB: Be wary for traps. If you spy one, try to get an enemy to trigger it.
Skyrim.esm|0347EC: I can see the battle-lust in your eyes. Doom will surely come to any who cross your path.
Skyrim.esm|0347ED: If it wasn't for all the dangers in here, this place could almost be cozy.
Skyrim.esm|0347EE: When I was a child, I dreamed about having a fort like this all to myself.
Skyrim.esm|0347EF: I wonder if we'll come across some bandits who've just finished roasting a rabbit on a spit. Maybe they'll have a keg of ale, too.
Skyrim.esm|0347F0: If my ma could see me now, she'd scold me good. She'd tell me I should know better than to go poking around in abandoned forts.
Skyrim.esm|0347F1: This smell of dank earth and worm-eaten wood is going to make me lose my last meal.
Skyrim.esm|0347F3: How pathetic, being forced to eke out a miserable existence in a place like this.
Skyrim.esm|0347F4: Just look at this place. Is this what passes for a fort in Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0347F5: The sooner we can get out of this filth-pit, the better.
Skyrim.esm|0347F6: These timber supports are rotted through. It's a wonder this place is still standing.
Skyrim.esm|0347F7: Check every crate, chest and barrel you see. Who knows, maybe a few of them have something valuable inside.
Skyrim.esm|0347F8: Did you bring a torch? These old forts can get pretty dark inside.
Skyrim.esm|0347F9: There are forts like this all over Skyrim. The Imperials abandoned most of them a long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|0347FB: Just look at this place. I've seen better workmanship from Nord children only ten winters old.
Skyrim.esm|0347FD: So this is what passes for Imperial craftsmanship. Try not to sneeze, or you might bring the whole fort down.
Skyrim.esm|034802: What is that stench? Rotting wood, perhaps? That can't be good.
Skyrim.esm|034803: You'd have to be pretty desperate to want to make a home in this dank hole.
Skyrim.esm|034805: I hope that when I die, I don't end up as a shambling corpse.
Skyrim.esm|034806: Outlaws and brigands are the lowest of the low. They prey on their own countrymen for profit.
Skyrim.esm|034809: It isn't just your blood that a vampire wants, it's your mortal soul.
Skyrim.esm|03480A: Fighting draugr is as much about controlling your fear as anything else.
Skyrim.esm|03480F: These wizards are mad beyond reason. They understand only violence.
Skyrim.esm|03482B: You can easily break a blade on the metal skin of a Dwemer construct.
Skyrim.esm|0348F4: By Shor, what do you want?
Skyrim.esm|034945: Astrid, I've been hearing some, er... interesting things, from the others... about what you did when you left the Sanctuary last week...
Skyrim.esm|034947: Tell me, brother. Are the stories true?
Skyrim.esm|034948: Tell me, sister. Are the stories true?
Skyrim.esm|034949: Your latest exploit certainly has the Sanctuary talking...
Skyrim.esm|03494A: But to be outnumbered twenty to one...
Skyrim.esm|03494B: You infiltrated the position, eliminated the target, and made it look like an accident. Impressive. I'd say you earned your bonus on that one!
Skyrim.esm|03494C: To kill triplets, secretly, and then have the whole thing blamed on the mother. Remarkable.
Skyrim.esm|03494D: You're referring to that blacksmith contract?
Skyrim.esm|03494E: You must be talking about the caravan job...
Skyrim.esm|03494F: Word certainly travels fast, doesn't it?
Skyrim.esm|034950: The temple contract? Well, I certainly won't deny my involvement. He he he he he.
Skyrim.esm|034951: The Redoran contract? Yes, you heard correctly. In and out of Vvardenfell in less than forty eight hours.
Skyrim.esm|03496B: It was a stupid plan, and now Rigel is so mad she won't even let us cut him down from there!
Skyrim.esm|03496C: I tried to tell him that he wouldn't get past the traps, but he wouldn't listen...
Skyrim.esm|03496D: Aye, and she's added more traps now as well... A moment of silence for our fallen friend...
Skyrim.esm|03499D: The crucial nature is pure apparent. Mundane sight traded for another sort.
Skyrim.esm|034B32: Generally posting information is need to know only.
Skyrim.esm|034B33: Her daughter was sent to Whiterun, the skirmishes there have been violent. The whiterun legate, he needed to know the Stormcloak positions.
Skyrim.esm|034B34: He's the sorry bastard who's going to be executed. A gate guard who let Ulfric Stormcloak escape the city, after he killed the High King.
Skyrim.esm|034B35: There's no greater honor than to serve. If you're serious about enlisting, speak to Legate Rikke in Castle Dour.
Skyrim.esm|034B36: I... I've been trying to find the time and place to let her know.
Skyrim.esm|034B37: I'm sorry, I can't help you.
Skyrim.esm|034B38: If you've come to Solitude to join the Legion, speak to Rikke. If not, just keep your nose clean while you're here... or you'll end up like Roggvir.
Skyrim.esm|034B39: Yes, Sir. Although I'm not sure you'll be happy to know.
Skyrim.esm|034B3B: I told her. I told her the imperial army would kill her. That this was a war of Jarls and Imperial Generals and we had no part in it. She just wouldn't hear it. It was brave of you to come to me with this. I can't thank you for it, not for this, but you have done me a service.
Skyrim.esm|034B3C: You got Aldis to tell you something? I knew you would. What did he say?
Skyrim.esm|034B3D: Need something for a potion? You're certainly in the right place.
Skyrim.esm|034B3E: If you want to know that bad then fine.
Skyrim.esm|034BCA: Do you mind? I'm busy doing the fishstick. It's a very delicate state of mind!
Skyrim.esm|034C13: Unscrupulous daring. It's why you fell in love with me, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|034C14: It was a suicide mission! Astrid said as much. Yet here you stand, hale and hearty.
Skyrim.esm|034C40: I work silver, like my father, and his father before him.
Skyrim.esm|034C41: My wife Kerah runs our jewelry stall in the market.
Skyrim.esm|034C42: Silver-Bloods tax everything now that they run the city.
Skyrim.esm|034C44: Are you looking for a gift for a friend, perhaps? Or... a lover?
Skyrim.esm|034C45: Perhaps a silver necklace or a silver ring? Easy to wear and soft against the skin.
Skyrim.esm|034C46: I have a necklace that would look lovely on you, or on a... special someone.
Skyrim.esm|034C73: That's it. I'm placing you under arrest.
Skyrim.esm|034C74: This is your last warning. Get out, or get arrested.
Skyrim.esm|034C75: You're not supposed to be here...
Skyrim.esm|034C84: But, I've got the key to the Sabre Cage! Look!
Skyrim.esm|034C85: Take down that one with your bare hands too?
Skyrim.esm|034C86: What's that supposed to prove? Get back on patrol!
Skyrim.esm|034C87: Wasn't our last catch a Cave Bear?
Skyrim.esm|034C88: Why, you don't think I could do it?
Skyrim.esm|034C89: Troll's blood was he a tough one!
Skyrim.esm|034C8B: So, you're saying you brought that cat down alone?
Skyrim.esm|034CAB: You will be called on to take swift action. Rise to the challenge, and discover what you are capable of.
Skyrim.esm|034CB1: You are on the right path, and you will prevail.
Skyrim.esm|034CB5: It is imperative that you return to your College at once.
Skyrim.esm|034CB6: You have done well thus far, but trying times are ahead.
Skyrim.esm|034CF3: By Sithis, you slew everyone! Well, except for that one you kept alive, to serve as witness. Does your wickedness, or your skill, know no bounds?
Skyrim.esm|034CF4: Bah! A child could have accomplished it. Such is the power of the arcane. If you only knew of the spells I didn't use. He he he he he.
Skyrim.esm|034CF5: What? You think me too old, too feeble? There is more knowledge in me than in two of you pups put together. And knowledge... knowledge is my weapon.
Skyrim.esm|034CF6: Once again, you have underestimated me. You see the man, brutish and hulking. But I am wolf as well. Deliberate. Cunning.
Skyrim.esm|034CF7: When I am become wolf, I am treachery incarnate. And then, then... there is no one who can stand between the hunter and his prey.
Skyrim.esm|034CF8: When I first received the blessing of the wolf, my creator gave me a valuable piece of advice. "Arnbjorn," he said. "Never lose!" And I never do.
Skyrim.esm|034CF9: He he. Don't forget that I am Shadowscale. I was, quite literally, born to kill.
Skyrim.esm|034CFA: It was a simple matter, really, when one has mastered the art of camouflage. Scale becomes stone, becomes shadow. Then... death.
Skyrim.esm|034CFB: My greatest challenge was deciding which weapon to use, for I am prone to indecision. Bow, dagger... axe? In the end, I opted for my claws.
Skyrim.esm|034CFC: In truth? It was so easy, I feel a bit guilty. Once again, the sweet, innocent child earned more trust than was wise.
Skyrim.esm|034CFD: You give me too much credit. For how could I fail? I am, after all, just a little girl. What threat could I possibly pose? He he he he he.
Skyrim.esm|034CFE: I am the perfect weapon. My innocent, childish form grants me nearly unrestricted access. Poison or a good bite usually takes care of the rest.
Skyrim.esm|034CFF: I am but a humble instrument of Sithis. Our Dread Father guides me. Therefore, failure is an impossibility.
Skyrim.esm|034D00: In the end, I relied on a woman's natural weapons - beauty and seduction. The rest was a formality. Though, a rather wet, messy formality...
Skyrim.esm|034D01: When one can divine the future, success may not be assured... but it can at least be anticipated. It helps that I've already foretold my own death.
Skyrim.esm|034D9F: What's he doing poking his nose in there?
Skyrim.esm|034DA0: What's she doing poking her nose in there?
Skyrim.esm|034DA1: Maybe he's looking for something to eat?
Skyrim.esm|034DA2: Maybe she's looking for something to eat?
Skyrim.esm|034DAA: What's this? It thinks it can just throw refuse wherever it likes?
Skyrim.esm|034F94: Skyrim isn't very friendly for Argonians, so we mostly just keep out of the way.
Skyrim.esm|034F95: Shahvee knows the dock doesn't look nice, but fresh air is good for the gills.
Skyrim.esm|034F96: Good honest work staves off the cold.
Skyrim.esm|034F97: I like to bring Grimvar down here sometimes. It reminds him how lucky he is.
Skyrim.esm|034F99: When Grimvar asks me why women are being killed around the city. I don't even know what to say to him.
Skyrim.esm|034F9A: Ever tried taking care of children? It can be a nightmare.
Skyrim.esm|034F9B: We can't thank you enough for helping us get this office running.
Skyrim.esm|034F9C: Hey, it's our hero! Seen any more of those pirates around here?
Skyrim.esm|034F9D: I appreciate everything you've done for us, but I really am quite busy.
Skyrim.esm|034F9F: The Stormcloaks are gone, but Talos will never be forgotten. I don't care what the Thalmor say!
Skyrim.esm|034FA0: We're the only Temple in Skyrim that can openly worship Talos.
Skyrim.esm|034FA1: Those awful Thalmor can't touch us, thanks to Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|034FA2: I can't believe the Empire would turn its back on Talos.
Skyrim.esm|034FA3: Spare a coin for a poor old woman, milord?
Skyrim.esm|034FA4: Spare a coin for a poor old woman, milady?
Skyrim.esm|034FA5: Spare a coin? Talos rewards the generous.
Skyrim.esm|034FA6: I hope the wind dies down a bit before tonight. Hard when you've got to sleep outside.
Skyrim.esm|034FA7: Nervous being alone at night. Especially these days, with the Butcher prowling the streets.
Skyrim.esm|034FA8: Looking for shipping? We'll always offer better service than the East Empire Company.
Skyrim.esm|034FA9: These lazy Argonians better get their tails moving.
Skyrim.esm|034FAA: You hear about the Shatter-shield girl? It's a tragedy.
Skyrim.esm|034FAB: Torbjorn wants the shipping operations to bring in cash, and I make it happen.
Skyrim.esm|034FAC: Come see me in the marketplace tomorrow. You won't find better prices in all of Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|034FAD: I don't haggle. If you want a lower price, look elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|034FAE: I'd travel more, but who wants to deal with all the soldiers these days?
Skyrim.esm|034FAF: If you're looking to get a drink, Candlehearth Hall's got warm fires and strong ale.
Skyrim.esm|034FB0: Horses. All day long. Do you have any idea how dull horses are?
Skyrim.esm|034FB1: If the guards would listen to Viola, they'd have caught the Butcher by now.
Skyrim.esm|034FB2: I love my husband, but he can be a bit... dull at times.
Skyrim.esm|034FB3: Ugh. I'll need to bathe thoroughly to get this stench off of me.
Skyrim.esm|034FB5: Real Nords like to prove their mettle in the pit.
Skyrim.esm|034FB6: Just between you and me, I wouldn't mind if the Empire just up and left for good.
Skyrim.esm|034FB7: I'd love get one of those Imperial weasels under my boot...
Skyrim.esm|034FB8: I've been a bit out of sorts since... since our daughter was taken from us.
Skyrim.esm|034FB9: Not really sure what to do with myself anymore. I just miss her so much.
Skyrim.esm|034FBA: I try not to think of her, but sometimes the simplest thing will remind me.
Skyrim.esm|034FBB: No mother should have to bury her daughter.
Skyrim.esm|034FBC: This is the Hall of the Dead. Someday you'll end up here.
Skyrim.esm|034FBD: My job's simple enough. The dead don't really complain much.
Skyrim.esm|034FBE: Luckily Windhelm is cold, so the dead don't rot as quickly. Sometimes the burial has to wait for the ground to thaw, though.
Skyrim.esm|034FBF: Another Dark Elf. Just what Windhelm needs.
Skyrim.esm|034FC0: Maybe you should just move along.
Skyrim.esm|034FC1: This here's Candlehearth Hall. Great room's upstairs, an' there's a bed for rent on the ground floor.
Skyrim.esm|034FC2: Mind you don't get fresh with Susanna. She plays the flirt, but it's just to get good tips.
Skyrim.esm|034FC3: Got some fresh-baked bread an' good cheese, if you're after a bite to eat.
Skyrim.esm|034FC4: Be on the lookout. The Butcher could be around any corner!
Skyrim.esm|034FC5: Captain Lonely-Gale has been avoiding me, I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|034FC6: After what happened to those other women, I'm worried about my own safety.
Skyrim.esm|034FC7: Women murdered time and again, and all the guards care about is the war.
Skyrim.esm|034FC8: How can Elda hate the dark elves when she knows so little about them?
Skyrim.esm|034FC9: You didn't hear it from me, but if you want a real drink, try the Cornerclub in the Gray Quarter.
Skyrim.esm|034FCA: Did you know that candle on the mantle hasn't gone out in over a hundred years?
Skyrim.esm|034FCB: Stand away from me, or I'll crack you like an egg.
Skyrim.esm|034FCC: What are you looking at? Scared of strong women?
Skyrim.esm|034FCD: You look mighty tasty. Better not stick around for supper.
Skyrim.esm|034FCE: Working in Windhelm is great. I get to see Ulfric up close.
Skyrim.esm|034FCF: I couldn't ask for a better teacher than Oengul.
Skyrim.esm|034FD0: I never expected Ulfric to live up to his legend, but he does.
Skyrim.esm|034FD1: It's a new day in Windhelm. The Stormcloaks are gone, and so is their prejudice.
Skyrim.esm|034FD2: We Argonians do the work the Nords don't want to do.
Skyrim.esm|034FD3: The water is cold, but it's a lot better than having to work up in the dry air.
Skyrim.esm|034FD4: Shatter-Shield, East Empire Company... we'll work for any ship that pulls in, as long as they can pay.
Skyrim.esm|034FD5: The Nords don't appreciate us, but so what? I don't appreciate them right back.
Skyrim.esm|034FD6: Look, we don't have much love for your kind down here. Probably best if you just left.
Skyrim.esm|034FD7: Haven't seen you here before, egg brother. Welcome!
Skyrim.esm|034FD8: The docks can be a dangerous place. Don't expect me to save you if you fall in.
Skyrim.esm|034FD9: Huh? What's that now? Leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|034FDC: The young folks are too happy to break their backs for the Nords. They forget what it was like to swim free.
Skyrim.esm|034FDD: Do you know my father? He's really important. He even talks to the Jarl sometimes.
Skyrim.esm|034FDF: All the Argonians at the docks are nice to me. Good thing, too, or father would run them off!
Skyrim.esm|034FE0: They say dragons are coming back. I want to kill one!
Skyrim.esm|034FE1: I made my fortune as a sea captain, but now I'm retired.
Skyrim.esm|034FE2: The townsfolk call me "captain" because I used to be a sailor.
Skyrim.esm|034FE3: I lost my family years ago. Truth is, I don't really know what keeps me going.
Skyrim.esm|034FE4: Did Viola send you to bother me?
Skyrim.esm|034FE5: Serving as Steward to Ulfric Stormcloak was a great honor, but I don't miss his temper.
Skyrim.esm|034FE6: The Stormcloaks are finding victory across the land.
Skyrim.esm|034FE7: I'm not much of a strategist, but Lord Ulfric listens to my counsel all the same.
Skyrim.esm|034FE8: If you have any news of the western holds, take it to Ulfric immediately.
Skyrim.esm|034FE9: Terrible shame, what happened to those women.
Skyrim.esm|034FEA: I was one of the best soldiers in the Stormcloak army, until I took a sword through the chest.
Skyrim.esm|034FEB: I once killed six men, single-handed, while rescuing my brothers-in-arms from ambush by an Imperial patrol.
Skyrim.esm|034FEE: I'm not too proud to admit that I need a bit of help from time to time.
Skyrim.esm|034FEF: Can't say I mind the sight of all the Imperial guards. It's a change for the better.
Skyrim.esm|034FF0: I like to share my good fortune with those who deserve it.
Skyrim.esm|034FF1: Trader dropped by recently with some lovely pieces, if you'd like to have a look.
Skyrim.esm|034FF2: Pardon the mess. We're really trying to do better around here.
Skyrim.esm|034FF3: "Windhelm's an easy posting!" they said. Guess they didn't count on Ulfric's little uprising.
Skyrim.esm|034FF4: Can you believe the company won't even dock ships here anymore?
Skyrim.esm|034FF6: I really can't thank you enough for your help.
Skyrim.esm|034FF7: I still can't believe Isabella's gone. She was such a helpful young lass.
Skyrim.esm|034FF8: Now more than ever, the world needs tales of heroism.
Skyrim.esm|034FF9: Know any tales of nobility and courage? I'm keen to record them.
Skyrim.esm|034FFD: Apparently some Nord women were murdered. Not sure why I should care.
Skyrim.esm|034FFE: At long last, Windhelm is free. No longer must we suffer persecution at the hands of Ulfric Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|034FFF: It's not the cold of Skyrim that gets to me. It's the stink from the people.
Skyrim.esm|035000: When I've made enough money, I'm going to return to Morrowind in high style.
Skyrim.esm|035001: Both my daughters. Now my wife? How much is one man supposed to take?
Skyrim.esm|035002: Poor Nilsine has been wrecked since her sister was killed.
Skyrim.esm|035003: Seeing a good tavern brawl might be just the thing to lift my spirits.
Skyrim.esm|035004: Brond's one of the most savage fighters I've ever seen...
Skyrim.esm|035006: What I lack in strength, I make up for with tenacity.
Skyrim.esm|035008: You need anything, come see me. I'm as resourceful as I am discreet.
Skyrim.esm|035009: I have a lot of respect for Ambarys Rendar, but his views are a little extreme.
Skyrim.esm|03500A: You look like someone who appreciates nice things.
Skyrim.esm|03500B: Oh, pardon me. Was I in your way?
Skyrim.esm|03500C: I hope someday we'll get to know each other better.
Skyrim.esm|03500D: I see why the Nords like Skyrim. This crisp air is wonderful for the lungs!
Skyrim.esm|03500E: You seem like such a nice person. I'd love to hear more about you.
Skyrim.esm|03500F: Can't believe Ulfric's gone. If not for Hermir, I'd have left the city by now.
Skyrim.esm|035011: Hermir's become a decent smith. Like to think I had a hand in that.
Skyrim.esm|035012: With my swords in their hands, Ulfric's men will send those legion dogs running.
Skyrim.esm|035013: I lost my twin sister a while back. Have you ever lost anyone close?
Skyrim.esm|035015: My father says we need to just get on with our lives. Like it's that easy.
Skyrim.esm|035016: I've been a lot of things in my life. Pit fighter's the one I'm best at.
Skyrim.esm|035017: We should get a dragon for the Pit. I'd love to fight one.
Skyrim.esm|035018: I've been fighting in the Pit for years. That should tell you something.
Skyrim.esm|035019: Ulfric once tried to get me to fight for him, but marching bores me.
Skyrim.esm|03501A: Steel. Barehanded. Armor. Naked. It's all fighting. I can kill you with a weapon or by myself. No difference.
Skyrim.esm|03501B: With Ulfric dead and the Stormcloaks gone, who will have the courage to champion the cause of Talos?
Skyrim.esm|03501C: Talos created the Empire. You'd think they'd show him more respect.
Skyrim.esm|03501D: The way I see it, Ulfric's fighting a holy war.
Skyrim.esm|03501E: Someday all of Skyrim will worship Talos openly, as we do here.
Skyrim.esm|03501F: Whatever you've heard I can do is probably true.
Skyrim.esm|035020: If Ulfric needs a favor, he has it. Anyone else better pay well for the service.
Skyrim.esm|035021: I'd explain my research, but you'd just be confused.
Skyrim.esm|035022: Yes, I'm a powerful wizard. No, I won't put on a magic show for you.
Skyrim.esm|035023: My job? Well, I'm the Watcher of the Wheel! Which is... exactly what it sounds like. I watch the Wheel.
Skyrim.esm|035024: Watching the wheel isn't as simple as you might think. Have to inspect all the way around the ice, make sure it's not melting.
Skyrim.esm|035025: Don't get too many folks up here these days. Sometimes I think they've forgotten about me.
Skyrim.esm|035026: My time is limited, so speak quickly.
Skyrim.esm|035027: They call me a war hero. I'm no hero, I'm just a soldier who didn't want to die.
Skyrim.esm|035028: If Ulfric had his way, anyone who wasn't a Nord would be shipped right out of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|035029: The sooner this war ends, the sooner we can all get on with our lives.
Skyrim.esm|03502A: Welcome to the House of Curiosities! I offer a brief tour for a few coins, or you can simply browse at your leisure.
Skyrim.esm|03502B: Welcome, friend, to Calixto's House of Curiosities!
Skyrim.esm|03502C: My sister and I once traveled the world to assemble our collection. Such adventures we had!
Skyrim.esm|03502D: I don't care much for the war one way or the other.
Skyrim.esm|035031: Name's Nils. I'm the cook up at Candlehearth Hall.
Skyrim.esm|035032: Can't just throw the big logs on the fire. Got to have the little bits, too.
Skyrim.esm|035033: When I'm cutting wood, I pretend there's an Imperial soldier's head on the stump.
Skyrim.esm|035034: Hello, hello, hello. Interested in fighting in the Pit? The rules are strict, but if I don't see it, it didn't happen, know what I mean?
Skyrim.esm|035035: It's very important to Huki that all the battles are fair. What's more important to me is that my pay is fair.
Skyrim.esm|035036: Prove your valor in the Pit! If you haven't got any valor, prove your pocketbook!
Skyrim.esm|035037: When Ulfric was killed, justice was done. That might sound harsh, but it's how I feel.
Skyrim.esm|035038: The Gray Quarter's not much to look at now, but it wasn't always so bad.
Skyrim.esm|035039: We might be poor, but is it really any better out there?
Skyrim.esm|03503A: Not a day passes that I don't wonder if life would be better in Morrowind.
Skyrim.esm|03503B: I don't see why we should live as an underclass to the Nords. If anything, they owe us.
Skyrim.esm|03503C: Must you bother me now? I've almost figured out where it is.
Skyrim.esm|03503D: Alchemy is simple. Unless, of course, you are simple. Then I can't help you.
Skyrim.esm|03503E: My master can be a bit short-tempered, but I've learned so much from him.
Skyrim.esm|03503F: I have a decent mind for potions, but I've also learned how to craft magical items.
Skyrim.esm|035040: It's good to spend some time on solid land.
Skyrim.esm|035041: I'm not proud of Hrefna's father. If she knew him, she wouldn't be either.
Skyrim.esm|035042: Derkeethus used to take me diving way down deep in the river. I miss him.
Skyrim.esm|03505F: I... knew Veren and Thorek. They were my friends. Is this punishment for my past? Is it Mara's will to torment me so?
Skyrim.esm|035071: Is there... something else you need?
Skyrim.esm|035072: You must deliver those items to your superior. And I... I must get out of this foul place.
Skyrim.esm|03508B: Yes, that will be fine. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|03508C: No, I don't think so. Not today.
Skyrim.esm|03508D: Malur, I need you to take care of something.
Skyrim.esm|03508F: Well, he's been very busy. Perhaps today he'll have some free time.
Skyrim.esm|035090: Again? Are you sure? He still hasn't answered your last several requests.
Skyrim.esm|035092: I haven't even said what it is.
Skyrim.esm|035093: Will you be needing anything special today?
Skyrim.esm|0350CE: I shall inform you the moment something noteworthy happens.
Skyrim.esm|0350D0: Why don't you see if Arch-Mage Aren is interested in joining me for a meal?
Skyrim.esm|0350D1: Doesn't really matter. Thanks, but no.
Skyrim.esm|0350DF: Arnbjorn is safe, and for that you have my thanks. But what of the fool? Is Cicero dead?
Skyrim.esm|0350E0: Oh, I like that! Very good, very good! Creative! But killing me would be a mistake! Oh yes. You would displease our mother, hmm? For she's your mother too, isn't she... Listener? Walk away! Let poor Cicero live! Tell the pretender Astrid you did the job! Stabbed, strangled, drowned poor Cicero! One little itty bitty lie!
Skyrim.esm|0350E2: Excellent. Once again, you've proven yourself a born assassin. Tell you what. Why don't you hold onto Shadowmere a while longer? He's a fine steed, and hasn't been ridden nearly as much as he should lately. And now that this Cicero mess has been mopped up, we can get back to the matter at hand, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0350E3: Traitor? Me? Silly assassin. So confused, so confused... and they say I'm mad! If I'm a traitor, so are you! Have you not heard the maiden's voice? Are you not the Listener? So walk away! Let poor Cicero live! Tell Astrid you did the job! Stabbed, strangled, drowned poor Cicero! A tiny white lie for a dark assassin!
Skyrim.esm|0350E4: Kill me or don't, Listener! The power is yours! But spare poor Cicero, and a friend you will have! Oh, such a friend, until we enter the Void...
Skyrim.esm|0350E6: Oh, you prefer to listen, eh? Of course, of course! The Listener listens! A joke! A funny joke! I get it. Then listen to this - don't kill me. Let poor Cicero live! I attacked the strumpet Astrid, I did! And I'd do it again! Anything for our mother! Return to the pretender, tell her I'm dead! Tell her you strangled me with my own intestines! Ha ha! But lie! Yes, lie! Lie, and let me live!
Skyrim.esm|0350E7: You caught me! I surrender! Ha ha ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|0350EA: There is no news, I'm afraid.
Skyrim.esm|0350EB: So, what's the latest exciting news about goings-on in Winterhold?
Skyrim.esm|0350EE: None? Nothing at all? Nothing from the College, even?
Skyrim.esm|0350EF: With the Empire's help, Winterhold will be back on its feet in no time.
Skyrim.esm|0350F0: I do what's necessary so we can get by. Have to, since I can't count on my brother for anything.
Skyrim.esm|0350F1: What is it doing? Walking around nearly naked...
Skyrim.esm|03510B: Bassianus is still so terrified of Jofthor. If he knew that I'd keep him from hunting the poor boy down, he'd take Fastred to Riften without a second thought. Let him know that I'll watch out for them. I just want my daughter to be happy.
Skyrim.esm|03510D: She would? If she's willing to deal with him, then everything would be all right. I'll... I'll go speak to Fastred. Post-haste!
Skyrim.esm|03510E: Have you spoken to my parents yet? Please, my love depends on it!
Skyrim.esm|03510F: That's the trouble. I know Faleen from the Keep, but I have no idea what sorts of things she likes. It's not a simple matter. I could offend her fairly easily by bringing up the wrong subject. I've seen it happen before.
Skyrim.esm|035110: As do I. It's a puzzling situation.
Skyrim.esm|035233: It's too cold for my bones, but that never seems to stop you, dear.
Skyrim.esm|035234: Another gorgeous gray day, right dear?
Skyrim.esm|035235: Kharag, take a break and drink with me! Those trees aren't going anywhere!
Skyrim.esm|035236: Why so glum, my ugly friend? Did we run out of mead?
Skyrim.esm|035237: Hjorunn, the last time you tried anything, your fist broke on my head!
Skyrim.esm|035238: Just as long as you don't breathe on me, Hjorunn. I'll be out for hours.
Skyrim.esm|035239: I have to work twice as hard because you drink up all our profits.
Skyrim.esm|03523A: Can you pay attention to work for once, instead of your mug?
Skyrim.esm|03523B: I'll see if I can't get some work done inside, where it's warm.
Skyrim.esm|03523C: Guess we can't afford to miss that much work, can we? Oh well...
Skyrim.esm|03523D: True enough, you cur. Back to work, I suppose...
Skyrim.esm|03523E: I'm gonna go play soldier with a tree!
Skyrim.esm|03523F: That's nice, son. Just don't stomp on the crop like last time!
Skyrim.esm|035240: What did you say? I ought to break off one of those tusks and use it as my drinking horn!
Skyrim.esm|035241: Don't make me to defend the honor of my mead! It's been my friend a lot longer than you have!
Skyrim.esm|035293: Kill me if you must. But I can't promise I won't haunt you till the end of your days if... if the Dread Lord lets me. Ha ha ha ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|0352D7: Simple... I'm going to cause a distraction and you're going to steal Madesi's silver ring from a strongbox under his stand. Once you have it, I want you to place it in Brand-Shei's pocket without him noticing.
Skyrim.esm|0352D8: Oh, but that's where you're wrong, lad. Wealth is my business. Maybe you'd like a taste?
Skyrim.esm|0352D9: Oh, but that's where you're wrong, lass. Wealth is my business. Maybe you'd like a taste?
Skyrim.esm|0352DA: I've got a bit of an errand to perform, but I need an extra pair of hands. And in my line of work, extra hands are well-paid.
Skyrim.esm|0352DB: I'm saying you've got the coin but you didn't earn a septim of it honestly. I can tell.
Skyrim.esm|0352DC: When I'm done here, I'd be more than happy to sell you an elixir.
Skyrim.esm|0352DD: Please, please... hold all your questions until after I've finished!
Skyrim.esm|0352DE: Oh, excuse me, milord. I'll be glad to sell you an elixir after I'm done with these fine people.
Skyrim.esm|0352DF: Glad to see you finally came to to your senses. Ready to make some coin?
Skyrim.esm|0352E0: Running a little light in the pockets, lad?
Skyrim.esm|0352E1: Never done an honest day's work in your life for all that coin you're carrying, eh lass?
Skyrim.esm|0352E2: It's all about sizing up your mark, lad. The way they walk, what they're wearing. It's a dead giveaway.
Skyrim.esm|0352E5: Sorry... I usually have a nose for this kind of thing. Never mind then, lad. If you change your mind, come find me.
Skyrim.esm|035379: This place has had you shaking in your armor ever since we arrived.
Skyrim.esm|03537B: I'm fine, I'm fine. Let's move on.
Skyrim.esm|03537D: Quiet! I think I heard something.
Skyrim.esm|035596: My daughter Adara is also my apprentice. She's a quick study.
Skyrim.esm|035598: Stormcloaks or Imperials. Either way, all they want is everything for free and you to lick their boots.
Skyrim.esm|035599: You want a cut of venison or mutton? Of course you do.
Skyrim.esm|03559A: Don't try to haggle with me. Buy or leave.
Skyrim.esm|03559B: The secret to chopping meat is never washing your hands.
Skyrim.esm|03559F: I am the Jarl of Markarth, now. The Silver-Blood family is finally where it should have always been.
Skyrim.esm|0355A0: I'm Thongvor's steward now. Every coin in this city is ours.
Skyrim.esm|0355CB: All right, then. Let's put that to the test. The group I represent has its home in the Ratway beneath Riften... a tavern called the Ragged Flagon. Get there in one piece and we'll see if you've really got what it takes.
Skyrim.esm|0355CC: Look, I'll make this simple for you. The group I represent has its home in the Ratway beneath Riften... a tavern called the Ragged Flagon. When you make up your mind, come find me there and we'll talk about your future.
Skyrim.esm|0355CE: Arrogant, eh? Play it however you want, but listen up. The group I represent has its home in the Ratway beneath Riften... a tavern called the Ragged Flagon. When you come to your senses, find me there and we'll discuss your future.
Skyrim.esm|0355CF: Bah. My organization's been having a run of bad luck, but I suppose that's just how it goes. But never mind that, you did the job and you did it well. Best of all, there's more where that came from... if you think you can handle it.
Skyrim.esm|0357F5: Thongvor's made me his housecarl. Remember, me killing you is legal.
Skyrim.esm|0357F9: My family owns this inn, but I've put Kleppr in charge. Right rat for the right job, I say.
Skyrim.esm|0357FA: Thongvor may be the head of the family, but I'm the hands.
Skyrim.esm|0357FB: We control every piece of this city. The people should be calling us kings.
Skyrim.esm|0357FC: If someone joins the Forsworn, we haul him to the mines.
Skyrim.esm|0357FD: Every prisoner in the Reach is just another worker for our family.
Skyrim.esm|0357FF: I'm married to Thonar Silver-Blood. Keep that in mind while you're speaking to me.
Skyrim.esm|035800: I married Thonar for the money, and I'm not the least bit ashamed to admit it.
Skyrim.esm|035806: I'm just a servant. Don't pay me any mind.
Skyrim.esm|035807: Working in the Treasury House isn't so bad. I earn good money.
Skyrim.esm|035809: Ildene has been working in the Treasury House since before I was born.
Skyrim.esm|03580A: You just let old nana know if you need anything, all right dear?
Skyrim.esm|035811: You learn a new word like a master... you truly do have the gift.
Skyrim.esm|035812: Lorkhan's eyes! Look at that big bastard! Keep your head down, let's see what it does.
Skyrim.esm|035814: Stay under cover. This is what we came for. We need to see what happens.
Skyrim.esm|035815: Steady. Let's see what it does.
Skyrim.esm|035816: I don't know exactly. We need to find the ancient burial mound. Let's ask around town.
Skyrim.esm|035856: Bring up some more mead, my boy. This cask's nearly tapped!
Skyrim.esm|035859: Yes, father. Which cask should we open?
Skyrim.esm|03585C: It's been a good week, my boy! Loud customers mean good cheer, and good cheer means a full till! You know, I ought to retire and leave you to run the Skeever, son!
Skyrim.esm|035861: I'm bored! Let's play a game!
Skyrim.esm|035862: Just bring any of them, lad! Can't risk thirsty customers without mead to drink, even for a second!
Skyrim.esm|035863: Any of them! Just hurry up! No time for a debate when customers are thirsty!
Skyrim.esm|035869: Aww, I'm hungry! Can we get some food, instead?
Skyrim.esm|03586A: I'm not playing with you, Kayd! You play mean!
Skyrim.esm|03586B: Father says I shouldn't play with you anymore.
Skyrim.esm|03586E: You're on! Loser buys the next round!
Skyrim.esm|03586F: In your dreams! If I win, the next round's on you!
Skyrim.esm|035871: I could run the Skeever! You even said I'm almost as smart as you!
Skyrim.esm|035872: Why not let me run the Skeever? I already know lots about it!
Skyrim.esm|035873: What about "Auntie Faida" in Dragon Bridge? She runs a bar, and you said she's one of your favorite girls!
Skyrim.esm|035874: But you always say you want more girls in the bar to begin with!
Skyrim.esm|035875: But I'm sure those Skyrim dogs would stand down if the keepers of the tales showed their patriotism!
Skyrim.esm|035876: Maybe if we stopped singing about their silly Nord heroes, the rebels would stop making such a fuss!
Skyrim.esm|035877: That's it! I'm not retiring, ever! End of discussion!
Skyrim.esm|035878: Well, you see, that's a little complicated, and... you know what? Never mind. Guess I'm not retiring anytime soon.
Skyrim.esm|03587F: Viarmo, the college really must take a stand on the war!
Skyrim.esm|035880: Viarmo, don't you think it's time that the Bards College officially declared support for the empire?
Skyrim.esm|035882: Don't be a silly girl! Running a bar's no job for a young lady.
Skyrim.esm|035883: Hah! A girl running a bar? Don't be ridiculous, Minette!
Skyrim.esm|035884: Yes, yes, fine. Just hurry! Mustn't keep the customers thirsty!
Skyrim.esm|035885: I don't know, father... There's a whole world out there I want to see.
Skyrim.esm|035886: You'd just spend your time here, bossing me around and yelling for more drinks. You're practically retired already...
Skyrim.esm|035890: A round for all of my friends!
Skyrim.esm|035892: Lady Ateia, it is the official position of the college to remain neutral in all political affairs. We cannot risk our history by choosing sides.
Skyrim.esm|035893: Now, now, our duty is to record the great tales of history, not to pick sides in them. Best to stay out of the warfare, I feel.
Skyrim.esm|035894: Aia, please be quiet. Viarmo and I are discussing matters of national politics.
Skyrim.esm|035895: Aia, this is a delicate matter. Why don't you review your lessons and be silent for a while.
Skyrim.esm|035896: Can I buy you a drink, darling?
Skyrim.esm|035897: So, um, mind if I buy you a drink?
Skyrim.esm|03589B: Good idea, sis. Maybe you ought to be in charge!
Skyrim.esm|03589D: Bring the Blackbriar 195 Berry-Blend. It should be at its peak right now, and if it doesn't get served soon, it'll only go bad.
Skyrim.esm|03589E: We just got a cask of Old Gold 200 that has a crack in it. Better serve it now before it goes bad.
Skyrim.esm|03589F: Fat Toad 190 is always a crowd-pleaser. Father could probably charge extra.
Skyrim.esm|0358A7: Pantea, perhaps you should attend to your students, and leave the politics to me.
Skyrim.esm|0358A8: That is precisely why we remain neutral. Ours is to tell tales of empires, not to make or break them.
Skyrim.esm|0358A9: Bet I can reach the bottom of this flagon before you!
Skyrim.esm|0358AA: Bet I can down this ale faster than you!
Skyrim.esm|0358AC: You heard the man! Drink and be merry!
Skyrim.esm|0358AD: Let's hear it for our kind benefactor!
Skyrim.esm|0358AE: Not if you were the last man in the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0358B0: You can buy me whatever you want!
Skyrim.esm|0358B1: Aw, that's so sweet of you! Thanks!
Skyrim.esm|0358B2: Being friends with the bartender's son has its advantages, huh?
Skyrim.esm|035B0E: A man's heart is his own burden to bear, so I will honor your request, though it casts a bit of gloom on an otherwise glorious day.
Skyrim.esm|035B10: Well, I suppose some kind of speech is in order.
Skyrim.esm|035B12: There is much to do, and I need every able bodied man and woman committed to rebuilding Skyrim. A great darkness is growing, and soon we will be called to fight it, on these shores or abroad. The Aldmeri Dominion may have defeated the Empire, but it has not defeated Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|035B13: And it is for these reasons that I cannot accept the mantle of "High King." Not until the Moot declares that title should adorn my shoulders will I accept it.
Skyrim.esm|035B14: Eh, not so bad. Nice touch about the High King.
Skyrim.esm|035B1C: It's a foregone conclusion, you know.
Skyrim.esm|035B25: Come, Galmar. We've still much work to do.
Skyrim.esm|035B31: It was you who fought the Thalmor and their puppets who would have us deny our gods and our heritage.
Skyrim.esm|035B32: Come, at the very least walk out with me.
Skyrim.esm|035B36: And of course, we couldn't have done it without you. May the gods preserve you.
Skyrim.esm|035B37: But more than that, it was you who fought for Skyrim, for our right to fight our own battles...
Skyrim.esm|035B40: The Imperials aren't going to leave us alone. They still have camps in the hills. They'll continue to strike out at us, whenever and wherever they can.
Skyrim.esm|035B41: And indeed, there are many that call us heroes.
Skyrim.esm|035B45: And indeed, there are many that call me hero.
Skyrim.esm|035B4A: Alduin, thuri! Boaan tiid vokriiha suleyksejun kruziik?
Skyrim.esm|035B55: That's great. I owe you one. Thanks!
Skyrim.esm|035B56: Beg pardon. You dropped some armor over there. Don't suppose you'd mind if I... took it?
Skyrim.esm|035B57: Oh. Well, you should probably go get it before someone steals it.
Skyrim.esm|035B58: We're here to teach you a lesson.
Skyrim.esm|035B59: This'll teach you not to take things that don't belong to you!
Skyrim.esm|035B71: Don't you worry about her. I've sent my best men to round her up.
Skyrim.esm|035B73: Don't you worry about him. I've sent my best men to round him up.
Skyrim.esm|035B9F: Thank you, I thought so, too.
Skyrim.esm|035BA0: But it is all of you who are the true heroes!
Skyrim.esm|035BA1: On the contrary, you exceed the rest. But, I will honor your request. The gods know of your victories, and they themselves will glory in your deeds.
Skyrim.esm|035BA2: I'll go gather the men in the courtyard.
Skyrim.esm|035BA5: I am indeed Ulfric Stormcloak, and at my side the man we know as Stormblade, and the world knows as the Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|035CB5: I am indeed Ulfric Stormcloak, and at my side the woman we know as Stormblade, and the world knows as the Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|035D4F: I'll be damned, you did it!
Skyrim.esm|035D51: That was well done. Come on. I've been wanting a closer look at one of these buggers.
Skyrim.esm|035D52: I owe you some answers, don't I?
Skyrim.esm|035D53: Kill the damn dragon! Unless you have a better idea?!
Skyrim.esm|035DA3: Yes, what about the Lady Elisif? Will she put aside her personal hatred for me, and her misplaced love for the Emperor and his coin, so that the suffering of our people will end? Will she acknowledge that it is we Nord's who will determine Skyrim's future? Will she swear fealty to me, so all may know that we are at peace, and a new day has dawned?
Skyrim.esm|035DA5: Yes, what about Jarl Erikur? Will he cast aside the Imperial coin falling into his coffers? Will he acknowledge that we Nords deserve to determine our own future? Will he swear fealty to me, that all may know we are at peace, and a new day has dawned?
Skyrim.esm|035DA6: I'm not afraid of the remnants of the Legion, in time they'll all give up and go home. What I fear, is that the Thalmor will see our victory here and turn greater attention to our shores. We must be prepared to face them.
Skyrim.esm|035DA8: Very good. Come, the people await us.
Skyrim.esm|035DAA: May the gods preserve us all.
Skyrim.esm|035DAD: And now, I present to you, Ulfric Stormcloak, hero of the people, liberator and High King of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|035DC9: Stormblade. I want you to have my sword, a token of my appreciation. Now then. The men will expect a speech. Will you stand by my side? I wish to honor you, Dragonborn and the truest of Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|035DE5: The men will expect a speech. Will you stand by my side? I wish to honor you, Dragonborn and the truest of Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|035DEB: Then it is settled. The Jarl will continue to rule Solitude, I will garrison armies here to ward off Imperial attempts to reclaim the city. And in due time, the Moot will meet, and settle the claim to High King once and for all.
Skyrim.esm|035DF0: To return to our glory and traditions, to determine our own future!
Skyrim.esm|035DF1: It was you who fought a dying Empire who sunk its claws into our land, trying to drag us down with it.
Skyrim.esm|035DF2: There's no shame in such thoughts. A man without doubts is a man without a conscience. But rest assured. We've won Skyrim's right to determine her own future. And for that, every Nord praises us. But I will heed your wishes.
Skyrim.esm|035DF3: It was you who fought your kin who didn't understand our cause, who weren't willing to pay the price of our freedom.
Skyrim.esm|035E08: This is worse than I thought...
Skyrim.esm|035E0B: If my analysis is correct, the dragon buried near Kynesgrove will be the next to come back to life.
Skyrim.esm|035E0C: We need to find that burial mound before its dragon comes back to life.
Skyrim.esm|035E0E: Come on. Let's go see if there's a mythical dragon buried up there.
Skyrim.esm|035E0F: We need to find the burial mound. Let's ask in the inn.
Skyrim.esm|035E12: So, you made it out of there alive. Ancano was right... you are dangerous. I'm afraid I'll have to take that Staff from you now. Ancano wants it kept safe... oh, and he wants you dead. Nothing personal.
Skyrim.esm|035ED6: What was that you said, venomous wife of mine?
Skyrim.esm|035ED7: I've worked in the Treasury House for almost twenty years.
Skyrim.esm|035EDA: So the Stormcloaks took the Reach? Maybe Talos really is on their side.
Skyrim.esm|035EDC: The selection seems small, but we can provide whatever you need. Just ask.
Skyrim.esm|035EDD: No, I'm not Arnleif. Yes, I know it's called Arnleif and Sons.
Skyrim.esm|035EE2: Lisbet handles the customers. I just check the shipments.
Skyrim.esm|035EE3: Feel free to take a look around the store.
Skyrim.esm|035EE4: I've been at Arnleif and Sons all my life. It's a good store.
Skyrim.esm|035EE5: Old Arnleif would be proud of Lisbet. She's kept this place running, thick and thin.
Skyrim.esm|035EE7: I brought in my cousin Cosnach to help me handle shipments.
Skyrim.esm|035EEA: "Check the shipments" Lisbet says. "There are no shipments" I says.
Skyrim.esm|035EEB: No breaks for a good worker out here.
Skyrim.esm|035EED: If I can't get you something, I'm sure my wife will bellow at me until I can.
Skyrim.esm|035EF2: Now you just tell me if you need something.
Skyrim.esm|035EF3: You come right to me if you find an unwashed floor or lumpy bed.
Skyrim.esm|035EF5: Isn't my son Hreinn such a good worker? Not like his father at all....
Skyrim.esm|035EF6: Don't mind the yelling. My parents are always going at each other.
Skyrim.esm|035EF7: I'm just here to clean up.
Skyrim.esm|035EFB: My father Kleppr is the one you want to talk to. I just clean up.
Skyrim.esm|035EFE: Poor Banning. Twice as much work here in the stables without him.
Skyrim.esm|035F00: You treat a horse well, it'll take you to Oblivion and back.
Skyrim.esm|035F02: Stormcloaks took Markarth. It is good to see Talos worshipped openly again.
Skyrim.esm|035F03: Songs are just old memories that help the mead go down quicker.
Skyrim.esm|035F16: You should see that Orc over in Understone Keep, have him forge you some new armor...
Skyrim.esm|035F1C: Sorry. You already have an animal with you.
Skyrim.esm|035F1D: War dogs cost gold, friend. Come back when you have some.
Skyrim.esm|035F43: Haven't seen you here before, egg sister. Welcome!
Skyrim.esm|035F81: Well... you probably should go collect it before someone else does, shouldn't you?
Skyrim.esm|035F84: Well what did you go dropping it for? Got my hopes up for nothin'...
Skyrim.esm|035F85: You dropped it by mistake? Well, if I was you, I'd go grab it now before somebody does.
Skyrim.esm|035F86: Aww. I thought you were done with it.
Skyrim.esm|035F87: Well... fine. I wasn't really gonna do anything with it anyway. Hmph.
Skyrim.esm|035F88: Well, you'd better go pick it up then, before someone snatches it.
Skyrim.esm|035F89: Stay away from my family if you value your life.
Skyrim.esm|035F8A: Good. But know that further dishonor to my family will mean your death.
Skyrim.esm|035F8B: You stay away from my family. Or you die. Be smart. Stay away.
Skyrim.esm|035F8C: This had better be the last I hear of you disrespecting my family.
Skyrim.esm|035F8D: Mark these words carefully, fetcher. Stay away from my family, or I will paint the ground with your blood.
Skyrim.esm|035F8E: Any further dishonor done to my kin, and I'll put you in the ground.
Skyrim.esm|035F8F: That wasn't so hard, now was it.
Skyrim.esm|035F90: You! Fetcher! I should kill you for assaulting my family. But I will accept your apology...
Skyrim.esm|035F91: You know who I am? You attacked one of my clan. I've come to accept your apology... or your death.
Skyrim.esm|035F92: You! You hurt my family. I don't like when my family gets hurt. Say you're sorry. Or say goodbye.
Skyrim.esm|035F93: You attacked one of my family. What do you have to say for yourself?
Skyrim.esm|035F94: You can't attack my family and get away with it, fetcher! I will have an apology, or your head on a spike. Choose.
Skyrim.esm|035F95: Dishonorable dog. You attacked one of my kin. So now you're going to apologize... or I'm going to lop off your head.
Skyrim.esm|035F96: Here's the situation. You attacked my family. So now you're going to apologize.. or I'm going to kill you. I hope we understand each other...
Skyrim.esm|035F97: Really, the nerve of some people, leaving their trash wherever they please.
Skyrim.esm|035F98: What kind of filthy barbarian just throws their trash on the ground?
Skyrim.esm|035F99: Oh, by all means. Just leave your refuse wherever you see fit.
Skyrim.esm|035F9A: There's a place for trash, and that's not it.
Skyrim.esm|035F9B: In my day, we knew how to dispose of trash properly.
Skyrim.esm|035F9C: What's the matter with you? You think you can just go dropping your junk wherever you like?
Skyrim.esm|035F9D: Look at this one. Spoiled as a child, I'll bet. Allowed to leave trash just lying around...
Skyrim.esm|035F9E: I swear, is there no end to these uncouth savages, just throwing their trash wherever they please...
Skyrim.esm|035F9F: Hrmmm. Pick up your trash, fetcher.
Skyrim.esm|035FA0: I never did understand the local custom of simply leaving one's trash wherever is convenient...
Skyrim.esm|035FA1: Who does that? Who just leaves their garbage lying about?
Skyrim.esm|035FA2: Gah! Quit throwing your garbage everywhere.
Skyrim.esm|035FA3: One person's trash is another's treasure. Don't be surprised if you find that gone...
Skyrim.esm|035FA4: Umm... You dropped a piece armor over there. I was kind of hoping that I could, you know, have it?
Skyrim.esm|035FA6: Excuse me. I'm assuming that armor you got rid of wasn't an accident, but just in case, I wanted to make sure I could take it.
Skyrim.esm|035FA7: That armor you got rid of. I can have it, yeah?
Skyrim.esm|035FA8: Pardon. I saw you toss a piece of armor. You wouldn't mind if I take it, then?
Skyrim.esm|035FA9: Couldn't help but notice, that, um... you dropped some armor. Just wanted to check with you before I took it. Is that all right?
Skyrim.esm|035FAA: Hey. That armor. You got rid of it, yeah? I can take it, no problem?
Skyrim.esm|035FAB: If you're not going use that armor you dropped... do you mind if I take it?
Skyrim.esm|035FAE: What's he rummaging around in there for?
Skyrim.esm|035FAF: What's she rummaging around in there for?
Skyrim.esm|035FB0: Look at him, rummaging through refuse. Wonder what he's lost... besides his mind.
Skyrim.esm|035FB1: Look at her, rummaging through refuse. Wonder what she's lost... besides her mind.
Skyrim.esm|035FB2: Tsk. What a man will do for a few coins...
Skyrim.esm|035FB3: Tsk. What a woman will do for a few coins...
Skyrim.esm|035FB4: Picking through garbage. Has he no shame?
Skyrim.esm|035FB5: Picking through garbage. Has she no shame?
Skyrim.esm|035FB6: What's he digging around in there for?
Skyrim.esm|035FB7: What's she digging around in there for?
Skyrim.esm|035FB8: What's he doing? Looking for rubies in rubbish? Get a job!
Skyrim.esm|035FB9: What's she doing? Looking for rubies in rubbish? Get a job!
Skyrim.esm|035FDF: Are you out of your mind?
Skyrim.esm|035FE0: If you value your well being, you will offer an apology...
Skyrim.esm|035FE2: You may care nothing of honor, but I do. I'm not leaving without an apology.
Skyrim.esm|035FE3: You sure you want it to come to this?
Skyrim.esm|035FE4: In your death may you find the honor you lacked in life.
Skyrim.esm|035FE6: Say you're sorry. Say it now.
Skyrim.esm|035FE9: You're making a fatal mistake. Apologize, and you may yet live...
Skyrim.esm|035FEB: I will not tolerate this offense against my kin.
Skyrim.esm|035FEC: Are you mad? Apologize, or I will gut you like a horker.
Skyrim.esm|035FED: Then you are fool, and will die for it.
Skyrim.esm|035FEE: Honor may mean nothing to you, but it means much to me. Your apology, or your life.
Skyrim.esm|035FEF: Only your death or an apology will restore my family's honor. Last chance.
Skyrim.esm|035FF0: Then I guess we're done talking.
Skyrim.esm|035FF1: Perhaps I haven't made myself clear. Your best and least painful course of action is to apologize and promise to leave my family in peace.
Skyrim.esm|035FF2: No? You sure? 'Cause we're reaching the point of no return here...
Skyrim.esm|035FF3: Annnddd... Point of no return reached.
Skyrim.esm|035FF6: Thanks! I'm going to go put it on right now.
Skyrim.esm|035FF9: Well great! Let's see anyone try to hurt me now...
Skyrim.esm|035FFA: Best luck I've had all day.
Skyrim.esm|035FFB: I'm going to go get it right now and wear it. Many thanks!
Skyrim.esm|035FFC: What'd you pick up there? Oh, I wish I saw that first. You're so lucky!
Skyrim.esm|035FFD: Hey, someone had to take it, right?
Skyrim.esm|035FFF: Lucky for you that was just sitting there for the taking.
Skyrim.esm|036000: Oh, wow! That was just sitting there? I wish I found it first.
Skyrim.esm|036002: You just find that lying around? Must be your lucky day.
Skyrim.esm|036003: He might find something good in there. One man's trash is another's treasure.
Skyrim.esm|036004: She might find something good in there. One man's trash is another's treasure.
Skyrim.esm|036005: The poor dear must truly be hungry to comb through rubbish like that. Hope he finds something edible.
Skyrim.esm|036006: The poor dear must truly be hungry to comb through rubbish like that. Hope she finds something edible.
Skyrim.esm|036007: Sometimes it pays to look in every nook and cranny.
Skyrim.esm|036008: Sometimes it pays to look in every nook and cranny.
Skyrim.esm|036009: When you're hungry, you'll look anywhere for food. Can't blame him.
Skyrim.esm|03600A: When you're hungry, you'll look anywhere for food. Can't blame her.
Skyrim.esm|03600B: Desperate times call for desperate measures. Maybe he found what he was looking for in there.
Skyrim.esm|03600C: Desperate times call for desperate measures. Maybe she found what she was looking for in there.
Skyrim.esm|03600D: How unfortunate he has to dig through refuse like that. Someone should give him a piece of bread.
