Skyrim.esm|03600E: How unfortunate she has to dig through refuse like that. Someone should give her a piece of bread.
Skyrim.esm|03600F: You never know what you'll find laying around in a crate or barrel. People toss the darnedest things in them.
Skyrim.esm|036010: You never know what you'll find laying around in a crate or barrel. People toss the darnedest things in them.
Skyrim.esm|036013: Some folk - they got no shame.
Skyrim.esm|036014: Well now! That's a shamefully absurd way to go around in public.
Skyrim.esm|036015: Ummm... they'd lock me up if I went around like that.
Skyrim.esm|036016: What is that half-naked fetcher trying to prove?
Skyrim.esm|036019: That's not something you see every day...
Skyrim.esm|03601A: Gracious! This one is certainly unaccustomed to such sights...
Skyrim.esm|03601B: Well now! Aren't you putting on quite the show?
Skyrim.esm|03601C: Shameful, really. I blame the parents.
Skyrim.esm|03601D: Oh, dear. Would you look at that? How embarrassing.
Skyrim.esm|03601F: Gah! Look over there. I can't believe it.
Skyrim.esm|036021: It takes a lot of confidence to walk around naked. Trust me on that.
Skyrim.esm|036023: Crazy person... Something must be wrong with its head, to strut around like that.
Skyrim.esm|036024: They're half-naked. That ain't right. Somebody's gotta say somethin'...
Skyrim.esm|036026: Somebody get that idiot some armor...
Skyrim.esm|036027: A bit much, going around like that...
Skyrim.esm|036029: Never see me strutting around like that!
Skyrim.esm|03602B: Whoa... Not even I'm that drunk...
Skyrim.esm|03602D: Somebody get that fool a blanket.
Skyrim.esm|03602E: Despite the shame, this one can't help but stare.
Skyrim.esm|03602F: By Ysmir's beard! I didn't know it was gonna be a party!
Skyrim.esm|036030: For shame! You should be locked up for strutting around like that.
Skyrim.esm|036031: Oh my. Been a lot of years since I've seen something like that...
Skyrim.esm|036032: Got to admire the chops it takes to walk around like that.
Skyrim.esm|036033: Not my thing, but who am I to judge...
Skyrim.esm|036034: Wait until the boys hear about this.
Skyrim.esm|036035: Now that's something else, right there!
Skyrim.esm|036037: You left this back there. Figured you'd be wantin' it. Here ya go.
Skyrim.esm|036038: I think you accidentally mislaid this, dear. Here you go.
Skyrim.esm|036039: You dropped this. Good thing I found it and not someone else, otherwise you might have lost it for good.
Skyrim.esm|03603A: You left this over there. You want it back, right? Here.
Skyrim.esm|03603B: I think you dropped this! Good thing I brought it to you, huh?
Skyrim.esm|03603D: You should take more care, leaving things lying about. Here.
Skyrim.esm|03603E: Excuse me. You misplaced this, I believe? There you go. Take care now.
Skyrim.esm|03603F: You left this over there. You're lucky - someone else might have kept it for himself. Here you go.
Skyrim.esm|0361C1: We've all heard that one before, Bryn! Quit kidding yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0361C2: Dying breed, eh? Well what do you call that then!
Skyrim.esm|0361C3: Give it up, Brynjolf... those days are over.
Skyrim.esm|0361C4: I'm telling you, this one is different...
Skyrim.esm|0361C5: It's time to face the truth, old friend. You, Vex, Mercer... you're all part of a dying breed. Things are changing!
Skyrim.esm|0361DB: You need to meet with him and give him whatever you think you can't live without while you're inside the embassy. The Thalmor are paranoid and thorough. You won't get in the front door with anything but the clothes on your back.
Skyrim.esm|0361DC: Don't worry, it will all be waiting for you when you get back. Just make sure you get back out of there alive with the information we need. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0361DD: Really? You're who she picked? I hope she knows what she's doing.
Skyrim.esm|0361DE: Good. I have your invitation to the party. But the only way you're going to get past the guards is if they really believe you're an invited guest. Which means you need to look the part, and not be armed to the teeth. Here, put this on.
Skyrim.esm|0361DF: Okay. I'll get this inside the Embassy for you. I've got to go. I'll find you at the party, don't worry.
Skyrim.esm|0361E0: This is no time for cold feet. I've put everything in your hands here, not least Malborn's life. Now do whatever it is you need to do, and let's get going before we miss our chance!
Skyrim.esm|0361E2: You can't go to a party at the Thalmor Embassy dressed like that.
Skyrim.esm|0361E3: Have you given Malborn the gear you want to smuggle into the embassy?
Skyrim.esm|0361E4: Your job is to get into the party without being fingered as a spy. I'll take care of the rest.
Skyrim.esm|0361E5: Here's the deal. I can smuggle some equipment into the Embassy for you. Don't plan on bringing anything else in with you. The Thalmor take security very seriously. Give me what you can't live without, and I'll make sure to get it into the Embassy. The rest is up to you.
Skyrim.esm|0361E6: When you're ready, I'll keep the rest of your gear safe until you get back. You'll only have what Malborn smuggled in for you, plus whatever you can pick up inside.
Skyrim.esm|0361E7: I'll give you a few more minutes. I can't wait here all day. It's my neck on the line, remember.
Skyrim.esm|03626A: Well, well... color me impressed, lass. I wasn't certain I'd ever see you again!
Skyrim.esm|03626C: The Hag's Cure is here for all your discreet needs...
Skyrim.esm|03626D: A little bit of old Reach magic can cure whatever ills you....
Skyrim.esm|03626E: Oh yes... you'll need one of my special brews...
Skyrim.esm|036270: Not scared of an old woman now, are you?
Skyrim.esm|036275: I'm Bothela's assistant. She's the one you should talk to if you need an herb or a potion.
Skyrim.esm|036276: There's so much to learn about the shop. I'll never get it all straight.
Skyrim.esm|036278: Why can't the men in this city leave me alone?
Skyrim.esm|03627B: Alms. I said "alms" you backbiter. Give a crippled worker a helping hand.
Skyrim.esm|03627F: They don't like us talking to people when we work.
Skyrim.esm|036284: Can't talk. Mulush will beat me if I stop working.
Skyrim.esm|036285: Can't you see I'm working? Beat it.
Skyrim.esm|036288: I oversee Markarth Smelter. Worker gets out of line, I get him back in line.
Skyrim.esm|036289: These workers. Always complaining. Always excuses.
Skyrim.esm|03628A: Shipment of silver this month is behind. Nepos is to blame, not me.
Skyrim.esm|03628B: I tell Thonar I can only smelt what Nepos and his miners dig up.
Skyrim.esm|03628C: I'm in charge of the prisoners here.
Skyrim.esm|03628D: No funny business. I'm watching you.
Skyrim.esm|03628E: You do something out of line, I'll put you down.
Skyrim.esm|036298: Don't reach for a shiv around me, or we'll both regret it.
Skyrim.esm|03629E: Whenever I get out of here, I'm going to go kill a Nord.
Skyrim.esm|03629F: I was sent here because someone said I was a Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|0362A0: I wonder how grandma Bothela is doing. She's probably worried sick about me.
Skyrim.esm|0362A3: I left behind my daughter Uaile when I was taken.
Skyrim.esm|0362A4: How old is my daughter now, I wonder? In prison, you lose track of time.
Skyrim.esm|0362A5: You need to not bother me.
Skyrim.esm|0362A7: You serve your time by digging. So dig.
Skyrim.esm|0362A9: I got locked up one too many times. Now I'm in for life.
Skyrim.esm|0362AA: I swear, if I ever get out of here, I'm giving up thieving. Really.
Skyrim.esm|0362C3: So far so good. Let's hope nobody saw us slip out. We need to pass through the kitchen. Your gear is hidden in the larder. Just stay close and let me do any talking, got it? Follow me.
Skyrim.esm|0362C4: Tss! Get out of here, I saw nothing.
Skyrim.esm|0362C5: Rules, is it, Tsavani? I didn't realize that eating Moon Sugar was permitted.
Skyrim.esm|0362C6: Your gear is in that chest. I'll lock the door behind you. Don't screw this up.
Skyrim.esm|0362C7: Who comes, Malborn? You know I don't like strange smells in my kitchen.
Skyrim.esm|0362C8: A guest, feeling ill. Leave the poor wretch be.
Skyrim.esm|0362C9: Perhaps I should ask the Ambassador...
Skyrim.esm|0362CA: A guest? In the kitchens? You know this is against the rules...
Skyrim.esm|0362CB: Of course. Let me see if we have another bottle of that. I'll be waiting by the door for everyone to be distracted.
Skyrim.esm|0362CC: Hurry it up. I've got to get back before I'm missed.
Skyrim.esm|0362CD: Come on. If someone misses me at the party, we're both dead.
Skyrim.esm|0362CE: I need to lock the door behind you or the patrols might notice something's wrong.
Skyrim.esm|036353: I tried to tell them. They wouldn't listen. Fools. It's all come true... all I could do was watch our doom approach...
Skyrim.esm|036356: Dragons, pah. They can be killed. The Blades killed many in their early days as dragon-slayers. No, the dragons are merely the final portent of the End of Days.
Skyrim.esm|036357: Oh, yes! The prophecies make clear the signs that will precede the end times. One by one, I have seen them fulfilled.
Skyrim.esm|036358: You're right, I forget myself. I've lived without hope for so long... The prophecies are clear. Only the Dragonborn can stop Alduin.
Skyrim.esm|036359: What? You're... can it really be true? Dragonborn? Then... there is hope! The gods have not abandoned us! We must... we must...
Skyrim.esm|03635A: Then... there is hope after all. For so long, all I could do was watch our doom approach, helplessly.
Skyrim.esm|03635D: The Blades knew, once upon a time. Alduin's Wall is the key. Yes, yes! Don't move. Don't move. I have it here, somewhere... never thought it would be any use, but now...
Skyrim.esm|036360: We must go, quickly now. Take me to Delphine. We have much to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|036361: Important? Far more than even she realizes! If you are Dragonborn, then...
Skyrim.esm|0365ED: No! You're wrong. You must be wrong. Laelette may have met her fate out in the marsh. I refuse to believe Alva had anything to do with this. There is no way you can prove it to the Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0365EE: Like I said. It got hot and smoky. Now it's cold and quiet. I wish someone would find me.
Skyrim.esm|0365EF: That's cheating. We're playing hide and seek. You have to find me before Laelette does.
Skyrim.esm|0365F0: Don't you remember? His name is Hroggar.
Skyrim.esm|0365F3: I can't tell you. She might hear me. She's so close. If you can find me first, I can tell you.
Skyrim.esm|0365F4: I thought Laelette left to join the war. I hadn't heard from her since she left.
Skyrim.esm|0365F6: Perhaps we can go another few decades without a disaster. That would be nice.
Skyrim.esm|0365F7: The exact nature of my research is a secret, I'm afraid, but it really is quite valuable.
Skyrim.esm|0365F8: I don't think anyone here truly understands how useful my work will be.
Skyrim.esm|0365F9: When I've finished what I'm working on, everyone will understand its significance.
Skyrim.esm|0365FA: We have survived difficult times, and are better mages for it. We owe you that, Arch-Mage. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0365FB: Illusion really is the least appreciated of the schools of magic.
Skyrim.esm|0365FD: If only I could be free of the mundane, day-to-day activities life so often requires.
Skyrim.esm|0365FE: I'm not one for commendations, but I might've lost everything that matters. So... thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0365FF: Hundreds of years have gone into assembling this collection. It's going to stay pristine, understand?
Skyrim.esm|036600: You need a book, you talk to me. Otherwise you're going to find yourself in a lot of pain.
Skyrim.esm|036601: I don't care if you wrote it yourself -- if you want a book from here, you go through me.
Skyrim.esm|036602: The Arcanaeum is always accepting new volumes. I'll take what I can get.
Skyrim.esm|036604: We all have you to thank for saving the College. Your position is well-deserved.
Skyrim.esm|036605: I can't wait to see who succeeds Mirabelle. I've no doubt she'll be promoted to Arch-Mage at some point.
Skyrim.esm|036606: Certainly a few of my colleagues aren't quite as talented as they'd like to believe.
Skyrim.esm|036607: Savos Aren is wise beyond his years. He knows real talent when he sees it.
Skyrim.esm|036608: Nasty business with that Ancano fellow. Pity that I was unable to assist, but I was... otherwise occupied.
Skyrim.esm|036609: There are few places where one can pursue my type of work without fear of persecution.
Skyrim.esm|03660A: That 'incident' in Morthal was just a misunderstanding. No matter what the guards claim.
Skyrim.esm|03660B: The people of Skyrim have no appreciation for what we do here. Not that I care.
Skyrim.esm|03660C: Should you ever fall victim to an unfortunate accident, I promise your remains will be put to good use...
Skyrim.esm|03660E: Restoration is a perfectly valid school of magic, and don't let anyone tell you otherwise!
Skyrim.esm|03660F: Why are my research materials constantly disappearing?
Skyrim.esm|036610: Once again, I have found very insulting notes left in my personal effects. Simply barbaric.
Skyrim.esm|036611: Just because I don't fit in here is no reason for others to ridicule me behind my back.
Skyrim.esm|036612: I think the College is due for a change in leadership.
Skyrim.esm|036613: If you're working on any experiments, please clean up after yourself.
Skyrim.esm|036614: Just like anywhere else, it's all about who you know. Lucky for you, you know me.
Skyrim.esm|036615: I hope I can count on you if I ever need a favor.
Skyrim.esm|036616: The Arch-Mage triumphant! The College... Skyrim... All of Tamriel owes you a great debt!
Skyrim.esm|036617: There are some experiments the College frowns on. If they're aware of them, of course.
Skyrim.esm|036618: If you ever need something, especially something hard to come by, let me know.
Skyrim.esm|036619: I've spent a long time building up a list of contacts for goods from all over Tamriel.
Skyrim.esm|03661A: I am always available should you need me, Arch-Mage.
Skyrim.esm|03661B: The College will be here for you, no matter what. We always look after our own.
Skyrim.esm|03661C: Education is never appreciated until after the fact. Young people never understand this.
Skyrim.esm|03661D: Have you, by chance, seen my research notes? I seem to have misplaced them.
Skyrim.esm|03661E: What you learn here will last you a lifetime. Several, if you're talented.
Skyrim.esm|03661F: It's no secret that the College's reputation in Skyrim is... tainted.
Skyrim.esm|036620: Please don't do anything to anger the locals. They don't need additional reasons to hate us.
Skyrim.esm|036621: It is not the place of the College to become involved in local politics.
Skyrim.esm|036622: Please don't bother the Arch-Mage unless it's absolutely necessary.
Skyrim.esm|036623: If there are any problems, let me know. It's my job to keep things running smoothly.
Skyrim.esm|036624: Day-to-day operations are my responsibility, which means if you become a problem, you get sent to me.
Skyrim.esm|036625: Mine is not a popular position, but someone needs to keep things running smoothly around here.
Skyrim.esm|0366F5: Really? How did we afford that? Besides, I like the gown I already have.
Skyrim.esm|0366F6: Oh certainly, dear sister. We couldn't afford to have our customers look like the simple pigs they are, now could we?
Skyrim.esm|0366F7: Miss Stentor, watch your tongue in the Jarl's court!
Skyrim.esm|0366F8: Svari, Jala said you stole an apple from her cart yesterday. Is this true?
Skyrim.esm|0366FA: What news have you heard from the war, Irnskar?
Skyrim.esm|0366FC: Sir, we can't just keep trading blows with the rebels like this - it's only strengthening their resolve.
Skyrim.esm|0366FE: My dear Bryling. Barring any... fancy and folly... the good people of Haafingar are surely capable of dealing with the odd highwayman or stray dog.
Skyrim.esm|0366FF: Rest up, scum. Probably getting executed tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|036700: I'm not just talking about my subjects. I'm talking about my people: the proud Nords of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|036701: Maybe we'll have better luck tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|036703: New orders for the soldiers, Legate. Make sure they're distributed as noted.
Skyrim.esm|036704: You're right, of course. But when this war is over, I'll gladly resign and we can stop sneaking around like this.
Skyrim.esm|036706: Steward, when is my appointment to meet with General Tullius? There are some issues we must speak about.
Skyrim.esm|036707: I mean, the Redguards fought off the Aldmeri and the Empire, and now Hammerfell is an independent state. That's all we Nords want for Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|036708: They aren't my "people." I'm with the Empire because they pay me. And that means I'm out here, and you're in there. So shut up, scum!
Skyrim.esm|036709: I'm afraid "acceptable" just isn't acceptable anymore. I'm sure they can spare a few more coins in monthly rent.
Skyrim.esm|03670A: I suppose. But Svari, we don't steal. We're better than that. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|03670B: I know of a ship coming in from Hammerfell. Some cargo might get "lost"...
Skyrim.esm|03670C: He uses all of Haafingar's resources, and doesn't answer to me in the slightest? This hardly seems fair.
Skyrim.esm|03670D: Big shipment coming in later tonight. I need you three working late.
Skyrim.esm|03670F: Nonsense, dear. I've had more visitors than ever seeking the wisdom of the hearth goddesses.
Skyrim.esm|036710: I can commission one from the Radiant Raiment. It won't be cheap, but if you want the best, they're the only choice.
Skyrim.esm|036711: I'm on my feet all day. Can you please do this one thing for me?
Skyrim.esm|036712: Yes, and I'm also aware of how necessary my divinations and wards have been in the city's defense. My experiments are all I ask for in return.
Skyrim.esm|036713: Madame Endarie insisted. She made it free of charge, in honor of your lenience about last month's rent discrepancies.
Skyrim.esm|036714: Your compassion is admirable, but you see, we cannot help them all. The poor must learn from the examples of the gods and raise themselves up.
Skyrim.esm|036715: Indeed I have, sir. Elven weapons for their armies, sir. Elven material, but poor workmanship, like most of what we get from them.
Skyrim.esm|036716: On threat of what, exactly? This city wouldn't run without me! Now if you'll excuse me, my work awaits.
Skyrim.esm|036717: Fihada, where did you learn to make bows so well?
Skyrim.esm|036718: You won't like it, my lady.
Skyrim.esm|036719: What are you going to do after you graduate? I'm thinking of joining the Legion. I hear they're looking for skalds. Just think. Me - a war bard.
Skyrim.esm|03671A: I'm serious. If Erikur knew about us, he'd force you to resign from your position. And without you, he'd have the jarl bent around his little finger.
Skyrim.esm|03671B: I'm serious. If Erikur knew about us, he'd force you to resign from your position. And without you helping the Jarl, Solitude would be weaker.
Skyrim.esm|03671C: As always, Lord, your loyalty is a shining example to us all.
Skyrim.esm|03671D: Vivienne, could you check out supplies of mandrake root?
Skyrim.esm|03671E: Sister dear, could you try sewing these sleeves a little tighter? We can't have our customers looking sloppy because of your work.
Skyrim.esm|03671F: I told you you wouldn't like the news.
Skyrim.esm|036720: As you know, Solitude's coffers are much depleted by the war efforts. Thane Erikur, you have a strong head for business. What do you suggest?
Skyrim.esm|036722: What could be more important than hearing the prayers of the faithful, and answering the questions of the dedicated?
Skyrim.esm|036723: Yes, Brother. I'm sure they'll agree it's a fair price to pay in order to avoid interacting with you on a daily basis.
Skyrim.esm|036724: Simple. Let the Empire fight its own war, with its own funds, and without hijacking our supplies and soldiers. Let Haafingar rebuild.
Skyrim.esm|036725: Kynareth has blessed you with tremendous compassion, Silana. You will find a way, I'm sure of it.
Skyrim.esm|036726: I fear attendance is down. There are so few worshippers coming to see us. Could it be that the war has weakened their faith?
Skyrim.esm|036727: Lady Ateia has already arranged for me to perform in the court of the Jarl of Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|036728: In that case, I'm pretty sure it tried to buy me a drink last night.
Skyrim.esm|036729: I swear, those scheming elves charge us twice what anyone else would pay.
Skyrim.esm|03672A: Thane Bryling, what do you have to report about the state of Haafingar's defenses?
Skyrim.esm|03672B: Trouble on the roads and cities means less commerce. Less commerce means less money and more starving people. And that means more crime.
Skyrim.esm|03672C: Yeah, we'll get the valuables out of there, no problem!
Skyrim.esm|03672D: Oh, yes sir. I feel many of Solitude's families are having trouble getting by, and the wealthy do nothing to help them!
Skyrim.esm|03672E: This is an unfortunate but unavoidable ebb in revenue. But as long as we continue to support the Empire, our sacrifices will be well rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|03672F: Don't you think I know that? But these commands come straight from Cyrodiil. We must maintain order and put the rebellious jarls in their place, before we have a full-scale war on our hands.
Skyrim.esm|036730: You'd make a better Imperial ranger than me. I may have charmed the locals, but my fellow soldiers hated my waggling tongue.
Skyrim.esm|036731: We'll make it, Greta. We always have so far...
Skyrim.esm|036732: Scary, thinking of those boots with an army of Elven weapons at their command. Let's be thankful they're half a world away, eh?
Skyrim.esm|036734: I'm afraid he's had to reschedule, my grace. By all accounts, waging war takes his every moment.
Skyrim.esm|036736: My lady... Surely you're not that naive. Such foolishness would only leave us defenseless. The rebels would storm the palace in an afternoon.
Skyrim.esm|036738: It's all we've got for today. Don't be greedy.
Skyrim.esm|03673A: "Fair" is a word rarely applied to politics, my grace. I'm afraid you must learn to accept this.
Skyrim.esm|03673B: Mistress, the people of Solitude are destitute. Many are starving and desperate for work. We need to be more active, to reach out to them.
Skyrim.esm|03673C: The news is the same as it's been for the rest of the war, your grace. Our borders are secure, but at the cost of the guards of our towns and roads.
Skyrim.esm|03673D: Slow's the only way to do it! Work too hard and you'll warp the wood.
Skyrim.esm|03673E: Passably. Aia is my star pupil. None of the others can hold a candle to her. Especially not that wretched Illdi.
Skyrim.esm|03673F: Some of them have a long way to go, but they're mostly passable. Don't let them hear I said that, though!
Skyrim.esm|036740: Bunch of distractions, if you ask me. They just interrupt the rest of my work.
Skyrim.esm|036742: Hey, jailor! What I don't understand is why you're siding with the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|036743: My contact, she says Hammerfell steel is much desired by certain parties.
Skyrim.esm|036744: Very well. But I suspect her grace will not be pleased.
Skyrim.esm|036745: I barely make enough for us to eat as it is, Father. You can't just drink it all away.
Skyrim.esm|036746: That was suspiciously nice of her. I thought she hated everyone.
Skyrim.esm|036747: I'll talk with Jala, dear. I'm sure she'll accept a potato as repayment.
Skyrim.esm|036748: Why should we settle for half a fin when we could just take the whole whale?
Skyrim.esm|036749: I'm well aware that I won't like it. But it's my duty to keep my people safe. Tell me.
Skyrim.esm|03674B: Well, did you bring back any mead?
Skyrim.esm|03674C: Sir, this is costing lives of both Imperial loyalists and Skyrim citizens alike. And it's winning the rebels support where they had none before.
Skyrim.esm|03674D: Maybe. I'll see if I feel like it.
Skyrim.esm|03674E: Humph. That's only a problem for people who can't afford to hire their own security.
Skyrim.esm|036750: Spare a bite for a poor old veteran?
Skyrim.esm|036751: Please, ma'am, I've got nowhere to go...
Skyrim.esm|036752: I lost my eye defending the Empire... Spare some grub for a veteran?
Skyrim.esm|036754: Come now. You'll want to show up in Sovngarde refreshed. You got an eternity of merrymakin' ahead of you.
Skyrim.esm|036755: Guess the headsman's been busy with all of your friends. Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|036756: Falk, you shouldn't have come. You know what'd happen if people would find out about us.
Skyrim.esm|036758: Come on, Heimvar. My son could stoke the forge better than you do!
Skyrim.esm|036759: You're making progress, Heimvar. But my boy could still work the bellows better than you do!
Skyrim.esm|03675A: Shoo! Go away! You get nothing from me.
Skyrim.esm|03675B: You bother me again, maybe you will not walk away. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|03675C: Sorry, I have everyone I need at the warehouse. Times are tight.
Skyrim.esm|03675D: Just a little one! I was hungry!
Skyrim.esm|03675E: That is correct. Those orders have been delivered by special courier. I can't trust some of our normal distribution channels.
Skyrim.esm|03675F: Sister, how is my fortune coming along?
Skyrim.esm|036760: Again? He can't simply cancel an appointment with the jarl! He serves in my capital city!
Skyrim.esm|036761: So it's okay if your people rebel against the Empire, but not mine?
Skyrim.esm|036762: Oh, we'll make sure it's emptied out by morning. You can trust us!
Skyrim.esm|036764: Huh. Fair or not, they'll pay it, and that's all I need to care about. Bear that in mind, Sister.
Skyrim.esm|036765: Father, could you clean up the house a little while I'm away?
Skyrim.esm|036766: It's the root that looks like an ugly little man.
Skyrim.esm|036767: Steward, I will need the following reagents for my studies. Please see to them.
Skyrim.esm|036768: Good. And while you're at it, perhaps you should sew your mouth shut when our fine customers are within earshot, hrm?
Skyrim.esm|036769: My lady, I'm pleased to inform you that you have a new gown for court, specially tailored by madame Endarie.
Skyrim.esm|03676A: Not that I mind the break, of course. But, still...
Skyrim.esm|03676B: I suppose in wartime, people aren't looking to part with their old junk...
Skyrim.esm|03676D: Silana, you don't seem yourself. Is something the matter?
Skyrim.esm|03676E: Of course they do, brother. You keep raising their rent.
Skyrim.esm|03676F: And? I've seen jarls come and go. This one only became Jarl because her husband died. She'll be replaced in a matter of months.
Skyrim.esm|036770: She does, my lady. But apparently, she hates you just a little less than the others.
Skyrim.esm|036771: I see you have meditated on this subject quite thoroughly. I shall not stop you, but please, try to attend to your temple duties as well.
Skyrim.esm|036772: Even a fine weapon is only as good as its wielder, sir. But they should fetch a high price from the Imperial army.
Skyrim.esm|036773: Wish I could learn some of that charm. I'm just good with bows, not people.
Skyrim.esm|036774: Possession of the holds change on an almost daily basis, but I can report that Haafingar is still secure. Your people are safe.
Skyrim.esm|036776: I ain't begging. I just need a job. Anything.
Skyrim.esm|036777: You'd better hope the war's over by then. The Stormcloaks would eat you alive!
Skyrim.esm|036778: One more reason to pray for the fighting to end then, my love.
Skyrim.esm|03677C: I know, my lady. I just needed to see you outside of court, away from all of the politics.
Skyrim.esm|03677D: Profits continue to rise. The warehouse continues to bring in good revenues, and rent on your stores in the Well is acceptable.
Skyrim.esm|03677E: Listen to you, speaking of sacrifice. You've never gone hungry a day in your life, Erikur!
Skyrim.esm|036780: We barely have enough for ourselves...
Skyrim.esm|036781: Here, it's all I can spare...
Skyrim.esm|036782: We took you in, didn't we? We would be glad to extend our welcome to any other who showed your dedication. What more could we do?
Skyrim.esm|036783: Yeah, but can he hammer the anvil for 8 hours straight?
Skyrim.esm|036784: Sure, but I bet he can't hold his mead as well as me!
Skyrim.esm|036787: That clumsy maid at the palace spilled wine on my best suit. Sister, I believe it's time for a replacement.
Skyrim.esm|036788: The Stormcloaks only rebel because the Empire uses Skyrim as its personal larder. The more they take, the more support the rebels gain!
Skyrim.esm|036789: Ah, that must be it. So many of the men are serving in battle. I shall pray to Stendarr that they return victorious.
Skyrim.esm|03678A: Hmph. Music tames the most savage beasts. Even Nords.
Skyrim.esm|03678B: Your concerns are duly noted, Legate, but unfounded. I assure you, this war will be over in another six months.
Skyrim.esm|03678C: The same place I learned my charm and quick wits: I used to be an Imperial ranger, my boy!
Skyrim.esm|03678D: You there. Have you performed your voice exercises today?
Skyrim.esm|03678E: Your solo performance is about a week away. I expect to hear your early rendition tomorrow, understand?
Skyrim.esm|03678F: Pardon me, but I trust you've been working on your poem, on the classical heroes of Skyrim, yes?
Skyrim.esm|036791: Melaran, you lazy milk drinker, have you finished studying the imports from Black Marsh?
Skyrim.esm|036792: I don't see any reason why we'd need them. As long as those dogs stay outside of our borders, we're fine.
Skyrim.esm|036793: Try not to play favorites, Lady Ateia. It does a disservice to the whole class.
Skyrim.esm|036794: Of course not! Complacency is the bane of art. They're in good hands with you, Inge.
Skyrim.esm|036795: I'm sorry to break it to you, Giraud, but students are an unfortunate necessity of a college. Do try to persevere.
Skyrim.esm|036796: Yes, well, see that you keep at it. That is all.
Skyrim.esm|036797: I'll be the judge of that. Now, back to your practice.
Skyrim.esm|036798: Patience, Illdi. We study the heroes of the past so we can have better understanding of the heroes of today... and tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|036799: I'm sure it will be fine. Just don't be late.
Skyrim.esm|03679A: That may well be our only answer. I will empower my Steward to seek out capable individuals to secure Haafingar. Thank you, my Thanes.
Skyrim.esm|03679B: I respectfully disagree, sir. Stendarr and Zenithar may demand strength from them, but Mara and Dibella demand compassion from us.
Skyrim.esm|03679C: And what would my impetuous Thane Bryling suggest, instead?
Skyrim.esm|03679D: Sir, these are missing commands for about half of the commanders.
Skyrim.esm|03679E: Yes, Lady Ateia. My voice is strong as a Dragonborn's!
Skyrim.esm|03679F: Always! Perfection takes dedication, after all!
Skyrim.esm|0367A0: Yes, madam. I know I have a lot to work on.
Skyrim.esm|0367A1: I've converted the classic tale of Eyldi the Bear to be performed with drums. I think you'll be impressed!
Skyrim.esm|0367A2: At this point, I hardly see how your advice would improve it. It's flawless.
Skyrim.esm|0367A3: Oh, thank you! I've been working on an original piece. I hope it can stand up to the classics!
Skyrim.esm|0367A4: Yes sir, Professor Gemane! It's a war epic about Hoag Merkiller's last stand!
Skyrim.esm|0367A5: Must it be written? Poetry is so dull when it isn't set to music.
Skyrim.esm|0367A6: Why do we always focus on retelling the old stories? There are so many new stories being made, right now...
Skyrim.esm|0367A8: It's okay, dear. More work than ever down at the blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|0367A9: We'll make it through, dear. We always do.
Skyrim.esm|0367AA: Who needs customers and their old junk?
Skyrim.esm|0367AB: Father oughtta punch them in the mouth! That'd get people in here!
Skyrim.esm|0367AC: Right you are, elf! Or even from the rebels, if they've got enough money to make a competitive offer.
Skyrim.esm|0367AD: I'm afraid he can. His authority comes from the emperor. Who you also serve, technically.
Skyrim.esm|0367AE: I know you and father Rorlund have many duties to attend to in the temple, but sometimes I feel that there are more important matters to attend to.
Skyrim.esm|0367AF: That is quite enough. Perhaps I will raise these issues when I have an audience with General Tullius.
Skyrim.esm|0367B1: Sure has been quiet around the shop lately.
Skyrim.esm|0367B2: We hardly ever get new customers anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0367B4: Give him time. In a couple years, he'll be the age you were when you started!
Skyrim.esm|0367B5: Right you are! Guess I'll keep training you... for now.
Skyrim.esm|0367B6: How are your students coming along?
Skyrim.esm|0367B7: Miss Stentor, these are quite expensive. I trust you know how limited the hold's funds are at this time?
Skyrim.esm|0367B8: I know, and I appreciate it dearly. But I can't help but feel we could be doing more...
Skyrim.esm|0367B9: The Stormcloaks are far from the only threat to the hold. The roads are beset by bandits and beasts... and now there's even talk of bloody dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0368D5: Your wish is my command! As long as it doesn't involve much physical activity. I seem to be having trouble staying on my feet at the moment.
Skyrim.esm|0368E0: Yeah, that was a shame. But now Alva takes care of me. And I take care of her.
Skyrim.esm|0368E2: That's true, but I'm in a new house now. A better house.
Skyrim.esm|0368E3: I have Alva to take care of now. I don't need Helgi anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0368E4: Let me know if you find out anything about the fire.
Skyrim.esm|0368E5: I hope Alva is not what you think.
Skyrim.esm|0368E6: He was cruel to her. He would beat her every night. It's no wonder she fled in the middle of the night.
Skyrim.esm|0368E7: She came by so Alva could kiss her goodbye. Then she left to join the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|0368E9: I hear the steward asked you to look into the fire.
Skyrim.esm|0368EA: Have you found out anything about Alva?
Skyrim.esm|0368EB: I hear you put Laelette out of her misery.
Skyrim.esm|0368F0: You speak to Igmund. Son of Hrolfdir. Jarl of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0368F1: Markarth is not without its problems, but the Empire is here to maintain order.
Skyrim.esm|0368F2: Forsworn have troubled the Reach for too long. I intend to deal with them.
Skyrim.esm|0368F5: I'm Igmund's uncle as well as his steward.
Skyrim.esm|0368F8: The Forsworn know every stone in the Reach. We have to wait them out.
Skyrim.esm|0368FA: As his housecarl, Igmund's security is my chief concern.
Skyrim.esm|0368FC: Igmund's father was killed by Forsworn. I won't rest until they've paid with their blood.
Skyrim.esm|0368FD: Raerek is far too cautious, but I'll agree with him on any measure that keeps this city safe.
Skyrim.esm|0368FF: I learned to forge when I was a pup, then I joined the Legion and mastered it.
Skyrim.esm|036900: I served in the Legion with Thongvor Silver-Blood. He's a good man and a tough soldier.
Skyrim.esm|036901: I'm the Jarl's personal blacksmith, but that doesn't mean I can't sell you a few pieces for a fair price.
Skyrim.esm|036902: I'm Ghorza's assistant. I help her with the forge.
Skyrim.esm|036903: I'm not very good for anything more than smithing nails right now.
Skyrim.esm|036904: One time, I almost cut a finger off sharpening a blade. I hate blood.
Skyrim.esm|036908: The artifacts I've discovered in Nchuand-Zel will make history one day.
Skyrim.esm|036909: I've only ever lost a few guards to Steam Centurions. Good odds, considering...
Skyrim.esm|03690A: I used to travel so much before all this Forsworn nonsense. Such an inconvenience...
Skyrim.esm|03690B: I'm Calcelmo's nephew. I help him around the laboratory.
Skyrim.esm|03690C: I'm afraid to touch half of the things we keep in the tower.
Skyrim.esm|03690D: Don't let my uncle go on about his theories. You'll be here all night.
Skyrim.esm|03690E: I try not to think about how my uncle got all the Dwemer Centurion parts.
Skyrim.esm|03690F: Before you say anything, no, I am not from the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|036911: I have meals to cook for the entire castle and almost no help.
Skyrim.esm|036913: I have to work, or Anton will rip my head off.
Skyrim.esm|036914: Anton sure looks like he's from the Reach, but don't tell him that.
Skyrim.esm|036917: Only reason Jarl keeps Anton on is because he cooks up a storm.
Skyrim.esm|036918: Don't mind the dogs, they get anxious when they haven't had dinner.
Skyrim.esm|036919: You should talk to Anton if you need anything. He's the master chef.
Skyrim.esm|03691B: Even the dead need company. That's what Arkay provides.
Skyrim.esm|03691C: We all come into Arkay's light in the end, but there's no need to rush.
Skyrim.esm|03691D: Markarth is a city of wealth, but gold doesn't follow you in death.
Skyrim.esm|03691F: You have the honor of addressing a member of the Thalmor. Bask in it.
Skyrim.esm|036920: The Aldmeri Dominion is the only thing keeping the Empire on its feet.
Skyrim.esm|036922: This war is such nonsense. All this fuss over their false human god.
Skyrim.esm|0369EE: Okay, look. If I'm caught saying anything bad about the meadery or the family, I'm a dead man.
Skyrim.esm|0369F7: I understand. Hopefully I'll be able to save my meager earnings up to buy them one day.
Skyrim.esm|0369F8: She's an absolute pleasure to work for! In fact, I almost feel guilty accepting pay from her. Why, just this last week I was commenting to my fellow meadery workers just how lucky we are to be working for such a charitable family.
Skyrim.esm|0369FA: Sure am! I thank my lucky stars every time I look to the night sky that Maven took me in and gave me this opportunity.
Skyrim.esm|036A00: I... I... I don't know what to say. Such kindness is unheard of in these times. Here. I want you to accept this as payment for your sacrifice and I... well, we both thank you.
Skyrim.esm|036A01: Nivenor is my wife. How I was ever able to meet such a gorgeous creature is one of life's mysteries. We're... at odds about my charity. She thinks the people in this town have become lazy and expecting a handout. I try to include her in my lessons from Maramal over at the Temple of Mara, but she prefers to shop instead. Different strokes, eh?
Skyrim.esm|036A05: Lady Maven's been developing the secret recipe for Black-Briar Mead over the last several decades. She uses a very secret blend of spices, fresh honey from Goldenglow Estate and specially constructed aging casks to ensure the best flavor. Even when you get to the last sip in your flagon, it'll taste just as good as the first!
Skyrim.esm|036A24: It's going to take a lot more than that.
Skyrim.esm|036A27: Damn it! Sorry. I'm not very good at this. Look, just don't say anything to Maven, will you? I've spent weeks trying to get that right and if she sees how stupid I am, she'll get rid of me.
Skyrim.esm|036A2B: Yes, actually. I'm partnered with Maven Black-Briar. I put up a chunk of the gold she needed to get the place off the ground. We've been staggeringly successful so far. In fact, I'm almost surprised how quickly she's generated business for us. My name may not be on the label, but some of my blood, sweat and tears are in every drop. Uh, figuratively of course.
Skyrim.esm|036A2D: Yeah... fired. Sure. That's what I meant.
Skyrim.esm|036A2E: Every Argonian wedding proposal band is supposed to have three flawless amethysts as part of their design. Two of the gems, the ones on the outside, represent the couple to be married... one for the husband, one for the wife. The amethyst in the center represents The Hist, the entity that all Argonians consider a sacred part of our lives.
Skyrim.esm|036A2F: Three in fact. They're my own recipe. Brought them over here from my days as a bartender in Gideon. First is the "Velvet Lechance" which is a mixture of blackberry, honey, spiced wine and a touch of nightshade... perfectly safe, I assure you. Second, we have the "White-Gold Tower" which is heavy cream with a layer of blended mead, lavender and dragon's tongue on top. Last, and only for the bravest of souls, we have the "Cliff Racer" which is Firebrand Wine, Cyrodiilic Brandy, Flin and Sujamma.
Skyrim.esm|036A30: Only the best in all of Riften! I own Riften Fishery. Started out with a single fishing pole and now I have an entire building and two boats to my name. Not bad for a middle-aged Nord, eh? Too bad I haven't had the same success helping this city shed its poverty. Nivenor thinks I'm wasting my time trying, but I disagree.
Skyrim.esm|036A31: I was hoping you'd offer. I didn't think it was my place to ask such a favor of you. I thank you.
Skyrim.esm|036A33: That is the truth, my friend! Maven is a saint and don't let anyone tell you otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|036A34: Outsiders aren't trusted in Markarth. Best keep that in mind.
Skyrim.esm|036A35: Can't believe I'm posted here. Even the beds are made of stone.
Skyrim.esm|036A36: Don't mistake my political beliefs as a substitute for my family values. They're still my parents, and I love them dearly. I just think they've let my sister's death skew their outlook on Ulfric and what he really brings to the table with this war. I'm not so certain that Ulfric's intentions are as noble as they think. I think Ulfric cares about Ulfric and the rest is a smokescreen. I'd... appreciate if you kept my opinion between us. The last thing I want to do is have my parents "lose" another child.
Skyrim.esm|036A37: Come back and see us again.
Skyrim.esm|036A38: If you'll excuse me, I have other things to attend to.
Skyrim.esm|036A39: Let's see those Stormcloaks try to break into this canyon.
Skyrim.esm|036A3B: The Empire didn't think we could take the Reach, but here we are.
Skyrim.esm|036A7F: Prison's not so bad. Less complaints when I kill people.
Skyrim.esm|036A80: What do you want? Have digging to do.
Skyrim.esm|036A81: Other prisoners get suspicious when we talk to each other, so keep it down.
Skyrim.esm|036A84: I'm innocent. The Nords framed me. I was nowhere near that murder weapon.
Skyrim.esm|036A85: Jarl decided I was too much of a problem. Threw me in here with the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|036A89: Support Ulfric or die trying. There's nothing in between.
Skyrim.esm|036A8C: It's been good talking to you.
Skyrim.esm|036AF4: Good, now maybe I can get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|036C27: Come back and see us again!
Skyrim.esm|036C29: Remember to give at the temple.
Skyrim.esm|036C2C: I trained our daughter Lilija as a healer... to one day tend the Shrine of Talos and replace me as a Priestess of Talos.
Skyrim.esm|036C2E: If you've got the coin, you're welcome here. Otherwise, hit the road.
Skyrim.esm|036C30: Welcome to the Bee and Barb, milord. If I can interest you in one of our special drinks, you let me know.
Skyrim.esm|036C44: Welcome to the Bee and Barb, milady. If I can interest you in one of our special drinks, you let me know.
Skyrim.esm|036C45: If you're hungry, Marise sells fresh fish... caught by yours truly.
Skyrim.esm|036D33: It is my personal mission to ensure that the Bards College preserves the great deeds of the past, in both poetry and song.
Skyrim.esm|036D34: I'm so glad you got back from Olaf's Tomb okay. They gave you a tougher task then they gave me.
Skyrim.esm|036D35: I can always find time to share a word with a warrior.
Skyrim.esm|036D36: The Jarl is not to be disturbed with common affairs of court. You direct those concerns to me.
Skyrim.esm|036D37: Someday, a gallant hero will ride up and take me away. Someday...
Skyrim.esm|036D4B: Is that all? My friend, you've come to the right person. You could say that causing a scene is somewhat of a specialty of mine. Stand back and behold my handiwork.
Skyrim.esm|036D4C: Wonderful! I can begin to repay your generosity immediately. Say on, friend.
Skyrim.esm|036D4D: Stand back and watch a true professional wreck a party.
Skyrim.esm|036D4F: Of course! I would do anything for you, my one and truest friend! What do you need from me?
Skyrim.esm|036D50: Ah, the one generous soul amongst a gathering of pinch-pennies and lick-spittles! If there's anything I can ever do for you, do not hesitate to call upon me!
Skyrim.esm|036D51: My friend, you are very perceptive! I have a powerful thirst that cannot be slaked! And none of the waiters will bring me a drink. Elenwen must have told them to cut me off, the frigid bitch. Afraid I'll cause another scene, I suppose...
Skyrim.esm|036FE0: Aye. A gift from the Black-Briars after they kicked the previous family out... place called Riftweald Manor. He never stays there, just pays for the upkeep on it. Hired some lout by the name of Vald to guard the place.
Skyrim.esm|036FE8: Then I have an important task for you. I need you to break into Mercer's home and search for anything that could tell us where he's gone.
Skyrim.esm|036FEB: Be careful, lad. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you.
Skyrim.esm|037059: Ooh. What a fun trick. Teach me, teach me!
Skyrim.esm|03705A: For someone called the "Listener," you do an awful lot of shouting...
Skyrim.esm|037063: What? Why would the stranger attack innocent Cicero?
Skyrim.esm|037065: What have I done, Listener? Has Cicero offended you?
Skyrim.esm|037067: By Sithis, you will pay for that!
Skyrim.esm|037069: What is this madness? Rexus, help me!
Skyrim.esm|03706B: Wait! That's not how this is supposed to work!
Skyrim.esm|03706C: What are you doing? I thought we were friends!
Skyrim.esm|037078: How may I serve you, my Listener?
Skyrim.esm|03708B: Just... just stop. And I'll tell you where I've hidden my treasure.
Skyrim.esm|03708E: It's... in a hollowed-out rock. In the tundra. A bit west of Whiterun. Now please... please stop.
Skyrim.esm|03708F: Oh gods, you've taken everything I have. Just kill me and be done with it...
Skyrim.esm|03710F: Why are you people doing this?
Skyrim.esm|037112: This... this is about the inheritance, isn't it? All right! I'll tell you where I've hidden it.
Skyrim.esm|037164: It's in a hollowed-out tree stump. In the marsh, south of Solitude. Now please, you'll let me go now. Right?
Skyrim.esm|037189: Why don't you be a dear and let me go now. All right? I won't say a word to anyone. Our little secret. I promise.
Skyrim.esm|03718A: You... you can't do this to me...
Skyrim.esm|0371A9: Do you have any idea who I am? When my father hears about this, he will gut you.
Skyrim.esm|0371AA: Ah, now I understand. This is about that my father's hidden cache, isn't it? The dowry for my wedding.
Skyrim.esm|0371B5: Ha! Well it's safely hidden away, in a hollowed-out tree stump. Just outside Riften, tucked away nice and secure. You'll never find it.
Skyrim.esm|0371B6: Damn it. I gave it away, didn't I?
Skyrim.esm|0371BF: I'll tell you anything. Anything! Just please stop... hurting me...
Skyrim.esm|0371C6: I just...  can't take the pain. Not a second longer.
Skyrim.esm|0371C7: I'll give you money. Treasure. I have some things hidden away. Take them all. They're yours. Just please... stop.
Skyrim.esm|0371D4: Just north of Windhelm. There's a hollowed-out rock. I have some things hidden in there. Take them! They're yours.
Skyrim.esm|0371D8: I've told you everything I can. I don't know what else you could want...
Skyrim.esm|03726A: What do you expect to find? My juggling balls? He he.
Skyrim.esm|0372B7: Hey, that's my lint! Ha, Cicero is just joking. You can have it.
Skyrim.esm|0372DA: If I had anything worth giving, I'd have offered it already.
Skyrim.esm|0373C7: See if they have any cheese. I'm getting a bit... peckish.
Skyrim.esm|0373C8: You're putting your hand... in my pocket? Have you gone complete cracked?
Skyrim.esm|0373CD: This how you get your thrills? Feeling up an old man?
Skyrim.esm|037425: I haven't been touched like that since I visited a certain brothel in the city of Sentinel...
Skyrim.esm|037429: It would seem you're not the accomplished pickpocket you think you are...
Skyrim.esm|03742A: What do you hope to find? Sweets and my dolly? Or maybe some of my spare Nightshade?
Skyrim.esm|037437: You're not actually... picking my pocket? Well, you've got guts. I'll give you that.
Skyrim.esm|0376A4: It's clear that memories are far too short.
Skyrim.esm|0376A5: And it's clear money matters more to Dagur than honor.
Skyrim.esm|0376A6: Eirid's a little girl who doesn't understand that they're responsible for destroying our home.
Skyrim.esm|0376A7: Because they don't care about you or I, cub. They think they're better than all of us, and that we can just be thrown away.
Skyrim.esm|0376D1: This is what it's come to. No one seems to care what they've done to our home.
Skyrim.esm|0376D2: Eirid says the mages at the College are nice people, but you said they're bad.
Skyrim.esm|0376D3: Why did they do that? Why would they be so evil?
Skyrim.esm|0376D5: Looks to be another cold day.
Skyrim.esm|0376E5: Not like this. Feels colder than usual. Bet it's those damned Mages.
Skyrim.esm|0376F2: That... wizard is still at the Inn. I can't believe Dagur allows him to stay there.
Skyrim.esm|0377C3: It's... it's like watching an artist create a masterpiece.
Skyrim.esm|03786F: You are an inspiration. Do you know that?
Skyrim.esm|03798A: She's the Master-Wizard here. Arch-Mage Aren may be in charge, but Mirabelle runs the place. You'd do well to remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0379CA: Is that... the best... you can do...
Skyrim.esm|0379E7: Ow... It hurts when I laugh. He he...
Skyrim.esm|0379F7: It will... take more than that... to stop me...
Skyrim.esm|0379FF: Oh, but the Listener jests with poor Cicero. You already have company. You don't need my help at all.
Skyrim.esm|037A02: I am honored to be chosen.
Skyrim.esm|037A04: But you already have a traveling companion. I fear I would just get in the way.
Skyrim.esm|037A26: Oh, well... You seem to already have assistance. Surely you mean to remain inconspicuous...
Skyrim.esm|037AD9: And we understand a rebel garrison is close to abandoning the resistance. We need to send them a little encouragement.
Skyrim.esm|037AEE: And we hear a handful of Imperials are realizing they are Stormcloaks at heart. We need to send them a little encouragement.
Skyrim.esm|037B03: First, if we can assassinate their commander, we'll be able to strike them while they're busy tucking their tails between their legs.
Skyrim.esm|037B04: First, our intelligence suggests one of the rebel garrisons has become disaffected. If we play our cards right, we should be able to turn them.
Skyrim.esm|037B45: What an amazing day this has been. I hope you're enjoying the festivities. I'm... I'm just so overwhelmed. Such kindness... for me.
Skyrim.esm|037B51: Ha ha! The news is everywhere! Vittoria Vici, the Emperor's cousin, butchered at her own wedding! Well done! Let's see his eminence try to ignore this.
Skyrim.esm|037B53: Ha ha ha ha! Yes, yes indeed! Hail the Imperial Legion! Hail the Stormcloaks! Hail Solitude and the Eight Divines! On this glorious day, we honor everyone! Ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|037B54: Oh, no need to be shy! My day is your day! Please - eat, drink, make merry! We're all friends here!
Skyrim.esm|037B5A: Her name is Vittoria Vici. She oversees the East Empire Company's business holdings in Solitude. The wedding is being held in that city, at the Temple of the Divines. Her death will cause an uproar, which is exactly what we want. Vici is likely to address her guests frequently, as is the wedding custom. Kill her when she does that, and I promise you a significant bonus. Now go. And give my best to the bride.
Skyrim.esm|037B5B: Oh! Well, you tell your Stormcloak friends "hello" right back at them! This is a day of healing! Legion, Stormcloaks - all are welcome here!
Skyrim.esm|037B5C: Oh, Asgeir! My husband! My dear, sweet husband! Why would someone do this to you? Why? This was supposed to be the happiest day of my life!
Skyrim.esm|037B5E: Well aren't you a pretty thing? Should my new bride by worried? He he he he.
Skyrim.esm|037B5F: With Vici's murder, you've started us down a path the Dark Brotherhood hasn't traveled in centuries. The assassination of an Emperor. And now, your reward. A unique spell to summon a legend of the Dark Brotherhood. His soul serves us now in death, as his body once did in life. Ah, and of course, a bonus, for killing Vici while she addressed her guests, as instructed. Gold... simple and pure. Spend it as you will. Now then. Time to proceed to the next stage of the plan. Go and speak with Gabriella. She's been helping me arrange your next contract.
Skyrim.esm|037BB9: Orgnar, hold down the bar for a minute, will you?
Skyrim.esm|037BBA: Sky Haven Temple. An ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden deep in the crags of the Reach. That's where we'll find Alduin's Wall.
Skyrim.esm|037BBD: You see, right here. Sky Haven Temple, constructed around one of the main Akaviri military camps in the Reach, during their conquest of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|037BBE: This changes everything, of course. There's no time to lose. We must locate...  let me show you. I know I had it here, somewhere...
Skyrim.esm|037BC2: One moment, I know, time is of the essence, but mustn't leave secrets for the Thalmor... there's one more I must bring...
Skyrim.esm|037BC3: Esbern. What are you getting at?
Skyrim.esm|037BC4: Delphine! I... it's good to see you. It's been... a long time.
Skyrim.esm|037BC6: This is where they built Alduin's Wall, to set down in stone all their accumulated dragonlore. A hedge against the forgetfulness of centuries.
Skyrim.esm|037BC9: Well, then. You made it, safe and sound. Good. Come on, I have a place we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|037BCA: Let's pretend we haven't. What's Alduin's Wall and what does it have to do with stopping the dragons?
Skyrim.esm|037BCF: Now then. I assume you know about...
Skyrim.esm|037BD0: Alduin's Wall was where the ancient Blades recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy. Its location has been lost for centuries, but I've found it again. Not lost, you see, just forgotten. The Blades archives held so many secrets... I was only able to save a few scraps...
Skyrim.esm|037BD2: Do you know what he's talking about?
Skyrim.esm|037BD4: I don't like being out in the open like this.
Skyrim.esm|037BD6: I'll need this... No, no, useless trash... where'd I put my annotated Anuad?
Skyrim.esm|037BD7: Well, I guess that's good enough... let's be off...
Skyrim.esm|037BD9: A wise and foresighted policy, in the event. Despite the far-reaching fame of Alduin's Wall at the time - one of the wonders of the ancient world - its location was lost.
Skyrim.esm|037BDA: Ah! Here it is. Come, let me show you.
Skyrim.esm|037BDB: It's good to see you, too, Esbern. It's been too long, old friend. Too long.
Skyrim.esm|037BDC: You mean... you don't mean to say you haven't heard of Alduin's Wall?
Skyrim.esm|037CD2: Shor's beard! He's going after the Eyes of the Falmer? That was Gallus's pet project. If he gets his hands on them, you can be certain he'll be gone for good and set up for life.
Skyrim.esm|037CD5: We've scoured the town and I've spoken to every contact we have left. No sign of Mercer. Any luck on your end?
Skyrim.esm|037CD8: Agreed. He's taken everything the Guild has left, and to go after one of the last greatest heists is just an insult. I've spoken to Karliah, and made amends for how the Guild's treated her. Now she wishes to speak with both of us. Quickly, we have no time to lose.
Skyrim.esm|037D6B: Better question would be "what did he leave." Mercer took everything. Even all of our plans are gone.
Skyrim.esm|037D6E: Stabbed in the back. It's like the Dark Brotherhood all over again.
Skyrim.esm|037D71: If I see Frey, I'll pluck his eyes from his skull with my bare hands!
Skyrim.esm|037D72: We can talk after I find out what's going on.
Skyrim.esm|037D73: I'm relieved Brynjolf has an open mind or that could have gotten bloody.
Skyrim.esm|037D77: I can't believe this is happening, lad.
Skyrim.esm|037DBC: What's that? Oh yes, yes. Thank you, I will.
Skyrim.esm|037DBD: Did she, perhaps, mention any dangerous research she might be working on? Anything that could get her in trouble?
Skyrim.esm|037DBE: Can you believe we're here? Saarthal, of all places.
Skyrim.esm|037DBF: Are you looking for any specific volumes, Drevis?
Skyrim.esm|037DC0: I'm surprised that the College is allowed to excavate here.
Skyrim.esm|037DC1: Can I help you with anything, Nirya?
Skyrim.esm|037DC2: I'm here to help you with books, Nirya. That's all.
Skyrim.esm|037DC3: Ah, yes. Of course. No, I don't need anything.
Skyrim.esm|037DC4: Hmm? Oh, no. No, not really. I... Actually, I've forgotten what I was looking for.
Skyrim.esm|037DC5: I was wondering. Has Faralda been in recently? Borrowed any books that you can recall?
Skyrim.esm|037DC6: I bet your family would be amazed, right?
Skyrim.esm|037DC7: Amazed isn't the right word. They might find this... offensive.
Skyrim.esm|037DC8: Ah. Well, then. Let me know if you need anything.
Skyrim.esm|037DCA: You know, this is one of a very few places where you can receive a real education in the schools of magic.
Skyrim.esm|037DCB: I think my parents reserved a spot for me here at the College on the day I was born.
Skyrim.esm|037DCC: There's a lot of pressure for me to excel in my studies. I hope I can live up to expectations.
Skyrim.esm|037DF7: My sweet Leanora, I will never let anything happen to us. I promise.
Skyrim.esm|037DF8: It brings me joy to hear that, but what possessed you to say that so suddenly?
Skyrim.esm|037DF9: It will have to do. Besides, if it came to blows, I'm sure the Snow-Shod's would come out here and assist us.
Skyrim.esm|037DFA: Just worry about keeping the fields tended and let the Jarl worry about the war.
Skyrim.esm|037DFB: My darling, you've made me the happiest woman in all Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|037DFC: I suppose so. I just fear they won't make it out here in time.
Skyrim.esm|037DFD: I've already lost a family, and we're about to start a new one. I don't want that to happen again.
Skyrim.esm|037DFE: I'm not so sure the few guards they've assigned to the farm are enough.
Skyrim.esm|037DFF: My husband and I were just recently married.
Skyrim.esm|037E00: I'm originally from Cyrodiil, but I left there after I had a falling out with my family.
Skyrim.esm|037E01: This was the first door in Skyrim I knocked upon, and little did I know my future husband would be answering the door.
Skyrim.esm|037E02: I like the farm life. It's a huge change from living in a city.
Skyrim.esm|037E03: Addvild's made me a happy woman. I wouldn't want to be with anyone else in the world.
Skyrim.esm|037E62: I'm dealing with a few new research proposals, evaluating the results of three long-term projects, and keeping an eye on our new Apprentices.
Skyrim.esm|037E63: This is why I avoid being social.
Skyrim.esm|037E64: So, you don't need anything then.
Skyrim.esm|037E66: Oh! Well... I... I'm sorry. Yes, we have been working on something together.
Skyrim.esm|037E67: Your schedule is always clear, Arch-Mage. I see to that.
Skyrim.esm|037E68: I only wish to escape the constant glaring of my "colleagues" and discover an opportunity for repose. Your Arcanaeum is so lovely and quiet...
Skyrim.esm|037E69: Forgive me, but at what point does it become our concern?
Skyrim.esm|037E6A: There were also two items requiring disciplinary action recently.
Skyrim.esm|037E6B: Don't fret, my fanged acquaintance. I shan't trouble you a bit.
Skyrim.esm|037E6C: Ah, yes. True. Well, thank you.
Skyrim.esm|037E6D: Oh, my dear Urag. You're trying so hard to help. I do hope you're not disappointed.
Skyrim.esm|037E6E: You've been spending a fair bit of time with Sergius lately, I see.
Skyrim.esm|037E6F: Never. The College cannot become mired in local politics. What happens inside these walls is our worry. What happens outside is theirs.
Skyrim.esm|037E70: Everything's under control, then? Right. Keep up the good work.
Skyrim.esm|037E71: Calm down! Why are you over-reacting? I was going to ask if the two of you are collaborating on research!
Skyrim.esm|037E72: Was there something specific you needed?
Skyrim.esm|037E74: "Disappointed" is not the word I would choose, Colette. Excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|037E75: Ah, my dearest Urag! How are you today, my green-skinned friend?
Skyrim.esm|037E76: Sir, the Imperial Legion has again requested assistance. What shall I tell them?
Skyrim.esm|037E77: Is there anything new I should be aware of, Mirabelle?
Skyrim.esm|037E78: What? Why, the audacity! How dare you imply such a thing!
Skyrim.esm|037E7A: I'm positive whatever I require will be revealed to me on its own, in due time.
Skyrim.esm|037E7B: The same thing we've told them before, and the same thing we've told these Stormcloaks. It's not our concern.
Skyrim.esm|037E7C: Mirabelle, would you please make sure my schedule is clear for the next few days? I'm working on something important.
Skyrim.esm|037EB6: Arch-Mage, the Synod have contacted us again, asking if we've any word on their researchers. What shall I tell them?
Skyrim.esm|037EB7: That hasn't changed, has it? Then just tell them again.
Skyrim.esm|037EB8: No, I don't think so. Respond to their request, and we'll leave it at that.
Skyrim.esm|037EB9: Err, excuse me Enthir. Might I have a moment?
Skyrim.esm|037EBA: Yes, err, apologies for the intrusion. I was wondering, err, if you could possibly, ah, procure a few select items for me.
Skyrim.esm|037EBC: Oh, no. Err, wouldn't do to say at all. Not, err, not now. Not at all. I have far too many things to do still.
Skyrim.esm|037EBD: Really, Arniel? Because I seem to recall doing just that for you recently, at which point you assured me that you'd cover my expenses.
Skyrim.esm|037EBE: What did you tell them last time?
Skyrim.esm|037EBF: So, Arniel. How's that little project of yours coming along? Any progress worth mentioning?
Skyrim.esm|037EC1: That they had arrived, asked for information which was provided to them, and went on their way with no suggestion of further interaction.
Skyrim.esm|037EC2: Should we consider investigating the matter?
Skyrim.esm|037EC5: And that, my nervous little friend, has not happened. Would you care to comment on that, perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|037EC6: Oh, that. Err, yes. It's... It's quite promising, I believe. Definitely on the right track. Results, err, results should be soon.
Skyrim.esm|037EC7: Ahh, hmm. Yes. I, err, I was unaware that I had forgotten that. I will, err, I mean I'll take care of that as soon as possible.
Skyrim.esm|037EC8: Thanks to you, the people of Skyrim will continue to have a place to study magic. Even if most of them resent you for it.
Skyrim.esm|037EC9: Nords don't trust magic, or those that use it. Made it difficult for me growing up.
Skyrim.esm|037ECA: They won't say much, but most people have very strong opinions about the College and those that study here.
Skyrim.esm|037ECB: Don't expect the local Nords to take kindly to you once they find out you're from the College.
Skyrim.esm|037ECC: J'zargo is pleased, and more than a little jealous, to greet the new Arch-Mage.
Skyrim.esm|037ECD: J'zargo will go wherever he can to learn more. Oblivion itself, if need be.
Skyrim.esm|037ECE: Khajiit are not known as mages, so J'zargo has much to prove.
Skyrim.esm|037ECF: J'zargo can learn magic from these men and elves, but they cannot learn to be as dashing as he. J'zargo comes out on top.
Skyrim.esm|037ED0: I think we could blow up half of Eastmarch, and people would still come to us for enchantments.
Skyrim.esm|037ED1: The local Nords may not like us very much, but they don't seem to mind having access to enchanted weapons and armor.
Skyrim.esm|037ED2: Savos doesn't care for politics. He likes to believe the College is removed from the rest of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|037ED3: If he could, Savos would float the the entire College above Skyrim, for all the Nords to see.
Skyrim.esm|037ED4: Right. And what did you say the point of this little venture was?
Skyrim.esm|037F1E: Yes, I'll be ready in a moment, I just need one more thing...
Skyrim.esm|037F1F: Fine! I'll just... leave the rest of it. Let's go, then, if you're in such a hurry.
Skyrim.esm|038064: Some fool is opening a museum in Dawnstar. They say he's paying people to collect artifacts from that old Mythic Dawn cult.
Skyrim.esm|038066: Silus keeps filling his house with those Mythic Dawn things. Creepy, I tell you.
Skyrim.esm|038069: I can't believe Silus is collecting Mythic Dawn artifacts. Right here in Dawnstar, no less.
Skyrim.esm|038245: Your son chose his side, and he chose poorly. And now he's gone. Such is the way of war. The sooner you accept his loss, the better.
Skyrim.esm|038246: Come on, father. There's nothing more to be said here.
Skyrim.esm|038247: Do you believe this old hag?
Skyrim.esm|038248: Face it, cow! Your stupid son is dead! He died a Stormcloak traitor. And you... you best keep your mouth shut before you suffer the same.
Skyrim.esm|03824C: Nothing? And what of my son? Hmm? What of Thorald? Is he nothing? So don't talk to me about suffering!
Skyrim.esm|03824E: Foolish old woman! You know nothing! Nothing of our struggles, our suffering!
Skyrim.esm|03824F: I will never accept his death! My son still lives. I feel it in my heart. So tell me, Battle-Borns, where is he? Where are you holding my Thorald?
Skyrim.esm|038250: "Holding him"? Why I've got him in my cellar. He's my prisoner.
Skyrim.esm|038272: If she's come this far from home, it must be important. Lead on. I just need to report back to camp by sunrise.
Skyrim.esm|038276: Our fires are visible for miles. We're in a low valley. Sitting ducks.
Skyrim.esm|038277: Of course I am. What brings you here?
Skyrim.esm|038278: Don't even bother with that one. Leave it to the orcs.
Skyrim.esm|038279: I'm searching for my Fenrig. He was marching with Gjukar's men, who they say were wiped out here. I've turned over every body, though, and I can't find him. Please help me look. He has a bright red beard and hair.
Skyrim.esm|03827A: It doesn't matter. I'm here. We're together now. And we will be forever.
Skyrim.esm|03827C: But that battle isn't expected until tomorrow... Ruki, what's going on?
Skyrim.esm|03827F: How wonderful. Like the sea, their love roils and swells, but brings life and nourishment to all.
Skyrim.esm|038280: I think I've got a handle on it, but Orichalcum is confusing.
Skyrim.esm|038281: Are you a fool? Look at the bodies around you. Even if you're blind, surely you smell the blood.
Skyrim.esm|038282: We're expected to fight there tomorrow. Gjukar elected to camp here for the night. I don't like it, though.
Skyrim.esm|038283: I had heard that Gjukar's men were wiped out. I came to find you.
Skyrim.esm|038284: Oh, right. That makes sense. Kind of a messy metal, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|038285: Come, help me search for Fenrig.
Skyrim.esm|038286: Have you been learning your ores?
Skyrim.esm|0382B8: We are done talking about this.
Skyrim.esm|0382B9: Wh- no... I.... Oh, by the Eight, fine. But only to the edge of town!
Skyrim.esm|0382BD: I wonder why they only stole Lucan's golden claw. I mean, we have plenty of things in the shop that are worth just as much coin. Lucan found the claw about a year after he opened the store. He never quite explained where he got it. He's a tricky one.
Skyrim.esm|0382BE: So this is your plan, Lucan?
Skyrim.esm|0382C3: Oh really? Well I think your new helper here needs a guide.
Skyrim.esm|0382C5: Well what are you going to do then, huh? Let's hear it!
Skyrim.esm|0382C7: I remember you. You're the stranger. Father says I'm not supposed to talk to strangers.
Skyrim.esm|038308: Those thieves must be mad, hiding out there. Those old crypts are filled with nothing but traps, trolls, and who knows what else!
Skyrim.esm|038309: Yes. So now you don't have to go, do you?
Skyrim.esm|03834A: Well one of us has to do something!
Skyrim.esm|038563: Have you thought about my invitation?
Skyrim.esm|038564: No, I really don't. It'll be a while before I'm ready for that. I'll be on my way now.
Skyrim.esm|038565: It was an old Nord proverb. It said, the best way to get over the loss of a lover is to find a new one.
Skyrim.esm|038566: Captain Lonely-Gale! I've been looking for you everywhere!
Skyrim.esm|038567: Viola, dark elves sleep just like the rest of us.
Skyrim.esm|038568: Did you notice that the snow settles in a different direction near the murder sites?
Skyrim.esm|038569: You wouldn't think it from the racket that Cornerclub makes.
Skyrim.esm|03856A: This is more delicate work than you're used to.
Skyrim.esm|03856B: Hello, captain! Can you spare a moment? I heard something the other day that I wanted to share with you.
Skyrim.esm|03856C: Can I help with it? Maybe just the cuirass?
Skyrim.esm|03856D: I've thought about it, but I'm not sure it's such a good idea.
Skyrim.esm|03856E: I did that engraved piece just last month!
Skyrim.esm|03856F: Did you look behind the forge? I think I saw it there.
Skyrim.esm|038571: Oh... yes. Now that you mention it, I think... sorry about that.
Skyrim.esm|038573: Do you think those dark elves had anything to do with the murders? The attacks all happened at night, after all.
Skyrim.esm|038574: Did you use my good hammer?
Skyrim.esm|038575: No, I want to take my time with it.
Skyrim.esm|038576: I'm sure the guards have the matter well in hand. Now, if you'll excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|038577: Yes, I think that must be it. My wife hasn't been gone that long, after all. Ask me again on another day.
Skyrim.esm|038579: And now you've found me. Can I help you?
Skyrim.esm|03857A: Don't you think all your tramping around there investigating might have something to do with it?
Skyrim.esm|03857C: This is the royal armor, lass. Maybe in a few years.
Skyrim.esm|03857D: I didn't lose a lover, Viola, I lost my wife. It's not the same thing.
Skyrim.esm|038580: Oh, I see. Is it still too soon?
Skyrim.esm|038581: Well, if it works in the one case, why not the other? Don't you want to at least give it a try?
Skyrim.esm|038582: Blast, girl, I told you to leave my tools alone. You have yours and I have mine.
Skyrim.esm|038583: I... well. Perhaps. But still! It's curious.
Skyrim.esm|038732: Come to gawk at the Dark Elf?
Skyrim.esm|038733: Well look at us. Like two gray specks in the snow.
Skyrim.esm|038734: I mean no offense. I am but a humble farmer. Not a... a great wanderer, like you.
Skyrim.esm|038735: If you beat up my sister Sissel, I won't tell.
Skyrim.esm|038736: I'm the older sister, by nearly five minutes. Sissel's barely worthy to walk in my shadow.
Skyrim.esm|038739: I wish I could be an adventurer like you, and go wherever I want.
Skyrim.esm|03873A: Some day soon I'll stop being afraid. Jouane is teaching me magic. He says I'm real good.
Skyrim.esm|03873C: Do yourself a favor and don't have children. They're good for nothing at all.
Skyrim.esm|03873D: Curse my good-for-nothing daughters! My wife was twice the woman they'll ever grow to be. She gave her life birthing them. What a waste.
Skyrim.esm|03873F: You look like you've seen your share of adventure. I envy you that. In fact, maybe you can help me with something.
Skyrim.esm|038740: My father, Mralki, manages the inn. He used to be a soldier, but he left that life behind.
Skyrim.esm|038742: I only take real coin here, no handouts and no bartering.
Skyrim.esm|038744: That fool son of mine, Erik, wants to be an adventurer. He a good-natured lad, but he's got no idea how dangerous the world really is.
Skyrim.esm|038745: Name's Ennis. If you've got friends who do business in Whiterun, they might know me.
Skyrim.esm|038747: You here from the city? I visit Whiterun from time to time, mostly to make deals.
Skyrim.esm|038749: Did you see those guards? Get out of line, and you'll have them to deal with.
Skyrim.esm|03874A: You look like the adventurous sort. You should talk to Erik, the innkeeper's son. He could use your help.
Skyrim.esm|038762: Alright now. Everyone back away. Let the law handle this.
Skyrim.esm|038765: You there. What do you know about this?
Skyrim.esm|038766: If anyone saw anything, step forward. Otherwise, step away from the body!
Skyrim.esm|038767: Resisting arrest will mean your death.
Skyrim.esm|03876A: Not sure, let's watch and find out.
Skyrim.esm|03876C: Is that so? Well, perhaps a night in jail might loosen your tongue. Hmmm?
Skyrim.esm|03876D: Alright, you are now under arrest.
Skyrim.esm|038770: Hmm... If you happen to recollect anything about the incident, you be sure to report to the captain of the guard. I've got my eye on you.
Skyrim.esm|038A3A: What's Karliah been filling your head with? Tales of thieves with honor? Oaths rife with falsehoods and broken promises? Nocturnal doesn't care about you, the Key or anything having to do with the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|038A3B: Then the die is cast, and once again my blade will taste Nightingale blood!
Skyrim.esm|038A3C: When Brynjolf brought you before me I could feel a sudden shift in the wind. And at that moment, I knew it would end with one of us at the end of a blade.
Skyrim.esm|038A3D: Then it appears the shadows shroud more than your presence... they blind your wisdom as well. Our actions have always been one in the same; both of us lie, cheat and steal to further our own end.
Skyrim.esm|038A45: It's clear you'll never see the Skeleton Key as I do... as an instrument of limitless wealth. Instead you've chosen to fall over your own foolish code.
Skyrim.esm|038A46: Revenge is it? Have you learned nothing from your time with us? When will you open your eyes and realize how little my actions differ from yours?  Both of us lie, cheat and steal to further our own end.
Skyrim.esm|038AB3: There's someone that wants to see him put out of business permanently. That's all you need to know. Now, you tell me when you're ready and we'll get started.
Skyrim.esm|038ADF: Then perhaps you care to explain why you are standing so close a dead body with your weapon drawn?
Skyrim.esm|038AE0: I see... and this "force of habit," does it also compel you to murder?
Skyrim.esm|038AE3: Let's show these rebels what real soldiers look like.
Skyrim.esm|038AE4: But don't underestimate the Stormcloaks. Many are Legion veterans. They may be traitors, but they know how to fight.
Skyrim.esm|038B0A: Hmmm... You can go, but you stay on your best behavior now. I've got my eye on you.
Skyrim.esm|038B5D: All right, meat, it's time for you to earn your freedom.
Skyrim.esm|038C6D: There's just one more target before we strike out at the Emperor. Have you by chance heard of the "Gourmet"? Read his cookbook? It's become quite a phenomenon. The Gourmet is scheduled to cook for the Emperor at a special dinner. You'll kill him, steal his Writ of Passage, and assume the roll of master chef. Festus has been spearheading this part of the assassination plan. He's close to uncovering the identity of the Gourmet. You should report to him.
Skyrim.esm|039166: The College would agree with you, but do you have any idea how many innocent lives were cut short, just so Azura could have revenge? We're nothing to the Daedra. Pawns to move around, praise, and punish as they see fit.
Skyrim.esm|039167: All right, but hurry. Who knows if Azura is watching us?
Skyrim.esm|039168: Give me a minute to examine the Star. I'll see what we'll need to do.
Skyrim.esm|03916C: Stay away! Last time you hurt me.
Skyrim.esm|03916E: I don't want to fight you again.
Skyrim.esm|039170: Hey! You're the one that hurt me!
Skyrim.esm|039178: You! Don't hurt me! Not again!
Skyrim.esm|03917D: I don't want any trouble. Not after what you did to me last time.
Skyrim.esm|03917E: You! Back to inflict more pain on me?
Skyrim.esm|039183: I hope you're not planning to attack me again...
Skyrim.esm|039184: If you provoke me, I will be forced to unsheathe my claws.
Skyrim.esm|0391A3: I don't want to be hurt again!
Skyrim.esm|0391A5: You wouldn't attack an old woman again, would you?
Skyrim.esm|039623: Yes, the claw. I know how it works. The claw, the markings, the door in the Hall of Stories. I know how they all fit together! Help me down, and I'll show you. You won't believe the power the Nords have hidden there.
Skyrim.esm|039625: You found it? Ha ha ha. There it is. Strange... it seems smaller than I remember. Funny thing, huh? I'm going to put this back where it belongs. I'll never forget this. You've done a great thing for me and my sister.
Skyrim.esm|039626: You did it. You killed it. Now cut me down before anything else shows up.
Skyrim.esm|03962C: Yes, we did have a bit of a... break-in. But we still have plenty to sell. Robbers were only after one thing. An ornament, solid gold. In the shape of a dragon's claw.
Skyrim.esm|039630: You here to attack me? I'm ready for you this time.
Skyrim.esm|039632: I better get back to cleaning the store. What a mess.
Skyrim.esm|03963A: Don't let my sister do anything foolish.
Skyrim.esm|03963B: Show those thieves not to steal from Lucan Valerius!
Skyrim.esm|03963C: The sooner you find the claw, the sooner our lives can get back to normal.
Skyrim.esm|03963D: You're back from Bleak Falls. Did you find anything?
Skyrim.esm|039641: Get me down. Get me down!
Skyrim.esm|039642: Lucan over in the Riverwood Trader says thieves broke into his store. Strange, he says they hardly took anything.
Skyrim.esm|039644: Did you hear? The Riverwood Trader was robbed! Poor Lucan must be torn to pieces over it.
Skyrim.esm|039672: Everybody ready? We charge on my signal.
Skyrim.esm|039674: Time to show what you're really made of.
Skyrim.esm|0396B9: You have a lot of nerve coming near me again. I'm still sore from what you did to me.
Skyrim.esm|03976B: Things look quiet enough here. Come on. There's my uncle.
Skyrim.esm|039772: My daughter Adara is going to be a fine silversmith when she's older.
Skyrim.esm|039773: I'm going to be a silversmith one day, like my mother and father.
Skyrim.esm|039774: Being an apprentice is great. I get to swing a hammer at things.
Skyrim.esm|039775: Mama doesn't like me jumping up and down in the house, but I can't help it. I swear.
Skyrim.esm|039777: A man like me makes a lot of enemies. People are always trying to cheat me.
Skyrim.esm|039778: I paid extra for the biggest room in the Silver-Blood Inn. I need space to stretch these arms.
Skyrim.esm|03977A: I love hearing Ogmund sing at the inn. He's a true skald.
Skyrim.esm|03977B: This one's claws are ready for you should you assault me again.
Skyrim.esm|03977D: If you try to hurt me again, I may have to kill you.
Skyrim.esm|039780: Attack me again and I'll hunt you down!
Skyrim.esm|039783: Ah! No more! Don't hurt me again.
Skyrim.esm|0397B0: Come back to gloat? I've been beaten by worse than you.
Skyrim.esm|0397EE: Don't come near me! I don't want to get hurt again.
Skyrim.esm|03982F: You dare approach me here? Are you mad? You cannot hope to defeat me!
Skyrim.esm|039830: The Eye has grown unstable. It cannot remain here, or else it may destroy your College and this world. It must be secured. Ancano's actions prove that the world is not ready for such a thing. We shall safeguard it... for now. You now have the opportunity to maintain your College, and carry on with your lives. You have our gratitude, Arch-Mage.
Skyrim.esm|039831: We knew you would succeed. Your victory here justifies our belief in you. You have proven yourself more than worthy to guide the College of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|039834: A great many things obscured to you are quite clear to us. We have long believed you would prevail.
Skyrim.esm|039848: It is time for us to go.
Skyrim.esm|039995: I hope you're not planning to attack me again...
Skyrim.esm|039999: You fool. You don't stand a chance.
Skyrim.esm|03999E: Sigh... I'm not proud of it. I'm terrified of that place and Aunt Agna knows it. My Da locked me in there in a drunken rage when he left us... three days in there eating the offerings left for our dead before Aunt Agna found me.
