Skyrim.esm|039A12: Uh, oh. You ain't gonna hurt me again, are ya?
Skyrim.esm|039B79: You're the brute that hurt me before.
Skyrim.esm|039B7A: Hey! Don't get mad and hurt me again.
Skyrim.esm|039B7C: Look, I don't want to get in another brawl with you.
Skyrim.esm|039B7D: You wouldn't attack an old man again, would you?
Skyrim.esm|039B7F: I don't go down easy if you raise your hand against me again.
Skyrim.esm|039B81: Well, if it isn't the bully that goes around hurting people.
Skyrim.esm|039B82: Ah! Are you planning on hurting me again?
Skyrim.esm|039B92: If it's a fight you want, I'm ready this time.
Skyrim.esm|039C31: I guess you had your reasons.
Skyrim.esm|039C34: If I can just... stand up...
Skyrim.esm|039C36: I guess I can look the other way, this time.
Skyrim.esm|039C39: Help yourself, by all means. Who cares if I starve?
Skyrim.esm|039C3A: I guess you can have that.
Skyrim.esm|039C3B: Oh, what a clever thief you are.
Skyrim.esm|039C3C: You really shouldn't be in here, but I guess since it's you...
Skyrim.esm|039C3D: I'll let you stay. This time.
Skyrim.esm|039C3E: You know you're not supposed to be in here right now.
Skyrim.esm|039C41: I didn't know you were so cold-blooded...
Skyrim.esm|039C75: I told you to weed the garden by sunset, and you didn't do it. Now you're in big trouble!
Skyrim.esm|039C77: You're going to get it, Sissel!
Skyrim.esm|039C7C: Papa told you to do that, not me! Now leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|039C80: If you've got some business in Rorikstead, you should start by speaking to Jouane.
Skyrim.esm|039C81: No offense intended, but I don't think we have anything to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|039C82: Peace and prosperity are all the good folk of Rorikstead want in life.
Skyrim.esm|039C84: Do enjoy your time in Rorikstead. This close to the Reach, well, we are truly blessed to have such fertile ground.
Skyrim.esm|039CB6: Quintus, could you fetch me a copy of The Firmament? I need to compare the old star charts to today's.
Skyrim.esm|039CB7: Yes, master, I know. I just want to keep up my own projects as well.
Skyrim.esm|039CB8: There's a clear spot just west of Riften. You know the area?
Skyrim.esm|039CB9: Don't waste time apologizing, just find the book!
Skyrim.esm|039CBA: Erm... no. Not yet. I'm sorry, I was reading up on the effects of distillation.
Skyrim.esm|039CBB: I'm sorry. What would you like to talk about?
Skyrim.esm|039CBE: I see you are eager to carry the light. As you venture, Mara fills my mind ever more with visions of love in peril. Embers lie nestled in stone, needing only fuel to bloom to a flame that will warm all around them. Go to Markarth. There you'll find Calcelmo, wise, acid, and reclusive. Help him to emerge and state his intentions. This is the prayer heard by the goddess and relayed to her servants.
Skyrim.esm|039CC0: You can waste your own time, not mine. An apprentice obeys his master. A good one does, at least.
Skyrim.esm|039CC1: Damn it, child, didn't I tell you to gather my notes on sub-material properties?
Skyrim.esm|039CC2: I've been there before, but I don't know it well.
Skyrim.esm|039CC3: What? You've been lollygagging in idle reading?
Skyrim.esm|039CC4: Hows about we figure out some way to keep warm tonight?
Skyrim.esm|039CC5: Yes, master. I'm sorry, I'll get back to those notes straight away.
Skyrim.esm|039CC6: I've been thinking about building that cabin we talked about.
Skyrim.esm|039CC7: Yes, and I'm working on it, but I'm also trying to...
Skyrim.esm|039CC8: Did they really record the star positions from Curalmil's tomb? There's no way it's that precise.
Skyrim.esm|039CC9: Just wondering when you'll be ready to give it up and settle down. Seems like you're always working.
Skyrim.esm|039CCA: Well that's where I want to build. In the fall forest. Give it an easterly view. The sunrise is almost as pretty as you are.
Skyrim.esm|039CCD: Tell me about the Great War. I hear you were in the battle of...
Skyrim.esm|039CCE: Don't be so sure. Now, have you finished grinding the dried snowberries?
Skyrim.esm|039CD0: I'm sorry to always be so hard on you, boy. I just worry I don't have much time left.
Skyrim.esm|039CD1: That sounds lovely. You're very sweet.
Skyrim.esm|039CD3: I just feel like if I'm not here, then something could go wrong. Nils is a hard worker, but I wouldn't trust him to run things.
Skyrim.esm|039CD4: When I ask you for something, I expect it to get done.
Skyrim.esm|039CD5: Confound it, boy, I know what I'm doing. It will help me determine the season of his burial, which I can cross-reference.
Skyrim.esm|039CD6: Elda, honey, let's not talk about the war. It's in the past.
Skyrim.esm|039CD7: One of these days he'll have to learn.
Skyrim.esm|039CD9: The cornerclub's been filling up at night lately. I'll bet Elda up at Candlehearth is jealous.
Skyrim.esm|039CDA: I know, master. Don't say such things -- there is always time.
Skyrim.esm|039CDB: Master, I have a question about infusions of arrowroot.
Skyrim.esm|039CDC: How long have you run this place, again?
Skyrim.esm|039CEC: Here you go, sir. The finest Colovian brandy. Is there anything else I can get for you?
Skyrim.esm|039CED: You ready yet? I need to get back before I'm missed. Remember, you won't have anything except what I smuggle in for you.
Skyrim.esm|039DF0: By the Eight, keep your voice down! Do you want the entire village to learn our secrets?
Skyrim.esm|039DF1: Shh shh. It's fine, child. It's fine. But we must be cautious, hmm? What we do, the things I teach you. The others wouldn't understand.
Skyrim.esm|039DF2: Hmph. Most certainly not. But perhaps I can teach you how to put some candles out. We'll start there.
Skyrim.esm|039DF3: I was just wondering. The next time we meet, do you think maybe you could teach me some fire magic? Nothing dangerous! Maybe a candle lighting spell?
Skyrim.esm|039DF4: I understand. I'm sorry. I just get so excited thinking about it. So... can we. Do some fire magic?
Skyrim.esm|039DF6: Oh! I'm... I'm sorry. I didn't mean to upset you.
Skyrim.esm|039E35: We should tread carefully. I wouldn't be surprised if he's left behind a few surprises for us.
Skyrim.esm|039E36: Mercer's been here, I hope we aren't too late.
Skyrim.esm|039F0B: No, no. I'll... I'll wait outside.
Skyrim.esm|039F0C: But, I need that money! I earned it.
Skyrim.esm|039F0E: Do not presume, Gissur. You are most useful, but do not presume. We have other informants who are less... offensive.
Skyrim.esm|039F0F: Everyone talks, in the end. Now, I have work to do. Leave me to it, if you ever want to see the rest of your payment.
Skyrim.esm|039F10: That would probably be best. Now get out!
Skyrim.esm|039F11: You'd like to come downstairs with me, is that it, Gissur? Shall we loose his bonds and put you in a cell together? You can ask him anything you like, and see how he answers.
Skyrim.esm|039F12: I have my own expenses you know...
Skyrim.esm|039F13: Can I... I could help you. He'd talk to me. He trusts me.
Skyrim.esm|039F14: You'll get the rest of your money when we confirm his story. As agreed.
Skyrim.esm|039F15: So he has talked! I knew it!
Skyrim.esm|039F16: But no one else has brought you such valuable information, have they? Etienne, he's talked, hasn't he? He knows where that old man is you're looking for, he told me himself.
Skyrim.esm|039F17: He can't treat me like that. He needs me.
Skyrim.esm|039F18: Rulindil, pah! One day, the shoe will be on the other foot.
Skyrim.esm|039F19: I knew he was telling the truth. I always can tell.
Skyrim.esm|039F1A: They're scared of the dragons, too, aren't they? Paying too well. They don't know anything.
Skyrim.esm|039F1B: Etienne must know something related to the dragons... who else might know? Greasy Thom... no, they'd see right through him...
Skyrim.esm|039F58: I just... I just know it. I can feel it in my heart. You have to believe me. Please, visit me at my home. I can tell you the whole story.
Skyrim.esm|039F59: Not here. Let's speak at my home, where we can be alone, unwatched.
Skyrim.esm|039F5A: It's not wise to discuss it here. Please, if you truly wish to help, meet me at my home. I'll tell you the whole story.
Skyrim.esm|039F5B: All I can think about is my son, my Thorald... they say that he was killed, but I know better. I know my son is alive! Those Battle-Born... they're in with the Imperials. They know it too, and yet they lie to my very face!
Skyrim.esm|039F74: You have a job to do.
Skyrim.esm|039F75: You want to get paid? Do what I hired you for.
Skyrim.esm|039F79: I've been looking for you. Got something I'm supposed to deliver - your hands only.
Skyrim.esm|039F80: I have a letter here from Quintus Navale in Windhelm. He said it was urgent.
Skyrim.esm|039F81: Looks like that's it. Got to go.
Skyrim.esm|03A065: I can't believe it's over. Twenty-five years in exile and just like that, it's done. All that remains is to ensure the safe return of the Skeleton Key.
Skyrim.esm|03A066: I'm afraid it's not that simple. When the Skeleton Key was stolen from the Twilight Sepulcher, our access to the inner sanctum was removed. The only way to bring it back will be through the Pilgrim's Path.
Skyrim.esm|03A067: Brynjolf is needed back at the Thieves Guild to keep order while you're away. And I... I can't bear to face Nocturnal after my failure to protect the Key. I'm afraid you'll have to face the end of your journey alone.
Skyrim.esm|03A068: It wasn't created for the Nightingales. It was created to test those who wished to serve Nocturnal in other ways. As a consequence, I have no knowledge of what you'll be facing.
Skyrim.esm|03A092: I don't like it. What are they waiting for?
Skyrim.esm|03A094: No, but I know they're out there. No other way they can come. Now shut it or you'll ruin our ambush.
Skyrim.esm|03A096: Maybe they're so scared of you they ran away.
Skyrim.esm|03A145: And there it is, friends! The ugly truth! We are the children of man! Talos is the true god of man! Ascended from flesh, to rule the realm of spirit!
Skyrim.esm|03A147: Come on now, we discussed that. I wouldn't be traveling all of Skyrim. It'd just to Whiterun and back, that's it.
Skyrim.esm|03A14A: And deserve our praise you do, for we are one! Ere you ascended and the Eight became Nine, you walked among us, great Talos, not as god, but as man!
Skyrim.esm|03A14B: The very idea is inconceivable to our Elven overlords! Sharing the heavens with us? With man? Ha! They can barely tolerate our presence on earth!
Skyrim.esm|03A14C: Have you given my idea any more thought? You said you'd let me know yesterday, and that obviously didn't happen.
Skyrim.esm|03A14E: "I breathe now, in royalty, and reshape this land which is mine. I do this for you, Red Legions, for I love you."
Skyrim.esm|03A14F: Terrible and powerful Talos! We, your unworthy servants, give praise! For only through your grace and benevolence may we truly reach enlightenment!
Skyrim.esm|03A150: Aye, love. Love! Even as man, great Talos cherished us. For he saw in us, in each of us, the future of Skyrim! The future of Tamriel!
Skyrim.esm|03A151: Oh, I don't know. Yes, we'd make more money, but I don't like the idea of you traveling alone out there with the war and talk of dragons.
Skyrim.esm|03A152: Tomorrow. I'll let you know tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|03A154: But you were once man! Aye! And as man, you said, "Let me show you the power of Talos Stormcrown, born of the North, where my breath is long winter."
Skyrim.esm|03A155: Today, they take away your faith. But what of tomorrow? What then? Do the elves take your homes? Your businesses? Your children? Your very lives?
Skyrim.esm|03A16E: That dark elf wants to go on ahead, let him. Better than us risking our necks.
Skyrim.esm|03A16F: Sweet breath of Arkay, thank you.
Skyrim.esm|03A170: Does it look like I can move? You have to cut me down, first.
Skyrim.esm|03A177: So we're just supposed to sit here while Arvel runs off with that golden claw?
Skyrim.esm|03A1C9: Fine. Whatever you want. Can't you just kill me and get it over with? There's this old man. I've seen him around Riften. I don't know where he lives. I don't know his name. Yes, he matches the description of this Esbern guy you're looking for. Maybe it's him. Maybe it isn't. That's all I know, okay?
Skyrim.esm|03A1CA: Whatever you want. But let's go, now.
Skyrim.esm|03A1CB: Why don't you just kill me already.
Skyrim.esm|03A1CC: No problem. Thanks for springing me. I owe you. Look me up in Riften if you make it out.
Skyrim.esm|03A1CD: I guess you know what you're doing. I've got to find a way out of here.
Skyrim.esm|03A1CE: I damn well hope so. If it helps you twist them up, I'm glad to help. They're after some old guy named Esbern. Something to do with dragons, I gathered it from listening to them talk when they thought I was out. I've seen a guy in Riften who they seem to think is him. Not much to go on -- I don't even know where he lives, or his name. But they seemed pretty excited about it. That's it. Now, let's get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|03A1CF: Yeah, sure, okay. Come on, this way. I've seen the guards use it to get rid of bodies. It must lead somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|03A1D0: What? Who... what do you want then?
Skyrim.esm|03A1D1: I told you, I don't know anything else about it.
Skyrim.esm|03A1DF: You fool, why should I share the treasure with anyone?
Skyrim.esm|03A6A0: Please, let me know if it seems safe to leave.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A1: He is? I'm not sure how to feel about that.  Perhaps it's for the better.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A2: I left for a few days in Riften. When I came back Bashnag accused me of trying to sell him out to the Thalmor. I was cursed and chased off.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A3: Thank you. When I find my daughter, it will be thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A4: Bashnag cursed me. I can't cast a spell to save my life right now. I'll have to wait until the way out is safe.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A5: Thanks for getting me out of that. Bashnag's coven chased me out and left me for dead down here. Nobody lasts too long with the spiders. Please, will you help me get out of here? If the coast is clear, I'll make a break for it.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A6: I am, and I don't want to hear your moral judgments about it, either. I wouldn't have had to study in secret in the old days.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A7: My daughter was sent to the orphanage there when I stood accused of illegal necromancy. I had no choice. Run or die.
Skyrim.esm|03A6A8: Bashnag's circle of mages. We - well "they" live here. They made it pretty clear that I'm no longer welcome.
Skyrim.esm|03A8B1: You know. Where do you get your goods? I am always amazed at how you manage to sell things so cheaply.
Skyrim.esm|03A8B2: Oh dear. What should I do?
Skyrim.esm|03A8B3: Sir, there continues to be unrest in the Gray Quarter.
Skyrim.esm|03A8B4: Fine, then. I guess some Dunmer are content to be their pets.
Skyrim.esm|03A8B5: Oh, I'm sorry to hear that. Is everything all right?
Skyrim.esm|03A8B7: Well that much is good. Let's finish this first war before starting the next one, eh?
Skyrim.esm|03A8B8: What's the current spirit among the dark elves?
Skyrim.esm|03A8B9: Here, let me get you some mead. This one's on me.
Skyrim.esm|03A8BB: That's really none of your business...
Skyrim.esm|03A8BC: Yes, sir. I'll try to contain her.
Skyrim.esm|03A8BF: A navy, my lord. We could stop the influx of Imperial troops to Solitude, and choke their northern lines.
Skyrim.esm|03A8C0: But imagine -- fleets of ships sailing from Windhelm, all bearing the Stormcloak flag! It would be glorious!
Skyrim.esm|03A8C2: Of course. Something that seems to be in short supply these days.
Skyrim.esm|03A8C3: Is there any news from High Rock?
Skyrim.esm|03A8C4: I know, dear. He's a busy man, though, and loyal to the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|03A8C5: Let me know if you hear anything more substantial?
Skyrim.esm|03A8C6: Bring it to me straight away when you find it. Don't dally. I can keep it safe.
Skyrim.esm|03A8C8: I'm still not sure I understand what you're asking.
Skyrim.esm|03A8C9: Oh, Jora. Good to see you. I was wondering if you might ask Lortheim something for me.
Skyrim.esm|03A8CA: Blasted dark elves. I don't suppose you could tell them that I presently have larger concerns? Such as all of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|03A8CB: Here's what's going to happen. I'm going to pretend I didn't hear that, and you're going to leave me alone. Deal?
Skyrim.esm|03A8CC: What's new with the Cruel-Seas, Suvaris? Have they given you one of those helmets with the horns on them yet?
Skyrim.esm|03A8CD: If it's all the same to you, Scouts-Many-Marshes prefers to eat and drink in peace.
Skyrim.esm|03A8CE: I'll think on this, but I make no promises.
Skyrim.esm|03A8CF: Well, sir, the hold of Falkreath is fairly vulnerable, and blocks the only road from Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|03A8D1: I just don't know what to do anymore. He's hardly ever in the temple. I see him at breakfast, and that's it.
Skyrim.esm|03A8D7: Calixto, someone left an interesting ring in here a few weeks ago. Would you like a look?
Skyrim.esm|03A8DA: Look, Ambarys, I just came here for a drink. I don't need any trouble.
Skyrim.esm|03A8E0: I don't care if she can find a living dwarf -- I can't have her stirring up trouble. We have enough problems as it is.
Skyrim.esm|03A8E1: We need to move faster. Keep them off balance.
Skyrim.esm|03A8E2: How would you like a drink?
Skyrim.esm|03A8E3: I'm diverting Gar and Haddring's men to join him.
Skyrim.esm|03A8E4: Calm yourself, Galmar. Save it for the battlefield.
Skyrim.esm|03A8E5: How much do they pay you per day?
Skyrim.esm|03A8E6: Why do the Nords bother you so much, Ambarys?
Skyrim.esm|03A8E7: I can ask no more. Thank you, my lord!
Skyrim.esm|03A8E8: Eight septims per day, plus lodging.
Skyrim.esm|03A8E9: We can't march on Solitude. Not yet. One thing at a time.
Skyrim.esm|03A8EA: Don't you ever find it demeaning, working for that Nord family?
Skyrim.esm|03A8EB: I'll consider it. Many of the people I deal with are... secretive. You know how it is.
Skyrim.esm|03A8EC: So what's the news on the street, Captain?
Skyrim.esm|03A8ED: It's been better, I'll be honest with you.
Skyrim.esm|03A8EF: Of course, my dear. It may prove to be quite the curiosity! One must always be alert.
Skyrim.esm|03A8F0: It's working, Galmar. Our patience has won us friends and allies. And our armies are systematically taking care of the rest.
Skyrim.esm|03A8F2: Well, I'll want to meet them next time they're around. Maybe you can give an introduction.
Skyrim.esm|03A8F4: Our men are getting massacred out there. Damn Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|03A8F5: What do you want from me? I work for them ok? We're not friends. They pay me, and I get the job done. That's it.
Skyrim.esm|03A8F6: Sorry, my scaly friend, you've come to the wrong place. Around here we don't ignore what's happening in the world.
Skyrim.esm|03A8F9: I suppose we shouldn't be surprised. They've never had many problems with the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|03A8FC: I think that's just their way. The Nords don't like anybody who's not a Nord, but they're not bad people, deep down.
Skyrim.esm|03A901: Galmar thinks we should double our men in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|03A902: Not a peep. Those prissy Bretons can't be made to lift a finger to help their neighbors.
Skyrim.esm|03A903: Either pour another drink or keep moving, Ambarys.
Skyrim.esm|03A905: Touchy. Maybe some Dunmer lives in you yet.
Skyrim.esm|03A906: Oh, just a matter of finding the right suppliers.
Skyrim.esm|03A907: See that you do. I will not let Windhelm descend into chaos over a few overactive imaginations.
Skyrim.esm|03A908: Oh, I doubt that very much. Who would load their ships, then?
Skyrim.esm|03A909: And I suppose the Cruel-Seas would be just the clan to provide ships and men?
Skyrim.esm|03A90A: Carry on, Lonely-Gale. And loosen yourself a bit.
Skyrim.esm|03A90B: They don't seem to be very sympathetic to our cause, sir.
Skyrim.esm|03A90C: To be sure, my lord, I am not skilled in the arts of war or military tactics.
Skyrim.esm|03A90D: Well, I seem to have misplaced it at the moment. But it was white, like alabaster, with three bright rubies set in it.
Skyrim.esm|03A90E: Captain, I've been receiving complaints about this Giordano woman.
Skyrim.esm|03A911: I... I'm sorry, dear, I've got some other customers. I'll be back around in a minute.
Skyrim.esm|03A913: Eight septims for breaking your back, and then you live in squalor. How much is your dignity worth?
Skyrim.esm|03A917: But you're able to see the whole in the parts, and for that I'm grateful.
Skyrim.esm|03A918: Look around you. Don't you know what their little war is about? They want all non-Nords out of Skyrim. That means you and me.
Skyrim.esm|03A919: Niranye, I just have to know -- what is your secret?
Skyrim.esm|03A91A: As restless as ever, sir, but I don't see them taking any kind of incendiary action. Not soon, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|03A91C: It will work if we can pull Hran's men from the south. And find a bit of luck.
Skyrim.esm|03A91D: Of course! I undersand completely. You just put us in a room, and I'll do all the talking. No worries at all.
Skyrim.esm|03A91E: The great stronghold of Narzulbur welcomes you, outsider.
Skyrim.esm|03A91F: Come to purchase ebony? Our Gloombound Mine runs thick with black veins.
Skyrim.esm|03A920: The blessings of the mine have been many, but I have no mate to share them.
Skyrim.esm|03A921: Been married many times, but the gods keep taking them from me.
Skyrim.esm|03A922: I've read the entrails many times, but can't fathom why Mauhulakh's wives keep finding tragedy.
Skyrim.esm|03A923: Urog and Dushnamub were such lovely children. I held them as they watched their mothers die after birth. They know me best, now.
Skyrim.esm|03A924: Mauhulakh was such a darling boy. Until he got older and started having opinions.
Skyrim.esm|03A925: Yatul and I tend to dote on Mauhulakh. You know how aunts can be with their favorite nephew.
Skyrim.esm|03A926: Mauhulakh. He wants to be a leader but spends too much time moping over women. Bolar and I should be all he needs.
Skyrim.esm|03A927: Orc chiefs should be strong and stand alone. They need no companions. Progeny is a poor excuse.
Skyrim.esm|03A928: I know I sound abrasive. It's just that I love Mauhulakh so much that I want us to be happy together.
Skyrim.esm|03A929: My brother keeps trying to spin these fantasies to me about my aunts and our father. He's crazy.
Skyrim.esm|03A92A: Yatul's been teaching me to hunt. I'm still learning, but she's the best teacher I could have.
Skyrim.esm|03A92B: Sometimes I wonder if my aunts are right about my father -- he does seem weak.
Skyrim.esm|03A92C: New to Narzulbur? If you're smart, you'll keep moving. There is a wicked shadow over this place.
Skyrim.esm|03A92D: Haven't you seen how my aunts act with my father? It's beyond strange.
Skyrim.esm|03A92E: I never knew my mother, but I doubt even she acted as affectionate with my father as Yatul and Bolar do.
Skyrim.esm|03A92F: My sister is blind. I've told her she shouldn't spend so much time with Yatul, but she won't listen. Thinks she's nice.
Skyrim.esm|03A930: Gadba and me came to work the mine. The plan is to get rich and then go back to Largashbur and take over!
Skyrim.esm|03A931: Mauhulakh brought us in when he found ebony in the mine. Not enough men in town to work it, so here we are.
Skyrim.esm|03A932: One of these days we need to head back to the Rift. Get some of that good Riften mead.
Skyrim.esm|03A933: Mul is the smart one. I go where he tells me.
Skyrim.esm|03A934: Mining is tough, but Mul says the money is good so we mine.
Skyrim.esm|03A935: When we go back to Largashbur we'll be kings. I like Narzulbur, but don't tell Mul.
Skyrim.esm|03A936: Oh, thank the gods you're in Windhelm, Calixto!
Skyrim.esm|03A937: The dark elves are uneasy. There's a killer on the streets. And Viola Giordano won't leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|03A938: Jorleif, I want you to send a messenger to our western camps.
Skyrim.esm|03A93B: Don't bother. I don't have much chance of seeing my husband these days. He's always up in the palace with Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|03A93C: What about being loyal to his wife?
Skyrim.esm|03A93D: Yes, sir. I've been trying to quiet her, but she's insistent. She thinks she can find out who's been killing the women...
Skyrim.esm|03A93F: I'm not seeking advice right now, friend. Just send the message.
Skyrim.esm|03A940: Those milk drinkers? Might as well be elves. Think they're better than us.
Skyrim.esm|03A941: But most of their supply routes come south, from Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|03A942: Be very careful with that. It sounds as if it may be the Death Ring of Dro'farahn. A ring of the most dangerous sort.
Skyrim.esm|03A944: Why do you even need to ask? They treat your people as bad as they do mine.
Skyrim.esm|03A945: I used to have five good men who worked the mill with me. Then this war started and they all ran off. Idiots with mead in their blood.
Skyrim.esm|03A946: There's plenty of good wood around here, but I just can't harvest it all by myself. Everyone able-bodied is fighting.
Skyrim.esm|03A947: Of course, without functioning mills, who will make the arrows for our soldiers? Men have no sense of irony.
Skyrim.esm|03A948: Welcome, welcome, welcome! Don't see many strangers out here. Come to get your hands dirty on a real farm?
Skyrim.esm|03A949: Nothing tastes better than food you've grown yourself. Something magic about dirt and sunshine making food.
Skyrim.esm|03A94A: The weather's harsh, but I like to think my sunny personality is what helps the grain grow.
Skyrim.esm|03A94B: Bolfrida is certainly grating, but she pays well. Thankfully nobody from the city can see me out here.
Skyrim.esm|03A94C: Farm work is hard work. It's good to relax when I can, but I still have to listen to my brothers harping about "injustices."
Skyrim.esm|03A94D: Of course working for Bolfrida isn't my first choice. But what else can a Dunmer do out here?
Skyrim.esm|03A94E: My sister and I both work for the Nords. Our brother is ashamed of us, but at least we can affort to eat.
Skyrim.esm|03A94F: Starting to get too old to work all day, but hopefully Master Hlaalu will let me slow down a bit.
Skyrim.esm|03A950: Every now and then someone from the city comes by. Think they aren't used to seeing a Nord working for a Dark Elf.
Skyrim.esm|03A951: Better to work in the cold than in the heat, I say. Though some sun would be nice.
Skyrim.esm|03A952: Master Cruel-Sea comes and works the fields sometimes. I don't think the Mistress likes it, but she won't come out here to catch him.
Skyrim.esm|03A953: Once the sun goes down, it's just me and the dogs. They keep me warm, and don't talk too much.
Skyrim.esm|03A954: I wonder if I could put the dogs to work somehow. But I don't really have the heart to force them.
Skyrim.esm|03A955: Regardless. It appears Skyrim must stand alone. Again.
Skyrim.esm|03AA6E: Yes, Listener. I won't move unless you give the word.
Skyrim.esm|03AA6F: I'll remain in this location until ordered otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|03AA78: Oh, of course. Mother needs me, after all.
Skyrim.esm|03AA79: I will return to the Dawnstar Sanctuary immediately, Listener.
Skyrim.esm|03AA7A: Yes, Listener. I'll be here if you need me.
Skyrim.esm|03AA7B: Yes, Listener. I'll return to the Dawnstar Sanctuary without delay.
Skyrim.esm|03AA7C: Of course. But if you need me, just give the order.
Skyrim.esm|03AA7D: Yes, Listener? How may I serve you?
Skyrim.esm|03AA7E: Listener. It is an honor to stand before you.
Skyrim.esm|03AA7F: How may I serve you, my Listener?
Skyrim.esm|03ADAA: And what does the Empire do? Nothing! Nay, worse than nothing! The Imperial machine enforces the will of the Thalmor! Against its own people!
Skyrim.esm|03ADAB: For we are the children of man! And we shall inherit both heavens and earth! And we, not the Elves or their toadies, will rule Skyrim! Forever!
Skyrim.esm|03ADAC: So rise up! Rise up, children of the Empire! Rise up, Stormcloaks! Embrace the word of mighty Talos, he who is both man and Divine!
Skyrim.esm|03ADAE: Good enough. I'll just confiscate any stolen goods you're carrying, then you're free to go.
Skyrim.esm|03ADB3: As soon as you distract the guards, I'll open the door.
Skyrim.esm|03AEBB: Hmm. I guess I'll defer to your judgment on this one, but I think it's a mistake.
Skyrim.esm|03AEBE: Maybe so, but we shouldn't be making concessions before negotiations even start. Makes us look weak.
Skyrim.esm|03AEBF: I'm glad we see eye to eye on this.
Skyrim.esm|03AF49: What? You, alone, reasoning with the Thalmor? That's madness, friend. You're serious, aren't you?
Skyrim.esm|03AF4B: Are you sure? I doubt you stand a chance against the Thalmor. I can have a group of men ready at a moment's notice. Many would be willing to help rescue my brother.
Skyrim.esm|03AF4C: Very well. I'll give you a chance. But if you can't do it, I will.
Skyrim.esm|03AF4D: Exactly. Together we stand more of a chance of making this work. Make your way to Northwatch Keep in Haafingar. I'll meet you near there, and we'll plan our assault.
Skyrim.esm|03AF50: We kill every Thalmor between us and Thorald, and we bring him out alive. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|03AF51: How do we know he's not spying for the Battle-Born? This was foolish!
Skyrim.esm|03AF52: How do we know she's not spying for the Battle-Born? This was foolish!
Skyrim.esm|03AF53: Anything, anything at all would be a start. Then we can figure out how to get to him. I'm sorry for doubting you, friend. You have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|03AF54: Avulstein, put that down! He's here to help us find Thorald!
Skyrim.esm|03AF57: I can't take any more of this. No weapons, please. Let's just talk.
Skyrim.esm|03AF58: Please, I'm desperate. This is my brother's life! I won't let them take it away! I beg of you, look for proof of Thorald's capture. The Battle-Borns have it, I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|03AF59: Mother, what's the meaning of this? Who have you brought into our home?
Skyrim.esm|03AF5A: I know that Thorald's not dead. I just know it. The Imperials have him, and are keeping him somewhere. But I don't know where. Those damned Battle-Borns... they know something. They're hiding it. They practically taunt us with it. They must have some sort of proof! There's got to be something in that house of theirs. I just know it. I don't dare leave the house myself. So I need your help.
Skyrim.esm|03AF5E: My brother, Thorald. He was fighting for the Stormcloaks, and went missing. Everyone assumes he's dead. But he's not. I just know it. The Imperials captured him, and they're holding him someplace. The Battle-Borns know where. I'd go and search their house for proof, but they want me too. I'd be captured, and then we'll never know what happened to him. So I need someone to find that proof for me.
Skyrim.esm|03AF5F: I knew it! Let me see, please! The Thalmor? By the Nine, it's worse than I thought. So... Northwatch Keep. Then we know where to hit them.
Skyrim.esm|03AF60: We can't trust anyone! Who knows what they'll do if they find me here.
Skyrim.esm|03AF61: Anything that confirms that Thorald isn't dead, but is being held captive. I know those Battle-Borns have it. They'd be hiding it, of course. Wouldn't want it to get out that they've been lying this whole time. But you might be able to butter them up enough to lower their guard.
Skyrim.esm|03AF78: I cannot thank you enough for rescuing me from this place. I suspect I'd never again see the light of day otherwise. But why would you risk your life for me, a stranger?
Skyrim.esm|03AF79: I never thought I'd see another friendly face again. We need to get to safety as soon as possible. Let's move.
Skyrim.esm|03B2BF: Talos the mighty! Talos the unerring! Talos the unassailable! To you we give praise!
Skyrim.esm|03B2C0: We are but maggots, writhing in the filth of our own corruption! While you have ascended from the dung of mortality, and now walk among the stars!
Skyrim.esm|03B439: Trust in me, Whiterun! Trust in Heimskr! For I am the chosen of Talos! I alone have been anointed by the Ninth to spread his holy word!
Skyrim.esm|03B470: Excellent! We've hit 'em where it hurts. Repairs will be needed when we take over, but at least the Stormcloaks won't benefit in the meantime.
Skyrim.esm|03B492: I'm one of the last members of the Blades. A very long time ago, the Blades were dragonslayers, and we served the Dragonborn, the greatest dragonslayer. For the last two hundred years, since the last Dragonborn emperor, the Blades have been searching for a purpose. Now that dragons are coming back, our purpose is clear again. We need to stop them.
Skyrim.esm|03B493: The first thing we need to do is figure out who's behind the dragons. The Thalmor are our best lead. If they aren't involved, they'll know who is.
Skyrim.esm|03B495: I'm not sure yet. I have a few ideas, but I'll need some time to pull things together... Meet me back in Riverwood. If I'm not back when you get there, wait for me. I shouldn't be long. Keep an eye on the sky. This is only going to get worse.
Skyrim.esm|03B496: Interesting. Same dragon... Damn it, we're blundering around in the dark here! We need to figure out who's behind it all!
Skyrim.esm|03B497: Go ahead. Whatever you want to know. Nothing held back.
Skyrim.esm|03B498: Not a damn thing. I was just as surprised as you to find that big black dragon here.
Skyrim.esm|03B499: Nothing solid. Yet. But my gut tells me it can't be anybody else. The Empire had captured Ulfric. The war was basically over. Then a dragon attacks, Ulfric escapes, and the war is back on. And now the dragons are attacking everywhere, indiscriminately. Skyrim is weakened, the Empire is weakened. Who else gains from that but the Thalmor?
Skyrim.esm|03B49A: We need to find out if the Thalmor are behind the dragons coming back. We need to get into the Thalmor Embassy, somehow...
Skyrim.esm|03B59B: If I have to hold your hand through everything, maybe you aren't cut out for this kind of work. It's under your feet, lad. Just follow the smell.
Skyrim.esm|03B59C: The walls have ears and you're being tested, lad. We'll talk in the Ragged Flagon.
Skyrim.esm|03B59E: The walls have ears and you're being tested, lass. We'll talk in the Ragged Flagon.
Skyrim.esm|03B66C: Isn't it obvious? As the Night Mother's Keeper, he believes he's entitled to the rule of this Sanctuary. Cicero will cite our independence as the need to revert to the Old Ways. He'll claim we're undisciplined, unruly. Heretical, even. Ironically, the Night Mother could prove to be just as much a victim. The queen in a fool's twisted game of chess.
Skyrim.esm|03B66D: I don't believe so. But I'll be able to instruct you further after you've returned from the chamber. It depends on what you learn. And make haste! You must be in place before Cicero and his... ally arrive! They could be planning another meeting at any time!
Skyrim.esm|03B66E: It's Cicero. Ever since he arrived, his behavior's been... Well, erratic would be an understatement. I do believe he is truly mad. But it's worse than that. He's taken to locking himself in the Night Mother's chamber, and talking. To someone. In hushed, but frantic tones. Who is he speaking with? What are they planning? I fear treachery.
Skyrim.esm|03B66F: That's the real question, isn't it? The jester enters, seals the door, and the conversation begins. So someone must be waiting for him inside. Any one of us could enter that chamber silently. Unnoticed. But who amongst us would dare conspire against the Sanctuary? The very thought breaks my heart.
Skyrim.esm|03B69A: Yeah. I bet I know your guy. He's hiding out in the Ratway Warrens. Paying us good coin for nobody to know about it.
Skyrim.esm|03B69C: Lot of people looking for him. Don't know his name, but he's paid good money for nobody to know he's down here.
Skyrim.esm|03B69D: He's holed up in the Ratway Warrens. Be careful. You're not the only one looking for him.
Skyrim.esm|03B69E: Huh. Lot of old guys around. I don't really know how I can help.
Skyrim.esm|03BB7A: What about you? Have you... have you spoken to anyone? No.... No, of course not. I do the talking, the stalking, the seeing and saying!
Skyrim.esm|03BB7C: Are we alone? Yes... yes... alone. Sweet solitude. No one will hear us, disturb us. Everything is going according to plan.
Skyrim.esm|03BB7D: The others... I've spoken to them. And they're coming around, I know it. The wizard, Festus Krex... perhaps even the Argonian, and the un-child...
Skyrim.esm|03BB7E: And what do you do? Nothing! Not... not that I'm angry! No, never! Cicero understands. Heh. Cicero always understands! And obeys!
Skyrim.esm|03BB7F: You will talk when you're ready, won't you? Won't you...
Skyrim.esm|03BC57: Help! Can anyone hear me? I'm stuck down here.
Skyrim.esm|03BC58: You, you there! Help me, I'm trapped.
Skyrim.esm|03BC5C: Cut me down, or you'll never get the claw!
Skyrim.esm|03BC5D: Where are you... get me down from this!
Skyrim.esm|03BC5E: Sorry, but I have a treasure to get to...
Skyrim.esm|03BC61: I don't know what you overheard, but the Riverwood Trader is still open. Feel free to shop.
Skyrim.esm|03BC6F: I heard Lucan yelling the other day about a thief breaking into his store. It wasn't me, honest!
Skyrim.esm|03BC93: Ironic, right? The old enemies assume that every calamity must be a plot by the other side... Even so, we've got to find Esbern before they do. He'll know how to stop the dragons if anybody does. Do they know where he is?
Skyrim.esm|03BC94: He's holed up in the Ratway Warrens. Hardly ever leaves the place - has someone bring him food and such. Crazy old coot, from what I've heard. For that to stand out down here, he must really be off his nut.
Skyrim.esm|03BC95: Riften, eh? Probably down in the Ratway, then. It's where I'd go.
Skyrim.esm|03BC98: You mean, aside from wanting to kill every Blade they can lay their hands on? Esbern was one of the Blades archivists, back before the Thalmor smashed us during the Great War. He knew everything about the ancient dragonlore of the Blades. Obsessed with it, really. Nobody paid much attention back then. I guess he wasn't as crazy as we all thought.
Skyrim.esm|03BC99: Really? That seems hard to believe. You're sure about that?
Skyrim.esm|03BC9F: Dangerous-looking elves who didn't give their names. Draw your own conclusions.
Skyrim.esm|03BCA0: You'd better get to Riften. Ask around the Ragged Flagon, in the Ratway. It's at least a good starting point.
Skyrim.esm|03BCA1: You'd better get to Riften. Talk to Brynjolf. He's... well-connected. A good starting point at least.
Skyrim.esm|03BCA2: Well... there is guy like that. He's paid good money for nobody to know he's down here, but I guess I can tell you.
Skyrim.esm|03BCA3: You're right, you're right. I just... I was sure it must have been them. If not the Thalmor, who? Or... what?
Skyrim.esm|03BCA7: Esbern? He's alive? I thought the Thalmor must have got him years ago. That crazy old man... Figures the Thalmor would be on his trail, though, if they were trying to find out what's going on with the dragons.
Skyrim.esm|03BCE4: You think I don't know that? If anyone learned of this meeting, my corpse would swing over Solitude for a decade! But what I ask of you... it must be done. There is too much at stake.
Skyrim.esm|03BCE5: No, she certainly wouldn't. And... for whatever reason, she chose to relay Motierre's information to you. I don't know exactly what's going on here, if you're the Listener, or this is some fluke, or what. But what we now have before us...
Skyrim.esm|03BCE6: Yes, um.... So it would seem. Well, I won't waste your time. I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the work I'm offering has more significance than anything your organization has experienced in, well, centuries.
Skyrim.esm|03BCE7: In the year 3E 41, Emperor Pelagius Septim was murdered in the Temple of the One in the Imperial City. Cut down by a Dark Brotherhood assassin. His killing ushered in, shall we say, a necessary change in Imperial policy. There are those now who wish for a similar change. I am sorry, but that's all I'm at liberty to say.
Skyrim.esm|03BCE8: The logistics of such an endeavor will be staggering, I know. There will be several minor assassinations necessary. Leading up to the... main target. As I mentioned, the amulet I gave you is exceptionally valuable, and will certainly cover the costs of all these other unpleasantries. The sealed letter explains everything else, in detail. It should be delivered to your superior. Whomever that may be.
Skyrim.esm|03BCE9: I'm thinking we need that amulet appraised. I want to know where it came from, how much it's worth, and if we can actually get away with selling it. And, there's only one man who can give us what we need - Delvin Mallory. He's a fence, a private operator. Works out of the Ratway, in Riften. Give me the letter. Bring Mallory the amulet. Find out everything you can, and sell it if he's willing. He'll offer a letter of credit - that's fine. Delvin Mallory and the Dark Brotherhood have... history. He can be trusted.
Skyrim.esm|03BCEA: As I said, I want you to kill several people. You'll find the targets, as well as their manners of elimination, quite varied. I'm sure someone of your disposition will probably even find it enjoyable. But you should know that these killings are but a means to an end. For they pave the way to the most important target. The real reason I'm speaking with a cutthroat in the bowels of this detestable crypt. For I seek the assassination of... ...the Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|03BCEB: I performed the Black Sacrament, contacted you people, because I thought you guaranteed discretion. Is this no longer so?
Skyrim.esm|03BCEC: Oh, I know. I know. But I'm so glad you're here. Please, allow me to state my business. Surely your time is as valuable as my own. I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the most important work your organization has had in, well... centuries.
Skyrim.esm|03BCEE: By Sithis, you're not joking. To kill the Emperor of Tamriel... The Dark Brotherhood hasn't done such a thing since the assassination of Pelagius. As a matter of fact, no one has dared assassinate an Emperor of Tamriel since the murder of Uriel Septim, and that was two hundred years ago...
Skyrim.esm|03BCF0: You're back. Good. All right, so? Did you meet this Motierre? What did he want?
Skyrim.esm|03BCF2: Oh, wonderful. You don't know how happy I am to hear you say that. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. It's as if the very stars have finally aligned. But I digress. Here, take these to your, um... superior. Rexus. The items.
Skyrim.esm|03BCF3: Oh, my furtive friend. When Emperor Titus Mede II lies dead, there will be gold... a fortune in gold. But so much more! It is said that the Dark Brotherhood, in recent years, has been in decline. That you lack the power, wealth, and respect of days past. Is it not so? If you do this, if you kill the Emperor... Oh, how the masses will fear and respect you.
Skyrim.esm|03BCF4: Right, then. You prefer to listen, is that it? Well, you must represent the Dark Brotherhood. I certainly wasn't expecting anyone else. So I'll cut right to the chase. I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the most important work your organization has had in, well... centuries.
Skyrim.esm|03BCF5: It's a shocking request, I know. But it is inside the purview of what you Dark Brotherhood types do. Isn't it? If history is to be believed? You must understand. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. Now you're here, as if the very stars have finally aligned. But I digress. Here, let me give you these. They are to be delivered to your, um... superior. Rexus. The items.
Skyrim.esm|03BCF6: You're damn right we'll accept it. If we pull this off, the Dark Brotherhood will know a fear and respect we haven't seen in centuries. You think I'd abandon an opportunity to lead my Family to glory? But this is all so much to take in. I need time to read the letter, and figure out where we go from here. And this amulet. Hmmm...
Skyrim.esm|03BCF7: That is correct. What I ask is no small thing, of course. But you represent the Dark Brotherhood. This is... what you do? No? You must understand. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. Now, it's as if the very stars have finally aligned. But I digress. Here, take these. They need to be delivered to your, um... superior. Rexus. The items.
Skyrim.esm|03BD2B: I need you to deliver some false orders to the Stormcloak commander in Dawnstar. But first we need to get our hands on some rebel orders to make the forgeries.
Skyrim.esm|03BD96: What's that you said? Dragonborn? Then... there really is hope after all? You'd better come inside. Quickly now. Thalmor agents have been seen in the Ratway.
Skyrim.esm|03BD97: What?! No, that's not me. I'm not Esbern. I don't know what you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|03BD98: Delphine? How do you... so you've finally found her, and she led you to me. And here I am, caught like a rat in a trap.
Skyrim.esm|03BD9A: I'm very dangerous! Don't make me come out there and hurt you!
Skyrim.esm|03BD9B: I'm not opening this door, so you might as well leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|03BD9C: If my friends find out you've been bothering me, you'll be very sorry!
Skyrim.esm|03BDBC: So, he says to petition Malacath for relief? Interesting.
Skyrim.esm|03BDBF: The kind that curse you, haunt you in the dead of night. There is nothing you can do, nothing anyone can do. Shagrol, once the leader of this tribe, unhappy with his demise, has dealt with Malacath himself to curse me, curse us all.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC0: Yes, but he offered it as an idea. Perhaps he hopes it will be helpful, but does not wish to risk personal failure. You should do as he says. Go to Malacath's shrine, northwest of here, and ask for his help. See what happens.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC1: Forgive Ugor's harsh words. She is merely doing as she has been told.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC2: Spirits! Ghosts! I am cursed, haunted, and I can do nothing. Can you fix that? Of course you cannot. No one can. It is my lot as Chief of this tribe, and so I suffer quietly.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC3: The ears of the wind? Malacath himself said this to you? But what does it... Ah! Yes! I see! A ritual is necessary! This message is for me. I understand now. I have work to do. You, I shall need your help. I need a Daedra's heart, at once! Quickly, quickly!
Skyrim.esm|03BDC4: His shrine lies northwest of Largashbur. I am unsure that he will listen, but it cannot hurt.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC5: Perhaps. But he spoke of this "curse" to you, which means it has deeply affected him. It may be worth attempting contact with Malacath after all. Go to Malacath's shrine, southeast of Largashbur. See if perhaps he will aid us after all.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC6: We cannot carry on this way! You know we are doomed if we do not do something!
Skyrim.esm|03BDC7: You? An outsider? I'd sooner slit my own throat than accept help from you. To even consider it would be such a sign of weakness that I might as well abandon my tribe now.
Skyrim.esm|03BDC8: Yamarz charged me with keeping outsiders away from Largashbur. You would have me disobey him?
Skyrim.esm|03BDC9: Atub! Meddlesome woman... You would help us? Help me? Tell me, outsider. Can you banish spirits? For that is what I need.
Skyrim.esm|03BDCA: You were charged with keeping us inside the walls. Have faith, Ugor. I only wish the best for our tribe.
Skyrim.esm|03BDCC: Our chief is weakened. He will not rest, will not sleep. He suffers, and so the tribe suffers. Yamarz is proud, and refuses to see that we need outside help. He needs someone to confide in. If it will not be one of us, perhaps it will be you. Go to him.
Skyrim.esm|03BDCD: Halt! You have no business here, outsider. Leave at once.
Skyrim.esm|03BDCE: Ugor, no! This may be the one we need.
Skyrim.esm|03BDCF: We need nothing from outsiders! Yamarz will provide for us.
Skyrim.esm|03BDE8: Speak with Nazir and see if he has any contracts available. I need time to read Motierre's letter.
Skyrim.esm|03C1A6: Yes. For matters of such gravity, we need to know where you stand. Or who you stand with.
Skyrim.esm|03C1A7: Where did you learn of that? Who have you been talking to?
Skyrim.esm|03C1AA: Ah, the prophecy. Be wary of prophecy. Some are meant as a guide. Some as a warning. Are you sure you can tell one from the other?
Skyrim.esm|03C383: Iron-Breaker Mine is three times the worth of that Quicksilver Mine and its fat-head owner.
Skyrim.esm|03C384: My husband Leigelf is dead. I... I never thought I would miss that oaf so much.
Skyrim.esm|03C385: My miners are as dumb as rocks are grey.
Skyrim.esm|03C386: The Mortar and Pestle has cures for every illness, from Ataxia to Witbane.
Skyrim.esm|03C387: A Stamina Potion maybe? Popular with young men.
Skyrim.esm|03C38A: Frost took my husband years ago. He was a good man.
Skyrim.esm|03C38C: I will serve Dawnstar as I always have.
Skyrim.esm|03C38D: Horik and I have lived here ever since I retired. He's a good soldier.
Skyrim.esm|03C390: My duty is to guard Brina Merilis.
Skyrim.esm|03C391: I've been in the Legion all my life. I'm content to serve.
Skyrim.esm|03C393: You should speak to the Jarl if you need something, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|03C394: I'm just one of the miners. I don't want any trouble.
Skyrim.esm|03C397: I'm no one special, just a miner who swings a pickaxe for his drinking money.
Skyrim.esm|03C398: Beitild thinks I drink on the job. Well, what does it matter if I do? Huh?
Skyrim.esm|03C39C: Quicksilver Mine is the best mine in Dawnstar, and you can tell those Iron-Breaker miners I said so.
Skyrim.esm|03C39E: Need a dagger? No? How about a claymore?
Skyrim.esm|03C39F: I trained in Hammerfell during my journeyman years. Redguard smiths are second to none.
Skyrim.esm|03C3A1: I'll forge you a weapon tomorrow. Right now, I'm closed.
Skyrim.esm|03C3A2: I traveled far and wide when I was younger. Glad to have settled down finally.
Skyrim.esm|03C3A3: I help my husband Rustleif run the forge. He's a marvel with steel.
Skyrim.esm|03C3A4: I married Rustleif when he was in Hammerfell, apprenticing under my father.
Skyrim.esm|03C3A6: We discovered recently that I am with child. I'll need to stop working next season.
Skyrim.esm|03C3AB: I work in the mine, just like everyone else.
Skyrim.esm|03C3AC: Used to be twice as many miners working here before all this trouble started.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B0: I mine. Work the smelter. Ain't much else to tell.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B1: Been mining all my life. Quicksilver is as much my home as anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B4: The nightmares in Dawnstar have lifted, and I hear you helped that happen. You have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B5: The Pale will throw those Imperial backbiters up against the wall when they come for us.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B7: I'm Skald's housecarl and head of his guard in Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B8: Skald is very... vocal about his hatred of the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|03C3B9: I served in the Great War against the Thalmor. I remember how bravely a Legionnaire can fight.
Skyrim.esm|03C3BA: Just a servant of Skald the Elder. Not worthy of time or consideration.
Skyrim.esm|03C3BB: My family has served Skald Felgeif's family for generations.
Skyrim.esm|03C3BC: There's no honor in being a Jarl's servant. No Sovngarde waits for dust cleaners.
Skyrim.esm|03C3BD: I'm here to lend my magic to the people of Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|03C3BE: I served in the Great War as a Legion battlemage. I've no desire to go back.
Skyrim.esm|03C3BF: I'll work my spells and potions for whoever is willing to leave Dawnstar in peace.
Skyrim.esm|03C3C2: The Windpeak Inn features the finest bard in all of Dawnstar. Me.
Skyrim.esm|03C3C3: I'm sorry. My mind was just somewhere else. Did you need a room? Drink?
Skyrim.esm|03C3C4: Oh, do you need a room?
Skyrim.esm|03C3C5: My daughter Karita sings to the guests when she can. She has her mother's voice.
Skyrim.esm|03C3C7: Dawnstar is the last port of call before Solitude. Damn rebels aren't taking it.
Skyrim.esm|03C3C8: We won't give up this port to the Imperials without a fight, don't you worry.
Skyrim.esm|03C3C9: Dangerous post, Dawnstar. If the bandits don't get you, the animals will.
Skyrim.esm|03C48E: I'm aspiring to earn that title, yes. However, the road ahead is paved with loose cobbles and deadly pitfalls. Master Elgrim says I'm a natural; that I have a unique talent. But I fear his approval masks his intolerance for my mistakes. My errant formulas have cost Master Elgrim a fortune in ingredients; some of which are almost irreplaceable.
Skyrim.esm|03C48F: Well, that's just about the best news I've heard all week. I was going to give this to whomever killed her for me, but I suppose you've earned it by bringing me this information. This key opens one of my drop spots in the basement of the meadery. Take anything inside you like.
Skyrim.esm|03C492: Yes, Master Elgrim will be pleased I've restored his stock of these rare ingredients. I feel I owe a bit more than some meaningless coin for all your diligence. Here, this key unlocks my supply chest at Elgrim's Elixirs. Feel free to take what you need from time to time. I'll be refilling it once every few days or so.
Skyrim.esm|03C493: Nirnroot is the easiest. It grows only by the water and makes a unique chiming noise. The only drawback is it won't regrow after harvesting. Nightshade is native to the pine forest and marshy tundra regions of Skyrim and has a distinctive starburst-like violet flower. Last we have deathbell which only grows in marshy tundra terrain. Its inverted bluish-purple flower bunches are unmistakable.
Skyrim.esm|03C494: Mother thought I should be taught a lesson for airing the family's dirty laundry in public.
Skyrim.esm|03C4BF: She was a young woman... buxom, with long flowing black hair. She used to sing the most lovely songs to me when we were courting. I've never heard a voice so beautiful. All that talent will go to waste when I'm finished with her.
Skyrim.esm|03C4C0: It's exhilarating to observe the effects of my potions on the body. Watching the heart stop... the eyes go blind. We're made up of thousands of parts with thousands of different functions all working in tandem to keep us alive. Yet if only a single part of our imperfect machine fails, life fails. It makes one realize how fragile... how flawed we are. You ask why I'm so fascinated? The irony... the irony that the same world that gave us life provides us the means to die.
Skyrim.esm|03C4C1: To restore Master Elgrim's supply I would need 20 deathbell, 20 nightshade and 20 nirnroot. Am I sensing that you may be interested in gathering these rare ingredients for me?
Skyrim.esm|03C4C5: I'm sorry, I don't know what you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|03C4C7: That means more time for my experiments and less time in the field. Much appreciated.
Skyrim.esm|03C4C9: That's the spirit. Find her and I promise you'll be well compensated.
Skyrim.esm|03C4CB: You've done me a great service, my friend. Allow me to compensate you for the information. This key opens one of my stashes in the basement of the meadery. Help yourself to any of its contents.
Skyrim.esm|03C4D0: Please, I beg you, don't tell Sibbi where I am. He'll kill me! Sibbi's been spreading lies about me, you must hear me out.
Skyrim.esm|03C4D2: Cut out the middleman, eh? Shrewd, very shrewd. You sure you don't have some Black-Briar blood in you?
Skyrim.esm|03C4DD: You idiot. You came all the way down here for that?
Skyrim.esm|03C4DE: When I find that bitch, I'm going to wring her little neck.
Skyrim.esm|03C4DF: Visit me anytime. I'm not going anywhere soon.
Skyrim.esm|03C4E2: Don't forget to check your supply chest from time to time.
Skyrim.esm|03C4EA: My experiments can't be completed until you've brought me those ingredients. Do hurry.
Skyrim.esm|03C4EC: Now was it two parts giant lichen or three? Hm...
Skyrim.esm|03C4ED: Well, well. Aren't you a sight for sore eyes. I've always got time for lovely ladies.
Skyrim.esm|03C4F2: Hm? Sorry, just thinking about my experiments.
Skyrim.esm|03C574: Dov wahlaan fah rel. We were made to dominate. The will to power is in our blood. You feel it in yourself, do you not? I can be trusted. I know this. But they do not. Onikaan ni ov dovah. It is always wise to mistrust a dovah. I have overcome my nature only through meditation and long study of the Way of the Voice. No day goes by where I am not tempted to return to my inborn nature. Zin krif horvut se suleyk. What is better - to be born good, or to overcome your evil nature through great effort?
Skyrim.esm|03C575: The Blades are wise not to trust me. Onikaan ni ov. I would not trust another dovah.
Skyrim.esm|03C585: Steady now. The Legion has faced down worse than a few dusty old bonewalkers. We're not leaving here until we get what we came for.
Skyrim.esm|03C586: Don't give them a chance to warn the others!
Skyrim.esm|03C587: Be careful men. There's bound to be more up ahead.
Skyrim.esm|03C588: Is this what killed that Stormcloak over there? Can't be. It looks like it's been dead for a hundred years.
Skyrim.esm|03C589: What in the nine holds is that?
Skyrim.esm|03C6F8: Of course you'll get a cut. We take care of our own. Now if you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Get going.
Skyrim.esm|03C6F9: If you were expecting a palace, then maybe you're not cut out for this line of work. Our methods involve secrecy and discretion. Now... if you're done bellyaching like a child, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
Skyrim.esm|03C6FA: If I have to hold your hand through everything, maybe you aren't cut out for this kind of work. It's under your feet, lass. Just follow the smell.
Skyrim.esm|03C6FB: No risk, no reward. That's the way it goes, so you better get used to it. Now... if you're done bellyaching like a child, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
Skyrim.esm|03C6FC: Judging from how well you handled those shopkeepers, I'd say you've done more than simply prove yourself. We need people like you in our outfit.
Skyrim.esm|03C700: That's the spirit! Larceny's in your blood... the telltale sign of a practiced thief. I think you'll do more than just fit in around here.
Skyrim.esm|03C79C: If you're looking for a challenge, you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|03C79D: A few drinks, a few laughs and a contest. What could be better?
Skyrim.esm|03C79F: Maybe you just aren't up to the challenge...
Skyrim.esm|03CCEB: You made it! We need to get inside. Follow me!
Skyrim.esm|03CCEC: Come on! We need to get inside before that dragon comes back!
Skyrim.esm|03CD26: The path to Paarthurnax lies through this gate. I will show you how to open the way.
Skyrim.esm|03CD7D: It's good to hear you say that. I wish my wife was as simple as you.
Skyrim.esm|03CD80: You'll shut your mouth and get back to work, or you won't be a miner or a soldier by the day's end. Hear me?
Skyrim.esm|03CD81: I'm just worried about raising a child in the middle of this war of yours.
Skyrim.esm|03CD82: With you by my side, is that right Horik?
Skyrim.esm|03CD84: What about your family? There must have been someone waiting for you back at the farm. Wondering if you were ever coming home?
Skyrim.esm|03CD87: If you don't mind me saying, Leigelf. You're working too hard. Go home.
Skyrim.esm|03CD88: What is this feud you have with your wife? What started this?
Skyrim.esm|03CD89: You don't want to talk about it, or you don't know?
Skyrim.esm|03CD8A: Your people's war, I mean. The Nords. What kind of life would we be giving our little one if we stayed here?
Skyrim.esm|03CD8B: So much for old soldiers settling down to a life of luxury, eh?
Skyrim.esm|03CD8E: With respect, Jarl. You can't ask everyone to throw themselves against trained Legionnaires. Let's leave the fighting to the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|03CD8F: You never talk much about yourself, do you Horik? I swear, these past 30 years you've been at my side, and I think I barely know you.
Skyrim.esm|03CD94: You weren't. There's just not much to it.
Skyrim.esm|03CD96: I see. You're a good soldier, Horik. I'm sorry for intruding.
Skyrim.esm|03CD97: If we win. This war is going to drag on for years. I can feel it. We need to prepare for a long struggle ahead.
Skyrim.esm|03CD9B: We're at war with Leigelf? Isn't he your husband?
Skyrim.esm|03CD9C: Drafted into a war, how could my life get worse?
Skyrim.esm|03CD9D: Hammerfell? I'm not against the idea, but why?
Skyrim.esm|03CD9F: Where else would I go? Dawnstar is my home and working here is my job.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA0: Work hard, now! The pride of Iron-Breaker Mine is on the line.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA1: No. If we must fight, we will, and you will lead us to victory. As you always have.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA2: Pride of the what? This isn't the Legion, boss. We're miners, not soldiers.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA4: What's to say? I was born on a farm, joined the Legion when I came of age, and I've served you ever since.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA5: I'll, uh, just take that as a compliment.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA6: War of mine? It's not my war, Seren!
Skyrim.esm|03CDA8: Of course. I would have it no other way.
Skyrim.esm|03CDA9: Never. Not until we turn this mine around, drive Beitild out of business, and force her to apologize to me for everything she's done!
Skyrim.esm|03CDAA: I don't want to talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|03CDAB: I don't want to talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|03CDAC: Rustleif, I've been wondering. Maybe after the baby is born, we should move to Hammerfell.
Skyrim.esm|03CDB0: I... well, let me think about it.
Skyrim.esm|03CDB1: No. Can't say there was. My parents died when I was young. You and the Legion have been my only family since.
Skyrim.esm|03CDB4: You're right. Why dilute the glory with too many souls fighting over it? We'll drive out the Empire and be celebrated as heroes when we win.
Skyrim.esm|03CDB6: I'm fine, Horik. I just... are you worried about this war?
Skyrim.esm|03CDB7: Not hot enough. Does your Skyrim ever get warm, husband?
Skyrim.esm|03CDB8: Thank you for sticking by me.
Skyrim.esm|03CDB9: We are poised for glory, Jod. The Empire will never take Dawnstar while every last man, woman, and child here draws breathe!
Skyrim.esm|03CDBB: Enough of your dire predictions! We will talk about this another time.
Skyrim.esm|03CDBC: Are you all right? You seem tired.
Skyrim.esm|03CDBE: No he isn't! He's the enemy, and we are going to crush him.
Skyrim.esm|03CF3B: So, feeling loose? How about you run a job for me.
Skyrim.esm|03CF40: I'm busy. Don't you have something to do?
Skyrim.esm|03CF41: I'm not here to hold your hand. Figure this one out for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|03CF42: You need advice? Talk to Brynjolf... I got better things to do.
Skyrim.esm|03D151: I'd had Eorlund forge this for Thorald. It was to be a present for his return. I suppose he can't have it now. Why don't you take it? We can always make another for the day when this war ends and Thorald can come home.
Skyrim.esm|03D153: With Eorlund gone, I'm afraid I can't offer you much in the way of thanks. But please, take this. It's the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|03D154: Is he? You've saved him?! I must see him at once!
Skyrim.esm|03D155: What? After all this, I can't even see him? How... How do I know you're telling me the truth, and not just what I want to hear?
Skyrim.esm|03D156: "...for it bears aloft next summer's seeds." That's my boy. So it's true, then. For now, it's enough to know that he's alive. I can find peace in that. Thank you, dear friend. You've given me back my son.
Skyrim.esm|03D1B2: Thank you, Madena. I appreciate your assistance.
Skyrim.esm|03D1B3: Brings back memories. Do you have any orders for me, Legate?
Skyrim.esm|03D1B5: Glory is meaningless if they have no home to return to. I urge you to lower our commitments to the front until I'm sure we're safe.
Skyrim.esm|03D1B6: Uh, yes sir. That's me. The oafish servant.
Skyrim.esm|03D1B7: Exactly, Bulfrek. You always know your place.
Skyrim.esm|03D1B8: Considering how the Great War ended, I think we need to be cautious about what we rely on.
Skyrim.esm|03D1B9: Skald, if you don't mind sir, I have a request.
Skyrim.esm|03D1BA: Skald, I've been meaning to ask you for a leave of absence. I wish to return to High Rock until this war is over.
Skyrim.esm|03D1BC: You keep pledging more and more of our guard to the frontlines. The way this is going, we won't be able to defend our own borders.
Skyrim.esm|03D1BD: Maybe you're right. I guess the battles I won yesterday won't win today's wars.
Skyrim.esm|03D1BE: So you say. We'll see if that's true when the Stormcloaks make their move.
Skyrim.esm|03D1C0: I, well, no of course not. I would be glad to help.
Skyrim.esm|03D1C1: I won't have you rob our soldiers of their chance to drive out the Empire. I'm done discussing this, Jod.
Skyrim.esm|03D1C2: I'm sorry. I just don't feel up to entertaining anyone just now.
Skyrim.esm|03D1C5: Madena, I was wondering if you could help me with something. Your magic would be invaluable.
Skyrim.esm|03D1C6: Why would they be looking at me? Are they upset about the service?
Skyrim.esm|03D1C7: I told you before, Skald, I have no interest in taking sides in this war. I've no desire to be killed.
Skyrim.esm|03D1C9: Madena, can we count on your spells to assist us in this war? The Empire is bound to bring their battlemages against us.
Skyrim.esm|03D1CA: Just like old times, huh Horik? You and me fighting in a war over some forsaken scrap of land.
Skyrim.esm|03D1CB: I know old men sometimes have pains in their legs when they rise from bed. I know a few spells that could help.
Skyrim.esm|03D1CC: What good is a court mage if she refuses to fight? Where's your sense of duty?
Skyrim.esm|03D1CD: You wish to leave now? In Skyrim's moment of triumph, just before we cast off the yoke of Imperial rule?
Skyrim.esm|03D1CE: Well, it's a winding road up the mountain just ahead. You'll know you're in the right place once you spot the old watchtower. Once you get to the tower, head north. Bleak Falls Barrow should be just around the corner further up.
Skyrim.esm|03D1D1: Actually, I was just wondering if you could make sure people are tended to if they get hurt. I'm sure you wouldn't object to that.
Skyrim.esm|03D1D2: It'll do you good! Maybe you could visit Irgnir or Fruki on your way out? I've seen them looking at you when they come in for drinks....
Skyrim.esm|03D1D3: Bulfrek, I have an order of swords and armor from the local blacksmith coming here. I need every piece polished to a fine shine.
Skyrim.esm|03D1D4: I'll tell you the same thing I told Skald. I'm here to help the people of Dawnstar, not play politics. Point your suspicions of treachery elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|03D1D5: Why would I do that? There's work to be done here.
Skyrim.esm|03D1D7: Safety? Don't I have you to handle the town's safety? What is this about, Jod?
Skyrim.esm|03D1D8: You, have a request of me, Bulfrek? This should be interesting.
Skyrim.esm|03D1D9: I've seen you walking with a bit of pain sometimes, Horik.
Skyrim.esm|03D1DA: If this is about battle magic, forget it. I've told every Jarl I've served under that I won't....
Skyrim.esm|03D1DC: Yes, Legate. No one is getting through us.
Skyrim.esm|03D1DD: I don't think that's needed, Horik. It's the same strategy we used for every major defense during the Great War. It'll serve just as well now.
Skyrim.esm|03D1DE: I'm sorry, Legate. I didn't want to imply anything about your ability to lead. You're a hero to the Empire, and I....
Skyrim.esm|03D1DF: Yes, my Jarl. We could hardly waste anyone else's precious time on such a tedious task.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E1: I want you to know I don't trust you, Madena. And I'll be watching you.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E2: Nonsense. The best place for our men is driving the Imperials back. I will ensure that men from Dawnstar get every opportunity for glory.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E3: Well, I was thinking sir, with the war going on and all. Maybe, I could enlist? Fight the Imperials?
Skyrim.esm|03D1E4: Yes, that's exactly what I'm asking. I'm not a Nord, I'm a Breton, and I have no wish to fight this war.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E5: Shor's bones, I hope you're right.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E6: We should go over our strategy again in case the Stormcloaks attack.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E8: When I came here, my only duties were to cure crop diseases and occasionally light a bonfire on holidays. I didn't sign up to kill.
Skyrim.esm|03D1E9: I was wondering, father. Maybe you should take a day off and head into town. I could watch the inn while you're away.
Skyrim.esm|03D1EB: No, Karita. I don't think I could.
Skyrim.esm|03D1EC: Still suspicious of me. Well, don't say I never offered.
Skyrim.esm|03D1EF: This is the bridge out of town. The path up the mountain to the northwest leads to Bleak Falls Barrow. I guess I should get back to my brother. He'll throw a fit if I take too long. Such a child....
Skyrim.esm|03D1F2: I don't think so, Madena. There will be no deserters in this hold, and that's final.
Skyrim.esm|03D1F3: Father, do you want to talk? About mother?
Skyrim.esm|03D1F6: I'm sure she would want you to be happy, father. Entertaining the guests and drinking and making your lewd jokes like before.
Skyrim.esm|03D1F7: My Jarl, I have concerns about the safety of Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|03D1F8: I said I'm fine, Madena. I won't have some court mage fixing me up like some magical experiment.
Skyrim.esm|03D1F9: Keep the guard alert. I want to make sure no townspeople get hurt if there's trouble.
Skyrim.esm|03D1FA: Fine, but this isn't over Madena. We'll talk again.
Skyrim.esm|03D1FD: A servant? Enlisting in the Stormcloaks? Ah, Bulfrek, that's why I like having you about. You're always good for a laugh.
Skyrim.esm|03D1FE: Oh, don't be such an old stick, Horik. I was joking. I can do that, you know. Redo the battle plans. I'll look over them later.
Skyrim.esm|03D1FF: Well, at the first sign of battle I'll be comfortably sitting in my room, waiting for it to end, so don't you worry.
Skyrim.esm|03D200: You have the claw. Oh, you have to tell Lucan at once. Hurry!
Skyrim.esm|03D201: Hurry up and tell Lucan you have the claw. He'll be so excited!
Skyrim.esm|03D233: Please, you can't leave me in here! If you let me out I can show you where they hide their best treasure.
Skyrim.esm|03D234: Please! Help me get out of this cage. I don't know why those things locked me up and I don't want to wait around to find out.
Skyrim.esm|03D237: One of those things has the key to this cage. If you get me out of here I'll help you slaughter the rest of those crows.
Skyrim.esm|03D23A: Hey! Over here! Get me out of here!
Skyrim.esm|03D23B: Hey you! Let me out! I can help!
Skyrim.esm|03D28D: Yours was not an easy transformation. But you're still alive, so congratulations. We even have a celebration planned for you.
Skyrim.esm|03D28E: The bastards... somehow they managed to kill Skjor.
Skyrim.esm|03D29B: Ah, you're from the College? I've been waiting for you. Please make sure this arrives in a timely manner. And don't scratch it up.
Skyrim.esm|03D29C: Oh, good. I've been waiting for you. Here you go, it's all ready. Now, Sergius knows what I want. I'm hoping he can get it done quickly.
Skyrim.esm|03D4C5: I'm glad they didn't find my lucky dagger. It's time to gut these rotten birds. Lead the way!
Skyrim.esm|03D4C6: Actually... on second thought, I'll just kill you!
Skyrim.esm|03D4C7: Now that we've got that out of the way, I'll take everything you have... or your life.
Skyrim.esm|03D4CC: Not now! It's time to get back at these crows.
Skyrim.esm|03D4CD: No! Don't leave me here! I beg you to let me out!
Skyrim.esm|03D4CE: Please! I'll pay you. I'll give you whatever you want. Just don't leave me here!
Skyrim.esm|03D4CF: Hurry! Get me out of here!
Skyrim.esm|03D4D0: Use the key to open the door! We have to be quick!
Skyrim.esm|03D6CB: Whatever you're selling, I'm not buying.
Skyrim.esm|03D6D1: Always a pleasure, boss. What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|03D6D4: So, what can I do for ya today?
Skyrim.esm|03D6D5: Still have quite a good bit of jobs available if you're looking for some extra coin.
Skyrim.esm|03D6D6: Need some gold? I may have some work for you.
Skyrim.esm|03D6D8: Got lots of jobs available. Interested?
Skyrim.esm|03D6D9: Talk to me again if you prove yourself to Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|03D6DA: Come back later and I may have some work for you.
Skyrim.esm|03D6DB: I think Brynjolf can give you all the information you need.
Skyrim.esm|03D6DF: Can I interest you in anything? Some fine armor perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|03D6E0: Be glad to repair any damage to your Guild armor.
Skyrim.esm|03D6E4: So, name your poison! Ha ha ha. Oh, I never get tired of that one!
Skyrim.esm|03D6E5: Need some sort of toxin for a special occasion? That's where I come in.
Skyrim.esm|03D6E6: I sell all sorts of poisons for almost every occasion.
Skyrim.esm|03D6EA: Looking for the best smith in Skyrim? You found it.
Skyrim.esm|03D6EB: Need your weapon repaired? Or could I interest you in a new one?
Skyrim.esm|03D6EC: They don't call me the Ember-Master for nothing. What do you need?
Skyrim.esm|03D6F3: Skilled, lethal and good-looking. Yep, it's a curse.
Skyrim.esm|03D6F4: If you're in the market for the finest bows money can buy, you're talking to the right person.
Skyrim.esm|03D6F5: I've got all sorts of weapons for the discerning marksman. What can I get you?
Skyrim.esm|03D6F7: I've been a part of this Guild for as long as Mercer has been Guild Master.
Skyrim.esm|03D6F8: I might not be as big as some of my fellow Nords, but in a fight they can barely lay a hand on me.
Skyrim.esm|03D6F9: My specialty is pickpocketing. All I need is a rich mark, a moon-lit sky and I'm in my element.
Skyrim.esm|03D6FA: I wouldn't mind teaching the ways of the pickpocket... if the gold was right.
Skyrim.esm|03D6FB: I miss the glory days of this place. We had more coin stacked in the vaults than we even knew what to do with.
Skyrim.esm|03D6FD: Ugh. The wine in Skyrim tastes like urine compared to the fine vintages we had in Valenwood.
Skyrim.esm|03D6FE: I don't miss home one bit. I'm glad to leave that life behind me.
Skyrim.esm|03D6FF: I'd much rather earn a living liberating others of their coin this way than spending my days as a carpenter like my father.
Skyrim.esm|03D700: It's not hard to fit in and make friends when you have the coin to make it happen.
Skyrim.esm|03D701: I don't bother with silly pickpocketing jobs when I can make far more at the gambling table.
Skyrim.esm|03D705: Our Guild Master graces me with a visit. What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|03D706: Back with good news I trust.
Skyrim.esm|03D708: Out with it. Time is money.
Skyrim.esm|03D709: Need a few spare septims? I got the cure right here.
Skyrim.esm|03D70A: I have work if you've got the backbone.
Skyrim.esm|03D70B: Ready for some work or you just going to wander around all day?
Skyrim.esm|03D70C: I have no business with you.
Skyrim.esm|03DA83: This place is getting worse and worse.
Skyrim.esm|03DA84: Delvin, please tell me you have good news.
Skyrim.esm|03DA85: You tell this sorry bunch of thieves that they need to put down their flagons and get to work if they want to keep their status!
Skyrim.esm|03DA86: Yeah, I know what you mean. If things don't start getting better soon, I may look for work elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|03DA87: You stupid cow. You don't know what you're missing.
Skyrim.esm|03DA88: Oh, really? Well then... how can I make that happen?
Skyrim.esm|03DA89: Thank you, Vex. You've always been the most reliable one in the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|03DA8D: So, what do you think, my dear? Could a beautiful woman like you ever see yourself with an old codger like me?
Skyrim.esm|03DA8E: Well, if you don't want to hear it, that's your loss.
Skyrim.esm|03DA8F: She said that she barely had enough coin to cover it and I should come back later. Can you imagine that?
Skyrim.esm|03DA90: All right. I have everything you asked for all ready to go.
Skyrim.esm|03DA91: You seem to be a bit closer to Brynjolf than the rest of us. Is he giving you any idea why this place seems to be falling apart?
Skyrim.esm|03DA92: So is it true, bandits get... you know, real friendly with the wildlife?
Skyrim.esm|03DA93: I've picked up some new clients and did a few jobs on my own, but I'm not sure if that will cover our expenses.
Skyrim.esm|03DA94: No, but I know that you're going to turn up missing if you keep up this kind of talk.
Skyrim.esm|03DA95: So, Thrynn, you were a bandit, huh?
Skyrim.esm|03DA96: What about Mercer? Have you asked him?
Skyrim.esm|03DA97: I think I know, but go ahead.
Skyrim.esm|03DA98: Your idea to start a brothel, right? Do you want me to tell you exactly what I think?
Skyrim.esm|03DA99: I trust you've applied the appropriate pressure in order to change their mind?
Skyrim.esm|03DA9A: Yes, I'm afraid you're right. Such a shame...
Skyrim.esm|03DA9B: Do we have anyone reliable to send out there? I don't want to look the fool again in front of her, Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|03DA9C: Remind me why we even bother to allow him to be a part of the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|03DA9E: Well, what the hell is Mercer going to do about this mess? Have you even talked to him?
Skyrim.esm|03DA9F: Two reasons. He's rich and because we need every sword we can get right now.
Skyrim.esm|03DAA0: There's no need to be concerned. We've been through more difficult times than this.
Skyrim.esm|03DAA1: That's impossible. I distinctly heard that lout in the tavern say it was a full shipment of furs. They should be worth a fortune.
Skyrim.esm|03DAA2: I'll speak to the Jarl and see what I can do. Just keep them at bay a little longer.
Skyrim.esm|03DAA3: If I told you that, I'd be lying. We haven't had a bite in weeks. Our clients are starting to get angry.
Skyrim.esm|03DAA4: So, the ending of my tale I began earlier...
Skyrim.esm|03DAA5: So, Sapphire. Can I do anything for you, or more to the point... to you?
Skyrim.esm|03DAA6: I tried to fence some goods to Tonilia, and you know what she told me?
Skyrim.esm|03DAA7: Hold a moment, Rune. What did you think about my idea?
Skyrim.esm|03DAA8: If he does, he isn't telling me a thing. I've asked, but all I get from him are more questions.
Skyrim.esm|03DAAA: No, and I'm not about to either. I value my life you know... you may want to remember that for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|03DAAB: Brynjolf, something stinks around here and it's more than just the Ratway. We all know it. The question is, what's Mercer going to do about it?
Skyrim.esm|03DAAC: I think if Brynjolf catches wind of it, he'll boot you right out of here... so drop it already!
Skyrim.esm|03DAAD: I don't understand it either. They were a sure thing, I swear.
Skyrim.esm|03DAAF: If I wanted to hear a tall tale, I'll read a children's storybook. Get some better stories, Vipir.
Skyrim.esm|03DAB1: Mercer is being Mercer. If he has a plan to get us out of this rut, then he isn't sharing it with me.
Skyrim.esm|03DAB2: What in the name of Oblivion is going on in this place?
Skyrim.esm|03DAB3: Is this your tale of single-handedly fighting off a pack of trolls or my favorite... where you bedded four women on the same night?
Skyrim.esm|03DAB4: Hey, I still have a contact in Valenwood that may have some work for us. You're welcome to join me.
Skyrim.esm|03DAB5: Vipir "the Fleet." The only man who's foolish enough to name himself after his bedroom prowess.
Skyrim.esm|03DAB7: I hope this deal goes through. I'm putting quite a bit of coin on the line... and the last two you set up fell through.
Skyrim.esm|03DAB8: I have. I think we may have to turn this one over to the Dark Brotherhood.
Skyrim.esm|03DAB9: Maven has been nagging us about her request. Any thoughts?
Skyrim.esm|03DABA: You just better hope this one isn't another waste of my coin.
Skyrim.esm|03DABB: Yes, I know. That last job was my fault. I obviously thought Niruin was ready, but I was wrong.
