Skyrim.esm|03DABC: You idiot. He said "firs" not "furs." It was a damn logging caravan!
Skyrim.esm|03DABD: Sorry Mercer, they no longer wish to be involved in our operation.
Skyrim.esm|03DABE: Delvin, that last shipment we heisted on the Cyrodiil frontier was worthless!
Skyrim.esm|03DABF: Well, I see your wounds have healed but your pride still needs some time.
Skyrim.esm|03DAC1: Oh my... umm, you won't let Mercer hear about this will you? Ahem.
Skyrim.esm|03DAC2: When you're in your grave and I'm standing over it. Now get out of my way!
Skyrim.esm|03DAC3: Pretty angry actually. I can't believe I botched the Goldenglow job. That should have been like stealing a sweetroll from a baby.
Skyrim.esm|03DAC4: Brynjolf, have you had a chance to speak to our contact in Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|03DAC5: I think we need to prepare for the worst. The coffers are dwindling and I can barely keep our contacts happy.
Skyrim.esm|03DAC6: Normally I'd agree, but we've already lost so many contacts across Skyrim. If we lose the ones in Riften, we may as well close up shop.
Skyrim.esm|03DD27: Thank you so much for taking care of those thieves. The Riverwood Trader is back to the way it used to be!
Skyrim.esm|03DD28: It means so much to us to have the claw back where it belongs. Thank you!
Skyrim.esm|03DD2A: I'll add it to the list of things I need to take care of. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|03DD2B: There are more books to be recovered and secured here in the Arcanaeum.
Skyrim.esm|03DD2C: There's more enchanting to be done. You can acquire whatever it is and bring it back here, and I'll take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|03DD2D: It's about time someone arrived. Here, make sure this is handled properly.
Skyrim.esm|03DD2E: Oh yes, that. Here you go. I hope this can be ready soon.
Skyrim.esm|03DD2F: Yes, yes. Let's keep our voices down, all right? Just make sure this gets there.
Skyrim.esm|03DF64: I've got a letter and a lot of gold. Something about it being your inheritance? Oh, and... sorry for your loss.
Skyrim.esm|03E2DB: The Forsworn are as wild as wolves. Practice those old magics, so some folk say.
Skyrim.esm|03E2E3: There is no greater madness than those who seek to become vampires. That's a dark bargain if there ever was one.
Skyrim.esm|03E301: I'd rather fight bandits than draugr. At least bandits know when to stay dead.
Skyrim.esm|03E311: You can't argue with these mages. They'll attack you on sight.
Skyrim.esm|03E31B: It probably took some Dwemer craftsman a year to build one of those things.
Skyrim.esm|03E31E: There may be no enemy more foul than a walking corpse.
Skyrim.esm|03E321: These ruins and their mechanical inhabitants are all that remain of the Dwemer.
Skyrim.esm|03E322: It's said that the Forsworn make dark pacts with the Hagravens, and that is why they can use magic.
Skyrim.esm|03E323: There is no adversary more deadly than a vampire. They are as cruel as they are cunning.
Skyrim.esm|03E324: Bandits are desperate men. They have nothing to lose, and they fight like it.
Skyrim.esm|03E325: What some call devotion to the dark arts, I call foolishness.
Skyrim.esm|03E327: I would find the Dwemers' mechanical guardians fascinating if they didn't try to kill everything on sight.
Skyrim.esm|03E4B6: Keep digging! Ugh... If Lu'ah Al-Skaven can raise an army of Draugr, then why do I have to babysit them?
Skyrim.esm|03E4B7: They burned his body before I could raise him... it should have been returned to me...
Skyrim.esm|03EA5C: No, I haven't seen him since the dragon showed up.
Skyrim.esm|03EA79: I hate those damn things. Too many eyes, you know?
Skyrim.esm|03EA81: No, I haven't seen him since the dragon showed up.
Skyrim.esm|03EAC5: You fighter types are always underfoot. You want me to clean? Then scat!
Skyrim.esm|03EBDB: I'll be sure to tell her.
Skyrim.esm|03EBDC: Just promise me when you get some more in, I'll have the first crack at them.
Skyrim.esm|03EBDD: I doubt Ulfric would let Thalmor into Windhelm without a fight. But please, do continue. How would you help us?
Skyrim.esm|03EBDE: Oh, I see. Yes, yes of course I'll take a look. Just bring them by whenever it's convenient for you.
Skyrim.esm|03EBDF: We've got another dozen ripening on the vine. They should be ready in a few days.
Skyrim.esm|03EBE0: I've been meaning to speak to you, Niranye. Rumor is, you've got ties to the Thieves' Guild.
Skyrim.esm|03EBE1: Well, I've heard awful stories about these Thalmor, and how they're rounding up everyone who still worships Talos. The interrogations are quite merciless, from what I've heard. I fear for you and your husband, should the Thamlor come here. But perhaps there's a way I can help.
Skyrim.esm|03EBE2: I have some things that belonged to my daughter, Friga. She... she won't be needing them any more.
Skyrim.esm|03EBE3: Well enough, I suppose. Nilsine bought up most of the tomatoes, and was asking if we could get more.
Skyrim.esm|03EBE4: You've done more than enough already. You've been a good friend in a time of need, and we won't forget that. I'm sorry I lost myself for a moment there. I just need some time to get over the loss.
Skyrim.esm|03EBE5: You know, I'm not sure. My husband's been tending to the vines, so I'll have to ask him.
Skyrim.esm|03EBFE: Is that so? And what were you thinking, exactly?
Skyrim.esm|03EC3A: Yes, that's right. Whether or not I'm interested depends on what you've got, of course.
Skyrim.esm|03EC9A: Oh, I see. Well, I'm sorry to have troubled you about it. Just... know that your efforts are appreciated.
Skyrim.esm|03EC9B: I... I'm so sorry, Torbjorn. You must be suffering terribly. I wish there was some way I could help.
Skyrim.esm|03EC9D: Is that new batch of tomatoes ready for sale yet, Hillevi?
Skyrim.esm|03EC9F: I understand you purchase used goods, as well as sell them?
Skyrim.esm|03ECA3: I'm not looking for special treatment, Aval. All I did was talk to Ulfric. For all I know, he's already forgotten the conversation. I wouldn't get your hopes up.
Skyrim.esm|03ECA4: You're such a wet blanket, captain. Can't a girl have a little fun from time to time?
Skyrim.esm|03ECA5: Hello, Jora. I'm glad to see you. In fact, I was just thinking about you and your husband.
Skyrim.esm|03ECA6: But I insist. and with good reason. Malthyr told me that you've been speaking to Ulfric about our situation. He said you're going to help us. So few Nords have ever offered to help us, a discount is the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|03ECAA: My little Friga loved to cook, but she was terrible at it. Then one day, out of nowhere, she made the most amazing leek soup. She watched while we ate it, and when she saw how much we loved it, she was... so proud...
Skyrim.esm|03ED2F: By the gods! What are you doing here?
Skyrim.esm|03ED46: If that business proposition includes smuggling, forget it. The sailors I know are plenty good at finding trouble without any help from you.
Skyrim.esm|03ED47: I'll be sure to stress your urgency. It seems like every farmer in Skyrim is short on supplies these days.
Skyrim.esm|03ED48: The eyes of the law are everywhere these days. The guards are looking for a killer, but who knows what else they might turn up? Good day.
Skyrim.esm|03ED4B: That's not necessary. I'll pay the same price as anyone else.
Skyrim.esm|03ED4C: It was my pleasure, Torbjorn. I enjoy cooking, and after what you and Tova have been through, it was the least I could do.
Skyrim.esm|03ED4D: Tell him I'll pay double the normal price. We've been getting by with one broken pitchfork, but it's falling apart.
Skyrim.esm|03ED4F: Not that I'd know anything about it, of course, but it seems to me that the last thing a thieves' guild would want is to draw attention to itself.
Skyrim.esm|03ED50: This is Stormcloak territory. If you're one of those craven Imperials, don't expect a warm welcome.
Skyrim.esm|03ED51: If you're here to join the Stormcloaks, the palace is inside.
Skyrim.esm|03ED52: Loyal Nords are always welcome here. Anyone else better watch their step.
Skyrim.esm|03ED8A: Hrm. You go find your own wreck. This one's mine.
Skyrim.esm|03ED8D: If you're an Imperial spy, better turn around right now.
Skyrim.esm|03ED96: Friend Brunwulf, how good to see you! If you see anything you like, just let me know and I'll glad offer you a discount.
Skyrim.esm|03EDDF: I wanted to thank you again for the mince pie you sent over. It was delicious.
Skyrim.esm|03EDE4: And how may Ma'randru-jo assist you, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|03EDED: I was wondering if you still had many associates in the shipping profession. I have a business proposition for any sailors who might be looking to make a few extra septims.
Skyrim.esm|03EDEE: Revyn put the word out to his suppliers but with the war on, tools are scarce. Both sides have been snapping them up.
Skyrim.esm|03EDEF: It's a bad time to be a criminal, what with these murders going on. In fact, thieves have been known to kill, if there's a profit in it. This war's been hard on all of us.
Skyrim.esm|03EDF1: We accept the risks that come with our faith, but I'll keep your offer in mind. Thank you, Niranye.
Skyrim.esm|03EDF3: A pleasure to see you as well, Niranye. What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|03EDF4: Did you have any luck tracking own some more pitchforks?
Skyrim.esm|03EDF5: One should never pay too much attention to rumors, Torbjorn. You have piqued my curiosity, however. Please, do continue.
Skyrim.esm|03EDF6: Go back to Morrowind, Dark Elf maggots! You're not welcome here!
Skyrim.esm|03EDF7: Get out of our city, gray-skins! This is Nord land!
Skyrim.esm|03EE34: We don't want your kind here, dark elves!
Skyrim.esm|03EE40: This place reeks of gray-skin filth!
Skyrim.esm|03EE41: You like living in this filthy slum, dark elves? Maybe you should go back to Morrowind, where you belong!
Skyrim.esm|03EE42: What is it Ulfric's always saying... "On my honor as a Nord, I swear it." (Laughter).
Skyrim.esm|03EE43: Well, let's just say that I know some people who are experts at helping others disappear when they need to. They are discreet and thorough, though not inexpensive. I hope you'll keep that in mind, should the worst come to pass.
Skyrim.esm|03EE4B: Captain Lonely-Gale, it's always a pleasure to see you. In fact, I'm glad you're here. There's something I'd like to ask you.
Skyrim.esm|03EF0B: Why are you here? Get away from me!
Skyrim.esm|03F05A: We're used to getting by on scraps, but the Shatter-Shields want everything for nothing.
Skyrim.esm|03F05C: No. I'm not. I feel like my scales are clawing into me. Maybe you could help an old Argonian out? I heard some of the sailors talking about how they stashed a bottle of Double-Distilled Skooma in the Gnisis Cornerclub. I just need a taste.
Skyrim.esm|03F05D: I yield. By the honor of Clan Shatter-Shield, the Argonians will be paid as much as any other worker.
Skyrim.esm|03F062: Torbjorn Shatter-Shield. I wish someone would beat the coin out of his fat fists. He clings to every Septim. He says an Argonian's labor is only worth a tenth of a "proper Nord worker." My people are not slaves!
Skyrim.esm|03F066: Thank you. I'll be waiting for that bottle.
Skyrim.esm|03F067: Easy. Maybe I was being too harsh on them. By the honor of Clan Shatter-Shield, they will be paid fairly. You have my word.
Skyrim.esm|03F07A: Thank you so much. Here. Let me show you a thing or two about not attracting attention to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|03F07B: We will make do somehow. We always have.
Skyrim.esm|03F07E: Well, my friend, if you think those boots needs more coin, I'll make it happen. But I'm doing this for you, not them.
Skyrim.esm|03F083: Those boots aren't worth the septims I do pay them. I'm not giving them coin I could give to good, Nord workers.
Skyrim.esm|03F085: You can try, but we've gotten nowhere talking to him ourselves.
Skyrim.esm|03F0B4: This is Markarth, traveler. Safest city in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|03F139: You here to see the Jarl? No sudden moves, understand?
Skyrim.esm|03F150: Whole dwarven city underneath the keep. Nchuand-Zel. Best steer clear if you don't want a Steam Centurion carving you up.
Skyrim.esm|03F158: By the Hist, if I don't get something soon, I don't know how I'll get through the week.
Skyrim.esm|03F15A: Bold words, but true. Fine, you win. I'll pay the Argonians more coin.
Skyrim.esm|03F15B: They don't work now. Why should I waste more money?
Skyrim.esm|03F15E: You talked Torbjorn down? I'm impressed. Here, I was saving this for a rainy day. Maybe now I can afford a few more for my people. Take it.
Skyrim.esm|03F160: Paying the wages yourself? Well, I won't say no to gold. By my family's honor, this money will go to the Argonian workers.
Skyrim.esm|03F1F5: Well now. What have we here? A lost little lamb?
Skyrim.esm|03F402: Considering most of us are now dead, I assumed as much. And before you ask, no - I don't think it was you. Well, maybe I did, but you saving my sorry hide just now sort of erased any doubts. So thanks.
Skyrim.esm|03F403: So you are alive. I was starting to wonder.
Skyrim.esm|03F404: Listener. I am your only salvation. Come. Embrace me.
Skyrim.esm|03F406: You've got that right. Only a matter of time before we're roasted alive. Come on!
Skyrim.esm|03F4FC: Watch out for pressure plates here.
Skyrim.esm|03F502: I'll let you live. This time.
Skyrim.esm|03F504: I didn't think you had the stomach for it.
Skyrim.esm|03F517: The door up ahead will be locked. Klotha, one of the hagravens, will have the key on her.
Skyrim.esm|03F51E: Who are you? What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|03F51F: You're making me... nervous. Is there something you need?
Skyrim.esm|03F520: Yes, yes. What is it? We're all very busy here...
Skyrim.esm|03F521: I'm really not in the mood. State your business, or take your leave.
Skyrim.esm|03F522: Let me guess - you have some "business" to discuss...
Skyrim.esm|03F523: Is the line for my next performance starting already? Come on, don't be shy. Plenty of room for everyone.
Skyrim.esm|03F880: Do not be so sure about that. Beware - the Blades may claim to serve the Dragonborn, but they do not. They never have. As for me, I kept from you only what you were not yet ready to know. Are still not ready to know, as your question reveals.
Skyrim.esm|03F881: If you wish to stand alone, so be it. If you wish for our help, you'll answer my question.
Skyrim.esm|03F884: Perhaps not. But this Shout was used once before, was it not? And here we are again. Have you considered that Alduin was not meant to be defeated? Those who overthrew him in ancient times only postponed the day of reckoning, they did not stop it. If the world is meant to end, so be it. Let it end and be reborn.
Skyrim.esm|03F886: The Dragonrend Shout is evil. Would you cure one evil with another?
Skyrim.esm|03F887: The Blades! Of course. They specialize in meddling in matters they barely understand. Their reckless arrogance knows no bounds.
Skyrim.esm|03F888: Keep moving, stay focused on your goal, and you will reach the summit.
Skyrim.esm|03F889: The Blades were born of dragon-hating crusaders. They still carry that legacy although they may not remember from whence it came. They carried out a genocide of dragons when they first came to Tamriel, and now they will do it again, with your help.
Skyrim.esm|03F88A: I am as my father Akatosh made me. As are you... Dovahkiin.
Skyrim.esm|03F88B: Drem Yol Lok. Greetings, wunduniik. I am Paarthurnax. Who are you? What brings you to my strunmah... my mountain?
Skyrim.esm|03F88C: Yes. Vahzah. You speak true, Dovahkiin. Forgive me. It has been long since I held tinvaak with a stranger. I gave in to the temptation to prolong our speech.
Skyrim.esm|03F88D: That may be what you are here for, Dragonborn. But stopping Alduin is one thing. Helping you wipe out dragons again is quite another. Dragons are part of the natural order of the world. The Blades thought to change the order to suit themselves, by wiping out the dragons. But again, we see that the natural order will be restored, one way or another. Perhaps we would have dragon allies against Alduin if the ancient Blades had not acted so arrogantly.
Skyrim.esm|03F88E: They see me as master. Wuth. Onik. Old and wise. It is true I am old...
Skyrim.esm|03F88F: So. You have made your way here, to me. No easy task for a joor... mortal. Even for one of Dovah Sos. Dragonblood. What would you ask of me?
Skyrim.esm|03F890: Drem. Patience. There are formalities which must be observed, at the first meeting of two of the dov.
Skyrim.esm|03F891: Where did you learn of a Shout used to defeat Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|03F892: No. I cannot teach it to you because I do not know it.
Skyrim.esm|03F9E1: True... But qostiid - prophecy - tells what may be, not what should be. Qostiid sahlo aak. Just because you can do a thing, does not always mean you should. Do you have no better reason for acting than destiny? Are you nothing but a plaything of dez... of fate?
Skyrim.esm|03F9E2: Evenaar bahlok. There are many hungers it is better to deny than to feed. Dreh ni nahkip. Discipline against the lesser aids in qahnaar... denial of the greater.
Skyrim.esm|03F9E6: Not until introductions are complete. Zin. Heyv. Test your Thu'um against mine, Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|03F9E7: You are bold, wunduniik. You dare enter a Dovah's home without permission.
Skyrim.esm|03FA13: Pruzah. As good a reason as any. There are many who feel as you do, although not all. Some would say that all things must end, so that the next can come to pass. Perhaps this world is simply the Egg of the next kalpa? Lein vokiin? Would you stop the next world from being born?
Skyrim.esm|03FA14: Tell me. Why do you come here, volaan? Why do you intrude on my meditation?
Skyrim.esm|03FA3D: Krosis. No. I know little of what has passed below in the long years I have lived here. You are likely better informed than I.
Skyrim.esm|03FA3E: This is the most sacred mountain in Skyrim. Zok revak strunmah. The great mountain of the world. Here the ancient Tongues, the first mortal masters of the Voice, brought Alduin to battle and defeated him.
Skyrim.esm|03FA3F: Do you know why I live here, at the peak of the Monahven - what you name Throat of the World?
Skyrim.esm|03FA40: Ah. I have expected you. Prodah. You would not come all this way for tinvaak with an old dovah. No. You seek your weapon against Alduin.
Skyrim.esm|03FA41: Yes. There were a few of us that rebelled against Alduin's thur... his tyranny. We aided the humans in his overthrow. But they did not trust us. Ni ov. Their inner councils were kept hidden from us. I was far from here on the day of Alduin's downfall. But all dov felt the... sundering of Time itself.
Skyrim.esm|03FA42: Vomindok. I do not know. Perhaps in the very doing they erased the knowing of it from Time itself. The dov are children of Akatosh. Thus we are specially... attuned to the flow of Time. Perhaps also uniquely vulnerable. I warned them against such a rash action. Even I could not foresee its consequences. Nust ni hon. They would not listen.
Skyrim.esm|03FA43: I cannot tell you in detail. I never heard it used. Kogaan. It was the first Thu'um created solely by mortals. It was said to force a dragon to experience the concept of Mortality. A truly vonmindoraan... incomprehensible idea to the immortal dov.
Skyrim.esm|03FA46: Yes and no. Viik nuz ni kron. Alduin was not truly defeated, either. If he was, you would not be here today, seeking to... defeat him. The Nords of those days used the Dragonrend Shout to cripple Alduin. But this was not enough. Ok mulaag unslaad. It was the Kel - the Elder Scroll. They used it to... cast him adrift on the currents of Time.
Skyrim.esm|03FA47: Drem. Patience. I am answering, in my way.
Skyrim.esm|03FA48: True. But few now remember that this was the very spot where Alduin was defeated by the ancient Tongues. Vahrukt unslaad... perhaps none but me now remember how he was defeated.
Skyrim.esm|03FA49: Tiid krent. Time was... shattered here because of what the ancient Nords did to Alduin. If you brought that Kel, that Elder Scroll back here... to the Tiid-Ahraan, the Time-Wound... With the Elder Scroll that was used to break Time, you may be able to... cast yourself back. To the other end of the break. You could learn Dragonrend from those who created it.
Skyrim.esm|03FA6E: Save the niceties for now. I have no intention of getting invested in someone who may be dead tomorrow. If you're still breathing in a few weeks, I'm sure we'll be the best of friends.
Skyrim.esm|03FA8B: Ooh, cocky. I give you three days before you screw up and someone runs a knife across your throat. But who knows? I've been proven wrong before.
Skyrim.esm|03FA8C: Ahh, going for the mysterious and brooding thing, hmm? That may work for your targets, but you'll find I'm not so easily impressed.
Skyrim.esm|03FA8D: You've got people to kill, my friend. Best get to it.
Skyrim.esm|03FDBE: Morthal used to be a quiet place. But then, the world used to make sense.
Skyrim.esm|03FE0E: You ask me, we'd be better off without that wizard in town.
Skyrim.esm|03FE1E: This isn't the time or place for magic, or whatever it is Falion does.
Skyrim.esm|03FF66: You're tougher than you look. You got me, fair and square.
Skyrim.esm|03FF67: I didn't mean nothing by it! Just... leave me alone! What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|03FF6A: I don't have to take this from the likes of you.
Skyrim.esm|03FF9D: So the Blades have survived yet another disaster of their own making, and continue to meddle in things they do not understand.
Skyrim.esm|04000B: Looks like the boss is at it again.
Skyrim.esm|04000C: Don't say that to loud, you don't want it getting back to him.
Skyrim.esm|04000E: He's on one of his drunk rants again. Except this time he's challenging everyone to a duel over in the hole.
Skyrim.esm|04000F: We've got him locked in there now, but he's still challenging anyone that looks crooked at him.
Skyrim.esm|040010: Someone should let that cat lose.  That'd shut him up real quick.
Skyrim.esm|040118: What in the world was that racket? Is everything all right?
Skyrim.esm|04011A: Anything! Anything at all that might be of interest. That's why I adore this location... we have no idea what we're going to find. And if, along the way, my message about the dangers of magic should happen to sink in for a few students, that would be a happy coincidence.
Skyrim.esm|04011B: You found what? Hmm. Well now. This is all very fascinating, but I don't recall asking to you to touch anything in here. I suppose you'd better show that to Tolfdir nonetheless. And please, ask before tampering with anything else.
Skyrim.esm|04011C: We're particularly interested in the prevalence of magical seals placed on the tombs here. It's rather unlike anything we've encountered.
Skyrim.esm|04015D: I'm not exactly built for manual labor. Now come on, you've almost got it.
Skyrim.esm|040161: I think so. Just hold on a moment.
Skyrim.esm|040162: Hurry, Nazir! I'm telling you, he's in there!
Skyrim.esm|040163: Hurry, Nazir! I'm telling you, she's in there!
Skyrim.esm|040164: You must speak with Astrid. Here, in the Dark Brotherhood Sanctuary.
Skyrim.esm|040165: I'm going... as fast... as I can, you stupid she-devil. I don't see you... helping...
Skyrim.esm|040356: Oh, really? So that Dark Elf took my ring. So typical of his kind. I think the Jarl should hear about this. Maybe double his taxes. No, triple them. That should teach those people a lesson. You deserve something for sniffing out that thief. Here.
Skyrim.esm|040359: Of course nothing in here is stolen. Only a careless, shameful, idiotic fetcher would do something as stupid as to buy pilfered goods.... Oh by Azura, I've made a terrible mistake. I bought a gold ring, and Viola Giordano has been missing a ring that looks just like it.
Skyrim.esm|040361: Be that as it may, we have no other choice. You need to remain unseen! Now go! Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
Skyrim.esm|040364: You must understand. If Cicero is turning the others against me... against us... Our Family would not survive such division.
Skyrim.esm|040365: So you understand my fear. If Cicero's planning something, and conspiring with one of our own, it would be disastrous.
Skyrim.esm|040368: Sorry, I thought Skald the Elder sent you. We've got an order to fill, and he's not known for patience. Look, if you're heading towards Dawnstar anytime soon, can you give the Jarl this note? Let him know he'll get his lumber.
Skyrim.esm|040379: Thank you. Thank you! Azura's Prophecy always guide you to fortune. Speaking of fortune. Here. Everything I earned from my last shipment. It's yours.
Skyrim.esm|040389: It's not that easy. She'd go to the Jarl if she knew I was even remotely involved. You have to get it to her some other way. Look, it's dangerous, but if you sneak the ring into her house. Put in a dresser or something. I will make it worth your while.
Skyrim.esm|04038B: Ah yes, Adonato's new work. Shorter than I expected, but words must be judged on merit, not length. I'll look over this later. Why don't you take this, for your trouble?
Skyrim.esm|040397: Don't like it? Too bad. This is our city. Ours! Don't think I can take you? One hundred septims says I can punch you back where you came from.
Skyrim.esm|040398: My axe is yours, outlander. Just say the word.
Skyrim.esm|040399: You. You a Dark Elf lover? Get out of our city, you filthy piece of trash.
Skyrim.esm|04039B: You damn gray-skin. Go back to Morrowind!
Skyrim.esm|04039C: All right. Fists only. And none of that magic stuff, either. Let's go!
Skyrim.esm|04039E: I said a hundred gold! Damn broke elves and damn broke elf-lovers.
Skyrim.esm|0403D6: Maybe so, but healthy paranoia has saved this Sanctuary before, and my gut's telling me that demented little fool is up to something.
Skyrim.esm|0403F6: No objections, then? Good. Go now. Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
Skyrim.esm|0403F7: They'll never think to check it. Who in their right mind would hide inside? Now go. Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
Skyrim.esm|040B66: I'm prepared to negotiate my surrender!
Skyrim.esm|040CC0: I don't think you were followed. Come on. I have a plan.
Skyrim.esm|040CC9: Now then. Here's my invitation, I don't have a poisoned dagger strapped to my thigh, et cetera, et cetera.
Skyrim.esm|040CDD: Ah! A fellow latecomer to Elenwen's little soiree.
Skyrim.esm|040E8D: You're asking me? She promised that she was sending someone who knew what they were doing... If you actually want to get out alive, I'd bring whatever you need to move quietly, and kill quickly.
Skyrim.esm|040F25: Before the Great War, the Blades helped the Empire against the Thalmor. Our Grand Master saw them as the greatest threat to Tamriel. At the time, that was true. Maybe it still is. So we fought them in the shadows, all across Tamriel. We thought we were more than a match for them. We were wrong.
Skyrim.esm|040F63: You'd do that for a stranger? I don't know what to say. Lead on, I suppose.
Skyrim.esm|040F64: Hello? You! Over here! Please, I need help!
Skyrim.esm|040F67: You'd really risk your life for a stranger's honor? I don't even know what to say. Thank you, my friend. I'll wait here.
Skyrim.esm|040F6D: Much better. Thanks, friend. I wouldn't have lasted much longer without that.
Skyrim.esm|040F72: Please, do you have a potion you can spare?
Skyrim.esm|040F73: Please! I'm not sure I'll make it on my own.
Skyrim.esm|040F74: Step lightly. Who knows if those things can hear us or not.
Skyrim.esm|040F75: Be careful. They're faster than you'd think.
Skyrim.esm|040F76: That is a lot of blood.
Skyrim.esm|040F77: Not sure how much longer I can hold out here.
Skyrim.esm|040F78: I really got myself into it this time.
Skyrim.esm|040F7D: Thank Kynareth for you, stranger. I'd almost given up hope of walking away from here.
Skyrim.esm|040F7E: I just need a little to get going.
Skyrim.esm|040F9F: That's when the fun starts. You'll have to slip away from the party without raising the alarm. Then you'll need to find Elenwen's office and search her files. Malborn should be able to point you in the right direction.
Skyrim.esm|04101A: I just need to rest a moment, right here on this lovely rock.
Skyrim.esm|041030: Inside, my friend, inside! Where only the women and drinks are cold.
Skyrim.esm|04104A: Awful damn cold up here. And only the guests are allowed inside. Damn foreigners and their inhospitable ways.
Skyrim.esm|04106F: What are you out here talking to me for? Party's inside.
Skyrim.esm|0410D2: I'm paid to wait to take you home. Enjoy yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0410D3: The lady's already paid the fee. We leave when she gives the word.
Skyrim.esm|0410D4: Let me handle that. You'll have a real invitation, don't worry. As long as you can act the part of a Thalmor toady, you'll get past the guards.
Skyrim.esm|0410D7: They're the high elves that rule the Aldmeri Dominion. Sworn enemies of the Empire and everything it stands for. Thirty years ago, they invaded the Empire and came close to destroying it. They smashed the Blades in the process. Since then, they've been working to weaken the Empire any way they can.
Skyrim.esm|0410D8: What? Something you're still confused about?
Skyrim.esm|0410DB: Of course. Just bring it to me when you find it.
Skyrim.esm|0410DC: Don't worry about Malborn. He's not a dangerous character like you, but he hates the Thalmor at least as much as I do. He's a wood elf - the Thalmor wiped out his family back in Valenwood during one of their purges that we never hear about. Luckily they don't know who he really is, or he wouldn't be serving drinks at the Ambassador's parties.
Skyrim.esm|0410FA: They're the rulers of the Aldmeri Dominion - what used to be the Imperial provinces of Summerset Isle and Valenwood. The Thalmor take the arrogance of high elves to the extreme - they believe they are the rightful rulers of all of Tamriel. For a century or more, the Thalmor had been picking away at the Empire. Valenwood was the first, then the province of Elsweyr. But even the Blades didn't see the Great War coming. We underestimated the Thalmor, and they destroyed us.
Skyrim.esm|0410FB: Welcome. I don't believe we've met. I am Elenwen, the Thalmor Ambassador to Skyrim. And you are...?
Skyrim.esm|0411DA: I prefer to arrive early. Often the day before the party. So as not to miss out on any of the drinking.
Skyrim.esm|0411DB: There's not enough drinking in the world today, wouldn't you agree?
Skyrim.esm|0411F5: I guess I was just hearing things.
Skyrim.esm|0411F6: I hope there aren't any more.
Skyrim.esm|04124D: I will just... rest here a moment. Although it is damnably cold out here. I don't look forward to the trip back down...
Skyrim.esm|041253: Perhaps we might share a carriage after the party? I know, I know - we've just met. But think, by the time the party is over we'll no doubt be fast friends. I look forward to it.
Skyrim.esm|04126B: Don't mind me. I'm perfectly insensible to the cold.
Skyrim.esm|04126C: I'll go after you. Although these fellows know me. I never miss one of Elenwen's parties, you see.
Skyrim.esm|04126D: Of course not. Purely a formality. Now, I need to see your invitation.
Skyrim.esm|0412F1: So that's your plan, huh? Even if you could survive, by the time you got inside, whatever documents they had would be long gone. We're there for information, remember? Trust me, I've been doing this for a long time. My way is better.
Skyrim.esm|041301: The faction that rules the Aldmeri Dominion. The ones who almost destroyed the Empire during the Great War, thirty years back. There's no worse enemy to humankind in Tamriel. The Empire barely survived the last war. The Thalmor don't intend to lose the next one.
Skyrim.esm|041311: Yes, yes. Now to find myself a drink.
Skyrim.esm|041327: My lateness is due more to getting lost on the way up this gods-forsaken mountain than to any desire to actually arrive late.
Skyrim.esm|041329: I've figured out how we're going to get you into the Thalmor Embassy.
Skyrim.esm|04132C: I've been doing this a long time, remember? While the Thalmor's been looking for me, I've been watching them.
Skyrim.esm|04138A: Everything's in order. Welcome back, sir.
Skyrim.esm|0413F1: And arriving by carriage, no less! I salute you, sir!
Skyrim.esm|0413F2: And arriving by carriage, no less! I salute you, my good lady!
Skyrim.esm|04146A: I have told Idgrod of your concerns. She'll look after you all. Please, go back to your business.
Skyrim.esm|04146C: What's the Jarl going to do about it?
Skyrim.esm|041473: Morthal has enough problems as it is!
Skyrim.esm|0414BC: We've no need for wizards in our midst!
Skyrim.esm|0414BD: Ah, you saw that, did you? Life in Morthal has been troubled lately. The people are uncertain, restless. They merely look to the Jarl for leadership.
Skyrim.esm|0414DF: There's news of rebellion against the Empire. Strange noises have been heard in the marshes at night. And then, the tragedy with Hroggar's home. The men simply seek wisdom. Everything will be fine.
Skyrim.esm|0414E2: The world's going mad, and our Jarl does what? She hides inside with her 'visions'. We need a leader, not some mystic!
Skyrim.esm|0414E4: How can I? She never speaks with us. She's allowed this wizard, Falion, to live in our midst practicing gods-know-what. And now there's talk of rebellion, and dragons? What help could Idgrod be against them? No, we can only rely on ourselves. You'd do well to remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0414E5: How are we supposed to feel safe in our own homes?
Skyrim.esm|041560: I... I don't know. Ancano is gone, but whatever he's done to the Eye doesn't seem to have stopped. I have no idea what to do!
Skyrim.esm|041561: I knew you could do it!
Skyrim.esm|04158E: Yes. I had Falk call you back because you were at the summoning, you have a connection to Potema now. You must deal with her. I can provide you with help for her minions though.  This should help you deal with the Catacombs themselves.
Skyrim.esm|041631: I'll see you back in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|04172B: I don't know. Beem-Ja has looked after me since I was knee-high to him. I need to rest, and then I think I'm going back to High Rock.
Skyrim.esm|04172D: Go on without me. I think I need to be alone for a while.
Skyrim.esm|0418F4: You've been at it for five minutes. Here, let me try.
Skyrim.esm|0418F6: I think I almost got it.
Skyrim.esm|041CDD: Thank you, sir. Go right in.
Skyrim.esm|041CE2: Thank you, ma'am. Go right in.
Skyrim.esm|041E09: Oh I know. Isn't it wonderful? I bet she squirmed like a skeever when you pulled them out of your pocket. I think things are going to be a lot different around here from now on and I have you to thank for it. Here, I want you to have this. It was my father's but I'm certain you'll put it to good use.
Skyrim.esm|041E0B: Yeah, you're right. Maybe it's not the best idea.
Skyrim.esm|041E0C: Oh, this is going to be great! You need to get the Marks of Dibella from Bolli, Hofgrir and Indaryn. Not sure how you're going to do that, but try your best. Then just confront Haelga with them and the rest works itself out.
Skyrim.esm|041E0D: I'm not sure what it's made from, but I can tell you that once you've had a taste, you'll never crave anything else. The Dunmer came up with the stuff a long time ago. Very illegal and very hard to come by. I got mine off of a merchant ship in Solitude. Worst mistake I ever made.
Skyrim.esm|041E15: That would be a bad idea. I'd be too likely to attract the wrong kind of attention. But they don't know you at all, yet.
Skyrim.esm|041E16: Bolli is a good man, he pays us well and watches out for us. I used to work at the Black-Briar Meadery, but Indaryn and I never saw eye-to-eye. That elf has no patience at all. It's probably for the best anyway; I'm far better at fishing than working in a meadery.
Skyrim.esm|041E19: Almost anyone in Riften is in dire need of those missives. Speak to them, child. You will know who will listen and who will sin. A word of advice, keep away from the Keep and the city guards. They follow their own path and have already forsaken Her.
Skyrim.esm|041E1A: Now that the Imperial blight has poisoned Riften, the worship of Talos is strictly forbidden by the terms of the White-Gold Concordat. They can defile our shrine, they can arrest our people and they can silence our voices, but the Empire will never destroy our spirit. As long as we keep Talos in our heart, his legacy will never die.
Skyrim.esm|041E20: Very well. I suppose I'll just curl up somewhere until this passes as usual.
Skyrim.esm|041E21: What? How... where did you get these? No, don't tell me. I don't want to know. Look, we need to keep this quiet... between you and me, okay. No one else needs to know about it. If word got out that I'm practicing my Dibellan Arts in Riften, they'll run me out of town. Here, take this and don't mention a word of this to anyone, especially Svana!
Skyrim.esm|041E22: What, did you suddenly grow a conscience? Suit yourself.
Skyrim.esm|041E23: Look, it was one night, okay? I think she slipped something in my drink. I haven't even been back to the Bunkhouse since. Just take this damn thing. I never want to see it again.
Skyrim.esm|041E24: I don't want a bad reputation in Riften, but Haelga's out of control. She wanted to try it in the stables so I said yes. If you want the mark that badly then go ahead and take it.
Skyrim.esm|041E2A: If word gets around that I bedded her, they'll lose all respect for me at the meadery. Take the mark if you want it. It's not worth all this hassle anyway.
Skyrim.esm|041E2C: My job at the Riften Fishery is in danger. The owner, Bolli, said that if I show up for work in this condition one more time, then I'm out. I don't mean to do this to myself, but I can't help it. I tried some skooma a year ago, and ever since then, I can't stop! If you could give me a healing potion, I could cleanse this poison from my body and get back to my life.
Skyrim.esm|041E2D: Your kindness will never be forgotten. Here, take this. It's all I can offer for what you've given me.
Skyrim.esm|041E2E: Mara is the handmaiden of Kyne, the Goddess of the Storm and the Mother of Men. She is the bearer of love and the patron of marriage. If you wish to make a donation to her Benevolence, speak to Maramal and ensure your true love will endure for all eternity.
Skyrim.esm|041E36: Work? I suppose you can call it that. I call it slavery. I work my fingers to the bone keeping this disgusting place clean. Ever since my parents died and Haelga took me in, it's been a nightmare. How was I to know she was such a wretched woman? So now I'm stuck living here while those pigs she calls customers grope me and say the most awful things.
Skyrim.esm|041E3D: Next time think before you speak.
Skyrim.esm|041E3F: Thank you. I'd almost given up hope. Here, take this. It's all I can offer for what you've given me.
Skyrim.esm|041E40: I have no idea what you're talking about. I'd never sleep with anyone besides my good wife, Drifa.
Skyrim.esm|041E42: Huh? What are you talking about? I don't even know what that is.
Skyrim.esm|041E43: You're crazy! How dare you make such an accusation!
Skyrim.esm|041E49: It's not just the work, it's everything about her. She's disgusting. I think she takes her worship of Dibella a bit too seriously. Do you know she's slept with three different men in the last month alone? What kind of a woman would do such a thing? Just for once, I'd like to see her squirm... to rub her nose in it.
Skyrim.esm|041EBF: Such a shame. Perhaps another time then.
Skyrim.esm|041EC3: Actually, there is, but I don't think I could get away with doing it. She'd kill me if she found out. You see, I happen to know after she makes love, she gives her partner a token of her affection called a Mark of Dibella. If you confront her with three of the Marks, she'd be so embarrassed... well, I don't know what she'd do!
Skyrim.esm|041EC5: Hand what over? I have no idea what you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|041EC8: Look, you must have me confused with someone else.
Skyrim.esm|041ED1: Welcome to the Thalmor Embassy. Your invitation, please.
Skyrim.esm|041EF2: You aren't making any sense. Hand what over?
Skyrim.esm|041EF3: You must present your invitation to be admitted to the party.
Skyrim.esm|041EF5: Or else what? You don't scare me.
Skyrim.esm|041EFA: You have a death wish or something?
Skyrim.esm|041F02: You're threatening me? Oh, that's rich!
Skyrim.esm|041F10: Were the Greybeards any help? Did they know this anything about the Shout used against Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|041F11: Ask Esbern. If he doesn't know himself, he'll know where to find it out.
Skyrim.esm|041F12: Ah, indeed? There's a pretty puzzle. Not the kind of thing you'll find in your local bookshop. Let me think... Perhaps the College of Winterhold. What learning there is in Skyrim is gathered there. One of the mages there may be able to put you on the trail of this Elder Scroll.
Skyrim.esm|041F13: An Elder Scroll? That's not going to be easy to come by. Esbern's your man for that kind of thing. He may be able to at least point you in the right direction.
Skyrim.esm|041F14: Did he, now? That doesn't sound like my nephew at all. I doubt he'd be mixed up with the likes of you.
Skyrim.esm|041F15: I'd be glad to help a friend of Hadvar. But you didn't answer my question - how do you know him?
Skyrim.esm|041F16: Hadvar? How do you know him?
Skyrim.esm|041F4C: You'd do well to remember who's really in charge around here.
Skyrim.esm|041F5C: You'd do well to remember who's in charge around here.
Skyrim.esm|041F5D: May you return to Her Benevolence in safety.
Skyrim.esm|041F68: You represent Mara now. Set an example for all.
Skyrim.esm|041F6A: I don't know why I bother.
Skyrim.esm|041F6B: Try and get those marks without violence please. I don't want to be responsible for their deaths... or yours.
Skyrim.esm|041F6C: Sure, leave. Not like you care anyway.
Skyrim.esm|041F6D: Remain vigilant and don't trust anyone.
Skyrim.esm|041F70: You've been kind, I thank you.
Skyrim.esm|041F71: Any healing potion will do. Please hurry.
Skyrim.esm|041F72: What am I going to do?
Skyrim.esm|041F73: A Dreth never forgets a friend.
Skyrim.esm|041F78: A Dreth never forgets a traitor. You'll pay for this!
Skyrim.esm|041F79: I believe you have a delivery to make.
Skyrim.esm|041F7B: I'm Riften's appointed Steward. How can I help you?
Skyrim.esm|041F7C: Steward Hemming Black-Briar at your service.
Skyrim.esm|041F7E: How may I help you, my daughter?
Skyrim.esm|041F7F: Has the Warmth of Mara been given to Riften?
Skyrim.esm|041F82: Lady Mara bids you welcome to Her Benevolence.
Skyrim.esm|041F83: There's just one more thing I need from you. To fully absorb Gathrik's power I require a blood sacrifice. Your blood should be good enough. This will go easier for us both if you don't bother fighting back.
Skyrim.esm|041F84: Fool. I should thank you. I knew the girl wouldn't be strong enough to help me kill Gathrik alone.
Skyrim.esm|041F85: New around Riften? Take my advice. Stay at the Bee and Barb... the Bunkhouse isn't for you.
Skyrim.esm|041F86: Any luck getting the marks from those men?
Skyrim.esm|041F88: Sorry about the mess. Keeping this place clean is quite a chore.
Skyrim.esm|041FC1: Go right on in. The party has already started.
Skyrim.esm|041FC2: I'm sorry, sir, I'm on duty.
Skyrim.esm|041FC3: I'm sorry, ma'am, I'm on duty.
Skyrim.esm|041FC4: We're not supposed to talk to the guests.
Skyrim.esm|041FC7: Guests are advised to remain inside the Embassy.
Skyrim.esm|041FC8: What are you doing here? You're supposed to be meeting Malborn in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|041FC9: Not here. I told you to meet me in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|041FCA: I'm just doing my duty, sir.
Skyrim.esm|0420B0: We're in this together. Stay close.
Skyrim.esm|0420D2: Let's clear the place out first. Then we'll gather our loot.
Skyrim.esm|0420F1: Look, I never needed treasure. My father owns land; our family is wealthy. I just wanted adventure. Not this.
Skyrim.esm|04216F: Yeah, I don't know how I feel about what goes on down there.
Skyrim.esm|042170: I bet Naris gets a kick out it. Nasty fella he is.
Skyrim.esm|042171: Ah, the sweet smell of burnt flesh. Nothing better really.
Skyrim.esm|042172: They drink all day and night and what do I do?
Skyrim.esm|04217A: They die too quickly. Maybe I'll try and hold off on the paralysis next time. Give me more of a moving target. Good practice.
Skyrim.esm|04217B: Why do they need me to do this?  We use magic, not weapons.
Skyrim.esm|04218E: You better quiet down before you end up there. Then I'd have to work the forge.
Skyrim.esm|04219B: I swear they're making me do this for a laugh. At least I'm not dealing with the prisoners down below.
Skyrim.esm|0422C1: Oh, it's you again. What in blazes do you want?
Skyrim.esm|0422C2: What can I get you? Mead? Wine?
Skyrim.esm|0422C7: Looking for a place to stay?
Skyrim.esm|0422C8: You have some nerve coming back here after what you did to Keerava.
Skyrim.esm|0422C9: Is there something I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|0422CF: Be brief Imperial, there's only so long I can stand being downwind of your stench.
Skyrim.esm|0422D0: Hm. You're not from around here. Imperial spy perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|0422D1: If your allegiance lies with the Empire, we haven't much to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|0422D2: Ah! A fellow Nord! I trust you're bringing the fight to the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0422D9: How can a humble servant of Talos help you today?
Skyrim.esm|0422E0: Sorry, I'm afraid I can't help you much right now... I'm currently in mourning.
Skyrim.esm|0422E2: You've caught me at a bad time. I've lost someone dear to me.
Skyrim.esm|0422E7: I'm the brewmaster around here. What did you want?
Skyrim.esm|0422E8: I'm quite busy. Need something or can I get back to work?
Skyrim.esm|0422E9: Yeah? What do you want? If this is about the meadery, we can talk later.
Skyrim.esm|0422ED: Welcome! Allow me to recommend the Black-Briar Mead... the purest brew in all of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|0422EE: We serve only the best here... Black-Briar Mead. May I get one for you?
Skyrim.esm|0422EF: I'm off work at the Black-Briar Meadery. I'll be back there in the morning.
Skyrim.esm|0422F6: Hey, don't look at me that way! Who are you to judge me?
Skyrim.esm|0422F7: So what if I have a problem? I know how to deal with it!
Skyrim.esm|0422F8: Maramal thinks he's so smart; tells me that Mara will cleanse the filth from my blood. Ha! What does he know?
Skyrim.esm|0422F9: If Indaryn finds out, he'll have me thrown out of the Meadery for certain. So don't breathe a word of it to him. Not a word!
Skyrim.esm|0422FA: Oh what am I going do? I can barely afford to stay at the Bunkhouse.
Skyrim.esm|0422FB: My n'wah of a father thought he could pull my strings... "continue the Hlaalu tradition." Bah! I left all that nonsense behind.
Skyrim.esm|0422FC: I'm going to stop. That's what I'm going to do! Then everyone will see that I'm not as useless as they think.
Skyrim.esm|0422FD: I'm selling the best mead in town for cheap. Interested?
Skyrim.esm|0422FE: Wilhelm was pleased with the delivery, well done.
Skyrim.esm|042305: Well met. I don't think they're hiring anyone at the moment, so if you're looking for work, you're out of luck.
Skyrim.esm|042306: The trees... they're beautiful here. Reminds me of autumn at home.
Skyrim.esm|042307: The fall forest makes me miss my childhood; playing among the towering trees in Valenwood and living with my brethren.
Skyrim.esm|042308: Seeing the sorry state of this city almost makes me regret my decision to leave my home when my apprenticeship completed.
Skyrim.esm|042309: I was fortunate enough to be guided by one of Silvenar's master armorers until he passed.
Skyrim.esm|04230A: On his deathbed, my master urged me to take over his business but I decided against it. I chose to wander Tamriel instead.
Skyrim.esm|04230B: Now here I am, working for this dreary city's Fishery. I sometimes wonder why I don't just move on.
Skyrim.esm|04230C: Hello. Welcome to the Pawned Prawn.
Skyrim.esm|04230D: We both own the Pawned Prawn together, but he's singlehandedly kept the place running despite all our problems.
Skyrim.esm|04230E: Bersi's afraid that someone's making a move to take the Pawned Prawn from us. I think he's being paranoid.
Skyrim.esm|04230F: All the crime and corruption in Riften is really getting to him... and now that we hear rumors about the dragons, he's been very depressed.
Skyrim.esm|042310: I just care about my husband so much and I hate to see him in this state.
Skyrim.esm|042311: Welcome to the Pawned Prawn. What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|042312: I have all sorts of interesting items for sale. Have you looked around?
Skyrim.esm|042313: Pickings may be a bit slim today, but if you see anything you like I'll make you a good deal.
Skyrim.esm|04231A: I presume you're bothering me for a good reason?
Skyrim.esm|04231B: What in the name of Oblivion do you want?
Skyrim.esm|04231C: I don't have time for idle chit-chat. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|04231F: Fancy yourself the adventuring type, eh? It's just as well, I doubt your combat prowess would prove valuable in a real battle anyway.
Skyrim.esm|042320: I was trained by some of the finest warriors in all Skyrim to wield a blade as if it was an extension of my own arm.
Skyrim.esm|042321: My mother spares no expense where I'm concerned. I'm heir to the family fortune after all.
Skyrim.esm|042322: Once I'm in charge I have bigger aspirations than just Skyrim. I plan to bring Black-Briar Mead to all of Tamriel.
Skyrim.esm|042323: My children, Sibbi and Ingun, are being groomed to run the family business one day as well.
Skyrim.esm|042324: Sibbi will prove to be a fine leader one day, but Ingun... that girl has her mind somewhere other than business.
Skyrim.esm|042325: Maven's disappointed that Ingun's heart isn't in the family business, but she says "Ingun still has her uses." Not sure what that means.
Skyrim.esm|042326: Have you come to gawk at me or is this a social call?
Skyrim.esm|042327: Well, if it isn't my little problem-solver.
Skyrim.esm|04232F: My mind is on my experiments today. What did you need?
Skyrim.esm|042335: Welcome back to the temple, messenger.
Skyrim.esm|042336: How may I help you, my son?
Skyrim.esm|04233B: What is it you seek, my child?
Skyrim.esm|042341: New in town, eh? Here for the fishing I'd guess.
Skyrim.esm|042342: Thanks again for the delivery to Markarth. What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|042348: If you're looking for a handout, you're not going to get one from me.
Skyrim.esm|042349: If you're looking for a handout, you'll want to speak with my husband. If you're looking for company, I suppose I could do worse.
Skyrim.esm|04234A: Bolli's around somewhere, planning to feed the poor with his nonsense about Mara.
Skyrim.esm|04234B: With my husband's new found religion, what I do with our coin and what I do all day seem less important to him. Suits me.
Skyrim.esm|04234C: At this rate, he'll give the entire Riften Fishery away to charity. Then how will I live?
Skyrim.esm|04234D: Such a waste of time preaching to the poor. If they're that hungry, they should just grow some food or something.
Skyrim.esm|04234E: I spend every Friday at the fishery, so I know the meaning of hard work. All those forms to fill out and file; it's exhausting!
Skyrim.esm|04234F: Mark my words. If Bolli thinks he's giving all of our wealth away to some poor slob, I'll leave him high and dry.
Skyrim.esm|042350: I'm feeling much better thanks to your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|042351: I can see that disapproving look in your eye.
Skyrim.esm|042356: If you're looking for a bed, this is the wrong place.
Skyrim.esm|042357: You looking for something or what?
Skyrim.esm|04235D: Hey! Good to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|04235E: I'd appreciate it if you'd keep your hands to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|04235F: The Bunkhouse isn't an inn. I'd recommend the Bee and Barb.
Skyrim.esm|042363: If there's anything you wish to purchase, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|042364: Have anything you wish to sell? I pay fair prices for all sorts of goods.
Skyrim.esm|042367: I'm beginning to think leaving Mournhold was a mistake.
Skyrim.esm|042368: If Riften is the best Skyrim has to offer, I may as well turn back right now and just head for home.
Skyrim.esm|042369: Working at the Riften Fishery is tough, but it puts some coin in my pocket.
Skyrim.esm|04236C: Please help me, I'm going to lose my job at the Riften Fishery.
Skyrim.esm|04236D: Oh, my aching head. Why do I do this to myself?
Skyrim.esm|04236E: You have no idea what it's like to have everything, then watch it all disappear.
Skyrim.esm|04236F: I'm feeling much better now thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|042370: Did you bring anything? The pain...
Skyrim.esm|042374: Welcome to Riften, traveler. I hope the road fared well for you.
Skyrim.esm|042375: What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|042376: I feel so helpless out here. The people of Riften depended on me, and I failed them.
Skyrim.esm|04237B: Out of my way. The son of the Jarl has no time for idle conversation with travelers.
Skyrim.esm|04237D: If this isn't about official city business, you're wasting my time.
Skyrim.esm|042381: Come to gloat, have we? To poke fun at the jarl's youngest son?
Skyrim.esm|042382: I can feel you laughing behind my back.
Skyrim.esm|042383: Did Harrald send you to taunt me?
Skyrim.esm|042387: I'm afraid my days as the steward of Riften has come to an end.
Skyrim.esm|042388: As Riften's Steward I'm afraid I can provide only limited assistance, but speak your mind.
Skyrim.esm|042389: Welcome to Mistveil Keep. I'm Jarl Laila's steward, Anuriel.
Skyrim.esm|04238D: As Jarl Laila's housecarl, I would ask that you maintain a respectable distance from her at all times.
Skyrim.esm|04238E: Did you have some sort of official business here?
Skyrim.esm|04238F: I'd request that when speaking to Jarl Laila, you show the proper respect.
Skyrim.esm|042390: Being in exile is the most humiliating thing I've experienced in my life.
Skyrim.esm|042391: I killed scores of the Empire's men before I was forced to surrender.
Skyrim.esm|042392: The only reason I stopped fighting was a plea from Laila, otherwise I'd would have fought to the death.
Skyrim.esm|042394: I know you. Where was it? Hm. Oh! You were getting something for me?
Skyrim.esm|042395: Excuse my disorganization, but I'm in the middle of some delicate experiments.
Skyrim.esm|042399: Nothing like the smell of a white-hot blade, eh?
Skyrim.esm|04239A: Looking for some armor? A weapon perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|04239F: Here to purchase some armor or a weapon?
Skyrim.esm|0423A0: I used to live at the orphanage here in Riften. Grelod said my ma and da left me there when I was a baby... that they didn't want me.
Skyrim.esm|0423A1: Balimund took me in and has been so kind to me. I don't know why, but he thinks I can be a great blacksmith like him.
Skyrim.esm|0423A2: At first I don't know why he picked me. I never even held a hammer in my life! But the first time I picked it up, it just felt right!
Skyrim.esm|0423A3: Folks in town say that Balimund is the greatest blacksmith in all of Tamriel. I hope I can live up to that some day and make him proud of me.
Skyrim.esm|0423A4: Welcome back! Good to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|0423A7: Visitors like us are rare in this part of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0423AA: I've never seen Mjoll this upset about anything. The city really gets to her.
Skyrim.esm|0423AB: Mjoll told me that it isn't this bad everywhere. Maybe one day I'll venture out and see if she's right.
Skyrim.esm|0423AC: Mjoll's brought me nothing but safety and piece of mind... and some lessons with the blade.
Skyrim.esm|0423AD: I hope that someday this city will be free from corruption and we can all live in peace.
Skyrim.esm|0423AE: Do you have any idea how busy I am? My mixture could be burning as we speak!
Skyrim.esm|0423AF: You here for lessons like Ingun? Or did you wish to buy something?
Skyrim.esm|0423B0: Take a look at my supplies if you must, but do hurry... I'm not getting any younger you know!
Skyrim.esm|0423B4: Thanks again for delivering that ore sample.
Skyrim.esm|0423B5: Where's my head? I never even greeted you properly.
Skyrim.esm|0423B6: I apologize in advance for my husband's crankiness; I promise you he means well.
Skyrim.esm|0423B9: Please, please. I need but a septim. A septim is all I ask. Is that so bad?
Skyrim.esm|0423BA: I can't take this place anymore. The trees... all the colors, but the city, so dark. Why? It makes no sense.
Skyrim.esm|0423BB: So cold, so hungry, so tired.
Skyrim.esm|0423BC: No one cares about us! No one! We could die and no one would ever even know! They'd just throw our corpses in the canal!
Skyrim.esm|0423BD: Are you scared of death? I am. I hear everything just stops... forever. It just all goes away, but you don't realize it. I don't want to die.
Skyrim.esm|0423BE: Sure, sure. I'm a dirty beggar... why would you want to even speak to me.
Skyrim.esm|0423BF: To Oblivion with all of you! You rich piles of dung walking by us like we're not there! Pretending we don't exist! How dare you!
Skyrim.esm|0423C0: You think you know pain? Do you!? Try watching your wife and daughter burn alive after they've been ravaged by brigands! That's pain, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|0423C1: Edda... she's crazy as a loon, but at least she doesn't have the mind to realize what a horrible world this is. She's the lucky one.
Skyrim.esm|0423C2: Just leave Riften. Go far, far away and never return. This place is nothing but a rotten, stinking corpse that's sat in the sun far too long.
Skyrim.esm|0423C3: Greetings, honored friend. Could I interest you in some fine jewelry?
Skyrim.esm|0423C4: If anything pleases the eye, don't hesitate to make me an offer.
Skyrim.esm|0423C5: If you have any jewelry to sell, I'd be glad to pay you a fair sum in gold for it.
Skyrim.esm|04270D: You won't even live to regret this!
Skyrim.esm|04270E: I'll gut you like a horker!
Skyrim.esm|042717: Ooh, now that one must have hurt!
Skyrim.esm|04271E: I'll be back, and you'll be dead!
Skyrim.esm|042721: I'll live to drink another day!
Skyrim.esm|0427C6: You're wrong to come here, stranger. No rest in Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0427CB: These dreams are real I tell you. Real!
Skyrim.esm|0427CE: What is it? I haven't had much sleep.
Skyrim.esm|0427CF: If I get any rest soon, I'll count myself richer than a king.
Skyrim.esm|042841: He needs to die. He deserves to die. And it falls to you to kill him. Until he's dead... well, I'm sorry, but we would dishonor our oaths as Blades if we continued to help you.
Skyrim.esm|042842: That's fine. We needed his help. Now we don't, and it's long past time for him to pay for his crimes. And he's not just any dragon. He was the right hand of Alduin. He committed atrocities so infamous they are still remembered, thousands of years later.
Skyrim.esm|042843: Have you decided to do the right thing, Dragonborn?
Skyrim.esm|042844: Make your choice, Dragonborn. You're either with us or against us.
Skyrim.esm|042845: I wish there were another way, Dragonborn, but it falls to you to serve justice upon Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|042846: Justice can be harsh. But it is still justice. Paarthurnax deserves to die.
Skyrim.esm|042847: There's one more thing. We know about Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|042848: I knew we could count on you! Glad to have you back on our side.
Skyrim.esm|042849: At long last. An ancient evil is avenged. The shades of many Blades salute you this day.
Skyrim.esm|04284A: Paarthurnax. The dragon, that the Greybeards have been protecting for all these years.
Skyrim.esm|04284B: Ah, you're back. I hope you've made some progress on tracking down this Shout we need to defeat Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|042862: I will not lie to you, there is some risk involved. The last time the Torpor was imbibed could have been decades ago. But I swear upon Lady Mara that I will do everything within my power to prevent any harm from befalling you.
Skyrim.esm|042863: Quite amazing, yes. Alchemy and the blessings of a Divine distilled down into a ingestible liquid. Sadly, I have yet to see it function in person.
Skyrim.esm|042864: Yes. The Torpor grants an ability the priests of Vaermina called "The Dreamstride"; using dreams to travel distances in the real world.
Skyrim.esm|042865: No, all my research points in the opposite direction. This is the exact liquid the priests would drink. The only question in my mind is whether you will be granted The Dreamstride. Otherwise, the worst that could happen is a bad aftertaste. I swear upon Lady Mara that I would never let any harm befall you.
Skyrim.esm|042866: I assure you, the Dreamstride is well known in Vaerminian Lore. Sadly, I have yet to see it function in person.
Skyrim.esm|042867: As a sworn priest of Mara, the elixir won't work for me. The Torpor will only work for Priests of Vaermina, or the unaffiliated.
Skyrim.esm|042891: The Jarl does little to protect us these days.
Skyrim.esm|0428D6: All well and good. But did you know that he was Alduin's chief lieutenant in ancient times? Responsible for terrible atrocities? It's true that his crimes are long in the past, but justice does not count the passage of years.
Skyrim.esm|0428D7: He's a dragon. Not just any dragon, but the right hand of Alduin, responsible for many atrocities during the ancient Dragon War.
Skyrim.esm|0428D8: I'm afraid there's a further problem. A serious one. I've discovered who the Greybeards' leader really is.
Skyrim.esm|0428DA: Here's the big picture. He helped Alduin enslave our ancestors. He may have betrayed Alduin in the end, but that makes him worse, not better. We can't afford to give Paarthurnax the opportunity to betray us in turn, and return to his old master.
Skyrim.esm|0428DB: Paarthurnax was the author of many atrocities during the Dragon War - crimes great enough to be remembered for thousands of years. True, he turned traitor to Alduin and helped overthrow the Dragon Cult, but that does not excuse or expiate his previous deeds. Whether or not he has truly repented, or merely acted to save himself, justice demands that he pay with his life.
Skyrim.esm|0428DC: The Blades have been hunting him for centuries, but he was protected by the Greybeards and then the Emperors. Justice demands that he die for his crimes. Until he is dead, I'm afraid my oath as a Blade prevents me from offering you aid and comfort.
Skyrim.esm|042B0B: So I've heard. Thanks to you, this should be the last time they forget to pay. One small detail though - I need all of the gold they gave you. No one skims around here. Come back when you've got every last coin.
Skyrim.esm|042E30: A new face! Such a welcome sight in dreary old Morthal. Welcome friend, welcome!
Skyrim.esm|042E31: Don't fret over the gloom, there's nothing to fear! Old Lurbuk will brighten your spirits -- just lend him your ear!
Skyrim.esm|042E32: The good people of Morthal have yet to embrace my talent.
Skyrim.esm|042E33: Try as you may, they won't say "good day," but will just walk away. Worry not, Morthal! Lurbuk is here to stay!
Skyrim.esm|042E34: The fair Jonna does me a great courtesy by allowing me to entertain the guests of her noble establishment.
Skyrim.esm|042E35: I, Lurbuk the Great, will win the hearts of the people of Morthal one day, mark my words!
Skyrim.esm|042E36: Welcome to the Moorside. If you need anythin', I'll be 'round. Good to have a customer.
Skyrim.esm|042E37: There ain't much to offer, but if you wanna place dry to spend the night, I'll rent you a room.
Skyrim.esm|042E38: Runnin' an inn weren't my plan. But Falion decided to move here, so I joined him.
Skyrim.esm|042E39: I take what little business I can git. I'm grateful for anyone willin' to spend money here.
Skyrim.esm|042E3A: Ignore the Orc. He pays, so I let him stay.
Skyrim.esm|042E3B: You'll want to be careful travelin' at night. The marsh is a dangerous place.
Skyrim.esm|042E3C: So, life has brought you to Morthal, and to me. What purpose this serves, we will no doubt see. Welcome.
Skyrim.esm|042E3D: Tamriel is full of wisdom and magic, if one is willing to look for it. Few have their eyes open.
Skyrim.esm|042E3E: I speak, and others hear. It is my hope that instead they listen.
Skyrim.esm|042E3F: In Morthal, there is little to rule. But there is much to teach. And so, I am Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|042E40: All this talk of rebellion and strife. Too many are focused on what might be instead of what is. It tires me to talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|042E41: There is too much to learn in the world, too much to know, to fight over "laws" that have no real purpose.
Skyrim.esm|042E42: We're safe here now. But what of Morthal?
Skyrim.esm|042E43: I do what I can to see that Idgrod's time isn't wasted, and that she only has to deal with important matters.
Skyrim.esm|042E44: Too many bother my wife with their petty problems, and then don't even understand the advice she gives them.
Skyrim.esm|042E45: The only thing that matters to me is keeping my wife safe. I won't let anyone harm her.
Skyrim.esm|042E46: Morthal doesn't need any trouble. You keep your nose clean, understand?
Skyrim.esm|042E47: My mind weighs heavy enough. Leave me to my food.
Skyrim.esm|042E48: Morthal has enough problems without you bothering me.
Skyrim.esm|042E49: I'm under a lot of pressure. If you don't leave me alone, I won't be held responsible.
Skyrim.esm|042E4B: Please don't mind Joric. He's not mad, really he's not.
Skyrim.esm|042E4C: I don't... I haven't mother's gift, not quite, but I've seen things.
Skyrim.esm|042E4D: Someday I'll be Jarl. Mother reminds me often. I hope I can help the people of Morthal the way she has.
Skyrim.esm|042E4E: It's difficult sometimes... To know whether what I see is real or not.
Skyrim.esm|042E4F: You're different, aren't you? Not like anyone else. Not in Morthal, or Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|042E50: I don't understand. Why can't we go home?
Skyrim.esm|042E51: Gorm thinks I'm mad. He doesn't say it, but I know he thinks it. Is he right?
Skyrim.esm|042E52: My mother sees things. She doesn't know why, but she does. Only sometimes, she doesn't see me at all.
Skyrim.esm|042E53: It's Falion. He... He did it. I don't know what it was, but he did it.
Skyrim.esm|042E54: Keep your wits about you. Morthal's hardly a safe place.
Skyrim.esm|042E55: I'm just a poor man trying to make a living. I'd leave Morthal were it in my power.
Skyrim.esm|042E56: This damned war has cost us... me... everything.
Skyrim.esm|042E57: My boy's to grow up without a mother? What life is that? It's not right, I tell you.
Skyrim.esm|042E58: Hasn't been seen for a while. She just vanished without a trace.
Skyrim.esm|042E59: Have you seen my mother? I'm still waiting for her to come home.
Skyrim.esm|042E5A: Papa says that I shouldn't keep waiting. Why not? Mama has to come home some time.
Skyrim.esm|042E5C: Who are the Stormcloaks, and why would mama want to spend time with them instead of me?
Skyrim.esm|042E5D: I know you for what you are. This town's blood is ours. Leave it! Now we must return to our roles lest the sheep suspect the wolves.
Skyrim.esm|042E5E: The people here work so hard, and they never allow themselves a moment of fun. It's such a pity. It won't last forever though.
Skyrim.esm|042EC7: These people are like cattle. All they do is work, sleep and eat.
Skyrim.esm|042EC8: Gathering plants, picking mushrooms, brewing potions. It all gets so dreary after a while. This town needs something exciting to happen.
Skyrim.esm|042ECA: Thank you for saving me from Alva. I was completely under her evil spell. I'll never forgive myself for what happened to Helgi and my wife.
Skyrim.esm|042ECB: Alva's been good to me ever since... well, since the fire.
Skyrim.esm|042ECC: I just try and do my job, and not to think about what's happened. Try to find what happiness I can.
Skyrim.esm|042ECD: Ignore the talk of spirits and creatures and such. It's just superstition.
Skyrim.esm|042ECE: You are new to Morthal. We have not spoken before. This is interesting.
Skyrim.esm|042ECF: The changes in Morthal are of little consequence to me. So long as the laws are upheld, I do not care who does the upholding.
Skyrim.esm|042ED0: Morthal is a troubled place, and it is my duty to see it rest in peace.
Skyrim.esm|042ED1: Please remain indoors at night. It is dangerous to go outside.
Skyrim.esm|0439F9: Think the guards will be coming down, soon?
Skyrim.esm|0439FA: Nearly broke my back out in the mines today. Damn Nords should bleed for putting us through this!
Skyrim.esm|043A01: You make one move towards my skooma, and I shiv you.
Skyrim.esm|043A03: They keep the doors locked when they come down. You kill them, you're trapping us here with no food.
Skyrim.esm|043A05: Think maybe we should jump the guards when they come down next?
Skyrim.esm|043A06: Have any skooma? I need to get something from Grisvar.
Skyrim.esm|043A0C: Ah, it's not been more than a few days since they came down the last time.
Skyrim.esm|043A98: When the orcs invaded the temple, I fled. I left my brothers and sisters here to die.
Skyrim.esm|043CFB: Oh, half the field should do.
Skyrim.esm|043D01: I told you I don't like you going there. I've seen scavengers picking at the mammoths. They could be dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|043D07: I just think pollinating would be easier if I could do a lot of plants at once.
Skyrim.esm|043D08: I had a look at that boneyard the other day, Vantus. The one to the Northwest.
Skyrim.esm|043D0A: That should be all right. How much space were you planning?
Skyrim.esm|043D0E: You know, I was thinking of expanding the herb garden. Make room for some new plants I'm planning.
Skyrim.esm|043D0F: Half the field? What could need half the field?
Skyrim.esm|043D10: Uh... why don't we talk about this later?
Skyrim.esm|043D27: I hadn't asked you because I was afraid you'd say no. I appreciate your help. Here, I'm entrusting you with the dagger. Bring it to Andurs and he'll take care of the rest.
Skyrim.esm|043D28: If you're trying to ask if I tend the hall of the dead alone, then the answer is yes. You think anyone around here is going to lift a shovel to help me? Guess again. "Leave the dirty work to old Alessandra." Look, I didn't ask for this job, I got stuck with it thanks to my father.
Skyrim.esm|043D29: Yes. Perhaps one day I'll be able to make the journey, but for now I need to tend the dead.
Skyrim.esm|043D2A: Let's see. Last time I used the Dwemer Spoon was at my dear friend Boti's house in Ivarstead... Fellstar Farm I believe it's called. The orichalcum ingot should still be in Winterhold at the Frozen Hearth Inn. I don't know why I didn't just take it with me. And last is the soul gem. I left that one in Windhelm at the White Phial alchemy shop. It was a good trade too. Ah well.
Skyrim.esm|043D2B: You don't have to, because it's utter nonsense. It's never going to work. No, no, no. What I need to do is to reverse that. Draw the harmonic energy into the reagent and then apply it with the proper incantation. What was I thinking? It would never work that first way. Why didn't you tell me?
Skyrim.esm|043D2C: I felt a caress on my cheek some time ago... I thought it to be a trick of the wind, but I think it was a message from my father. We've made peace with one another without even uttering a single word and I'm content for the first time in my life. I thank you. Here, please accept this as a token of my appreciation for your selfless action.
Skyrim.esm|043D2D: I'll tell you why you won't tell me. Because you were absolutely right not to. It's a fundamental rule of magic and it would be ridiculous. You can't draw from harmonic energy without a soul siphon. Since you're so well informed I'm certain you've solved the problem with thermic drift, right?
Skyrim.esm|043D2E: What's not to get? It's as simple as Traven's First Axiom of Magic... or was it Galerion's Law of Casting. Hmm. I'll tell you what, let me find out where that's from. I'll need to look through my library... and get you an answer. Umm... what were we talking about?
Skyrim.esm|043DE2: Yes, I could use some help with that. An assistant perhaps? No, no, no. Maybe a familiar. No, too messy. Well, at any rate, organization is not my strong suit. Not at all. I need as much help as I can get. In fact, if you could retrieve a few things for me, I could start on my next set of experiments.
Skyrim.esm|043DE3: A shame. Perhaps another time then.
Skyrim.esm|043DE4: Excellent. I can't wait. Well... why are you still standing here? Oh, right. You need to know where they are! That would help.
Skyrim.esm|043DE5: As you can imagine, I tend to forget things often. Leave things around. I really must learn to put these things away. What I need is my Dwemer Stirring Spoon, my Orichalcum Ingot and my Grand Soul Gem. In fact, I could use them immediately. Bring those items back to me and I'll be happy to experiment on you. No, no... that isn't right. I'd be happy to reward you.
Skyrim.esm|043DE6: My father was a Priest of Arkay. All my life was spent around the dead, being forced to learn the proper burial rituals and prayers. I never had any friends; who'd want to spend time with "the little weird girl who hangs around dead people?" So I grew up with a miserable trade. This is all I know how to do... prepare and inter the deceased.
Skyrim.esm|043DE7: Please, come back and speak to me anytime.
Skyrim.esm|043DE8: Be cautious, the pilgrimage to the Shrine is not an easy one.
Skyrim.esm|043DE9: I'd ask you maintain civility while you're here.
Skyrim.esm|043DEC: You're welcome in Riften as long as you continue to obey our laws.
Skyrim.esm|043DEF: I'm keeping my eye on you.
Skyrim.esm|043DF0: I have to remember... ward first, then summon.
Skyrim.esm|043DF1: Okay, now where did I put those spiders?
Skyrim.esm|043DF2: Someone actually interested in what I'm doing? Amazing! Well, allow me to explain. My experimentation involves a magical construct and a reagent that will allow the construct to maintain a field of permanent harmonic energy!
Skyrim.esm|043DF3: I'd like to make the journey to the Hall of the Dead in Windhelm and give my ceremonial dagger to Andurs, the caretaker. I believe once it's interred with my father's remains I'll be at peace with his memory.
Skyrim.esm|043DF4: Items? What items? Let me see those. Hmm. I'm not sure how you managed it, but these are exactly what I need to start my next set of experiments. Wonderful! Well, at least allow me to reward you for your uncanny foresight.
Skyrim.esm|043DF8: The pilgrimage is complete, I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|043DF9: Anything else before you make the journey?
Skyrim.esm|043DFA: Come to laugh at the grave tender?
Skyrim.esm|043E01: Did we have an appointment? No... it was a delivery? I can't remember.
Skyrim.esm|044045: I understand. But you're being paranoid. I'm inspecting security, not charging off into battle. There's not a lot that can go wrong.
Skyrim.esm|044046: Son, when the Emperor's safety is concerned, anything could go wrong. Off with you, now. And good travels.
Skyrim.esm|044047: Farewell, father. I'll return as soon as I'm able.
Skyrim.esm|044048: Well, this is it, then. Look after yourself, Gaius. You're doing your duty, and I'm proud of you. But you'd better come back to me. You hear?
Skyrim.esm|044049: Oh, Faida. I may travel alone, but you know I carry you always in my heart. I'll see you soon.
Skyrim.esm|04404A: No, so your best plan is to follow him after he leaves Dragon Bridge. However, there is one other option... We know there's an actual written travel schedule, somewhere in the Penitus Oculatus outpost. Securing that would, obviously, be most beneficial...
Skyrim.esm|04404B: It's a small town, with one rather unique feature - it houses Skyrim's only Penitus Oculatus outpost. It was established by Commander Maro when he first arrived in Skyrim, several months ago. When he began preparing for the Emperor's initial visit.
Skyrim.esm|04404C: Father, you worry too much. I'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|04404D: Yes. Do not kill Gaius Maro in Dragon Bridge, or on the road. Kill him in one of the other major cities he'll be visiting. There, the body will be discovered quickly, as will the letter implicating Gaius Maro and the Stormcloaks in the Emperor's assassination. Do that, and Astrid has authorized me to grant you a rather unique bonus. It is a special token, to be given to Olava the Feeble, in Whiterun. Olava is an old and dear friend, and a powerful seer. The token entitles you to a reading of your future. It's an opportunity one should not pass up.
Skyrim.esm|04404E: I know you will. But all the same, remember everything I said. Stay alert, and when you get to the cities, make your observations and move on.
Skyrim.esm|04421A: Let those that watch from Sovngarde envy us this day!
Skyrim.esm|04421D: Hold, Alduin on the Wing! Sister Hawk, grant us your sacred breath to make this contract heard!
Skyrim.esm|04421F: He will come. He cannot ignore our defiance. And why should he fear us, even now?
Skyrim.esm|044220: We shall see soon enough. Alduin approaches!
Skyrim.esm|044221: We've bloodied him well. Four of his kin have fallen to my blade alone this day.
Skyrim.esm|044223: But none have yet stood against Alduin himself. Galthor, Sorri, Birkir...
Skyrim.esm|044225: No. We will deal with Alduin ourselves, here and now.
Skyrim.esm|044226: You do not understand. Alduin cannot be slain like a lesser dragon. He is beyond our strength.
Skyrim.esm|04422A: It's no use! Use the Scroll, Felldir! Now!
Skyrim.esm|04422C: Yes, the World-Eater is gone... may the spirits have mercy on our souls.
Skyrim.esm|04422E: They did not have Dragonrend. Once we bring him down, I promise I will have his head.
Skyrim.esm|04422F: Felldir! We agreed not to use it!
Skyrim.esm|044230: I never agreed. And if you are right, I will not need it.
Skyrim.esm|044231: Why does Alduin hang back? We've staked everything on this plan of yours, old man.
Skyrim.esm|044372: No, I'm sorry. I'll just... I'll know it if I find it. Does that make sense?
Skyrim.esm|044373: Is there something you need, dear?
Skyrim.esm|044374: All right, can you describe it to me?
Skyrim.esm|044375: If it does to you, dear, that's all that matters.
Skyrim.esm|044376: Do you like magic? Falion says I'm good at learning. I like learning about magic.
Skyrim.esm|044377: My mother and father died years ago. Falion takes care of me now.
Skyrim.esm|044378: I asked Falion if someday I could learn from the mages in Winterhold, but he doesn't like that idea.
Skyrim.esm|044379: If you need any alchemy supplies, I'll do my best to provide them. Assuming I haven't used them all up.
Skyrim.esm|04437A: Jorgen and I both work hard, all the time. Morthal is important to both of us.
Skyrim.esm|04437B: I don't get many customers, but that leaves me time to try things out. Experiment, I guess.
Skyrim.esm|04437C: Unless it's about the mill, I don't have time to talk. Too much to do.
Skyrim.esm|04437D: We've gone from a Jarl lost in her own head to a Jarl that cares for nothing but herself. Lot of good that does us.
Skyrim.esm|04437E: I just want to be left alone to do my job, and to enjoy what little free time I have when I have it.
Skyrim.esm|04437F: I... I'm not sure. I think perhaps, but I don't know what it is.
Skyrim.esm|0443E4: You can have anything except the scroll of course.
Skyrim.esm|0443E5: Oh alright.  Well I'll be here for a bit if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0443E6: If Vokun is as powerful as I think, I may need a little help um ... dealing ...with him.
Skyrim.esm|0443E7: I'm Anska.  Are you here to help?
Skyrim.esm|0443E9: Great, with the two of us this should be easy.
Skyrim.esm|0443EA: I've finally located Vokun's crypt! The scroll I'm looking for just has to be here.
Skyrim.esm|044433: That's right. Whole group of the natives called the Forsworn rose up against the Nords. Drove them out. Then Ulfric Stormcloak came with his militia.
