That must have been a sight to see, him shouting folks to death. You'd think that'd be the end of it, but those Forsworn are still in the hills, and they're as wild as wolves.
Skyrim.esm|044436: It's the whole reason Ulfric revolted against the Empire. Well, the first reason anyway. Forsworn had taken over Markarth, and Ulfric and his men drove them out. Empire promised they'd be free to worship Talos afterwards. Pity no one told the Elves. Whole group of those Thalmor came and they demanded Ulfric's arrest.
Skyrim.esm|044437: That's right, at least according to them scholars. Stonework's clearly not Nord, that much is for sure. Old Elven fella named Calcelmo is up in Understone Keep's the one you want to talk to for more about that. Wrote all those books.
Skyrim.esm|04443A: Before the Nords came, we worshipped the old gods, had our own kingdom. Times have been good and bad since then, but some folks couldn't handle not ruling their own land. Those are the Forsworn. The Forsworn follow the old ways, but some of those were best forgotten. Blood sacrifices, communing with Daedra. It's the road to ruin.
Skyrim.esm|04443B: It's an old city. Legends say Dwarves built it first. Then after they vanished, the natives moved in. Finally, the Nords took over around Tiber Septim's time. That bent a few folks' arms the wrong way.
Skyrim.esm|04443C: What's this? Oh, that's right, the timbers from Anga's Mill. I swear, that Aeri and her men would make fine Stormcloak recruits. Pity we can't spare the lumber to conscript them. Here. Take this. For your time and all that.
Skyrim.esm|04443D: That would be Jarl Igmund. Young for a ruler of a Hold, especially the Reach. Wasn't surprising he stayed loyal to the Empire. His father helped capture Ulfric Stormcloak after the Markarth Incident.
Skyrim.esm|04443E: Since the Stormcloaks took over, Jarl Thongvor is in charge. It'll be lean times for the Reach with a Silver-Blood ruling it, that's for certain.
Skyrim.esm|04444B: Well, look at you, thanks. Take it to Voada, she handles all the dogs for the Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|044489: My family has been doing it for years. You need one out in the Reach. Don't want to be alone when some Forsworn comes swinging. I personally trained a whole litter for the Jarl. That reminds me, I was going to deliver some dog food to the Keep. Spiced beef. Their favorite.
Skyrim.esm|04448C: You need a reliable horse in the Reach. That's what we have.
Skyrim.esm|04448E: Traveling in the Reach is dangerous. You need a good carriage.
Skyrim.esm|044491: City of Markarth sure is a beauty. No doubt about that.
Skyrim.esm|04449D: A war dog is good company and good protection.
Skyrim.esm|044622: Whatever happened to Ennodius? He just up and left.
Skyrim.esm|044624: Oh, I've heard this one before. Watch out Divines, poor Kodrir curses you all for giving him life!
Skyrim.esm|044628: Watch your mouth with me you pup! Who do you think you are?
Skyrim.esm|04462E: You're all heart, Kodrir. I bet Lady Mara is weeping a special tear for you right now.
Skyrim.esm|04462F: Don't know why you're asking me. But I'm glad that damn Imperial is gone. Something wrong about him.
Skyrim.esm|044630: I swear that woman Aeri doesn't appreciate all the work I do for her damn mill.
Skyrim.esm|044632: I run the mill, so I'm a busy woman. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|044633: A good lumberjack is quiet, hard-working, and honest.
Skyrim.esm|044635: I used to work at Anga's Mill, but I... I had to leave.
Skyrim.esm|044637: Someone's after me I tell you. They're watching me. Somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|04463A: I don't need to talk to you. Get lost.
Skyrim.esm|04463B: I work at the mill. Chop trees, carry logs, saw lumber, you know.
Skyrim.esm|04463D: Aeri's the boss. Should talk to her if you want work.
Skyrim.esm|04464A: My cousin's out fighting dragons, and what do I get? Guard duty.
Skyrim.esm|044666: Been so long since I killed a Stormcloak, my sword arm's getting flabby.
Skyrim.esm|044683: I cannot thank you enough for all of the help.
Skyrim.esm|0447F9: When I grow up, will I go to the College in Winterhold?
Skyrim.esm|0447FA: It's just a dream, child. Nothing more.
Skyrim.esm|0447FB: When I'm known throughout Tamriel, you'll regret this decision.
Skyrim.esm|0447FC: Nah. Just... don't. Leave me outta it.
Skyrim.esm|044800: Oh, of course! I'm sorry, I didn't mean to say that it's not.
Skyrim.esm|044801: Oh Hroggar, you know I'm always hungry. Don't worry, I'll eat when I need to.
Skyrim.esm|044802: Hush now. Think no more of it.
Skyrim.esm|044803: No. They ain't. And they aren't gonna. If you weren't payin' for your room, I'd have thrown you out a long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|044804: Well dear, you know where to find me if it ever becomes too much for you.
Skyrim.esm|044809: Jonna, do you think the townspeople are warming to my serenades?
Skyrim.esm|04480A: Still the same. I tell you, something's got to be done.
Skyrim.esm|04480B: It was scary. It was like it was real!
Skyrim.esm|04480C: I'm thinking of composing a song about you, Jonna. Would you prefer that I proclaim your beauty, your strength, your...
Skyrim.esm|04480E: I was there only for a short time. Much of what I have learned has been on my own. And I will teach you. You have no reason to go there.
Skyrim.esm|04480F: Good. Concentration is paramount when dealing with magical forces.
Skyrim.esm|044810: Morthal will be mine to govern someday. I have a great deal to learn.
Skyrim.esm|044811: I had a bad dream again last night.
Skyrim.esm|044812: But I'm learning about magic, and that's what they do there. Shouldn't I go?
Skyrim.esm|044813: When the time is right, friend. When we're sure.
Skyrim.esm|044814: Yes, but they'll come around eventually. You'll see.
Skyrim.esm|044817: Oh, I quite like living in the hall. Mother says it's an honor to live there and serve Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|044818: Have you been working on your concentration?
Skyrim.esm|044819: Because an unfocused mind will almost certainly be obliterated. Destroyed by the forces you attempt to control and yield.
Skyrim.esm|04481A: Thank you, dear. It must be nice to get out of the hall every now and then.
Skyrim.esm|04481B: The longer we wait, the worse it gets.
Skyrim.esm|044820: Oh no! I couldn't. Mother says they are a burden we must bear for our gift.
Skyrim.esm|044821: Oh, yes. All the time. They come with the visions, as always.
Skyrim.esm|044822: Eat? No, I don't think I'm... hungry. Not now, at least.
Skyrim.esm|044823: No, I don't think that will be necessary.
Skyrim.esm|044824: Let's talk about it some other time.
Skyrim.esm|044825: Have no fear. I believe it is within you to excel in your studies. But you must learn to concentrate!
Skyrim.esm|044826: How are the headaches, dear? You or your mother still having them?
Skyrim.esm|044827: Right. Okay, tell me if you change yer mind.
Skyrim.esm|044828: I dreamt that you went away. You made monsters.
Skyrim.esm|044829: I feel wonderful, just like every night.
Skyrim.esm|04482A: Umm... Did you want somethin' to eat?
Skyrim.esm|04482C: But you went there, didn't you? Isn't that where you learned?
Skyrim.esm|04482D: Do you need anything? Are you hungry?
Skyrim.esm|04482E: Are you sure you don't want something for them? I may be able to brew you something to help.
Skyrim.esm|04482F: No interest in being immortalized in song? Remembered and praised for all eternity?
Skyrim.esm|044830: What can't you get me, sweetie?
Skyrim.esm|044831: Oh, I shouldn't be surprised if they come 'round. And if you're never seen or heard from agin.
Skyrim.esm|044832: The College is not the only place to learn about magic.
Skyrim.esm|044968: Suleyki mulaag, Paarthurnax. My power has waxed, while yours has waned.
Skyrim.esm|044969: Dovahkiin! Use Dragonrend, if you know it!
Skyrim.esm|044BD9: So, you're Brynjolf's new protege, eh? Don't look like much to me.
Skyrim.esm|044BDA: It's one thing to say you've got the skills to be a thief. It's another to actually use them.
Skyrim.esm|044BDB: It's been a long time since anyone joined us. Seems like people are in a rush to leave.
Skyrim.esm|044BDC: I hear even the simple jobs are becoming difficult. It never used to be like this.
Skyrim.esm|044BDD: Less coin in everyone's pocket means less coin in mine.
Skyrim.esm|044BDE: Something has to change soon or I may as well pack this place up and leave it for the skeevers.
Skyrim.esm|044BDF: You better have coin to pay for your drinks. There's no handouts here.
Skyrim.esm|044BE0: Got a lot of nerve coming down here. People tend to get hurt in the Ratway.
Skyrim.esm|044BE1: I wouldn't stay here long if I were you.
Skyrim.esm|044BE2: There's a reason the city guard doesn't come down here you know.
Skyrim.esm|044BE3: Don't make trouble or else I'll ask Dirge to "walk" you out.
Skyrim.esm|044BE4: Even if you're one of us, you better not make trouble.
Skyrim.esm|044BE5: I don't care if you're best buddies with the Guild Master. I'll still smash in your skull if you try anything.
Skyrim.esm|044BE6: The last person that made trouble for Vekel ended up floating in the canal... catch my drift?
Skyrim.esm|044BE8: Stay outta trouble, or there's gonna be trouble.
Skyrim.esm|044BE9: Last person who made trouble for Vekel ended up with a smashed head.
Skyrim.esm|044BEA: Vekel doesn't like strangers snooping around the Flagon.
Skyrim.esm|044BEB: There's two things to do at the Flagon: spend coin and then get out.
Skyrim.esm|044BEC: If it isn't the best brawler in Skyrim. Good to see you again!
Skyrim.esm|044BED: Too lazy to walk, eh? Well, you've found the right place.
Skyrim.esm|044BF2: If it's horses you want, you'll want to speak to Hofgrir at the stables.
Skyrim.esm|044BF3: Hofgrir is a great teacher. I've learned so much about horses from him.
Skyrim.esm|044BFF: There's a lever, right there in the center. Hurry, before more of them show up!
Skyrim.esm|044C01: What? The bo... Oh. Oh dear. I shouldn't have taken them, I know! It was stupid. I was stupid. It won't happen again. Help me get out of here, and I'll help you find them. Please!
Skyrim.esm|044C02: You have to let me out. They're going to do something awful to me!
Skyrim.esm|044C03: Thank you, thank you! I promise I'll help. And then I'll go back to the College and I'll beg them to let me back in.
Skyrim.esm|044C04: I don't have them anymore. She took them... The Caller. She's the one who put me in here! Please, let me out of here!
Skyrim.esm|044C05: Please, please let me out of here!
Skyrim.esm|044C06: Yes, yes! Did Arch-Mage Aren send you? He sent you to rescue me, didn't he?
Skyrim.esm|044C4F: Lost funt. You are too late, Alduin!
Skyrim.esm|044C50: You may have picked up the weapons of my ancient foes, but you are not their equal!
Skyrim.esm|044C52: Bahloki nahkip sillesejoor. My belly is full of the souls of your fellow mortals, Dovahkiin. Die now and await your fate in Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|044CED: Meyz mul, Dovahkiin. You have become strong. But I am Al-du-in, Firstborn of Akatosh! Mulaagi zok lot! I cannot be slain here, by you or anyone else!
Skyrim.esm|044D0D: I'll tell you one thing, if you don't make peace with them soon you may get a walkout on your hands. Then we're both in trouble.
Skyrim.esm|044D0E: If only Maven would stop raising the price on it maybe I could afford to stock more cases.
Skyrim.esm|044D0F: Talen my friend, up for some fishing this week?
Skyrim.esm|044D10: Oh come now, what's a dragon or two compared to a day full of fishing!
Skyrim.esm|044D11: Why don't they listen to me, Keerava? Why won't they do as I say?
Skyrim.esm|044D12: Keerava, we're running low on Black-Briar Mead.
Skyrim.esm|044D13: That's not funny, Bolli. People are scared.
Skyrim.esm|044D14: I don't know Bolli. It's getting dangerous outside the walls.
Skyrim.esm|044D15: We could always look for another brand.
Skyrim.esm|044D16: Don't worry, the time will come when we can walk away from here.
Skyrim.esm|044D18: They always try to take advantage of me. Give them an inch and they'll take a mile.
Skyrim.esm|044D1A: I hope so. I can't stand this city anymore.
Skyrim.esm|044D1B: Maybe they're afraid of you. Maybe it's your breath. How should I know?
Skyrim.esm|044D1C: Damn it, Talen. We still don't have enough this year to get out of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|044D1D: That isn't an option. Maven sees me serve a competitor's brand here and we may as well dig our own graves.
Skyrim.esm|044DD2: You have wax in your ears? I said no one dies. We're not assassins. Well guess what... now you've inherited their debt. Don't come back to me until you have every last coin.
Skyrim.esm|0452EE: To call it a guild is ridiculous. How can people who would betray one another over a gold coin be considered part of an association? They're the worst kind. Even the Dark Brotherhood abides by a strict set of rules and tradition. These thieves are just rabble. What do you think?
Skyrim.esm|0452EF: Many years ago I lost my blade, "Grimsever," within a Dwemer ruin. I took it as a sign that I was wasting my days in search of wealth. You and I are alike. We seek challenge and great fortune. But for me, that's where the similarities end. You see, Riften is my great beast to be slain and my fortune comes from gratitude and trust.
Skyrim.esm|0452FD: I was adventuring in the depths of the ruins when I was attacked by a massive construct... like nothing I had ever seen. When the colossus struck, Grimsever was knocked from my grasp and I was wounded badly. It was only through blind luck that I was able to crawl away from the Dwemer abomination and make my way to the surface. I must have collapsed, because the next thing I remember is Aerin standing over me tending to my wounds.
Skyrim.esm|045304: It was lost years ago in a Dwemer ruin. Without it, I feel almost as defenseless as a newborn. I don't think I'll ever be able to find a replacement.
Skyrim.esm|045305: Hardly! In fact, I don't know how I get anything done with all of the distractions I have to put up with. My wife Hafjorg, well, she's a necessary evil I suppose. Not a lick of talent when it comes to alchemy, but she keeps me fed. Then there's Ingun Black-Briar. Bright young thing and a worthy apprentice, when she isn't wasting my rare ingredients.
Skyrim.esm|045311: I couldn't ask you to undertake such a trecherous journey, it would be a fool's errand. But I see that spark in your eye and I know better than to warn you away. Grimsever rests with the Dwemer ruin of Mzinchaleft. Tread carefully, friend... those same ruins almost took my life.
Skyrim.esm|04532D: I see I still have much to learn. Lead on.
Skyrim.esm|045388: Just stay away from me and I'll forget we had this discussion.
Skyrim.esm|045389: Watch yourself around here, it isn't safe.
Skyrim.esm|0453C2: Yes, quite a shame. It was a fine weapon.
Skyrim.esm|0453DB: Its been difficult. I've taken the burdens of this city's problems upon myself and I keep running into impossible obstacles. Corruption, lies and deceit are the order of the day here. If it wasn't for Aerin, the young man who saved my life, I think I'd already have given up long ago.
Skyrim.esm|0453EB: Word is Ulfric Stormcloak escaped from the Imperials. War's only going to get worse, now.
Skyrim.esm|0453EC: They say a dragon attacked Helgen. Sounds like hogwash to me.
Skyrim.esm|0453ED: We've got something of a love triangle here in Riverwood. Faendal and Sven, both vying for Camilla's affections.
Skyrim.esm|0453EE: Oh, you wanted the attic room? Why didn't you say so the first time? The thing is... we don't actually have an attic room. But I think I know what you mean. Go ahead and make yourself at home.
Skyrim.esm|0453EF: Next to the door, the room on the left.
Skyrim.esm|0453F3: Ain't used to strangers here in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|0453F4: You're pretty, I'll give ya that. Just stay away from my husband, Alvor.
Skyrim.esm|0453F5: Alvor and I, we got a daughter. Little Dorthe.
Skyrim.esm|0453F6: My girl likes to work the forge with her papa.
Skyrim.esm|0453F7: All of that blacksmithing... It's not good for a little girl. Ain't... ladylike.
Skyrim.esm|0453F8: Dorthe needs to spend more time in the garden. I could use the help.
Skyrim.esm|0453F9: It don't matter what I grow - my Alvor, he'll eat anything.
Skyrim.esm|0453FA: Maybe I'll do turnips next. Hmmm... Or maybe radishes?
Skyrim.esm|0453FF: New to Riverwood? If you're looking for work, go see Hod at the mill.
Skyrim.esm|045400: We're a small village, but we work hard, and take care of our own.
Skyrim.esm|045401: I own the lumber mill, and pay a fair wage. See my husband Hod if you're looking for work.
Skyrim.esm|045402: Only worry we've got is the war reaching this far.
Skyrim.esm|045403: Years past, the lumber was used for houses or furniture. Now, the soldiers just buy it all.
Skyrim.esm|045404: The thought of my wood being used for weapons... Makes me sick.
Skyrim.esm|045405: No use thinkin' of things I can't change. Too much work to be done...
Skyrim.esm|04540A: You look like a traveler. Someone that has seen far away places and heard new stories.
Skyrim.esm|04540C: I've been trained as a skald, as was my father, and my father's father before him.
Skyrim.esm|04540E: You should come hear me play at the Sleeping Giant Inn. For a small sum you can even pick the song.
Skyrim.esm|045410: My time at the Bards College was brief. A prodigy has little need for formal education.
Skyrim.esm|045411: I'm composing a new song. A ballad, for Camilla Valerius.
Skyrim.esm|045415: Some day Camilla will know of my affections. Some day...
Skyrim.esm|04541A: A new face. And a right fair one at that.
Skyrim.esm|04541B: A new face. And a right ugly one at that. No offence.
Skyrim.esm|04541C: Got business here in Riverwood, eh? There are worse villages.
Skyrim.esm|04541D: At least the damned war hasn't gotten here. Yet.
Skyrim.esm|04541E: We work hard, try to do right by our families.
Skyrim.esm|04541F: Me, I've got my axe, my wife Gerdur and my boy Frodnar. That's all I need.
Skyrim.esm|045420: Woodcutting's my trade. Best log splitter from here to Riften.
Skyrim.esm|045421: It's all in the way you hold the axe. Caress it, like any wench, and she'll do ya right.
Skyrim.esm|045422: You met Camilla yet? Wouldn't mind getting my hands on those trunks. He he he he.
Skyrim.esm|045423: Now you, lass... You've got some beautiful trunks yourself... Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|045424: Well... I'll let you go about your business.
Skyrim.esm|04542A: You're new around here, so I'll go easy on ya. But don't get on my bad side.
Skyrim.esm|04542B: Get me mad, and I'll prank ya. And not a little. A lot.
Skyrim.esm|04542C: Ah, everybody around here's so serious.
Skyrim.esm|04542D: My friend Dorthe doesn't like my pranking. "You're being mean, Frodnar!" So what if I am?
Skyrim.esm|04542E: Mother wants me to work at the mill. But I ain't nobody's slave.
Skyrim.esm|04542F: You gotta have better things to do than talk to me all day...
Skyrim.esm|045436: Greetings, sister Elf. Good to see a familiar face so far from home.
Skyrim.esm|045437: Greetings, brother Elf. Good to see a familiar face so far from home.
Skyrim.esm|045438: Did I see you talking to Sven? Maybe not. Maybe...nevermind. But I would stay away from him if I were you.
Skyrim.esm|045439: There's beauty here unmatched in all of Skyrim, to be sure.
Skyrim.esm|04543A: Maybe you've already met Camilla Valerius?
Skyrim.esm|04543B: If only Camilla shared my affections. Or even knew about them.
Skyrim.esm|04543C: Are you a hunter, by chance? The hills surrounding the village are ripe with game.
Skyrim.esm|04543D: I work at the lumber mill, but I escape into the forest whenever possible.
Skyrim.esm|04543E: I miss Valenwood, but the wilds here are... serviceable.
Skyrim.esm|04543F: I don't like to brag, but I once took down a bear at three hundred yards. In a blizzard.
Skyrim.esm|045441: We got rooms and food. Drink, too. I cook. Ain't much else to tell.
Skyrim.esm|045442: Need a room, talk to Delphine.
Skyrim.esm|045445: I ain't lookin' to make friends.
Skyrim.esm|045447: You like Skeever liver. No? Tough luck.
Skyrim.esm|045448: Food's hot. Never said it was good.
Skyrim.esm|04544B: You're that visitor, been pokin' around.
Skyrim.esm|04544C: I'm the innkeeper. It's my business to keep track of strangers.
Skyrim.esm|04544D: We don't get a lot of travelers here in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|04544E: Most of my customers are locals, come to relax after a hard day's toil.
Skyrim.esm|04544F: So what's your story? Just here to... relax?
Skyrim.esm|045450: You look like you could use an ale.
Skyrim.esm|045451: If you're staying the night, I've got a free room.
Skyrim.esm|045452: Wanderer like you must have quite an appetite. Some food?
Skyrim.esm|045457: You're a strapping young man. Don't be a stranger.
Skyrim.esm|045458: The men here like my attention, and I like theirs. Best know your place.
Skyrim.esm|045459: You're not from around here. That makes two of us.
Skyrim.esm|04545A: I came here from the Imperial Province, to work with my brother Lucan.
Skyrim.esm|04545B: It got bad back in Cyrodiil. The war with the Thalmor ruined... everything.
Skyrim.esm|04545C: I came to Skyrim looking for a better life. So what did I get? Another war.
Skyrim.esm|04545D: I just want to find a good husband, and start a family of my own.
Skyrim.esm|04545F: The Riverwood Trader is everything you need in a general store.
Skyrim.esm|045460: My sister Camilla or I will be happy to help you.
Skyrim.esm|045461: Maybe you're looking for something... particular?
Skyrim.esm|045462: We carry just about anything you could need...
Skyrim.esm|045463: I know some of it looks like junk, but everything's a treasure in the right light.
Skyrim.esm|045468: Ain't every day we get visitors in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|045469: Whatever you need. By Ysmir, if it's simple and strong, I can forge it.
Skyrim.esm|04546A: You lookin' for a new blade?
Skyrim.esm|04546B: Iron or steel is what you need. Leave that fancy Elven stuff to the Elves.
Skyrim.esm|04546C: Iron and steel mostly. I might even have some of your fancy Elven pieces lying around somewhere...
Skyrim.esm|04546D: I could forge you a warhammer that would crush a giant's head like a walnut.
Skyrim.esm|04546E: You the sneaky type? Got some nice daggers around here somewhere...
Skyrim.esm|045473: Papa says I'm too friendly with strangers, but you seem all right.
Skyrim.esm|045474: My father is Alvor, the blacksmith. I'm his assistant. I mean, apprentice.
Skyrim.esm|045475: Some day I'm going to forge my own sword!
Skyrim.esm|045476: Papa says I'm not ready for weapons. So I make lots of horseshoes and hobnails.
Skyrim.esm|045477: Mama doesn't think I'm girlish enough. But that's just stupid girl talk.
Skyrim.esm|045478: Papa may have wanted a son, but he got me. He's a lucky man.
Skyrim.esm|045479: Ain't nothin' a boy can do that I can't do better.
Skyrim.esm|04547C: Nobody believes me, but I tell you, I saw a dragon!
Skyrim.esm|04547D: It's my boy Sven you need to listen to. He sings at the inn, most every night.
Skyrim.esm|04547E: Sven has the voice of a snow lark. Only good thing he got from his father.
Skyrim.esm|04547F: My boy fancies that Camilla Valerius. She'll come around soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|045480: Camilla, I've seen her talking to that Faendal. Filthy elf needs to keep to his own kind...
Skyrim.esm|045481: What happened to my Skyrim? Everywhere you look, cats, lizards and elves. It's sickening.
Skyrim.esm|045482: I raised my Sven to be strong, and proud. A true Nord.
Skyrim.esm|045483: You're kinda fuzzy. What's wrong with ya?
Skyrim.esm|04548C: I'm spoken for, you know. So don't get any ideas.
Skyrim.esm|045869: The war keeps most folks away, these days.
Skyrim.esm|0458BA: So I like to have a little fun, so what?
Skyrim.esm|04590A: Grrr. Fine, let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|04590C: You bring an outsider here, and now insist I call on Malacath for help, when he has clearly forsaken me? You try my patience, Atub.
Skyrim.esm|04590D: Doing nothing will not grant our tribe relief from this curse. We must try.
Skyrim.esm|045918: Hmm, yes. But your su'um is strong. I do not doubt that you can convince him of the need.
Skyrim.esm|045919: Lot krongrah. You truly have the Voice of a dovah. Alduin's allies will think twice after this victory.
Skyrim.esm|04591A: Ni liivrah hin moro. True, this is not the final krongrah - victory. But not even the heroes of old were able to defeat Alduin in open battle. Alduin always was pahlok - arrogant in his power. Uznahgar paar. He took domination as his birthright. This should shake the loyalty of the dov who serve him.
Skyrim.esm|04591B: Yes... one of his allies could tell us. Motmahus... But it will not be so easy to... convince one of them to betray him. Perhaps the hofkahsejun - the palace in Whiterun... Dragonsreach. It was originally built to house a captive dovah. A fine place to trap one of Alduin's allies, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|045C49: Why, why have you done this to us? I cannot protect you if you murder us. This is the only warning you will receive.
Skyrim.esm|045D03: Then I would be glad to help you with your mad dragon-trapping scheme. But getting both sides to agree to a truce will be difficult at this point. The bitterness has gone too deep. Maybe... hmm... what of the Greybeards? They are respected by all Nords. High Hrothgar is neutral territory. If the Greybeards were willing to host a peace council... then maybe Ulfric and Tullius would have to listen.
Skyrim.esm|045D04: No. I can't risk weakening the city while we are under the threat of enemy attack. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|045D05: I must have misheard you. I thought you asked me to help you trap a dragon in my palace.
Skyrim.esm|045D06: What do you think the Imperials would be doing while this dragon is busy slaughtering my men?
Skyrim.esm|045D07: Do you think the Stormcloaks will sit idle while this dragon is slaughtering my men and burning down my city?
Skyrim.esm|045D09: Yes. This was ages ago, you understand. There were more of us then. Before the bruniikke - the Akaviri - came and killed all my zeymah. I used to visit him from time to time. Nearly crazed by loneliness and captivity. Tiiraz sivaas. He did not even remember his own name. I do not know how he came to be caught. But the bronjun... the Jarl... was very proud of his pet. Paak! The hofkahsejun has been known as Dragonsreach ever since.
Skyrim.esm|045D0A: According to legend. Although I never thought to put the tale to the test. Jarl Olaf One-Eye it was, who later became High King. They say he Shouted it into submission in single combat atop Mount Anthor and brought it back to Whiterun. Numinex was the dragon's name. That's his skull decorating the main hall.
Skyrim.esm|045D93: What you're asking for is insane. Impossible! You want me to let a dragon into the heart of my city, with the threat of war on my doorstep?
Skyrim.esm|04601F: Perhaps... Perhaps it might. If nothing else, you could be an excellent sacrifice to Malacath. You really want to help? You go offer yourself at Malacath's Shrine. Petition him for help. He might not devour you whole.
Skyrim.esm|046021: Are you blind, or just stupid? You think the tribe has not seen you talking to me? You think they would not know? Do not mistake our life for one of stupidity. It would be the last mistake you make.
Skyrim.esm|046022: Hrmph. Breaking a curse is not a simple thing. It is dangerous. But... To have won against Malacath himself... That would be seen as real power.
Skyrim.esm|046023: You persist? Fine. You go tell Malacath to lift this curse from me. See how well he listens to you. Offer yourself up as a sacrifice while you're there.
Skyrim.esm|046024: I am the one cursed by Malacath. It is my burden. I bear it, and continue to lead, and the others see how strong I am. That is how I rule.
Skyrim.esm|046214: Yes, because a worthless City Orc is just what we've been waiting for. Finally, we're saved!
Skyrim.esm|046219: Don't make a mess of this.
Skyrim.esm|04621A: I must focus now. This ritual is very important.
Skyrim.esm|04621B: I will stay right here and wait for you to bring me what I need.
Skyrim.esm|04621C: Do you bring word from Malacath?
Skyrim.esm|04621D: Do whatever Yamarz asks of you. It cannot make things worse.
Skyrim.esm|04621E: Speak to Yamarz. Convince him to let you help him.
Skyrim.esm|04621F: You should not be here, outsider.
Skyrim.esm|046220: We have angered Malacath, and so we must be punished.
Skyrim.esm|046221: Our tribe has survived this long without you interfering. We'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|046778: You've got to tell them! We weren't with you! This is a mistake!
Skyrim.esm|046789: Let's go. Shouldn't keep the gods waiting for us.
Skyrim.esm|04678D: Why do you think? End of the line.
Skyrim.esm|046810: Steady. I don't know what's happening. Let's watch and wait.
Skyrim.esm|046A6F: You've been in Riften for quite some time now, Lady Mjoll. I fear that you soon may leave us.
Skyrim.esm|046A70: Yes, my lady. I will pass the good news on to her.
Skyrim.esm|046A71: I'm relieved to hear you say that. If you were to leave, I'd miss you terribly.
Skyrim.esm|046A72: This is the last warning. You do it again, and you'll be sleeping in Beggar's Row with the rest of the riff-raff!
Skyrim.esm|046A73: Romlyn, I've been meaning to speak to you. I did a count at the meadery and we're four cases short... again.
Skyrim.esm|046A74: That s'wit. One day, he'll regret pushing all of us around.
Skyrim.esm|046A75: I'm not accusing you of anything. Yet.
Skyrim.esm|046A76: Hemming, I need you to bring a note to Sibbi. I need him to do something for me.
Skyrim.esm|046A77: All I'm saying is Indaryn's going to push someone too far one day and they're going to push back... hard.
Skyrim.esm|046A78: For your sake, I hope you're right. Because if it is you, and Maven finds out, the Dreth family is going to be one member smaller.
Skyrim.esm|046A79: I understand. It just isn't me. I'm not a warrior and I don't think I ever will be.
Skyrim.esm|046A7A: I know that! I can promise you when he's ready, he'll find the cell door unlocked.
Skyrim.esm|046A7B: Please, milady, it was only because he threatened me. He said he'd have me thrown in the prisons if I didn't give him what he wanted! I can't afford to just give my jewelry away!
Skyrim.esm|046A7C: The Dark Elves and the Wood Elves have never been the best of friends. Be patient, they'll come around soon enough!
Skyrim.esm|046A7D: We already have people that do that and they make quite a bit of coin for it.
Skyrim.esm|046A7E: I have a single one left, quite expensive, but also quite elegant.
Skyrim.esm|046A7F: You need to be easier on Asgeir. All he wants to do is please you.
Skyrim.esm|046A80: I tolerate your son because he'll do anything I ask, without question. I'd expect the same from my own son.
Skyrim.esm|046A81: Chin up, Ungrien! Remember, you have a roof over your head and food in your belly thanks to the very same Dark Elves. Be thankful.
Skyrim.esm|046A83: That's right. He is your son. He has his own hopes and dreams and we have no right to tell him otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|046A84: I love when you spin that yarn. Gets better every time you add something new.
Skyrim.esm|046A85: What would you have me do, woman? Cross the Guild? Take them all on one by one?
Skyrim.esm|046A86: Anuriel seems to think that seeing me on the streets might bolster the city's morale.
Skyrim.esm|046A87: Laila is as clueless as she is stupid. No. If anything's to be done about this, we have to deal with it ourselves.
Skyrim.esm|046A88: Spices? I'm certain I have no idea what you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|046A89: Begging your pardon milady... I know these have something to do with Dibella, but whatever are they for?
Skyrim.esm|046A8B: What would you have me do, send him to the gallows for treason? He's your brother, Harrald.
Skyrim.esm|046A8D: So, is this what it's come to? You'd have your own flesh and blood hanged just to solidify your grip as the next Jarl?
Skyrim.esm|046A8E: Madesi, my friend. How have things been since last week's incident?
Skyrim.esm|046A8F: Lady Mjoll, if you lost a gold ring anywhere in this city I'm afraid it's likely never to be seen again. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|046A90: Outrageous! Who does she think she is?!
Skyrim.esm|046A91: Was this before or after the Mythic Dawn attacked the Emperor and Valen single-handedly fought them off? Just shut up and drink, Romlyn.
Skyrim.esm|046A92: Oh, I'm so sorry. I'm certain Bolli would be happy to lend you a few septims.
Skyrim.esm|046A93: Then put the thumbscrews on him and hit him... hard.
Skyrim.esm|046A94: Lady Maven, how may I help you?
Skyrim.esm|046A95: Here's another septim, Madesi. How much more do I need?
Skyrim.esm|046A96: When are you going to stop by the temple, Madesi?
Skyrim.esm|046A97: You stupid lizard! I said give me some more drink, or I'll have your head on a pike!
Skyrim.esm|046A98: Begging your pardon my lady, but I strongly recommend against it.
Skyrim.esm|046A99: They laughed at me again, Dinya. They simply don't understand.
Skyrim.esm|046A9A: Yes, yes, I know. I just wish to show them there's more to life than drowning your sorrows in a goblet of mead.
Skyrim.esm|046A9B: It fills me with joy to hear you're with child, Dinya. They said it wouldn't... that it couldn't happen, but Mara has shown them they're mistake.
Skyrim.esm|046A9C: It only takes but a moment to say a silent prayer to Mara. Please come by soon; not for me, but for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|046A9D: You were guided here; you were always meant to be here. That's the way of things.
Skyrim.esm|046A9E: Of course. Your training isn't complete yet.
Skyrim.esm|046A9F: I believe it to be a falsehood perpetrated by the Empire in order to weaken the citizen's confidence in your ability to rule.
Skyrim.esm|046AA0: My only concern is your safety, mother. One can only imagine the sordid types he may be conspiring with to bring about your downfall.
Skyrim.esm|046AA1: I... I'm sorry. I could have sworn I sealed it.
Skyrim.esm|046AA2: Then I thank Lady Mara with all my heart; for you, for our child and for our love.
Skyrim.esm|046AA3: Well, make sure we keep the patrols out night and day, just to be certain.
Skyrim.esm|046AA4: Well, let's all raise a flagon to Ulfric; future King of Skyrim and bringer of poverty to the working class!
Skyrim.esm|046AA5: Posh. I'm not paying for it. Put it on Bolli's account if you please.
Skyrim.esm|046AA7: Drink up, my boy! Drink to those who have fallen! May their souls find their way to Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|046AA8: Next time a Black-Briar asks for something, I suggest you respond quickly and affirmatively.
Skyrim.esm|046AAA: Hush, Vulwulf... let the boy speak.
Skyrim.esm|046AAB: I look forward to it then.
Skyrim.esm|046AAC: I'll rest easy when we've driven every last Imperial pig back across the Jerall Mountains! Now, do me the honor I deserve as your father and drink up!
Skyrim.esm|046AAD: Maybe I'll show you one day, Madesi. Have them delivered to the Bunkhouse, won't you?
Skyrim.esm|046AAE: Of course it is. That's what happens when you're partners with a corrupt Imperial whore like Maven.
Skyrim.esm|046AAF: What's become of us? We used to be inseparable... always fighting back to back and letting nothing stand in our way. You've changed, brother. And not for the better I'm afraid.
Skyrim.esm|046AB0: Come now brother, don't act all high and mighty. I know you could care less about the war one way or another. The things you say are just an act to please mother.
Skyrim.esm|046AB1: Father, I promise you, this has nothing to do with Empires or politics. It's just business.
Skyrim.esm|046AB2: It's only a bit of fun. You're a lovely young thing, you should try it sometime.
Skyrim.esm|046AB3: Mother, what can I do to make father happier? It seems no matter how successful I become he doesn't care.
Skyrim.esm|046AB4: You mean Sibbi? You must be joking! All he wants to do is bed you and be done with it. You may as well get it over with and let him.
Skyrim.esm|046AB5: Xhuth! Black-Briar or not, say that again and so help me you're going to need more than your mother to protect you from me.
Skyrim.esm|046AB6: Svana! Has that skooma-addled idiot Niluva paid us her rent yet? Or does she have more excuses?
Skyrim.esm|046AB7: Why do you insist on defending that woman? All she does with her money is spend it on that Dark Elf garbage!
Skyrim.esm|046AB8: Ah, Nivenor. What can I sell you on this fine day?
Skyrim.esm|046AB9: Bersi still refuses to cooperate. What did you want to do?
Skyrim.esm|046ABA: Hey, Svana... I hear you like Dark Elves. Perhaps I could should you what a real Dark Elf can do to you.
Skyrim.esm|046ABB: Perhaps. Perhaps not. I'm disappointed in you brother. You had it all. You had it all and your mouth cost you everything.
Skyrim.esm|046ABC: Ah, so my reputation as a lover precedes me. Care to reconsider?
Skyrim.esm|046ABD: Sorry Hemming, I've no interest in becoming your valet.
Skyrim.esm|046ABE: So, Brand-Shei... business as awful for you as it is for all of us?
Skyrim.esm|046ABF: I would never knowingly place Laila in danger, you know that too. Why do you always question my judgment? You don't trust me.
Skyrim.esm|046AC0: Oh, yes... spices. That was a special order for someone in Whiterun. Should be along any day now. Don't let it worry you, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|046AC1: I've told you, my interest in the family business is quite low. I wish to be much more than just a merchant.
Skyrim.esm|046AC2: Mother, how long must we put up with Saerlund spouting his nonsense about the Empire?
Skyrim.esm|046AC4: I see you still have the run of the place, traitor.
Skyrim.esm|046AC5: Have I told you about Valen, my dear?
Skyrim.esm|046AC6: Much appreciated, Lady Drifa, but I'll manage.
Skyrim.esm|046AC7: Riften has problems of its own. Now is not the time for exploration and discovery.
Skyrim.esm|046AC9: Aerin, I owe you my life. It would take a threat to all of Skyrim for me to depart.
Skyrim.esm|046ACB: Do you know what Indaryn told me? He said if I'm late one more time he'll kick me right out the door!
Skyrim.esm|046ACC: What are you saying? You think I had something to do with that?
Skyrim.esm|046ACD: Lady Haelga, so pleased to see you. Your Marks of Dibella are ready.
Skyrim.esm|046ACE: Excellent, then they'll be no need to devote any resources to stopping it. Thank you, Anuriel. That will be all.
Skyrim.esm|046AD0: The Dreth family doesn't steal, Indaryn.
Skyrim.esm|046AD1: Sibbi? He's in prison... you put him there.
Skyrim.esm|046AD2: I don't know if I can stand the abuse here much longer. They treat us like outcasts.
Skyrim.esm|046AD3: Hemming, I want you to personally supervise the next shipment.
Skyrim.esm|046AD4: I've done what I needed to do keep mother happy. It doesn't matter what I really believe.
Skyrim.esm|046AD5: Then by Talos, you do what you feel you're meant to do. I'll take care of your father for you.
Skyrim.esm|046AD6: I was hoping you could help me. It seems I've lost a ring that also means quite a bit to me. It's made of gold with an inscription on the inside that says "sand elske."
Skyrim.esm|046AD7: Why do you tolerate him, mother? All he contributes to this family is embarrassment and disgrace.
Skyrim.esm|046AD8: Wretched Dark Elves. It's just my luck I ended up working under one of them.
Skyrim.esm|046AD9: I don't trust them. Come to think of it, I don't trust you either, but at least you're family.
Skyrim.esm|046ADA: It would please me if he'd take up arms and fight for to save his homeland. Is that too much to ask my own son?
Skyrim.esm|046ADD: You keep giving and giving Bersi, and they'll keep taking and taking. When is it going to end?
Skyrim.esm|046ADE: You can't deny the fact that he needs to give back to his people; to fight to keep us free. You healed the wounded, Nura. You saw their life's blood spilling into the mud, giving back to the land. No. I'm sorry, I've worked too damn hard to let his life go to waste.
Skyrim.esm|046ADF: You know I don't mean that. We need to find a way out of this. Perhaps talk to Laila...
Skyrim.esm|046AE0: Drifa? I was looking through our books and there's an entry for "spices." Says we spent 300 septims. What is that?
Skyrim.esm|046AE1: Of course he cares. You just have to understand that your father was a soldier and he'll always be a soldier. He just wants you to follow in his footsteps.
Skyrim.esm|046AE2: I'm sorry my lady. The stresses of all this research... it's becoming more than I can bear.
Skyrim.esm|046AE3: Madesi, what's this bill for 300 septims!
Skyrim.esm|046AE4: No. Of course you're right, Unmid. I'll stay here for now.
Skyrim.esm|046AE5: Unmid, I wish to travel about the city. Prepare an escort please.
Skyrim.esm|046AE6: I'm afraid not. I don't deal in stolen goods.
Skyrim.esm|046AE7: Harrald, I have need of you.
Skyrim.esm|046AE8: We got a few pieces of jewelry at the Prawn, Madesi. Want to come take a look at them later?
Skyrim.esm|046AE9: But of course! Tell her she'll have it. Maven is one of the few people I can trust and I'd be delighted to give her my blessing.
Skyrim.esm|046AEA: How dare you take that kind of tone. You're in service to me, remember?
Skyrim.esm|046AEB: He killed six Imperial Guards before they dragged him into the prisons.
Skyrim.esm|046AEC: My lady, Maven Black-Briar has sent word that she requires your seal in order to expand her shipments into Morrowind.
Skyrim.esm|046AEF: It's been brought to my attention that the poison known as skooma may be present in our city. What do you know of this?
Skyrim.esm|046AF0: Don't worry, Shadr, your secret is safe with me.
Skyrim.esm|046AF1: Very well. Keep at it. You may go.
Skyrim.esm|046AF2: I trust you, I'm just concerned about her safety. That's all. Don't be angry with me.
Skyrim.esm|046AF3: It's for Marise, but don't say a word to her!
Skyrim.esm|046AF4: Yes, I've heard such things. Anuriel assures me they're just fabrications and there's nothing down there save a few stray skeever.
Skyrim.esm|046AF5: I've known Bersi a long time. Even before he opened that rubbish heap he calls a shop. Give him one more week to think about it.
Skyrim.esm|046AF6: Sorry, my lady. I've been looking but I'm convinced Saerlund says truly believes what he is saying.
Skyrim.esm|046AF7: Unmid, my sweet. Are we still meeting later for... combat training?
Skyrim.esm|046AF8: I think you've had plenty, Vulwulf. Maybe you should head on home.
Skyrim.esm|046AF9: Tell me something. Do you enjoy spending Lady Maven's gold for her? It certainly seems that way.
Skyrim.esm|046AFA: If that's what it will take then so be it.
Skyrim.esm|046AFB: Anuriel, would you please stop declaring that the city is safe for the Jarl to roam? You and I both know that isn't true.
Skyrim.esm|046AFC: Is it true that you refused to sell one of your... trinkets to Hemming?
Skyrim.esm|046AFD: This situation with the sewers beneath the city. I hear people have entered this place and have gone missing. Do you know of this?
Skyrim.esm|046AFE: Sorry Lady Dinya, but I've been busy of late.
Skyrim.esm|046AFF: At least I've chosen a side instead of acting like a spectator. One day, you'll be forced to make a choice. And I hope it's the right one, brother.
Skyrim.esm|046B00: I'm looking for a necklace. Do you have anything with garnets?
Skyrim.esm|046B01: Only thirty more and it'll be all yours. If I might ask, who is the lucky woman?
Skyrim.esm|046B02: So, Talen. Have you reconsidered my offer?
Skyrim.esm|046B03: I will hear no more of this, Harrald. Do you understand me? No more!
Skyrim.esm|046B04: Ungrien! I just found another spoiled keg! What's the matter with you?
Skyrim.esm|046B06: It's my pleasure. If you see the Thieves Guild harassing anyone else, you be sure and let me know.
Skyrim.esm|046B07: I'm so sorry my husband. You must remember, they're at the Bee and Barb to drink, not to listen to a sermon.
Skyrim.esm|046B09: You're a good man, Maramal. We're lucky to have you watching over us.
Skyrim.esm|046B0A: You're forgiven. Now, I want to know if you've made any progress with my son. Is he cursed? Has he been possessed?
Skyrim.esm|046B0B: And to think I was wandering the ash wastes only two years ago. I never knew my path would lead to you.
Skyrim.esm|046B0D: What's the matter, I thought you people were used to indentured servitude.
Skyrim.esm|046B0E: Ever since you scared off that thug from the Thieves Guild, not one of them's bothered me. We're lucky to have you here.
Skyrim.esm|046B0F: Oh, splendid! I can't wait to use them.
Skyrim.esm|046B10: How are things at the Meadery, my dear?
Skyrim.esm|046B11: I'm not angry with you. Tell you what, lover. Meet me in my chambers later and we can discuss the situation in private.
Skyrim.esm|046B12: Grelka! Lower your voice before you get us both in trouble.
Skyrim.esm|046B13: Take it easy, father. You're making a scene.
Skyrim.esm|046B14: It's going very well, actually. The war hasn't even slowed down our production or shipping at all.
Skyrim.esm|046B16: Begging your pardon, milord. Your wife has run up quite an account in the last few months.
Skyrim.esm|046B17: Vulwulf! The Empire may be our enemy, but Asgeir is still our son and we're a long way from Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|046B18: Aunt Haelga, why you... demean yourself with these people you barely know? They show no real love for you.
Skyrim.esm|046B19: It has everything to do with politics. Asgeir my son, you have so much to learn.
Skyrim.esm|046B1A: No! I'm saving myself for someone special. Someone who loves me dearly.
Skyrim.esm|046B1B: Do you realize how delicate the experiment is you've interrupted by sending for me? I may need to throw out the components and start again.
Skyrim.esm|046B1C: You're wrong! Sibbi and I are in love and you're just jealous!
Skyrim.esm|046B1E: I wish I could, milady. Sadly, I haven't sold enough of my own jewelry let alone buy some from you. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|046B1F: She said she'll have it soon. She still has her job at the meadery for now, so she'll be able to pay us.
Skyrim.esm|046B20: Stolen? How can you be certain? I may have just misplaced it somewhere in the city.
Skyrim.esm|046B22: Just be patient, aunt. She'll have it, I promise.
Skyrim.esm|046B23: You're an animal, Tythis... keep away from me.
Skyrim.esm|046B24: You're disgusting! Don't you even think of laying a finger on me or I'll cut off something you can't live without.
Skyrim.esm|046B25: You sure? It was in your handwriting. If you can't keep the books straight then let me do them.
Skyrim.esm|046B26: Worse. The Khajiit Caravans are the only way to get supplies into the city, and even those are becoming more infrequent.
Skyrim.esm|046B27: Ingun, how much longer do you intend to waste your life concocting your foolish little potions?
Skyrim.esm|046B28: Your interests lay where I wish them to lay otherwise my favorite daughter may end up homeless and without a septim to her name. Is that clear?
Skyrim.esm|046B29: You've done what you've had to for yourself, not for mother. Wise up, Harrald. There's a storm on the horizon and it will blow through here with all its fury. It's time to choose a side.
Skyrim.esm|046B2A: My lady, there could be agents of the Empire looking for an opportunity like that to have you killed. Are you willing to take that risk?
Skyrim.esm|046D60: Dirge! Did you take out the trash like I asked?
Skyrim.esm|046D61: No, that won't be necessary. You'll have compensation for your loss.
Skyrim.esm|046D62: I know, I was late. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|046D63: I'll look around the house and if I find it, I'll bring it to you right away.
Skyrim.esm|046D64: Oh dear, I didn't mean to say she isn't bright. It's the things she makes here, the things she says and all those poor little animals she tests them on. It's just not right.
Skyrim.esm|046D65: Yep. She said that the Jarl was planning a surprise raid on us, but she talked her out of it. Said she saved us a lot of trouble.
Skyrim.esm|046D66: It means I have a business to run, I need to have the books straight and you're barely ever here!
Skyrim.esm|046D67: Aerin, have you seen my ring? The one with the inscription?
Skyrim.esm|046D68: I suspected as much. The real question now is do we do something about it ourselves or do we speak to the Jarl?
Skyrim.esm|046D69: Elgrim, honey. I'm concerned about your new pupil. She seems very... odd.
Skyrim.esm|046D6A: Maven! Always a pleasure to receive you. What can I help you with today?
Skyrim.esm|046D6B: Sigh. What is it now, dear?
Skyrim.esm|046D6D: That she did. Very good. I'll tell Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|046D6E: I don't know what I'm going to do with you. I can't just let this go unpunished.
Skyrim.esm|046D6F: No, I haven't. Don't tell me you've lost it.
Skyrim.esm|046D71: Tell me you looked through the trash before you did that.
Skyrim.esm|046D73: Yes, indeed. An entire caravan shipment of your mead taken by Imperial soldiers. What of it?
Skyrim.esm|046D74: Thank you, Laila. I wanted to discuss the protection of my shipments. I'm certain you're aware of the loss I sustained a fortnight ago.
Skyrim.esm|046D76: Don't you "dear" me! I had a bowl of Vampire Dust on my worktable not an hour ago and after you swept in there it's gone!
Skyrim.esm|046D77: Laila, Riften is my home, but if my meadery can't be safe here, I'll just move it elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|046D78: Quite. To be brief, I need compensation for the lost shipment. Since Riften was responsible for it, Riften should pay for it. I have payments to make you realize. If I can't make them, I can't make Black-Briar Mead.
Skyrim.esm|046D79: No, no, no. I need this job! This is the last place in town to have work.
Skyrim.esm|046D7A: Ah, I see. Well, just be careful around my things!
Skyrim.esm|046D7B: Sigh. Very well. You're lucky Maramal has placed me in a very forgiving mood.
Skyrim.esm|046D7C: Wujeeta, you can't keep showing up here late. Bolli's just going to get someone to replace you.
Skyrim.esm|046D7D: Bolli, I was looking at the finances and I noticed you've been donating gold to the Temple of Mara. Are you out of your mind?
Skyrim.esm|046D7E: Maramal's been helping me through a rough patch. I just wanted to pay him back. It's not like he's keeping it!
Skyrim.esm|046D7F: Well, I'm also certain you're aware that our own city guard failed to provide the protection required to ensure safe passage of the shipment.
Skyrim.esm|046D80: Indeed. We lost three soldiers in that attack. A sad day for Riften.
Skyrim.esm|046D82: Take it easy, Vekel. How long we known each other? Yeah. I checked it. All the stuff is in the safe.
Skyrim.esm|046D83: Please, don't get rid of me! I need the coin. I... I'll work for free for the rest of this week. Please!
Skyrim.esm|046D84: Well, you were right. These so called 'Gutter Saints' live in the sewers beneath.
Skyrim.esm|046D85: Uh huh. I'm sure he runs outside with the coin and throws it up in the air so Mara herself can catch it. Come off of it, Bolli.
Skyrim.esm|046D86: Job's done, boss. Gave the package to her just you said.
Skyrim.esm|046D87: I hope not. That ring is irreplaceable.
Skyrim.esm|046D88: And just what's that supposed to mean?
Skyrim.esm|046D89: For goodness sake, Bolli! You barely lift a finger yourself when you're here except to point at things for your workers to do. Let them do the work, that's what you pay them for!
Skyrim.esm|046D8A: Nothing, nothing. Just straighten yourself out before you find yourself headed back to Black Marsh.
Skyrim.esm|046D8B: You may be my wife, but this is my business, so just do your job. Don't worry, you'll still have plenty of gold left over to waste on trinkets.
Skyrim.esm|046D8C: Yeah. Threw it in the lake like you said.
Skyrim.esm|046D8D: Decided to show up for work today, eh?
Skyrim.esm|046D8E: Good. Did she say anything we need to know?
Skyrim.esm|046D8F: Damn it, woman! Ingun is the most promising alchemist I've ever seen and I won't have you scaring her off!
Skyrim.esm|046D90: Hafjorg! Where did you put it woman?!
Skyrim.esm|046D91: I say we go down there and clear the place out, just like old times.
Skyrim.esm|046D93: No, my friend. That would be reckless. We have to help, but help in a way that doesn't directly place anyone in harm's way. For now, give this "Ratway" a wide berth.
Skyrim.esm|046D95: Oh my. Elgrim, it's on the shelf to the right of the table, as always.
Skyrim.esm|046EE5: That's it! Now, wait until he's well inside!
Skyrim.esm|046EE7: You better know what you're doing!
Skyrim.esm|046EE8: Steady! Steady, now! Keep under cover until it's down!
Skyrim.esm|046FA6: No. As if trade wasn't poor enough. Now there's violence in the streets. It was the Forsworn, wasn't it? I lose dozens of shipments every season to those barbarians. Now they're in the city? Gods help us.
Skyrim.esm|046FA7: I inherited the store from my husband. His father was Arnleif. My husband, Gunnar, died about five years ago. Forsworn attack. I miss that stubborn oaf.
Skyrim.esm|046FAA: You have stepped on us long enough!
Skyrim.esm|046FAC: We are the rightful rulers of Markarth!
Skyrim.esm|046FAD: Thank you. I'll have a sum of gold waiting for you when you return.
Skyrim.esm|046FC5: Come on in. The Silver-Blood Inn has plenty of strong drink and clean rooms.
Skyrim.esm|04743C: So... wait. Cicero is alive? And we're all friends again? I swear, I can hardly keep up...
Skyrim.esm|04743D: I like it here. We should have moved to Dawnstar ages ago.
Skyrim.esm|047442: I can't wait to see what the future brings.
Skyrim.esm|047446: You know, I can still sell you some potions and such, or train you in alchemy.
Skyrim.esm|047449: Why am I not surprised to learn Cicero is alive? I still can't stomach the little clown, but if you've welcomed him here, I won't question it.
Skyrim.esm|04744B: I'll follow you till I'm dust in the breeze. You do realize that?
Skyrim.esm|047450: This place is already starting to feel like home. Must be all the bloodstains.
Skyrim.esm|047454: The Dark Brotherhood is on the rise again, Listener. I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|0479A8: Aye, the last thing we want to be doing is crossing our clients.
Skyrim.esm|0479AC: You claim this recruit possesses an aptitude for our line of work. If so, let him prove it.
Skyrim.esm|0479AD: The Guild depends on an arrangement of influential people to keep things running smoothly.
Skyrim.esm|0479AE: Wait a moment, you're not talking about Goldenglow, are you? Even our little Vex couldn't get in.
Skyrim.esm|0479AF: Hmm? Oh, yes. Since Brynjolf assures me you'll be nothing but a benefit to us, then you're in. Welcome to the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0479B1: Hmph, yeah I did. That wood elf s'wit... he's a lot smarter than I expected. Can you believe that fetcher had more than tripled the guard? There must be eight of them in there. It was like he was daring us to come and get him.
Skyrim.esm|0479B2: Goldenglow Estate is critically important to one of our largest clients. However, the owner has suddenly decided to take matters into his own hands and shut us out. He needs to be taught a lesson. Brynjolf will provide you with the details.
Skyrim.esm|0479F5: It's a wonderful house, my love. If you don't mind, I'm going to start a small store. Something to do whenever you're on the road.
Skyrim.esm|047AD6: Fine! I'll take care of it, just please leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|047CA6: Every night, I walk around the Gray Quarter and let them gray-skins know what I think of 'em.
Skyrim.esm|047CA7: We ought dig a big hole, throw all them dark elves and Argonians in it, and let 'em tear each other to pieces.
Skyrim.esm|047CA8: Only thing I hate worse than those scale-backs are them filthy gray-skins!
Skyrim.esm|047CA9: The Gray Quarter's a disgrace. Them dark elves live like animals.
Skyrim.esm|047CAA: Can't see why we let them dark elves live inside the walls. We should kick 'em out like we did to the scalebacks.
Skyrim.esm|0480BF: Of course... your pay. Haha! You're smart as a whip, lass. Keep doing right by us and there's plenty more where that came from.
Skyrim.esm|0480EF: Avulstein, put that down! She's here to help us find Thorald!
Skyrim.esm|0480F2: I owe you my life, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0480F3: We're getting Thorald back, no matter what.
Skyrim.esm|048194: Astrid... By the sands, I still can't wrap my head around it...
Skyrim.esm|048196: If I hadn't heard it with my own ears I wouldn't have believed it. How could Astrid have done this to us? Strangely, I feel only pity for her...
Skyrim.esm|04826B: He lives in seclusion on the very peak of the mountain. He speaks to us only rarely, and never to outsiders. Being allowed to see him is a great privilege.
Skyrim.esm|04826C: You weren't ready. You still aren't ready. But thanks to the Blades, you now have questions that only Paarthurnax can answer.
Skyrim.esm|048293: Only Paarthurnax, the master of our order, can answer that question, if he so chooses.
Skyrim.esm|048294: To get the answers you seek, you must travel to the top of the mountain and speak to our master, Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|0483D8: Only those whose Voice is strong can find the path. Come. We will teach you a Shout to open the way to Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|0483EC: Sigh... fine, take whatever you want, so long as you help me get rid of Vals.
Skyrim.esm|0483ED: Hey! Those belong to my family!
Skyrim.esm|0483EE: Careful, there's a pressure plate around here. I've triggered it before and got pelted with darts.
Skyrim.esm|0483EF: Okay, I think this door should lead to the main burial chamber where Vals is holed up. Let's get in there and put and end to this.
Skyrim.esm|0485C5: As I promised, my men stand ready. The great chains are oiled. We wait on your word.
Skyrim.esm|0485C7: My men know what to do. Make sure you do your part. I'm putting my city in your hands.
Skyrim.esm|0485C8: Now that it's upon us, I'm rather looking forward to the challenge. I'll be famouser than Olaf One-Eye!
Skyrim.esm|0489E8: Don't leave me, for Arkay's sake!
Skyrim.esm|048A7C: Oh really? Well, I'm not going to hold my breath. The thieves are up in Bleak Falls Barrow, northeast of town. I'd take you there, but with Lucan gone, I'm busy with the store.
Skyrim.esm|048B30: Nobody knows his true identity. Well, nobody except Gaius Maro, and you've already taken care of him. I do know the Gourmet was classically trained in traditional Breton cuisine in High Rock. So that's where he must have met Anton Virane. In any event, our anonymous chef shares a passion for food with all of Tamriel. And that anonymity will prove the Emperor's undoing.
Skyrim.esm|048B31: Shouldn't you be out ruining someone's special day?
Skyrim.esm|048C8C: Yes, well. Perhaps the proprietor of this establishment can assist you. Please, you're starting to... frighten me.
Skyrim.esm|048C8D: "Uncommon Taste." It was published a few years ago, and became a sensation throughout the Empire almost overnight. The recipes were hailed as both practical and decadent. I've made a few of the Gourmet's dishes myself, actually. His roast mutton with Redguard berry sauce is to die for. But his chocolate pate gives me the runs.
Skyrim.esm|048C8E: Gourmet? Why I... Um... Oh dear. You're going to kill me now, aren't you?
Skyrim.esm|048C8F: What? The Emperor? But I... Oh. Oh, by the gods, no. No, you can't do this. You can't!
Skyrim.esm|048C90: Whatever it is, I'm sure I can't be of help. I'm just here on... holiday.
Skyrim.esm|048C91: Yes. Gaius Maro, on orders from his father, arranged for the Gourmet to come to Skyrim months ago. To serve as the Emperor's personal chef. But the Emperor canceled his journey at the last minute. Maro was smart, kept the Gourmet around, in case things changed. And so, the Gourmet has remained in seclusion, somewhere in Skyrim... on indefinite hold. Probably feels like a prisoner. But you'll fix that.
Skyrim.esm|048DC9: The Ebonmere is a conduit through which Nocturnal influences our world. Not through magic or blessings, but purely through luck.
Skyrim.esm|048DCA: Whatever is affecting the Nightingale Sentinels is starting to affect me too. As I get closer to the Ebonmere I begin to feel myself slipping away. Even right this moment, I feel strange... when I don't think I should be feeling anything at all.
Skyrim.esm|048DD3: So it's done. You've killed the Gourmet. And now Titus Mede II is as good as dead.
Skyrim.esm|048F01: Ah, indeed. I'm no master of the Voice like these worthy gentlemen, but it is written here in this scroll.
Skyrim.esm|048F02: Zu'u bonaar. You went to a great deal of trouble to put me in this... humiliating position. Hind siiv Alduin, hmm? No doubt you want to know where to find Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|048F03: Aam? Serve you? ...no. Ni tiid. If and when you defeat Alduin, I will reconsider. Hmm... krosis. There is one... detail about Skuldafn I neglected to mention.
Skyrim.esm|048F04: Horvutah med kodaav. Caught like a bear in a trap...
Skyrim.esm|048F05: Unslaad krosis. Innumerable pardons. I digress. He has travelled to Sovngarde to regain his strength, devouring the sillesejoor... the souls of the mortal dead. A privilege he jealously guards... His door to Sovngarde is at Skuldafn, one of his ancient fanes high in the eastern mountains. Mindoraan, pah ok middovahhe lahvraan til. I surely do not need to warn you that all his remaining strength is marshalled there. Zu'u lost ofan hin laan... now that I have answered your question, you will allow me to go free?
Skyrim.esm|048F08: Ah, don't you see? The names of dragons are always three Words of Power -- Shouts. By calling the dragon with the Voice, he will hear you wherever he might be.
Skyrim.esm|048F09: Zok frini grind ko grah drun viiki, Dovahkiin. Ah. I forget. You do not have the dovah speech. My... eagerness to meet you in battle was my... undoing, Dovahkiin. I salute your, hmm, low cunning in devising such a grahmindol - strategem.
Skyrim.esm|048F0A: Shout for Odahviing from the Dragonsreach courtyard. After that, it's up to you.
Skyrim.esm|048F0B: I anticipated the problem. While you were arranging this meeting, I was busy in the library of Sky Haven Temple.
Skyrim.esm|048F0D: Rinik vazah. An apt phrase. Alduin bovul. One reason I came to your call was to test your Thu'um for myself. Many of us have begun to question Alduin's lordship, whether his Thu'um was truly the strongest. Among ourselves, of course. Mu ni meyye. None were yet ready to openly defy him.
Skyrim.esm|048F0E: He's not compelled to, but dragons are prideful by nature and loath to refuse a challenge. Your Voice in particular is likely to intrigue this dragon, after your victory over Alduin. I think it very likely that he will be unable to resist investigating your call.
Skyrim.esm|049324: The gods have granted us peace for the moment. But that won't last long. I'm sure trouble will be stirred up soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0493F9: I know I ran ahead with the claw, but I need help!
Skyrim.esm|049402: Is... is someone coming? Is that you Harknir? Bjorn? Soling?
Skyrim.esm|049404: Get it away from me. Get it away!
Skyrim.esm|049405: Don't let it get me. Help!
Skyrim.esm|049449: You do have a plan for luring a dragon here, yeah?
Skyrim.esm|04944A: Go ahead and call this dragon of yours. We're ready.
Skyrim.esm|04944B: What are you waiting for? Let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|04949F: You think you can take me?
Skyrim.esm|0494A1: I'll mount your head on my wall!
Skyrim.esm|0494A2: Ha ha! I love a challenge!
Skyrim.esm|0494A5: You won't get the best of me!
Skyrim.esm|0494AA: You ready to die today? Huh?
Skyrim.esm|0494AF: This is the end for you!
Skyrim.esm|0494B8: Not quite good enough, are you?
Skyrim.esm|0494BB: I'll show you what a real Orc can do!
Skyrim.esm|0494BC: Pray to Stendarr while you can!
Skyrim.esm|0494BD: I'll shatter your bones like glass!
Skyrim.esm|0494BE: I'll show you a real fight!
Skyrim.esm|0494C2: You're a fool to fight me!
Skyrim.esm|0494C5: I'm going to crush you like a bug!
Skyrim.esm|0494C6: You won't get away from me!
Skyrim.esm|0494CE: This is the end for you!
Skyrim.esm|0494D0: This is the part where you fall down and bleed to death!
Skyrim.esm|0494D1: I'll see you dead and buried!
Skyrim.esm|0494D5: You'll beg me to kill you before I'm done!
Skyrim.esm|0494D8: Just give up! You can't win!
Skyrim.esm|0494D9: I'll kill you, I swear it!
Skyrim.esm|0494DA: I'll fight you to the end!
Skyrim.esm|0494E5: I'll paint the snow with your blood!
Skyrim.esm|0494E7: I haven't lived this long to be beaten by you!
Skyrim.esm|0494E8: I can still put up a fight!
Skyrim.esm|0494E9: I've fought colds tougher than you!
Skyrim.esm|0494EA: I've got some fight left in me!
Skyrim.esm|0494EB: I may be old, but I can put up a fight!
Skyrim.esm|0494EC: I won't go down so easily!
Skyrim.esm|0494ED: Look what you've made me do!
Skyrim.esm|0494EF: You need to learn some manners!
Skyrim.esm|0494F1: I've still got some fight in me!
Skyrim.esm|0494F6: I'll kill you where you stand!
Skyrim.esm|0494F7: And you thought I was just a pretty face.
Skyrim.esm|0494F9: I'll raise a cup to your grave!
Skyrim.esm|0494FB: Remember... hit the one in the middle!
Skyrim.esm|049500: Yield, and I may still show mercy!
Skyrim.esm|04951B: Just shut it and keep an eye out for trouble.
Skyrim.esm|04951C: What if Arvel doesn't come back? I want my share from that claw!
Skyrim.esm|049B9B: You won't live to see tomorrow!
Skyrim.esm|049B9D: I'll kill you if I have to!
Skyrim.esm|049B9E: You won't get the best of me!
Skyrim.esm|049BA1: Give up while you still can!
Skyrim.esm|049BA6: I'll have your head for a trophy!
Skyrim.esm|049BA7: Pray to your gods for mercy!
Skyrim.esm|049BAF: Come on! Let's see you put up a fight!
Skyrim.esm|049BB0: Come on, show me what you've got!
Skyrim.esm|049BB1: You think I'm afraid of you?
Skyrim.esm|049BB4: You think you stand a chance?
Skyrim.esm|04A20C: The Greybeards? What do those old hermits want with me?
Skyrim.esm|04A20D: Most of the Legion is tied down on the border with the Aldmeri Dominion. The Emperor can't afford to risk weakening Cyrodiil's defenses. From the Imperial City, our war here is just a sideshow. An interlude before the main event against the Thalmor resumes.
Skyrim.esm|04A20E: Why? There's nothing to discuss as long as that traitor Ulfric is in arms against his rightful Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|04A20F: Fair enough. We're driving the Stormcloaks back well enough at the moment, but we're already overstretched.
Skyrim.esm|04A210: No, you're right. After all this time, Ulfric still holds almost half of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|04A211: Well... we could use some breathing space to regroup. Things haven't been going well for us lately.
Skyrim.esm|04A212: Yes. Yes, fine, I'll come to this Greybeard council. For all the good it will do.
Skyrim.esm|04A378: I've seen enough to know their fabric. The warp of air, the weft of time. But no, it is not in my possession.
Skyrim.esm|04A379: The Daedric prince of the unknown. Hermaeus Mora. I thought there were no secrets left to know. Until I first spoke to him. He asks a price -- to work his will. A few murders, some dissent spread, a plague or two. For the secrets I can endure. In time, he brought me here. To the box. But he won't reveal how to open it. Maddening.
Skyrim.esm|04A60B: At last. I've been waiting for someone more fit to carry out my will. The child is spirited, but lacks... agency.
Skyrim.esm|04AFA1: Next, I need a little investigating to confirm the possibility of blackmailing the Jarl's Steward.
Skyrim.esm|04AFA2: Next, I need dirt on the Jarl's Steward. Heard there's blackmail potential there.
Skyrim.esm|04AFA7: First off, seems the Jarl's Steward might be vulnerable to blackmail. Need to investigate that possibility.
Skyrim.esm|04AFA8: First off, it seems that we might be able to blackmail the Jarl's Steward. I need to find out for sure.
Skyrim.esm|04B220: Then I must do this alone.
Skyrim.esm|04B222: By the gods! I know this looks bad. Let me explain...
Skyrim.esm|04B224: Good. Follow me. We have to fight our way up. I don't think anyone else here is about to have a change of heart.
Skyrim.esm|04B255: Legate. We represent the Emperor here.
Skyrim.esm|04B257: What would the Empire want in return?
Skyrim.esm|04B258: Ulfric, you can't seriously expect us to give up Markarth at the negotiating table.
Skyrim.esm|04B25A: You're lucky I respect the Greybeards' council, Galmar!
Skyrim.esm|04B25B: Wait. General, you don't intend to just hand over Markarth to that... traitor!
Skyrim.esm|04B25C: You know exactly... no. Not this time.
Skyrim.esm|04B25D: Ha! Skyrim will never bow to the Thalmor!
Skyrim.esm|04B261: She's supposed to be on our side?
Skyrim.esm|04B262: You lost the trust of the people of Skyrim, Elisif. You had your chance to make the right choice when the war started.
Skyrim.esm|04B263: You hope to gain in council what you've been unable to take in battle, is that it?
Skyrim.esm|04B264: No. We'll stay. Out of respect for the Dragonborn and our Greybeard hosts.
Skyrim.esm|04B265: But she is to observe, nothing more. We are not negotiating with her, is that clear?
Skyrim.esm|04B266: Yes, that'd be entirely out of character.
Skyrim.esm|04B267: Very well, Ulfric. Enjoy your petty victory.
Skyrim.esm|04B268: I'm sure Jarl Ulfric does not expect something for nothing.
Skyrim.esm|04B26B: Now that that's settled, may we proceed?
Skyrim.esm|04B26C: We've taken it before. I'm only asking for what will be ours soon enough anyway.
Skyrim.esm|04B26D: Since we're all here at your request, I'd like to hear what you think Markarth is worth.
Skyrim.esm|04B26F: Yes, let's get down to it.
Skyrim.esm|04B271: Sorry, sir. It won't happen again.
Skyrim.esm|04B272: First, let's be clear. This council wasn't my idea. I think it's a waste of time. You are a traitor to the Empire, and deserve a traitor's death.
Skyrim.esm|04B274: Ulfric, why so hostile? After all, it's not the Thalmor that's burning your farms and killing your sons.
Skyrim.esm|04B2B3: I've only looked through a portion of this section. You, uh, you can look around in the chambers just north of here. Try and be careful, all right? We don't want to damage anything.
Skyrim.esm|04B2B4: Ah, yes. You. I remember you. You're going to help? That's fine. Just... Just don't make a mess of my work.
Skyrim.esm|04B2B8: J'zargo finds nothing but dust in these ruins.
Skyrim.esm|04B2B9: To think, my ancestors destroyed the homes of Onmund's ancestors. So much bloodshed.
Skyrim.esm|04B2BA: I wonder if everyone else would be so happy to rifle through the bones of their ancestors.
Skyrim.esm|04B2BB: You must be one of the new apprentices, then? Fine, fine. Just... just don't make a mess of my work, all right?
Skyrim.esm|04B46F: I didn't want to, but she attacked to try and keep me from leaving. I just can't be a part of this place any more! The things we do here - evil, evil things. No more!
Skyrim.esm|04B470: I'm going up there to stop her. This cycle must end. I don't know what brought you here, but I could use your help, if you're willing.
Skyrim.esm|04B471: I called it home, now I know that was a mistake. I am, or was part of a coven serving the Hagravens that live here. So was she.
Skyrim.esm|04B4B6: Just round up the rest of the magical artifacts so that I can catalog them.
Skyrim.esm|04B564: Hardly worth giving up Markarth for, but not a bad starting point.
Skyrim.esm|04B569: You're right. Ulfric isn't in any position to ask for Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|04B56A: Hmm. The Rift would help secure our communications with Cyrodiil... and threaten Ulfric's southern flank...
Skyrim.esm|04B56B: It's true that Whiterun is dangerously exposed as long as the Stormcloaks hold Markarth...
Skyrim.esm|04B704: Ah, the sneaky type, eh? I think we can fix you right up.
Skyrim.esm|04B706: Yes, lad, you've been raised into the Companions, I hear. I can fashion whatever kind of weapon you want. Finest forge in Skyrim, right here before you.
Skyrim.esm|04B70C: Now that's a smart weapon. Light, strong, useful. Crack some skulls for me.
Skyrim.esm|04B70D: Speak with Miss Salma, if you wish to speak.
Skyrim.esm|04B70E: Sure you can handle that? Ha! Of course you can.
Skyrim.esm|04B710: A blade! It will be as sharp as Fralia's tongue!
Skyrim.esm|04B71D: We should be inside, loading up on treasure. Worry-scales here thinks we should wait, though. Don't get any ideas, either. We were here first.
Skyrim.esm|04B71E: Gold, silver. Gems, maybe? Only one way to find out!
Skyrim.esm|04B71F: Not without us, you aren't! I'm sure there's enough for everyone, after all. Come on, Beem-Ja. We're going.
Skyrim.esm|04B85E: This is a good hiding spot.
Skyrim.esm|04B85F: I found you! Na na na, na na na!
Skyrim.esm|04B991: Fair enough. Although I'm beginning to wonder whose side you're really on. Still...
Skyrim.esm|04B992: Fair enough. I was hoping you could put aside your loyalties for the greater good, but I see you're firmly in Ulfric's camp. Still...
Skyrim.esm|04B993: I'm glad you agree. I was starting to wonder whose side you were on here.
Skyrim.esm|04B994: Well. You surprise me. I was expecting you to favor Ulfric. Maybe this council will achieve something after all.
Skyrim.esm|04B995: Better than nothing, I suppose. But Ulfric will need to offer a lot more if he wants me to give up Markarth without a fight.
Skyrim.esm|04B996: In exchange for Markarth, the source of most of Skyrim's silver? Hardly. Riften seems like a better choice to me. Well-fortified, easily resupplied across Lake Honrich. Plus all the mead we can drink.
Skyrim.esm|04B997: In exchange for Markarth, the source of most of Skyrim's silver? Hardly. I might rather consider Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|04B99C: I've never liked having the rebels right on Solitude's doorstep. Retaking Hjaalmarch would help secure our supply lines...
Skyrim.esm|04B99D: Regaining Pale Pass would help our supply situation considerably... and Whiterun is dangerously exposed with the enemy in Falkreath...
Skyrim.esm|04B99E: Having another port would ease our supply situation considerably...
Skyrim.esm|04B99F: Winterhold would allow us to directly threaten the rebel supply lines out of Windhelm...
Skyrim.esm|04B9C7: I don't know about the end of the world, but this dragon situation has gotten out of hand. If this truce will help the Dragonborn here put an end to that menace, we both gain. Remember that, Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|04B9C8: If he's right about Alduin... we both have just as much to lose here, Tullius, remember that.
Skyrim.esm|04B9C9: He grows more powerful with every soldier slain in your pointless war!
Skyrim.esm|04B9CD: Stop! Are you so blind to our danger that you can't see past your petty disagreements?
Skyrim.esm|04B9D0: Think carefully where your loyalties lie... brother.
Skyrim.esm|04B9D1: I don't know why I expected even-handed treatment from you.
Skyrim.esm|04B9D2: Alduin has returned! The World-Eater! Even now, he devours the souls of your fallen comrades!
Skyrim.esm|04B9D3: A very pretty speech, but what does it have to do with...
Skyrim.esm|04B9D4: You don't really expect compensation every time a farm gets burned in a war that you started, do you Ulfric?
Skyrim.esm|04B9DF: And I advise you both to listen to what he has to say, before you do anything rash.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E0: I expected better from you Dragonborn. I came here in good faith, despite your known Imperial sympathies.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E1: You're different from what I expected, Dragonborn. You treat us with respect, despite your known Imperial sympathies.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E2: Think hard about your loyalties, brother. I expected better from you.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E3: Think hard about your loyalties, sister. I expected better from you.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E4: Well done, brother. If you can hold these Imperials to their promises, we might be able to reach an agreement.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E5: Well done, sister. If you can hold these Imperials to their promises, we might be able to reach an agreement.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E6: Is he with you, Delphine? If so, I advise you to tell him to watch his tongue.
Skyrim.esm|04B9E7: Don't you understand the danger? Don't you understand what the return of the dragons means?
Skyrim.esm|04B9EB: You always were a fool, Ulfric. You'll never get a better chance to salvage something from the ruins of your ambition.
Skyrim.esm|04B9ED: I think you're on the wrong side in this war, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|04B9F0: That's a lie! My legionnaires are disciplined, unlike your...
Skyrim.esm|04B9F1: You slaughtered them - the women, the children! You butchers!
Skyrim.esm|04B9F2: Can you not put aside your hatred for even one moment in the face of this mortal danger?
Skyrim.esm|04B9F9: Here you sit arguing about... nothing! While the fate of the land hangs in the balance!
Skyrim.esm|04B9FA: Markarth is off the table. Your rabble is on the verge of defeat. We have no need of a truce at such a price.
Skyrim.esm|04B9FB: We've made you a fair offer. Are you serious about these talks, or are you just here to posture?
Skyrim.esm|04BA23: So that's why you're here, Ulfric? You dare to insult the Greybeards by using this council to advance your own position?
Skyrim.esm|04BA25: I'm willing to let the Dragonborn be the judge of the fairness of my request.
Skyrim.esm|04BA26: Markarth remains allied to the Empire, under the authority of Jarl Igmund.
Skyrim.esm|04BA27: Riften remains allied to the Stormcloak cause, under the authority of Jarl Laila Law-Giver.
Skyrim.esm|04BA28: Markarth will be handed over to Ulfric's forces. Jarl Igmund will step down, and Thongvor Silver-Blood will become the Jarl of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|04BA29: Riften will be handed over to Imperial control. Jarl Laila Law-Giver will step down, and Maven Black-Briar will become the Jarl of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|04BA2A: Do you hear that, Tullius? The blood of the innocent cries out for weregild.
Skyrim.esm|04BA2B: And so the Empire runs roughshod over Skyrim, as usual.
Skyrim.esm|04BA2C: What? And you call yourself a son of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|04BA2E: These are the terms currently on the table.
Skyrim.esm|04BA2F: Elisif! I said I'd handle it.
Skyrim.esm|04BA31: The Stormcloaks will withdraw from the Reach, allowing Jarl Igmund to return to Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|04BA32: The Stormcloaks will withdraw from the Rift, allowing Imperial troops unhindered access. Jarl Laila Law-Giver will step down, and Maven Black-Briar will become the Jarl of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|04BA33: The Empire will withdraw its troops from the Reach, allowing Ulfric's forces unhindered access to Markarth. Jarl Igmund will go into exile, with Thongvor Silver-Blood taking his place as Jarl of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|04BA34: The Empire will withdraw its troops from the Rift, allowing the return of Jarl Laila Law-Giver.
Skyrim.esm|04BA35: Ulfric will withdraw his forces from Hjaalmarch, allowing Jarl Idgrod Ravencrone to return to Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|04BA36: Ulfric will withdraw his forces from Falkreath, allowing Jarl Siddgeir to return.
Skyrim.esm|04BA37: Ulfric will allow Imperial forces into the Pale. Skald the Elder will go into exile, and Brina Merilis will assume the Jarlship.
Skyrim.esm|04BA38: Ulfric will allow Imperial troops to enter Winterhold. Korir will replace Kraldar as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|04BA39: The Empire will allow Ulfric's forces to enter Hjaalmarch. Idgrod Ravencrone will go into exile, with Sorli the Builder replacing her as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|04BA3A: The Empire will allow Ulfric's forces to enter Falkreath. Jarl Siddgeir will step down, and Dengeir of Stuhn will take his place as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|04BA3B: The Empire will withdraw its troops from the Pale, allowing Jarl Skald the Elder to return from exile.
Skyrim.esm|04BA3C: The Empire will withdraw its troops from Winterhold, allowing Jarl Korir to return from exile.
Skyrim.esm|04BA3F: I'm sure you have something in mind.
