Skyrim.esm|04BA40: I shouldn't agree to terms that so blatantly favor the Empire. I have no choice, though, under the circumstances. But once Alduin is defeated - then it will be the Empire's turn. Remember - "Evgir Unslaad."
Skyrim.esm|04BA43: You treat us fairly. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|04BA44: Spoken like a true son of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|04BA46: You've dealt with us fairly so far. You've heard our demand. What say you?
Skyrim.esm|04BA48: General, this is outrageous! You can't be taking this demand seriously! I thought we were here to discuss a truce!
Skyrim.esm|04BA4A: These terms blatantly favor the rebels. Everyone here knows that. But the Empire will accept them, until the dragon menace is dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|04BA4B: What makes you think I'll give that to you, if I wouldn't give you Markarth?
Skyrim.esm|04BA4C: Where do these demands stop, Ulfric? Do you expect me to surrender all of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|04BA4D: We want compensation for the massacre at Shor's Stone.
Skyrim.esm|04BA4E: You surrender Hjaalmarch to us, and take Idgrod Ravencrone with you. Sorli the Builder will take over as Jarl of Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|04BA4F: You surrender Falkreath to us. Siddgeir steps down, and Dengeir of Stuhn resumes the Jarlship.
Skyrim.esm|04BA50: You withdraw from the Pale, and allow Jarl Skald the Elder to return to Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|04BA51: We want Imperial forces to withdraw from Winterhold, and allow Jarl Kraldar to return from exile.
Skyrim.esm|04BA52: Bah! This is a waste of time. I can see that we won't get better terms from this council. So be it. The sons of Skyrim at least put the greater good above our own interests.
Skyrim.esm|04BA53: Hjaalmarch will be turned over to Ulfric, with Sorli the Builder assuming the Jarlship.
Skyrim.esm|04BA54: Falkreath will be turned over to Ulfric, and Dengeir of Stuhn will return as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|04BA55: Imperial forces will withdraw from The Pale, and Jarl Skald the Elder will be allowed to return to Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|04BA56: Imperial troops will withdraw from Winterhold, and Kraldar will return as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|04BA57: The Stormcloaks will withdraw from Hjaalmarch, and Idgrod Ravencrone will resume the Jarlship.
Skyrim.esm|04BA58: Falkreath will be returned to Imperial control, under the authority of Jarl Siddgeir.
Skyrim.esm|04BA59: The Pale will be turned over to the Empire. Brina Merilis will assume the Jarlship in Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|04BA5A: Winterhold will be turned over to the Empire, although the College itself will retain its traditional neutrality.
Skyrim.esm|04BA5C: But I at least will negotiate in good faith.
Skyrim.esm|04BA5E: If we're not going to get Markarth, we need you to give us something.
Skyrim.esm|04BA5F: You know as well as I do that that we can't hand over Riften on these terms.
Skyrim.esm|04BA60: These terms are not acceptable. You know that.
Skyrim.esm|04BC9B: Oh, I get it. This is a test, right? You want to see how well I know the Potage le Magnifique. Well then, I think  a pinch of frost salts should do the trick, right? Now what?
Skyrim.esm|04BC9C: Hmmm... horker. So delicious. I swear, is there a soul alive who doesn't enjoy the taste of... Sorry, I didn't mean to get carried away. There we go, one cup of diced horker meat. I have to say, the stew seems done. Add anything else, and we may dilute the distinct flavors. So... is that it?
Skyrim.esm|04BC9D: You're a High Elf! I... I never would have guessed it. So many of your recipes are clearly aimed at the commonfolk. It just seems...
Skyrim.esm|04BC9E: My choice, then? Very well. I think we should add some Chaurus eggs. For additional flavor.
Skyrim.esm|04BC9F: Excellent. They should be ready for us now. And if I may say so... it has been an honor, getting a chance to prepare a meal with, well... the best chef in the entire Empire. I'll carry the stew pot, and lead the way up to the dining room. I'm sure the Emperor and his guests are dying to meet you.
Skyrim.esm|04BCA0: Maybe we should start over, hmm? We want the Gourmet's famous Potage le Magnifique to be perfect, after all. So, what is the first special ingredient?
Skyrim.esm|04BCA1: Well, I'm just surprised the Gourmet is a Redguard. I had you pegged for a Wood Elf, honestly. You know, because of your use of...
Skyrim.esm|04BCA2: Just go right into the tower, then straight ahead to the kitchen. Gianna's expecting you.
Skyrim.esm|04BCA3: Ooookay.... Um.... Let me see. Thistle? Yes, let's try that. Just a sprig or two...
Skyrim.esm|04BCA4: My choice, hmm? All right, then - tomatoes. Simple, yet infinitely flavorful. Don't you agree? Well, I have to say, the stew seems done. Add anything else, and we may dilute the distinct flavors. So... is that it?
Skyrim.esm|04BCA5: Ooohh.... how decadent. I never would have guessed it. What next?
Skyrim.esm|04BCA6: Really? Oh, I use Nirnroot as a special seasoning all the time as well. What a wonderful idea. Okay. Now what?
Skyrim.esm|04BCA7: I guessed right! I... I always imagined the great Gourmet was a Wood Elf. You see, only someone with a knowledge of woodland herbs could combine...
Skyrim.esm|04BCA8: Oh! Yes, but of course. Ahem. The Emperor has requested your signature dish - the Potage le Magnifique. I've taken the liberty of getting it started. But the cookbook only says so much, and everyone makes the Potage differently. I would be honored if we could make it... the Gourmet's special way. The base broth is already boiled. We can get started right now. So... which ingredient should I add next?
Skyrim.esm|04BCAA: Yes. For Sithis... and the Night Mother. You are the Listener after all, hmm? So let's not waste any more time. Go now to Castle Dour in Solitude. Present the Gourmet's Writ of Passage to the officer in charge, Commander Maro. I'm sure you remember him. You'll gain unrestricted access to the kitchens, and then the Emperor. You're posing as a chef, so you'll be able to poison his meal rather easily.
Skyrim.esm|04BCAB: Well, I don't mean to seem disappointed, but... I just never figured the Gourmet was an Imperial. I had dreamed of something more exotic, maybe...
Skyrim.esm|04BCAC: Not another delivery. I told you people, our stocks are fine. Now put whatever you have over there, then get out!
Skyrim.esm|04BCAD: Ah, of course. I suspected as much.
Skyrim.esm|04BCAE: You... you're an Argonian! Where did you learn to cook like that? Surely not in Black Marsh. Why even the...
Skyrim.esm|04BCAF: Heh, I'm sorry. Of course. It's your most famous recipe, after all. All right then, your secret ingredient's been added. And if I may say so... it has been an honor, getting a chance to prepare a meal with, well... the best chef in the entire Empire. I'll carry the stew pot, and lead the way up to the dining room. I'm sure the Emperor and his guests are dying to meet you.
Skyrim.esm|04BCB0: Here, take this - it's called Jarrin Root. All it takes is one taste, and the effects are quite immediate. The Emperor will be serving Sithis before he even knows he's dead. Once Mede has been killed, escape through the upper door, and across the bridge. I've "arranged" for it to be unguarded once the alarm is sounded. Now go, my friend. Go, and fulfill your destiny as Listener.
Skyrim.esm|04BCB1: The... Gourmet? Oh! Finally! When I heard the Gourmet was being brought in to cook for the Emperor, I could hardly believe it. It's just...
Skyrim.esm|04BCB2: Stop right there. The tower is off limits until further notice.
Skyrim.esm|04BCB5: Hmph. Look at you. So calm. So confident. I do believe you were born for this task. So let's begin. Go now to Castle Dour in Solitude. Present the Gourmet's Writ of Passage to the officer in charge, Commander Maro. I'm sure you remember him. You'll gain unrestricted access to the kitchens, and then the Emperor. You're posing as a chef, so you'll be able to poison his meal rather easily.
Skyrim.esm|04BCB6: A... giant's toe? You're... sure about that?
Skyrim.esm|04BCB7: Oh yes. And I've decided you will have the honor.
Skyrim.esm|04BCB8: You're a Nord! I never would have guessed it! But where in the world did you learn to cook like that? Here in Skyrim? Or maybe somewhere less...
Skyrim.esm|04BCB9: Of course! I.... I'm sorry. One giant's toe. There. What, um... What next?
Skyrim.esm|04BCBA: It's a rare plant, found only on the island of Stros M'Kai. It can be served like any vegetable - cut up, put in a salad, perhaps boiled in a stew. One taste will lead to nearly instant death, so don't even think about it.
Skyrim.esm|04BCBB: A Septim? As in... a gold coin? Really? Ah, I see now. That would give the Potage le Magnifique a slightly metallic - but delicious - aftertaste. Simply brilliant. I have to say, the stew seems done. Add anything else, and we may dilute the distinct flavors. So... is that it?
Skyrim.esm|04BCBC: What's this now? ...order of his eminence... ...possessor of these papers... the "Gourmet"... By Azura. The Gourmet! I... I'm sorry! Your clothes... of course... I should have realized! Please, excuse my ignorance.  Gianna, the castle chef, has been eagerly awaiting your arrival. You should proceed to the kitchens straight away.
Skyrim.esm|04BCBD: Well, I just never expected the Gourmet to be a Breton. It almost seems too obvious. Some of the greatest cooks have been Bretons, and...
Skyrim.esm|04BCBE: I... Well, I just can't believe the Gourmet is a Dark Elf. How difficult it must have been for you in Morrowind. The food there is...
Skyrim.esm|04BCBF: Oh, I have the utmost faith in your success, believe me. So let's begin. Go now to Castle Dour in Solitude. Present the Gourmet's Writ of Passage to the officer in charge, Commander Maro. I'm sure you remember him. You'll gain unrestricted access to the kitchens, and then the Emperor. You're posing as a chef, so you'll be able to poison his meal rather easily.
Skyrim.esm|04BCC0: Oh? What is this, some kind of herb? Are you sure? The Potage tastes perfect as it is. Any other ingredient might...
Skyrim.esm|04BCC1: It's just... Well... you're an Orc! Ha ha ha! If the people of the Empire only knew! I mean, now I understand your need for secrecy. Most would...
Skyrim.esm|04BCC2: Vampire dust? Seriously? Hmm... yes. I guess I can see how that would add a more... earthy texture. And, oddly enough, we do have some on hand... All right. What next, then?
Skyrim.esm|04BCCE: Please, help Yamarz. Do whatever you can. Our tribe depends on you.
Skyrim.esm|04BCD1: Please be careful here. The site isn't entirely secured.
Skyrim.esm|04BCEB: Hey. You wanna play a game?
Skyrim.esm|04BCEC: Figures. You elders are always so boring.
Skyrim.esm|04BCED: Really? All right! You pick. Tag or hide-and-seek?
Skyrim.esm|04BCEF: I know all the good spots! You don't stand a chance! You stand right there and count to 10. No peaking. Ready? Okay go!
Skyrim.esm|04BD97: I wish we could offer more help, but our last attack went sour and we're all that is left. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|04BD98: Finally, reinforcements have arrived. I was expecting more though, you'd better be good.
Skyrim.esm|04BD99: Fine then.  The blood of my family is on your hands as well as Brurid's.
Skyrim.esm|04BE52: You made your decision. Now live with it.
Skyrim.esm|04BE53: Now, back to the matter at hand.
Skyrim.esm|04BE54: Pay no attention to him. A ponderous windbag, like most Imperial generals.
Skyrim.esm|04BE55: The Dragonborn has spoken, Tullius. Markarth will be ours.
Skyrim.esm|04BE57: Legate, with me. We have a lot of work to do.
Skyrim.esm|04BE58: Your actions here were a disgrace. You betrayed your oath to the Emperor at every turn.
Skyrim.esm|04BE59: You... you disappointed me. You gave too much away. More than was necessary to secure this truce.
Skyrim.esm|04BE5A: The Empire showed its true colors today, eh? Don't worry. I was once loyal to the Empire, too, before my eyes were opened. Come see me in Windhelm when all this is over, and I'll find a place for you.
Skyrim.esm|04BE5B: I think you may have joined up with the wrong side, Dragonborn. You showed yourself a true son of Skyrim here today.
Skyrim.esm|04BE5D: Fair? This is war, soldier. War's about winning, not being fair to the enemy.
Skyrim.esm|04BE5E: Now we'll see if there's anything behind your talk of good faith.
Skyrim.esm|04BE5F: Welcome, brother! I'm glad that your eyes have finally been opened to the Empire's true nature.
Skyrim.esm|04BE60: I'm sorry to hear that. I thought your eyes had finally been opened to the Empire's true nature.
Skyrim.esm|04BE62: I know you, Ulfric. If I hand over Markarth, you'll be ready with a new demand.
Skyrim.esm|04BE64: You're losing this war, and you know it.
Skyrim.esm|04BE65: You'll never defeat the Empire, and you know it. But you're willing to sacrifice thousands for your own selfish ambition.
Skyrim.esm|04BE66: By Talos, that's an excellent question. I hope you haven't forgotten that little detail?
Skyrim.esm|04BE67: Jarl Vignar, I assume you are familiar with the Dragonborn's plan?
Skyrim.esm|04BE69: This is quite an achievement, Dragonborn. And Jarl Ulfric. Markarth is ours!
Skyrim.esm|04BE6A: I admit, I really didn't expect these talks to succeed. Getting the Empire to agree to anything at all is quite an achievement.
Skyrim.esm|04BE6B: Yes, I'm ready to do my part.
Skyrim.esm|04BE6C: Just say the word, and my men will help you spring this trap.
Skyrim.esm|04BE72: You are hereby dishonorably dismissed and removed from the rolls of the Imperial Legion.
Skyrim.esm|04BE73: I'm sure that will be comforting to your comrades that gave their lives for the territory you just handed to Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|04BE74: You should think harder on your oath to the Emperor next time you're deciding what's right.
Skyrim.esm|04BE88: Oh, no. Nothing like that. You're calling on her for business.
Skyrim.esm|04BE8A: Let me take a look at what you found.
Skyrim.esm|04BE8B: Ha ha. If it was like that she wouldn't be asking for you, she'd be calling on the Dark Brotherhood. It's just business.
Skyrim.esm|04BE8C: Word on the street is Goldenglow's been hit. Good job, lad.
Skyrim.esm|04BE8D: Word on the street is Goldenglow's been hit. Good job, lass.
Skyrim.esm|04BE8E: Don't sound so eager, it's not a social visit. It's business.
Skyrim.esm|04BE8F: Aye. Aringoth sent the city guard packing and fortified the entire island. In fact, Vex barely made it out of there alive. You should talk to her about it before you go.
Skyrim.esm|04BE90: We had an arrangement with Maven. We kept an eye on Goldenglow Estate to make sure the honey kept flowing. If the workers had a dispute, we'd rough them up. If competitors tried to buy honey from Aringoth, we'd steal the shipments. In return, Maven allowed us to extort Aringoth and bring in a huge payout.
Skyrim.esm|04BE91: That's between you and Maven and I prefer to keep it that way. Just keep your ears open and your mouth shut and you'll do fine.
Skyrim.esm|04BE92: That's between you and Maven and I prefer to keep it that way. Don't worry about it. Maven's business dealings usually involve quite a bit of gold for her people.
Skyrim.esm|04C088: We can just sacrifice another disciple. Apprentice Haerlon will be no great waste.
Skyrim.esm|04C089: We can't take another villager from the surface so soon. I told you to prepare everything properly!
Skyrim.esm|04C08A: More souls are needed for the Star. The last one died before he could be harvested.
Skyrim.esm|04C09E: You look like you've just seen a ghost. Please tell me you haven't. Cicero said he spoke to the Night Mother... but she spoke to you?
Skyrim.esm|04C09F: Okay, let's all take a deep breath. Cicero said he spoke to the Night Mother, but she spoke to you? Please tell me this is some kind of sick joke.
Skyrim.esm|04C15D: I'm going to Movarth's Lair to kill that monster. Are you with me?
Skyrim.esm|04C15E: Morthal is full of cowards! Let's go kill that monster.
Skyrim.esm|04C293: We're not done talking. You fell for that hussy Esmerelda, with the dark feathers - didn't you? I won't let her have you!
Skyrim.esm|04C2B3: Choose your path and your journey will be complete.
Skyrim.esm|04C2B4: This Agent of Strife can send forth a tendril of pure darkness into the heart of another, causing great injury to them. At the same time, this tether will bolster the agent's own life force, making him stronger.
Skyrim.esm|04C2B5: The Agent of Subterfuge utilizes shadow to cloud the judgement of those around him. By weaving the darkness to their will, this agent can manipulate others into fighting for the Nightingale for a limited time.
Skyrim.esm|04C2B6: The Agent of Stealth is the master of remaining unseen. They are able to manipulate the darkness and use it to their advantage. On moonlit nights or in darkened rooms, this agent literally becomes invisible.
Skyrim.esm|04C2BE: I return from the war to find my home overrun with bandit filth.
Skyrim.esm|04C3D5: You are the Listener! You are the Listener! I have served mother well, I have!
Skyrim.esm|04C3D6: I don't even know what to think anymore...
Skyrim.esm|04C3D7: Are you excited? Oh, you should be. Things are finally looking up!
Skyrim.esm|04C3D8: Ha! Surely you're pulling Cicero's leg! Wait... the Listener is serious? Oh. Well of course the Night Mother will speak to you again! She might speak now, or later, or... I don't know! But speak she will! The Night Mother is part of you now! You get to hear voices inside your head! The rest of us should be so lucky.
Skyrim.esm|04C3D9: Oh... Well... yes. I did. I did indeed. I tried to listen. Tried so hard. But the Night Mother never spoke to poor Cicero. The silence became almost... maddening. Oh, but that was then! This is now! You're the Listener, and the Night Mother chose you for a reason, I'm sure! Cicero will remain the happy Keeper.
Skyrim.esm|04C3DA: Oh, the Listener, well... listens! The Night Mother speaks to you. Guides you on your path. But when the Night Mother speaks, the Listener must obey. You must! For her word is the will of Sithis. And Sithis is the Dark Brotherhood incarnate.
Skyrim.esm|04C425: This is my realm. I won't let you take it from me!
Skyrim.esm|04C429: I was a sorceress of no small skill before I came into Azura's faith. Afterwards, her magic sustained me. I could easily provide my spells to your cause. Protect you from harm. Defeat your enemies.
Skyrim.esm|04C42A: Ah, my disciples have sent me a fresh soul. Good. I was getting... hungry. Wait. There's something different about you.
Skyrim.esm|04C42D: Are you ready for me to show Azura her Star and receive her wisdom?
Skyrim.esm|04C42E: While you were in the Star, Azura gave me a vision. Her last, she said. I have never been without Azura's foresight since escaping Morrowind. I don't know what to do. If you need me, I'd be honored to accompany you, Guardian of the Star. It would give me a purpose.
Skyrim.esm|04C42F: Leave this shrine, blasphemer, before I kill you for defiling Azura's artifact.
Skyrim.esm|04C430: She wishes to speak to you herself. Please. Place your hands on the altar, and you will hear her voice.
Skyrim.esm|04C431: Just glad all that business with Malyn is over with. What did you need?
Skyrim.esm|04C432: I don't like doing business with Azura's faithful. Make it quick.
Skyrim.esm|04C433: Are you ready to give me the Star?
Skyrim.esm|04C434: Azura. Mother of Roses. Goddess of Dusk and Dawn. Your chosen champion has returned your Star to you.
Skyrim.esm|04C435: No. She said my part was over. That my fate had moved beyond the Twilight, and I was on my own. I will tend Azura's shrine when you do not need me. I still have my duties, but for the first time I feel... alone.
Skyrim.esm|04C436: And who are you to challenge me? I have conquered mortality itself. I've spat in the eyes of the Daedric Lords. This is my realm now. I've sacrificed too much to let you take it from me!
Skyrim.esm|04C469: I've seen better. Well, maybe not.
Skyrim.esm|04C470: You sure? All right. I'll keep guard here.
Skyrim.esm|04C485: She finally decided to return it to her father. Bless her heart. I will make sure this finds its way to his tomb as soon as possible. Thank you for bringing it, my child.
Skyrim.esm|04C486: I had to for honor's sake, but I had waited too long. My father died before he could see my training to completion. When I finally became a priest, they gave me the new high priest's dagger, but I already owned my father's. So you see, I have to return it. It breaks tradition, and I need to tell him that I finally did what he wanted.
Skyrim.esm|04C487: A Priest of Arkay is entrusted with a ceremonial dagger once they've completed their training. It usually given by the head priest who sanctified the ritual. In my case, the head priest happened to be my father. The thing is, I never wanted to follow in his footsteps... I some ways I still don't.
Skyrim.esm|04C4B1: Malborn? Ah... very good. I think we understand each other.
Skyrim.esm|04C4B3: Well, we'd better get back to our rounds.
Skyrim.esm|04C4B4: Of course. I've told you before not to bother me with such trifles.
Skyrim.esm|04C4B6: Move on, friend. I have nothing to sell today.
Skyrim.esm|04C4B7: Move on, friend. I have nothing to sell today.
Skyrim.esm|04C4B8: Do I have your permission to uncork the Arenthia red...
Skyrim.esm|04C4B9: Ah yes. I remember your name from the guest list.
Skyrim.esm|04C4CB: I was trying to get to Morrowind. The Dunmer don't care much for the Thalmor and I figured I'd be safe there. As safe as anywhere,that is. But there's a Khajiit hanging around the main gate. I didn't like the way he was looking at me. I'm sure he's Thalmor. Just waiting for me to leave so he can follow me and kill me in a lonely place.
Skyrim.esm|04C4CC: It's just that we've run out of the Alto wine.
Skyrim.esm|04C4CD: Sure, fine. Thanks for nothing. You already got what you needed from me.
Skyrim.esm|04C4CE: No, you can't ever be too paranoid where the Thalmor is concerned. Look what happened to the Blades. The Blades! If the damn Blades are no match for the Thalmor, what chance do I have? Please, just go check it out. You'll be saving my life.
Skyrim.esm|04C4D0: You will? Really? Well, okay, here's the situation.
Skyrim.esm|04C4EC: He looks so... sweet. Go ahead. Have the first bite.
Skyrim.esm|04C58C: You'd be saving my life. Again. I know I probably seemed ungrateful... I've just been so scared. Expecting a knife at my throat any minute.
Skyrim.esm|04C593: Madame Ambassador, I'm so sorry to interrupt...
Skyrim.esm|04C594: Trying to stay alive. I think time's running out though. I'm pretty sure the Thalmor know where I am. Maybe you can help me. You know, by way of making amends for getting me into this mess.
Skyrim.esm|04C5E1: Why struggle? The Thalmor will only send more like me.
Skyrim.esm|04C5F1: Have you? All good, I trust. But you have me at a disadvantage. I'm afraid I know nothing about you...
Skyrim.esm|04C6BC: Well, there's an old sewer tunnel that dumps into the lake on the northwest side of the island. That's how I slipped in there. Should still be unguarded.
Skyrim.esm|04C6BD: Watch yourself out there. The mercenaries Aringoth hired are trained killers. Just ask our poor little Vex.
Skyrim.esm|04C6BE: The honey they collect on that hunk of rock goes into making Black-Briar Mead; just about the best tasting mead in all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|04C6BF: I wouldn't bother smacking at those hives with a weapon. Set 'em on fire and they'll go up like kindling.
Skyrim.esm|04C6C0: I'd like to know why you're wasting time asking about it when you should already be on your way.
Skyrim.esm|04C77F: Don't worry, I'm mad at her, too. And at myself for ever agreeing to get involved in her insane scheme.
Skyrim.esm|04C781: Please, tell me more about yourself. What brings you to this... to Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|04C782: Don't worry, my parties are always well-stocked with the finest food and drink available in this country. But first, you were introducing yourself.
Skyrim.esm|04CA80: I count my blessings every day.
Skyrim.esm|04CA81: Here you go, ma'am. The finest Colovian brandy. Is there anything else I can get for you?
Skyrim.esm|04CB5A: We seem to have run out of Colovian brandy. I'll check in the wine cellar for more when I get a moment.
Skyrim.esm|04CB7F: Here you go, miss. Colovian brandy.
Skyrim.esm|04CB8E: I'm sorry, but I think you've had enough for the moment.
Skyrim.esm|04CB9B: Trying to stay ahead of the Thalmor assassins. I'm not a hero like you. I don't stand a chance.
Skyrim.esm|04CB9C: My apologies. We'll have to get better acquainted later. Please, enjoy yourself.
Skyrim.esm|04CBA0: That's... great news. I'd... I'd better go. Right now. Before they find me again. This is my chance. Thank you! Here... I stole a lot from the Thalmor over the years. You may as well have some of it.
Skyrim.esm|04CBA8: I was trying to get to Morrowind. The Dunmer don't care much for the Thalmor and I figured I'd be safe there. As safe as anywhere, that is. But there's a Khajiit hanging around the main gate. I didn't like the way he was looking at me. I'm sure he's Thalmor. Just waiting for me to leave so he can follow me and kill me in a lonely place.
Skyrim.esm|04CBAE: I was trying to get to Morrowind. The Dunmer don't care much for the Thalmor and I figured I'd be safe there. As safe as anywhere,that is. But there's a Khajiit hanging around the main gate. I didn't like the way he was looking at me. I'm sure he's Thalmor. Just waiting for me to leave so he can follow me and kill me in a lonely place.
Skyrim.esm|04CBB4: Oh, it's you. I didn't expect to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|04CC45: I suppose that's the fairest deal we're likely to get.
Skyrim.esm|04CC46: It seems I have no choice but to let the Dragonborn decide. Although I'm starting to doubt your fairness.
Skyrim.esm|04CC47: Don't hand me a mug of sheep's piss and call it mead. These terms are still not acceptable.
Skyrim.esm|04CC48: Come on, Tullius. You have to give a little ground here. The dragons are a threat to us both.
Skyrim.esm|04CDB2: Thank you for your help.  Did you see my family inside?
Skyrim.esm|04CDB3: He's a rogue mage who defected at the beginning of the war.  I turned him in but apparently he escaped and now this.
Skyrim.esm|04D0D4: Please, I don't have anything to pay you with! Don't destroy my urn!
Skyrim.esm|04D0E6: If I had it, I'd pay! There's no need to do that!
Skyrim.esm|04D0F3: No! You're going to break it!
Skyrim.esm|04D10F: I can't believe you're doing this! Stop!
Skyrim.esm|04D1F6: Did you hear? The Hall of the Dead is closed. Brother Verulus isn't the most popular man in town right now.
Skyrim.esm|04D1F9: Thanks again for saving my life. I think I'm done with venturing outside of Markarth, though.
Skyrim.esm|04D241: Names no longer matter. You may refer to me as The Caller. Now, do you have a reason for making such a mess?
Skyrim.esm|04D242: Oh, now we're all please and thank you, are we? I'm afraid we're well beyond pleasantries. I'll allow you the opportunity to turn around, walk out that door, and never come back. I suggest you move quickly.
Skyrim.esm|04D243: So you're just one of Aren's lackeys? That's disappointing. You show real promise. You come here, kill my assistants, disrupt my work... You've annoyed me, so I don't think I'll be giving you anything.
Skyrim.esm|04D244: So, you're the one who barged into my home and laid waste to my projects. How nice to meet you.
Skyrim.esm|04D245: I'm afraid you don't have anything worth trading. Now, you can go back to your College and leave me be, or I can kill you. Your choice.
Skyrim.esm|04D2D3: Brother! Mara's mercy, it's good to see you!
Skyrim.esm|04D2D4: Hod! Come here a minute. I need your help with something.
Skyrim.esm|04D2D5: And who's this? One of your comrades?
Skyrim.esm|04D318: Is there somewhere we can talk? There's no telling when the news from Helgen will reach the Imperials...
Skyrim.esm|04D545: Ah. I see. Well, then I'll just stay behind you a little bit, and follow along quietly.
Skyrim.esm|04D546: An arrangement? After you've destroyed so much? Fine. Leave me the elf. You may go with your books.
Skyrim.esm|04D547: How could you just leave me here?
Skyrim.esm|04D548: I've got to get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|04D54B: Yes, of course. I said I'd tell you, didn't I? The Caller will have them. She was most interested in one of the volumes. Although not interested enough to keep me from being locked up.
Skyrim.esm|04D56D: But is it safe for you to be here? We heard that Ulfric had been captured...
Skyrim.esm|04D574: Ralof! What are you doing here? Ah... I'll be right down.
Skyrim.esm|04D5FB: Not a comrade yet, but a friend. I owe him my life, in fact.
Skyrim.esm|04D5FE: Not a comrade yet, but a friend. I owe her my life, in fact.
Skyrim.esm|04D609: Looks like nobody here knows what's happened yet. Come on. Gerdur's probably working in her lumber mill.
Skyrim.esm|04D6B6: Gerdur, I'm fine. At least now I am.
Skyrim.esm|04D6BD: No! Don't pity me. I deserve whatever fate the Dread Lord has in store. I betrayed you... and now Maro has betrayed me. Fitting... I just wanted things... to stay the way they were. Before Cicero, before the Night Mother. Before... you. I thought I could save us. I was wrong. But you're alive! So there's still a chance. A chance to start over, rebuild. That's why I did... this. Don't you see? I prayed to the Night Mother! I am the Black Sacrament.
Skyrim.esm|04D6BE: Don't you see? It was me. I set you up, wanted you dead. I betrayed you, the Night Mother... everything I hold dear. And now Maro has betrayed me. I just wanted things... to stay the way they were. Before Cicero, before the Night Mother. Before... you. I thought I could save us. I was wrong. But you're alive! So there's still a chance. A chance to start over, rebuild. That's why I did... this. Don't you see? I prayed to the Night Mother! I am the Black Sacrament.
Skyrim.esm|04D6BF: I'm saying you were right. The Night Mother was right. The old ways... they guided the Dark Brotherhood for centuries. I was a fool to oppose them. And to prove my... sincerity, I have prayed for a contract. You lead this Family now. I give you the Blade of Woe, so that you can see it through. You must kill... Me.
Skyrim.esm|04D6C1: Yes. I set you up. I wanted you dead. I betrayed you, the Night Mother, and everything I hold dear. And now Maro has betrayed me. I just wanted things... to stay the way they were. Before Cicero, before the Night Mother. Before... you. I thought I could save us. I was wrong. But you're alive! So there's still a chance. A chance to start over, rebuild. That's why I did... this. Don't you see? I prayed to the Night Mother! I am the Black Sacrament.
Skyrim.esm|04D6C2: Ssshhh.... Please. There is much... I have to say. And... not much time... I'm sorry. So very sorry. The Penitus Oculatus... Maro... He said that by giving you to them, he would leave the Dark Brotherhood alone. Forever. By Sithis, I was such a fool. All of this... it's all my fault. You are the best of us, and I nearly killed you... as I've killed everyone else...
Skyrim.esm|04D785: The number of graves in this cemetery never decreases.
Skyrim.esm|04D786: War is not new to Falkreath. You have only to stand in our cemetery to see that.
Skyrim.esm|04D78A: Dengeir deserves more respect than his nephew, Jarl Siddgeir, affords him.
Skyrim.esm|04D78C: Without birth there is no life, without death there is only stasis. Such is the truth of Arkay.
Skyrim.esm|04D863: Are you all right? Can you walk? I need you on your feet. We're in trouble here.
Skyrim.esm|04D872: Ancano! Stop this at once! I command you!
Skyrim.esm|04D875: I don't care what it is, I want it down now! I want to know what he's doing in there!
Skyrim.esm|04D877: Excellent. I'd suggest that we go retrieve it immediately, but right now we have more pressing matters. Ancano has somehow locked himself in the hall. He's up to something, and I intend to put a stop to it. Help us get through this, will you? We're throwing everything we have at it.
Skyrim.esm|04D886: Ancano is doing something with that thing... the Eye. We can't stop him! I haven't seen Savos since the explosion. He must've been blown clear, and he may be injured. I need you to find the Arch-Mage, and I need you to do it quickly. Get moving!
Skyrim.esm|04D89E: Ancano. He's in there, doing... something. We don't know what. We're trying to get in now. I will have his head for this, I assure you. Help take this down, will you? We're throwing everything we can at it.
Skyrim.esm|04D8AF: Then you are weak and inconsequential.
Skyrim.esm|04D8B7: Ahhhhgh. Wearing flesh is so... distasteful. You have caught my attention, mortal. That is most unwise.
Skyrim.esm|04D8B8: Shhh! Be still. Beware her anger!
Skyrim.esm|04D8BA: My favor? My good pleasure is not some trophy for mortal sport! I should slay you for such insolence!
Skyrim.esm|04D8BB: Were you certain in your purpose, you would have no need to apologize. You have much to learn... But you have proven your will is strong and your heart is devoid of pity. So, perhaps you are not entirely useless... We shall soon find out.
Skyrim.esm|04D8BE: The Lord of Plots, Deceiver of Nations, Devourer of Trinimac. The Queen of Shadows, Goddess of Destruction, He-Who-Destroys and She-Who-Erases... Many are our lord's lofty names, but they befit only us mortals. Intone her mighty names from now until the end of time - it is for naught. Names mean nothing to our Lord. She only cares for those who care for themselves, whose hearts are full of purpose, whose lives are full of deeds.
Skyrim.esm|04D8C1: We are forged by Boethiah's example. We carve our will upon the world through our tongues and our blades. You? You are nothing. An empty vapor, dispersed by the slightest breeze.
Skyrim.esm|04D8C2: A well placed word, or a well placed dagger. Both can achieve equal purposes.
Skyrim.esm|04D8C4: Though we live in shadow, we carve indelible signs of our passing into the world.
Skyrim.esm|04D8C6: We are Lord Boethiah's faithful. Have you come to test your mettle?
Skyrim.esm|04D8C9: If you desire a glimpse of our Dark Mistress, then this is what you must do. First, prove your tongue can wield a lie. Find someone. Gain his trust. Lead him to the shrine above. Next, instruct your thrall to touch the Pillar of Sacrifice. Its magic will ensnare him, rendering him helpless. Then, girded with certitude, plant the ceremonial blade deeply, so that the waters of his heart wash over you. If your will is strong enough, it will stir Boethiah in her Dark Mansion, and she will appear before us.
Skyrim.esm|04D8CA: That remains to be seen. But if it is so, and if Boethiah appears, then we will welcome you as one of us.
Skyrim.esm|04DA15: He gets the books, I get you. It's very simple.
Skyrim.esm|04DA17: You wouldn't do that, would you? You wouldn't leave me here with her!
Skyrim.esm|04DA18: Fine. Take your books and leave at once. If you ever come back, I won't be so gracious.
Skyrim.esm|04DBB7: I can promise you if it comes to that, and my back's to the wall, I won't hesitate.
Skyrim.esm|04DBB9: She's the mistress of night and darkness and the patron of every thief in Tamriel.
Skyrim.esm|04DBBB: Again, you have to think differently. Haven't you ever noticed how our luck behaves? Like a novice picking an impossible lock or a blind man suddenly turning to face you as you reach for his pocket? It's through these subtle means that Nocturnal influences us.
Skyrim.esm|04DBBC: Nocturnal isn't one for worship and reverence. There are no priests and no sermons, no services and no alms. She influences our luck and in return demands payment.
Skyrim.esm|04DBBD: You're closer to understanding than you realize. The only difference is she doesn't demand payment in the traditional sense and sometimes the cost can be quite high. Whether you know it or not, Nocturnal dictates how well we perform as rogues.
Skyrim.esm|04DBBE: Nocturnal's whim is the greatest mystery to everyone. There have been volumes written on the subject. Does she exact payment when we die? When we suffer does she revel in our misery? No one knows. The return certainly seems worth the risk though.
Skyrim.esm|04DBBF: He was a scholar, a master thief and a natural leader. Everyone respected him and followed him without question. It was Gallus who inducted me into the Nightingales and honed my skills to a razor sharp point. I owe everything to him. We were... very close.
Skyrim.esm|04DBC0: Gallus once said he felt comfortable around me; able to let his guard down. I can't help but think that I'm responsible for what happened to him.
Skyrim.esm|04DBC1: Mercer lied to the Guild, branded me a murderer and slandered my name across his network of contacts. For twenty-five years I ran, never sleeping in the same place twice and carefully covering my tracks. Mercer doesn't need to die... he needs to feel the cold sting of fate as his life crumbles in front of him and he's hunted by the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|04DCB2: Strangers like you bring war. I've seen enough war.
Skyrim.esm|04DCB3: These graves hold the sons and daughters of Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|04DCB6: Show respect while you're here and you and I will be fine.
Skyrim.esm|04DCB7: You have the look of a killer.
Skyrim.esm|04DCB8: Arkay helps us find peace with death.
Skyrim.esm|04DCBB: Indara and I like to keep to ourselves and run our farm.
Skyrim.esm|04DCBC: My wife, Indara, has a gift with animals.  It makes her a better farmer than anyone I've ever seen.
Skyrim.esm|04DCBD: This war isn't needed. Imperials like my wife can be hard to handle but we Nords could work with them. I should know.
Skyrim.esm|04DCBE: Runil has been a true friend to my wife and I in our time of need.
Skyrim.esm|04DCBF: I don't know if the war is Arkay's will or man's, but it's none of my doings.
Skyrim.esm|04DCC2: My husband Mathias and I tend Corpselight Farm.
Skyrim.esm|04DCC3: Falkreath is a hard place and tending a farm here is thankless work.
Skyrim.esm|04DCC5: Mathies is a kind and wise man. The Nords could use more like him. So could the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|04DCC6: How could people see all the graves in Falkreath and still want to make war?
Skyrim.esm|04DDAA: Are you attempting to threaten me? After I've been so hospitable? Well, then you won't be leaving here at all.
Skyrim.esm|04DE32: Zu'u ni tahrodiis. It was you that lured me here and took me prisoner... vobalaan grahmindol. I have done nothing to earn your distrust.
Skyrim.esm|04DE35: Hin aar, orin nu. And yet here I am, still your prisoner.
Skyrim.esm|04DE36: Indeed. Orin brit ro. I cannot leave here until you defeat Alduin, which you cannot do without my help.
Skyrim.esm|04DE37: Ahraan. You wound me, Dovahkiin. I may not tell the whole truth, but I am no liar. Go and see for yourself. Zu'u ni bo nol het. I will be here... unless Alduin returns before you do.
Skyrim.esm|04DE38: You have reconsidered my offer, hmm? Onikaan kron?
Skyrim.esm|04DE39: Hey, when you make up your mind, just let me know. I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|04DE3A: Saraan uth - I await your command, as promised. Are you ready to see the world as only a dovah can?
Skyrim.esm|04DE3B: Your funeral. Someone else is gonna have to help you get him back in there again.
Skyrim.esm|04DE3C: Zok brit uth! I warn you, once you've flown the skies of Keizaal, your envy of the dov will only increase.
Skyrim.esm|04DE3D: Only this. You have the Thu'um of a dovah, but without the wings of one, you will never set foot in Skuldafn. Of course... I could fly you there. But not while imprisoned like this.
Skyrim.esm|04DE3E: Ah. Well. Hmm... krosis. There is one... detail about Skuldafn I neglected to mention.
Skyrim.esm|04DE3F: This seems like a really bad idea to me.
Skyrim.esm|04DE40: Onikaan koraav gein miraad. It is wise to recognize when you only have one choice. And you can trust me. Zu'u ni tahrodiis. Alduin has proven himself unworthy to rule. I go my own way now. Free me, and I will carry you to Skuldafn.
Skyrim.esm|04DE41: You sure about that? You want to let that dragon loose after all the trouble to catch him in there?
Skyrim.esm|04DE42: Kreh zini! The freedom of the sky beckons! Yet I stay here, nau gol, as promised.
Skyrim.esm|04DE85: Steel's good, but loyalty's better. I'm loyal to Dengeir first and the Empire second.
Skyrim.esm|04DE87: The war bleeds Skyrim. It bleeds her dry and I think none of us are the better for it.
Skyrim.esm|04DE8C: Someone new, perhaps someone looking for me to mix something up for them?
Skyrim.esm|04DE8E: If either these Nords or Imperials had some red in their blood this war would be over.
Skyrim.esm|04DE92: Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
Skyrim.esm|04DE93: A hearty welcome to Falkreath!  All of Skyrim's true sons are welcome here!
Skyrim.esm|04DE94: Stay out of my way, boot.
Skyrim.esm|04DE98: I can't believe we let provincials like you wander Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|04DE9A: You'll find my brother Solaf and I are the only true Nords in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|04DE9E: Well met. Unlike my brother, I've no dislike of strangers. Met lots of 'em while I was a Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|04DE9F: Don't mind Bolund. He's young and gets riled easily.
Skyrim.esm|04DEAD: I have little need for mindless thralls. My servants serve their own purposes. But, if their will is insufficient, nor suitably aligned with mine, I simply destroy them. Which brings me to my present concern.
Skyrim.esm|04DEAE: You have earned the right to call yourself my Champion. And now you must perform the task for which I created you. You have already proven to possess the needed skills. Go to Solitude. Stalk the Jarl and slay her. I have spread rumors of an assassin. It will make your job harder, but will allow me to place blame where I desire it. You will need stealth and lies to get past her guards. You will assassinate her swiftly and quietly. And if combat finds you, survive the escape. Succeed, and you may keep your title and my armor, and I shall write your name upon the Tablet of Absolute Blackness. Failure is not an option.
Skyrim.esm|04DEAF: Well done. You have proven the strength of your will and your tongue's gift for lies. You have shown ferocity and prowess in combat. Now the time has come for a final proving. Are you able to cast aside your honor and strike with the hidden blade?
Skyrim.esm|04DEB0: I am loath to utter his name... one cannot erase a thing if it has a name to be remembered.
Skyrim.esm|04DEB1: You have done well, my Champion. You have earned my respect, a feat few manage and live to tell about it. Now go. I have strings to pull that require my full attention. You may pursue your own course wherever it leads you. Remember always this: You need answer to no authority, neither mortal, nor divine... As you will it, so it shall be.
Skyrim.esm|04E195: We better get moving. Come here. Let me see if I can get those bindings off.
Skyrim.esm|04E19D: We'll meet again in Sovngarde, brother.
Skyrim.esm|04E19E: Here we are, found a key. Let's see if it opens that door.
Skyrim.esm|04E1A0: This one's locked. Let's see about that gate.
Skyrim.esm|04E1A2: There you go. You may as well take Gunjar's gear. He won't be needing it any more.
Skyrim.esm|04E1A3: That's it! Come on, let's get out of here before the dragon brings the whole tower down on our heads.
Skyrim.esm|04E1A5: Maybe one of these Imperials had the key.
Skyrim.esm|04E1A6: Looks like we're the only ones who made it.
Skyrim.esm|04E1A7: I'm going to see if I can find some way out of here.
Skyrim.esm|04E1AB: Damn. No way to open this from our side.
Skyrim.esm|04E281: Shor's bones! A handsome man in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|04E282: You're going to have the men around here wrapped around your finger in no time.
Skyrim.esm|04E285: Can I get you a drink?
Skyrim.esm|04E288: I wouldn't complain if we could string together a few sunny days now and then.
Skyrim.esm|04E28B: I escaped fighting in Cyrodiil only to have it track me down again in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|04E28E: Try not to judge Falkreath too harshly, visitor - the war has been hard on us.
Skyrim.esm|04E292: I'm sorry, I should really go now.
Skyrim.esm|04E293: I can't really help you, I'm just Dengeir's maid.
Skyrim.esm|04E295: I was Jarl once, but I was encouraged to step down when I took sides with the Stormcloaks. It was an imperial plot, I tell you!
Skyrim.esm|04E296: If you're sympathetic to the Empire, you'll be wise to put Falkreath far behind you.
Skyrim.esm|04E297: Stendarr protect Falkreath until Skyrim's Sons rise.
Skyrim.esm|04E298: Enemies and ears. Both are everywhere.
Skyrim.esm|04E299: The war has changed Falkreath, even our neighbors can be our enemies.
Skyrim.esm|04E29C: I protect the Jarl, whoever that might be.
Skyrim.esm|04E29D: Falkreath's cemetery reminds of us that war is not new here.
Skyrim.esm|04E29E: Being Housecarl of Falkreath is an honor.
Skyrim.esm|04E29F: Treat the Jarl with respect, and you'll be welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|04E2A1: I'm the Steward here.  I serve under Jarl Siddgeir and Jarl Dengeir before him.
Skyrim.esm|04E2A4: Siddgeir can sometimes act too quickly but he does listen to Helvard and I.
Skyrim.esm|04E2A5: For Falkreath and I this is just one more war in a long line.
Skyrim.esm|04E2A6: Yes? What is it that you want?
Skyrim.esm|04E2A8: Boland speaks much about the war and the Stormcloaks but he knows who provides.
Skyrim.esm|04E2AA: The Stormcloaks want freedom but don't understand its price.
Skyrim.esm|04E2AC: There are opportunities everywhere.  Why don't you go find them.
Skyrim.esm|04E2AD: No time, I have business to conduct.
Skyrim.esm|04E397: Maven Black-Briar's gone out of her way to try and have me shut down, but fortunately I have a good relationship with Whiterun's Jarl. Every year, I sell more Honningbrew and Maven loses customers. Know why? My mead tastes better.
Skyrim.esm|04E399: Faith? I don't have faith in anyone. All I care about is cause and effect. Did the job get done and was it done correctly. There's no gray area.
Skyrim.esm|04E39A: Exactly. With Sabjorn in prison, his meadery will be forced to close. Then I swoop in and take over the place. No more competition.
Skyrim.esm|04E3A1: This is exactly what I'm talking about. Once again Brynjolf sends me someone with no backbone, no determination.
Skyrim.esm|04E3A4: I bought some poison. I was going to have my lazy, good-for-nothing assistant Mallus handle it, but he seems to have vanished. If you plant this in the vermin's nest, it should stop them from ever coming back.
Skyrim.esm|04E3A5: Word on the street is that poor Sabjorn has found himself in Whiterun's prison. How unfortunate for him.
Skyrim.esm|04E3A6: Not a day goes by that I don't regret letting Sabjorn get as far as he did. In only a few short years, he's taken that bile he calls mead to market and a chunk of my profits with it! I can't imagine where he found the gold to take it to market so quickly.
Skyrim.esm|04E3AA: So, you're the one. Hmm. You don't look so impressive.
Skyrim.esm|04E3AC: The only reason we're having this conversation is due to Brynjolf's assurance you won't botch another assignment. He claims you possess some sort of uncanny aptitude for your line of work. Quite frankly I find that hard to believe.
Skyrim.esm|04E3AD: I hope not. This is an important job. I have a competitor called Honningbrew Meadery that I want to put out of business. I also want to know how they managed to get the place up and running so quickly.
Skyrim.esm|04E3B0: Is that confidence I hear... or is it arrogance? Strange how often they're confused. You have to understand, it's been a long time since Brynjolf's sent me anyone I can rely on.
Skyrim.esm|04E4C6: But a single candle can banish the darkness of the entire Void. If not you, then someone else. My beacon is sure to attract a worthy soul. But if you are wise, you will heed my bidding.
Skyrim.esm|04E4C7: It is time for my splendor to return to Skyrim. But the token of my truth lies buried in the ruins of my once great temple, now tainted by a profane darkness skittering within.
Skyrim.esm|04E4C8: Can you not hear me? Are your ears full of hate? The defiler waxes! Go now, with haste. Take my beacon to Mount Kilkreath.
Skyrim.esm|04E4C9: A new hand touches the beacon.
Skyrim.esm|04E4CA: It matters not. The plant cares nothing for the rays that bring it the warmth of the sun. As you carry Dawnbreaker, so will my light touch the world.
Skyrim.esm|04E4CB: Of course you will. I have commanded it!
Skyrim.esm|04E4CC: The Necromancer Malkoran defiles my shrine with vile corruptions, trapping lost souls left in the wake of this war to do his bidding. Worse still, he uses the power stored within my own token to fuel his foul deeds. I have brought you here, mortal, to be my champion. You will enter my temple, retrieve my artifact, and destroy the defiler.
Skyrim.esm|04E4CD: Guide my light through the temple to open the inner sanctum and destroy the defiler.
Skyrim.esm|04E4CE: Malkoran is vanquished. Skyrim's dead shall remain at rest. This is as it should be. This is because of you. A new day is dawning. And you shall be its herald. Take the mighty Dawnbreaker and with it purge corruption from the dark corners of the world. Wield it in my name, that my influence may grow.
Skyrim.esm|04E4CF: May the light of certitude guide your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|04E4D0: Malkoran has forced the doors shut. But this is my temple, and it responds to my decree. I will send down a ray of light. Guide this light through my temple and its doors will open.
Skyrim.esm|04E4D1: Go now, the artifact must be reclaimed and Malkoran destroyed.
Skyrim.esm|04E64D: What is it? I said I didn't wish to... be disturbed.
Skyrim.esm|04E80A: I think perhaps it might be best if you lead the way. My eyes aren't what they used to be.
Skyrim.esm|04E811: Don't worry, I'll stay right with you.
Skyrim.esm|04E841: This is all very strange. I've never seen anything like this.
Skyrim.esm|04E84C: You mess with the Silver-Blood family, you end up here.
Skyrim.esm|04E84D: I catch you stealing anything, I'll break your neck.
Skyrim.esm|04E84E: Now this is real guard duty. Just throw them in the mine and be done with it.
Skyrim.esm|04E9B5: The World-Eater waits within the mist! Bring word to Shor's hall of our hard fate!
Skyrim.esm|04E9B6: Turn back, traveler! Terror waits within this mist. Many have braved the shadowed vale but vain is all courage against the peril that guards the way.
Skyrim.esm|04E9B7: I'll try to hold to your hopeful purpose. Quickly, before this encompassing fog once more snares me in the World-Eater's net.
Skyrim.esm|04E9B8: Near Giants' Gap, in the gloom before dawn, we marched, unsuspecting into the Imperials' trap. Then we stood and fought, our shield-wall defending until by dawn's light the Legion's ranks wavered. But I never knew if nights-end brought victory - a swift-flying arrow to Sovngarde carried me.
Skyrim.esm|04E9B9: I do not know - but none have passed through. Alduin, his hunger insatiable, hunts the lost souls snared within this shadowed valley. Can you lead the way to where Shor's hall waits, beckoning us on to welcome long sought?
Skyrim.esm|04E9BB: I saw it fair when first I trod this long-sought path. The pain and fear vanished, dreamlike, and a vision beckoned - Shor's hall, shimmering across the clouded vale. But quenched was hope by the shrouding mist - my mind is darkened. I've lost the way and wander blindly. Hurry! Before Alduin your life devours bring word to Shor's hall of our hard fate!
Skyrim.esm|04EC47: I want to be an adventurer like you, but my father says I can't. He says that he needs me to stay here and work the farm, and even if he did let me be an adventurer, we couldn't afford to buy armor. His name's Mralki, and he's the innkeeper here. I hope you can change his mind.
Skyrim.esm|04EC48: I'll take your challenge, but if I win I get to start a new life as an adventurer, and with no objections from you.
Skyrim.esm|04EC49: Thank you. I can't stand the thought of being trapped in this village for the rest of my days.
Skyrim.esm|04EC4B: Strong, yes, but how clever are you? I'll tell you what, if you can open the lock on my chest, you can have everything in it.
Skyrim.esm|04EC4C: Some folk here will tell you I'm sheltering my boy too much, but I ask you, isn't it a father's duty to keep his children safe?
Skyrim.esm|04EC4D: I was a different man, once -- strong, commanding, ah, and young. But the Great War was a long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|04EC4E: You look like you've seen your share of adventure. I envy you that. In fact, maybe you can help me with something. Will you hear me out?
Skyrim.esm|04EEEC: Hmmm. I don't think you've got enough to cover my expenses with Silver-Bloods, much less convince me give my words away.
Skyrim.esm|04EF17: This is insane! I didn't steal anything! I never saw this ring before in my entire life!
Skyrim.esm|04EF1A: All right, Brand-Shei. Turn out your pockets, we know you have it.
Skyrim.esm|04EF1B: I'm telling you, I don't... wait, what's this ring? This isn't mine!
Skyrim.esm|04EF1C: We can do this one of two ways. You can walk with me up to the keep, or I can drag your lifeless body. Your choice.
Skyrim.esm|04EF1E: Have what? What in blazes are you talking about?
Skyrim.esm|04EF20: That's right. It isn't yours. You're under arrest, Brand-Shei.
Skyrim.esm|04EF22: Don't play stupid. I said turn out your pockets... now!
Skyrim.esm|04F41B: Wait a second. Looks like there's something in this cage.
Skyrim.esm|04F41D: A storeroom. See if you can find any potions. We'll need them.
Skyrim.esm|04F41E: No, I haven't seen him since the dragon showed up.
Skyrim.esm|04F421: Damn, that dragon doesn't give up easy.
Skyrim.esm|04F424: Shor's bones. We were too late. Damn the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|04F74E: Don't you know? What drew you here? Surely your dreams showed you the way? The Hall of Valor, where heroes wait to follow Shor to the final battle.
Skyrim.esm|04F7DB: Astrid is dead. It is as it should be. May she find redemption in the Void. But while you live, the Dark Brotherhood lives. We must fulfill our contract. Emperor Titus Mede II must be eliminated. Speak with Amaund Motierre at the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. He will know the true Emperor's location. But first, inform Nazir of your plans. For you are the Listener, and must bind this Family together.
Skyrim.esm|04F8C6: I hope your stay in Markarth treats you better.
Skyrim.esm|04FA0F: Underneath the College. It's not the nicest place, so if you go down there, please do be careful.
Skyrim.esm|04FA10: The what of what? No. No idea. If it's important, maybe Tolfdir knows. He seems to know a lot.
Skyrim.esm|04FA11: Very well. It's not something often discussed, as it might be... misunderstood by the locals. The Augur is down in the Midden, below the College. I don't recommend going down there unless it's absolutely necessary.
Skyrim.esm|04FA20: What brings you, wayfarer grim, to wander here, in Sovngarde, souls-end, Shor's gift to honored dead?
Skyrim.esm|04FA43: Ah! It's been too long since last I faced a doom-driven hero of the dragon blood.
Skyrim.esm|04FA44: Do not mistake the night-shrouded thief's stealthily-taken spoils, stolen and unearned, for a warrior's plunder, won in honorable battle. Your doom already binds you to your dark mistress, but your errand I will not hinder, if my wrath you can withstand.
Skyrim.esm|04FA46: You trespass here, shadow-walker. Shor does not know you. Perhaps before the end you will earn the right to pass this way. Welcome I do not offer, but your errand I will not hinder, if my wrath you can withstand.
Skyrim.esm|04FA47: I welcome the chance to challenge the blade of Ysgramor's heir, honored shield-brother to Kodlak Whitemane, whom I've watched for in vain.
Skyrim.esm|04FA48: Well met, mage of Skyrim. The Nords may have forgotten their forefathers' respect for the Clever Craft, but your comrades throng this hall. Here in Shor's house we honor it still.
Skyrim.esm|04FBEF: There is no help for you here.
Skyrim.esm|04FBF0: There is no solace in knowing what is to come.
Skyrim.esm|04FBF2: Your perseverance will only lead you to disappointment.
Skyrim.esm|04FBF3: Still you persist? Very well, you may enter.
Skyrim.esm|04FCDA: Never done an honest day's work in your life for all that coin you're carrying, eh lad?
Skyrim.esm|04FCDB: Running a little light in the pockets, lass?
Skyrim.esm|04FCDC: Your pockets... they're a little low on coin. I can tell.
Skyrim.esm|04FD3C: Indeed. And so you have come looking, though you do not know why. Like others before you, you blindly follow a path to your own destruction. The Thalmor came seeking answers as well, unaware they will be his undoing. Your path now follows his, though you will arrive too late.
Skyrim.esm|04FD3E: You seek that which all who wield magic seek. Knowledge. You shall find this: Knowledge will corrupt. It will destroy. It will consume. You seek meaning, shelter in Knowledge. You will not find it. The Thalmor sought the same thing, and it shall lead to his end as it has so many others.
Skyrim.esm|04FD52: You will hear me out, then? Good. You will kill me, and I have accepted that fate. But regardless of your path through life, I sense in you a certain... ambition. So I ask of you a favor. An old man's dying wish. While there are many who would see me dead, there is one who set the machine in motion. This person, whomever he or she may be, must be punished for their treachery. Once you have been rewarded for my assassination, I want you to kill the very person who ordered it. Would you do me this kindness?
Skyrim.esm|04FD53: Well, I don't know. It's in the Solitude Inlet - that's the channel just north of the docks. I suppose you should go to the docks and... swim?
Skyrim.esm|04FD55: Oh, you can save your sinister bravado. I'll not go to my grave whimpering like an infant. I know why you're here. I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is. But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Skyrim.esm|04FD56: Tsk. How unfortunate. I had hoped you would honor an old man's dying wish. Well, let's be done with it then.
Skyrim.esm|04FD58: Thank you. Now, on to the business at hand I suppose, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|04FD59: Hmph. Pity. I had hoped to obtain even a modicum of justice. Well, on to the business at hand then, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|04FD5A: By the gods. You... you're alive! But I had heard... your Sanctuary... Please! You mustn't think I had anything to do with that! I wanted the Emperor dead! The true Emperor! I still do! It was Maro! He...
Skyrim.esm|04FD5B: You mean, after all that's transpired, the Dark Brotherhood will still... honor the contract? Why, this is astounding news! Wonderful news! The Emperor is still in Skyrim, but not for long. He's onboard his ship, the Katariah, moored offshore in the Solitude Inlet. But you must hurry! If you can get onboard that ship. Kill Titus Mede II, as contracted... I will reveal the location of the dead drop that holds your payment.
Skyrim.esm|04FD5C: Well? I won't fight you, so we may as well get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|04FD5D: I thank you for your courtesy. You will kill me, and I have accepted that fate. But regardless of your path through life, I sense in you a certain... ambition. So I ask of you a favor. An old man's dying wish. While there are many who would see me dead, there is one who set the machine in motion. This person, whomever he or she may be, must be punished for their treachery. Once you have been rewarded for my assassination, I want you to kill the very person who ordered it. Would you do me this kindness?
Skyrim.esm|04FD5E: You and I have a date with destiny, it would seem. But so it is with assassins and emperors, hmm? Yes, I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is. But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Skyrim.esm|04FD5F: Well. Perhaps you'll at least consider it, hmm? Now, on to the business at hand...
Skyrim.esm|04FD60: But of course. You and I have a date with destiny. But so it is with assassins and emperors, hmm? Yes, I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is. But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Skyrim.esm|04FD61: Come now, don't be shy. You haven't come this far just to stand there gawking.
Skyrim.esm|04FD6A: All right. Had I known the deal would bring me this much trouble, I never would have accepted the gold.
Skyrim.esm|04FDA2: Ha! Well, I certainly do feel that way. Now, if, um... you'll get going?
Skyrim.esm|04FDA3: I know! I know! I received the news not moments ago! Ha ha! This is glorious! My friend, you may not realize it, but you have served the Empire, indeed all of Tamriel, in ways you cannot possibly imagine. Ah, but you care little for politics, am I right? You want money! And money you shall have! Your payment waits for you at a dead drop. It is inside an urn, in the very chamber where we first met, in Volunruud. Now please, go. Collect your money, and let us never look upon one another again. Our business, thank the gods, is concluded.
Skyrim.esm|04FDA5: You assassins and your riddles. It really does get very annoying, you know. Now if you'll excuse me...
Skyrim.esm|04FDA7: Your payment awaits. And don't worry, I'm not stupid enough to betray you.
Skyrim.esm|04FDA8: Yes? No? Nothing? Very well, then. If you'll just... get going now...
Skyrim.esm|04FDAA: Surely you're joking? This is the true Emperor, not some half-Septim lookalike. He'll be surrounded by elite bodyguards, I'm sure. You'll have your work cut out for you. Still... everyone assumes the Dark Brotherhood has been completely destroyed. They may be a bit more lax than usual.
Skyrim.esm|04FEEA: Look, that's all I know. I never promised you I'd have all the answers. Now, since our transaction is done, I'll be on my way.
Skyrim.esm|04FEEB: I was approached by a woman who wanted me to act as the broker for something big. She flashed a bag of gold in my face and said all I had to do was pay Aringoth for the estate. I brought him the coin and walked away with her copy of the deed.
Skyrim.esm|04FEEC: Not at all. I tend not to ask too many questions when I'm on the job. I'm sure you understand. However, I did notice she was quite angry and it was being directed at Mercer Frey.
Skyrim.esm|04FEEF: In this business we rarely deal in names; our identity comes from how much coin we carry.
Skyrim.esm|04FF04: It is done. Emperor Titus Mede II lies dead, and the Dark Brotherhood yet lives.
Skyrim.esm|05036F: Oh, no. No, that won't do at all. Not my problem. Not even a little. Take it to Tolfdir, he's supposed to be looking after you lot.
Skyrim.esm|050370: That's nothing you need to concern yourself with.
Skyrim.esm|050371: Huh? That sounds ominous. Are you getting yourself into even more trouble? Did you ask Mirabelle? She runs things around here, after all.
Skyrim.esm|05039A: Shor's favor has found you, Dragonborn. The Hall of Valor, heart's-ease, awaits.
Skyrim.esm|05039D: We cannot fight the foe in this mist!
Skyrim.esm|05039E: You fought well. I find you worthy.
Skyrim.esm|05039F: It is long since one of the living has entered here. May Shor's favor follow you and your errand.
Skyrim.esm|0503A0: It's a little gift from the dwarves. Who knew they would end up helping our cause? We have to move. We'll talk more once we see the sky.
Skyrim.esm|0503A1: I say you've earned an early pardon. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|05049F: My brothers, we have been here long enough. It's time to leave Cidhna Mine and continue our fight against the Nords.
Skyrim.esm|0504A5: Through this gate, just beside my quarters, is a tunnel. A tunnel that leads right through the old Dwarven ruins of Markarth, into the city. Well, what do you say, my brothers?
Skyrim.esm|0504C4: Good. I can always count on you, can't I?
Skyrim.esm|0504C5: I can always count on you, can't I?
Skyrim.esm|0504C6: This will require stealth and discretion. The Jarl's guards won't take kindly to anyone rummaging through her Steward's private quarters.
Skyrim.esm|0504C7: You'll need to be careful with this one. The Jarl's men won't look kindly on you rummaging through the steward's quarters.
Skyrim.esm|0504E8: First on my list, is a problem with our supply caravans being constantly harassed by a giant. Need to deal with that hairy brute. And soon.
Skyrim.esm|0504E9: The last few supply caravans have been attacked... by a giant. As if I didn't have enough to deal with. Need that damn hairy brute straightened out.
Skyrim.esm|0504EA: And then there's a tiny problem with a giant that's been raiding our supply caravans... You heard me right. A giant. Need to put a stop to that.
Skyrim.esm|0504EB: And then there's a tiny problem with a giant attacking our supply caravans. A giant! As if I didn't have enough to deal with.
Skyrim.esm|0504FB: I can't believe we're finally getting out.
Skyrim.esm|050524: Come on, move it. We have Nords to kill.
Skyrim.esm|05072D: Ha! I knew it, you rascal! By Sithis, the old man was worth the trouble.
Skyrim.esm|05072E: Well? What word of the Emperor?
Skyrim.esm|05072F: Ha! Clearly, we should have killed that old bastard years ago!
Skyrim.esm|050730: Truly? Could you have brought us more wondrous news? Recent events notwithstanding, this is a happy day for us, my friend. Despite your misfortunes, you stayed true to the Dark Brotherhood. You've saved us all, and for this you have my eternal thanks. Now, of course I must ask... Killing the Emperor... How much did Motierre pay for such a thing?
Skyrim.esm|050731: Ha! Remarkable! Well, the old bastard certainly made it worth your while, didn't he?
Skyrim.esm|050732: Oh. Is that all? Really? Come now! You're joking with old Nazir! Surely the Emperor's life was worth more than that.
Skyrim.esm|050733: Pfft. You'd think an emperor would fetch at least twice that. Damn that motherless cheapskate, Motierre. In any event, here's my advice. Go to Riften's Ratway, find Delvin Mallory. I believe Astrid had you visit him once before, no? Mallory is an expert "obtainer of goods." We can use the money to repair and refit this Sanctuary. Make a true home for us, eh?
Skyrim.esm|050781: I'll be fine. I just need a minute to catch my breath. Find Savos...
Skyrim.esm|05092C: What if I told you about a large shipment of gold and weapons?
Skyrim.esm|05092F: So. You're an Imperial spy... Interesting... I have no strong love for the Stormcloaks but I can't say I'm all that fond of the Empire, either. This war of yours makes it difficult for a resourceful woman to advance her interests. Perhaps we can find a way for both of us to prosper from this unfortunate discovery?
Skyrim.esm|050930: So. You're one of Ulfric's spies... I can't deny the man is right about a few things... But I've seen first hand what Ulfric is capable of, given the chance. Suffice it to say, he is no friend to Markarth, and no friend of mine. My first and only loyalties are to my nephew, and to this city.
Skyrim.esm|050932: Good work. I'll send some men with a wagon to collect the prize. We can use the weapons here, and I'll pass on the coin to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|050933: Well done! I'll send men with a wagon to collect our prize. We'll put the weapons to use here, and I'll send the coin back to Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|050934: Good job! I knew I gave the mission to the right person.
Skyrim.esm|050935: Good job! Ulfric was right about you. I'm glad to have you with us!
Skyrim.esm|050936: I suppose you're here to extort something from me? Is that it? Well, what is it you want?
Skyrim.esm|050948: Stand fast! His strength is failing! Once more, and his might will be broken!
Skyrim.esm|050950: Again, as one! The World-Eater fears us!
Skyrim.esm|050951: We can shatter his power if we Shout together!
Skyrim.esm|050955: One more time, and the World-Eater must face us!
Skyrim.esm|050956: His power crumbles - do not pause for breath!
Skyrim.esm|050957: Again, and we break his grim hold over Shor's realm, and redeem it for eternity!
Skyrim.esm|050959: Does his strength have no end? Is our struggle in vain?
Skyrim.esm|05095A: We must clear this confounding mist.
Skyrim.esm|05095B: Alduin will hide in this lightless mist until we Shout together, and shatter his power.
Skyrim.esm|05095C: We must Shout together these shadows to dispel.
Skyrim.esm|05095D: We're ready, Dragonborn, our wrath to unleash.
Skyrim.esm|050A21: What's going on? What happened in there?
Skyrim.esm|050A22: It 'may be' in danger? Take a look. I don't think there's much question. Let's get down there. We have to do something.
Skyrim.esm|050A23: We'll go with you, but when this is all over I want an explanation.
Skyrim.esm|050A24: I'll go with you, but when this is all over I want an explanation.
Skyrim.esm|050AD8: Look, in case you hadn't noticed, these aren't the glory days of the Guild. We've lost more clients in the last decade than I'd care to count, but Maven's stuck with us through throughout the entire ordeal. I'll be damned if I'm going to lose her to... well, to whatever they are.
Skyrim.esm|050AD9: If you were to bring me the deed to Goldenglow Estate, so I was the full owner, that would be a step in the right direction.
Skyrim.esm|050ADB: Goldenglow Estate provided a huge payout for us before Aringoth locked us out. Maven looked the other way as long as the honey kept flowing and we kept him in line.
Skyrim.esm|050ADC: Since I know losing the farm's payments will harm the Guild, I intend to make a substantial contribution for compensation. I take care of my friends.
Skyrim.esm|050ADD: Let's not kid ourselves. Black-Briar Mead is swill. The only thing keeping it successful is Maven. Word on the street was that Honningbrew tasted better, cost less and Sabjorn didn't shove it down everyone's throat. If Sabjorn ever managed to push Maven off her pedestal, we'd all be in trouble.
Skyrim.esm|050ADF: You're going to have to buy him off; it's the only way to get his attention. If that fails, follow him and see what he's up to. If I know Gulum-Ei, he's in way over his head and you'll be able to use it as leverage.
Skyrim.esm|050AE0: It would seem our adversary is attempting to take us apart indirectly by angering Maven Black-Briar. Very clever.
Skyrim.esm|050AE1: Yes, we spoke at length while you were in Whiterun dealing with our meadery situation. I hope for all your sakes you find out who's behind this supposed "plot."
Skyrim.esm|050AE2: Having Goldenglow kept in line by the Thieves Guild has kept the honey flowing with minimal cost. Now, mysteriously, some sort of anonymous adversary shows up and takes the Estate out of my hands.
Skyrim.esm|050AE3: When you've been doing business like I have for decades, you quickly discover that the only person you can trust is yourself.
Skyrim.esm|050C83: Damn ears... playing tricks on me again. Can't stand this sea air!
Skyrim.esm|050C84: You're trailing me? I knew I heard something.
Skyrim.esm|050C85: Ha! I knew it was you all along.
Skyrim.esm|050C86: Following me, huh? You're not very good at this are you?
Skyrim.esm|050C87: You may as well give up, I see you there.
Skyrim.esm|050C8B: I swear, if I'm being followed...
Skyrim.esm|050C8C: I swear I feel like I'm being watched.
Skyrim.esm|050C8D: If this keeps up, I'm headed back to the Winking Skeever.
Skyrim.esm|050C8E: I don't have time to chase shadows, I've got better things to do.
Skyrim.esm|050C9A: She got sick, last winter, when the snows came. And she just... she never got better. Not all year. One night she fell asleep and...  never woke up. So now I'm all alone. And the Jarl said I had to go to Honorhall Orphanage. It's not fair!
Skyrim.esm|050C9B: Folk been hearing some strange stuff coming from the Aretino place. Chanting. Talk is, little Aventus is trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood...
Skyrim.esm|050C9C: A little boy, Aventus Aretino, got sent to the orphanage. Now there's talk he's trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood. No good can come of that...
Skyrim.esm|050C9D: I was walking by the old Aretino Residence, heard the little boy, Aventus? He was chanting. It was the Black Sacrament, I tell ya.
Skyrim.esm|050C9E: Hear about the little Imperial boy, Aventus Aretino? The stupid whelp is trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood...
Skyrim.esm|050C9F: I've never been more sure about anything in my entire life. Someone like Grelod doesn't deserve to live one more day. She's a monster.
Skyrim.esm|050CAC: Your corpse will feed my spiders for weeks. Now die, you fool!
Skyrim.esm|050CAD: What? Who are you? How did you get this far?
Skyrim.esm|050CC8: I never would have believed it, if I hadn't seen it with my own eyes...
Skyrim.esm|050CC9: It may be dead now, but... where did it come from?
Skyrim.esm|050CCA: In all my years, I've never seen such a thing...
Skyrim.esm|050CCC: What manner of magic is this?
Skyrim.esm|050CCE: Look out! Don't get too close!
Skyrim.esm|050CD3: It's like the ancient legends. Dragonborn...
Skyrim.esm|050CD4: It can't be... You... You took its very soul...
Skyrim.esm|050CD5: By the gods... I don't even know what to say...
Skyrim.esm|050DA9: Please hurry. To be honest, I'm kind of lonely here. As much as I hated getting sent to Honorhall, I really miss my friends there...
Skyrim.esm|050DAA: Have you heard about Aventus Aretino? He's a little boy, lives in Windhelm. They say he's been trying to get in touch with the Dark Brotherhood...
Skyrim.esm|050DAB: Been talk of a little boy in Windhelm, name of Aventus Aretino. Get this - he's been trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood. Foolish lad...
Skyrim.esm|050DAC: I heard tell of a lad in Windhelm. Aventus Aretino. They say he's been doing the Black Sacrament, trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood. Damn fool.
Skyrim.esm|050FA0: Arcturus took some men deeper in, wanted to be sure. We should maybe... assist them.
Skyrim.esm|050FA1: Suppose not. But what's taking the others so long? The sooner we get out of here, the better. Smoke's getting bad. This place'll be raging soon.
Skyrim.esm|050FAE: Dunno. One of these corpses. Does it matter?
Skyrim.esm|050FE1: You assist all you like. And get roasted for your trouble. I'll guard the exit.
Skyrim.esm|051090: First, I need to get a handle on rebel operations. Need to discretely liberate some documents from their headquarters.
Skyrim.esm|051091: First, I need to know what the Imperials are planning. Need to slip into their headquarters, grab plans, maps, something useful like that.
Skyrim.esm|051098: I also would like to get a handle on what the rebels are planning - slip into their headquarters and steal their war plans.
Skyrim.esm|051099: Also, I need a peek inside Imperial headquarters, ideally lifting some documents detailing their plans. I need to know what to concentrate on.
Skyrim.esm|05118A: What's happening? What did you people do?
Skyrim.esm|05118B: You brought this on us! You!
Skyrim.esm|05118C: Please, whatever you did, just make it stop!
Skyrim.esm|0513E6: There's a cave here. Perfect hideout for bandits or worse.
Skyrim.esm|0513E7: It's a Nordic ruin. Thousands of years old.
Skyrim.esm|0513EE: What's going on out there? What happened?
Skyrim.esm|0513EF: Did you make something bad happen?
Skyrim.esm|0513F0: I'm not seeing things again, am I?
Skyrim.esm|0513F1: Whatever it is you're up to at the College, you need to fix this at once!
Skyrim.esm|0513FA: What do you want? You have no business being in here.
Skyrim.esm|051400: You did? Really? This is the best news I've ever gotten! I mean, I knew the Dark Brotherhood was good... just not that good! You killed the old hag before I even asked! Oh, and please, take this as payment. It's an old family heirloom, supposed to be pretty valuable. It should fetch you a nice price. Thank you again!
Skyrim.esm|051402: Thank you again for resolving my... problem.
Skyrim.esm|051418: You've taken up enough of my time. Now leave.
Skyrim.esm|051419: Think you can intimidate me? In my own orphanage? Ha! Not bloody likely.
Skyrim.esm|05142B: We'll go with you, but when this is all over I want an explanation.
Skyrim.esm|05142C: I'll go with you, but when things are back to normal I want an explanation.
Skyrim.esm|05142D: Well? Is everything out there all right?
Skyrim.esm|05142F: What's going on? What happened in there?
Skyrim.esm|051430: That's certainly an understatement. I've never seen anything like this! Well, I suppose we can't just stand here. We'd better do something.
Skyrim.esm|05169C: Do you want me to hold your hand as well? You're going to have to sneak over to Madesi's stall and use your lockpick on the strongbox. Then when you have the ring, you pickpocket Brand-Shei and leave it behind.
Skyrim.esm|051792: All right. I'll take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|051793: I'll handle it. No need to blow things out of proportion.
Skyrim.esm|051794: Please, just let me handle it. It's not a problem.
Skyrim.esm|051795: Okay, okay. I'll deal with it.
Skyrim.esm|051797: Samuel is getting to be a problem. Fancies himself something of a "leader" to the other children. Put an end to the nonsense, or I will.
Skyrim.esm|051798: Hroar's crying is keeping me up at night. I'll give you one chance to talk the tears out of him, or he's getting the belt.
Skyrim.esm|051799: I can't abide Samuel's constant talk of his parents. "They're coming for me." "This is only temporary." The boy must accept his fate.
Skyrim.esm|05179A: Seven septims are missing from my purse. I know it was Runa. You get those coins back, or by Stendarr, I'll see her rot in the dungeon.
Skyrim.esm|05179B: This place is filthy. I've a mind to cancel all town privileges unless those brats start pulling their weight.
Skyrim.esm|05179C: The stores are running low. We'll need to water down the milk again. Besides, don't want the little darlings getting fat.
Skyrim.esm|05179D: We haven't had an adoption in months. At this rate, we'll be stuck with these brats forever. We need to get the word out. A sale, if need be.
Skyrim.esm|05195A: This was a mighty deed! The doom of Alduin encompassed at last, and cleansed is Sovngarde of his evil snare. They will sing of this battle in Shor's hall forever. But your fate lies elsewhere. When you have completed your count of days, I may welcome you again, with glad friendship, and bid you join the blessed feasting.
Skyrim.esm|05195B: I am Tsun, shield-thane to Shor. The Whalebone Bridge he bade me guard and winnow all those souls whose heroic end sent them here, to Shor's lofty hall where welcome, well-earned, awaits those I judge fit to join that fellowship of honor.
Skyrim.esm|051974: It's got to be somewhere around here! Now where did I put it?
Skyrim.esm|051975: Oh, and I wasn't entirely truthful before. This is actually going to hurt a lot. You see, you have to be dead in order for the spell to take hold.
Skyrim.esm|051976: Don't worry though. It'll be relatively painless...relatively.
Skyrim.esm|051977: Hhhmmm...maybe I put it over here.
Skyrim.esm|051978: Ooooh...yes...yes. You've fallen for my trap! Greed makes a great lure. Odd you didn't die from the fall though. Most do.
Skyrim.esm|051979: Oh, and don't mind my assistant here. He may have seen better days, but he's still more help to me dead than he was alive.
Skyrim.esm|05197A: Ah...Here it is. Now, if you'd be so kind as to stand still this will be much easier for me to do.
Skyrim.esm|05197B: Come on! You know you want what's in that chest. Just walk toward it!
Skyrim.esm|05197C: Just walk toward the chest! Gravity will do the rest.
Skyrim.esm|05197D: I know you're up there. Go get the treasure. Doesn't it look pretty?
Skyrim.esm|05197E: Alright, let's see if we have something here to end this as quickly as possible.
Skyrim.esm|051A45: Call it what you want. You don't pay, then you're not getting in.
