Skyrim.esm|051A69: All right, calm down, no need to for that. I'll let you in right away, just need to get the gate unlocked.
Skyrim.esm|051AC2: Are you excited to see what's in there? This is important to you Nords, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|051AC3: Don't get any ideas. We're here on official business, remember?
Skyrim.esm|051AC4: Well, this is highly unusual. And very interesting.
Skyrim.esm|051AC5: Like anyone would even notice, in all this mess.
Skyrim.esm|051AC6: As some of you may know, Saarthal was one of the earliest Nord settlements in Skyrim. It was also the largest.
Skyrim.esm|051AC7: I think, given the state of Skyrim, some might disagree.
Skyrim.esm|051AC8: There's no gold, nothing shiny at all.
Skyrim.esm|051AC9: That doesn't begin to describe it.
Skyrim.esm|051ACA: I just don't know that it's right for us to be in here like this, picking through the ruins .
Skyrim.esm|051ACC: I'm betting bodies. Lots and lots of bodies. And maybe some valuables...
Skyrim.esm|051ACD: It's thousands of years old. Who knows what we might find?
Skyrim.esm|051ACE: You found what? Hmm. Well now. Yes, this might actually be something interesting. You'd better show that to Tolfdir right away.
Skyrim.esm|051AD1: I'm not sure what I was expecting, but this isn't it.
Skyrim.esm|051AD2: Sacked by the elves in the infamous "Night of Tears," not much is known about what happened to Saarthal.
Skyrim.esm|051AD3: Don't sound so disappointed. You wouldn't be allowed to take it even if there was.
Skyrim.esm|051AD4: Not excited, no. Some very bad things happened here.
Skyrim.esm|051AD6: Oh, right. That whole thing with the elves. Well, it all worked out in the end, didn't it?
Skyrim.esm|051AD7: What do you think we'll find in there?
Skyrim.esm|051AD8: Why in the world would this be sealed off? What is this place?
Skyrim.esm|051AD9: What... What was that? Everything seemed to shimmer for a moment.
Skyrim.esm|051ADB: You know, there might be gold somewhere in here.
Skyrim.esm|051ADD: I find it hard to believe this excavation was approved.
Skyrim.esm|051ADF: It's going to take forever to sift through all this.
Skyrim.esm|051AE0: I'll be amazed if we find anything useful here.
Skyrim.esm|051AE1: Well, certainly none of this will benefit my research...
Skyrim.esm|051AE2: This is an exciting opportunity for us. To be able to study such an early civilization, and the magics they used...
Skyrim.esm|051B10: I think you'd be a little smarter than that.
Skyrim.esm|051EE6: Don't be sorry for me. I just - if I would have known it would come to this. Mother...
Skyrim.esm|051EE7: I've thought this through, and it's the only way. She's too far gone, too corrupted by the Hags. At least in death she can be free again. Enough. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|051EE8: All right. Mother is just outside this door. I have a plan.
Skyrim.esm|051EE9: Keep Mother's staff. Think of it as payment. I want to leave everything from this cursed place behind. Goodbye. And thank you.
Skyrim.esm|051EEA: I'll present you as the sacrifice. Just play along. While mother begins the ritual, I'll strike. Then, together we can... we can kill her.
Skyrim.esm|052248: Don't know. Creepy fella, black robe. Couldn't see his face. Paid me a pretty sum to get that into your hands, though.
Skyrim.esm|05225B: Our secret ingredient, if you will. A battle-mage of fearsome cunning. Runs the Blood Horkers, and makes sure the battles end right-side up.
Skyrim.esm|05225C: Come now, dearie, surely we aren't all that bad.
Skyrim.esm|05225D: Sorry isn't good enough, Orthus. There's a lot of gold at stake here, and sniveling isn't going to get it back.
Skyrim.esm|05225E: Finally, someone around here that can get things done. Since we're short on soldiers you'll be coming with us, too. No arguments. Anything else you want to say?
Skyrim.esm|05225F: Just... leave me alone! I've brought your mead, now drink it and shut your filthy mouths.
Skyrim.esm|052260: Well, here we are. We think.
Skyrim.esm|052261: Aye, the war keeps the Empire and the locals too busy to worry about us. Haldyn calls it "our golden age."
Skyrim.esm|052262: Haldyn will take my hide for this, but Skyrim's been feeling a bit confining lately. We draw council at Japhet's Folly. The fools in Windhelm won't go near it, which is fine by us. If you'll pardon me now, my crew needs gathering. Should be in Hammerfell before the other Horkers know who let slip their secret.
Skyrim.esm|052263: Oh, we've got other things in mind for our mouths, sweetie.
Skyrim.esm|052264: Haldyn? I've heard of the man, but never guess he'd join up with some pirates. This could be even more dangerous than I thought. Head to the ships. We'll plan the assault on the way.
Skyrim.esm|052266: Know about them? Lass, you're looking at them.
Skyrim.esm|0522B7: It was a truly terrifying scene. Beautiful, in some respects.
Skyrim.esm|0524CD: I knew he wasn't gone, I just knew it! Please, get that to Avulstein right away.
Skyrim.esm|0524CE: If only you could help bring my son back to me.
Skyrim.esm|0524CF: Thorald's disappearance has left our family in shambles.
Skyrim.esm|0524D0: I never thought I'd see another friendly face again. We need to get to safety as soon as possible. Let's move.
Skyrim.esm|0524D1: Thorald's disappearance has been hard on all of us, but Avulstein has taken it badly. He's afraid of being taken as well, so he hasn't left the house. All he talks about is Thorald. Please, help Avulstein if you can. Something has to be done, or we're all going to lose our minds.
Skyrim.esm|0524D2: Are you joking? Even if we had a prisoner by that name, the only way he'd be released is by order of the Imperial Legion. Now I suggest you turn around and go back the way you came.
Skyrim.esm|0524D3: Are you joking? Even if we had a prisoner by that name, the only way he'd be released is by order of the Imperial Legion. Now I suggest you turn around and go back the way you came.
Skyrim.esm|0524D4: The Thalmor? Do you have any idea what you're asking? I'm sorry, that's just not possible. It would cause far too many problems.
Skyrim.esm|0524D5: The Thalmor? Do you have any idea what you're asking? I can only imagine the headaches this is going to cause... Here, take this. Assuming they even honor it, you should be able to get your prisoner out.
Skyrim.esm|0524D6: What? Well... This seems to be in order. Very well, follow me.
Skyrim.esm|052549: Does it matter? You're warm, dry... and still very much alive. That's more than can be said for old Grelod. Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0525EF: Will do. Don't stray too far away or else we'll come after you.
Skyrim.esm|052720: Almost ever since he became leader of the tribe. He killed Shagrol in combat, which none of us thought possible. But the winner is made Chief. That is how it is done.
Skyrim.esm|052721: They threw me in here until they were ready to use me in one of their experiments. This wasn't supposed to happen. I thought they wanted my help, not to use me as a test subject!
Skyrim.esm|052722: No, it's the levers in the center there. Just make sure you don't pull the wrong one. Please, hurry. I don't like being in here!
Skyrim.esm|052723: Let's just get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|052724: Curses aren't enough, now must I suffer you too? What is it?
Skyrim.esm|052726: See? I knew Aren wouldn't leave me in here! Hurry and get me out of this cage before they come for me!
Skyrim.esm|052749: No problem, but if you get too far away, we're going to catch up.
Skyrim.esm|05274B: All right, but don't wander ahead too far or else we'll be forced to come find you.
Skyrim.esm|052792: My previous champion displeases me. It is time he is replaced - in the traditional fashion.
Skyrim.esm|052793: An astute observation. Skyrim is a beautiful and harsh mistress, but her people cling to such a petty notion of honor.
Skyrim.esm|05286C: No, no! This can't be happening!
Skyrim.esm|0529DB: The sealed letter will explain everything that needs to be done. The amulet is quite valuable - you can use it to pay for any and all expenses.
Skyrim.esm|052CAB: Just head north - you can't miss it. It's the capital of Whiterun Hold. Jarl Balgruuf still hasn't declared for one side or the other, so at least you won't run into any Imperials along the way.
Skyrim.esm|052CAC: Cross the river and then head north. You'll see Whiterun on its hill as you pass the falls.
Skyrim.esm|052CAF: Of course. Here's a key to the house. Stay as long as you like. If there's anything else you need, just let me know. But what's this all about? What are you two doing here?
Skyrim.esm|052CB0: Ralof? How do you know him?
Skyrim.esm|052CB1: Like I said, I'm glad to help in any way I can. Help yourself to whatever you need, within reason.
Skyrim.esm|052CB2: You did? We heard that Ulfric was captured... ... no, sorry, but in times like these I can't just take the word of a stranger who wanders into town claiming to be my brother's friend.
Skyrim.esm|053064: I'm afraid not. There are preparations to make and Gallus's remains to lay to rest. I promise to join you there as soon as I can.
Skyrim.esm|053065: Ah yes, quite an amusing anecdote actually. I caught him trying to break into my laboratory.
Skyrim.esm|053066: Besides the fact that there are only a handful of people in Tamriel that even recognize the language? I'm fairly certain he was planning some sort of a heist that involved a deep understanding of the Falmer language. Sadly, we never had the opportunity to speak about the details.
Skyrim.esm|053067: Well, for the thrill of course. He was quite clear that he felt more in his element climbing through a window rather than hunched over a dusty tome.
Skyrim.esm|053068: Ironically, I pointed him in the same direction I pointed you. To Markarth and Calcelmo. I'm only hoping whatever means he used to learn the language will still be available to you.
Skyrim.esm|053069: Please, there isn't much time. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|05306A: Please, make it brief. We have much to do.
Skyrim.esm|05306B: Back, eh? And how was our friend Calcelmo?
Skyrim.esm|05306C: Can I help you with anything else?
Skyrim.esm|05306D: Ah yes, my matchmaker. What is it I can do for you?
Skyrim.esm|05306E: Remember, speak only to Enthir. Trust no one else.
Skyrim.esm|053070: If you have further need of me, you can find me in the College.
Skyrim.esm|053072: He was a dear friend of mine and a surprisingly astute pupil of academia... I was devastated when he was killed. I suppose that risk always coexisted with his line of work, I just never thought his luck would run out.
Skyrim.esm|05310D: Wish I could say the same for us. Tolfdir and I can try and keep this contained. You need to get your hands on the Staff of Magnus. Now.
Skyrim.esm|05310E: I certainly hope so. I don't know how long we can keep this up. At least tell me where you're going, so we can send someone after you if you don't come back.
Skyrim.esm|05310F: I've got to stay here, to see if we can get to Ancano and stop this. I need someone I can trust to do it. Besides, you're the one having all of these visions, aren't you?
Skyrim.esm|053114: It's coming loose. I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|053115: That's it. Keep hacking at it.
Skyrim.esm|0531A3: We must hasten to Riften before Mercer can do any more damage to the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0531A6: I'll make for Riften and scout the situation; see if I can discover what Mercer's up to. When you're ready, meet me at the Ragged Flagon. In the meantime, I wanted you to have this. It belonged to Gallus, but given the circumstances I think he'd approve.
Skyrim.esm|0531A8: I felt the same way when Gallus first revealed these things to me. I think given time, you'll understand what I mean.
Skyrim.esm|0531A9: Everything that represents Nocturnal's influence is contained within the walls of the Sepulcher. Now it seems Mercer's broken his oath with Nocturnal and defiled the very thing he swore to protect.
Skyrim.esm|0531AA: You've come this far, so I see no harm in concealing it any longer. The Twilight Sepulcher is the temple to Nocturnal. It's what the Nightingales are sworn to protect with their lives.
Skyrim.esm|05325C: Ah yes, excellent samples. Thank you. This coin should compensate you for all the bother.
Skyrim.esm|05325E: I need someone to take this small keg of Black-Briar Mead to Wilhelm at the Vilemyr Inn in Ivarstead. Drop it off and he'll trade you something for the delivery. Keep it as payment for the legwork. Oh, and if you bump into Indaryn from the meadery, keep your mouth shut. He'd rat us out in a second.
Skyrim.esm|05325F: No, I'm sorry. You must have me mistaken for someone else. I've never even heard that name before.
Skyrim.esm|053260: Sibbi and I were to be married. We were supposed to be happy together forever. Then I found that wretched poetry from Svana. When I confronted Sibbi about it, he threatened to kill me. I was scared so I told my brother Wulfur. He went to talk to Sibbi for me and... and... Sibbi killed him. My brother wasn't even armed! When I heard what Sibbi did, I fled. Now you've found me, and you're going to tell him where I am.
Skyrim.esm|053264: No, no friend. You've got it all wrong. I'm not selling cheap mead. I'm selling good mead for cheap... Black-Briar Mead. I sell cases of it for half of what the inns and taverns pay through the Meadery. All I need is someone to deliver it for me. If anyone saw me leave Riften, they'd get suspicious.
Skyrim.esm|053266: Oh, splendid! Just splendid! Mara smiles upon us this day. Allow me to give you her gift... a true blessing to aid you in times of need. Despair not, her warmth will protect you always!
Skyrim.esm|053269: When you told me of your intent to join the Thieves Guild, I honestly didn't think we'd have much to speak about ever again. Yet here you are with my weapon that you could have easily sold or kept for yourself. Forgive me for my hasty opinion of you, I see that I was wrong and that there's much I can learn from you. If you'd permit me, I'd be honored to accompany you in your travels for a time.
Skyrim.esm|05326A: Exactly! And now I have to stare at these bars for eight months. Can you believe it? I was about to let that wench marry into the richest family in Riften, and this is how she repays me. I'd do anything to have that whore's head on a platter. All I need to know is where she is.
Skyrim.esm|05326B: Look, we all have our flaws; mine is that women can't get enough of me. So I had this little affair going on while I was betrothed to a beautiful girl named Svidi. Well, she finds out and she tells her brother, Wulfur. Then her brother attacks me with a knife... I mean, I had to defend myself!
Skyrim.esm|05326C: Aye, I suspected as much but I've never been able to catch that little sneak with proof.
Skyrim.esm|053270: So, the whore thinks the Black-Briar reach ends at the border of Skyrim, eh? She'll soon discover her mistake.
Skyrim.esm|053271: That fetcher! I gave him the same keg just this morning and told him to store it in the cellars. Oh, he'll rot in prison for this one I promise you. As soon as Maven finds out, it's all over for him. Here, take this as a bit of a reward for nabbing our little thief.
Skyrim.esm|053272: Smithing's been in my blood for generations. I owe my success to my forefathers and their recipe for flame. The secret is my forge. It consumes fire salts, a strange mineral that burns as hot as Red Mountain lava.
Skyrim.esm|053273: Well, it was. Sadly, this forge is dying and I've used the last of my fire salts. If I can't feed it soon, it may grow cold.
Skyrim.esm|053275: Good, good. Just point me in the right direction and I'll do the rest.
Skyrim.esm|053276: All right, pipe down. You want the whole blessed place to hear you? The arrangement called for a trade. I hope you'll find this suitable.
Skyrim.esm|053278: Oh my, how wonderful! Lady Mara will be so pleased! Here you are. Now make sure you give out every single one, we can't afford to let even one lost soul stray from Her warmth.
Skyrim.esm|053279: Good, my experiments are at a critical stage and these are just what I need to finish. Here's some coin for all the trouble.
Skyrim.esm|05327A: Ivarstead? So close to Riften? She's an even bigger fool than I thought.
Skyrim.esm|05327C: Riften is drowning in a sea of sin and wickedness and it's my personal mission to let everyone know that the warmth of Mara can see them through. All I need is a messenger to bring Her words to these poor souls by distributing these missives.
Skyrim.esm|05327D: I met her when I wandered into Riften a few years ago. Been smitten with her ever since. In fact, I'm thinking of asking her to marry me. In Black Marsh, tradition dictates we present a potential mate a unique wedding ring to represent our future bond. I already have the gold band, but all I need are three flawless amethysts to complete the setting.
Skyrim.esm|05329C: Perfect. This is just what I needed. Here's some gold for your time.
Skyrim.esm|05329F: Mara only helps those who can afford her tithes. This is nonsense.
Skyrim.esm|0532A1: Excellent, I needed some more kindling for my kitchen hearth.
Skyrim.esm|0532A5: Whatever you're peddling, I'm not buying.
Skyrim.esm|0532A6: Are you serious? You're as crazy as Maramal.
Skyrim.esm|0532A7: Look, the only religion I believe in is the good old septim. Everything else is just a waste.
Skyrim.esm|0532A8: Ugh. Get this awful paper away from me, by Talos!
Skyrim.esm|0532A9: Oh, a new pamphlet from Dinya? I'll be glad to read it. Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0532AA: Huh? Oh. Sure, whatever. I prefer Dibella's teachings myself.
Skyrim.esm|0532AB: Thank you. Umm, blessings upon you too.
Skyrim.esm|0532AC: I wouldn't even clean my tail with this trash.
Skyrim.esm|0532AD: Unless Mara wishes to come down and help me with these urchins, I don't really give a damn about her blessings.
Skyrim.esm|0532AE: Thank you. I'll share this with Aerin.
Skyrim.esm|0532B5: Hmmm. Never read anything from the temple before. I suppose there wouldn't be any harm in reading it.
Skyrim.esm|0532E0: I don't have time for this nonsense.
Skyrim.esm|0532E2: Seems like a noble cause, I'll look it over.
Skyrim.esm|0532E4: If only more people would read this, things might be better in Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0532E5: I don't understand how paper will help the dregs of this city, but suit yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0532E6: Do you take me for a fool? Get this drivel away from me!
Skyrim.esm|0532E7: Thank you. Blessings of Mara upon you as well.
Skyrim.esm|0532E8: Interesting. I'll look it over. Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0532E9: I hold my own beliefs, but thanks anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0532EA: Mara this and Mara that. Where is she when hundreds of Stormcloaks are dying for no good reason, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0532EB: Not really my usual read, but I'll give it a try.
Skyrim.esm|0532EC: Mara can't help me. No one can.
Skyrim.esm|0532ED: Oh, interesting. I'll read it this evening.
Skyrim.esm|0532EE: I have no desire to seek religion.
Skyrim.esm|0532EF: I visit the temple sometimes, but perhaps not often enough. Thanks for bringing this to me.
Skyrim.esm|0532F0: Thank you for the blessing, I'll put this to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0532F1: Oh, well thank you. I'll try and read this if I can.
Skyrim.esm|0532F2: Are you out of your mind?
Skyrim.esm|0532F3: I think I have some more of these padding my mattress.
Skyrim.esm|0532F4: I have no time for Marla or Mara or whatever her name is... there's work to be done!
Skyrim.esm|0532F5: Had only Mara saved that poor animal...
Skyrim.esm|0532F6: I'll give it a try, thanks very much.
Skyrim.esm|0532F8: You look like you could use a drink. What do you say to some cheap mead?
Skyrim.esm|0532FC: Ah finally, someone besides the stupid guards to talk to.
Skyrim.esm|0532FE: My experiments continue to go well thanks to you. How can I return the favor?
Skyrim.esm|053300: Lady Mara's messenger returns. What can I help you with, my child?
Skyrim.esm|053301: Oh, it's you. Come to extort more from me?
Skyrim.esm|053303: Come to see Balimund perform miracles with steel, eh?
Skyrim.esm|053304: Oh, it's you. Not here to rob me are you?
Skyrim.esm|053320: Enthusiasm. Good. Here's the key to the dig site.
Skyrim.esm|053321: I'll let you in if you agree to something for me. There's a giant spider in Nchuand-Zel. My workers call her "Nimhe," the poisoned one. If you deal with Nimhe, I'll let you into both the excavation site and my Dwemer Museum. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|053322: Head on in. Keep your nose clean, and you won't have any problems with us.
Skyrim.esm|053323: Correct. It's a private place where I can catalog my research. Occasionally, the Jarl will come in and have questions, but that's thankfully rare.
Skyrim.esm|053324: Persistent aren't you? Oh, very well. Who am I to stand in the way of curiosity?
Skyrim.esm|053325: You're our favorite kind of traveler, then. The market area is just inside in the gate.
Skyrim.esm|053326: It means "Dwarven Museum." Lost race of people that built the city. You want to know more? Ask Calcelmo. He's usually in front of the ruins, just down the tunnel.
Skyrim.esm|053327: Jarl Igmund sits on the Mournful Throne. You can find him in the keep, in the back end of the city inside the mountain.
Skyrim.esm|053328: Calcelmo. He's the Jarl's magical advisor, but he spends most of his time studying the ruins. If you want in, Calcelmo has to give his say-so. He's usually in front of Nchuand-Zel. Just down the tunnel.
Skyrim.esm|053329: See, there you go. Getting involved. Don't ask too many questions in Markarth. Safer for everyone that way.
Skyrim.esm|05332A: Because Calcelmo doesn't want anyone to go in. He runs the museum, he makes the rules. You have a problem with that? Go talk to him. You'll find him in front of the ruins during the day. Just down the tunnel.
Skyrim.esm|05332B: You're working with the Daedra? Right. Now tell me the one about the Argonian Maid and the lusty baron.
Skyrim.esm|05332C: First time in Markarth, traveler? Take my advice. You see anything, don't get involved. The city guard will take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|05332D: You have my gratitude. That spider had been interfering with my excavations for months. As promised, here's the key to the Dwemer Museum. Mind the displays. Some of them are quite fragile.
Skyrim.esm|05332E: Halt. The Dwemer Museum isn't open to anyone without the court wizard's permission.
Skyrim.esm|053335: Things are so much quieter with my darling Frabbi dead.
Skyrim.esm|053336: Oh, very well. Probably safest that way.
Skyrim.esm|05340B: I bleed for the people of the Reach!
Skyrim.esm|05343D: It seems that a predator has invaded their home, and someone needs to clear the beast out.
Skyrim.esm|05343E: A citizen of the Reach has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|05343F: A citizen of Haafingar has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053440: A citizen of Hjaalmarch has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053441: A citizen of Falkreath has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053442: A citizen of Whiterun has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053443: A citizen of the Pale has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053444: A citizen of the Rift has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053445: A citizen of Eastmarch has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053446: A citizen of Winterhold has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053492: I really must get back to my duties. This fort isn't going to clean itself.
Skyrim.esm|053497: Well, I'm not blind to the fact that you've been instrumental in improving my affairs of the heart. If you wish, you may enter my tower to view the museum section, but I'm afraid the laboratories are still off limits. That's the best I can offer.
Skyrim.esm|053498: Oh, be certain to purchase my Falmer translation tome when it's completed!
Skyrim.esm|05349A: Preposterous! That research represents years of personal toil in some of the most dangerous Dwemer ruins in Skyrim! You must be mad to think I'd allow anyone to see it before it's completed.
Skyrim.esm|05349D: Look, I'm very busy, so this better be important.
Skyrim.esm|05349E: I've already provided you with the key to my museum, what else could you possibly want?
Skyrim.esm|0534E5: Ha! All right, I'm writing that in. I'm not sure about the court but I find that much more probable than Olaf capturing a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0534E6: And they got the orders backwards. Ha ha ha.  Yes, I'm writing that in.  It should be good for a laugh from the court.
Skyrim.esm|0534E7: Elisif has forbidden the Burning of King Olaf, a Festival put on by the Bards College. We need to change her mind. To convince her I want to read King Olaf's Verse. A part of the Poetic Edda, the living history of Skyrim. Unfortunately the verse was lost long ago.
Skyrim.esm|0534E9: It means I can't read it to the court. Without the verse I won't be able to convince Elisif of the importance of The Burning of King Olaf Festival. If she isn't convinced of the festival's importance then she won't reverse her decision to stop the effigy burning. It means that the Burning of King Olaf, which the Bards College has held for time immemorial, won't be happening.
Skyrim.esm|0534EB: Welcome to the Bards College. I am the headmaster here. How may I help you?
Skyrim.esm|0534EC: "O, Olaf, our subjugator, the one-eyed betrayer;" "death-dealing demon and dragon-killing King." "Your legend is lies, lurid and false;" "your cunning capture of Numinex, a con for the ages."
Skyrim.esm|0534EE: "Olaf gave orders, Winterhold disguises. An attack on Solitude total destruction to follow." "His men dressed up and then went out to fight, but they reversed Olaf's orders much to Winterhold's sorrow."
Skyrim.esm|0534EF: Thank you yet again. I will make sure our applicant, who was instrumental in... recovering the poem will be well rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|0534F0: I have to admit I didn't think it would actually be there.  Now let's take a look at this... Oh. Oh-no. This won't do at all. The copy is incomplete, it's aged to the point that parts are unreadable. And the parts that are readable... well... bardic verse has come a long way since ancient times.
Skyrim.esm|0534F1: Soon, soon. These things must be done properly, you will be inducted as part of the Festival itself. I need you to go speak to Jorn. He was preparing the Effigy of King Olaf. Tell him to finish the preparations, the Festival is back on.
Skyrim.esm|0534F2: I'm a bard, trained at the Bards College in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.
Skyrim.esm|0534F3: I'm a bard, from the Bards College here in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.
Skyrim.esm|0534F4: I thank you and the college thanks you, Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0534F5: "Olaf grabbed power, by promise and threat;" "From Falkreath to Winterhold, they fell to their knees;" "But Solitude stood strong, Skyrim's truest protectors." "Olaf's vengeance was instant, inspired and wicked."
Skyrim.esm|0534F6: "No shouting match between dragon and man, no fire or fury did this battle entail." "Olaf captured a fast-sleeping dragon, a mighty feat which the meek would not fail."
Skyrim.esm|0534F7: I find that highly unlikely... but the court will love it. I'm writing it in.
Skyrim.esm|0534F8: Make it up? That doesn't seem appropriate... I suppose I could copy his style based on what you brought me but I have no idea what happened in between these verses.
Skyrim.esm|0534FA: Hmmm, Olaf did become king and Numinex escaped later. It's entirely possible... and I'm sure the court will like that, I'm writing it in.
Skyrim.esm|0534FB: It's not the most exciting answer but the most likely. I'll write it in.
Skyrim.esm|0534FC: It has a few final lines but that's all we needed to add. I need to head to court immediately and present this. You should come.
Skyrim.esm|0534FE: Unbelievable! You have done us a great service here. I can't begin to thank you enough.
Skyrim.esm|053502: Oh that is exciting! I'm sure the court and the Jarl will love it. I'm writing it in now.
Skyrim.esm|053504: Always a pleasure to meet a prospective bard. You should be aware that many apply but we accept very few people. When possible, we ask applicants to perform tasks the college needs completed. In this case, I do have a task befitting an aspiring bard...
Skyrim.esm|053505: That sounds good. I'm not sure the court will believe that Olaf had wizard powers but we shall see.
Skyrim.esm|053509: You have proven your point, Viarmo.  The festival is truly a celebration of Solitude and a condemnation of false kings.
Skyrim.esm|05350B: As you may be aware Elisif's husband High King Torygg was recently killed. Elisif mourns her husband deeply and she feels that a festival that burns a King in effigy is... distasteful. I've tried to convince her the festival is many centuries old and celebrates Solitude but I need proof. I believe King Olaf's verse will provide that proof.
Skyrim.esm|05350C: "So ends the story of Olaf the liar, a thief and a scoundrel we of Solitude commit to the fire." "In Solitude bards train for their service, they also gather each year and burn a King who deserves it."
Skyrim.esm|05350F: Look, I'm getting out of here. Normally, spiders make my skin crawl, but magic spiders? Blech.
Skyrim.esm|053510: Furthermore, I believe that such a fine poem deserves some payment of Patronage. The college will be moderately rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|05352D: Rexus will now give you two items which must be passed along to your superior.
Skyrim.esm|05352E: Is there... something else you need?
Skyrim.esm|053535: Climb down that ledge and see if you can...
Skyrim.esm|053536: Karliah, when will you learn you can't get the drop on me?
Skyrim.esm|053539: He's here and he hasn't seen us yet. Brynjolf, watch the door.
Skyrim.esm|05353A: Aye, lass. Nothing's getting by me.
Skyrim.esm|053550: The old gods give me strength!
Skyrim.esm|0537C7: I'm in the mood to ssssiiiiinnnggg! What about you, friend? Care to join me?
Skyrim.esm|0537C8: Please, just... just leave me be!
Skyrim.esm|0537C9: The power of Mzinchaleft is mine! Begone from here!
Skyrim.esm|0537CA: By the love of the Nine, what is it with you sycophants and hangers on? The jarl is busy! And so am I, for that matter.
Skyrim.esm|0537CB: By the gods! Somebody help me!
Skyrim.esm|0537CC: You do realize there's only one way this can end.
Skyrim.esm|0537CD: Go away! This is my wreck! Mine!
Skyrim.esm|0537CE: I'm busy, and you're bothering me. So go away.
Skyrim.esm|0537CF: I find your presence... unnerving. Please depart.
Skyrim.esm|0537D0: Purchase something or leave. Those are your options.
Skyrim.esm|0537D1: You really don't want to be loitering here, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|0537D5: Good work. A simple job, but it keeps the coin rolling in. Here's your share.
Skyrim.esm|0537D6: I'm sure that the owner of that place is relieved. They certainly came through with the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0537DA: A citizen of Skyrim has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|0537E5: Good luck. And try not to get yourself killed.
Skyrim.esm|0537E7: Will there ever come a time when the people of Skyrim no longer need our services? I think not.
Skyrim.esm|0537E8: You reek of death, my friend. I salute you.
Skyrim.esm|053902: You're going to happen by and lend poor old Sabjorn a helping hand. He's going to give you the poison to use on the pests, but you're also going to dump it into the brewing vat.
Skyrim.esm|053A1E: Go. Out. Before I change my mind.
Skyrim.esm|053A20: What? Why? But... you must have said something to Erikur! You lied to him and now here I am! You're a heartless monster!
Skyrim.esm|053A23: No... please, you have to help me! You don't understand what they do to people here!
Skyrim.esm|053A2A: You must be new. We don't talk to each other here.
Skyrim.esm|053A2C: You here to see Rulindil, too, eh? None of my business of course.
Skyrim.esm|053A44: Ah! You made it! Good to see you, my friend. I'll never forget how you saved my life from those Thalmor maniacs.
Skyrim.esm|053A4F: So you made it out. Good for you. Don't expect me to thank you. I still remember what you did to me.
Skyrim.esm|053A50: Oh... thank you. I don't understand what's happening. I can't stay here. What will I do now?
Skyrim.esm|053A51: Ah. You must mean that one over there. He's not one of us. Thalmor filth, eh? Giving all Khajiit a bad name.
Skyrim.esm|053A57: What did you tell him? Why did you do this to me?
Skyrim.esm|053A59: You've got to help me! Please!
Skyrim.esm|053A63: I don't know where I'll go. Somewhere far away from here.
Skyrim.esm|053A64: Maybe this is a way out?
Skyrim.esm|053ACE: Maybe one of the guards had a key?
Skyrim.esm|053ACF: There's got to be some way out of here!
Skyrim.esm|053F08: It seems that predators have taken up residence and have been attacking farmers and travelers. Someone's got to head out there and take care of the beasts.
Skyrim.esm|053F09: The Jarl of the Reach has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F0A: The Jarl of Haafingar has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F0B: The Jarl of Hjaalmarch has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F0C: The Jarl of Falkreath has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F0D: The Jarl of Whiterun has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F0E: The Jarl of the Pale has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F0F: The Jarl of the Rift has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F10: The Jarl of Eastmarch has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F11: The Jarl of Winterhold has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|053F16: This is going to be terrible for business...
Skyrim.esm|054139: Both of the buildings are connected by tunnels made by the pests infesting the meadery. There's an entrance to it in the basement storeroom of the warehouse that used to be boarded over. I've already removed the boards so the meadery would get infested. That's where you should start.
Skyrim.esm|05413D: Are you kidding me? Look at this place. I'm supposed to be holding a tasting of the new Honningbrew Reserve for the Captain of the Guard. If he sees the meadery in this state, I'll be ruined.
Skyrim.esm|05413E: Mallus is the best deal I've ever made. Lent him a bit of gold some time ago I knew he'd never be able to pay back. Nothing like free labor to make operating costs cheaper.
Skyrim.esm|05413F: I made the mistake of borrowing coin from Sabjorn. He's allowing me to pay it back, but he's working my fingers to the bone! He treats me like a slave... I have to do every nasty, dirty job in the meadery.
Skyrim.esm|054140: Once Sabjorn is out of the way, Maven has plans for this place. One way or another, we don't want the pests coming back. Consider it just more of the dirty work. I did my part getting them in there, now you need to clear them out.
Skyrim.esm|054142: Remember, Sabjorn will be needing a helping hand. Make it look good.
Skyrim.esm|054143: Good luck. I'll be here if you need me.
Skyrim.esm|054146: Well... yes. I mean, all of the gold didn't come from me. I have a silent partner that helped with the finances.
Skyrim.esm|054216: One of the Jarls has asked for our help.
Skyrim.esm|054217: Good work. The mindless cretins didn't stand a chance.
Skyrim.esm|054407: You want in, you need to cooperate.
Skyrim.esm|0545D0: Keep at it. We need to work twice as hard now that fat-headed Leigelf has declared war!
Skyrim.esm|054686: I don't know, but this place is full of them. Every one I've encountered has been enchanted or the like.
Skyrim.esm|0546CD: Tell Cicero the time has come. Tell him the words he has been waiting for, all these years: "Darkness rises when silence dies."
Skyrim.esm|054741: What? What treachery! Defiler! Debaser and defiler! You have violated the sanctity of the Night Mother's tomb! Explain yourself!
Skyrim.esm|054742: Poor Cicero has failed you. Poor Cicero is sorry, sweet mother. I've tried, so very hard. But I just can't find the Listener.
Skyrim.esm|054759: These look like good quality, too. I know it's not the glamorous, heroic work, but don't be surprised if I ask you to do it again.
Skyrim.esm|05475B: Shame. But, I'm sure one of the whelps around here will take it up.
Skyrim.esm|05475E: Unfortunate. Let's be more careful next time.
Skyrim.esm|05475F: I'm ever so grateful. Please, give my regard to the rest of the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|054760: And I have already received word that the client is satisfied. You have done well for yourself, and for the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|054761: Another job well done. Your payment is secure, and you have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|054762: I expected no less of you. And once again, you have not disappointed.
Skyrim.esm|054763: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Cave Bear pelts.
Skyrim.esm|054764: I knew you could be counted on. Do what must be done.
Skyrim.esm|054766: Good. Do not spare them your blade.
Skyrim.esm|054767: Nothing but a bunch of lowly bandits. Cowards, who will take little effort.
Skyrim.esm|054768: The restless dead. Not pretty, but if you don't stop them, they'll move on to the locals.
Skyrim.esm|054769: The Falmer. There have been reports that they've been attacking people at night.
Skyrim.esm|05476A: It's a problem with the Forsworn. Unpredictable, unknowable, and dangerous. Be careful.
Skyrim.esm|05476B: It's a clan of giants. The war is driving them out of their lands, and they've started attacking settlements.
Skyrim.esm|05476C: You'll be clearing a nest of those Hagraven things. I've never faced one myself, but I hear... disturbing things about them.
Skyrim.esm|05476D: Oddly enough, Spriggans. Usually they let alone, but for some reason, this particular grove isn't.
Skyrim.esm|05476E: Bloodsuckers. Vampires. Take every precaution. One bite, and you could end up as one of them.
Skyrim.esm|05476F: Rogue wizards. I've never trusted magic types. The College is bad enough. Destroy them.
Skyrim.esm|054771: There's some trouble in Eastmarch. Farmers getting troubled by some bad types who've built up a stronghold in the area.
Skyrim.esm|054772: There's some trouble in Falkreath. There's always some sort of trouble down there, but now it's come to a head.
Skyrim.esm|054773: There's some trouble in Haafingar. The Legion's too busy with the war, and they're hiring us to take care of their problems.
Skyrim.esm|054774: We've been hired to deal with some pests in Hjaalmarch. Someone's got them riled up, and you need to put an end to it.
Skyrim.esm|054775: There's some trouble in The Pale. We need you to go in and clear out an enemy stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|054776: There's always some sort of trouble in The Reach, but a local warlord has raised the stakes. We have to handle the situation.
Skyrim.esm|054777: The Rift is seeing a lot of trouble from some riff-raff. We need to head in and break them up.
Skyrim.esm|054778: We've got trouble right here in Whiterun Hold. Nothing we can't handle.
Skyrim.esm|054779: There's trouble up in Winterhold. I'm a little surprised anyone's complaining about it, but money is money.
Skyrim.esm|05477B: I don't know if you're capable, and I don't have time to find out. If Kodlak will vouch for you, I might have some work you could do.
Skyrim.esm|05477C: Kodlak is the one who figures out who can be a Companion. So talk to him. It's out of my hands.
Skyrim.esm|05477D: So you think you have what it takes? Huh. Lucky for you, I'm not the one who makes that decision. Talk to Kodlak. Who knows, maybe he's in a generous mood.
Skyrim.esm|05477E: Aren't you already running a job for Aela? Come talk to me when you finish that up.
Skyrim.esm|05477F: I've already given you a job, now get to it.
Skyrim.esm|054780: Aren't you already running a job for Farkas? Come talk to me when you finish that up.
Skyrim.esm|054781: I've already given you a job, now get to it.
Skyrim.esm|054782: Aren't you already running a job for Vilkas? Come talk to me when you finish that up.
Skyrim.esm|054783: I've already given you a job, now get to it.
Skyrim.esm|054784: Aren't you already running a job for Skjor? Come talk to me when you finish that up.
Skyrim.esm|054785: I've already given you a job, now get to it.
Skyrim.esm|054795: Get to the College, before it's too late!
Skyrim.esm|054796: There's no time! You have to get back to the College as fast as you can! Mirabelle is dead, and they're losing ground fast. I'd be there now if I hadn't seen... I had one of your 'visions', I think. Go on, get moving! I'll try and clear a path for you.
Skyrim.esm|05479C: I prefer working in silver. More skill needed than forging iron or steel.
Skyrim.esm|05479D: The bloodiest beef in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|054829: Alright everyone! Keep your guard up. Let's move out!
Skyrim.esm|054833: Alright, let's move, men! We've got more...
Skyrim.esm|054834: We'll stay here and see if we can find anything else that could be of use.
Skyrim.esm|054840: Auxiliary, do what you do best. See if you can find some way to get that gate open.
Skyrim.esm|054842: Steady, legionnaires! We've been through worse. Just keep your guard up!
Skyrim.esm|054843: Follow me. Let's see where this leads.
Skyrim.esm|054855: How's it going? You find anything yet?
Skyrim.esm|054856: Keep looking. There's got to be some way to get that gate open.
Skyrim.esm|05485E: This may sound obvious, but do you see a lever anywhere?
Skyrim.esm|05485F: Alright legionnaires! Spread out and see what we've got.
Skyrim.esm|05486A: Madanach. I've brought what you asked for.
Skyrim.esm|05486E: Good work. Get ready while I have a word with our favorite outsider.
Skyrim.esm|054874: I had Kaie recover all the things the Nords stole from you. You better get ready before we break out into the city. And take this. It's blessed with the old magicks. Something to remember me by.
Skyrim.esm|054875: It wasn't easy getting all your things. A few guards had to have their throats slit.
Skyrim.esm|0549CC: And you... you're coming with me to Dragonsreach. We'll see how quickly your memory clears in the city's prisons. Now move.
Skyrim.esm|0549CD: You assured me this place was clean! I'll see... see to it that you remain in irons for the rest of your days!
Skyrim.esm|0549CE: Well, Sabjorn. Now that you've taken care of your little pest problem, how about I get a taste of some of your mead?
Skyrim.esm|0549D0: I... I don't know. What's wrong?
Skyrim.esm|0549D1: Well it's about time! I had to stall the captain until you were finished.
Skyrim.esm|0549D2: Why are you standing here then? I've told the captain of the guard we'll have the meadery cleared for the tasting and he could be here any minute.
Skyrim.esm|0549D3: Help yourself, milord. It's my finest brew yet... I call it Honningbrew Reserve. I think you'll find it quite pleasing to your palate.
Skyrim.esm|0549D4: I beg you... please. This is not what it seems!
Skyrim.esm|0549D6: By the Eight?! What... what's in this?
Skyrim.esm|0549D7: Silence, idiot! I should have known better... to trust this place after it's been riddled with filth.
Skyrim.esm|054A0A: Sometimes I hear Yngvar singing in his room. He's actually quite good.
Skyrim.esm|054A0B: There isn't an inch of this building that I haven't cleaned up.
Skyrim.esm|054A0F: I remember old Arnleif building each shelf in this store himself.
Skyrim.esm|054A11: There's no shipments coming in, so I do nothing. Just drink.
Skyrim.esm|054A12: This is the Silver-Blood Inn. I'll let you figure out who owns it by yourself.
Skyrim.esm|054A13: Unlike my husband Kleppr, I watch after our guests.
Skyrim.esm|054A14: I'll bend Kleppr's ear if there's anything wrong with a room you rent.
Skyrim.esm|054A16: I've seen every corner of Skyrim, but nothing is as beautiful as the battlefield.
Skyrim.esm|054A17: I have potions for disease, love sickness, irritating children...
Skyrim.esm|054A18: I spill just a bit of metal, and Mulush takes it out of my hide.
Skyrim.esm|054A19: If a man has time to complain, he has time to work.
Skyrim.esm|054A1A: I advise Igmund the same way I advised his father. Caution, caution, caution.
Skyrim.esm|054A1C: Ghorza is the one you should ask if you need anything.
Skyrim.esm|054A1D: I have things to study, what is it?
Skyrim.esm|054A1F: The Jarl gives me two incompetent assistants. Worthless.
Skyrim.esm|054C87: Yeah, but Twice-Killed has more of a ring to it!
Skyrim.esm|054C88: Yeah, I was lucky though. After Vex tore the guy to pieces, she gave me a potion. If she hadn't of been there... Anyway, after I got back to the Guild, I decided to start calling myself Twice-Killed.
Skyrim.esm|054C89: Got into a scrap a few years ago at the Ragged Flagon. Some idiot who stole himself a guard's sword and kept boasting about it. He's making too much racket and I'm trying to get Vex drunk. So I tell him to keep it down and he just runs me through.
Skyrim.esm|054C8A: Brynjolf sent you to rub it in?
Skyrim.esm|054C8C: Waste of talent throwing me in here, right?
Skyrim.esm|054C8E: Can't wait to get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|054C8F: What? What are you looking at?
Skyrim.esm|054C99: You didn't need to leave so soon.
Skyrim.esm|054CA4: Give Vex a kiss for me.
Skyrim.esm|054CA5: Hey, if you're in good with Maven you've pretty much got it made. She's got friends in high places, and I don't just mean in Skyrim. So getting the guards to throw me in here as a "punishment" was about as easy as falling off a log.
Skyrim.esm|054CA6: Because big, bad Brynjolf doesn't like the way I play thief. Look, he says we needed coin, so I got coin. Who cares if people got hurt? Blood washes off septims. Now I'm sitting in here counting skeever droppings while he's drinking Black-Briar mead with Maven.
Skyrim.esm|054E73: They wouldn't dare give Ulfric a fair trial. Treason, for fighting for your own people! All of Skyrim would have seen the truth then.
Skyrim.esm|054E75: Any friend of Ralof's is a friend of mine.
Skyrim.esm|054E76: I can hardly believe it myself, and I was there.
Skyrim.esm|054E77: I can't remember when I last slept...
Skyrim.esm|054E78: But then... out of nowhere... a dragon attacked...
Skyrim.esm|054E79: Nonsense. You and your friend are welcome to stay here as long as you need to. Let me worry about the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|054E7B: Thanks, sister. I knew we could count on you.
Skyrim.esm|054E7C: Go and watch the south road. Come find us if you see any Imperial soldiers coming.
Skyrim.esm|054E7D: Where to start? Well, the news you heard about Ulfric was true. The Imperials ambushed us outside Darkwater Crossing. Like they knew exactly where we'd be. That was... two days ago, now. We stopped in Helgen this morning, and I thought it was all over. Had us lined up to the headsman's block and ready to start chopping.
Skyrim.esm|054E7E: You don't mean, a real, live...
Skyrim.esm|054E81: I ought to get back to work before I'm missed, but... did anyone else escape? Did Ulfric...
Skyrim.esm|054E87: Don't worry. I'm sure he made it out. It'll take more than a dragon to stop Ulfric Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|054E8C: I'll let them into the house and, you know, show them where everything is...
Skyrim.esm|054E8F: Hmph. Help them drink up our mead, you mean.
Skyrim.esm|054E92: Uncle Ralof! Can I see your axe? How many Imperials have you killed? Do you really know Ulfric Stormcloak?
Skyrim.esm|054E95: As strange as it sounds, we'd be dead if not for that dragon. In the confusion, we managed to slip away. Are we really the first to make it to Riverwood?
Skyrim.esm|054E97: Nobody else has come up the south road today, as far as I know.
Skyrim.esm|054E98: I told you my sister would help us out.
Skyrim.esm|054E99: I hope to see you in Windhelm. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|054E9A: Come on. I'll feel better once we're under cover in Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|054E9B: Do you think that dragon is still flying around here somewhere?
Skyrim.esm|054E9C: I almost can't believe it... a real, live dragon...
Skyrim.esm|054E9D: Did you really see a dragon?
Skyrim.esm|054E9F: I'm glad to help any way I can.
Skyrim.esm|054EA0: Good luck, brother. I'll see you later.
Skyrim.esm|054EA1: Look at you, almost a grown man! Won't be long before you'll be joining the fight yourself.
Skyrim.esm|054EA4: Now, Ralof, what's going on? You two look pretty well done in.
Skyrim.esm|054EA6: Good. Maybe we can lay up for a while. I hate to put your family in danger, Gerdur, but...
Skyrim.esm|054EAA: It's been a hard day cleaning. Good, honest work I say.
Skyrim.esm|054EAB: If you see any signs of Talos worship, it's your obligation to report them to me.
Skyrim.esm|054EC0: The Goldenglow Estate job has undoubtedly interrupted the supply of honey I need to make my mead. Sabjorn could use this interruption to his advantage and collect a larger share of the market. I can't have that.
Skyrim.esm|054F88: King Olaf was Olaf One-Eye? He famously captured the dragon Numinex and took him to Dragonsreach. What do we say really happened?
Skyrim.esm|054F89: Strange. According to history Solitude attacked Winterhold, but Aesgeir seems to be saying Olaf reacted. What do we say happened?
Skyrim.esm|054F8B: "Olaf sent word to Solitude's Jarl, cajoling and mocking and begging for a fight." "From Winterhold Olaf had sent every message, upon attack he was nowhere in sight."
Skyrim.esm|054F8C: "Because Solitude would not soon bend knee, Olaf would hurt them while his status accrued." "He sacked Winterhold his only true ally, and used magic bold to blame Solitude."
Skyrim.esm|054F8D: "No shouting match between dragon and man, no fire or fury did this battle entail." "Olaf struck a deal to make himself king, Numinex let go though none tell that tale."
Skyrim.esm|054F8E: "No shouting match between dragon and man, no fire or fury did this battle entail." "Olaf was Numinex in human form, on moonless nights he would spread wings and sail."
Skyrim.esm|054F91: What? So Cicero wasn't talking to anyone else. Just... the Night Mother's body? And the Night Mother, who, according to everything we know, will only speak to the person chosen as Listener... just spoke. Right now... ...to you?
Skyrim.esm|054F92: When I heard Cicero screaming, I knew you'd been discovered. I feared the worst. Are you all right?
Skyrim.esm|055042: Please hurry. The Captain of the Guard isn't patient when it comes to tasting time.
Skyrim.esm|055043: Remember, you don't get paid until they're all dead.
Skyrim.esm|055044: I haven't heard even a scratch at the door. Have you done it?
Skyrim.esm|055045: What is it now? We have a tasting to attend to!
Skyrim.esm|0550CD: We'd be better off without the Silver-Bloods. They send their brute, Yngvar, to shake some coins from me every few days. And Betrid Silver-Blood is always asking for more potions to fuel Thonar's "ambitions." Gives him a temper fiercer than any bear.
Skyrim.esm|0550CF: How about a septim? Wait, make that two septims, I feel like living it up.
Skyrim.esm|0550D0: Come on, you outsiders always have plenty of gold on you. Spare a piece!
Skyrim.esm|0550DF: Poor Cicero is stuck. Can't you see? I was transporting my dear, sweet mother. Well, not her. Her corpse! She's quite dead. I'm taking mother to a new home. A new crypt. But... aggh! Wagon wheel! Damnedest wagon wheel! It broke! Don't you see?
Skyrim.esm|0550E0: Oh. Oh yes! Yes, the kindly stranger can certainly help! Go to the farm - the Loreius Farm. Just over there, off the road. Talk to Loreius. He has tools! He can help me! But he won't! He refuses! Convince Loreius to fix my wheel! Do that, and poor Cicero will reward you. With coin! Gleamy, shiny coin!
Skyrim.esm|0550E2: Pay me? You think this is about money? Have you seen the man? He's completely out of his head. A jester? Here, in Skyrim? Ain't been a merryman in these parts for a hundred years. And he's transporting some giant box. Says it's a coffin, and he's going to bury his mother. Mother my eye. He could have anything in there. War contraband. Weapons. Skooma. Ain't no way I'm getting involved in any of that.
Skyrim.esm|0550E3: That Cicero feller? Hmph. Tell me something I don't know. Crazy fool's already asked me about five times. Seems he's not satisfied with my answer. Why can't he just leave us alone?
Skyrim.esm|0550E4: Is something wrong. "Is something wrong?" she asks. Yes, something is bloody well wrong! Or maybe you missed the demented little man in the jester's garb, down by the road? Goes by the name of Cicero? Crazy fool's asked me to fix his broken wagon wheel five times. He won't take no for an answer. Why can't he just leave us alone?
Skyrim.esm|0550E5: Is something wrong. "Is something wrong?" he asks. Yes, something is bloody well wrong! Or maybe you missed the demented little man in the jester's garb, down by the road? Goes by the name of Cicero? Crazy fool's asked me to fix his broken wagon wheel five times. He won't take no for an answer. Why can't he just leave us alone?
Skyrim.esm|0550E6: Ha! Wonderful! Like I said, the guard is down by the road. You really can't miss him. You tell him that jester is up to no good, and he'll deal with it. Then come back here, and I'll toss a few coins your way, yeah?
Skyrim.esm|0550E8: What? And just who in Mara's name are you, anyway? Hmm? Come here, telling me my business. And for what? To help a... a... a fool!
Skyrim.esm|0550E9: You... you did? He has? Oh stranger! You have made Cicero so happy! So jubilant and ecstatic! But more! Even more! My mother thanks you! Here, here. For your troubles! Shiny, clinky gold! A few coins for a kind deed! And thank you! Thank you again.
Skyrim.esm|0550EA: Agh! Bother and befuddle! Stuck here! Stuck! My mother, my poor mother. Unmoving. At rest, but too still!
Skyrim.esm|0550EB: Poor mother... Her new home seems so very far...
Skyrim.esm|0550EC: Hmph. Fine. Suit yourself. But you're wrong, anyway. Just look at the fool. I don't know what it is, but he's damn well guilty of something.
Skyrim.esm|0550ED: Oh, for the love of Mara. What now?
Skyrim.esm|0550EE: You need to report that crazy jester to the guard. He should be patrolling the road.
Skyrim.esm|0550EF: I thought we'd talked enough. Unless you've reconsidered my offer? That Cicero needs sortin'...
Skyrim.esm|0550F6: Stendarr's Mercy to you. If my Horgeir doesn't give up the mead this time, I'll hit him over the head with it. Here, for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|055101: Jewelry for sale. Redguard craftsmanship in every piece.
Skyrim.esm|055102: You there, uh, the finest silver in Markarth is sold right here.
Skyrim.esm|055103: Maybe you could buy a gift for a friend? A best friend?
Skyrim.esm|055104: With Lisbet gone, I'll be taking care of you.
Skyrim.esm|055105: Let me know if there's something you need to buy.
Skyrim.esm|055106: I'm keeping Arnleif and Sons going, as best I can.
Skyrim.esm|055107: Never thought I'd be running the store. I'm not used to it.
Skyrim.esm|055108: My worthless husband is gone, so I run the inn now.
Skyrim.esm|055109: I can't believe the mess Kleppr left things in. I have twice the work with him gone.
Skyrim.esm|05510E: I've taken over the Hag's Cure, since Bothela is gone.
Skyrim.esm|05510F: I'm not sure if half these potions work. But they should. I think.
Skyrim.esm|055110: I wish Bothela was still running the store. I'm not ready for this.
Skyrim.esm|055111: I run the forge now. Kind of... scary.
Skyrim.esm|055112: Making weapons and armor isn't like making nails at all.
Skyrim.esm|055113: You need a sword? Uh, how about a small sword? Nail-sized?
Skyrim.esm|055114: Oh, excuse me. With my uncle gone, things are a bit of a mess.
Skyrim.esm|055115: You're going to try to talk sense into that Orc? I'll believe it when I see it.
Skyrim.esm|0551A8: Have you met my husband? Well don't bother, he's a drunk.
Skyrim.esm|0551AB: Shor's Crown, that woman never gives up. I guess that's why I married her. You're a real friend saving this mead from her. Here, let me show you how to block a punch that might be coming from some angry spouse, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0551E6: I'm not half the wizard my uncle was, but I still have a few spells worth buying.
Skyrim.esm|0551E7: Did you need something? A staff? Some enchanting materials?
Skyrim.esm|0552BA: What? Are you telling me how to treat my workers? Do you have any idea how much metal the Silver-Blood family wants us to smelt?
Skyrim.esm|0552BC: He promised me, swore on his ancestors, that he'd give up the mead. And what do I find out? He's been stashing drink in a cave nearby. He even has some wolves guarding it. I swear, I should just cut out his tongue. That would be the end of it.
Skyrim.esm|0552BE: It's not luck, it's mead I'm cursing.
Skyrim.esm|055367: He's a damn tyrant. Always being goaded on by the Silver-Bloods to get more work out of us. They pay us next to nothing. We get beaten if we make a mistake, and where are our kind Nord rulers? What are they doing? Typical.
Skyrim.esm|055369: Me and Hathrasil mostly. Mulush barks at us all day, but I wouldn't call that honest work. A few people in the Warrens used to work here. Degaine broke some bones a while back. Eltrys quit. Cairine is too sick.
Skyrim.esm|05536A: Mulush. Big Orc fella. Can't miss him.
Skyrim.esm|05536B: Let me know if you see anything you like.
Skyrim.esm|05538E: Don't get involved in that nonsense. Just want to work.
Skyrim.esm|0553C5: We've lost a lot of good people to that damn crusade. Drive out the Nords, they say. Well, the Nord ain't driven out.
Skyrim.esm|0554D9: I guess I can spare him a few beatings. Deal.
Skyrim.esm|0554DA: The only thing I understand is you bothering you.
Skyrim.esm|0554DB: All right, all right. You sound like a damn Legion officer, but I get it. I'll give him a break. This better not hurt our quota.
Skyrim.esm|0554DD: I'm in charge of the smelter. Not you. So why don't you lay off?
Skyrim.esm|0554FE: If you want to try, but I'll keep my dagger sharpened just in case. You can help yourself to any drink you find, but bring me the bottle of Dragon's Breath Mead. It's his favorite, so I know he has one there.
Skyrim.esm|055511: I don't believe it. Someone sticking up for us. Here. It's a week's wages for me, but you've earned every coin.
Skyrim.esm|055512: Wait. You win. I'll ease off on the beatings, just leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|05551D: Think you can mess with an Orc of the Reach? Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|05567E: What? Oh! How could I have possibly forgotten. Your reward, of course... of course. Here you are. So sorry about that.
Skyrim.esm|05567F: Ah, Bolli finally got someone out here. Well it's about time. I've been waiting for weeks. Well, thank you for bringing it I suppose. Have a good day.
Skyrim.esm|055698: Good. Kleppr will pay you as soon as you arrive with the letter. Here you are.
Skyrim.esm|05569E: Lazy good-for-nothing, isn't he? Sent you down here when I'm a stone's throw from the keep? Here's the sword. Tell Harrald to stop slaying walls and perhaps his blade will stay in better condition!
Skyrim.esm|0556A0: I sell my catch across a good portion of Skyrim... it's worth its weight in gold to me. The only untapped market left is Markarth. However, I just got word that Kleppr at The Silver Blood Inn might be interested. If you're headed that way, I would appreciate you delivering a purchase agreement for me. You'd be paid, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0556AD: Took your sweet time getting it to me. Here you go, for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0556B8: Hmph. I should have known better than to ask a knave.
Skyrim.esm|0556D9: But you have indulged my weakness for speech long enough. Krosis. Now I will answer your question.
Skyrim.esm|0556DA: Nid. If you wish me to answer your question, you must answer mine.
Skyrim.esm|0556DB: And so, perhaps, your destiny will be fulfilled. Who can say? Dez motmahus. Even to the dov, who ride the currents of Time, destiny is elusive. Alduin believes that he will prevail, with good reason. Rok mul. And he is no fool. Ni mey, rinik gut nol. Far from it. He began as the wisest and most far-seeing of us all.
Skyrim.esm|0556DC: Yes. Alduin... zeymah. The elder brother. Gifted, grasping and troublesome as is so often the case with firstborn. But why? Why must you stop Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|0556F5: You don't want to go up to that farmhouse, citizen. It's a terrible sight. The farmer and his wife, slaughtered in the night. So many knife wounds...
Skyrim.esm|0556F7: Did he now? Nobody pilfers property on my watch. My thanks for bringing this to my attention, citizen. Don't worry, I'll take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0556F8: What? How dare that little fool! And right under my nose! My thanks for bringing this to my attention, citizen. Don't worry, I'll take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0556F9: Ha ha! Oh, I knew I had a good feeling about you! Here, my friend. A few septims, to show my thanks, hmm? And may that twisted little jester rot in a cell for a fortnight!
Skyrim.esm|0556FA: What? An assault. On my watch. By the gods, he'll not get away with this. My thanks for bringing this to my attention, citizen. Don't worry, I'll take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0556FC: The farmer is at his farm! Where else would he be? Loreius is his name. Talk to him. Convince him to help poor Cicero.
Skyrim.esm|0556FD: Poor mother... she's been waiting here so long... Stupid farmer and his stupid tools! Why won't he help us?
Skyrim.esm|0556FE: Unfair! Unjust! Cicero has done nothing wrong! Loreius will pay for his treachery! Oh, how he will pay... And you! You were no help! Not at all!
Skyrim.esm|0556FF: I will wait for Loreius! Oh yes, mother and I will wait right here, right here until he fixes our wheel.
Skyrim.esm|055700: Well? Is it done? Have you reported the fool to the guard?
Skyrim.esm|055701: Ha! That'll teach the little jester to go bothering the decent, hardworking folk of Skyrim. Fool never should have come here.
Skyrim.esm|055702: You can tell Cicero I'll be along presently. I just need to do a couple things here first, and gather my tools.
Skyrim.esm|055703: You need something talk to my husband. Vantus Loreius. Or just Loreius, if it's all the same to you.
Skyrim.esm|055704: Would you now? Well, spit it out. What's the matter?
Skyrim.esm|055771: Paaz. A fair answer. Ro fus... maybe you only balance the forces that work to quicken the end of this world. Even we who ride the currents of Time cannot see past Time's end. Wuldsetiid los tahrodiis. Those who try to hasten the end, may delay it. Those who work to delay the end, may bring it closer.
Skyrim.esm|055788: If you must know, I was practicing in the yard when the guard I was sparring with feinted when I specifically told him to duck! My blade bit right into the stonework next to the keep and needed serious repairs. Thankfully our smith Balimund works wonders. In fact, if you'd fetch my blade from him I'd be most appreciative.
Skyrim.esm|0557AA: Did you get that purchase agreement to Markarth yet?
Skyrim.esm|0557AC: There's nothing like fishing on Lake Honrich, you should try it sometime.
Skyrim.esm|0557AE: Let me out of here, damn it! I'm not a criminal!
Skyrim.esm|0557B4: This is what happens to you when you speak your mind in this city.
Skyrim.esm|0557B8: They threw me in here because I called Ulfric a liar; a deceiver.
Skyrim.esm|0557B9: Ulfric doesn't care about Talos. He yearns for the throne of Skyrim and he'll say anything to rally people to his side.
Skyrim.esm|0557C0: But now, because of Ulfric's greed, countless lives have been lost.
Skyrim.esm|0557C8: On second thought, maybe I'm better off in here. At least I'll die with a clear conscience.
Skyrim.esm|05585A: Sure, my voice could use a rest.
Skyrim.esm|05585B: Sure, what can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|05585C: You look like an adventurous one. I bet we're kindred spirits, you and I.
Skyrim.esm|05586A: Aaah... yes! Sossedov los mul. The dragonblood runs strong in you. It is long since I had the pleasure of speech with one of my own kind.
Skyrim.esm|05586E: By long tradition, the elder speaks first.
Skyrim.esm|05586F: If you can see your destiny clearly, your sight is clearer than mine. Dahmaan - remember, Alduin also follows his destiny, as he sees it. But, I bow before your certainty. In a way I envy you. The curse of much knowledge is often indecision.
Skyrim.esm|055870: Drem. All in good time. First, a question for you. Why do you want to learn this Thu'um?
Skyrim.esm|055872: You were there for Roggvir's execution...
Skyrim.esm|055873: I'm a little busy at the moment. I've got all sorts of errands to run.
Skyrim.esm|055972: Mama, why are you and papa fighting?
Skyrim.esm|055973: Then you're talking to the wrong man. You want that elf in the keep. Calcelmo. The only thing I'll tell you about Markarth is stay out of our business. We have enough around here without some outsider causing trouble.
Skyrim.esm|05597E: Hahaha! You have much to learn of the dov, then. There is nothing else but philosophy to a dovah. It is no accident that we do battle with our Thu'um, our Voices. There is no distinction between debate and combat to a dragon. Tinvaak los grah. For us it is one and the same.
Skyrim.esm|05597F: Haven't attacked each other yet. Good a sign as any.
Skyrim.esm|055980: Markarth. Is it bloody enough for you?
Skyrim.esm|055981: You have not answered my question. Why do you need to learn this Thu'um?
Skyrim.esm|055982: What am I supposed to do? Never leave the house? You want that, Endon?
Skyrim.esm|055983: That's right. An Orc stronghold. Kept to ourselves. Lived as our ancestors would have wanted. Wasn't enough for me, though. I left. Didn't want to end up as the third wife of some lowly chieftain.
Skyrim.esm|055985: It's right there in the name of the most powerful family in Markarth. The Silver-Bloods. They have a whole mine filled with prisoners to dig up silver ore, gets smelted by workers they pay, and they own half the city. I work for them. The inn is named after them. When the guards make an arrest, they check with them first.
Skyrim.esm|055986: You strike your metal like a new mother giving her babe a light slap. Hit it harder.
Skyrim.esm|055987: Think we should buy more horses next season?
Skyrim.esm|055988: That's about the only harmless thing you can do in this city. Silver-Blood Inn is the place to go. And once you've had your fill of mead, do us all a favor and go home. Markarth has enough trouble.
Skyrim.esm|055989: Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as mortal, but as dovah!
Skyrim.esm|05598B: Then you've found your home, friend. Blood and silver are what flows through Markarth. Welcome.
Skyrim.esm|0559B8: You want to give up now? Out of fear? Is that what we're teaching our daughter?
Skyrim.esm|0559BA: How are the new breeds getting along with my dogs?
Skyrim.esm|0559C6: No. I live in a nice house in Solitude where the air isn't filled with smoke from the silver smelters and golden-haired girls shine my shield. Been here ever since I left the Bards College. Turns out a few muscles earn more gold than a damn lute. The Silver-Bloods pay me, let me crack a few skulls, and keep the guards off my back. That's all I need.
Skyrim.esm|0559DC: Come, Dovahkiin. Nin Yol. Strike me with the fire of your Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|0559DD: Why do you delay? Are you Dovahkiin or not?
Skyrim.esm|0559DE: Do not be afraid. Faasnu. Let me feel the power of your Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|0559E7: Then turn around and go back where you came. City doesn't need you, doesn't want you. Blood and silver are what flows through Markarth. That's the way it is. That's the way it'll stay.
Skyrim.esm|0559FC: If you can't pound the dusk out of your iron, it'll break when it's finished. Hit. It. Harder.
Skyrim.esm|0559FD: Grew up in a stronghold. Every Orc girl tended the forge at some point. But a girl has to grow up, and life in the stronghold asked for a lot in return, so I joined the Legion. Mastered my trade there.
Skyrim.esm|0559FE: Wait, I can still fight. No, no I can't.
Skyrim.esm|0559FF: We're not fighting we're just.... Why don't I get you something to eat? Mama and papa will talk later.
Skyrim.esm|055A00: You a fighting girl? How about a little bet?
Skyrim.esm|055A01: Think you can go blade to blade with me? You'd be dead in six seconds.
Skyrim.esm|055A02: Why are you bothering me, kinsman?
Skyrim.esm|055A03: Why are you bothering me, outsider?
Skyrim.esm|055A04: I don't. Go bother Kleppr at the Silver-Blood Inn, he handles the newcomers. And by Ysmir, if you find work, just do what you're told. There's enough trouble in Markarth already.
Skyrim.esm|055A07: You're closing the stall, and that's final.
Skyrim.esm|055A08: Jarl is who you want to talk to. Always coin to be made killing off some Forsworn in the hills. As long as you don't mind going axe-to-axe with a bunch of Daedra-worshipping savages who practice the dark arts.
Skyrim.esm|055A09: I'd rather spend the gold on a new litter of pups, but the horses bring in more gold.
Skyrim.esm|055A0A: I'm sorry, Ghorza. I just... flinch, and uh....
Skyrim.esm|055A0B: Just fists. No weapons, no magic... no crying. Let's go!
Skyrim.esm|055A0C: Why don't you come back when you actually have a hundred gold?
Skyrim.esm|055A0D: And why not? I could beat anyone in this city, bare-handed. A hundred gold says I knock your hide to the ground.
Skyrim.esm|055A0F: If I was threatening you, believe me, you'd know it. You outsiders never learn. If the Forsworn don't get you, the guards will throw you in Cidhna Mine to work your fingers off. Think that's water in the river? Blood and silver are what flows through Markarth, friend.
Skyrim.esm|055A10: The Empire has always been good to the Orcs, but this war of theirs is none of my concern.
Skyrim.esm|055A14: Didn't I speak clear enough? Out of my way.
Skyrim.esm|055A15: Blood and silver, friend. Blood and silver.
Skyrim.esm|055A16: Just turn around and leave the city. That's the smart move.
Skyrim.esm|055A17: Got an axe here. Good for cleaving up Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|055A19: You'll want some thick armor if you're traveling the Reach alone.
Skyrim.esm|055A1B: You'd think an apprentice could forge a few hinges. Not Tacitus.
Skyrim.esm|055A2D: The who? I'm sorry, I have no idea what you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|055A9E: Time is of the essence here, please hurry back.
Skyrim.esm|055A9F: Then we show them Gallus's journal and hope for the best. Remember, we have proof and all he's got is his word.
Skyrim.esm|055AA0: Keep your eyes open. I'm not sure what to expect when we enter the cistern.
Skyrim.esm|055AA1: The time for talk has passed. We must hurry.
Skyrim.esm|055AA2: I'm glad you're here. I think some of these people are beginning to suspect who I am. Are you ready to face the Guild?
Skyrim.esm|055AA3: Are you finished your other business and ready to face the Guild?
Skyrim.esm|055AD5: This can't be true. I've known Mercer too long...
Skyrim.esm|055AD8: It says Mercer's been stealing from our vault for years. Gallus was looking into it before he was murdered.
Skyrim.esm|055AD9: Use your key on the vault, Delvin. We'll open it up and find out the truth.
Skyrim.esm|055ADA: What's in that book? What did it say?
Skyrim.esm|055ADC: I have Gallus's journal. I think you'll find its contents disturbing.
Skyrim.esm|055ADD: By the Eight! It's gone, everything's gone! Get in here, all of you!
Skyrim.esm|055ADE: Now what's this so-called proof you speak of?
Skyrim.esm|055ADF: Wait just a blessed moment, Bryn.
Skyrim.esm|055AE0: He didn't need to pick the lock.
Skyrim.esm|055AE1: You better have a damn good reason to be here with that murderer.
Skyrim.esm|055AE2: It's true, Brynjolf. Every word. Mercer's been stealing from the Guild for years, right under your noses.
Skyrim.esm|055AE4: That door has the best puzzle locks money can buy. There's no way it can be picked open.
Skyrim.esm|055AE5: No tricks, Karliah or I'll cut you down where you stand.
Skyrim.esm|055AE6: I've used my key, but the vault's still locked up tighter than a drum. Now use yours.
Skyrim.esm|055AE7: Please, lower your weapons so we can speak. I have proof that you've all been misled!
Skyrim.esm|055AE8: There's only one way to find out if what the lass says is true. Delvin, I'll need you to open the Vault.
Skyrim.esm|055B5C: Faida's teaching me the innkeeper's trade. She runs the Four Shields.
Skyrim.esm|055B5D: Got nothing to say to strange folk.
Skyrim.esm|055BF6: I can hardly believe it. But there's no time. Whatever happened here has affected Winterhold as well. I need you to get out there and make sure everything's all right. I'll do what I can here. Go now, go!
Skyrim.esm|055BF7: Are you all right? What happened in there?
Skyrim.esm|055BF8: By the Nine... Is he responsible for this? The Arch-Mage, dead? There's more. Something's happened to Winterhold. It must be whatever Ancano did. You need to get out there and make sure it's safe. Quickly now, quickly! I'll find Mirabelle and see if we can't put a stop to this!
Skyrim.esm|055CDF: He's my son. I just... I feel it in my heart. You'd understand if you had children.
Skyrim.esm|055CE5: It's hardly a secret that they have ties to the Empire, and hate anyone who speaks out against it. Thorald did worse. He took action against the Empire, and the Battle-Borns knew it.
Skyrim.esm|055D2E: The gold, the jewels... it's all gone.
Skyrim.esm|055D30: Vex! Put it away... right now. We can't afford to lose our heads... we need to calm down and focus.
Skyrim.esm|055D32: Delvin, Vex... watch the Flagon. If you see Mercer, come tell me right away.
Skyrim.esm|055D77: Wuuthrad was the blade held by Ysgramor when he came back from Atmora. An axe of great vengeance. Each fragment is precious.
Skyrim.esm|055DF1: With open arms, with cheers and song, with joy in my heart and tears in my eyes! Praise be to Talos, this is a glorious day for Whiterun and for all Skyrim! Our liberators have come at last!
Skyrim.esm|055DF2: I'm sure my brother Vignar is, since they made him Jarl after throwing Balgruuf out. Comes the end of the day, Imperials and Stormcloaks ain't much different. Both sides want to tell you how you should live your life. Guess I'd rather hear it from a real Nord than some Emperor down south.
Skyrim.esm|055DF5: Of course they have! I've made no secret of my loyalty to the Empire. I'm no coward. Twice I've been robbed, and good luck getting any kind of justice from Vignar now that he's the Jarl. He probably told the Stormcloaks to do it. Lately, they've been threatening to send my whole family into exile if we're caught collaborating with the Empire. They think we're spies! Needless to say, these rebels and I have no love for each other.
Skyrim.esm|055DF6: Pleased enough, I suppose. At least they appreciate the ways and traditions of the Nord people. Can't say I ever much liked the idea of the Empire telling us what to do an' how to do it.
Skyrim.esm|055DF8: No doubt he thought it was the only way to make his point. And also because he knew he could.
Skyrim.esm|055DFA: If it's the war you're referring to, I'm on the side of Whiterun. No doubt General Tullius and his friends in the Empire will tell you that I owe them my loyalty, and perhaps I do. Ulfric Stormcloak would say that I owe my allegiance to the Nord people as they fight for Skyrim's independence. Perhaps this is also true. The day might come when I am forced to draw my sword for one side or the other. But that day has not come yet.
Skyrim.esm|055DFB: If the gods are merciful, they've found a safe place to hide in the city. I hope Vignar doesn't find them. He'll ask a king's ransom, I'm sure.
Skyrim.esm|055DFC: I serve Jarl Baalgruuf as steward.
Skyrim.esm|055DFD: After Whiterun is retaken, Ulfric will pay in blood for his insolence.
Skyrim.esm|055FA9: We're causing no trouble. All we ask is to look for her.
Skyrim.esm|055FAA: I don't care what you're doing, after what happened you're lucky I don't toss you in jail. Now get lost.
Skyrim.esm|055FAB: Look, you've already been told you're not allowed here. Turn around and go back the way you came.
Skyrim.esm|055FAC: We will be back. This is not over.
Skyrim.esm|055FAD: She gets the books, I get you. It's very simple.
Skyrim.esm|055FC3: Trying to make Mercer look bad in front of Maven, eh? Clever lass. Was there anything else?
Skyrim.esm|055FC4: Look, before I have you help track Mercer down I need to know what you learned from Karliah. I mean everything.
Skyrim.esm|055FC5: Before Mercer took over, Gallus started collecting every bit of material he could on locations the Guild could heist. Museums, keeps, estates... you name it. By the time Mercer took over the Guild we must have had a few dozen.
Skyrim.esm|055FC6: Aye. I feared that was the case. From that last entry in Gallus's diary, it looks like he was getting close to exposing Mercer to the Guild. Anything else?
Skyrim.esm|055FC7: What? Nightingales? But, I always assumed they were just a tale... a way to keep the young footpads in line. Was there anything else she told you?
Skyrim.esm|055FC8: I don't have a clue. That door is impenetrable. Without two keys, it's impossible to open. I have a key, Delvin has a key, and Mercer has a key. That's it. There are no other copies.
Skyrim.esm|056155: All I can tell you for now is that he's abused his authority as a Nightingale.
Skyrim.esm|056156: As a Nightingale, I took an oath and was sworn to secrecy. Be patient and your time will come.
Skyrim.esm|056170: Never said that. Just that we don't get many of you wanting to join, that's all.
Skyrim.esm|056178: Changed your mind? You ready to pledge your blood and honor for Skyrim's freedom?
Skyrim.esm|0562E0: Hey, I'm not done talking to you yet. Come back here!
Skyrim.esm|0562E5: I don't know for sure. I spoke out against the Aldmeri Dominion publicly; I suspect that's why these men were hired to hunt me down.
Skyrim.esm|0562E6: You think I'd be in hiding if this was something I trusted town guards to handle? These men are ruthless. Cunning, deceitful... they'll pay off whoever they can. I can't trust anyone here in Whiterun. Guards and Jarls can be bought. And the Alik'r are close; I'm running out of time, so I'm choosing to trust you.
Skyrim.esm|0562F1: Without them at our backs, we'd be in serious trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0562FA: Of course... your pay. Haha! You're smart as a whip, lad. Keep doing right by us and there's plenty more where that came from.
Skyrim.esm|0562FD: No! I'm not about to let you ruin everything I've worked so hard for!
Skyrim.esm|0562FF: All eyes are on you, lass. Don't disappoint us.
Skyrim.esm|056300: Let me show you the operation, then you can ask your questions.
Skyrim.esm|056309: Let me put it to you this way. Nothing happens in Riften without Maven's consent. One word from her and you could spend the rest of the fourth era in prison.
Skyrim.esm|05630E: I don't believe you. That's not your way.
Skyrim.esm|056380: Thurri du hin sille ko Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|056381: Brit grah. I had forgotten what fine sport you mortals can provide!
Skyrim.esm|056382: You are brave. Balaan hokoron. Your defeat brings me honor.
Skyrim.esm|056551: Yes, Yes. It is an ancient and ruined city. Built by the Dwemer. The dwarf folk. It is nearby, but I'd stay clear. People say it is quite dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|0565E1: Indeed. Avanchnzel is as much a library as it is a city. Built to hold the vast memories of the Dwemer.
Skyrim.esm|0565E3: Why are all these metal... things... ignoring us?
Skyrim.esm|0565E5: Enough. Their services will be more than adequate. Let us continue.
Skyrim.esm|0565E7: So we grab it and we get out, right?
Skyrim.esm|0565E9: I assure you, it is. The knowledge contained in the Lexicon created everything you see around you.
Skyrim.esm|0565EA: You said they were sleeping. I said they were waiting. It seems a few of them were "waiting" for someone to try and take the Lexicon.
Skyrim.esm|0565ED: The Lexicon had better be worth all this trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0565F0: We're almost there. I can hear the Lexicon. Come on. Quickly!
Skyrim.esm|0565F1: I can't hold these things! We need to leave!
Skyrim.esm|0565F3: And yet none of us can pick a damned lock.
Skyrim.esm|0565F4: I thought you said these things were "sleeping"?
Skyrim.esm|0565F5: Ours. Soon the Lexicon's knowledge will be ours.
Skyrim.esm|0565F6: The way out is right there! Run, damn it!
