Skyrim.esm|0565FA: We are close now. Can you hear the Lexicon calling out?
Skyrim.esm|0565FD: Drennen, you do understand that the Lexicon at the bottom of this place holds the accumulated memories of centuries of Dwemer.
Skyrim.esm|056600: It is large, indeed. I did not anticipate Avanchnzel's size or the time needed to uncover the entrance.
Skyrim.esm|056601: We are thieves, you fool. We're stealing the cube.
Skyrim.esm|056602: I told you we should have hired a thief.
Skyrim.esm|056603: I have the Lexicon! Where's the podium?
Skyrim.esm|056606: I did not come all the way here just to walk out empty-handed.
Skyrim.esm|056607: Calm yourself, Breya. We are closing in on the Lexicon. I can feel it calling. We'll continue without Drennen.
Skyrim.esm|056608: So we're not turning back, you fool.
Skyrim.esm|05660B: I don't like this place. It feels like we're being... watched.
Skyrim.esm|05660C: It's a simple job, Drennen. We get in, we steal the Lexicon, we leave. Don't get jumpy.
Skyrim.esm|05660D: Let the field mouse run. Your pay will be double.
Skyrim.esm|05660E: My pay will be nothing if we die in here!
Skyrim.esm|05660F: You sure that's power we're meant to have?
Skyrim.esm|056610: Once we have the Lexicon, we'll need to take it to its podium. I know what to do from there. Soon the Lexicon's knowledge will be mine.
Skyrim.esm|056613: If you two cannot handle this, we can find others to hire.
Skyrim.esm|056614: Maybe we should turn back. I don't want to sleep here.
Skyrim.esm|056AA8: Seriously, you don't know? That was Ulfric Stormcloak himself.
Skyrim.esm|056AA9: I was assigned to Ulfric's guard. We were on our way to Darkwater Crossing, in the south of Eastmarch. The Imperials were waiting for us. As pretty an ambush as I ever saw. We were outnumbered five to one, at least. Ulfric ordered us to stop fighting. Didn't want us all to die for nothing, I guess. I thought they were taking us south to Cyrodiil. Parade us in front of the Emperor. But then we stopped in Helgen, and you know the rest.
Skyrim.esm|056AAA: Surely even down in Cyrodiil people have heard news of the war in Skyrim? The Nords are tired of spending our blood fighting the Empire's wars, and paying for the Empire's decadence with our taxes. Ulfric is our rightful High King. He's leading the fight to drive the Empire out of Skyrim, once and for all.
Skyrim.esm|056AAB: Don't even say such things! I hope that dragon stays away from Riverwood. We don't need that kind of trouble.
Skyrim.esm|056AAC: What? Oh, hardly! I don't think even Ulfric could pull a dragon out of his pocket. Lucky for us it attacked when it did, eh? I wasn't looking forward to getting a shave from the Imperial headsman.
Skyrim.esm|056AAD: So you saw a dragon, did you? Tell me, what was it like? As big as a house?
Skyrim.esm|056AAE: Oh, right, they said you were captured crossing the border. Still, I'm surprised you haven't heard of Ulfric. He's our leader - the leader of the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|056AAF: I bet my uncle Ralof is tougher than you are.
Skyrim.esm|056AB0: Well I'll be! That'd be a sight to see. Not that I actually want to see one, understand. I hope that dragon stays far away from here.
Skyrim.esm|056AB1: The Empire may have been good for Skyrim once upon a time, but those days are long past. Banning the worship of Talos was the last straw. Thalmor everywhere, dragging people off for honoring our own gods! I'm glad Ralof is helping drive them out of here. If I was a bit younger, I might have joined the fight myself.
Skyrim.esm|056AB2: I've got enough to worry about, taking care of my family. I don't concern myself with such matters.
Skyrim.esm|056AB3: Right. The leader of our fight against the Empire. I forget that most people don't know what he looks like, except for those Imperial wanted posters.
Skyrim.esm|056AB4: Sure, sure. It's just, you know, not every day I meet someone who's laid eyes on a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|056AF0: Impressive work. We started the attack once you made the fog scamper away like that. Hope you didn't catch any of the fire. We can head back whenever you're set. There's celebrating to be done!
Skyrim.esm|056AF1: Name is Stig Salt-Plank, one of the captains of the Blood Horkers.
Skyrim.esm|056AF2: I've got a top man on the job right now. He should be returning any moment.
Skyrim.esm|056AF3: I've got a sharp woman on the job right now. She should be returning any moment.
Skyrim.esm|056AF4: Hard to say. The weather's taken a bit of a turn, here. Haldyn's been known to obscure his hideaways with magic. If we take care of him, the way should be clear to properly assault the island.
Skyrim.esm|056B1E: You haven't heard of the civil war in Skyrim? I guess down in Cyrodiil people have other things to worry about. It's pretty simple. Ulfric founded the Stormcloaks years ago, as a sort of private army to advance his ambitions. He's always used the ban on the worship of Talos to stir people up against the Empire. He never succeeded in getting much support, so a few months ago he murdered the High King! That got the Empire's attention.
Skyrim.esm|056B1F: A masterstroke by General Tullius! He's only been in charge here for a few months, but he's turned things around for the Empire. We've been trying to catch Ulfric since the war started, but he always seemed to slip through our fingers... like he knew we were coming. This time, the General turned the tables on him. Ulfric rode right into our ambush with only a few bodyguards. He surrendered pretty meekly, too. So much for his death-or-glory reputation. I thought we were taking Ulfric back to Cyrodiil, but I guess the General changed his mind. You know the rest.
Skyrim.esm|056B20: Right, the leader of the Stormcloaks. They claim to be fighting for Skyrim's freedom, but the war is really all about Ulfric wanting to be High King of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|056B21: I guess that wasn't such a big deal elsewhere in the Empire, but here it's caused a lot of resentment. Native son and all that. Even I'll admit it hasn't been the Empire's finest hour. But it wasn't like the Emperor had any choice, did he? If he hadn't signed the peace treaty with the Thalmor, they would have destroyed the Empire - then where would Skyrim be? That's the part that Ulfric's supporters always conveniently forget about. Unless the Empire stands together, the Thalmor will destroy us all.
Skyrim.esm|056B22: It's from that treaty that ended the Great War, remember, when the Emperor was forced by the Thalmor to outlaw Talos worship. We didn't pay much attention to it when I was a boy - everyone still had their little shrine to Talos. But then Ulfric and his "Sons of Skyrim" started agitating about it, and sure enough the Emperor had to crack down. Dragging people off in the middle of the night... one of the main causes of this war, if you ask me.
Skyrim.esm|056B23: Oh, right, you were caught trying to cross the border into Skyrim, weren't you?
Skyrim.esm|056B24: People are rightly stirred up about the damn Thalmor being allowed to roam around arresting people, just for worshipping Talos. But was it worth tearing Skyrim apart, and maybe destroying the Empire? No, Ulfric will have a lot to answer for in the end. Nords have always supported the Empire, and the Empire has always been good for Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|056B25: I just hope the war doesn't come to Riverwood. There's enough trouble in the world without those Stormcloaks stirring up more.
Skyrim.esm|056B26: So did I! If the damn Stormcloaks somehow found one, or woke it up... the war might be about to take an ugly turn. Hard to believe it was just a coincidence, that the first dragon anyone's seen for centuries attacks just as Ulfric was about to be executed.
Skyrim.esm|056B27: You didn't know? That was Ulfric Stormcloak and his top lieutenants.
Skyrim.esm|056BA3: See if you can find some, and I'll try to find some more interesting work by the time you get back.
Skyrim.esm|056BA4: The Companions have been asked for assistance, and we shall answer. Particularly when the pay is as good as this. Get moving. I shudder to think what might happen if you're too late.
Skyrim.esm|056BA5: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Bear pelts.
Skyrim.esm|056BA6: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Snow Bear pelts.
Skyrim.esm|056BA7: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Sabre Cat pelts.
Skyrim.esm|056BA8: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Snow Sabre Cat pelts.
Skyrim.esm|056BA9: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Ice Wolf pelts.
Skyrim.esm|056BAA: We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Wolf pelts.
Skyrim.esm|056BAB: A citizen of the Reach has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BAC: A citizen of Haafingar has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BAD: A citizen of Hjaalmarch has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BAE: A citizen of Falkreath has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BAF: A citizen of Whiterun has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BB0: A citizen of the Pale has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BB1: A citizen of the Rift has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BB2: A citizen of Eastmarch has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BB3: A citizen of Winterhold has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|056BB4: A citizen of Skyrim has been kidnapped.
Skyrim.esm|057996: Be careful on the way to Solitude. Damn Stormcloaks could be anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|057997: We'd better keep moving. I feel like that dragon might be up there watching us right now.
Skyrim.esm|057998: I'm going to rest up here a while before heading to Windhelm. Be careful.
Skyrim.esm|057999: We'd better keep moving. I don't want to run into any Imperials who know what happened at Helgen.
Skyrim.esm|05799E: The rebels have agreed to cease hostilities until we hash out this treaty. Suffice it to say I'm not holding my breath.
Skyrim.esm|0579A6: You've proven your strength, warrior. Let's avoid any more bloodshed.
Skyrim.esm|0579A7: I think you and I have some things to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|057A4D: Yes. According to Giraud, our histories keeper, the portion of the Edda dealing with King Olaf might still exist in the Dead Man's Respite. I need you to retrieve the poem.
Skyrim.esm|057A4E: That's tremendous! I have to admit I didn't fully believe it still existed. Now let's take a look at this... Oh. Oh-no. This won't do at all. The copy is incomplete, it's aged to the point that parts are unreadable.
Skyrim.esm|057A72: Always something going wrong. Mill needs more lumber or the saw needs sharpening.
Skyrim.esm|057BCB: Even the Imperials might eventually decide to patrol their perimeter. Get yourself ready!
Skyrim.esm|057BCE: That's what I like to hear.
Skyrim.esm|057BD0: I've found the final resting place of the Jagged Crown. Can you believe it? Well, I'm almost certain. We're headed to Korvanjund. But, if old King Borgas is indeed buried there, we're bound to run into trouble of some kind. Your fellow Stormcloaks are already assembling outside the tomb. I'll meet you there as soon as I'm finished up here.
Skyrim.esm|057BD1: What's the Legion doing here? Damn Imperial spies... Well, should be fun for us, at least. They don't seem to know we're here yet. Ready to spill some Imperial blood for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|057DBF: Follow me. Quickly and quietly now. I want their guts on the ground before they even know we're here.
Skyrim.esm|057DC0: But do not make the mistake of underestimating the Legion. Plenty of them are Nords same as us.
Skyrim.esm|057E23: My wife Beitild is dead. Can't say I miss that old sabre cat.
Skyrim.esm|057F7B: So polite! So nice! Cicero likes you. The Night Mother is sure to like you too. Oh, we're going to be fast friends. Fast friends.
Skyrim.esm|057F7C: Do you mistrust poor Cicero? I am, as you say, just an innocent jester. A fool! Oh, but my work is not foolish, no. For I am our mother's Keeper.
Skyrim.esm|057F7D: Oooh... So silent. So menacing. A true assassin of the old way. Cicero likes you. Oh yes, Cicero likes you, indeed.
Skyrim.esm|057F7E: Keep talking, little man, and we'll see who gets "punished."
Skyrim.esm|057F7F: I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes! And you helped me! You helped poor Cicero! You talked to Loreius, got him to fix my wheel! Oh, you may have pleased me, but you have surely pleased the Night Mother. And our mother, she will never forget.
Skyrim.esm|057F80: I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes! Oh, but you didn't help poor Cicero! You helped that stupid farmer! Stupid Loreius! Oh, the guard said cruel things about us. Untrue things...
Skyrim.esm|057F81: Wait, oh wait. I know you! Yes, yes. From the road! Cicero never forgets a face.
Skyrim.esm|057F82: Another member of the Family! Hello, hello. So very good to meet you.
Skyrim.esm|057F83: No time to talk now, my friend. No time. Out mistress needs tending. Keeping.
Skyrim.esm|057F84: Why talk? Cicero has nothing to say to you. Call it a matter of... trust.
Skyrim.esm|057F85: Oh, I do wish we could get to know each other better. But not now! I must tend to mother. See her settled.
Skyrim.esm|057F87: He said I attacked someone! On the road! But it never happened. Never. Sweet mother and I were just waiting. My knife was sheathed!
Skyrim.esm|057F88: He said I was transporting weapons! For the war! In mother's coffin! He made me... open it! Indignity! Sacrilege!
Skyrim.esm|057F89: He said I stole something! Me! Poor Cicero. A common thief. The very thought is insulting. One might even say... maddening.
Skyrim.esm|057FA0: We meet at last, my dear lady.
Skyrim.esm|057FA1: After all this, I have to pick up and leave again? If you really think this is the only way, I trust you. Let's not waste any time.
Skyrim.esm|057FA4: But where will I go? I can't keep running forever!
Skyrim.esm|057FA5: Oh, come now. You didn't really expect to manipulate people forever, did you? Your luck had to run out sometime.
Skyrim.esm|057FB1: Please, our tribe suffers and we need help.
Skyrim.esm|057FB3: Our Chief, Yamarz, was once a strong and proud warrior. Now he is stricken, cursed. He is weak, and so our tribe is weak. The giants sense this, and intrude on our territory. Now they assault our very home. Yamarz refuses help, but I sense that you may be just what we need.
Skyrim.esm|05809A: Good. They'll think twice before crossing us while their command structure is in disarray.
Skyrim.esm|05809B: I pray Talos overlooks our less than honorable approach. But he knows our purpose is just and true. I'm glad you made it out alive.
Skyrim.esm|05809C: Good work. This was a double victory. We took a garrison away from the Stormcloaks and further proved the hollowness of their cause.
Skyrim.esm|05809D: Excellent. That's one less settlement in Imperial hands, and one more sign to all that our cause is true.
Skyrim.esm|0580A0: They're mercenaries, only in it for the money. They're led by a man named Kematu. Get rid of him, and the rest will scatter. I don't dare show my face, lest they recognize me, so you'll have to find out where they are.
Skyrim.esm|0580A1: We're looking for information about a woman -- a foreigner in these lands. She's a Redguard, like us. We believe she may be hiding in Whiterun, but we're checking everywhere to be sure.
Skyrim.esm|05829E: Aela's laid claim to your services, and I've learned the hard way not to upset her. Best you stick to her for now.
Skyrim.esm|05829F: Aela said you were hers, and I wasn't supposed to interfere. She scares me.
Skyrim.esm|0582A0: And there is more work to be done, but I fear that Kodlak's gotten wind of our recent efforts. He's asked to see you. My advice? Always be honest with the old man, but don't tell him anything he doesn't need to know.
Skyrim.esm|0582D1: The Silver Hand. They finally found enough courage to attack Jorrvaskr. We fought them off, but... The old man... Kodlak... he's dead.
Skyrim.esm|0582D2: Have you heard the story of how we came to be werewolves?
Skyrim.esm|0582D4: You shall have no Shield-Brother this time. But the spirit of Ysgramor goes with you, to restore the honor of his legacy.
Skyrim.esm|0582D6: Do you have the fragments of Wuuthrad, still? I'll need to prepare them for mounting again.
Skyrim.esm|0582D7: And so slain the beast inside of me. I thank you for this gift. The other Harbingers remain trapped by Hircine, though. Perhaps from Sovngarde, the heroes of old can join me in their rescue. The Harrowing of the Hunting Grounds. It would be a battle of such triumph. And perhaps some day, you'll join us in that battle. But for today, return to Jorrvaskr. Triumph in your victory. And lead the Companions to further glory.
Skyrim.esm|0582D9: That's what I've spent my twilight years trying to find out. And now I've found the answer. The witches' magic ensnared us, and only their magic can release us. They won't give it willingly, but we can extract their foul powers by force. I want you to seek them out. Go to their coven in  the wilderness. Strike them down as a true warrior of the wild. And bring me their heads. The seat of their abilities. From there, we may begin to undo centuries of impurity.
Skyrim.esm|0582DB: They did not believe the change would be permanent. The witches offered payment, like anyone else. But we had been deceived.
Skyrim.esm|0582DC: The boy has a nugget of truth, but the reality is more complicated than that. It always is.
Skyrim.esm|0582DD: One of the fiercest battles I've ever seen.
Skyrim.esm|0582DF: The others have probably prepared Kodlak's funeral by now. Come up to the Skyforge to pay your respects.
Skyrim.esm|0582E0: The Companions are nearly five thousand years old. This matter of beastblood has only troubled us for a few hundred. One of my predecessors was a good, but short-sighted man. He made a bargain with the witches of Glenmoril Coven. If the Companions would hunt in the name of their lord, Hircine, we would be granted great power.
Skyrim.esm|0582E2: In any case, I have a task for you.
Skyrim.esm|0582E3: No, but they made off with all our fragments of Wuuthrad. But you and I are going to reclaim them. We will bring the battle to their chief camp. There will be none left living to tell their stories. Only songs of Jorrvaskr will be sung. We will avenge Kodlak. And they will know terror before the end.
Skyrim.esm|0582E4: We'll get to that. It's not so simple as just killing them, though.
Skyrim.esm|0582E6: This is not about your desires, lad.
Skyrim.esm|0582E7: The witches didn't lie, of course. But it's more than our bodies.
Skyrim.esm|0582E8: Don't get too flowery on me, lad.
Skyrim.esm|0582E9: Of course. My fellow Harbingers and I have been warming ourselves here. Trying to evade Hircine.
Skyrim.esm|0582EA: Return Wuuthrad to Ysgramor. It should open the way.
Skyrim.esm|0582EC: Mind you, it's no business of mine what each Companion does in the name of honor. But this sneaking around. It doesn't befit warriors of your standing. Aela knows better, and so should you.
Skyrim.esm|0582ED: Good. Now move quickly. And don't leave any of them alive.
Skyrim.esm|0582EE: Aye, that sounds like him. As in all matters of faith, though, the reality is more complicated than one believer would tell you.
Skyrim.esm|0582F0: Lass, I know what you've been up to.
Skyrim.esm|0582F1: I hope it was important, because it means you weren't here to defend him.
Skyrim.esm|0582F2: The disease, you see, affects not just our bodies. It seeps into the spirit. Upon death, werewolves are claimed by Hircine for his Hunting Grounds. For some, this is a paradise. They want nothing more than to chase prey with their master for eternity. And that is their choice. But I am still a true Nord. And I wish for Sovngarde as my spirit home.
Skyrim.esm|0585F9: They should be back shortly. You're welcome to wait here if you like.
Skyrim.esm|0585FA: This temple is dedicated to Mara, and the light of love she shines on us all. I'm only a humble acolyte, though. If you wish to avail yourself of Mara's blessing, you should speak with Dinya Balu or Maramal.
Skyrim.esm|058656: Get yourself kitted out with what you need. Welcome to the fight, brother!
Skyrim.esm|0587F4: Aye, lass... and I've come to a decision. Mercer Frey tried to kill both of you, he betrayed the Guild, murdered Gallus and made us question our future. He needs to die.
Skyrim.esm|0587F5: Then it's all true... everything I heard in the stories. The Nightingales, their allegiance to Nocturnal and the Twilight Sepulcher.
Skyrim.esm|0587F6: From the moment you were struck with my poisoned arrow at Snow Veil Sanctum, my path changed its course. Perhaps I couldn't bring Mercer back alive, but together, we were able to clear my name and to put Gallus's memory to rest. I'd always intended Mercer's fate to ultimately be decided by the Guild, and it seems they've spoken.
Skyrim.esm|0587F7: Brynjolf, the time has come to decide Mercer's fate. Until a new Guild Master is chosen, the decision falls to you.
Skyrim.esm|0587F8: We have to be very careful, Brynjolf. Mercer is a Nightingale, an Agent of Nocturnal.
Skyrim.esm|0587F9: Just outside of Riften, beyond the Southeast Gate is a small path cut up the mountainside. At the end of that path is a clearing and an old standing stone. I'd ask you both meet me there.
Skyrim.esm|0587FA: Time's wasting and Mercer's still out there. Let's get this show on the road.
Skyrim.esm|0587FB: I'm just as puzzled as you, lad.
Skyrim.esm|0587FC: I think we can trust Karliah. Let's see what she's on about.
Skyrim.esm|0587FE: Not now, lad. Karliah said it was important.
Skyrim.esm|0587FF: Not now, lass. Karliah said it was important.
Skyrim.esm|058800: We'll speak when the Oath is complete.
Skyrim.esm|058B37: Stay your hand, warrior! It's no secret why you're here and you have proven your skill in combat. Let us talk a moment, and no one else needs to die. I think we can all profit from the situation in which we find ourselves. My men will not attack you, if you will lower your weapons.
Skyrim.esm|058B38: Not on the way back. Once she gets there, it's not up to me to decide what's done with her. And as for you, I owe you a portion of the reward, don't I? Well, here you go. Spend it wisely, and if I may... Don't allow yourself to be fooled by a pretty face. You're better than that.
Skyrim.esm|058B39: Now, we'll take our friend here back to Hammerfell, where she will pay the price for her treason.
Skyrim.esm|058C43: Irgnir, get a hold of yourself. They're just dreams. Please tell her, Erandur.
Skyrim.esm|058C44: It's the same dream over and over again. You think that's normal? It's evil I tell you!
Skyrim.esm|058C45: Erandur, she has a point. You keep telling us no harm will follow these dreams, but they must be an omen.
Skyrim.esm|058C47: It's a curse! It has to be! I've got to get out of this town.
Skyrim.esm|058C48: No. I don't have anything for bad dreams, so don't ask.
Skyrim.esm|058C49: Lady Mara will save us from these nightmares. You'll see.
Skyrim.esm|058C4A: Can't sleep well no matter how much drink I put in me.
Skyrim.esm|058C4B: I envy you travelers. Dreams aren't affecting anyone not from here.
Skyrim.esm|058C4C: If you're here about the nightmares, I'm afraid my pleas to the College of Winterhold go unheard.
Skyrim.esm|058C4D: Seems like no one is getting a decent night's rest in Dawnstar, stranger. Best buy your steel and move on.
Skyrim.esm|058C4E: Unless you're here to solve this nightmare problem, I don't need you.
Skyrim.esm|058C4F: Need a room? Don't worry. Nightmares don't seem to happen for travelers.
Skyrim.esm|058C52: You come to Dawnstar at a bad time. Tempers are... fiery.
Skyrim.esm|058CF4: I don't know how you made it this far, but you'll go no farther.
Skyrim.esm|058EBB: So, this is Nightingale Hall. I heard about this place when I joined the Guild, but I never believed it existed.
Skyrim.esm|058EBC: I have two others that wish to transact the Oath; to serve you both in life and in death.
Skyrim.esm|058EBE: My Lady, I've come before you to throw myself upon your mercy and to accept responsibility for my failure.
Skyrim.esm|058EBF: This is enough to make your head spin, eh?
Skyrim.esm|058EC0: I think we should trust the lass and take the deal.
Skyrim.esm|058EC1: This isn't about religion or destiny. This is nothing more than a business transaction between yourself and Nocturnal. Consider this an extremely risky job but with a massive potential for profit, and you'll do fine.
Skyrim.esm|058EC3: I'm trying to understand why I'm here, lass. I'm no priest, and I'm certainly not religious. Why pick me?
Skyrim.esm|058EC6: It's my hope that you will, yes.
Skyrim.esm|058EC8: The assumption that the Nightingales were just a myth was seeded within the Guild on purpose. It helped avert attention from our true nature.
Skyrim.esm|058EC9: This isn't about religion, Brynjolf... it's business.
Skyrim.esm|058ECA: I call upon you Lady Nocturnal, Queen of Murk and Empress of Shadow... hear my voice!
Skyrim.esm|058ECB: Listen, lad. There's one last piece of business we need to settle before we go after Mercer... the leadership of the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|058ECC: Listen, lass. There's one last piece of business we need to settle before we go after Mercer... the leadership of the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|058ECE: Lady Nocturnal, we accept your terms. We dedicate ourselves to you as both your avengers and your sentinels. We will honor our agreement in this life and the next until your conditions have been met.
Skyrim.esm|058ECF: This is the headquarters of the Nightingales, cut into the mountainside by the first of our kind. We've come to seek the edge we need to defeat Mercer Frey.
Skyrim.esm|058ED2: If you'll follow me, I'll try to explain on the way.
Skyrim.esm|058ED4: What's wrong, Brynjolf? I can almost hear your brow furrowing.
Skyrim.esm|058ED7: This is Nightingale Hall. You're the first of the uninitiated to set foot inside in over a century.
Skyrim.esm|058F85: Damn him - the old bear was right! Did you run into any trouble?
Skyrim.esm|058F8A: Get the crown. We will keep an eye out for more of those dustmen.
Skyrim.esm|058F8B: That crown should be around here somewhere. Just keep an eye out for the bonewalkers...
Skyrim.esm|058FD0: What? Alain is... is dead? I... I'm grateful. It's just this... this isn't at all how I imagined things would play out. There was Alain, but I was considering more, and... Ah, well. Dead is dead. Right? My thanks to you, assassin. Here's the gold I was going to pay for the job. You earned it, after all.
Skyrim.esm|058FD1: The anticipation is killing me. Can you figure out who has the contract? Which one will you choose?
Skyrim.esm|059091: Just try it. Maybe a little time inside Cidhna Mine will get rid of all that fat on you.
Skyrim.esm|059092: Picking on me again, you backbiting Nord? Tired of those prissy nobles turning down your rotten slabs of venison?
Skyrim.esm|059093: Maybe we could spend a little bit more and buy some meat, Endon.
Skyrim.esm|059095: How dare you. No one turns down cut meat from Hogni Red-Arm!
Skyrim.esm|059098: Maybe. Let me think about it.
Skyrim.esm|05909A: Hogni Red-Arm, more like Hogni Fat-Arm.
Skyrim.esm|05909C: Markarth has changed, but the inn is as lively as ever.
Skyrim.esm|05909F: Alms. Give me some meat, you fat Nord!
Skyrim.esm|0590A1: The city hasn't changed that much, dear. Same old dwarven stones gathering the same old moss.
Skyrim.esm|0590A5: What did you say to me, Reachman?
Skyrim.esm|0590AA: The stones aren't what bother me, love. The Forsworn are tearing the Reach apart.
Skyrim.esm|0590B1: Let's not talk about this over dinner.
Skyrim.esm|0590B3: I said give me some meat. I'm hungry, and the Divines know you don't need more food in that huge gut of yours.
Skyrim.esm|0590B8: You little twig. I'll snap you in two!
Skyrim.esm|0590B9: Look at you, begging on the streets. Learn a trade.
Skyrim.esm|0590BA: Stew again, papa? I want steak.
Skyrim.esm|059631: Dear sister, I need you to steal into that chamber, and eavesdrop on their meeting. It'll be no use clinging to the shadows. They'll see you for sure. No, you need a hiding place. Somewhere they'd never think to look. Like inside the Night Mother's coffin.
Skyrim.esm|059634: Ah, at last. I've been anxiously awaiting your return.
Skyrim.esm|059636: Yes, I know. As does Astrid. You have done well, and have earned both your reward, and a bonus, as I may have mentioned. But you should know that we have a more pressing matter to deal with. It's... Cicero. There's been an incident. You should proceed into the Sanctuary. I'll let Astrid explain.
Skyrim.esm|059643: Yes, I know. As does Astrid. And you've earned your payment, though the bonus is forfeit, as I'm sure you're aware. But you should know that we have a more pressing matter to deal with. It's... Cicero. There's been an incident. You should proceed into the Sanctuary. I'll let Astrid explain.
Skyrim.esm|0596F0: Be on your guard. I've no idea what to expect inside the College.
Skyrim.esm|0596F1: The power to unmake the world at my fingertips, and you think you can do anything about it?
Skyrim.esm|0596F2: Head for the Great Hall, quickly! Don't stop for anything!
Skyrim.esm|0596F3: If it can help us get inside the College, and reach Ancano, we'll have to hope that there's a way to stop all this.
Skyrim.esm|0596F4: You've come for me, have you?
Skyrim.esm|0596F5: You're the one with the staff, my boy.
Skyrim.esm|0596F6: You're the one with the staff, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|0596F7: Come then. See what I can do now.
Skyrim.esm|0596F8: You think I don't know what you're up to? You think I can't destroy you?
Skyrim.esm|059A81: You, too, huh? I'd much rather a straight up fight than creeping around a place like this.
Skyrim.esm|059A82: Good to see you, my friend! I knew you'd make a proper Legionnaire. I'm glad you'll be with me in there. I don't like the looks of this place... and I'm not talking about the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|059A83: These old ruins... They don't want us here, the ancient ones. Better left alone.
Skyrim.esm|059A84: But never mind all that. We're the Emperor's soldiers. We'll do our duty no matter what stands in our way, right?
Skyrim.esm|059AD6: Your family's success burdens you, does it?
Skyrim.esm|059AD7: Remember that old rusty sword I had? Damn thing must have had over a hundred chips in it.
Skyrim.esm|059AE6: Well, if you had learned to swing it correctly. A sword is an extension of the arm, not a meat cleaver.
Skyrim.esm|059AE7: Sometimes I miss the Legion, back when the Empire stood up for Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|059AEA: Hello, my friend. Come to visit the forge?
Skyrim.esm|059AF4: I was about to say how impressed I was that you always managed to put it back together.
Skyrim.esm|059AF9: I grow tired of counting coin all day. Put steel in my hand, not gold.
Skyrim.esm|059AFA: It's fine. Much better than the rickety tools I had in the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|059AFB: No politics, Thongvor. I told you before.
Skyrim.esm|059EE1: These old ruins... My father always told me to stay away from them. Good advice, I'd say.
Skyrim.esm|059EE2: You, too, huh? I'd take a straight fight with the whole Legion over creeping around a place like this.
Skyrim.esm|059EE3: But never mind all that. We've got a job to do, and nothing's going to stand in our way.
Skyrim.esm|059EE4: The Stormcloaks are here in force. Stick close and follow orders so we don't stir up the whole place against us.
Skyrim.esm|059EE5: The Imperials are here in force. Stick together.
Skyrim.esm|059EE6: I'm glad I'll have you beside me in there. This place chills my blood, and I don't care who knows it.
Skyrim.esm|059EE7: You must be the new recruit. Welcome to the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|059EE8: I don't know what we're doing here, but orders are orders.
Skyrim.esm|059EE9: I keep thinking I see something moving in the shadows...
Skyrim.esm|059EEA: I hope we don't run into anything worse than Stormcloaks in here.
Skyrim.esm|059EEB: It's the waiting that gets me.
Skyrim.esm|059EEC: Nobody allowed in. Ulfric Stormcloak's orders. Consider yourself warned.
Skyrim.esm|059EED: You must be the new unblooded. Welcome to the fight, brother.
Skyrim.esm|059EEE: You must be the new unblooded. Welcome to the fight, sister.
Skyrim.esm|059EEF: I don't know what we're doing here. Ulfric must have his reasons.
Skyrim.esm|059EF0: I keep thinking I see something moving in the shadows...
Skyrim.esm|059EF1: I hope we don't run into anything worse than Imperials in here.
Skyrim.esm|059EF3: Don't interfere with Imperial Legion business. Now move along, unless you want three inches of steel in your gut.
Skyrim.esm|059F2C: Only a few things came today. Cedran said he'd drop it off later.
Skyrim.esm|059F2D: Just keep at it, or Mulush will beat you.
Skyrim.esm|059F32: Cedran said he'd... what am I paying you for? Next time, you bring the shipment in yourself.
Skyrim.esm|059F33: I don't want any of you thieving workers even thinking about stealing any silver.
Skyrim.esm|059F37: What do I pay you for, to stand around all day? You're supposed to be bringing goods to the store.
Skyrim.esm|059F38: Careful. You spill one drop of silver, I'm taking it out of your hide.
Skyrim.esm|059F3B: If I see you sneaking off with even one speck of silver, I'll break your leg and throw you into the river.
Skyrim.esm|059F3E: I'm being as careful as I can.
Skyrim.esm|059F46: Did you just talk back to me? I'm not paid to listen. I'm paid to keep you working. Now work.
Skyrim.esm|059F48: There are no goods to bring to the store. That's what I'm saying.
Skyrim.esm|059F4B: Careful of the molten metal. That gets on you, kiss your fingers goodbye.
Skyrim.esm|059F4E: Mulush, we need a break. The heat from the smelter is too much.
Skyrim.esm|059F51: I know what I'm doing. Stop telling me.
Skyrim.esm|059F54: Are you complaining? Work. If you have strength to whine, you have strength to smelt.
Skyrim.esm|059F59: Where are my goods? Did the shipment come in?
Skyrim.esm|059F5A: Divines, I'm tired. This is too much work.
Skyrim.esm|059F5B: I could smelt metal in half the time as you weak-willed pink-skins. Do I have to do everything?
Skyrim.esm|05A091: What about you? Are you ready to transact the Oath with Nocturnal?
Skyrim.esm|05A092: What sort of arrangement? I need to know the terms.
Skyrim.esm|05A0AD: Aye, there's always a catch. But at this point, I suppose there isn't much to lose. If it means the end of Mercer Frey, you can count me in.
Skyrim.esm|05A0B8: I don't like the look of this place.
Skyrim.esm|05A0C0: Good. After I open the gate, please stand on the western circle.
Skyrim.esm|05A0C5: Okay, lass. We've got these getups on... now what?
Skyrim.esm|05A0CC: In return, you'll be required to defend the Twilight Sepulcher and everything within when the need arises. More importantly, upon your death, your spirit will be bound to the Twilight Sepulcher as one of its guardians.
Skyrim.esm|05A0CF: By transacting the Oath with Nocturnal, you're entering into a business deal. You'll be provided all of the power and knowledge befitting a Nightingale. You're free to use those powers as you see fit to further your own goals or the goals of the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|05A0D4: Beyond this gate is the first step in becoming a Nightingale.
Skyrim.esm|05A0D5: The terms are quite simple, Brynjolf. Nocturnal will allow you to become a Nightingale and use your abilities for whatever you wish. And in return, both in life and in death, you must serve as a guardian of the Twilight Sepulcher.
Skyrim.esm|05A0D6: Once the Oath has been struck, the terms are binding. Knowing this, are you ready to undergo the ceremony?
Skyrim.esm|05A187: By the gods... what was that?
Skyrim.esm|05A188: I don't know what he's doing, but he won't get away with it.
Skyrim.esm|05A189: Now's not the time. We need to put a stop to whatever it is that's going on.
Skyrim.esm|05A306: Be careful who you talk to.
Skyrim.esm|05A308: My children all grew up and left Dawnstar to make their fortune.
Skyrim.esm|05A309: Most of the guards in Dawnstar carry swords forged from his very smithy.
Skyrim.esm|05A30D: No Empire is going to take the Jarlship away from this Son of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|05A30E: Skyrim will not bow to a false king any longer. Ulfric is the true king.
Skyrim.esm|05A30F: The tenacity of the Imperial Legion is legendary. We need to be ready for them.
Skyrim.esm|05A310: Oh, it's just wonderful being Skald's servant. He's not demanding in the least.
Skyrim.esm|05A311: I had hoped life would be simpler here, but war seems to find a way of following me.
Skyrim.esm|05A409: Dear brother, I need you to steal into that chamber, and eavesdrop on their meeting. It'll be no use clinging to the shadows. They'll see you for sure. No, you need a hiding place. Somewhere they'd never think to look. Like inside the Night Mother's coffin.
Skyrim.esm|05A40F: Our order will not forget your deeds.
Skyrim.esm|05A410: You have done the world a great service.
Skyrim.esm|05A6A0: You... you were at Helgen. One of the prisoners. What makes you think I'm interested in someone like you for the Legion?
Skyrim.esm|05A6A3: Are you now? You were at Helgen, one of the prisoners. Although no one seems to know how you got mixed up in that affair. Normally, I would have welcomed someone like you -- resourceful, clearly no friend of the Empire. But I've had some disturbing reports of you... seen talking to General Tullius in Castle Dour, for example. So what do you have to say for yourself? How can I be sure you're not an Imperial spy?
Skyrim.esm|05A6A6: Well... you did show remarkable initiative. And you didn't have to bring the crown to me... But let me warn you - joining the Legion is for life. Any previous loyalties... I trust we understand each other. You can prove your new allegiance to me, by delivering an important message to the Jarl of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|05A6A9: You'd do well to get out of here before I change my mind and have you locked in irons...
Skyrim.esm|05A6AB: You... what? How could you possibly... Oh, so you're trying to play both sides, is that it? What do you really want?
Skyrim.esm|05A6B1: You're not short of guts, are you? What's your game? Why would Tullius waste resources getting the crown only to give it to me? Poisoned perhaps? No. He's not that cunning. Alright. I believe you. You aren't the first to turn away from the Empire in disgust. But remember - you're one of us now, forever. Whatever Imperial loyalties you may have had... that's all in the past, forgotten. Now, to prove yourself to me, I have a task for you. Take my axe, and deliver it to the Jarl of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|05A6B7: The Imperials wouldn't have just let you walk away. Remember that.
Skyrim.esm|05A6B9: So you as good as admit you're working for the Imperials. Yet you walk in here, and ask to join me. What are you playing at?
Skyrim.esm|05A864: I suppose. But what about the old man? Son killed, family name ruined... and he acts as if nothing even happened. Think he's even fit for duty?
Skyrim.esm|05A865: I'm going to pretend I didn't hear that. Commander Maro is the best the Penitus Oculatus has ever produced. You should be half the man he is.
Skyrim.esm|05A866: You misunderstand. It's just... I feel for him, is all. To carry a weight like that. It's got to take its toll. That happened to me, I'd be a wreck.
Skyrim.esm|05A867: This city is crawling with Imperial Legion, and you know what? I'm still nervous.
Skyrim.esm|05A868: All these damn Stormcloaks. They're making me nervous. Never in a million years did I imagine they'd gain control of Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|05A86B: Lazy and useless, the lot of them. They can't even keep the Stormcloaks in line. No way I trust them with the Emperor's life.
Skyrim.esm|05A86C: I'm all for diplomacy, but the sooner the Emperor deals with this civil war mess, the sooner we can get back to Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|05A86D: You worry too much. The Emperor's never been safer. You think assassination attempts are planned overnight? We discovered the plot. End of threat.
Skyrim.esm|05AE8B: I'm afraid so. There's a problem with one of the Jarls here in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|05AE8C: Well, that's good to know. I suggest you tell your people at the College to keep a tighter leash on their mages.
Skyrim.esm|05AE8D: We have, of course, severed all ties with the mage in question, but the Jarl isn't appeased. So, the mage needs to be "taken care of," if you understand me.
Skyrim.esm|05AE8E: Ah, so they've finally sent someone to deal with that lunatic. Well, is it taken care of?
Skyrim.esm|05AE8F: He's been complaining of a member of the College running amok in his hold.
Skyrim.esm|05AE90: She's been complaining of a member of the College running amok in her hold.
Skyrim.esm|05AE9D: Well, yes. But the Key isn't only restricted to physical barriers. All of us possess untapped abilities; the potential to wield great power, securely sealed within our minds. Once you realize the Key can access these traits, the potential becomes limitless.
Skyrim.esm|05AE9E: Now that you've transacted the Oath, it's time to reveal the final piece of the puzzle to you; Mercer's true crime.
Skyrim.esm|05AE9F: Very true. In our line of work, it's quite rare we set out to return a stolen item to its rightful owner.
Skyrim.esm|05AEA3: I suggest you talk to Karliah first. She has some news that'll turn your stomach.
Skyrim.esm|05AEA4: Mercer was able to unlock the Guild's vault without two keys because of what he stole from the Twilight Sepulcher... the Skeleton Key. By doing this, he's compromised our ties to Nocturnal and in essence, caused our luck to run dry.
Skyrim.esm|05AEB7: Even if these carvings tell us where the crown is, I'm sure we're going to have to find a way through this door.
Skyrim.esm|05AEB8: Careful! I wouldn't do that again unless you know for sure it's going to open.
Skyrim.esm|05AEB9: And this must be the Hall of Stories.
Skyrim.esm|05AEBA: One thing at a time soldier. Focus on our primary mission. We're searching for the crown.
Skyrim.esm|05AEBB: Maybe there's something on the claw itself that could help us.
Skyrim.esm|05AEBC: See what you can figure out auxillary, I'm going to check out these carvings over here. Let me know if you find something.
Skyrim.esm|05AEBE: Oh... I've heard of this. These walls are supposed to show the history of the ancients who built this place.
Skyrim.esm|05AEBF: Looks like we weren't the first ones here, either.
Skyrim.esm|05AEC0: Hmm... what is that? Some kind of stone claw? I wonder what it's used for.
Skyrim.esm|05AEC1: Too bad we can't read these carvings.  Who knows what secrets we'd uncover?
Skyrim.esm|05AEC2: Looks like the door will open if we turn these rings the right way.
Skyrim.esm|05AFE3: I do. Sadly, I'm one of the few traditional Saxhleel Jewelers that remains in Tamriel; it's becoming a lost art. Only trouble is gathering materials has become hazardous. The roads to the mines and to the other cities are thick with bandits... and worse. Would you be interested in gathering some materials for me? I'd certainly be happy to compensate you.
Skyrim.esm|05AFFE: The job pays well, so I hope I can make arrangements soon. We could really use the gold.
Skyrim.esm|05AFFF: Good, these will make wonderful settings for my rings.
Skyrim.esm|05B000: No, I think I have enough here to get started. Here, let me give you this. It's the least I can do for all your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|05B001: Madesi was actually my original Saxhleel name. Unlike others, I decided to embrace tradition and keep it that way.
Skyrim.esm|05B002: You would? Oh, that would be wonderful. Speak to Filnjar in Shor's Stone. He's got some sort of ore sample he wants us to identify and was willing to pay us well for it.
Skyrim.esm|05B04D: Forgive me. It's a term I haven't used in some time. While most in Tamriel call my kind Argonian, we prefer the term Saxhleel.
Skyrim.esm|05B062: Forgive me. It's a term I haven't used in some time. While most in Tamriel would call us Argonian, we prefer the term Saxhleel.
Skyrim.esm|05B063: Thank you. I'll be sure to get Elgrim to examine this as soon as his... busy schedule allows. Here, I had him make you a few things for all that travel.
Skyrim.esm|05B0CC: Perfect. Melting this down will give me plenty of gold for those necklaces I started.
Skyrim.esm|05B0D4: Oh! No, I didn't mean it that way. Elgrim can barely put his on his own shoes let alone have the energy to pursue a young girl. He's been gushing about how talented Ingun is at alchemy. He'd never say it to her face; that's not his way. I just think Elgrim's taken it upon himself to mentor the young thing so he focuses a lot of attention on her.
Skyrim.esm|05B0D5: Redbelly is supposed to be nothing but an iron mine. Been working it for years. Then right before the spiders had moved in, we found that chunk of ore. Never seen anything like it. I want to know what I'm dealing with before I start tearing it out of the ground.
Skyrim.esm|05B17F: I know how to mix a few basic potions, but I'm nowhere near as skilled as a master alchemist. I've picked up a few tricks by reading the tomes scattered all over the shop, but I don't have the knack to pull half of the formulas off.
Skyrim.esm|05B1D0: I'm seeking a small list of items to complete my next creation: two flawless sapphires, a mammoth tusk and a chunk of gold ore.
Skyrim.esm|05B1D1: Have you managed to make it out to Shor's Stone?
Skyrim.esm|05B1D7: I guess we'd better try this way.
Skyrim.esm|05B1DA: We'd better push on. The rest of them will have to find another way out.
Skyrim.esm|05B1E7: Go ahead. I'll follow your lead and watch your back.
Skyrim.esm|05B1E8: This place has seen better days, but it's not my fault.
Skyrim.esm|05B1EC: Hold up. There's a bear just ahead. See her?
Skyrim.esm|05B1F0: Better to stick together down here.
Skyrim.esm|05B1F5: No going back that way, now.
Skyrim.esm|05B1F6: That thing was a dragon. No doubt. Just like the children's stories and the legends. The harbingers of the End Times.
Skyrim.esm|05B1F8: I'd rather not tangle with her right now. Let's try to sneak by. Just take it nice and slow, and watch where you step.
Skyrim.esm|05B200: That looks like the way out! I knew we'd make it!
Skyrim.esm|05B206: The ivory from this tusk will be perfect for the inlay on my amulets.
Skyrim.esm|05B211: When Elgrim said we were setting up shop down here, I told him he would catch his death. The dirt, the moisture... it's disgusting. Who wants to spend their life living like a skeever? But that old bellyacher just fed me some nonsense about light ruining his mixtures and being close to the water... agh.
Skyrim.esm|05B212: It's about time; I sent them a letter weeks ago. That Elgrim... he'd forget his own pants if his wife didn't help him dress. Here you go. Tell Hafjorg that she can chip off whatever she thinks is a fair trade for their time.
Skyrim.esm|05B248: Did you ever have to fight off bandits, Frida? Like in your stories?
Skyrim.esm|05B24A: Leigelf is a good man, and I'm not running out on him now.
Skyrim.esm|05B250: You mean Hildolf the Skull-Splitter? Remember when we rounded up the townspeople and dragged him out of that cave?
Skyrim.esm|05B251: Why, I once fought alongside your father when we were both younger. Saved him from getting his fool head sliced off.
Skyrim.esm|05B253: Oh, got a bit of interest in your boss do you?
Skyrim.esm|05B256: Those were the days. I'd never seen my husband fight so bravely before.
Skyrim.esm|05B25A: Aye, to your husband. Divines bless his memory.
Skyrim.esm|05B25D: No, not that it's any of your business.
Skyrim.esm|05B260: Oh, he was young, and handsome. I had half a mind to go after him, myself, before I met my husband.
Skyrim.esm|05B261: Oh, dearie, it was so long ago. But I do remember the celebration we had afterwards. Mead never tasted so good.
Skyrim.esm|05B262: Did I ever tell you about the day when Skald the Elder was Skald the Younger?
Skyrim.esm|05B264: Thoring, do you remember the time when that wolf pack came clamoring into town?
Skyrim.esm|05B265: Tough day at the mines. I swear the ore is getting harder to dig.
Skyrim.esm|05B26A: I can't imagine Skald ever being young.
Skyrim.esm|05B26D: How could I forget? That old alpha male almost took off my leg.
Skyrim.esm|05B273: Never did find out why they came sprawling into town like that.
Skyrim.esm|05B275: I was a beauty in my day, dearie. Don't act so surprised.
Skyrim.esm|05B276: That's why I come here to talk to you. You're all heart.
Skyrim.esm|05B279: Animals in The Pale don't need a reason to attack Dawnstar. It's just their nature.
Skyrim.esm|05B27A: Dawnstar has always held its own against the bandits and wild animals of The Pale.
Skyrim.esm|05B27B: You should quit working in the eastern mine. Leigelf's no good to work for.
Skyrim.esm|05B27E: Now, who was it that kidnapped the jarl's son 20 years ago?
Skyrim.esm|05B2B3: I swear, if I wasn't around, Elgrim would forget to eat his meals. How we make any coin at all is beyond me. In fact, I need to arrange for someone to pick up an ore sample for me in Shor's Stone... goodness knows Elgrim won't lift a finger to do it.
Skyrim.esm|05B2DC: Be careful, the road to Shor's Stone could be dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|05B2DD: Don't forget us if you need supplies for your journey.
Skyrim.esm|05B3CA: It's always good to see someone I can count on.
Skyrim.esm|05B3CB: The general expects so much with so little.
Skyrim.esm|05B3CC: The sooner we end this rebellion, the sooner I'll be able to sleep at night.
Skyrim.esm|05B3CD: Ulfric... what have you done my old friend?
Skyrim.esm|05B3CE: Stormcloaks... even the name of their rebellion stinks of Ulfric's vanity. He wants to be king. That's all this is about. One man's ambition.
Skyrim.esm|05B3CF: I don't fight for the glory of the Empire, nor do I glory in the death of my kinsmen. I fight to bring peace and order to my homeland.
Skyrim.esm|05B3D0: Damnit! Why can't Ulfric see he's tearing Skyrim to pieces?
Skyrim.esm|05B3D1: Sometimes I wonder if the gods have abandoned us... as we have abandoned them.
Skyrim.esm|05B3D2: What I do, I do for Skyrim and her people.
Skyrim.esm|05B3D3: When we destroy the rebellion, perhaps the Thalmor will call off their damned inquisitions. It's just making things worse.
Skyrim.esm|05B3D4: Sometimes I miss being out there in the middle of the action.
Skyrim.esm|05B406: Always good to see someone I can trust.
Skyrim.esm|05B407: Sometimes I wonder if Ulfric understands what I'm dealing with out here.
Skyrim.esm|05B408: That godless Emperor can shove his damn treaty into the steaming nether-reaches of my you-know-where.
Skyrim.esm|05B415: My hatred for the Empire is exceeded only by my hatred for their witch-elf puppet-masters.
Skyrim.esm|05B416: What will Skyrim have become if her children submitted to tyranny?
Skyrim.esm|05B418: The Empire would have us become their slaves as they have become slaves to the Thalmor. I will not live as a slave, nor will I die as one.
Skyrim.esm|05B41A: The Empire is weak. That is why we fight them. Skyrim must be ruled by the strong.
Skyrim.esm|05B41B: Some say Ulfric desires to be king and nothing more... have they not seen that his anger floats on a sea of tears?
Skyrim.esm|05B41C: Countless dead haunt countless sleepless nights... when Skyrim becomes whole again, perhaps so will I.
Skyrim.esm|05B41D: When we have reclaimed our homeland from the Imperials, we will take the fight to the Thalmor. They are the true enemy. Make no mistake.
Skyrim.esm|05B41E: If there was someone else Ulfric could trust with all this planning nonsense, I would be out there breaking heads with the rest of you.
Skyrim.esm|05B490: Buy it? This? An Elder Council amulet? Oh yes. Oh yes, indeed. Uh, wait just one moment... Here... is a letter of credit. Usable, by Astrid only, for any service or item I can provide. As per our standard arrangement. You bring that back to your lovely mistress. With my regards.
Skyrim.esm|05B491: The Elder Council... Oh, now that explains quite a bit. Motierre, you naughty, naughty boy. Hiring the Dark Brotherhood to help you rise beyond your station. Delicious. Was Mallory willing to buy the amulet?
Skyrim.esm|05B492: Let's see... Where oh where did you get this? No, don't answer - I don't want to know. This is an amulet of the Emperor's Elder Council. Specially crafted for each member. Worth a small fortune. Ain't somethin' you'd give up lightly. Look, it ain't my business to tell the Dark Brotherhood its business, but if you killed a member of the Elder Council, you'd better belie-
Skyrim.esm|05B493: Good, you're back. Well, what did Mallory have to say? Is the amulet authentic?
Skyrim.esm|05B494: Oh. Oh, I see. Well, you're makin' friends all over, ain't ya? So, uh... how is Astrid doin' these days? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up. Ah, but business! Of course. What kind of business?
Skyrim.esm|05B495: Oh. Oh, I see. Well now, how is Astrid doin' these days, uh? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up. Ah, but we can discuss that later, yeah? What does the Brotherhood need?
Skyrim.esm|05B496: Somethin' ya need? Anything for the guild.
Skyrim.esm|05B497: Ah, now you must be lost. Best ya scurry off while you're able. The Ratway, well, it has a habit of swallowin' up the uninvited.
Skyrim.esm|05B5C4: Poor Cicero. Dear Cicero. Such a humble servant. But he will never hear my voice. For he is not the Listener.
Skyrim.esm|05BE45: I knew I could count on you.
Skyrim.esm|05BE47: We all have faith in you.
Skyrim.esm|05BEBB: Good. And remember - keep the gods, and they'll keep you.
Skyrim.esm|05BEBC: Excellent. Put the fear of the Nine into those motherless dogs.
Skyrim.esm|05BEBD: That's what I wanted to hear. Good luck out there.
Skyrim.esm|05BEC4: We do this for the good of Skyrim and her people. Never forget that.
Skyrim.esm|05BF27: Oh, Rexus is no mere servant. He has been with the Motierre family since I was a child. I trust the man with my life.
Skyrim.esm|05BF28: I have vowed to serve Amaund Motierre until my dying breath. Best remember that.
Skyrim.esm|05BF34: Good. Make it it happen, soldier.
Skyrim.esm|05BF36: The whole of Whiterun is ripe with paranoia and tensions. The Jarl's court is right to fear the power I hold behind this door. The Jarl trusts few, and they will be his undoing. The dark child knows of what I speak. Let him guide your path.
Skyrim.esm|05BF37: Show the rebels the futility of their cause.
Skyrim.esm|05BF38: As long as the rebels are willing to die fighting the Empire, we must be willing to oblige them.
Skyrim.esm|05BF39: Peace may yet return to Skyrim. But until then, we fight.
Skyrim.esm|05C2FC: I hope you're just playing dumb. I'm the one who left the note in Ustengrav.
Skyrim.esm|05C2FD: Yes. We're very old enemies. And if my suspicions are correct, they might have something to do with the dragons returning. But that isn't important right now. What is important is that you might be Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|05C2FF: Good. The whole point of being in hiding is to appear to be someone you're not.
Skyrim.esm|05C302: Speak, if you have aught to say worth the listening.
Skyrim.esm|05C61E: Yes. Congratulations, you are now a full-fledged member of the Bards College. Due to your adventurous nature several of the college professors have some things they wish you to do. What's more, Elisif has declared the Burning of King Olaf should become a weekly event. And finally, there's the matter of the Patronage that Elisif wanted me to give you.
Skyrim.esm|05C61F: Not many come to see me. What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|05C620: I know many things. I have studied things beyond the reach of most humans, traveled the Oblivion planes, seen things one should not see. I have met Daedra and Dwemer and everything in between and I know enough to see a vampire where others would see a man.
Skyrim.esm|05C621: Great. Looks like you'll be one of us soon.  We'll gather outside the College for the Burning of King Olaf.
Skyrim.esm|05C622: Ah the bard-to-be. Did Viarmo send you?
Skyrim.esm|05C81D: Could you... could you give her this letter, and say it's from Sven? I think I've matched that Nord's lack of cleverness perfectly.
Skyrim.esm|05C81E: I appreciate your help. Please, take this. Some gold I've saved up from working at the mill.
Skyrim.esm|05C81F: What is that blowhard up to now? So, he thinks he can turn Camilla against me? Two can play at this. Here. Give this letter to Camilla and tell her it's from Sven. Let's see what she thinks of him after reading that.
Skyrim.esm|05C820: You may be right. Camilla may see Faendal as little more than a friend, but why take chances? Here. Give Camilla this letter. It's full of venomous nonsense. Tell her it's from Faendal.
Skyrim.esm|05C822: I've been thinking... maybe Camilla needs a little help seeing Sven for what he is.
Skyrim.esm|05C823: Camilla Valerius knows I'm the best man in Riverwood. That elf is kidding himself if he thinks she would choose him over me. I've seen him sneaking over to the Riverwood Trader to speak with her when I'm not around. He's wasting his time.
Skyrim.esm|05C824: Is that sarcasm? I've heard better wisecracks from Orgnar. Still, you have a point. Camilla letting Faendal visit her isn't a good thing for me. Here, let me give you a particularly venomous letter. Say it's from Faendal. That should get Camilla to stop inviting the elf over.
Skyrim.esm|05C825: He's a bard, so he says. Occasionally he finds time to do his job here at the mill. Thinks his ballads and sonnets are going to convince Camilla Valerius to marry him. As if she would say "yes." An intelligent, beautiful woman like her wouldn't fall for that nonsense... I hope.
Skyrim.esm|05C826: I will be back at your house if you need me, Thane.
Skyrim.esm|05C829: Looks like you've already got someone with you.
Skyrim.esm|05C82D: This is your initiation into the coven. Go ahead. Have the first bite.
Skyrim.esm|05C82E: I don't need your jokes right now, but maybe you have a point. Maybe Camilla needs some help seeing Sven for what he is.
Skyrim.esm|05C82F: Tell me as soon as you've given Camilla the letter.
Skyrim.esm|05C831: That letter ought to convince Camilla to never speak to Faendal ever again.
Skyrim.esm|05C832: Faendal thinks he can woo Camilla Valerius away from me. She's already mine, I keep telling him.
Skyrim.esm|05C833: Another poem, I'll bet. He does know how to make a girl blush. What's this? If that oaf thinks all I'm going to do is stay in that filthy house of his and clean, I'll.... You can tell Sven that he already has a mother. I'm not speaking to him anymore.
Skyrim.esm|05C834: He what? What are you talking about? Why that jealous idiot. Thought he could just manipulate me into never seeing Sven again? Thank you for telling me the truth. You should speak to Sven. I'm sure he'll want to thank you, too, for defending his name.
Skyrim.esm|05C835: What is this? The elf playing at treachery? Shocking. As if I would write this dribble. How about we play a bit of turnabout with Faendal? Here. I had to use simple words, but this letter should convince Camilla it's from him.
Skyrim.esm|05C836: From Faendal? That's strange. He's never written to me before. What, what is this? Is this what he really thinks about me? Well, you can tell that... that long-eared ass not to come around the store anymore. He's not welcome.
Skyrim.esm|05C837: Now that's what I like to hear. Good work. I think you deserve some gold from my tips at the inn.
Skyrim.esm|05C838: As you will, my Thane. I will protect you with my life.
Skyrim.esm|05C83A: What are you talking about? What's in the letter? Oh my. He... he wanted me to think Faendal wrote this? Thank you for telling me the truth. Could you talk to Faendal, as well?  I'm sure he'll want to thank you for standing up for him.
Skyrim.esm|05C9D3: A dragon is attacking the Western Watchtower.
Skyrim.esm|05C9D4: Now what do you say? Shall we go kill us a dragon?
Skyrim.esm|05C9D5: That's a fair question. None of us have ever seen a dragon before, or expected to face one in battle.
Skyrim.esm|05C9D6: But we are honorbound to fight it, even if we fail. This dragon is threatening our homes... our families.
Skyrim.esm|05C9D7: But Housecarl... how can we fight a dragon?
Skyrim.esm|05C9D8: You heard right! I said a dragon!
Skyrim.esm|05C9DC: I don't much care where it came from or who sent it. What I do know is that it's made the mistake of attacking Whiterun!
Skyrim.esm|05C9FE: They are rare things, I have one I picked up during my research of the Oblivion realms but it is a precious item.
Skyrim.esm|05C9FF: I met several of your kind during my studies of life-extending magics. I even  considered becoming a vampire myself. In the end, vampirism would endanger my ward Agni which would defeat the intended purpose.
Skyrim.esm|05CA00: It requires a filled black soul gem. You will need to kill someone. When you have a gem and have filled it return to me and I will perform the ritual. I will bring life to your dead body, vampire.
Skyrim.esm|05CA01: They say Falion of Morthal has studied vampires, draugr and all matter of undead.
Skyrim.esm|05CA10: Very good. I hope this will allow us to maintain our reputation.
Skyrim.esm|05CA16: You've been suspended from the College for what you did. You'd better talk to Tolfdir if you want to get it sorted out.
Skyrim.esm|05CA17: I'm sorry, but you've been suspended from the College. I can't be seen talking to you right now.
Skyrim.esm|05CA18: I would hate to see our reputation further tarnished.
Skyrim.esm|05CBFC: "Because Solitude would not soon bend knee, Olaf would hurt them while his status accrued." "He sacked Winterhold in dragon form, and bent their minds to blame Solitude."
Skyrim.esm|05CC6C: Ah, Viarmo.  I assume you are here to petition for the reinstatement of the Burning of King Olaf Festival.
Skyrim.esm|05CC6D: I am, Jarl. I wish to present King Olaf's verse from the Poetic Edda. Recovered this very day from the Bards Tomb.
Skyrim.esm|05CC6E: Ah, you mentioned something that would convince us the festival should take place but I didn't expect King Olaf's lost verse. Please proceed.
Skyrim.esm|05CCB5: Well done. They never knew what hit them.
Skyrim.esm|05CCB7: You have your orders. Get moving.
Skyrim.esm|05CCB8: We had the advantage of surprise this time, but don't expect it to last. Let's move out.
Skyrim.esm|05CCB9: Take that crown back to Solitude, soldier.
Skyrim.esm|05CCBA: Get the crown already. We don't need to hang around here any longer than necessary.
Skyrim.esm|05CCBB: The crown should be around here somewhere. Spread out, and keep your eyes open.
Skyrim.esm|05CDEE: Enough, Maramal. We've all heard of the dragons and their return. There's no need to use them as an excuse to harass our customers.
Skyrim.esm|05CE1E: Put down your flagons filled with your vile liquids and embrace the teachings of the handmaiden of Kyne.
Skyrim.esm|05CE1F: Very well, Talen. I'll remove myself from this den of iniquity.
Skyrim.esm|05CE20: No, no... Maramal... we talked about this. Talen...
Skyrim.esm|05CE21: People of Riften, heed my words. The return of the dragons is not mere coincidence.
Skyrim.esm|05CE22: We're not kicking you out, just keep the sermons at the temple and let us all sin in peace.
Skyrim.esm|05CE23: Keerava, certainly we can come to some sort of an understanding. These people must be made aware of the chaos they've sown.
Skyrim.esm|05CE24: This is one of the signs. The signs that Lady Mara is displeased with your constant inebriation.
Skyrim.esm|05CE8F: Quick, I can cut you loose inside the keep.
Skyrim.esm|05D2E2: She what? Oh, I should've known she'd try it again! Thanks for warning me. I'll see to it this is taken care of.
Skyrim.esm|05D2E3: Well, I suppose I could use a hand with something, yes. Faralda's working on something, and I want to know what it is. I want her notes. You think you could get them for me, without her knowing?
Skyrim.esm|05D2E4: I'm still waiting for those notes, if you can get them.
Skyrim.esm|05D2E5: Yes, yes it is. You have a problem with that? Look, either you get the notes or I'll figure out another way to do it. Just come find me if you can get your hands on them.
Skyrim.esm|05D2E6: Good, good. She didn't see you take them? Al right, this should be enough to figure out what she's up to. Then I can make sure it doesn't work. Good work. Here, take this, and keep quiet.
Skyrim.esm|05D2E7: I'll throw some money at you. Good enough? Just get the notes and bring them to me. If you can't do it, I'll find some other way to manage it.
Skyrim.esm|05D2FE: You're the one that got out of Helgen, aren't you? I could use anyone who's tangled with a dragon before. Where do you think you'll be the most help - scouting ahead or supporting the rest of my men?
Skyrim.esm|05D2FF: Well? You going to scout ahead or travel with me?
Skyrim.esm|05D31E: No, and I'm not expecting any.
Skyrim.esm|05D31F: J'zargo continues to struggle with wards. You have time to practice?
Skyrim.esm|05D320: My family didn't really approve of me coming here at all.
Skyrim.esm|05D321: No, not really. Couriers don't seem to like delivering letters here.
Skyrim.esm|05D323: Have you heard from your family recently?
Skyrim.esm|05D324: Still no word from your family?
Skyrim.esm|05D325: J'zargo understands. You hesitate because you know J'zargo will be better than you. Very well.
Skyrim.esm|05D326: Maybe later. I'm sort of busy now.
Skyrim.esm|05D327: Oh. Well, that's... awkward. Sorry to have brought it up.
Skyrim.esm|05D328: Not at all. It means I'm not being hounded about my progress at the College.
Skyrim.esm|05D329: Really? That must be so nice. I came all the way to Skyrim just to get away from my family's expectations.
Skyrim.esm|05D474: Damn thieves. As if things weren't bad enough.
Skyrim.esm|05DB9B: I call upon Oblivion realms. The home of those who are not our ancestors. Answer my plea!
Skyrim.esm|05DB9C: Very well. Meet me at the summoning circle in the marsh at dawn. We shall banish the creature you have become.
Skyrim.esm|05DB9D: Stand in the center of the circle.
Skyrim.esm|05DB9E: As in death there is new life, in Oblivion there is a beginning for that which has ended.
Skyrim.esm|05DBA0: I would not sell it to just anyone but I'll make an exception for you. The cost to replace the gem is quite high.
Skyrim.esm|05DBA1: You are human again. Like the rest of us.
Skyrim.esm|05DBA3: I call forth that power! Accept the soul that we offer!
Skyrim.esm|05DD43: That may be true, but I ordered Irileth to bring back proof. You'd best report back to her at once.
Skyrim.esm|05DD44: There's no refusing a summons from the Greybeards. If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your power.
Skyrim.esm|05DD45: So what happened at the watchtower? Was the dragon there?
Skyrim.esm|05DD46: Help Irileth kill this dragon before it can attack Whiterun. There's no time to lose.
Skyrim.esm|05DD4A: You sure took care of that dragon. Dragonborn or not, that's good enough for me.
Skyrim.esm|05DD4B: I'm proud to be your battle-brother, Dragonborn. Whiterun doesn't need to fear with you on our side.
Skyrim.esm|05DD4C: If you really are Dragonborn, then Skyrim needs you. Go to High Hrothgar and talk to the Greybeards.
Skyrim.esm|05DD4E: Good enough. We'll meet up near the watchtower. Don't take any chances until we get there.
Skyrim.esm|05DD4F: That was the hairiest fight I've ever been in, and I've been in more than a few. I don't know about this Dragonborn business, but I'm sure glad you're with us. You better get back to Whiterun right away. Jarl Balgruuf will want to know what happened here.
Skyrim.esm|05DD50: I'm taking command here for the moment. You head back to Whiterun and let the Jarl know what happened.
Skyrim.esm|05DD51: We'd better go make sure that bastard is really dead. Collect us a trophy or something to bring back to Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|05DD52: Scatter and get behind cover! We need to hurt it, somehow! If you've got a bow or spells, now would be a good time to use them!
Skyrim.esm|05DD97: We stand at the tomb of Hevnoraak, who has been dead for many generations. I fear, however, that his return may be at hand.
Skyrim.esm|05DD98: You would do this? Three vessels in the tomb below hold the power to vanquish Hevnoraak. Before anything else, we need those. Bring them to me - but be careful, mortal. They are guarded by Hevnoraak's minions. They will stop at nothing to see their master return.
Skyrim.esm|05DD99: Leave, stranger. Evil stirs in this place. I fear for the security of the very land if it should break free.
Skyrim.esm|05DD9B: You've found the vessels. I worried that I had sent you to your death.
Skyrim.esm|05DD9D: I have been holding him here. While he regains strength, however, I am fading. By now I can barely contain his power, much less defeat him if he awakens. But with the help of a living champion I may be able to succeed.
Skyrim.esm|05DDB0: I'm just as puzzled as you, lass.
Skyrim.esm|05DDFD: Aye, there will be blood today. I can promise you that.
Skyrim.esm|05DDFE: Crime is one thing, murder is another. Mercer will answer for this.
Skyrim.esm|05DDFF: I'd just assume die before I'd let Mercer get those gems.
Skyrim.esm|05DE00: Do the Falmer also have no sense of smell? This place reeks!
Skyrim.esm|05DE01: The Eyes would go a long way in helping get the Guild back on its feet.
Skyrim.esm|05DE02: I can't wait to get my hands around that bastard's neck.
Skyrim.esm|05DE03: By Talos, Mercer has a lot to answer for.
Skyrim.esm|05DE04: With any luck, this will be Mercer's final day alive.
Skyrim.esm|05DE05: This place, it's like a maze. How did the dwarves find their way around?
Skyrim.esm|05DE06: This is just like old times.
Skyrim.esm|05DE07: Keep as quiet as you can. The Falmer may be blind, but they can still hear us.
Skyrim.esm|05DE08: If Mercer leaves here with the Eyes, we'll never see him again.
Skyrim.esm|05DE09: We have to get the Key back.
Skyrim.esm|05DE0A: Mercer's here. I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|05DE0B: Be careful, there's no telling what lurks within these walls.
Skyrim.esm|05DE0D: There's little time for talk, what do you want?
Skyrim.esm|05DE0E: Yes, yes, what is it now?
Skyrim.esm|05DE0F: You're quite noisy for someone who claims to be a thief.
Skyrim.esm|05DE12: If you see Karliah, don't hesitate. Kill her.
Skyrim.esm|05DE13: That's enough of this unnecessary chatting, we need to keep going.
Skyrim.esm|05DE14: Let's get going. I want her head on my mantle.
Skyrim.esm|05DE15: Have your weapon at the ready. I'm certain Karliah knows we're here.
Skyrim.esm|05DE16: Keep your eyes open and your mouth shut.
Skyrim.esm|05E2C0: As always. The Nords don't ever stop wanting things enchanted.
Skyrim.esm|05E2C1: Why, because I'm a Dunmer? We didn't all grow up in dwarven ruins, you know.
Skyrim.esm|05E2C2: Perhaps, but it just means more work for me.
Skyrim.esm|05E2C3: I've had yet another complaint. Please stop barging in on experiments in progress.
Skyrim.esm|05E2C4: Do you think you might, err, have a moment to look at something for me?
Skyrim.esm|05E2C5: Yes, I just thought perhaps you'd have, err, come across something like this before.
Skyrim.esm|05E2C7: Don't even talk to me, Gane.
Skyrim.esm|05E2CA: Well, then. Fine. Have a nice day.
Skyrim.esm|05E2CC: At least it's more money for the College.
Skyrim.esm|05E2CD: My dear, I am merely observing the proceedings.
Skyrim.esm|05E2CE: Some days I marvel that you haven't managed to blow yourself up.
Skyrim.esm|05E2CF: Well then "observe" from a greater distance. You're making people uncomfortable.
Skyrim.esm|05E2D0: If this is another dwarven trinket, I keep telling you I'm not familiar with them.
Skyrim.esm|05E2D2: What are you up to, Gane? Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|05E2D3: Why should I want anything? Why can't I simply be sociable with my colleagues?
Skyrim.esm|05E2D5: Well, err, yes. Sorry to have troubled you.
Skyrim.esm|05E2F6: No, my lord. It was just circling overhead when I left. I never ran so fast in my life... I thought it would come after me for sure.
Skyrim.esm|05E2F7: I've already ordered my men to muster near the main gate.
Skyrim.esm|05E2F8: One last thing, Irileth. This isn't a death or glory mission. I need to know what we're dealing with.
Skyrim.esm|05E2FB: Irileth, you'd better gather some guardsmen and get down there.
Skyrim.esm|05E2FD: Good work, son. We'll take it from here. Head down to the barracks for some food and rest. You've earned it.
Skyrim.esm|05E963: You think so? Let's see who the real woman is here.
Skyrim.esm|05E98F: What news? Why isn't Fjola with you?
Skyrim.esm|05E9AA: I hope my son has found some nice, well-mannered girl to settle down with.
Skyrim.esm|05E9AB: Damn soldiers always trample my crops.
Skyrim.esm|05E9AC: My husband just gets cranky when he doesn't get enough sleep.
Skyrim.esm|05E9AD: I've lived through rebellion, Forsworn, undead, witches, wizards, hagravens, and marriage.
Skyrim.esm|05E9AE: We're getting back to work as best we can.
Skyrim.esm|05E9B0: Any day now more draugr are going to come out of that crypt.
Skyrim.esm|05EB13: This is the bridge out of town. The path up the mountain to the northwest leads to Bleak Falls Barrow. I guess I should get back to my brother. He'll throw a fit if I take too long. Such a child....
Skyrim.esm|05EDA1: I... I'm sorry Haelga, I can't. Not yet.
Skyrim.esm|05EDA2: I'll pay you back, if it means that much.
Skyrim.esm|05EDA9: I'm telling you, Vulwulf, it's the investment you've been waiting for.
Skyrim.esm|05EDAA: That old place? I could care less about it. Its you I want, Bolli.
Skyrim.esm|05EDAB: The only reason you have the gold for your "business" is because I gave it to you.
Skyrim.esm|05EDC0: If you want to go out to Whiterun and burn that meadery down, go right ahead. And when the guard traces it back here, and Maven is implicated, I'll be sure to tell her it was your idea.
Skyrim.esm|05EE35: The Dragonborn is said to be uniquely gifted in the Voice - the ability to focus your vital essence into a Thu'um, or Shout. The Greybeards are masters of the Way of the Voice. If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your gift.
Skyrim.esm|05EE36: There's no question about that. It was a mighty deed. You've earned a place of honor among the heroes of Whiterun. But there must be more to it than that. Did something... strange... happen when the dragon died?
Skyrim.esm|05EE37: I knew I could count on Irileth. But there must be more to it than that.
Skyrim.esm|05EE38: So it's true. The Greybeards really were summoning you.
Skyrim.esm|05EE39: We were just talking about you. My brother needs a word with you.
Skyrim.esm|05EE3B: Dragonborn? What do you know about the Dragonborn?
Skyrim.esm|05EE3D: You heard the summons. What else could it mean?
Skyrim.esm|05EE3E: Not just the men. The Greybeards seem to think the same thing.
Skyrim.esm|05EE3F: Didn't you hear the thundering sound as you returned to Whiterun? That was the voice of the Greybeards, summoning you to High Hrothgar! This hasn't happened in ... centuries, at least. Not since Tiber Septim himself was summoned when he was still Talos of Atmora!
Skyrim.esm|05EE40: Masters of the Way of the Voice. They live in seclusion high on the slopes of the Throat of the World.
Skyrim.esm|05EE42: I told you, I can't just pick up and leave. I have my wife, my business... not to mention you have the Bunkhouse.
Skyrim.esm|05EE43: Come on, think about it. She'd reward us!
Skyrim.esm|05EE44: When are you going to take me away from here Bolli?
Skyrim.esm|05EE45: If you think I'd would make a move like that without asking Maven, you're out of your mind. Doing that would be a fatal mistake.
Skyrim.esm|05EE46: Is it really me, or is it your wife? You need to make a choice Bolli. The honey or her.
Skyrim.esm|05EE47: Don't worry, I'll treat those shipments as if I owned them myself.
Skyrim.esm|05EE48: So, what do you think of my plan. You in or out?
Skyrim.esm|05EE49: Haelga! That's just it. I can't keep up with you. Your Dibellan techniques... they're exhausting.
Skyrim.esm|05EE4A: It's only because I've had an excellent teacher.
Skyrim.esm|05EE4B: Shor's Beard! You're right! I'll do it... provided you supervise the shipments personally.
Skyrim.esm|05EE4C: Aerin, you never cease to amaze me. You always see the good in everything.
Skyrim.esm|05EE4D: Oh, Bolli you silly man. You weren't saying that when you were licking that honey off my...
Skyrim.esm|05EE4E: Exactly. What better way to help the cause than to put weapons into the hands of young Nord warriors dying for their homeland?
Skyrim.esm|05EE4F: I think some of them do, Lady Mjoll. They're just too frightened to step up and do something about it like you are.
Skyrim.esm|05EE50: Haelga, we need to stop seeing each other.
Skyrim.esm|05EE51: Sometimes I'm not certain that the people of Riften want to be helped.
Skyrim.esm|05EE52: It's not about the coin, it's about honoring your legacy by picking up arms and keeping this land free. The sooner you remember that, the better.
Skyrim.esm|05EE53: I'm sorry you disapprove, father. But I've made up my mind. I'm not a warrior, I'm a businessman.
Skyrim.esm|05EE54: So I pay for the weapon shipments and you oversee their distribution to the Stormcloaks?
Skyrim.esm|05EFB0: If you want to die, so be it.
Skyrim.esm|05F1AB: Did he now? I can only hope. You still have the witches' heads? Excellent. Throw one of them into the fire. It will release their magic, for me at least.
Skyrim.esm|05F3F9: That's the spirit. I'll get all the details from Galmar when he gets back. Now then...
Skyrim.esm|05F3FA: The blood of our fallen heroes will make Skyrim free, never doubt it. Also, never doubt me or Galmar. Now then...
Skyrim.esm|05F3FB: Damn it. What were they doing there? Imperial spies are everywhere. Never forget that...  I trust you gave them a thrashing? Now then...
Skyrim.esm|05F3FF: Gretta, Engar, stay and guard the entrance. We don't want any Imperial reinforcements taking us by surprise.
Skyrim.esm|05F402: The rest of you lot are with me.
Skyrim.esm|05F403: You two, stay and guard the entrance. We don't want any Stormcloak reinforcements taking us by surprise.
Skyrim.esm|05F51A: What? You have it? No... No, as tempting as it is, it won't do to have both sides angry at us. The College has had enough trouble staying out of the war. No need to make it worse. You take that book to General Tullius or Ulfric and let them have their little triumph. It will be delivered back here to me soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|05F5B1: Look around, see if you see anything unusual. Any signs of the mound being disturbed. Anything.
Skyrim.esm|05F5B2: We hurt them in the past, and they have long memories.
Skyrim.esm|05F5BB: So you were paying attention. I arranged to have Farengar recover the dragonstone for me. It's what I do. I make things happen from behind the scenes. After all, here you are.
Skyrim.esm|05F5BC: Hmm. There's no simple answer to that. You want the long version or the short version?
Skyrim.esm|05F6E3: There's the stories about Helgen, bandits, skirmishes between the Stormcloaks and the Imperials... it almost seems endless.
Skyrim.esm|05F700: Orgnar. I'm travelling. You've got the inn 'til I get back.
Skyrim.esm|05F702: The Thalmor ambassador, Elenwen, regularly throws parties where the rich and connected cozy up to the Thalmor. I can get you into one of those parties. Once you're inside the Embassy, you get away and find Elenwen's secret files. I have a contact inside the Embassy. He's not up for this kind of high-risk mission, but he can help you. His name's Malborn. Wood elf, plenty of reason to hate the Thalmor. You can trust him. I'll get word for him to meet you in Solitude, at the Winking Skeever -- you know it?
