While you're doing that, I'll work on getting you an invitation to Elenwen's little party. Meet me at the Solitude stables after you've arranged things with Malborn. Any questions?
Skyrim.esm|05F70A: Well, whatever you're doing, keep doing it and you'll have me as a customer for life.
Skyrim.esm|05F725: If we could get into the Thalmor Embassy... it's the center of their operations in Skyrim... Problem is, that place is locked up tighter than a miser's purse. They could teach me a few things about paranoia...
Skyrim.esm|05F73A: Did you do everything I said, Shadr?
Skyrim.esm|05F73B: My dear, you have no idea who you're trifling with. Now I suggest you turn around and rattle someone else's cage before you get hurt.
Skyrim.esm|05F73D: It's true. Wasn't much of a thief though. Made enough noise to wake the Red Mountain.
Skyrim.esm|05F73E: All right, all right. Just bring it to me tomorrow and I'll fix it for you.
Skyrim.esm|05F7BE: That's a shame. What's causing them all the trouble?
Skyrim.esm|05F7D6: Is it true you chased a thief from your home? I overheard Aerin mentioning it.
Skyrim.esm|05F7D9: Why should I pay for a repair on something I just bought?
Skyrim.esm|05F816: You know very well what I'm talking about. You're saying that I only married Bolli for his gold and that I don't really love him.
Skyrim.esm|05F83E: Dinya, about that incense you requested? Its not arriving anytime soon. The Khajiit caravans are having tough times getting through.
Skyrim.esm|05F84C: Can't tell you. It's a bit of a trade secret I picked up when I was living in High Rock.
Skyrim.esm|05F84D: I'm watching you, Grelka. One slip up and I report you to the guards. You got that?
Skyrim.esm|05F84E: You may have everyone else impressed around here, but you don't impress me. You couldn't touch me if you wanted to.
Skyrim.esm|05F858: Well met, Brand-Shei. I've come by to browse your wares today.
Skyrim.esm|05F871: Fine. It'll be five septims to repair it.
Skyrim.esm|05F873: I'd still keep an eye on my purse if I was you.
Skyrim.esm|05F874: I don't know what he's talking about. He must be seeing things.
Skyrim.esm|05F894: Maven. How's the criminal underworld treating you this fine day?
Skyrim.esm|05F8C9: Grelka, I wanted to talk about that suit of leather armor you sold me. The fasteners are already coming loose.
Skyrim.esm|05F8D1: If that's true, then we have much more to fear than the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|05F8E5: Thanks for that bushel of apples, Marise. They're wonderful. How did you keep them so fresh?
Skyrim.esm|05F925: Aerin tells me you might be dealing arms to the Thieves Guild. Is this true?
Skyrim.esm|05F937: Interesting. Keep your ears open for me and tell me if you hear anything more. I'll make it worth your while.
Skyrim.esm|05F970: Perhaps they moved on to greener pastures. Whatever the reason, lets be glad they're scarce.
Skyrim.esm|05F97A: Oh, well... I suppose that's a noble cause. I'll settle this bill at the end of the day. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|05F97B: Hey. What's the matter with you? You have no right to spread rumors around town about me.
Skyrim.esm|05F97D: You've heard too? They say the town was completely destroyed by a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|05F97E: Um... okay. Well, the boots aren't here yet. Hopefully on the next Khajiit caravan.
Skyrim.esm|05F97F: I'll contact my man tonight and let you know what he says.
Skyrim.esm|05F980: I heard a traveler say that Helgen was destroyed by one of those creatures. Didn't leave a soul alive they said.
Skyrim.esm|05F981: No. I'm surprised actually. I thought they had a stranglehold on the city, but in the last few years, it hasn't been that bad.
Skyrim.esm|05F9DF: I believe she's trying to look as ravishing as possible for you, milord.
Skyrim.esm|05F9EC: So while I was in Ivarstead, I heard news that something terrible happened at Helgen.
Skyrim.esm|05F9EF: Always be prepared. You never know if you might get a split in one, or lose one, then where would you be? No sir, that's not happening to me.
Skyrim.esm|05F9F0: You watch the market, you hear the rumors around Riften. What have you heard about these dragons?
Skyrim.esm|05FA30: They haven't been by the Bunkhouse, but I'm certain they will. How about you?
Skyrim.esm|05FA33: May I ask what all the honey's for? I mean, you used to buy almost a jar a week. Are you cooking or something?
Skyrim.esm|05FA35: What's with all the boots and shoes you keep buying? You must have bought twenty pairs in the last two years.
Skyrim.esm|05FA36: I don't care what it costs. I need four longbows and four score of arrows. Just make it happen.
Skyrim.esm|05FA3A: Has the Thieves Guild visited you yet, Haelga?
Skyrim.esm|05FA43: Well, well. You've slain my followers. I should destroy you for your impudence! On the other hand, you've weeded out the weak from among those who would deign worship me. You could be the one I seek... if your will is strong, lure a thrall to my shrine. Convince him to embrace the Pillar of Sacrifice. Then slay him. Do this, and I shall favor you.
Skyrim.esm|05FA4C: I thought it was strange that Aringoth stopped coming into town. I suppose he's having some difficulties out there.
Skyrim.esm|05FA53: Hey, I told you not to ask any questions. Now, here. For your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|05FA54: Brand-Shei... have you managed to find those boots for me?
Skyrim.esm|05FA56: I don't usually deal in weapons, but I know someone who can get them for me. You mind paying a little extra to get them fast?
Skyrim.esm|05FA5D: Relax, Marise... I have no quarrel with you. I just wish to speak a moment.
Skyrim.esm|05FA5E: Chin up, Brand-Shei. Lady Mara will provide us strength and guidance through these difficult times.
Skyrim.esm|05FA6D: I'm afraid it's going to be a while before I can get any more Goldenglow Honey.
Skyrim.esm|05FAFB: Well, he's quite obviously mad. Whether or not that makes him dangerous is another question entirely. Do I trust him? Not in the slightest.
Skyrim.esm|05FAFC: Yes. The man came by at noon just like you said. I gave him the package and he gave me this pouch for you. The package was heavy... I needed his help getting it on his horse. What was inside?
Skyrim.esm|05FAFD: Make them all pay for what they've done to me...
Skyrim.esm|05FB0D: I'm watching you, Maven Black-Briar. One day, you'll slip up and your Imperial friends won't be there to catch your fall.
Skyrim.esm|05FB10: Good. Maybe that will teach them to leave honest citizens alone.
Skyrim.esm|05FB19: Grelka, how fast can you get your hands on some longbows?
Skyrim.esm|05FB1A: Oh! Miss Maven. What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|05FC7C: I am called Boethiah. Many names have mortals given me. But I am not interested in names. I am interested only in deeds.
Skyrim.esm|05FC7F: I abide only those whose will is aligned to my own. Those who oppose my desires perish in the most dreadful of ways. I have a purpose for which you might prove useful. But first I must test the deftness of your guile, and the strength of your purpose. Make a thrall. Lure him to my shrine. Prove your will is stronger. Convince him to embrace the Pillar of Sacrifice, and then slay him without mercy. Do this and I shall remember you and bestow an important task upon you. Do this not, and I shall forget you, and your soul will waste in obscurity.
Skyrim.esm|060022: Oh, thank you! Divines bless your kind heart.
Skyrim.esm|060023: I am sworn to carry your burdens.
Skyrim.esm|06009C: Was I correct in hearing that you were traveling to the grove of the Eldergleam?
Skyrim.esm|06035D: Yes. According to Giraud, our histories keeper, the portion of the Edda dealing with King Olaf might still exist in Dead Man's Respite. I need you to retrieve the poem.
Skyrim.esm|060360: Furthermore, I believe that such a fine poem deserves some payment of Patronage. The college will be moderately rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|060361: Furthermore, I believe that such a fine poem deserves some payment of Patronage. The college will be generously rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|060362: Furthermore, I believe that such a fine poem deserves some payment of Patronage. The college will be very generously rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|0604EF: Falion had his work to keep him busy, and I needed somethin' to do. So, here I am.
Skyrim.esm|0604F0: I've tried to explain to Lurbuk that his "singin'" is gonna get his throat cut.
Skyrim.esm|0604F1: For now, looking after Joric is my job.
Skyrim.esm|0604F2: Some say the swamp took her. Part of me wishes it were that simple.
Skyrim.esm|0604F3: There's enough real troubles in the world without making new ones up.
Skyrim.esm|060513: Indeed. But I abide only those whose will is aligned to my own. Those who oppose my desires perish in the most dreadful of ways.
Skyrim.esm|060514: A god? Assuredly not! When was the last time you saw evidence of Aedra in the world? No, we Daedra are far more powerful than any god. And among my brethren, I am the most feared.
Skyrim.esm|060518: Is that so? You should be. Though a heart without fear is something I can use... Perhaps you are the one I seek. We shall soon find out.
Skyrim.esm|060519: Do you wish to test yourself against me?
Skyrim.esm|06051A: Tell me, why have you slain this one, who trusted you, here upon my shrine?
Skyrim.esm|06051B: Tell me, why have you slain those who profess to follow in my ways?
Skyrim.esm|060642: You idiot. Do you even know who I am? The most recognized scholar on the Dwemer in all of Tamriel, and you people keep bothering me! I... I'm sorry I... I got too excited. I'm in the middle of some very... stressful work, and I shouldn't have yelled. How can I help you?
Skyrim.esm|060643: Only that they break an old woman's heart. So many friends, so many kin, all lost trying to rebuild a past that was over long ago.
Skyrim.esm|060644: I told you I'm not hiring any more guards. Why do you people always bother me when I'm trying to finish my research?
Skyrim.esm|060646: That's a true Nord hero. A legend. So great that the Divines themselves lifted his soul into the heavens and made him a god.
Skyrim.esm|060647: Well, the Priesthood of Arkay always accepts generous donations.
Skyrim.esm|06064C: Magic. That's what they always want. Never history. Never the secrets of the lost races. No, just little spells and enchantments.
Skyrim.esm|06064E: There's a war on and you don't know what I'm talking about? The Empire is destroying everything Skyrim stands for. Jarl Igmund can refuse to see me all he wants. The Silver-Blood family will be heard.
Skyrim.esm|06064F: We've discovered that some of the dead have been... eaten. Flesh has been chewed off, bones were snapped to get at the marrow inside. We haven't caught anyone or anything yet. It's like it knows when I'm there. If you can get to the bottom of this, the Priesthood of Arkay will reward you. Take my key, and be careful.
Skyrim.esm|060650: Nchuand-Zel? The ruins underneath Markarth? The wealth of artifacts that I've based two human lifetimes of research on?
Skyrim.esm|060651: Finally, someone with decent sense. The Empire is destroying everything Skyrim stands for. Honor. Pride. Mighty Talos. Jarl Igmund can't ignore us for much longer. Not if the Silver-Blood family has a say.
Skyrim.esm|060652: All right. I was going to suggest the Jarl hire someone to sort this mess out, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|060654: What are you doing here? The excavation site is closed. I don't need any more workers or guards.
Skyrim.esm|060655: Tacitus? Boy's an Imperial. I keep showing him how to treat the iron, but he never gets it. In the Legion, the Imperial smiths used to read books when they couldn't set their hammers straight. Wisdom of the old, I guess. If you find a book called "The Last Scabbard of Akrash," could you bring it to me? Tacitus sure isn't learning by example.
Skyrim.esm|060656: You can tell Jarl Igmund that the Silver-Blood family won't tolerate his cowardice for much longer.
Skyrim.esm|06065A: Arkay. One of the Eight Divines. God of the Cycle of Birth and Death. It is his shrine people pray to when they visit the Hall. That his divinity will watch over their ancestors on the way to the next life.
Skyrim.esm|06065B: He used the Thu'um. Shouted those Forsworn from the walls. Then he led a band of Nord warriors in to take the city. Just like Talos himself at the Battle of Old Hroldan, reclaiming the Reach from those heathen natives.
Skyrim.esm|06065C: Thank you again. I can't believe I was this close to being killed.
Skyrim.esm|060679: Books. Don't know why the Imperials like these so much. This should help the boy, though, Malacath willing. Here, let me show you how an Orc learns to smith.
Skyrim.esm|06067A: New to Skyrim? I was confused at first, too. The Nords call their mausoleums the Hall of the Dead. It's where people in the city are buried. Fathers. Forefathers. Mothers. Foremothers.
Skyrim.esm|0606E5: Sorry, mother. I'll focus on my work.
Skyrim.esm|0606E6: I served in the Legion. Perhaps I'll show you some of their technique one of these days.
Skyrim.esm|0606E7: Imagine the heads of your enemies as you forge. Let your hatred work the steel.
Skyrim.esm|0606E9: Ah, is there any weapon finer than an Orc blade?
Skyrim.esm|0606EB: No, mother. It's no wonder our enemies fear the sight of an Orc warrior.
Skyrim.esm|0606ED: The metal has been stubborn today. It refuses to bend.
Skyrim.esm|0606EE: You'll come of age soon. Time to be married to another chief.
Skyrim.esm|0606EF: Save your anger for the forge. The steel cannot hear your whining.
Skyrim.esm|0606F0: I won't be married to some weak-willed Orc chieften. I'd sooner join the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|0606F1: Burguk is a fine chief. He brings strength and wealth to the stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|0606F3: You should see my mother, Sharamph, if you need a cure or a poison.
Skyrim.esm|0606F5: Mother tells me to shape metal with focus and strength.
Skyrim.esm|060741: What of it, he says. Your Empire is destroying everything Skyrim stands for. Honor. Pride. Mighty Talos.
Skyrim.esm|060744: What of it, she says. Your Empire is destroying everything Skyrim stands for. Honor. Pride. Mighty Talos.
Skyrim.esm|06075A: They started the Great War. Nearly destroyed the Imperial City. Then the Emperor took the coward's way out and surrendered. Now the Elves dictate everything we do, and they don't want us acknowledging a "mere human" like Talos as the god he is.
Skyrim.esm|06077D: Your kind started the Great War. Nearly destroyed the Imperial City. Then the Emperor took the coward's way out and surrendered. Now you people dictate everything we do. Don't want us acknowledging a "mere human" like Talos as the god he is.
Skyrim.esm|060790: You there. You another Imperial puppet? Answer me.
Skyrim.esm|0607BD: We own Cidhna Mine. Best prison in Skyrim, and source of half the wealth in this city. We keep the Reach safe. We flood Markarth with wealth, but does the Jarl listen to us when we want a say? No. He's busy forgetting that Ulfric Stormcloak saved this city from the Forsworn. Ulfric's a hero, not a damn criminal.
Skyrim.esm|0607C4: Ondolemar is the commander of the Thalmor in Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0607D2: Worship of Talos is strictly forbidden.
Skyrim.esm|0607D3: Arkay keep you and protect you.
Skyrim.esm|0607D4: Thank you. He can be a bit difficult, but I'm sure he'll appreciate your help.
Skyrim.esm|0607D5: He founded the Empire, and now they've turned their back on him. To appease the Elves. Elves! Do the Elves rule Skyrim, or do the Nords?
Skyrim.esm|06097D: Pretty simple. Break into a house, grab a specific item and get out before they realize what's going on. The catch is you can't kill anyone who lives there or you forfeit the payment. You ready for some good old-fashioned burglary?
Skyrim.esm|06097E: We're looking to recover an heirloom for a client. They claim ownership of it, and as long as they're paying we don't really care. You in?
Skyrim.esm|06097F: We've been trying to get this item for a while, but no one with the skills has come along to claim it. Can I count on you to get it?
Skyrim.esm|060980: Have a house with a good bit of valuables inside, but one particular item's caught my eye. You up for it?
Skyrim.esm|060981: Got a tip on a desirable item at this location. We want you to break in there and bring it to us. What do you think?
Skyrim.esm|060982: Good, got a buyer in a rush for this item. If you hurry we can turn it around and make a quick profit. Can you get it?
Skyrim.esm|060983: Sure, sure. We're always looking to get our hands on items from all over Skyrim. How about this one?
Skyrim.esm|060984: As long as you can keep things quiet and don't get yourself killed in the process, this place has exactly what we're looking for. You in?
Skyrim.esm|060985: We've actually stolen this item before and somehow it ended up back in some other mark's house. Guess it's time to reclaim it, eh?
Skyrim.esm|060998: If you wish to gain Boethiah's attention, you must slay a thrall upon her shrine.
Skyrim.esm|06099A: You were weak. Now you are dead. What signs have you left of your passing for us to remember you by?
Skyrim.esm|06099B: He founded the Empire, and now they've turned their back on him. To appease your kind, Elf. Do your people rule Skyrim, or do the Nords?
Skyrim.esm|060C89: Smart woman. Now come along with us. We'll take any stolen goods, and you'll be free to go. After you pay the fine, of course.
Skyrim.esm|060C8B: Then let me speed your passage to Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|061347: Whoa, there. I don't know where you're going, but stay out of that mine. It isn't safe.
Skyrim.esm|061348: You did? We were waiting for the Jarl to send an entire regiment to clear that mine. Now we can get back to work. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|061349: We're going to need an army to clear out that crypt.
Skyrim.esm|06134C: We tunneled into an old Nordic crypt. The whole place is crawling with draugr. We're not mining up any ore with those undead around, so I wouldn't go down there if I were you.
Skyrim.esm|06134D: We tunneled into an old crypt. We're warning strangers to keep out.
Skyrim.esm|06134E: I'm not going into the mine with draugr lurking down there.
Skyrim.esm|06134F: Guards are useless. They just stand around waiting for a full regiment to come and help.
Skyrim.esm|061350: We're keeping the mine closed until that crypt is clear.
Skyrim.esm|061351: I hear talk about a regiment coming to clear the crypt out. We'll see if that happens.
Skyrim.esm|061352: There's still plenty of ore left in that mine. What a waste.
Skyrim.esm|06138D: When a client needs us to eliminate another, we don't operate like the Dark Brotherhood. We find alternate means to get them out of the way. We send you to plant some phony evidence in the mark's house, and then tip off the town guard... the rest takes care of itself. Sound like you can handle it?
Skyrim.esm|06138E: Someone wants to see this mark suffer. They're paying a good bit of coin to have us plant the evidence in their house. You in?
Skyrim.esm|06138F: This one should be easy. Get in, plant the goods and get out. Quiet and neat. Sound like your kind of job?
Skyrim.esm|061390: The mark must have ticked-off our client badly. He's impatient and keeps asking if the job's done. Can you get him off my back?
Skyrim.esm|061391: Got a good one here. Personal score to settle between myself and this mark. You up for it?
Skyrim.esm|061392: Brynjolf finally got some dirt on the mark and the evidence to back it up. Think you can give him a hand?
Skyrim.esm|061393: This one angered someone pretty high up in the keep. Can I count on you to get this done for me?
Skyrim.esm|061394: Got another personal score to settle and I'd consider it a favor if you'd take the job. Think you can help me out?
Skyrim.esm|061395: Sure, sure. Got one that should be pretty easy, as long as you keep your weapon sheathed. Can you handle it?
Skyrim.esm|061396: Yeah. Someone ticked off Maven and she wants to teach them a lesson... our way. You in?
Skyrim.esm|0614EC: Those draugr are going to come up and kill us all.
Skyrim.esm|0614ED: Fine, but don't say I didn't warn you.
Skyrim.esm|0614EE: The guards say no draugr have come out of the mine.
Skyrim.esm|0614EF: Don't be such a coward. We haven't seen any draugr since the mine was cleared. Be grateful.
Skyrim.esm|0614F0: They haven't moved out of the caves. I think we're safe.
Skyrim.esm|0614F1: That's comforting. Glad they're keeping watch instead of getting rid of the draugr.
Skyrim.esm|0614F2: I don't think it's safe mining a cave on top of that crypt.
Skyrim.esm|0614F4: "We're safe" he says. You'll eat those words when a draugr is tearing into your face.
Skyrim.esm|0614F5: There's a war on. We need every scrap of ore we can get, so we're going to keep digging. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|0614F6: Those draugr are going to be back. I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|06150A: Feels good to be mining again.
Skyrim.esm|06150B: How can you say that? This mine was filled with draugr not too long ago.
Skyrim.esm|06150C: Steel your nerves. The draugr are gone. Focus on your mining.
Skyrim.esm|06150D: I feel like any moment, a draugr is going to come and chop my head off.
Skyrim.esm|06183D: Most of the establishments in Skyrim keep their transactions recorded within business ledgers. Your job would be to change the numbers in those books so the shortfalls from our other jobs look legit. Feel like doing a bit of writin'?
Skyrim.esm|06183E: This business is a regular target for the Guild. They keep pretty shoddy records, so the job should be a cinch. You in?
Skyrim.esm|06183F: We want to waylay a shipment on the way from this business without them even noticin' anything went missin'. Want the job?
Skyrim.esm|061840: Yeah, sure. Got a business on tap that could use a little number rearrangin'. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|061841: Okay, got just the one for you. Ledger's just beggin' to be changed and the Guild could use the coin. How about it?
Skyrim.esm|061842: Have a client lookin' to have a rival's business ledger changed. Don't know the reason, and don't want to know. Count you in?
Skyrim.esm|061843: Got a personal request from Brynjolf on this numbers job. He says we may hit 'em hard later, and he wants to prep their books. Help us out?
Skyrim.esm|061844: These marks keep their ledgers all neat and tidy, but they're ripe for some change, if you know what I mean. Take it?
Skyrim.esm|061845: All right. Got a simple one. Just get in, change the books and be back in time for last call. So how's about it?
Skyrim.esm|0618A5: With Vici's murder, you've started us down a path the Dark Brotherhood hasn't traveled in centuries. The assassination of an emperor. And now, your reward. A unique spell to summon a legend of the Dark Brotherhood. His soul serves us now in death, as his body once did in life. Unfortunately, in your zeal, you failed to eliminate Vici while she addressed her guests, as directed. I'm sorry, but your bonus is forfeit. Now, then. Time to proceed to the next stage of the plan. Go and speak with Gabriella. She's been helping me arrange your next contract.
Skyrim.esm|0618A9: Make sure to get yourself something to drink.
Skyrim.esm|0618AA: You hungry? There's plenty of food. Eat up, now.
Skyrim.esm|0618AB: What's going on? Why would somebody do that?
Skyrim.esm|0618AC: When my aunt got married, they had a cake that was three feet tall. I got really sick that day.
Skyrim.esm|0618AD: I heard Vittoria Vici is the Emperor's cousin. I wish he came to the wedding. I never saw a real live emperor before.
Skyrim.esm|0618AE: By the gods. The blood! The blood!
Skyrim.esm|0618B0: I just can't wait to be a bride someday. Someday...
Skyrim.esm|0618B1: I didn't do it! You saw! I didn't do it!
Skyrim.esm|0618B2: I've eaten more in the last two hours than I have in the last two weeks.
Skyrim.esm|0618B3: If I'm really lucky, I'll get to kiss the bride for good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0618B4: By all the gods, the groom has been killed. At his own wedding! I can only think of my own husband...
Skyrim.esm|0618B5: A marriage of both love and political advantage. A rare thing indeed.
Skyrim.esm|0618B7: Oh, no no no... This is so wrong...
Skyrim.esm|0618B8: I'm so glad Vittoria and Asgeir chose a public reception. That way the entire city can share in their holy bond.
Skyrim.esm|0618B9: Oh, did you miss the ceremony? It was rather quick, actually. The couple seemed more concerned with their reception.
Skyrim.esm|0618BB: I just came to sing! This is a horror show!
Skyrim.esm|0618BC: Ahem. Mi mi mi mi. Pardon me. Just, um, warming up. Never know when I may be called upon for a ballad.
Skyrim.esm|0618BE: What my daughter sees in that... barbarian, I'll never know.
Skyrim.esm|0618BF: The thought of paying a dowry to that - that... Stormcloak turns my stomach.
Skyrim.esm|0618C0: My son is dead! At his own wedding! Only those Imperial dogs would have dared do something like this! Damn them all to Oblivion!
Skyrim.esm|0618C1: They say this marriage is the first step towards peace. Who in Shor's name wants peace? Pfft.
Skyrim.esm|0618C2: My boy, he always did have an eye for the pretty ones. That Vittoria ain't bad, for an Imperial.
Skyrim.esm|0618C3: By Ysmir, this is horrible! I never wanted the girl dead, I swear!
Skyrim.esm|0618C4: To think, my little boy is married. And to an Imperial. Oh, they grow up so fast.
Skyrim.esm|0618C5: If my Asgeir is happy, then I'm happy. Really.
Skyrim.esm|0618E7: There are all sorts of people wandering around carryin' their valuables on their person... just waitin' for someone like you to pick their pocket. So, you in for a little fishin'?
Skyrim.esm|0618E8: Sure, I've got a mark for you. Stole something quite important from a friend of the Guild and we take care of our own. You in?
Skyrim.esm|0618E9: One of our marks seems to have an item of value we want to add to our vault. Get it for us and you'll receive the usual pay. Sound good?
Skyrim.esm|0618EA: Got ourselves a freelancer thinks he can muscle in on our territory. How about you teach him some manners, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0618EB: Maven wants this mark's most prized possession. The bloody fool carries it on his person all the time. So, think you can handle it?
Skyrim.esm|0618EC: Brynjolf wants this mark's head on a pike, but we'll settle for what the bastard's carryin' instead. Can I count on you?
Skyrim.esm|0618ED: Got a mark taxed with carryin' a heavy item. Care to lighten' the load?
Skyrim.esm|0618EE: This brilliant soul decided to pick a fight with a fellow Guild mate. Time for them to pay the piper. Will you help out?
Skyrim.esm|0618EF: I've got a mark who just got an item from a relative outside of Skyrim. They probably should have been quieter about it. You in?
Skyrim.esm|061B25: We'd better stick together down here.
Skyrim.esm|061B2D: There may be a way out, down this way.
Skyrim.esm|061B37: I guess that'll teach me to try to reason with a Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|061B3F: Let's see where this goes. We can't be the only survivors.
Skyrim.esm|061B40: Find yourself a weapon. You're going to need it.
Skyrim.esm|061B7A: We'd better get out of here. I don't think this place is going to stand up to much more of this.
Skyrim.esm|061B81: Don't worry. We'll get out of here, somehow.
Skyrim.esm|061D73: I... it's true, isn't it? You really are Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|061F08: What an interesting idea. I think if someone were to do something like that, there would definitely be a certain amount of loot to be shared... Yes, if someone were to put out the lighthouse fire and then meet me on the docks afterwards... I could certainly direct them to that loot.
Skyrim.esm|061F9A: My time has come. I am old. Too old to become chief. It would be wrong for me to take wives at this age. So I will die. Malacath has given me a vision of a glorious death. I am to wait here until it finds me. As you can see, it has not yet arrived.
Skyrim.esm|061F9C: Great! Thanks! Bastard stole from me. I'll catch him though!
Skyrim.esm|061FC1: Yes! How did you get this? Never mind. Here, it's not much, but it's something. I'm going to track down that thief and murder him!
Skyrim.esm|061FC3: You should leave. I don't want you scaring off my good death.
Skyrim.esm|061FC4: Perhaps... Are you sure about this?
Skyrim.esm|061FCA: I'm not kidding. I'll hurt you!
Skyrim.esm|061FCB: No time to talk. Snitch or double-cross me and I'll kill you. I mean it!
Skyrim.esm|061FD3: Well, if you do, grab him and yell. Bastard stole from me.
Skyrim.esm|061FD5: Indeed. One should find his death while he can still call himself a proper man. We Orc men are not like these Nords and Imperials who carry on until they are grey and feeble and their hair falls out. To cling to something past its usefulness is unseemly, how much more so when that thing is you?
Skyrim.esm|061FF7: I am waiting for a good death.
Skyrim.esm|061FFD: If you are not here to grant me a good death, then you can leave.
Skyrim.esm|061FFE: Take this and hold onto it. I'll be back for it later. I'll pay you good. Don't lose it.
Skyrim.esm|062016: Did you see someone run past just now?
Skyrim.esm|062021: Yes. Were I to simply lay down and die, it would not please Malacath.
Skyrim.esm|0620E5: You're passing through Solitude? Maybe you're looking to make some easy gold, yes?
Skyrim.esm|062167: What?! This is exactly why... don't they understand it's hard enough to fight a war without these juvenile attempts to undermine authority?
Skyrim.esm|062169: You're leaving? Okay, we'll just work on the verse in a bit.
Skyrim.esm|06216A: I'll tell everyone we're ready, but we'll start the festival at dusk. Come talk to me after dark. We'll get the festival started when you do.
Skyrim.esm|06216B: My coat? What do you need my coat for?
Skyrim.esm|06216F: If you say so. If this were anyone but you I would lock them up. Take it. Take the coat, have your fun. I'll see you at the festival.
Skyrim.esm|062238: Well then, what are you waiting for? Get in there and earn your pay.
Skyrim.esm|06223A: Any progress on finding the mask?
Skyrim.esm|06223C: You there! As an officer of the Imperial Legion, I require your assistance to complete my mission.
Skyrim.esm|06223D: And what, you couldn't read it yourself? Fine, give it here a moment.
Skyrim.esm|0622BB: Everyone in town knows about the Black Door, and to never go near it.
Skyrim.esm|0622BC: Did you hear? People have been seen coming in and out of the Black Door. Divines have mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0622CB: I hear people were spotted using the Black Door. Gods have mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0622D0: Ainethach's dead, but that doesn't mean we can stop working.
Skyrim.esm|0622D1: Sauranach mine is open again. How am I supposed to handle all this work?
Skyrim.esm|062498: I think we're almost out. Come on!
Skyrim.esm|062499: Let's take our time. I think we can sneak by her if we're careful.
Skyrim.esm|06249A: We must be near the entrance for that bear to have made its den here.
Skyrim.esm|06249F: I hate those things. Too many eyes or something.
Skyrim.esm|0624A0: Looks like we're on our own now.
Skyrim.esm|0624A1: Since nobody's trying to kill me just now, maybe we should rest for a minute.
Skyrim.esm|0624A3: Let's see if we can find a way out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0624A5: I'm glad to be out of that place. I hope this leads somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0624A6: These bastards call themselves Imperial Legionnaires...
Skyrim.esm|0624A9: Look around. Grab anything that looks useful.
Skyrim.esm|062CEF: Excellent work. Now, I trust you're sharp enough to see that the sword doesn't match the description of the Ebony Blade you may know. It has languished too long outside the winds of alliance and betrayal.
Skyrim.esm|062D7B: I told you, I know everything about this castle. For some reason that door is special. Only two people can open it, Balgruuf and Farengar, the court wizard. How you get it from them is up to you. Nobody would notice if Farengar went missing, I promise you.
Skyrim.esm|062F4A: I don't even care any more. You want to check it out? Fine. I'll sell you the thing for fifty gold, and then it's your problem.
Skyrim.esm|062F4B: Fine, here you go. You get killed on account of this thing, it's not my fault. Got it? But, I suppose, if it does actually turn out to be worth something, let me know, will you?
Skyrim.esm|062F4C: Oh, I'd gladly sell it to you if you had the money. But you don't, do you?
Skyrim.esm|062F4D: Yeah, it was stupid of me. I shouldn't have believed the story and just refused the trade. But I didn't, and now I'm stuck with this worthless junk.
Skyrim.esm|062F4E: Some line about this claw thing and Yngol Barrow. He said it was worth more than its weight in gold if I took it back there. Something about placing it back in Yngol's chamber, I don't know. What was I thinking? Even if it were true, I'm not setting foot in some ancient tomb, filled with who knows what.
Skyrim.esm|062F4F: Very funny. I'm sure you've noticed that Winterhold isn't exactly overflowing with people. So I'll sell whatever I can. If it's worth putting a price on, you can bet I'll do it.
Skyrim.esm|062F50: My pa says elves are bad and not to trust 'em.
Skyrim.esm|062F51: I have a very close relationship with Savos Aren, the current Arch-Mage.
Skyrim.esm|062F52: Most of our business comes from folks traveling to and from the College.
Skyrim.esm|062F53: I've been burned a few times, made a few bad deals, but mostly I come out on top.
Skyrim.esm|063410: If that's what she wants, then that's what she'll get.
Skyrim.esm|063411: There is one... Yngvar. He's quite popular with the ladies. Thankfully Faleen is not quite his type, but they've been friends for some time, and he may have some ideas. Please, ask him what she likes. It's my only chance.
Skyrim.esm|063412: Wait... you mean I should just... tell her? Tell her how I feel?
Skyrim.esm|063413: The dawn surely opens upon you, child. You must bear its light that all may see.
Skyrim.esm|06382A: Helping out Ainethach, huh? Give me a reason not to plunge a dagger in your chest.
Skyrim.esm|06382C: I want you sellswords out of my mine.
Skyrim.esm|06382D: No. This is my land. You expect me to just hand it over to those Silver-Blood thugs?
Skyrim.esm|06382F: You haven't heard of the Silver-Blood family? They're the most important people in the Reach. They practically own the city of Markarth. And we're here on their coin.
Skyrim.esm|063830: Forsworn attacked a while back. Jarl had no men to spare, so the Silver-Bloods generously offered to send us to clean things up. We just want that old native to hand over his land in payment for our services. Then we'll leave, and everyone can get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|063832: Watch your tongue, native. We'll leave when we're sure there's no Forsworn here.
Skyrim.esm|063833: Oh, and when would that be, I wonder? When I sell my land to the Silver-Bloods?
Skyrim.esm|063834: That won't be the last we hear from the Silver-Bloods, but at least we can get back to work. Thank you. You've done a good thing for me and my workers. Here. This is for you.
Skyrim.esm|063836: A family of thieves. Running their corrupt empire from Markarth. The Silver-Blood family own half the Reach, and they won't stop until they own all of it.
Skyrim.esm|063837: Robbery is what it is. The Silver-Bloods are trying to muscle in on my land. Forsworn attack the mines, and suddenly all these sellswords show up to "help." They won't let anyone work until I sell it off.
Skyrim.esm|063838: The Silver-Bloods have made you a very generous offer for this pile of dirt. I suggest you take it.
Skyrim.esm|063839: The mine is closed. Get lost.
Skyrim.esm|06383A: I've been sent by Silver-Bloods to keep this mine "safe."
Skyrim.esm|063AAE: Are you all right, dear? I thought I saw a tear in your eye a moment ago.
Skyrim.esm|063AB1: What I meant to say was... I love having an Orc here in the barracks. They're so... pleasant.
Skyrim.esm|063AB2: Nothing, Ragnar. Please... don't hurt me.
Skyrim.esm|063AB4: I've been thinking, would you like to join me in hunting?
Skyrim.esm|063AB5: We're doomed. The mine is going to stay closed, and we'll up be carted of to Cidhna Mine as slaves.
Skyrim.esm|063AB6: I can afford to buy meat now. I prefer working in the mines, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|063AB8: Oh, I'm fine, Enmon. I just... I still miss her.
Skyrim.esm|063AB9: Why do your people claim the Reach is theirs? The Orcs have been in these lands just as long.
Skyrim.esm|063ABB: But why did they take her? What could they want with our little girl?
Skyrim.esm|063ABE: Only criminals get sent to Cidhna Mine. They're not going to send us there.
Skyrim.esm|063ABF: Tell me, Ragnar, what do you think of the Orcs?
Skyrim.esm|063AC0: You wouldn't understand, Lash. Your people aren't smart enough for politics.
Skyrim.esm|063AC1: I... I'm sorry, Belchimac. I didn't mean that.
Skyrim.esm|063AC2: You want me to hunt with you? I thought you preferred being alone.
Skyrim.esm|063AC5: I miss her, too, but I'm glad she's safe.
Skyrim.esm|063AC7: Oh, they'll find some way to send us there. It'll be just my luck.
Skyrim.esm|063AC9: You have strong arms. You'd make a good Orc.
Skyrim.esm|063ACB: Is that so? I'll remember that next time we're in the mine tunnels... alone.
Skyrim.esm|063ACC: Your arms and legs are fit for catching strong, agile prey. I could use your help.
Skyrim.esm|063ACD: Cunning warriors and skilled workers. It's good to have you here, Lash.
Skyrim.esm|063ACE: You're always talking about the Forsworn. You aren't thinking of joining them, are you?
Skyrim.esm|063AD0: So am I. I hope she's happy at the temple.
Skyrim.esm|063AD2: I don't know. The guards are out looking for her, now. They'll be back soon.
Skyrim.esm|063AD5: Did... did I say you weren't smart? I meant to say that I'm not smart, Lash. I'm just not smart enough to answer your questions.
Skyrim.esm|063AD6: I'd be honored, but I have work to do in the mines.
Skyrim.esm|063AD7: Maybe I will. What do you think about that?
Skyrim.esm|063AD8: Um... thank you, Lash. You'd... make a fine Nord?
Skyrim.esm|063AD9: I asked what you thought of the Orcs, not about me.
Skyrim.esm|063ADB: You keep saying that. What if she's gone forever? What if they've done something to her?
Skyrim.esm|063ADD: Enmon, dear, do you have any regrets? About letting her go?
Skyrim.esm|063AE1: When will Sanuarach Mine be open?
Skyrim.esm|063AE2: Don't say words you don't mean.
Skyrim.esm|063AE3: I'm warning you. Those people are killers. You'd never last a day with them.
Skyrim.esm|063AE4: Oh. I'm sorry. Well, it's good to have you here, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|063AE8: No... no, we did the right thing. The temple will take good care of her, and one day she'll be helping all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|063AED: I don't like bunking with Orcs. It's not... clean.
Skyrim.esm|063AEF: You're right. She'll be a great Sybil, won't she?
Skyrim.esm|063AF0: Damn Nords. Always getting in the way of us honest folk.
Skyrim.esm|063AF1: I don't know. Whenever those Silver-Blood men decide to leave.
Skyrim.esm|063AF3: When are those Silver-Blood men going to leave?
Skyrim.esm|063AF4: The house is so... empty, Enmon... and quiet.
Skyrim.esm|063AF9: If you want me to leave, you could challenge me to a fight. I promise to stop once you've lost an arm.
Skyrim.esm|063AFB: What did you say? You lazy coward, I'll cut your throat.
Skyrim.esm|063AFC: I haven't seen you hunting in a while.
Skyrim.esm|063AFD: Of course she will. She's our daughter, after all.
Skyrim.esm|063AFE: Never. They've shut us down forever, Enmon. The Divines have abandoned us.
Skyrim.esm|063B50: Someone bring me a drink, quick!
Skyrim.esm|063B51: Fine, fine. Get your hands off me. I'll be a good boy now.
Skyrim.esm|063B52: Wouldn't want to offend our Thalmor overlords, would we?
Skyrim.esm|063B53: Attention, everyone! Could I have your attention, please! I have an announcement to make!
Skyrim.esm|063B54: I propose a toast to Elenwen! Our mistress! I speak figuratively, of course. Nothing could be more unlikely than that someone would actually want her in their bed. Although... most of you are already in bed with her! But again... I speak figuratively, of course!
Skyrim.esm|063E2D: Just when you think things couldn't get any worse.
Skyrim.esm|063E2E: Of course. I rented a bed there. Where else would I sleep?
Skyrim.esm|063E32: Sounds good. May I owe you for them?
Skyrim.esm|063E35: Good. You could be our first line of defense since you live outside the city walls, so we're depending on you.
Skyrim.esm|063E36: Brand-Shei, did you hear about Helgen? It's... it's gone! A dragon burned it to the ground!
Skyrim.esm|063E37: Grelka, have you had anyone come by and try to sell you an iron war axe?
Skyrim.esm|063E38: Marise, I've come to warn you that the Thieves Guild has been harassing shopkeepers again.
Skyrim.esm|063E39: So, Tythis... you coming by the Bunkhouse later?
Skyrim.esm|063E3C: I have two more bushels for you, Marise. That should keep you well-stocked.
Skyrim.esm|063E3D: If he paid what he owes, I wouldn't have to say anything. Besides, what in Oblivion do you care?
Skyrim.esm|063E3E: Yes, Lady Mjoll. If Riften gets attacked, we'll retreat inside the city gates and bar the door just like you said.
Skyrim.esm|063E3F: I'll see what I can do.
Skyrim.esm|063E42: Religion isn't going to save me. So unless Mara is going to start raining coin from the heavens, why don't you mind your own business.
Skyrim.esm|063E43: Well, there's Ingun Black-Briar, Maven's daughter. Strange girl. She likes to spend time down in Elgrim's Elixirs working on her alchemy.
Skyrim.esm|063E44: One of them came in and demanded payment. Bolli tried to tell them no; stood up to them, but...
Skyrim.esm|063E45: Sigh. Yes, as usual. I'll be there.
Skyrim.esm|063E46: Shadr, that's so sweet... I'd love to.
Skyrim.esm|063E48: What's this nonsense I've been hearing? How dare you call Bolli a deadbeat!
Skyrim.esm|063E49: Have you prepared the stables like I suggested, Shadr?
Skyrim.esm|063E4C: That's fine. I have a special occasion coming up, and I'd like to have it.
Skyrim.esm|063E4F: You poor thing. You should come by the temple, speak to Maramal.
Skyrim.esm|063E54: No, no... not at all. Mara believes that the spark of a union comes from inside us, not from our appearance. Tell you what... if you still feel unsure, come by the temple sometime and we'll talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|063E55: Did something happen at the Pawned Prawn?
Skyrim.esm|063E57: Will we be dining together this evening after my work at the fishery?
Skyrim.esm|063E5A: Naw... he's fine. Look, I wanted to ask if you'll... um, if you'd like to... well, like to have a drink with me at the Bee and Barb sometime?
Skyrim.esm|063E5E: Maven Black-Briar, the matriarch of the family, represents everything that's wrong with this city. She's bribed countless officials, has friends back in the Imperial City and freely associates with the Thieves Guild. I've tried everything I can to protect Riften's citizens from her family, but to no avail.
Skyrim.esm|063E5F: If I see him, I'll make sure he doesn't do anything brash. Don't worry.
Skyrim.esm|063E61: The Argonian sugar-cane wine? It's pretty hard to get around here. Very expensive.
Skyrim.esm|063E63: What's so good about it? I've got deadbeats who don't pay me, the city guard looking over my shoulder and Maven Black-Briar breathing down my neck.
Skyrim.esm|063E64: Of course he wasn't you stupid whore; he's a man. Now I'm not going to warn you again. Is that clear?
Skyrim.esm|063E65: There's someone I like very much in Riften. I think I'd like to marry him someday, but... well, he's not of my kind. Would Mara be angry?
Skyrim.esm|063E71: I've come to warn you that the Thieves Guild seems to be back to its old tricks again.
Skyrim.esm|063E73: I'm well. How are things over at the stables? Old Hofgrir giving you a hard time?
Skyrim.esm|063E75: Try it out? What would you need a leather harness for anyway?
Skyrim.esm|063EBD: It's Bersi. He's getting so angry about the corruption in Riften, he said he's going to walk right up to Maven and give her a piece of his mind.
Skyrim.esm|063EBE: Brand-Shei, do you think you can find me some theilul?
Skyrim.esm|063EC2: Feel free to ask me anything you like.
Skyrim.esm|063EC3: Why in goodness name would you have... oh never mind, just bring them by my house later and I'll see what I can do.
Skyrim.esm|063EC4: Nivenor, my darling. What a pleasant surprise.
Skyrim.esm|063EC5: Hi, Marise. How are you today?
Skyrim.esm|063EC6: Oh, good. I wanted to try that out as soon as possible.
Skyrim.esm|063EC8: What is it, Drifa? What's the matter?
Skyrim.esm|063EC9: Maven, I... please, don't hurt me.
Skyrim.esm|063ECA: Haelga, I like you, so I'm only going to say this one time. If you ever try and "practice the Dibellan arts" with Hemming again, I'll make sure that pretty little face loses its beauty.
Skyrim.esm|063ECC: When I got close, the mercenaries Aringoth hired to protect the estate just waved me off. I don't think he wants visitors anymore.
Skyrim.esm|063ECD: I don't know. All we can do is hope the Eight will favor us and have these creatures stay far away from Riften.
Skyrim.esm|063ECE: Oh, Haelga... there you are. Your special order from Hammerfell came in off the last Khajiit caravan.
Skyrim.esm|063ECF: Mjoll! I'm glad I found you.
Skyrim.esm|063ED0: Turning him in to the guards? Have you joined Mjoll as official peacekeepers of Riften? You cost me a lot of coin.
Skyrim.esm|063ED2: Dinya? I was reading that missive you gave me and I was wondering something about marriage.
Skyrim.esm|063ED3: Leather? If they're armored, you should talk to Balimund over at The Scorched Hammer.
Skyrim.esm|063ED5: Do you... do you think that could happen here? I mean, could a dragon come here and burn the city down?
Skyrim.esm|063ED6: Oh I get it. "It's missing so Grelka must have it." I wish this town would stop pointing their fingers at me. There is a Thieves Guild in town you know.
Skyrim.esm|063ED7: I hope that doesn't mean things are going to get worse around here.
Skyrim.esm|063ED9: Maven, please. I didn't know it was your man. Had I known, I would have looked the other way, I swear.
Skyrim.esm|063EDA: Good, that must be very comfortable for you. I think mine's a bit too small. Perhaps we could trade?
Skyrim.esm|063EDB: You know I can't tell you that. As soon as I do, and you have a few meads in you, everyone will know.
Skyrim.esm|063EDD: Bountiful. Although I would like to have anchored a bit closer to Goldenglow Estate.
Skyrim.esm|063EDE: Yes, I heard. I think all of Skyrim knows by know.
Skyrim.esm|063EDF: Well, we had one go missing from the Pawned Prawn, and I was just wondering...
Skyrim.esm|063EE0: I've heard. Bolli and Keerava were speaking of it just a little while ago.
Skyrim.esm|063EE1: I don't like being made a fool of, Brand-Shei. Not one bit.
Skyrim.esm|063EE2: I have plenty of room in my bed.
Skyrim.esm|063EE3: Of course. Say, how are you going to sell all those fish before they spoil?
Skyrim.esm|063EE4: Oh come off it, Niv. We both know you married that fisherman for his gold. You don't need to put on an act for my benefit.
Skyrim.esm|063FE7: Aha! Here we are. Honored guests, I present to you - the Gourmet!
Skyrim.esm|063FEC: You, an assassin for the Dark Brotherhood, have just made an attempt on the Emperor's life. Would have succeeded, had it been the real man.
Skyrim.esm|063FED: Quite. Yet that recent business with the young officer. Maro, was it? How dreadful. The son of your commander, plotting your assassination.
Skyrim.esm|063FEE: Surprised? So was I, when a member of your "Family" came to me with the plan. We worked out a deal, you see. An exchange.
Skyrim.esm|063FF0: Ah, the Potage le Magnifique. So delicious. My friends, as Emperor, I of course reserve the right of first taste.
Skyrim.esm|063FF1: Yes, an unfortunate turn of events, that. But an isolated incident. And I have been assured that the fault was with the man's son alone.
Skyrim.esm|063FF3: I get you, and the Dark Brotherhood gets to continue its existence.
Skyrim.esm|06408C: Kill him. And make sure there's nothing left to bury.
Skyrim.esm|06408F: Kill her. And make sure there's nothing left to bury.
Skyrim.esm|0640CD: By the gods! The Gourmet has killed the Emperor! Attack!
Skyrim.esm|0640F6: By the gods! The Gourmet and the chef have poisoned the Emperor! Get them!
Skyrim.esm|064114: You mean the wedding? My cousin's apparent murder? An unfortunate misunderstanding, no more. Cold mead, hot tempers... these things happen.
Skyrim.esm|064116: But you know what? I've changed my mind. How about this? I kill you, and butcher each and every one of your miserable little friends?
Skyrim.esm|064165: Good, good. Does it say where he may be headed?
Skyrim.esm|064166: No, nothing. At least nothing I'm aware of. Well... If I'm being honest, I haven't exactly been discreet lately in expressing my frustration with this whole situation. Obeying the Night Mother. You being the Listener. It's ridiculous. No offense. Cicero may have overheard me talking to one of the others about the Night Mother. It's possible I was... not entirely respectful. But to go this far. To attempt to murder the leader of a Sanctuary. Cicero must pay with his life. There is no other option.
Skyrim.esm|064167: The Dawnstar Sanctuary? Whatever for? Never mind, it doesn't matter. You need to leave. Now. Every moment counts, so I want you to take my horse. His name is Shadowmere. You'll find him outside, by the pool. Let's just say he's... one of us. Find Arnbjorn. Make sure my husband's all right. And then, send that jester's twisted little soul to the Void, in as many pieces as possible.
Skyrim.esm|06416C: But aren't even you the least bit nervous? After everything that's happened?
Skyrim.esm|0641D2: Truth is, we are in no danger whatsoever. Killing an Emperor can be useful, but befriending one? Now that's beneficial - as I'm sure you'd all agree.
Skyrim.esm|06422E: Here we are. Gods, I'm nervous. We'll go in in just a moment. Please, I'll serve. You just stand there and... be amazing.
Skyrim.esm|064266: That man was, by far, the most insufferable decoy the Emperor has ever employed. I'm glad he's dead. Ah, but I'm even happier that you killed him.
Skyrim.esm|064268: Your Sanctuary's being put to the sword right now. That's what I think of this "deal." You killed my son! All of you! And now you'll pay the price.
Skyrim.esm|064616: Any idea how I can get my daughter's head out of the clouds?
Skyrim.esm|064765: I can't decide if things are about to get worse or better.
Skyrim.esm|064766: At least we don't have a dragon to worry about, for the moment.
Skyrim.esm|06477A: No. I'm a Breton from High Rock, and I refuse to be mistaken for a filthy native. I don't care if we share the same ancestors, these people are savages. Half of them are Forsworn barbarians, and have you seen what they eat? What have I done for the gods to take me so far from my beloved Daggerfall?
Skyrim.esm|06477B: We're the ruling body of the Aldmeri Dominion. Saviors of Mer. Victors of the Great War. The Empire exists because we allow it to exist, and I'm here to make sure the Jarl of Markarth remembers that.
Skyrim.esm|06477C: Damn Elves! You'll pay for this!
Skyrim.esm|06477E: The Thalmor saved all of Elven-kind during the Oblivion Crisis. We've been watching over our lands for 200 years. We re-founded the Aldmeri Dominion, an alliance between us and our Bosmer cousins. You might know them from their common name. Wood elves. We intend to prove the superiority of Mer over Man, one century at a time.
Skyrim.esm|064781: Ogmund the skald. He's old, respected, and I know for a fact that he worships Talos in his home. But the Jarl has been hesitant to call for his arrest. I want you to break in to his home and find evidence.
Skyrim.esm|064783: Ah, there we have it. This will be all I need to have Ogmund taken care of. I would like to personally thank you on behalf of myself and the Thalmor for your assistance. Here, for your work.
Skyrim.esm|064784: Depends on what you need. A light armor is good for travel. Less to carry. A heavy armor weighs you down, but no true warrior goes without it. If you want some heavy armor, I might have a deal we could work out. I need a Daedra's Heart. Jarl wants me to cool his new sword in blood. You get me a heart, I get you some armor. Interested?
Skyrim.esm|064785: It's a religious matter. The Thalmor do not recognize Talos as a god. He was only a man, and does not deserve to a place in our pantheon. The Empire has agreed to accept our beliefs, and its citizens have a responsibility to cease their heretical worship. You're awfully inquisitive, aren't you? I like that. Perhaps you'd care to solve a little problem I'm having?
Skyrim.esm|064786: He's the real head of this family. He may not be the eldest, but all the work that keeps us respected is handled by my Thonar.
Skyrim.esm|064787: Anything. Served in the Legion with my sister, Ghorza, for over 10 years. We've seen every piece you can imagine. Have you met her? She runs the forge near the smelter. Just don't bother talking to her apprentice. Boy has no talent.
Skyrim.esm|064788: As if this craggy, wretch of a city could give birth to a superiorly bred Mer such as myself. No, I'm not from Markarth. I was sent here to lead the Thalmor's interests in this corner of Skyrim. It's my mission to root out all Talos worship in this city.
Skyrim.esm|064789: Good. I'd prefer using water to cool my metal, but it's what the Jarl wants. Here. Some of my best armor. Wear it well.
Skyrim.esm|06478A: Jarl can refuse to see me all he likes. He can't ignore us forever.
Skyrim.esm|06478B: My husband Thonar handles all our family's business. He's an important man.
Skyrim.esm|06478C: The Dominion is here, and we're watching you.
Skyrim.esm|06478D: The Thalmor know how to deal with Talos worshippers.
Skyrim.esm|0648EF: Help us and you may earn a pardon.
Skyrim.esm|0648F0: Hey, weren't you on the carts with the Stormcloaks? Ah, well, if Hadvar vouches for you I guess it must be all right.
Skyrim.esm|0648F1: I saw Caius.. that dragon just grabbed him and... I don't know how I made it out of there...
Skyrim.esm|0648F2: Nothing for it but to keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|0648F3: Better down here than out there.
Skyrim.esm|0648F4: A dragon.... First in a thousand years....
Skyrim.esm|0648F5: We need to find a way out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0648F8: Thank Talos you came along... I thought I was going to die down here.
Skyrim.esm|0648F9: Imperial dogs... there's nothing that's too low for them.
Skyrim.esm|0648FA: You were with us on the carts, weren't you? Not your lucky day, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0648FB: I wonder if there's any more Imperials hiding down here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0648FC: Ralof seems to like you. That's good enough for me.
Skyrim.esm|0648FD: What do you make of it... the dragon? What does it mean?
Skyrim.esm|064900: I hope someone knows where we're going.
Skyrim.esm|064901: Better down here than out there.
Skyrim.esm|064902: Just keep putting one foot in front of the other.
Skyrim.esm|064903: I think we're almost out. Come on!
Skyrim.esm|064904: Take it slow and careful, and I think we can sneak by her.
Skyrim.esm|064905: We must be near the entrance for that bear to make its den here.
Skyrim.esm|064907: I've got your back, don't worry.
Skyrim.esm|064909: Biggest damn spiders I've ever seen.
Skyrim.esm|06490A: The others'll have to find another way out.
Skyrim.esm|06490B: This is the longest day I can remember.
Skyrim.esm|06490C: Let's see if we can find a way out of here.
Skyrim.esm|06490D: We'd better stick together down here.
Skyrim.esm|06490E: I sure hope there's a way out somewhere down here.
Skyrim.esm|06490F: Here's the noble Empire in all its glory. Pah.
Skyrim.esm|064912: There may be a way out, down this way.
Skyrim.esm|064913: We'd better get moving before any more Imperials show up.
Skyrim.esm|064914: Let's see where this goes. Better than going back outside with that dragon.
Skyrim.esm|064915: Get yourself a weapon. I'm going to need your help if we run into any more Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|064916: We've got to find a way out of here.
Skyrim.esm|064917: Don't worry. We'll get out of here, somehow.
Skyrim.esm|064918: I don't think this day is ever going to end.
Skyrim.esm|064919: Better in here than out there.
Skyrim.esm|06494F: You're doing me quite a favor. I won't forget it. I'm sure she'll be angry. Brace yourself.
Skyrim.esm|064950: Can't do that and keep an eye on things here at the same time. But... You could take word to her in Solitude, let her know it'll be coming soon. I'd make it worth your while. No one would notice a few missing ingots out of the next shipment.
Skyrim.esm|064951: You could take word to her that there'll be a shipment soon. Buy us some time and keep her off our backs. In exchange, I'm willing to let a few ingots go missing. Maybe more than a few.
Skyrim.esm|064952: That's good news. I was starting to fear something had happened. You have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|064953: Well, no. But that's not the point. Bryling owns the mine, and she's not selling. We make her some decent coin running this place. Keeps her living well in Solitude. At least, when we can get shipments to her. We're late on the last one. I expect her to start breathin' fire any moment now.
Skyrim.esm|064954: Is everything all right? I've not heard from Sorli or Pactur in some time.
Skyrim.esm|064955: I'm just protecting Bryling's investment. She's the owner, even though we're the ones here doing all the work. She's already come down hard on us for missing shipments. Likely to do it again, since we're late.
Skyrim.esm|064956: No, and before you ask, it's not for sale.
Skyrim.esm|064957: Won't be 'some other time' at this rate.
Skyrim.esm|064963: Hroggar's house fire? Aye. He lost his wife and daughter in the blaze.
Skyrim.esm|064964: Hroggar's house fire? He lost his wife and daughter in the blaze.
Skyrim.esm|064965: They think he did it to get rid of his wife so he could be with Alva. He moved in with her the day after the fire. Even I find that suspicious.
Skyrim.esm|064966: Lust can make a man do the unthinkable. The ashes were still warm when he pledged himself to Alva.
Skyrim.esm|06497A: Hey, greenskin... close your mouth. Some of us are trying to eat over here.
Skyrim.esm|06497B: They say... well, they say he murdered someone. Something to do with Svidi.
Skyrim.esm|06497C: You have a kind heart; much too kind for Riften. Don't worry about me, I'll be just fine.
Skyrim.esm|06497D: Don't let it get you down. Indaryn will eventually tire of picking on you and move on to someone else.
Skyrim.esm|06497E: Are you sure that's the real reason? It wouldn't have anything to do with others making an Argonian unwelcome, because if it is...
Skyrim.esm|06497F: Svana, I heard that Sibbi was put in jail. What happened?
Skyrim.esm|064980: And do what? Losing a job from the Black-Briar family would make me a marked man. No one would ever take me on anywhere else.
Skyrim.esm|064981: The cold stone, the dampness... it suits me, Svana.
Skyrim.esm|064982: Maybe it's time you quit then.
Skyrim.esm|064983: Madesi, why don't you just take a bed here? There's no need to sleep in Beggar's Row.
Skyrim.esm|064984: I can't take it anymore, Tythis. Indaryn keeps pushing me around and I'm not even doing anything wrong.
Skyrim.esm|064985: If he wasn't a Black-Briar, I'd march right over to the jail and teach Sibbi some manners... but then I suppose I'd end up like Wulfur.
Skyrim.esm|064986: You're not going to goad me into a fight so I'll get kicked out, Grelka. Eat your food and leave me in peace.
Skyrim.esm|064987: Visiting Riften? All are welcome at the Temple of Mara.
Skyrim.esm|064988: Please to meet you, I'm a priestess at the Temple of Mara.
Skyrim.esm|064989: I really need your help! I don't want to lose my job at the fishery! What am I going to do?
Skyrim.esm|06498A: Or perhaps you should crawl back into whatever swamp you came from, lizard man.
Skyrim.esm|06498B: No, it was Svidi's brother, Wulfur. She's in hiding now. Somewhere far from here I'd suspect.
Skyrim.esm|06498C: There's no need to be rude, Grelka. If you don't care for Argonians, then perhaps you should take residence somewhere else.
Skyrim.esm|06498D: Come to think of it, I haven't seen Svidi in a while. Wait... he didn't...
Skyrim.esm|0649A9: Don't have to tell me twice. But then, I'm not the one that buried iron under these mountains.
Skyrim.esm|0649AA: I can go back to my life for now. And I have you to thank for it. I won't forget this.
Skyrim.esm|0649AB: A risky game to play, friend. But now Kematu's gone, and the rest of his snakes should slither back to their desert.
Skyrim.esm|0649AC: You could have warned me you were baiting me into a trap. But you're right. This should scatter the rest of them. I can go back to my life for now. You have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0649AD: You liar! How could you do that to me!
Skyrim.esm|0649B5: Dragonborn. It is you. Welcome to High Hrothgar.
Skyrim.esm|0649B6: Come, we will do your next test inside the hall.
Skyrim.esm|0649B7: I am Master Arngeir. I speak for the Greybeards. Now tell me, Dragonborn, why have you come here?
Skyrim.esm|0649DF: Well, you got Maven, she pretty much runs the whole operation. She's got friends in high places if you know what I mean. She's also got ties to the Dark Brotherhood and the Thieves Guild, so basically no one can touch her. Just remember, if she tells you to do somethin'... you damn well better do exactly what she says.
Skyrim.esm|0649E0: Yeah? Well, I got news for you; there's nothing to see here. Last thing the Black-Briars need is some stranger stickin' their nose where it doesn't belong.
Skyrim.esm|0649E1: I don't know you. You in Riften lookin' for trouble?
Skyrim.esm|0649E2: Slip up and I'll gut you like a fish.
Skyrim.esm|0649E4: Pleasure doin' business with you. So what do you want to know?
Skyrim.esm|0649E5: You don't have enough to earn my loyalty.
Skyrim.esm|0649E8: Then we're speakin' the same language. Good. So what do you want to know?
Skyrim.esm|0649E9: Then you're stupider than you look. You better watch your ass around here.
Skyrim.esm|0649EA: You can pretend not to hear me all you want... but you better stay out of the Black-Briars' business.
Skyrim.esm|0649EB: Hey, you. Get over here, I want to talk to you.
Skyrim.esm|0649ED: Stay out of the Black-Briars' business and you'll live longer.
Skyrim.esm|0649EE: You aren't worth my time. Piss off.
Skyrim.esm|0649EF: I'm through with you. Get out of my way.
Skyrim.esm|0649F1: The Black-Briars have Riften in their pocket and the Thieves Guild watchin' their back, so keep your nose out of their business. Me? I'm Maul. I watch the streets for 'em. If you need dirt on anythin', I'm your guy... but it'll cost you.
Skyrim.esm|0649F3: Yeah, walk away like a good doggie. Just stay out of the Black-Briars' business and maybe we'll give you a bone.
Skyrim.esm|0649F4: That's the wrong answer. Last thing the Black-Briars need around here is some troublemaker tryin' to steal a piece of the action.
Skyrim.esm|0649F5: Suit yourself. I'll be at the docks if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0649F6: Don't say something you'll regret. Last thing the Black-Briars need is some loudmouth tryin' to meddle in their affairs.
Skyrim.esm|0649F7: You kiddin'? My brother Dirge works in their hideout. I used to run with them myself, but took a job with Maven after they started hittin' a rough patch. If you want to get in on that action, find Brynjolf in the marketplace. I'm sure he could use someone like you.
Skyrim.esm|0649F8: All right, all right, let's not get too hasty now. So what do you want to know?
Skyrim.esm|0649F9: Ha ha! Don't make me laugh.
Skyrim.esm|064AFA: Always sad when someone has to die.
Skyrim.esm|064B11: All right, let's give this a try. Ready your ward...
Skyrim.esm|064B55: It doesn't matter if they're in the city. We fight them where they live, we won't have to fight them here.
Skyrim.esm|064B56: Very well, you may approach the Mournful Throne, but watch your words.
Skyrim.esm|064B57: It's not that simple, Igmund. There are reports of Forsworn here in the city.
Skyrim.esm|064B58: You. Who are you to approach the Jarl of Markarth?
Skyrim.esm|064B59: The only thing we need to discuss is how many men it'll take to flush them out of the hills.
Skyrim.esm|064B5A: My Jarl, we need to discuss the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|064B5B: I don't know you, and I don't sell what precious property we have to outsiders. But if you're willing to work, and you prove to be reliable, I might deem you worthy to live here. My steward will handle things from there.
Skyrim.esm|064B5C: Yes. The finest court in all of High Rock. You think politics here are something? Well, we Bretons invented politics. That is where I learned to cook, and where I belong. Not here, with all these dogs.
Skyrim.esm|064B5E: Nothing to say. Fine, but I'm watching you.
Skyrim.esm|064B63: My mother and father yell a lot. Always have.
Skyrim.esm|064B64: My father is more generous than he lets on. Gives me plenty of spending money.
Skyrim.esm|064B66: Drinks here are watered down, but they're cheap.
Skyrim.esm|064B67: Treat my people fairly, and I will do the same to you.
Skyrim.esm|064B68: They live here too, my Jarl. That's what I'm telling you.
Skyrim.esm|064B69: You've honored my father's memory in more ways than I have words to say.
Skyrim.esm|064E71: Do what you will. Cicero has no fight left. In the end, Sithis will judge us both.
Skyrim.esm|064E72: You're back. Good. You'll want to hear this.
Skyrim.esm|064E73: Do me a favor and kill that little jester twice. Just to make sure.
Skyrim.esm|064E8F: I don't, I'm afraid. Ranmir didn't talk much about her after she'd left. He seemed convinced that she'd run off with someone named Vex. That's all I remember.
Skyrim.esm|064E90: Like I told you, last time I saw her she was headed for Hobs Fall Cave. No idea what happened to her.
Skyrim.esm|064E91: See, Ranmir was involved with a pretty young woman named Isabelle Rolaine. The two of them were very happy together, I thought. One day, she just disappeared. Ranmir was convinced she'd run off with someone else, leaving him behind. He turned to drinking, and what's left of him is what you see here every day.
Skyrim.esm|064E92: By the Eight. She's dead, isn't she? And it's all my fault.
Skyrim.esm|064E93: This... This is really from her, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|064E94: Well, I'm getting to that. Just let me explain.
Skyrim.esm|064E95: Not him again. He came nosing around Riften a while back, making a lot of noise about me. He was... persuaded to leave before he found me. Funny, I'd have thought she would've gone back to him by now.
Skyrim.esm|064E96: Huh? Ha. Sure you did. Dagur put you up to this? It don't matter now. None of it does. Just leave me be.
Skyrim.esm|064E98: Oh, I'm so glad you changed your mind. It's about Ranmir.
Skyrim.esm|064E99: Did she really run off and do this? For me?
Skyrim.esm|064E9A: I... I have some thinking to do. Thank you for this.
Skyrim.esm|064E9B: Oh, he was a different person. Happy, outgoing... Like anyone who's in love.
Skyrim.esm|064E9C: Don't get me wrong, I don't have a problem with him being here all the time. He mostly keeps to himself, and so far he's been able to pay. I just hate to see what's happened to him. He hasn't always been like this, you know. Drunk, I mean.
Skyrim.esm|064E9D: I'm not sure. I hoped that maybe if she could be found, if he had some sort of explanation, that maybe he could put himself back together. I'm in no position to go searching for her, but perhaps you could.
Skyrim.esm|064E9E: A what? Don't you go pulling my leg. Give it here.
Skyrim.esm|064E9F: Well, ain't gonna find her here. Haven't seen her in a while.
Skyrim.esm|064EA0: Well, not exactly. Listen, we're friends, right? I wondered if I could ask a favor of you.
Skyrim.esm|064EA1: Please, leave me alone. I need to be by myself.
Skyrim.esm|064EA2: After you talk to Brynjolf and Mercer, come back and see me.
Skyrim.esm|064EA4: Ah. Well, some other time, perhaps.
Skyrim.esm|064EA5: Unless it's gold for another round of drinks, I don't wanna hear it.
Skyrim.esm|064EA6: She came through a while ago, wanted to know where she could get her hands on something valuable. Anything, really. I told her it was a bad idea, that she wasn't cut out for what'd be necessary, but she wouldn't hear it. So I gave her some advice, pointed her towards Hobs Fall Cave and that was that.
Skyrim.esm|064EA7: What? Don't you... Don't you say that name to me. Don't you dare. Ever.
Skyrim.esm|064F69: For the moment we have ceased hostilities. We'll see what these Imperials have to say. I doubt we'll come to any lasting agreement.
Skyrim.esm|064F6B: Things are quiet for the moment. That won't last long. As soon as a new campaign starts, I want you there. Stay close. See me tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0653DB: Did you reconsider? Will you talk to my father?
Skyrim.esm|0653EE: Well, if you change your mind, I'd still like your help.
Skyrim.esm|0653F6: Seemed like the best way to see the world and make some coin. Sure I'm a little green, but what I lack in experience, I make up for in courage.
Skyrim.esm|0653F8: Can't blame you for having better things to do than help some farmer you don't know.
Skyrim.esm|0653F9: I can't thank you enough, friend. I'll hope you'll come back to Rorikstead soon and pay me a visit. Maybe we can swap stories about our adventures over a mug of ale at the inn!
Skyrim.esm|0653FA: Are you new around here? You should go to the inn if you need a place to stay.
Skyrim.esm|0653FD: Sissel thinks she's so special. No way she's more special than me.
Skyrim.esm|06541A: I had a dream that there was a good dragon. He was old and gray, but he wasn't scary.
Skyrim.esm|065494: Most days, I do all I can to stay away from my sister and my father. The beating's the same from either one.
Skyrim.esm|065496: I got enough troubles, so don't go adding to them.
Skyrim.esm|0654B7: I spend every day tending the fields, and what do my daughters do? Nothing! Complain and caterwaul! Sleep and eat! Useless!
Skyrim.esm|0654C0: Did you have any luck talking to my father?
Skyrim.esm|0654C2: I hope you can change my father's mind. He can be a little stubborn.
Skyrim.esm|0654C4: If you need a meal or a room, I've got both.
Skyrim.esm|065524: You talk to Reldith yet? She's like a mother to me.
Skyrim.esm|065525: Reldith is the hardest-working farmer in Rorikstead, but I'm the one with a head for business.
Skyrim.esm|065526: We're honest, hardworking folk here, and we don't suffer beggars or thieves.
Skyrim.esm|065527: City folk might look down on us, but what's wrong with farming? Everything we've got, we had to earn with our own sweat.
Skyrim.esm|065528: Sorry, I don't mean to be rude but Jouane's the one who deals with people. I'm afraid I lost my charm years ago.
Skyrim.esm|065529: If you're thinking of setting up to farm, you picked the right place.
Skyrim.esm|06552A: I'm sure it's hard to believe, but I was once a bold adventurer like you, ready to face any danger.
Skyrim.esm|06552B: Have you spoken to Erik? His father won't let him go out and seek his fortune in the world. Maybe you can help.
Skyrim.esm|06552C: Good to see you again! How would you like to hire a brave, if untested, mercenary?
Skyrim.esm|06552D: At first I thought I'd miss farming. I guess I've had my fill of it, because I don't miss it at all.
Skyrim.esm|06552E: It just feels right somehow, having this new life as an adventurer. It's what I was meant to do.
Skyrim.esm|06552F: This new armor fits me like a glove.
Skyrim.esm|065530: If I can make enough money here in Skyrim, I think I'll travel to Hammerfell and see what work I can get there.
Skyrim.esm|065705: Not yet. You know you're asking for some pretty strange stuff, don't you? It's not easy to find.
Skyrim.esm|065706: Always looking on the bright side, aren't you.
Skyrim.esm|065707: You assured me that you could find it, however.
Skyrim.esm|065708: I did, and I will. It's just tougher than I thought.
Skyrim.esm|065709: You looking for anything in particular this time?
Skyrim.esm|06570A: I certainly hope so, otherwise I'm wasting my time.
Skyrim.esm|06570B: No, without Ranmir around we're running out of stock far less often than we used to.
Skyrim.esm|06570D: Perhaps, perhaps. I'm not quite sure what I need.
Skyrim.esm|06570F: Well, you know the drill. You don't see it here, it's gonna cost you extra for me to get it.
Skyrim.esm|065710: I'm fully aware of the terms of our... arrangement.
Skyrim.esm|065711: Yeah, well I don't see anything.
Skyrim.esm|065712: Things are easier now that I don't have to worry about Ranmir anymore.
Skyrim.esm|065713: I still can't believe you were able to help Ranmir pull himself together.
Skyrim.esm|065714: Without all our money going to drink, I can afford to expand my selection a bit.
Skyrim.esm|065715: Have you gotten the items I requested from you?
Skyrim.esm|06571B: You got anything in here worth buying?
Skyrim.esm|06571C: Same as ever. Trying to keep from freezing to death, trying to keep food on the table.
Skyrim.esm|065733: That's right. Welcome to Markarth, traveler. Thank the Divines you can choose to live somewhere else.
Skyrim.esm|065734: Be careful not to fall on your way out.
Skyrim.esm|065737: Arnleif and Sons Trading Company. I'm a porter. Just some muscle hired to move heavy packages. But we don't get any shipments in. Forsworn attack every caravan. So all I do is sit here and drink.
Skyrim.esm|0658D1: Not while I'm still breathing, it's not.
Skyrim.esm|0658D9: The Legion will be staying here for quite some time. I don't expect there to be any further violence.
Skyrim.esm|0658E6: Ulfric Stormcloak! You are guilty of insurrection, murder of Imperial citizens, the assassination of King Torygg, and high treason against the Empire. It's over.
Skyrim.esm|0658E9: Brunwulf. Yes, I've sent men to protect him and bring him here. Windhelm will need a government quickly if we are to prevent more violence.
Skyrim.esm|0658EE: Well Ulfric, you can't escape from me this time. Any last requests before I send you to... to wherever you people go when you die.
Skyrim.esm|0658EF: I am proud of all of you. All hail the Emperor. All hail his Legionnaires!
Skyrim.esm|0658F1: Well, the men will be expecting some kind of speech. And we'll need to hand the city over to that Free-Winter fellow.
Skyrim.esm|0658F2: Come, Rikke. There's still much to be done.
Skyrim.esm|0658F3: Well? What are we waiting for?
Skyrim.esm|0658F7: We couldn't have done this without you. The Empire glories in your accomplishments...
Skyrim.esm|0658F8: Enough! You are traitors and will die traitors' deaths. Stand down and face public execution, or advance and face summary execution by my hands. It matters little to me. Either way I'll be sending your heads back to Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|0658FA: In appreciation for your exemplary service, I am doubling your pay and compensation to the widows of your fallen comrades.
Skyrim.esm|0658FE: I pray you're right, Legate. In the meantime, we'll continue to root them out and put them to the sword.
Skyrim.esm|065900: Here, use my sword to do it. I made sure to sharpen it for the occasion.
Skyrim.esm|06591A: No... But I belong to her.
Skyrim.esm|06591C: We are turning the city over to Brunwulf Free-Winter, an honorable and faithful man. Many of you will be staying in Windhelm to aid the Jarl in restoring order and stamping out any embers of rebellion that may still smolder here.
Skyrim.esm|06591D: Song or not, I just want it done.
Skyrim.esm|065929: Skyrim doesn't belong to you, Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|065931: His head will be sent to Cyrodiil where it will adorn a spike on the walls of the Imperial City. Let this day be a final warning to all who would still call themselves Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|065932: It'll make for a better song.
Skyrim.esm|065936: Oh, and Legate. It's been an honor to have you at my side. As a token of my appreciation, I want you to keep my sword. And now, to deliver a speech.
Skyrim.esm|065938: There's bound to be resistance. There are many Stormcloak camps tucked away in the hills. They'll no doubt strike whenever and wherever they can. But without Ulfric to inflame their passions, they'll settle down and return to their homes eventually.
Skyrim.esm|065939: I'll never surrender Skyrim into the hands of a corrupt and dying Empire.
Skyrim.esm|06593E: Let the Dragonborn be the one to do it.
Skyrim.esm|065940: I hope we haven't just created a martyr.
Skyrim.esm|065949: The rebellion is over. Ulfric Stormcloak is dead.
Skyrim.esm|06594B: Well, I'm not quite done myself.
Skyrim.esm|06594F: Step aside Galmar. We're here to accept Ulfric's surrender.
Skyrim.esm|065950: Attention! General Tullius has an announcement!
Skyrim.esm|0659AE: My spies report the Jarl's Steward, Anuriel, has arrangements with the Thieves Guild that would be rather embarrassing if made public. I need you to find evidence of her corrupt activities, and use it to gain a measure of her "cooperation."
Skyrim.esm|0659AF: Rumor has it the Jarl's Steward, Raerek, is a faithful Talos worshipper, if not a true son of Skyrim - he still supports the Empire, after all. But, if confronted with proof of his belief, you might be able to "persuade" him to aid our cause, indirectly of course. Wouldn't want to sully his reputation, would we?
Skyrim.esm|065AE1: I told you everything I know. What else did you want?
Skyrim.esm|065AE3: I thank you for your help, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|065AE6: I should be the one thanking you. You've solved more problems in the short time you've been here then anyone in my court has their entire careers. You've been a beacon of hope in these dark times for my people and I will never forget it. Therefore, by my authority as Jarl, I pronounce you Thane of Riften and award you all of the benefits befitting your station. Congratulations.
Skyrim.esm|065AE7: I expect that you'll distance yourself from the affairs of state. In other words, keep your nose out of my business and we'll do just fine. Just remember that you're only a part of my court, this doesn't make you one of the family. I guess all that's left is to bestow the title. What were those words again? Doesn't matter... the title is yours. I henceforth name you Thane of Riften. Don't disappoint me.
Skyrim.esm|065AE9: Well done! Your continued efforts have been of great benefit to the people of Riften. Allow me to present you with this compensation for your selfless efforts.
Skyrim.esm|065AEA: I'll send my men to secure any of the remaining goods you left behind. We can't have that substance falling into the wrong hands now, can we? Here, this should suffice as your cut of the... or rather, a reward for your actions. Now, if you'll excuse me...
Skyrim.esm|065AEC: Much like Mjoll, you've become champion of our hold; helping people with their difficulties and providing assistance for their needs. As the Jarl of Riften, I feel it's my duty to honor your selfless behavior by honoring you with the title Thane of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|065AED: Oh don't pretend to be modest. I'm talking about your new role as champion. Apparently, you've taken my people under your wing. If you think you can steal my throne by winning the hearts of the people, that's where you're wrong. Keep that in mind. However, since it wouldn't behoove me to make you disappear, I'd rather have you on my side as Thane of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|065AF0: Ah, there you are. I was wondering when you'd visit us again. I've been informed by my steward that you've made quite an impression in the Rift; several of our citizens have expressed their admiration.
Skyrim.esm|065AF1: So, I've been hearing your name in court quite a bit lately. I've been informed by my steward that you've made quite an impression in the Rift; the people won't stop blathering about you.
