Skyrim.esm|065AF4: You've already taken all the necessary steps in becoming eligible for the title. Therefore all that remains is to bestow it upon you.
Skyrim.esm|065AF5: You damn well better. I've checked with my court and apparently you're fully eligible... there's nothing standing in the way of presenting you the title.
Skyrim.esm|065AF6: Before I can present you with the title, there's but a single requirement you must fulfill. A Thane of Riften is expected to maintain permanent residence within the city walls. My steward has such a home available to you for purchase. I think you'll find the accommodations to be most pleasing. When the house is yours, return to me and I will present you the title.
Skyrim.esm|065AF7: Not so fast, there's a matter of residence to be discussed. In order to have the title bestowed upon you, you need to purchase a house within Riften. And by the Eight, aren't you fortunate that my steward has just the place for you to buy. After the place is yours, come back to me and we'll deal with the rest of the nonsense.
Skyrim.esm|065AF8: I think I've said enough. Now leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|065AF9: Be careful, Niluva is a bit... aggressive when she's cornered.
Skyrim.esm|065AFA: Then allow me to officially welcome you to Riften. You'll make a fine addition to our city. All that remains now is to bestow the title upon you.
Skyrim.esm|065AFB: Hmm? What's this about? Oh, yes. Of course... the whole thane thing. I'd forgotten. If you're ready then, can we get this out of the way?
Skyrim.esm|065C24: Not at all. It's good to be put in your place once in a while. I guess I shouldn't boast so much... it gets expensive!
Skyrim.esm|065C25: Sure, I don't mind taking some more of your gold. Just let me know when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|065C26: I'll fight you again, but not until you have the gold to back up your bet.
Skyrim.esm|065C27: I don't blame you. If I were in your shoes, I'd chicken out too. Well, offer still stands if you ever want to have a go.
Skyrim.esm|065C28: I don't believe it. I guess you were right!
Skyrim.esm|065C29: All right, remember... this is a clean fight. No weapons, no tricks. And none of those fancy magic spells, either. Here we go!
Skyrim.esm|065C2A: Well come back when you earn some more so I can take it from you!
Skyrim.esm|065C2D: Got horses that need shoeing, so make it quick.
Skyrim.esm|065C2E: Oh! Sounds like I've got a challenger. Tell you what. How about a friendly wager, just between us. I bet you my entire purse of septims I can beat you in a fistfight. You match the bet and we fight until one of us gives in.
Skyrim.esm|065C2F: Bah, I'm not afraid of anything out here. Walls are just a weakling's hiding spot. Now me, I'm no weakling. In fact, I bet I've got the strongest arms in all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|065C41: Forgive me milord, I didn't recognize you! Please proceed.
Skyrim.esm|065C42: Forgive me milady, I didn't recognize you! Please proceed.
Skyrim.esm|065C44: Sure, that'll do just fine. Go on ahead.
Skyrim.esm|065C45: That's just insulting. Not even a proper bribe?
Skyrim.esm|065C46: Oh, my mistake then. Sorry, you can go on ahead.
Skyrim.esm|065C47: Sure, and I'm Queen Barenziah. Now tell me another one.
Skyrim.esm|065C48: Hey, you're not supposed to be down here. Official business only.
Skyrim.esm|065C49: Is it daylight out? Night? I lose track down here.
Skyrim.esm|065C4A: The prisoners are in rare form, as usual.
Skyrim.esm|065C4B: Watch your step in here. The stonework gets slippery.
Skyrim.esm|065C4C: Now that the Empire's arrived in Riften, we've finally established a launching point into Morrowind... just in case.
Skyrim.esm|065C4D: I'm kind of busy, but if you need any potions or ingredients, let me know.
Skyrim.esm|065C4E: I'll be over here if you need any potions or ingredients.
Skyrim.esm|065C4F: Hello there, can I pour you a Black-Briar Mead?
Skyrim.esm|065C50: Ah good, back for some more Black-Briar mead, eh?
Skyrim.esm|065C51: Welcome to the Pawned Prawn. Come on in, take a look around.
Skyrim.esm|065C52: Welcome back to the Prawn. Let me know if you need anything.
Skyrim.esm|065C61: What are you doing down here?
Skyrim.esm|065C62: Ignore me all you want, you still aren't getting in.
Skyrim.esm|065C69: I trust the day's found you well, thane.
Skyrim.esm|065C70: The fiercest of the remaining rebels will continue to harass us, but by in large, the people here desire peace. What I'm not so sure about is the peace we've made with the Thalmor. But we'll keep that between the two of us, alright?
Skyrim.esm|065C71: There are those who still call themselves Stormcloaks, who continue to fight us, and bring misery to the people... but they are few in number. The ordinary citizen will be happy to get back to life as normal, to have their families return home. And they should enjoy it while they can... I suspect all of Tamriel will again be called to arms in the not too distant future.
Skyrim.esm|065C72: There will be peace for a time, during which we must rebuild Skyrim into the land it once was. Strong. Self-reliant. The center of mankind. Because getting rid of the Empire was only half the problem. Soon, the elves will again seek to rule the world. We must ready ourselves to fight them. For it will be Skyrim that shall lead Tamriel in those dark days, when the fate of the world is finally determined.
Skyrim.esm|065C73: Peace? When has any Nord ever known peace? Skyrim breeds warriors. It's who we are, it's in our blood. And that's as it should be. In the darkest days ahead, it'll be us Nords who lead the fight against the elves. It's only a matter of time before their pointy ears start itching to rule the world again. It'll be Skyrim that leads the charge. Mark my words. And that time around, mankind will either succeed once and for all or die trying.
Skyrim.esm|065C74: Ulfric is dead and his army destroyed. There's not much more I could ask of you. The gods, however, I suspect, may have plans for you elsewhere. That said, Stormcloak true believers are still operating out of hidden military camps in the hills. If you encounter any on you travels, kill them. With luck, they'll soon lose heart and return to their homes and families. If not, we'll be forced to put them all to the sword.
Skyrim.esm|065C75: The Legion's work is far from finished, but you are free to roam. I suspect you'll be of greater good to Skyrim out there, in the world. If while you're out there, you stumble into any Stormcloak military camps, I expect you to destroy them. I've reports they are hiding in the hills.
Skyrim.esm|065C76: True sons and daughters of Skyrim reign in every city, we've crippled the Imperial Legion, and cut off its head by killing General Tullius himself.
Skyrim.esm|065C77: No. I trust you'll do more good out there in the world, wherever you damn well please to go. You're a true son of Skyrim. You'll know what to do. To begin with, killing any Legionnaire you find out in the wilderness. I've reports they've hidden military camps out there.
Skyrim.esm|065C78: No. I trust you'll do more good out there in the world, wherever you damn well please to go. You're a true daughter of Skyrim and'll know what to do.
Skyrim.esm|065C79: No, I suspect Skyrim to be my home for many years. Can't say I'll ever get used to the damn cold, or understand these Nords... but, I've come to respect them. The harshness of Skyrim has a way of carving a man down to his true self.
Skyrim.esm|065C7A: The General's put me in charge of assisting the new governments. I may understand things here in Skyrim better than the General, but I'm a soldier at heart, not a politician. But I'll do my best. This is the land of my birth, the land that shaped me. I'm proud to have any part in making it strong again.
Skyrim.esm|065C7B: No, not yet. We'll wait for the Moot to name me High King. It'll be better for all that way. But, that doesn't mean I won't start acting like it. There's much to do. The new Jarls need help building armies, and enforcing their right to rule. The Empire may try to reclaim Skyrim, and I need everyone ready for that. Though the biggest threat, of course, is the elves.
Skyrim.esm|065C7C: Heh. Ulfric's put me in charge of keeping an eye on the new Jarls. You know, make sure they're generally following orders. Can't say I'm looking forward to that. But, I'll also be training men and women for their new armies. The Empire might send over a few Legionnaires to make sure we're serious, and of course, we'll be taking the fight to the elves soon enough. We'll need trained, disciplined and creative warriors ready for all that.
Skyrim.esm|065C7D: I'm ready. I made my peace with the gods long ago.
Skyrim.esm|065C7E: What are you waiting for? Do it!
Skyrim.esm|065C7F: With me, you kill Skyrim's last hope. Mark my words. You'll all be serving elven masters soon. And know then, that I would have stopped them.
Skyrim.esm|065C80: By the gods, stop toying with me and do it!
Skyrim.esm|065C81: We're attacking Riften. Meet the soldiers gathering for the attack. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|065C82: We're mounting our final attack on Windhelm. You're needed on the front line. Get a move on soldier!
Skyrim.esm|065C83: We're taking Dawnstar. Get down there and join the rest of the men gathering for the attack. Now soldier!
Skyrim.esm|065C84: We're taking Winterhold. Get over there and join the rest of the men gathering for the attack. Now soldier!
Skyrim.esm|065C85: We're taking Whiterun. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Skyrim.esm|065C86: We're mounting our attack on Markarth. Get over there and join the brothers gathering for the attack. This is it now! Talos be with you.
Skyrim.esm|065C87: We're mounting our final attack on Solitude. Get over there and join the rest of the army. They'll be launching the attack soon. Talos be with you!
Skyrim.esm|065C88: We're taking Falkreath. Get over there with the others. Fight well or die well.
Skyrim.esm|065C89: We're liberating Morthal. Join the brothers gathering for the attack. Talos be with you.
Skyrim.esm|065C8A: You've little time to glory in your accomplishments. We're gathering for our final assault on Windhelm. Report to our camp in Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|065C8B: You've no time to glory in your accomplishments. We're gathering for our final assault on Solitude. I need you there. Report to our camp in Haafingar.
Skyrim.esm|06617C: Oh my. That... You... you go first.
Skyrim.esm|066189: What've you been staring at your feet this whole time? You see that big city up ahead of you? Markarth? Head over there, unless you feel like helping my wife pick crops. And then 50 years later you'll wake up and still be here.
Skyrim.esm|06618A: What kind of question is that? Look at me! Is this a young man's face? I've been here 50 years, and nothing is going to move me off my land.
Skyrim.esm|0661BC: As part of your initiation, Master Einarth will allow you to tap into his understanding of "Ro."
Skyrim.esm|0661BD: Well, that is how the rest of us learn Shouts. As Dragonborn, you can absorb a slain dragon's life force and knowledge directly.
Skyrim.esm|0661BE: But learning a Word of Power is only the first step... you must unlock its meaning through constant practice in order to use it in a Shout.
Skyrim.esm|0661BF: All Shouts are made up of three Words of Power. As you master each Word, your Shout will become progressively stronger.
Skyrim.esm|0661C0: Ro means "Balance" in the dragon tongue. Combine it with Fus - "Force" - to focus your Thu'um more sharply.
Skyrim.esm|06624B: Do you think the Forsworn will come here? I mean, if they attacked Kolskeggr...
Skyrim.esm|06624C: Those Forsworn were fierce fighters. Fiercer than I could have imagined.
Skyrim.esm|06624D: Thank the Divines we survived that.
Skyrim.esm|06624E: Well I'm glad you were there. I don't know how I would have gotten past the Forsworn without you.
Skyrim.esm|06624F: That I do. If you want to grab a pickaxe and dig up some iron, feel free. I'll pay for anything you bring me.
Skyrim.esm|066250: Guess we'll have two more mouths to feed for a while.
Skyrim.esm|066251: Don't have much to say unless you're here to mine metal.
Skyrim.esm|066252: Did you hear that? Pavo was driven out of his mine. Almost everyone is dead.
Skyrim.esm|066253: What if the Forsworn come here? We aren't safe.
Skyrim.esm|066254: You should speak with my husband. Skaggi runs everything.
Skyrim.esm|066257: I'm one of miners. Plenty of ore to dig up around here.
Skyrim.esm|066258: Don't have much to say to you. Got work.
Skyrim.esm|066259: It's amazing how much work goes into the little things, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|06625A: I'm just here to dig. Not much else to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|06625B: Thank Malacath we got out of Kolskeggr alive.
Skyrim.esm|06625C: Don't know what I would have done without Pavo. Man's braver than he admits.
Skyrim.esm|06625D: Another day, another settlement gets attacked. No place in The Reach is safe from the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|06625E: I'm the hardest worker in Left Hand. Better than any man.
Skyrim.esm|06625F: Hope Pavo will be able to go back to his mine soon. Everyone needs a home.
Skyrim.esm|066260: Whatcha doing in Left Hand? Do you dig up rocks, too, like my mom?
Skyrim.esm|066261: Let's just hope that doesn't happen.
Skyrim.esm|066322: Backup! I need some help in here!
Skyrim.esm|0663C8: Now that our "ghost" has been unmasked, I hope we see more visitors in town than just the occasional pilgrim.
Skyrim.esm|0663C9: Narfi's happy now. Maybe Narfi can sleep. Sleep all night. Sleep, sleep, sleep.
Skyrim.esm|0663CA: I wish I could make my deliveries more often, but the road's getting dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|0663CB: I'm going to put those pelts you gave me to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0663CC: So, have you shown those furry menaces that I mean business?
Skyrim.esm|0663CD: These damn bears are driving me crazy!
Skyrim.esm|0663CE: I swear, if one more thing goes wrong, I'm going to burn my mill to the ground!
Skyrim.esm|0663CF: Miss Temba is much happier now that you took care of those bears. She may not look like she is, but I can tell.
Skyrim.esm|0663D0: Those bears are making Miss Temba so angry. I hope you can do something about them.
Skyrim.esm|0663D1: Hello, my friend? What can I do for you on such a fine day?
Skyrim.esm|0663D2: Such a fine day, smell the air! Isn't it wonderful?
Skyrim.esm|0665CF: I protest! This is an insult to the dignity of my person! This time I'm completely innocent!
Skyrim.esm|0665D0: Oh, Ambassador Elenwen, that won't be necessary. It is just Razelan, after all. I'm sure he'll behave himself for the rest of the evening, won't you dear?
Skyrim.esm|0665D1: Absolutely not! I protest... uh, that is, yes, of course. I still don't understand what just... oh, never mind.
Skyrim.esm|0665D2: The head office will hear of this, Razelan! You're a disgrace to the East Empire Company, and to the Empire itself!
Skyrim.esm|0665D3: Of course! I mean... assuming you don't want me to kill someone for you. I might have to draw the line there.
Skyrim.esm|0665D5: Just play along. How dare you! You disgusting pig!
Skyrim.esm|0665D6: You must be new around here. Bad manners to ask such a direct question, at one of Elenwen's little soirees. But I have nothing to hide. I'm in from the south, on business. And if you want to do business in Tamriel these days, well, you'd better get used to cozying up to the Thalmor. Like it or not.
Skyrim.esm|0665D7: Razelan. And you promised to behave yourself this time.
Skyrim.esm|0665D8: Ah, pardon me, friend. I didn't see you standing there. Allow me to introduce myself: Razelan. Imports and exports, by trade. Observer of human nature, by avocation.
Skyrim.esm|0665D9: Well. Anything to relieve the boredom. As long as it doesn't get back to me.
Skyrim.esm|0665DA: Remove him. He's disturbing the other guests.
Skyrim.esm|0665DC: Ah, the nectar of the gods! If there's ever anything I can do for you, just name it!
Skyrim.esm|0665DD: Indeed. Being a drunken boor is thirsty work. And I can't seem to find my way back to the bar...
Skyrim.esm|0665DE: I find these parties such a dreadful bore.
Skyrim.esm|0665DF: One thing everyone can agree on. This war is bad for business.
Skyrim.esm|0665E0: The East Empire Company has interests all across Tamriel, inside and outside the Empire's borders. We try not to get involved in politics. However, as the head of the company it's my job to make sure we maintain good relationships with prospective partners and investors.
Skyrim.esm|0665E1: What? I didn't... hmm? No, listen, you must have misunderstood... I don't mean to say I wouldn't be interested... what I mean is...
Skyrim.esm|0665E2: What does a fellow need to do to get a drink around here?
Skyrim.esm|0665E4: My friend! A toast to a beautiful friendship!
Skyrim.esm|066648: Mara be praised! Nightcaller Temple is only a short walk from Dawnstar. Come, we must hurry.
Skyrim.esm|066649: I understand. If you should change your mind, I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|06664C: Turn my back on these people when their lives could be at stake? I should say not. Those dreams are not just visions, they're a footprint left by the Daedric Lord Vaermina as she drains these people of their memories!
Skyrim.esm|066657: In any other circumstance, I'd agree with you. However, these aren't normal dreams. The Daedric Lord Vaermina's voracious hunger for memories is responsible for these visions. The nightmares are an echo of her presence as she feeds.
Skyrim.esm|066658: Who can say? Perhaps she collects them for display like works of art in a nonsensical art gallery. Whatever the case may be, her intentions are far from benevolent.
Skyrim.esm|06696B: Then allow me to introduce myself.
Skyrim.esm|06696C: I am Savos Aren, Arch-Mage of the College of Winterhold. I am quite content to see nearly any aspect of magic explored and investigated here. But I do not and will not approve of any research or experiments that cause purposeful harm to your fellow members of the College. Are we clear?
Skyrim.esm|06696F: You are relatively new here, are you not? I have noticed you, but we have not spoken.
Skyrim.esm|066970: You are new here, are you not? We have not spoken.
Skyrim.esm|066972: No. No I can't. I have enough to keep me busy. View it as a chance to hone your craft.
Skyrim.esm|066973: New here too, eh? It's good to see another Nord. I feared I'd be the only one. Almost doesn't feel like Skyrim, being so far away from the rest of the world here.
Skyrim.esm|066974: New here too, eh? I'd hoped I wouldn't be the only Nord, but I should've known better.
Skyrim.esm|066976: Not often, no. Some risks must be taken, to be sure. I am simply trying to avoid untimely deaths. We also must make an effort to avoid worsening what Skyrim thinks of us.
Skyrim.esm|066977: I'm sure you can figure it out on your own. If you can't, perhaps Urag has a book you can read.
Skyrim.esm|066978: I can teach you, but I won't be held responsible for what you do with the knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|06697A: Well, there's an Imbuer here in the Great Hall.
Skyrim.esm|06697B: Well, there's an Imbuer in the Great Hall.
Skyrim.esm|06717B: No harm is meant to you, happy fool. Right here! I see it in your face! The snakes writhe behind your eyes! Get away, get away from me!
Skyrim.esm|06717C: You take it back then? You retract your statement? Well?
Skyrim.esm|06717D: Well, now. There's no need to ruin the party on my account. I don't think he'll be bothering me again.
Skyrim.esm|0674D2: Excellent. I can move more soldiers through the area now that I know those scouts are dead.
Skyrim.esm|0674D3: Good job! With those Imperial scouts out of the way, I can be more aggressive about where I send our men.
Skyrim.esm|0674E6: I'm in the middle of something. But don't go far. I'll need you soon.
Skyrim.esm|0674E7: I've got my hands full with something. Don't go far. I'll need you soon.
Skyrim.esm|06767A: Yes. I have something a little different planned this time. But it's not for everyone to hear. Meet me in the Underforge tonight. We will speak more.
Skyrim.esm|06767B: The Eldergleam has blessed us with a sapling.
Skyrim.esm|0676DD: You're sweet. I'm just not ready for that sort of thing right now. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|0676DE: Blood from a stone, Klimmek. I've been out there every morning. They just aren't biting.
Skyrim.esm|0676DF: When I want your opinion, I'll ask for it. Otherwise, mind your business.
Skyrim.esm|0676E0: I'm sorry. I was curious... I didn't believe the stories. I won't go over there ever again.
Skyrim.esm|0676E1: You're hardly throwing her to the wolves. She just wants to see the world... just like I did when I was her age.
Skyrim.esm|0676E3: Come on, everyone in town knows that you spend more time with Fastred than fishing in the river.
Skyrim.esm|0676E4: I heard Temba yelling at you yesterday. Why do you let her treat you like that?
Skyrim.esm|0676E5: Gwilin! I told you to cut those boards at twice arm length! My arm's length. Not yours.
Skyrim.esm|0676E6: See that you don't. I promised to keep you safe and I'm not going to break my word.
Skyrim.esm|0676E7: And if she decides to leave Ivarstead, what then? No, no... I can't bear the thought.
Skyrim.esm|0676E8: What's the point? I'm not very good, and it's only for the same few people every night.
Skyrim.esm|0676E9: Have you spoken to Fastred yet?
Skyrim.esm|0676EA: That's none of your business and I appreciate if you wouldn't mention it again.
Skyrim.esm|0676EB: I'm so sorry Miss Temba. Perhaps if you would have been more specific...
Skyrim.esm|0676EC: Klimmek is doing just fine. You should follow your heart.
Skyrim.esm|0676ED: If you'll pardon the expression, she's far more bark than bite.
Skyrim.esm|0676EF: I need you to pitch in a little more around here, dear.
Skyrim.esm|0676F0: If you have a moment, I've come up with a few ideas I wanted to share.
Skyrim.esm|0676F1: Sigh. Everyone isn't as short as you are... what good are these boards to someone of the proper height!?
Skyrim.esm|0676F2: Perhaps, but abuse is abuse. I don't think I could stand it.
Skyrim.esm|0676F3: No. I'll tell you, Wilhelm, if I could sweep her away from here tomorrow I'd do it, but Klimmek still has so much to learn.
Skyrim.esm|0676F4: I know, but I don't want to let them down. I guess I better start thinking about it soon.
Skyrim.esm|0676F5: I help, I do the chores. What else would you have me do?
Skyrim.esm|0676F7: Perhaps one day, but not today.
Skyrim.esm|0676F8: There's much more to worry about in life then a bit of a temper, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|0676F9: I think you play beautifully. It would be a shame if you stopped.
Skyrim.esm|0676FA: I'm becoming sick to death of your ideas. Not one of them has paid off since you moved in.
Skyrim.esm|0676FB: I thought you played wonderfully last night.
Skyrim.esm|0676FC: I know your sights are set beyond this town, but for now, while you're under our roof, I need you to pitch in a little more.
Skyrim.esm|0676FD: Fastred, I wanted to talk with you.
Skyrim.esm|0676FE: Look, I'm merely suggesting a few things that could expand our business.
Skyrim.esm|0676FF: Wilhelm said he saw you speaking to one of the pilgrims on the 7,000 Steps. I told you that I didn't want you to go up there.
Skyrim.esm|067700: All right, Wilhlem. I suppose it's the least I can do for you.
Skyrim.esm|067701: You heading up to High Hrothgar soon, Klimmek?
Skyrim.esm|067703: Thank you Klimmek. That's too kind.
Skyrim.esm|067704: I know what you said, but I only wanted to speak to him for a moment.
Skyrim.esm|067705: "Our" business? As I remember things, it was you that showed up at my doorstep looking for a job and a place to live. Try and remember that.
Skyrim.esm|067706: I think you should continue with your performances.
Skyrim.esm|067707: I think I saw it again. That... ghost. It was over by the barrows.
Skyrim.esm|067708: You have to let Fastred follow her heart. You can't tell her what to do.
Skyrim.esm|067709: I wish I didn't have to. It's quite a difficult climb.
Skyrim.esm|06770A: Another batch of logs completely ruined. I swear, these bears have it out for me.
Skyrim.esm|06770B: The Greybeards are a solitary lot. I don't think they've ever ventured outside their monastery. We get the occasional pilgrim passing through here on their way to the summit, but almost all of them have returned disappointed.
Skyrim.esm|06770C: I've been to the monastery many times, but I've never even laid eyes on one of the Greybeards. Not that I'd care to. Being masters of the Thu'um, they could kill you by uttering a single word. Well, not that they would. They seem peaceful, but I wouldn't want to provoke them.
Skyrim.esm|06770D: Klimmek brings food supplies up to them once every few weeks when the weather permits it. Other than that, they seem like a quiet lot. Don't really know too much about them.
Skyrim.esm|06770E: It's frightening living below their monastery. Sometimes I swear I can hear strange noises rolling down from up there. It sounds like thunder, but there's never any rain. What do you make of that?
Skyrim.esm|06770F: The Greybeards stay away from me, and I stay away from them. Suits me just fine.
Skyrim.esm|067710: Always thought it was odd that there's a layer of thick clouds covering the peak of the mountain above the monastery. Not sure what's up there, but I bet the Greybeards know.
Skyrim.esm|067711: They call the path to the monastery the 7,000 Steps. Can you imagine? I'm not certain if I could even make it to the top without collapsing from exhaustion.
Skyrim.esm|067712: The Greybeards are a strange bunch. I heard they live their entire life without uttering a single word; can you even imagine?
Skyrim.esm|067713: I've always fancied a journey up the 7,000 Steps to the monastery. Anything to break the boredom of living in this town. I envy you.
Skyrim.esm|067714: Perhaps when you're finished here this evening, we could...
Skyrim.esm|067715: We're behind again. I need you to catch another two bushels by week's end if I'm to make another delivery.
Skyrim.esm|067716: That thing's evil, Lynly! I told you to keep away from there!
Skyrim.esm|067717: She's our only child, Boti. Am I to throw her to the wolves?
Skyrim.esm|067718: I hardly think the bears are clever enough to hold a grudge against you, Miss Temba.
Skyrim.esm|067719: Take your time then. It isn't as though the Greybeards are going to complain.
Skyrim.esm|067CC6: Ah, and let me guess. He wants his horse. Well, that's going to be a bit of a problem. You see, I don't really outright "own" Frost...
Skyrim.esm|067CC7: No idea, but whatever he did it must have angered Maven. No guard would lock up a Black-Briar without the old lady's permission.
Skyrim.esm|067CC8: Then I think we're done talking.
Skyrim.esm|067CC9: That is correct. You see, Frost belongs to the Black-Briar estate. Technically everything in the estate is owned by my mother, Maven. My plan was to take the horse from our lodge and deliver it to Letrush at the stables. Obviously, that didn't exactly work out as I'd planned... Still, I don't want to cross Letrush. Tell you what. Steal the horse and deliver it, and you can have the second half of the payment.
Skyrim.esm|067CCB: I'm aware of who stole Frost. And why.
Skyrim.esm|067CCC: Ah, you have returned. How goes the task I gave you?
Skyrim.esm|067CCD: I have faith that you'll return with King Olaf's Verse.
Skyrim.esm|067DDA: Secondly, some rebel scouts that have seen too much are moving through the hold. Need to take them out before they report back to their leaders.
Skyrim.esm|067DDB: Secondly, we got a couple of Imperial spies roaming through the hold. Need to put an end to that.
Skyrim.esm|067DDC: First off, we've learned the location of some rebels scouting our positions throughout the hold. Need to take them out.
Skyrim.esm|067DDD: First off, there's a couple of Imperials skulking about the hold. Need to cut their throats so they can't make their reports.
Skyrim.esm|067E83: Congratulations on your wedding. I'm so happy for both of you.
Skyrim.esm|067EC5: No, wait! I was just... catching my breath... why wouldn't I tell you again? I don't even know anything...
Skyrim.esm|067EC6: Hmm. Very well. I'm putting my reputation on the line for you.
Skyrim.esm|067EC8: There's an old man. He lives in Riften. He could be this Esbern you're looking for, but I don't know. He's old and seemed kind of crazy. That's all I know.
Skyrim.esm|067EC9: No... for pity's sake... I've already told you everything...
Skyrim.esm|067ECA: I don't know his name. Like I've told already a hundred-
Skyrim.esm|067ECB: Don't presume upon our friendship. My first loyalty is to the Thalmor, as you well know.
Skyrim.esm|067ECE: You know the rules. Just answer the questions.
Skyrim.esm|067ED0: Your insults and provocations have gone far enough! I'd kill you where you stand if I wasn't bound by my oath as an officer of the Aldmeri Dominion.
Skyrim.esm|067ED1: And where can we find this nameless old man?
Skyrim.esm|067ED2: To maintain relationships. By the way, I don't know who you are, but unless you want me to blow your cover, I'd advise that you avoid me for the rest of the party.
Skyrim.esm|067ED3: Stop. Please. I don't know anything else. Don't you think I'd have told you already?
Skyrim.esm|067ED4: Start at the beginning, as usual.
Skyrim.esm|067ED5: How dare you speak of the Thalmor in such a disgusting manner!
Skyrim.esm|067ED8: That's it? Sure. Shouldn't be too hard. I usually end up making a fool out of myself without even trying.
Skyrim.esm|067ED9: This is very irregular. I trust that whatever you're doing doesn't compromise my position in any way?
Skyrim.esm|067EDA: Like I said, I don't know! I've seen him down in the Ratway. Maybe he lives down there, but I don't know for sure.
Skyrim.esm|067EDF: I don't recall seeing you at one of these before. And I know everyone who's anyone in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|067EE0: That's General Tullius himself. He can't stand Elenwen, but she insists that he attend these parties. Just to piss him off, no doubt.
Skyrim.esm|067EE1: See that disgusting drunk over there? A high-ranking official in the East Empire Company, believe it or not.
Skyrim.esm|067EE2: That will be all for now. I must say I continue to be disappointed in your lack of cooperation. I hope next time you will do better.
Skyrim.esm|067EE3: What else do you want from me? I've already told you everything. Listen, if you let me go I can take you to Riften, show you where-
Skyrim.esm|067EE4: If you persist in this stubbornness I'll have...
Skyrim.esm|067EE5: Forgive me, Ambassador. I allowed this... fool to provoke me. The fault is mine. There is no need for further disruption on my account.
Skyrim.esm|067EE6: Silence. You know the rules. Do not speak unless spoken to. Master Rulindil will ask the questions.
Skyrim.esm|067EE8: What? I didn't... hmm? No, listen, you must have misunderstood... I would never openly insult your... that is to say...
Skyrim.esm|067EE9: There are those in the Empire who would wish to evade their obligations to help root out the Talos heresy. Fortunately, those most opposed to the Emperor's wise policy have now branded themselves traitors as well as heretics. I am here to remind the ruling classes of Skyrim that their loyalty to the Emperor requires cooperation with the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|067EEB: It's a necessary part of my job to mix with the upper classes of Skyrim, such as they are.
Skyrim.esm|067EEC: The degeneracy of the Empire is on display here in this room.
Skyrim.esm|067EED: It's only a matter of time before your whole rotten Empire collapses of its own decay. No offense.
Skyrim.esm|067EEE: None of these people cares a whit about the religious aspects of this war. Another sign of the degeneracy of your Empire.
Skyrim.esm|067EEF: At least Ulfric's men are willing to fight for their principles, barbaric as they may be.
Skyrim.esm|068169: We need to go upstairs, that's where she is! That's where the books are!
Skyrim.esm|06816A: Are you quite sure? I don't want to get in the way, but...
Skyrim.esm|06816B: Well yes, I did. And thank you so much for helping me. But... I rather thought that if I were to help, I might... Well, I might be allowed to return to the College. I'm not sure where else I'd go.
Skyrim.esm|06816C: Wait, what? I thought I was going to help you!
Skyrim.esm|06816D: But... I rather thought that if I were to help, I might... Well, I might be allowed to return to the College.
Skyrim.esm|06816E: No, no! This is the wrong way!
Skyrim.esm|068195: Hjalti? Is that you? I've been waiting.
Skyrim.esm|068196: Trust like that is not freely given.
Skyrim.esm|068197: The most well known stronghold is Dushnikh Yal. Here, I'll mark it down for you.
Skyrim.esm|068198: You promised me, Hjalti. You promised that when we sacked Hroldan, you would make me your sworn brother. And I've waited. Even after the enemies' arrows dug into my chest and their hammers crushed my bones. I've waited. Give me your sword, Hjalti. That we may become brothers as you promised.
Skyrim.esm|0681B8: Hmph. As I was saying... You'll need to kill the Gourmet, of course. But what's more, you'll need to get his Writ of Passage, so you can take his place. Oh, and if you can, hide his body. The longer it takes for anyone else to determine his real identity, the better. Now off with you!
Skyrim.esm|0681B9: I believe so. Therefore, you are to obtain that information from Virane - who the Gourmet really is, and where he can be found. Oh, and when you're done with Virane, kill him. Loose ends and all that. Next, you'll have to... Are you still paying attention?
Skyrim.esm|0681BA: There you are. Took your sweet time dealing with Cicero, hmph? Now let's get down to business. Astrid told you about the Gourmet, I hope?
Skyrim.esm|0681BB: Don't be so petulant! In my, um, investigations, I came upon this. It's a copy of the Gourmet's cookbook. Ah, but not just any copy. It's signed you see! Seems to be a message from the Gourmet to one Anton Virane. I've tracked Virane to the keep in Markarth. It would seem he's the cook.
Skyrim.esm|0681BC: Quite so. But first you'll need to learn who he is. Assuming, of course, that the Gourmet actually is a "he." Could be a woman for all we know.
Skyrim.esm|0681BD: You know, maybe I was wrong about you after all. Anyone who's smart enough to shut up and listen can't be all bad. In my, um, investigations, I came upon this. It's a copy of the Gourmet's cookbook. Ah, but not just any copy. It's signed you see! Seems to be a message from the Gourmet to one Anton Virane. I've tracked Virane to the keep in Markarth. It would seem he's the cook.
Skyrim.esm|0681BE: Ah, indeed I do! Indeed I do! In my, um, investigations, I came upon this. It's a copy of the Gourmet's cookbook. Ah, but not just any copy. It's signed you see! Seems to be a message from the Gourmet to one Anton Virane. I've tracked Virane to the keep in Markarth. It would seem he's the cook.
Skyrim.esm|0681C8: It's tough, with just the two of us. There's hardly ever time to make the occasional delivery to the taverns of Skyrim. We could use a pair of hands and a strong back. There'd be a little coin in it for you.
Skyrim.esm|0681CA: I hope you have good news for Mirabelle.
Skyrim.esm|0681CB: Please, make sure Winterhold is safe!
Skyrim.esm|0681CC: Find Savos, and get him back here if you can!
Skyrim.esm|0681CD: I need you to take a case of mead to the Frozen Hearth in Winterhold. The delivery fee is yours to keep.
Skyrim.esm|0681CE: Another case of mead needs to find its way to the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. The delivery fee is yours to keep.
Skyrim.esm|0681CF: Candlehearth Hall is running low on our famous mead. Can you drop off this case? The gold is yours, as always.
Skyrim.esm|0681D0: Ah, good. I've been waiting on that. Here's the gold for it. Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0681D1: Ah, good. I've been waiting on that. Here's the gold for it. Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0681D2: Ah, good. I've been waiting on that. Here's the gold for it. Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0681D3: That'd be great. There's a case of our soon-to-be famous mead that needs to get to Winterhold. Get it there, and you can keep the delivery fee.
Skyrim.esm|0681EC: I'll speak with Aela about how you handled yourself. Come back later.
Skyrim.esm|0681EF: I'll speak with Farkas about how you handled yourself. Come back later.
Skyrim.esm|068364: I spend every waking moment making sure the mine is put to good use.
Skyrim.esm|068366: This is not where Teeba-Ei was meant to live out the rest of his days.
Skyrim.esm|068377: Best batch of Spiced Wine I ever had.
Skyrim.esm|068378: I thought the festival wasn't happening this year.
Skyrim.esm|068379: Thanks for convincing Elisif to put this on again.
Skyrim.esm|06837D: Jorn is the best of the bunch, you should hear him sing.
Skyrim.esm|06837E: I heard you're the reason we get to have this.  Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|068384: I wish you luck in finding the verse.
Skyrim.esm|068399: Surrender immediately or you both die.
Skyrim.esm|06839A: Listen up, spy! You're trapped in here, and we have your accomplice.
Skyrim.esm|06839B: He's got to be here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|06839C: Good luck. You're on your own now.
Skyrim.esm|06839E: Let's go, let's go! Before anyone notices us.
Skyrim.esm|06839F: If you like. But we need to find a way to unlock that trap door.
Skyrim.esm|068511: Yes, yes, for the hundredth time, I am a Breton. I was born in High Rock. And then I came here. I am not a Reachman!
Skyrim.esm|068512: Please. Please just let me be...
Skyrim.esm|068514: The... the Gourmet? Never! I don't know what led you here, but nothing will betray my trust. I'll take the secret of the Gourmet's identity to my grave.
Skyrim.esm|068515: Who are you? What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|068516: The Dark Brotherhood? Now... now wait a minute. Let's not get hasty. I mean, surely my friend wouldn't want me to endanger my own life. Right? Look, his name is Balagog gro-Nolob. He's an Orc! The Gourmet's an Orc! He's staying at the Nightgate Inn. That's all I know! Now... now you'll let me go. Right?
Skyrim.esm|06851A: Okay. All right. Wonderful. You're welcome! I'll just be on my way, then...
Skyrim.esm|068942: A sword? I remember a legend that Tiber Septim had attacked one of the enemy camps before he came to Old Hroldan. It could be there. I'll mark it down on your map.
Skyrim.esm|068943: There's a... ghost. Just wandering around my inn like it owned the place.
Skyrim.esm|068944: Yes. I've heard stories that Old Hroldan was haunted, but no ones seen a ghost here since the Great War. He's from the battle, I just know it. He's one of Tiber Septim's soldiers... back from the dead.
Skyrim.esm|068947: Mara have mercy that gave me a fright.
Skyrim.esm|06894C: Hjalti? I've never heard of anyone named Hjalti. Tiber Septim had many names. Maybe that's one of them?
Skyrim.esm|06894E: It's been an honor to serve you, brother. Remembers our lessons from the sword masters of Alcaire? Let me show you a few things you may have forgotten before we leave Hroldan.
Skyrim.esm|068953: Do you think the ghost is one of... Tiber Septim's dead men?
Skyrim.esm|068955: All of a sudden, the ghost disappeared. What happened to it?
Skyrim.esm|068956: Do you think that ghost is coming back? It seems to have vanished.
Skyrim.esm|068957: I was just getting used to that ghost being here, and poof, now he's gone.
Skyrim.esm|068958: I've been waiting for you. Hjalti.
Skyrim.esm|068959: I fought by your side. To take back the Reach from the savages.
Skyrim.esm|06895A: Do you remember me now, Hjalti?
Skyrim.esm|06895F: I work for Eydis. She runs the inn.
Skyrim.esm|068960: Skuli's father is off fighting for the Stormcloaks. I'm sure he's just fine.
Skyrim.esm|068B69: Look. I gave you a contract, and you completed it. Barely. Now let's go back to you being insufferable, and me being... me.
Skyrim.esm|068B6B: Come now, you've got your orders. What else could you possibly need?
Skyrim.esm|068B6E: Ah, good to see you, brother. Gearing up to kill the Emperor? Exciting times. Exciting times, indeed.
Skyrim.esm|068B6F: Ah, good to see you, sister. Gearing up to kill the Emperor? Exciting times. Exciting times, indeed.
Skyrim.esm|068B70: So, the prodigal murderer returns. And the Gourmet?
Skyrim.esm|068B71: Ah, you're back. Ready to try again, are we? Let's start from the top. You were sent to kill the Gourmet. And he is...
Skyrim.esm|068B72: Hmph. I was wrong about you. I see that now. Maybe we all were. Here's your payment. And... a little something else, as well. From me. Consider it my way of apologizing. For being so damned curmudgeonly! It's called the Nightweaver's Band. I wore this for years. I want you to have it now. It'll give your magic and sneakiness some much needed "oomph." Now, you'd better get a move on and see Astrid. It's time! Time for the final stage of this grand and glorious operation.
Skyrim.esm|068B73: Remember, the Markarth Keep. That's where you'll find Anton Virane. Probably cooking up all of the Gourmet's recipes, passing them off as his own.
Skyrim.esm|068B74: All you've got to do is find and kill one lousy chef. The clock is ticking...
Skyrim.esm|068B76: Yes, well. Perhaps someone else in the village can assist you? Please, you're starting to... frighten me.
Skyrim.esm|068B77: So I gathered. It seems a certain Orc has disappeared. Which means you not only killed the Gourmet, but disposed of the body as well. You've got the Writ of Passage too, I see. Splendid, splendid... Ah, and word has come in from Markarth that the keep's cook has met an untimely demise. You performed your duties to the letter.
Skyrim.esm|068B78: Right, which we had already established. And you managed to hide the body as well? Good. You've got the Writ of Passage too, I see. So there's that. And Anton Virane has suffered an untimely fate, as well. Good. Well, looks like you completed the contract as directed. Finally. A little sloppy, but, well... done is done. Here's your gold, with a little extra for hiding the body. Now go see Astrid. The final stage of the contract has come. The Emperor must fall.
Skyrim.esm|068B79: Good. And his Writ of Passage? Oh wait, let me guess - you don't have them. Fool! Amateur! Without those papers you'll never be able to present yourself as the Gourmet! Off with you! Back to the body to retrieve them!
Skyrim.esm|068B7A: Good, good. And you've got the Writ of Passage too, I see, so that's done. But why? Why, pray tell, have I received word that Anton Virane is still walking around Markarth, hale and healthy as the day he was born? Back to Markarth with you! Virane must be silenced! He's a potential witness, you witless dagger jabber!
Skyrim.esm|068B7B: Good, good. And you've got the Writ of Passage, too, I see. So there's that. I've also received word from Markarth. It would seem Anton Virane has suffered an untimely fate, as well. Good. But unfortunately, that inn is abuzz with the news of somebody killing one of their guests! What, hiding the body was too much for you to handle? Bah! Useless. Here's your gold. Now go see Astrid. The time to move against the Emperor has finally come. Sithis help us all.
Skyrim.esm|068BC5: Speak with Ulfric, he's expecting word from Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|068CBB: Got one all saddled and ready to go. What are you willing to pay?
Skyrim.esm|068CC0: Not today. Sold my last one. Pretty sure I sold it to you.
Skyrim.esm|068CC2: Yeah, I got one for sale.
Skyrim.esm|068CC3: You could if I had one. Sold my last available horse to someone that looked an awful lot like you.
Skyrim.esm|068CEE: Off with ya then. I've got work to do.
Skyrim.esm|068CF1: You've got a deal. It's the one with the saddle.
Skyrim.esm|068CF2: Then come back when you've got that much.
Skyrim.esm|068CF4: That'll do. She's all saddled up for you.
Skyrim.esm|068CF5: When you get it, she's yours.
Skyrim.esm|068F29: Oh dear. Darkwater Crossing needs medicines for its miners? I'll see about getting this order filled right away. Here, a few septims for you.
Skyrim.esm|068F2A: Well, well, you actually did what you said you were going to do. I'm impressed. Here, let me teach you a thing or two about Alteration magic. It's the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|068F31: He'll be fine, I just hate worrying about his worrying.
Skyrim.esm|068F32: My clan's shield. So Lenne really did stumble into that cave and was eaten by monsters. In honor of his memory, let me show you a few shield techniques. And tell me if you're leaving town. Be good to travel again.
Skyrim.esm|068F33: He's been in Kynesgrove forever. Great storyteller. Keeps everyone's spirits up. Owes me a lot of coin for drinks. I keep telling him not to worry about it, but he's got the stubborn Nord blood. Damn fool doesn't have the coin to pay me back, so he just beats himself up over it, and then he gets thirsty, and it cycles all over again.
Skyrim.esm|068F39: It's not any of your bother, so I don't see what you're apologizing for.
Skyrim.esm|068F3B: If you want to go to the trouble, feel free. I suppose I could offer you a bit of free Alteration training when you get back.
Skyrim.esm|068F3D: Poor Roggi. If you're feeling charitable, buy the Nord a mead. He's good for it.
Skyrim.esm|068F3F: That's right. Used to explore all over. I'm the one who originally found the mine Darkwater Crossing was built around. I was actually scouting a bit the other day, old times sake. Spied a brigand heading back to his camp. Up for a little hunting? My husband is being a sad stick, so I can't go myself.
Skyrim.esm|068F57: Nosy one, huh? I was a mage back in Morrowind. I lend my talents to the mines here, helping them keep the tunnels safe. By Azura, it gets hot in there. I use Frost Salts for a potion to keep the heat bearable, but I'm out.
Skyrim.esm|068F59: I get your point. Tell Iddra I'm sorry for being so stubborn about the coin.
Skyrim.esm|068F5B: It'll be good to not have to hear him whining about his debts. Roggi pawned a few of his things back when he still had things to pawn. Take them.
Skyrim.esm|068F5C: A true Nord would settle it himself, but at least this way Iddra gets her coin. Thank you. Tell her I'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|068F60: This isn't about me paying Iddra back, this is about me being able to pay her back with my own coin.
Skyrim.esm|068F61: This brings back memories. Come back when you're done and maybe I'll be up to showing you a few tricks.
Skyrim.esm|068F62: I guess if you're telling me to stop worrying about it, maybe I should just let it go. Thank you, friend.
Skyrim.esm|068F63: What do you think I am, a beggar? My family has always paid back every single coin we owed, going back to Tiber Septim's time.
Skyrim.esm|068F64: According to my grandfather at least. He swore that version of the legend was true, even found the cave old Lenne stumbled into. Hey, if you find it, I could show you some of the clan's favorite fighting moves. They're all shield techniques.
Skyrim.esm|068F6A: Fine. You sound like a priest of Mara, but I get your point. Tell him to forget about the gold.
Skyrim.esm|068FD2: You know how to throw a punch, I'll give you that.
Skyrim.esm|068FD3: What? No. All right. I won't worry about the coin anymore, just stop giving me that look.
Skyrim.esm|068FDF: If you could convince him that I don't care about his tab, it'd certainly make me feel better.
Skyrim.esm|068FE0: This mine is only a few years old, but I've been digging up ore for over a century. We're getting to the point where the tunnels are getting deep into the rock. People complain of bad coughs and foul air. I've seen this before. We need some medicines to combat the ore dust. I was about to send a message to Windhelm for aid.
Skyrim.esm|068FE2: Yes. I've wandered a bit, but I always come back to Kynesgrove. My clan was one of the first to settle here. Legend says that my forefather Lenne discovered the mines after he stumbled into a dark cave to relieve himself. Too much mead. Of course, there's another story of Lenne losing the family shield in a different cave, so it depends on who you believe.
Skyrim.esm|068FE7: I used to be an adventurer in my day, then I settled down. Got married.
Skyrim.esm|068FE9: Thank you. Take it to Quintus, in the White Phial.
Skyrim.esm|06958C: Did you clean the linens, yet?
Skyrim.esm|06958D: Yes. I'm sure he's doing well there.
Skyrim.esm|06958E: Last time you said he was fighting for the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0695AE: I did. Those mead stains are always tricky.
Skyrim.esm|0695BA: He's off fighting the war, dear. For the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|069647: Here's your pay. It's never enough for what we do, but war gives us warriors its own kind of benefits, doesn't it?
Skyrim.esm|069648: Are you sure? It's rightfully yours, but this money could go to getting better weapons and armor, and making repairs to the settlements we control.
Skyrim.esm|06965A: Now the Thalmor will be hunting me for the rest of my life. I hope it was worth it.
Skyrim.esm|06965B: I should have known this would end badly. I can't believe I let Delphine talk me into this.
Skyrim.esm|06965C: Let's get out of here, for pity's sake!
Skyrim.esm|06965D: I saw guards dragging bodies over here. Might be a way out.
Skyrim.esm|06965E: We've got to get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|06965F: You saved my life. I won't forget it. Look me up if you're ever in Riften.
Skyrim.esm|069660: You didn't have to help me, so... thanks.
Skyrim.esm|06966C: Finally, both of my mines are open again. Time for everyone to get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|069672: Trust me, we have nothing to say to one another.
Skyrim.esm|069685: Use your Unrelenting Force shout to strike the targets as they appear.
Skyrim.esm|069687: Strike the targets as they appear. Breath and focus.
Skyrim.esm|069F1F: Don't you dare walk away from me, you slut! Do you know who I am?
Skyrim.esm|069F20: Ha. Don't toy with me. I may just have to seize my opportunity with both hands, ha ha ha. But we'll play it your way for now.
Skyrim.esm|069F21: There's a likely-looking filly. Even if she is an elf.
Skyrim.esm|069F23: Um, thank you. Sir. Was there anything else I can do for you?
Skyrim.esm|069F24: Ha. We'll see. I do have my eye on her, but she seemed a bit cold when I spoke to her earlier. Maybe just playing hard to get, eh?
Skyrim.esm|069F25: You there! Serving girl! What's your name, dear?
Skyrim.esm|069F26: Mmm, yes... Brelas, is that the one? I hope you're not thinking of cutting me out, are you? Haha. I'm warning you, when I get my heart set on something I'm not easily put off.
Skyrim.esm|069F27: You must be new to Skyrim, or you'd already know who I am. I have a stake in most anything of importance in Solitude. I feel that it's time to put aside the grievances of the past, and let peace and prosperity flourish between the Empire and the Aldmeri Dominion. I try to do my little part. Besides, just between you and me, it's making me pots of money, hahaha.
Skyrim.esm|069F28: Oh... not at the moment. Maybe later. Don't go far.
Skyrim.esm|069F29: Uh, Brelas, sir. Did you need a drink? Something to eat?
Skyrim.esm|069F2A: So. You think you can toy with me, is that it? No, my dear. I have my heart set on you, and I always get what I want.
Skyrim.esm|069F2B: Haha. I knew it! No woman yet born has been able to resist my charms for long. Thank you, my friend. I owe you one.
Skyrim.esm|069F2C: What? I'm sorry, sir, but you must have misunderstood. I'm not permitted to leave the party.
Skyrim.esm|069F2E: Oh, I don't think so. I think I understand perfectly. Don't worry, my dear, I will make any necessary excuses to your employer.
Skyrim.esm|069F2F: Really? Yes, maybe that would be just the thing. She seemed to be playing hard to get when I spoke to her earlier. Go on. See what she says. Now you've gone and gotten my hopes up again.
Skyrim.esm|069F30: Have you talked to Brelas yet? I'm not a patient man, you know.
Skyrim.esm|069F31: Did you see that serving girl? I hear elf women are insatiable...
Skyrim.esm|069F32: For the man who knows how to make money, war can be a most profitable venture.
Skyrim.esm|069F33: The Great War is long past. It's time the Empire and the Aldmeri Dominion put aside their differences. Prosperity is good for everyone.
Skyrim.esm|069F34: Elenwen always throws quite a party. I never miss one if I can help it.
Skyrim.esm|069F35: No, I'm sorry, but I can't go anywhere with you. I won't. Please, I must get back to my duties.
Skyrim.esm|069F36: No, no, that's not what I'm interested in right now. I just wanted to get a better look at you. I like what I see, my dear. And believe me, I don't say that to everyone. I'm very discriminating when it comes to the female form.
Skyrim.esm|069F9A: Mistress Elenwen, it's not true! I did nothing!
Skyrim.esm|069F9B: What did you tell him? Why did you do this to me?
Skyrim.esm|069F9C: I demand that you have this wench removed from my presence at once!
Skyrim.esm|069F9D: Well, I'm glad that little unpleasantness is over.
Skyrim.esm|069F9E: Sir, you must tell her! You don't know what they'll do to me! Please!
Skyrim.esm|069FA0: Now you're going to be sorry you crossed me.
Skyrim.esm|069FA1: Ugh. Erikur, right? He was talking to me earlier. I could tell what he was after. I hate working these parties. Some of the guests are nice, but there's always a few like Erikur. Please tell him to leave me alone. Politely. I'm sure you'll have better luck getting through to him than I would.
Skyrim.esm|069FA2: Here you go, sir. Colovian brandy.
Skyrim.esm|069FA3: Is that so, Erikur? And you with such delicate sensibilities. It must have been most upsetting.
Skyrim.esm|069FA4: Elenwen! This servant girl has been throwing herself at me in a most disgusting manner.
Skyrim.esm|069FA5: I hope you're enjoying yourself, sir.
Skyrim.esm|069FA6: I hope you're enjoying yourself, ma'am.
Skyrim.esm|069FA7: What can I get for you?
Skyrim.esm|069FA9: Well. Whatever the truth of it, I'm sure a few words with Master Rulindil will have a salutary effect. Take her downstairs.
Skyrim.esm|069FEA: Go now to Castle Dour in Solitude. Present the Gourmet's Write of Passage to the officer in charge, Commander Maro. I'm sure you remember him.
Skyrim.esm|06A032: Look, it's all very simple. The Dwemer, they were uhh, masters of magic in a way we can't even begin to conceive of. Their greatest achievement, however, has also been their greatest, shall we say... Mystery.
Skyrim.esm|06A033: I don't know, I don't know! I've already agreed to pay him quite handsomely for the... well, the item in question. Just... Do whatever it takes to get him to stop lording this over me.
Skyrim.esm|06A034: If you're getting involved in this, that'd be where you start looking. Why is this suddenly so important, anyway?
Skyrim.esm|06A035: Ah, yes. Umm, no. No, I don't think so. Not at this point. It's just that, well I'm not even really sure it's going to amount to anything just yet. All theoretical right now, you understand. But the implications are quite intriguing, I think. I simply need some experimental results to confirm my suspicions.
Skyrim.esm|06A036: Terrible, just terrible. I vastly underestimated the heat transfer necessary, and have destroyed my only working model. I'm at a loss.
Skyrim.esm|06A037: Well, yes. The designs for my Convector were based on real dwarven machines, so there may still be some in existence. I'd go looking myself, but uhh... Yes, there are calculations to double- and triple-check. Many calculations. Perhaps you could seek out these Convectors in Skyrim ruins? I can even teach you the spell to heat them. It's very particular, and very important.
Skyrim.esm|06A038: You think you can do what he can't, and I'll sell it to you. Then you're free to run off to him with it, if you like.
Skyrim.esm|06A039: With all due respect, Arch-Mage, I'm not in a position to just give this away. I told Arniel what I wanted in exchange for it, and he was unwilling to come up with it. Now, if you're here on his behalf, I'm afraid I'm going to have to insist on my original payment.
Skyrim.esm|06A03A: Have you lost your mind? Think, please, just for a moment, about what he would do if he knew its true value. Do you really believe he would honor our agreement, or go running off to the highest bidder? Please, you must set this right. I'm begging you.
Skyrim.esm|06A03B: It... It's very valuable. Critical to my work, and one-of-a-kind. That's all I care to say for now.
Skyrim.esm|06A03C: Well, yes. I mean, no. That is, it might, but I'm not completely sure. I'm no tonal architect, I've only read their writings. Scraps, really. The soul gem by itself isn't enough, you see. It needs to be altered, purified. The dwarves had machines for this sort of thing. I attempted to build my own, based on designs and using parts you helped to provide. My very own Dwarven Convector. It worked, but not fully. And the uhh... The device was destroyed in the process.
Skyrim.esm|06A03D: Well, I... Don't you see? Unlocking this mystery... The story alone is worth more riches than you can even imagine. And perhaps... well, I don't wish to speculate, but... What if we could contact the dwarves? What if they're still out there? Imagine!
Skyrim.esm|06A03E: I had retained Enthir's services in, uhh, acquiring something specific. But now he's refusing to deliver. Could you perhaps speak with him, and see if we can come to some sort of arrangement?
Skyrim.esm|06A03F: Nothing, nothing at all! Arniel already overpaid for... whatever it is.
Skyrim.esm|06A040: I don't know. He never told me. He made most of the arrangements himself, he just needed it transported from Morrowind. That's what I agreed to help with. If I'd known it was so important to him, I'd have charged him more.
Skyrim.esm|06A041: What? No, no. Goodness, no! At least, I don't think so. No, it's just... It's just something I'd prefer to keep to myself.
Skyrim.esm|06A042: Did I ask you to? Did you hear me say that?
Skyrim.esm|06A044: Oh dear. He's so afraid to talk to me he's sending you to do it? I should've expected as much. I'm afraid you've been a little mis-informed. I have something of mine, that I was going to sell to Arniel.
Skyrim.esm|06A045: Hmm? No, no. No! I'm too close now. Can't take the time. Though... Perhaps there's another option. I, umm, I may require further assistance from you.
Skyrim.esm|06A046: No, and at this rate, I never shall! Enthir is once again holding out on me. He refuses to deliver yet another item absolutely essential to my work! I am close, so close! You must force him to see reason!
Skyrim.esm|06A047: Do you? Wonderful. I was afraid I'd have to give up, and when I'm so close... Thank you for this. I have a great deal of work ahead of me.
Skyrim.esm|06A049: You're not listening to me. I don't have it. I set up the whole deal. A courier was supposed to be bringing it from Morrowind. He never arrived. Okay? The deal was already done, the thing just never showed up. I can't help that!
Skyrim.esm|06A04A: No, no no no. This can't be happening.
Skyrim.esm|06A04B: Just... Just bring me some Dwemer cogs. I need to examine them for the methods used in forging them. Ten of them should be enough for me to get a better understanding of how they were crafted. That's all I need, all right?
Skyrim.esm|06A04C: Oh, by the Divines, you too? I've already told him. I don't have it!
Skyrim.esm|06A04D: He has some simple-minded courier delivering it?
Skyrim.esm|06A04E: Wonderful, just wonderful! I knew I could count on you! Now this spell is not for defending yourself, but it's just right for heating the Convector. At least three seconds' burn is necessary. Just place the soul gem inside, and heat the Convector. At least three seconds. You'll almost certainly need to find multiple Convectors. I suspect it may be due to the nature of the soul gem versus the crystals this was intended for. Destroys the equipment in the process. But, ahh... It's effective. Three times, and the crystal should be ready for the next step. Isn't this exciting?
Skyrim.esm|06A04F: Their disappearance, of course! Instant, unexplainable. The stories have fascinated me... Ever since I was young, I wanted to know what really happened. After years and years of work, I'm closing in on a way to investigate what happened through experimentation. Only now... Well, it's simply a disaster.
Skyrim.esm|06A050: I heard one of them was just arrested trying to sneak into the city. If he's locked up in the jail, perhaps you can get it out of him. Please, I know I'm asking you to do something difficult, maybe even dangerous. I just don't know who else I can trust.
Skyrim.esm|06A051: You've seen them? Then waste no time, head there at once! The longer they're around, the more danger I'm in! Please, kill Kematu and end this nightmare for me!
Skyrim.esm|06A052: Last I heard he intended to pass through Eastmarch on his way here.
Skyrim.esm|06A053: Last I'd heard, he was crossing the border from Morrowind into The Rift.
Skyrim.esm|06A054: It can't be lost. It just can't! It's irreplaceable, do you understand? Without it, I cannot finish my work. All these years, so much time, so much energy... You must find it! It cannot end this way! Not after I've worked so hard!
Skyrim.esm|06A055: What? I... I don't understand. Where is it?
Skyrim.esm|06A056: Ah, that's excellent news. This will be most helpful in furthering my research. I'm afraid I can't offer you much right now, but please take this. And perhaps, if this is all successful... Well, let's not get ahead of ourselves. It's too early to be getting excited. Still, this is most encouraging. Thank you again. If I require anything else, I shall be sure and let you know.
Skyrim.esm|06A057: I simply need more pieces of Dwemer equipment to study. It's nothing dangerous... At least, not necessarily. And please, let's just... Let's keep this between us.
Skyrim.esm|06A058: I have a... friend, let's say, who's interested in a staff. A particular one. It's nothing dangerous, so don't worry. I'm not even sure why he wants it, but then that's not really my concern. He's willing to pay for it. The problem is that it'll be a little difficult to acquire, seeing as how it's in the hands of someone who isn't exactly friendly. So, you bring that staff back here, and I'll consider it payment for the item Arniel was after.
Skyrim.esm|06A059: You mean the official sort of business? Hmm, well... no. No, not really.
Skyrim.esm|06A05A: As a matter of fact, there is something you can do for me.
Skyrim.esm|06A05B: What? Oh, no. No, no. I need more time to research the dwarven machinery. Thank you for offering, though.
Skyrim.esm|06A07C: I don't know. Are you not listening at all? It was a simple task, so I didn't keep close tabs on it like I do with the dangerous stuff.
Skyrim.esm|06A081: Without boring you with the, ahh, details, I am attempting to re-create the circumstances of the event. The dwarves were very clever, tapping into and drawing power from the Heart of Lorkhan itself. Lacking the heart of a dead god myself, I was attempting to substitute a rather specific soul gem. The ahh, the one you helped me acquire.
Skyrim.esm|06A082: But... I can't help but notice that you're doing quite well for yourself, rising through the ranks and all. It may not be "official", but there is a, uhh, project I've been working on. I wonder if perhaps you could assist with it.
Skyrim.esm|06A084: Precisely! What could be worth studying about a long-lost civilization?
Skyrim.esm|06A085: Well, well. All right, you delivered on your end, so I'll deliver on mine. Here's the gem Arniel was after. Can't imagine what he expects to do with that... it's warped beyond any ability to capture a soul.
Skyrim.esm|06A170: Now? I announce to all of Markarth that I have returned. Don't worry about your name. They'll know who to blame and fear after today. It'll take years, but I'll organize the Forsworn again. We'll reclaim our land, and then, when power is ours, we'll have peace. A kingdom. Until then, let me offer you a warning. Beware the Forsworn. No place in the Reach is safe from us now.
Skyrim.esm|06A26D: Here you go. Your continued assistance to the cause is appreciated.
Skyrim.esm|06A26E: I wish more had the dedication and strength of character that you demonstrate. I'll be sure this gets put to good use.
Skyrim.esm|06A26F: I surrender! Give me a few hours to gather my household, and I'll depart. You have my word.
Skyrim.esm|06A3D4: I assume you saw the Gildergreen? Big tree, all burnt up, in the middle of the Wind District. It's part of the Temple of Kynareth. People want Danica to do something about it, but I don't know what they expect.
Skyrim.esm|06A3DA: Don't forget the ranting and raving. About the Night Mother, how she was the true leader of the Dark Brotherhood, and Astrid was just a "pretender."
Skyrim.esm|06A3DB: This is not the time to keep Astrid waiting.
Skyrim.esm|06A3DC: It's true, I'm afraid. Cicero was a little whirlwind, slashing this way and that. It would have been funny, if he weren't trying to murder us all.
Skyrim.esm|06A6FA: Gone? Every one of them? Why, that's incredible.
Skyrim.esm|06A701: Moved right in and made themselves at home. Did it overnight. I'm not even sure exactly where they came from. No one's been hurt yet, and they seem to be staying in the mine, but who knows how long that will last? If we can't get back into the mine, our town is sunk.
Skyrim.esm|06A702: One day we were cracking rocks in the deepest parts of the mine and the next day they were all over the place. I'd give my eye teeth if it meant getting those damn spiders out of the mine!
Skyrim.esm|06A704: Everything was going great in Redbelly. Then the damn spiders showed up.
Skyrim.esm|06A705: Have you ever been to Darkwater Crossing? Terrible name for such a beautiful place.
Skyrim.esm|06A76B: I need someone that cares about me more than they care about themselves. That person isn't you.
Skyrim.esm|06A76C: What makes you so sure? What if I'm right?
Skyrim.esm|06A76D: I'll have you know I can haul up just as much iron as you in a day and still manage to clean up my messes.
Skyrim.esm|06A76E: I've been watching the way you mine and I have to say, I'm very impressed.
Skyrim.esm|06A76F: You two will just have to learn to get along until we can build you another house.
Skyrim.esm|06A770: It's no feast in their neck of the woods either. Last time I was up there, father said they were barely scraping by.
Skyrim.esm|06A771: Quit your bellyaching. We've all got our problems.
Skyrim.esm|06A772: I owe it all to my father who started me swinging the pickaxe when I was a barefoot little girl.
Skyrim.esm|06A773: All I'm asking is that you clean up once in a while. How you can find anything in your half of the place is beyond me.
Skyrim.esm|06A774: Well, it shows. Your skill rivals some of the greatest miners I've known.
Skyrim.esm|06A775: You think so, huh? Maybe you'd like to put some septims behind that boast.
Skyrim.esm|06A776: I know there's ore still in there, I can smell it. If you think you're right then you can pack up and leave any time you like.
Skyrim.esm|06A777: Thank you. It's nice to know someone admires me for more than just my appearance.
Skyrim.esm|06A778: Grelka, do you fancy a walk tomorrow?
Skyrim.esm|06A779: This civil war is taking its toll on all of us. Miners are some of the hardest hit.
Skyrim.esm|06A77A: And I'm telling you, you're wrong.
Skyrim.esm|06A77B: I've been cracking rock ever since I was able to hold a pickaxe and I'm telling you, this mine is drying up.
Skyrim.esm|06A77C: You think living with Grogmar is easy? Did you see how he lives?
Skyrim.esm|06A77D: I'd rather spend more time in the mine hauling up iron then doing woman's work keeping the house clean.
Skyrim.esm|06A77E: Odfel, I've told you time and time again. I'm not interested in a relationship with you.
Skyrim.esm|06A77F: At this rate that'll never happen.
Skyrim.esm|06A780: How are your parents faring, Grelka? Doing better than we are I would guess.
Skyrim.esm|06A782: And why not? I'm handsome, strong and smart. What else could you possibly need?
Skyrim.esm|06A783: That's what scares me. I'm afraid I'll travel to Darkwater Crossing and find it burned to the ground.
Skyrim.esm|06A79B: What? That little tease! Leading me on and turning cold at the last minute. I don't think so.
Skyrim.esm|06A79C: It's very nice to meet you. You'll have to excuse Orthus here. He so rarely gets back to civilization. Stuck in Windhelm, poor fellow.
Skyrim.esm|06A79D: Oh, don't worry. I'll let you make it up to me. Now, where can we go for a little privacy, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|06A79E: Don't worry, Orthus. I'm doing my best to get you reassigned to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|06A79F: Hello, my dear. I got your message. Where shall we go?
Skyrim.esm|06A7A0: I'm sorry, sir. I didn't mean to give you the wrong impression earlier. I meant no disrespect.
Skyrim.esm|06A7A1: I should be back in Windhelm. I've got work to do.
Skyrim.esm|06A7A2: Did you know Vittoria is the Emperor's cousin? Best stay on her good side.
Skyrim.esm|06A7A3: Did you know Vittoria's getting married? They say the Emperor might even come to Skyrim for the wedding!
Skyrim.esm|06A7A4: Just between us, I've got more important things to do than attend these meaningless parties.
Skyrim.esm|06A7A5: I'm ready for this party to be over.
Skyrim.esm|06A7A7: The Empire is fortunate to have the support of the Thalmor during this difficult time, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|06A7A8: Mark my words, the war will be over in a matter of months. Once Ulfric sees the full might of the Empire, I'm sure he'll come to his senses.
Skyrim.esm|06A7A9: I don't understand why General Tullius hasn't been recalled. The man is clearly incompetent. I have half a mind to tell him so myself.
Skyrim.esm|06A7AA: Trust me, there's no place better to forge relationships with the movers and shakers of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|06A7AB: Vittoria! There's someone I'd like you to meet.
Skyrim.esm|06A7AC: I wasn't expecting to see you here! I assume you have your reasons. Everyone does.
Skyrim.esm|06A7AD: Look around the room and you'll see what we're up against. Just between you and me, a lot of what Ulfric says about the Empire is true.
Skyrim.esm|06A7AE: I swear Elenwen holds these parties just to make the Empire look bad. Almost makes me want to join the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|06A7AF: The only reason I'm here is because my refusal to attend would cause a diplomatic incident.
Skyrim.esm|06A7B1: I don't remember seeing you at one of these before.
Skyrim.esm|06A7B2: Do I know you? I didn't think so.
Skyrim.esm|06A7B3: Oh, don't worry about me. I relish the challenge.
Skyrim.esm|06A874: Those Dark Elf refugees will take over the city if we aren't careful.
Skyrim.esm|06A886: You're joking, right? Those bone-walkers will tear you limb from limb. But if you want to get yourself killed, go ahead.
Skyrim.esm|06A88B: Fine, but there's a whole crypt of draugr underneath the mine. We're not going in there until the Jarl's men come to clear them out, so I wouldn't go down there.
Skyrim.esm|06A88C: Seems Kolskeggr Mine got hit by Forsworn. Those madmen get more bold every season. Pavo and Gat are the only survivors. I'll be sending word to the Jarl about this, but I don't know when help will come.
Skyrim.esm|06A897: Was. Forsworn came in the night. Killed everyone. Gat and I are the only ones who escaped. Hopefully Skaggi will send word to the Jarl and something will be done.
Skyrim.esm|06A8BD: I'm sure Pavo would be grateful, and we'd all rest a little easier. But that's nonsense. You can't handle a group of Forsworn by yourself. If their axes don't get you, their magic will.
Skyrim.esm|06A8BE: I'd be grateful. Kolskeggr is my home. But, I wouldn't go there if I were you. There's a whole group of them with axes and magic on their side. You'd never stand a chance.
Skyrim.esm|06A8CC: They just call themselves the people of the Reach. Lot of common blood with the Bretons, by the looks of them. Used to have their own kingdom, till the Nords came, now they're at the bottom of the hay pile.
Skyrim.esm|06A8CF: Skaggi is in charge, not me.
Skyrim.esm|06A8D5: That it is. Good to be home. Let me know if you dig up any ore. We'll pay a fair wage.
Skyrim.esm|06A8D6: Are you serious? I say more threatening words to my husband.
Skyrim.esm|06A8DA: You speak to Burguk, chief of Dushnikh Yal.
Skyrim.esm|06AA59: May Kynareth's winds blow you to fortune.
Skyrim.esm|06AA5C: What's happened to the Gildergreen is a travesty.
Skyrim.esm|06AA5D: Please move quickly. Eldergleam awaits us.
Skyrim.esm|06AA5E: Is it much further to the sanctuary?
Skyrim.esm|06AA5F: I can't believe I've finally made it here.
Skyrim.esm|06AA60: It's like it was in my dreams.
Skyrim.esm|06AA61: I can almost hear its heart.
Skyrim.esm|06AE39: What are you doing that for?
Skyrim.esm|06AE40: If you were anyone else, I'd make you leave.
Skyrim.esm|06AE41: You're not really supposed to... you know, be here.
Skyrim.esm|06AE43: I didn't really want that anyway.
Skyrim.esm|06AEA4: It's time for you to go.
Skyrim.esm|06AF3A: That is for you to discover. We can show you the Way, but not your destination.
Skyrim.esm|06AF3B: You are not the first. There have been many of the Dragon Blood since Akatosh first bestowed that gift upon mortalkind. Whether you are the only Dragonborn of this age... that is not ours to know. You are the only one that has been revealed thus far. That is all I can say.
Skyrim.esm|06AF3C: You have shown that you are Dragonborn. You have the inborn gift. But do you have the discipline and temperament to follow the path laid out for you? That remains to be seen.
Skyrim.esm|06AF3D: We are honored to welcome a Dragonborn to High Hrothgar. We will do our best to teach you how to use your gift in fulfillment of your destiny.
Skyrim.esm|06AF3E: We are here to guide you in that pursuit, just as the Greybeards have sought to guide those of the Dragon Blood that came before you.
Skyrim.esm|06AF3F: We are the Greybeards, followers of the Way of the Voice. You stand in High Hrothgar, on the slopes of Kynareth's sacred mountain. Here we commune with the voice of the sky, and strive to achieve balance between our inner and outer selves.
Skyrim.esm|06B136: I'm still sore where I took that arrow. I hate archers.
Skyrim.esm|06B137: This imperial armor is damn heavy.
Skyrim.esm|06B138: I'm sick of waiting. When are we going to see some action?
Skyrim.esm|06B139: Hope I don't draw guard duty.
Skyrim.esm|06B13A: I bet we have rabbit again. I'm getting sick of rabbit.
Skyrim.esm|06B13B: We're running out of bread. I hope the supplies get here soon.
Skyrim.esm|06B143: Yeah, I know what you mean.
Skyrim.esm|06B145: You'd rather be plowing a field? Not me.
Skyrim.esm|06B146: Go tell the commander. I'm sure the boot in your arse will take your mind off your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|06B147: You must be all right if you've got time to complain.
Skyrim.esm|06B149: What the rebels like to forget, is that the Empire's what's keeping the Dominion out of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|06B14A: Can't wait to kill another of Ulfric's boys.
Skyrim.esm|06B14C: Next Imperial I see is dead.
Skyrim.esm|06B14D: We'll show those faithless dogs who this land belongs to.
Skyrim.esm|06B150: Keep an eye out for trouble. It's only a matter of time.
Skyrim.esm|06B155: Can't wait to gut one of those rebels.
Skyrim.esm|06B156: Soon. I can almost taste it.
Skyrim.esm|06B157: Well? Where the hell are those damned Stormcloaks? Let's get this over with already.
Skyrim.esm|06B158: If those rebels think they can best us, they have another think coming.
Skyrim.esm|06B159: By the Nine, I hate the waiting. The goddamned waiting.
Skyrim.esm|06B15A: Can't wait to kill another Imperial.
Skyrim.esm|06B15B: Gah. Let's do this already. I'm itching to kill something.
Skyrim.esm|06B15C: What's taking them so long? Did they get lost? Let's get this over with already.
Skyrim.esm|06B15D: Can't wait to see the look on their faces when they find us here waiting to spill their blood.
Skyrim.esm|06B163: Not sure you belong here, citizen.
Skyrim.esm|06B165: If it isn't Ice-Veins. Hi there.
Skyrim.esm|06B169: Think you might be in the wrong place, friend.
Skyrim.esm|06B1A7: Why aren't you at your post, Justiciar?
Skyrim.esm|06B1A8: You there. I don't think I've seen you around before.
Skyrim.esm|06B1E6: Come here. Let me see your face.
Skyrim.esm|06B1E7: Seems a bit short to me.
Skyrim.esm|06B501: The Emperor is going to love it. How can he not?
Skyrim.esm|06B502: Gianna will be overjoyed to meet you. She's in the kitchens.
Skyrim.esm|06B523: I'm not stealing anything. Besides, some jail time isn't going to make me roll over and play dead.
Skyrim.esm|06B524: Well then, if you don't want the extra gold, you can certainly let Letrush keep it as a gesture of goodwill.
Skyrim.esm|06B528: Which is utterly useless at the moment. You need to fill it with a soul.
Skyrim.esm|06B543: What? No! No, you don't understand! There... there's been some kind of mistake! I... Aggghhh!
Skyrim.esm|06B943: That'll teach you to break the law on my watch.
Skyrim.esm|06B944: I thought it was finally going to be a quiet day.
Skyrim.esm|06B945: You really thought you could get away with it?
Skyrim.esm|06B947: Move along. Nothing to see here.
Skyrim.esm|06B948: Well, this is a nice mess you've gotten me into.
Skyrim.esm|06B949: That could have turned out better.
Skyrim.esm|06B94A: I can't believe I got caught.
Skyrim.esm|06B997: A few days ago, some thieves from Riften broke in here and stole it! Can you imagine? Took the only thing of value we had. If you have the guts to head into the Ratway and get my bow back, I'll pay you what I can.
Skyrim.esm|06B999: Thanks again for getting my bow back from those thieves. Next time they come knocking I'll be ready.
Skyrim.esm|06B99A: After we were robbed, Dravin's become more bitter than ever. He hates this place.
Skyrim.esm|06BBA2: No, it can't be... oh my... and all this time, I was thinking he'd been unfaithful to me. I feel so stupid.
Skyrim.esm|06BBA5: So, any luck finding my louse of a husband?
Skyrim.esm|06BBA6: I can't believe my mother's gone. Now what'll I do?
Skyrim.esm|06BBA8: He said he was going east towards the border of Morrowind to trade some of our grain to some folks at Broken Helm Hollow. I waited and waited, and he never came back. Probably shacked up with some elven whore. Good riddance to him I say. If I only knew where he was, I'd sure give him a piece of my mind.
Skyrim.esm|06BC17: You're the one who's coming to Japhet's Folly with us, right? You ready to go?
Skyrim.esm|06BC2D: I must be on my way now.
Skyrim.esm|06BC2E: Well then, you'd better have something I really want to buy.
Skyrim.esm|06BC2F: Let's just take a look, then.
Skyrim.esm|06BC30: I hope you've got some coin.
Skyrim.esm|06BC31: All right, let's not overdo it. Meet me at my quarters.
Skyrim.esm|06BC32: Let me give you a bit of advice. You want to work on something that's not officially allowed? Don't tell anyone you're doing it. You need something you're not officially supposed to have, you come see me. You tell anyone that I told you, and I'll fry you from the inside out. Got it? Good.
Skyrim.esm|06BFFC: Protect yourself! We're under some kind of attack!
Skyrim.esm|06BFFE: We never should have brought that thing here!
Skyrim.esm|06BFFF: I hope this isn't something I did.
Skyrim.esm|06C000: If this is somehow your fault...
Skyrim.esm|06C0E2: Welcome, people of Solitude! We of the Bards College are pleased to be here to celebrate the Burning of King Olaf.
Skyrim.esm|06C0E3: The festival would not have been possible without the dedication and hard work of our latest applicant.
Skyrim.esm|06C0E4: With the lighting of the effigy...
Skyrim.esm|06C0E5: He becomes a full-fledged member of the Bards College. Please welcome our newest bard!
