Skyrim.esm|06C1DC: Good luck. Lucan and I will be waiting for you back in the shop.
Skyrim.esm|06C3D6: Two hundred years ago, I would have lain down my life for the Unholy Matron. But that age has long since passed. Astrid is my matron now.
Skyrim.esm|06C3D7: Without the Night Mother, there is no Dark Brotherhood. The matron deserves our unwavering support and respect. Though I admit, I find her Keeper a bit too exuberant for my liking.
Skyrim.esm|06C3D8: The witless fool and his pet corpse? Take a guess.
Skyrim.esm|06C3D9: Their arrival is the best thing that's happened to this Sanctuary in years. Astrid is an effective leader, but we've been rudderless for too long. Scrounging for contracts, abandoning the Tenets. The Dark Brotherhood has been reduced to a group of common cutthroats. Frankly, it's embarrassing.
Skyrim.esm|06C3DA: Truthfully? I'm not entirely sure. It's not that I don't have the utmost respect for Sithis, and the Night Mother, and the ancient ways. I do. But I've never been much for religion. I kill because it's what I've been trained to do. It's all I know. So long as Astrid is happy, I'm happy.
Skyrim.esm|06C3DB: I don't like mimes, minstrels, thespians, acrobats, jugglers, troubadours, or tumblers. Flutists give me a headache. I particularly hate jesters. As a rule, I'm also not crazy about the corpses of old women. For the Night Mother, I'll make an exception. But Astrid is the only mistress I serve.
Skyrim.esm|06C3DE: Look. If you're trying to get to know me - don't. I don't like you, and I never will. It's nothing personal. But... My wife trusts you, and I trust her judgment. So you can't be all bad. Here's all you need to know: I'm a werewolf. I like killing things. I love Astrid. I hate annoying people. And the color blue gives me a headache.
Skyrim.esm|06C3DF: I might ask the same of you. For you are more than mortal. You are moon-born. You are wolf. So you're a Companion, then? I can't imagine you got your gift anywhere else. I hope you have better luck with them than I did. Oh yes, I was once brother to the Companions. Let's just say they found my methods "unsettling." The Dark Brotherhood, obviously, feels differently.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E0: What a curious question. Well, I enjoy moonlit nights, taking long walks on the beach, knitting and unicorns. In fact, I once took a seaside stroll, on a moonlit night, and discovered a unicorn... which I proceeded to stab in the throat with a crochet needle. I'm a woman of refined, yet simple tastes.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E1: I'm just a little girl! The Dark Brotherhood killed my mama and papa, and then they took me captive! Please, please help me! Rather convincing, don't you think? In truth, I'm no more a little girl than you are. I was once, of course. Three hundred years ago. Vampirism tends to keep one remarkably... fresh.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E2: Hmph. I like to say I was born with a wand in my hand. Well, not literally - you can see how painful that would have been for Mother. But I was a prodigy! Casting simple spells by one, completing complex incantations by three. Resurrecting corpses by seven! Ha! When I was thirteen I accidentally burned down the family home. Bit of lightning gone awry. Ah, but soon after I mastered my gifts. Completely. I went on to teach at the College - left after two years. Too simple. Too safe. None of them truly understood the glory of the Destruction school...
Skyrim.esm|06C3E3: Once, I was a Shadowscale. An assassin in service to the King of Black Marsh. Trained by the Dark Brotherhood since the day of my hatching. Ah, but that was a lifetime ago. Today, I am the last of my kind. My order is extinct. Now, I happily serve Astrid, and this Sanctuary. I am a trained killer, and once again have been given purpose. Life is good.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E4: This Sanctuary has been my entire life since I was a young woman. That's when I first discovered my, shall we call it "aptitude," for elimination. I had an uncle, you see. He made certain... unwanted advances. So I killed him. And liked it. Then I killed again. And liked it even more. And so on. I was recruited by the Dark Brotherhood, and have been here ever since. I met my husband Arnbjorn, rose to leadership. And now here we are.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E5: Ah, well, I'm Redguard, as I'm sure you've noticed. Though not like most of my kind you'll find in Skyrim, that's for damned sure. I am a child of Hammerfell, born of the sands of the mighty Alik'r. In that great desert, blood is a shade of crimson I can scarce describe. My past is long and storied, and not altogether proud. But the Dark Brotherhood saved me from myself. And I have never looked back.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E7: Me? Oh, Cicero is just the Keeper! I... keep! I look after our matron, you see. The Night Mother. I keep her clean, and protected, and happy... But I am not the Listener. Oh no. There is no Listener. Not yet! But some day, some day, some day I pray, that one will come to hear her say... The words.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E8: Me? Oh, Cicero is just the Keeper! I... keep! I look after our matron, you see. The Night Mother. I keep her clean, and protected, and happy... But I am not the Listener. Oh no. You are the Listener! You heard the words! And everything will soon be put right.
Skyrim.esm|06C3E9: Yeah. Follow my wife's orders, and don't do anything stupid.
Skyrim.esm|06C3EA: You must speak to that Muiri woman, correct? I have heard rumors in Windhelm. She was recently implicated in a thievery, along with her lover. The lover will likely be your target. If memory serves me correctly, he leads a group of bandits. So this may be rather interesting for you.
Skyrim.esm|06C3EB: The contracts I dole out are fairly simple - go to a location, and kill the target. But this is different. The jobs Astrid gives you will be more important, and usually more complex. Just keep your wits about you. And for Sithis' sake, don't get killed.
Skyrim.esm|06C3EC: You're headed to The Hag's Cure, right? It's a good shop. You should examine their wares. Never pass up the chance to buy a good potion... or poison. And one more thing. Have fun. You'll be committing a dark deed, but there's no reason you shouldn't enjoy yourself. You're only young once. Trust me, I know.
Skyrim.esm|06C3ED: Yes. Just because you're now a professional assassin, don't think you have to skulk around in the dark like a skeever and stab people. Do what I do. Walk up to your target, introduce yourself, melt their skin off, and then run like the wind. Works every time.
Skyrim.esm|06C3EE: You're being sent to meet with a contact directly, is that correct? What a most unusual occurrence. Astrid usually performs this duty herself. I can only assume she wants to test the full breadth of your abilities. So be polite, and professional, and represent us well.
Skyrim.esm|06C3EF: She said that? If I find anyone working against my wife, or this Sanctuary, I'll beat them to death with their own severed head. Of course, they'd probably already be dead... But you know what I mean!
Skyrim.esm|06C3F0: You're joking. While I can understand if some family members wish to honor the Old Ways once more, surely no one would oppose Astrid's rule.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F1: Does she? Listen, brother... Astrid is my mistress, and I worship the ground she walks on. But this Cicero business has her a bit... paranoid.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F2: Does she? Listen, sister... Astrid is my mistress, and I worship the ground she walks on. But this Cicero business has her a bit... paranoid.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F3: Oh! Well, I hope she doesn't suspect me. I mean, I've spoken with Cicero a bit, and he's quite charming. But I'd never go against Astrid. Never.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F4: Ha! And would that surprise anyone? This Sanctuary's a mess. Wouldn't surprise me if somebody's talking to the fool on the sly. Making deals. And no, before you ask, it's not me. I know which side my bread is buttered on.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F5: To what end? Usurping Astrid's authority as leader of the Sanctuary? Hmmm... Yes, I can see her concern. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F6: Oho! The Listener wants my counsel! I thought you were above the opinions of mere mortals, now that you commune with dead old women.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F7: Just remember Astrid's commitment to her Sanctuary, to her Family. A Family that now counts you as one of its own. Honor Astrid above all others.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F8: You may be the Listener, or you may just be a lunatic who's started hearing voices. Either way, Astrid is still your mistress. Not the Night Mother.
Skyrim.esm|06C3F9: The man you need to speak with, his name is Motierre? That's a very old and powerful Breton family, firmly established in Cyrodiil. Most curious...
Skyrim.esm|06C3FA: So you're the Listener, eh? Well, that hardly seems fair. You just got here. The Night Mother should have spoken to me. Any idiot could see that. But, I will serve gladly, as that's my lot in life. Now let's see. Volunruud. Well, that's an old crypt, infested with draugr. So plan accordingly.
Skyrim.esm|06C3FB: If the Night Mother truly spoke to you, if you really are the Listener... your destiny was written in the Void. Astrid is right to fear your power.
Skyrim.esm|06C3FC: You've got to kill a soft little woman on her wedding day. What's next? Murdering a crippled orphan in his sleep?
Skyrim.esm|06C3FD: Your contract sounds simple, which means it certainly will not be. Getting into that reception will be easy, but have you thought about getting out?
Skyrim.esm|06C3FE: Slaughtering the Emperor's cousin? At her wedding? Those guards will be out for blood. Wouldn't surprise me if the crowd jumped in too.
Skyrim.esm|06C3FF: It's no fair. I love weddings. Astrid should have given this contract to me. Instead, she's got me visiting cities, assessing security. How boring. But listen, I think I can help you. The wedding reception is outside the Temple of the Divines, right? In Solitude? If so, the bride will probably address the crowd from the balcony. It overlooks the courtyard. Well, there's an old statue there. I've seen it. Hanging right over where she'll be standing. Old means weak. Weak means it could fall. Hmmm?
Skyrim.esm|06C400: Ah, killing her will be easy. She'll be so happy, she'll never see it coming. I should know, I killed a bride once. It was our honeymoon. Tepid, I said! The bathwater should be tepid! Lukewarm is not tepid! Close, maybe, but there is a difference! Stupid cow.
Skyrim.esm|06C401: I hope you don't mind, but I took the liberty of surveying the site of the reception. There's a small parapet, just opposite the balcony, that would prove an excellent spot for a long-range kill. I left something there for you. If you're inclined to take that approach.
Skyrim.esm|06C402: What's the matter, Listener? Night Mother not feeling very chatty these days? You need help from your flesh and blood lessers? You've got to track a man, kill him, and then frame him for a crime. This contract's perfect... for me. Clearly, my lovely wife made a mistake.
Skyrim.esm|06C403: Astrid had me investigate the Penitus Oculatus. Their members are highly trained, effective warriors. Be on your guard, brother.
Skyrim.esm|06C404: Astrid had me investigate the Penitus Oculatus. Their members are highly trained, effective warriors. Be on your guard, sister.
Skyrim.esm|06C405: Killing Gaius Maro in a city will be challenging because of all the guards, but fights, even murder, are fairly common. Killing him secretly will certainly make things easier on you, but if there are witnesses, so be it.
Skyrim.esm|06C406: Normally I talk to a victim before I end his life, but I wouldn't recommend it this time. Maro will be alert to danger. Talking may spook him.
Skyrim.esm|06C407: Remember, your target is Gaius Maro, the son. Not Commander Maro, the father! We need him alive. But the son... Ha! Scorch his face off!
Skyrim.esm|06C409: Yes, do not underestimate Cicero. A man like that, small and foolish, is easy to mock. To underestimate. Don't make the same mistake I did.
Skyrim.esm|06C40A: Going after Cicero now would be a fool's errand. That little jester is currently being digested in Arnbjorn's stomach, mark my words.
Skyrim.esm|06C40B: Cicero destroyed any sympathy I may have had for him when he wounded Veezara. Kill the fool and be done with it.
Skyrim.esm|06C40C: All right, so maybe I was wrong. Maybe Cicero coming here wasn't the best thing that could have happened. But even I didn't anticipate this. Well, you know the old saying. When life gives you lemons... ...go murder a clown.
Skyrim.esm|06C40D: I feel only pity for Cicero. He must die, of course, but can any of us truly blame him for his actions? We mocked him, disrespected the Old Ways. Our actions were shameful. Surely, the Night Mother weeps this day.
Skyrim.esm|06C40E: The last thing you need is my help. Listener or not, you've proven yourself time and again. To Astrid, to the Family... and to me.
Skyrim.esm|06C40F: Hmm... Well, assuming this Gourmet is just a cook, it will probably be an easy kill. Just be discreet - alerting others will be the real threat.
Skyrim.esm|06C410: Hmph. I wouldn't worry too much about this Virane giving you the information you need. Give him a good scare, and he'll squawk like a Hagraven. That said... If your attack on Virane is witnessed, those Markarth guards are going to be none too happy...
Skyrim.esm|06C411: Yes - don't kill the Gourmet! Oh, I know you have to, but it makes me sad all the same. I love the Gourmet's recipes. The world will soon be a blander, less flavorful place.
Skyrim.esm|06C412: You've been directed to dispose of the Gourmet's body, is that correct? I often find water to be a suitable hiding place. A lake, a pool... the sea.
Skyrim.esm|06C413: Yes. You're killing the Emperor himself. The Dark Brotherhood will get one shot at this, and you've been given the honors. So don't screw up.
Skyrim.esm|06C414: You get to kill the Emperor. The bloody Emperor. You'd better enjoy this one. You owe us all that much. So make it bloody, painful, and spectacular.
Skyrim.esm|06C80E: Are you a sellsword? I don't like your kind. No honor. No loyalty. Unreliable. But I can't turn away a potential ally in these times. Let's see if you're better than a common mercenary. My scouts have tracked down one of the Forsworn leaders, hiding out in their blood-soaked camps. Deal with them.
Skyrim.esm|06C80F: Yes. I earned the right by slaying my father in single combat. One day, I will have a son who will challenge me when I become too old to lead, and the stronghold will have a new chief. Until then, I alone have the right to have wives and children. It ensures our strength.
Skyrim.esm|06C810: For your help, I'm sending word to the Orc strongholds that you're to be trusted. You should be welcome there now.
Skyrim.esm|06C811: I'm drunk, and it's been a while since I had a good fight. A hundred septims says I can take ya, bare-handed.
Skyrim.esm|06C812: The strongholds keep to themselves. Skyrim hasn't always been kind to the Orcs. For an outsider like you to be accepted, an Orc would have to make you Blood-Kin, and send word to the chiefs.
Skyrim.esm|06C815: Hey, I may have had a few, but I can still count. You don't have a hundred septims on you.
Skyrim.esm|06C816: I'm impressed. Forsworn usually make short work of mercenaries, but you took care of them. Here is your reward.
Skyrim.esm|06C818: You need steel? Speak to my forge-wife, Gharol.
Skyrim.esm|06C819: Need a poison for your hunting? My mother, Murbul, knows several.
Skyrim.esm|06C81A: This stronghold respects strength, and strength alone.
Skyrim.esm|06C81B: I have an old cure for Rockjoint. Common in little Orc children.
Skyrim.esm|06C81C: If you wish to trade, wait for the sun to rise.
Skyrim.esm|06C81D: You should see Gharol if you're looking for a blade or armor.
Skyrim.esm|06C81E: I am the hunts-wife of this stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|06C820: Blades to kill your prey and your rivals.
Skyrim.esm|06C821: We can trade when there's light outside.
Skyrim.esm|06C84D: Of course I'm sure! He was asking about Esbern.
Skyrim.esm|06C84E: Of course I'm sure! She was asking about Esbern.
Skyrim.esm|06C84F: He lives somewhere in the Ratway Warrens. Must be crazy or desperate to live down there. Maybe both.
Skyrim.esm|06C850: And now you pay for meddling in the Thalmor's affairs.
Skyrim.esm|06C851: That's him! That's who was asking about Esbern! Kill him!
Skyrim.esm|06C852: That's her! That's who was asking about Esbern! Kill her!
Skyrim.esm|06C853: Doesn't matter. We'll kill them either way.
Skyrim.esm|06C85B: Pff. Go away, if you know what's good for you.
Skyrim.esm|06C85C: I got nothing to say to you.
Skyrim.esm|06C85D: I'm not looking for a conversation.
Skyrim.esm|06C85F: Don't know his name, but there is a guy like that hiding out down here. Paid plenty for nobody to know about it. You're not the first person to come in here asking about him, either, so be careful.
Skyrim.esm|06C860: Yeah, I think I know who you mean. He's paid good money for nobody to know he's down here, but I guess I can tell you.
Skyrim.esm|06C861: Down in the Ratway Warrens. Nice place, if you don't mind the stench, rats, and assorted lowlifes as neighbors.
Skyrim.esm|06C862: That's interesting. Why do I care? You want a drink, or maybe just a few broken bones?
Skyrim.esm|06C863: Never heard of anybody like that. But if you want to hide out in Riften, the Ratway's where you'd be. There's a sort of tavern down there, the Ragged Flagon. Where all the lowlifes in Riften get drunk and knife each other. You might try there, if you can get through the Ratway alive.
Skyrim.esm|06C864: He's in the Flagon right now. Get ready.
Skyrim.esm|06C865: She's in the Flagon right now. Get ready.
Skyrim.esm|06C97C: If you cause problems, I'll have to ask you to leave.
Skyrim.esm|06C97D: You're making my patrons nervous. Put the weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C97E: Put that thing away. You're making people nervous.
Skyrim.esm|06C97F: We've all seen your little weapon. We're impressed. Now, please put it away.
Skyrim.esm|06C980: It's time to put the weapon away now. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|06C981: It is making patrons nervous. It needs to put the weapon away, now.
Skyrim.esm|06C984: You can bring weapons in, but you can't use 'em in here. Got it?
Skyrim.esm|06C985: If it's not too much to ask, put the weapon away? Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|06C986: I trust I won't have to, but I'll bust your head in if you cause problems for my patrons.
Skyrim.esm|06C987: While a good fight is always entertaining, my patrons don't like blood in their food. Put the weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C988: If you don't mind, my patrons would appreciate it if you put the weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C98A: Don't break anything with that weapon, okay?
Skyrim.esm|06C98B: You break it, you buy it.
Skyrim.esm|06C98D: If that weapon breaks anything, you will be buying it.
Skyrim.esm|06C98E: This is a proper store. Start acting like a proper customer. Sheathe that weapon.
Skyrim.esm|06C98F: Don't start swinging that thing in here.
Skyrim.esm|06C990: If that weapon breaks anything of mine, I'll break something of yours.
Skyrim.esm|06C991: Put that thing away, or get out.
Skyrim.esm|06C992: You should probably put that away before you knock something over.
Skyrim.esm|06C993: Break my stuff with that weapon, and I'll break you.
Skyrim.esm|06C995: Fine weapon, but you wouldn't mind putting it away, I'm sure.
Skyrim.esm|06C996: Look. You're making my customers nervous. Put the weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C997: You. Weapon. Put it away. Now.
Skyrim.esm|06C998: Don't start swinging that thing in here. You break it, you buy it.
Skyrim.esm|06C999: Brandishing your weapon is not going to help you negotiate a better sale...
Skyrim.esm|06C99B: That weapon breaks my wares, we're gonna have us a real problem...
Skyrim.esm|06C99C: You won't be intimidating me into lower prices. Put that thing away.
Skyrim.esm|06C99D: Walking around with your weapon out is bound to make people nervous. We understand each other?
Skyrim.esm|06C99E: If that weapon causes problems for my customers, I cause problems for you. Got it?
Skyrim.esm|06C99F: Unless you're here to sell that weapon of yours, why don't you put it away.
Skyrim.esm|06C9A0: Come on. Put the weapon away. You're making people nervous.
Skyrim.esm|06C9A1: Why the drawn weapon? Is it contemplating violence?
Skyrim.esm|06C9A2: Neat weapon. You're not going to hurt anybody, though. Right?
Skyrim.esm|06C9A3: I trust you'll put your weapon away?
Skyrim.esm|06C9A4: You planning on using that weapon?
Skyrim.esm|06C9A5: Civilized people don't brandish their weapons unless they plan to use them.
Skyrim.esm|06C9A6: Umm... you're not planning on using that weapon, are you? At least not on me... right?
Skyrim.esm|06C9A7: You'd better know how to use that weapon if you plan on swinging it around in here.
Skyrim.esm|06C9A8: Perhaps you'd care to put your weapon away? Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|06C9A9: There a reason you're walking around with an unsheathed weapon?
Skyrim.esm|06C9AA: This one wonders why you brandish your weapon so?
Skyrim.esm|06C9AB: Might want to put it away that weapon... unless you plan on using it.
Skyrim.esm|06C9AC: You got no respect, strutting about with that weapon out, threatening everyone.
Skyrim.esm|06C9AD: Dear. You should put that weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C9AE: You got guts walking around with that weapon out. Looking for a fight? Is that it?
Skyrim.esm|06C9AF: Put that thing away before someone gets hurt!
Skyrim.esm|06C9B1: Now, now... no need for weapons.
Skyrim.esm|06C9B2: Nice weapon you have there. But I don't suppose you'd consider putting it away?
Skyrim.esm|06C9B3: What's this? Is it spoiling for a fight? If not, it should hide its weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C9B4: You want to fight? That why you have your weapon out?
Skyrim.esm|06C9B5: Wow. Nice weapon! Can I hold it? I promise not to break anything. Honest!
Skyrim.esm|06C9B7: You're not planning on hurting anyone with that weapon there, are you?
Skyrim.esm|06C9B8: So... carrying that weapon around like that makes you feel safe, does it?
Skyrim.esm|06C9B9: Uh. Hey. That weapon there is just for show. Right? I mean, you aren't going to hurt me or anything... right?
Skyrim.esm|06C9BA: In some parts of the world, wielding a weapon openly like that is an invitation to combat...
Skyrim.esm|06C9BB: Ooh, real tough, wagging your weapon around...
Skyrim.esm|06C9BC: I suggest you put away your weapon. Wouldn't want to cause a... scene.
Skyrim.esm|06C9BD: You're not planning on using that weapon in here, I hope.
Skyrim.esm|06C9BE: That's mighty bold, strutting about like that, looking for a fight. This one will oblige if he must.
Skyrim.esm|06C9BF: Oh ho! What's with the weapon? Looks like it's about to get rough in here.
Skyrim.esm|06C9C0: Damn fool parading around with your weapon out. You planning on hurting someone, huh?
Skyrim.esm|06C9C1: You're making people a bit nervous. Should consider putting that weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C9C2: Ever see someone killed with their own weapon? Would you like to?
Skyrim.esm|06C9C3: Walk into the the wrong place with your weapon drawn? You might not walk out again....
Skyrim.esm|06C9C4: It's probably best if you put that weapon away.
Skyrim.esm|06C9C5: Nice weapon you got there. Care to put it away?
Skyrim.esm|06C9E1: We believe the place in our vision to be Karthwasten. Now move quickly. The girl must be brought to the temple as soon as possible.
Skyrim.esm|06C9E4: The sisters are communing with Dibella. They can't be disturbed. You'll have to come back another time.
Skyrim.esm|06C9E6: The ceremony you so rudely interrupted was the Exalted Protocol of the Dibellan Sybil. I don't expect you to know what that means. Suffice to say that our Sybil was recently lost to us. Through the Protocol, we have seen the home of the next Sybil, to the north, in a small village pressed against the stone. If you can travel there, and retrieve our young Sybil, your transgression will be forgiven.
Skyrim.esm|06C9E7: You have committed a breach, and must pay the penalty.
Skyrim.esm|06C9E9: You just can't leave well enough alone, can you?
Skyrim.esm|06C9EA: So be it. We gave you a choice.
Skyrim.esm|06C9EB: So be it. We gave you a choice.
Skyrim.esm|06C9EC: Luckily for you, there are more pressing matters. We could make an example of you, but perhaps you could be put to better use.
Skyrim.esm|06C9ED: Unfortunately for you, we don't allow men into the inner sanctum.
Skyrim.esm|06C9EE: Unfortunately for you, we don't allow the uninitiated into the inner sanctum.
Skyrim.esm|06CCC5: Really, I think there's been a mistake.
Skyrim.esm|06CCCC: May I just speak with her, please, to clear all this up?
Skyrim.esm|06CCCE: Just let me out of here. I only wanted to help you!
Skyrim.esm|06CCCF: Okay, now can you let me out? Please? I won't tell anyone it was you.
Skyrim.esm|06CCD0: Just open the cage, and I'll leave quietly. Won't make a sound, won't bother you at all.
Skyrim.esm|06CCD3: She said you stay in there, and that's final.
Skyrim.esm|06CCE2: You should not have meddled in the Thalmor's affairs.
Skyrim.esm|06CCE6: There's the Blades agent! Kill him!
Skyrim.esm|06CCE7: There's the Blades agent! Kill her!
Skyrim.esm|06CCE8: That one right there, walking out the door. He was one of them asking about that old man you're looking for.
Skyrim.esm|06CCE9: Various shady types I've never seen before. I'd watch my back if I was you.
Skyrim.esm|06CD0E: I'll give you something to cry about if you don't quiet down.
Skyrim.esm|06CD0F: Well, I guess you'll have to.
Skyrim.esm|06CD10: If you really think you can manage... I mean, I'd hate to leave now and find out later you met some horrible fate at the hands of the Caller. But if you insist, I suppose I'll be on my way. Thank you, and good luck.
Skyrim.esm|06CD20: Treasure, that's what! Get off your scaly rump and let's go.
Skyrim.esm|06CD22: How can you just sit here and wait? We should be in there!
Skyrim.esm|06CD23: We must gather our strength. Who knows what's inside?
Skyrim.esm|06CE4F: What in Arkay's name was that?
Skyrim.esm|06CE50: What sort of power was that?
Skyrim.esm|06CE56: I haven't heard a true shout in generations!
Skyrim.esm|06CE58: Time to end this little game!
Skyrim.esm|06CE5F: I saw you do that, you know.
Skyrim.esm|06CEE2: Don't just stand there! Kill him!
Skyrim.esm|06D197: When you Shout, you speak in the language of dragons. Thus, your Dragon Blood gives you an inborn ability to learn Words of Power.
Skyrim.esm|06D198: Their Voices are too powerful for anyone not trained in the Way to withstand. Even a whisper could kill you.
Skyrim.esm|06D1C5: Don't take any chances with this one. Use the Jarrin Root to poison the Emperor's food. You're disguising yourself as a chef - it only makes sense.
Skyrim.esm|06D1EC: You show promise. Remain focused, and you will fulfill your potential.
Skyrim.esm|06D1EF: You have the ability, but you need to learn discpline if you wish to fulfill your potential.
Skyrim.esm|06D202: Approach Master Borri and he will gift you his knowledge of "Wuld."
Skyrim.esm|06D204: Approach Master Borri and he will gift you his knowledge of "Nah."
Skyrim.esm|06D205: Approach Master Borri and he will gift you his knowledge of "Kest."
Skyrim.esm|06D26B: Yeah, well, don't expect many more stories like that in my future. I'm getting old. Being that amazing takes too much damn effort.
Skyrim.esm|06D26C: Hmph. Been awhile since I've actually cleaved someone in two. Forgot how hard it was to get the scimitar back out.
Skyrim.esm|06D26D: Let that be a lesson to you young pups. There is no challenge that magic cannot overcome.
Skyrim.esm|06D26E: Come now, dear husband. You above all people should know what I'm capable of.
Skyrim.esm|06D26F: Sometimes I just need to remind everyone why I'm the one in charge.
Skyrim.esm|06D270: I guess I got tired of handing out contracts to everyone else, while I stayed home playing den mother. It was good to get out, and have some fun.
Skyrim.esm|06D271: I'd be lying if I said I didn't enjoy myself. But more importantly, I made this Sanctuary some much needed gold. In the end, that's all that matters.
Skyrim.esm|06D272: Well, I just did what had to be done. Nothing more. Nothing less. I'm just glad I lived to tell the tale.
Skyrim.esm|06D273: Ah, contracts like that are few and far between, it seems. Makes me long for the old days...
Skyrim.esm|06D274: Well... While it's true I prefer sorcery's more destructive nuances, we mustn't forget the many spells with simpler, more practical applications. Sure, you poison the Emperor, but what then? Why not summon an Atronach to cause some chaos? Or turn invisible and slip away. Use your imagination!
Skyrim.esm|06D275: What an honor, the assassination of an Emperor. And all because you are the Listener, favored by the Night Mother, anointed by Sithis. I am so very happy for you. If not a bit... envious.
Skyrim.esm|06D276: Astrid has arranged for your escape, no? Then you have nothing to worry about. Get in, kill the Emperor, and get out. Then, we celebrate.
Skyrim.esm|06D277: It took all the favors, bribes, and blackmails I could muster, but I've secured your exit out of the keep. Just follow my instructions, and the Dark Brotherhood will be back on top. All thanks to you... Listener.
Skyrim.esm|06D2AE: As I said, you will know you are ready when your Voice can open the path to him.
Skyrim.esm|06D3B6: Dragons have always been able to Shout. Language is intrinsic to their very being. There is no difference in the dragon tongue between debating and fighting. Shouting comes as naturally to a dragon as breathing, or speaking. In mythic times, when mortalkind was in great need, the goddess Kynareth granted us the ability to speak as dragons do. For most people, long years of training are required to learn even the simplest Shout. But for you, the dragon speech is in your blood, and you learn it almost without effort.
Skyrim.esm|06D3C2: Five. Our leader, Paarthurnax, lives alone on the peak of the Throat of the World. When your Voice can open the path, you will know you are ready to speak to him.
Skyrim.esm|06D43C: If we want to stay on top in Skyrim, the Guild needs that paragon back. Without it, we're just a bunch of petty thieves.
Skyrim.esm|06D480: Not exactly. The crown is what the Guild calls a paragon; an object that enhances our thieving abilities. At least, that's what I'm told it does... there hasn't been a paragon in this Guild for hundreds of years.
Skyrim.esm|06D481: Do I look like a merchant to you? You're going to have to take that up with Vex in the Thieves Guild. Get in good with them and you'll find her.
Skyrim.esm|06D4A6: The name's Maul; I watch the streets for the Black-Briars. If you need dirt on anything, I'm your guy.
Skyrim.esm|06D4A8: Only way you could have found one of those is by stealin' it. Guess you'll fit in around here better than I thought. So what do you want to know?
Skyrim.esm|06D4A9: Dirge told me you made it into the Guild. You need dirt on anything, you talk to me.
Skyrim.esm|06D4AA: What did you want to know?
Skyrim.esm|06D4AB: Back again? What is it this time?
Skyrim.esm|06D517: You want to talk? Finish those jobs for Brynjolf first.
Skyrim.esm|06D539: Get in good with Brynjolf, then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|06D918: Be sure and introduce yourself to your new Family members. They're all very eager to meet you.
Skyrim.esm|06D919: Walk always in the shadow of Sithis.
Skyrim.esm|06D91E: You require the services of the Fool of Hearts?
Skyrim.esm|06D920: How may I serve the Listener?
Skyrim.esm|06D93B: I was sure I heard something.
Skyrim.esm|06D940: When I find you, you're dead.
Skyrim.esm|06D942: I know you can hear me!
Skyrim.esm|06D948: You're a disgrace to your own kind!
Skyrim.esm|06D949: Your kind has no place here!
Skyrim.esm|06D94A: You should've stayed in your precious forests, elf!
Skyrim.esm|06D94C: I'm gonna wear that tail as a belt!
Skyrim.esm|06D94D: You'll make a fine rug, cat!
Skyrim.esm|06D952: I can do this all day!
Skyrim.esm|06D953: This is what I've trained for!
Skyrim.esm|06D954: No one can save you now!
Skyrim.esm|06DA82: This city is corrupt! You're all corrupt!
Skyrim.esm|06DA84: Can I interest you in some fine goods from Morrowind?
Skyrim.esm|06DE0D: No, I suppose we haven't. Brelyna Maryon, of House Telvanni. First of my family to leave Morrowind in a long time. Now I'm here to study Conjuration. That's what's important.
Skyrim.esm|06DE15: Dragons, is it? Does that make you the fabled "Dragonborn" we've heard mention of?
Skyrim.esm|06DE17: You must first prove your worth, and then I shall lead you across.
Skyrim.esm|06DE2C: It's quite simple really. There are some who shall not be dignified by being named, who have implied that it is not. That is to say, it's been suggested that resources used for my study of Restoration could be put to better use. Any other use, in fact. It's not just healing cuts and bruises, you know. The undead are a very real, very dangerous threat and Restoration magics can keep them at bay. Not to mention the benefit of wards! How can they continually overlook wards! It's baffling!
Skyrim.esm|06DE32: I've just been asked enough questions. I'm here to learn, not to explain myself over and over. Forgive me, I didn't mean to assume.
Skyrim.esm|06DE33: You risk death by doing so, and you shall not gain entry.
Skyrim.esm|06DE34: I... I see. Well, I'm glad we've established this early, rather than me hearing about it years or decades later when the whispers behind my back are loud enough.
Skyrim.esm|06DE36: Before you even ask, yes I have an ancestry steeped in magic, and no I don't want to talk about it. Yes, I know Winterhold used to be full of my kind, and no I don't care that they're all gone now. Does that cover everything?
Skyrim.esm|06DE3B: Do not cross the bridge unaided!
Skyrim.esm|06DE3C: By Sheor, no. Those archaic policies died out with the Mages Guild, and were never enforced here. Necromancy, as any other type of magic, is a tool to be used. Of course, non-mages may not see it that way, so we don't go around flaunting it.
Skyrim.esm|06DE3D: Good, good. Not that I require validation, of course. There are just some - and I shan't name names - who would disagree. It can make life difficult when one's colleagues fail to recognize the research one engages in.
Skyrim.esm|06DE3E: The key to wards is that they take time to charge. Not long, but you can't start casting with a fireball coming at you. Also, if your ward isn't negated by an incoming spell, it will recharge itself after a moment or two. Just be careful. Casting wards requires constant concentration, and you can easily find yourself drained of your magicka reserves.
Skyrim.esm|06DE7B: The research I've done seems to indicate that Skorm Snow-Strider left part of his journal here after laying siege.
Skyrim.esm|06DE7C: I believe it should indicate how to enter the blocked off sections of the stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|06DE7D: I should warn you, Forelhost is quite haunted, but I'm sure you can handle it. Return to me here once you've obtained the staff.
Skyrim.esm|06DE95: If you require assistance with summoning techniques, please let me know. If you would, please also contain your summons to the College grounds. The locals do not need to be agitated further by Atronachs roaming their town. The summoning of undead is even less advisable outside of the College.
Skyrim.esm|06DEAF: Please. Come in. Musn't talk in the Ratway.
Skyrim.esm|06DEB0: Come in, come in. Too many ears out there.
Skyrim.esm|06DEB2: That's better. Now we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|06DEC2: Please, follow me. I'll escort you inside the College.
Skyrim.esm|06DEC3: A mage is only as good as what he knows. I try and make sure as much knowledge is available as possible. We've been keeping this collection since the Second Era. Books have come and gone during that time, but it's mostly intact.
Skyrim.esm|06DEC4: You consider Restoration a valid school of magic, worthy of study, don't you? Don't you?
Skyrim.esm|06DEC5: You do indeed have the power. I'm impressed.
Skyrim.esm|06DF9A: Of course I do. If I didn't, most of these books would've been burned to ashes or dissolved to nothing before the Third Era. Mages need to be reminded to be careful around research materials.
Skyrim.esm|06DFA3: So you have the Voice, then? I would be quite impressed to see that.
Skyrim.esm|06DFB8: You are now in the Arcanaeum, of which I am in charge. It might as well be my own little plane of Oblivion. Disrupt my Arcanaeum, and I will have you torn apart by angry Atronachs. Now, do you require assistance?
Skyrim.esm|06E008: Didn't you know? Before the Oblivion Crisis, many elves called Winterhold their home. More visited the College from Morrowind every year. After, growing distrust of magic made life difficult for many. Some left rather than endure the growing hatred from the local Nords. Others returned home after the Red Year, when Vvardenfell erupted and caused much destruction. Winterhold itself died in the years between then and now. What's left out there is a husk. Only the College really remains.
Skyrim.esm|06E009: Can you? You have the power of the Voice? Now I'm interested. By all means, show me.
Skyrim.esm|06E00A: Well... No. Not really. Not directly, I mean. But I know things are said behind my back. Conversations cease when I enter rooms. I'm quite aware of what's going on here, and I will not stand for it!
Skyrim.esm|06E016: The gods will be the judge of that.
Skyrim.esm|06E017: I won't take no for answer!
Skyrim.esm|06E018: Stop it! This is a one-on-one duel!
Skyrim.esm|06E019: I thought you were a wizard. Hit me with a spell!
Skyrim.esm|06E01B: It's no fun unless you fight me with magic.
Skyrim.esm|06E01C: This is a magic duel. Ma-gic. You know, with spells.
Skyrim.esm|06E01D: You call yourself a wizard? I challenge you to a duel!
Skyrim.esm|06E259: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E25A: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E25B: What about you, Festus? How did that last contract turn out?
Skyrim.esm|06E25C: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E25D: I am not adorable, it was not funny, and he wasn't a merchant. He was a Khajiit monk, a master of the Whispering Fang style.
Skyrim.esm|06E25E: Ha ha ha ha! Again! Again! Do the part where he tries to buy you some candy.
Skyrim.esm|06E25F: Tried a new spell. Little something I've been working on in my spare time. Came "this" close to turning that priest inside out. Damned messy.
Skyrim.esm|06E260: But now he's dead... and I have a new loincloth.
Skyrim.esm|06E261: Oh ya, very good. Very good. My it is dark down here. Oh, but you are so beautiful. Such a lovely smile. Your teeth... your teeth! No! Aggghh!!
Skyrim.esm|06E262: Oh yes, please, kind sir. My mama and papa left me all alone, and I'm so very hungry. I know a shortcut to the candy shop. Through this alley.
Skyrim.esm|06E263: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E264: Oh, yes, please, old man. Regale us with your tales of wizardry...
Skyrim.esm|06E265: Oh, a big doggy chasing a little kitty! How adorable!
Skyrim.esm|06E266: And what of your latest, Arnbjorn. Something about a Khajiit? Merchant was it?
Skyrim.esm|06E267: Okay, okay. Wait. Here we go. "Ooh, you are such a pretty little girl. Would the sweetie like a sweetie? Oh yes, how about some chocolate?"
Skyrim.esm|06E268: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E269: Oh Babette, but you are so wicked.
Skyrim.esm|06E26A: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E26B: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|06E26C: Ah, the young and stupid. Always mocking the experienced and brilliant. My contract went very well, I'll have you know.
Skyrim.esm|06E78E: Yes, yes, you're the new Family member. Let's make this easy - consider me the cranky old uncle nobody talks to. You go your way, I'll go mine.
Skyrim.esm|06E78F: There you are! Astrid's told me all about you. Heard she pulled the ole' "Choose your victim" gag with you. Ah, I love that one...
Skyrim.esm|06E790: Well, well. It's raw meat. Oh, my beautiful wife has told me all about you.
Skyrim.esm|06E791: New brother. May I offer my most sincere welcome to our Family.
Skyrim.esm|06E792: New sister. May I offer my most sincere welcome to our Family.
Skyrim.esm|06E793: Welcome, my brother. Welcome to our Sanctuary. You have made the right choice, joining us, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|06E794: Welcome, my sister. Welcome to our Sanctuary. You have made the right choice, joining us, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|06E796: I'll give you a week before you end up dead in a ditch.
Skyrim.esm|06E797: You certainly have a strange energy about you. I look forward to following your progress.
Skyrim.esm|06E798: You just trust in Astrid, follow her orders, and you'll do fine here.
Skyrim.esm|06E799: You're going to love it here. We have a lot of fun, and we look out for each other. Just like any family.
Skyrim.esm|06EBD6: So what are you doing here? Lorkhan's eyes, you can't let the Thalmor get to him first! Get back to Riften!
Skyrim.esm|06EBD7: Did you tell him you were Dragonborn? I didn't think so. Lorkhan's eyes, do I have to do everything myself? Get back to Riften before the Thalmor find him!
Skyrim.esm|06EBD8: Well. They've been hunting down Blades since the Great War, on general principle. But if you mean me, now, in particular... maybe they've started to get an inkling of what the return of the dragons means. I don't suppose they want the world to end any more than we do. Or at least, they'd prefer to end it on their terms.
Skyrim.esm|06EBD9: Really? He's safe? Where is he?
Skyrim.esm|06EBDA: Haven't you been paying attention to anything I've said? Only you can finally defeat Alduin and avert the end of the world! If we can just find out what this Dragonrend Shout is, you may have a chance. Or not. There's no guarantee with prophecy. Merely hope.
Skyrim.esm|06EBDB: It's the prophecy, don't you see? Only a Dragonborn can stop Alduin and avert the end of the world! But... I don't yet know how you can stop him. The prophecy doesn't say. But Alduin's Wall does. I hope.
Skyrim.esm|06EBDC: Not yet, not yet. When we get to Riverwood... with Delphine... I'll tell you both then, when I've collected my thoughts. I never expected... well, suddenly here you are and now I'm too muddled to explain myself. I still need some time to get used to the idea that there might be hope for us after all.
Skyrim.esm|06EBDD: Hasn't she told you? We were both Blades, back when that still meant something. Now we're just fellow fugitives from the Thalmor. I didn't know she was still alive, but I'm not surprised. She was a survivor.
Skyrim.esm|06EBDE: What? You left him on his own? With the Thalmor after him? Go make sure he's safe and bring him here!
Skyrim.esm|06EBDF: Mara's mercy, then he's safe! Come on, we have a lot to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|06EEE1: That which is unknown can create unease, even fear. It is to be expected. Some fear our new resident wizard. As they grow to know him, they will accept him. Time will prove me right.
Skyrim.esm|06EFF0: I'm too weak right now. Between the spiders and the coven, I'd be dead before escaping the cave.
Skyrim.esm|06EFF1: These are no dainty college mages. Bashnag has the skill of a magister and the mind of a warlord. This is a dangerous bunch. They're good company if you want to study necromancy in peace, though.
Skyrim.esm|06F003: I want to get to my son. He's all I have left.
Skyrim.esm|06F169: Well? Get out of here and mind your rounds.
Skyrim.esm|06F176: Why didn't you say so? Take over my post. Make sure nobody enters the Solar.
Skyrim.esm|06F209: Oh. All right... I guess I'll just keep practicing on my own. Sorry to bother you.
Skyrim.esm|06F20A: Come on. I don't want to hit you until you have your ward ready.
Skyrim.esm|06F20C: Really? Great! Okay. So, first, you raise your ward and I'll throw a little spell at it. That way I can see the "right" way to do things.
Skyrim.esm|06F20D: I've finally found you! I want to join the College of Winterhold. But I need help with my wards. Can you teach me?
Skyrim.esm|06F20E: All right. That should do it. I think I'm ready to try the College again. Thanks!
Skyrim.esm|06F20F: Great! Now I'm going to cast some spells at you. Keep your ward up so I can study it.
Skyrim.esm|06F210: I think I see what I was doing wrong...
Skyrim.esm|06F417: Guess it was nothing after all.
Skyrim.esm|06F41D: That'll teach you to cross me.
Skyrim.esm|06F433: You can't get away from me!
Skyrim.esm|06F6B9: I did. Your time, it seems, has come.
Skyrim.esm|06F6BA: Yes, and we have something special for you this time.
Skyrim.esm|06F6BC: Last week a scholar came to us. He said he knew where we could find another fragment of Wuuthrad. He seemed a fool to me, but if he's right, the honor of the Companions demands that we seek it out.
Skyrim.esm|06F6BD: Don't know. He just said he needed to talk to you before you do anything else. I don't like making him angry, but there is some work for you if you want it.
Skyrim.esm|06F6BE: He didn't say, but you'd better move fast. There's probably something I could find for you to do, but best to check with him.
Skyrim.esm|06F6BF: I don't know, but he sounded impatient.
Skyrim.esm|06F6C0: So I'm told. Try and keep up.
Skyrim.esm|06F6C2: Aela will be your Shield-Sibling on this venture, whelp.
Skyrim.esm|06F6C3: Farkas will be your Shield-Sibling on this venture, whelp.
Skyrim.esm|06F6D6: That's a load off my mind. Here's your pay.
Skyrim.esm|06F6E9: Well, for that much, I don't think Malacath would turn his scowl on me. All right, outlander. My axe is yours.
Skyrim.esm|06F6FA: My honor isn't so cheap, outlander.
Skyrim.esm|06F7CF: Life's hard enough with all these men propositioning me. But that bard is the worst.
Skyrim.esm|06F7D2: You're right. Malacath teaches us to meet our fate. Perhaps this is mine.
Skyrim.esm|06F7DC: What do you know about my people, outlander?
Skyrim.esm|06F7DD: You've done a fine thing for me. You know, I used to be quite a warrior in my day. Let me show you a thing or two.
Skyrim.esm|06F7DE: My husband. We used to go out in the wilds and collect ingredients together. He used to call me his "pretty Juniper." It's quite romantic, if you're an alchemist. Frost took the old fool a few seasons back, while he was looking for the Ring of Pure Mixtures. I found the ring's resting place after he died, but I'm far too old to go exploring some cave, now.
Skyrim.esm|06F7E7: Good. I'll have a sack of coin waiting for you when you get back.
Skyrim.esm|06F7EA: You're right. Look at me, talking in circles like some old wise woman. Very well, outlander. You have my axe when you need it.
Skyrim.esm|06F7EE: Yes, but I am honor-bound to stay here.
Skyrim.esm|06F7EF: If you want to try, go right ahead. I don't think anything will get through that thick skull of his, though.
Skyrim.esm|06F7F2: Yes, but I must stay here and wait for the day when I'll be married and sent to another stronghold. It is our way, and I am proud to follow my people's traditions, but I can't help but feel chained.
Skyrim.esm|06F7F6: I couldn't. It would shame my chief and my mother and foremothers.
Skyrim.esm|06F7F9: I may have accidentally let a prisoner escape. The leader of one of those bandit groups. I told everyone he died during questioning, and I need you to track him down and make sure he doesn't show up to disprove that.
Skyrim.esm|06F7FE: No. A chief's daughter marries who her chief wishes. It ensures the stronghold receives a fair trade in goods for my absence.
Skyrim.esm|06F800: I do. There is a freedom the roads give you that the stronghold does not. But I have a place here, and I wouldn't want to dishonor my clan by leaving again like some young pup.
Skyrim.esm|06F802: I couldn't. It would dishonor the place the chief has made for me.
Skyrim.esm|06F805: You wouldn't be a sellsword, would you? I have a little problem you could solve.
Skyrim.esm|06F806: I am Larak's daughter. Soon, I'll be of age and will be sent to marry an Orc chief in some distant stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|06F807: I am an Orc and a warrior, but I have no desire to become chief.
Skyrim.esm|06F808: Are you looking to work? The mines are nearby. You bring me ore, I'll give you coin.
Skyrim.esm|06F80A: I did. I was known as Ghorbash gro-Dushnikh in those days, in honor of my homeland. Many Orcs leave the stronghold to join the Legion. They welcome our skill in battle and smithing, and we welcome a chance to see the world.
Skyrim.esm|06F815: Thank you. Here, let an old alchemist pass on some of her secrets to you.
Skyrim.esm|06F816: Too many drinking games, too many bets, and I'm too old to ever raise the coin on my own.
Skyrim.esm|06F817: Me? I'm Burguk's brother. I came back after serving many years in the Legion. By then, Burguk had already become chief, and he welcomed me home as family, not a rival as is tradition.
Skyrim.esm|06F818: You bested me, an Orc chief. Impressive.
Skyrim.esm|06F819: It's been so long since I tasted my own blood. Sour.
Skyrim.esm|06F81C: Damn addicts always stealing from me.
Skyrim.esm|06F81E: I am not weak. Very well, outlander, I will go with you whenever you are ready.
Skyrim.esm|06F81F: You dare challenge a child of Malacath?
Skyrim.esm|06F826: I can take care of myself. A Whiterun woman learns how to handle a few idiot men early in life.
Skyrim.esm|06F82A: Works for me. Tell Octieve the debt's settled.
Skyrim.esm|06F82E: I guess you're right. A Nord shouldn't be so proud to turn down a kind soul's generosity. Tell Iddra I'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|06F8A4: That is a fine idea. The stronghold would need the gold when I left to marry, anyway. I am ready when you are.
Skyrim.esm|06F94A: Debts are debts. Either he pays them, or his kind do.
Skyrim.esm|06F955: All right, all right. Have it your way. Tell Octieve to forget about the debt.
Skyrim.esm|06F95F: You should see my forge-wife, Shuftharz, if you need weapons or armor.
Skyrim.esm|06F960: My mother, Sharamph, knows many remedies you may wish to take with you.
Skyrim.esm|06F962: You remind me of myself a few years ago. Let me show you a little bit about wearing light armor. It's helped me out whenever I was scouting. Now I've got the itch to travel again. If you're leaving town, I wouldn't mind coming with for a while.
Skyrim.esm|06F963: It must be the Stormcloaks! Find them!
Skyrim.esm|06F96F: My pa always warned me about these things!
Skyrim.esm|06F970: Damn dustmen! Why don't you stay dead?
Skyrim.esm|06F971: My place is chief and my challenge is to lead my people.
Skyrim.esm|06F973: Do not allow Ataxia to fester. A smart warrior keeps a potion to cure diseases at all times.
Skyrim.esm|06F975: Just what I was afraid of!
Skyrim.esm|06F97A: If you wish to trade, see me during daylight.
Skyrim.esm|06F97B: Shuftharz can forge you some armor, outlander.
Skyrim.esm|06F97C: How about an axe? Malacath smiles on the axe-wielder.
Skyrim.esm|06F98E: The forge is strong and so is our chief, Larak. I'm content.
Skyrim.esm|06F998: Then I suggest you "collect" it. Until you pay the fine, you are effectively cut off from all Dark Brotherhood duties and contracts.
Skyrim.esm|06F999: Good. I'll spread the word that you've made reparations. Now let's forget the past and get back to business...
Skyrim.esm|06F99A: Excellent. Now we can get back to business. I think it's best we just forget this ever happened and move on, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|06F99B: Not ready? Well, my arrogant little sibling, until you pay the fine the Dark Brotherhood holds nothing for you. No contracts. No rewards. No respect.
Skyrim.esm|06F99C: I understand, Listener. And I hope you understand that until the fine is paid, I must insist you refrain from all Dark Brotherhood business.
Skyrim.esm|06F99D: You disrespected the Brotherhood. And it'll cost you. You want back into the Family, you pay the price. 500 gold, and all is forgiven.
Skyrim.esm|06F99E: You may be Listener, and head of the Sanctuary, but your recent behavior sets a bad example. I must insist you pay a fine of 300 gold.
Skyrim.esm|06F99F: If the Tenets were still in place, you would be required to defeat the Wrath of Sithis. Now you just need to pay a fine to Nazir. Ah, the Old Ways...
Skyrim.esm|06F9A0: When you disrespect your Family, you disrespect yourself. Don't you care for anything at all? Prove that you do - speak with Nazir, make reparations.
Skyrim.esm|06F9A1: Bah. If it were up to me, we'd cut off your hands and never let you back in this Sanctuary. But no. All you have to do is pay Nazir a fine. Pathetic.
Skyrim.esm|06F9A2: Ah, don't worry, we all like to break the rules sometimes. Just see Nazir and pay the fine. Seems I do that at least once a month...
Skyrim.esm|06F9A3: There are so few rules here. All we ask is that you don't disrespect your Family. But for some reason, you have. Best talk to Nazir and pay the fine.
Skyrim.esm|06F9A4: Idiot. I may not have a lot of rules, but that doesn't give you free rein to do as you please. You want to resume your duties? Go talk to Nazir.
Skyrim.esm|06FA12: I'm not a coward, outlander. You want me to prove it? Fine. My axe is yours. To the most dangerous corners of Skyrim and back.
Skyrim.esm|06FABA: All right. I'll lay off him. Just leave.
Skyrim.esm|06FB0F: It's got to be the Imperials! Find them!
Skyrim.esm|06FC72: Well, aren't you sweet? I'd be happy to give you some alchemy training in exchange for the ring, if you bring it to me.
Skyrim.esm|06FEB1: It should be careful with those flames.
Skyrim.esm|06FEB2: You're not supposed to play with fire, you know.
Skyrim.esm|06FEB3: Take care not to set anything on fire.
Skyrim.esm|06FEB4: You're not afraid you'll set fire to something?
Skyrim.esm|06FEB5: What kind of fool plays with fire like that?
Skyrim.esm|06FEB6: You're not planning on burning anything with that, are you?
Skyrim.esm|06FEB7: Careful not to torch anything important.
Skyrim.esm|06FEB8: I do hope you know what you're doing with those flames...
Skyrim.esm|06FEB9: Try not to set anything on fire, all right?
Skyrim.esm|06FEBA: Fire is not a thing to be played with...
Skyrim.esm|06FEBC: What's the matter with you? You want to start a blaze?
Skyrim.esm|06FEBD: Dear, you really ought to be careful with fire.
Skyrim.esm|06FEBE: Better not set anything on fire.
Skyrim.esm|06FEBF: What do you think you're doing? Knock it off with the flames already.
Skyrim.esm|06FEC1: Is it getting hot, or is it just me?
Skyrim.esm|06FEC2: Whoa. Hope you know what you're doing with those flames there.
Skyrim.esm|06FEC3: It is planning on starting a fire?
Skyrim.esm|06FEC4: Hey now, you be careful with that fire.
Skyrim.esm|06FEC5: I wish I could play with fire like that.
Skyrim.esm|06FEC7: Mind yourself with that fire. Don't burn anything down, now.
Skyrim.esm|06FEC8: Have a fascination with fire, do we?
Skyrim.esm|06FEC9: Umm... don't suppose you'd want to take that fire somewhere else? You know... somewhere away from me?
Skyrim.esm|06FECA: Careful with those flames, unless you plan on torching something.
Skyrim.esm|06FECC: I do hope you know what your doing with those flames. It would be quite a shame if something caught fire.
Skyrim.esm|06FECD: Don't set anything on fire, all right?
Skyrim.esm|06FECF: Don't go setting the place on fire, okay?
Skyrim.esm|06FED0: Plan on burning everything down, do you? Damn fool.
Skyrim.esm|06FED1: You should be more careful with those flames.
Skyrim.esm|06FED2: Those flames get out of control, and you're gonna wish they hadn't.
Skyrim.esm|06FED3: Careful, now. Wouldn't want someone to get... burned.
Skyrim.esm|06FED5: You've got that fire under control. Right?
Skyrim.esm|070207: Really? I dunno... I don't feel invisible.
Skyrim.esm|070209: Come on. Hurry it up already. The waiting is killing me.
Skyrim.esm|07020A: Hey... So are you ever going to make me invisible or what?
Skyrim.esm|07020C: Really? I mean... I still feel like I'm here.
Skyrim.esm|07020D: All right! I knew you'd do it. This is going to be so fun. You can't even imagine!
Skyrim.esm|07020E: Ooh, you're a wizard! I mean, you cast that spell just now, yeah? I was hoping you could make me invisible or something. Can you? Make me invisible?
Skyrim.esm|07020F: I can't wait to be invisible.
Skyrim.esm|070210: Awww. And here I thought you could do it... Well, I'll just have to think of something else, then. Bye.
Skyrim.esm|070215: Okay, great! Now I'm finally going to have some fun around here. Bye!
Skyrim.esm|070374: I can't believe it's come to this.
Skyrim.esm|070375: Bunch of foreign troublemakers. Wandering around, getting into mischief, scaring the citizens. They've been told to stay out of the city, but keep trying to get back in.
Skyrim.esm|070376: I've told you, we can't talk out in the open. Come with me.
Skyrim.esm|07038E: What do you think you're doing?
Skyrim.esm|070394: Give it to me quickly, before they see us together. You've riled up the priestesses, and the guards don't take kindly to that. Here's your share. Nicely done.
Skyrim.esm|070435: I write drama, friend. The legends and history of Skyrim made to excite and inspire. Poor Giruad Gemane at the Bards College has been waiting for my latest work, "Olaf and the Dragon," for weeks now. Roads are a mess.
Skyrim.esm|070437: I was the finest scout in the Legion, once. Tracked down all the places in the wilds that were causing people trouble. Lost my helmet in one of the old caves. My pretty, pretty helmet. Life just got worse after that. Kept losing things. So many things....
Skyrim.esm|07043E: You're sure? When you see Giruad, ask for a tip. I'm sure he'll be in a good mood after reading this.
Skyrim.esm|07044D: Oh, you could? I sure would love to have it again. Could show you some of my old scouting tricks.
Skyrim.esm|070490: Ah, Berit's ashes. He was a good man. Not many warriors grow to a fine, old age. Thank you for bringing this to me. I'll make sure he gets his rites. Here, for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|070574: Look at it. That's real Nord craftsmanship, that is! Let me show you an old trick I learned as a scout. Makes it harder for the enemy to notice you.
Skyrim.esm|0705B4: If you're one of the Jarl's men, you'll get your lumber. Anga's Mill pays its debts.
Skyrim.esm|070DE6: Here. For you. For my hatchling of the heart.
Skyrim.esm|070DE7: I found this. I thought you might like it.
Skyrim.esm|070DE8: I wanted to give you this. A token of my appreciation.
Skyrim.esm|070DE9: Here. It isn't much, but... I hope it's the thought that counts.
Skyrim.esm|070DEA: I saw this and thought of you. It would be a waste on anyone lesser. I hope you like it.
Skyrim.esm|070DEB: Um... I was thinking you might like this. Here. It's nothing really.
Skyrim.esm|070DEC: In dark times friends bring light. This is for you. Thank you for your friendship.
Skyrim.esm|070DED: I had to get this for you when I saw it. I hope you like it.
Skyrim.esm|070DEE: I'm glad we're friends. I have something for you. Here.
Skyrim.esm|070DEF: This one is honored by your friendship. I hope this gift does honor to you.
Skyrim.esm|070DF0: Here's to friendship. A gift! If you don't like it, too bad.
Skyrim.esm|070DF1: You're always a sight for sore eyes. Here, I've been hanging on to this for you.
Skyrim.esm|070DF2: I've been holding on to this for you. I hope you like it.
Skyrim.esm|070DF3: I have something I want to give to you. Here.
Skyrim.esm|070DF4: Here. For you. Don't thank me, just take it.
Skyrim.esm|070DF6: You always brighten my day a little. Here. I hope you like this.
Skyrim.esm|070DF7: It's really great we're friends. I was hoping to find some way to thank you. I hope this does the trick.
Skyrim.esm|070DF8: You are like my brood to me. Here. A token.
Skyrim.esm|070DF9: You're a good friend. Here. Take this. You'll like it.
Skyrim.esm|070DFA: So I found something. Here. Take it. I think you'll like it.
Skyrim.esm|070DFC: I've been wanting to say thanks for being a friend. Here. It's not much, but I was thinking you might like to have this.
Skyrim.esm|070DFD: You're not tiresome like the rest of the people I have the displeasure of dealing with on a regular basis. Here. I'd like you to have this.
Skyrim.esm|070DFE: So... I've been thinking... I'm grateful we're friends. I'd like you to have this. It's not much, but I hope it's something you like.
Skyrim.esm|070DFF: A true friend is a gift. Here. I'd like you to have this.
Skyrim.esm|070E00: You know... you ain't so bad. Here. Take this...
Skyrim.esm|070E01: When I found this, I immediately thought of you. Here. A small token of my gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|070E02: Here. I'd like you to have this as a token my friendship.
Skyrim.esm|070E03: You bring a smile to this one's eyes and peace to his heart. Take this. A small token of appreciation.
Skyrim.esm|070E04: In a world of fools, you stand out above the rest. Here, take this small sign of my friendship.
Skyrim.esm|070E05: Here. Something I want you to have. Now, don't embarrass either of us by saying something sappy. Just... take it.
Skyrim.esm|070E06: I've been holding onto this for you. Here! I hope you like it.
Skyrim.esm|070E07: Here. I want you to have this.
Skyrim.esm|070E08: I wanted you to have this. A token of my affection. Don't mean we're engaged or nothin'.
Skyrim.esm|070E09: Here. Take this. I hope you like it.
Skyrim.esm|070E0A: I got this for you. It's nothing big, but I hope you like it. I want you to know I appreciate your friendship.
Skyrim.esm|070E74: Can't prove it. But I could use your help, being unbiased and all. See if you can find anything in the burned out house. I'll pay you to figure it, whether or not he's guilty.
Skyrim.esm|070E75: Bear fat in the hearth fire, if you believe Hroggar. I do, but some folk here think he set the fire himself.
Skyrim.esm|070E76: I won't let you hurt Alva!
Skyrim.esm|070E77: The poor child is probably beggin' for us to string up her murderin' father. Best tell the Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|070E78: The spirit world is strong in this place. Look to the graveyard. That is where you'll find her.
Skyrim.esm|070E7A: They think he did it to get rid of his wife so he could be with Alva. He moved in with her the day after the fire. Even I find that suspicious.
Skyrim.esm|070E7B: Hroggar's house? He lost his wife and daughter in the blaze.
Skyrim.esm|070E7C: Who's there? Is that you, father?
Skyrim.esm|070EBE: You mean... there's still a chance? But how? Our plan has gone to ruin, everyone is dead, the Family...
Skyrim.esm|070EBF: Ah, yes, I can imagine you'd want to settle that score. Last I heard he was at the Solitude Docks, conducting the Emperor's departure.
Skyrim.esm|071051: You found it? I've been looking for that blade for months. You took on all those bandits yourself? Thank you. Here, my father had a favorite sword and shield technique. Let me teach it to you, in honor of his memory.
Skyrim.esm|0711C8: It's an evil substance. Whoever's selling it to you should be brought to justice.
Skyrim.esm|0711C9: Next time you speak to him, tell him to clean up his own messes. I'm not about to jeopardize this position for a bunch of low-life thugs.
Skyrim.esm|0711CA: If I cared for your opinion on our affairs, I'd ask. Now, if you'll excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|0711CB: Maul, can I ask a personal favor of you?
Skyrim.esm|0711CC: Don't worry about it, Svana. It's my problem... no need for you to get involved.
Skyrim.esm|0711CD: If you'd stop wasting your coin on that skooma, you'd have enough gold to live on.
Skyrim.esm|0711CE: Ingun! If you want to remain in my shop as an apprentice I expect you to act like one. No exceptions!
Skyrim.esm|0711CF: The execution will proceed as planned.
Skyrim.esm|0711D0: Yes, and I sent it with our fastest courier and under the cover of night, exactly as you specified.
Skyrim.esm|0711D1: Yes, milady. Sorry, milday. They will be completed.
Skyrim.esm|0711D2: Sorry, Tythis. I'll get it to you somehow.
Skyrim.esm|0711D3: Very well, I'll keep practicing them.
Skyrim.esm|0711D4: Any word about our little problem in the Ratway?
Skyrim.esm|0711D5: Well that depends on what the favor is, now doesn't it.
Skyrim.esm|0711D6: No, mother. The execution will proceed as planned.
Skyrim.esm|0711D7: Look, if you're in some sort of trouble, I could talk to Haelga.
Skyrim.esm|0711D8: Yes, she's been grooming herself for this for quite a long time.
Skyrim.esm|0711D9: How do you do it, Valindor? How do you work at Black-Briar Meadery and the Fishery without getting exhausted?
Skyrim.esm|0711DA: Ingun, you clumsy fool! You've tainted our entire supply of Painted Troll Fat. You have no idea how hard that was to get.
Skyrim.esm|0711DB: It's Hemming. He's making advances and I can't bear the man's presence. Is there anything you can do?
Skyrim.esm|0711DC: No, no... please, I'm just tired is all. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0711DE: The coin isn't just for the men, there's a lot of people that need to be compensated in order to make this happen.
Skyrim.esm|0711DF: Oh, it's not so terrible. I actually find the work quite relaxing.
Skyrim.esm|0711E0: I'm sure my mother can compensate you for it.
Skyrim.esm|0711E1: It's surprising the Council is allowing a Nord rule Riften under the Imperial flag. That can't be their first choice.
Skyrim.esm|0711E2: Your mother seems to have taken to her seat quite well.
Skyrim.esm|0711E3: I'll talk to him, but he's stubborn. He's like his mother; he sees what he wants and he won't quit until he has it.
Skyrim.esm|0711E4: Good. If I need to light a few fires in order to get what we need to defend this place from the Stormcloaks, so be it.
Skyrim.esm|0711E5: Let's just say my mother knows which pockets to line and keep it at that.
Skyrim.esm|0711E6: But, mother... this is quite a sizable contribution and the crime doesn't befit such a harsh punishment.
Skyrim.esm|0711E8: Anything you can do to keep his filthy hands off of me would be a welcome change.
Skyrim.esm|0711E9: There's still the matter of the prisoner to discuss. Now, his family is willing to contribute handsomely if we release him.
Skyrim.esm|0711EA: Relaxing? They must be working you to the bone! You should rest before you do yourself in.
Skyrim.esm|0711EB: I'll draft a letter to Rythe. If you could have it sent to Cheydinhal for me, that would make up for your blunder.
Skyrim.esm|0711EC: Not to worry, dear Svana. I promise you I can handle it, but your concern is quite flattering.
Skyrim.esm|0711ED: I've spoken to Brynjolf and he assures me it'll be taken care of.
Skyrim.esm|0711EE: So, have you thought about what I said?
Skyrim.esm|0711EF: So how are things with you and Bolli?
Skyrim.esm|0711F0: I'll make sure it's sent right away.
Skyrim.esm|0711F1: My agents are telling me that we've appropriated the caravan from Hammerfell... with minimal losses.
Skyrim.esm|0711F2: And a hefty cut for yourself too I'm sure. Just see to it the items are secured and the rest disposed of properly this time.
Skyrim.esm|0711F3: I'm sorry, milady. The components for those elixirs are quite volatile and in some cases, very dangerous to handle. I must take care.
Skyrim.esm|0711F4: Hemming, have you dispatched that letter I gave you yesterday?
Skyrim.esm|0711F5: Wujeeta, you really need to stop using that skooma. It's very bad for your health and I wouldn't want to see anything happen to you.
Skyrim.esm|0711F6: I don't understand, Elgrim. The formulas you're giving me are for novices but I've advanced well passed that point.
Skyrim.esm|0711F7: For what it's costing me, I should be handed the merchandise on a silver platter.
Skyrim.esm|0711F8: Why are the potions I requested not completed?
Skyrim.esm|0711F9: Excuses are not acceptable in my court. When I ask for something I expect results, and I expect them immediately.
Skyrim.esm|0711FA: I find the concept of sleeping with you not only repulsive, but morally unsettling. Does that answer your question?
Skyrim.esm|0711FB: How do you think? He's a pig. All I am to him is a warm body to lay with.
Skyrim.esm|0711FC: If there was any justice in the world, I'd just get this over with and die peacefully.
Skyrim.esm|0711FD: Niluva, you promised me you'd pay me back yesterday. So what's the story?
Skyrim.esm|0711FE: Until you can do those formulas with your eyes shut, you'll never be ready to master the more difficult ones.
Skyrim.esm|0711FF: I'm almost certain he'll reach the Imperial City tomorrow, so we should have an answer by the end of the week.
Skyrim.esm|071200: You listen to me. I'd be careful wagging that pretty little tongue at me like that otherwise I may just have it cut off.
Skyrim.esm|071201: I can't, Valindor. It won't let me go. It's taking over my life.
Skyrim.esm|071202: Are you going soft on me, Hemming? Perhaps I made the wrong choice installing you as steward.
Skyrim.esm|071203: This is an utter waste of time.
Skyrim.esm|071204: Nivenor! How can you say that? He gives you everything you ask for.
Skyrim.esm|071205: Just give me a few more days and I promise I'll come up with it.
Skyrim.esm|071206: Just stay away from me and maybe I won't weave a spell to shrink something that matters to you.
Skyrim.esm|071207: Wujeeta, you're worrying me. You look so... well, so sad.
Skyrim.esm|071440: I have a letter here for you.
Skyrim.esm|0716DC: That is your choice. We will not force you. But to join the Circle, your blood must be as ours. Meet us here when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|0716EF: Not here. The Underforge. Come on.
Skyrim.esm|07175F: There's a new guard nosing around the meadery. Can you do something about it?
Skyrim.esm|071761: I don't want to settle. I like things the way they are.
Skyrim.esm|071762: I need you to do me a favor, Dirge.
Skyrim.esm|071763: You need to tell your boys to place nice, Delvin. Almost threw two of them into the well last night.
Skyrim.esm|071764: Come on, Ton. Marry me. Let me take care of you.
Skyrim.esm|071765: All right, Delvin. What gives with that case of Shadowbanish you sold me?
Skyrim.esm|071766: Gladly. He won't bother you anymore, I promise.
Skyrim.esm|071767: You did? Thanks. I get you the rest of your coin by tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|071768: Vekel, you have no shame. I'll get you the rest like I said. I want that hammer.
Skyrim.esm|07176A: Sure, Ton. You know I'm good for it.
Skyrim.esm|07176B: I can take care of myself.
Skyrim.esm|07176C: Sure, it tastes great. Only one problem. You said it can make the darkness as clear as day when you drink it. Guess what? It doesn't.
Skyrim.esm|07176D: Maybe. That stuff's gettin' tough to bring across the border with all the Argonian patrols.
Skyrim.esm|07176E: Yeah, funny thing about that. The price was a hundred higher than I expected.
Skyrim.esm|071770: How dare you! After all we've been through together? How can you do this to me!
Skyrim.esm|071772: They aren't my boys. Do I look like the leadin' type?
Skyrim.esm|071773: Puttin' together another shipment from Morrowind, Vekel. Lookin' for anythin' special?
Skyrim.esm|071776: Look, I need to know. What's going on between you and Brynjolf?
Skyrim.esm|071777: Am I responsible if the vintage was off on that case? Sorry, you know the rules Vekel. No refunds.
Skyrim.esm|071778: Well if it turns up, I'll be willing to buy.
Skyrim.esm|07177C: Good news. I found that dwarven hammer you were looking for.
Skyrim.esm|07177F: If you could spare a septim, I'd be grateful.
Skyrim.esm|071780: Help out an old man here with some coin, would you?
Skyrim.esm|071781: Hey, can I get a gold or two?
Skyrim.esm|071782: I could sure use some coin.
Skyrim.esm|071785: Edda, you know well I barely have any coin to call my own. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|071786: You must be starving. Here, spend it well.
Skyrim.esm|071789: Yes, of course. This war is causing your woes, it's hardly your fault.
Skyrim.esm|07178B: Are you joking? Go earn a living and stop loafing!
Skyrim.esm|07178C: I would if I could, but I can barely make ends meet.
Skyrim.esm|07178F: Maybe you should look for work instead of begging for coin.
Skyrim.esm|071791: Oh you poor thing! Come by the temple and my husband and I will keep you fed.
Skyrim.esm|071792: Edda, Edda. There's no need for this. Just come by the temple and we'll care for you.
Skyrim.esm|071794: I've told you before, and I'll tell you again. Leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|071795: Ugh, you reek. Get out of my way.
Skyrim.esm|071797: Oh, very well. Just try and clean yourself up.
Skyrim.esm|071798: Get away from me, you filthy woman.
Skyrim.esm|071799: Why can't you just settle down and run the Flagon with me?
Skyrim.esm|07179A: That's all they are... rumors. So quit worrying.
Skyrim.esm|07179B: Please milady, can you spare a gold coin for an old woman?
Skyrim.esm|07179C: Please my lord, can you spare a gold coin for an old woman?
Skyrim.esm|07179E: Please, I don't have anything to eat.
Skyrim.esm|0717A1: Well, if some Moon Sugar should fall into your lap...
Skyrim.esm|0717A3: Snilf, it's me. I don't have a roof over my head either, remember?
Skyrim.esm|0717A4: Oh if only I could provide more than this coin. I hope it'll do.
Skyrim.esm|0717A5: Again? Buy some food this time will you?
Skyrim.esm|0717A7: Another uncounted causality of this war. Here you are, you poor man.
Skyrim.esm|0717A9: You're disgusting, stay away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0717AA: If I had even a pot to piss in, I'd gladly share. Sorry.
Skyrim.esm|0717AB: Yes, I hope this helps you.
Skyrim.esm|0717AC: Absolutely. Here you are. Be well.
Skyrim.esm|0717AD: Ugh, you smell horrible. Get yourself cleaned up.
Skyrim.esm|0717AF: Oh you poor thing, you must be starving. Come to the temple later and let us feed you.
Skyrim.esm|0717B0: Snilf, I told you begging isn't the answer. I expect to see you at the temple later.
Skyrim.esm|0717B2: If you want to awaken from your next slumber, I suggest you leave me be.
Skyrim.esm|0717B3: What? No. Get away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0717B4: I suppose. Just get something to eat and stay away from the Bee and Barb for a change.
Skyrim.esm|0717B5: Very well, please use it for food.
Skyrim.esm|0717B7: What do you mean? That wine tastes finer than anythin' Surilie Brothers ever made.
Skyrim.esm|0717B8: You know what I mean. Tell your boss that rough is rough, but when someone pulls a dagger fun time is over.
Skyrim.esm|071A88: Money, if that is what you wish. Now tell me where she is!
Skyrim.esm|071A92: We cannot go after her, not without drawing attention. You must bring her to us. Lead her to the Whiterun stables, outside of the city. We will wait for you there.
Skyrim.esm|071AB0: You have? Where? Tell me now!
Skyrim.esm|071AD6: She sold the city out to the Aldmeri Dominion. Were it not for her betrayal, Taneth could have held its ground in the war.
Skyrim.esm|071ADA: The other noble houses discovered her betrayal and she fled. They want her brought back alive.
Skyrim.esm|071ADF: The resistance against the Dominion is alive and well in Hammerfell, and they want justice.
Skyrim.esm|071C28: We're here for Alduin's Wall, right, Esbern?
Skyrim.esm|071C29: Yes, of course. We'll have more time to look around later, I suppose. Let's see what's up ahead.
Skyrim.esm|071C2A: Fascinating! Original Akaviri bas-reliefs... almost entirely intact!
Skyrim.esm|071C2B: Amazing... you can see how the Akaviri craftsmen were beginning to embrace the more flowing Nordic style...
Skyrim.esm|071DE1: Ysgramor was the hero who started the Companions. Wuuthrad was his weapon. He came from the ancient homeland and killed all the elves. But not all of them, because some of them are still here.
Skyrim.esm|071DE2: Not everyone, but all the Circle are. It's a secret to everybody.
Skyrim.esm|071DE3: He'll answer any questions you have. Try not to disappoint. Or to get him killed.
Skyrim.esm|071DE4: She'll answer any questions you have. Try not to disappoint. Or to get her killed.
Skyrim.esm|071DE6: Bad people who don't like werewolves. So they don't like us either.
Skyrim.esm|071DE7: I watch you to make sure you are honorable. If you are honorable and strong, then I can call you brother.
Skyrim.esm|071DE8: So I'm told. Let's see if you impress.
Skyrim.esm|0721A6: Leave this place... Leave this place...
Skyrim.esm|072659: Many battles have been fought here over the centuries. Graves were dug and monuments built, and the town grew around these. In time, Falkreath came to be known as the heroes' graveyard. Many noble Jarls and renowned warriors asked to be buried here. Falkreath's reputation has faded somewhat, but the graves remain, reminding us of the one inevitable truth of life.
Skyrim.esm|07265A: It was Dengeir who made me a steward, long ago when he was young. When Dengeir's health began to fail, young Sidgeir was appointed Jarl in his stead. It was simpler just to keep me as steward. Sidgeir has no interest in running his hold, and so leaves such matters to me. As for my part, I do my best to see that folk are treated fairly.
Skyrim.esm|07265B: Difficult? I am a Jarl. I eat the most succulent meat, drink the finest ale and hunt with the best hounds in the hold. Meanwhile, my very capable steward sees to the needs of the smallfolk and ensures that my hold runs smoothly. Being a Jarl could hardly be simpler. You should try it sometime.
Skyrim.esm|07265E: My uncle Dengeir was Jarl until a short time ago. His old age and failing health caught up with him, and he stepped down.
Skyrim.esm|072664: There's two more of them ahead.
Skyrim.esm|072666: That door up ahead... looks perfect for hiding an ambush. Be ready.
Skyrim.esm|072669: Bone chimes... clever. Rigged to wake the draugr I'd bet. Don't blunder into any of them.
Skyrim.esm|07266B: The stench in here... this place smells of death. Be on your guard.
Skyrim.esm|07266D: Karliah always was a nimble minx... slipping passed these draugr must be child's play for her.
Skyrim.esm|07266E: Pull the chain over there, and watch out for the spikes. Looks like Karliah reset all of the traps.
Skyrim.esm|07266F: We're on the right track. She's been through here as well.
Skyrim.esm|07271D: Karliah's been through here. What does she expect to find?
Skyrim.esm|072766: And, once more, I prove Commander Maro the fool. I told him you can't stop the Dark Brotherhood. Never could.
Skyrim.esm|072767: Hmph. Now there's an excellent question. An excellent question, indeed. Truth is, as soon as the Emperor sets sail,  I'm resigning my position.
Skyrim.esm|072768: The honor has been mine. You should be proud of what we've accomplished here. The Dark Brotherhood is no more. And the Emperor, finally, is safe.
Skyrim.esm|072769: And the outpost at Dragon Bridge?
Skyrim.esm|07276A: Very good. And you'll be returning to... Well, if you don't mind me asking, where exactly will you be going now, sir?
Skyrim.esm|07276C: Oh... I see. Well then, let me be just say that it's been an honor serving under you Commander Maro.
Skyrim.esm|07276D: It'll be shuttered by the end of the month.
Skyrim.esm|0727D3: What can I get for you? You made it in. Good. As soon as you distract the guards, I'll open this door and we can get you on your way. Let's hope we both live through this day.
Skyrim.esm|07283A: The undercroft is ahead... ugh, Necromancers. Be careful.
Skyrim.esm|07283B: The library is just through here. We're getting close now.
Skyrim.esm|07283C: Yes, you certainly do. You'll be going back to the College, then? I, uhh... I think perhaps I'll wait a bit before venturing there myself. Let this whole thing blow over, you know. Give certain parties a chance to forget what I may have done. Still, you've gotten me out of that cage, and I won't forget it. Thank you again, and good luck to you.
Skyrim.esm|07283D: Have the books, do you? I mean, I'm very glad. That's wonderful. You could, you know, let me out of this cage now.
Skyrim.esm|072979: Year seven, I think? Surprised I haven't cut my own throat out by now, but something keeps me going.
