Skyrim.esm|0729C0: First it was 6 months, then a year, then 2 years. Now I'm in for life.
Skyrim.esm|0729ED: Me? No, I'm too important to the war effort to risk life and limb in there... Besides, that's what I'm paying you for.
Skyrim.esm|0729EE: Oh! Um, he he... Hold on... I... I can explain...
Skyrim.esm|0729EF: Wait, it's haunted? I don't like ghosts... let me think about this for a minute...
Skyrim.esm|0729F3: Wait... who in blazes is this?
Skyrim.esm|0729F4: Valmir, you rat. You're not a soldier at all! Must be one of the Thalmor!
Skyrim.esm|0729F6: Why don't you go in there and get it?
Skyrim.esm|072A0F: It's just across the bridge and up that hill.
Skyrim.esm|072A10: We're close now. I can see the camp.
Skyrim.esm|072A11: My men? What did you do? You'll die for this.
Skyrim.esm|072AA7: Murder. Banditry. Assault. Theft. And lollygagging.
Skyrim.esm|072AF5: The first time? Thieving. The second time? Thieving. The third time? Thieving. It kind of keeps going like that.
Skyrim.esm|072AF8: No. It's some sort of crazy contraption Mercer commissioned for quick escapes. I'd wager a well-placed shot at the ramp's mechanism would lower it in a hurry.
Skyrim.esm|072AF9: Sure, but he'll ask for a whole lot. I mean, you are asking him to betray Mercer Frey. Your best bet would be to erase his debt with Maven Black-Briar. If you talk to her, she might be able to give you the details. Of course, you could just run him through and take what you need off of his corpse... I could care less.
Skyrim.esm|072AFA: That'd be Vald. A real piece of work, that one. Mercer's holding something over his head, keeping him loyal. Talk to Vex. She used to know him very well... if you catch my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|072AFB: Good question. I've only set foot inside a few times myself and that was in Mercer's company. If you can get past his trained watchdog, I think your best bet might be the ramp to the second floor balcony in his backyard.
Skyrim.esm|072AFC: I can't believe it! How did ya' talk her into this? Never mind, I don't care. I'm just glad I don't gotta' work for Maven anymore. Here, ya' did me a favor, I guess I owe you one.
Skyrim.esm|072AFD: You ain't got enough coin to make my problems go away unless ya' can talk Maven Black-Briar into letting me outta' my debt. You try and set one foot in this yard, and I'll cut it off.
Skyrim.esm|072AFF: This is Mercer Frey's place and he don't like visitors. Now go away.
Skyrim.esm|072B00: That pig? Oh, I have info on him. More than you care to know.
Skyrim.esm|072B02: No visitors. No way. Now piss off, before ya' make me mad.
Skyrim.esm|072B03: Vald? Good side? I think you have the wrong person. The only thing Vald understands is gold. A man after my own heart.
Skyrim.esm|072B18: Don't want to talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|072BD1: And what makes you think that?
Skyrim.esm|072BD2: All this talk of dragons returning to the world makes me more than a little nervous.
Skyrim.esm|072BD9: Soon it will be time to replenish our supply of water.
Skyrim.esm|072BDA: Ah, I see. I've just remembered that I saved a few septims in case of an emergency. This is starting to feel like one. Tell Erik that I've changed my mind, and we'll visit Whiterun soon to fit him for armor. And please don't hurt me!
Skyrim.esm|072BDB: Between the war and the return of the dragons, I wonder if we should not leave Skyrim and ply our trade elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|072BDD: Yes, I know just what you mean. My toes are numb from the cold more often than not.
Skyrim.esm|072BE5: I'm a Forsworn. One of our raids went bad, and I got captured. Would've been fine dying for the Reach with my kinsmen, but the Silver-Bloods want their ore mined, so they threw me in here.
Skyrim.esm|072BE6: A couple of years? I wasn't a Forsworn when I came, but I joined up when I got in. Easier that way.
Skyrim.esm|072BE7: Guards say I murdered someone. I was just sleeping off some mead in my aunt's house, but that didn't stop them from arresting me. I don't even know who they said I killed.
Skyrim.esm|072BFF: The soldiers? From which side, the Stormcloaks, or the Imperials?
Skyrim.esm|072C00: Not only that, but these Nords have no love for us. They look upon us and see only thieves and smugglers.
Skyrim.esm|072C69: We are fortunate. We travel lightly, and can escape at a moment's notice. We have no home that a dragon can burn to the ground.
Skyrim.esm|072C6A: Yes, of course you are right. Still, I would give much for even a few grains of moonsugar.
Skyrim.esm|072C71: As the saying goes, you do not need to be faster than the dragon; you need only be faster than your friends.
Skyrim.esm|072C81: My bones ache for the moonsugar. It has been too long since I tasted it.
Skyrim.esm|072CF8: The war? That is not a reason to leave, it is a reason to stay.
Skyrim.esm|072CFC: That is true. Perhaps I should be more grateful for this opportunity. Not many Khajiit can say that they have visited Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|072CFD: You would give your own coin to help my son? I'm moved by your compassion. Your kind deed will not go unrewarded, my friend. Tell Erik I've changed my mind, and we'll visit Whiterun soon to fit him for armor.
Skyrim.esm|072CFE: Hm. I had not thought of it that way.
Skyrim.esm|072D00: We have been in this land for so long, I have forgotten what it feels like to walk on warm sand.
Skyrim.esm|072D01: I wonder if we should offer discounts to the soldiers.
Skyrim.esm|072D08: I feel the same. I do not think Khajiit were made for the cold.
Skyrim.esm|072D09: S'rendarr be merciful! What ill fortune that this should happen now, while we are trying to make a living.
Skyrim.esm|072D0A: Control yourself! Do not frighten away our customers with your fits.
Skyrim.esm|072D0B: I suppose if we do, we will find out just how fast each of us can run.
Skyrim.esm|072D0D: If the river is not frozen to ice, perhaps.
Skyrim.esm|072D0E: That may be, but what of the war? That is not so easy to escape from.
Skyrim.esm|072D18: In truth, I do not. I have always dreamed of seeing new lands, and it does not hurt that we are making a good profit.
Skyrim.esm|072D19: My son? Erik? What about him? Did something happen? Is he alright?
Skyrim.esm|072D27: Hah, you have the foresight of a Khajiit in a sandstorm. If we offer a discount to both sides, the winners will persecute us for helping the losers.
Skyrim.esm|072D28: Hah! I wish that I had your optimism.
Skyrim.esm|072D9B: And we will lose customers. Let us hope that if there are dragons, there are only a few and they are not too foul-tempered.
Skyrim.esm|072D9C: It has been a profitable journey, and yet I long for the warm sands of Elsweyr more and more each day.
Skyrim.esm|072D9D: I wish I could say that I did not, but in every part of Skyrim, there is talk of dragon sightings. It is no coincidence.
Skyrim.esm|072D9E: My ears hear the truth in your words, and yet my body shakes with the need.
Skyrim.esm|072D9F: Because war is good for business, of course.
Skyrim.esm|072DA0: Moons protect us! I hope we do not encounter one of them on the road.
Skyrim.esm|072DA1: The road takes us past many cold rivers. We can fill our skins there.
Skyrim.esm|072DA2: I... I cannot deny the truth of what you say. Erik is all the family that I have left, but it's wrong to hold him back as I have. Go and tell Erik that I've changed my mind. I'll see about borrowing some money in Whiterun for his armor.
Skyrim.esm|072DA3: I admit, the dragons are a concern, but we can avoid them if we do not stay in one place for too long.
Skyrim.esm|072DF3: Do you ever find that you regret making this journey?
Skyrim.esm|072DF4: But the world is a dangerous place. Erik has no idea what he's getting himself into. What's more, he's got no armor. I'll have to save up the money for it.
Skyrim.esm|072E04: Both. One or the other is bound to win the war eventually. Why not curry favor now, so that the victor will look upon us kindly?
Skyrim.esm|072E0F: Guards brought me in about 12 years ago. Was running a good group of bandits up until then. But these Forsworn. They're nothing like the men I cobbled together. They're real killers.
Skyrim.esm|072E15: You should just serve your time. Find an ore vein and start digging.
Skyrim.esm|072E17: You're not in here for life, like I am.
Skyrim.esm|072E1B: I'm just another Forsworn sent to rot here in this mine.
Skyrim.esm|072E1C: You're talking to a killer, remember that.
Skyrim.esm|072E22: What do you want, new blood?
Skyrim.esm|072E31: It is a pity, but it cannot be helped. Perhaps if we continue to show that we can be trusted, they will open their cities to our caravans.
Skyrim.esm|072E33: I feel pity for these Nords. When the dragons attack cities and towns, they will lose everything.
Skyrim.esm|072E34: Put the thought out of your mind, and do not speak of it again.
Skyrim.esm|072E39: I welcome you, friend. How may I serve you?
Skyrim.esm|072E3A: The wind blows bitter cold in this place.
Skyrim.esm|072E42: The longer we travel the roads of Skyrim, the more empty this land seems.
Skyrim.esm|072E44: The warm sand of Elsweyr is far away from here.
Skyrim.esm|072E45: The roads of Skyrim lead to many dangers.
Skyrim.esm|072E46: We are creatures of the desert. The north wind chills us to the bones.
Skyrim.esm|072E4C: There is much beauty in this place.
Skyrim.esm|072E4D: How high the mountains of Skyrim rise!
Skyrim.esm|072E58: It is a strange sight, the white flakes that sometimes fall from the sky.
Skyrim.esm|072EB2: The air is so clear in Skyrim, one can see forever.
Skyrim.esm|072EB3: A welcome to you, fellow traveler.
Skyrim.esm|072EB4: Khajiit welcomes you, and also your coin.
Skyrim.esm|072EB5: Do you seek to make a purchase?
Skyrim.esm|072EBB: Khajiit has wares, if you have coin.
Skyrim.esm|072F03: Warm sands, friend. How may this one serve you?
Skyrim.esm|072F04: Please, come and browse our goods.
Skyrim.esm|072F05: May your bed be warm and your food sweet.
Skyrim.esm|072F06: You honor Khajiit with your presence.
Skyrim.esm|072F07: Many fine things are here for you, friend.
Skyrim.esm|072F08: Come and see what is for sale, traveler.
Skyrim.esm|072F0A: May the sun warm your path, friend.
Skyrim.esm|072F0B: Speak to one of the others if you wish to buy something.
Skyrim.esm|072F0D: Bring your coins, but do not bring trouble.
Skyrim.esm|072F0E: Khajiit is always watching for thieves and pickpockets.
Skyrim.esm|072F0F: Khajiit is ready for any troublemakers.
Skyrim.esm|072F10: If you have come to see our wares, speak to the others.
Skyrim.esm|072F14: Khajiit is just a guard, and has no wares to sell.
Skyrim.esm|072F15: Blessings of the moons upon you, traveler.
Skyrim.esm|072F16: Some come to buy, but others come to steal. Khajiit shows no mercy to thieves.
Skyrim.esm|072F17: Khajiit are not welcome in the cities, so we make our camps outside the walls.
Skyrim.esm|072F18: There is no trust for Khajiit in this place.
Skyrim.esm|072F19: Do you believe all this talk of dragons? Can the ancient ones truly have returned?
Skyrim.esm|072F7B: You know that our supply is limited. It could be a season or more before we get a new shipment from the south.
Skyrim.esm|073094: Many thanks. Here - a token of gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|073095: Thanks! Here - this is for you.
Skyrim.esm|073096: I'm grateful for your efforts. Here, take this.
Skyrim.esm|073097: Thanks a lot. Here - a little something for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|073098: Here, take this small token of appreciation for your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|073099: Oh. Good job. Here... My way of saying thanks.
Skyrim.esm|07309A: Good. Take this token of my gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|07309B: Very well. Here, take this for your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|07309C: Thank you. Please, take this for your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|07309D: Well done. Here, a token of appreciation.
Skyrim.esm|07309E: Thank you. Honor dictates something in return. I hope you find this pleasing.
Skyrim.esm|07309F: I wasn't sure it'd ever happen. Here. I want you to have this. My way of saying "thanks."
Skyrim.esm|0730A0: Finely done. Here, please accept this gift as a sign of my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0730A1: Good. Take this gift. We're even now.
Skyrim.esm|0730A2: Wonderful! Here, take this. I hope it's fair compensation.
Skyrim.esm|0730A4: I couldn't be more pleased. Take this small token of my appreciation.
Skyrim.esm|0730A5: I'm truly grateful. Please accept this as a token of my appreciation.
Skyrim.esm|0730A6: It has done a good thing. It will take this gift as a token of gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|0730A7: Ah, good, good. Now please, take this. Just my way of saying thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0730A8: Thanks a lot! Here. Take this. For all your hard work.
Skyrim.esm|0730A9: Good job. Please accept this in return.
Skyrim.esm|0730AA: Much obliged. Here. It's not much, but take it anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0730AB: I'm surprised you succeeded. Please accept this as payment.
Skyrim.esm|0730AC: Excellent! Thank you... Here, take this as, um, a sign of my gratitude. It's not much, but I hope it's enough.
Skyrim.esm|0730AD: Your deed was true, and so is my gratitude. Please take this in return for your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|0730AE: Really? Good show. Here, take this. You know, as a thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0730AF: Fine. Please accept this token of gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|0730B0: I'm so glad you succeeded. Here. Please take this for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0730B1: Excellent. And now, please accept this token of appreciation.
Skyrim.esm|0730B2: Many thanks. And please, take this gift. I hope it's a fitting token to honor the deed.
Skyrim.esm|0730B3: Hmph. Hadn't expected you had it in you. Here. Take this for a job well done.
Skyrim.esm|0730B4: Well done, indeed. Please accept this as a sign of my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0730B5: Very good. But you've created a debt between us. Here. Take this. We are even now.
Skyrim.esm|0730B6: My joy knows no bounds. Here. A little payment for your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|0730B7: Good job. Take this in return.
Skyrim.esm|0730B8: I can't thank you enough. Here, take this. It's not much, but please accept it as a token of my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0730B9: Now, let us put this behind us and speak of it no more.
Skyrim.esm|0730BA: So we're not going to talk about this any more, right? Because I don't want to.
Skyrim.esm|0730BB: This is last time we speak of this.
Skyrim.esm|0730BC: Let's both get on with our lives now, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0730BD: Now, let's pretend this never happened, shall we?
Skyrim.esm|0730BE: Umm... So, the business is done. This is the last we'll talk about it, right?
Skyrim.esm|0730BF: The deed is done. We need speak of it no longer.
Skyrim.esm|0730C0: Now let us turn our conversation away from such unpleasantness.
Skyrim.esm|0730C1: Now let's, um, not talk about this anymore. Okay?
Skyrim.esm|0730C2: Now, let us bury the memory of these dark deeds.
Skyrim.esm|0730C3: Now, so we're clear - this is the last time we talk about this.
Skyrim.esm|0730C4: Now, as far as I'm concerned, none of this ever happened.
Skyrim.esm|0730C5: Now we can both pretend this whole thing never happened.
Skyrim.esm|0730C6: I trust you'll keep your mouth shut about this.
Skyrim.esm|0730C7: Now the whole thing's over and done with. So let's stop talking about it.
Skyrim.esm|0730C9: Now... I trust we'll never have to speak of this again. To anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0730CA: Now I just want to put the whole nasty business behind me.
Skyrim.esm|0730CB: Now we can put this all behind us, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0730CC: Now we never talk about it again. Got it?
Skyrim.esm|0730CD: Now let's not talk about it anymore, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0730CE: Now... This conversation never happened. Understood?
Skyrim.esm|0730CF: Anyway, it's done. Time to move on.
Skyrim.esm|0730D0: But enough of this. Such things are best forgotten.
Skyrim.esm|0730D1: Now let's just, you know, try to forget any of this ever happened. All right?
Skyrim.esm|0730D2: What happened is between you, me, and Azura. Let's keep it that way.
Skyrim.esm|0730D3: It's done. No more... no more talking about it.
Skyrim.esm|0730D4: Now, let us turn our thoughts to more pleasant matters.
Skyrim.esm|0730D5: Now, let's just pretend this never happened.
Skyrim.esm|0730D6: I hope you'll agree that these dark deeds are best left forgotten...
Skyrim.esm|0730D7: Now, let's pledge never to speak of this again.
Skyrim.esm|0730D8: But let's not talk about it anymore. What's done is done.
Skyrim.esm|0730D9: Now... I don't really want to talk about it anymore. So let's not.
Skyrim.esm|0730DA: Now our business together is done.
Skyrim.esm|0730DB: I trust we'll never need to speak of this again... to anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0730DC: Let's go our separate ways now, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0730DD: I'm just glad to put all this behind me. So let's never speak of it again, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0731D1: Mirabelle will be waiting for you.
Skyrim.esm|0734A0: Then the judgment of this Circle is complete.
Skyrim.esm|0734A1: His heart beats with fury and courage that have united the Companions since the days of the distant green summers.
Skyrim.esm|0734A2: Let it beat with ours, that the mountains may echo and our enemies may tremble at the call.
Skyrim.esm|0734A4: And would you raise your sword in his honor?
Skyrim.esm|0734A5: And would you raise your sword in her honor?
Skyrim.esm|0734A6: I would stand at his back, that the world might never overtake us.
Skyrim.esm|0734A7: I would stand at her back, that the world might never overtake us.
Skyrim.esm|0734AD: Would you raise your shield in his defense?
Skyrim.esm|0734AE: Would you raise your shield in her defense?
Skyrim.esm|0734AF: It stands ready to meet the blood of his foes.
Skyrim.esm|0734B0: It stands ready to meet the blood of her foes.
Skyrim.esm|0734B2: I stand witness to the courage of the soul before us.
Skyrim.esm|0734B3: This man has endured, has challenged, and has shown his valor.
Skyrim.esm|0734B4: This woman has endured, has challenged, and has shown her valor.
Skyrim.esm|0734B5: Well, boy, you're one of us now. I trust you won't disappoint.
Skyrim.esm|0734B6: Well, girl, you're one of us now. I trust you won't disappoint.
Skyrim.esm|0734B7: And would you raise a mug in his name?
Skyrim.esm|0734B8: And would you raise a mug in her name?
Skyrim.esm|0734BA: Brothers and sisters of the Circle, today we welcome a new soul into our mortal fold.
Skyrim.esm|0734BB: I would lead the song of triumph as our mead hall reveled in his stories.
Skyrim.esm|0734BC: I would lead the song of triumph as our mead hall reveled in her stories.
Skyrim.esm|073EB7: For the glory of the Empire!
Skyrim.esm|073EBA: I'll water the ground with your blood.
Skyrim.esm|073EC0: They're in! Don't let them gain further ground!
Skyrim.esm|073EC1: Keep it together! There's not many of us left!
Skyrim.esm|073EC3: Yeaaaah! Come on, we can take them!
Skyrim.esm|073EC5: That's it! We're pushing them back!
Skyrim.esm|073EC6: Fall back, fall back! Regroup and fall back!
Skyrim.esm|073EC7: We've got them pinned in! It's only a matter of time now. Keep fighting!
Skyrim.esm|073EC8: This is it! Hold at all costs! Don't give them any more ground!
Skyrim.esm|073ECA: Someone put an arrow in that damn wizard!
Skyrim.esm|073ECE: Along the fence, do you see them?
Skyrim.esm|073ECF: They got archers over there by the fence!
Skyrim.esm|073ED0: Wizard! Hiding over there by that fence!
Skyrim.esm|073ED1: They're over there, next to that house!
Skyrim.esm|073ED2: By that rock over there. See 'em?
Skyrim.esm|073ED3: They're hiding over by those stables!
Skyrim.esm|073ED6: Mage up there! In the tower!
Skyrim.esm|073ED9: They got archers up along the wall!
Skyrim.esm|073EDA: Look! Up on the wall. A wizard!
Skyrim.esm|073EDB: Look sharp. There they are. In the window!
Skyrim.esm|073EDD: They've got a mage hiding in that window up there!
Skyrim.esm|073F98: I may not be fond of Letrush, but even I've got to admit he's got the upper hand.
Skyrim.esm|073FB9: I've been hunting and fishing in these parts for years.
Skyrim.esm|073FBA: It's not like my poaching is hurting anyone. The Jarl can hardly eat every deer now can he?
Skyrim.esm|073FBB: There's enough for all of us if you aren't greedy.
Skyrim.esm|074316: All right, just because it's you, we'll try again.
Skyrim.esm|074318: I can't believe you're speaking to me, after what you've done. You broke my heart.
Skyrim.esm|074684: I took that prancing fool into my home, and this is how he repays me?
Skyrim.esm|074747: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|074748: You can't kill what's already dead, mortal.
Skyrim.esm|07474D: There is no pain in the Void!
Skyrim.esm|07474E: You would shut out the darkness?
Skyrim.esm|07474F: You wish to kill me? Someone has already had that honor.
Skyrim.esm|074756: Fall before the will of Sithis!
Skyrim.esm|074768: You might just make it. But for now, you're still a whelp to us, new blood. So you do what we tell you. Here's my sword. Go take it up to Eorlund to have it sharpened. And be careful, it's probably worth more than you are.
Skyrim.esm|074769: Now that's impressive. You're mightier than you look.
Skyrim.esm|07476A: What do you think this is? We don't do battle with magic around here, new blood. Now come at me.
Skyrim.esm|074773: Alduin's wings, they did darken the sky. His roar fury's fire, and his scales sharpened scythes.
Skyrim.esm|07478B: Do you agree to be bound together, in love, now and forever?
Skyrim.esm|07478C: It is from her love of us that we first learned to love one another. It is from this love that we learn that a life lived alone is no life at all.
Skyrim.esm|07478D: We gather here today, under Mara's loving gaze, to bear witness to the union of two souls in eternal companionship.
Skyrim.esm|07478F: It was Mara that first gave birth to all of creation and pledged to watch over us as her children.
Skyrim.esm|074790: May they journey forth together in this life and the next, in prosperity and poverty, and in joy and hardship.
Skyrim.esm|0748DA: Men ran and they cowered, and they fought and they died. They burned and they bled as they issued their cries.
Skyrim.esm|074922: We need saviors to free us from Alduin's rage. Heroes on the field of this new war to wage.
Skyrim.esm|074984: And if Alduin wins, man is gone from this world. Lost in the shadow of the black wings unfurled.
Skyrim.esm|074988: But then came the Tongues on that terrible day. Steadfast as winter, they entered the fray.
Skyrim.esm|074989: And all heard the music of Alduin's doom. The sweet song of Skyrim, sky-shattering Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|074997: And so the Tongues freed us from Alduin's rage. Gave the gift of the Voice, ushered in a new Age.
Skyrim.esm|0749B1: If Alduin is eternal, then eternity's done. For his story is over and the dragons are... gone.
Skyrim.esm|0749B6: I've taken the liberty of visiting the cities, and spreading some nasty rumors about Gaius Maro. To make his treachery that much more believable.
Skyrim.esm|0749B7: Believe it or not, Festus likes you. He senses your power, your potential. Follow his instructions to the letter, and you may even earn his respect.
Skyrim.esm|0749B8: Boy, did you miss all the excitement.
Skyrim.esm|0749B9: There's too much of an obsession these days with knives and arrows. You can kill someone just as easily with a well-placed spell.
Skyrim.esm|0749BA: I believed in Cicero. But he's betrayed us all.
Skyrim.esm|0749BC: It only hurts when I laugh. He he he. Owww...
Skyrim.esm|074A09: Our hero, our hero, claims a warrior's heart.
Skyrim.esm|074A0A: With a Voice wielding power of the ancient Nord art.
Skyrim.esm|074A0D: It's an end to the evil, of all Skyrim's foes.
Skyrim.esm|074A10: For the darkness has passed, and the legend yet grows.
Skyrim.esm|074A16: I tell you, I tell you, the Dragonborn comes.
Skyrim.esm|074A3F: Really? Now that is interesting... Then you should know that Louis and I have a certain... history. Tell you what. Go along with his plans for now. I'll keep an eye on you both and, when the time is right, I'll settle accounts with Louis.
Skyrim.esm|074A4C: I'm not exactly... comfortable around guards or jails. Definitely not when associates of mine have been locked up. No, I'll meet you outside of town.
Skyrim.esm|074A50: You'll know, You'll know the Dragonborn's come.
Skyrim.esm|074DB1: Why are you here? Killing Gaius Maro is but the first step of your contract - you must also plant the incriminating letter on his body...
Skyrim.esm|074DE1: Well, yes. Why wouldn't I be? Are you... interested in me?
Skyrim.esm|074DE2: Wonderful. Where to begin? Mara is the Goddess of Love. The temple spreads her gifts by tending to the sick, the poor, and the lost. We also perform wedding ceremonies for all the loving couples in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|074DE3: It's settled then. Brief as life can be in Skyrim, at least we'll have each other.
Skyrim.esm|074DE6: Yes. If you were ready, of course. Are you familiar with how marriage works in Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|074DE7: Typically, love in Skyrim is as earnest as the people who live here. Life is hard, and short, so there is little room for long courtship. A person who is looking for a spouse simply wears an Amulet of Mara about the neck, showing they are available. When another person shows interest, and the two agree to be together, one of them comes to the Temple and we arrange a ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|074E26: Oh. I'm sorry I said anything, then.
Skyrim.esm|074E2B: Yes, but it's no easy matter. But you don't need to share the worries of an old warrior. This day is to rejoice in your bravery!
Skyrim.esm|074E2E: Well, I grow old. My mind turns towards the horizon. To Sovngarde. I worry that Shor won't call an animal to glory as he would a true Nord warrior. Living as beasts draws our souls closer to the Daedric Lord Hircine. Some may prefer an eternity in his Hunting Grounds, but I crave the fellowship of Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|07508B: Yes... yes, that's an excellent idea. Who knows what other lost treasures we might discover?
Skyrim.esm|07508C: Well, I'm going to look around some more. See what the old Blades left for us.
Skyrim.esm|075090: Have you ever heard of a Shout that can knock a dragon out of the sky?
Skyrim.esm|075091: Let's go see if Esbern's right about Alduin's Wall.
Skyrim.esm|075093: Only if you don't know how to use it. All the great heroes have had to learn to use their power. Those that shrank from their destiny... well, you've never heard of them, have you? And there are the villains - those that misused their power. There's always a choice, and there's always a risk. But if you live in fear of what might go wrong, you'll end up doing nothing. Like the Greybeards up on their mountain.
Skyrim.esm|075094: Good. The Greybeards can teach you a lot, but don't let them turn you away from your destiny. You're Dragonborn, and you're the only one who can stop Alduin. Don't forget it.
Skyrim.esm|075095: I know the prophecy by heart. Once all Blades knew it.
Skyrim.esm|075097: If they had their way, you'd do nothing but sit up on their mountain with them and talk to the sky, or whatever it is they do. The Greybeards are so afraid of power that they won't use it. Think about it. Have they tried to stop the civil war, or done anything about Alduin? No. And they're afraid of you, of your power. Trust me, there's no need to be afraid. Think of Tiber Septim. Do you think he'd have founded the Empire if he'd listened to the Greybeards?
Skyrim.esm|075098: Here are the Akaviri - the Blades - you see their distinctive longswords. Now they kneel, their ancient mission fulfilled, as the Last Dragonborn contends with Alduin at the end of time.
Skyrim.esm|07509A: You'll have to speak up. I'm a little deaf in my right ear.
Skyrim.esm|07509D: Right. Good thing they've already let you into their little cult. Not likely they'd help Esbern or me if we came calling. We'll look around Sky Haven Temple and see what else the old Blades might have left for us. It's a better hideout than I could have hoped for. Talos guard you.
Skyrim.esm|07509E: Just think. We're the first to set foot here in thousands of years.
Skyrim.esm|07509F: I can't wait to see what's inside!
Skyrim.esm|0750A0: Patience. Give me a moment and I'll explain everything.
Skyrim.esm|0750AC: Let's see what Esbern has to say.
Skyrim.esm|0750AD: We must be getting close. We should proceed cautiously.
Skyrim.esm|0750AE: Are you sure this is the right way?
Skyrim.esm|0750AF: I've told you where Sky Haven Temple is. I'll trust you to get us there in one piece.
Skyrim.esm|0750B0: I must speak to Delphine at once. There's no time to lose.
Skyrim.esm|0750B1: So this is where Delphine has been hiding all these years? Hmm.
Skyrim.esm|075354: I'm afraid the Winterhold you see before you is somewhat... underwhelming. I can assure you, though, that it was quite something in its prime. An early capital of Skyrim, you know. Sadly, the Oblivion Crisis took its toll on Winterhold, in more ways than one. Then, the Great Collapse swallowed most of our beloved city.
Skyrim.esm|075355: What's there to tell? It's mostly gone now, thanks to those damned mages in the College. Someday there'll be proof they caused the Great Collapse. Most of a city just dropping off into the sea... That doesn't just happen.
Skyrim.esm|075362: Mortals call it Dawnbreaker, for it was forged in a holy light that breaks upon my foes, burning away corruption and false life. You will enter my shrine, destroy Malkoran, and retrieve this mighty blade.
Skyrim.esm|075375: Do I look like a guide? Only thing I do is sell meat. Now, are you going to buy or not?
Skyrim.esm|07537C: Oh, that's right. I keep forgetting to pick that up. Poor Kerah, such a patient woman. Now, you're supposed to get something for your trouble, aren't you? How about some gold? People seem to like that, I notice.
Skyrim.esm|07537E: Oh, by the Divines is it that obvious? I swear, if my Gunnar was alive to see me now. This store would finally be on its feet if I hadn't lost my last shipment to Forsworn attacks. A specially-made Dibella statuette. You aren't for hire, are you? A sellsword? I'll pay you if you can recover that statuette from the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|075383: Ah, there it is! This little gold delight is going to keep us afloat for a while. Thank you. Here's something for your hard work.
Skyrim.esm|07538E: My family came here centuries ago. We've made a good living smithing the silver that flows through the city. But you probably wanted to know about the fancy carvings and stonework, didn't you? Travelers always do. Here, I have a delivery I need to make to Calcelmo. Why don't you take it to him? He knows the history of Markarth better than anyone.
Skyrim.esm|07538F: To protect my research, for one. There are more cutthroat scholars out there who would steal my findings if they got the chance. And the excavations into Nchuand-Zel are dangerous. Dwemer machines and traps still function even after thousands of years.
Skyrim.esm|0753BF: You don't have to tell me that.
Skyrim.esm|0753C0: I used to think so, too.
Skyrim.esm|0753C1: Oh... hello there. Didn't notice you come up. I was focusing my magicka. Or at least, I thought I was. I don't really know what I'm doing.
Skyrim.esm|0753C2: See, my grandfather was a wizard. My father wouldn't talk about him much. But I found some of his things in the attic. Including this staff. After that I thought I was turning into a wizard too, because dead things near me would come to life again for while. I even went to the College of Winterhold. But they just laughed at me. The magic is in the staff, not in me they said.
Skyrim.esm|0753C4: I grant you a new life!
Skyrim.esm|0753C6: This should work. I've done it before.
Skyrim.esm|0753C7: Gah! This staff is useless... I'm useless. The whole world is useless!
Skyrim.esm|0753C9: In fact. Keep it. I must have used up the magic in the staff or something. It's just a dumb stick of wood now.
Skyrim.esm|075C05: Better to stick together down here.
Skyrim.esm|075C08: Better to stick together down here.
Skyrim.esm|075C6F: Those kids are dead anyway. Killing me won't bring them back. I, on the other hand, have done quite a bit with my time on Mundus. Release me, and I'll share a bit of my wealth with you.
Skyrim.esm|075C70: Where I belong is back aboard my ship, on the Abecean. You've no hope of defeating me, pup. Don't bother. Why not broker a bargain, instead?
Skyrim.esm|075C71: Ah, I was beginning to think the day would never come. So you broke the binding spell, did you? Matters little. Their souls are trapped in Oblivion all the same, and here I stay.
Skyrim.esm|075C72: There's a smart lad. Take the map. The loot's hidden there. It'll show itself when you get near.
Skyrim.esm|075C73: There's a smart lass. Check the map. The loot's hidden there. It'll show itself when you get near.
Skyrim.esm|075C74: Where am I? You! Out with it, then.
Skyrim.esm|075C75: Years back, I had to ferret away some booty nearby. We'd sunk an Imperial Galleon off the coast. Good plunder, but we needed to travel light. We stashed what we couldn't carry, and I hid it with a trick I know. I can point you in the right direction, and we'll each go our separate ways.
Skyrim.esm|075C76: Have it your own way, meat.
Skyrim.esm|075C77: Names have power, lad. You should know by now. Just say "Velehk Sain, I release you.", and "Poof!", I'm on my way.
Skyrim.esm|075C78: Names have power, lass. You should know by now. Just say "Velehk Sain, I release you.", and "Poof!", I'm on my way.
Skyrim.esm|075C79: Clever try, scab, but it won't be that easy!
Skyrim.esm|075CA2: Under the authority of Mara, the Divine of Love, I declare this couple to be wed. I present the two of you with these matching rings, blessed by Mara's divine grace. May they protect each of you in your new life together.
Skyrim.esm|075CA3: You've embarrassed the temple and spat in the face of love. What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|075CA4: Ah, here's the blushing bride now. Let's begin the ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|075CA5: Ah, here's the proud groom now. Let's begin the ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|075CA6: Do you agree to be bound together, in love, now and forever?
Skyrim.esm|075CAB: It's our wedding day. I feel like all of Skyrim is focused on just the two of us.
Skyrim.esm|075CAF: "Forgiveness is the heart of Mara's blessings." Very well. We shall re-arrange your wedding. Tomorrow. Same time as before.
Skyrim.esm|075CB1: You're just going to walk away from your betrothal? Get out of the temple.
Skyrim.esm|075CD0: We must hurry if we're going to defeat Ancano.
Skyrim.esm|075CDE: What's the bloody hold up in there? How long can it take to run through a few scraggly assassins?
Skyrim.esm|075CDF: If they were easy to kill, don't you think we would have done it years ago? If our men aren't out in a few minutes, they're dead, trust me.
Skyrim.esm|075CE1: Anyway, I can smell the smoke. They've already set the place alight.
Skyrim.esm|075CE4: And so ends the Dark Brotherhood, once and for all. About bloody time.
Skyrim.esm|075D2D: Well I think your betrothed might feel otherwise. The two of you will need to work this out before the temple will marry you.
Skyrim.esm|0762A8: Few outsiders earn the right to enter our strongholds. Speak with more respect, or we might forget your gifts. Feel free to trade with our smiths for Orc weapons and armor. Train with my warriors, or purchase poisons from the wise woman.
Skyrim.esm|0762A9: What are you doing here, outlander? We don't deal with your kind.
Skyrim.esm|07632B: Malacath blesses us with this land. It's not for your kind.
Skyrim.esm|07632C: We don't know you, and we don't want you here.
Skyrim.esm|07632D: I don't believe it. You found the Forgemaster's Fingers. You've impressed me, outlander. No one else has returned from this task before. By the Code of Malacath, I name you Blood-Kin to the Orcs. Let it be known among the strongholds that you are family, now.
Skyrim.esm|07632E: Feel free to trade with our smiths for Orc weapons and armor. Train with my warriors, or purchase poisons from the wise woman. Welcome to our stronghold, Blood-Kin.
Skyrim.esm|076331: You've no right to be here. This place is for Orcs. And Orcs alone.
Skyrim.esm|076332: Outlanders should learn to not wander where they don't belong.
Skyrim.esm|0763FB: It's the rebels! Defend the entrance!
Skyrim.esm|07641B: The Legion is here! Defend the entrance!
Skyrim.esm|07664E: The original Companions. Their finest warriors rest with Ysgramor. You'll have to prove yourselves to them. It's not that you're intruding. I'd wager they've actually expected us. They just want to be sure that you're worthy. Be ready for an honorable battle.
Skyrim.esm|0766D0: You have no business in our stronghold, outlander.
Skyrim.esm|0769C9: Babette, my girl - pack your things. We're moving.
Skyrim.esm|0769CA: Amaund Motierre? But that would mean...
Skyrim.esm|0769CB: I was hoping you'd say that.
Skyrim.esm|0769CC: By Sithis, what a mess. I guess this is the end.
Skyrim.esm|0769CD: You just worry about fulfilling the contract. I'll take care of things on this end.
Skyrim.esm|0769CE: I'll admit, I'm having trouble coming to terms with all of this. I'll just follow Nazir's lead.
Skyrim.esm|0769CF: Please, make haste. The Emperor's ship won't stayed moored forever. We won't get a chance like this again.
Skyrim.esm|0769D0: Well I'll be damned. We heard you were dead. Motierre's in the back room, if you've got business.
Skyrim.esm|0769D1: Don't you have all the information you need? Please, the Emperor isn't going to kill himself. Hmph. Now wouldn't that be a blessing...
Skyrim.esm|0769D2: What? Well - what did she say?
Skyrim.esm|0769D3: Hmph. All right, then. Go. Go, my Listener. Find out what that slimy bastard Motierre has to say, then send the Emperor to Sithis. Ah, but when you're done, there's no use returning here, is there? I was thinking... the Dawnstar Sanctuary. We could make a proper home there. Listen, when you're finished with this Emperor business, meet Babette and me there. I'll find some way to move the Night Mother. Don't worry. Now go! And come back with a barrel full of gold, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|076B35: Derkeethus, from Darkwater Crossing. Should have known better than to swim all the way to the falls. Please, get me out of here!
Skyrim.esm|076F19: Ain't many visitors come to the dungeon. Every now and then Sybille Stentor will come by looking for... volunteers.
Skyrim.esm|076F1A: There isn't someone less busy who can help you?
Skyrim.esm|076F47: It's bad luck to talk about that place. Jarl's been looking for someone who ain't superstitious though. Be lookin' for a fool if you ask me.
Skyrim.esm|076F48: Have you seen Laelette? Have you seen my wife?
Skyrim.esm|076F4A: Laelette! She's dead! Ysmir's beard! She's...she's a vampire!
Skyrim.esm|0771A6: So you were right. Go tell the Jarl. Hurry!
Skyrim.esm|0771A7: Hroggar blames his wife for spilling bear fat in the fire. Many folk think he set the fire himself.
Skyrim.esm|0771A8: On rumor and gossip? No. But you, a stranger, might find the truth for us. Sift through the ashes that others are too fearful to touch. See what they tell you. Should you prove him guilty or innocent, I will reward you.
Skyrim.esm|0771A9: Hroggar's house? He lost his wife and daughter in the blaze.
Skyrim.esm|0771AA: You saw her ghost? I'm glad it was you and not me. She's buried outside of town. That's where I'd look.
Skyrim.esm|0771AC: Lust can make a man do the unthinkable. The ashes were still warm when he pledged himself to Alva.
Skyrim.esm|077262: Now, I am afraid I must leave you. We will continue to watch over you, and guide you as best we can. It is within you to succeed. Never forget that.
Skyrim.esm|077263: You set this chain of events in motion at Saarthal.
Skyrim.esm|077264: I'll be the one asking the questions. All you need to know is that the Psijic Order is a rogue organization, believing themselves to be above the law. They have clashed with the Aldmeri Dominion before, and I have no intention of allowing that to happen here.
Skyrim.esm|077265: We believe the your efforts should be directed towards dealing with the aftermath, but we cannot predict what that will be.
Skyrim.esm|077266: Unfortunately, the future is as obscured to us as it is to you. The overwhelming power of the Eye makes it difficult for us to see.
Skyrim.esm|077267: Please do not be alarmed. I mean you no harm.
Skyrim.esm|077268: You must understand, the Psijic Order does not typically... intervene directly in events. My presence here will be seen as an affront to some within the Order, and as soon as we have finished, I will be leaving your College. I'm all too aware that my arrival has aroused suspicion, especially in Ancano, your Thalmor associate. Nevertheless, my Order will not act directly. You must take it upon yourself to do so.
Skyrim.esm|077269: I... I am unsure. He is somewhere within the College. Surely one of your colleagues must know his location. I am sorry I cannot provide you with further help, but this conversation requires a great deal of effort on my part.
Skyrim.esm|07726A: As you may have learned, this object... The Eye... is immensely powerful. This world is not ready for it. If it remains here, it will be misused. Indeed, many in the Order believe it has already... Rather, something will happen soon, something that cannot be avoided.
Skyrim.esm|07726B: Technically, that is true. But I still report to the Aldmeri Dominion, and I cannot ignore this situation. Don't worry, you can return to your petty squabbles and meaningless 'research' as soon as this matter is resolved.
Skyrim.esm|07726C: I merely wish to talk to you.
Skyrim.esm|07726D: I've given us a chance to speak privately, but I'm afraid I can't do this for long. We must be brief. The situation here at your College is of dire importance, and attempts to contact you as we have previously have failed. I believe it is due to the very source of our concern. This object... The Eye of Magnus as your people have taken to calling it. The energy coming from it has prevented us from reaching you with the visions you have already seen. The longer it remains here, the more dangerous the situation becomes. And so I have come here personally to tell you it must be dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|07726E: I fear I have already overstepped the bounds of my Order, but I will offer this: seek out the Augur of Dunlain here in your College. His perception may be more coherent than ours.
Skyrim.esm|07726F: He was once a student here at the College. Now he is... something different.
Skyrim.esm|077270: I'm afraid it's not that simple.
Skyrim.esm|077271: I presume you refer to Ancano's distaste for the Psijic Order? The Thalmor see our Order as a threat because we have power, and we will not allow them to control us. I assure you that we mean you no harm.
Skyrim.esm|077357: The same could be said for any of us, could it not? Are you so guiltless as to stand in judgment upon Paarthurnax? Listen to your inner Voice before acting. Do not allow the Blades to bully you into doing something you will later regret. After all, repentance is something you deny to Paarthurnax by meting out your so-called justice.
Skyrim.esm|077358: Now you see why I've warned you against them! Bloodthirsty barbarians!
Skyrim.esm|077359: So be it. If he believes it is necessary for you to learn this... we will bow to his wisdom.
Skyrim.esm|07735A: Kynareth has placed the Voice of wisdom within you, Dragonborn. All you need do is learn to listen to it, and your path will be clear.
Skyrim.esm|07735B: So... you spoke to Paarthurnax. The dragonblood burns bright within you. Did he tell you what you wanted to know? Did he teach you the Dragonrend Shout?
Skyrim.esm|07735C: Such blasphemies are the calling of mages, not followers of the Way. Take your question to the College of Winterhold. They may be able to help you.
Skyrim.esm|07735D: We have never concerned ourselves with the Scrolls. The gods themselves would rightly fear to tamper with such things. As for where to find it... such blasphemies have always been the stock in trade of the mages of Winterhold. They may be able to tell you something about the Elder Scroll you seek.
Skyrim.esm|07735E: This is how you repay our trust? Paarthurnax's trust? His noble spirit... the wisdom of ages... his deep understanding of our existence... all gone, and for what? A mindless vendetta urged on you by a cabal of Akaviri barbarians! Begone, before even my philosophy is tested beyond the breaking point. We are men of peace, and you are not.
Skyrim.esm|07735F: You're learning, Dragonborn. Doing nothing can be the wisest choice, although strangely often the most difficult. I trust that you will make the right choice, in the end. Listen to the Voice that Kynareth has placed within you, and your path will be clear.
Skyrim.esm|077362: My oath binds me, Dragonborn. Until Paarthurnax is dead, you aren't welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|077363: Do the right thing. Paarthurnax deserves to die.
Skyrim.esm|077364: We have nothing to talk about until Paarthurnax is dead.
Skyrim.esm|077365: You shouldn't be here, unless you've decided to bring Paarthurnax to justice.
Skyrim.esm|077366: What further business do you have with us?
Skyrim.esm|077367: You have made your choice, Dragonborn. You are not welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|07736F: By the gods... you! But it can't be. You're dead. You... Yeaaaahhhh!
Skyrim.esm|077370: Oh, for the love of Mara, would you please just go?
Skyrim.esm|077376: You know, I like it here. We should have moved to Dawnstar ages ago.
Skyrim.esm|077377: I can't wait to see what the future brings.
Skyrim.esm|077378: I'll follow you till I'm dust in the breeze. You do realize that?
Skyrim.esm|077379: This place is already starting to feel like home. Must be all the bloodstains.
Skyrim.esm|077421: What? How did you get this far?
Skyrim.esm|077423: What? No! Get back in your cage!
Skyrim.esm|077426: Is there anything more revolting than spiders? Too many legs...
Skyrim.esm|077498: Hroggar's fate is in your hands.
Skyrim.esm|07749A: Do they? How delightful! I'm lucky to have a man that is so devoted to me.
Skyrim.esm|07749B: Mother's calling me. It's time for me to sleep now. I'm so tired. Thank you for making her feel better.
Skyrim.esm|077590: Give me just a moment, and I'll have this open.
Skyrim.esm|077C22: What? That flower-chewer's died? Then let me out. My tower!
Skyrim.esm|077C23: Ah, I have a pretty staff. Help me find Petra, wring her neck, pluck her eyes. Take my prize staff, I just want my tower back!
Skyrim.esm|077C25: Oh. Yes. You all look the same.
Skyrim.esm|077C26: Because, because you are such a nice Breton, yes!
Skyrim.esm|077C28: Petra! Evil Petra put me here, stole my tower. Hate her, chew her bones! Let me out, kind, kind meat.
Skyrim.esm|077C29: Because you are such a very nice Orc?
Skyrim.esm|077C2A: Live in my tower, roost in my home. Mix pretty poisons and collect shiny eyeballs, yes.
Skyrim.esm|077C2B: Yes, kind morsel. Let us go up, and mind Melka on the way.
Skyrim.esm|077C2D: Don't leave poor Melka to die.
Skyrim.esm|077C2F: We go up, yes? Always up.
Skyrim.esm|077C30: Do you like my pretty tower?
Skyrim.esm|077C31: Press only middle button! Clever trick, yes? Nobody ever thinks of the middle.
Skyrim.esm|077DD5: He didn't want to hit the bar like the rest! Ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|077DDC: Oh my... that's the worst one yet. How about we stop talking and keep our eyes open like Gulum-Ei said?
Skyrim.esm|077DE7: What, seriously? Come on, I thought that was funny!
Skyrim.esm|077DEC: You'd better find that chest that fell off the boat or Gulum-Ei will have your hide!
Skyrim.esm|077DED: I know! I know! Just shut up and let me look, okay?
Skyrim.esm|077EFA: Hagraven up ahead. Pull the lever when you're ready and we'll take her.
Skyrim.esm|077EFB: Locked. Damn! One of the hags nearby will have the key. You'll have to take it from here. I'll keep watch here - unless you can pick the lock.
Skyrim.esm|077F5F: I'll have to take your word on that one, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|077F61: And Meieran thinks that we should go there.
Skyrim.esm|077F65: I don't want to hear another word about what you're learning from the elf! It's high time you learned to be a Nord, too!
Skyrim.esm|077F6B: I've been in Skyrim my whole life. Why would I want to leave?
Skyrim.esm|077F6C: That it was black and gross and nobody wanted to live there anymore.
Skyrim.esm|077F72: I think I like the lake here just a little more.
Skyrim.esm|077F74: He's a half-decent farmer. Ask him to teach you to farm!
Skyrim.esm|077F7C: Do you ever want to go to Morrowind, Sondas?
Skyrim.esm|07864B: Yes, you're right. I should have, but I didn't know what to say. When the orcs raided the temple, I fled. I left my brothers and sisters behind to die.
Skyrim.esm|07864C: Behold the Skull of Corruption, the source of Dawnstar's woes. We must reach the inner sanctum and destroy it. Come, there's no time to lose.
Skyrim.esm|07864D: Now I can show you the source of the nightmares. Over here.
Skyrim.esm|07864E: Be careful... we're certain to find more of the awakened within.
Skyrim.esm|078650: I've spent the last few decades living in regret and seeking redemption from Mara. And by Her Benevolence, I will right my wrongs.
Skyrim.esm|078651: And what would you have me say? Sorry for following the misguided teachings of a mad Divine? Sorry for stealing memories from children? Do you realize when the orcs attacked, I was only concerned with myself? I fled... and left my brothers and sisters behind to die.
Skyrim.esm|078661: I wonder what Irileth is so excited about?
Skyrim.esm|0786E8: I'll send word to the strongholds to let you in as one of our own.
Skyrim.esm|07870A: Gladly. By the Code of Malacath, I name you Blood-Brother to the Orcs.
Skyrim.esm|07870B: Gladly. By the Code of Malacath, I name you Blood-Sister to the Orcs.
Skyrim.esm|078724: Give him a chance to speak. He's trying to help us.
Skyrim.esm|07873B: You're gouging me for what little coin I make and you can't even protect yourselves? Ridiculous!
Skyrim.esm|07873F: They owe our organization some serious coin and they've decided not to pay. I want you to explain to them the error of their ways.
Skyrim.esm|078740: No, and I never will. Now get out of my inn!
Skyrim.esm|078741: Then go back to your boss and tell him what I've already told him so many times, I lose count. His request is denied... I'm not paying him any more gold. This madness has to end.
Skyrim.esm|078742: Look, everything was all just a misunderstanding. I didn't mean to tell Brynjolf to go jump off the pier. You'll tell him I said sorry, yes? Take this. Every single coin I owe is there, I swear it.
Skyrim.esm|078745: I know who you are. You've been terrorizing the entire city. Please, there's no need for that here. Message understood. Here, I even have the payment!
Skyrim.esm|078746: I knew when we were married, this would come between us one day. I don't want to lose what we have, so I'll pay him gladly.
Skyrim.esm|078748: Oh! There's no need for that. I know why you're here. Tell Brynjolf not to worry about it anymore. Oh! And look, I even have the payment I owed. Here, take it.
Skyrim.esm|078B12: Oh... yes... um... thank you. Here you go. Some gold for your assistance and your, discretion.
Skyrim.esm|078B39: Master Wulfgar will now gift you with his knowledge of "Dah."
Skyrim.esm|078B3A: With all three words together, this Shout is much more powerful. Use it wisely.
Skyrim.esm|078B3B: You are ready to learn the final word of Unrelenting Force, "Dah," which means "Push."
Skyrim.esm|078B3C: We will now see how you learn a completely new Shout.
Skyrim.esm|078B3D: I see that you have already begun to learn the Whirlwind Sprint Shout.
Skyrim.esm|078B3E: Master Borri will teach you "Wuld," which means "Whirlwind."
Skyrim.esm|078B3F: Master Borri will teach you "Nah" - "Fury" - the second word of Whirlwind Sprint.
Skyrim.esm|078B41: Master Borri will teach you "Kest" - "Tempest"- the third word of Whirlwind Sprint.
Skyrim.esm|078B4F: Have you no thought beyond the blooding of your blade?
Skyrim.esm|078B51: You worry too much, brother. Victory will be ours.
Skyrim.esm|078B52: Hakon! A glorious day, is it not!
Skyrim.esm|078B53: The battle below goes ill. If Alduin does not rise to our challenge, I fear all may be lost.
Skyrim.esm|078B90: This library used to be filled with arcane volumes. Now look at it; almost everything's been burned. I hope the tome we need is still intact.
Skyrim.esm|078B92: Sorry, I'm not buying any goods until you straighten this out with Vex.
Skyrim.esm|078C1C: What? The Augur? Oh no, I'm quite sure that's... He's nothing I'm involved in. No, no. Mirabelle Ervine... Or Tolfdir. They're qualified to discuss him. Not me, definitely not.
Skyrim.esm|078C1D: It's not enough that you've stolen the spotlight with this Eye thing, now you're looking for the Augur? I'm not sure what you're playing at, but you'd do well to remember your place around here.
Skyrim.esm|078C1E: The Augur? Tragic story, really. At least, the way I heard it. But that was a long time ago. It was some experiment gone horribly wrong. Ghastly results, I was told. And his ghost still roams the halls, they say. Of course, on further reflection, that may have simply been an attempt to scare me. Hmm. Perhaps I'll ask Tolfdir what really happened. I understand he was here at the time.
Skyrim.esm|078C1F: I've never heard of anything like that. Did you ask Tolfdir? He's been here a long time, and seems to know almost everything.
Skyrim.esm|078DDD: Comes with living to a ripe old age, people start thinking there's something magical about you. Then, the insults. Still, a little knowledge of plants and potions can get you by. Not that anyone likes to admit buying things from you. See? I even have a little potion for the Steward. Mind handing it over to him? Just say it should solve that problem he has.
Skyrim.esm|078DF7: Daar sul thur se Alduin vokrii. Today Alduin's lordship will be restored. But I honor your courage. Krif voth ahkrin. Die now, in vain.
Skyrim.esm|078F35: Sweetheart, did you move my pickaxe?
Skyrim.esm|078F37: Ever think about spending more time around town?
Skyrim.esm|078F38: I'm just worried about what he's teaching her.
Skyrim.esm|078F39: I guess I should join you more often.
Skyrim.esm|078F3A: It's at the south end near that loose rockpile.
Skyrim.esm|078F3B: Sondas, does Hrefna ever mention Meieran to you?
Skyrim.esm|078F3C: And I found a new deposit of amber. Would have been nice to have some help pulling it out.
Skyrim.esm|078F3D: Oh, it's no trouble. She's a smart little girl. Might talk your ear off, but she's fun.
Skyrim.esm|078F3E: I wouldn't worry about that. He's a good elf. I trust him.
Skyrim.esm|078F3F: You used to love finding new places.
Skyrim.esm|078F40: So you can be helpful sometimes.
Skyrim.esm|078F42: Not when there's so much to see!
Skyrim.esm|078F43: Well, I know she thinks the world of you.
Skyrim.esm|078F46: I still do. I just like to have food waiting for me when I come home.
Skyrim.esm|078F47: Verner, you should have come out with me yesterday. I found the most amazing tree growing right out of a cave.
Skyrim.esm|078F48: Well, I'll try to be a good influence, then.
Skyrim.esm|078F72: What is it with you and all these questions? Very well, ask away.
Skyrim.esm|078F73: All right, I'll entertain you a bit more. Go on.
Skyrim.esm|07933C: Come to gawk at the monster?
Skyrim.esm|079800: Excellent. I have a couple tasks for you.
Skyrim.esm|079801: Good. I have some things that need doing.
Skyrim.esm|0799D3: Several months ago I ordered a special silver mold from some artisans in Valenwood by way of a Khajiit Caravan. Well, it never arrived. Later I found out that it was robbed by a group of bandits led by someone named Rigel Strong-Arm.
Skyrim.esm|0799D4: Yes, indeed. The only thing I can tell you is that the mold was taken to the bandit's hideout... a small cottage called Pinewatch. I'm not sure how many men they have inside, but I trust that won't be an issue for you. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0799D5: Exactly. Look, this mold is irreplaceable. I'll pay you well for its return and I can also prove to be quite a valuable ally to the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0799D6: I thought I was clear when I said I didn't know.
Skyrim.esm|0799D7: Endon's mold? I'm afraid you have the wrong place, my friend. You'll find no silversmiths here. I assure you, I have nothing of value. I'm nothing but a poor woodcutter just trying to make ends meet.
Skyrim.esm|0799D8: You've more than proven that the Thieves Guild is back on its feet in our city and earned every bit of your reward. Tell Delvin that he can count on me to provide the influence around here with the right people when he needs it. If you're ever looking to sell any illegally obtained merchandise, I'd also provide my services as a fence. It's the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|0799D9: No harm done. Tell this Endon fellow I hope he finds what he's looking for.
Skyrim.esm|0799DA: Well, can't say exactly where the mold is, but something does spring to mind. It seems there's this strange button on the wall opposite the fireplace in my house. Always wondered what it was for. Think I'm going to head out now. Take a long vacation from woodcutting, you know? Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0799DB: Oh thank goodness! I wasn't sure where else to turn...
Skyrim.esm|0799DC: So that's how it's going to be, huh? Fine. Take your best shot!
Skyrim.esm|0799DD: Well uh, Endon is a silversmith in Markarth, so I just assumed...
Skyrim.esm|0799DE: Look, I talked to the Jarl, the Steward, the Housecarl... everyone. They all told me their resources were spread too thin right now. Word on the street says that the Thieves Guild is coming back into its own in Markarth so I figured...
Skyrim.esm|079ADA: Before my ma and da passed, I told them that one day, I'd become the best trader in Skyrim. I met one of the caravan leaders, Ma'dran. He said he'd help get me started if I could bring him a mammoth's tusk. Easier said than done.
Skyrim.esm|079ADC: You'd do that? If you find one, I could teach you a thing or two about trading. Help you get a fair bargain in the future.
Skyrim.esm|079AE2: Thank you. This should turn that old cat's gaze. Maybe I'll be able to become a caravaneer myself, one day. As promised, let me show you a thing or two about bargaining. Don't want some shifty merchant giving you a raw trade.
Skyrim.esm|079AEB: You were just going to prove that you're Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|079B0E: Now we will see how quickly you can master a new Word.
Skyrim.esm|079B16: You don't remember me, Hjalti? Two campaigns we served together. You saved my life time and time again.
Skyrim.esm|079B17: I cannot go into battle without a sword, Hjalti.
Skyrim.esm|079B18: I long to taste battle again.
Skyrim.esm|079B19: Watch your tongue, stranger. We don't take backtalk in this inn.
Skyrim.esm|079B1A: Sure thing. It's yours for a day.
Skyrim.esm|079BA4: Then you leave me no choice!
Skyrim.esm|079BA8: It's time. The Skull must be destroyed. If you'll stand back, I'll perform the ritual granted to me by Lady Mara.
Skyrim.esm|079BA9: No! You mustn't listen to them. They speak only lies and deception. Had we aided them in releasing the Skull, they would use it to wreak havoc across Skyrim. They... they had to die.
Skyrim.esm|079BAC: And had they succeeded, Dawnstar's fate would be sealed. You have a unique way of looking at things, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|079BAD: Enough of your lies! I can't allow you to destroy the Skull, Priest of Mara.
Skyrim.esm|079BAE: No. I... I was scared. I wasn't ready to sleep.
Skyrim.esm|079BAF: You're a traitor. You left us to die and then ran before the Miasma took you.
Skyrim.esm|079BB0: Yes. You're right. If they needed to die so that Dawnstar could live, then it was worth the price.
Skyrim.esm|079BB1: I no longer use that name. I'm Erandur, Priest of Mara.
Skyrim.esm|07A0DA: Nivahriin joorre! What have you done? What twisted Words have you created?! Tahrodiis Paarthurnax! My teeth to his neck!
Skyrim.esm|07A0DD: To feed my power when I come for you in Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|07A1E7: As my Thane, I'm sworn to your service. I'll guard you, and all you own, with my life.
Skyrim.esm|07A1E8: What do you want me to carry?
Skyrim.esm|07A1EA: I'll head back home if you need me.
Skyrim.esm|07A4AD: The others do not speak. Their Voices are too powerful for you to withstand.
Skyrim.esm|07A50B: If that's what you want. Come find me at the College if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|07A50C: So you think you can make it on your own, then? We'll just see about that.
Skyrim.esm|07A50D: If that's what you want. Watch your back out there, and if you need me again, you know where I'll be.
Skyrim.esm|07A50E: It is a lonely thing, facing all the dangers of Skyrim by yourself. Come and find me if you decide that you miss my companionship.
Skyrim.esm|07A50F: You're letting me go? What's wrong, is my skill with a blade making you look bad? Hah! I'm kidding, friend. Until we meet again!
Skyrim.esm|07A510: I'm a mage, not a pawnbroker, you know.
Skyrim.esm|07A511: I am an apprentice wizard, not a pack mule! Oh very well, but make it quick.
Skyrim.esm|07A512: Sure, feel free to hand me all the gold you want.
Skyrim.esm|07A513: If that is what you wish.
Skyrim.esm|07A515: Try not to walk into any spike-filled pits or filthy skeever dens.
Skyrim.esm|07A517: I am the shadow at your back.
Skyrim.esm|07A518: Good! Let's go find something to fight.
Skyrim.esm|07A51A: Patience isn't exactly my strong suit, but alright.
Skyrim.esm|07A51C: Don't make me wait too long. I get bored very easily.
Skyrim.esm|07A51E: I'll wait here. Just don't start any trouble without me.
Skyrim.esm|07ACE1: I can't believe we lost Markarth. Igmund's family has ruled there for generations.
Skyrim.esm|07ACE2: Stormcloaks have taken the Reach, but we'll return. The Empire will bring order.
Skyrim.esm|07ACE3: Igmund is alive, and I'll keep it that way, Jarl or not.
Skyrim.esm|07ACE4: What can an old skald do for you, huh?
Skyrim.esm|07ACE5: The finest jewelry in all of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|07ACE6: Hey you. Yeah, you. Fresh meat for your stomach.
Skyrim.esm|07ACE7: Igmund was weak. We'll bring the order that the Empire should have.
Skyrim.esm|07ACEF: Let the Forsworn ravage the Reach. Talos favors us. Our victory is assured.
Skyrim.esm|07ACF2: The Silver-Blood family will be heard.
Skyrim.esm|07ACF3: We'll be sending generous resources to Ulfric. As much as the Reach can bleed.
Skyrim.esm|07ACF8: Keeping this place secure is as simple as breaking a few spines.
Skyrim.esm|07ACF9: Used to have run-ins with the guards, now they have run-ins with me.
Skyrim.esm|07ACFB: Arnleif and Sons Trading Company buys and sells anything you wandering types need.
Skyrim.esm|07B0D3: What? An intruder? Attack, my lovelies!
Skyrim.esm|07B0D4: Ah, another plaything for my collection.
Skyrim.esm|07B0D5: What's happening? No! Leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|07BC23: Come, my men stand ready on the great porch.
Skyrim.esm|07BC24: Let's do this. Summon this dragon of yours.
Skyrim.esm|07BC29: This is getting ridiculous. If we can't get this open soon, the boss will have our heads.
Skyrim.esm|07C13E: Get back! Get back! We need to trap it, not kill it!
Skyrim.esm|07CD30: You'll need to get your hands on some Balmora Blue from Sabine Nyette down by the docks. She's the first mate on another ship, the Red Wave. Once you get your hands on it, I want you to plant it in Captain Volf's footlocker. I'll take care of the rest.
Skyrim.esm|07CD31: It's about time you got here. I'm not accustomed to dealing with people who are unreliable. That's why I asked Delvin to send me his best.
Skyrim.esm|07CD32: Nothing raises my ire more than having an agreement broken. It's bad for business and it wastes time. Captain Volf of the Dainty Sload has decided to test my patience on this matter by neglecting to honor a trade agreement we had established.
Skyrim.esm|07CD33: Captain Volf is ashore right now and I want the authorities waiting for him when he gets back. Now get going... I don't want to see your face until the job's done.
Skyrim.esm|07CD34: Yes, I know. In fact, by now, Captain Volf should be on his way to the prisons. Our contract is complete. Here's a token of my gratitude for your efforts. Oh, convey my compliments to Delvin for me. Tell him I'll be happy to reopen whatever doors he needs in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|07CD35: Hmph. That remains to be seen.
Skyrim.esm|07CD36: I need you to help me show him the error of his ways by sneaking on board the Sload and planting some contraband.
Skyrim.esm|07D01E: It's a well-known fact that the steward keeps all of the records on Whiterun's prisoners in a large book. My sources tell me you can find that book in a study in his quarters. Change Arn's entry in the book to anything you want and they'll be forced to let him go after he serves a few weeks.
Skyrim.esm|07D01F: The city guard in Solitude is seeking Arn for a serious crime. When he fled here, he was arrested for drunken behavior. Can you imagine? Fortunately, his identity isn't known to the authorities in Whiterun, so there's still a chance to save him.
Skyrim.esm|07D020: Arn? Never heard of him. I guess that means you're finished. Here's your payment. Tell Delvin that he has my support and all the weight it carries in Whiterun from now on. I think he'll be quite pleased.
Skyrim.esm|07D021: A close friend of mine. We fought together on the battlefield for many years until old age got the better of us. Now it's up to me to save him one more time... this time from the executioner's block in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|07D022: Hold a moment. This is more than a simple prison break. I want to have Arn's name stricken from the record books permanently. I'm setting him up with a new identity. It's the only way to throw the guard permanently off his trail.
Skyrim.esm|07D023: I have eyes within Dragonsreach. They tell me that all correspondence from other holds are sent to the Jarl's private chambers.
Skyrim.esm|07D024: If it was easy, I would have hired a local thug instead of a professional. You see both of these items are kept inside Dragonsreach, and they don't allow visitors inside the Jarl's or the Steward's chambers. One more thing. If you get caught, I can't afford to be connected to you. Remember that before you do anything stupid.
Skyrim.esm|07D025: You're here! And not a moment too soon. If anything should happen to Arn, there'll be hell to pay.
Skyrim.esm|07D026: The job is two-fold. First, steal a letter that was sent from Solitude warning Whiterun's guard to be on the lookout for Arn. The second is to change Arn's name in the prison registry to his new identity.
Skyrim.esm|07D415: I'd almost given up hope. Let me get the door...
Skyrim.esm|07D427: Very true! Hmm. I wonder... do you know the Jarl's palace in Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|07D428: Oh, this was thousands of years ago, before the Akaviri crusaders cleansed Skyrim of dragons. Skyrim's mountains were still infested with dragons. One of the early Nord kings caught a dragon and kept it in Dragonsreach.
Skyrim.esm|07D429: Well, you see, the palace - Dragonsreach - was originally built to hold a captive dragon. Hence its name.
Skyrim.esm|07D42A: Yes, exactly! Most people don't know that it was originally built to hold a captive dragon - hence its name.
Skyrim.esm|07D42B: He must have returned to Sovngarde to feed on the souls of the dead. If you don't find him soon, he'll return, stronger than ever!
Skyrim.esm|07D42C: Yes, yes. We must think. He must have some means of travelling to and from Sovngarde. But no tale tells where that might be.
Skyrim.esm|07D42D: I'm still amazed that your plan worked.
Skyrim.esm|07D42E: You'll be the toast of Whiterun for this!
Skyrim.esm|07D42F: No one can doubt that you truly are Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|07D431: Something to tell the grandkids, eh?
Skyrim.esm|07D432: I can't believe I'm still alive.
Skyrim.esm|07D433: I admit, I doubted you, but no more. You've made a dragon submit to your will. A feat to rival the legendary Olaf One-Eye! What glory!
Skyrim.esm|07D434: Lure it into the trap! I'm counting on you!
Skyrim.esm|07D435: Do whatever you're going to do before it kills us all!
Skyrim.esm|07D436: My men are ready. Now it's up to you.
Skyrim.esm|07D437: This was your crazy idea, huh?
Skyrim.esm|07D438: You better know what you're doing.
Skyrim.esm|07D439: I don't know how you talked the Jarl into this.
Skyrim.esm|07D43B: You could trap him there, exactly.
Skyrim.esm|07D671: The only name I have is Niranye. Has a house right here in Windhelm. That's where you should begin looking.
Skyrim.esm|07D672: My daughter Fjotli... she was murdered a few months ago. Left her laying on the ground in a pool of her own blood.
Skyrim.esm|07D673: Well, like I told Delvin, I think we can help each other. You recover what I'm looking for and take out a rival Guild in the process.
Skyrim.esm|07D674: It pains me to see this locket... to be reminded of  Fjotli once more, but I'm glad it's back where it belongs. Tell Delvin that if he still desires to have my support for the Thieves Guild in Windhelm, he's got it.
Skyrim.esm|07D675: No need to explain, I'm well aware of your Guild's methods. It took me weeks, but I finally tracked down the killer. A bloody Altmer at that. Can you imagine?
Skyrim.esm|07D676: Exactly. One of the pieces stolen from her was a silver locket, a Cruel-Sea family heirloom. I want it back.
Skyrim.esm|07D677: Let's just say I'm a firm believer in an eye for an eye and leave it at that. He fancied himself a thief in some sort of a new Guild forming around here. Gave me some valuable information before... well, you know.
Skyrim.esm|07D905: The Stormcloaks will be here any minute! Hurry! Get inside and lock your doors!
Skyrim.esm|07D906: Citizens! Go home and lock your doors!
Skyrim.esm|07D907: It's not safe out here! Return to your homes immediately!
Skyrim.esm|07D908: The imperial butchers are on their way. Get home and lock your doors!
Skyrim.esm|07D909: What are you people doing outside? Go home. Now!
Skyrim.esm|07D90A: Go home and lock your doors.
Skyrim.esm|07D935: I feel the hunger inside of you. Gnawing at you. You see the dead and your mouth grows wet. Your stomach growls.
Skyrim.esm|07D939: If it's about the Hall of the Dead, no, you can't go in there.
Skyrim.esm|07D93F: Not many would walk blindly into a crypt, smelling of steel and blood, but not fear.
Skyrim.esm|07D940: Arkay keeps the cycle. He watches us in life and death.
Skyrim.esm|07D941: Arkay will guard our eternal rest. His servants shall protect the dead from harm.
Skyrim.esm|07D942: Protect us, Arkay, from the foul and unclean who seek to disturb the dead's rest.
Skyrim.esm|07D943: Let us sleep, sweet Arkay, by your side. Let no harm come to us.
Skyrim.esm|07DCB3: Who enters? Will nobody save poor Melka?
Skyrim.esm|07DCB4: Careful here, there's a trick to this. Trick is to not bleed to death. Ah, and there's a lever, too.
Skyrim.esm|07DE91: Look, I... I... You're right. You're right. Feller might be nutters, might not. But fact is, he needs help. I turn him away, what kind of man am I, hmm? Look, um... Thanks. And I'm sorry for my unneighborly reaction. If you talk to Cicero, you be sure and tell him I'll be down to help soon.
Skyrim.esm|07DE92: Well, there is something. There's a guard, patrols the road that crazy jester is on. He passes by pretty often. You could report the fool. You know, say he's done something against the law. That will at least get rid of him for me. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|07E64B: Look out! The prisoners are loose!
Skyrim.esm|07E8B5: Walk always in shadow, my Listener.
Skyrim.esm|07E8E7: Ready when you need me, Listener.
Skyrim.esm|07E96D: Dovahkiin. You have tasted the Voice of the Greybeards, and passed through unscathed. High Hrothgar is open to you.
Skyrim.esm|07E97A: Forgive me if I don't appear relieved... this temple has taken its toll on me.
Skyrim.esm|07E97B: First, an incantation to remove the barrier.
Skyrim.esm|07E97D: There's no need to thank me. What you did was for the people of Dawnstar. If anyone should be thanked, it should be you.
Skyrim.esm|07EA22: I'd constructed a meager shrine to Mara in the antechamber where we entered. My intention was to spend the rest of my years here, burying the past and praying for forgiveness. But instead, I wish to offer my services to you. If you ever wish to journey with me, I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|07EA23: Being a humble servant of Mara, I have little in the way of gold or coin, but perhaps I can offer you something better... companionship.
Skyrim.esm|07EA24: Yes. The Skull has been destroyed and Dawnstar is safe.
Skyrim.esm|07EA25: In time, I believe I will.
Skyrim.esm|07EA56: Come. You, too. We can escape through the tunnels.
Skyrim.esm|07EA59: Not without a fight it isn't!
Skyrim.esm|07EA5A: I assume everyone's gone ahead of us as planned. I just swept through the castle, no one's here. Now. We really must be going, sir.
Skyrim.esm|07EA5B: As soon as we can muster our forces, we'll be back. That's the battle you need to fight. Not this. This will be suicide. And to what end?
Skyrim.esm|07EAA6: And just why would you be doing that? Oh, it doesn't matter now. Gavros is never coming back, and he was supposed to have the crystal. Without that, we're lost. It's all been for nothing. So congratulations, whoever you are, you've found the Synod. Just me, alone, surrounded by angry machines and angrier Falmer.
Skyrim.esm|07EAA7: It didn't work the first time. I tried to tell Gavros, but he wouldn't listen. "No, it won't be too cold" he said. Well, I was right, wasn't I? Focused completely wrong by the time we got here! The cold had warped it! Gavros had to cart it all the way back to Cyrodiil. Left the rest of us here to fend off the damnable Falmer.
Skyrim.esm|07EAA8: What are you doing here? What've you done with Gavros?
Skyrim.esm|07EAA9: Really? Well, I do recall warning them it might not be the safest of locations. I hope this doesn't become a problem for us. Were you able to learn anything?
Skyrim.esm|07EAAA: Hmm. I can't say I've any idea what they were up to. Have you found this observatory? You might be able to learn something from it. Keep looking, but be careful.
Skyrim.esm|07EAAB: It was the Falmer, wasn't it? Curse them! They've ruined everything! If Gavros is gone, there's no hope. He was supposed to return with the crystal... Without that, all our efforts are wasted. And you. If you're here for treasure, or wisdom, or anything, I'm afraid you've wasted your time.
Skyrim.esm|07EAAC: I can't imagine what that might be, but if it's safe, you might see if they were successful. You could get some useful information about whatever they were up to. Sounds like it won't be of use to them now, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|07EAAD: I trust that you'll inform me as soon as the Staff is recovered.
Skyrim.esm|07EAAE: I hope Mirabelle is helpful in locating the Staff.
Skyrim.esm|07EAAF: Have you managed to track down the Synod, or the Staff of Magnus?
Skyrim.esm|07EAB0: I am on official business of the Grand Council of the Synod. That's all you need to know. I might well ask the same of you, running around by yourself in this dangerous place.
Skyrim.esm|07EADB: You're the one Mazzaka described to me.
Skyrim.esm|07EADC: Ha! I'm afraid the loot's already been moved. You've been useful to the Blackbloods, though. You've earned a quick death.
Skyrim.esm|07EADE: It's appreciated. The captains around here will have one less thing to worry about. Here's your reward.
Skyrim.esm|07EADF: I'm Captain Aldis. I'm here to help you take down these bandits.
Skyrim.esm|07EAE0: You are exactly what I was looking for.
Skyrim.esm|07EAE3: My brother told me you were coming. I'm supposed to give you what you've earned.
Skyrim.esm|07EAE5: First you murder my sister, then you massacre my men!
Skyrim.esm|07EAE8: You've betrayed me, murdered my sister and massacred my men!
Skyrim.esm|07EAE9: We're stretched thin in Solitude, and I don't expect these bandits to be much challenge. Are you ready?
Skyrim.esm|07EAEA: I've been away from Solitude for as long as I can. But if you decide to finish these bandits off, you'll be rewarded for your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|07EAED: To Oblivion with your loot! This ends now!
Skyrim.esm|07EAEF: You don't have what it takes to kill me.
Skyrim.esm|07EB20: Did you get a look at that thief that they sent to break into the place? What I wouldn't give for a night with her.
Skyrim.esm|07EB21: I can't believe how lucky we got landing this job. It's a far cry from working for the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|07EB22: Aringoth sold Goldenglow? What's that idiot thinking? He has no idea the extent of Maven's fury when she's been cut out of a deal, but I'm certain he'll find out. If only this parchment had the buyer's name instead of this odd symbol. Any idea what that might be?
Skyrim.esm|07EB23: No idea. Maybe he got tired of getting stung by these damn bees.
Skyrim.esm|07EB24: Keep your eyes open, they'll try to hit us again... I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|07EB25: Sure. Then you wake up in the morning with your purse empty and your gear stolen. No thanks.
Skyrim.esm|07EB26: You're telling me. Playing nursemaid to a bunch of bees and drinking mead all day beats dying on some battlefield.
Skyrim.esm|07EB28: Worthless mercenaries. I didn't think Maven or Mercer would allow me to get away with this, but I had little choice.
Skyrim.esm|07EB29: I can't. If I do, I may as well cut my own throat.
Skyrim.esm|07EB2A: I'll give you one good reason. Maven Black-Briar. Burn all the hives and she'd have to import honey for Black-Briar Meadery which would cut into her profits.
Skyrim.esm|07EB2B: Agh... so bored! Only one fight in the last few weeks? This is gonna' drive me crazy.
Skyrim.esm|07EB2C: Fine. Take it! Once the new owner finds out I gave in, I'm as good as dead anyway.
Skyrim.esm|07EB2D: If they do, we'll send them crying back to their boss again.
Skyrim.esm|07EB2E: Have you seen Refnir? I told him to check out the well drain, but he never reported back.
Skyrim.esm|07EB2F: So why did he sell this place? I mean look at it. This is how to live.
Skyrim.esm|07EB30: Aringoth said as soon as the deed goes through, we're done here. Then we'll head south and look for more work. Deal?
Skyrim.esm|07EB31: Blast. Well, I'll check my sources and speak to Mercer. But for now, you're off to speak to Maven Black-Briar. She asked for you by name.
Skyrim.esm|07EB32: I've already said too much. I gave you what you came here for, now go. Leave me in peace.
Skyrim.esm|07EB33: Probably got drunk and fell into the lake. Who cares.
Skyrim.esm|07EBD6: Everyone is this city is so on edge. I could use some good news, Skulvar.
Skyrim.esm|07EBD7: Can you close up the stand early today, mama?
Skyrim.esm|07EBD8: You don't prepare the meat with any of those strange Elven spices, do you?
Skyrim.esm|07EBD9: Listen, Braith... Why don't you talk to your mother about this, okay? There's a good girl.
Skyrim.esm|07EBDA: Shor's beard, boy! You forget where you're standing? What kind o' business we're running?
Skyrim.esm|07EBDC: Look, come back tomorrow, okay? If I have anything left that's not sold or spoiled, you can have it.
Skyrim.esm|07EBDD: Not now, Braith. Go... go ask your father.
Skyrim.esm|07EBDE: Braith. I.... I've come to tell you that I'm not afraid of you anymore.
Skyrim.esm|07EBDF: Nice try, blossom, but I'm not a knight, I'm a mercenary. The answer's still no. Besides, your mother would miss you too much.
Skyrim.esm|07EBE1: I can't. Not tonight. I need to go home. I swear, my father is starting to suspect something.
Skyrim.esm|07EBE2: Well if my layabout manservant fed me more, that wouldn't be a problem.
Skyrim.esm|07EBE3: I'm just no good at this. You said it yourself. Gods, I don't even like horses. And if it's true what they're saying about dragons...
