Skyrim.esm|07EBE4: I tell you? Saw that wild mare again yesterday, just outside the walls.
Skyrim.esm|07EBE5: Oh. Okay. So we should try to... what? Capture it?
Skyrim.esm|07EBE6: Oh Jon, stop dallying. You know you have to go. It's why Mara put you on this earth. You think a few miles can truly keep us apart?
Skyrim.esm|07EBE7: A ballad? Oh, now I know you're joking with me.
Skyrim.esm|07EBE9: You mean you won't forget about me the moment I'm out of sight? Get yourself a nice old rich husband, have sixteen babies?
Skyrim.esm|07EBEA: Perfect, my dear. Absolutely perfect. I'll see you then.
Skyrim.esm|07EBEB: Aye, that it has. Trust me, mum - once you've killed a man from a horse, you'll never want to fight on the ground again. Ain't no goin' back.
Skyrim.esm|07EBED: Don't worry, brother. I'm sure he's fine. We've waited longer before receiving a letter.
Skyrim.esm|07EBEF: That's what I thought. Now get out of my way, baby. I got things to do.
Skyrim.esm|07EBF0: No handouts today, Brenuin. Now scat. You'll scare off my paying customers.
Skyrim.esm|07EBF1: Our stock of arrows is running a bit low.
Skyrim.esm|07EBF2: I... I don't have any money.
Skyrim.esm|07EBF3: Tsk. And to think, I was going to feed you cabbages and apples again. Yes, little fairy, we can go to the tavern for supper.
Skyrim.esm|07EBF4: Three weeks? You're mad! You can't keep the stand closed for that long!
Skyrim.esm|07EBF6: Papa, I want to talk to you...
Skyrim.esm|07EBF7: You've got the heart of Mara herself, you know that? Thank you, Carlotta. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|07EBF8: Dragons? You want to go out and fight dragons? Are you completely off your nut?
Skyrim.esm|07EBF9: Ahhhh... No, no Elven spices. Just some salt, to preserve it. You're not afraid of salt, are you?
Skyrim.esm|07EBFB: I've been thinking of hunting out beyond the boundaries of Whiterun hold.
Skyrim.esm|07EBFC: Lillith - always a pleasure. Just browsing, or were you looking for something particular?
Skyrim.esm|07EBFD: I'm afraid not, little fairy. The fruit has about another day left before it spoils. We've got to sell as much as we can, as soon as we can.
Skyrim.esm|07EBFE: Ha! Four more geldings sold. Sound like good news to you?
Skyrim.esm|07EC00: That's your imagination running wild. Nobody knows how we feel about each other.
Skyrim.esm|07EC01: Don't make me laugh, baby Battle-Born. You'll be afraid of me till the day I kill you.
Skyrim.esm|07EC02: Tsk. You've uncovered my master plan. Now I suppose you'll never leave.
Skyrim.esm|07EC03: Why thank you for that most colorful assessment, Skulvar. So we'll be needing fresh horses, then?
Skyrim.esm|07EC04: Mother? Can I talk to you?
Skyrim.esm|07EC05: Oho! Manservant? Is that what I am now?
Skyrim.esm|07EC06: Don't suppose there is. But you don't have to be so damned prickly.
Skyrim.esm|07EC09: Might be worth riding out to Solitude, see if a messenger ship's come in.
Skyrim.esm|07EC0A: Aye, mum. That we will. Mares, stallions, donkeys - I don't think it matters. Pretty sure I can sell any steeds we get.
Skyrim.esm|07EC0B: So I'm, uh... writing a song for you...
Skyrim.esm|07EC0F: Papa, the next time you go away, I want to come with you.
Skyrim.esm|07EC10: It's all true, I swear it. You can hear it when it's done. I'll just need a year or two smooth the rough edges.
Skyrim.esm|07EC11: Do you have anything even remotely fresh?
Skyrim.esm|07EC12: What are you gonna do if I don't? Huh? Gonna cry to your papa?
Skyrim.esm|07EC14: Oh, just one apple, Carlotta? Just one tiny apple. I'll pay you tomorrow, I swear it.
Skyrim.esm|07EC16: Why not? Business has been terrible. Most folk would rather hunt their own meat than buy ours.
Skyrim.esm|07EC18: All right. I'll fletch some more when I get the time.
Skyrim.esm|07EC19: I'm not "afraid" of anything. I simply prefer quality merchandise. No, no this meat won't do at all. Too much fat, and it all smells... wrong.
Skyrim.esm|07EC1C: You'd better give me five septims, right now, or I'm going to bloody your nose...
Skyrim.esm|07EC1D: Hmm. So I haven't. All right, I'll buy the ingredients tomorrow and we can have it for dinner.
Skyrim.esm|07EC1F: Funny you should ask. I'm in the market for a new locket. Something in polished silver, maybe. Big enough to hold, say, a folded-up letter?
Skyrim.esm|07EC20: You... You're wrong! You can't hurt me anymore...
Skyrim.esm|07EC21: Don't talk about my father like that, Jon. And the answer's still no. But tomorrow. Definitely.
Skyrim.esm|07EC23: Come on, father, how hard can it be? They're just... big lizards. And those scales and teeth, they must be worth a fortune...
Skyrim.esm|07EC24: Excellent. I'll send the proper letters, then, and have some fresh horses delivered straightaway.
Skyrim.esm|07EC25: Fancy a game of Iron Hearts later?
Skyrim.esm|07EC26: Nonsense. That's just an excuse for you to avoid working, and "commune with the greenwood like our fathers of old" and blah blah.
Skyrim.esm|07EC2A: My mistake. A manservant wouldn't let me starve or freeze. I let you live here, and now I'm two steps closer to the grave.
Skyrim.esm|07EC2B: If it'll put your mind at ease, then I agree. But not now, all right? We need the business from the stand.
Skyrim.esm|07EC2C: Suit yourself, then. The woods are that way. Outside the walls, walk straight ahead. Can't miss them. Good luck on your hunt.
Skyrim.esm|07EC2D: That's what you said last week, and the week before that. Now go, before I call a guard.
Skyrim.esm|07EC2E: When I go away, I go to war. That's no place for a little girl.
Skyrim.esm|07EC2F: So have you spoken to that man from the Bards College?
Skyrim.esm|07EC31: I can assure you, it's all fresh. I hunt the game myself, every day.
Skyrim.esm|07EC32: You ain't made me that stew in a long time.
Skyrim.esm|07EC33: Why Jon Battle-Born. You're writing me a song? Does it somehow involve blood, or beheadings, or the honor of my forebears?
Skyrim.esm|07EC34: And what might you be looking at?
Skyrim.esm|07EC35: Maybe you'd like to try the horker? Or some beef?
Skyrim.esm|07EC36: No! Please! I really don't have any money, Braith! I'll give you ten septims tomorrow. Just don't hit me.
Skyrim.esm|07EC37: There's a chill in the air. Ysmir's beard, it gets colder in here every day.
Skyrim.esm|07EC38: Good. I'll do the same. We've still got plenty of shafts from that Riverwood shipment.
Skyrim.esm|07EC3A: We manage to break that mare and bring her back here, we can sell her for a small fortune.
Skyrim.esm|07EC3B: Oh no? So you're gonna fight back if I punch you? Huh? You're gonna hit a girl?
Skyrim.esm|07EC3C: Congratulations, brother. You've been living in Skyrim so long, you've become a Nord.
Skyrim.esm|07EC3D: Old man, without me, you would have withered away a long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|07EC3E: Big lizards? By Shor, your mother gave birth to a half-wit. That is the end of this discussion.
Skyrim.esm|07EC40: Again? You just had an "extended hunt" last month. To the Pine Forest, wasn't it.
Skyrim.esm|07EC41: Here it comes again. Not happy with a roof and a trade, no. You want to be something entirely... different.
Skyrim.esm|07EC44: I wanted to ask you something. It's about a boy...
Skyrim.esm|07EC47: I want to see you when you're done working.
Skyrim.esm|07EC48: You going to lose horribly, and then say you let me win?
Skyrim.esm|07EC49: No, no! Don't do that. No need to do that. I'm going. See? I'm leaving. No guards, please.
Skyrim.esm|07EC4A: Well, that's where I started. But it turned into something of a ballad.
Skyrim.esm|07EC4B: Oh look, it's little baby Battle-Born.
Skyrim.esm|07EC4C: Yes, father, I did. And I watered her, and got fresh feed, and refitted the old saddle, just like you asked. Is there anything else?
Skyrim.esm|07EC4E: Ten septims tomorrow, baby Battle-Born. Or I'm gonna bloody your nose and your lip.
Skyrim.esm|07EC4F: Every knight needs a squire. I could help you with your sword, things like that.
Skyrim.esm|07EC50: No, not yet. But I will. I just haven't... gotten around to it yet.
Skyrim.esm|07EC52: That's just you getting skinnier every day, old man. You're losing your padding. You need to eat more.
Skyrim.esm|07EC53: Yes, son. We should try to capture it.
Skyrim.esm|07EC54: Which, the mutton or the vegetable?
Skyrim.esm|07EC55: It's been two months, and no word from father.
Skyrim.esm|07EC56: At least the Nords don't shirk their responsibilities.
Skyrim.esm|07EC57: Oh, the fruit looks so good. So juicy.
Skyrim.esm|07EC5A: Ahhh, I see. I think I know just what you need. I don't have anything like that here now, but my Eorlund - he can make you one right quick. When I get home, I'll have him start on it right away. Come back to pick it up in, oh... three days?
Skyrim.esm|07EC5E: Can we at least go to the tavern for supper?
Skyrim.esm|07EC5F: Indeed. I do hate to admit it, but if nothing else, all the fighting lately has been a complete blessing to this business.
Skyrim.esm|07EC61: That was three days. I'm thinking more like three weeks.
Skyrim.esm|07EC62: Hmph. Damn you for being right.
Skyrim.esm|07EDD8: Oh, I didn't see you there.
Skyrim.esm|07EDD9: We were excavating this old tomb when we opened a chamber full of corpses. The team took one look, turned tail, and ran.
Skyrim.esm|07EDDA: In any case, if I can't get past the creatures in that room, I can't recover the treasure. I need more manpower.
Skyrim.esm|07EDDB: Obviously, this treasure will fetch a good deal of gold, and I'm willing to let you share in the reward when we find it.
Skyrim.esm|07EDDC: Legend says this tomb holds a treasure that bestows great power on the one who discovers it. Beyond that, not much more is known.
Skyrim.esm|07EDDD: Excellent. With my brains and your brawn, that treasure will soon be mine... uh, ours.
Skyrim.esm|07EDDE: Well, probably not like these. The ones in there are all still walking around.
Skyrim.esm|07EDDF: First, I need you to clear the main chamber of those monstrosities. I'll stay here until you're finished, planning our next move.
Skyrim.esm|07EDE0: Bear in mind, even if you clear out the tomb, you'll still need a key to get to the treasure. A key I happen to possess.
Skyrim.esm|07EDE1: Of course I'm troubled. I'm on the verge of incredible wealth, but my cowardly work force ran at the first sign of danger.
Skyrim.esm|07EDE2: Bah! You're just as bad as the rest of them.
Skyrim.esm|07EDE3: Say, that's not a bad idea. You look like you could handle yourself against these beasts.
Skyrim.esm|07EDE4: I'll tell you what. You help me get the treasure and I'll split it with you. Deal?
Skyrim.esm|07F265: So, what book should I read to you tonight?
Skyrim.esm|07F266: That's all right, grandmother. I like staying home with you.
Skyrim.esm|07F267: You've got no sense of humor, you know that? Why don't you pick up where you left off with "The Real Barenziah."
Skyrim.esm|07F268: It's that Braith girl again, isn't it? Has she been bothering you again? I've a mind to speak with her mother.
Skyrim.esm|07F269: Eorlund, dearest, are you hungry? Would you like me to fix you something?
Skyrim.esm|07F26A: No! Please, grandmother, don't do that. I'm fine. Really. Can we just... not talk about this anymore?
Skyrim.esm|07F26D: When will you be makin' some more jewelry, love? Some silver lockets and amulets would fetch a nice price at the stand.
Skyrim.esm|07F26F: A venison pie, maybe? With a nice cup of mulled mead?
Skyrim.esm|07F272: Lars, dear, why don't you go outside and play? It's a beautiful day.
Skyrim.esm|07F3F7: You there. We are looking for someone in Whiterun, and will pay good money for information.
Skyrim.esm|07F413: The Silver Hand have taken over the old fort at Gallows Rock. They always make such easy prey.
Skyrim.esm|07F414: Old fort. Abandoned for years. They're like jackals, making their home anywhere they can find.
Skyrim.esm|07F426: Please tell me you have news of my son.
Skyrim.esm|07F427: Don't worry, citizen. I'm on top of that little matter we discussed.
Skyrim.esm|07F428: I'll soon arrange transportation for our little friend. Don't worry, he'll rot in a cell and pay for what he's done.
Skyrim.esm|07F6B6: We've failed. The city is lost. They're through the gates and will be here soon.
Skyrim.esm|080102: You only face failure once more...
Skyrim.esm|080103: Nivahriin muz fent siiv nid aaz het.
Skyrim.esm|080104: You... You are not Aren, are you? Has he sent you in his place?
Skyrim.esm|080105: Have you returned, Aren? My old friend?
Skyrim.esm|080106: You do not answer... Must I use this guttural language of yours?
Skyrim.esm|080107: Did he warn you that your own power would be your undoing? That it would only serve to strengthen me?
Skyrim.esm|080108: What? Are you... Are you sure? The staff is there? That can't be a coincidence.
Skyrim.esm|08010A: Do you seek to finish that which you could not?
Skyrim.esm|08010B: Wo meyz wah dii vul junaar?
Skyrim.esm|080611: Not sure. Feeling like taking another trip.
Skyrim.esm|080612: What's your wife think of your trips?
Skyrim.esm|080615: What did I tell you about snooping around my wards?
Skyrim.esm|080616: Again? But there shouldn't be any earthstreams over there.
Skyrim.esm|080617: No, I've been busy trying to keep the whole mine from collapsing, if that's all right with you.
Skyrim.esm|080619: They're none of her business, and none of yours either.
Skyrim.esm|08061A: I'd like the fresh air better, but this isn't so bad.
Skyrim.esm|08061C: Woman, there's a vein of malachite as thick as my leg back there.
Skyrim.esm|08061D: Well I nearly lost my eyebrows to it.
Skyrim.esm|08061F: Not too bad. Careful around the more obvious scorchers.
Skyrim.esm|080620: I don't know. Maybe because it makes the mead taste that much better at night.
Skyrim.esm|080621: Found another one of those vents in back near the scaffold.
Skyrim.esm|080622: And if you don't let me secure the steam channel, your leg could get blown off. So go dig at the other corner for a bit.
Skyrim.esm|080623: How do you stay cheery down in these blazes?
Skyrim.esm|080625: All right, I'll take a look at it.
Skyrim.esm|080628: As usual. I've always got an eye on the rocks.
Skyrim.esm|080629: You going to be at the inn tonight?
Skyrim.esm|08062A: You're busy. I'll come back later.
Skyrim.esm|08062B: Roggi tell you about the scorcher we found under the scaffolding?
Skyrim.esm|08062C: Is the new shaft cleared yet?
Skyrim.esm|08062D: Well, you send that blowhard to me if he steps over the line.
Skyrim.esm|08062E: She hasn't had a chance to look at it yet. Just be careful over there.
Skyrim.esm|08069D: So you're interested in my potions and ingredients?
Skyrim.esm|08069F: Usual guild rate. Let's take a look at them.
Skyrim.esm|0806A0: Bow and arrows for the mighty huntsman.
Skyrim.esm|0806A2: Drink for the thirsty, food for the hungry.
Skyrim.esm|0806A4: Oh, a bit of this and a bit of that.
Skyrim.esm|0806A6: Spells and incantations for those with the talent to cast them.
Skyrim.esm|0806A7: Clothes and raiment for the discerning customer.
Skyrim.esm|0806BE: I'm sorry to drag you into Markarth's problems, but after that attack in the market, I'm running out of time. You're an outsider. You're dangerous-looking. You'll do.
Skyrim.esm|0806C6: The Forsworn? Strange. Well, I hope the Eight give you more peace in the future, for what it's worth.
Skyrim.esm|080C0F: When you're ready, meet me at Snow Veil Sanctum.
Skyrim.esm|080C10: Exactly. For the Guild to survive, they need Maven's support. This Karliah must have spent a lot of time and resources planning this.
Skyrim.esm|080C24: Maven Black-Briar needs Goldenglow's honey for her mead. She's been using the Guild to keep the Estate under her thumb. If the owner cuts the Guild out of the picture, he's cutting Maven out of the picture... which she can't afford. If I was Maven, I'd blame the Guild for weakening and not being able to handle the place.
Skyrim.esm|081179: I have need of a warrior. Only the most ruthless, cunning, and agile will do.
Skyrim.esm|08117A: I have a special task for whichever of you is left standing.
Skyrim.esm|08117E: But which among you truly exceeds the rest?
Skyrim.esm|08117F: You have gathered here night after night, sacrificing fools upon my shrine, and sparring aimlessly with yourselves.
Skyrim.esm|08131C: 'Course not. Dogs I could train. But a half-starved wolf? Not a chance.
Skyrim.esm|08131D: ...Yeah. We can't have that rheumy-eyed fool as our watchman, even if he is Hajvarr's uncle. Time we dealt with both of them.
Skyrim.esm|08131E: Right. Lucky if it doesn't rip our throats out.
Skyrim.esm|081320: Look out! The wolf is loose!
Skyrim.esm|081321: We strike tonight, at dusk. Be ready.
Skyrim.esm|0813A7: No, no. That's the beauty of the whole plan. We're going to get Sabjorn to give it to us. The meadery has quite a pest problem and the whole city knows about it. Pest poison and mead don't mix well, you know what I mean?
Skyrim.esm|0813A8: If this plan works, not only is my debt gone, but I'll be set up for life. Maven and I worked out a little deal. If Sabjorn ends up in jail, she's going to take over his meadery. And guess who gets to run the Black-Briar Meadery in Whiterun? You're looking at him.
Skyrim.esm|0813AA: Well, whoever this mysterious marking represents, they'll regret starting a war with me. You should bring this information to the Thieves Guild immediately. There's also the matter of your payment. I believe you'll find this more than adequate for your services.
Skyrim.esm|0813AB: Have you lost your mind? I thought it was clear I didn't want anyone killed. Now I have to spend a fortune to keep the whole thing quiet. Guess where that money comes from.
Skyrim.esm|0813AC: You'll just have to wait until after the captain's finished. I suppose you can wait around if you must.
Skyrim.esm|0813AD: Maven and I spent weeks planning this. All we need is someone like you to get in there and get it done. Now get going before Sabjorn grows a brain and hires someone else to do the dirty work.
Skyrim.esm|0813AE: So, Maven wants to hunt down Sabjorn's private partner, huh? You're welcome to take a look around Sabjorn's office. He keeps most of his papers stashed in his desk. Here, this should help.
Skyrim.esm|0813AF: I don't think that could have gone any better. Anything else you need before you head back to Riften?
Skyrim.esm|0813B0: Silent means I don't tell and it's none of your business.
Skyrim.esm|0819D4: Very well. But should the hunger call out to you, I'll be waiting outside Reachcliff Cave. Tell the people of Markarth that their dead are safe. My work is done here.
Skyrim.esm|0819D5: You were young when you first tasted human flesh, weren't you? A brother or sister had died? An accident, of course. Then the hunger set in. Curiosity. What's the harm in just one bite? It's okay, now. You've found a friend who understands you. You can let go of your guilt.
Skyrim.esm|0819D8: It's inside Reachcliff Cave. But the dead have stirred from their slumber recently, and I was forced here. Meet me there. We will fight our way to Namira's embrace together. Until then, tell the people of Markarth that their dead won't be disturbed, anymore. We have bigger plans ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0819DA: Blessed are the forgotten. The wretched.
Skyrim.esm|0819DB: The dead exist to feed the living.
Skyrim.esm|0819DD: Divines preserve you, you're a hero. We'll re-open the Hall right away. Here. Take my amulet as a reward.
Skyrim.esm|081C4C: Listen. Hear me and obey. A foul darkness has seeped into my temple. A darkness that you will destroy.
Skyrim.esm|081C4F: Restore to me my beacon, that I might guide you toward your destiny.
Skyrim.esm|081C50: Look at my temple, lying in ruins. So much for the constancy of mortals, their crafts and their hearts. If they love me not, how can my love reach them?
Skyrim.esm|081C51: But first, you must restore to me my beacon. I shall guide you unto it. Find it and return here. And great shall be your reward.
Skyrim.esm|081C52: Return my beacon to Mount Kilkreath. And I will make you the instrument of my cleansing light.
Skyrim.esm|081C53: You have found my beacon. But it is not time for rejoicing. The defiler continues to profane my temple. Return to my figure on Mount Kilkreath, and I shall make you the instrument of my cleansing light.
Skyrim.esm|081C54: Do you prefer heedlessness to my luminous glory? Go! Take my beacon to the mount above Kilkreath.
Skyrim.esm|081C56: Fool! He is perverting my Light. A Light that could illuminate Skyrim and her people.
Skyrim.esm|081C7E: Not here. We will meet at Reachcliff Cave.
Skyrim.esm|081CC5: I was hoping you would say that.
Skyrim.esm|081D00: You've come. The draugr infesting Namira's sanctuary are inside.
Skyrim.esm|081DDF: Wuuthrad was the blade held by Ysgramor when he cut through the elven hordes that once swarmed this land. He made it safe for our kind.
Skyrim.esm|081DE0: This is a simple errand, but the time is right for it to be your Trial. Carry yourself with honor, and you'll become a true Companion.
Skyrim.esm|081DE1: I hope I didn't scare you.
Skyrim.esm|081DE2: There's a fine line between respect and boot-licking, new blood. But I like your spirit. We've decided this will be your Trial. Do well, and you'll be counted among the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|081DE3: Wuuthrad is a relic of Ysgramor. Through that weapon, we trace our line straight to the first harbinger of makind in this land. What fragments we have are displayed in honor, but we always seek more.
Skyrim.esm|081F0E: Clear Namira's sanctuary of the undead.
Skyrim.esm|08256F: Yes? Make it quick, I'm a busy man.
Skyrim.esm|082A10: Did he now? Does he have information about our wonderful discovery?
Skyrim.esm|082A11: This marvelous object. It practically radiates magicka, and yet it's unlike anything I've felt before.
Skyrim.esm|082A12: I just can't seem to tear myself away. Whatever this is, its beauty is like nothing I've ever seen before. If you'd allow me to indulge myself for a moment, I thought I might make a few observations...
Skyrim.esm|082A13: Ah, yes. Well, I'll be sure and stop by the Arcanaeum later.
Skyrim.esm|082A14: Is that the one about something buried beneath Saarthal? Something that men and mer fought over? I'll have to make a point of re-reading it. I don't recall the details.
Skyrim.esm|082A28: Why? Because it's old, and full of dead things? Clearly you've never spent any time in Morrowind.
Skyrim.esm|082A29: No. There's no chance anyone in authority approved this. Our ancestors should be allowed to rest in peace.
Skyrim.esm|082A3E: I'll not go down without a fight!
Skyrim.esm|082A3F: Gods, you'd better have something good up your sleeve. I've got nothing left.
Skyrim.esm|082A40: We never should have brought that thing here...
Skyrim.esm|082A41: I'll be amazed if any of us survive this.
Skyrim.esm|082FB8: We offer this soul to The Revenant: he who watches over the opponents of Arkay and his power over life and death. Fie upon Arkay and his followers!
Skyrim.esm|082FBA: You have to help me, they are going to kill me!
Skyrim.esm|08304B: The Blades have always guarded and guided the Dragonborn. But we'd forgotten why. Now our purpose is clear. I'm all that's left of the Blades, and you're the Dragonborn. Together we're going to stop these dragons, if we have to put every last one of them back in the ground.
Skyrim.esm|08304C: Part of the cost of peace. Emperor Titus Mede saved his Empire at a very high price. A price that included the Blades. The Nords didn't like the peace treaty much either. Thirty years later, the Empire ends up with the Stormcloak rebellion on its hands. No doubt exactly as the Thalmor intended.
Skyrim.esm|08304D: We remember what most don't - that the Dragonborn is the ultimate dragonslayer. You're the only one that can kill a dragon permanently by devouring its soul.
Skyrim.esm|08304E: The dragonstone was a map of ancient dragon burial sites. I've looked at which ones are now empty. The pattern is pretty clear. It seems to be spreading from the southeast, down in the Jeralls near Riften. The one at Kynesgrove is next if the pattern holds.
Skyrim.esm|08304F: The Thalmor happened. They've been hunting down every Blade they can find for the past thirty years. Thanks to the White-Gold Concordat, they've been able to operate throughout the Empire with impunity.
Skyrim.esm|083050: When I was young, our leaders became obsessed with the Thalmor threat. They believed that if and when we found a Dragonborn, we would need to protect her against the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|083052: Something's happening to bring the dragons back to life. And I need you to help me stop it.
Skyrim.esm|083053: The Thalmor. We're very old enemies. And if my suspicions are correct, they might have something to do with the dragons returning. But that isn't important right now. What is important is that you might be Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|083055: And now you have it. No harm done. I knew the Greybeards would send you for the horn if they thought you were Dragonborn. Taking it was the only way I could be sure this wasn't a Thalmor trap.
Skyrim.esm|08306F: You're right. They always get what they want, somehow. Fine. Tell Atar I'll sell.
Skyrim.esm|083070: Someone has to stand up to them. I won't sell.
Skyrim.esm|083071: That I will, stranger. This is more than the Silver-Bloods are paying us. We'll clear out.
Skyrim.esm|083072: Don't think I'm being paid already? Get lost.
Skyrim.esm|083074: Do that and I'll make sure you get a cut.
Skyrim.esm|083075: I'm not from here. I'm a sellsword. These are my men.
Skyrim.esm|083076: Dealing with a mob wouldn't be good for my coin purse. Fine. We're leaving.
Skyrim.esm|083078: My family has owned Karthwasten for generations. Rare for anyone in the Reach that isn't a Nord. Not that it does me any good. Nords think I'm working for the Forsworn. Forsworn think I'm working for the Nords.
Skyrim.esm|083079: You can try, but I'd keep your sword-arm ready if things get ugly.
Skyrim.esm|08307B: Good work. Here's your cut. I guess this finishes up our contract with the Silver-Bloods, so we'll be heading out.
Skyrim.esm|08307D: Easy. I'll sell. Just don't hurt anyone.
Skyrim.esm|08307F: Ainethach needs to get rid of those Silver-Blood. Then maybe we can go back to work.
Skyrim.esm|083080: You should talk to Ainethach. He runs the mines. Used to anyway.
Skyrim.esm|083081: You should talk to Ainethach if you have business with the mine. Not that there's much these days.
Skyrim.esm|083082: Forsworn attacks are bad enough, but those sellswords are worse.
Skyrim.esm|083083: That's more gold than they're offering. Fine. I'll sell.
Skyrim.esm|083085: What do you want? Can't people leave us alone?
Skyrim.esm|083087: Karthwasten has enough problems. Leave us alone.
Skyrim.esm|083088: I work in the mines. I hunt. Everything else I do is my business.
Skyrim.esm|083089: I'm Ainethach's right-hand man. I help supervise the mines.
Skyrim.esm|08308A: You should talk to Ainethach. He's in charge.
Skyrim.esm|08308B: Don't have nothing to say to you, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|08308C: Karthwasten doesn't need outsiders prying in our business.
Skyrim.esm|08308D: We're in control of this mine. No sudden moves.
Skyrim.esm|083090: Whoa. Wait a second. I was just kidding. We'll leave. We'll leave!
Skyrim.esm|083091: I thought today was going to be boring.
Skyrim.esm|083249: Skyrim is the birth place of the Empire. We won't let a bunch of thugs rob her of her lineage.
Skyrim.esm|08324A: General Tullius expects much of me, and thus I expect much of my men.
Skyrim.esm|08324B: In these grave times each Legionnaire must be his better self at all times and under all conditions.
Skyrim.esm|08324C: The Empire is man's last bastion. If Ulfric wasn't blinded by lust for power, he'd see we are on the same side.
Skyrim.esm|08324D: Were the Stormcloaks to prevail in dismantling Imperial rule in Skyrim, who would be her protector? No. We will crush this rebellion. We must do.
Skyrim.esm|08324E: Ulfric would expunge from Skyrim citizens whose only crime was to be born of a non-Nordic woman. That is unacceptable to free men everywhere.
Skyrim.esm|08324F: Every Legionnaire's secret weapon lies in this: knowledge of himself. And like any blade, he must be forged between the hammer and the anvil.
Skyrim.esm|083250: On the coldest of nights, in the steam rising from my breath, I see the many faces of the dead.
Skyrim.esm|083251: We stand out here in the cold, as brothers, warmed by the knowledge that we fight for the rights of every free citizen of the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|083257: Look deeply into the faces of the warriors around you. These are the true sons and daughters of Skyrim. See her strength and nobility in their eyes.
Skyrim.esm|083258: The Empire would have us become slaves to the Thalmor. We are not slaves. We are men. Free men who will slay anyone who begs to differ.
Skyrim.esm|083259: It was anger that brought each of us here, but it is love that keeps us fighting.
Skyrim.esm|08325A: The measure of a man is taken at the moment of his death. And know this for truth, the ghosts of many heroes walk among us.
Skyrim.esm|08325B: One lives for the things he is willing to die for.
Skyrim.esm|08325C: My hatred for the Empire is bested only by love for my countrymen.
Skyrim.esm|08325D: The Emperor would turn Skyrim into a puppet of the Dominion. I for one would not care to dance for the amusement of those foul elves.
Skyrim.esm|08325E: That we have forgotten them, this I fear the gods will remember.
Skyrim.esm|08325F: Before we wage war upon our foes, we must wage a war against our lesser selves. Cleave to your higher voice and with it shatter your foes.
Skyrim.esm|083447: Certainly! It's a fine tune and a good request!
Skyrim.esm|0834EA: Because Tolfdir says so. He says there are things to learn here.
Skyrim.esm|0834EB: Hopefully we can learn something from the experience. How the ancient Nords used magic, maybe even what happened to this place.
Skyrim.esm|0834EC: Not really. Just that it's very, very old. It was one of the first cities of men in Tamriel, I think.
Skyrim.esm|0834ED: Oh, yes. J'zargo hopes to find things that will make him a more powerful mage here. Hopefully small things that fit inside pockets, and will not be noticed if they are missing.
Skyrim.esm|0834FA: For a time he served his purpose, but that time has ended. He uses my gifts for his own amusement, and the only amusement I tolerate is my own.
Skyrim.esm|0834FB: You will find him holed up at Knifepoint Ridge, where he plays king to bandits and highwaymen.
Skyrim.esm|0834FC: You may keep my Ebony Mail, a token of my appreciation to my new champion. Its gifts will resonate with your talents. Now go. I have strings to pull that require my full attention. You may pursue your own course wherever it leads you. Remember always this: As you will it, so it shall be.
Skyrim.esm|0834FD: Kill every single member of his band. Do so as silently and invisibly as you can, for this is not about you. You are to be my instrument in this. Slay him in the coldest of blood. Do not give him the dignity of defending himself. Once his corpse lays cold upon the ground, and all trace of his followers erased, retrieve my Ebony Mail. A gift fit only for my true champion.
Skyrim.esm|083866: If you're looking for anything to sell for a fair price, I run the Pawned Prawn over by the market.
Skyrim.esm|083867: If you're ever in the market, I deal in specialty goods from Morrowind at one of the stands.
Skyrim.esm|083868: Come see me in the market if you're looking for the finest custom jewelry in all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|083A8D: You disappoint me. I expected someone from outside Riften to be different, but I see corruption's reach holds little regard for boundaries. Enjoy the city. It will suit you well.
Skyrim.esm|083A8E: Ah ha! So you're a student of theoretical applied harmonics! Putting aside Ralston's Constant of Universal Inversion for a moment, how would you approach the problem? Draw the harmonic energy into the reagent or allow it to generate its own field?
Skyrim.esm|083A90: That's a shame. Too many people in Riften share your view and look where its getting them. It's going to take more than just myself and Aerin to help this city... it has to want to help itself first.
Skyrim.esm|083A91: You know, I'm beginning to respect you. Other than Aerin, you seem to be the only other person I've met I might be able to trust. Since we share the same opinion of them, let me warn you that they're recruiting others to join their "guild" and you should be cautious.
Skyrim.esm|083A92: It was Aerin who found me dying outside of a Dwemer ruin. If he hadn't brought me here and nursed me back to health, I would have died. When my strength returned and he told me about the problems plaguing Riften, I decided fate brought me here for a reason... so I stayed.
Skyrim.esm|083A93: Astonishing. To think you actually were able to reach it and yet return to tell the tale. Rarely in my travels have I encountered those who possess skills equal to your own. I see I still have much to learn. If you'd permit me, I'd be honored to accompany you in your travels for a time.
Skyrim.esm|083A94: Are you completely insane? Swallow a soul gem? That has to be the most brilliant and unexpected solution I've heard in a long time. It solves all of my problems and keeps the field stable! Now all I need... wait, what were we talking about?
Skyrim.esm|083A95: Of course! Nine is the most basic of magical principles. Oh, how stupid of me. That completely takes care of all your basic problems, magicka burn, dimensional collapse and thermic drift! You've done it! Now, if I can only remember what we were originally talking about.
Skyrim.esm|083A96: The Thieves Guild has been pushing hard to regain a foothold in this city. Rumor has it they're falling apart from the inside.
Skyrim.esm|083A97: Please be careful, I don't wish to be the reason for your death.
Skyrim.esm|083A9C: Calipers? That's utterly ridiculous. Maybe long ago, you could just find calipers in every household across Tamriel, but not anymore. Hold on, you've given me a brilliant idea. Just as calipers hold materials in place, a soul siphon can hold magic fields in place. Genius! And I'm sure you've completely worked out how to counteract complete dimensional collapse, right?
Skyrim.esm|083AA1: The blue thing? But even the simplest harmonic field is composed of entirely... Wait just a moment. The blue thing. Yes, yes, yes! I can see your point. Draw out the necessary energies using a Jarol Apparatus! But wait. What about soul reversal? You must be aware of the consequences when using the apparatus near an open magic field.
Skyrim.esm|083AA2: Ah, you're back. What have you discovered?
Skyrim.esm|083AA3: Well met, friend. How can I help you?
Skyrim.esm|083AA4: You're a stranger here too, eh?
Skyrim.esm|083AA5: Seven. Astonishing. I hadn't considered that. I mean, it would take months... years to work out the differentials. But what a revolutionary concept. Wait, wait. I can see a minor problem in your theory. Without a soul siphon, how would you prevent permanent magicka burn?
Skyrim.esm|083FF7: He requests more barrels of ichor to be delivered to the Arcanex tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|083FF8: Orchendor promised a place where we would be accepted and taken care of.
Skyrim.esm|083FFA: We've been here so long and what do we have to show for it?
Skyrim.esm|083FFB: I'm sorry to wake you, my Brother. I have a request from Orchendor.
Skyrim.esm|083FFC: I know you can't hear me, brother. I don't like what we've become.
Skyrim.esm|083FFD: Keep your eyes and ears open my Brothers and Sisters.  Orchendor needs us to keep vigilant while he convenes with Peryite in the Arcanex.
Skyrim.esm|084000: Yes, yes, as with you. Now be off.
Skyrim.esm|08401B: Yes, show yourself to us. Until that day, we will continue to devote our lives to you and suffer for you.
Skyrim.esm|084055: I know this place will eventually kill us. I have come to accept that. But I will always regret that day I introduced you to Orchendor.
Skyrim.esm|0840B9: Yes, my Brother. Forgive me. May the blessing of Peryite be with you.
Skyrim.esm|0840BA: Yes, a sign. We are lost without your guidance. On the ninth of Rain's Hand our prayers went unanswered, yet here we stand, not faltering in our belief that soon you will show yourself to us.
Skyrim.esm|0840BB: But who am I kidding, I know you wouldn't let me leave, brother. You truly believe in Orchendor and his promises.
Skyrim.esm|0840BC: Now leave me to my sleep. My strength and patience are waning.
Skyrim.esm|0840BD: Yes, a blessing. For many months we have sought your guidance, yet we have heard nothing from you. If we do not please you, give us a sign so that we may understand why.
Skyrim.esm|0840BE: I want out. I want to leave this place. I long for the fresh air of the Reach. This place only makes us sicker.
Skyrim.esm|0840C0: Peryite, heed our call if you deem us worthy. Our leader, Orchendor, has led us here and for that we are thankful. He has shown us that our suffering is not a punishment, but a blessing.
Skyrim.esm|0840C1: Forgive me for saying this, brother, but I have not felt Peryite's presence.
Skyrim.esm|0840C2: This is the reason you wake me? I already have Orchendor's request taken care of.
Skyrim.esm|0840C3: He promised Peryite would be present at all times and give us comfort in our suffering.
Skyrim.esm|084794: The Skyforge? Aye, my clan-fathers have worked it since the first Gray-Manes came to Whiterun. Skyforge Steel is all the Companions will use, for good reason.
Skyrim.esm|084796: Why? Who says I have to be? Skyforge Steel is my art and honor. The Companions need the best, so they come to me.
Skyrim.esm|084797: Battle-Born is a name out of legend, sung in the songs of old and heard in deeds of valor for a hundred generations. "For Clan Battle-Born!" they cry in the mead halls. "First into the fray and last to quit their ale!" We've got honor. Wealth and titles, too. Aye, and buxom women to warm your bed on a cold Skyrim night. Now if that don't answer your question, then no words will.
Skyrim.esm|084798: Uncivilized, pelt-wearing axe-draggers. It's a wonder they don't still live in a cave like those ancestors they keep boasting about. It's true the Vignar Gray-Mane and his lot are one of Whiterun's oldest and, depending on who you ask, most respected clans. I don't dispute that. But it's one thing to take pride in your lineage, and another to cling blindly to the past. What they call honor and tradition, I call ignorance.
Skyrim.esm|084799: Money, you laggard! What else? It always comes down to coin. The Gray-Manes have deep roots in Whiterun, but so do we. Difference is, we're rich, and Vignar hates it. Oh, and they hate it, too. All their big talk of pride and honor, and what have they got to show for it? Beggar's rags and stale bread. We've got the same pride, the same honor, and we've got wealth. No wonder they envy us.
Skyrim.esm|084803: Look, I know the others think I'm a bit daft for sayin' stuff like this, but I'm gonna give it to you straight. Somethin' out there is piss-drunk mad at us. I don't know who or what it is, but it's beyond just you and me. We've been cursed.
Skyrim.esm|084804: Lyin' to my face isn't gonna win you any favors with me, I can promise you that. On the other hand, takin' some of these extra jobs off my hands, now that'll get you to the top of my list.
Skyrim.esm|08483B: You can get out there and start making a name for us again... make them start fearing us like they did long ago. And, while you're at it, make a little bit of coin on the side. Not a bad deal, eh?
Skyrim.esm|084841: I'm not going to sugar-coat it for you. We're in a bad way down here.
Skyrim.esm|084842: Then we understand each other... good. Now, it's time to get your feet wet and I don't want to waste a lot of time talking about anything but business.
Skyrim.esm|084845: Brynjolf, huh? Lemme guess. He plucked you off the street and dropped you into the thick of things without tellin' you which way is up. Am I right?
Skyrim.esm|084847: Simple. Do as you're told and keep your blade clean. We can't turn a profit by killing. You should talk with Delvin Mallory and Vex. They know their way around this place and they'll be able to kick some extra jobs your way. Oh, and talk to Tonilia in the Flagon... she'll set you up with your new armor.
Skyrim.esm|084848: Look around you. The Flagon, the Guild... it's all fallin' apart. A few decades ago, this place was as busy as the Imperial City. Now, you're lucky if you don't trip over a skeever instead.
Skyrim.esm|084849: Who knows. Old Delvin thinks it's some kind of a curse. I think he's crazy. If you want my opinion I say it's just plain old bad luck.
Skyrim.esm|08484A: Now you I like. I've been getting sick of the pushovers that usually walk in here. I think you and I are going to get along just fine. Now, it's time to get your feet wet and I don't want to waste a lot of time talking about anything but business.
Skyrim.esm|0848EC: Before we begin, I want to make two things perfectly clear. One, I'm the best infiltrator this rathole of a Guild's got, so if you think you're here to replace me, you're dead wrong. And two, you follow my lead and do exactly as I say... no questions, no excuses.
Skyrim.esm|0848ED: Welcome to the family, lad. I'm expecting you to make us a lot of coin, so don't disappoint me.
Skyrim.esm|0848EE: Welcome to the family, lass. I'm expecting you to make us a lot of coin, so don't disappoint me.
Skyrim.esm|08497B: Please. You're in the presence of a born huntsman. I can hear the sweat sliding down your forehead.
Skyrim.esm|08497C: I have my ways, but it's not easy these days. The Blackblood Marauders have been plundering ships off the coast. A lot of people been hurt by their foolishness.
Skyrim.esm|084983: Oh, very brisk. When the snow sets in, even the hardiest Nord needs some spiced wine to warm the blood.
Skyrim.esm|084985: Down at the docks. There are a few good spots, if you can avoid the East Empire Company workers and those Red Wave thugs.
Skyrim.esm|084987: Well, as it turns out, I had a pet skeever when I was a boy and he used to wink.
Skyrim.esm|0849AF: Radiant Raiment has the finest attire in all of Skyrim. That is how we get business.
Skyrim.esm|0849EF: Nothing at all. It's very... nice. If you want something with a little more style, however, you should see what we have available.
Skyrim.esm|084A1B: Ah... To be honest, no. But High King Torygg supported them, and now her lady Elisif has thrown in with them. You could say I'm a King's man at heart. Hmph. I guess that's "Jarl's man" now. My loyalty lies with Solitude. So where Jarl Elisif leads, I follow.
Skyrim.esm|084AB3: I'll tell you what we do. We spit in that curse's face and turn things around down here. Put things back the way they were. That's where you come in. I've got plenty of work available that could guide us down the road to recovery. All you need to do is ask and we can both come out of this smellin' like a rose.
Skyrim.esm|084AB4: See, that kind of attitude comes from someone who wants to get rich and stay alive long enough to enjoy it. We're goin' to get along nicely. So, if you've got the nerve, I've got plenty of extra jobs to help get the Guild back on its feet.
Skyrim.esm|084ABA: Easy, huh? So, should I just hand you purses of coin or are you going to work to earn your keep? Let me give you a small bit of advice. Nothing in this line of work is easy. If it was, every dreg in the Ratway would be robbing Riften blind. If something's being handed to you on a silver platter, then there's a catch... steal the platter instead. You get it?
Skyrim.esm|084B89: I'm afraid there's not much to be said. Winterhold is hardly what it used to be. You can talk to Birna if you need to buy anything. She doesn't have much, but could certainly use the coin. And then there's our little inn. Most of the business we get is from folks here to visit the College, though even that's dried up some.
Skyrim.esm|084D06: It's a school that teaches music, poetry, that sort of thing. Most bards in Skyrim have been to the college at some point. If you're thinking of applying, talk to Viarmo in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|084D07: That's about the truth of it. Write tales about the sights I've seen. The battles I've fought. Earned quite a name for myself when I was younger. The Bards College still teaches some of my work to their students.
Skyrim.esm|084D6B: Welcome. Look around. We've got everything you could want in life. At least... that's what Beirand always tells me.
Skyrim.esm|084D6C: Beirand's my husband. He works the forge up at Castle Dour. I barely see him nowadays, what with him filling orders for the army.
Skyrim.esm|084EF9: Fruit, fresh off the boat! The freshest in Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|084F19: Get your fresh fruit and produce here!
Skyrim.esm|084F23: Apples, tomatoes, leeks and cabbages! Fresh and ripe!
Skyrim.esm|084F30: Fresh Fish! Straight from the Fjords!
Skyrim.esm|084F4D: The finest fish, here! Caught daily!
Skyrim.esm|084F59: Spiced Wine to warm the blood!
Skyrim.esm|084F5D: Keep the cold at bay with our spiced wine!
Skyrim.esm|084F5E: Spiced wine, the taste of home and hearth!
Skyrim.esm|08509E: Oh, master. It's your time to do with as you please, but what about those who depend on you so much?
Skyrim.esm|0850A4: Tendrils of thought may wind their way through this realm, but those tendrils bind our reality together.
Skyrim.esm|0850A5: A method to your madness and your madness is the method and the melody...
Skyrim.esm|0850A8: I found the bone! I've brought it to you but still I am refused...
Skyrim.esm|0851FD: It is done. The defiler is defeated. Take Dawnbreaker from its pedestal.
Skyrim.esm|085497: The glimmer of life is gone...
Skyrim.esm|085499: Hmm? Does someone live among the dead?
Skyrim.esm|08549B: Time to end your foolish charade!
Skyrim.esm|08549C: Mortal fool! No one hides from the dead!
Skyrim.esm|08549E: Gone. The living are right to fear the dead.
Skyrim.esm|08549F: There is no more life here.
Skyrim.esm|0854A6: I still sense your presence, living fool...
Skyrim.esm|0854A7: ...retire some day... get myself an island...
Skyrim.esm|0854A8: ...never enough gold... just need one good haul...
Skyrim.esm|0854A9: ...might pay off my bounty this time... walk into the city a free man...
Skyrim.esm|0854AA: ..told him to just hand over the gold, but did he listen? Oh, no... they always have to fight back...
Skyrim.esm|0854AB: ...I'll never do another span in that stinking jail... rather die than give myself up...
Skyrim.esm|0854AC: ...he he he... two threes? What was he thinking? Fool never saw me pull that ace out of my boot...
Skyrim.esm|0854AD: ...need me some skooma... the imported stuff... just one last time...
Skyrim.esm|0854B4: I'm going to... put you down!
Skyrim.esm|0854B5: Ha! You can never truly stop me!
Skyrim.esm|0854B6: Ugh! Only a fool tries to kill the dead!
Skyrim.esm|0854BD: By Ysmir, you won't leave here alive!
Skyrim.esm|0854BE: Don't just stand there! Kill her!
Skyrim.esm|0854BF: Well well. Another maggot to squash beneath my boot.
Skyrim.esm|0854C3: Such an abrupt end to our game.
Skyrim.esm|0854C5: Ohh... but I will have your blood...
Skyrim.esm|0854C6: You'll be so much easier to rob when you're dead...
Skyrim.esm|0854C7: You cannot even conceive of the power I wield...
Skyrim.esm|0854C8: Come. Come and face the Forsworn...
Skyrim.esm|0854C9: Soon you will know the cold of death...
Skyrim.esm|0854CA: The endless sleep comes for us all...
Skyrim.esm|0854CF: Lookee here. Looks like we got ourselves a hero.
Skyrim.esm|0854D0: You picked a bad time to get lost, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0854D4: All the living shall fear the dead!
Skyrim.esm|0854D5: Ugh! That it? That your best?
Skyrim.esm|0854D6: By Ysmir, you'll pay for that!
Skyrim.esm|0854D8: Agh! I been wounded worse by better!
Skyrim.esm|0854D9: Ha! I've suffered worse during training!
Skyrim.esm|0854DA: That's it? That's all you've got?
Skyrim.esm|0854F0: Their aim could be steadier, but they're battle ready.
Skyrim.esm|0854F1: Perhaps, but necessary. My cousin, Fura, is somewhere out there in Skyrim right now, fighting Ulfric Stormcloak and his damned insurrectionists. She wouldn't have to be there at all, if it weren't for Roggvir.
Skyrim.esm|0854F2: I shouldn't be. Mama says if I do, people are going to ask about uncle Roggvir. Maybe if I don't talk, they won't either.
Skyrim.esm|0854F3: And why wouldn't I be? We sing tales of kings, queens and their politics, 'tis true. But do you know who really makes history? The person who writes it.
Skyrim.esm|0854F4: Thank you. I'm still at the beginning of my lessons but Inge... Dean Six Fingers... says I'm quite good with the flute.
Skyrim.esm|0854F5: Aye. I'm the headsman here in Solitude. It's my job.
Skyrim.esm|0854F6: She still mourns greatly for her husband, High King Torygg. So she leaves many of the courtly details to me.
Skyrim.esm|0854F7: All my life. I practically grew up in the Hall. My father and mother were the priest and priestess of Arkay before me, you see. I believe I was put here to protect this place from the darkness that pervades Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0854F8: Aye, but I'll be here in Solitude for longer still. Fihada's skill as a fletcher drew me to this place. I wanted to learn. And there is still much of Skyrim I wish to see. I hear there is a city, Wind-Helm, that is nearly white with... What do you call it? Ah yes! Snow. And far to the west lies a ruin. Volskygge. Inside, there is said to be a wall, black as night. And it speaks! In the ancient Dragon tongue! There are wonders in this place I never dreamed imaginable.
Skyrim.esm|0854F9: What? You see a man fallen on hard times, and you think he's been like that forever? I was there. I fought in the Great War. I was on the field at Anvil. And when I was struck down, they left me. You understand? They left me for dead. I didn't leave them.
Skyrim.esm|0854FA: Then you didn't know Roggvir, and you have no idea what it means to be a Nord of Skyrim! He did the honorable thing, even when he knew it would cost him everything. My brother was the best man in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0854FB: I'm a Thane. In the future, you may want to gain that information before talking with someone. Risk of looking like an idiot, and all that.
Skyrim.esm|0854FC: Yes. My older brother and the person you probably want to be speaking to right now. But really, since Torygg's death, Falk Firebeard wields the true power of the Jarl's court. My brother is nothing more than a pompous little fop.
Skyrim.esm|0854FD: I make sure Erikur keeps breathing.
Skyrim.esm|085504: This little adventure of yours has cost me a number of test subjects. I need more, and you'll do nicely.
Skyrim.esm|085506: I think I can teach you a thing or two.
Skyrim.esm|085507: I think I can teach you a thing or two.
Skyrim.esm|085508: I can certainly share a bit of what I know.
Skyrim.esm|0855F0: I thought it high time I caught up with you.
Skyrim.esm|0855F1: Hold on, my boy. Hold on!
Skyrim.esm|0855F2: Hold on, my dear. Hold on!
Skyrim.esm|0855F3: I'm just doing what I'm told.
Skyrim.esm|0855F4: Gods, it's cold. They'd better find something good in there to make this worthwhile.
Skyrim.esm|0855F5: As long as the College keeps paying, I'll keep digging.
Skyrim.esm|0855F6: For thousand-year old ruins, they're not much to look at.
Skyrim.esm|0855F7: Can't imagine what the College expects to find in here.
Skyrim.esm|0855F8: I've never seen anything like this in Nordic ruins before. Why, just look at all these coffins! This bears closer inspection. I'd like to stay a while and examine this. You, however, should press on. See if you can find whatever this vision of yours mentioned. But if it is truly dangerous, be careful. Go on ahead. I'll be sure and catch up with you before long.
Skyrim.esm|0858C8: Oh come now, this is mead... not some wine to be sipped and savored.
Skyrim.esm|0858CB: Then this is beyond coincidence. First Aringoth and now Sabjorn. Someone's trying to take us down by driving a wedge between Maven and the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0858CC: You... you're in charge here until I can sort this all out.
Skyrim.esm|0858CD: Look, I assure you, this is all just a huge misunderstanding!
Skyrim.esm|0858CE: Shor's Bones! What... what's in this?
Skyrim.esm|0858CF: Sabjorn keeps that locked up tight. If you can get through that way, go right ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0858D1: I trust you have good news for me.
Skyrim.esm|0858D2: Why aren't you at Honningbrew Meadery?
Skyrim.esm|0858D3: Shouldn't you be on your way to Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|0858D4: Out with it. What happened at the meadery?
Skyrim.esm|0858D5: I'm certain Maven wishes you to be in Whiterun, not wasting time here.
Skyrim.esm|085C4C: Talk to me again when you've cleaned up your mess.
Skyrim.esm|085C4D: Come on, now! I want those arrows dead center! The lives of your fellow soldiers are counting on it.
Skyrim.esm|085C4E: Concentrate. The battlefield is full of distractions. Ignore them! Focus on your targets.
Skyrim.esm|085C4F: Some day soon, you will all see battle. And what you do here, today, may well save your lives!
Skyrim.esm|085D2F: Okay, okay, I'll tell you. I get my skooma from Sarthis Idren. He has some sort of a setup over at the Riften Warehouse. You can't get inside though. They've kept that place locked up tight since the war began.
Skyrim.esm|085D31: Excellent. Here, this is the key to the warehouse. See if you can drop in on this dealer of poison and convince him to close down his operation permanently.
Skyrim.esm|085D32: Good. Here, take the key to the warehouse. Pay dear old Sarthis a visit and tell him Maven sends her regards, would you?
Skyrim.esm|085D34: You can't threaten me any worse than they have!
Skyrim.esm|085D35: Yes, I'm afraid we're aware of Sarthis's presence in the warehouse. Unfortunately, we're certain he has informants within the city guard. Every time we've made a move to arrest him, he's escaped. However, if you'd take care of this discreetly, you might be able to surprise him without warning.
Skyrim.esm|085D36: Ah yes, Sarthis... a thorn in my side for the last few years. I've tried repeatedly to eradicate that meddler, but he has ears within the city guard. Gives us the slip every time we raid the warehouse. However, if you'd wish to eliminate that burden for me... well, let's just say it would go a long way with earning my favor.
Skyrim.esm|085D38: It's got nothing to do with the coin... this is my life we're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|085D39: I overheard Bolli say that only the Jarl carries the key to the warehouse. When I meet Sarthis there, he's usually waiting for me outside with his bodyguard.
Skyrim.esm|085D3A: If it wasn't for skooma, I'd already be on my way out of this horrible city. All my gold... completely gone. Now I have to start over. I'll never use skooma again! Although I suppose a little mead now and then would be harmless...
Skyrim.esm|085D3D: Look, I don't think I should say. I mean, they could kill me!
Skyrim.esm|085D3E: Hm. Well, if you change your mind, come see me again.
Skyrim.esm|085D3F: Let me give you a word of advice. Think about your choices carefully from now on. You'll go much farther in Riften with me on your side.
Skyrim.esm|085DF1: I can't say I'm happy with your decision. If you change your mind, let me know.
Skyrim.esm|085DF2: I'm very disappointed. I'd advise you to rethink that decision soon.
Skyrim.esm|085DF3: Good. Report back to me when your task is complete.
Skyrim.esm|085DF4: Good. Make sure not one of them escapes alive.
Skyrim.esm|085DF5: Then there's no time to lose. It won't take long for Sarthis's associates to learn of his death. The source of the poison must be destroyed once and for all, otherwise another dealer will simply take his place. Do this task for me and you'll be well on your way to a title in Riften.
Skyrim.esm|085DF6: Then I want you to head out there immediately and eliminate all of them. I can't afford to have another Sarthis blunder into Riften because they think we're too lazy to disrupt their pitiful operation. Do this task for me and you'll be well on your way to a title in Riften.
Skyrim.esm|085DF7: It's nice to receive good news for a change. Did you recover anything else from the warehouse worth mentioning?
Skyrim.esm|085DF8: Results speak volumes over words and you've just proved it. Did you happen to discover where the fetcher was getting his skooma from?
Skyrim.esm|085FBC: I should say so. I'd be careful in the future before you confront someone without all of the facts!
Skyrim.esm|085FBD: Come on, what are trying to do? Insult me?
Skyrim.esm|08649A: I knew the Greybeards would send you there if they thought you were Dragonborn. They're nothing if not predictable. When you showed up here, I knew you were the one the Greybeards sent, and not some Thalmor plant.
Skyrim.esm|08649C: I know they are. I've visited their ancient burial mounds and found them empty. And I've figured out where the next one will come back to life. We're going to go there, and you're going to kill that dragon. If we succeed, I'll tell you anything you want to know.
Skyrim.esm|08649E: Exactly. Nobody even remembers our name these days. We used to be known across Tamriel as the protectors of the Septim Emperors. Those days are long gone, though. For the last two hundred years, we've been searching for the next Dragonborn to guide and guard, as we are sworn to do. But we never found one. Until now.
Skyrim.esm|086786: I can't wait to see Sabjorn squirm.
Skyrim.esm|086787: You're supposed to be at the meadery.
Skyrim.esm|08681C: Start changing it over to the Black-Briar Meadery West as soon as possible. That was Maven's part of the deal. She's put me in charge of keeping the mead flowing, so that's exactly what I'm going to do. If you're in the area and you ever need anything fenced, you just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|08682C: What is there to know? It's in the temple, you bring it out. You bring it to me, I pay you. Now get moving.
Skyrim.esm|08682D: My little girl. Always strong. Do you know where you have to go?
Skyrim.esm|08682F: I'm to go to the temple in Markarth. I... I'll have to leave you and mother.
Skyrim.esm|086830: You're... probably right. Just hurry, please. Who knows what those things are doing to her.
Skyrim.esm|086833: Let's get to the Forsworn camp. Her life could be in danger.
Skyrim.esm|086834: Let's find Fjotra. This place gives me the creeps.
Skyrim.esm|086835: It's our honor to have you there.
Skyrim.esm|086836: Fjotra, dear. Did they hurt you?
Skyrim.esm|086837: This man is going to take you to Markarth. Are you ready?
Skyrim.esm|086838: This woman is going to take you to Markarth. Are you ready?
Skyrim.esm|086839: Of course not. My spirit remains strong.
Skyrim.esm|086852: You were not invited here. You are not welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|086854: Fine. Let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|086855: We're here to arrange a temporary truce to allow the Dragonborn here to deal with the dragons. Nothing more.
Skyrim.esm|086856: I just want to make clear that the only reason I agreed to attend this council was to deal with the dragon menace.
Skyrim.esm|086857: We have as much right to be at this council as all of you. More, actually, since we were the ones that put the Dragonborn on this path.
Skyrim.esm|08685A: Who would like to open the negotiations?
Skyrim.esm|08685C: We have no need to negotiate when the rebels are so clearly losing the war.
Skyrim.esm|08685D: So. Arngeir, is it? You know why we're here. Are you going to let us in or not?
Skyrim.esm|08685E: Delphine, we're not here to rehearse old grudges.
Skyrim.esm|086864: Enough posturing, Tullius. If you came to talk, let's get on with it.
Skyrim.esm|086865: She did what had to be done. It was long overdue. My only regret is that I wasn't able to kill him myself.
Skyrim.esm|086866: He did what had to be done. It was long overdue. My only regret is that I wasn't able to kill him myself.
Skyrim.esm|086867: For now. The Blades' memory is long, as you know.
Skyrim.esm|086868: If it were up to you, the Dragonborn would sit dreaming on this mountain doing nothing!
Skyrim.esm|086869: The matter at hand is urgent. Alduin must be stopped. You wouldn't have called this council if you didn't agree. We know a great deal about the situation and the threat that Alduin poses to us all. You need us here if you want this council to succeed.
Skyrim.esm|08686A: I know what path you set her on. And now Paarthurnax's wisdom is lost to us, just when we need it most.
Skyrim.esm|08686B: I know what path you set him on. And now Paarthurnax's wisdom is lost to us, just when we need it most.
Skyrim.esm|08686C: We know what path you've set her on. But she has made a different choice. Paarthurnax is still safe from your malice.
Skyrim.esm|08686D: We know what path you've set him on. But he has made a different choice. Paarthurnax is still safe from your malice.
Skyrim.esm|08686E: Were you? The hubris of the Blades truly knows no bounds.
Skyrim.esm|08686F: We want compensation for the massacre at Barleydark Farm.
Skyrim.esm|086870: We want compensation for the massacre at Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|086871: We want compensation for the massacre at Rorikstead.
Skyrim.esm|086872: We want compensation for the massacre at Karthwasten.
Skyrim.esm|086873: We want compensation for the massacre at Old Hroldan.
Skyrim.esm|086874: We want compensation for the massacre at Kolskeggr Mine.
Skyrim.esm|086875: We want compensation for the massacre at Stonehills.
Skyrim.esm|086876: We want compensation for the massacre at Frost River Farm.
Skyrim.esm|086877: We want compensation for the massacre at Granite Hill.
Skyrim.esm|08687A: We want compensation for the massacre at Half-moon Mill.
Skyrim.esm|08687B: We want compensation for the massacre at Heljarchen.
Skyrim.esm|08687C: We want compensation for the massacre at Anga's Mill.
Skyrim.esm|08687D: We want compensation for the massacre at Loreius Farm.
Skyrim.esm|08687E: We want compensation for the massacre at Whistling Mine.
Skyrim.esm|08687F: We want compensation for the massacre at Ivarstead.
Skyrim.esm|086880: We want compensation for the massacre at Heartwood Mill.
Skyrim.esm|086881: We want compensation for the massacre at Sarethi Farm.
Skyrim.esm|086905: You can't just leave me here!
Skyrim.esm|086906: Fie. We beseech you. Darken this gem so that we may use its power to restore The Order of the Black Worm.
Skyrim.esm|086909: Grant us this power so that in your name we may vanquish our enemies.
Skyrim.esm|086C9C: Yes, yes. Which is why I allowed this... violation of all our traditions.
Skyrim.esm|086C9E: We want control of Markarth. That's our price for agreeing to a truce.
Skyrim.esm|086C9F: But regrets are pointless. Here we are. Take your seat at the council table and let us see what wisdom we can find among these warriors of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|086CA0: In exchange for Riften, the jewel of Skyrim? Hardly. Whiterun seems like a better choice to me. From there we can threaten to undermine the whole Imperial position.
Skyrim.esm|086CA1: In exchange for Riften, the jewel of Skyrim? Hardly. How about the Reach? Gaining control of the silver mines would be invaluable.
Skyrim.esm|086CA3: Besides, Jarl Laila will never agree to...
Skyrim.esm|086CA4: Peace? I doubt it. They may put their weapons down for a moment, but only to gather strength for the next bloodletting. They are not yet tired of war. Far from it. Do you know the ancient Nord word for war? "Season unending"... so it has proved.
Skyrim.esm|086CA5: I'm glad you agree. I was starting to wonder whose side you were on here.
Skyrim.esm|086CA6: Well. Good! You favor the Empire less than I feared. Maybe we will be able to come to some agreement here after all.
Skyrim.esm|086CA7: I should not have agreed to host this council. The Greybeards have no business involving ourselves in such matters.
Skyrim.esm|086CA8: I did ask. Although I'm beginning to wonder whose side you're really on. Still...
Skyrim.esm|086CA9: Fair enough. I thought you might be able to see past your Imperial loyalties in the interests of peace, but I guess not. Still...
Skyrim.esm|086CAA: Galmar. We will do whatever I decide is in the best interests of Skyrim. Are we clear?
Skyrim.esm|086CAB: Better than nothing, I guess. But Tullius will need to offer a lot more if he wants me to give up Riften without a fight.
Skyrim.esm|086CAD: Hjaalmarch would allow us to directly threaten the Imperial supply lines out of Solitude...
Skyrim.esm|086CAE: Blocking Pale Pass would cut one of the main Imperial supply routes... and Whiterun is dangerously exposed with the enemy in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|086CAF: Hmm... the Empire is bringing in a lot of supplies through Dawnstar. Taking the Pale would stretch them pretty thin...
Skyrim.esm|086CB0: I've never liked having the Imperials right on Windhelm's doorstep. Retaking Winterhold would help secure our supply lines...
Skyrim.esm|086CB1: You should be glad enough of a chance to lick your wounds at this point in the war.
Skyrim.esm|086CB2: So, you've done it. The men of violence are gathered here, in these halls whose very stones are dedicated to peace.
Skyrim.esm|086CB6: It has its advantages. I've never liked having the Empire perched on my doorstep. And Riften would be easy to hold...
Skyrim.esm|086CB7: Yes. With Markarth in our hands, we'll be able to raid the Imperial supply lines out of Solitude...
Skyrim.esm|086CBC: You're right. Tullius isn't in any position to ask for Riften.
Skyrim.esm|086CBD: Not quite worth handing over Riften, but not a bad starting point.
Skyrim.esm|086CC3: This council was your idea, eh? So tell us, what do you think Riften is worth?
Skyrim.esm|086DCC: Have you killed them all yet?
Skyrim.esm|086DCD: Then we've no time to lose. Follow me!
Skyrim.esm|086ED4: You haven't been able to take it back yet. Why should we give it up now?
Skyrim.esm|086ED5: I'm sure General Tullius does not expect something for nothing.
Skyrim.esm|086ED7: Jarl Ulfric, what would you want in return?
Skyrim.esm|086ED9: You heard what she said, Tullius.
Skyrim.esm|086EDA: Of course not! What will you offer for Riften, Tullius? Some empty promises? Maybe some more Imperial bluster?
Skyrim.esm|086EDB: Aye! We haven't forgotten Markarth, Tullius!
Skyrim.esm|086EDC: Riften must be returned to Imperial control. That's our price for agreeing to a truce.
Skyrim.esm|086EDD: But I accepted the Dragonborn's invitation to this council, and I at least will negotiate in good faith.
Skyrim.esm|086EDE: Ulfric, you can't be taking this demand seriously? We can hold Riften against anything the Empire can throw at it.
Skyrim.esm|086EDF: Riften is off the table. The Empire barely has a toehold left in Skyrim. We don't need to pay such a high price for this truce.
Skyrim.esm|086EE0: We've made you a fair offer. If you're really serious about this truce, you need to reconsider.
Skyrim.esm|086EE2: First, let me be clear. The sons of Skyrim know from bitter experience that talking to the Empire is a waste of time, or worse. Imperial promises are simply a way to lull you while they prepare a dagger for your back.
Skyrim.esm|086EE3: Elisif, are you really going to be a party to this foolishness? Do you really think Ulfric will just turn Riften over because some Imperial general barked an order?
Skyrim.esm|086EE4: That's quite an opening demand, Tullius.
Skyrim.esm|086EE5: Come on, Tullius, you can't seriously expect us to just hand over Riften at the negotiating table.
Skyrim.esm|086EEA: I expected better of you, Dragonborn. You swore an oath to serve the Emperor. Don't forget it.
Skyrim.esm|086EEB: I don't blame you, Dragonborn. You made the best of a bad situation.
Skyrim.esm|086EEC: You disappoint me, Dragonborn. I accepted your invitation to this council, despite your known association with the rebels. But it seems you have no intention of dealing fairly with me.
Skyrim.esm|086EED: I don't blame you, Dragonborn. You treated us fairly despite your known rebel sympathies.
Skyrim.esm|0870BB: Wh... What? A crime? Me? Preposterous! Poor Cicero is just standing here! Cicero loves order. Oh yes! He would never disrespect the law!
Skyrim.esm|0870BC: All right, so Cicero attacked that harlot, Astrid! But what's a fool to do, when his mother is slandered and mocked? Surely the Listener understands!
Skyrim.esm|0870BD: If it's any comfort, I do feel slightly bad about Veezara. Stupid lizard got in my way! But please tell me that hulking sheepdog has bled to death.
Skyrim.esm|0870BE: Cicero admits, he thought the Listener would be dead by now. Heh. Maybe we could just forget all this? Hmm? Let bygones be bygones? What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|0870BF: Damn that Loreius. Oh, how he will pay for this treachery...
Skyrim.esm|0870C0: You there! Fool! Hold fast. You've been accused of a crime against the laws of this land. What say you in your defense?
Skyrim.esm|0870C1: And now we come to the end of our play. The grand finale.
Skyrim.esm|0870C2: You look like a crazy man to me. And what's in that coffin, hmm? Maybe some time in a cell will help loosen that lying tongue.
Skyrim.esm|0870C3: Oh, but this isn't at all what Mother would want. You kill the Keeper or I kill the Listener? Now that's madness.
Skyrim.esm|0870C4: Listener! Is that you? Oh, I knew you'd come. Send the best to defeat the best. Astrid knew her stupid wolf couldn't slay sly Cicero.
Skyrim.esm|0870C5: Brrr! Chilly! You'll enjoy this. Not an original part of the Sanctuary, per se. Let's call it a "forced addition." Forced by what? Oh, come and see!
Skyrim.esm|0870C6: You're... still alive. Cicero respects the Listener's abilities, of course, but could you at least slow down a bit? I'm not what I used to be. Heh.
Skyrim.esm|0870C7: Oh thank you, kind stranger. Thank you for helping Cicero and his poor, stranded mother.
Skyrim.esm|0870C8: He he. That'll teach the little loony.
Skyrim.esm|0870C9: A jester. In these parts. Man must be completely off his tree.
Skyrim.esm|0870CB: Ouch! Pointy pointy! My home is well defended. I always have been a stickler for details. Get it? "Stick-ler." Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Oh, I slay me!
Skyrim.esm|0870EE: Another trap, most likely. Things are dangerous... three of the guards died when the lower hall flooded with steam last week.
Skyrim.esm|0870F0: Better them than us. We lost three good men to the traps up there, and for what- just to see another damn spider clanking around?
Skyrim.esm|0870F3: I... oh, all right. If there is a thief, he won't leave this tower alive. But shouldn't we inform Master Calcelmo?
Skyrim.esm|0870F4: Well, at least this post is safe enough. Come on, let's get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|0870F6: Everyone, fall back! Run for it!
Skyrim.esm|0870F9: You heard me, captain- our work must not be disturbed. Kill any intruders on sight. Call for backup if you need it. But let no one through.
Skyrim.esm|0870FE: What a waste! There must be a dozen men up there!
Skyrim.esm|0870FF: What do you think this is?
Skyrim.esm|087104: Yeah. Calcelmo called in his entire expedition team- said he needed every man he could get to protect his research.
Skyrim.esm|087158: Hmm. These doors seem to be the only thing left between me and the treasure.
Skyrim.esm|087159: There'd better be. I didn't come all this way to leave empty handed.
Skyrim.esm|08715A: There must be a way to activate these doors deeper in the ruins. Here, take this key. It should open the doors on either side of this chamber.
Skyrim.esm|08715B: At last, the treasure is mine! All mine!
Skyrim.esm|0871B1: You'll never see the sun again, you hear me? No one escapes Cidhna Mine. No one.
Skyrim.esm|0871BA: We warned you, but you just had to go and cause trouble. Now we have to pin all these recent murders on you. Silence witnesses. Work. Work. Work.
Skyrim.esm|0874F3: He's proud of his service, Skald. The Legion taught us loyalty. And we're loyal to Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0874F5: Your man Horik is wearing his old Legion armor. What should I make of that?
Skyrim.esm|0875C9: This is our homeland, Tullius. All the blood spilled in this war is on your head.
Skyrim.esm|0875CA: Come see me in Windhelm when this is all done. There's always work for those willing to fight for Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0875CB: Don't listen to him. Ulfric's been telling lies about himself for so long, he's actually starting to believe them.
Skyrim.esm|0875CC: You've disgraced yourself and our cause. You are no longer fit to be known as a son of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0875CD: You've disgraced yourself and our cause. You are no longer fit to be known as a daughter of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0875CE: Come see me in Solitude when this is all done. The Emperor can always use another good soldier.
Skyrim.esm|0875CF: Well said! You may be fighting on the wrong side in this war.
Skyrim.esm|0875D2: You made your decision. Now live with it.
Skyrim.esm|0875D3: If you think that winning a few battles means you're winning the war, you're even worse at strategy than diplomacy.
Skyrim.esm|0875D6: What? Have you forgotten your oath to the Emperor?
Skyrim.esm|0875D7: As I expected, you favor your friends.
Skyrim.esm|0875D8: Jarl Baalgruf, I assume you are familiar with the Dragonborn's plan?
Skyrim.esm|0875D9: This is quite an achievement, Dragonborn. And General Tullius. Riften is ours!
Skyrim.esm|0875DA: I admit, I really didn't expect you'd be able to arrange a truce. Getting Ulfric to agree to anything at all is quite an achievement.
Skyrim.esm|0875DB: I know how this works, Tullius. I've learned the value of Imperial promises the hard way. Never again.
Skyrim.esm|0875DC: Your actions here were a disgrace. You betrayed Skyrim at every turn.
Skyrim.esm|0875DD: You... you disappointed me. You gave too much away. More than was necessary to secure this truce.
Skyrim.esm|0875DE: Very honorable, I'm sure, but you should not put your own honor above the freedom of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0875E0: Excellent! You aren't the first to have a change of heart, after seeing what Ulfric's reckless pride is doing to Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0875E1: Shor's bones, where will these demands end?
Skyrim.esm|0875E3: I know you, Ulfric. You have no intention of coming to terms.
Skyrim.esm|0875E4: Welcome, sister! I'm glad that your eyes have finally been opened to the Empire's true nature.
Skyrim.esm|0875E5: I'm sure that will be a great comfort to the families of your comrades who died to free the land you just gave back to the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0875E6: You should think harder on your commitment to Skyrim's freedom next time you're deciding what's right.
Skyrim.esm|0875E7: I think you may have joined up with the wrong side, Dragonborn. You showed yourself a true daughter of Skyrim here today.
Skyrim.esm|0875E8: You're losing this war and you know it.
Skyrim.esm|0875E9: Well that's an excellent question. You haven't overlooked that little detail, have you?
Skyrim.esm|0875EC: I'm impressed at your even-handed treatment. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0875ED: I knew I could count on you.
Skyrim.esm|0875EF: I'm sorry to hear that. I thought you'd finally seen through Ulfric's lies.
Skyrim.esm|0875F0: I'm willing to let the Dragonborn decide the fairness of my request.
Skyrim.esm|0875F1: I guess I have no choice but to let the Dragonborn decide. Although I'm starting to doubt your fairness.
Skyrim.esm|0875F3: Now, back to the matter at hand.
Skyrim.esm|0875F4: I suppose that's the fairest deal we're likely to get.
Skyrim.esm|0875F6: Spoken like a true daughter of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|0875F7: You slaughtered the very people you claim to be fighting for! True sons of Skyrim would never do such things.
Skyrim.esm|0875F8: You're here to blacken the Empire's name, and I won't be party to it.
Skyrim.esm|0875F9: Come on Galmar. We have a lot of work to do.
Skyrim.esm|0875FA: If we're not going to get Riften, we need you to give us something.
Skyrim.esm|0875FB: You know as well as I do that that we can't hand over Markarth on these terms.
Skyrim.esm|0875FC: You know I can't agree to these terms. The Emperor would repudiate this treaty and I would be recalled.
Skyrim.esm|0875FD: Don't hand me a mug of sheep's piss and call it Colovian brandy. These terms are still not acceptable.
Skyrim.esm|0875FE: Come on, Ulfric. You have to give a little ground here. The dragons are a threat to us both.
Skyrim.esm|087601: Damned Imperial lies! My men would never stoop to such methods, even in retaliation for your butchery at...
Skyrim.esm|087602: Just say the word, and my men will help you spring this trap.
Skyrim.esm|087604: I should have known better than to expect you to give us a fair hearing.
Skyrim.esm|087605: Why would I give you that, if I wouldn't give you Riften?
Skyrim.esm|087606: What next, Tullius? Shall I just hand over all of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|087607: For what it's worth, I'm sorry to see you go. You fought well for us.
Skyrim.esm|087608: Ulfric showed his true colors today, eh? Don't worry. You aren't the first to be taken in by his self-serving lies.
Skyrim.esm|087609: I think you may have joined the wrong side. You showed yourself to be a true friend to the Empire today.
Skyrim.esm|08760A: We get Riften back. Now we'll see if there's anything behind your fine words.
Skyrim.esm|087613: What? And you call yourself a daughter of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|087614: But the difficulty remains - how to lure a dragon to Dragonsreach at all?
Skyrim.esm|087615: But I can see now that this is not a negotiation at all.
Skyrim.esm|087616: If you think this war is nearly won, you're as deluded as your Emperor when he signed away our freedom to the Thalmor. You can trample us underfoot but we will rise again!
Skyrim.esm|087617: If you think you can hold Markarth, you're as deluded as your Emperor when he signed away our freedom to the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|087618: If you think we'll just hand over Riften, you're as deluded as your Emperor when he signed away our freedom to the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|087619: Soon enough I'll have you back under the headsman's axe, and this time there won't be any dragon to save you.
Skyrim.esm|08761A: As always, the Empire's fine words are worth nothing!
Skyrim.esm|08761C: You heard what she said, Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|08761D: You did the best you could, brother. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
Skyrim.esm|08761E: You did the best you could, sister. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
Skyrim.esm|08761F: As for you, General Tullius, I see now that Galmar was right. Talking to the Empire is just as useless as ever.
Skyrim.esm|087620: Skyrim will never again bow to your false Empire!
Skyrim.esm|087621: Let's go, Galmar. I should have listened to you in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|087622: You always were a fool, Ulfric. You're no better at diplomacy than you are on the battlefield.
Skyrim.esm|087623: You've been even-handed so far. What do you say to our demand?
Skyrim.esm|087624: So, Dragonborn. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|087625: Ah. I believe I can be of help here.
Skyrim.esm|087626: Yes, I'm ready to do my part.
Skyrim.esm|087627: You may think this war is nearly won. You're wrong. I've been fighting you with a mere handful of troops. Once the Emperor is convinced of the need, then you will see the full strength of the Imperial Legion.
Skyrim.esm|087628: If you think you can hold Riften, you're as deluded as you've always been.
Skyrim.esm|08762C: We want compensation for the massacre at Barleydark Farm.
Skyrim.esm|08762D: We want compensation for the massacre at Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|08762E: We want compensation for the massacre at Rorikstead.
Skyrim.esm|08762F: We want compensation for the massacre at Karthwasten.
Skyrim.esm|087630: We want compensation for the massacre at Old Hroldan.
