Skyrim.esm|087631: We want compensation for the massacre at Kolskeggr Mine.
Skyrim.esm|087632: We want compensation for the massacre at Stonehills.
Skyrim.esm|087633: We want compensation for the massacre at Frost River Farm.
Skyrim.esm|087634: We want compensation for the massacre at Granite Hill.
Skyrim.esm|087637: We want compensation for the massacre at Half-moon Mill.
Skyrim.esm|087638: We want compensation for the massacre at Heljarchen.
Skyrim.esm|087639: We want compensation for the massacre at Anga's Mill.
Skyrim.esm|08763A: We want compensation for the massacre at Loreius Farm.
Skyrim.esm|08763B: We want compensation for the massacre at Whistling Mine.
Skyrim.esm|08763C: We want compensation for the massacre at Ivarstead.
Skyrim.esm|08763D: We want compensation for the massacre at Shor's Stone.
Skyrim.esm|08763E: We want compensation for the massacre at Heartwood Mill.
Skyrim.esm|08763F: We want compensation for the massacre at Sarethi Farm.
Skyrim.esm|087640: We want Hjaalmarch back, and Idgrod Ravencrone restored as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|087641: You withdraw from Falkreath, and allow Siddgeir to resume his rightful place as Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|087642: We want the Pale returned to Imperial control. The traitor Skald the Elder will be replaced with a more loyal candidate.
Skyrim.esm|087643: We want Kraldar removed as Jarl of Winterhold, and an Imperial candidate put in his place.
Skyrim.esm|087644: Never mind. I can see that these are the best terms we'll get from this council. So be it. The Empire at least puts the greater good above our own interests.
Skyrim.esm|087645: Think carefully where your loyalties lie... sister.
Skyrim.esm|0876C2: All right men, spread out and search the tower. No telling who- or what- we're dealing with here.
Skyrim.esm|0877D9: I'll deal with my uncle. Just... go! Scour this place from top to bottom!
Skyrim.esm|0877DA: Yes, I'm positive I heard something.
Skyrim.esm|087906: Not a single man or woman in Dawnstar has gotten a good night's rest in days. We keep having the same nightmare, over and over. If people aren't tired, they're angry, and if they aren't angry, they're afraid. It's made keeping order a mess. I hear Mara has sent one of her priests to the inn to try to calm people down, but I don't know how much words will do now.
Skyrim.esm|087907: That's right. Dawnstar. My Dawnstar is plagued with nightmares. I haven't slept properly in days. That priest of Mara who came here before you says the Divines will cure us. Well, until they do, I don't have any business to discuss with outsiders.
Skyrim.esm|087909: You haven't heard? All of Dawnstar hasn't had a decent night's rest in days. People are having nightmares they swear are real. They go to bed, but they wake up more tired than yesterday. I've heard that a priest of Mara has come, saying that the Divines will lift this curse somehow, but that remains to be seen.
Skyrim.esm|08790A: Nightmares. I haven't met a single man or woman who doesn't admit to having them. A bad dream I can handle, but everyone having the same bad dream, every night? It's a curse, I say. I hear there's a priest of Mara visiting the inn. Maybe the Divines will cure us.
Skyrim.esm|08790B: It's these nightmares. Me and every one of my workers can't catch a nod without having one. They're almost... real. They say a priest of Mara has arrived in town. We'll see if faith can cure this curse.
Skyrim.esm|08790D: New in town? You're lucky. Dreams aren't affecting travelers. Everyone else though.... They're horrible. Imagine dying over and over again in your dreams night after night. They say a priest of Mara is visiting the Windpeak Inn. Says the Divines will take care of everything. We'll see about that.
Skyrim.esm|08790E: Everyone here is having nightmares. I have miners tripping over their own feet from lack of sleep. I heard there was a Priest of Mara who was supposed to be looking into it. We'll see if anything comes of that.
Skyrim.esm|08790F: The Jarl is... tired. Be mindful of that.
Skyrim.esm|087911: Kyne's guardian troll awaits you. Complete this trial and you'll have proven yourself a true hunter.
Skyrim.esm|087912: Then you finally know what it means to be a true hunter, in the Nord way. For this you have earned the Blessing of Kyne, and can count me as a friend.
Skyrim.esm|087932: May you walk on warm sands.
Skyrim.esm|087936: What do you want, little elf?
Skyrim.esm|087941: That's no small amount of coin, friend. I suppose I can come with you for a short venture. Lead on.
Skyrim.esm|087948: Treasure, you say? I suppose the Jarl won't mind if I'm gone for a little while. Lead on.
Skyrim.esm|08794C: They say that something happened in the Hall of the Dead. Brother Verulus has the whole place closed.
Skyrim.esm|087956: You've done it. The shrine is ours again. Now we need to prepare a grand feast to welcome you to Namira's coven. You will have the honor of bringing a fresh kill for the main course. And I know the perfect person.
Skyrim.esm|087957: You are looking for Arkay's protection while you delve some dank tomb, I take it? My duties keep me busy in Markarth. I don't know if I can help you.
Skyrim.esm|087959: I think you need to calm down before I summon the guards.
Skyrim.esm|08795A: Soon, Namira's blessing will be yours.
Skyrim.esm|08795E: We will sate the hunger together.
Skyrim.esm|08795F: Don't fear the unknown, the hidden, the wretched.
Skyrim.esm|087960: Bring Verulus here. The Lady of Decay will do the rest.
Skyrim.esm|08796F: Is it? This is disappointing. J'zargo hoped there might be one who could keep pace with his accomplishments.
Skyrim.esm|087971: I am an advisor to the Arch-Mage. The Thalmor wish to promote relations with your College. I am at the Arch-Mage's disposal if he requires advice.
Skyrim.esm|08797D: Ah, this is good. But J'zargo will know them first. It is good to finally be around mages who may be able to keep up with J'zargo.
Skyrim.esm|087986: Another new Apprentice, I see. Are you of the sort that believe you're here to change the world? Or are you only in it for yourself? I assure you that I shall be watching you -- all of you -- very closely.
Skyrim.esm|08798C: You... you have? Already? Well, J'zargo is surprised. And pleased! There is real competition here. This is good.
Skyrim.esm|08798D: You are new, yes? Have you mastered the Expert level destruction spells yet?
Skyrim.esm|08798E: He didn't like the Arch-Mage. Hated dealing with him, hated having to answer to him. Falion said he hated what this place was becoming. So he just left. Aren found a note on his desk, and that was it. I understand he settled down in Hjaalmarch, and his presence there caused something of a problem.
Skyrim.esm|087990: Not within society, no. Many who practice within the school of Conjuration do so on their own, secretly. Falion is the only one I know who is public about what he does. He was once my teacher here, but left the College years ago.
Skyrim.esm|087991: Perhaps not as often as he should. In time, I believe he will be more trusting.
Skyrim.esm|087992: There is much for J'zargo to be sure of. There is skill in magic, there is charm, and there is a strong will. J'zargo will be successful, of this there is no doubt.
Skyrim.esm|087993: Now that stability has been established between the Dominion and the Empire, we seek only to help Skyrim. To guide its people through this time of transition, to help lead them to a better future.
Skyrim.esm|087994: I have many important things on my mind. Your concerns are not among them.
Skyrim.esm|087995: Are you quite sure we need to be speaking?
Skyrim.esm|087996: I'm disappointed to say I've learned little during my time here.
Skyrim.esm|087997: I had hoped your scholars would be on a level comparable with my own colleagues. They are not.
Skyrim.esm|08799B: Oh, but you are wrong. The only reason you could disagree is because you are losing so badly you cannot see it.
Skyrim.esm|087B55: I need to get back to Markarth. Here, take this. It's the least I can do for saving my life.
Skyrim.esm|087B56: Thank you. I don't know what's going on, but I think I owe you my life.
Skyrim.esm|087B58: Sweet Breath of Arkay, were these the cannibals that were plaguing the Hall of the Dead?
Skyrim.esm|087B5A: Don't worry, Arkay's protection is absolute.
Skyrim.esm|087B5B: Don't worry, the Eight are watching over us. I hope.
Skyrim.esm|087B5D: Let me speak to our guest.
Skyrim.esm|087B6D: You must speak with the Farmhand at Chillfurrow farm.
Skyrim.esm|087B6F: The Dawnstar Sanctuary? That where you lot are holing up in now? Hmm... Tell you what. It'll cost you - a lot - but I can help you out. What exactly are we talking about here?
Skyrim.esm|087B7B: So you're the... the assassin? I need you to kill the Visiting Noble at Dragonsreach, in Whiterun. Here's the gold. I... I hope it's enough.
Skyrim.esm|087B7C: So you're the one from the... Dark Brotherhood? I need you to kill the Big Laborer at Katla's Farm. Here's the gold for the kill. Make it painful.
Skyrim.esm|087B7D: You must be the... assassin. I need you to kill the Itinerant Lumberjack, in Morthal, at the logging camp. Here, this is all the gold I have.
Skyrim.esm|087B7E: You're the assassin, right? Look, there's this Reckless Mage in the Frozen Hearth Inn, in Winterhold. I want him dead. This gold should cover it.
Skyrim.esm|087B7F: You're the one? From the... the Dark Brotherhood? The man I need killed is a Seasoned Hunter. He's just outside Falkreath. Here's the gold...
Skyrim.esm|087B80: You're here! I was wondering if that Black Sacrament even worked. I need you to kill a woman, a Poor Fishwife, on the Riften Docks. Here's the gold.
Skyrim.esm|087B81: Dark Brotherhood? You look the type. Listen, there's this Grim Shieldmaiden walking the streets of Markarth. Kill her. Now take this gold, and go.
Skyrim.esm|087B82: Are you the assassin? From the...? Well, there's this Traveling Dignitary, in Solitude. I need her... you know. This gold will cover it, right?
Skyrim.esm|087B83: I'm glad you're here. I need you to kill a Coldhearted Gravedigger, tends to work in the Hall of the Dead in Windhelm. This is all the gold I have.
Skyrim.esm|087B84: Good, you're here. Look, in Ivarstead, there's this Beautiful Barbarian. I need her dead. Don't ask why. Just do it. This gold should cover it.
Skyrim.esm|087B85: Would it kill you to let a man go about his business in peace? Really, the nerve...
Skyrim.esm|087B86: Look, I'm busy. Please go bother somebody else.
Skyrim.esm|087B87: Did you want something? I'm quite busy.
Skyrim.esm|087B88: I'm dying for a rest. I swear, I could lie down right now, and just sleep forever.
Skyrim.esm|087FD6: All right, you want to go?
Skyrim.esm|088738: Yes, let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|08873A: We're here to arrange a temporary truce to allow the Dragonborn here to deal with the dragons. Nothing more.
Skyrim.esm|08873C: The only reason I agreed to attend this council was to deal with the dragon menace.
Skyrim.esm|08873D: I'm glad we agree on this.
Skyrim.esm|08873E: I have no authority to negotiate a permanent settlement, unless Ulfric is ready to sue for peace and turn himself over to Imperial justice.
Skyrim.esm|08873F: The sons of Skyrim will live up to their agreements. As long as the Imperials hold to theirs.
Skyrim.esm|088740: We have nothing to say to each other. Paarthurnax's murder is beyond my powers of forgiveness.
Skyrim.esm|088741: Master Arngeir, are you going to just let him continue to...
Skyrim.esm|088743: Maybe so, but bringing her here is a deliberate provocation. We need to show Tullius we won't be pushed around.
Skyrim.esm|088744: You should be glad enough of a chance to lick your wounds at this point in the war.
Skyrim.esm|088745: I consider even talking to the Empire a generous gesture.
Skyrim.esm|088746: Hmm. It feels like a mistake to me, but I'll bow to your judgment on this.
Skyrim.esm|088747: We have no interest in negotiating with the Empire. It's only a matter of time before they are driven out of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|088748: There's nothing else to talk about, unless the Empire is finally ready to renounce its unjust claim to rule over the free people of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|08874A: I have something to say first.
Skyrim.esm|08874B: The Empire can live with these terms, yes. For a temporary truce, until the dragon menace is dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|08874C: We're off to a good start.
Skyrim.esm|08874D: Are you done? Did you just come here to make speeches? Or can we get down to business?
Skyrim.esm|08874E: Is everyone here? Let's get this started.
Skyrim.esm|08874F: Don't worry, I'll come to this peace council of yours.
Skyrim.esm|088750: I never thought I'd ever willingly stand in the same room with Tullius again. At least not without a weapon in my hand.
Skyrim.esm|088751: I'll come to your council. But I doubt any good will come of it.
Skyrim.esm|088752: When has any good ever come from talking to the Empire?
Skyrim.esm|088753: I'm glad I finally got a chance to see this place.
Skyrim.esm|088754: If you can arrange an end to the fighting, Dragonsreach is at your disposal.
Skyrim.esm|088756: If you can arrange an end to the fighting, Dragonsreach is at your disposal.
Skyrim.esm|088757: I understand this council was your idea, Dragonborn. I'm afraid I don't hold out much hope for success.
Skyrim.esm|088758: We should get started. Alduin is only getting stronger.
Skyrim.esm|088759: We must make them see their danger, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|08875A: We consider even sitting down to talk with these rebels more than generous.
Skyrim.esm|08875B: You're not supposed to be in here.
Skyrim.esm|08875C: I knew he wouldn't be able to resist.
Skyrim.esm|08893E: I know it's a bit strange. Not exactly a name to bring comfort to the sick and ailing who come to buy a poultice or salve. But what you must understand about Falkreath is that our town is defined, for better or wrose, by the large and ancient cemetery here. That's why the inn is called Dead Man's Drink, the farm is called Corpselight Farm, and so on. I suppose it's sort of a running joke.
Skyrim.esm|088947: For one thing, I won't empty Falkreath's coffers to buy myself fine clothes an' expensive mead. For another, I won't get mixed up with criminals and other sorts who take advantage of the honest folk for profit. I might be old, but I haven't forgotten that a Jarl's first duty is to look after his people.
Skyrim.esm|088948: You think some Emperor sitting on a gilded throne in Cyrodiil is going to know what's best for Skyrim? The Imperial City's so far from here, it might as well be on one of the moons. And yet the Empire thinks it can tell us what to do an' how to live. I'm no man's fool. I know Ulfric Stormcloak's selfish and power-hungry, but he's the devil I know. Does that put it plain enough for you?
Skyrim.esm|088949: I was once, but not any more. I was wounded in a skirmish up near Windhelm. This was years ago, mind you. Now, maybe I don't pick up a blade in Ulfric's name no more, but I still hold with his ideals. Skyrim is Nord land, and we ought to rule it. We don't need no help from the Empire on that count.
Skyrim.esm|08894A: His name's Bolund. He's the one who works the lumber mill down by the river. If you meet him, don't expect much in the way o' conversation. He ain't one for talking, especially with folk he don't know.
Skyrim.esm|08894B: Comes down to trust, I expect. After Dengeir gave up his seat to his nephew, he didn't trust anyone. Everywhere he looks, he sees assassins, spies an' plots against him. I'm the only one he confides in. So, when he was made Jarl again, he didn't wait a moment to name me his Steward. He said I was the only choice.
Skyrim.esm|088960: I was Dengier's personal guard for years. I risked my hide more than once to protect him. Why would I do that? For money? Because I swore an oath? No, it was because he was a good man and a true friend. A true Nord places loyalty and honor above all else.
Skyrim.esm|088965: Well, my family back in Hammerfell didn't approve of my interest in lethal poisons and death in general. So I left, and wandered north. When I found this town, with its huge cemetery, I felt right at home. I opened the shop and I've never looked back. This is where I belong.
Skyrim.esm|088966: Only a hearty soul travels the road these days.
Skyrim.esm|088968: Be mindful. My maid Tekla has been known to pass information to that pompous nephew of mine, Siddgeir.
Skyrim.esm|088969: My nephew has always been handed things, even the title of Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|08896A: Try not to judge Falkreath too harshly, visitor - the war has been hard on us.
Skyrim.esm|08896B: Aye, not so long ago. Some'll tell you I stepped down on account of my old age. Truth is, I found out about all the Imperial spies crawling around Falkreath. A few well-placed bribes, and they bought themselves some friends. Suddenly the nobles are calling for a new Jarl, and all the while their pockets are jingling with the sound of Imperial septims. They named me Thane, and they got my nephew Siddgeir in my place. A real friend to Cyrodiil, that one.
Skyrim.esm|088986: You're the one who put out the fire in the Lighthouse, right?
Skyrim.esm|088989: For once you'll actually pay for your crimes.
Skyrim.esm|08898A: Once again, the Empire takes the blame for the crimes of others.
Skyrim.esm|08898B: We're ready, Dragonborn. Just say the word.
Skyrim.esm|08898C: I'd give a week's pay to see the look on Ulfric's face again, when he had to swallow the terms of the truce.
Skyrim.esm|08898D: I hope this truce gives you what you need. It won't last.
Skyrim.esm|08898E: As soon as I get back to Whiterun, I'll get my men ready.
Skyrim.esm|08898F: When I return to Whiterun, I'll get my men ready to help trap this dragon.
Skyrim.esm|088992: The look on Tullius's face while the Greybeard read the terms was worth the trip.
Skyrim.esm|088993: I don't much like this truce. Feels like a betrayal to me.
Skyrim.esm|088994: We're counting on you to make this all worthwhile.
Skyrim.esm|088995: I sacrificed a lot here today for this plan of yours.
Skyrim.esm|088996: That could have gone worse. Now it's up to you to deliver.
Skyrim.esm|088997: You'd better be able to deliver, Dragonborn. I sacrificed a lot here today.
Skyrim.esm|088998: It's good to be back in High Hrothgar after all these years.
Skyrim.esm|088999: I'd just as soon get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|08899A: You've done well, to even get the two of them in the same room.
Skyrim.esm|088C71: That was good work. Deeja's in the hold of the ship. Go down two levels.
Skyrim.esm|089423: Mortal. I am Namira, the Lady of Decay. Your consumption of the blood and bile of Arkay's own is pleasing to me. I give you my ring. Wear it, and when you feast on the flesh of the dead, I will grant you my power.
Skyrim.esm|089424: Wallow in your wretchedness, my newest champion.
Skyrim.esm|089425: Your mockery will be the end of you. And on that day, another servant will feast on your entrails and discover a ring clenched in her teeth. Be grateful for what time I give you until then.
Skyrim.esm|08942A: Don't be shy. You should be the one to carve.
Skyrim.esm|08942C: It's an honor to see you, the keeper of Namira's ring.
Skyrim.esm|08942D: You're one of Namira's faithful now.
Skyrim.esm|0896A7: Listen, when you're ready to do something useful, find me outside Haemar's Shame, in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|089947: I'll see you at Northwatch Keep, friend.
Skyrim.esm|089948: Of course. I should've known. Avulstein was behind this, wasn't he?
Skyrim.esm|089949: Of course. I should've known. This was all Avulstein's idea, wasn't it? Where is he?
Skyrim.esm|08994A: I cannot stay. Not here, not in Whiterun. It likely won't be safe for Avulstein, either. Our best hope now is to fall in with the ranks of the Stormcloaks. But would you do me a favor? Tell my mother to suffer the winter's cold wind, for it bears aloft next summer's seeds. She'll know what it means. I thank you again, friend. You've given me my life back.
Skyrim.esm|08994B: I don't dare return to Whiterun. But my mother... She'll need some consolation. Tell her... tell her to suffer the winter's cold wind, for it bears aloft next summer's seeds. She'll understand. Though shall mourn my brother, I thank you, friend. You've given me my life back.
Skyrim.esm|08994C: A wise decision, but I fear he may not be safe there anymore. They'll go looking for me, and Whiterun will be the first place they search.
Skyrim.esm|08994D: So he might still be in there? I don't dare go back in now. I'll just have to hope that in the confusion he can slip out.
Skyrim.esm|08994E: Avulstein... you stupid boy... What is my freedom worth, if the price is my brother's life?
Skyrim.esm|089982: Yes, the incense. Fetch for me a deathbell flower, one silver ingot, a flawless ruby, and some vampire dust. Then I will show you how.
Skyrim.esm|089983: Ah, a wanderer, yes? No? Pilgrim, perhaps? Have you come to commune with Peryite, Taskmaster and blighted Lord?
Skyrim.esm|089984: He is the pus in the wound. Oh, proper ones curl their noses, but it's pus that drinks foul humors and restores the blood. I worship Peryite, yes, because sometimes the world can only be cleansed by disease.
Skyrim.esm|089985: Not everyone has the stomach required to entreat my Lord. But Kesh likes you, friend. There is a way Peryite may speak to us who will take Him in. If you wish to commune with Him, we'll need the incense.
Skyrim.esm|0899B5: My guards told me. Poor Margret. And witnesses report that the attacker was shouting some nonsense about being a Forsworn. I want to make things clear. Markarth has its troubles, but there are no Forsworn in this city. They're only a threat in the hills and along the roads, where they live. Everywhere else, we are in control.
Skyrim.esm|0899B7: It's unfortunate that we live in such violent times, but there you have it. Now I've been told that the attacker claimed to be a Forsworn. It's nonsense. More likely he was just sick in the head. So don't concern yourself with it. Markarth is safe. You have my word.
Skyrim.esm|089A8B: Astrid did the right thing, and now it's your turn. End her suffering.
Skyrim.esm|089BA4: I suppose it would be inappropriate of me to ask how you obtained this, so I simply won't. A rubbing, eh? Odd. I expected notes.
Skyrim.esm|089BA5: I undertstand. Now, let me take a good look at this. Over here, please.
Skyrim.esm|08A1F7: If I am to die today, so be it. But you'll not soon forget the Dark Brotherhood.
Skyrim.esm|08A917: Oh, excuse me, milady. I'll be glad to sell you an elixir after I'm done with these fine people.
Skyrim.esm|08AF48: Hmm. Yes, it is quite sad. A young boy, Aventus Aretino, lost his mother recently, and was sent to an orphanage. But he has returned home, and people have heard strange chanting. They say it's the Black Sacrament, the ritual to contact the Dark Brotherhood. Why a little boy would want to contact a group of murderers is beyond me, but he is inviting evil into this city.
Skyrim.esm|08AF49: Oh, Grimvar... always with the nonsense. No, no, of course not. Those are just tales...
Skyrim.esm|08AF4A: Fine. Then I'll invite him out to play. He lives right there. I'm going to knock on his door...
Skyrim.esm|08AF4B: Then it's true, what everyone is saying? That Aventus Aretino is doing the Black Sacrament? Trying to summon the Dark Brotherhood?
Skyrim.esm|08AF4C: All right. I won't deny it, child. What you heard is true. But Aventus Aretino walks a dark path. His actions can lead only to ruin.
Skyrim.esm|08AF4E: Ha! Then I'm right. I knew it. He's trying to have somebody killed!
Skyrim.esm|08AF50: No, child! Wait! That boy, that house - they're cursed.
Skyrim.esm|08AF9E: You mean before he died? We had a break in. Thieves didn't touch anything on the shelves, or any coin, just one thing. A solid gold ornament, in the shape of a dragon's claw.
Skyrim.esm|08AF9F: Thanks for bringing the claw back.
Skyrim.esm|08AFA0: Thanks for bringing the claw back.
Skyrim.esm|08AFA1: You could? I've got some coin coming in from my last shipment. It's yours if you bring my claw back. If you're going after those thieves, you should head to Bleak Falls Barrow, northeast of town.
Skyrim.esm|08AFA2: You could? I've got some coin coming in from my last shipment. It's yours if you bring my claw back.
Skyrim.esm|08AFA7: Lucan's dead. I guess I'm in charge of the Riverwood Trader now.
Skyrim.esm|08AFA8: I guess Lucan will never see the golden claw again now.
Skyrim.esm|08AFA9: Thanks. I guess this was the least you could do after what happened to Camilla.
Skyrim.esm|08AFAA: Thanks. I only wish Camilla was here to see it. Here, take this. You've done a good thing for the Riverwood Trader.
Skyrim.esm|08AFAB: Thanks. I guess this was the least you could do after what happened to Lucan.
Skyrim.esm|08AFAC: Thanks. I only wish Lucan was here to see this again. Take this. You've done a good thing for the Riverwood Trader.
Skyrim.esm|08AFB8: They don't have to be perfect. Just send them over when they're ready.
Skyrim.esm|08AFB9: I can only hammer so fast, Gerdur.
Skyrim.esm|08B008: I try to keep Grimvar safe, but these are dangerous times.
Skyrim.esm|08B00C: Now. Enough. We will speak no more of this. I am the only friend you need.
Skyrim.esm|08B594: Faralda can teach Destruction spells, and offers training in that school.
Skyrim.esm|08B596: Oh my, that's quite a difficult question. Never underestimate the ability to influence others. Whether they're friends or foes, they're almost all susceptible to Illusion spells. They can turn the tide of a battle in a moment's notice. There are also spells that help in avoiding conflict altogether. Invisibility is tricky to master, but exceptionally useful.
Skyrim.esm|08B597: Well, that depends on what you're looking for.
Skyrim.esm|08B598: It's lies. All of it. I don't care what she told you, it's all lies.
Skyrim.esm|08B599: But you can hear me, obviously. Something still isn't quite right. At least I'm making progress.
Skyrim.esm|08B59A: Confound it all. I was quite sure I was invisible, or at least transparent.
Skyrim.esm|08B59B: Well, look at the evidence. Nords generally don't trust magic, so it's not off to a good start. Throw in the Oblivion Crisis, which was caused by magic-users, and the troubles now with the Aldmeri Dominion, who are elves and magic users. And finally take the fact that the College is the only thing left standing after most of Winterhold was destroyed. It's all fairly damning.
Skyrim.esm|08B5A6: Not at all. I consider it a blessing. My family was convinced coming here was a death sentence, or worse. It took years of insisting that this is what I'm meant to do.
Skyrim.esm|08B5A7: And finally, there's Colette. She may be... difficult to get along with, she's very knowledgeable when it comes to Restoration magic.
Skyrim.esm|08B5B2: Magic is shunned by most. If it can't be swung over your head and used to crack skulls, most Nords want nothing to do with it. Magic is seen as something for elves, and weaker races.
Skyrim.esm|08B5B6: If you can find him and focus his attention, there's a great deal Drevis can teach you about Illusion magic.
Skyrim.esm|08B5D7: Faralda. Don't act like she didn't say anything. I know her, I know what she's been up to.
Skyrim.esm|08B5D8: Don't let Tolfdir fool you; he's the pre-eminent scholar on Alteration. One of the best in Tamriel, and always willing to pass on his knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|08B5D9: Phinis is one of the best Conjurers in all of Skyrim, and can help with spells from that school.
Skyrim.esm|08B5DA: Certainly. Destruction spells come in different forms, each useful for various purposes. Concentration spells can be cast immediately, though they're weaker. Good for a tight spot, and less costly if you miss. You'll also need to decide whether to devote yourself to a single type of spell. Focusing solely on flame spells will make them more powerful, but you may find yourself at a disadvantage against foes immune to fire.
Skyrim.esm|08B5E1: And here you go... your payment, just as I promised. The way things have been going around here, it's a relief that our plan went off without a hitch.
Skyrim.esm|08B5E2: I didn't think you'd get pinched. Can't say I'm surprised with the way things have been going around here.
Skyrim.esm|08B5EA: Mutual advantage! Any fool can see that. For centuries, we have benefited from the Empire's protection, and prosperous trade with the south. In exchange, the people of Cyrodiil sleep peacefully, knowing their northern border is guarded by the fiercest warriors in all Tamriel.
Skyrim.esm|08B636: Not at all. I consider it a blessing. My family was convinced coming here was a death sentence, or worse. It took years of insisting that this is what I'm meant to do.
Skyrim.esm|08B637: Mages in Cyrodiil are all about politics. The Synod and the College of Whispers are too busy guarding secrets to bother to teach. Skyrim was not J'zargo's first choice, but Winterhold is removed from politics, dedicated to study. This is the place for J'zargo to become great.
Skyrim.esm|08B638: So far, if only because no one is telling me how great I ought to be. I just want to learn, I don't want to think about what's expected of me.
Skyrim.esm|08B643: Well, there are three of them. Arranged in three tiers, as you may have noticed. The Plains District is so named because it's the closest of the three to the plains that surround the city. The inn and marketplace are found there. Most of the city's residents live in the Wind District, which is the middle of the three. Dragonsreach occupies the highest, which we call the Cloud District. From here, the Jarl can see all that goes on within his city.
Skyrim.esm|08B6D8: We got the mill up and running before we found out that the big stand of trees on the hill was sacred. Now we can't cut them, and can't afford to buy another mill somewhere else. So we scrounge whatever we can from around the tundra.
Skyrim.esm|08B6D9: A lifetime of study, you might say. The best comes from Riften, but I haven't been there in years. Ever tried making your own? It's... not easy. Trust me.
Skyrim.esm|08B6DA: Those falls... dangerous place. Derkeethus thought he could find some good fish in the pools. Haven't heard from him in a long while.
Skyrim.esm|08B6DC: Not much to it, really. We fish, and keep to ourselves. Try to keep from upsetting the Empire or the Stormcloaks, and so far the war's left us alone.
Skyrim.esm|08B6DD: A bunch of lazy rabble trying to make a living. Can't get better workers in because everyone's afraid of the tomb up above the mine. Dead dragons... it hasn't moved since Ysgramor and it's not going to start now. Superstitious milk drinkers.
Skyrim.esm|08B6DE: So it seems the milk drinkers were right. That dragon was nearly the death of us. All we can do now is rebuild.
Skyrim.esm|08B6DF: I'm lucky enough that none of my friends have been killed. Of course we have soldiers coming through all the time.
Skyrim.esm|08BAC3: We love you, Grelod. Thank you for your kindness.
Skyrim.esm|08BAC4: Now, what do you all say?
Skyrim.esm|08BAC6: We love you, Grelod. Thank you for your kindness.
Skyrim.esm|08BAC7: And one more thing! I will hear no more talk of adoptions! None of you riff-raff is getting adopted. Ever! Nobody needs you, nobody wants you.
Skyrim.esm|08BAC8: We love you, Grelod. Thank you for your kindness.
Skyrim.esm|08BAC9: Those who shirk their duties will get an extra beating. Do I make myself clear?
Skyrim.esm|08BACA: That's better. Now scurry off, my little guttersnipes.
Skyrim.esm|08BACB: We love you, Grelod. Thank you for your kindness.
Skyrim.esm|08BACF: That, my darlings, is why you're here. Why you will always be here, until the day you come of age and get thrown into that wide, horrible world.
Skyrim.esm|08BBCA: Aventus Aretino. He was sent to live here after his mother died. He escaped. And... Before he left, he said he was going to do the Black Sacrament. You know, to call the Dark Brotherhood. So they'd come and kill old Grelod! Word out on the street is that he went home to Windhelm, and people have heard strange chanting coming from his house. He's really doing it!
Skyrim.esm|08BBCB: A little boy who lived here. Aventus Aretino. He was nice, but really quiet, and sad, because his mother just died. He ran away. Back home to Windhelm. Samuel told me he's trying to get some murderer people to come here and kill Grelod. I really hope he does.
Skyrim.esm|08BBCC: Oh, Aventus Aretino. He was sent here when his mother died, but he ran away. I heard he went back to his house in Windhelm, and is trying to get the Dark Brotherhood to come and murder Grelod. That would be so wonderful.
Skyrim.esm|08BBCD: Aventus Aretino? He only lived here for awhile. I think his mother got sick and died or something. Anyway, he's gone now. He ran away. But Samuel told me he's back home in Windhelm, and is doing some kind of dark ritual. He's trying to summon some killers to come get old Grelod.
Skyrim.esm|08BBCE: Please, mister. You got to get me out of here. Ever since Aventus left, Grelod's been even meaner than usual. I swear, she's going to kill one of us.
Skyrim.esm|08BBCF: Please, lady. You got to get me out of here. Ever since Aventus left, Grelod's been even meaner than usual. I swear, she's going to kill one of us.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD0: I used to hate it here, but it's not so bad anymore. Constance is real good to us.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD1: It's too bad the orphanage ain't letting us kids get adopted. I'd be the best son ever.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD2: I was named after the sound a lion makes. You know: rrroooaaaarr! Really.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD3: I can't believe it! Grelod is dead! She's really dead!
Skyrim.esm|08BBD4: My parents dropped me off here a year ago. But they're coming back to get me. They said so.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD5: You look kind of scary. But nobody is scarier than old Grelod.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD6: Constance Michel says we have to be careful of people we don't know.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD7: I'm really afraid of Grelod. When Aventus escaped, she was so mad. I got double the beatings that day.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD8: I hate it here. Grelod the Kind is the meanest person in all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|08BBD9: Kill one person, and you can solve so many problems. I wonder at the possibilities.
Skyrim.esm|08BBDA: I'm strong, and quick, and can do any chore you want. Come on, adopt me. Please?
Skyrim.esm|08BBDB: Come to get yourself a kid? You're out of luck. No way Grelod will let any of us get adopted. Especially not since Aventus took off.
Skyrim.esm|08BBDD: Constance don't care much for strangers in the orphanage. Afraid someone might hurt one of us kids. Which, I gotta admit, is kind of nice.
Skyrim.esm|08BBDE: Why's someone like you want to hang around a place like this?
Skyrim.esm|08BBE0: Grelod ain't going to like some stranger just hangin' around. Not that I care.
Skyrim.esm|08C458: I'll be at the market come morning. You better be there too.
Skyrim.esm|08C459: I'm ready when you are. Just give the word.
Skyrim.esm|08CA5C: You should try that bit on the barkeep. Maybe get yourself a free drink.
Skyrim.esm|08CA5E: That amount should allow me to readily acquire a drink.
Skyrim.esm|08CA81: Such a kind nibble you are.
Skyrim.esm|08CACF: We have lived in Skyrim since before the Nords came. Our way is simple, all work to make the tribe strong. Only the bravest are allowed to lead, and to marry. A great chief is able to attract many wives and raise bold children. Wise women like myself are mothers of chieftains. We guide the tribe and advice our kin on what is most pleasing to Malacath.
Skyrim.esm|08CBB7: He is our creator and master. The Daedric Lord of the Sworn Oath and the Bloody Curse. Malacath teaches us to honor all words with action. To demand blood from your enemies and strength from your allies. He watches over all Orcs as his children. A truth those who leave the stronghold are quick to forget.
Skyrim.esm|08CBB8: It is said that an Orc first learns to wield a hammer in her mother's womb. By tradition, a mother always teaches her children how to smith. If a chief has a second wife, she is called the "Forge-wife" in honor of this.
Skyrim.esm|08CBBD: The Code of Malacath. It is both tradition and law in the strongholds. Break the code, and you must pay Blood Price. The code is not written anywhere. It is known and carried on by the Orcs, and all who live in the strongholds follow it. For an outlander, following it is simple. Do not steal or attack anyone in the stronghold, or be ready to pay in gold or blood.
Skyrim.esm|08CBE3: Thank you! Thank you! I'll never speak of this to anyone, I promise!
Skyrim.esm|08CBE9: Well it's about damn time. Capturing a woman from her home, keeping her bound like an animal. Ridiculous...
Skyrim.esm|08CBEB: Ah, you see? That wasn't so hard, now was it? Let's the two of us just forget we ever met, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|08CBED: Please! Release me! Cut my bonds!
Skyrim.esm|08CBEF: You cut these bindings this minute! You hear me? You cut these bindings right now!
Skyrim.esm|08CBF0: Come now, my friend. Haven't we played this game long enough? Untie me, and we can all go our separate ways.
Skyrim.esm|08CDA5: Ah, well, I'm Redguard, as I'm sure you've noticed. Though not like you. Hmph. No, definitely not like you. I am a child of Hammerfell, born of the sands of the mighty Alik'r. In that great desert, blood is a shade of crimson I can scarce describe. My past is long and storied, and not altogether proud. But the Dark Brotherhood saved me from myself. And I have never looked back.
Skyrim.esm|08CDB1: Don't you sweet talk me, Alvor. Not after last night. I'll bring you water when I'm done with my other chores.
Skyrim.esm|08CDB2: She'll make a fine wife someday.
Skyrim.esm|08CDB3: Woman, you'll be the death of me yet. Fine, take your time. I'll just die of thirst in the heat of this forge.
Skyrim.esm|08CDB4: Care to have a drink with me, sweet Sigrid?
Skyrim.esm|08CDB5: So that's a no I take it?
Skyrim.esm|08CDB6: Aye. She's helping me in the house, and even helps her father at the forge.
Skyrim.esm|08CDB7: Drunk again, Embry? Don't let Alvor hear you talking to me that way. He'll stick your head in his forge.
Skyrim.esm|08CDB8: Thank Shor I have a lot of years before I have to worry about that!
Skyrim.esm|08CDB9: It seems like Dorthe grows a head taller every season.
Skyrim.esm|08CDBA: Sigrid! Love of my life! When you get a spare moment, I could use some water.
Skyrim.esm|08CDD6: Glad the antidote is taking effect.
Skyrim.esm|08CF81: You must go to a fortress, endangered by water, yet untouched by it. Inside, you will find an elven mage who can turn the brightest star as black as night. It is cryptic, I know, but Azura's signs are never wrong. I believe the fortress may refer to Winterhold. Ask if they know this elven enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|08D25C: Calcelmo's orders- no one gets through to the tower. Kill any intruders on sight. Call for backup if you need to.
Skyrim.esm|08D292: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|08D2AF: I am Sanguine, Daedric Prince of Debauchery! I know, I know, how could I lie to you? Well, how could I trust you until we've shared a few drinks? But it wasn't long before I realized you'd make a more interesting bearer of my not-quite-holy staff than this waste of flesh.
Skyrim.esm|08D2BE: You came to visit! It's so much better here with Grelod gone. Thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|08D2BF: I'll go back to the Orphanage in a while. I'll give them time to, you know... clean up the mess.
Skyrim.esm|08D2C0: When I grow up, I'm going to be an assassin. That way I can help lots of children, just like you.
Skyrim.esm|08D2C1: Someone lied. Someone told the guard poor Cicero did something he did not. We were detained, delayed! The Night Mother kept from her new home! But you wouldn't know anything about that, would you? Hmmm? No. No... Of course not. Because then Cicero would get angry, and that would be... ugly. Now Loreius. Loreius has learned the meaning of ugly.
Skyrim.esm|08DC20: Lost it? Well, at least you showed signs of initiative by telling me. I guess I shouldn't be surprised with the way things are going around here.
Skyrim.esm|08E660: Yamarz has demanded we stay inside the walls. We cannot leave. I must petition Malacath for relief. This curse must be lifted. But I cannot travel to Malacath's shrine. The ritual must be done here, and I do not have the materials I need. I beg of you, can you bring me Troll Fat and a Daedra Heart? I have no wish to depend on a stranger, but I have no choice.
Skyrim.esm|08EC35: Nords don't care much for magic. The College in Winterhold is about the only place it's not looked down on. Otherwise you could talk to the Jarl's pet wizard in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|08EC36: The College of Winterhold is the only place in Skyrim open-minded enough to teach magic. Although Ulfric's court wizard has been known to take on apprentices once in a while.
Skyrim.esm|08EC37: Magic? Goodness, not here! Nothing dangerous like that! That's all kept to that College up in Winterhold. Better that than dealing with Ulfric's wizard like some do.
Skyrim.esm|08EC38: Magic is for the weak. Elves, I mean. They keep to that College in Winterhold. They know their place. Of course there is Farengar. He might be the Jarl's wizard, but at least he's a Nord.
Skyrim.esm|08EC39: Pfft, I should've guessed as much. Your kind sticks to that College in Winterhold, as well they should. You'd never think to learn from a Nord like the Jarl's wizard.
Skyrim.esm|08EC3A: There's a College in Winterhold that teaches magic. It's very secretive, though, and you know how people hate secrets. The Jarl's court wizard was taught there. That one's full of secrets.
Skyrim.esm|08EC3B: Magic? That's dangerous. You want to learn that, go to that College in Winterhold. They teach that stuff there. Hey! I know. The Jarl's wizard could teach ya. He's lots closer.
Skyrim.esm|08EC3C: I've got no interest in magic users. No use for their kind at all. They're way up north in Winterhold, and that's fine with me. I don't even like our Jarl havin' a  court wizard.
Skyrim.esm|08EC3D: Bit of advice, folks here don't care for magic or those that use it. If you're intent on it, check at the College up in Winterhold. Either that or go see the Jarl's court wizard.
Skyrim.esm|08EC3E: Looking to blow yourself up? I hear that's what magic does to you. There's a College in Winterhold that teaches magic, unless that blew up too. Be a lot safer just to have the Jarl's wizard teach you a thing or two.
Skyrim.esm|08EC3F: Magic is nothing but trouble. If you're set on it, there's that College in Winterhold or Ulfric's court wizard. But don't say I didn't warn you.
Skyrim.esm|08EC40: The people of Skyrim don't like magic much, so about the only place you're likely to find a teacher is the College of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|08EC43: That'd be the College in Winterhold. I suppose you'll be headed there, then. Well, don't let me get in your way.
Skyrim.esm|08EC44: Seen enough magic in my day to know to stay away from it. I'd stay away from the Jarl's wizard in Dawnstar too. If you wanna be a damn fool, go to the College in Winterhold instead.
Skyrim.esm|08EC45: You lookin' for Falion? Why don't you go bother those mages at their College in Winterhold and leave my brother alone?
Skyrim.esm|08EC46: Any aspiring mage would do well to look for the College in Winterhold. Head there, and you'll get what you're after. Although our Jarl's humble wizard might be willing to help you too.
Skyrim.esm|08EC47: Head north to Winterhold. There's a College there, they teach magic. Most Nords don't care for them. Else you could go see the Jarl's wizard. Might be able to help you.
Skyrim.esm|08EC48: Magic? You're on the wrong side of Skyrim. You want Winterhold... that College there. They teach it. Otherwise you can try the Jarl's wizard. I wouldn't, though.
Skyrim.esm|08EC49: Winterhold. You want the College there. They teach quite a bit.
Skyrim.esm|08EC4A: Oh, I think you'll find that magic is rare in Skyrim. Winterhold, though... The College there has it in abundance. Look there if you wish to learn.
Skyrim.esm|08EC4B: Not here. Definitely not here. You want to learn, join the College in Winterhold. They'll deal with it.
Skyrim.esm|08EC4C: Magic is... frowned upon in Skyrim. Only the mages at the College of Winterhold teach it freely. If you wish to learn, join them.
Skyrim.esm|08EC4D: A prospective student, are you? Well, I'm afraid I'm not much good at teaching. You should try your luck at the College of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|08EC4E: I have no time or patience for a student. Perhaps the College of Winterhold will grant you admission.
Skyrim.esm|08F141: Come see my collection of Gray-Mane's finest trinkets! You'll not find their like in all Tamriel!
Skyrim.esm|08F142: Never much cared for that Balgruuf myself. He was in the Empire's pocket, so to speak.
Skyrim.esm|08F143: Shiny trinkets for your good lady! All crafted by the great Eorlund Gray-Mane himself!
Skyrim.esm|08F144: My Eorlund is the finest blacksmith in Skyrim, and that's not just a wife's boasting.
Skyrim.esm|08F145: Chops and steaks fit for a jarl! Come and see my selection of finest-quality meats!
Skyrim.esm|08F146: The finest cuts, fresh from the wilds!
Skyrim.esm|08F147: Fresh-baked loaves, still warm from the oven!
Skyrim.esm|08F148: Ripe fruit and fresh vegetables for sale! Straight from the fields and orchards of Whiterun to your table!
Skyrim.esm|08F1AD: Damn! This place is coming down! Quick, get the Skeleton Key and the Eye and lets get out of here!
Skyrim.esm|08F1AE: We have to find another way out of here before the place fills with water!
Skyrim.esm|08F1B0: No luck there, lass. Something must have fallen on the other side of the door because it isn't moving!
Skyrim.esm|08F341: First off, there's an Orc hanging around causing me headaches. Something about a vision quest he needs help with.
Skyrim.esm|08F342: First off, there's a damn Orc hanging around getting in the way. He's been yammering on about needing help with some kind of vision quest.
Skyrim.esm|08F343: And then there's this Orc who's been hanging around causing me headaches. Something about a vision quest he needs help with.
Skyrim.esm|08F344: And as if that wasn't enough. I got this damn Orc hanging around, getting in the way, yapping about needing help with some kind of vision quest.
Skyrim.esm|08F3D4: I thought you might not remember your first trip here. You had a big night. I think you've definitely earned the staff.
Skyrim.esm|09003E: I've seen documents dating back to the late Second Era. Beyond that, it's hard to say. Events in the last several hundred years resulted in a significant loss of our history, I'm afraid.
Skyrim.esm|09028C: They mean to take our history and crush it all in the name of some damn treaty. The soil beneath our feet is steeped in the blood of our ancestors. We've fought for this land and... died for it. I lost my own daughter to the Imperial dogs, and by Talos I'll not lose anyone else!
Skyrim.esm|09028D: Lilija joined the Stormcloaks many years ago, when the first of the skirmishes broke out across Skyrim. I was so proud of her. She served as a Battle Maiden, using her healing arts to help those that fell on the battlefield. Never even lifted a blade in her life. It didn't matter. The Imperial soldiers cut her down... killed her like a dog and left her body to rot in the mud. And that's why I won't rest easy until every single Imperial soldier joins her, and Ulfric sits upon the throne of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|09028E: Suit yourself. If you change your mind, I'll be around.
Skyrim.esm|09028F: It was my sister, Lilija. She served as a Battle Maiden with the Stormcloaks. We recently heard she'd been killed... I... I find it difficult to talk about. Perhaps you'd be better off speaking to my parents about it. Anyway, I'm glad my work at the Black-Briar Meadery keeps me busy. Helps me keep my mind off of things.
Skyrim.esm|090290: Sure. How do you think I get my hands on the good stuff and offer it so cheap? You think old Maven is just giving it away to anyone? Good thing she makes so much money. Makes it easier for merchandise like this to fall through the cracks.
Skyrim.esm|090292: My father was a Stormcloak soldier in his younger days. Now he just walks the streets spewing his hatred and propaganda. My mother is the Priestess of Talos. She tends to the shrine and teaches his ways. I'm just not so sure she's delivering the right message.
Skyrim.esm|090295: Sorry, fresh out. Maybe next time.
Skyrim.esm|090296: Absolutely. Did you still want to buy one?
Skyrim.esm|090297: Nothing but the best. Black-Briar Mead... fresh from the vat. Best of all, you don't have to pay the ridiculous prices Maven charges. So what do you say to that?
Skyrim.esm|090298: Riften's biggest exports used to be fishing and honey, until the Black-Briar family moved in. Now the meadery is the biggest game in town.
Skyrim.esm|090299: The city guard has been tearing Riften apart looking for the source of our skooma problem. I'd hate to be in their shoes if they get discovered.
Skyrim.esm|09029A: Sibbi Black-Briar is furious that his ex-lover tried to murder him. He's leaving no stone unturned searching for her.
Skyrim.esm|09029B: Mjoll the Lioness used to adventure across Skyrim before she settled down in Riften. I hear it all ended when she lost her enchanted blade.
Skyrim.esm|09029C: Crime is at an all-time high in this city thanks to the Thieves Guild. Don't we have enough problems with the war going on?
Skyrim.esm|09029D: If Maven Black-Briar keeps raising prices on mead, I'll have to resort to watering it down just to make ends meet.
Skyrim.esm|09029E: If it's true that the dragons have returned, what will it mean for all of us? Is this how it all ends?
Skyrim.esm|09029F: Here you go. Oh, and keep it quiet. This stuff isn't exactly easy to get a hold of at these prices, if you catch my drift.
Skyrim.esm|0904AD: Oh, I always knew my Thorald was alive. Ain't a man in Skyrim who can keep a Gray-Mane down.
Skyrim.esm|0904AF: Welcome to Warmaiden's. Now you, my girl, have definitely come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0904B3: Best if... you didn't mention to anyone that you saw me here.
Skyrim.esm|0904B5: Remember - don't trust the Imperials. No matter what honeyed lies they tell you.
Skyrim.esm|0904B7: You take care of yourself, now.
Skyrim.esm|0904B8: If you need any jewelry, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0904B9: If you need a drink, just holler.
Skyrim.esm|0904BB: Come back anytime if you need a remedy.
Skyrim.esm|0904BC: Do forget to see me at my shop sometime - Arcadia's Cauldron.
Skyrim.esm|0904BD: Need anything else, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0904BE: I think I've got some mugs need scrubbin'.
Skyrim.esm|0904C0: If there's nothing else, I have work to do...
Skyrim.esm|0904C1: It's the leaving that's hardest, I know.
Skyrim.esm|0904C2: Some parting advice - women love sonnets.
Skyrim.esm|0904C5: Remember, I'll give you the best deals, or die trying.
Skyrim.esm|0904C6: Next time, come see my at my shop. I've got some things you'll just love.
Skyrim.esm|0904C8: We'll have to have a pint together sometime, you and me.
Skyrim.esm|0904CA: Remember, I've got the freshest meat in all of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0904CC: Thank you very much for your business.
Skyrim.esm|0904CF: You go on your way now, boy.
Skyrim.esm|0904D0: You go on your way now, girl.
Skyrim.esm|0904D5: Keep your eyes open for those Stormcloak dogs, yeah?
Skyrim.esm|0904D6: Do be careful, child. Skyrim is not as safe as it used to be.
Skyrim.esm|0904D7: By the way, you might want to stop in at Warmaiden's. These days, folk need all the protection they can get.
Skyrim.esm|0904D9: The Stormcloaks are living on borrowed time, believe me.
Skyrim.esm|0904DA: May your sword be sharp, and your tongue sharper.
Skyrim.esm|0904DB: Keep a song in your heart, and a blade in your boot.
Skyrim.esm|0904DF: May all your possible futures be bright.
Skyrim.esm|0904E0: Remember, fresh fruit and vegetables every day. Mostly.
Skyrim.esm|0904E2: I've really got to be going.
Skyrim.esm|0904E3: I think I hear my daughter calling...
Skyrim.esm|0904E7: Stay vigilant! Our enemies are legion!
Skyrim.esm|0904E8: Goodbye. And if you happen to see Mikael, tell him I was asking about him...
Skyrim.esm|0904EE: Finally. I got stuff to do, ya know.
Skyrim.esm|0904F0: Damn it, I lost my page...
Skyrim.esm|0904F1: The blessing of Arkay be upon you.
Skyrim.esm|0904F2: Walk always in the light, in these days of darkness.
Skyrim.esm|0904F5: Don't let the door slam into your backside on the way out.
Skyrim.esm|0904F6: Now if ya don't mind, my house could do with a bit of dusting.
Skyrim.esm|0904F7: Grace of Kynareth be with you.
Skyrim.esm|0904F8: Travel great Skyrim, and Kynareth travels with you.
Skyrim.esm|0904F9: You need to be careful in this city. Never know who you can trust, these days.
Skyrim.esm|0904FB: Right, I really am busy. So...
Skyrim.esm|0904FC: If there's nothing else, I really do have important matters tend to.
Skyrim.esm|0904FD: If you run into my husband Nazeem, remind him that he's not married to himself.
Skyrim.esm|0904FF: You know, if you've got the aptitude, you should join the Mages College in Winterhold...
Skyrim.esm|090500: Remember, your mind is the best weapon you have.
Skyrim.esm|090502: Don't forget to check inside the shop if you need anything.
Skyrim.esm|090504: I've got my eyes on you.
Skyrim.esm|090505: Mind yourself in front of the Jarl, or we'll have serious problems.
Skyrim.esm|090507: You always keep us in mind when you need a fresh horse, yeah?
Skyrim.esm|090509: Back to work, I guess. There's never a shortage of dung to shovel around here.
Skyrim.esm|09059E: Oh she will, mark my words. And don't you let her.
Skyrim.esm|0905A7: No? Well, then she hasn't gotten to you yet. But she will. And you don't you believe a word of it.
Skyrim.esm|090733: If there's any more I can help you with, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|090734: Be warned, the Jarl is in a foul mood since the Stormcloaks overran Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|090735: The Jarl is, as you can imagine, very busy. Perhaps I can assist you?
Skyrim.esm|090736: The task of Steward is more important than you realize. The Jarl often asks for my invaluable council.
Skyrim.esm|090737: I'm kept so busy, I don't get to see my daughter as often as I'd like. Perhaps you've met her? Adrianne? She owns the shop, Warmaiden's.
Skyrim.esm|090A42: I'm the commander of the guard here in Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|090A43: My boys'll keep you safe. Don't you worry.
Skyrim.esm|090A44: You're not thinking of causing trouble here in this beautiful city, now are you? 'Cause I'd strongly advise against such foolishness.
Skyrim.esm|090CFC: How the tree came to grow there is a bit of a mystery... Some say that when Vvardenfell erupted, a piece was blown to the middle of Skyrim and from the crater grew the tree. I've also heard that it was a spore that fell from an island floating in the sky, but that just sounds like nonsense. All I know is that the sap makes you feel as healthy as a cave troll, but slow as a drunk horker. And that and it fetches good coin.
Skyrim.esm|090DEA: You've shown those monsters that they will no longer be tolerated in the Pale. Good work. Here, a reward from your Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|090DEB: My mother taught me. She was a Bard, from the college in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|090DED: Drowning your troubles in mead? A lot of folks are doing that these days. You'll want the Windpeak Inn. As for work, you could try to catch Skald in a good mood, but your best bet would be working in the mines. They're on opposite ends of town.
Skyrim.esm|090DEE: Now that's what I like to see. Someone who gets things done. Here. A little something for you.
Skyrim.esm|090DF0: I am. I came into Arkay's light after the Great War. So many died by my hands, my magics. I'm fortunate that Kust and the people of Falkreath have come to accept me as one of their own. Could you do an old elf a favor? I misplaced my journal in a cave I was exploring. I'll pay for its return.
Skyrim.esm|090DF1: Seems Brina Merilis and the Legion came and now she's Jarl. That's a relief. I don't like the fighting, but Brina is the woman for the job.
Skyrim.esm|090DF2: I'm a writer by trade. Not that the people of Skyrim do much reading.
Skyrim.esm|090DF3: Skald supports Ulfric's rebellion, and he doesn't like having a couple of old Legionnaires living in his town. He's a fool. A dragon has attacked Helgen. Dawnstar needs the Empire now more than ever.
Skyrim.esm|090DF5: Giants are a common problem in the Pale. The Empire always demanded I leave them be, but the Empire isn't here now. Go and slay one of the beasts. It should let them know that Dawnstar is not to be trifled with.
Skyrim.esm|090DF7: You've proven reliable. There's a group of bandits in my Hold that I... may have had a few discreet dealings with. The cut they were giving me was good at first, but now it's time to clean things up. Go and take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|090DF8: Teach them to stop paying me. Here. You deserve a reward for your service.
Skyrim.esm|090DF9: It was supposed to be an honor, but very well.
Skyrim.esm|090DFA: You better. I hate people who waste my time.
Skyrim.esm|090DFC: That I am. My father left it to me after he died in that Great War against the Elves. I don't care what's going on in the world. Rebellion. Dragons awakening. The mill is enough trouble for me to worry about.
Skyrim.esm|090DFF: That would be Jarl Skald the Elder. He's over in the White Hall, probably talking about Ulfric like he's the second coming of Talos.
Skyrim.esm|090E00: Jarl Brina Merilis is in charge, thanks to the Empire. Best hide any Talos amulets you have.
Skyrim.esm|090E01: Blessing of Arkay to you for your help.
Skyrim.esm|090E02: Falkreath is known for its graveyard, traveler. We've buried more dead than you can imagine. I just finished giving my old war companion, Berit, a proper burial by fire. Old hard head hated the idea of being lost cold in the ground. Could you deliver the ashes to Runil, the priest of Arkay? He'll know the right blessings to make for Berit's soul.
Skyrim.esm|090E03: If you don't mind digging, I own the Quicksilver Mine. It's a better life than working in that damned Iron-Breaker Mine with all those milk drinkers.
Skyrim.esm|090E04: Then I don't have time for you.
Skyrim.esm|090E05: Oh, thank you. The dogs were getting restless. Here, this is for you.
Skyrim.esm|090E06: I own Iron-Breaker Mine. We make twice as much coin as those horkers over in Quicksilver Mine, and don't let anyone tell you otherwise. I pay a good wage for any iron ore you dig up, if you're looking for work.
Skyrim.esm|090E07: If you've got an axe, you can always make gold chopping wood. Just bring me everything you cut.
Skyrim.esm|090E08: Don't talk to me. You could be one of them.
Skyrim.esm|090E0A: That would be Skald the Elder. He's been running Dawnstar ever since he was a boy, and he never lost that brat's attitude. Turned on the Empire first chance he got. Now we're all part of this rebellion.
Skyrim.esm|090E0B: Brina Merilis came in with the whole damn Legion and deposed Skald. Couldn't have happened to a better man.
Skyrim.esm|090E0C: Thank you. It's a collection of regrets that I try not to forget. Here, I promised you this.
Skyrim.esm|090E0D: Work? I demand tribute before you can be considered for any real tasks. How about drink? None of that local piss. Real Black-Briar Mead, fresh from Riften.
Skyrim.esm|090E0E: Watch your hands around the Legate.
Skyrim.esm|090E0F: If it's about the mine, I'm all ears.
Skyrim.esm|090E10: You looking for a shield? Best thing between you and the enemy, friend.
Skyrim.esm|090E11: Don't forget getting a good pair of gauntlets. Broken hands can't hold a sword.
Skyrim.esm|090E13: I've been in charge of Dawnstar for over 35 years.
Skyrim.esm|090E14: My only aim is to keep the peace.
Skyrim.esm|090E15: Let me know if you need a drink. That's what we're here for.
Skyrim.esm|090E16: Sorry if my father seems a bit distracted. A lot's happened.
Skyrim.esm|090E17: You should talk to Rustleif. Get some proper steel.
Skyrim.esm|090E18: Old lady Frida is a grouch, but she sure can whip up a cure for the Rattles fast.
Skyrim.esm|090E19: Beware of Frostmere Crypt, friend. Heard some bandits are living in the ruins now.
Skyrim.esm|090E1A: Giants have been spotted up near Red Road Pass. Best to keep away and avoid it entirely.
Skyrim.esm|090E1D: Dawnstar's got its problems, but as long as the ore is good, I'll stay.
Skyrim.esm|090E1F: Quicksilver ore is rare. Worth digging.
Skyrim.esm|090E20: Been working in Iron-Breaker since I was a boy.
Skyrim.esm|090E21: Iron's a good, stout metal. A fine thing for a Nord to dig.
Skyrim.esm|090E23: Mining's an honest trade. No real tricks to it.
Skyrim.esm|090E24: I work for the Windpeak Inn. You should head on inside.
Skyrim.esm|090E25: Thoring runs the inn. Speak to him.
Skyrim.esm|090E9F: First of all, I'd like to drum up some more support from the local populace. I have someone in mind.
Skyrim.esm|090EA0: First of all, there's someone in particular I'd like to win over to our cause.
Skyrim.esm|090EA5: Then there's a little matter of some recruiting. I have someone in mind I'd like to join our campaign.
Skyrim.esm|090EA6: I need to send someone out to do a little recruiting. There's someone in particular I'd like to join us.
Skyrim.esm|090EB3: Good job. The more citizens that join the fight against the rebels, the sooner they will see the futility of their rebellion.
Skyrim.esm|090EB4: We grow that much stronger with each son and daughter of Skyrim who joins us. Down with the Emperor!
Skyrim.esm|090EB5: Thanks for taking care of that. I don't think that will be the last we see of that Orc. It's always good to gain allies.
Skyrim.esm|090EB6: Thanks for dealing with that orc. Something tells me that's not the last time we'll see him. And, something tells me that might not be a bad thing.
Skyrim.esm|090EC8: Beware! The World-Eater waits within the mist!
Skyrim.esm|090ECA: In life I felled foes unnumbered, fearless in battle; but no blade can defend against the terror of this gloom-stricken valley.
Skyrim.esm|090ECB: Do you know the way? I'm weary and lost.
Skyrim.esm|090ECC: Turn back! Before he finds you!
Skyrim.esm|090EE1: Sounds like easy money to me. You're on.
Skyrim.esm|090EE2: You there! You look like a sympathetic soldier. I require your assistance!
Skyrim.esm|090EE3: So you're the one who's been down there killing all my wolves. You're gonna die for that!
Skyrim.esm|090EE4: Let's see... found the Dragon Cult... laid siege... people already dead... poisoned themselves... blah, blah, blah...  ah! Here it is!
Skyrim.esm|090EE5: Oh... well... of course they'd disavow all knowledge... spies are everywhere.
Skyrim.esm|090EE7: So the Dragon Cult poisoned their own well, which explains the ghosts. However, Skorm thought the well was the key to entering the inner areas.
Skyrim.esm|090EE8: Get him! Rip his throat out!
Skyrim.esm|090EEA: That all happened in the First Era, so the well should be clear of poison by now. It says the key to the well is likely somewhere in the crypt.
Skyrim.esm|090EEE: I'll do you ten gold at three to one on the white one.
Skyrim.esm|090EF1: The General has sent me to obtain a powerful weapon for the war effort. This was the last great bastion of the Dragon Cult, and their leader's mask was reported to be quite powerful.
Skyrim.esm|091527: Now, cast the ward spell, and keep it up.
Skyrim.esm|09152A: You have to keep your ward up, or else this won't work. I don't want to hurt you.
Skyrim.esm|091705: Everybody pushes me around. Well no more!
Skyrim.esm|091707: Don't worry about it. Keep it.
Skyrim.esm|091708: You could have asked before just taking it.
Skyrim.esm|0917AA: There is no one else, but very well. I cannot force you to accept your destiny.
Skyrim.esm|0917AC: Good. All will be revealed in time. Keep playing your part, and Azura will reward you.
Skyrim.esm|0917AE: Stendarr's Mercy. You found it. Azura's Star. Maybe this is my chance to make things right. I can finish Malyn's work, the way we had meant to before his madness. It would mean cutting the Star off from Azura. Only black souls would be able to enter it once we finished.
Skyrim.esm|0917B1: Who sent you? Was it the College? The Jarl? We agreed there would be no more questions.
Skyrim.esm|0917B4: Azura is no ordinary Daedra. She commands an entire realm inside of Oblivion. The more Malyn worked on the Star, the more she was able to damn him. It started slowly at first. Malyn would see things that weren't there. Then he would yell at students over words they hadn't said. Then one day I walked in and Malyn had... killed a student, and in a horrific moment of inspiration, he started using her soul for his work.
Skyrim.esm|0917B9: There was an... unfortunate incident a few years ago. An elven mage named Nelacar was exiled from the College over it. He's still in town, getting by plying spells to the people inside the Frozen Hearth. Disgraceful.
Skyrim.esm|0917BA: The mages are up in the College. You can ask them, if they'll let you in. Then again, Nelacar might know. He's an old-timer from the College, but he has a room here.
Skyrim.esm|0917BC: I mentioned how the Star is a soul gem, only it never gets depleted? There's another rule the artifact follows. You can only store white souls in the Star, belonging to the lesser creatures. Azura's magic won't allow black souls to enter it. As a mortal, Malyn's soul was black, so part of his work was breaking past Azura's rules. He was close before... well, I already told you.
Skyrim.esm|091824: Psijics? Here now? What in Talos' name...
Skyrim.esm|091825: We've got to find a way to put a stop to this.
Skyrim.esm|091826: I hope whatever you're going to do will work.
Skyrim.esm|091828: What are we going to do? How do we save the College?
Skyrim.esm|091836: I hope you know what you're doing.
Skyrim.esm|09184B: I can't lose all my research. There has to be a way to save the College.
Skyrim.esm|09184C: You really think the Staff of Magnus is the key?
Skyrim.esm|091850: How long can we keep this up?
Skyrim.esm|091852: J'zargo knows that you will save the College. You must.
Skyrim.esm|091853: J'zargo is sure this is the end. And this was supposed to be a safe place...
Skyrim.esm|091856: If we can't stop this, what happens to the rest of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|091857: I've never seen anything like this, in all my years.
Skyrim.esm|09185F: Can you imagine the kind of power behind this?
Skyrim.esm|091860: We never should have pulled that thing out of Saarthal.
Skyrim.esm|09186B: Do you think Ancano knew all along what the Eye was capable of?
Skyrim.esm|09186C: What are you doing here? You need to get that Staff, or else we don't stand a chance!
Skyrim.esm|0918CF: The College has undertaken a fascinating excavation in the ruins of Saarthal nearby. It's an excellent learning opportunity.
Skyrim.esm|0918D0: Well, I think this is an excellent start. I'd like you all to continue practicing with wards, please.
Skyrim.esm|0918D1: I suggest we meet there in a few hours, and see what awaits us inside. That's all for now, thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0918D2: I think perhaps we're ready to begin exploring some of the various applications of magic throughout history.
Skyrim.esm|0918D7: I got no quarrel with you, but I won't turn one down, neither.
Skyrim.esm|0918D8: Keep walkin', softgut. I'm more woman than you can handle.
Skyrim.esm|0918D9: Welcome to Warmaiden's. Now don't let the name worry you - we got plenty o' steel for fightin' men.
Skyrim.esm|0918DA: I'll always be a commander, even if those Stormcloak dogs have stripped away my power.
Skyrim.esm|0918DB: The Legion will regain control of this city. Mark my words.
Skyrim.esm|0918DC: I'm commander of the guard, now.
Skyrim.esm|0918DD: The Stormcloaks can keep this city safe better than those Legion lapdogs.
Skyrim.esm|0918DF: The security in Whiterun is terrible. Shameful, is what it is.
Skyrim.esm|0918E0: The guards in this city are all lazy and undisciplined. Things need to change...
Skyrim.esm|0918E1: Commander Cauis walks around Dragonsreach like a preening rooster. I could do his job blindfolded....
Skyrim.esm|091942: I've been pouring over the plans you brought us, and I'm convinced the Eyes of the Falmer are in the dwarven ruins at Irkngthand. Karliah and I will meet you there. Prepare yourself, lad. This will be a fight to remember!
Skyrim.esm|091943: Well, we have a bit of an errand to run before your coronation, so don't get sentimental on me now.
Skyrim.esm|091944: I've been at this game a long time, my friend. A long time. I've stolen trinkets from nobles and framed priests for murder. I'm good at what I do, maybe even one of the best. But it's all I know. I've never been one to lead. Never desired it, never cared for it. Don't want it.
Skyrim.esm|091945: Then it's decided. When this entire sorry affair is over, we'll handle the details. Until then, we have quite the task ahead.
Skyrim.esm|091946: Then it's decided. When this is all over and Delvin's contacts assure me that we've regained our footing in Skyrim, we'll handle the details. Until then, we have quite the task ahead.
Skyrim.esm|091947: Ha! Spoken like the leader of the Thieves Guild. Just don't lose yourself in the role. We have a bit of an errand to run before your coronation.
Skyrim.esm|091948: Look. Everyone in the Guild admires what you've done. Maybe they won't come out and simply tell you, but I promise you it's true. And now they know Mercer never genuinely cared about the Guild. He lacked the loyalty you obviously possess. I can't think of anyone better.
Skyrim.esm|091949: Karliah and I had a long discussion before you arrived here. Thanks to your efforts, the Guild's been restored to it's full strength and you've exposed the traitor in our midst. As a result, we both feel that you'd make an excellent replacement for Mercer as leader of the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|09194A: Karliah and I had a long discussion before you arrived here. Thanks to your efforts, Mercer's treachery has been exposed. After we deal with him, all that remains is restoring the Guild to its full strength. As a result, we both feel that you have the potential of replacing Mercer as leader of the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|091AE3: These fissures and cracks aren't encouraging. Malyn obviously was growing more desparate once he left the College.
Skyrim.esm|09232E: The Stormcloaks are destined to win this fight. Talos is with us, and when you fight under a Divine's blessing, victory is assured. Dawnstar has a history of being the site of battles that have crippled the fate of the Empire. I hope this remains true.
Skyrim.esm|092330: The only reason dragons are attacking is because Talos is angry with us for turning our backs on him. As soon as we drive the Empire out of Skyrim, the dragons will vanish. Mark my words.
Skyrim.esm|092331: It all started here. This rebellion. When the Empire lost the Reach during the Great War, we became desperate. We promised a group of Nord militia free worship in exchange for their help retaking the Hold. Then the Elves found out about it. We were forced to arrest all of them. Ulfric Stormcloak, their leader, used the whole thing as proof that the Empire had abandoned Skyrim. The rebels called it "The Markarth Incident." It was the founding day for the Stormcloaks, and where this war really started.
Skyrim.esm|092332: Yes. Ever since mother died a few winters ago. I play songs and help with the drinks when I have the time.
Skyrim.esm|092333: Well, you are lovely to look at, and there's so many things I could teach you, but I'm afraid the Temple isn't taking in new students.
Skyrim.esm|092335: The White-Gold Concordat. Our treaty with the Elves after the Great War forbids free worship of Talos. But that's what we promised Ulfric and his men. It seems foolish looking back, but at the time we were hoping the Elves wouldn't find out. So when they did find out, it was either we arrest Ulfric and the militia, or enter into yet another war with the Aldmeri Dominion. The choice was clear. And now, Ulfric threatens the thinly veiled peace we sacrificed so much for.
Skyrim.esm|09233C: The Imperials were weak. They created this Forsworn problem, but now that we're in charge we'll solve it. Once the war is over, I'll petition Ulfric to send the militia and we'll cleanse the Reach of this menace. Then the Forsworn will be where they belong. In the mines, bringing us wealth.
Skyrim.esm|092341: When the Aldmeri Dominion invaded the Imperial City, the Legion all but turned a blind eye to the other provinces. Many of the disgruntled natives of the Reach used the opportunity to depose the Empire, and founded what they called an independent kingdom. It was little more than a chaotic uprising, but the Reach was removed from Imperial authority for two years before we reclaimed it. But the leaders of the uprising refused our offers of peace. They fled into the hills and became the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|092346: Yes. Their history and culture is all around us in Markarth. A race of stonecutters, artisans, and engineers. They invented machines and built elaborate underground cities where they researched powers to rival the gods themselves. And then, at a time we are still not sure when, they disappeared. The whole people, all at once. Leaving behind only their works.
Skyrim.esm|092347: The stone structure the Dwarves have left makes us invincible. Let the dragons singe a few carpets. They'll never topple Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|092349: Yes, I know. We have some advantages if they come here. The city is built into the mountainside, so it's hard to reach into even from the skies. And everything is made of stone. Even the beds. We'll have no trouble from dragon's fire here.
Skyrim.esm|09234A: The Empire has stretched itself too far. This rebellion in Skyrim is one problem, but solving it won't fix everything. But that's the kind of talk an old warhorse shouldn't be making. Don't worry. I have my problems with the Empire, but I'm still a soldier first. We'll keep peace and order in Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|09234B: Ah, now that is a good question. Where does Hogni get the finest, freshest meat from all over the Reach? The answer is simple. Orcs. There is a stronghold nearby whose hunters can butcher a deer in seconds. Now having an Orc for a wife would be something, yes. Strong and bulky. Too bad they don't let people go beyond the walls. Let me show you where they are. Maybe they welcome you. Maybe they put an arrow in your face. Who knows?
Skyrim.esm|09234C: Through neglect. During the Great War, they let the unwashed natives retake the Reach and declare their own sovereignty. The Stormcloaks were forced to retake the Reach on our own, but that taste of independence emboldened them. And the Forsworn were born. In the end, we took back Markarth, but those damn Forsworn still stalk the hills and roads, looking for revenge.
Skyrim.esm|09234D: No. We're completely exposed. Bows and arrows are in short supply. Every building is made of thatch and wood. I've given standing orders to the guards to prepare water bucket runs to the harbor in case of a fire, but that's all we can do.
Skyrim.esm|09234E: If you could call it that. The Jarl can make all the demands in the world, I'm not setting one foot on the battlefield. I fought in the Great War. I cut enough young lives short, I'm not going to do it again.
Skyrim.esm|09234F: A heathen. Lovely. Oh, don't put on that angry look. I'm just teasing you. Dibella is the Divine of Beauty. She is a patron deity to artists and bards and those who seek life's simple pleasures. The temple offers her blessings to pilgrims and instruction in enjoying all life has to offer.
Skyrim.esm|092350: I'm sorry, the Temple of Dibella is closed. You can receive your blessing, if you wish, but the other sisters are in seclusion.
Skyrim.esm|0923F7: There is no escape. Courage is useless.
Skyrim.esm|092441: Why have taxes not come in from Granite Hill, Nenya? We should send some guards down there.
Skyrim.esm|092442: I don't know about you, tekla, but all my prayers are silent and who I pray to is still my own to decide.
Skyrim.esm|092443: Most of my friends are buried here.
Skyrim.esm|092444: Narri, do you think the war will ever end?
Skyrim.esm|092446: Hmmf. Maybe you're the one talking to him then...
Skyrim.esm|092447: Falkreath is a fine place to be, Bolund.
Skyrim.esm|092448: Bolund, you're going to die of thirst if you keep calling me that.
Skyrim.esm|092449: I suppose so. Seems like it was just turning summer yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|09244A: Falkreath is our home, a place where we have good jobs under a good Jarl. Until the war comes to us we stay put.
Skyrim.esm|09244B: I wish I could afford to pay you both, Narri.  I really do.
Skyrim.esm|09244C: Do you ever get tired of long nights at the mill, Zaria?
Skyrim.esm|09244D: Preparing the defenses is almost impossible lately, every time we get a new war report our needs change.
Skyrim.esm|092450: Sir, I will shape them up but with the war on it is unwise to reduce their wages any further.
Skyrim.esm|092452: It is a hard time for all of us. I hope that you too can find some sanctuary here, Mathies.
Skyrim.esm|092453: Ah, Indara. Arkay's blessing upon you and your husband. Spend as much time here as you need.
Skyrim.esm|092454: They are only a little late, Jarl. The trade routes are affected by the war, we should give them another day.
Skyrim.esm|092455: There are a lot of good men buried here, Kust.
Skyrim.esm|092457: The impertinence! You never would have talked to me like that when I was Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|092458: Oh, the Hagraven feather and so on. You can throw all those out.  You see...
Skyrim.esm|092459: Don't let my brother get to you, Tekla. You're doing a fine job around here.
Skyrim.esm|09245A: Narri! This mead tastes like water and the meat has gone off.
Skyrim.esm|09245B: I think the war is just an excuse. If it does end there will be a new reason and a new place.
Skyrim.esm|09245C: Hey, Lod, how is the worst farmer in the hold doing?
Skyrim.esm|09245D: Tekla. The Hall is filthy. See to your cleaning.
Skyrim.esm|09245F: You know how farming is here, long days and little to show for it.
Skyrim.esm|092460: The trade routes are affected as well. Procuring supplies is a guessing game. Weapons sell well though.
Skyrim.esm|092462: Do you think Tekla has been talking to Siddgeir again?
Skyrim.esm|092463: At some point we should hire Tekla on here. If she could just spend less time serving Dengeir...
Skyrim.esm|092464: Do you ever get tired of the long days on the farm, Mathias?
Skyrim.esm|092465: The way this war is going you'll soon have plenty of veterans like me to buy drinks.
Skyrim.esm|092466: We can't afford to lose any men if we wish to keep Falkreath protected.
Skyrim.esm|092467: The graveyard has been lonely. Few pay homage to the dead.
Skyrim.esm|092469: The mead is same as it ever was and the meat is fresh, maybe your sense of taste has "gone off."
Skyrim.esm|09246A: Thank you, Runil. Your support means much to us.
Skyrim.esm|09246B: Very well, but when their emissary arrives I want words with him.
Skyrim.esm|09246C: Men aren't good or bad. They're just men.
Skyrim.esm|09246D: Mathies. I wanted to say I'm sorry about what happened to your girl.
Skyrim.esm|09246E: How's the Corpselight treating you Mathies?
Skyrim.esm|09246F: We had this same argument every week, even when you were Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|092470: Ah Tekla, you're a pretty little mouse.
Skyrim.esm|092471: Tekla! It's good to see you, girl. How's Dengeir treating you?
