Skyrim.esm|092472: But what about the banning of Talos worship? Isn't that worth fighting about?
Skyrim.esm|092474: Mmmm, careful with that, Nenya, our merchants could be selling weapons to an invading force.
Skyrim.esm|092475: Hmmm, well don't take what you don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|092476: Sure it is, Lod. Sure it is.
Skyrim.esm|092477: Keep them coming and keep them strong, Valga.
Skyrim.esm|092478: Hey gossip, bring me a drink.
Skyrim.esm|09247A: The Gentle Repose is barren, it was barren the day my father claimed the land and it's barren now.
Skyrim.esm|09247C: We've been over this, Den. Tekla does not share your secrets with Siddgeir.
Skyrim.esm|09247E: Falkreath is where people go to die. We could be fighting, taking back Nord land!
Skyrim.esm|09247F: I would think a man of your military training would be able to do with less but so be it.
Skyrim.esm|092480: The fields will need cutting soon.
Skyrim.esm|092481: Alright, but if you ever want to leave that old curmudgeon I've got plenty for you to do at Gentle Repose.
Skyrim.esm|092482: Few want to be reminded of mortality in a time of war, Kust.
Skyrim.esm|092484: Helvard! The guards are looking sloppy today. I want you to reduce their wages for the month.
Skyrim.esm|092485: The temple is the one place my wife finds peace these days, Runil.
Skyrim.esm|092486: The way this war is going there might not be anyone left to drink soon.
Skyrim.esm|092491: Please place your hands on the altar. Azura wishes to speak with you.
Skyrim.esm|092517: Looks like he's gone for good this time. But I don't think we should stick around to see if he comes back.
Skyrim.esm|092518: Draugr creeping down the mountain to climb through my window at night, that kind of thing. I admit, I still don't much like the look of it.
Skyrim.esm|09251A: There he goes. Looks like he's gone for good this time.
Skyrim.esm|09251B: I guess you get used to it.
Skyrim.esm|092A26: There once was a hero named Ragnar the Red, who came riding to Whiterun from ole Rorikstead!
Skyrim.esm|092A27: And the braggart did swagger and brandish his blade, as he told of bold battles and gold he had made!
Skyrim.esm|092A28: Thank you, thank you. No, please, hold your applause.
Skyrim.esm|092A29: But then he went quiet, did Ragnar the Red, when he met the shieldmaiden Matilda who said...
Skyrim.esm|092A2A: "Oh, you talk and you lie and you drink all our mead! Now I think it's high time that you lie down and bleed!"
Skyrim.esm|092A2B: And so then came the clashing and slashing of steel, as the brave lass Matilda charged in full of zeal!
Skyrim.esm|092A2C: And the braggart named Ragnar was boastful no moooooree... when his ugly red head rolled around on the floor!
Skyrim.esm|092A2D: Thank you, good patrons of the Bannered Mare! Thank you!
Skyrim.esm|092A2E: One more song, what say you? Yeah? All right, then!
Skyrim.esm|092A41: You'll never see the sun again. No one escapes Cidhna Mine. No one.
Skyrim.esm|092A43: We're going to make them pay.
Skyrim.esm|092A44: Those bastards will regret raising their weapons against Jorrvaskr.
Skyrim.esm|092A45: Let's get back to Jorrvaskr. We'll want to pay our respects to Kodlak's spirit.
Skyrim.esm|092A46: Well aren't you in luck. He's the next piece of gutter scum on the docket. Between you and me, I hope you cleave his arrogant head open.
Skyrim.esm|092A47: We don't just let people rot in cells until they've served their time. Here, you fight. If you win, you go free. If you lose, well, you'll know the freedom of Sovngarde if you're lucky.
Skyrim.esm|092A5E: You'd better give me five septims right now, or I'm going to bloody your nose...
Skyrim.esm|092A5F: I... I don't have any money.
Skyrim.esm|092A60: Ten septims tomorrow, baby Battle-Born. Or I'm gonna bloody your nose and your lip.
Skyrim.esm|092A61: No! Please! I really don't have any money, Braith! I'll give you ten septims tomorrow. Just don't hit me.
Skyrim.esm|092B19: You sound like my ma. Figured I was lying dead in a field somewhere, is that it?
Skyrim.esm|092B1A: By Ysmir, it's true. A jester - funny suit and all. Just north, by the Loreius Farm. Fool had a coffin in a cart, with a broken wheel.
Skyrim.esm|092B1D: Well, sounds like you've had quite the journey, Uthgerd. But you were gone longer than usual. I got to thinking maybe you weren't coming back...
Skyrim.esm|092B1E: Thought did cross my mind, yeah. War brewing, talk of a riot in Helgen, and now jesters? Skyrim's gone mad, girl. You need to be careful out there.
Skyrim.esm|092B1F: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|092CBF: I'm sorry, I'm just... you're right. Let me explain.
Skyrim.esm|092CC1: Stendarr's mercy - you aren't one of them. Please, you've got to help me!
Skyrim.esm|092CC2: All right, snowback. Who are you, and what are you doing in my tower?
Skyrim.esm|092CC3: Fjola?  How do you know that name?
Skyrim.esm|092CC9: Better men have tried. Let's finish this.
Skyrim.esm|092CCA: I don't know. I never expected the old bag to follow me so far. Just get rid of him. But don't kill him. I don't hate the man. I just want him to go back to his farm and forget about me.
Skyrim.esm|092CCE: The fool's my husband. I'm Fjola - or I was, once. Don't you see? I left that old scab. Came home to Skyrim, and found this rabble of bandits.
Skyrim.esm|092CCF: One of the guards dropped this key while I hid. I wager it will come in handy for you. Please hurry!
Skyrim.esm|092CD0: Take my wedding band. Stuhn only knows why I've kept it this long. He'll recognize it. Tell him whatever you think will convince him to leave. The ring itself is worth a few bits. Keep it as thanks for cutting off the last vestige of an unwanted fate.
Skyrim.esm|092CD1: Until you can find out whether Fjola is here there's nothing I care to to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|092CD2: And it won't end here. I'd die before I went back.
Skyrim.esm|092CD4: Didn't take much to prove myself and knock them into shape. Now every free blade from here to Windhelm wants to sign on for a piece.
Skyrim.esm|092CD5: You monster!  I'll kill you for this!
Skyrim.esm|092CD6: I didn't expect to see you again. What became of my doting husband?
Skyrim.esm|092CD7: Her marriage band? Then there's hope - she was here! I have to move on if I want to find her. Please, take the coin I promised. Blessings of Stendarr on you!
Skyrim.esm|092CDA: That... I gave it to her on our wedding day. Help me, Divines... it's true isn't it? I'm sorry. Keep the ring. I cannot bear the weight of them in my heart, and I must make the journey alone now. Thank you for... thank you.
Skyrim.esm|092CDB: It's my wife, Fjola!  I think she's being held in this tower.
Skyrim.esm|092CDC: How did... no, forget it. I don't care to know. I'm free of the little man, and that's what matters.
Skyrim.esm|092CDD: Thank you! I don't have much, but I can give you some coin for the trouble.
Skyrim.esm|092CDF: Honestly? I have no idea. But I've looked all over and the kidnappings here started around when she disappeared. So I have to hope...
Skyrim.esm|092CE0: I owe you a debt, and I aim to repay it. We'll see each other again someday. For now, goodbye, and thanks.
Skyrim.esm|092CE1: Fjola, my wife, left the farm on errands and never came back. It's been months, and I've been searching for her since.
Skyrim.esm|092CE2: I managed to sneak past the guards and get this far, but... I don't think I can go on. I'm no warrior! Please, can you look for her?
Skyrim.esm|092CE3: I heard a rumor that these bandits were ransoming captives, so I thought she may be here.
Skyrim.esm|092D9E: Now if you don't mind, I've got a city to keep.
Skyrim.esm|092D9F: Maybe we'll meet in the field. Just pray we're on the same side.
Skyrim.esm|092DA0: If you run into any Stormcloaks, kill a dozen for me.
Skyrim.esm|092DA1: You should be talking to my Steward.
Skyrim.esm|092DA2: I'm the Jarl, not a barkeep. Speak your business.
Skyrim.esm|092DA3: Quickly now, or would you prefer I let Whiterun fall to the Stormcloaks?
Skyrim.esm|092DA4: You might be a guest in Dragonsreach, but you're starting to try my patience.
Skyrim.esm|092DA5: All this standing around is rubbish. We should be taking the fight to the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|092DA6: I'm not a man, I'm a weapon in human form. Just unsheathe me, and point me at the enemy.
Skyrim.esm|092DA7: My brother takes council from Proventus Avenicci? What a laugh. He takes council from his daughter, and she's a bloody blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|092E04: I understand if you need some time to prepare. Let me know when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|092E07: I could soul trap you. Place you inside the Star. Then bring you back once Malyn has been dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|092E08: Malyn's soul is beyond hope because his body is dead. But I'll be keeping you just slightly alive on this end. But be careful. If you die inside the Star, your soul will be disrupted. There's nothing anyone can do for you if that happens.
Skyrim.esm|092E09: A special type of soul trap. Placing you inside the Star without fully killing you. Once Malyn has been dealt with, I'll bring you back.
Skyrim.esm|092E0A: First, I'll need to grab hold of your soul.
Skyrim.esm|092E0C: Okay. Stand very still. This might sting.
Skyrim.esm|092E10: The Star is being used to sustain Malyn's soul. I can't fix it while that fragment of him is inside.
Skyrim.esm|092E11: Precisely. But it's not as simple as enchanting Malyn away. He's put up barriers. Souls are only allowed in, not out. If we were to... somehow... send a soul inside the Star. One that was ready to overpower Malyn from the inside....
Skyrim.esm|092E13: Greetings, mortal. You have followed my guidance through the veils of Twilight and rescued my Star from Malyn Varen. But his soul still resides within, protected by his enchantments. Until he is purged, my artifact is useless to you.
Skyrim.esm|092E14: He did it. He actually managed to trap his own soul inside the Star, but it's falling apart. It needs more and more souls to sustain itself.
Skyrim.esm|092E15: Malyn's soul should be in the Star. Theoretically, purging him will be just like killing a ghost anywhere else. I'll have a magical tether to your soul while you're inside. Once you're done, it'll be as simple as pulling you back into your body.
Skyrim.esm|092EC6: I welcome the chance to challenge the blade of Ysgramor's heir, honored shield-sister to Kodlak Whitemane, whom I've watched for in vain.
Skyrim.esm|092F22: Unless you intend to bury someone, this isn't the place you're looking for, traveler.
Skyrim.esm|092F23: Falkreath was once full of wanderers and warriors like yourself. Those were better days.
Skyrim.esm|092F24: Skyrim's history of war is well documented here in Falkreath's graveyard.
Skyrim.esm|092F25: My brother Dengeir can be... difficult. Take his words with a grain of salt.
Skyrim.esm|092F2A: He's already dead. Worse, the enchantment keeping him intact requires black souls. I don't know how many people he must have killed. If we don't do something, the enchantment will eventually fade, and the Star will slip back into Oblivion. Azura will have the last laugh.
Skyrim.esm|093129: I am charged with protecting the Jarl from any and all threats. Powerful men have many enemies. More so, in times of strife. So you can imagine the dangers. Oh yes, there have been attempts on the Jarl's life. More than one would-be assassin has met his end at the tip of my blade.
Skyrim.esm|09312A: Balgruuf and I share a battle bond. We met as youths, and forged our friendship in the fires of war. When he became Jarl, I insisted on serving as his protector. He had no cause to argue. Gods, you are curious. Almost... dangerously so.
Skyrim.esm|09312B: Hello, my friend! If you seek knowledge about mighty Talos, you have most certainly come to the right person. In mortal life, Talos was a Nord possessed of unmatched tactical skill, limitless wisdom and the power to see into men's hearts. Talos mastered the power of the Voice, and with it he united the lands of men into a great Empire. In southern lands, he was known by the name Tiber Septim. Here in Skyrim, we honor him by his proper Nord name. So great was his reign in life, when he ascended to the heavens he was made lord of the Divines. If you want to know more, I'm sure you can find any number of tomes on the subject.
Skyrim.esm|09312C: That is an old tale, but well known to those of us who serve in the keep. Long ago, there lived a mighty hero of the Nords. Olaf One-Eye. Olaf did battle with a fearsome dragon named Numinex. The struggle between Olaf and the dragon culminated in a mighty duel atop Mount Anthor, with Olaf the victor. Olaf returned in triumph to Whiterun. By his decree, the city's keep was rebuilt as a prison for Numinex. And so, from that time until this, our great keep has been called "Dragonsreach."
Skyrim.esm|09312D: Ulfric Stormcloak's head on a pike. Do you think Ulfric really cares about Skyrim's independence, or the welfare or its people? I promise you, he doesn't. He's nothing more than a barbarian renegade, whose lust for power has cost the lives of countless innocents.
Skyrim.esm|09312E: Ah, splendid! Our fine city has a proud history, and I'm happy to share what I know. The history of Whiterun begins with Jorrvaskr, the hall of the Companions. For a long time, it was the only building on the mountain. As time passed, a thriving community sprang up around Jorrvaskr, as was common with mead halls. Today, Whiterun is the jewel of Skyrim. I can say without boast that is the greatest city in all the holds.
Skyrim.esm|09312F: I'll put it simply, so you'll understand. I advise the Jarl in matters of magic. If the hold is faced with any threats or mysteries of a magical nature, I am called upon to explain things and suggest a course of action. Hopefully that answer will satisfy you. Good day.
Skyrim.esm|093130: Let them come! I have no fear, for Talos is my ally and I am his prophet. His word is upon my lips, his voice in my throat.
Skyrim.esm|093131: With good planning and constant vigilance. Even now, my court wizard, Farengar, continues his research into ways we might drive back these terrors. We must also have ample reserves of water to combat the fires that will surely spread after an attack. But our greatest weapon? Courage. For if we cannot kill the beast, we must at least have the tenacity to drive it back. Until brave heroes step forward to destroy these monsters, that is the best we can do.
Skyrim.esm|093132: Somewhat, yes. At first it seemed a distant thing, heard only in the idle speech of guards and traders. When the wounded soldiers began to return from battle, I did what I could to help them. As more of the sick and injured came to the temple, my work as a healer became more important than my duties as a priestess. I wish only an end to the fighting, so that I can tend to the temple once more.
Skyrim.esm|093133: If I said that was a tedious question, would you be smart enough to know what I meant? Probably not. As long as I'm left alone to pursue my research, I don't care who gives the orders. Now, I'm a busy man and your pointless questions are boring me terribly. Good day.
Skyrim.esm|093134: Sadly, no. My work affords me few opportunities for such an adventure. Perhaps some hero will bring one to Dragonsreach, like old Olaf One-Eye once did. What a fascinating conversation that would be!
Skyrim.esm|093135: Because the so-called Emperor is a coward! That's right, I said coward! Oh yes! He agreed to banish the worship of Talos at the tip of an Aldmeri sword. They called it the "White-Gold Concordat." Well, I call it blasphemy! A true son of the Empire would never have turned his back on our greatest hero, not at any price. Well, let me tell you something, friend. Cyrodiil is a long way from here, and in Skyrim, we will never forsake mighty Talos!
Skyrim.esm|093136: Take your pick. Dragons, Stormcloak assassins, ambitious and unscrupulous nobles... Sometimes I think his own children want him dead. And those are just the physical threats. Assaults on his sanity are another matter entirely. Every day, he deals with obsequious functionaries and incompetent bureaucrats. Sadly, I'm not allowed to eliminate them. Well, not yet, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|093137: Indeed. The temple here in the city is my charge. The goddess's divine blessings have no doubt helped make Whiterun a thriving and prosperous city. After all, it is she who brings rain to our crops and fair weather on the harvest days.
Skyrim.esm|0931B8: That's right. Old inn to the south. The oaf doesn't even bother to visit these days. What am I supposed to do if the dragons burn down the farm? You're a traveler, right? I have this letter for him. Let him know how much of a failure he is.
Skyrim.esm|0931B9: Good. You make sure the lug tips you when he gets it.
Skyrim.esm|0931BB: Typical. Well don't mind me, then.
Skyrim.esm|0931BC: Orc poisons are to be feared, if you wish to trade.
Skyrim.esm|0931BD: What's this? I guess some things never change. Thanks for bringing this to me. Here, for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0932AD: I'm not sure I like these Alik'r warriors hiding out here. They seem like trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0932C5: Keep it to yourself. They're not paying us to talk.
Skyrim.esm|0932C8: They'll be gone as soon as they've found whoever they're looking for. And we'll have all the more coin.
Skyrim.esm|0934DF: The Stormcloaks are here. In force. Get down to the front line. This is it. We must hold the city. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|0934E0: The Imperials are here. All lined up pretty-like in their shiny armor. We'll put a few dents in those bastards before we give up the city. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|0934E2: Yes. Braith bullies me all the time. All the elders think I'm a milk drinker 'cause I can't fight. Nobody understands. If you can get Braith to leave me alone, I'll be your best friend... forever! I'll pay you, too! My whole life's savings - two septims!
Skyrim.esm|0934E4: Is it cold in here? I should put more wood on the fire...
Skyrim.esm|0934E5: Oh, I'm just a servant, dear. You want to talk to one of the Companions, I'm sure.
Skyrim.esm|0934E6: I've been tending to the warriors of Jorrvaskr for as long as I can remember.
Skyrim.esm|0934E7: I see everything, hear everything and know everything.
Skyrim.esm|0934E8: Oh, I'm a good keeper of secrets. Have to be, in this hall.
Skyrim.esm|0934F6: I don't know what you did, but Braith won't even look at me now! So thanks!
Skyrim.esm|0934F7: I don't fight Lars no more, if that's what you're worried about.
Skyrim.esm|0934F8: Okay, okay! I'll leave him alone! I was just kidding around. Besides, if he'd only kiss me, I wouldn't have to beat him up all the time...
Skyrim.esm|093569: That they might get themselves killed.
Skyrim.esm|09356A: Enough with the training. I want to go fight something.
Skyrim.esm|09356B: That's why it's best to not mistake foolhardiness for bravery.
Skyrim.esm|09356E: I'm a warrior, not a blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|09356F: Tiny blades don't hurt enough. A sword needs weight.
Skyrim.esm|093570: It's called a shortsword, oaf. It's quicker. Sharper. Like a razorfish against a whale.
Skyrim.esm|093571: But aren't the Circle in charge? And don't you lead them?
Skyrim.esm|093573: Just those same ruffians I was bringing in for the Shatter-Shields. So much wasted breath in that crew.
Skyrim.esm|093575: Are you prepared for tonight's hunt?
Skyrim.esm|093576: I worry that you've been spending too many nights out.
Skyrim.esm|093577: I'm imagining you just standing your ground and letting them tire themselves out.
Skyrim.esm|093578: I've heard some rumors... about you and Skjor.
Skyrim.esm|093579: Exactly. When you have a big heavy weight swinging around your upper body, your lower body has to compensate or you'll fall right over.
Skyrim.esm|09357A: Do you think I could be a member of the Circle? Some day? If I work hard?
Skyrim.esm|09357B: Aren't they slow? I can get in and out before you have one swing.
Skyrim.esm|09357C: Well which is it? Look farther or closer?
Skyrim.esm|09357E: Skjor has been a warrior for a long time. You would be wise to listen to him. Any warrior that gets to be old is either fearsome or a coward. I'll let you find out which Skjor is.
Skyrim.esm|09357F: Brave hearts beat lesser ones. That's the decider.
Skyrim.esm|093580: They're lording it over us all the time, though.
Skyrim.esm|093581: Worried one of them is going to take your place?
Skyrim.esm|093582: Well you can either get better with a bow or try to learn from me.
Skyrim.esm|093584: My title is Harbinger, child. I am no man's master.
Skyrim.esm|093585: No fighting just yet. Not till Skjor says so. We train now.
Skyrim.esm|093588: How are the young ones coming along?
Skyrim.esm|093589: Can't you just tell me if the grip is bad?
Skyrim.esm|09358A: I've been hunting on my own for years.
Skyrim.esm|09358B: I guide the Companions. The Circle advises me. But we are all free warriors. Only our honor binds us together.
Skyrim.esm|09358C: Just have them focus on the calm in the battle. Control the rage, don't let it control you.
Skyrim.esm|09358D: All I need is one to cut you in half.
Skyrim.esm|09358F: Where I go is my business. If you have a concern about my honor, bring it before the Circle.
Skyrim.esm|093590: Well, when you put it that way...
Skyrim.esm|093591: I'm telling you, it works. Let them ring their own ears on my shield, then scoop them up off the ground.
Skyrim.esm|093592: How do you manage to hold your ground like that?
Skyrim.esm|093593: I've been thinking we need to be more... discreet.
Skyrim.esm|093594: Don't worry about such things. Just focus on your own honor. Bring glory to the Companions, and that glory will return upon you.
Skyrim.esm|093595: Let's test that out some time.
Skyrim.esm|093597: Come on now, boy, you know what I meant...
Skyrim.esm|093598: Should I be holding the blade further away than I would a smaller one?
Skyrim.esm|093599: Tradition is very important to me, son.
Skyrim.esm|09359A: Young warriors are smart to listen to older ones. They've lived.
Skyrim.esm|09359B: Some of them might try. But that's not what I'm worried about.
Skyrim.esm|09359C: Forgive me. You're just the one in charge, and it's what I'm used to.
Skyrim.esm|09359D: Easy for you to say. You get to fight.
Skyrim.esm|09359E: Why can't I train with Aela?
Skyrim.esm|09359F: You're a strong warrior, Skjor. Your heart is fiery and that serves you well in battle.
Skyrim.esm|0935A0: What weapon do you like, Harbinger?
Skyrim.esm|0935A2: Skjor's heard rumors of a bear the size of a mammoth in Hammerfell. We're going to seek it out.
Skyrim.esm|0935A3: We all have different means to glory, Stonearm. Well done.
Skyrim.esm|0935A4: Some are too happy to fight. Blood runs hot.
Skyrim.esm|0935A5: If Eorlund made it, it's more likely you're gripping it wrong. Find me tomorrow and we can go over it.
Skyrim.esm|0935A6: I just want to make sure I'm moving up.
Skyrim.esm|0935A7: You have to remember, it's not about strength. It's about leverage.
Skyrim.esm|0935A9: I've never held a knitting needle.
Skyrim.esm|0935AD: It will feel that way for a while. But eventually you'll get used to it, and then you'll get faster with it. After that, the shortsword will feel like a knitting needle when you pick it up again.
Skyrim.esm|0935AE: There's nothing wrong with what we're doing.
Skyrim.esm|0935AF: Well, no. I'm just saying there's more to defense than muscle.
Skyrim.esm|0935B0: And if you want to keep having ears, you'll pretend you didn't.
Skyrim.esm|0935B2: If you keep it in close, you'll have more control, but it can be harder to balance a strike.
Skyrim.esm|0935B3: What kind of weapon do you prefer? I like the smaller, quicker blades.
Skyrim.esm|0935B6: No one is in charge here. Each Shield-Brother is his own man, each Shield-Sister her own woman.
Skyrim.esm|0935B7: To me as well. I think we don't see the same tradition at play here.
Skyrim.esm|0935B8: What glorious prey. Come back alive, and with stories to tell!
Skyrim.esm|0935B9: One day when you and I are on the Circle, we can change how things are run around here.
Skyrim.esm|0935BA: But before I fought, I had to train.
Skyrim.esm|0935BB: You'll do all right some day. Today you need to keep training, though.
Skyrim.esm|0935BC: Because you wanted to learn the longer blades. Aela's never used anything bigger than a dagger.
Skyrim.esm|0935BD: When I was young, I favored huge swords, much like you. Hard to lift these days, but I still enjoy the heft of a good hammer.
Skyrim.esm|0935BE: I've got a bad grip on my shield, could you take a look at it?
Skyrim.esm|0935BF: You only use the little swords.
Skyrim.esm|0935C0: And they always come to regret it.
Skyrim.esm|0935C1: I remember when you were the same way. The more they train, the more they'll cool down.
Skyrim.esm|0935C2: I'm sorry. I didn't mean to snap. Just... these hunters. We're all on edge.
Skyrim.esm|0935C3: This isn't the military, son. There are no ranks.
Skyrim.esm|0935C6: You might do well to learn a smaller weapon. Vary your style a bit.
Skyrim.esm|0935C7: Heard you had a bit of trouble near Solitude last week.
Skyrim.esm|0935C9: You know the old man doesn't like it. Let's just not be too obvious.
Skyrim.esm|0935CA: I guess that comes down to footwork, then.
Skyrim.esm|0935CB: Well why do I have to listen to Skjor, then?
Skyrim.esm|0935CC: Yes, you're very fond of saying that. You know I like hammers and axes.
Skyrim.esm|0935CD: It's not exactly forbidden, but even so... it's not true. So that's that.
Skyrim.esm|0935CE: Big swords should always beat little swords.
Skyrim.esm|0935D0: If you want to lead this pack when I'm gone, you have to see farther. Look closer.
Skyrim.esm|0935D1: I don't mean to confuse you.
Skyrim.esm|0935D2: I don't think that's how the Circle works.
Skyrim.esm|0935D3: It's just... I think our fighting styles are similar.
Skyrim.esm|0935D5: But even the bravest of men consider their actions. I want you to survive long enough to take the mantle.
Skyrim.esm|09360E: Good. We'll take a short break, then move on to wide-area destruction spells.
Skyrim.esm|0936CD: You - over here, quick. Before any of them show up!
Skyrim.esm|0936CE: Please, stranger! I need your help!
Skyrim.esm|093746: Great.  Here you go. Come back later and I'll have other things.
Skyrim.esm|093747: I knew he was troubled but one of the Thalmor? I wish I could say I did not believe it. Thank you for telling me.
Skyrim.esm|093748: Burying the past is much harder than burying remains. I'm... I'm afraid of what you will think of me if I tell you about the things I've done.
Skyrim.esm|093749: First off, let's keep this between us. I picked these up from the graves in town so don't go blabbing about it to Kust. You have to understand, Gentle Repose is a fallow festering place I should have left a long time ago. This is one of the few ways I make any money. Right now I've got a book that I think you might like.
Skyrim.esm|09374A: Hey friend, you wouldn't happen to be looking for unusual items would you?
Skyrim.esm|09374B: Although I have not practiced in a long time I was once a wizard. Even when I was young my magic was undeniably powerful. It was that power that brought me to the attention of the Thalmor. In the Summerset Isles it is a great honor to be chosen to serve the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|09374C: I knew he was troubled, but one of the Thalmor? I wish I could say I did not believe it. Thank you for telling me.
Skyrim.esm|09374D: They are the governing council of the Aldmeri Dominion. They lead us to attack the Empire and forced the Empire to agree to stop worshiping Talos. It was during the war with the Empire that I was at the height of my power. As a Thalmor battlemage I laid waste the enemy. Men, women, children, no one was spared my wrath. I destroyed whole villages.
Skyrim.esm|09374E: I knew he was troubled, but one of the Thalmor? I wish I could say I did not believe it. Thank you for telling me.
Skyrim.esm|09374F: Yeah, if your interested then I'll give you the book for free. If you like it you can come back later and I'll have other stuff. I mean, it don't cost me anything to let you have one and the dead won't notice their stuff missing. heh.
Skyrim.esm|093750: Thank you. May Arkay bless you. I won't forget this.
Skyrim.esm|093751: I knew something was up. I'll make sure he doesn't disrespect this place anymore. Honesty is a rare thing to find these days. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|093752: I dream about those days still. Except I'm not me. I can see myself there and I try to stop what I know is going to happen but... Many of the Empire's troops were Nords at that point. Some of them could even have been from around here.
Skyrim.esm|093773: My Star has been restored and Malyn's soul has been consigned to Oblivion. You have done well, mortal. As was destined, you are free to use my Star as you see fit.
Skyrim.esm|093775: Have faith, mortal. I will be watching over you.
Skyrim.esm|093776: Go ahead. Take it. I'd sooner finally put this chapter of my life behind me. Congratulations. You've altered the fate of a Daedric artifact and lived.
Skyrim.esm|093778: Now? You could use it for what we meant it for. Using black souls for enchanting. The Black Star will never decay, but it can no longer hold the white souls of lesser creatures. You keep it. I'd sooner finally put this chapter of my life behind me. Congratulations. You've changed the fate of a Daedric artifact and lived.
Skyrim.esm|093779: Easy there. The numbness will pass. Just don't strain yourself. We've done it. The Star has been cut off from Azura, and Malyn's soul finally has some measure of peace, even if it is in non-existence.
Skyrim.esm|09377B: Are you ready to enter the Star and purge the soul of Malyn Varen?
Skyrim.esm|09377D: Farewell, mortal. Know that Azura will be guarding over the threads of your fate in the Twilight.
Skyrim.esm|09377E: Oblivion has been watching you since the day you were conceived, mortal. Do not think that your life has been served by your will alone. Go now. I have seen the threads of your fate in the Twilight, and you still have much to accomplish.
Skyrim.esm|09377F: Azura is watching over you. She has seen this before it ever happened.
Skyrim.esm|093780: All will happen as it has been foretold.
Skyrim.esm|093781: The Twilight has foreseen all of this. Everything will be revealed in time.
Skyrim.esm|093782: I told you what you wanted to know. What else could you want?
Skyrim.esm|093786: This has all been foreseen, and we must play our parts.
Skyrim.esm|093787: Eventually, the Star will fade back into my realm in Oblivion, but I doubt you have the hundred or so years it would take to wait. No, only one option remains. I will send you inside the Star. You will banish Malyn's soul there. Tell me when you are ready, mortal.
Skyrim.esm|09378C: I don't deal with any College applicants these days, so don't bother asking.
Skyrim.esm|09378D: My days at the College are long behind me, but I prefer to stay close by.
Skyrim.esm|09378E: Dagur and I have an understanding. He gives me privacy, and I make sure my experiments don't blow up his inn.
Skyrim.esm|093809: Only if I can help it. But damn if I ain't almost sober. Hey. In the Bannered Mare, there's this bottle of Argonian Ale. The good stuff. Get it for me. They won't even know it's gone. Come on...
Skyrim.esm|093A6A: The front gate is locked so you'll need to use the mine entrance behind us.  Open the front gate and we'll try and give you some support.
Skyrim.esm|093A6B: You ask a lot of questions for a mercenary.  Just get in there and open the gate, we'll try and provide help once that's done.
Skyrim.esm|093A6C: I suspect Brurid planned this, there's been bad blood between us since the king's death. But to invade my home, kidnap my family...
Skyrim.esm|093A6D: Good, the place is crawling with bandits.  We lost some good men trying to take it earlier.
Skyrim.esm|093A6F: I will take back my family's castle.
Skyrim.esm|093A70: Agreed, we can't afford to try again.
Skyrim.esm|093A72: My family is everything to me.  Clear the fort and on top of the posted reward you can have whatever items you'd like.
Skyrim.esm|093BC0: I could not raise him, but I will raise an army to avenge his defilement!
Skyrim.esm|093BC3: You will not stop me from killing those who wage this pointless war!
Skyrim.esm|093BC6: Thank you for releasing us from her spell.
Skyrim.esm|093BC8: Rise from your graves to defeat this worm!
Skyrim.esm|093BC9: Now we can rest in peace once again. Come Fjori, my love...
Skyrim.esm|093BCB: I shall have vengeance for the death of my husband. Rise!
Skyrim.esm|093FDA: I'm going to eat well tonight, my darling!
Skyrim.esm|093FDB: No! You can't be here! You're all dead! I already killed you over and over!
Skyrim.esm|093FDC: Maybe I'm just getting soft in my old age, but I actually believe you.
Skyrim.esm|093FDD: That's a touching story. Now get outta my face.
Skyrim.esm|093FDE: A friend, huh? Well... then it's probably okay if I tell you.
Skyrim.esm|093FDF: I guess you don't hear too well. I never heard of this "friend" of yours.
Skyrim.esm|093FE0: There's no need for that. Not like he's a friend of mine or anything. Just calm down.
Skyrim.esm|093FE1: You've got a lot of nerve. But not a lot of common sense.
Skyrim.esm|093FE2: Easy there. I don't want that kind of trouble. Guy ain't worth it.
Skyrim.esm|093FE3: Yeah. That's what it's going to take.
Skyrim.esm|093FE4: Ah. Ha ha! Come here, come here come here come here!
Skyrim.esm|093FE5: Yes, yes. Closer. I won't bite. Promise.
Skyrim.esm|093FE6: Come over here. Please pretty please?
Skyrim.esm|093FE7: I have a present for you. A nice, shiny present.
Skyrim.esm|093FE9: Ah... so tender. Come here, you juicy morsel!
Skyrim.esm|093FEA: Go away. Go away. Go away!
Skyrim.esm|093FEB: It's hopeless. He told me. He's right, you know.
Skyrim.esm|093FEC: Their fires lit up the night. All across Lake Rumare, like stars come to earth. It was beautiful, really.
Skyrim.esm|093FED: They were golden, even when they were dead. But their blood was red. I knew it would be.
Skyrim.esm|093FEE: You don't think there's any hope, do you? I don't. He's right.
Skyrim.esm|093FEF: He explained it all to me. Before, when I still understood. I don't remember, but I still know.
Skyrim.esm|093FF0: He'll tell you. Just ask. You don't even need to ask. He'll tell you anyway.
Skyrim.esm|093FF1: I finally understand what it all means. What it was all for. The killing. And the dying.
Skyrim.esm|093FF2: I had a medal. It had someone's face on it. I threw it away, after. It didn't help.
Skyrim.esm|093FF3: Well... when you put it that way, I think I do know the old guy you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|093FF4: Big talk for someone with a light purse.
Skyrim.esm|093FF5: You know what? I think I do know the guy you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|093FF6: Maybe it would. If you had it.
Skyrim.esm|093FF8: Ha ha ha ha! No! Never find me!
Skyrim.esm|093FF9: Bucket. Knife. Book. Inkpot. Stone. No, no, no.
Skyrim.esm|093FFB: Knife. Yes. Book. Yes. Bucket. Yes. Inkpot? No.
Skyrim.esm|093FFC: Ah... so tender. Come here, you morsel!
Skyrim.esm|093FFD: Ah... so tender. Come here, you morsel!
Skyrim.esm|09403C: I don't expect you to be capable of even that.
Skyrim.esm|09403D: Winterhold has lost much of its history, and with that much of its power. I aim to change that. I've heard rumors of the resting place of the Helm of Winterhold, the very same helm that Jarl Hanse wore in the First Era. Hanse was in line to be High King of Skyrim, you know. Having that might get the ear of the other Holds and give me some authority.
Skyrim.esm|09403E: Nothing. I'm busy, and I don't like being bothered unless I'm expecting something from you. Oh, don't give me that look. Fine. I supposed I can find some menial task to set your mind on. Hmm.... We have something of a vampire problem. Bodies have been found with blood drained. I know of a den nearby you can wipe out as a precaution.
Skyrim.esm|094040: If I needed something from you, you would know it.
Skyrim.esm|094041: You're right. What would my forefathers think if they knew I wasn't paying my debts? Tell Haran I'll bring her the gold I owe.
Skyrim.esm|094042: And I told you I'd pay it when I'm good and ready.
Skyrim.esm|094045: Me? Nothing. And I hope to keep it that way. The Jarl is convinced I have some connection with the College, so he goes easy on me. Maybe you'd like to help me keep up appearances? Old Nelacar has a mage's staff in his room in the Frozen Hearth. It'd be good to have that staff in case the Jarl starts wondering if I actually have a real connection to the College.
Skyrim.esm|094048: Go right ahead. He just might be sober enough to listen to you.
Skyrim.esm|094049: Ranmir beg a drink from you, yet? That's gold wasted, friend.
Skyrim.esm|09404B: Thanks. I'll be waiting for it.
Skyrim.esm|094050: I picked up the trade when I was a girl. One of the temple healers staying here on a pilgrimage showed me the basics. She had a book with her, "Song of the Alchemists," that I used to read over and over. I miss having a copy.
Skyrim.esm|094051: That I am. Train the recruits. Turn boys into men. That sort of thing. Thinking of asking the General for some training books. Give the soldiers something to do while they're on rest. Something besides drinking. Ever seen a book called "The Mirror"? We used to hand it out to boys looking to learn basic shield work.
Skyrim.esm|094052: Want me to prove it? I bet a hundred gold I can take you, bare-handed.
Skyrim.esm|094074: He owes this tavern enough coin to burn it down, build it back up, then burn it down again just for laughs.
Skyrim.esm|094075: My brother is... sensitive. He has trouble focusing his attention on things. We were taking him to the Temple of Kynareth in Whiterun, just so the healers could make sure he was in good health, and they say he's fine. Are you heading to Whiterun? Could you take this letter to the priestess, Danica Pure-Spring? It's a note about Joric's health.
Skyrim.esm|094077: Who are you to say what I should do? I'll pay her back when I'm ready.
Skyrim.esm|094079: This should come in handy. Here. Let me show you a few tricks I've learned about buttering up royalty.
Skyrim.esm|09407B: I'm sure we'll get our gold at some point. Not like he has anywhere to go.
Skyrim.esm|09407C: Can you believe that this hold used to contend for the throne of the High King? Now perhaps others will listen. Here, your reward as promised. You have Winterhold's thanks.
Skyrim.esm|09407D: Thank you. He's not a bad man, just bad with his coins and his drink. My husband Dagur could tell you stories, there. I have a couple of Ranmir's things that he traded to us back when he was still paying regularly. I say you've earned them.
Skyrim.esm|094085: Filthy creatures, aren't they? Living in the darkness, like they do. So uncivilized. I prefer finery. Like this. Here, for your trouble. And I suppose I should show you a little about Illusion magic for free, shouldn't I?
Skyrim.esm|094086: I won't say no to that.
Skyrim.esm|094087: I'm not about to take on debts from some milk drinker I don't know.
Skyrim.esm|094088: You're a real fighter. I like that. You ever need my steel by your side, you just ask.
Skyrim.esm|094089: You got me, fair and square. Tell Haran I'll pay.
Skyrim.esm|09408A: I'm the best warrior in Morthal, and that's no boast.
Skyrim.esm|09408C: It's amazing how much you can learn from a book. Here. Let me show you some of the things the temple healers taught me about potions.
Skyrim.esm|09408D: Don't know what the Redguards see in books, but this will make my Seren happy. Thank you. Let me show you some tricks around the forge.
Skyrim.esm|09408F: Then I have no work for you.
Skyrim.esm|094094: Be glad you're not training under me with that mouth of yours.
Skyrim.esm|094096: Joric's sister? Poor child. Magic runs deep in that family's bloodline. It's not always a blessing. Thank you for this. Something for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0940C6: Just your own two hands. Weapons and magic are out. Now let's see what you got!
Skyrim.esm|0940C7: Come back when you have the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0940C8: Really? I'd be happy to show you a bit about herbs if you find the book.
Skyrim.esm|0940CB: Calm down there, friend. I'll pay! Tell Haran that she'll have her gold.
Skyrim.esm|0940CD: Good. I'll have a sum of gold waiting for your return.
Skyrim.esm|0940CF: What was that? Some kind of foreign move?
Skyrim.esm|0940EC: Oh yes. You're a fine fat one, aren't you!
Skyrim.esm|0940ED: No, no, no. I've got more blood than you know!
Skyrim.esm|0940EE: Give me just one... little... bite!
Skyrim.esm|0940EF: Ah, yes. No fat on this one!
Skyrim.esm|0940F1: I've been waiting for you, my sweet one.
Skyrim.esm|09414A: That's the first bit of good news I've had since I set sail a year ago. Here's your cut.
Skyrim.esm|09414C: Sailors are what happened to them. They got drunk and ended up losing the merchandise in some cave. If you're not afraid of little exploring, I'll pay good gold to get those salts.
Skyrim.esm|09414D: By inheritance, as my men are fond of saying. Can't blame them, my mother ran the Sea Squall as strictly as she ran the house. I've been trying to find new areas of business, trying to win the sailors' respect, but I keep having problems. Take the last trade we did. Some Fine-Cut Void Salts from the College of Winterhold. Plenty of coin if you know who to sell it to.
Skyrim.esm|09414E: Fine work. Here's your pay. Don't spend it all on mead.
Skyrim.esm|094151: The North Wind is my mistress, and I'm her master. I don't tolerate any disloyalty. You looking for work? Got a sailor who thought running a gang of thieves would be more money than hauling my cargo. You make an example of that gang, I'll give you a year's worth of pay.
Skyrim.esm|094171: Well, not yet. The Empire hasn't been in charge for that long, and real change takes time. Still, having a just and honorable man like Brunwulf in charge can only be an improvement. Unlike Ulfric, Brunwulf has shown a great willingness to work with the other races and make us feel more at home here. For the first time in a long while, I truly believe that there are brighter days ahead.
Skyrim.esm|094172: You have no idea. Did you know it was his decree that forbade the Argonians from living inside the city walls? I hope in his next life, he's reborn as an Argonian forced to live in a slum because of some bigoted Nord dictator. I'm joking, of course, but I'm a lot happier seeing the Empire running things in Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|094173: Yes, markedly so. Ulfric and his Stormcloaks made no secret of their disdain for the Dark Elves. In fact, they didn't seem to care much for anyone who wasn't a Nord. The folk of the Empire have always had a more cosmopolitan view of the world, so I'm glad to see them in charge.
Skyrim.esm|094174: I've taken the first steps toward doing so already. I met with several of the Dark Elves to discuss improvements in the Gray Quarter, but we have no real plans as of yet. First we'll need to refill our coffers and stockpile stone and wood. The war took a heavy toll on Windhelm's resources. But I swore an oath to our Dunmer friends that, for as long as I sit on the throne of Windhelm, their needs will not be ignored.
Skyrim.esm|094175: I'd like to, but it's not as simple as that. Most of the folk in the city believe as Ulfric did, that outsiders should not be trusted. Until those people learn to accept the Argonians, they must remain outside, for their own safety. Old habits don't die easily, and we Nords can be as stubborn as stone.
Skyrim.esm|094176: Every day is a struggle, but a Nord doesn't back down from a fight when the cause is honorable. I just wish things were simpler. In my days as a soldier, I knew who the enemy was, and how to defeat him. Unfortunately, a sword can't repair damaged buildings, feed hungry people or undo years of prejudice.
Skyrim.esm|094177: I don't care what the Imperials say -- when Ulfric Stormcloak died, one of Skyrim's greatest heroes was lost. Now at last, the Thalmor will get their way. The worship of Talos will finally be stamped out. Or so they think. Those true sons and daughters of Skyrim will never cease to worship mighty Talos. If we cannot do so in our temples, we will do so in our hearts. Oblivion take the Thalmor! I'll never give up Talos, not even if it means my life. Let them come and make me a martyr!
Skyrim.esm|094178: Aye, that I do. I ain't sayin' he was perfect, but he knew what it meant to be a Nord. The Empire's always taken us for granted. Nords died by the thousands in the Great War, and what did the Empire do? They outlawed the worship of Talos, Skyrim's most beloved son. There's thanks for you. I guess what I'm sayin' is, Ulfric stood up for us, an' for all Skyrim. So who's goin' to stand up for us now?
Skyrim.esm|094179: I almost didn't. I wasn't sure I could stomach having some Imperial tell me how to live my life. I was ready to pack my things when Hermir came to me. She wanted to make sure I'd finish teaching her the smithing trade. That girl's determination won me over. I suppose it didn't hurt that she said she only wanted to learn from the best smith in Skyrim. I couldn't let her run off to that pretender Eorlund Gray-Mane, could I?
Skyrim.esm|09417A: You'd better have a damned good reason for being here...
Skyrim.esm|09417B: It takes a brave soul to wander onto my deck. It'll take more than that to leave in one piece.
Skyrim.esm|09417C: I'd say you're either brave or stupid, but I can already tell which one it is.
Skyrim.esm|09417D: You must have a death wish.
Skyrim.esm|09417E: You reek. A little friendly advice - take a bath and get some new clothes.
Skyrim.esm|09417F: Whatever you're about to say, don't bother. I don't want to hear it.
Skyrim.esm|094180: What hole did you crawl out of?
Skyrim.esm|094181: Out of my way, or I'll cut you. Deep.
Skyrim.esm|094182: In some ways, yes, but there is often opportunity in chaos. When a new government takes charge, records can be lost, taxes can be forgotten and laws aren't always enforced. The new guards are often too busy learning their responsibilities to notice a few smuggled goods or minor robberies. Not that I'd ever get involved in any of those kinds of things, of course.
Skyrim.esm|094184: To receive the blessing of Talos, you have only to pray at the altar.
Skyrim.esm|094185: You know about Talos? He founded the Empire!
Skyrim.esm|094186: I wish the Nords, Argonians and dark elves in Windhelm got along better.
Skyrim.esm|094187: I don't care that we lost, I'm just glad the war is over.
Skyrim.esm|094189: All my goods are legitimate, which is more than I can say for some.
Skyrim.esm|09418A: Things have been a lot worse around here since Ulfric took over.
Skyrim.esm|09418B: Everyone says Eorlund Gray-Mane is the best smith in Skyrim. I plan to change a few minds about that.
Skyrim.esm|09418C: I've spent my life serving Talos. I don't plan to stop now, no matter what the Empire says.
Skyrim.esm|09418E: It'll take time to rebuild, but the Nords are no strangers to hard work.
Skyrim.esm|094196: The dark elves and Argonians won't soon forget Ulfric's prejudice, but I'm doing what I can to repair that rift.
Skyrim.esm|094197: Being a Jarl isn't something I ever wanted, but if I can help these people, then I will.
Skyrim.esm|094198: Got no time for a Nord who thinks he's better than everyone else. Keep walking.
Skyrim.esm|094199: I thought the the Gray Quarter would be a haven for my kind. I was wrong.
Skyrim.esm|09425B: Fools! You will not hold me here any longer, Valdar!
Skyrim.esm|094397: I spit on you, dead sister.
Skyrim.esm|094398: Ah, my parlor. No finer place to boil eyes, no...
Skyrim.esm|0946F0: A fateful errand.  No few have chafed to face the Worm since first he set his soul-snare here at Sovngarde's threshold. But Shor restrained our wrathful onslaught - perhaps, deep-counselled, your doom he foresaw.
Skyrim.esm|0946F1: No shade are you, as usually here passes, but living, you dare the land of the dead. By what right do you request entry?
Skyrim.esm|0946F6: From Adrianne? Ah, this must be that weapon for the Jarl. Poor girl, so eager to prove herself. I'll present it to Balgruuf when his mood is... agreeable. Thank you. Please, take these few coins, for services rendered.
Skyrim.esm|0946F7: Aye, that I do. I've got to, if I hope to be as good as Eorlund Gray-Mane some day. In fact, I just finished my best piece of work. It's a sword. I made it for the Jarl, Balgruuf the Greater. It's a surprise, and I don't even know if he'll accept it. But... Listen, could you take the sword to my father, Proventus Avenicci? He's the Jarl's steward. He'll know the right time to present it to him.
Skyrim.esm|0947CF: None may pass this perilous bridge 'til I judge them worthy by the warrior's test.
Skyrim.esm|0949C3: Goodness, no! They're simply scholars, they mean no one harm. I've had... several conversations with Arch-Mage Aren over the years. He's perfectly polite, if a bit guarded.
Skyrim.esm|0949C4: Just about eighty years ago, there was a terrible disaster. The cliffs overlooking the Sea of Ghosts collapsed, taking most of Winterhold with them. In the middle of it all, the College was practically untouched. Many of the survivors were suspicious. Some believed the mages were behind the whole thing, and others felt they could have at least prevented it. Arch-Mage Aren assures me that his people had nothing to do with it, and I believe him. But Winterhold never recovered.
Skyrim.esm|0949C5: You may have noticed the College, just to the north there. Very prestigious place. Mages from all over Tamriel traveled here to seek knowledge. After the Oblivion Crisis was over, many felt that magic users were to blame. Elves, to be specific. It created a great deal of tension. A good many dark elves were driven from the city, and people became... uncomfortable with the presence of the College.
Skyrim.esm|094A24: Yeah, but raising Draugr to take on both the Imperials and the Stormcloaks? That's just crazy.
Skyrim.esm|094A25: I think it's sweet Lu'ah is so worked up over her dead soldier...
Skyrim.esm|094A26: True, but I'm not going to be the one to try to oppose her...
Skyrim.esm|094C4D: Shor's Bones! Look at that monstrosity.
Skyrim.esm|094C4E: This is where we saw Mercer. We must be getting close.
Skyrim.esm|094C4F: I can hear water rushing through these pipes. We must be beneath a lake.
Skyrim.esm|094C50: Aye, lass... and we'll be ready for him. Let's keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|094C51: Looks like we can take the low road or the high road across this chamber. Your choice.
Skyrim.esm|094C52: Aye. Whatever you want to do, we're with you.
Skyrim.esm|094C53: The stench... this place reeks of Falmer.
Skyrim.esm|094C54: It's a Dwarven Centurion. Very tough and very deadly.
Skyrim.esm|094C56: So this is what we heard. The entire tower collapsed.
Skyrim.esm|094C58: Falmer patrol up ahead. I don't think they know we're here.
Skyrim.esm|094C59: Look at the size of this place. Have you ever seen anything like it in your life, lass?
Skyrim.esm|094C5B: Brynjolf and I found them like that. Mercer's doing. We have to catch up to him before it's too late.
Skyrim.esm|094C5C: This must be their hive. We'll have to keep silent if we want to avoid drawing their attention.
Skyrim.esm|094C5D: The only reason to do that would be to block pursuit. It must be Mercer. We'll have to find another way around.
Skyrim.esm|094C5E: Damn it! There's no way through.
Skyrim.esm|094C5F: Mercer must have triggered these pistons. We're going to have to search for a way to open them.
Skyrim.esm|094C60: He's close. I'm certain of it. We must prepare ourselves.
Skyrim.esm|094C63: Even the Falmer don't deserve the pain these implements must have inflicted. The dwarves were a cruel race.
Skyrim.esm|094C65: I'll bet my last septim that there's a mechanism around here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|094C66: He's toying with us. He wants us to follow.
Skyrim.esm|094C68: Then this is it. We do this for Gallus and for the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|094C69: We can take the beast on or sneak around. It's your call, lad. We're right behind you.
Skyrim.esm|094C6A: We can take the beast on or sneak around. It's your call, lass. We're right behind you.
Skyrim.esm|094C6B: Can't say that I have. Imagine the riches hidden within these walls.
Skyrim.esm|094C6C: There's a mass of the Falmer in this chamber. We can sneak through or take them down... I don't care. As long as we get to Mercer.
Skyrim.esm|094D7C: It's the past! Dead oaths on dead lips. Let it stay there.
Skyrim.esm|094D7D: Your ancestors wouldn't want this, Silus!
Skyrim.esm|094D7F: Why should I hide from it? This is my family's legacy!
Skyrim.esm|094D80: There's a new museum opening up in Dawnstar. The owner is asking me to hand out invitations to travelers.
Skyrim.esm|094E33: My Amulet of Arkay. It's the source of my divine powers and also a sacred badge of office. I misplaced it in the catacombs. I'd look for it, but I've been hearing noises down there. I fear the dead have become... restless. Without that amulet, I'm powerless to confront them. Would you be willing to search for it?
Skyrim.esm|094E34: Ah, splendid, splendid. It's for a special brew I'm working on. A love elixir like none other. Maybe I'll test it on Farengar first... Oh, but I suppose you expect some compensation. Um... here, these potions should suffice.
Skyrim.esm|094E35: I'll make sure the Emperor himself hears of all you've accomplished. You've made Skyrim a better place.
Skyrim.esm|094E36: The leader of the Dark Brotherhood? You mean Astrid? Dead? And this is no jest? Ha! This is a stroke of good fortune. Long have I watched the Dark Brotherhood's movements... waiting for the time to strike. That time is now! My agents have recently acquired the passphrase to their Sanctuary. It is, "Silence, my brother." Every assassin in that hole must be put down! You, my friend. You've slain their leader. This honor should be yours. Do this, and you will be rewarded most handsomely!
Skyrim.esm|094E37: We have a chance to destroy the Dark Brotherhood, forever. It's all up to you.
Skyrim.esm|094E38: By all I hold holy... Can it be true? Have you actually done it? This is a great day, my friend. You have struck a blow against darkness that will not soon be forgotten! Here, as promised - a most generous reward!
Skyrim.esm|094E3A: You did what? By the gods, you're serious. You'd better report this to Commander Maro right away. He's at the Penitus Oculatus outpost, up in Dragon Bridge.
Skyrim.esm|094E3B: I believe I am, yes. Technically speaking, of course. The city is also home to a priest, priestess, an alchemist, and I'm sure others who practice. Ah, that reminds me. Speaking of alchemists, I have some frost salts for Arcadia. She asked me to obtain them for one of her potions. Would you be so kind as to deliver the frost salts for me? I'm sure Arcadia will provide some form of recompense...
Skyrim.esm|095123: The Jarl has put out a bounty on some bandits. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Skyrim.esm|0951BB: Mercer was able to knock this thing down? Gods...
Skyrim.esm|0951BC: It's the Key, Brynjolf. In his hands, there's no telling what he's capable of.
Skyrim.esm|0954C5: Not now. If I don't pull night duty, I'll drink a round with you at the Sleeping Giant.
Skyrim.esm|0954C6: What? What is it now, mother?
Skyrim.esm|0954C7: If he does, tell him how bad the food is. And how dangerous it is.
Skyrim.esm|0954C8: It was as big as the mountain, and black as night. It flew right over the barrow.
Skyrim.esm|0954C9: Look old woman. If your boy wants to learn how to fight, we'll teach him. Now off with you.
Skyrim.esm|0954CA: You're always thirsty. Just like you're always drunk. Now let me get back to my rounds.
Skyrim.esm|0954CC: A dragon! I saw a dragon!
Skyrim.esm|0954D1: Has my son Sven asked to join the war?
Skyrim.esm|0954D2: My favorite drinking buddy! Let's get some mead.
Skyrim.esm|0954D3: Dragons, now, is it? Please, mother. If you keep on like this everyone in town will think you're crazy.
Skyrim.esm|095584: Has the Bloodmoon called you, fellow Hunter?
Skyrim.esm|095585: The prey is strong. Stronger than the hunters. But more will come. Bring him down, for the glories of Lord Hircine.
Skyrim.esm|09564A: Can it be? You've brought me my amulet? Oh, thank Arkay. And thank you. Oh, dear friend, please, take this gold for your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|095676: What the... we've been betrayed! Look out!
Skyrim.esm|095677: No. I will have no peace until these hunters are dead.
Skyrim.esm|0956B1: This is the resting place of Ysgramor. And his most trusted generals. You should be cautious.
Skyrim.esm|0956B3: Don't get too flowery on me, lass.
Skyrim.esm|0957F7: Oh it's you. The one from the market.
Skyrim.esm|095813: Don't kill me.... I don't want to die!
Skyrim.esm|09583F: We drink to our youth, to days come and gone. For the age of aggression is just about done.
Skyrim.esm|095840: We'll drive out the Stormcloaks and restore what we own. With our blood and our steel we'll take back our home.
Skyrim.esm|095841: Down with Ulfric the killer of kings. On the day of your death we'll drink and we'll sing.
Skyrim.esm|0958B1: J'zargo is eager to hear how the scrolls are working.
Skyrim.esm|0958B2: J'zargo has seen that Tolfdir likes you. You must be very skilled with magic, yes? J'zargo is working hard, but cannot do it alone. Can you help with this?
Skyrim.esm|095C37: Brynjolf's been sending idiots like you down here for years looking for their hideout. Funny thing is his stupid Thieves Guild never counted on me and my partner blocking the way. Now, empty your pockets or I'll pick the gold off your corpse.
Skyrim.esm|095C38: Hey, you. Stop right there! Empty your pockets or end up as skeever food.
Skyrim.esm|095C39: I'm going to check the entrance to the Ratway. Be right back.
Skyrim.esm|095CB1: Don't worry, Hewnon will let you pass.
Skyrim.esm|095CB2: We won't give you any more trouble.
Skyrim.esm|095CB8: Sorry to have bothered you. I'll just be out of your way now.
Skyrim.esm|095D2E: Ignoring me, huh? Suit yourself. Get 'em Hewnon!
Skyrim.esm|095D2F: You worry about bashing people's heads in, I'll worry about the Guild. Okay?
Skyrim.esm|095D31: This far? We're livin' in a sewer. You said we'd have a house as big as the Black-Briars' by now.
Skyrim.esm|095E7C: You aren't going to pass. You're going to give me everything you have. Whether you leave here in one piece is up to you.
Skyrim.esm|095E7D: Why are you always acting like such a big baby? I've gotten us this far.
Skyrim.esm|095E7E: I dunno, Drahff. They'd skin us alive if they knew we were doin' this.
Skyrim.esm|095E7F: No need to get hasty. I... I was just testing you. You can go on ahead. Let 'em go, Hewnon. All clear.
Skyrim.esm|096200: J'zargo has worked hard on learning new spells, trying new things. This will make J'zargo stand out as a mage. But J'zargo works so hard, there is no time to test these new spells. If you help with the testing, this will make learning easier. J'zargo will give you scrolls, you will use them and report the results. This is a good plan, no? You tell J'zargo when you are ready.
Skyrim.esm|096439: You should buy some produce from Carlotta's stall. Most of it comes from my farm.
Skyrim.esm|09643A: You should stop by my farm if you get a chance. See what life's really about.
Skyrim.esm|09643B: There's a nip in the air. Cold won't be good for my crops. Never is.
Skyrim.esm|09643C: I own the Pelagia Farm, just outside the city walls. Nimriel takes good care of things for me.
Skyrim.esm|09643D: I had my years of war and fighting. Senseless, all of it. All I want now is a rake and a patch of good soil.
Skyrim.esm|096504: You'll need to do better than that.
Skyrim.esm|096507: ...gonna start keepin' a knife in my boot... tired of gettin' disarmed...
Skyrim.esm|096508: ...should have known she was lying... said she'd wait for me... but they never wait...
Skyrim.esm|096509: ...lyin' little harlot... that brat ain't mine... could be anyone's... won't get one rusty septim from me...
Skyrim.esm|09650A: ..."go to the college" da said... "use your smarts" he said... like I'm supposed to figure out which college he meant...
Skyrim.esm|096531: ...hmph, wizards... Now that's power... Bet they got that "secret" magic... can turn wood to gold... yeah... Wish I could turn wood to gold...
Skyrim.esm|096532: ...mead, mead, mead... ...kill 'em to get some beer every now and then? ...stupid bees and their stupid honey...
Skyrim.esm|096533: ...picking pockets, now that's the real art... ...no rough stuff, just take it and go... ...hmph, don't sound like much fun, though...
Skyrim.esm|096534: ...once was a woman, as fair as an evenin', of springtime in old Stros M'Kai...
Skyrim.esm|09653F: ...with three beers down, the Orc did frown, and bid the Elf goodbye... ...for none could know, 'twas not for show, and someone had to die...
Skyrim.esm|096540: ...kill him, he talks to me like that again... ...get him while he's sleeping... ...or poison his meat... ...see how he likes that...
Skyrim.esm|09654C: I swear to you, unnatural magics are coming from that cave! There are strange noises and lights! We need someone to investigate!
Skyrim.esm|09654F: Your eminence, my scrying has suggested nothing in the area. Dragon Bridge is under imperial control. This is likely superstitious nonsense.
Skyrim.esm|096550: Then we will immediately send out a legion to scour the cave and secure the town. Haafingar's people will always be safe under my rule.
Skyrim.esm|09655D: Die already so I can take your stuff!
Skyrim.esm|096587: Whatcha gonna do? Huh? Whatcha gonna do?
Skyrim.esm|096588: Gonna split your belly like an old woman's purse!
Skyrim.esm|09678D: Oh, I can smell your blood...
Skyrim.esm|09678F: Tell you what. You start running so I can stab you in the back.
Skyrim.esm|096791: Can't wait to count out your coin...
Skyrim.esm|096792: Fire, frost, or lightning - you will suffer at my hands!
Skyrim.esm|096793: Don't you see? I am master of the arcane!
Skyrim.esm|096796: First you... then all of the Reach!
Skyrim.esm|096797: The Forsworn will reclaim what is rightfully ours!
Skyrim.esm|096798: Foolish human. Your time is at its end!
Skyrim.esm|09679E: Time for you humans to learn your place!
Skyrim.esm|09679F: Soon, all Nords will be thralls to the Thalmor!
Skyrim.esm|0967A0: Nord beast! Both your life and lands are now forfeit!
Skyrim.esm|0967AA: Behold, the future. Behold, the Thalmor!
Skyrim.esm|0967BF: Ha ha ha ha ha! You wish to defeat me? Impossible.
Skyrim.esm|0967CE: Don't you see? Elven supremacy is the only truth!
Skyrim.esm|0967D4: Death is the only way out of your misery!
Skyrim.esm|0967E0: You are but a dog, and I am your master!
Skyrim.esm|0967E2: Ha! You can't resist, can you? Let's see... this one, I think. J'zargo has designed it to be an excellent flame cloak, with a twist. It is especially potent against the undead, and should burn them to a crisp in no time. J'zargo gives you plenty of these scrolls, so you try them and return when you can say whether they work.
Skyrim.esm|0967F2: We were betrayed by one of our own kind. In fact, I'm to blame for what's happened here.
Skyrim.esm|0967F3: I haven't heard that name in a long time. How do you know of me?
Skyrim.esm|0967F5: I was blinded. Blinded by dark treachery masquerading as friendship. Perhaps if I had been more vigilant, then Mercer Frey wouldn't have lured me to my fate and stolen the Skeleton Key.
Skyrim.esm|0967F6: I'm afraid so. I'm weakening, and I can feel myself slipping away. The years without restoration of my power have taken their toll. Whatever damage has been caused can only be corrected by following the Pilgrim's Path to the Ebonmere and replacing the Key.
Skyrim.esm|0967F7: I don't recognize you, but I sense that you're one of us. Who are you?
Skyrim.esm|0967F8: The Sepulcher isn't merely a temple or a vault to house the Key. Within these walls is the Ebonmere... a conduit to Nocturnal's realm of Evergloam. When Mercer stole the Key, that conduit closed, severely limiting our ties to her.
Skyrim.esm|0967F9: With the Ebonmere closed, and their sudden severance from the realm of Evergloam, I fear they've undergone a drastic change. They're shadows of their former selves. They no longer remember their true purpose or their original identities.
Skyrim.esm|0967FA: Nothing would bring me more pride than to return the Key, but I'm afraid it's impossible. From the moment I arrived here, I've felt myself... well... dying.
Skyrim.esm|0967FB: My spirit didn't manifest itself in the Sepulcher immediately, so fortunately I wasn't present when the Ebonmere was sealed. However, ever since that day, I've felt my power waning... slowly draining away.
Skyrim.esm|0967FC: The last of the Nightingale Sentinels, I'm afraid. I've defended the Sepulcher alone for what seems like an eternity.
Skyrim.esm|0968C4: I'll try and make this quick!
Skyrim.esm|096D13: You've arrived at last. The hero who prevented me from being bound returns to my fold.
Skyrim.esm|096D14: You've arrived at last. The heroine who prevented me from being bound returns to my fold.
Skyrim.esm|096D15: I have much to thank you for, little one. When you die I will raise you and you can take your place by my side.
Skyrim.esm|096FB6: You've done the Guild a great deed. And although they may not show it, I'm certain they appreciate your sacrifices. My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
Skyrim.esm|096FB7: Were I able to provide it, I'd shower you in wealth for what you've done. My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
Skyrim.esm|096FB8: Yes, absolutely. Your skill is your own... don't let anyone ever tell you otherwise. But Nocturnal, she influences our luck; nearly imperceptible assistance we get when we ply our trade.
Skyrim.esm|096FB9: Precisely. There are a few who still call Nocturnal "Lady Luck" and for good reason.
Skyrim.esm|096FBA: Think about the Guild... about the state it was in when you began. Think about all the little things you might have heard. A pick breaking when it shouldn't have, the clouds in the nighttime sky clearing at the wrong moment... Our access to those bits of luck are what separates us from common bandits.
Skyrim.esm|096FC6: That burned paper is all that remains of the fabled Mysterium Xarxes. The blasphemous book written by Mehrunes Dagon himself. It's said that Mankar Camoran used the book to open a portal to a Paradise where all his followers would live forever.
Skyrim.esm|096FC7: Feel free to look around. Come talk to me when you're ready to discuss that job I mentioned.
Skyrim.esm|096FC8: The Commentaries on the Mysterium Xarxes were written by the Mythic Dawn cult leader, Mankar Camoran. He promised a paradise awaited his followers when they died, that they would be reborn by Mehrunes Dagon's side.
Skyrim.esm|096FCE: The tapestries hung here and outside were found in hideouts where members of the Mythic Dawn would meet and plot. The cult's greatest accomplishment was the assassination of the Septim Dynasty and the opening of the Oblivion Gates.
Skyrim.esm|096FD2: Those robes were worn during the Mythic Dawn's secret meetings, where they plotted to bring the Daedra Mehrunes Dagon into Tamriel. Each bolt of yarn used to make the robe was colored with a dye made from sacrificial blood.
Skyrim.esm|096FD5: Did you have any questions about the museum, or would you rather talk business?
Skyrim.esm|096FF8: At long last! Alduin's doom is now ours to seal - just speak the word and with high hearts we'll hasten forth to smite the worm wherever he lurks.
Skyrim.esm|096FF9: Hold, comrades - let us counsel take before battle is blindly joined.
Skyrim.esm|097250: Speak with the Nervous Patron at Candlehearth Hall, in Windhelm. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|097251: Speak with the Wary Outlaw at the Bee and Barb, in Riften. Accept his gold, and then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|097252: Yet another child has prayed to their mother.
Skyrim.esm|097253: If anybody asks, this meeting never took place.
Skyrim.esm|097263: But our work is just beginning. Approach me, Listener, and hear my words.
Skyrim.esm|097270: And here you are. I hope you've found the museum to your liking.
Skyrim.esm|09739C: Our master craftsman are nothing compared to the perfection of the Daedra. Look at how it shines. Here's your gold, as promised.
Skyrim.esm|09739D: A little history, first. After the Oblivion Crisis, a number of groups cropped up dedicated to wiping out the remnants of the Mythic Dawn. One of these groups found Mehrunes' Razor, the artifact of Dagon. They split it into three fragments and pledged to keep them apart forever. That was almost 150 years ago, and the pieces are still being kept by the descendants of that group. And they're right here, in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|09739E: Did you try the grip? Isn't it eerie how it seems to mold itself into your hand? Here's your gold.
Skyrim.esm|09739F: Don't know what you're talking about, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|0973A3: At least two of the owners, Ghunzul and Drascua, are dangerous marauders. And the third owner, Jorgen, I only know he lives in Morthal. Here are my notes about them. I'll gladly pay you for getting the pieces any way you can. No questions asked.
Skyrim.esm|0973A4: Look at them. The legendary sharpness of a deadric weapon. Marvelous. And here's your payment for the shards.
Skyrim.esm|0973A5: Finally, all the pieces of Mehrunes' Razor are in my hands. It's time I let you in on something. There's a fourth piece. That scabbard in the display case. Built to house the Razor. And there's more. I know how to put all the pieces together. We just need to take them to Dagon's shrine and contact the Lord of Change directly.
Skyrim.esm|09753F: I'll just be tending the museum if you need me.
Skyrim.esm|097543: What is it? Do you have one of the Razor fragments?
Skyrim.esm|097592: All right. Head to the pit when you think you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|097593: What is this, you think you're clever? If you want to fight your way out of here, you have to do it in the Pit. Gotta give the crowd a show. Luckily there's no shortage of Pit bait around here. Now get in there and earn your freedom.
Skyrim.esm|09759C: Why, perhaps the greatest crime spree Skyrim's ever known. There are pockets brimming with coin and coffers overflowing with riches ripe for the picking. We may be Nightingales, but in our hearts we're still thieves and we're damn good at what we do.
Skyrim.esm|09759D: I wouldn't take that to heart. It's her way. Think of her as a scolding mother continually pushing you harder to be successful; outwardly sounding angry but silently content. I assure you, had she been displeased with you, we wouldn't be having this conversation.
Skyrim.esm|09759E: This is Nocturnal's way of maintaining balance. If you ever feel the need to change your abilities, you can return to the Sepulcher and step onto a different circle. Be warned that once you've chosen, you can't reselect for at least a day.
Skyrim.esm|09759F: The circles at the base of the Ebonmere imbue you with powers befitting a Nightingale Agent. The crescent moon represents the Agent of Shadow, the half moon for the Agent of Subterfuge and the full moon for the Agent of Strife.
Skyrim.esm|0975A0: The Guild has welcomed me back with open arms. I feel like a void in my life has finally been filled. I only hope that this isn't an ending to things, but actually the beginning.
Skyrim.esm|0975A1: Now, your life as a Nightingale begins. Should the need arise, you'll be summoned to the Sepulcher in order to defend it.
Skyrim.esm|0975A2: I'm glad you were able to bring the Key back safely. Nocturnal seemed quite pleased with your efforts.
Skyrim.esm|0976B8: Yeah. I'm glad we can finally stand down- getting tired of looking over my shoulder all the time.
Skyrim.esm|0976D0: Well, it's all over now. Lieutenant said you could see the smoke from Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0976D1: I think we'll all feel better once we're back out to sea. Won't be long now.
Skyrim.esm|0976D2: Dragons, murder, rebellion... and we sail right into the middle of it with the Emperor on board.
Skyrim.esm|0976DD: Gods, I'll be glad to see the last of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|097A4E: Arniel, stay on your guard. We can't let harm come to these people.
Skyrim.esm|097D2D: You'd better get out of that Stormcloak gear. Today's not the day to go around dressed like a rebel.
Skyrim.esm|097EED: Only Dagon can declare if a pawn is worth keeping. I. Have. Spoken.
Skyrim.esm|097EEF: They were worshippers of Mehrunes Dagon, the Daedric Lord of Destruction and Change. The Mythic Dawn killed Uriel Septim VII and his heirs, triggering the events that lead to the Oblivion Crisis, when the Daedra invaded Tamriel. All that remains of the infamous cult, I've gathered in my museum.
Skyrim.esm|097EF0: I am pleased, mortal. I will give you my Razor. Use it to wreak havoc on Tamriel.
Skyrim.esm|097EF1: You. Mortal. You are worthy of speaking to. You have claimed the pieces of my Razor. It has been an amusing game to witness. But Dagon does not declare a winner while there is a pawn on the board. Kill Silus. He and his family have served their purpose.
Skyrim.esm|097EF2: Kill him. Take your rightful place as my champion, or I will crush you.
Skyrim.esm|097F73: I planned to kill Alain myself, you know. Nilsine, too. But lost my nerve. I even brewed a special poison, Lotus Extract. Maybe you could use it? Just coat your weapon with it. Then... you get the idea.
Skyrim.esm|097F7C: Spare me your pitiful pride. You are but a tool of my ambitions, mortal. Never forget that. Place your hands on my altar one last time, and you will witness the power of Mehrunes Dagon for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|097F7D: You have been a worthy tool. Fill the world with destruction in my name. Place your hands on my altar one last time, and you will witness the power of Mehrunes Dagon for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|097FCB: Your guy's hiding out in the Ratway Warrens and paying us good coin for nobody to know about it. Well, until now that is. Tell you what. Go ahead and deal with your business for now. When you're done, find me and we'll deal with mine.
Skyrim.esm|097FCC: Aye, you've got a point there.
Skyrim.esm|097FCD: Passing on a golden opportunity is worse.
Skyrim.esm|09820C: I may fall to your malice, but your fate is already written.
Skyrim.esm|09820D: You may strike me down, but your doom is near.
Skyrim.esm|09820E: You cannot defeat us! The Dragonborn still stands!
Skyrim.esm|09820F: I've waited an eternity for this day.
Skyrim.esm|098210: My debt is repaid at last.
Skyrim.esm|098211: I vowed to have your head.
Skyrim.esm|098212: Alduin escaped us long ago. Today we take our well-tempered revenge.
Skyrim.esm|098213: For a hundred lifetimes my heart has burned for revenge on Alduin too long delayed.
Skyrim.esm|098215: Your doom has come, damned worm!
Skyrim.esm|098216: Nowhere to hide! Hunter becomes our prey!
Skyrim.esm|098217: Die, World-Eater, in despair and fear!
Skyrim.esm|098218: Use Dragonrend, to drag him down!
Skyrim.esm|09821A: For Skyrim! For Shor! For Sovngarde's freedom!
Skyrim.esm|09821B: I've waited long for such a worthy opponent.
Skyrim.esm|09821C: The joy of battle burns in your heart!
Skyrim.esm|09821D: The path is closed, till your courage is tested.
Skyrim.esm|09821E: Prove worthy by warrior's test, and cross the bridge to the blessed hall.
Skyrim.esm|098220: Stand together and we shall defeat him!
Skyrim.esm|098222: No escape this time, foul worm!
Skyrim.esm|0983AF: Don't blame me if your feet get sore.
Skyrim.esm|0983B1: Didn't think you had the coin for it anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0983B2: Then off with ya. I've got better things to do.
Skyrim.esm|0983B3: Should have known you'd be a cheap one.
Skyrim.esm|0983B4: Then what are you wasting my time for?
Skyrim.esm|0983BF: Suppose so. Got one all saddled if you've got the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0983C5: If I had one. Sold my last one to you a bit ago.
Skyrim.esm|0983C6: Reckon you can. How much you willing to pay?
Skyrim.esm|0983C7: Nope. Sold my last one to some stranger.
Skyrim.esm|0983CC: It's for sale, if you've got the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0983D0: Not right now. You bought my last one.
Skyrim.esm|0983D1: That one with the saddle? How much are you offering?
Skyrim.esm|0983D2: Can't help you. They're all spoken for.
Skyrim.esm|0983D5: Don't mind if you do. What's your price?
Skyrim.esm|0983DC: If I had one you could. They're all spoken for.
Skyrim.esm|0983DE: I've got one all saddled up for you! What do you figure she's worth?
Skyrim.esm|0983ED: Not today. You should have come by earlier. Wait, didn't I sell me last one to you?
Skyrim.esm|0983EE: Sounds fair. She's yours. Like I said, she's all saddled.
Skyrim.esm|098410: When you got that much, we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|098411: Fair enough. You can have her. She's the one with the saddle.
Skyrim.esm|09841B: Didn't think you had the coin.
Skyrim.esm|09841D: That's your best offer? I suppose so. She's the one with the saddle.
Skyrim.esm|09841E: If you had it, you'd be riding and we wouldn't be talking.
Skyrim.esm|098422: That much? I mean, of course. If that's all you've got.
Skyrim.esm|098427: I'd sell her for that, but you ain't got it.
Skyrim.esm|098430: Sold. She's all yours. She's already saddled up.
Skyrim.esm|098431: That would work, except you ain't got.
Skyrim.esm|098437: Wish everyone haggled like you. She's all yours.
Skyrim.esm|098438: Too bad you don't have that much. Guess you'll have to walk.
Skyrim.esm|0985AC: Everyone on me. Let's show these Imperial milk drinkers what true Nords look like! Rrrrraaaaaarrrrr!!!!!
Skyrim.esm|0985AE: Whiterun's walls are tall, but they are old and crumbling, like the Empire whose Legion lines them.
Skyrim.esm|0985AF: They'll have set barricades to slow us down. Destroy them quickly, and take out the archers in the towers along the way.
Skyrim.esm|0985B0: The faster we take them out, the faster we can claim this city as ours!
Skyrim.esm|098633: Well, I'd better get back to work. You two make yourselves at home.
Skyrim.esm|098634: Well, I'd better get some sleep. Don't mind me, though. You two make yourselves at home.
Skyrim.esm|098644: Of course. Nothing gets done without my approval in this city. I have the Jarl's ear, and the guards in my pocket. Anyone makes trouble for me and I pay a visit to the Thieves Guild. Make me angry and I contact the Dark Brotherhood. You'd do well to remember that the next time you make such a stupid observation.
Skyrim.esm|098649: The Black-Briar family has always been allied with the Guild. Our connections with the Empire and within Skyrim make for a perfect fit. I dare say the Guild owes its survival as much to my family as it does to its own people.
Skyrim.esm|09864A: And you think that I care? Interesting. I don't believe in getting my hands dirty. You have people that handle those sort of things... use them.
Skyrim.esm|09864C: Mara's domain encompasses the emotions we strive the most to embrace; love, compassion and understanding. It's difficult to appreciate her gifts in these dark times, but you should consider her light a beacon in the storm.
Skyrim.esm|09864D: The question is, what isn't wrong with Riften. This city is corrupt. Rotten to the core. No one cares about anything except themselves and how much coin they can make off the misery of others.
Skyrim.esm|09864E: This heap of matchsticks is what everyone calls Haelga's Bunkhouse. Can you stay here? No. The Bunkhouse is for the working man, not some sort of a luxury inn for tourists.
