Skyrim.esm|098650: Of course not. You expect me to take care of it by myself? My niece Svana helps me with the chores. She'd be more help if she kept her head out of the clouds. She's been with me ever since her parents were killed by bandits and she was dropped in my lap.
Skyrim.esm|098651: Authorities? Have you been listening to what I said? They're all dirty. Every one of them. The only way to get things done in this city is to keep your head down and pay off the right people.
Skyrim.esm|09878F: The Forsworn have taken Kolskeggr Mine. We're the only ones left.
Skyrim.esm|098790: Troll's blood, what happened to you Pavo?
Skyrim.esm|098792: Gods.... You two head inside and get some rest. I'll send word to the Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|098794: I'm in charge of Left Hand Mine. You cause trouble, you answer to me.
Skyrim.esm|09888C: You've come far, mortal, but can you stand against my inner council?  Let's see!
Skyrim.esm|09888D: Don't applaud yourself too soon, worm!
Skyrim.esm|09888E: Rip the eyes from her head!
Skyrim.esm|09888F: Rip the eyes from his head!
Skyrim.esm|098B5C: Oh ho. Now that's the Dark Brotherhood I know and love. This for business or pleasure? Ah, what's it matter, right? I'll see that it gets done.
Skyrim.esm|098B5E: Yeah, we can do that. I'm thinking a sewer grate on the outskirts of Dawnstar. Leads to a tunnel, then inside. You'll be in or out, quick as a coney.
Skyrim.esm|098B60: You're a little light on coin there, my friend. No gold, no refit.
Skyrim.esm|098B61: Tell me what you need, and I'll get my people get on it.
Skyrim.esm|098B62: I'm afraid that's it, my friend. I've done all I can. I hope the place is at least livable now. Good luck with, your, um... murders.
Skyrim.esm|098B63: Banners, huh? Trying to pretty up the place? All right, you got it.
Skyrim.esm|098B65: Now, might I offer some advice? You should go to Riften and find Delvin Mallory. I believe Astrid had you visit him once before? Mallory is an expert "obtainer of goods." We can use the money to repair and refit this Sanctuary. Make a true home for us, hmm? You do that, and I'll see what I can do about recruiting some new additions to our Family...
Skyrim.esm|098B66: That'll come in handy, yeah? An alchemy station, maybe a little garden with some choice plants. All right, consider it done.
Skyrim.esm|098B68: Heh. Chamber fit for a king and all that, right? No problem. Give ya a big bed, decorate the room real nice, maybe even throw in a few extra baubles.
Skyrim.esm|098BBF: Do not fail Kodlak. He's earned his place here, and does not deserve to fall prey to Alduin's insatiable hunger.
Skyrim.esm|098BC0: Hail, Dragonborn. That honor is also mine - to our shared birthright you'll bring new glory!
Skyrim.esm|098BC1: Dragonborn! Have you come to cleanse Sovngarde at long last of Alduin's foul mist?
Skyrim.esm|098BC2: Why does Shor forbid us to face Alduin? I fear no worm, World-Eater though he be.
Skyrim.esm|098BC3: I long to fight that foul worm, but by Shor's command we curb our wrath.
Skyrim.esm|098BC4: The mist is gone! A glorious day!
Skyrim.esm|098BC5: The World-Eater is dead! The way is now clear!
Skyrim.esm|098BC8: You saved us all. We will sing of you forever.
Skyrim.esm|098D46: You'd better speak to the Jarl, then.
Skyrim.esm|098D49: Oh, yes? We'll get to that, after I've finished dealing with this dragon situation.
Skyrim.esm|098D4A: Oh, yes? I wonder what that old busybody wants now. Whatever it is, it will have to wait until after I've finished dealing with this dragon situation.
Skyrim.esm|098D4B: And I'm sure he told you it was urgent. Well, he's not the only Jarl in Skyrim with problems to deal with. Whatever it is, it will have to wait until after I've finished dealing with this dragon situation.
Skyrim.esm|098D4E: So Delphine keeps up the fight, after all these years. You'd better come in and tell me how you found me and what you want.
Skyrim.esm|098D54: Delphine? How do you ... ? This is just a trick to get me to open the door, eh? I told you to go away!
Skyrim.esm|098E45: A hard truth, that is. Let's drink to the fallen ones.
Skyrim.esm|098E47: Oh, Sven. You do know how to turn a girl's head.
Skyrim.esm|098E48: You need to choose between those two boys. It ain't fair to Sven or Faendal to keep tuggin' on their heart strings like that.
Skyrim.esm|098E49: Just stay away from her, or...or else.
Skyrim.esm|098E4A: It's bad enough my brother is harping on me to pick one. Don't you start too.
Skyrim.esm|098E4B: Ah, Camilla. Fair as the morning sun. But I ask you, how could she prefer your brooding to my romantic tunes?
Skyrim.esm|098E4C: Ay, we can get that for ya. We just finished the timber for those catapults.
Skyrim.esm|098E4E: A pint of your finest mead.
Skyrim.esm|098E54: With you as my muse, how could I not?
Skyrim.esm|098E55: I'm just sayin' you should pick one to settle down with.
Skyrim.esm|098E56: I got a new order in for shields. I'm gonna need some hardwood planks. None of that soft pine this time.
Skyrim.esm|098E57: War may be good for business, but it's hard on families.
Skyrim.esm|098F7F: Hey, haven't I seen you around the Flagon? I never expected to see another Guild brother down here. They grabbed me in the Ratway. The nerve of these bastards... they need to be taught a lesson.
Skyrim.esm|098F8A: They grabbed me in Riften. They seem to think I know something. They just kept asking me the same damn questions.
Skyrim.esm|0991D1: Would you two shut up before Brurid hears you messing around.
Skyrim.esm|0991D3: Funny coming from a woman who's breath smells like she just kissed a draugr.
Skyrim.esm|0991D4: And it shows, I've seen horkers with smaller guts.
Skyrim.esm|0991DC: It's been forever since I've eaten so well.
Skyrim.esm|099393: Blood on the wind today. Good hunt ahead of us.
Skyrim.esm|099394: You look strong. Come to Jorrvaskr and be a Companion!
Skyrim.esm|099395: I swear, every wanderer who shows up in Whiterun looks weaker than the last.
Skyrim.esm|099396: Fight with us and you'll have stories to tell. Come up to Jorrvaskr if you're worth anything in a fight.
Skyrim.esm|0993DA: So we're just supposed to sit here while Arvel runs off with that golden claw?
Skyrim.esm|0993DB: I know I ran ahead with the claw, but I need help!
Skyrim.esm|0993DC: Is... is someone coming? Is that you Harknir? Bjorn? Soling?
Skyrim.esm|0993DD: Just shut it and keep an eye out for trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0993DE: Sweet breath of Arkay, thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0993DF: That dark elf wants to go on ahead, let him. Better than us risking our necks.
Skyrim.esm|0993E6: What if Arvel doesn't come back? I want my share from that claw!
Skyrim.esm|0993E8: Where are you going? Come back!
Skyrim.esm|0993E9: Does it look like I can move? You have to cut me down, first.
Skyrim.esm|0993EA: You fool, why should I share the treasure with anyone?
Skyrim.esm|0993EC: Yes, the claw. I know how it works. The claw, the markings, the door in the Hall of Stories. I know how they all fit together! Help me down, and I'll show you. You won't believe the power the Nords have hidden there.
Skyrim.esm|0993FF: It's coming loose. I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|099438: Where are you going? Come back!
Skyrim.esm|09944C: Cut me down, or you'll never get the claw!
Skyrim.esm|09944D: Where are you... get me down from this!
Skyrim.esm|09944E: Sorry, but I have a treasure to get to...
Skyrim.esm|099460: Don't leave me, for Arkay's sake!
Skyrim.esm|099465: Get me down. Get me down!
Skyrim.esm|099475: Get it away from me. Get it away!
Skyrim.esm|099477: Don't let it get me. Help!
Skyrim.esm|0994EE: That's it. Keep hacking at it.
Skyrim.esm|0994EF: You did it. You killed it. Now cut me down before anything else shows up.
Skyrim.esm|0994F5: What? Who are you? Oh, never mind. Cut me down before that thing gets us!
Skyrim.esm|09957F: The... the books? Oh, I see. I thought perhaps... Well, I thought you'd come for me. But yes, the books. The Caller will have them. She was most interested in one of the volumes. Although not interested enough to keep me from being locked up.
Skyrim.esm|099580: Oh, you've saved me! Thank you so much. Who knows what they'd have done to me if you hadn't come along. I promise I'll help you get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|099581: Why did I ever come here?
Skyrim.esm|0996DB: Like I said, I'm glad to help in any way I can. But I need your help. We need your help.
Skyrim.esm|099761: Occasionally. But an Orc blade is a very personal thing. Anger, regret, pride. All these things are put into our steel. When my daughter Lash chose to leave the stronghold, I forged a sword to channel all my shame and grief. Honor me with a task, outlander. Bring the sword to my daughter, if you see her. It'll be all she needs to know.
Skyrim.esm|099763: So that's it, then. "Don't come back." Thank you. Here. You honored my mother's task and should be rewarded. Now, I need to be alone.
Skyrim.esm|099C58: Listen to your friend, Fruki. They are just dreams, my dear. I assure you that it is quite normal.
Skyrim.esm|099C59: Everyone, please. I'm doing what I can to end these nightmares. In the meantime all I ask is you remain strong and put your trust in Lady Mara.
Skyrim.esm|099CF3: The shrine of Namira is further inside. Keep going.
Skyrim.esm|099CF5: You've returned. What happened in the Hall of the Dead?
Skyrim.esm|099CF6: Find out what's happening in the Hall of the Dead. Arkay will watch over you.
Skyrim.esm|099CF7: Tread lightly in the Hall. We don't know what's in there.
Skyrim.esm|099CF8: It's all right. I will not shun you for what you are. Stay. I will tell you everything you have forgotten.
Skyrim.esm|099CF9: A lot of our kind block out the memory of their first meal. The shame is too much. But you don't need to hide anymore. Namira, the Lady of Decay, accepts you for what you are. She has a place for us, where we can sate our appetites without judgment.
Skyrim.esm|099CFB: A priest, filled with the taste of an easy life. Brother Verulus, from Markarth. Give him this gold. Tell him you need Arkay's help exploring an old cavern for treasure. And when he stands in Namira's presence, she will take care of the rest.
Skyrim.esm|099CFD: I will be waiting at Reachcliff.
Skyrim.esm|099CFE: Say hello to Verulus for me.
Skyrim.esm|099CFF: The shrine of Namira is beyond that door. Get ready.
Skyrim.esm|099D14: "...and unwavering obedience to the officers of his great Empire."
Skyrim.esm|099D15: "May those above judge me, and those below take me, if I fail in my duty."
Skyrim.esm|099D17: "Long live the Emperor! Long live the Empire!"
Skyrim.esm|099D18: Welcome to the Imperial Legion, soldier. Just remember, we take care of our own. Once you're in the Legion, you're in it for life. Speak to Beirand, he's normally out by the forge. He'll get you outfitted. Now, if I'm not mistaken. Legate Rikke has a special assignment for you.
Skyrim.esm|099D59: I've already said too much. If anyone overhears what we're saying, it could start a panic. I would simply ask that you trust me and help me end Dawnstar's nightmares.
Skyrim.esm|099D5A: I'm concerned that when this place is unsealed, the Miasma will dissipate and they'll awaken; both orcs and priests alike.
Skyrim.esm|099D5B: The Miasma was created by the priests of Vaermina for their rituals. It's a gas that places the affected in a deep sleep. Because the rituals would last for months or even years, the Miasma was designed to slow down the aging process.
Skyrim.esm|099D5C: Sadly, yes. The longer an individual is exposed to the Miasma, the more the mind can become damaged. Those who've been under the effect of it for extended periods of time have been known to lose their minds entirely. In some cases, a few never awoke at all.
Skyrim.esm|099D8A: I'm a strict follower of the old traditions. Honor, strength, deeds.
Skyrim.esm|099DA4: "...Jarl of Windhelm and true High King of Skyrim."
Skyrim.esm|099DA5: "As Talos is my witness, may this oath bind me to death and beyond..."
Skyrim.esm|099DA6: "...even to my lord as to my fellow brothers and sisters in arms."
Skyrim.esm|099DA7: Now you're one of us. Which means you get to tag along on a little trip with me. Oh, and here. You're a Stormcloak now, you ought to look the part.
Skyrim.esm|099F1E: How quickly you've advanced. Most impressive. There are spells yet to be learned, powerful spells that can more closely and more permanently bind creatures to your will. But there are risks. There are always risks. You must summon and command an unbound Dremora.
Skyrim.esm|099F26: You dare bring me here? You must be punished.
Skyrim.esm|09A02F: That's right! Don't worry, Uncle Ralof, I won't let those soldiers sneak up on you.
Skyrim.esm|09A032: Here's a key to the house. Stay as long as you like. If there's anything else you need, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|09A033: Don't worry about me. I know how to lay low.
Skyrim.esm|09A035: What is it, woman? Sven drunk on the job again?
Skyrim.esm|09A036: I'm glad to assist you in any way I can. But I need your help. We need your help.
Skyrim.esm|09A03C: Hush, Frodnar. This is no time for your games.
Skyrim.esm|09A03D: Aw, mama, I want to stay and talk with Uncle Ralof!
Skyrim.esm|09A79F: Need a trained war dog? Fiercest companion money can buy.
Skyrim.esm|09A7A0: I'll be handling the horses with Cedran gone. Miss that old man.
Skyrim.esm|09A7A1: I like the loyalty of a dog. Not as fickle as a wolf.
Skyrim.esm|09A7A2: You have to train a good war dog from a pup. Bite your arm clean off otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|09A7A3: Good. Here you are. He'll follow you to Oblivion and back, if it comes to that.
Skyrim.esm|09A7D0: He had a way of enthralling people and built up a loyal army of followers. Good men fell under his spell and were forever twisted. Made... evil. After that, fear was enough to keep them in line. He could torture and humiliate them and they would not rebel. Even in death, they serve him still.
Skyrim.esm|09A7DF: Hevnoraak planned his resurrection obsessively before dying. My duty in life and in death is to make sure he stays in this tomb for eternity.
Skyrim.esm|09A806: Did you find all of the vessels?
Skyrim.esm|09A823: Do not interrupt the ritual. Go empty the vessels and sit on the throne.
Skyrim.esm|09B00C: Your daughters are growing up fast, Lemkil. Seems like only yesterday they were newborn babes.
Skyrim.esm|09B00D: I've told you a hundred times, I lock that chest for a reason. I don't want you getting a head full of ideas about adventure and glory.
Skyrim.esm|09B00E: It pleases me to know that I can still be of use even in the twilight of my life.
Skyrim.esm|09B00F: Hey, people have paid money for stranger things. Take our cow dung - we sell that by the wagon load.
Skyrim.esm|09B012: I'll tell you who I don't envy - those poor children who lost their parents in this war.
Skyrim.esm|09B013: You smell like a pig's pen, too. What a stink! Have you been rolling in the dirt all day?
Skyrim.esm|09B014: You're right, it's none of your business. How about you keep your nose out of it?
Skyrim.esm|09B015: But I don't want to be safe! I'm not afraid of the dangers out there. The only thing I'm afraid of is wasting my life on this farm.
Skyrim.esm|09B016: Oh! Are you going to teach me how to turn into a mud crab?
Skyrim.esm|09B017: Maybe you're right. Even so, I can't help wondering if some other force it at work here.
Skyrim.esm|09B018: It's too fine a day to dwell on such things. Let's get back to work and forget about wars and orphans for awhile.
Skyrim.esm|09B01B: I just wanted to try on your old armor, from back when you were a soldier. I thought I might be old enough to wear it.
Skyrim.esm|09B01C: Now, clear your mind and breathe deeply. Good concentration is very important for spellcasting.
Skyrim.esm|09B01D: Well done! I don't know where I'd be without you, my old friend.
Skyrim.esm|09B01E: Hah! Who in their right mind would pay for dirt? You'd get laughed out of Whiterun for even trying it.
Skyrim.esm|09B020: There's times I think you're the coldest man in Skyrim. This is your own flesh and blood you're talking about.
Skyrim.esm|09B021: Another fine day at work in the fields. I don't envy those city folk who miss out on the joys of working the land.
Skyrim.esm|09B023: I'm not saying it's my business, but I have to ask. Why are you so cruel to your little girls?
Skyrim.esm|09B024: I know where this is going, son. As I've said many a time. the world is a dangerous place. You're much safer here at the farm, with me.
Skyrim.esm|09B025: Today I'll teach you a useful spell that might just save your life. If you should ever fall into a river, you'll be very glad you learned it.
Skyrim.esm|09B026: Luck? I think it has more to do with the hard work and good soil.
Skyrim.esm|09B027: I was lucky. If you hadn't rescued me, I'd probably be a thief, a mercenary or something even worse.
Skyrim.esm|09B029: Hah! And what would a witless oaf like you know about raising children?
Skyrim.esm|09B02A: I heard you sneaking around downstairs last night. Were you trying to break into my chest again, son? I've told you to leave it alone.
Skyrim.esm|09B02B: Wow! Then I could dive into a river and swim so far away that Britte and father could never find me.
Skyrim.esm|09B02C: The last wheat shipment fetched a fine price at market.
Skyrim.esm|09B02D: Maybe we should start selling the soil along with the dung. We could make a tidy profit.
Skyrim.esm|09B02F: They've grown, all right - lazier and more stupid.
Skyrim.esm|09B030: I'm old enough to decide how to live my life, and I'd be honored to wear your armor, if you'd just let me. I'm strong enough to handle it.
Skyrim.esm|09B031: Look at you! You're as filthy as a pig in a pen!
Skyrim.esm|09B032: When you're rich, you can waste as much money as you like on far-fetched business ideas. In the meantime, there's a farm to run.
Skyrim.esm|09B033: Nothing refreshes the soul like an honest day's labor in good, tilled earth.
Skyrim.esm|09B034: Looks like it's going to be a bountiful harvest once again. The gods continue to smile upon us.
Skyrim.esm|09B035: It's remarkable that our farms are so prosperous given the harsh winters of late.
Skyrim.esm|09B036: Father, how old were you when you left home?
Skyrim.esm|09B038: Looks like we're in for quite a harvest. We've had better luck with our planting than any village in the hold.
Skyrim.esm|09B039: Lives lost, crops burned, land ruined... there's no greater sin than war. And yet, you were an orphan, and you've made something of your life.
Skyrim.esm|09B03A: You're a liar! I won't listen to you. Leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|09B03B: You keep on like you are, you'll end up with two daughters who hate you.
Skyrim.esm|09B03C: Yes, that's your mother's side of the family talking. Just stay on for one more season, that's all I ask.
Skyrim.esm|09B03D: Oh, it's much better than that. I will teach you how to breathe underwater, with no need to turn into one of those ugly creatures.
Skyrim.esm|09B03F: The way our crops have been growing, Rorikstead must have the richest soil in the hold.
Skyrim.esm|09B14D: Go. Bring honor to yourself, and the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|09B14E: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle over in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|09B78B: Walkin' never hurt nobody. If you change your mind, I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|09B78C: Oh. Was it something I said? I can't afford to lower the price, but come back if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|09B792: Wish I did, but some outlander bought my last one.
Skyrim.esm|09B794: That's what I'm here for. What are you offering?
Skyrim.esm|09B796: Don't have any that are for sale right now.
Skyrim.esm|09B797: Yes! Yes, you can. She's all saddled up and everything.
Skyrim.esm|09B799: Good enough for me. It's the one with the saddle.
Skyrim.esm|09B79F: That would be a fair price. When you've got it, come see me.
Skyrim.esm|09B7A0: Wow! Didn't think I could get that for her. I mean, sure!
Skyrim.esm|09B7A1: Looks like you're a little short on coin though. Sorry.
Skyrim.esm|09B9CD: Before we enter, I must warn you about the dangers that could be lurking within. Years ago, this temple was raided by an orc war party seeking revenge... they were being plagued by nightmares just like the people of Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|09B9CF: No. Knowing they could never defeat the orcs, the priests of Vaermina released what they call "The Miasma," putting everyone to sleep.
Skyrim.esm|09BB34: I'm just not up for it, sorry. I'd rather stay here, have a glass of wine, and perform my rites to Arkay in peace.
Skyrim.esm|09BB35: The meal is on Namira's table. Go ahead. Carve.
Skyrim.esm|09BB36: Come with me. Our feast is about to begin.
Skyrim.esm|09BB37: I need to lay down. I'll just be a minute....
Skyrim.esm|09BB38: Why don't you lay down and rest, while we get the meal ready?
Skyrim.esm|09BB39: I've been invited to dinner.... I'm so hungry....
Skyrim.esm|09BB3A: Yes. I'm your friend, and I've invited you to dinner.
Skyrim.esm|09BB3B: You know, in the Priesthood of Arkay, we have a joke about an empty pocket being better than a full grave.
Skyrim.esm|09BB8D: You were told to find it, weren't you? If the stories about the staff are true, if it really can absorb a tremendous amount of power, maybe we can break through Ancano's magic.
Skyrim.esm|09BB8F: He... He gave me something just a little while ago. He told me it was from Labyrinthian, and that I would know what to do with it when the time came. I think... I think he meant this for you, then. I'm not sure why, but there was something very personal about it for him. Also, I think you should take this amulet. It belonged to Savos, but I think would do you the most good now. Take it, and get out of here. Bring back that staff before Ancano brings the whole College down around us.
Skyrim.esm|09BC01: Cicero can talk for a moment. Just a moment. Then Mother must be... tended to.
Skyrim.esm|09BC02: Oh, I do wish to get to know you better. But I must tend to mother. Maybe we can talk for just a moment...
Skyrim.esm|09BCA2: Cicero is at your service, oh great and powerful Listener!
Skyrim.esm|09BCA4: Oh yes, it is Cicero! You were a fool to spare me. What, did you think I would be grateful? Cicero should be Listener! Not you! Now you will die! ... Ahhh.... gotcha! Oh, Listener, you should see the look on your face! Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! Oh, Cicero has returned! Not to kill the kind Listener, but to serve, until one of us dies horribly, in service to our Mother! Best friends forever! In the meantime, I'll make myself at home in the Sanctuary. I'm sure Mother needs... tending.
Skyrim.esm|09BD36: I just cook, and clean, and do whatever it is they ask of me.
Skyrim.esm|09BD37: I can't even keep track of all the people who have been in and out of this fort. They come, they go. I barely notice.
Skyrim.esm|09BD38: Hard enough to keep up with who owns this fort. One side takes over, the other runs away. I tell them, "I ain't leaving! I come with the place!" Ha!
Skyrim.esm|09BD39: I remember one morning, long time ago, I woke up and the place was run by some Orcs. Went to bed that night, it was all vampires. Don't matter to me.
Skyrim.esm|09BD3A: Now, then. I been gabbin' long enough. Too much cleanin' to do.
Skyrim.esm|09BD63: You must excuse me. I shouldn't be here.
Skyrim.esm|09BD64: It is good to meet you in person.
Skyrim.esm|09BD65: Please, we do not have much time.
Skyrim.esm|09BDB0: Now that's how it's done! I doubt anyone in Skyrim could have weaved a better enchantment.
Skyrim.esm|09BDB4: Now that's how it's done! I doubt anyone in Skyrim could have weaved a better enchantment.
Skyrim.esm|09BDBD: Now that's how it's done! I doubt anyone in Skyrim could have weaved a better enchantment.

Skyrim.esm|09BDCE: Now that's how it's done! I doubt anyone in Skyrim could have weaved a better enchantment.
Skyrim.esm|09BDCF: Not bad. Reminds me when I first started smithing.
Skyrim.esm|09BDD3: Not bad. Reminds me when I first started smithing.
Skyrim.esm|09BDD6: Not bad. Reminds me when I first started smithing.

Skyrim.esm|09BDFE: Good job. I can see you know what you're doing.
Skyrim.esm|09BE00: Good job. I can see you know what you're doing.

Skyrim.esm|09BE0B: You made that from the remains of a dragon? Amazing.
Skyrim.esm|09BE0C: You made that from the remains of a dragon? Amazing.

Skyrim.esm|09BE1E: Watching you reminds me of my first days as an enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|09BE2C: Watching you reminds me of my first days as an enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|09BE2D: Watching you reminds me of my first days as an enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|09BE30: Watching you reminds me of my first days as an enchanter.

Skyrim.esm|09BE43: An excellent elixir. Your technique shows a command of the alchemical art.
Skyrim.esm|09BE4A: Yes, Listener! I will remain in this very spot until you tell me otherwise. Spiders could eat my face off and faithful Cicero would not move an inch!
Skyrim.esm|09BE4B: Home again, home again! I'll return to the Night Mother straightaway!
Skyrim.esm|09BE4C: Oh yes, Cicero and the Listener! On the hunt!
Skyrim.esm|09BE54: An excellent elixir. Your technique shows a command of the alchemical art.

Skyrim.esm|09C209: And so you fulfilled your destiny, which you once said you did not believe in.
Skyrim.esm|09C20A: But I cannot celebrate his fall. Zu'u tiiraaz ahst ok mah. He was my brother once. This world will never be the same.
Skyrim.esm|09C20B: So, it is done. Alduin dilon. The Eldest is no more, he who came before all others, and has always been.
Skyrim.esm|09C20C: Perhaps. At least it will continue to exist. Grik los lein. And, as you told me once, the next world will have to take care of itself. Ful nii los. Even I cannot see past Time's ending.
Skyrim.esm|09C20D: I am glad you believe that. At least it will continue to exist. Grik los lein. Even I cannot see past Time's ending to what comes next. Niid koraav zeim dinoksetiid. We must do the best we can with this world.
Skyrim.esm|09C20E: As for myself, you've proven your mastery twice over. Thuri, Dovahkiin. I gladly acknowledge the power of your Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|09C20F: And so Alduin's lordship passes to you. Thuri, Dovahkiin. I gladly submit to your mastery of the Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|09C212: But I forget myself. Krosis. So los mid fahdon. Melancholy is an easy trap for a dovah to fall into. You have won a mighty victory. Sahrot krongrah - one that will echo through all the ages of this world for those who have eyes to see. Savor your triumph, Dovahkiin. This is not the last of what you will write upon the currents of Time.
Skyrim.esm|09C213: Happy? No, I am not happy. Zeymahi lost ont du'ul Bormahu. Alduin was once the crown of our father Akatosh's creation.
Skyrim.esm|09C214: Zu'u Odahviing. Call me when you have need, and I will come if I can.
Skyrim.esm|09C215: Perhaps now you have some insight into the forces that shape the vennesetiid... the currents of Time. Perhaps you begin to see the world as a dovah.
Skyrim.esm|09C216: Of course. Alduin wahlaan daanii. I would not have helped you if I thought otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|09C217: You once told me you did not believe in destiny.
Skyrim.esm|09C218: Indeed, you saw more clearly than I - certainly more clearly than Alduin. Rok funta koraav.
Skyrim.esm|09C219: You did what was necessary. Alduin had flown far from the path of right action in his pahlok - the arrogance of his power.
Skyrim.esm|09C21A: Indeed.  Alduin wahlaan daanii. His doom was written when he claimed for himself the lordship that properly belongs to Bormahu - our father Akatosh.

Skyrim.esm|09C6E1: The approach to Riften is guarded by watchtowers. They will be full of archers. Move fast, and stay low.
Skyrim.esm|09C6E4: Ready now! Everyone, with me! For the Empire! For the Legion! Rrrrrraaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhh!!!!
Skyrim.esm|09C6F2: All that remains then is the exterior gate. Find a way through to open it and the city will be ours!
Skyrim.esm|09C88F: Did you see that madman? A Forsworn agent. Here in the city.
Skyrim.esm|09C890: The Reach belongs to the Forsworn!
Skyrim.esm|09C891: Think he worked down at the smelter. Lot of the laborers there are sympathetic to the Forsworn. They promise people they'll kill off all the Nords that rule over the Reach. Nothing but murderers and saboteurs.
Skyrim.esm|09C892: The Forsworn. Here inside the city.
Skyrim.esm|09C894: Didn't you just see that woman getting attacked in front of us? We're closed.
Skyrim.esm|09C895: We'll handle things from here. Move along.
Skyrim.esm|09C897: I said move along. The Forsworn are just a bunch of madmen. We have everything under control. Stay out of it.
Skyrim.esm|09C899: Margret. He... he killed her. Right in front of me.
Skyrim.esm|09C89A: She's a traveler, from the Imperial City. Came by every day looking for jewelry for her relatives back home. Why would anyone want to kill her? It doesn't make any sense.
Skyrim.esm|09C89B: I'm sorry. I don't think I can bare to sell anything right now.
Skyrim.esm|09C8B9: I should never have come to Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|09C8BA: This has ruined my entire day.
Skyrim.esm|09C9FF: Now you're being ridiculous. This is our city. Not theirs.
Skyrim.esm|09CA00: Someone was attacked in the market. Do you know who it was?
Skyrim.esm|09CA01: I think it was Margret. Poor woman, she was just visiting the city from Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|09CA02: Yeah, but she was a Nord. And a noble. Perfect Forsworn target.
Skyrim.esm|09CA03: You're being paranoid. We're in control of Markarth. Not the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|09CA04: The Jarl is refusing to dispatch men to the front lines. Do you think it's because of the Forsworn threat?
Skyrim.esm|09CA05: Please. Those savages are no match for us. The Jarl is just biding his time.
Skyrim.esm|09CA06: Biding his time until what? Until another person is lying dead in the streets? Don't be naive.
Skyrim.esm|09CA07: A Forsworn agent. Running loose in the city. The Jarl is going to hear about this.
Skyrim.esm|09CA08: The Jarl has things under control. Don't you worry.
Skyrim.esm|09CA09: This was their city once. They won't stop until we're all dead.
Skyrim.esm|09CC93: Are you joking? You just rented a room from me.
Skyrim.esm|09CC94: Have a good rest. It's the big room with the double bed.
Skyrim.esm|09CC95: What does this look like, the Temple of Mara? No gold, no bed.
Skyrim.esm|09CC9A: Are you joking? You just rented a room from me.
Skyrim.esm|09CC9B: Sure thing. It's yours for a day.
Skyrim.esm|09CC9C: What does this look like, the Temple of Mara? No gold, no bed.
Skyrim.esm|09CCCB: No... no, that is all. Now... now please. I find myself very weary all of sudden.
Skyrim.esm|09CCCD: Token, you say? Let me see. Oh, goodness me. You're a friend of Gabriella's, then. Well, I guess we both know why you're here.
Skyrim.esm|09CCCE: Hmph. Yes. Yes, I surely can. It's not something I do lightly, mind you, and it's not as specific as you might want. But yes. I will do this for you. Please, relax. Free your mind. Yes, that's it...
Skyrim.esm|09CCCF: There's a cave. No, not a cave. A... home? A place you feel secure. You will find safety there... sanctuary. I see snow, lit by the star of dawn. And you are not alone. There are others. A child of night... a stalker of the sands? Oh, but before you are family, there will be blood. Such blood. Wait! There's something else... A potential for adventure, and wealth. It is a ruin, ripe for the plunder. Deepwood Redoubt. Far to the northwest... Through there is... Hag's End. The last resting place of an assassin of old. A Dark Brother, who bequeaths his ancient earthly possessions... to you.
Skyrim.esm|09CCD0: Your destiny is dark. Oh yes, so dark. But much of it has been fulfilled. You have found sanctuary, and the comforting words of a mother's love. And you have companionship. A child of night, and a stalker of the sands. Together, you are a family. Wait! There's something else... A potential for adventure, and wealth. It is a ruin, ripe for the plunder. Deepwood Redoubt. Far to the northwest... It is the last resting place of an assassin of old. A Dark Brother, who bequeaths his ancient earthly possessions... to you.
Skyrim.esm|09CCD1: Oh, very well. But my energies are low. I warn you - I won't be able to see much. Give me... five gold, and I'll do what I can.
Skyrim.esm|09CCD2: Sorry, love, but I'm not a charity.
Skyrim.esm|09D595: I'm not the only one seeing this, am I?
Skyrim.esm|09D5E2: Not much further. Come, little thing. Serve me in death.
Skyrim.esm|09D6CF: Kind of fun to push people around sometimes, isn't it. Good work, brother.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D1: I'm cutting your pay for this job. Be more careful next time. This isn't a game.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D2: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle up in Haafingar.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D3: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle up in Hjaalmarch.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D4: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle down in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D5: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle right here in Whiterun Hold.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D6: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle out in the Pale.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D7: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle over in the Rift.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D8: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle over in Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|09D6D9: We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle all the way in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|09D6DA: I don't know what the fight is about, and that's not our business anyway. I just need you to go out there, look tough, and scare this milk drinker into submission. No more than that. I don't want to hear about a killing, understand?
Skyrim.esm|09D6DB: Excellent. Looks like you've really driven them into hiding. Keep this up. We'll wipe them out, yet.
Skyrim.esm|09D6DC: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|09D6DD: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around Haafingar.
Skyrim.esm|09D6DE: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around Hjaalmarch.

Skyrim.esm|09D6E0: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|09D6E1: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around the Pale.
Skyrim.esm|09D6E2: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around the Rift.
Skyrim.esm|09D6E3: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|09D6E4: I've caught wind that one of the brighter Silver Hand has been sniffing around Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|09D6E5: Shor's bones, boy. What did I tell you?
Skyrim.esm|09D6E6: Shor's bones, girl. What did I tell you?
Skyrim.esm|09D6E8: Are you here to save me?
Skyrim.esm|09D6E9: Perfect. Hopefully this will let us know more about their movement. I think we've got them on the run!
Skyrim.esm|09D6EA: I've been hearing rumors of Silver Hand activity.
Skyrim.esm|09D6EB: A group of them has holed up in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|09D6EC: A group of them has holed up in Haafingar.
Skyrim.esm|09D6ED: A group of them has holed up in Hjaalmarch.
Skyrim.esm|09D6EE: A group of them has holed up in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|09D6EF: A group of them has holed up in Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|09D6F0: A group of them has holed up in the Pale.
Skyrim.esm|09D6F1: A group of them has holed up in the Rift.
Skyrim.esm|09D6F2: A group of them has holed up in Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|09D6F3: A group of them has holed up in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|09D6F4: If you can sneak into their camp and steal his plans, we'll have the advantage.
Skyrim.esm|09D6F5: If you can sneak into their camp and steal her plans, we'll have the advantage.
Skyrim.esm|09D9D7: Yes! Yes! Return me to this realm!
Skyrim.esm|09D9D9: What! What are you doing?! You fools! You cannot bind me to your wills!
Skyrim.esm|09D9DA: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9DB: Long have you slept the dreamless sleep of death, Potema. No longer. Hear us Wolf Queen! We Summon You!
Skyrim.esm|09D9DC: I'm Sybille Stentor, court mage of Solitude, and according to my scrying you've managed to unleash Potema herself!
Skyrim.esm|09D9DE: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9DF: What did you do in Wolfskull cave?!
Skyrim.esm|09D9E0: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9E2: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9E3: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9E4: You ants don't have the power to bind me!
Skyrim.esm|09D9E6: As our voices summon you the blood of the innocent binds you Wolf Queen!
Skyrim.esm|09D9E8: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9E9: Listen you impudent cur. According to my scrying something happened at Wolfskull Cave that returned Potema to this world.
Skyrim.esm|09D9EA: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9EC: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9ED: Something is wrong. There is an intruder.
Skyrim.esm|09D9EF: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9F0: You weren't nearly as successful as you think, fool. According to my scrying the Wolf Queen has returned.
Skyrim.esm|09D9F1: Wolf Queen. Hear our call and awaken. We summon Potema!
Skyrim.esm|09D9F2: Just remember I'm here if you rethink your decision.
Skyrim.esm|09D9F3: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9F4: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09D9F6: Summoned with words. Bound by blood.
Skyrim.esm|09DA29: Whatever you do, stay away from that Black Door. Being near it chills you to the bones.
Skyrim.esm|09DA2A: I've heard that shadowy figures are being seen coming in and out of the Black Door. Gods help us.
Skyrim.esm|09DCCD: By Ysmir, this is just what we need. There'll be panic in the streets if this proves true.
Skyrim.esm|09DCCF: Panic we can handle, Balgruuf. It's the dragons I'm not so sure about.
Skyrim.esm|09DCD0: I've dispatched a rider to Helgen. We'll know soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|09DD43: She cannot hide from us forever.
Skyrim.esm|09DD44: If you locate her, find us in Rorikstead.
Skyrim.esm|09DD45: I really thought you'd be able to save me.
Skyrim.esm|09DD47: Let's get to the stables before they find me.
Skyrim.esm|09DD49: Do you have news of our quarry?
Skyrim.esm|09DD53: Well, I never thought I'd see the Guild this way ever again. The place is growin' into what it used to be when Gallus was runnin' the show. Contacts are springin' up all over and I've got young footpads beatin' down the door to join up. I got one last special request of you. Finish this one and all of Skyrim's ours for the takin'.
Skyrim.esm|09DD54: I don't know what your made of, but I've never seen anyone tackle this many jobs without endin' up dead or in jail. We've got half of Skyrim back under our wing and the coin's startin' to flow. Now, if you're ready, I got another special job for you.
Skyrim.esm|09DD55: I've got another special request and they specifically asked for you. Your makin' a name for yourself, that's for sure. But more important, you're makin' a name for the Guild. Keep it up.
Skyrim.esm|09DD56: Well, it appears your actions are startin' to make waves out there. People are talkin' about the Guild again. In fact, I just got a special job request in. I haven't gotten anythin' like it in years.
Skyrim.esm|09DD57: Got word from Torsten Cruel-Sea in Windhelm that a rival Guild is tryin' to get itself started. Not only that but they're givin' us thieves a bad name by murderin' their marks. Get over there and see what you can do.
Skyrim.esm|09DD58: We actually got a request from one of the most powerful families in Whiterun, the Battle-Born's. Make your way up there and look for Olfrid. He says he's got a matter that requires a delicate touch, and I suggested you for the job. Don't make me the fool and let me down, all right?
Skyrim.esm|09DD59: I want you to head out to Markarth and speak to Endon. He's a silversmith... has some kind of a shop there. His father was a good friend of the Guild back in the day, and we could really use his family's influence back on our side.
Skyrim.esm|09DD5A: I need you to make your way to Solitude and talk to Erikur. He's lookin' to run some kind of a shill job. Now, he's not exactly goin' to welcome you with open arms, but keep your cool. Do this job right and we've just made an important friend out there.
Skyrim.esm|09DD5B: Slow down, you have that special request in Windhelm to finish first. After that's done, I'll send you some more jobs.
Skyrim.esm|09DD5C: Before I'm goin' to give you any more work, you need to finish that job in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|09DD5D: I want you to finish that job in Whiterun first. Then we'll talk about more work.
Skyrim.esm|09DD68: I hear Endon's quite pleased to have his merchandise back. He's a powerful ally in Markarth. Should help the Guild regain a foothold in the west.
Skyrim.esm|09DD69: Olfrid sent ahead his compliments. Looks like his friend Arn is soon to be released thanks to his brand new identity. More importanly, he's pledged the full support of the Battle-Born clan to the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|09DD6A: Erikur assures me that Captain Volf will spend pretty much the rest of his life clapped in irons. Best of all, he's also pledged to open doors for us in Solitude and to get things rollin' again.
Skyrim.esm|09DD6B: Torsten was gratified the death of his daughter was avenged and the heirloom was returned.
Skyrim.esm|09DD6C: Torsten was gratified the death of his daughter was avenged and the heirloom was returned.
Skyrim.esm|09DD6D: The Guild's at full strength and we have you to thank for it. We've had our share of dark times, and now they seem like a distant memory. I'm goin' to tell you'll somethin' you'll never hear me say again, and if you tell anyone I told you, I'll deny it. When you walk through the Guild, don't be ashamed to hold your head high 'cause you're the best damn thief in the place.
Skyrim.esm|09DD6E: Success means that the Guild is gettin' stronger. We've picked up another merchant and a new recruit. Bein' a thief is like a second skin for you, ain't it? Never seen anythin' like it. If you don't watch out, you might even earn Vex's respect. Now that'd be something, eh?
Skyrim.esm|09DD6F: We're still growin' by leaps and bounds thanks to you. Another merchant's just moved into the space outside the Flagon. You're a natural. Never seen anythin' like it. We've got a long way to go, but don't let that bother you... it's a lot farther than we've been in years.
Skyrim.esm|09DD70: I think you'll find that the Guild is beginnin' to grow. We've had a merchant move into the space right outside the Flagon. It's nice to shake the cobwebs off and get things rollin' again. Keep this up and I can see a bounty headed our way.
Skyrim.esm|09DE8C: I can take you to any of the hold capitals.
Skyrim.esm|09DEA1: I'm a woman living in Skyrim with six children and no husband. I don't have the time or patience to be "nice." Do some people look down on me? Have I made some enemies? You're damn right.
Skyrim.esm|09DEA2: Okay, well... I guess it's possible. I've been selling my sword arm for years now. Killed a lot of people. Could be someone wanted revenge. But... But you're not going to kill me. Right?
Skyrim.esm|09DEA3: Fool! Don't you get it? I live in the shadow of death every day. A knife in every doorway, a nocked arrow on every rooftop! If one of my enemies wouldn't pay to have me killed, I'd take it as a personal insult.
Skyrim.esm|09DEA4: I'm kneeling here with my hands bound and a sack over my head, and you have the gall to ask me that? What do you think, genius?
Skyrim.esm|09DEA5: Please! I don't know! I mean, I'm a soldier. I've killed people. When I was ordered to. Maybe there were some times... some times I got carried away? But war is war. Right? Nobody could blame me for that. Could they?
Skyrim.esm|09DEA6: He he he he he he he. The real question is, "Would someone pay to have me killed... again." A day goes by without someone trying to gut me in the street, I get disappointed.
Skyrim.esm|09DEA9: I'll need to lock up the store. Thank you so much for agreeing to help us.
Skyrim.esm|09DEAA: Mara, I forgot how late it was. We'll need to lock up the shop.
Skyrim.esm|09DEAB: Many used to come to these caves. The forgotten. The wretched.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB1: I think there were twenty-four in all. Most people keep them as a curiosity. Some of the Guild members have tried to locate them over the years, but they haven't been successful... well, until now.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB2: Not like that it isn't. The stone was pried off of Barenziah's ceremonial crown by a thief in order to cover his tracks.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB3: When I told you the set had to be complete, I left out a small detail... I honestly didn't think you'd ever get this far. If the stones were remounted on the crown, it would be of great benefit to the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB4: I knew you had it in you. After all, finding the Stones was no easy feat. Well, I'm not one to welch on a deal, so here's your reward. I hope you put it to good use. I know I have.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB5: Look, I only buy things I can turn around quickly for a profit. And no one wants those stones unless they have the whole set. Tell you what, if you find the rest of them, talk to me again. Otherwise, keep it and maybe it'll bring you luck.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB6: Unbelievable! You must be the first person to set eyes on all twenty-four of them in... well, the last two eras.
Skyrim.esm|09DFB9: Been a while since I've seen one of those. What you've got there is a Stone of Barenziah.
Skyrim.esm|09DFBA: I have it on good authority that the crown is within Tolvald's Cave; dropped by a Dunmer caravan leaving Morrowind long ago. If you can bring it back, we could restore the paragon... and reap its benefits.
Skyrim.esm|09E006: As to Potema herself, find what's left of her body, likely a skeleton. Remove it from the Catacombs and bring it back to be sanctified by Arkay.
Skyrim.esm|09E007: Summoned in spirit form is not raised from the dead. She'll need help before she can return to the living. For the moment, the Wolf Queen has retreated to a place filled with dead eager to serve her. She has gone to her old Catacombs. A few days ago, one of her servants busted through a wall into the Temple of Divines. We'll need you to go into the Catacombs themselves.
Skyrim.esm|09E008: Excellent! These things do have a way of working out when people take action. I'll sanctify the remains.
Skyrim.esm|09E009: Good. Being at the summoning created a connection to Potema, you are the one to do this. I can provide you with some help with her minions.
Skyrim.esm|09E00A: You've returned. Good. What did you find at Wolfskull Cave?
Skyrim.esm|09E00B: I wish you well friend. Be careful.
Skyrim.esm|09E00C: In case Falk doesn't make it clear - Solitude owes you a debt of gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|09E00D: I hope Falk made it clear that Solitude owes you a debt of gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|09E00F: Take this payment - the Jarl would thank you but she very much wants to keep Potema's return quiet. Make no mistake we consider you a protector of Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|09E010: Take this payment - the Jarl would thank you but she very much wants to keep Potema's return quiet. Make no mistake we consider you a protector of Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|09E069: Pruzah wundunne wah Wuth Gein. I wish the old one luck in his... quest. But I doubt many will wish to exchange Alduin's lordship for the tyranny of Paarthurnax's "Way of the Voice".
Skyrim.esm|09E06A: If Alduin himself could not stand against your Thu'um, I feel no shame in my own defeat.
Skyrim.esm|09E073: Goraan! I feel younger than I have in many an age. Many of the dovahhe are now scattered across Keizaal. Without Alduin's lordship, they may yet bow to the vahzen... rightness of my Thu'um. But willing or no, they will hear it! Fare thee well, Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|09E0AE: It took me weeks to find that thieves' den. I can't stop now, and I can't get the sword on my own.
Skyrim.esm|09E0AF: So you're willing to starve your wife and daughter to reclaim some rusty old sword?
Skyrim.esm|09E0B1: I know your family's honor is important to you, but we can't afford it!
Skyrim.esm|09E0B2: I just need to hire one, maybe two good men. You won't starve.
Skyrim.esm|09E0B3: I'll put it plainly. You can claim your sword, or you can keep your wife. If you set foot outside the gate, I won't be here when you return.
Skyrim.esm|09E118: Careful if you cross the river to the east. Old Hajvarr Iron-Hand and his brigands are holed up in that cave, White River Watch.
Skyrim.esm|09E1AB: Thank you for your trust. I am eager to return to my homeland.
Skyrim.esm|09E587: I can send you into Azura's Star as soon as you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|09E65C: Good luck. Come to Windhelm and talk to Ulfric. You won't regret it.
Skyrim.esm|09E680: Good luck. If you get a chance, look me up in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|09F108: Okay, okay. No need to make rash decisions. Here's half. You get the rest when the job's done.
Skyrim.esm|09F154: Yell all you want. It's not going to change my mind.
Skyrim.esm|09F155: Oh really? And I don't suppose you'd just do it out of the kindness of your heart, would you? I hope you're not expecting to get paid until the job's done.
Skyrim.esm|09F157: Oh, very well. Here's half. You get the rest when the job's done.
Skyrim.esm|09F158: Well that's not how I operate, so forget it.
Skyrim.esm|09F15E: Now I've got to clean up this mess.
Skyrim.esm|09F15F: My only demand is that these vermin are permanently eliminated before my reputation is completely destroyed.
Skyrim.esm|09F275: I suppose there's no point in concealing the truth any longer. My knowledge of this temple comes from personal experience. I was a priest of Vaermina.
Skyrim.esm|09F799: It was my idea to replace one of the key elements with our focusing crystal. Months of enchantments went into it.
Skyrim.esm|09F79A: Ha! So you did. I was almost beginning to think Gavros had gone and gotten himself killed just to spite me. Well, with the Falmer skulking around I can't very well do this on my own. I guess you'll have to come with me. I'll explain on the way.
Skyrim.esm|09F79B: Let's just hope they got it right this time.
Skyrim.esm|09F79C: No matter what Gavros said, this was my idea first. The Council is going to know that when I get back.
Skyrim.esm|09F79D: I was the one who thought of using this... this Oculory. I don't know what the dwarves called it. Something unpronounceable, I'm sure.
Skyrim.esm|09F79E: You are, are you? Savos wouldn't even grant us an audience when we came to you, but now you come here expecting something from me? I don't much like this, I'll tell you. But you've saved my skin, so maybe I can overlook the past for now. Come on, I'll explain on the way.
Skyrim.esm|09F79F: It was a brilliant idea, really. Mostly mine, though Gavros took the credit. Secret, though. Official Synod business. Can't talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|09F7A0: From all our research, it seems they were intent on discerning the nature of the divine.
Skyrim.esm|09F7A1: Please, tell me you've found the crystal. I've no wish to die in this place.
Skyrim.esm|09F7A2: Unless you can find that crystal, there's no point in wasting more time with talk.
Skyrim.esm|09F7A3: You found... how in the world... That's it. That's it! I don't know who you are, but you may have just saved this little project... In fact, who are you, anyway?
Skyrim.esm|09F7A4: I don't know what sort of help you expect from me, but if you can actually find the crystal I'd certainly be grateful.
Skyrim.esm|09F7A5: This machinery, all of it, was designed to collect starlight, and then... I'm not sure. Split it, somehow?
Skyrim.esm|09F7E3: Fine. If you can't pay with coin, you'll pay with your life!
Skyrim.esm|09F7E4: If you insist. Come on boys. This'll be fun.
Skyrim.esm|09F7E5: The, um, Emperor thanks you... Now shove off! Er, rather, have a nice day, citizen.
Skyrim.esm|09F7E6: You there! Ah... citizen. This area is off limits. You are interfering in Imperial business. Yeah, so you'll have to pay us a fine... of say, 100 gold. Pay up, citizen.
Skyrim.esm|09F7E7: Go on, get out of here!
Skyrim.esm|09FBF9: I'm relieved you discovered a bottle intact; this place looks as though it was ransacked by the orcs. So... I've taken us this far, but you need to guide us the rest of the way. Drink.
Skyrim.esm|09FBFA: Dawnstar's fate rests in that tiny bottle. The longer we wait, the more damage Vaermina could be doing to those poor people. I understand your hesitation, but I promise you that it works.
Skyrim.esm|09FBFB: It should be in a small bottle, very similar to a potion. I'll begin searching up here.
Skyrim.esm|09FBFC: I call upon you, Lady Mara!
Skyrim.esm|09FBFD: Now that they've been dealt with, we need to find the Torpor.
Skyrim.esm|09FBFE: The Skull hungers. It yearns for memories and leaves nightmares in its wake.
Skyrim.esm|09FBFF: Indeed. My reverence for Vaermina's machinations should not take precedence over our mission. My apologies. The inner sanctum lies ahead. We must reach the Skull and put an end to Dawnstar's troubles. Lead on, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|09FC00: Grant me the power to break through this barrier and to send the Skull to the depths of Oblivion!
Skyrim.esm|09FC01: We're looking for a book of alchemical recipes called "The Dreamstride." The tome bears the likeness of Vaermina on the cover. It should be here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|09FC02: Barring any more interruptions, perhaps we can locate the information I need.
Skyrim.esm|0A0135: Please. I must remain uninterrupted if I am to complete the ritual. Have no fear, your time will come.
Skyrim.esm|0A0307: Speak with the Corrupt Agent at the Penitus Oculatus Outpost, in Dragon Bridge. Accept his gold, then kill the target. Hail Sithis!
Skyrim.esm|0A0308: Speak with the Scheming Servant at the Keep in Markarth. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|0A0309: Speak with the Desperate Gambler at the Barracks, in Windhelm. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|0A030A: Speak with the Blasphemous Priest at the Temple of Kynareth, in Whiterun. Accept his gold, then kill the target. Hail Sithis!
Skyrim.esm|0A030B: Speak with the Dishonored Skald at the Bards College, in Solitude. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|0A030C: Speak with the Indolent Farmer at the Vilemyr Inn, in Ivarstead. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|0A030D: Speak with the Griefstricken Chef, at the Windpeak Inn, here in Dawnstar. Accept his gold, then eliminate the target. Hail the Listener! Hail Sithis!
Skyrim.esm|0A030E: Speak with the Unemployed Laborer at the Frostfruit Inn, in Rorikstead. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Skyrim.esm|0A0329: Believe me, it wasn't anything I ever intended to do. I just... lost control. I tried to tell them, but none of them believe me. It's all on account of this blasted ring.
Skyrim.esm|0A0354: Don't wait around for me. I bet I'll beat you to Korvanjund even though you start first.
Skyrim.esm|0A0941: Cold as the inside of an ice wraith. That and some Imperials are camped out around the entrance, staying nice and warm around their fires.
Skyrim.esm|0A0942: Imperials? Well, if they're keeping themselves comfortable, let's slip in and send them to their graves that way.
Skyrim.esm|0A0944: Stormcloaks were already camped out around the entrance when we got here. They don't know we're here yet, though.
Skyrim.esm|0A0945: We're not leaving until we get what we came for.
Skyrim.esm|0A0948: No. And I'm not sure I'm better off for it now neither.
Skyrim.esm|0A094B: At least we got the damn crown. I hope it was worth it.
Skyrim.esm|0A094C: I wish the Legate would let us clear out of here. Place gives me the creeps.
Skyrim.esm|0A094E: At least we got the damn crown. I hope it was worth it.
Skyrim.esm|0A094F: I wish Galmar would hurry it up. We shouldn't be here.
Skyrim.esm|0A0C3E: That's the way I like it. Short and bloody. They never knew what hit them.
Skyrim.esm|0A0C3F: Enough talk. Let's go kill some Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0A0C40: We had the advantage of surprise this time, but things won't be so easy from here on out.
Skyrim.esm|0A0C91: There, there. It will pass. Going too long without a proper meal can make the stomach weak. Feel free to stay and mingle with your new coven, if you have the strength.
Skyrim.esm|0A0CB6: It's an honor, champion. Feel free to stay and mingle with your new coven.
Skyrim.esm|0A0CDC: I knew when you walked into the Hall of the Dead that you were special. And here you are. Champion of Namira. Keeper of her ring. You're everything I hoped you would be.
Skyrim.esm|0A0CE5: I can smell the blood. Good and sour.
Skyrim.esm|0A0D24: I love this part of the meal.
Skyrim.esm|0A0D5E: Yes, yes. We should proceed cautiously.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DDD: See what you can figure out, Unblooded. I'm going to check out these carvings over here. Let me know if you find something.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DDE: Draugr. Ain't you ever seen one before?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE0: What in the nine holds is that?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE1: I wouldn't do that again unless you know for sure it's going to open.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE3: But there isn't any other way through.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE4: Let's hope that's the last of them. Come on, let's see what's down this way.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE5: Is there anything on the claw that might show how to open this door?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE6: Steady now! They may be uglier than Imperials but they'll go down just the same.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE7: Maybe they're so scared of you they ran away.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE8: We'll stick around here for a while and see if we can find anything else useful.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DE9: Alright everyone! Keep your guard up. No telling what we'll find down here.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DEA: Come on, boys. Let's spread out and see what we've got.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DEB: Hmm, looks like this is as far as the Imperials got.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DED: Too bad we can't read these carvings. Who knows what secrets we'd uncover?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DEE: Even if one of these pictures tells us where the Crown is, I'm betting we're going to have to find a way through that door.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DEF: Time to show what you're really made of.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF0: Not so sure? Oh, then perhaps we should take a moment to look around a little, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF1: I don't like it. What are they waiting for?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF3: Oh... I've heard of this. They say these walls show the history of the ancients who built this place.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF4: You sure about that? Then please, be my guest and go strolling on in there. We'll stay here and watch your back...
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF5: That's done it! Alright, boys, let's get moving. We've got more...
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF6: You there, Unblooded, see if you can find another way through. We'll charge in to help as soon as we hear fighting.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF7: Pick a man and put him down. We attack on my signal.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF8: Hmm... that looks like some kind of claw from a statue. I wonder what it's used for.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DF9: I don't like the look of this. Perfect spot for an ambush. Ten to one they're just waiting for us on the other side.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DFC: Steady. A few dusty bonewalkers aren't going to stop us anymore than the Imperials could.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DFD: The crown must be around here somewhere. Spread out, and keep your eyes open.
Skyrim.esm|0A0DFE: One thing at a time. We're here for the Crown. Any of these carvings show a crown?
Skyrim.esm|0A0DFF: No, but I know they're out there. No other way they can come. Now shut up or you'll blow our ambush.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E00: Unblooded, do what you do best. See if you can find some way to get that gate open.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E03: Looks like the door will open if we turn these rings the right way.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E04: There may be some way to get through on the level above. Hurry!
Skyrim.esm|0A0E05: Maybe you can find a way through somewhere above us.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E06: You have your orders. Get moving.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E07: How's it going, Unblooded? You find anything yet?
Skyrim.esm|0A0E08: Keep looking, Unblooded. There's got to be some way to get that thing open.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E0A: Ah! The Hall of Stories... we must be getting close now.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E0E: Don't give them a chance to warn the others!
Skyrim.esm|0A0E0F: Careful, boys. There's bound to be more up ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E82: I'm glad we could come to an agreement.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E83: Excellent. Very good. This is exactly what I need to complete a series of studies I'm doing on Dwemer craftsmanship and magical resonance. I simply must have this! I'll pay you double what it's worth.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E94: Oh... well... I can see you have no appreciation for scholarship.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E95: Oh... what a terrible shame. Well, if you think you know where you left it and can retrieve it, I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E98: Hold on, there. I suppose, in the uh, interest of research... I can spare a few more coins. Double and a half its value. That's all I can muster.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E99: Ha! I'll see to it the Jarl hears of your abuse of a court scholar. Shame on you.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E9B: You are? Very good.... may I see it?
Skyrim.esm|0A0E9C: Well... I suppose important research is worth every coin. All right. You have yourself a deal. How's double and a half its value in trade?
Skyrim.esm|0A0E9D: With this the world could have a better understanding of the Dwemer! Come now, where's your sense of propriety? Extorting an old man like this.
Skyrim.esm|0A0E9E: A note from someone named Calcelmo, in Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0A0F3C: Wonderful. You've just made an important contribution to the advancement of a Dwemer scholarship.
Skyrim.esm|0A1794: I've got what I came for, I better get back to Dragon Bridge.
Skyrim.esm|0A17CB: Terrible shame, that. Seems we have a killer on the loose here in Windhelm. He's claimed three victims that I know of, though maybe there's more that haven't been found yet. All lovely young ladies, too. Let's hope the guards find that demon soon, and introduce him to the headsman's axe.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E1: If anything actually happens, call a guard.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E2: My men have their orders. But it's a big city, and there's a war on. Plus these murders. A few dark elves getting shoved around doesn't really amount to much.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E3: Well you see where we have to live. This forgotten alley. All the filth from the upper quarters flows downhill, like they say. Good luck getting one of the guards to help with anything. I tried to get Ulfric to even come down here to see the squalor, but the High Lord of His Mightiness couldn't find the time.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E4: If you're looking to pass the time, you can give Calixto a few septims and gawk at whater oddity he's come across lately. If you're need of goods, there's a market. If it's a soft bed and a hot meal you're after, there's Candlehearth Hall.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E5: None of that matters to me. Until someone takes a Dunmer, I let Windhelm deal with its own problems.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E6: Where else would we be? When the Red Mountain burned, you could scarcely breathe in Morrowind. So we came west. Windhelm is the first city on that road, and here we are. If we had known the Nords would be so unwelcoming, we may have kept walking.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E8: The Empire's elven puppetmasters never cared much for the idea of a human becoming a divine. So the worship of Talos is outlawed. Skyrim doesn't take kindly to being told what to do. And to try and forget Talos, well... they may as well ordered us all to cut off our ears.
Skyrim.esm|0A17E9: Watch that tongue, Rendar, or your whole lot could be down with the Argonians hauling ballast by tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0A18C5: Talos, who in life was known as Tiber Septim, united Tamriel and founded the Empire. He was rewarded for his deeds by being joined with the Divines in eternal glory. The only mortal to do so. We worship him that we might follow in his path. We can't literally join him in the heavens, but we may do our best to know them here on Nirn.
Skyrim.esm|0A1910: Get to Windhelm with the crown as quick as you can. Tell Ulfric he owes me a drink.
Skyrim.esm|0A1929: Like anyone, a crisis will push them towards devotion. I've been seeing a lot of the Shatter-Shields, since they lost their daughter.
Skyrim.esm|0A193B: There's an author named Adonato staying at the inn. He's published a few works of historical fiction. The clans Shatter-Shield and Cruel-Sea see most of the gold that passes through the city. And obviously you have Ulfric, the Jarl and future High King. Oh, and that busybody Viola Giordano. She's got her nose in everyone's business, which I guess is one way of being prominent.
Skyrim.esm|0A1959: If the guards in the Gray Quarter weren't a bunch of n'wahs, I wouldn't be here talking to you.
Skyrim.esm|0A195A: Well, nothing. At least not any more. Folk just call me that because of what I used to do. I was a sailor for many a year. I made my coin as a trader and in time, I bought my own ship. When I retired here in Windhelm, most folk already knew me as Captain Lonely-Gale, and the name just stuck.
Skyrim.esm|0A1A5D: We need to find out what Ancano's up to.
Skyrim.esm|0A1A65: That should do. Now - Inhale deeply.
Skyrim.esm|0A1A71: Yes, yes. This will create a fine fume.
Skyrim.esm|0A1A93: Ah, have you? Let Kesh have them.
Skyrim.esm|0A1F82: First, we're mounting an attack on the Fort. We aim to confiscate it from the rebels.
Skyrim.esm|0A1F83: First, we're attacking the fort. I want that fort.
Skyrim.esm|0A1F9E: Meet the soldiers preparing for the attack. Then join them in wiping out the rebels. Once you prevail we will garrison the fort.
Skyrim.esm|0A1F9F: Meet the brothers preparing for the attack. Then join them in wiping out the Imperials. Once you prevail we will garrison the fort.
Skyrim.esm|0A1FA0: Secondly, we're mounting an attack on the Fort. I want to gain a fortified position here before we launch our final attack on the capital.
Skyrim.esm|0A1FA1: Secondly, I want to take the fort here. Having a fortified position will make it easier for us to amass men for our ultimate attack on the capital.
Skyrim.esm|0A200E: Their spies must've found out we know about the Crown, and they don't want us to have it. But they won't stand in our way.
Skyrim.esm|0A200F: Listen up. Those Imperials aren't here by coincidence.
Skyrim.esm|0A2010: General Tullius is counting on us to bring back the Jagged Crown, and that's exactly what we're going to do.
Skyrim.esm|0A2011: I realize some of you may know men on the other side. But remember this. They are the enemy now and will not hesitate to end your lives either.
Skyrim.esm|0A2012: Ulfric the Pretender wants that crown, but we're not going to let him have it.
Skyrim.esm|0A2013: Listen up, legionnaires. Those Stormcloaks are here for the same reason we are.
Skyrim.esm|0A2014: Ulfric Stormcloak is counting on us to bring him back that crown, and that's exactly what we're going to do.
Skyrim.esm|0A2015: Keep your wits about you and watch your shield brother's back.
Skyrim.esm|0A2016: Good work! Lucky we had you with us today.
Skyrim.esm|0A2017: Must be here somewhere. Keep looking.
Skyrim.esm|0A2018: Draugr. Dammit. I hate those things.
Skyrim.esm|0A2019: Be careful, friend. This place doesn't seem too... forgiving.
Skyrim.esm|0A201A: Maybe that claw has a clue on it? You know, how to set the rings so the door will open.
Skyrim.esm|0A201B: Stick close. This place gives me the creeps.
Skyrim.esm|0A201C: All for some old crown? It's a strange war all right.
Skyrim.esm|0A201D: The Stormcloaks will never stop us.
Skyrim.esm|0A201F: Good work! Lucky we had you with us today.
Skyrim.esm|0A2020: That crown must be here somewhere. Keep looking.
Skyrim.esm|0A2021: My pa always warned me not to mess with places like this.
Skyrim.esm|0A2022: Careful. No telling what will happen the next time you mess with that door.
Skyrim.esm|0A2023: Maybe that claw has a clue on it? You know, how to turn the rings so the door will open.
Skyrim.esm|0A2024: Keep a sharp eye out. There's no telling what we might run into in here.
Skyrim.esm|0A2025: All for an old crown, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0A2026: The Imperials will be sorry they got in our way.
Skyrim.esm|0A2028: I know some of you are ex-legion and may know men on the other side. But remember this. They are the enemy now and they will not hesitate to kill you.
Skyrim.esm|0A2034: No! There's got to be more Skooma... Shut up! Shut up!  Don't lie to me J'zhar! You hid it! You always try to steal it from me!
Skyrim.esm|0A2035: Where is it? I know you were trying to keep it for yourself J'zhar... You always try to keep it for yourself!
Skyrim.esm|0A2036: No... No! You must be the one who took my skooma!
Skyrim.esm|0A27B0: Guardsman! What happened here? Where's this dragon? Quickly now!
Skyrim.esm|0A27B1: No! Get back! It's still here somewhere! Hroki and Tor just got grabbed when they tried to make a run for it!
Skyrim.esm|0A27B8: Kynareth save us, here he comes again...
Skyrim.esm|0A27BD: Zok morokei! More souls to feed my master's hunger.
Skyrim.esm|0A27BE: Let's go. We need to get to the Western Watchtower before it's too late.
Skyrim.esm|0A27BF: I'll see you at the watchtower as soon as the rest of the men have gathered.
Skyrim.esm|0A27C0: Follow me. We're assembling near the main gate.
Skyrim.esm|0A27C1: Talos save us! It's a dragon!
Skyrim.esm|0A27CE: Go. Quickly. Hide in the coffin before Cicero and the traitor meet.
Skyrim.esm|0A27CF: This is all just too much, too fast. Go get some work from Nazir. I need time to... to think...
Skyrim.esm|0A27D0: Remember, the Sanctuary is in the southern Pine Forest. Just beneath the road, and away from prying eyes.
Skyrim.esm|0A27D1: Why are you still standing there? Hurry, before the meeting begins.
Skyrim.esm|0A29A8: Are you daft? I told you not kill any of them. How much clearer do you need me to be? How about the gold? Did you remember to bring it or is that too difficult an instruction as well.
Skyrim.esm|0A29A9: So, job's done and you even brought the gold. Best of all you did it clean. I like that. Dumping bodies and keeping the guards quiet can be expensive.
Skyrim.esm|0A29AA: I suppose it's not a total loss then. Let me give you a piece of advice. If you want to make some serious coin in this business you need to listen to rules. Now, on to other things.
Skyrim.esm|0A29AB: Well done. And it would seem I owe you something in return. Here you go, I think you'll find these quite useful.
Skyrim.esm|0A29B1: I had to. The farmers are charging me double for the produce I sell.
Skyrim.esm|0A29B2: How goes the begging today, Brenuin?
Skyrim.esm|0A29B3: By Ysmir, I will. Gods know it's been too long since I tasted real meat.
Skyrim.esm|0A29B4: Did you raise your prices, Carlotta?
Skyrim.esm|0A29B5: Best you forget whatever you think you saw, elf. Such idle talk can prove hazardous to one's health.
Skyrim.esm|0A29B8: If they do, promise you'll actually purchase something for once.
Skyrim.esm|0A29B9: You're right. I'll have to remember that the next time I get a shipment in.
Skyrim.esm|0A2A6B: Would your good friend be named Jon, by any chance? I've spied the two of you talking quite intimately.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BAD: I'm sure you could charge double for those and still sell them all within a week.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB0: Got any sausages? I'm cooking a special breakfast for a friend, and they're his favorite.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB1: Just browsin' today. If the gods be kind, someone will take mercy on me and spare a few septims.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB2: Aren't they? I always set some aside for myself when the traders bring them up from the south.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB3: Ah, Olfina. How may I help you?
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB4: I don't suppose you're going to buy something this time?
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB5: Those green apples you sold me the other day were delicious.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB6: Make it quick, and there might be a bowl of hot stew in it for you.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB7: Maybe, but they wouldn't taste as good as a loaf of fresh-baked pumpernickel.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB8: The gods smile on them who show charity. Best you remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BB9: Exactly. Road patrols were cut in half because the Jarl needed soldiers. Now the bandits are bolder than ever. They've been raiding the farms for food, and supplies are scarcer than ever. Maybe I should sell swords and armor, instead.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BBA: Make it quick, then. I don't want your stink driving my customers away.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BBB: Certainly. I'll be sure to bring some back for you the next time I go hunting.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BBC: Let me guess, they're saying it's because of this trouble with the Stormcloaks?
Skyrim.esm|0A2BBD: That's right, mock the town beggar like everyone else. But I've got a right to browse, don't I? I ain't hurtin' anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0A2BBE: I've got a recipe for venison stew that I want to try out. Will you have venison anytime soon?
Skyrim.esm|0A2C3F: The Star is free to purify itself. Don't worry, mortal. I will return you before you are cleansed.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C40: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of the Reach. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl to watch over your home and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C41: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Whiterun. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl to watch over your home and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C45: Malyn's enchantments are broken. Hold on. I'm going to pull your soul out of there. Brace yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C48: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Haafingar. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl to watch over your home and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C51: Oh, forgive me, Thane. I didn't realize it was you. We'll look the other way this time, but even the Jarl's influence has its limits. Be more careful.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C5A: There is room in my court for a new Thane. It's an honorary title, mainly, but there are a few perks someone like you could make use of. However, I could only grant the title to someone who is known throughout my Hold, and who owns at least one piece of property in my city. You help my people and purchase a house from my steward, and I'll make you my Thane.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C6C: The Jarl has appointed me to be your housecarl. It's an honor to serve you.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C6E: I am your sword and your shield.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C70: What do you need, my Thane?
Skyrim.esm|0A2C71: Honored to see you again, my Thane.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C74: No one in the Jarl's court is above the charge of conspiring against the city. You're coming with us.
Skyrim.esm|0A2C7A: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Eastmarch. Congratulations. I grant you a personal Housecarl to watch over your home and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CBD: Hmph. When most would speak, you listen. You think. You understand that the only thing that matters is you following my orders. To kill.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CBE: Indeed. For you, my friend, seem to understand what's truly important. When I give an order to spill blood, you follow it. No questions. No remorse.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CBF: Oh. No, no, no. Don't you understand? Guilt, innocence, right, wrong.... Irrelevant. What matters is I ordered you to kill someone, and you obeyed.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CC0: Ah, the feisty goodwife. Quite the mouth on her. Someone must have wanted her dead... right?
Skyrim.esm|0A2CC1: The conniving Khajiit. Cat like that was sure to have enemies. It's no wonder you chose him.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CC2: The blubbering barbarian and the tenacious mother. Must have been one of them, right?
Skyrim.esm|0A2CC3: The timid warrior and the sly Khajiit. Must have been one or the other, right?
Skyrim.esm|0A2CC4: Hmm. The fearless wife and mother, and the underhanded Khajiit. Had to be one of them, right? Interesting choice.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CC5: Well, well. Aren't we the overachiever. Three possibilities, three victims. Must have been one of them, right? So why take chances...
Skyrim.esm|0A2CD5: Never you mind, then. I'll find a more charitable soul.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CD6: So you can spend it on drinks at the Bannered Mare? If it's food you need, ask for that instead.
Skyrim.esm|0A2CD7: Spare a coin for a fellow Nord who's down on his luck?
Skyrim.esm|0A30E5: I should lock up the store now. Thank you again.
Skyrim.esm|0A30E9: Mara, I forgot how late it was. Need to lock up the shop.
Skyrim.esm|0A311F: I can't believe it. You have them already? Here, take this. This should cover your end of the work nicely.
Skyrim.esm|0A3120: It's a good start, but you need all four of the volumes or the set isn't complete. Why don't you head back out to Yngvild and find the rest?
Skyrim.esm|0A3121: Yeah, but you're into gold. Let me know if you change your mind. I'm not going anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|0A3122: Then we have a deal. In his later years, Andoril moved into some ice caves known as Yngvild. He prefered the solitude in which to perform his, um... experiments.
Skyrim.esm|0A3123: I'm told they're the ravings of a madman... a wizard named Arondil and his peculiar cravings. It's said to be spread across four volumes and very hard to come by. My client is offering quite a reward for them, which I'd split with you.
Skyrim.esm|0A3124: Good. I was hoping you hadn't forgotten about it. As promised, here's your payment for bringing them to me. Got it as part of a fair trade from my client.
Skyrim.esm|0A3125: Now that you mention it, there is. I have a buyer with, shall we say, a very odd taste in literature looking for some particular books.
Skyrim.esm|0A3126: Recently, a woman was found... naked and shivering along the road to Dawnstar. The only information the guard could get from her were tales of strange experiments and Arondil scribbling in his journals.
Skyrim.esm|0A3127: Only rumors. He used to have a home in Dawnstar until they burned it down. The people say he was doing unspeakable experiments on reanimation... rituals only a necromancer would perform. After he made for Yngvild, he was never heard from again.
Skyrim.esm|0A31D4: Ah, there's nothing like fresh air and honest labor to bolster the spirit.
Skyrim.esm|0A31D5: So far, things are going well. Let's just hope the weather holds.
Skyrim.esm|0A31D6: True enough, but there's nothing wrong with a little adventure now and then. Not much excitement in working the soil every day.
Skyrim.esm|0A31D7: Those little girls of yours are growing like weeds, Lemkil.
Skyrim.esm|0A31D8: I'm trying something new, offering discounts for bulk purchases. So far, that seems to be working.
Skyrim.esm|0A31D9: Yes, and just like weeds, they're nothin' but trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0A31DA: Are you making good progress with the planting?
Skyrim.esm|0A31DB: I hear your crops sold well at the market. Well done, Ennis.
Skyrim.esm|0A3229: Thanks for the offer, Jouane, but I'm saving up for a new sword.
Skyrim.esm|0A322A: We don't need your charity, Breton. If my girls deserve something nice, it'll be their father who buys it.
Skyrim.esm|0A322B: How thoughtful! I'll come take a look after supper.
