Skyrim.esm|0A322C: Good day, Lemkil. I saw some nice dresses for your little girls while I was in Whiterun. I'd be happy to purchase them, if you like.
Skyrim.esm|0A322D: Glad to hear all my hard work has paid off. Maybe we'll fetch a better price for our crops next time we're in the city.
Skyrim.esm|0A322E: The last time I was in Whiterun, the tradesmen were talking about you. It seems your reputation as a businessman is growing.
Skyrim.esm|0A322F: Anything I can pick up for you on my next trip into the city, Erik?
Skyrim.esm|0A3230: I picked up a set of fine silver brushes in the city, Reldith. I thought you might be interested.
Skyrim.esm|0A344D: I'm a sailor on the Northwind. We're laying over in Windhelm for awhile.
Skyrim.esm|0A344E: A life on the river makes for weak legs, so I do a lot of walking when we dock.
Skyrim.esm|0A344F: Captain Kjar expects the best, but he's a fair man. He never fails to reward hard work and loyalty.
Skyrim.esm|0A3450: Never much cared for Windhelm. The place is depressing.
Skyrim.esm|0A345F: I'm a lot happier out here on the deck than I am wandering around in that ruin of a city.
Skyrim.esm|0A349B: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Winterhold. I grant you this weapon from the armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify the guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A349E: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Falkreath. Congratulations. I grant you this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A34A0: I hereby grant you permission to purchase property in my city. Talk to my steward if you're interested. And take this. A gift from the Jarl's personal stores.
Skyrim.esm|0A34A2: I do. A personal matter. My father died trying to negotiate with the Forsworn, back when that was possible. They took his shield to those Hagraven beasts they consort with. I need you to bring it home where it belongs. I warn you, it's dangerous work. No man of mine has ever faced a Hagraven and lived. I'll understand if you decline.
Skyrim.esm|0A34A3: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Hjaalmarch. Congratulations. I grant you this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A34A4: There is room in my court for a new Thane. It's an honorary title, mainly, but there are a few perks someone like you could make use of. However, I could only grant the title to someone who is known throughout my Hold. You help my people, and I'll make you my Thane.
Skyrim.esm|0A34A8: Then by my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of the Pale. Congratulations. I grant you this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A34B4: I heard those Forsworn out west live in caves. Can't be any worse than trying to live in Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0A34B5: Can't wait until we raise anchor. Anytime the boat's not moving, I start to get impatient.
Skyrim.esm|0A34B6: Sorry, but I'm not looking to hire any more crew right now.
Skyrim.esm|0A34B7: You don't know what freedom really means until you've lived on the river.
Skyrim.esm|0A34B8: Been on the river since I could walk. Wouldn't trade it for the world.
Skyrim.esm|0A34B9: While you're on my boat, you follow my rules. First rule is, don't touch anything that doesn't belong to you.
Skyrim.esm|0A34BA: I understand what potions do, but I don't always understand how they do it.
Skyrim.esm|0A34BB: Everyone thinks I'm too young to be a ship's captain, but I'm going to prove them wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0A34BC: I don't buy all that talk about ship captains having to be stern and strict. I want my crewmen to enjoy their work.
Skyrim.esm|0A34BD: No one in the Jarl's court is above the charge of conspiring against the city. You're coming with us.
Skyrim.esm|0A34F5: I never imagined we'd find something like this.
Skyrim.esm|0A34F6: Why is this buried so far within Saarthal?
Skyrim.esm|0A34F7: Well now... would you look at that.
Skyrim.esm|0A34FD: Have you seen Astrid? She needs to speak with you. It seemed urgent. Come talk to me afterward.
Skyrim.esm|0A34FE: Please. Not now. You'll know when I'm ready to discuss the issue.
Skyrim.esm|0A3509: I inherited the ship when my mother passed away. She spent her life on the water, and now I do, too.
Skyrim.esm|0A350D: I'm good with stars and charts, but I admit I've got some things to learn about leading men.
Skyrim.esm|0A353E: I plan to spend all the days I got left on the water. It's where I belong.
Skyrim.esm|0A3546: Once sailing gets in your blood, it don't get out.
Skyrim.esm|0A3547: I been a sailor for so long, I could navigate these rivers an' seas blindfolded.
Skyrim.esm|0A355A: Cap'n Wayfinder's as green as seaweed, but he's learnin'.
Skyrim.esm|0A355B: I don't like taking orders from a boy, but I need the gold.
Skyrim.esm|0A355C: I could run this ship better than that boy who calls himself a captain. Maybe one day I'll do something about it.
Skyrim.esm|0A355D: The captain inherited the Sea Squall from his mother when she died. Maybe I can inherit the ship if he dies...
Skyrim.esm|0A355E: Not a day goes by that I don't think about finding a new ship. I guess it's easier just to stay put.
Skyrim.esm|0A3BB3: Such is our way! Such is the ancient custom of Skyrim, and all Nords!
Skyrim.esm|0A3BB5: There was no murder! Ulfric challenged Torygg. He beat the High King in fair combat.
Skyrim.esm|0A3BB7: By opening that gate for Ulfric you betrayed the people of Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0A3BB8: Roggvir. You helped Ulfric Stormcloak escape this city after he murdered High King Torygg.
Skyrim.esm|0A3BB9: On this day... I go to Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C49: Let me know if there's anything else you need.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C4A: I'll show you to your room. Right this way.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C5C: You should run while you have the chance.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C5F: Must be my mind playing tricks on me.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C63: Hmm. I was sure I heard something.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C64: Ah... the power of the dragon is yours! There can be no doubt that you are the Dragonborn of prophecy.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C65: Alduin cannot ignore this for long.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C69: Stay alert. They may not have gone far.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C6A: Hmm. Gone. I don't like it.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C6B: Good. I can use the practice.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C7F: Could have been my imagination, I suppose...
Skyrim.esm|0A3C82: Oho, so that's where you went.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C8C: I'm going to check that out.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C8E: You'll have to do better than that.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C8F: Not bad... but it won't save you.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C92: I could use some help over here!
Skyrim.esm|0A3C93: Ah! I've heard of this Shouting, but never seen it. Fascinating...
Skyrim.esm|0A3C94: I've read the dragon language, but to hear it spoken is most interesting.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C95: This skill should prove very useful, Dragonborn. Fighting fire with fire, as the saying goes.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C96: The dragons will surely fear your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|0A3C9A: We've got to bring it down!
Skyrim.esm|0A3C9B: Don't let it get too close!
Skyrim.esm|0A3C9C: We've got to ground that bastard!
Skyrim.esm|0A3C9D: Come on! This is our best chance!
Skyrim.esm|0A3C9F: Now it's my turn, you son of a bitch.
Skyrim.esm|0A3CA3: Come on. What are you waiting for?
Skyrim.esm|0A3CA4: You're not afraid of me, are you?
Skyrim.esm|0A3CA5: Lorkhan's eyes, you're an ugly one, aren't you?
Skyrim.esm|0A3CA7: Did you think the Blades had been destroyed?
Skyrim.esm|0A3CA8: Now it's your turn, Thalmor bitch.
Skyrim.esm|0A3CA9: The Blades will never be defeated, Thalmor dog.
Skyrim.esm|0A3CAA: I could use a hand here.
Skyrim.esm|0A3CAE: I gave up this kind of thing a long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|0A3CAF: You can still run. It's not too late.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E04: Thalmor dog! Death is too good for you.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E2D: You'll die knowing it was the Blades that sent you to Oblivion.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E2F: You're starting to make me angry.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E5B: Sometimes the famous come to us. Sometimes men and women come to us to seek their fame. It makes no difference. What matters is their heart.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E5C: Here, let's have Farkas show you where you'll be resting your head.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E5D: Don't let Vilkas catch you saying that.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E5F: That attitude would get you far, if you were some stuffy merchant or a Jarl's footstool. Around here, you'll want to learn to live your own life. Remember, nobody rules anybody in the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E60: I'm in a bit of trouble here.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E61: Fine, I'll get someone else to take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E62: You have my brother and I, obviously. But I don't know if the rest will go along quite so easily.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E63: I need some help with this one!
Skyrim.esm|0A3E64: Woah, ease up there. We're rough, but there's no need to kill a Shield-Brother over a dispute. I like your fire, though. You'll make a fierce Companion. Let us hunt together sometime.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E65: Calm down there, whelp. Don't feel like you need to show off for our sake. Vilkas, here, will be the one testing your mettle.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E66: Looks like someone's been digging here. And recently. Tread lightly.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E67: I see you've been allowed to know some secrets before your appointed time. No matter. Yes, it's true. Not every Companion, though. Only members of the Circle all share the blood of the beast. Some take to it more than others.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E68: That may be so. This is Vilkas. He will test your arm.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E6C: This isn't a command. Just decency. Help out an old blacksmith. I've got to get back to my work.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E6F: Ah, a woman who lets her actions speak for her. I knew there was something I liked about you.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E70: Not actually a Companion myself, but none of them know how to work a forge properly, and I'm honored to serve them. My name is Eorlund Gray-Mane. I work the Skyforge. Best steel in all of Skyrim. All of Tamriel.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E71: You know this one? I saw him training in the yard with Vilkas.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E72: Oh you're back. Do you still think you could stand as a Companion?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E73: Of course. How are you in a battle, girl?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E74: Master, you're not truly considering accepting him?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E75: Master, you're not truly considering accepting her?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E77: This work isn't for everyone. Maybe you're more cut out for those snowberries up in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E78: Be careful around the burial stones. I don't want to haul you back to Jorrvaskr on my back.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E79: Oh, don't worry too much about it. They were all whelps once. They just might not like to talk about it. And don't always just do what you're told. Nobody rules anybody in the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E7A: Would you now? Here, let me have a look at you. Hm. Yes, perhaps. A certain strength of spirit.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E7B: I have a favor to ask.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E7C: But I still hear the call of the blood.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E7F: Not bad. Next time won't be so easy.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E80: I am nobody's master, Vilkas. And last I checked, we had some empty beds in Jorrvaskr for those with a fire burning in their hearts.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E82: Of course we did, icebrain. Show this new blood where the rest of the whelps sleep.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E83: Shor's bones... how did they find this place?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E85: Ah, good. I've been waiting for this. Good to see you made it up here.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E86: There you are. I had wondered where you got to.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E87: Nice to have a new face around. It gets boring here sometimes. I hope we keep you. This can be a rough life.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E88: New blood? Oh, I remember you. Come on, follow me.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E89: You've already turned down a job that I had for you. Work needs doing, and if you're not up to it, maybe you're not cut out to be a Companion.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E8A: Well, I'm not sure how they've managed it, but they have. No leaders since Ysgramor. Kodlak is the Harbinger, and he's a sort of advisor for the whole group, but every man is his own. Every woman, her own.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E8D: Ah, yes. I heard you gave him quite a thrashing.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E94: I'm guessing you're the newcomer then?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E95: Apologies. But perhaps this isn't the time. I've never even heard of this outsider.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E96: Skjor and Aela like to tease me, but they are good people. They challenge us to be our best.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E97: We all do. It is our burden to bear. But we can overcome.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E98: Not here. Out in the yard. Come on.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E99: The old man said to have a look at you, so let's do this. Just have a few swings at me so I can see your form. Don't worry, I can take it.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E9A: That's the spirit. Vilkas, here, will get started on that.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E9B: The quarters are up here. Just pick a bed and fall in it when you're tired. Tilma will keep the place clean. She always has.
Skyrim.esm|0A3E9C: You think running will save you?
Skyrim.esm|0A3E9F: All right, so here you are. Looks like the others are eager to meet you. Come to me or Aela if you're looking for work. Once you've made a bit of a name for yourself, Skjor and Vilkas might have things for you to do. Good luck. Welcome to the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F4F: No signs of any dragon right now, but it sure looks like he's been here.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F50: Spread out and look for survivors. We need to know what we're dealing with.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F7D: There weren't any dragons then, idiot. They're just coming back now for the first time in... forever.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F80: But the old tales tell of the Dragonborn who could kill dragons and steal their power. You must be one!
Skyrim.esm|0A3F83: I thought we were all dead for sure!
Skyrim.esm|0A3F84: We killed it! I can't believe it!
Skyrim.esm|0A3F85: Dragonborn? What are you talking about?
Skyrim.esm|0A3F87: Let's make sure that overgrown lizard is really dead.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F89: I never heard of Tiber Septim killing any dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F8A: That's right! My grandfather used to tell stories about the Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F8B: Do not be afraid. Your Shout will not harm us.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F8C: Shout at us, and let us taste of your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|0A3F8D: Strike us with the power of your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|0A4028: We'll meet back up at the Sanctuary! I'll be fine. Go!
Skyrim.esm|0A4029: If there's nothing else, I really must ask you to leave.
Skyrim.esm|0A402A: Today, the problems of Skyrim are not my problems. Nor are they yours. Today we are joined in peace and happiness. So please, enjoy yourselves.
Skyrim.esm|0A402B: These days, it seems there is dread everywhere, whether it be war or dragon. But I beg you - here, today, put such fears aside. Enjoy yourselves!
Skyrim.esm|0A402C: Grelod... murdered. Here. I... I need... air... You... you... Get out of here! Get out!
Skyrim.esm|0A402D: You really shouldn't be in here.
Skyrim.esm|0A402E: I'm sorry, but the children aren't up for adoption right now. You should go.
Skyrim.esm|0A402F: My only concern is for the children. The poor darlings have no one else.
Skyrim.esm|0A4030: I thank you all again for making this the best wedding a woman could ask for.
Skyrim.esm|0A4031: You have all helped make this a truly extraordinary wedding. All of my dreams have come true. So thank you. Thank you all.
Skyrim.esm|0A4032: Astrid ordered me to keep an eye on you. Figured you could use a hand when the chaos erupted. I'll try and hold them off as best I can. You get back to the Sanctuary!
Skyrim.esm|0A4035: I guess we should have expected this response, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0A4036: I just wanted to take this time to thank you all for being here. To thank you for sharing this wonderfully happy day with myself, and my new husband.
Skyrim.esm|0A4037: Are you all enjoying yourselves? Please, eat, drink, and make merry on this most happy of days.
Skyrim.esm|0A4050: Gotta have a sharp axe to cut through all that fat...
Skyrim.esm|0A4051: Hurry up with it, we've got a merchant in the pit to take care of after we carve this thing up.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A27: I'm glad you're coming with us. They say Talos favors you.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A28: That dragon should've stuck with killing Imperials. Now he's going to learn how real Nords fight.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A29: My cousin's stationed out at the watchtower. I hope he's all right.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A2A: I doubt any of us are coming back from this mission.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A2B: With Irileth, you either come back covered with glory or not at all.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A2C: You really think we can kill a dragon?
Skyrim.esm|0A4A2D: I'm looking forward to testing myself against this dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A2E: I hear you faced a dragon at Helgen and lived to tell about it. That ain't nothing.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A2F: We'd better keep moving. Who knows what that dragon is doing.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A30: I hope the tower holds out until we get there.
Skyrim.esm|0A4A32: Shor's bones, did it kill them all...?
Skyrim.esm|0A4A33: We'd better find out what's happened.
Skyrim.esm|0A4E80: I daresay you've learned all I can teach you. But... That doesn't mean there isn't more out there.
Skyrim.esm|0A4E81: Over the years I've heard things. Powerful magics lost to the ages, incredible spells that only the most skilled mages can master. This is the only thing I've ever found. I think it's better off in your hands.
Skyrim.esm|0A5469: We will do your next test in the courtyard. Follow Master Borri.
Skyrim.esm|0A546A: Master Borri has agreed to teach you the first Word of a new Shout. Attend to him.
Skyrim.esm|0A56D4: Attic room, eh? Well... we don't have an attic room, but you can have the one on the left. Make yourself at home.
Skyrim.esm|0A56E0: The bride has been murdered! Somebody help!
Skyrim.esm|0A59A6: La la la, la la la, la la la, la la la la. La la la, la la la la la laaaaaahhhh...
Skyrim.esm|0A59A7: La la la, do do do do do, dum de dum dum...
Skyrim.esm|0A59A8: Dum, di di dum, dum di dum, dum di di di di dum da da dum.
Skyrim.esm|0A59A9: It's time we put a little kink in the Emperor's security.
Skyrim.esm|0A6386: The boss is going to cut those creepy cave people to pieces!
Skyrim.esm|0A6387: Yeah! It'll teach them to steal from us! One of them took my good helmet.
Skyrim.esm|0A6388: I hope one comes running up here so I can stab it right in the face.
Skyrim.esm|0A6389: I would be fine if they didn't.  Have you seen their eyes? They don't have any! Gross.
Skyrim.esm|0A6834: Come out and face me, you coward!
Skyrim.esm|0A683C: Hiding? The shadows are my domain!
Skyrim.esm|0A68F7: I know you're here. Show yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0A6923: The afterlife will bring you darkness.
Skyrim.esm|0A6927: Bless you, Lady of Shadows... I live another day.
Skyrim.esm|0A6956: I send you to your rest.
Skyrim.esm|0A6957: You were no match for a Nightingale.
Skyrim.esm|0A695D: That wasn't a fight, it was an execution.
Skyrim.esm|0A6ABC: That was hardly worth my time.
Skyrim.esm|0A6AE2: Your feeble attempt to hide is insulting!
Skyrim.esm|0A6B44: Is that the best you can do?
Skyrim.esm|0A6B48: I claim your soul for Nocturnal!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BAF: The shadows will be your undoing!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BB5: I promise a swift but painful end!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BB8: I bring death from the shadows!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BB9: By Nocturnal's grace, I will defeat you!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BBA: I'll bury my blade in your heart!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BBB: Your death will be my triumph!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BBC: You're not a threat... merely an annoyance.
Skyrim.esm|0A6BBD: You've just written your own epitaph.
Skyrim.esm|0A6BBE: Your blood will adorn my blade!
Skyrim.esm|0A6BE9: It's easy to find things to sell. Things nobody will miss. Things from underground, or just left lying around in someone's house. I bet the two of us, we're very alike. So why not take the next step? We can help each other. My sister Deeja and I are treasure hunters. We like to collect things.
Skyrim.esm|0A6C52: I'll find you... it's only a matter of time.
Skyrim.esm|0A6C77: Coward. Come out and fight me!
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB0: It's good we hold the Reach. Were the rebels to gain control, the silver mines would give them coin to hire more thugs and expand their violence.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB1: We need to stem the flow of silver to the rebellion. Until we retake the Reach, the Stormcloaks will plunder it's mines to fund further violence.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB2: Hjaalmarch is an important buffer zone between the rebel forces and the capital. Ulfric will try to break through at some point.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB3: I'm losing my patience with this damn war. We must take Hjaalmarch back! The Stormcloaks will be beating upon the doors of the capital soon.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB4: Ulfric will want to take Whiterun from us. But we will hold fast. We can't give the rebellion free reign through the center of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB5: I can't believe we lost Whiterun. The rebels have a great advantage in controlling the center now. We must take it back!
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB6: By holding onto Falkreath, we control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. Ulfric will try for it soon. We'll be there to stop him.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB7: I fear losing Falkreath may give too much advantage to Ulfric. The rebels now control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. I want it back.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB8: Taking the Pale gives us another port in Dawnstar, and puts us within striking distance of Windhelm. That should make Ulfric a bit more cautious, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0A6CB9: Controlling the Pale gives Ulfric a port town and takes the pressure off of Windhelm. But it's only a matter of time before it's back in our hands.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CBA: Now that we have Winterhold, I can garrison some troops there until I'm ready to march on Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CBB: There's not much up there, but if we can regain Winterhold, our proximity to Windhelm should keep Ulfric wary and wasting troops garrisoned at home.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CBC: I almost can't believe it's over. The rebellion is squashed, Windhelm is free. I must admit, Ulfric put up a stronger fight than I was expecting.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CBD: We will oust Ulfric from Windhelm soon enough. And when we do, I'll have his head on a plate.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CBE: Taking control of the Rift was a great victory. Ulfric must be rather nervous with us so close to his door step.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CBF: We need the Rift. We need to regain control of it before we can march on Windhelm without worry about our rear guard.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC0: I almost can't believe it. The Empire has been defeated. The world bears witness to the power and righteousness of the sons and daughters of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC1: Soon enough we will march on Solitude. And on that day the heroes of Sovngarde will march with us, and the Empire will fall to its knees in fear.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC2: Now that the Empire has been driven from the Reach we can put a stop to the raping of her silver mines. That silver belongs in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC3: The Empire's ravenous hunger for Skyrim's silver makes them fierce. But rest assured, we'll win the Reach and wrest it from their greedy paws.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC4: Tullius must be getting nervous. Taking Hjaalmarch we're practically in his backyard now. As soon as we're able, we'll march on Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC5: We should take Hjaalmarch. It'll be an important pressure point on the Imperials. We'd be within spitting distance of the capital.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC6: We've driven the Imperials out of Whiterun. This is good. Very good. We now control the center. It's a powerful position. One I aim to keep.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC7: The Imperials have really dug into Whiterun. We'll wrest it from them soon enough. We must. Its central position is key.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC8: I'm proud we liberated Falkreath from the Empire. In many ways it's the heart and soul of Skyrim. Not to mention its rather strategic position.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CC9: The Empire thinks it holds Falkreath from us. But the souls of Skyrim's bravest are buried there and will fill our hearts and strengthen our blades.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CCA: Tullius has camps in the Pale, entertaining thoughts of battle. The Empire still underestimates the force of our cause. It will be their undoing.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CCB: I can't believe we let them take the Pale. Losing the port of Dawnstar is lessened only by the threat of Imperial forces being so close to Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CCC: I'm not sure why Tullius is wasting his time trying to take Winterhold from us. But if he wants to throw his men away, I'll gladly accept that gift.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CCD: I don't like Imperial troops holed up in Winterhold... such a close march to Windhelm. So I'm sending a campaign up there to take it back.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CCE: Damn Tullius. He's pocketed men in the Rift. It's dangerous having Imperials skulking about on the southern border of Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|0A6CCF: Losing the Rift gives Tullius easy access to the south of Eastmarch. Talos preserve us. We must take it back!
Skyrim.esm|0A6CD7: By defeating Ulfric at Whiterun we have solidified the allegiance of the Jarl. Perhaps the Stormcloaks will soon lose heart for this little rebellion of theirs once and for all.
Skyrim.esm|0A6D75: I guess I expected too much from you.
Skyrim.esm|0A6D76: Looks like I chose the right person for the job.
Skyrim.esm|0A6EFE: Now look what you've gotten yourself into. No worries. Just sit tight. I'll find the release.
Skyrim.esm|0A7036: Ah, you're back. It was good to fight alongside you. I'm sure people won't be forgetting that wedding anytime soon, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0A7037: She's dead! On my watch! This won't stand!
Skyrim.esm|0A7038: Oh my, yes. I absolutely love weddings. I haven't found my special someone yet, but there's still hope. Vittoria is, well, getting up in years...
Skyrim.esm|0A7039: Am I! Free food and wine, and nobody trying to throw me in the dungeon. I wish they did weddings here every day.
Skyrim.esm|0A703A: Not really. It's just a bunch of elders standing around, looking important. I wish something exciting would happen.
Skyrim.esm|0A703B: Oh. Why... why certainly. I keep telling myself I'm not losing a son, I'm gaining an Imperial. Daughter. An Imperial daughter...
Skyrim.esm|0A703C: No, I'm not having a good time. My boy just married a gods-forsaken Imperial. Skyrim is full of eager Nord women, and he beds down with the enemy.
Skyrim.esm|0A703D: The best. Vittoria's a good woman. Any man should be so lucky. I know my father's not happy about me marrying an Imperial, but that's his problem.
Skyrim.esm|0A703E: I most certainly am not. I've just lost my daughter to a Nord beast, and my nephew Titus is too busy playing Emperor to even show up.
Skyrim.esm|0A703F: I am, thank you. I do so enjoy singing at weddings. It's just one of the many duties we at the Bards College are called upon to perform.
Skyrim.esm|0A7040: So far. I just hope things remain calm. Wine gets drunk, tempers flare. This may be a joyous celebration, but it's shadowed by a lot of animosity.
Skyrim.esm|0A7041: Oh, yes. Presiding over the union of two lovers is one of my greatest responsibilities. It helps that Asgeir and Vittoria truly care for each other.
Skyrim.esm|0A7052: Skjor was looking for you before. Talk to him before doing anything else.
Skyrim.esm|0A7161: Thank you, Lisbet. It's been such a joy to dine with you all these years.
Skyrim.esm|0A7163: This is lovely, as always, Eola.
Skyrim.esm|0A7164: When was your first meal, Banning?
Skyrim.esm|0A7165: I never miss a good meal.
Skyrim.esm|0A7166: I'm just glad we had this, now. I was almost getting ready to eat my servant and blame it on the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|0A7167: Thank you for coming to dinner, Hogni.
Skyrim.esm|0A7168: Eola is a fine host. She really keeps the faithful together.
Skyrim.esm|0A7169: I always preferred the liver myself. My brother had such a juicy liver.
Skyrim.esm|0A716A: It's so good to have everyone around the table again.
Skyrim.esm|0A716B: The toes are my favorite part. I hope I get to take a few home with me.
Skyrim.esm|0A716C: Either way, I came back with meat for the table.
Skyrim.esm|0A716D: That must have been quite a surprise.
Skyrim.esm|0A716E: Shh. You mustn't speak about such things away from the shrine. Only you and your dinner guests can know.
Skyrim.esm|0A716F: Better if we don't talk about that. As far as anyone's concerned, we all had dinner at a nice inn along the road, and nothing strange happened.
Skyrim.esm|0A7170: Don't go talking about that. Only the dinner guests can know.
Skyrim.esm|0A7171: I always leave room for the main course, Eola.
Skyrim.esm|0A7172: My friend Marex. We were hunting, and I thought he was an elk.
Skyrim.esm|0A7173: Ah, yes. The courier. I guess you know my secret ingredient for that spiced beef, now.
Skyrim.esm|0A7174: You buy a war dog from me? There's a reason why my hounds are so eager to bite into people.
Skyrim.esm|0A7175: That's right. You're the sellsword. Thank you again. It's so nice to have help with my husband gone. Shame what happened to him.
Skyrim.esm|0A7176: One of my customers? Did you know I inherited the store from my late husband? Shame what happened to him. He had such good taste.
Skyrim.esm|0A7177: "The bloodiest beef in the Reach!" Ha ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|0A7178: Not too full on bread and wine, I hope?
Skyrim.esm|0A717A: Ah, the new disciple. I hear you have quite the appetite.
Skyrim.esm|0A717B: It's an honor, Keeper of the Ring.
Skyrim.esm|0A717C: You've brought us quite the meal.
Skyrim.esm|0A717D: You were given Namira's ring? A great honor.
Skyrim.esm|0A717E: I remember my first feast. I envy you.
Skyrim.esm|0A717F: I'm so glad Eola invited you to dinner.
Skyrim.esm|0A7180: Namira spoke to you? I knew this feast was going to be something.
Skyrim.esm|0A7181: Now this is going to be a good feast.
Skyrim.esm|0A7248: With good planning and constant vigilance. We must have ample reserves of water to combat the fires that will surely spread after an attack. But our greatest weapon? Courage. For if we cannot kill the beast, we must at least have the tenacity to drive it back. Until brave heroes step forward to destroy these monsters, that is the best we can do.
Skyrim.esm|0A7249: Ha! The most important question of all, that one. You'll want the Bannered Mare. See old Hulda, she'll keep you swimmin' in mead.
Skyrim.esm|0A724A: Ha! The most important question of all, that one. You'll want the Bannered Mare. See Olfina, she'll keep you swimmin' in mead.
Skyrim.esm|0A724B: I can't. Not tonight. I need to go home. I swear, my family is starting to suspect something.
Skyrim.esm|0A724C: Since Adrianne's death, I've had to buy them from the Khajiit caravans. Tried making some myself, but I'm no good with the forge and anvil. Anyway, our wares are strong and the prices fair. What else matters?
Skyrim.esm|0A724D: Curse you for bringing up that bitter memory. Not long ago it was my husband Eorlund. There was no finer smith in the nine holds. Now he's dead and gone and the Skyforge of Jorrvaskr lies cold.
Skyrim.esm|0A724E: Glad to see you're making new friends. I know Jon's death was hard on you.
Skyrim.esm|0A724F: Try Olfina, the new innkeeper at the Bannered Mare. It's just up the road, near the market. You should check in at Dragonsreach, too. There's so much going on these days, the Jarl or his Steward might have need of help.
Skyrim.esm|0A7250: Don't say that out loud. Not here. And the answer's still no. But tomorrow. Definitely.
Skyrim.esm|0A7251: I wish your mother were still alive. She could answer you much better. Let me think on it and we'll talk later.
Skyrim.esm|0A7252: Ah, splendid, splendid. It's for a special brew I'm working on. A love elixir like none other. Oh, but I suppose you expect some compensation. Um... here, these potions should suffice.
Skyrim.esm|0A7253: Here you stand, talking to a still grieving widow. Why? Am I that interesting? Or perhaps there are other things on your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0A7254: My brother Jon had the right of it - join the Bards College and avoid this pointless war. Too bad he died before he had the chance.
Skyrim.esm|0A7255: I...I miss our son terribly, especially since Eorlund died. Thorald... he went missing, fighting the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0A7256: Eorlund Gray-Mane was a stubborn fool. Our friendship ended when he turned his back on the Emperor. Now he's dead.
Skyrim.esm|0A7258: My wife was a good woman. She could be a little hard-headed sometimes, but I loved her just the same. Gods I miss her.
Skyrim.esm|0A7259: I don't claim to be the best blacksmith in Whiterun. Eorlund Gray-Mane was that and more, but now he's dead. All I ask is a fair chance.
Skyrim.esm|0A725A: Have you met my father? He was the steward, up at Dragonsreach when Balgruuf was Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0A725B: My father never thought I was smart enough to be a stablemaster. I just wish he was around to see me now.
Skyrim.esm|0A7260: Oh come now, resorting to petty tricks are we?
Skyrim.esm|0A7261: I don't have time for this.
Skyrim.esm|0A7262: There's no need to hide. I won't hurt you.
Skyrim.esm|0A7264: I can hear your heart beating.
Skyrim.esm|0A7266: I'll cut out your eyes and sell 'em for coin!
Skyrim.esm|0A7269: My blade thirsts for your blood!
Skyrim.esm|0A726A: Come on. Show me you've got what it takes!
Skyrim.esm|0A726B: You just made the last mistake of your life!
Skyrim.esm|0A7279: Come on, put up a fight!
Skyrim.esm|0A727F: Sure, pick on the old guy!
Skyrim.esm|0A7285: So, it's to the pain then!
Skyrim.esm|0A7286: Ah good, I could use some practice!
Skyrim.esm|0A728B: They picked on the wrong old man.
Skyrim.esm|0A728C: Haven't fought like that in years.
Skyrim.esm|0A728F: Didn't even work up a sweat.
Skyrim.esm|0A7291: That's what happens when you cross the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0A7293: I think they got the message.
Skyrim.esm|0A7294: Damn, just as I was getting warmed up.
Skyrim.esm|0A7296: And that's why I'm the best.
Skyrim.esm|0A7334: It's abandoned and it's always been abandoned.
Skyrim.esm|0A7335: Seems no one in this city has.
Skyrim.esm|0A7338: I was actually just about to head on inside. Be good to have someone watch my back. Follow me, and keep your eyes open. Daedra are powerful creatures and tricksters. Never know what you'll find.
Skyrim.esm|0A733A: Excuse me, but do you know anything about this house? Seen anyone enter or leave?
Skyrim.esm|0A75F1: Hmm. I guess that will have to do. You should pass for a real guest, at least until you open your mouth. Ready to board the carriage to the embassy?
Skyrim.esm|0A791E: You made it out alive, at least. Your gear's safe in my room, as promised.
Skyrim.esm|0A7920: Be careful. We need to find out what happened here.
Skyrim.esm|0A7B2F: There is an empty home in the city, but there was some... unpleasantness recently. I'm afraid it's not available just yet.
Skyrim.esm|0A7FE1: I'll buy everything you've dug up.
Skyrim.esm|0A7FE2: We could always use help picking crops if you need a few extra coins.
Skyrim.esm|0A7FEB: Mining is hard work, but we pay a fair price for any ore you dig up.
Skyrim.esm|0A8041: Well. You must have all those annoying gods on your side to have made it this far, whoever you are. Even I have to admit, that's pretty impressive.
Skyrim.esm|0A8057: I have watched you for some time, you know. The decisions you've made intrigue me, and I wonder if you are the proper agent for a task of mine.
Skyrim.esm|0A8058: Breathe deep, mortal. I would have you hear me well, so let these vapors fill your lungs.
Skyrim.esm|0A8059: In a sense, but no more poisoned than a fool after too much wine.
Skyrim.esm|0A81F2: Well, where is it? Forgive me, but I don't dare just trust your word, no matter who you are.
Skyrim.esm|0A81F9: I'm... I'm sorry you had to see that.
Skyrim.esm|0A81FA: Oh, no. Only the Circle have the beastblood. Prove your honor to be a Companion. "Eyes on the prey, not the horizon."
Skyrim.esm|0A81FB: No, only members of the Circle get this blessing. Right now we're just seeing if you're worth keeping around.
Skyrim.esm|0A81FC: It's a blessing given to some of us. We can be like wild beasts. Fearsome.
Skyrim.esm|0A81FD: Come on now, you're a sharp one. You must have heard of werewolves before. It's a blessing given to the most honored of the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0A8455: You'd better clear out before someone gets hurt.
Skyrim.esm|0A8456: The answer ain't gonna be any different the next time. Arrogant elven bastard.
Skyrim.esm|0A8457: Where is he? The one who was asking about the fugitive hiding in the Ratway?
Skyrim.esm|0A8459: Your lack of cooperation has been noted. We'll be back if we require further information. You won't get a second chance.
Skyrim.esm|0A845A: You're in the wrong place to be asking those kind of questions, pal.
Skyrim.esm|0A84FD: Vals Veran. My family has never really seen eye to eye with him and he has finally gone off the deep end. He's gone in to defile our family tomb by using our ancestors for his filthy dark elf necromancy.
Skyrim.esm|0A84FE: Great! I'll unlock the door and meet you inside, then you can lead the way.
Skyrim.esm|0A84FF: Fine, but I've locked the door so don't think you're getting in there without me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8500: No offense, I mean I'm sure you aren't into any of that kind of stuff. My aunt went in after him, but she hasn't come out yet and I'm afraid to go in by myself.
Skyrim.esm|0A8501: My aunt went in after him, but she hasn't come out yet and I don't think I can take him by myself.
Skyrim.esm|0A8502: Can you help me? He's in the tomb doing Gods know what with my dead relatives.
Skyrim.esm|0A8607: Really. We aren't interested in seeing all that. Remove yourself until you are properly attired!
Skyrim.esm|0A8608: You might consider putting on... something. Anything.
Skyrim.esm|0A8609: This one wonders - do you have no clothes, or just like the feel of the wind?
Skyrim.esm|0A860A: Gonna get cold tonight. Might want to at least cover your delicates.
Skyrim.esm|0A860B: Please. Remove your naked obscenity from the company of civilized folk.
Skyrim.esm|0A860C: Dear. You really ought to put some clothes on. You're embarrassing yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0A860D: Enough already. Put on some clothes. You're making people nervous.
Skyrim.esm|0A8610: You must either be crazy or supremely confident to go walking around with no clothes on...
Skyrim.esm|0A8611: Not sure you should be walking around naked.
Skyrim.esm|0A8612: It should be embarrassed to be uncovered so.
Skyrim.esm|0A8613: Umm. You got no clothes. You should get some.
Skyrim.esm|0A8616: Ought put on some clothes. Bound to upset some people...
Skyrim.esm|0A8617: Put on a cloth sack at the very least. You're making me... unseasonably warm...
Skyrim.esm|0A8619: You aren't as attractive as you'd like to believe.
Skyrim.esm|0A861A: Either I'm drunk, or you're naked. Possibly both.
Skyrim.esm|0A861B: Cover yourself. Have you no shame?
Skyrim.esm|0A861C: Next time, you might want to get dressed before leaving the house.
Skyrim.esm|0A861D: This one suggests donning some apparel.
Skyrim.esm|0A861E: Ysmir's beard, you're going to freeze to death...
Skyrim.esm|0A861F: Put on some damn clothes already!
Skyrim.esm|0A8620: You really should put on some clothes, you know.
Skyrim.esm|0A8621: All right, we've all seen enough. Put on some clothes.
Skyrim.esm|0A8622: The way you walk around at home is your own business. When you're at home...
Skyrim.esm|0A8623: What are you, some kind of barbarian? Don't care for armor?
Skyrim.esm|0A8624: It's probably not a good idea to walk around naked like that.
Skyrim.esm|0A8625: It should put on some coverings.
Skyrim.esm|0A8627: Put on some clothes, you damned fool.
Skyrim.esm|0A8628: You ought to cover your... unmentionables.
Skyrim.esm|0A8629: Even rags would be preferable to your current wardrobe. Or lack thereof...
Skyrim.esm|0A862A: Careful. They might put you away if you don't at least put on some pants.
Skyrim.esm|0A862B: I've seen enough. Put on some clothes.
Skyrim.esm|0A87D9: No matter. Another one will be along any time now.
Skyrim.esm|0A87DD: Well...that's disappointing. This one didn't survive the fall either.
Skyrim.esm|0A8802: I'm sorry! This isn't what I want!
Skyrim.esm|0A8803: I don't want to do this!
Skyrim.esm|0A8804: I don't have any choice! I'm sorry!
Skyrim.esm|0A8A19: Hey! What the... keep your damn magic to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A50: Keep your damn spells to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A51: That was pathetic. It didn't even do anything.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A52: Ah! Damn wizard. Keep your spells off of me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A53: Stop that! Keep your spells to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A54: Don't try to magic me, you damn wizard.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A57: Hey! What are you trying to pull?
Skyrim.esm|0A8A67: Don't know what that was, but don't try it again.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A6B: Magic is for the weak. Pick up some steel if you want to fight me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A6C: Wizards. There oughta be a law against them.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A6D: Whoa, whoa, whoa? What did you do?
Skyrim.esm|0A8A6F: Ah! Keep your spells away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A71: Ah! I'm just an old woman. Don't hurt me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A72: You stop that! I will not be violated by some... half-Septim magician!
Skyrim.esm|0A8A73: Oh! What did you just do?
Skyrim.esm|0A8A74: Hey! I didn't ask you to magic me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A76: Ah! Picking on an old man? What did I ever do to you?
Skyrim.esm|0A8A77: Oh! I don't feel any different. What did you do?
Skyrim.esm|0A8A7B: Stop it! I don't like magic.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A7C: Wow! That was fun. Do it again!
Skyrim.esm|0A8A7E: You there! That was uninvited. No more.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A80: What was that? Don't do it again.
Skyrim.esm|0A8A84: Huh... what was that? What'd you do?
Skyrim.esm|0A8A85: I did not ask for that! No more magic.
Skyrim.esm|0A8BEA: The Akaviri blood seal can only be opened with the right kind of blood. Your blood, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0A8BF1: The entrance must be past the Forsworn camp. Look for a canyon or cave entrance into the Karthspire.
Skyrim.esm|0A8C06: Ah, yes. The entrance seems to be near what's now known as the Karthspire. We'll have to see what we find when we arrive.
Skyrim.esm|0A8C16: Very good. I'll trust you to get there in one piece. Don't be long.
Skyrim.esm|0A8C57: Your call. Might be safer to travel separately - attract less attention that way. Don't worry, I'll get Esbern there in one piece. We'll wait for you near Karthspire. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D72: Oh yeah? I think so too. You're pretty smart. Hey, here. Have one on the house.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D76: Good. Now shut up and get out of my face.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D77: They call me Dirge, 'cause I'm the last thing you hear before they put you in the ground. Why? You think it's funny or something?
Skyrim.esm|0A8D78: What, you still think it's funny?
Skyrim.esm|0A8D79: I already told you. They call me Dirge, 'cause I'm the last thing you hear before they put you in the ground.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D7A: The only thing I heard is a rumor that a boy named Aventus Aretino, in Windhelm, has been attempting to contact the Dark Brotherhood. He used to live here in Riften at the Orphanage on the other side of town but I think he ran away. Can't say I blame him.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D7C: Maul? Yeah. That jerk works for Maven Black-Briar now. Left me down here watching this garbage heap. I guess he's better at all that talking stuff anyway. I'd just mess everything up.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D83: No, no. Sorry if I sounded so mean there. My brother always said I have a big mouth. Here, have one on me.
Skyrim.esm|0A8D87: Yeah, my problem is I'm too tired to punch your face in.
Skyrim.esm|0A91A2: Old Nord sacrifice place. Be careful around here. Let's look for a way to open the bars.
Skyrim.esm|0A91A3: Ah, the ceremonial juncture. The old Nords used to do sacrifices here. Look around for a way to open those bars.
Skyrim.esm|0A92B3: Oh, I'm sorry, did I imply that you'd be meeting for purposes other than court business? You must forgive me.
Skyrim.esm|0A92B5: Oh, very well. I can see there's no changing your stubborn Nord minds. But when this war is won, I will have my parade!
Skyrim.esm|0A92B6: You know that I support the Empire, as we all do. However, I fear General Tullius is underestimating the Stormcloaks. Too often the general has lost good soldiers because he did not take Ulfric and his men seriously. If this continues, and the worst comes to pass, Solitude will pay the price. The Empire is headquartered here, after all.
Skyrim.esm|0A92D1: I must protest, my Jarl. A parade would make you an easy target for a Stormcloak assassin. You're safer here in the palace.
Skyrim.esm|0A92D2: Speak your mind, Bryling. You're among friends here.
Skyrim.esm|0A92D3: A wise course of action, my Jarl. However, we have little food or water to spare.
Skyrim.esm|0A92DC: What a... fine idea, my Jarl. However, I would suggest we wait until the war has ended. After all, the General needs his soldiers out in the field, fighting battles against the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|0A9301: There's something that's been troubling me, Falk. I am hesitant to share it, but I feel that I must.
Skyrim.esm|0A9305: Steward Falk, I have a task for you. I want to begin stockpiling food and water in the event we come under siege, and I would like you to oversee this work personally.
Skyrim.esm|0A9329: It comforts me to know that, Bolgeir.
Skyrim.esm|0A9332: Is it that time again already? Very well, let's meet this evening and make the arrangements.
Skyrim.esm|0A9364: Make your way to our camp in the Rift. We have a few surprises for the Stormcloaks lined up.
Skyrim.esm|0A9365: Get over to our camp in the Reach. They need every able body they can muster.
Skyrim.esm|0A9375: Steward, I have an idea that might help to bolster the morale of our people in this difficult time. I think we shall have a grand parade, and let General Tullius march his troops from the Blue Palace to Castle Dour in all their finery.
Skyrim.esm|0A9377: No disrespect toward the general, but sometimes I wonder if he's got your best interests at heart. In any case he won't be turnin' me into no Imperial soldier. I know my place, and it's at your side.
Skyrim.esm|0A9378: I will make the arrangements, my Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0A9379: The Jarl would be wise to remember that it is her thanes - and my profits - that are paying for this war.
Skyrim.esm|0A937B: I'm aware of that, Bolgeir, and I appreciate your dedication. However, if we lose the war, the guardsmen of Solitude will make little difference in the end.
Skyrim.esm|0A937C: It's nearly time to collect taxes on your properties here in the city, Bryling. We'll need to visit each home for an assessment.
Skyrim.esm|0A937D: There's no need for an apology, and you're right about the war. Perhaps this is a matter best discussed with with the general in person.
Skyrim.esm|0A937E: We know full well about your investment in shipping ventures, Erikur. I've also heard troubling rumors about dealings between you and the crew of that pirate ship, the Red Wave. Thankfully, Jarl Elisif cares far less about your lost profits than she does about winning the war!
Skyrim.esm|0A9380: Told you that, did he? I'm sworn to protect you, my Jarl, but I'm the last line of defense. The first line is the walls around this city and the men who patrol them. Men who, I might add, we're precious short on.
Skyrim.esm|0A9381: Have faith, Bryling. When this war is over and Ulfric is dead, you'll see that you were jumping at shadows, and nothing more.
Skyrim.esm|0A9382: Then let's increase the taxes on the farms and merchants. We can't afford to lose the loyalty of the ship captains.
Skyrim.esm|0A9383: Stop it, both of you. You're acting like children.
Skyrim.esm|0A9384: Yes, I know. The general acted with my blessing. It is vital that we bring Ulfric to justice, but that cannot happen until his army of Stormcloaks is defeated utterly.
Skyrim.esm|0A9385: Yes, of course, but Ulfric's message is a powerful one. More of our people flock to his cause every day.
Skyrim.esm|0A9386: My apologies, Jarl Elisif. I was thinking only of the importance of the war effort.
Skyrim.esm|0A9387: General Tullius needs more money for the war effort. We need the funds to arm and provision more troops.
Skyrim.esm|0A9388: I should very much like to see you try it, my dear steward.
Skyrim.esm|0A9389: We lost another five guardsmen, my Jarl. If General Tullius continues to conscript our men and send them off to battle, we'll have none left to protect the city.
Skyrim.esm|0A938A: General Tullius does not run this city, Thane Erikur. Or have you forgotten your oaths of fealty?
Skyrim.esm|0A938B: I've heard the Empire plans to levy a tax on our trading ships. Falk, you've got to do something about this.
Skyrim.esm|0A938C: Hold your tongue, Wizard, or you're likely to lose it.
Skyrim.esm|0A938E: We don't have the luxury of hanging back to see who wins before choosing our friends, Bryling. You know this. And besides, no Nord with a shred of honor would consider it. We're no cowards.
Skyrim.esm|0A938F: General Tullius will never allow that. He's ordered all surplus food to be given over for the war effort.
Skyrim.esm|0A940C: Hmph. Some of you would be better off keeping quiet than flapping your gums on matters you don't know anything about.
Skyrim.esm|0A940D: Come on, Irileth, tell us, do you believe in this Dragonborn business?
Skyrim.esm|0A940E: What do you say, Irileth? You're being awfully quiet.
Skyrim.esm|0A9415: If you really are Dragonborn, like out of the old tales, you ought to be able to Shout. Can you? Have you tried?
Skyrim.esm|0A9418: They say the Dragonborn can Shout without any training. Like Talos himself...
Skyrim.esm|0A9419: Those born with the Dragon Blood in 'em. Like old Tiber Septim himself.
Skyrim.esm|0A941A: Here's a dead dragon, and that's something I definitely understand. Now we know we can kill them.
Skyrim.esm|0A941B: That was Shouting, what you just did! Must be. You really are Dragonborn, then...
Skyrim.esm|0A941C: Get under cover! The dragon - it's still around somewhere!
Skyrim.esm|0A941D: Hold on. I want to hear what Irileth's saying.
Skyrim.esm|0A957B: Yes, yes. I haven't got all day...
Skyrim.esm|0A9589: Sovngarde is a myth, you s'wit! And now you can join your ancestors in service to me!
Skyrim.esm|0A958A: Agna once told me there is a secret room deeper in where they buried disgraced members of the family. Maybe that will get us into the main chamber...
Skyrim.esm|0A958B: He's barred the door. Gods only know how he's... defiling the bodies of my ancestors in there!
Skyrim.esm|0A958C: This looks like the area Agna told me about. She said that the bear would show the way...
Skyrim.esm|0A958D: You found it! The passage goes through the sarcophagus... I don't like this... but we'd better keep going.
Skyrim.esm|0A958F: The dead should be made to serve the living, not the other way around!
Skyrim.esm|0A9590: We're not killing them, they were already dead. We're helping them back to Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|0A9591: I'll return my ancestors to Sovngarde, and you with them!
Skyrim.esm|0A9592: Vals Veran will die for what he's done.
Skyrim.esm|0A9593: That dark elf is about to be a dead elf.
Skyrim.esm|0A9594: No! Aunt Agna! Talos, why didn't I go in with her?
Skyrim.esm|0A95AD: I'm guessing you also don't remember coming in here and blathering incoherently about marriage or a goat. Which means you don't remember losing your temper and throwing trash all over the temple.
Skyrim.esm|0A95B4: Wow. You've really done it. The staff is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0A95B7: One down, my friend. One down.
Skyrim.esm|0A95B9: So says you. I think I've hit my limit on these things. Tell you what, one more and you win the contest.
Skyrim.esm|0A95BA: You know, you're a fun person to drink with. I know this great little place where the wine flows like water. We should head there. Hey, you don't look so good...
Skyrim.esm|0A95BB: Well come back when you do, my friend. Come back when you do.
Skyrim.esm|0A95BC: Ha! We'll see about that. This is a special brew, very strong stuff. Let's get started.
Skyrim.esm|0A95C5: You look like someone who can hold their liquor. How about a friendly contest to win a staff?
Skyrim.esm|0A95CB: I see. So you don't remember fondling the statuary, then?
Skyrim.esm|0A95D5: Yes, your head hurts and you don't remember where you are.
Skyrim.esm|0A95E1: You should tell her that her uncle is scum that betrayed his High King. Best she know now, Addvar.
Skyrim.esm|0A95F2: Svari, you need to go home. Go home and stay there until your mother comes.
Skyrim.esm|0A95F9: They can't hurt uncle Roggvir. Tell them he didn't do it.
Skyrim.esm|0A95FB: I've been waiting for Roggvir's execution. Talk to me later.
Skyrim.esm|0A95FD: Very well, Roggvir. Bow your head.
Skyrim.esm|0A9600: I have no words. Talk to Anwen.
Skyrim.esm|0A9603: Pick up your mess. Then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0A9604: I'm not going to talk to you until this mess is cleaned up.
Skyrim.esm|0A960C: I'll start round one. Down the hatch!
Skyrim.esm|0A9634: Got a pretty full stock of potions and alchemy reagents.
Skyrim.esm|0A9635: Ah, so you're an alchemist, then?
Skyrim.esm|0A9636: Hmm... Blades, helmets. Pretty much anything to suit your needs.
Skyrim.esm|0A9637: Looking to protect yourself, or deal some damage?
Skyrim.esm|0A9638: An archer, eh? Oh, I've got just what you need.
Skyrim.esm|0A9639: What are you hunting? Never mind. I don't want to know.
Skyrim.esm|0A963B: Let see what adornments I might have available...
Skyrim.esm|0A963D: Trinkets, odds and ends, that sort of thing.
Skyrim.esm|0A963E: Some may call this junk. Me, I call them treasures.
Skyrim.esm|0A963F: Everything I've got on display, really.
Skyrim.esm|0A9641: So, you wish to master the arcane arts...
Skyrim.esm|0A9642: Hmph. I had you figured for a mage. I think you'll appreciate this...
Skyrim.esm|0A9643: Breeches, gowns... clothes for any occasion, really.
Skyrim.esm|0A9687: Let's see if we can't find an outfit that suits you.
Skyrim.esm|0A9B35: Is there... something I can do for you?
Skyrim.esm|0A9B36: What can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|0A9B38: I don't owe you money, do I?
Skyrim.esm|0A9B3A: And what might you need? Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0A9B42: Come, come. I haven't got all day.
Skyrim.esm|0A9B47: I don't think you belong here.
Skyrim.esm|0A9B48: Leave now or suffer dire consequences.
Skyrim.esm|0A9B49: Get out of here before I call the guards!
Skyrim.esm|0A9B4B: I think it's time for you to get going.
Skyrim.esm|0A9B5B: That's a dangerous game you're playing.
Skyrim.esm|0A9B61: You're not going to shoot, right?
Skyrim.esm|0A9B6C: Shouldn't leave things around to trip over...
Skyrim.esm|0A9B6D: Oh my word, this is terrible.
Skyrim.esm|0A9EFF: Thank you for your help with Vals. Please, take this and leave me to see to Aunt Agna and my other family members.
Skyrim.esm|0A9F36: What? Who is this, Brother? Another of the smooth skins looking for food? But this one wasn't trapped with us...
Skyrim.esm|0AA0A6: You are trying my patience, citizen. Back away. Now.
Skyrim.esm|0AA0AF: Be careful, Mjoll. The Thieves Guild has Maven Black-Briar at her back. One snap of her fingers, and you could end up in Riften Jail... or worse.
Skyrim.esm|0AA0B0: I know. I just don't want you to leave; you're the only good thing that's happened to this city in a long time.
Skyrim.esm|0AA0B1: I had another run-in with the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0AA0B2: They represent the reason I'm here. I can't just ignore them, Aerin.
Skyrim.esm|0AA73A: You just going to stand there and take it? Fight back.
Skyrim.esm|0AA743: Who taught you to fight? Keep those hands up!
Skyrim.esm|0AA752: Don't let her get away with this!
Skyrim.esm|0AA758: Come on, she's not so tough.
Skyrim.esm|0AA77C: Yes, and I'm more than happy to give it to you. I mean look at this place. I've never seen so much wealth down here. You've made us all rich. Here you go... should fit you like a glove... boss.
Skyrim.esm|0AA78C: Well, that's it. Sorry if it isn't the ceremony you were hoping for, but we're not exactly known for throwing our coin around. After we're done here, head over to Tonilia and she'll set you up with your Guild Master Armor. Oh, and one last thing. Here, I want you to take this. It's sort of a tradition around here.
Skyrim.esm|0AA799: This is Helgen. I used to be sweet on a girl from here. Wonder if Vilod is still making that mead with juniper berries mixed in.
Skyrim.esm|0AA84C: What? Who are you? Oh, never mind. Cut me down before that thing gets us!
Skyrim.esm|0AA94B: Let's wait until Brynjolf's here before we begin.
Skyrim.esm|0AA9A8: I've heard Jaree-Ra is hanging around the Wells District, looking for new blood to hire.
Skyrim.esm|0AAA14: As I said, it's easy to find things nobody will miss. My sister Deeja and I are treasure hunters. You could be too.
Skyrim.esm|0AAA17: What? You want it for that hussy Esmerelda, with the dark feathers - don't you? I won't let her have you!
Skyrim.esm|0AAA18: Darling! I've been waiting for you to return, to consummate our love!
Skyrim.esm|0AAAC9: Haven't you talked to Astrid? She's by the pool, greeting the Night Mother and her little jester. She has a contract for you. Come see me afterward.
Skyrim.esm|0AAECC: There's no way out. There's no way out....
Skyrim.esm|0AB1F8: We left to help with the war and these filth bags swooped in and seized the place.  My family is in there.
Skyrim.esm|0AB3D3: What is the meaning of this?
Skyrim.esm|0AB3D5: What in the name of the gods are you doing?
Skyrim.esm|0AB3D8: Fool! What do you think you're doing?
Skyrim.esm|0AB3D9: Look... You shouldn't be doing that...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3DA: This is going to end horribly, I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|0AB3DB: Gods help us, this can't end well.
Skyrim.esm|0AB3DD: Oh, someone's going to get hurt, I just know it...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3DE: Damned wizards and their damned magic...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3DF: Oh goodness, someone's going to get killed.
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E1: Whoa, now... ease off on the magic...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E2: Oh, now, that can't be good...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E3: Someone stop this fool sorcerer before someone gets killed.
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E4: Ysmir's beard - I don't like the look of this...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E5: You shouldn't play around with magic like that...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E6: Mind your tricks, wizard. No need for someone to get hurt...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E7: I don't know if that's such a good idea...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3E8: Oh, I do not approve at all.
Skyrim.esm|0AB3EA: Crazy n'wah - you're trying to get someone killed?
Skyrim.esm|0AB3EB: Oh, great. I'm gonna get killed by a crazy wizard...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3EC: The world's gone mad with sorcerers now, is that it?
Skyrim.esm|0AB3EE: I think it's time to leave...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3EF: By Ysmir, this will not end well...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3F0: You're going to get us all killed, you damn fool!
Skyrim.esm|0AB3F3: Oh, this is not going to end well...
Skyrim.esm|0AB3F4: You there! What are you doing?
Skyrim.esm|0AB6EF: No. I don't have any idea. I was just shopping for a pendant for my sister back in the Imperial City.
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F0: He... he tried to kill Margret. Right in front of me.
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F1: By the Divines, the Forsworn are here in the city!
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F2: By the gods, that man nearly killed me. You saved my life. Thank you. Here, I was going to bring this to my sister, but I think you should have it.
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F3: No. She's such a nice woman. Comes by every day looking for jewelry for her family in Cyrodiil. Is it because she's a noble? Why would anyone want to kill her? It doesn't make any sense.
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F4: I've heard of them. Something about the men who live in the hills and attack caravans. I'm sorry. I don't know anymore. I'm just visiting here from Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F5: Oh, this pendant would look lovely on my sister.
Skyrim.esm|0AB6F6: Everyone stay back. The Markarth city guard have this all under control. There are no Forsworn here.
Skyrim.esm|0AB817: Of course I am. What do I look like, a tavern wench? Ah, don't let my words cut you too deep. I've just been working the forge too hard. Trying to set this sword right.
Skyrim.esm|0AB81A: I hope Arkay grants my wife some comfort. Thank you. Here. I always pay my debts.
Skyrim.esm|0AB81C: Look at that edge. Still sharp. I'll present this to the Jarl later. As for you, let me show you some smithing techniques.
Skyrim.esm|0AB81D: Whenever a group of marauders attack a Nord village, Ulfric is the first to sound the horn and send the men. But a group of Dark Elf refugees gets ambushed? A group of Argonians, or a Khajiit caravan? No troops. No investigation. Nothing. There's a group of cutthroats out there right now that Ulfric doesn't lift a finger to bring to justice, as long as they don't threaten Nord land.
Skyrim.esm|0AB822: That's a brave offer. I'd be happy to throw in my coin behind that. You deal with them, I'll make sure you get paid. Maybe this old soldier will throw in some lessons as well.
Skyrim.esm|0AB83B: Jarl wants it to look like an ancient blade. High Queen Freydis ruled over Windhelm in the Second Era, and her sword was legendary. Getting the real sword would be even better than making a copy, but no one wants to risk getting it from its resting place. If you find that sword, traveler, I'd be grateful.
Skyrim.esm|0AB840: I'd appreciate if you don't bother my wife, Tova. She's still in mourning.
Skyrim.esm|0AB859: Now that's good work. Everyone will be a lot safer with them gone, Dark Elf and Nord alike. Here, some gold as promised, and let me show you a bit about wearing heavy armor. Save you some aches on a long march.
Skyrim.esm|0AB85A: Our little girl died recently. I've been shouldering my days with strong mead, but nothing gives my Tova peace. I've been looking for an Amulet of Arkay to remind my wife that our child is with the gods now, but I can't find one.
Skyrim.esm|0AB983: Wow! Look at all the colors!
Skyrim.esm|0AB990: Keep that magic away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0AB991: Well hey, that's not something you see every day.
Skyrim.esm|0ABA9E: No funerals, just some pain. If you get caught, it's not my problem.
Skyrim.esm|0ABACA: No killing. I'm not hiring the Dark Brotherhood, here. If you get caught, it's not my problem.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAD5: Just cause some pain. I don't want a death on my conscience. If you get caught, it's not my problem.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAD6: Hurt him. I mean her. Whichever. No killing though. And I ain't paying your fine if you get caught.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAD9: I hope I can hear the screams. But don't let on it's from me! And I'm not paying your fine if you get caught.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAE0: Make it quick, but make it hurt. If you get caught by the guards, I don't know you.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAE2: Good. No killing, though. Don't get arrested. I won't help you.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAE3: Make it hurt. But not too much. No killing. And don't let the guards catch you.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAE4: It should hurt. A lot. But I'm not paying for murder. Oh, if you're caught, it's not my problem.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAE5: Good. Now remember - pain, and lots of it. But not to the death. If they arrest you, it's not my problem.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAE7: Now what am I supposed to do? I can't be seen in public like this. If I can even be seen at all...
Skyrim.esm|0ABAF2: Not bad. Now turn me back.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAF4: What did ya do that for?
Skyrim.esm|0ABAF5: Bah! Save your magic. A warrior should face her foe, not hide from him.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAF7: Ooohh... The things I could do now...
Skyrim.esm|0ABAF9: Magic! I'm not sure I approve.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAFB: You're a tricky one. Now fix it.
Skyrim.esm|0ABAFD: Hey! I can't see myself! What did you do to me?
Skyrim.esm|0ABAFF: Wizard tricks! An Orc faces the enemy. He doesn't hide from him.
Skyrim.esm|0ABB00: Stop that! How can I do my job if I'm invisible?
Skyrim.esm|0ABB01: Clever. To think, now I could... Well, never mind.
Skyrim.esm|0ABB02: Ahhh... no one will know I'm here now.
Skyrim.esm|0ABB03: What did you do to me? What am I made of? Air? Shadow? Oh, this is not good...
Skyrim.esm|0ABB06: What did you do! I'm...I'm see through! This better not be permanent. You can't just go around making women invisible like this!
Skyrim.esm|0ABB08: What have you done to me?
Skyrim.esm|0ABB09: Hey! Where did I go? I can't see myself!
Skyrim.esm|0ABB0C: Ah! I am now like the Shadowscales of old.
Skyrim.esm|0ABB4A: Hey, Longhammer, set us up with a round of... what in Oblivion happened in here?
Skyrim.esm|0ABB4B: Take the money, just let us go...
Skyrim.esm|0ABB4C: Shut up or they'll kill us, you idiot! Once they're gone I'll pick the lock...
Skyrim.esm|0ABB4D: Come on... there! I got it, let's go!
Skyrim.esm|0ABB4E: No! You'll never take me alive!
Skyrim.esm|0ABB77: Hmm. I could be a great hunter like this.
Skyrim.esm|0ABB90: Hey! What did you do to me? My body is gone!
Skyrim.esm|0ABBB5: What? Do I have mead on my chin?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC35: Are you a member of the College, or did you just steal those robes from a real wizard?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC54: You must be one of those wizards. From the College in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC7D: Wow! Look at that robe. Are you a wizard?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC7E: Those are College of Winterhold robes, aren't they?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC80: Fancy robes. You a wizard or somethin'?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC82: I like your robes. Are you a sorcerer? From the College?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC83: I heard you attacked someone in town recently. Thought you might be open to doing it again... for a tidy sum.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC8B: Heard you hit somebody in town not too long ago. I'll pay to do the same, to a certain special someone.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC8C: So you like to hurt people, huh? The town is still talking about what you did. You know, I'll pay you to attack one of my enemies...
Skyrim.esm|0ABC8D: Yeah. You beat people up. Got someone I want you to hurt. Don't worry, I can pay.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC8E: Uh... yeah. I know you don't mind hurting people. Just look what you did to... well, you know. I need someone to do that to someone. I'll even pay.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC8F: I heard you hurt someone in town not too long ago. I'll pay you to hurt someone else. Okay?
Skyrim.esm|0ABC90: I heard about what you did. You hurt someone. Bad. I got someone who deserves a beating, but I need someone else to do it for me. I can pay.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC91: Yeah. Uh, I know you hurt someone in town recently. I'd like to pay you to hurt somebody else. For me.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC94: Idiot! That wasn't the deal. You were just supposed to teach them a lesson. I am not paying you...
Skyrim.esm|0ABC95: They're dead? But now everyone will think I did it!
Skyrim.esm|0ABC96: What have you done? You weren't supposed to murder anyone! Gods...
Skyrim.esm|0ABC97: Good. Wait. Dead? That's not what I asked for. I... uh... you should leave. I need a drink...
Skyrim.esm|0ABC98: Dead? No, no, no, no, no! This is terrible. I don't know you. Get away from me!
Skyrim.esm|0ABC99: Shor's bones! What did you do that for? I would have hired the Dark Brotherhood if I'd wanted that! Get away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC9A: Really? What did you go and do that for? You... you need to go away.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC9B: No! Tell me you're joking. Sweet Mara, what have I done? Go away. Just go away.
Skyrim.esm|0ABC9C: Oh gods, what did you do that for? This is horrible. I'm not paying for murder. Get away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCAF: What have you done? They'll think I did it. Wait, I did do it! I'm not paying you! This is all your fault!
Skyrim.esm|0ABCB0: Ha! There's nothing like a few cuts and bruises to drive home a point, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0ABCB1: Good. Maybe things will be different around here from now on.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCB2: Good. Let's hope it's a lesson learned.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCB3: You what? Oh. That. Good. I hope there was a lot of blood.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCB4: It can't be traced back to me, can it? Was there lot's of crying? I hope so.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCB7: Really? Good. Was there a lot of screaming? Never mind. I don't want to know.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCC5: Were there tears? I hope everyone can see the bruises.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCD8: Good. I hope it hurt a lot, after all the pain I've been put through.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCFC: I suppose you think you're better than me because you can do that?
Skyrim.esm|0ABCFD: I hope you're not expecting a thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0ABCFE: A healing spell? Was that really necessary?
Skyrim.esm|0ABD00: My old bones don't hurt so much now.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD01: Healing magic? An Orc lets her wounds heal naturally...
Skyrim.esm|0ABD05: No need to do that now.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD0B: You heal away my scars and there's gonna be a problem...
Skyrim.esm|0ABD0C: That put a spring in my step.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD0D: Hey! You trying to pull a fast one on me?
Skyrim.esm|0ABD0F: I wasn't hurt, but that did make me feel better.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD10: Well, I only know what I've heard in the old tales. Shouting is an ancient form of magic - the use of one's voice to project their power. If you can Shout, you need to go see the Greybeards. They'll know what to do.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD11: A healing spell? Are you a priest?
Skyrim.esm|0ABD19: Don't go casting spells on a woman without asking her permission.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD1F: Hmm. Feel a bit better now. Thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD20: Was that some sort of... healing spell?
Skyrim.esm|0ABD22: Thanks! Not sure it was necessary, though.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD23: Well, in the old tales, the Dragonborn heroes would use the power of their Voice to defeat the enemies of Skyrim. Wulfharth was Dragonborn. Talos, too - the founder of the Empire, back in the good old days. In the very oldest tales, back from when there still were dragons in Skyrim, the Dragonborn would slay dragons and steal their power.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD24: Healing spells always leave me light-headed...
Skyrim.esm|0ABD25: They're the Masters of the Way of the Voice - of Shouting. They live up on top of the Throat of the World. If you're really Dragonborn, they'll want to talk to you. In the old stories, they always summon the Dragonborn for training.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD81: Ah! What did you do? Stop that!
Skyrim.esm|0ABD8A: Excellent. I think you'll be glad that you made this decision.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD8B: If you change your mind, you know where to find me.
Skyrim.esm|0ABD8C: Thank you. I'll be sure to put it to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0ABDAA: Wow! What was that? Do it again!
Skyrim.esm|0ABDAD: Ah! I feel like I just woke from a nap.
Skyrim.esm|0ABDF7: I hear you've been busy of late.
Skyrim.esm|0AC03F: The only way into Windhelm is across this bridge. Keep moving, stay close, and we'll be through their gates in no time.
Skyrim.esm|0AC040: We now fight our way to Castle Dour to cut the head off the legion itself! And in that moment, the gods will look down and see Skyrim as she was meant to be. Full of Nords who are mighty, powerful, and free!
Skyrim.esm|0AC042: The walls of Markarth may be high, but their age will have created weakness or secret entrances. Find them!
Skyrim.esm|0AC043: They've barricades to block us, but we'll tear through them and the Imperials behind them!
Skyrim.esm|0AC048: Beyond that are two exterior gates. Find a way to open them and we can take this city!
Skyrim.esm|0AC04C: Our objective is the drawbridge. If we can find a way to drop it, the city will be ours!
Skyrim.esm|0AC11B: See that ruin up there? Bleak Falls Barrow. When I was a boy, that place always used to give me nightmares.
Skyrim.esm|0AC11C: You don't seem to realize there's a war on. What you are is a damn Imperial spy!
Skyrim.esm|0AC11D: Remember, this isn't Stormcloak territory. If we're ahead of the news from Helgen we should be fine as long as we don't do anything stupid. If we run into any Imperials, just let me do the talking, all right?
Skyrim.esm|0AC11E: I'm glad you decided to come with me. We're almost to Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|0AC11F: You sure you won't change your mind? Riverwood isn't far. I'm sure my sister will be glad to help you out.
Skyrim.esm|0AC120: What do you think you're doing, walking around dressed like a damn Stormcloak traitor?
Skyrim.esm|0AC121: You've got a lot of nerve, walking around here dressed like an Imperial Legionnaire.
Skyrim.esm|0AC123: Is that right? Here's how the Legion deals with rebel scum like you.
Skyrim.esm|0AC124: I'm glad you decided to come with me. We're almost to Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|0AC125: You sure you won't change your mind? Riverwood isn't far. I'm sure my uncle will be glad to help you out.
Skyrim.esm|0AC126: See that ruin up there? Bleak Falls Barrow. I never understood how my sister could stand living in the shadow of that place.
Skyrim.esm|0AC127: Well. I'll let it slide this time. But you'd be smart to take that off. Someone might get the wrong idea.
Skyrim.esm|0AC128: Hmm. I still don't like it, but I guess I'll overlook it. This time.
Skyrim.esm|0AC129: Listen, as far as I'm concerned you've already earned your pardon. But until we get that confirmed by General Tullius, just stay clear of other Imperial soldiers and avoid any complications, all right?
Skyrim.esm|0AC94C: Oh? Well, I won't turn down your money.
Skyrim.esm|0AC94D: By helping me, you're helping the good folk of Whiterun. How could I say no to that?
Skyrim.esm|0AC94F: I'll take your money, but I make no promises of a large return on your investment. These are troubled times, after all.
Skyrim.esm|0AC955: Splendid! With the extra coin, I'll be able to purchase twice the volume in supplies that I normally do.
Skyrim.esm|0AC95A: Ah, the sound of clinking coins. Even better, the sound of clinking coins in my hand!
Skyrim.esm|0AC989: Yes. Your reward is waiting for you, mortal. Further down.
Skyrim.esm|0AC98B: Rusted. Dry. There was a time when this mace dripped with the blood of the feeble and the worthless. But a Daedric Lord has his enemies, and my rival Boethiah had her priest desecrate it. Left it here to decay. Until you came.
Skyrim.esm|0AC991: Yes. It's an altar. Men would come and sacrifice the wretched in my name. The weak would be punished by the strong. But a Daedric Lord has his enemies, and my rival Boethiah had her priest desecrate the altar. Until you came.
Skyrim.esm|0AC992: Know your place, mortal. Or Molag Bal may just send another champion to claim his mace from your broken corpse. Now, I have a soul in Oblivion to claim. Take care of the house while I'm gone. Ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0AC994: Rescue? No one knew where I was, when I was taken. Who sent you?
Skyrim.esm|0AC995: Now, I have a soul in Oblivion that needs claiming. Take care of the house while I'm gone. Ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0AC998: The Mace of Molag Bal! I give you its true power, mortal. When your enemies lie broken and bloody before you, know that I will be watching.
Skyrim.esm|0AC99C: Got a witchhunter in town, from the Priesthood of Stendarr. Keeps asking questions about that old abandoned house.
Skyrim.esm|0AC99D: A witchhunter from the Priesthood of Stendarr is in town. He's asking a lot of questions about that old abandoned house.
Skyrim.esm|0AC9A3: You there. Are you here to kill me? Slay the mighty Logrolf while he sits tied and helpless?
Skyrim.esm|0AC9A5: Fool! Did you think Molag Bal, the Lord of Domination, would so easily reward you? What do you see from that little cage? Speak.
Skyrim.esm|0ACCD3: Is someone there? Stay back! I don't want to attack you.
Skyrim.esm|0ACCD5: Stay back or I'll be forced to attack you!
Skyrim.esm|0AD034: Hmm, Esbern's right. Look like pressure plates.
Skyrim.esm|0AD035: And of course the symbol for "Dragonborn." That's the one that appears to have a sort of arrow shape pointing downward at the bottom.
Skyrim.esm|0AD036: We should be careful here. See these symbols on the floor?
Skyrim.esm|0AD037: Let's see... you have the symbol for "King"... and "Warrior"...
Skyrim.esm|0AD03C: These pillars must have something to do with it.
Skyrim.esm|0AD03D: Yes, yes! I think we must be close to the entrance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD03E: We've got to get this bridge down.
Skyrim.esm|0AD040: Whatever you did, it worked. Let's see what else those old Blades left in our way.
Skyrim.esm|0AD041: Yes. Definitely early Akaviri stonework here.
Skyrim.esm|0AD042: Given that Sky Haven Temple was sealed against the return of the Dragonborn, I would try turning all the pillars to that symbol.
Skyrim.esm|0AD044: I would suggest staying on the plates with the Dragonborn symbol.
Skyrim.esm|0AD046: There should be a way to turn this off.
Skyrim.esm|0AD048: Yes, that's it. The symbol on the pillar on the left.
Skyrim.esm|0AD049: Yes, that's it. The symbol on the middle pillar.
Skyrim.esm|0AD04A: Yes, that's it. The symbol on the pillar on the right.
Skyrim.esm|0AD07F: If you find that sword, please bring it to me.
Skyrim.esm|0AD080: Come back as soon as you have the helm.
Skyrim.esm|0AD081: Sneak that ring back to Viola before we both get caught.
Skyrim.esm|0AD082: Have you taken care of that... little problem?
Skyrim.esm|0AD083: Retrieve that shield at all costs.
Skyrim.esm|0AD084: Don't let Viola catch you in her house.
Skyrim.esm|0AD085: Execute every last bandit. Spare no one.
Skyrim.esm|0AD086: Good luck with the giant. Don't get stepped on.
Skyrim.esm|0AD087: I want no mercy for those Forsworn. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|0AD088: Vampires are clever hunters. More clever than you, definitely.
Skyrim.esm|0AD089: Remember. I need that bandit dead. Not alive.
Skyrim.esm|0AD08A: Bandits are cowards. Don't strain yourself chasing them all down.
Skyrim.esm|0AD08B: Show those marauders what Windhelm justice tastes like.
Skyrim.esm|0AD08C: If you find that ring, bring it here.
Skyrim.esm|0AD08D: That journal means a lot to me, if you find it.
Skyrim.esm|0AD08F: Hopefully, that story about Lenne's Shield is true.
Skyrim.esm|0AD09A: Don't forget. You find Queen Freydis's Sword, I'll reward you.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D1: It will keep it's hands to itself, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D2: Umm... are you in trouble? Is that why you're hiding like that?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D3: I hope you're not planning on picking my pocket.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D4: What? Why are you skulking about like that?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D5: Look at you, up to no good.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D6: You remind me of the Shadowscales, from the old histories.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D7: Are you playing hide and seek?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D8: I trust you aren't considering any... thievery.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0D9: You're not a thief trying to rob me, right?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0DA: You trying to hide? 'Cause I can see you plain as day.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0DB: You're a bad sneak. You're not creeping up on anybody like that.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0DD: You don't look so good. Are you feeling all right?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0DE: You should probably be in bed. You're looking a bit under the weather.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0DF: How do you feel? I think you might be sick.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E0: You look sick. I hope it's not contagious.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E1: Are you feeling all right, dear? You look a little ill.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E2: Don't take this the wrong way, but you look a little sickly.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E3: You're looking a little rough around the edges. You feeling all right?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E4: You look a bit peaked. You sure you shouldn't be home, in bed?
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E5: You look worse than I feel.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E7: You look awful. Better not get me sick.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E8: You all right? You look like you might be sick.
Skyrim.esm|0AD0E9: No offense, but you're not looking so good. Are you feeling ill?
Skyrim.esm|0AD106: I'm worried about my daughter. I'm keeping an eye on her for a little while before I pass on.
Skyrim.esm|0AD11E: I'm worried about my son. I'm keeping an eye on him for a little while before I pass on.
