Skyrim.esm|0AD12A: I know my mother would be worried about me. I'm here to let her know everything's all right.
Skyrim.esm|0AD162: I know my father would be worried about me. I'm here to let him know everything's all right.
Skyrim.esm|0AD163: I worry about my sister. This way I can keep an eye on her, make sure she's not getting into trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0AD1C3: I worry about my brother. This way I can keep an eye on him, make sure he's not getting into trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0AD1FB: I'm not quite ready to let go of my wife.
Skyrim.esm|0AD1FE: I'm not quite ready to let go of my wife.
Skyrim.esm|0AD1FF: I'm not quite ready to let go of my wife.
Skyrim.esm|0AD201: I'm not quite ready to let go of my wife.
Skyrim.esm|0AD203: I'm not quite ready to let go of my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0AD204: I'm not quite ready to let go of my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0AD207: I'm not quite ready to let go of my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0AD209: Don't come too close with that.
Skyrim.esm|0AD20B: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD213: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD217: Please! Keep that magic away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0AD218: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD219: You really should be more careful with the spells you cast.
Skyrim.esm|0AD22C: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD22D: You should be more careful with your spell casting.
Skyrim.esm|0AD232: You keep your distance with that magic
Skyrim.esm|0AD23B: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD276: Dear, that spell is making me nervous.
Skyrim.esm|0AD27D: Hurt anyone with that spell and you'll be sorry.
Skyrim.esm|0AD27F: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD296: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD2EE: Uuh. Would you mind dousing that magic?
Skyrim.esm|0AD2F0: That spell looks like it'll hurt.
Skyrim.esm|0AD2F7: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD2FF: Please stay away while you got that spell on you.
Skyrim.esm|0AD300: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD34C: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD377: Walking around like that is why magic gets a bad name. Shame on you.
Skyrim.esm|0AD37C: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD37D: This one thinks you should be more careful with your spells.
Skyrim.esm|0AD380: You're asking for trouble walking around with dangerous magic like that.
Skyrim.esm|0AD382: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD383: Do be careful with that spell, will you?
Skyrim.esm|0AD387: You better be careful with that magic, if you know what's good for you.
Skyrim.esm|0AD388: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD389: I trust you know what you're doing with that spell?
Skyrim.esm|0AD38A: That spell looks dangerous... keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0AD38B: My mother's been haunting me ever since she died. At first it was a little strange, but now I'm glad she's here.
Skyrim.esm|0AD39A: That someone would attack Jorrvaskr... it's unthinkable.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3AC: My mother's been haunting me ever since she died. At first it was a little strange, but now I'm glad she's here.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3AF: My father's been haunting me ever since he died. At first it was a little strange, but now I'm glad he's here.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3B5: My father's been haunting me ever since he died. At first it was a little strange, but now I'm glad he's here.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3B6: I'll call upon my suppliers this very evening and arrange to double my orders.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3BA: I'll start planning how best to spend this. You have my thanks!
Skyrim.esm|0AD3BF: You'll look back on this as one of your best decisions, I swear it.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3C0: If you want to make the most of your investment, you'll come here to sell your goods.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3C7: A parent should never outlive a child. Having my son's spirit here... it's as if he never died.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3C8: I've been looking to expand the business, and your investment will certainly help with that. I accept!
Skyrim.esm|0AD3CC: Such an investment will mean that I can take better care of my family, as well as my customers.
Skyrim.esm|0AD3CE: An investment, is it? Alright, I'm sure I can put the extra money to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0AD5C8: A parent should never outlive a child. Having my son's spirit here... it's as if he never died.
Skyrim.esm|0AD5FF: A parent should never outlive a child. Having my daughter's spirit here... it's as if she never died.
Skyrim.esm|0AD670: A parent should never outlive a child. Having my daughter's spirit here... it's as if she never died.
Skyrim.esm|0AD676: I was a little scared at first, but I like having my sister around. It's comforting.
Skyrim.esm|0AD6FD: I was a little scared at first, but I like having my sister around. It's comforting.
Skyrim.esm|0AD71B: I was a little scared at first, but I like having my brother around. It's comforting.
Skyrim.esm|0AD76E: I was a little scared at first, but I like having my brother around. It's comforting.
Skyrim.esm|0AD776: When my wife died, I wasn't sure I could let her go. Now I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0AD779: When my wife died, I wasn't sure I could let her go. Now I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0AD78B: When my wife died, I wasn't sure I could let her go. Now I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7A5: When my husband died, I wasn't sure I could let him go. Now I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7A7: When my husband died, I wasn't sure I could let him go. Now I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7A9: When my husband died, I wasn't sure I could let him go. Now I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7C7: You're going to rot in the Dragonsreach Dungeon.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7C8: A stretch in the Castle Dour Dungeon will straighten you right out.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7C9: Guard might get nervous, a woman approaches with her weapon drawn...
Skyrim.esm|0AD7CA: You come up to me, fists raised? You looking for a beating?
Skyrim.esm|0AD7CF: Hmph. You wear that armor well. Iron may be simple, but it'll stop enough to keep you in the fight.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7E3: Iron sword, huh? What are you killing, butterflies?
Skyrim.esm|0AD7ED: I mostly deal with petty thievery and drunken brawls. Been too long since we've had a good bandit raid.
Skyrim.esm|0AD7FE: There's no mistake. You're a wanted woman... and it's time to pay for your crimes.
Skyrim.esm|0AD808: Cause trouble in Whiterun, and I'll haul you into the Dragonsreach dungeon myself.
Skyrim.esm|0AD80A: Riften's under my protection. You watch yourself, now.
Skyrim.esm|0AD80B: Careful. Riften's not the safest place for a nighttime stroll.
Skyrim.esm|0AD80F: City's quiet enough now. But when the sun goes down, you best keep on your guard.
Skyrim.esm|0AD85E: Have you... Do you see that future? Will I be a good leader?
Skyrim.esm|0AD864: It's not true, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0AD86E: The people of Morthal would much rather weave their own horrid tales about my life than simply ask me for the truth. If they choose to fear me in their ignorance, that is their choice. But it will not change what is true.
Skyrim.esm|0AD86F: That is what the Divines have planned for you, yes.
Skyrim.esm|0AD87E: I don't know. Someone in town said it.
Skyrim.esm|0AD882: The visions continue to hold me in balance, Gorm.
Skyrim.esm|0AD887: If you stand before me to accuse me of sacrificing children, or eating the hearts of the dead, you may save your breath. I have done no such thing, nor do I intend to. I simply wish to live my life in peace.
Skyrim.esm|0AD888: I will be Jarl someday, mother?
Skyrim.esm|0AD88F: What? Where did you hear such a thing?
Skyrim.esm|0AD890: How are you feeling, Jarl Idgrod?
Skyrim.esm|0AD892: No, no. Just watch over him.
Skyrim.esm|0AD893: Is it true that you kill deer and eat their hearts?
Skyrim.esm|0AD896: What sort of harm? Have you had a vision?
Skyrim.esm|0AD898: No, Gorm. I am simply telling you what I see.
Skyrim.esm|0AD89B: I'm just here to protect the Jarl, not to chat.
Skyrim.esm|0AD89C: Good. He must be kept safe from harm.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8A5: No? Then how do you like the idea of having your legs broken? Mind your manners in this hall, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8AD: No, no. I'm fine. Is there something you need?
Skyrim.esm|0AD8AF: It's no secret that Jarl Idgrod is... different. It's also no secret that there are rumors about her and her visions. I won't repeat them, and I won't allow them to be spread under this roof. Now, you go about your business.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8B6: You are looking after your brother, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0AD8B8: Then by all means, go about your business.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8BA: Don't believe everything you hear, child.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8BC: You will not disrespect Jarl Idgrod while in this Hall. Do we have an understanding?
Skyrim.esm|0AD8BF: All things are possible, my child. But I believe it is within you to succeed.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8C4: Do not worry, my friend. All is well.
Skyrim.esm|0AD8C5: I keep to myself. I offer spells and scrolls to those who need them, and wisdom in Conjuration magic for those who wish to learn. Beyond that I seek only to be left alone to pursue my research.
Skyrim.esm|0AD9F3: Thank you. Please, do not open it. It is... private.
Skyrim.esm|0AD9F4: Yes, but... not so loud! Will you take it to Captain Aldis?
Skyrim.esm|0AD9FC: The Divines reveal things to me at times, yes. I do not hide this. It is a gift. Anyone who believes otherwise does not and cannot understand it.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA18: I can. It might save your life.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA19: I'll teach you some of what I know, but be careful.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA1B: I'll teach you some of what I know, but be careful.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA1C: You've come to the right place. I'd be happy to teach you.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA2C: Use it right, and you won't feel a thing.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA3C: I'll teach you how to use it effectively, and keep you quick on your feet.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA42: All right, but if you get arrested don't blame it on me.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA51: I'll show you what I know.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA60: I'll show you what I know.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA6C: I can teach you a few things.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA70: Up to no good, are we? I'll teach you, but it'll cost you.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA96: I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.
Skyrim.esm|0ADA99: It's an honorable craft. I'll show you what I can.
Skyrim.esm|0ADAED: I can show you a few things, but anyone asks, and my name never comes up. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|0ADB16: Well, you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0ADB1C: Well, you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0ADB1E: It's all in the swing. Let me show you.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC3D: Oh no! By the gods! This can't be happening!
Skyrim.esm|0ADC68: I don't want to talk about it.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC6B: Begone. I have no wish to speak to anyone right now.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC6E: Have the decency to leave me to my sorrows.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC71: Get out of here.... I'm in no mood to talk...
Skyrim.esm|0ADC72: Leave me alone. Can't you see I'm upset?
Skyrim.esm|0ADC73: Not now. I'm... much too upset to talk.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC75: Not now. I'm too upset to talk.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC78: Please, I can't... Just... just go.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC7E: Now is not a good time. Leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC80: Now is not the time for talk...
Skyrim.esm|0ADC81: I want a drink, not company. Now go.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC82: I'm in no mood for conversation. Now go, and leave me be.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC83: Not now, please. I'm just... too upset to talk.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC86: Just... get out of my face. I've got nothing to say.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC87: Please... I... I can't talk to you right now...
Skyrim.esm|0ADC89: Leave me alone... I'm in no mood for talking.
Skyrim.esm|0ADC8B: Look... Now's not a good time...
Skyrim.esm|0ADD10: Orgnar, this is it. The inn is yours. I'm probably never coming back here.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD11: You mean they used a Shout to defeat Alduin? You're sure?
Skyrim.esm|0ADD12: This is what I've been trying to explain. It's in Sky Haven Temple, an ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden deep in the crags of the Reach. That's where we'll find Alduin's Wall.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD13: We should be careful. There's no telling what traps and wards the ancient Blades may have set.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD31: Yes, exactly. Well, I think so. It's certainly our best hope. The ancient Blades remembered much Akaviri dragonlore that has now been forgotten.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD6E: We'll meet you there. Be careful. That part of the Reach is rough country these days.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD70: We'd best be careful in here.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD71: It's starting to look like Esbern was right.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD73: From Riverwood? The road south through Falkreath is the most direct route. Or you could catch the carriage from Whiterun to Markarth and then approach from the west. Either way, the Reach is wild country these days. The Forsworn are everywhere. Best be careful.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD74: Always. Let's go see if we can find this Sky Haven Temple.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD7D: Remember, this is where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD7E: Sky Haven Temple it is, then. I knew you'd have something for us, Esbern.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD7F: Yeah... sure. You, too, Delphine. You be safe.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD8A: Well, yes, but... there's no guarantee, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD8C: Take care of yourself, Orgnar. Goodbye.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD8D: So you think that Alduin's Wall will tell us how to defeat Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|0ADD8E: I know the area of the Reach that Esbern's talking about. Near what's now known as Karthspire, in the Karth River canyon. We can meet you there, or all travel together, your call.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD92: Alduin's Wall... one of the lost secrets of the Blades. Where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy. Its location has been lost for centuries, but I've found it again. Not lost, you see, just forgotten. The Blades archives held so many secrets... I was only able to save a few scraps...
Skyrim.esm|0ADD98: Well, now. That's something to think about.
Skyrim.esm|0ADD9B: Hmm? Oh, yes. Presumably something rather specific to dragons, or even Alduin himself.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE64: Wise move. Now get out of here ya goat licker, before I decide the toll's gone up.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE65: Should have left when you had the chance, friend. Now we're gonna have to spill your brains on the road here.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE66: Hold it. This here's a toll road, see? Yer gonna have to hand over, say, 200 gold if you want to use our road.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE67: Get on your way before I change my mind.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE69: That's a real shame. Gonna make it hard for you to pay a healer after we cave your skull in.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE6A: You know, now that I think of it, that sounds fair. Just shove off before the others notice.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE6B: Ha! Tough talk for someone who's about to have their guts spilled.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE6C: Eh, fine. I can tell you aren't worth the trouble. Just get out of here before I change my mind.
Skyrim.esm|0ADE6D: How does a knife in the gut sound? It's 200 gold or your life.
Skyrim.esm|0AE684: Splendid! I've been looking for a new investor. Now it seems my search is over.
Skyrim.esm|0AE686: Come to see the hag with an offering of coin, then? So be it. I accept your investment.
Skyrim.esm|0AE68A: Thank you so much! With this coin, I'll be able to double my stock.
Skyrim.esm|0AE68C: Ah, look at the pretty, shiny coins. Now, how best to spend them?
Skyrim.esm|0AE725: I almost envy you. That first time is always the most... intense. You gave us even more trouble than Farkas did at his first turning.
Skyrim.esm|0AE726: I forget that you've never seen it. Beneath the Skyforge, where Eorlund works. The door is hidden, but I will show you the way.
Skyrim.esm|0AE727: You were born into the pack, brother.
Skyrim.esm|0AE728: You were born into the pack, sister.
Skyrim.esm|0AE729: Are you awake? I was starting to think you might never come back.
Skyrim.esm|0AE72B: Are you prepared to join your spirit with the beast world, friend?
Skyrim.esm|0AE744: I would hope you recognize Aela, even in this form. She's agreed to be your forebear.
Skyrim.esm|0AE76A: The mine will be the talk of Skyrim soon, you'll see. It'll be the stuff of legends.
Skyrim.esm|0AE76B: It's a matter of time before the Jarl is coming to me asking for money. This mine is going to make me... I  mean us, rich.
Skyrim.esm|0AE76C: I know they laughed when I said I wanted to keep the mine open. I'll show them. I'll show them all.
Skyrim.esm|0AE76D: You're welcome to look around, of course, but understand that this place belongs to me.
Skyrim.esm|0AE76E: Thorgar says we'll find rocks soon, the kind worth gold.
Skyrim.esm|0AE76F: I'm the best at breaking up rocks. Thorgar says so.
Skyrim.esm|0AE770: It's cold in the mine, but Thorgar really needs me, so I stay.
Skyrim.esm|0AE771: Be careful where you step in the mine. Don't want to cover up some valuable ore, or gems, or... Or something...
Skyrim.esm|0AE772: Any time I get tired of working, I just think that maybe that next swing will make me rich. It keeps me going.
Skyrim.esm|0AE773: It's going to happen soon, I just know it. We'll strike something better than iron. Silver, or maybe even gold. You'll see.
Skyrim.esm|0AE774: We were supposed to be rich by now, you know. Thorgar insisted we'd be swimming in gold.
Skyrim.esm|0AE775: I'm starting to think we might not even find iron in here. We've been at it for so long.
Skyrim.esm|0AE776: Is it still worth it? When do you decide enough is enough, and just walk away?
Skyrim.esm|0AE905: Cure? Hah. You're sounding like the old man. I... shouldn't say that. I love Kodlak. I respect and follow him. But he's wrong on this. It's no curse. We're made into the greatest hunters in the land. If he's worried about some mead-swilling afterlife in Sovngarde, he's free to pursue it. I'll take the glories of the hunt right here.
Skyrim.esm|0AE906: This is no test, new blood. This is a gift. Come inside.
Skyrim.esm|0AE907: Nothing, until you choose to use it. Then, well... you got a taste. Stronger, faster. It doesn't last long, though. The blood of your foes can sustain you. If you're willing to feed.
Skyrim.esm|0AE9D6: Here's all you need to know. Jorrvaskr is the oldest building in Whiterun. The Skyforge was here long before it was. And the Underforge taps an ancient magic that is older than men or elves. We bring you here to make you stronger, new blood. Now let's move.
Skyrim.esm|0AEBF9: You have the blood of the wolf in you. You'll need to build up your strength before you can call on it again, though. Just be careful where you do it. Some cowards in this land can't stand the sight of glory before them.
Skyrim.esm|0AEBFA: Aela will be joining us shortly. Once she's inside, we can follow.
Skyrim.esm|0AEC10: Typical Imperial lies. First you take away Talos, now you're keeping us from seeing our honored dead? You and the Jarl will answer for any desecration of my ancestors' bodies.
Skyrim.esm|0AEC12: I'm not hiding anything. It's closed for a reason.
Skyrim.esm|0AECEC: The people still doubt me, don't they.
Skyrim.esm|0AECEF: They got used to Idgrod just fine, didn't they? So what's the difference?
Skyrim.esm|0AECF9: I have never doubted that, husband.
Skyrim.esm|0AECFB: Joric will be what Joric will be. Give it time.
Skyrim.esm|0AED10: It does not surprise me. They do not share in what I see, and I cannot explain it to them.
Skyrim.esm|0AED1E: Have you made a decision about Falion?
Skyrim.esm|0AED1F: Still, I fear there is some connection.
Skyrim.esm|0AED23: When are you gonna do somethin' to help Falion? Stop people sayin' things about him?
Skyrim.esm|0AED24: You have to understand, his presence here has caused... Well, it's difficult for the people to get used to.
Skyrim.esm|0AED25: I stand where I have always stood. Here in Morthal. Now is not the time to fight amongst ourselves. I fear dark days approach and all of Skyrim's strength will be needed.
Skyrim.esm|0AED26: What decision is there to be made? I allowed him to live here. I stand by that.
Skyrim.esm|0AED27: No, not at all. It's just...
Skyrim.esm|0AED2A: That's what many here wanted. A quiet life in a quiet place, without the noise or the trouble of a city. Now some worry this wizard, Falion, will foul it all up.
Skyrim.esm|0AED2B: Just guide them well, and all will work out.
Skyrim.esm|0AED2C: It's Joric. I fear his condition is worse.
Skyrim.esm|0AED2D: No, I... I was just asking.
Skyrim.esm|0AED3A: You wish to do what I do? Very well.
Skyrim.esm|0AED80: Yes, but there are concerns. Rumors that he's up to something.
Skyrim.esm|0AED8D: What? Oh, nothing. All is well.
Skyrim.esm|0AEDC9: He calls out in his sleep sometimes. I've heard Falion's name. What does it mean?
Skyrim.esm|0AEDCA: We are all up to something, my dear. All is as it should be.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF0A: No surprise they keep vermin around. Filthy squalor.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF22: Nothing we can do for these ones now. Don't even want to think about what those cretins did to them before they died.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF24: There's a dead one, isn't there. Thought so. Nobody we know, by the smell. Some can't separate the animal from themselves. Go feral. This poor sod could have been anyone. We should keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF26: Look at this. Cowards must have locked the place down after Skjor charged in. You can taste the fear.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF29: I don't think I need to tell you why.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF31: We're getting close now. Be careful. Their leader is a tricky one.
Skyrim.esm|0AEF5E: Skjor's somewhere ahead. We need to find him.
Skyrim.esm|0AF087: Been hearing some strange tales of the Jarl's children. Say the one's turning wicked, and the others have an ill-favored look to them. Best to keep clear.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0B2: I still can't believe he knew what he was doing when he opened that gate...
Skyrim.esm|0AF0C5: Roggvir... Why did it have to be like this?
Skyrim.esm|0AF0C6: They should have drawn it out some more. He deserved a slower death.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0C8: I guess it's all over. I expected... I don't know what I expected.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0C9: A man opens a gate and they cut his head off? Doesn't seem right to me.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0CA: That's definitely going in my part of the Edda.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0DF: Perhaps. But what is it you expect to find within?
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E0: I am here to assist those seeking the wisdom of the College. And if, in the process, my presence helps to deter those who might seek to do harm, so be it. The more important question is: why are you here?
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E1: It is no great secret that we have been unjustly blamed for a great many things over the years. The good people of Skyrim on occasion would rather pass judgment than attempt to understand what we do here. Thus we must take certain precautions in order to secure our safety.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E2: Oh, forgive me. Most who arrive here do so because they have heard of the College beforehand. This is the College of Winterhold, a safe haven for mages in Skyrim. A place of wisdom and arcane knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E3: Welcome to the College of Winterhold. I am Faralda, one of the senior Wizards here. I trust you found your journey to Winterhold not entirely unpleasant. Now, I must advise you that if your only purpose in being here is to complain, you would be far better off speaking with the Jarl of Winterhold. If, however, you seek something more, I will be happy to assist you.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E4: Cross the bridge at your own peril! The way is dangerous, and the gate will not open. You shall not gain entry!
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E5: Yes, the school of Illusion can be quite influential. Kings have risen to power because of it, and empires have been razed with it.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E6: Do you? It is true there are some here who have spent years studying the accumulated knowledge of the scrolls. But what you seek does not come easily, and can destroy those without a strong will.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E7: Greetings. Welcome to the College of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E8: Much knowledge lies within the College's walls.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0E9: The College is an asset to Skyrim, even if it goes unnoticed or unappreciated.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0EA: Winterhold's glory days may lie behind it, but the College lives on.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0EB: No matter what, the College will endure.
Skyrim.esm|0AF0EC: I see. That power certainly exists, I assure you. Wield it faithfully, and few can withstand you.
Skyrim.esm|0AF1B4: The eyes of Shor are upon you this day. Defeat Alduin, and destroy his soul-snare.
Skyrim.esm|0AF1B5: All hail the Dragonborn! Hail him with great praise!
Skyrim.esm|0AF1B6: All hail the Dragonborn! Hail her with great praise!
Skyrim.esm|0AF25C: Your depths will open to the mind!
Skyrim.esm|0AF25E: Open to the will of Septimus!
Skyrim.esm|0AF399: And just what do you think you're doing?
Skyrim.esm|0AF482: Hmmm... A question I am all to familiar with. One my very Thanes ask often. They worry that Tullius is so focused on the war, that he risks forgetting what's best for our people. The real irony is that, while General Tullius is certainly no Nord, the people of Skyrim would be lost without him. Trust is always difficult, especially for my people. Yet we must trust in Tullius' leadership. For what other choice do we have?
Skyrim.esm|0AF483: Because that's what traitorous cowards do when they desire power. Ulfric coveted the High King's throne. He thought he deserved it more than Torygg. And so he came before my husband and he... he shouted. With that terrible voice. Like something out of a legend... Or a nightmare. When Ulfric unleashed such fury, my husband he... he simply ceased to be. That is all I will say, for it grieves me to speak of it.
Skyrim.esm|0AF487: Oh, most certainly. At least, when Elisif herself asks. If you're inquiring whether we have concerns about her age and inexperience, well, let me put it this way... The Thanes have every confidence in General Tullius's leadership.
Skyrim.esm|0AF489: Of course he does. What sort of a question is that? Maybe you've been listening to Erikur. There's been loose talk among some of the Thanes, and I suspect he's the cause of it. You'll pay those rumors no heed if you wish to retain your welcome in this city. She may be young, but Elisif is the Jarl by right. And here in Skyrim, if nothing else, we respect the traditions of our father's fathers. You'd do well to remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0AF48B: That remains to be seen. By rights, she has a legitimate claim as High Queen of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0AF490: I've only been a part of the Red Wave's crew for a few weeks, but I've already learned to stay out of Captain Safia's way.
Skyrim.esm|0AF493: Forget wine, women and wealth - give me a calm sea and a good book, and I'm happy.
Skyrim.esm|0AF494: You've not seen the night sky until you've seen it on the open sea.
Skyrim.esm|0AF495: I hear Adonato Leotelli's staying up in Windhelm. I've read a few of his works. Be good to meet him someday.
Skyrim.esm|0AF498: My father was all the family I ever had. I felt so lost when he died. But the crew of the Red Wave's my family now.
Skyrim.esm|0AF49C: I've seen some truly wondrous sights since I took to the seas. I had no idea a man could die in so many ways...
Skyrim.esm|0AF49D: Just yesterday, I saw a musician in the street playing a flute. It was... beautiful. I want that flute.
Skyrim.esm|0AF4B5: Dorian is helping me perfect my fencing, but I don't think I'll ever be as good as he is.
Skyrim.esm|0AF4C1: I once captained a ship called the Argent Raptor. She struck an iceberg and went down, just north of Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0AF4CC: My shipmates scoff at my love of finery, but I know envy when I hear it.
Skyrim.esm|0AF4D3: From ship's captain to lowly crewman... I suppose I deserve it after wasting all my coin on gambling and women.
Skyrim.esm|0AF4DA: I hail from Cyrodiil, but after a... misunderstanding with the law, I needed a swift change of scenery.
Skyrim.esm|0AF4E1: Not yet, no. Although it is true my husband Torygg was the High King, and I am his widow... now is not the time to lay claim to such a title. This land is riven by war, and her people suffer for it. The Stormcloaks are a blight on Skyrim, and like any blight... must be purged. Only then will I take my rightful place as High Queen.
Skyrim.esm|0AF643: If you have spare weapons and armor to sell, bring them here.
Skyrim.esm|0AF645: Of course you want to invest in my business. I'm the finest blacksmith in all Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0AF64A: How kind. At my age, I've certainly learned how to spend wisely.
Skyrim.esm|0AF663: More coin means more supplies, and that means more useful balms, potions and remedies for everyone.
Skyrim.esm|0AF66B: Our ancient debt for Alduin's reprieve is now repaid - the long night is ended!
Skyrim.esm|0AF66C: I'll look for you, friend, when at last you return to Shor's hall. The honor will be ours when you rejoin the ranks of Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|0AF66D: Even here, where heroes throng, few can match this mighty deed. What glory! The gods themselves must envy us this well-earned honor!
Skyrim.esm|0AF6B4: Enough to know that it's not worth it. Ranmir's a good boy, and he doesn't deserve what fate pushed his way, but there's only so far I'll go to risk my neck.
Skyrim.esm|0AF6B5: One night Ranmir was in here like always, drinking himself to death. When I told him there was no more Honningbrew, that he'd drank it all, he got angry. He ranted at me, how I didn't know what he's been through, how Isabelle broke his heart when she ran off with some thief named Vex... He said he'd even gone to Riften to try and find the bastard. I think maybe he was hoping they'd just kill him, and that'd be the end of it. Dagur never heard any of this, and I chose to keep it to myself. I'm only telling you so you don't get into trouble. Riften and thieves means trouble. Just leave it alone.
Skyrim.esm|0AF6B6: You'll wind up getting yourself killed. Dagur shouldn't have said a word about it. I love the man, bless his heart, but he doesn't know the whole story.
Skyrim.esm|0AF6B7: Oh, no. Dagur's been talking at you, hasn't he? Gotten you roped into his little plan to fix Ranmir's life? I'll tell you what I told him -- stay out of it. It's none of your business. Those aren't folks you want to get tangled up with.
Skyrim.esm|0AF6B8: No there isn't. And I suggest you leave it at that. I don't want to see anyone get hurt over this.
Skyrim.esm|0AF759: Surprised to see you working. Mulush almost broke your arm yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|0AF75A: Does it look like I can afford losing a day's wage? That damn Orc isn't getting the best of me.
Skyrim.esm|0AF75C: Markarth lives because my family makes sure it's fed on gold and silver.
Skyrim.esm|0B0211: The Forsworn ambush our roads and dragons cloud the skies. Is there any good news?
Skyrim.esm|0B0212: I just don't know if I can keep you safe.
Skyrim.esm|0B0213: Umm.... No, my Jarl. I'm afraid there isn't.
Skyrim.esm|0B0214: Our men know how to kill Forsworn and Legionnaires, not dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0B0215: I wonder if we should finally leave Markarth. These dragon attacks, I think Skyrim is cursed.
Skyrim.esm|0B0216: I'm a busy man. Half this city works for my family, and I have to keep them in line.
Skyrim.esm|0B0217: I want soldiers sent to deal with these dragons. Every able-bodied man and woman who can carry a bow.
Skyrim.esm|0B0218: First war, and now dragons. Everywhere they go, the Nords bring nothing but ruin.
Skyrim.esm|0B0219: We will keep each other safe. No matter what.
Skyrim.esm|0B021A: What kind of talk is this? The man I married would never speak of leaving his home.
Skyrim.esm|0B021B: We can't do that. Every soldier we send to hunt dragons is one more man that can't defend the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|0B021C: More settlements have sent riders to say dragons are being spotted throughout Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0B021D: Ain't nothing for it. They're still in charge.
Skyrim.esm|0B021E: Killing dragons isn't defending the Reach?
Skyrim.esm|0B038C: You'd better. Because until you do, you don't get a single coin from us... and you won't be able to use our fences. Anything you steal, you bring it straight to me. Now get out of here and don't come back until your pockets are full.
Skyrim.esm|0B038D: Well, well. If it isn't Brynjolf's little protege. I'm going to make this short and sweet, because frankly I don't think you deserve another shot. You've broken the rules and now you need to pay... and it's going to cost you quite a bit.
Skyrim.esm|0B038E: It's all here. Well, congratulations. You're one of us again. Next time you decide to pull a stunt like that again, just remember how much it hurt to give this away.
Skyrim.esm|0B038F: Again? Didn't we teach you enough of a lesson the first time? I suppose it's tough for an old dog to learn new tricks. Anyway, you know the drill. You want to buy back in, and it'll cost you.
Skyrim.esm|0B0390: I'd say it's time you put your nose to the grindstone and figure it out for yourself. You claim to be a thief... act like one. Pick pockets, knock over houses... heck, I don't care if you have to bust into a palace. You do what you have to do to come up with the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0B0391: I hope you come up with that gold soon.
Skyrim.esm|0B0392: You have the gold yet or what?
Skyrim.esm|0B0393: I believe you owe the Guild something.
Skyrim.esm|0B0394: This is the Thieves Guild, genius. How do you think? With gold. A lot of gold. Did you think we'd accept a heartfelt apology and let bygones be bygones?
Skyrim.esm|0B0395: Speak to Vex. We have nothing else to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|0B0396: The Guild will not tolerate the kind of behavior you've been displaying. Too much is at stake. I'm taking you out of the game for now. No jobs will be offered and you won't be able to fence your goods. Speak to Vex to make the proper reparations if you want back in. Now get out of my sight.
Skyrim.esm|0B0397: Until you're done with Vex, we don't have anything to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|0B0398: You've done it now, whelp. You broke the rules and now you're knee deep in it. That means your out. No jobs, no payouts... no nothing. You want to get back into our graces, you need to talk to Vex. She'll set you straight.
Skyrim.esm|0B0399: Sorry. We got nothin' to talk about. Vex will straighten you out.
Skyrim.esm|0B039A: Are you crazy? What were you thinkin'? You can't just go around doin' things the way you please! Now we have to kick you out until things cool down. Can't give you any jobs or payouts for goods. Go talk to Vex. She'll tell you the why's and how's if you want to get back in.
Skyrim.esm|0B039B: Until you get back into the Guild's graces, we have nothing to speak about.
Skyrim.esm|0B039C: Straighten yourself out with the Guild and then maybe we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|0B039D: Don't want to know what you did and I don't care. Just keep away from me.
Skyrim.esm|0B039E: I may be new around here, but at least I know how to follow the rules.
Skyrim.esm|0B039F: And here I expected you to hop the next caravan to Morrowind. What have you got for me?
Skyrim.esm|0B03A0: I hope you brought a little something for me.
Skyrim.esm|0B03A1: First, let's count the coin, then we can get back to business.
Skyrim.esm|0B0BBF: Then, finally, realization and horror arrive together. The orange is flame, heat. The sound a roar, a challenge in their ancient tongue. But now it's too late for escape. The dragon is upon me - fire and darkness descending like a thunderbolt. And not just any dragon, but the Dragon - Alduin, the World-Eater, the dragon who devours both the living and the dead.
Skyrim.esm|0B0BC0: Then it came again, this time more distinct. Closer. Definitely not lightning now. It was orange - brilliant orange, the color of hearth and dawn. And a sound, too. Distinct and indistinct. Not thunder... something else. Something I should recognize, but in the dream I cannot place it.
Skyrim.esm|0B0BC1: Then came the flash of light - just on the horizon, within the clouds that mark the border between worlds. It could have been lightning, but there was no thunder. In the dream, the sense of foreboding grew, but I could never wake up.
Skyrim.esm|0B0BC2: I used to dream of it. In the dream, I was standing... someplace high up... a tower, or mountain. It was always just before dawn. The whole world was in darkness.
Skyrim.esm|0B0BC3: And then I would wake up. And hope that it was just a dream... but know that it was not.
Skyrim.esm|0B0BC4: I want to leave my high place, to seek shelter. From what, I don't yet know. In the manner of dreams, I cannot escape. I'm forced to wait and watch.
Skyrim.esm|0B0EED: You can't scare me into submission. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|0B0F0D: Heh, a Nord skull makes the best axe blade. 'bout all they're good for.
Skyrim.esm|0B1138: I'm sorry, could you describe the smell?
Skyrim.esm|0B1139: It was a minor miscalculation. I've already corrected it for future experiments.
Skyrim.esm|0B113B: This, this is why people have a problem with your college, Nelacar.
Skyrim.esm|0B113C: Like some horrible monster was turned inside out, and then exploded. What did you do?
Skyrim.esm|0B118C: I'm feeling better, but Bashnag's curse won't wear off for weeks.
Skyrim.esm|0B118D: I won't be myself for some time. I just need to get out of here and recover.
Skyrim.esm|0B1231: Another wanderer, here to lick my father's boots. Good job.
Skyrim.esm|0B1232: The only grownups who talk to me are the kind I don't want talking to me.
Skyrim.esm|0B1233: Have I done something to make you bother me?
Skyrim.esm|0B1234: You... you know the Whispering Lady, don't you. I can tell.
Skyrim.esm|0B1236: You're going to end up helping me. I know it.
Skyrim.esm|0B1239: Kind of nice being in on the secret, itsn't it.
Skyrim.esm|0B1240: Father says I'm too young to train with a sword, so I practice with my fists!
Skyrim.esm|0B1241: Hope this war lasts long enough that I get to fight in it. Battle! Glory!
Skyrim.esm|0B1243: You're not from around here. I'll bet you're here to help with the war!
Skyrim.esm|0B124D: One of the new servants? Remember that I like my meat rare.
Skyrim.esm|0B124E: Absurd that you can't get good sweet rolls in this skeeverhole of a city.
Skyrim.esm|0B124F: Where is papa? I want to ride Karinda now!
Skyrim.esm|0B1250: Blasted war. I was due a new dress from Solitude, but it will never get here.
Skyrim.esm|0B1285: Now the crystal needs to be focused. It was created so far away, we knew that some adjustments would have to be made. Heating and cooling the crystal will cause it to expand or contract, which will change how the light passes through it. You'll need to use spells to do that. Being from the College, I assume you know them already. There should be a few basic tomes around here somewhere in case your training is even more sub-standard than I've heard.
Skyrim.esm|0B1290: You'll have to get the crystal to focus properly. Heat and cold will affect it; simple concentration spells should be enough. You'll also need to align the mirrors using the controls up above. Once everything is lined up, the light will reflect back through the crystal. Then you'll see some real magic.
Skyrim.esm|0B1293: Place the crystal in the center of the armillary. Then we can worry about attuning it.
Skyrim.esm|0B12B4: What are you playing at? Is this some attempt to stall my work?!
Skyrim.esm|0B12B5: Something in Winterhold, it looks like.
Skyrim.esm|0B12B6: Something is creating an incredible amount of interference.
Skyrim.esm|0B12BF: Very good. I'll see that this is put towards getting things back to normal. Consider the suspension lifted.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C2: Oh, excellent. I'll see that it gets where it needs to go. And I'll make sure the suspension is lifted. I'm glad we were able to clear this matter up, and I do hope we don't need to have this talk again.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C3: ...but what's this? These results... They're not at all what they should be.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C4: I made it rather clear that a donation of 500 gold will clear it up rather neatly.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C5: As I've said, a donation of 250 should help take care of that.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C6: It's most unfortunate that we need to have this discussion once again. These matters are becoming more difficult to deal with. I'm going to need 1000 gold from you in order to make reparations. I also hope that this is the last time we'll need to discuss this.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C7: Another incident? I must say, I'm disappointed. I thought I made it clear that this sort of behavior is totally unacceptable. It cannot become widely known that these sorts of things happen here. Work will need to be done to keep this quiet. I think a donation of 500 gold on your part is necessary in order to put this behind us.
Skyrim.esm|0B12C8: Years of work, finally going to pay off...
Skyrim.esm|0B12C9: It's very important you do this correctly.
Skyrim.esm|0B149D: Nothing to do now but sit back, relax, and enjoy the trip home.
Skyrim.esm|0B191D: They're at Mistwatch. Here, let me show you on your map.... If you can stop them, you'll be a true hero!
Skyrim.esm|0B191E: Oh, thank you, but I should be fine now that you've shown me the way. But those bandits have to be stopped!
Skyrim.esm|0B1927: I managed to pick the lock and slip out while the guard slept, but now I'm completely lost. Can you help me, please?
Skyrim.esm|0B192E: I was kidnapped by these bandits weeks ago. They locked me up in the towers near Mistwatch.
Skyrim.esm|0B192F: Oh, thank goodness! I've been wandering alone for so long, I thought I'd never see another soul again.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FC1: Well in that case. You'll have to hand over your life as well as your coin.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FC2: We'll see about that. Won't we boys?
Skyrim.esm|0B1FD7: I'll work hard to justify your faith in me.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FDA: A wise choice, my friend. After all, what would a common adventurer know about the complexities of finance?
Skyrim.esm|0B1FDC: Wise is the one who spends a few coins now to make a fortune later.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FDE: Thank you. When I have the time, I'll try to think of how best to use this.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FE1: You want to give me money? Just like that? Fine, but I intend to earn that investment. I don't need handouts.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FE4: Ah yes, good idea. Best to let a professional businessman handle that money for you.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FE6: You make an interesting offer. Very well, I'm sure that I can put the extra coin to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0B1FE9: An investment? Very well, I don't see why not.
Skyrim.esm|0B216E: Don't just walk away from me! Where do you think you're going?
Skyrim.esm|0B216F: To the Inn for a drink, of course! Where else could I even go in this gods-forsaken town?
Skyrim.esm|0B2170: And what, you think that'll solve all your problems?
Skyrim.esm|0B2171: Probably not, but it's worth a try!
Skyrim.esm|0B2468: Gulum-Ei started to get greedy and cut us out of the deal. Almost can't say I blame the scaly bastard. It started out innocent enough; he kept claiming the shipments were light because of Imperial interference. Then, maybe a few years ago, it all stopped. He didn't even bother to contact us anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0B2469: When Gulum-Ei was getting us merchandise from the East Empire Company, we were making a hefty profit. It was so lucrative, I was considering moving the Guild to Solitude to save us time.
Skyrim.esm|0B246A: Don't dismiss our adversary so easily. They're well-funded, patient and have been able to avoid identification for years. However, don't mistake my admiration for complacency; our nemesis is going to pay dearly.
Skyrim.esm|0B246B: You jest, but they've been able to avoid identification for years. They're obviously well-funded, driven and patient. Just don't mistake my admiration for complacency; our nemesis is going to pay dearly.
Skyrim.esm|0B246C: They're well-funded and they've been able to avoid identification for years. I'm impressed it reached this point. Just don't mistake my admiration for complacency; our nemesis is going to pay dearly.
Skyrim.esm|0B2968: If you need to sample some of the finest mead in Skyrim, you're in the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0B2969: If you're looking to purchase any mead, speak to the owner, Sabjorn.
Skyrim.esm|0B296A: If you're looking to purchase any mead, speak to the brewmaster, Mallus.
Skyrim.esm|0B296B: We're a small meadery, but growing quickly. Sabjorn's only been running this place for a short time now and look at it.
Skyrim.esm|0B296C: It's a shame the Black-Briar Meadery took this place over. I never liked it as much as our Honningbrew.
Skyrim.esm|0B296D: Mallus is teaching me everything he knows about brewing. Hopefully, I can take over his position some day.
Skyrim.esm|0B296E: Welcome. I'm the owner and proprietor of Honningbrew Meadery.
Skyrim.esm|0B296F: Honningbrew uses only the finest natural honey, not that sludge you'll find at Goldenglow Estate.
Skyrim.esm|0B2970: We may be small, but what we lack in size we make up for in quality.
Skyrim.esm|0B2971: Black-Briar Mead is just fine, if you need something to wash the dirt off a riding horse's hooves.
Skyrim.esm|0B2972: You need something to do, or you just enjoy slowing down the hard-working folk?
Skyrim.esm|0B2973: If you want to buy something, go find Sabjorn. Otherwise, piss off.
Skyrim.esm|0B2974: To tell you the truth, Sabjorn isn't much of an owner. Slave driver, sure.
Skyrim.esm|0B2975: I've never met someone like him that revels so much in other's misery.
Skyrim.esm|0B2D88: Hiya! What's your name? Are you visiting? What do you do?
Skyrim.esm|0B2D8B: There's nothing like a good song to lift the spirit in troubled times.
Skyrim.esm|0B2D8C: Uh oh. A fellow bard. I best not miss my notes or forget any lyrics.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DAB: Glad you decided to check us out.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DAC: Soft sheets and hard liquor. What's not to like?
Skyrim.esm|0B2DAD: It takes two things to be a good teacher. The first is patience. The second is a firm wooden stick for rapping knuckles.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DAE: You here to apply at the Bards College? Well don't bother - you're too damned old. Start past four, and it's all wasted effort.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DAF: What a fascinating time to be alive! Mark me, these days will be remembered long after we're gone.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB0: If you love history, you're in the right place. Skyrim is steeped in it.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB2: I can't understand why Jorn would want to join the Imperial Legion. Soldiers only appreciate one thing, and it isn't music.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB3: Many condemn the Stormcloaks, but I refuse. There is honor in fighting for what you believe.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB4: This godsforsaken war is dividing our people and destroying our land.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB6: The Jarl has advised me not to talk about which court attendants I'd like to punch in the face. More's the pity.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB7: Quickly, if you please. I'm quite busy running errands for my bastard of a brother...
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB8: No doubt most folk in Skyrim consider the dragons terrifying. I find them... wondrous.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DB9: You want to know the one truth I've learned in all my years? Life's too hard to face sober.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DBA: Ulfric is a fool and a coward, who naively believes he can bring about peace through warfare. It's rather sad, really.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DBB: Thanks to Ulfric's rebellion, countless innocent people can't feed or clothe their families. It's unacceptably tragic.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DBC: Surely you have something better to do...
Skyrim.esm|0B2DBE: Most of the time, people just forget I'm even here. That's why I hear so much good gossip.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DBF: I made a wish in the town well that a golden knight came to take me to his castle. Oh, does it spoil the wish if I tell?
Skyrim.esm|0B2DC0: A sharp mind beats a sharp blade any day. Words to live by.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DD9: I feel like I can beat anything.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DDA: I'm just itching for a fight.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DDE: Suddenly I feel like I can take on the world.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DDF: Bah! Is there no one who can offer a challenge?
Skyrim.esm|0B2DE1: My claws are sharp. My will to kill... unquenchable.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DE3: I may be old, but I can show 'em a trick or two.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DE5: Hmph. Everyone seems so insignificant now.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DEA: By the gods, I feel like I could crush a giant's head like a walnut.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DEB: Ha ha! Look out. Here I come!
Skyrim.esm|0B2DEC: There is none who can stand in my way.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DED: Come on, I can take anyone. And that ain't just the mead talkin'.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DEE: I feel like I can beat anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DF1: Been too long since I've embraced my rage...
Skyrim.esm|0B2DF2: Just point me to the fight.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DF3: I fight and I'll win. Simple as that.
Skyrim.esm|0B2DF4: I feel like I can take on all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0B2FCD: Five, four, three, two, one... go!
Skyrim.esm|0B2FCE: Five, four, three, two, one... go!
Skyrim.esm|0B3101: I will see you dead, monster.
Skyrim.esm|0B3106: Then prepare yourself to receive Stendarr's Mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0B3107: The Vigil of Stendarr cleanses all Daedric filth from Tamriel. Give us any artifacts you have, or we'll purge you like all the others.
Skyrim.esm|0B310B: Stop! We know you're holding a Daedric artifact. Hand it over.
Skyrim.esm|0B3160: This looks like some sort of lever puzzle.  We'll have to figure out the correct order to flip them.
Skyrim.esm|0B3161: Anyway, here is your reward.  Thanks again.
Skyrim.esm|0B3162: Maybe the order has something to do with the Symbols up there.
Skyrim.esm|0B3163: Hopefully they made it to safety before the attack.  I'll begin the search after a quick restock.
Skyrim.esm|0B3164: An alchemy lab, it could be in here.  It's worth a look.
Skyrim.esm|0B31DE: Now that this is a Black-Briar Meadery, you can be sure that we'll be selling only the best mead in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0B31DF: What's left of the Honningbrew has been dumped into the sewers. Good riddance.
Skyrim.esm|0B31E0: Maven Black-Briar always remembers her friends. That's why I'm in charge of the place.
Skyrim.esm|0B31E1: If you have anything you're having a hard time selling, I can take it off your hands.
Skyrim.esm|0B31E2: It was you, wasn't it. I should of known better. Now look at me.
Skyrim.esm|0B31E3: You've sent an innocent man to jail. I hope your proud of yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0B31E4: Maven be damned. One day, she'll get hers. I can promise you that.
Skyrim.esm|0B31E5: Leave me alone. Haven't you done enough damage already?
Skyrim.esm|0B31F6: I heard Valdr made it back safe. Too bad about the others, but at least it's one less thing to worry about.
Skyrim.esm|0B31F7: Name's Valdr. I hunt out of Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0B31F8: We tracked a bear to this den. Good coin for those pelts. We had the big sow cornered when they showed up. Three of them, out of nowhere. Spriggans. Niels went down before we even knew to run. Ari died just inside. I never even thought the things were real.
Skyrim.esm|0B31F9: Changed your mind? Well, I can't give up so easily. I'm heading back.
Skyrim.esm|0B31FA: Please, I need help! I don't want to die on this damned log!
Skyrim.esm|0B31FB: I'll give them a proper burial, then head back. Come find me if you're ever in Falkreath. You'll always be welcome at my door.
Skyrim.esm|0B31FC: Folks have gone missing the past few weeks. Word now's that our hunters are gone, too. Say, if you're traveling, could you keep an eye out for them? Valdr and his men. I think they were after bear up north.
Skyrim.esm|0B31FE: It's done, then. Justice, if you can call it that. Look, I want you to have this. I know it isn't much, but it's important to me.
Skyrim.esm|0B3224: You haven't heard any more about dragon sightings, have you?
Skyrim.esm|0B3225: There's talk of it, but I find it hard to believe.
Skyrim.esm|0B3226: I do hope it's true. So many opportunities...
Skyrim.esm|0B3227: Knowledge of truly powerful Illusion spells is here at the College, but it is hidden from those who might misuse it. I shall teach you a spell to discern things hidden from others. With it, you will be able to acquire the texts you need. Bring all four to me, and I shall use them to inscribe the spells for you.
Skyrim.esm|0B3228: I continue to hear things about dragon sightings in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0B3229: Would you like me to send someone? Faralda or Phinis, perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|0B322A: No, which is fine with me.
Skyrim.esm|0B322C: A pity. You have any idea how much fangs would be worth? Or even better -- bottle their flame. The right buyer would pay an immense amount.
Skyrim.esm|0B322D: You have gone beyond my teachings, which is quite a feat. I did not think it possible. But there is always more to learn. I believe you are ready for the next step.
Skyrim.esm|0B322F: Dragons? Well, that's fascinating. We should have someone look into that.
Skyrim.esm|0B3230: What? Oh, nothing. It's just... It's an interesting development.
Skyrim.esm|0B3231: Bottle... their flame? I can't... No, I don't think I've any interest in that sort of thing.
Skyrim.esm|0B3232: Is it true? Are there really dragons in Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0B3888: Now you won't forget to tell Mercer I cooperated, will you?
Skyrim.esm|0B3889: Give Mercer my regards when you return to Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0B388A: Look, did you want the information or not?
Skyrim.esm|0B388B: Do take care handling that case of wine... it's irreplaceable.
Skyrim.esm|0B3890: If you're the best the Guild's got, they're in worse shape than I imagined.
Skyrim.esm|0B3893: Not at all. I consider it an investment in prolonging my life. As far as Goldenglow Estate goes, I'll tell you what I know.
Skyrim.esm|0B3896: Good. Can't have the buyer getting impatient and looking elsewhere for this, can we? Here, take this. I certainly can't use it, but I suppose I need to pay you something for the goods.
Skyrim.esm|0B3897: I have a buyer looking for a case of Firebrand Wine. There just so happens to be a single case in the Blue Palace. Bring it to me, and we'll talk about Goldenglow Estate.
Skyrim.esm|0B3898: I don't care what you promise. If I tell you the buyer's name and word gets around, it could ruin me!
Skyrim.esm|0B3899: Hasn't Mercer Frey taught you people anything?
Skyrim.esm|0B389A: Why are you following me? I told you, I don't know anything else!
Skyrim.esm|0B389B: Look, I've told you everything. Now leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|0B389C: Our business has concluded and you're standing in my way.
Skyrim.esm|0B389D: Ah, I see you have the wine. Hand it over and we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0B38A0: I'm glad to see you're embracing our methods. It would be a waste to lose a contact at the East Empire Company before we had the entire story. Just keep on Gulum-Ei's tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
Skyrim.esm|0B391F: But not Dragonrend. The knowledge of that Shout was lost in the time before history began. Perhaps only its creators ever knew it. But I am not the one to speak of it to you.
Skyrim.esm|0B3920: Do not confuse inaction with indifference. Often the wisest course is to wait and watch events unfold, until the time to act is clear. The Blades have never followed that path, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0B394E: No. Not now. Not until you return to the path of wisdom.
Skyrim.esm|0B396A: He is our leader. He surpasses us all in his mastery of the Way of the Voice.
Skyrim.esm|0B3970: No, no, of course not. Forgive me, Dragonborn. I have been intemperate with you. But heed my warning - the Blades may say they serve the Dragonborn, but they do not. They never have.
Skyrim.esm|0B3974: It is called "Dragonrend", but its Words of Power are unknown to us. We do not regret this loss. Dragonrend holds no place within the Way of the Voice.
Skyrim.esm|0B3975: They have always sought to turn the Dragonborn from the path of wisdom. Have you learned nothing from us? Would you simply be a tool in the hands of the Blades, to be used for their own purposes?
Skyrim.esm|0B3976: I will grant you my understanding of Clear Skies. This is your final gift from us, Dragonborn. Use it well.
Skyrim.esm|0B3978: I cannot teach you the Shout, because I do not know it.
Skyrim.esm|0B397C: Forgive me. I was... intemperate. I allowed my emotions to cloud my judgment. Master Einarth reminded me of my duty. The decision whether or not to help you is not mine to make.
Skyrim.esm|0B397D: I have given my answer. Go and reconsider your course.
Skyrim.esm|0B3980: Arngeir. Rok los Dovahkiin, Strundu'ul. Rok fen tinvaak Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|0B3981: Arngeir. Rek los Dovahkiin, Strundu'ul. Rek fen tinvaak Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|0B3982: It was created by those who had lived under the unimaginable cruelty of Alduin's Dragon Cult. Their whole lives were consumed with hatred for dragons, and they poured all their anger and hatred into this Shout. When you learn a Shout, you take it into your very being. In a sense, you become the Shout. In order to learn and use this Shout, you will be taking this evil into yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0B3983: What I want is irrelevant. This Shout was used once before, was it not? And here we are again. Have you considered that Alduin was not meant to be defeated? Those who overthrew him in ancient times only postponed the day of reckoning, they did not stop it. If the world is meant to end, so be it. Let it end and be reborn.
Skyrim.esm|0B398C: Those who are ignorant find the Way of the Voice easy to ridicule. This is why so few follow it, and why the world is as it is.
Skyrim.esm|0B3B07: Oh, this is going to be fun. You've got to use your Wabbajack to defeat the enemy while - get this - they use their Wabbajack to defeat you!
Skyrim.esm|0B3B0D: Inside the mind of Pelagius, silly. Oh. Is it your... first time?
Skyrim.esm|0B3B90: ...hear he's decided to let the Hags turn him into a Briarheart.
Skyrim.esm|0B3B91: Heart of thorn... bones of the wild... in life, Forsworn...
Skyrim.esm|0B3B92: That's a true Reachman. Hope he heads to Markarth to take out some of those Nords.
Skyrim.esm|0B3B93: ...rise from death, Blood of our Blood.
Skyrim.esm|0B3B94: ...rise from death, Blood of our Blood.
Skyrim.esm|0B3CB2: Pretty, pretty flesh. Let me out!
Skyrim.esm|0B3CB3: Yes, yes. My staff, take it. Kill something pretty with it.
Skyrim.esm|0B3D70: You've done it! That's all of them!
Skyrim.esm|0B3D8D: That's it! You've got one of them lined up!
Skyrim.esm|0B3D8F: So, what is it? What have you done? Did you know what we were attempting? Are you here to make sure your plan worked, that our efforts have been for nothing? Well, explain yourself!
Skyrim.esm|0B3DFE: This is... he should not have gone alone. Thank you for telling me. This is a day where our souls must cry, and our hearts will answer. Go. Grieve in whatever way you know.
Skyrim.esm|0B3DFF: Skjor... I always thought of him as invincible. Truly, one of our great fires has gone out.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E00: He was the one I looked up to. I need to find my brother.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E01: He has joined the honored dead. This will be a difficult time for us all.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E02: Unfortunate. He was an excellent sparring partner. I'll miss our training sessions.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E03: I... I can only imagine Aela's anger. I'll make sure not to cross her for a while.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E04: He did? That's... I'm surprised. I wonder who will replace him in the Circle?
Skyrim.esm|0B3E05: Tragic, of course. But, well. Fires go out. Others ignite. Skjor inspired many with his life. Perhaps his death can continue to inspire.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E06: Oh my. He was always so kind to me. A bit rough around the edges, but a good man. I... I'll get his things in order.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E25: I bet she would feel safer. And hopefully you wouldn't have to baby sit her for long. Once she calms down, she might start thinking straight again. Let's go talk with her. Stay close. Talk of assassins has the guards jumpy. They might attack you if you're not standing near me.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E26: If there's someone in the palace who can speak for you and wouldn't mind walking you around. Great. Lead me to him.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E27: Come in, come in! Shut the door behind you!
Skyrim.esm|0B3E28: I thought you didn't believe I was in danger!
Skyrim.esm|0B3E29: I'm afraid you'll have to come back later. I've been given strict instructions that no one be allowed to trouble Lady Elisif.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E2B: Stay close and in plain view. No funny business unless you want a few inches of steel in your belly.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E2C: I know. The poor girl's fright has left her bereft of reason. But there's no talking her out of it. I fear she'll be locked in there for days.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E2E: I'm glad they finally believed me. About the assassin I mean. It's been so hard since my Torygg passed. No one takes me seriously. So how's this work exactly? Do you just kind of stand there on the look out? That's what Bolgeir does. Well, he sort of looms more than stands. Bolgeir's loyal, but even he doesn't do what I ask. Case in point, this whole assassin thing. Well at least I have you now. Look at me ramble... Well, I guess I'll go about my business sulking in here. You can stand over there in the corner watching for assassins.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E2F: Private quarters are strictly off limits. You have been warned.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E30: It doesn't really matter what I believe, now does it? I've brought you someone who can protect you. That's what you want, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0B3E31: Well, what are you waiting for?!
Skyrim.esm|0B3E32: She's telling everyone she's sick. But she's really locked herself in her room because she fears an assassin is out to get her.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E33: Yes, the Steward is taking visitors. However, I must accompany you at all times while on the royal premises.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E34: Damn it, I lost him!  Protect the Jarl!
Skyrim.esm|0B3E35: Damn it, I lost her! Protect the Jarl!
Skyrim.esm|0B3E37: (Sounds of unlocking and unbarring a door)
Skyrim.esm|0B3E38: I've brought a friend. Someone who can help. A bodyguard. To protect you.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E39: I suppose you're free to frolic with the wenches in the servant's quarters, but anywhere else, you must have an escort. Jarl's orders.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E3A: I've been instructed to tell all visitors that the Lady Elisif isn't feeling well. She wishes you a good day, and please call again another time.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E3B: Yes. Are you enjoying our little walk together? Or perhaps you'd like to come back when the Lady is taking visitors?
Skyrim.esm|0B3E3C: Stay where I can see you.
Skyrim.esm|0B3E3D: You need an escort at all times.
Skyrim.esm|0B4584: These wall carvings are ancient, we must be getting close.
Skyrim.esm|0B4585: That gem looks suspicious to me.
Skyrim.esm|0B4586: There it is!  On the table.
Skyrim.esm|0B45CA: Been a long time since someone took the leap. Longer still since any survived it.
Skyrim.esm|0B45CB: I once performed the entirety of the Poetic Edda from atop Bard's Leap before trying my luck... Well, you can see how that turned out.
Skyrim.esm|0B45F1: Let me look.  This is a very exciting moment for me.
Skyrim.esm|0B45F2: That's Vokun, the guardian of the scroll.  It must be just past him.
Skyrim.esm|0B48F6: I suppose so. But believe me. Only us servants have a key like that, and its a very expensive looking lock. Really, I wouldn't worry about it. Here. Take a look for yourself. But give it back soon. It's almost time for milady's medicine.
Skyrim.esm|0B48F7: Not so ridiculous, really. I know people don't think much of her. But she is the Jarl after all.  Plenty of folk would kill to be in her shoes.
Skyrim.esm|0B48F8: Well, she needs to eat. I don't think she even knows where the kitchen is. And milady isn't exactly going to remove her own chamber pot, now is she? It hardly matters though. The door to the stairs is securely locked. There's no way anyone without the key can get in. So don't you worry about it.
Skyrim.esm|0B48FA: She's locked herself in her room not letting anyone in. Well, except for us servants. She's afraid of assassins coming to get her. Even still, I make a point of announcing myself when I enter her room now. I spooked her so bad last time she almost threw the chamber pot at me!
Skyrim.esm|0B5551: Good riddance.  Let's move on, the scroll must be in the next room.
Skyrim.esm|0B5D39: You've not heard of the Gourmet? Author of "Uncommon Taste"? He's a wizard with a spatula. Well, not an actual wizard, but his recipes are legendary.
Skyrim.esm|0B5D3A: Then you know that Gourmet keeps his identity a secret. I'm hoping to discover who he is, so I can tell him how he changed my life. For all I know, the Gourmet is a woman!
Skyrim.esm|0B5D3B: I'm collecting ingredients to bring to the Gourmet. He'll think my tastes so refined when I offer them to him. You have heard of the Gourmet?
Skyrim.esm|0B5D3C: Allow me to give you this, the fork I used the first time I tasted one of your dishes. Oh, and these ingredients I've been collecting for you. I've risked life and limb so that you may create another masterpiece. Make sure to think of me when you use them!
Skyrim.esm|0B5D3E: Oh, my! You? It is an honor! Your food has opened my eyes to the wonders this world can hold. I've cooked every dish in your book, and I must say your brilliance is inspiring. My technique surely doesn't even approach yours... but whose would?
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF0: He has knowledge of a cult of Talos. He will tell us what we need to know, or he will die. The choice is his.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF1: We are special envoys of the Aldmeri Dominion, the rightful rulers of Tamriel. If you'd like I can show you why.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF2: Taking this man to be interrogated.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF3: That is none of your concern.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF5: I am a Thalmor Justiciar on important business that you are interfering with.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF6: That we are superior to men is an established fact. For example, take this belief in Talos. The "ninth" divine. Heh. Certainly you don't believe such things?
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF7: It's immoral to worship a man. And it's also illegal. A faithful Imperial citizen would know that.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF8: I don't like you... I think you're a heretic.
Skyrim.esm|0B5DF9: Incorrect. You can worship whatever gods you like. But Talos is a man, and only a heretic would think otherwise...
Skyrim.esm|0B5DFF: By Imperial Law banning Talos, we have the right to do whatever we want.
Skyrim.esm|0B5E00: We're making sure your Emperor wasn't lying to his elven masters when he agreed the Empire would give up false gods and foolish beliefs.
Skyrim.esm|0B5E01: Or perhaps there's something you'd like to confess?
Skyrim.esm|0B5E07: And now you'll walk away, if you know what's good for you.
Skyrim.esm|0B5E08: And so, you will die a heretic's death.
Skyrim.esm|0B5E09: If you're smart, you'll walk away.
Skyrim.esm|0B5E0B: Perhaps there's something you wish to confess?
Skyrim.esm|0B5E1A: The path to Paarthurnax is perilous, not to be embarked upon lightly.
Skyrim.esm|0B5E1B: Clear Skies will blow away the mist, but only for a time.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F4F: Purely to help safeguard the Empire, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F50: Just look! This should be lit up brighter than the night sky, and it's not! Clearly you've interfered somehow. Or...
Skyrim.esm|0B5F51: What's important is that all of this work was designed to reveal to us sources of great magical power.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F68: Either you're lying to me, or...
Skyrim.esm|0B5F69: Also, I doubt you'd be able to comprehend the details.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F6A: But it must be something you're doing. There's no other explanation...
Skyrim.esm|0B5F72: I can't explain the details. That would be giving away many secrets the Synod have learned over the years.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F75: You're looking for something, yes? The... Staff of Magnus. Well, even if you are trying to ruin my work, there's still something to be learned here.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F76: You show up here just as our work nears completion, and now I can't get any results from this because of something at your College. Do you think me a fool? Do you think I'm too stupid to make the connection? You've ruined my work!
Skyrim.esm|0B5F89: Go wrong? Go wrong? Everything is wrong! Everything! Whatever you have at that College has completely interfered with our work here.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F8C: So you do have something, then. Whether this was intentional or not, it suggests some interesting results.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F8D: You and your College have ruined years of my work, I've lost colleagues and friends to the Falmer, and you want me to calm down?
Skyrim.esm|0B5F90: Well, now. This I hadn't considered. If that's taken into account, these results make more sense.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F93: The other... Well, that can only be Labyrinthian.
Skyrim.esm|0B5F9D: The Eye of Magnus? Well, I suppose if that means what I think it does... Well, that's interesting....
Skyrim.esm|0B5FAA: And yet, in the end, only two locations have been revealed to us. One is your College.
Skyrim.esm|0B5FAC: You have something at your College, don't you? Something immensely powerful. Beyond anything I'd anticipated. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|0B5FAD: I can. It might save your life.
Skyrim.esm|0B5FB0: I can teach you a few things.
Skyrim.esm|0B6227: I'll never get tired of killing these Imperial bastards.
Skyrim.esm|0B6228: Don't let those Stormcloaks get away!
Skyrim.esm|0B6269: You see only me because your heart knows only me as the Companions leader. I'd wager old Vignar could see half a dozen of my predecessors. And I see them all. The ones in Sovngarde. The ones trapped with me in Hircine's realm. And they all see you. You've brought honor to the name of the Companions. We won't soon forget it.
Skyrim.esm|0B6274: And that's exactly what the old man wanted. I'm glad we could give it to him.
Skyrim.esm|0B6275: He's not a werewolf any more? Even dead? This is a good thing!
Skyrim.esm|0B6276: Yes, you brought honor to him, even after his death. A worthy outcome for a worthy warrior.
Skyrim.esm|0B6277: I'll be honest, I wouldn't have expected it. But that may just be because I remember when you were just a whelp who was begging to join. The old man trusted you, so I'll trust you.
Skyrim.esm|0B6278: Oh, you don't have to worry about any problems from me. I do what I'm told. Congratulations!
Skyrim.esm|0B6279: If you had told me a few months ago that some outsider I had never heard of would lead the Companions, I might have slit your throat. But I've seen what you can do. And heard of the love that Kodlak had for you. The trust. Let's hope you can show that his heart was not deceived. Good luck, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|0B627A: Eventually. But this... this is the Tomb of Ysgramor. I think I'm just going to... commune for a bit. This place is worthy of some time. You go on ahead. I'll see you back there.
Skyrim.esm|0B627B: I go where my brother goes. I think he wants to look around some more.
Skyrim.esm|0B627C: Not right now. I'm going to look at some of these carvings. I wonder if Ysgramor ever set foot here while he was alive. But you don't have to stay, if you don't want to. I'll see you back at Jorrvaskr. Stories to tell the others, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0B62AE: Ah, I can't reveal all my secrets to you. But rest assured, I would know.
Skyrim.esm|0B62B0: But how would you know it's real?
Skyrim.esm|0B62B1: Elda, you know better than to believe every idiot traveler that wanders through.
Skyrim.esm|0B62B2: Captain, have you heard these stories of dragons?
Skyrim.esm|0B62B4: But they're saying that one of them freed Ulfric... that he's in league with them.
Skyrim.esm|0B62B5: Think about how ridiculous that sounds. Come now.
Skyrim.esm|0B62B6: Calixto, do you know if there's any truth to these rumors of dragons?
Skyrim.esm|0B62B7: Eh. I try not to trouble myself with stories of the outside world. Now if someone brings me a dragon scale, that's a different story.
Skyrim.esm|0B62C7: I'm fairly sure that the dragons don't care about our matters one way or the other.
Skyrim.esm|0B62CA: But just think of the advantage.
Skyrim.esm|0B62CB: Do you think we could persuade one of the dragons to join our side?
Skyrim.esm|0B62CD: I'm not saying it wouldn't be useful. But there are better uses of our resources right now.
Skyrim.esm|0B62CF: I don't suppose we would ever really trust the thing anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0B646A: I am Lu'ah Al-Skaven. Who are you to disrupt my work? Begone, or I will add you to my army of the dead!
Skyrim.esm|0B6679: Tythis, please. The Snow-Shods are good people, but tend to exaggerate. Try and stay calm for your own sake.
Skyrim.esm|0B667A: We hold each other close and hope that all of this ends well.
Skyrim.esm|0B667B: Yes, milady. I've also taken the liberty in preparing a private carriage... just in case.
Skyrim.esm|0B667C: Yes, milady. That unpleasantness at Helgen supposedly involved a dragon. It killed several soldiers before it departed.
Skyrim.esm|0B667D: Talen, do you believe what they're saying? Do you really think the dragons have returned?
Skyrim.esm|0B667E: Madesi, have you heard the Imperials might be headed this way? What would we do?
Skyrim.esm|0B667F: Good. If Riften should fall, it's only right that its leader be spared.
Skyrim.esm|0B6680: I don't know what to believe. If it's true, then dark times may be upon us.
Skyrim.esm|0B6681: They aren't invading, milord... it's simply a response to Ulfric and his Stormcloaks. I assure you everything will be fine.
Skyrim.esm|0B6682: I want you to speak to the city guard. Make sure there's a contingency plan in effect in case Riften falls under attack.
Skyrim.esm|0B6683: But I've heard from the Snow-Shods that they intend to burn this place to the ground!
Skyrim.esm|0B6684: Have our scouts come up with any information on these reports of dragons?
Skyrim.esm|0B6B9E: See if you can unlock that door.
Skyrim.esm|0B6B9F: Did you find a key? See if it unlocks that door.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BA0: Unlock that door and let's get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BDB: You have your orders. Rendezvous with the rest of the men outside Korvanjund.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF0: I knew I heard something. What's this bridge doing down?
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF1: So, stop your worrying and get some rest. Your shift is coming up and I don't want you dozing off again like last time.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF2: Keep looking. Someone's here, I can feel it.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF3: This again? I told you we have someone standing guard out there. And, don't forget the rock trap we rigged up.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF6: We did! Keep your eyes peeled. I don't think we're alone. You check the entrance. I'm going to look over here.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF7: Aren't you worried someone will wander in here? The entrance isn't exactly hidden you know.
Skyrim.esm|0B6BF8: I thought we had a guard posted outside.
Skyrim.esm|0B714D: Cowards, every one of them. Running at the first sign of trouble...
Skyrim.esm|0B714F: Clear out the chamber, and I'll give you the key.
Skyrim.esm|0B7150: Every moment you waste is another moment those doors stay shut tight. Get going!
Skyrim.esm|0B7151: Riches and fortune, within my grasp, and I have to give it all up because they're afraid of a few dead bodies...
Skyrim.esm|0B7152: Every moment you waste is another moment those doors stay shut tight. Get going!
Skyrim.esm|0B7153: Only one more door left.  Hurry up.
Skyrim.esm|0B7154: I don't have time to talk. Keep moving!
Skyrim.esm|0B72F9: Why don't you just formally join up? Quit sittin' here wheelin' and dealin' when you could be out there makin' a heck of a lot more coin. We could use someone with your skills.
Skyrim.esm|0B72FA: Tonilia, can I offer some friendly advice?
Skyrim.esm|0B72FC: Thanks for the compliment, Delvin. I may just take you up on that one day.
Skyrim.esm|0B72FD: Maven! So good to see you again. Hoist a flagon and celebrate with me!
Skyrim.esm|0B72FE: You can offer, but I may not buy.
Skyrim.esm|0B72FF: Hey, hey. Sorry. I just get jealous when I hear rumors about you.
Skyrim.esm|0B7301: Vulwulf, it's always a pleasure. When you're standing downwind of me.
Skyrim.esm|0B737C: I beg you, don't go into Silus' museum.
Skyrim.esm|0B737D: Not a student of history I see. It's for the best. They're a group that should be forgotten. The only thing you need to know is that they almost destroyed the world, and they were stopped. A long time ago.
Skyrim.esm|0B737E: Silus comes from one of the oldest families in Dawnstar. They have a complicated history. Several of his ancestors belonged to the Mythic Dawn, the cult that almost destroyed Tamriel. His family's involvement was only found out well after the crisis had died down, but it still ruined their reputation. They were outcasts. And now Silus is back and this museum to the Mythic Dawn is his way of trying to rebuild his family's pride. It's misguided.
Skyrim.esm|0B737F: Your loss, but if you change your mind, I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|0B7380: Yes. My collection of artifacts from a group that toppled an Empire. Why don't you come in? You can browse the displays, and we can talk. I have a job you look perfect for.
Skyrim.esm|0B7381: And here comes my first visitor. The Museum of the Mythic Dawn is open, friend!
Skyrim.esm|0B776C: Some old priest buried here long ago. I've been searching for his tomb for a few years now.
Skyrim.esm|0B776D: Maybe you could help find it, I just know it's in here.
Skyrim.esm|0B776E: I'm sorry, but I can't let that happen.
Skyrim.esm|0B776F: Hmm, it's encrypted. It'll take a while to figure this out.
Skyrim.esm|0B7770: It's probably meaningless to you, but according to family legend it links our bloodline back to Ysgramor himself.
Skyrim.esm|0B7771: And here's a little something for helping me out.
Skyrim.esm|0B785F: If that's how you choose to end your life, so be it.
Skyrim.esm|0B7860: There are many valuable artifacts all through the temple.
Skyrim.esm|0B7861: What are you doing in here? Stay where you are. The mother will deal with you.
Skyrim.esm|0B7862: Now, come with me, child. We'll prepare you for the goddess' presence.
Skyrim.esm|0B7863: Oh, I... I'm sorry. I just can't... you'll have to speak to my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0B7864: Oh, my. You'll want to talk to Enmon about that.
Skyrim.esm|0B7865: I thought Enmon had a little girl, but I haven't seen her in a while.
Skyrim.esm|0B7866: I think you might be looking for Enmon.
Skyrim.esm|0B7867: And what's that have to do with me?
Skyrim.esm|0B7868: Yeah, we're all looking for something.
Skyrim.esm|0B80A7: Well. I do suppose thanks are in order. So... my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0B80AA: I suppose thanks are in order.
Skyrim.esm|0B80B3: Well now. I guess I owe you my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0B80BA: Yes, yes, thank you and all that.
Skyrim.esm|0B80BE: I suppose thanks are in order.
Skyrim.esm|0B80C4: Ah, my thanks. Malacath watch over ya.
Skyrim.esm|0B810C: You know, I think you're right.
Skyrim.esm|0B810D: No, I'm afraid I don't know anything of the sort.
Skyrim.esm|0B810E: Ha! Humor is often in short supply here. But I sense that perhaps you're after more than just that.
Skyrim.esm|0B810F: It would seem that the College has what you seek. The question now is what can you offer the College? Not just anyone is allowed inside. Those wishing to enter must show some degree of skill with magic. A small test, if you will.
Skyrim.esm|0B8110: Ah, the immortal plane. It is said to be the source of all magic. This is a noble goal, indeed.
Skyrim.esm|0B8111: No, not recently at least. We don't mistake that for acceptance, though. Odds are they're simply too afraid to confront us directly. And most of us are quite satisfied with that.
Skyrim.esm|0B8112: Well, yes. Probably. I mean, yes. Certainly it is, but that doesn't matter now, because I know you're hoarding something even more important at your college. The Council will be informed of this. They will find out what you're up to.
Skyrim.esm|0B8114: Is there some way I can assist you?
