Skyrim.esm|0B8115: We are the only group left in Skyrim dedicated to the study of the arcane. There are others who study, to be sure, but they do so in private, and often in secret.
Skyrim.esm|0B8116: Well, you clearly possess the minimum aptitude required. Congratulations. I'll escort you up to the gate. You'll want to speak with Mirabelle Ervine right away. And welcome to the College of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0B8117: Am I? Am I? I think I've discovered more here than I could've hoped to. Your trickery won't confuse me!
Skyrim.esm|0B8119: I think you'll be a superb addition to the College. Welcome, Apprentice. I'll lead you across the bridge. Once you're inside you'll want to speak with Mirabelle Ervine, our master wizard. Please, follow me.
Skyrim.esm|0B811C: So, mage from Winterhold, despite your intentions I've beaten your little game. Even if all you've said here is lies, I know you have something in Winterhold the Synod Council will be very interested in. So fine, trudge off to Labyrinthian in search of your staff. I shall return to Cyrodiil and deliver my full report to the Council. This is not over, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0B811D: So the stories are true... you are Dragonborn! Normally you'd need to show some aptitude with one of the schools of magic, but you... I think there is much that we can learn from each other.
Skyrim.esm|0B811E: If it's true, and you do have the Voice, would you mind showing me? I've never seen it first-hand.
Skyrim.esm|0B811F: Dragonborn? It's been so long since we've had any contact with the Greybeards. Do you really have the Voice? I would be most impressed to see that.
Skyrim.esm|0B8127: Mind what - or who - you practice this on.
Skyrim.esm|0B8128: Your power over others shall be great, like mine.
Skyrim.esm|0B8135: I'm afraid your skills haven't improved enough to be considered.
Skyrim.esm|0B8136: If you can pass the test, then yes.
Skyrim.esm|0B813B: I told you, it's the wedding of Vittoria Vici, an extremely well connected merchant with the East Empire Company. The Emperor's cousin? Remember?
Skyrim.esm|0B813C: Why are we even going to a wedding at this far flung end of the Empire anyway?
Skyrim.esm|0B813D: Come along, no more stops, we need to find our way to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0B813E: Hopefully these gifts will put us in her good graces, secure that import deal, and lead the way to an audience with the Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|0B8159: Ha, what a coward. Didn't even put up a proper fight.
Skyrim.esm|0B815A: No, I guess not. Well, get out of here before I change my mind.
Skyrim.esm|0B815B: Yes, actually. You look like you're lousy with gold.
Skyrim.esm|0B815E: All right, hand over your valuables, or I'll gut you like a fish.
Skyrim.esm|0B815F: Hail, brother! I saw you walking there and thought you might need this.
Skyrim.esm|0B8160: Hail, sister! I saw you walking there and thought you could use this.
Skyrim.esm|0B8161: Hail, Nightingale! Luck has shined down on me and made me a rich man. Here is your cut, as promised.
Skyrim.esm|0B8162: A Nightingale? Oh, I had no idea. My apologies.
Skyrim.esm|0B8164: Well. I, uh, can see you're not one to be trifled with. Let this be a warning to you!
Skyrim.esm|0B8165: Nice try, but you don't scare me.
Skyrim.esm|0B8166: Of course! I've seen you at the Flagon. My mistake.
Skyrim.esm|0B8167: Then I guess that's your last request, fool!
Skyrim.esm|0B8168: I'm not going to ask again.
Skyrim.esm|0B81E4: The self-loathing enhances Pelagius' anger! Ah, but his confidence will shrink with every hit. You must bring the two into balance.
Skyrim.esm|0B81E5: With the threat gone Pelagius is under the delusion that he's safe, which means you've helped him out... sort of. And we're that much closer to home.
Skyrim.esm|0B81E6: The attacks he makes on himself can be seen here fully. They are always carried out on the weakest part of his fragile self.
Skyrim.esm|0B81E7: But the deepest, keenest hatred was for himself.
Skyrim.esm|0B81E8: Oho! I thought you'd never figure it out.
Skyrim.esm|0B81E9: The butler did it! Or is it the advisor? Whoever that man behind the throne was.
Skyrim.esm|0B81EA: Ah, now this is a sad path. Pelagius hated and feared many things. Assassins, wild dogs, the undead, pumpernickel...
Skyrim.esm|0B81EC: All you need to do is find something to wake our poor Pelagius up. You'll find his terrors easy to repel... but persistent.
Skyrim.esm|0B81ED: The objective here is simple, you simpleton!  Use your Wabbajack to defeat the enemy, while they do the same!
Skyrim.esm|0B81EE: Oh, but she taught her son well. Pelagius learned at a very early age that danger could come from anywhere. At any time. Delivered... by anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0B81EF: That woman wielded fear like a cleaver. Or did she wield a cleaver and make people afraid? I never get that part right...
Skyrim.esm|0B81F0: You've headed down the path of dreams. Unfortunately for you, Pelagius suffered night terrors from a young age.
Skyrim.esm|0B81F1: He would lie in bed, paralyzed with fear, imagining his own nightmares coming to life. Ooohhh... Scary.
Skyrim.esm|0B81F2: You see, Pelagius' mother was... well... let us say "unique." Although, I suppose in the grand scheme of things, she was fairly average for a Septim.
Skyrim.esm|0B81F3: Wonderfully done. Pelagius is finally ready to love himself... and continue hating everyone else.
Skyrim.esm|0B81F4: Don't get too comfortable here, mortal. You may actually earn your way out.
Skyrim.esm|0B81F5: Well now, that's something to crow about. With Pelagius up and about you're moving right along. We'll both be home in no time.
Skyrim.esm|0B81F6: Oh, good choice. Well, good for me. I find everyone being out to get you so terribly entertaining. You might find it... less so.
Skyrim.esm|0B8240: Thanks for clearing out that vampire nest. You saved Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|0B82E6: Looking for the blade? You aren't the first!
Skyrim.esm|0B82E7: You won't take my eye, man-thing!
Skyrim.esm|0B82E8: You're after that damn hilt? Go ahead, try and take it!
Skyrim.esm|0B82EA: It's no secret that my family were once members of the Mythic Dawn. One of my forefathers was even chosen to assassinate Uriel Septim himself. We hid from our past for years, became tradesman, people of coin and influence. But I realized that the Mythic Dawn's importance -- our importance -- to history cannot be denied. I'll see everyone in Tamriel remember that for a moment, we held the fate of the world in our hands, for good or ill.
Skyrim.esm|0B82EB: And here you are. Good. Come in, browse the display, and let's talk. I have a job you look perfect for.
Skyrim.esm|0B82EC: Never mind that. Madena is a good woman, but I have my own reasons for opening this museum, and I'm not changing my mind. I have a collection of artifacts from the group that toppled an Empire. Their importance to history cannot be forgotten. Why don't you come in? You can browse the displays, and we can talk. I have a job you look perfect for.
Skyrim.esm|0B82ED: Ah, yes. That scabbard. Notice the insignia? An Oblivion gate. A key symbol of Mehrunes Dagon, the patron daedra of the Mythic Dawn.
Skyrim.esm|0B82F9: You dare to betray us, Illia?
Skyrim.esm|0B82FA: Our sisterhood will be better off with you dead, brat.
Skyrim.esm|0B833B: You'll be able to run circles around those big, slow brutes in Heavy Armor.
Skyrim.esm|0B833C: Well, you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0B8382: More than you'll ever realize. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot.
Skyrim.esm|0B8383: My original intention was to use that arrow on Mercer, but I never had a clear shot. I made a split second decision to get you out of the way and it prevented your death.
Skyrim.esm|0B8384: There were three of us. Myself, Gallus, and Mercer. We were an anonymous splinter of the Thieves Guild in Riften. Perhaps I'll tell you more about it later. Right now, you need to head for Winterhold with the journal and get the translation. Here, take these as well, they may prove useful for your journey.
Skyrim.esm|0B8385: Farewell. I'll be certain to give Brynjolf your regards.
Skyrim.esm|0B8386: Enthir... Gallus' friend at the College of Winterhold. Of course... The only outsider Gallus trusted with the knowledge of his Nightingale identity.
Skyrim.esm|0B8387: The infighting had taken months to subside which gave her time to go into hiding and carefully cover her tracks. I spent thousands of septims and used every contact at my disposal, but it was as if she had simply vanished... like I said before, she was the best.
Skyrim.esm|0B8388: I wish I knew. The journal is written in some sort of language I've never seen before.
Skyrim.esm|0B8389: I saw what they did to Gallus. I wanted to use the Guild's resources to hunt down Karliah. The others didn't even care he was gone. Fortunately, I persevered and the other groups were either killed or they left Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0B838A: My purpose in using Snow Veil Sanctum to ambush Mercer wasn't simply for irony's sake. Before both of you arrived, I recovered a journal from Gallus' remains. I suspect the information we need is written inside.
Skyrim.esm|0B838B: The Guild was thrown into disarray. Several stepped up and tried to claim Gallus' former position as Guild Master. Sides quickly formed behind these men and the Ratway became a bloodbath.
Skyrim.esm|0B838C: All I had hoped was to capture Mercer alive.
Skyrim.esm|0B838D: Mercer must be brought before the Guild to answer for what he's done. He needs to pay for Gallus' murder.
Skyrim.esm|0B838E: I promise you, the thought crossed my mind. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot.
Skyrim.esm|0B838F: Without the antidote I administered, you'd be as still as a statue. I treated your wounds and didn't leave you defenseless. The poison on that arrow took me a year to perfect; I only had enough for a single shot and yet I used it on you.
Skyrim.esm|0B8394: Don't you walk away from me!
Skyrim.esm|0B839B: I don't know, Babette. Looks like he may have suffered a head wound. Best not let him out of our sight...
Skyrim.esm|0B839C: I don't know, Babette. Looks like she may have suffered a head wound. Best not let her out of our sight...
Skyrim.esm|0B839D: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Slow down. It's all right. You've been through a lot. Maybe you should just sit down for a bit...
Skyrim.esm|0B83A0: Damn it, there's got to be a way out of here!
Skyrim.esm|0B83A1: Nazir told me what happened in Solitude. You poor dear. You've been through so much...
Skyrim.esm|0B83A2: Oh! Well, in that case, lead on. I'm right behind you.
Skyrim.esm|0B83A3: She's here? By Sithis, I thought we'd lost her. Let's go!
Skyrim.esm|0B83B9: My family thanks you. Take this as a token of our gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|0B83BA: I expected too much. I'll seek someone else to salvage the family honor.
Skyrim.esm|0B83BB: Good. He will have fled to gather others of his kind.
Skyrim.esm|0B83BC: No. It's too late for the wardstone. Vighar must be destroyed. If this gets out, my family will suffer great shame.
Skyrim.esm|0B83BD: You may have seen the open grave in our graveyard. Most of the townsfolk think it's the work of a corpse thief. The truth is less pleasant. It is...was, the grave of Vighar, my own ancestor, dead for centuries now. He is also a vampire. My family is entrusted with keeping him buried, but recently someone stole the wardstone, and Vighar escaped.
Skyrim.esm|0B83BE: Destroy Vighar, my long dead vampire ancestor. Have you changed your mind?
Skyrim.esm|0B83BF: You were helpful before. I have need of your help again, if you're willing.
Skyrim.esm|0B85B1: I'm on a pilgrimage to the Shrine of Azura, the Goddess of Twilight. If you'll excuse me.
Skyrim.esm|0B85B2: I can't believe I just met the Gourmet!
Skyrim.esm|0B85B3: You know, I've heard the Gourmet is in Skyrim right now...
Skyrim.esm|0B85B5: It is the Champion of Namira! I was just about to eat. Here, you should have the first bite.
Skyrim.esm|0B85B6: In the mountains to the northeast. I'll mark it on your map.
Skyrim.esm|0B8617: I've heard of him. My father had suspicions about his connection to the Mythic Dawn. Guess they were true. I don't need this. My family wasted 8 generations keeping that Razor safe from a dead cult. As far as I care, it can stay locked in my house.
Skyrim.esm|0B8618: All at once! You're efficient. I like that. Here's your reward.
Skyrim.esm|0B8619: Best leave the past buried, I say.
Skyrim.esm|0B861A: Easy. Here. These are the keys. The hilt is in a chest in my house, just don't hurt anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0B861B: You want a fight? You got it.
Skyrim.esm|0B861C: Divines know the mill could use some new blades. All right. It's yours. Here's the keys. It's locked in a chest inside my house.
Skyrim.esm|0B861D: Why don't you take your coin and keep walking?
Skyrim.esm|0B861E: We just need the shards of the blade and the hilt, now.
Skyrim.esm|0B861F: We just need the pommel and the shards of the blade, now.
Skyrim.esm|0B8620: We just need the hilt and the pommel, now.
Skyrim.esm|0B8621: There's only the shards of the blade left to collect. Exciting, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0B8622: There's only the pommel left to collect. Exciting, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0B8623: There's only the hilt left to collect. Exciting, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0B8624: You want the hilt that bad? Fine. Here's the keys. It's locked in a chest inside my house.
Skyrim.esm|0B8625: I don't care, but my ancestors do. You can't have it.
Skyrim.esm|0B8626: Damn it. Why'd you have to walk into Morthal?
Skyrim.esm|0B8627: Fine. The hilt is in the chest inside my house. Here are the keys. Take that damn thing and go.
Skyrim.esm|0B862B: Guess I should have known. Maven and Sibbi have been at odds ever since she had him tossed into jail. Look, if I were you, I'd proceed carefully. Crossing Maven is one of the first things you learn not to do when you're down here. As far as the Guild's concerned this is between Sibbi and Maven, so we're going to officially stay out of it.
Skyrim.esm|0B862C: Who in their right mind would be stupid enough to pay you to steal from Maven?
Skyrim.esm|0B862D: I believe this gem is worth more than you are at the moment.
Skyrim.esm|0B8665: It was Atmah's idea to come here, at first. She talked me into it, and I convinced the others.
Skyrim.esm|0B8666: Savos is right. We can make it if we just stay alert.
Skyrim.esm|0B8667: We all knew this was the end. Without even opening the door, we knew what was behind it would kill us.
Skyrim.esm|0B8668: You keep talking like you're sure we'll find something useful in here.
Skyrim.esm|0B866D: Elvali died here. I don't even remember what killed her. One of the countless faceless horrors. I think she was glad, in that final moment.
Skyrim.esm|0B866E: Now you've put it all to rest, but it can't undo my mistakes. They can never be undone...
Skyrim.esm|0B866F: There were six of us. Full of ambition, eager to conquer the world.
Skyrim.esm|0B8670: We can't go back. Might as well go forward. We can still do this.
Skyrim.esm|0B8671: What happened after was my fault. All mine.
Skyrim.esm|0B8672: Can you imagine the looks on their faces when we come back?
Skyrim.esm|0B8673: We could have turned back. It could have ended there. But we kept going.
Skyrim.esm|0B8674: That's how I consoled myself for years, after I'd sealed this place shut and vowed never to let anyone open it.
Skyrim.esm|0B8675: But now it all comes out again...
Skyrim.esm|0B8677: I was in charge now. I pushed them on, insisting it would be worse to try and go back.
Skyrim.esm|0B8678: I can't believe we're doing this.
Skyrim.esm|0B8679: When it was over, Atmah, Hafnar, and I stared, pale-faced, at one another, unwilling to admit we'd made a terrible mistake.
Skyrim.esm|0B867A: If we'd all died there, if we'd loosed the thing on the world, who knows what might have happened?
Skyrim.esm|0B867B: I never meant for any of what happened here. Tried to seal it up, lock it away forever.
Skyrim.esm|0B867C: It's too late. There isn't enough of him left to go back in after.
Skyrim.esm|0B867D: Atmah cried to herself. Hafnar wouldn't look at either of us. And I kept telling them it would be all right.
Skyrim.esm|0B867E: Let's just get inside, see what's in there.
Skyrim.esm|0B867F: And then we were all fighting just to survive. None of us were prepared. It was amazing the rest of us survived.
Skyrim.esm|0B8680: It would seem I'm bound to this place. The bitter irony of it all -- my greatest failure, and even in death I can't escape it.
Skyrim.esm|0B8681: We barely made it out alive, and you want to go back in?
Skyrim.esm|0B8682: I've no idea what killed her, but I'm sure something did.
Skyrim.esm|0B8683: Don't forget, this whole idea was Atmah's to begin with.
Skyrim.esm|0B86B7: I had to leave them behind, had to sacrifice them so I could make it out alive.
Skyrim.esm|0B86C1: We... we have to go back. We can't leave Girduin...
Skyrim.esm|0B86CF: There were only three of us left. Takes-In-Light just sat down and gave up, and we left her there to die.
Skyrim.esm|0B86D0: You would care about that, since you're the Arch-Mage's favorite!
Skyrim.esm|0B86D1: One moment we joked about what we'd find below, the next he'd been ripped in half.
Skyrim.esm|0B86D2: I had no choice, don't you see?
Skyrim.esm|0B86DA: Against six College-trained mages? I think we'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|0B86DB: "If we can just make it through, it'll all be worth it", I told them. And the fools believed the words I myself didn't trust.
Skyrim.esm|0B86DC: We'll be back at the College before anyone even knows we're gone.
Skyrim.esm|0B86E9: Girduin died first. It happened so fast, none of us had a chance to react.
Skyrim.esm|0B86ED: "No matter what, we stay together", Hafnar said. I looked him in the eyes and lied to him.
Skyrim.esm|0B86F2: And what if... What if there are things guarding this place?
Skyrim.esm|0B86F5: Are we truly sure this is a good idea?
Skyrim.esm|0B86F6: We thought it would be so simple.
Skyrim.esm|0B86F7: Enchanted weapons, tomes of ancient knowledge, Shalidor's secrets themselves -- who knows what we could find!
Skyrim.esm|0B86F8: I... I don't know why I pressed the others on, convinced them to keep going.
Skyrim.esm|0B8714: Come on, we're finally here! Let's not waste any more time!
Skyrim.esm|0B8715: We were sure we'd find it all here, hidden away from time. Power, knowledge... All the things we didn't want to wait for.
Skyrim.esm|0B871B: None of our spells were potent enough, none of our wills were strong enough.
Skyrim.esm|0B871C: Given the history of this place, it's more than likely there's still some amount of power here.
Skyrim.esm|0B871E: Hafnar was covered in blood, but his stupid Nord pride wouldn't let him admit defeat.
Skyrim.esm|0B871F: Yes, well so do I. That's why I was hoping you could help.
Skyrim.esm|0B873C: Let him know that we've unearthed something... Well, I'm not sure. Something unique, let's say. It's clearly magical in nature, but like nothing I've ever seen before. He should be most interested.
Skyrim.esm|0B873D: I'm not really sure. Made and used by Magnus himself, if you believe those sorts of things. I believe I've heard it said that it's the only thing that could adequately contain his power. The sort of embellishment wizards of ancient times loved to make.
Skyrim.esm|0B873E: I warned them it might be dangerous. It's not my fault they didn't listen. As long as the Synod don't blame us for this mess, I have more pressing matters to deal with.
Skyrim.esm|0B873F: Not enough to bother with it. Arch-Mage Aren's approach to these things is just to let them sort themselves out. Although now it looks like you'll be doing the sorting. Good luck with that.
Skyrim.esm|0B8740: Oh, I think I'll be fine. We seem to have eliminated the most pressing threat. It certainly seems that whoever placed this here intended for it to be well guarded. I wonder why...
Skyrim.esm|0B8752: Your father's too easy on you, boy. I'd have you cutting wood at dawn if you were my son.
Skyrim.esm|0B8753: As do I. That man saved my life. I hope that someone helps him, as he once helped me.
Skyrim.esm|0B8754: And how is that old warrior doing these days?
Skyrim.esm|0B8755: Papa says I'm not old enough yet, so I help out by patrolling the town with Lucky here.
Skyrim.esm|0B8756: Oh dear. I wish there was something we could do to help him.
Skyrim.esm|0B8757: Father, how come Olda's so mean to everyone? She's only ever happy when she's with Lodvar, and she's not even married to him!
Skyrim.esm|0B8758: Yes, just this morning. It was from Logrolf. He wrote to thank us for the food we sent.
Skyrim.esm|0B875B: Haven't you got some chores to do?
Skyrim.esm|0B875C: Not well. He's convinced that his house is haunted, so he's sleeping on the street.
Skyrim.esm|0B875D: Did you read that letter from Markarth yet?
Skyrim.esm|0B875E: That's no business of yours, Son. Mind you keep your nose out of it.
Skyrim.esm|0B876C: Go ahead, try and fight back!
Skyrim.esm|0B8770: The elements are mine to command!
Skyrim.esm|0B8810: Exactly! Now you're beginning to see how our little system works. Maven sent word that you discovered something else while you were out there. Something important to the Guild?
Skyrim.esm|0B8811: Come on, everyone knows about you and Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|0B8812: Well, if you like I'd be happy to take over the operation for you. Take the source off your hands.
Skyrim.esm|0B8813: Well, everyone's wrong. Now why don't you just shut up about it.
Skyrim.esm|0B8814: And lose out just when the going is getting good? No thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0B8815: Hey Ton, you shouldn't cheat on Vekel. He's a good guy.
Skyrim.esm|0B8816: The only thing keeping me from having you killed is Brynjolf. He's stepped in and made amends. At least you remembered to bring me the information I requested. Give me that. This doesn't tell me much. The only thing that could identify Sabjorn's partner is this odd little symbol.
Skyrim.esm|0B8817: Yes, I'm going with you and together we're going to kill her. Here's your payment for Solitude. Prepare yourself and meet me at the ruins as soon as you can. We can't let her slip through our fingers.
Skyrim.esm|0B8818: I don't know where you find your sources, but that last shipment of skooma was some of the best quality I've seen in years.
Skyrim.esm|0B8819: This doesn't tell me much. The only thing that could identify Sabjorn's partner is this odd little symbol.
Skyrim.esm|0B881C: Because, even after all their posturing and planning, they've made a mistake. The parchment you recovered mentions a "Gajul-Lei." According to my sources, that's an old alias used by one of our contacts. His real name is Gulum-Ei. Slimy bastard.
Skyrim.esm|0B881D: Ah, just got lucky is all. Bumped into someone who knew someone else. The rest just writes itself.
Skyrim.esm|0B881E: I thought it was painfully obvious that Gulum-Ei was important to the Guild. I'm sorry to say your payment for the job will have to go to elsewhere since you feel like taking things in your own direction. Now, did Gulum-Ei give up any information on our buyer?
Skyrim.esm|0B881F: Welcome to the cozy little family. I'm one of the lookouts for the Guild. I watch Delvin's back.
Skyrim.esm|0B8820: If you're looking for extra work, talk to Vex or Delvin. They'll fix you up.
Skyrim.esm|0B8821: Stick to the rules and you'll end up rich. Break them and you're out on your arse. Simple, right?
Skyrim.esm|0B8822: If you're looking for conversation, The Flagon isn't the place.
Skyrim.esm|0B8823: Vekel and me, we have a thing going. So I recommend you keep your hands off the merchandise.
Skyrim.esm|0B8824: Vekel and me, we have a thing going. Try anything with him and it'd be bad for your health.
Skyrim.esm|0B8825: You don't impress me. So don't even try.
Skyrim.esm|0B8826: I warned you not to cross Maven. If I hadn't stepped in and paid amends, you'd be... well, I think you know. At least Sabjorn has found himself in Whiterun's prison. How unfortunate for him.
Skyrim.esm|0B89DE: Hear my Thu'um! Feel it in your bones. Match it, if you are Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|0B89E0: A gift, Dovahkiin. Yol. Understand Fire as the dov do.
Skyrim.esm|0B89E1: A gift, Dovahkiin. Toor. Understand Inferno as the dov do.
Skyrim.esm|0B89E4: A gift, Dovahkiin. Shul. Understand Sun as the dov do.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A04: You remind me of my cousin's cat. Killed that too!
Skyrim.esm|0B8A8A: In their tongue, he is Dovahkiin.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A8B: But no one wanted to believe. Believe they even existed.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A8C: Their defeat was merely a delay.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A8E: And when the truth finally dawns, it dawns in fire.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A8F: To the time after Oblivion opened.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A90: You should have acted. Theyâ€™re already here.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A93: When the sons of Skyrim would spill their own blood.
Skyrim.esm|0B8A94: The Elder Scrolls told of their return.
Skyrim.esm|0B8C95: Hmm... looks like it's all here. Okay, go on through. But stick to the road, and don't make any sudden moves.
Skyrim.esm|0B8C96: All right, that's far enough. You know how this works - toll here is 100 gold.
Skyrim.esm|0B8C97: Don't play dumb with me. Pay up, and you walk right through. Refuse, and you'll never see the other side.
Skyrim.esm|0B8C9C: Not my problem. You want through here, you pay. Or else.
Skyrim.esm|0B8C9D: I may have lost a drop or two of blood. Truth is, I'm not going anywhere like this. Hurts to breathe.
Skyrim.esm|0B8C9E: In these parts? Good luck. But don't come this way again.
Skyrim.esm|0B8CF2: These two aren't a problem anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0B8D0D: The Silver Hand. They finally had the nerve to attack Jorrvaskr. We got most of them, but I think a few stragglers made it out.
Skyrim.esm|0B8D10: Don't leave any of them alive.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EB8: I am a loyal citizen of the Empire. My parents were Legionnaires, and I've followed in their footsteps. But more importantly, I am a daughter of Skyrim and wish to see her made whole again.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EBA: I've been a daughter of Skyrim all my life. I love this land and her people. So do all the Nords that serve the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EBB: I'm a soldier, not a politician. And my personal beliefs are not yours to question.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EBC: I oppose tyranny. I oppose those who tell me how to live, what to think, and what to believe. I'm a man. Skyrim is man's homeland. That is a fact. A fact I'm proud of. There is no shame in that. Read your history.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EBD: If that's all, there's pressing concerns that need dealing with.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EBE: Not this Empire. The world's better without it. Certainly Skyrim is. I fought in the Imperial army in the War against the Dominion. I bled and spilt blood for the Empire. And for what? The Empire to bend its knee before those evil elf bastards? Signing a treaty meant to kill the heart of the Empire itself. To deny Talos? No. I will have no part of any such Empire. And I will gladly gut anyone who says otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EBF: Enough talk. There's much to do.
Skyrim.esm|0B8EC0: Reasons? Since when does a man need a reason to protect his family, to defend his homeland? It's the damn outlanders and Empire that need the reasons.
Skyrim.esm|0B90D8: No, how did you find me? Just give me more time, I... I can pay Maven back!
Skyrim.esm|0B9104: Indeed. If we ever get there.
Skyrim.esm|0B9106: Don't bother me, peasant. I've had a long journey and paid too much coin on these gifts to be late to Vittoria's wedding.
Skyrim.esm|0B9115: Hello there, fellow traveler. One itinerant minstrel and wandering wastrel, at your service.
Skyrim.esm|0B9126: Hello there, friend. How lucky for you to chance upon a bard on the road.
Skyrim.esm|0B9127: Ahh. Smell that fresh air? Truly, this is a good place to play a song.
Skyrim.esm|0B9128: The life of adventure and song is the life for us, eh, friend?
Skyrim.esm|0B9130: For a generous fellow traveler? But of course. What would you like to hear?
Skyrim.esm|0B9131: Sorry friend, but it looks like you're a bit light in the purse, and the wayward minstrel can't afford to do free performances.
Skyrim.esm|0B9147: I've got a message for you from Louis Letrush. This is for double-crossing him.
Skyrim.esm|0B9156: This has nothing to do with you, so back off.
Skyrim.esm|0B9166: Ugh. Speak to my husband, if you must.
Skyrim.esm|0B9175: Get away from me, you Skyrim trash.
Skyrim.esm|0B9179: You! You must have told Maven about the horse. If you'd just stuck to the plan, I wouldn't be in this mess.
Skyrim.esm|0B921B: Listen, wench, we're on to you. We know who you are, so you'd better come with us right now!
Skyrim.esm|0B9223: We've made a mistake. Now move along before that mouth of yours gets you into trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0B9225: They thought I was some other Redguard woman! They just wouldn't leave me alone. I hope they get lost in the wilderness.
Skyrim.esm|0B9243: I'm not going anywhere. Now back off before I do something drastic.
Skyrim.esm|0B9246: Brother, I think she's telling the truth...
Skyrim.esm|0B924E: I don't have time for this foolishness. Now come along quietly and we won't have to get rough.
Skyrim.esm|0B924F: Next time you're looking to pester someone, make sure it's the right person!
Skyrim.esm|0B925C: I don't know who you think I am, and I don't care. Leave me alone!
Skyrim.esm|0B928F: No Firebrand Wine yet? How disappointing.
Skyrim.esm|0B94B1: Krosis. Sorrowfully, no. It cannot be known to me.
Skyrim.esm|0B94B7: Your kind - joorre - mortals - created it as a weapon against the dov... the dragons. Our hadrimme, our minds cannot even... comprehend its concepts.
Skyrim.esm|0B94C5: Alduin komeyt tiid. What else would you seek? Alduin and Dovahkiin return together. But, I do not know the Thu'um you seek. Krosis. It cannot be known to me.
Skyrim.esm|0B94C6: Hmm. Yes. They are very protective of me. Bahlaan fahdonne. But I do not know the Thu'um you seek. Krosis. It cannot be known to me.
Skyrim.esm|0B94C8: Not hate. Distrust and fear. The Blades have always sought to turn the Dragonborn away from the path of wisdom.
Skyrim.esm|0B9647: Leave me to my work. Unless you plan to stay and help?
Skyrim.esm|0B96C4: Thank you again for all the help.
Skyrim.esm|0B96C6: Sometimes I just like to watch them burn.
Skyrim.esm|0B994A: Ari gave me this dagger when we first started hunting together. Always said it brought her luck. You should be the one to carry it now.
Skyrim.esm|0B9963: I'm thinking of going for a walk up the mountain later. Would you like to come?
Skyrim.esm|0B9964: Of course, Julienne. What's on your mind?
Skyrim.esm|0B9965: I won't forget. I'll do it after I get the dishes cleaned.
Skyrim.esm|0B9966: Yes, I think I'd enjoy a woman like your wife.
Skyrim.esm|0B9967: Faida came by today. She says you left the tavern last night without paying for your drinks.
Skyrim.esm|0B9968: Oh, that sounds nice. Yes, I think I'll join you.
Skyrim.esm|0B9969: Miss Faida, may I ask you a question?
Skyrim.esm|0B996A: Mind you don't forget to sweep up, Julienne. The floor's looking a bit dusty.
Skyrim.esm|0B996B: One of these days, we'll have to find you a good woman like my Olda.
Skyrim.esm|0B996C: Since my customers are mostly local, I just knock on their front doors the next day.
Skyrim.esm|0B996D: I must have forgotten. I'll take care of it tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0B996F: I'll bring a blanket and some wine.
Skyrim.esm|0B9970: Yes, it's a great shame. Gods willing, I'll find someone just like her one day.
Skyrim.esm|0B9971: See that you do. You're an embarrassment, Horgeir. I don't know why I married you.
Skyrim.esm|0B9972: What do you do when a customer can't afford to pay for a meal or a drink?
Skyrim.esm|0B9973: You've been working hard lately. You'll make a fine innkeeper someday.
Skyrim.esm|0B9974: Well then, it's too bad my wife is already taken.
Skyrim.esm|0B9BE1: You're interfering with official Thalmor business.
Skyrim.esm|0B9BE8: This is none of your concern, citizen.
Skyrim.esm|0B9BF1: You leave me no choice, citizen.
Skyrim.esm|0B9BF5: Are you looking for a fight?
Skyrim.esm|0B9BF6: Don't make me tell you again. Get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0B9BF7: Fine. Have it you way. A fight it is.
Skyrim.esm|0B9BF8: Alright, if it's a fight you want. You've got it.
Skyrim.esm|0B9CF9: M'aiq's father was also called M'aiq. As was M'aiq's father's father. At least, that is what his father said.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D02: M'aiq knows much, and tells some. M'aiq knows many things others do not.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D07: M'aiq carries two weapons, to be safe. What if one breaks? That would be most unlucky.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D09: M'aiq is always in search of calipers, yet he finds none. Where could they have gone?
Skyrim.esm|0B9D3F: M'aiq hears many stories of war... yet few of them are true.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D56: How does anyone know there was a city of Winterhold? M'aiq did not see it with his eyes. Did you?
Skyrim.esm|0B9D5A: Too much magic can be dangerous. M'aiq once had two spells and burned his sweetroll.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D5B: What does this mean, to combine magic? Magic plus magic is still magic.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D5C: It does not matter to M'aiq how strong or smart one is. It only matters what one can do.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D5F: Dragons were never gone. They were just invisible and very, very quiet.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D61: Werebears? Where? Bears? Men that are bears?
Skyrim.esm|0B9D62: Much snow in Skyrim. Enough snow. M'aiq does not want any more.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D65: Snow falls. Why worry where it goes? M'aiq thinks the snowflakes are pretty.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D68: Skyrim was once the land of many butterflies. Now, not so much.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D6C: M'aiq once walked to High Hrothgar. So many steps, he lost count.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D76: Once M'aiq got in trouble in Riften, and fled to Windhelm. It is good that nobody there cared.
Skyrim.esm|0B9D79: M'aiq can travel fast across the land. Some lazy types take carriages. It is all the same to M'aiq.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DA9: M'aiq does not understand what is so impressive about shouting. M'aiq can shout whenever he wants.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DD0: Your own fate will be your own choice. Just as always. Now be gone.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DD1: This is not about your desires, lass.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DD2: M'aiq saw a mudcrab the other day. Horrible creatures.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DD3: M'aiq loves the people of Skyrim. Many interesting things they say to each other.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DD4: Nords are so serious about beards. So many beards. M'aiq thinks they wish they had glorious manes like Khajiit.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DD9: M'aiq does not remember his childhood. Perhaps he never had one.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DDE: M'aiq is very practical. He has no need for mysticism.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DE0: Nords' armor has lots of fur. This sometimes makes M'aiq nervous.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DE4: M'aiq was soul trapped once. Not very pleasant. You should think about that once in a while.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DE7: Some say Alduin is Akatosh. Some say M'aiq is a Liar. Don't you believe either of those things.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DEF: Something strange happens to Khajiit when they arrive in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DF0: M'aiq has heard the people of Skyrim are better looking than the ones in Cyrodiil. He has no opinion on the matter. All people are beautiful to him.
Skyrim.esm|0B9DFA: Why do soldiers bother with target practice? One learns best by hitting real people.
Skyrim.esm|0BA03D: I have some preparations of my own to make. I'll meet you at the stone.
Skyrim.esm|0BA03E: I promise you, all your questions will be answered. You just have to trust me.
Skyrim.esm|0BA05E: M'aiq knows why Falmer are blind. It has nothing to do with the Dwemer disappearing. Really.
Skyrim.esm|0BA060: M'aiq has heard it is dangerous to be your friend.
Skyrim.esm|0BA084: The people of Skyrim are more open-minded about certain things than people in other places.
Skyrim.esm|0BA08F: Some like taking friends on adventures. M'aiq thinks being alone is better. Less arguing about splitting treasure.
Skyrim.esm|0BA092: Don't try blocking if you have two weapons. You will only get confused.  Much better to hit twice anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0BA095: M'aiq is tired now. Go bother somebody else.
Skyrim.esm|0BA0D9: Before you go, mortal. One final challenge. Ha ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|0BA0DA: You don't want to be part of history? Fine. I'll be at the shrine if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0BA0DB: You think to betray Mehrunes Dagon? Suffer!
Skyrim.esm|0BA0DC: Ever since I was a boy, I felt this strange sense of destiny surrounding the Mythic Dawn cult, and now I know what it is. Don't you see? Fate has led you to me and to the pieces. Dagon has to answer our call. We're so close. I'll meet you at the shrine.
Skyrim.esm|0BA11A: It's not working. Why don't you give it a try? Just put your hands on the altar.
Skyrim.esm|0BA11B: Mehrunes Dagon, the Lord of Change, we have brought your Razor to you. We beg you, please bring the blade's full glory to Tamriel again!
Skyrim.esm|0BA11C: The museum is doing well. I haven't told anyone about the... incident with the Razor.
Skyrim.esm|0BA11D: Good to see you in my museum again. I owe you my life.
Skyrim.esm|0BA11F: I never thought all I would want is peace and quiet, but I think our little adventure scared me straight.
Skyrim.esm|0BA120: Thank you. Here's your gold. I'll make a run for it back to Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0BA121: Fine. I'm not dying without a fight!
Skyrim.esm|0BA122: I keep gazing into the pommel gem. Not a single flaw.
Skyrim.esm|0BA129: I've had to be careful handling those blade shards you found. They're each sharp enough to sever a finger with no effort.
Skyrim.esm|0BA12A: It's strange holding a hilt that isn't connected to a dagger. Oh well.
Skyrim.esm|0BA12B: Wait. Wait. Don't kill me. There's another way. I can take the pieces back to my museum. Seal them in a display case. You get a generous amount of gold, I get to complete my collection, and nobody has to die.
Skyrim.esm|0BA12C: Good. You're here. I'll place the pieces on the altar, and Dagon should speak to us.
Skyrim.esm|0BA12D: I'll meet you at the shrine.
Skyrim.esm|0BA12E: Let me put the pieces on the altar. We'll see if Mehrunes Dagon can still be reached.
Skyrim.esm|0BA12F: Try putting your hands on the altar. Maybe Mehrunes Dagon will speak to you.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1D5: Ah. Well. After a little incident with a roguish lad and the daughter of a prominent Thane... Well, let's just say, best not.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1D6: Afraid not. But if you're serious about sharpening that tongue, you might try the Bard's College in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1D7: You have it! The blessed Wabbajack! You must use it on me. You must.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1D8: You're the one who returned blessed Sheogorath. I would be honored to be Wabbajacked by you, your grace.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1DA: Don't go to the Blue Palace. The master is on vacation. Don't listen to Dervenin. Don't do it.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1DB: The master has gone, but Dervenin will find him. Dervenin should not have gone.
Skyrim.esm|0BA1DC: Vacation is where the head is. That is what Dervenin told us about our master. He's cozy in his Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BA24C: If you wish to learn more about them, you're welcome to my tome on the subject. Should be on my table.
Skyrim.esm|0BA268: In the time before man, they were known as the Snow Elves. They lived in the sunlight and had a very prosperous society.
Skyrim.esm|0BA27E: Good. I lost more than a few acquaintances to the Falmer and I wish to see them avenged.
Skyrim.esm|0BA288: Why shouldn't I? The Falmer have killed more than a fair share of my acquaintances. They're animals... they show absolutely no pity or remorse.
Skyrim.esm|0BA28A: I've already provided you a key to the museum. You should consider it an honor. Few are allowed to browse my collection. I'm sorry, but that's as far as my generosity extends.
Skyrim.esm|0BA28E: Yes, I suppose you're right. Forgive me. I've lost several close friends to the Falmer and it tends to distort my view.
Skyrim.esm|0BA292: The Nords went to war with the Falmer in the First Era. Killed them by the thousands to drive them from their snowy homeland. The Falmer retreated underground and forged an uneasy alliance with the dwarves who ended up betraying them. This betrayal made them what they are now... horrible blind monstrosities with a burning hatred of any but their own kind.
Skyrim.esm|0BA294: Yes, I would say their culture quite possibly rivaled our own.
Skyrim.esm|0BA2FB: Don't look at me. I didn't kill her. I was just... making sure the body got buried.
Skyrim.esm|0BA2FD: You will pay for Malyn's death, Azura worshipper!
Skyrim.esm|0BA305: I found them near the old Dwemer bridge in the far west. I'll mark it down for you on your map for a small fee.
Skyrim.esm|0BA306: Hey there! Check out what my friend and I found. I'll sell some of it to you, if you want!
Skyrim.esm|0BA307: Thank you! You'll find the ruins right here.
Skyrim.esm|0BA30D: Why? Why do you pursue me?
Skyrim.esm|0BA516: As the sun ends the night, end the darkness of this soul, return life to the creature you see before you!
Skyrim.esm|0BA534: There will be a feast in our honor when we return.
Skyrim.esm|0BA535: It will be a good day. I can feel it in my gut.
Skyrim.esm|0BA536: The scent of victory is on the wind.
Skyrim.esm|0BA55B: Come back if you need a new ring or necklace.
Skyrim.esm|0BA55C: Hey, stop by my stall in the market sometime.
Skyrim.esm|0BA55F: Come back when you need more meat. Bring gold.
Skyrim.esm|0BA560: Need to get back to chopping.
Skyrim.esm|0BA561: Stay out of trouble in my city.
Skyrim.esm|0BA562: Stay clear of the Forsworn. Bloodthirsty savages.
Skyrim.esm|0BA563: I should get back to work. Money doesn't count itself.
Skyrim.esm|0BA564: Time to prepare some more records on the silver trade.
Skyrim.esm|0BA567: The blood of Markarth is silver.
Skyrim.esm|0BA569: Yes, I was bored talking to you anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0BA56D: I have records I need to tend to.
Skyrim.esm|0BA571: Come back whenever you need something.
Skyrim.esm|0BA572: If you need anything else, just ask.
Skyrim.esm|0BA573: I better get back to sorting the dry goods.
Skyrim.esm|0BA574: Now where did I put that shipment of forks...
Skyrim.esm|0BA575: I think I'm going to get another drink.
Skyrim.esm|0BA577: Remember. Ale is cheaper than blood in Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0BA578: Guards are free to search your room. Uh, just keep that in mind.
Skyrim.esm|0BA579: I should remind Kleppr to clean this place up.
Skyrim.esm|0BA57D: May the crags fail to trip your feet.
Skyrim.esm|0BA580: Live a life worth telling about.
Skyrim.esm|0BA581: I have a prison to run.
Skyrim.esm|0BA585: Now you let me know if you need a cure... or anything else.
Skyrim.esm|0BA587: I should get back to memorizing recipes.
Skyrim.esm|0BA588: Now, is lavender an herb or a poison? Hmm...
Skyrim.esm|0BA58A: Tell everyone you see to bring me some septims.
Skyrim.esm|0BA58B: If you'll excuse me, I have a city to run.
Skyrim.esm|0BA58D: Keep your eyes open for Forsworn when you're on the roads.
Skyrim.esm|0BA58E: Let me know if you need something forged.
Skyrim.esm|0BA590: I should heat up the forge. Why does it have to be so hot?
Skyrim.esm|0BA593: Yes, yes. I have a lot of research to do.
Skyrim.esm|0BA595: Goodbye. Be careful if you head into the ruins.
Skyrim.esm|0BA598: How can Mercer open up a vault that needs two keys? It's impossible. Could he pick his way in?
Skyrim.esm|0BA59C: Stop yammering and keep your eyes peeled. They could be here at any moment. If I were them I'd come straight here, sword swingin'!
Skyrim.esm|0BA59D: Can you believe one person came in here and took out this whole camp?
Skyrim.esm|0BABE2: Gerdur's family first settled here as woodcutters a few generations ago. She and Hod run the mill. I make a decent living sharpening axes and fixing the sawmill.
Skyrim.esm|0BB368: Looks like we've got ourselves another fool.
Skyrim.esm|0BB36A: Please help. I can pay you.
Skyrim.esm|0BB962: I think I have a few old spell books laying about. If you want more, you'll have to go to the College of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0BB963: And I've got better things to do than listen to more of your fantasies.
Skyrim.esm|0BB964: At the Bards College in Solitude. They train bards and skalds like myself. If you go there, talk to Viarmo. He's the headmaster.
Skyrim.esm|0BB965: There's the one about Tiber Septim and the dragon Nafaalilargus. I never bothered to learn it. The Blades killed them all centuries ago. Nobody asks to hear that lay anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA8C: Hmm... we've heard whispers of an enchanted pair of gauntlets, hidden and protected by dangerous men, beasts, or worse. They are called the Forgemaster's Fingers. Bring them to our chief, and he will decide if you're worthy to be Blood-Kin to the Orcs.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA8D: You do not remember the Daedric Lord of the Orcs? The keeper of the Sworn Oath and the Bloody Curse? We live by his code and follow his teachings. Spend some time with the wise woman and you'll remember.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA8E: There are a few great strongholds worthy to talk about. Dushnikh Yal, Mor Khazgur, Narzulbur, and Largashbur.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA8F: This is our stronghold. Here, we are free to live as Malacath intended, away from the prying eyes of the Nords. You must have been away from your kind for a long time to forget. Come, speak to the chief and the wise woman and remember.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA90: Halt, outlander. By the Code of Malacath, this stronghold belongs to the Orcs. Your kind are unwelcome.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA91: We admire your fighting spirit, Orc-Kin. You'll fit right in here. Come inside.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA92: Outlanders should mind their own business.
Skyrim.esm|0BBA95: This stronghold is for the Orcs. Leave. Now.
Skyrim.esm|0BBB2A: He is the Daedric Lord of the Orcs. The keeper of the Sworn Oath and the Bloody Curse. We live by his code and follow his teachings. An outlander like you wouldn't understand.
Skyrim.esm|0BBB2B: Welcome, child of Malacath. Your kin bid you welcome to our stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|0BBB2C: We won't kill you, outlander, but we don't want you here. Remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0BBB2D: This is our stronghold. Here, we are free to live as Malacath intended, away from the prying eyes of the Nords. You're not an Orc, or Blood-Kin, so stay out. We only help our own.
Skyrim.esm|0BBC8A: I don't think going back is a good idea.
Skyrim.esm|0BBC8C: You're right. This is all my fault. Should we turn around, head back?
Skyrim.esm|0BBC8E: This is insanity. We never should've come here.
Skyrim.esm|0BBC8F: We stay together, no matter what. Agreed?
Skyrim.esm|0BBC90: Dead. Something grabbed her from behind. Gone before I could do anything.
Skyrim.esm|0BBC91: We're not going to make it, are we?
Skyrim.esm|0BBC92: Where's Elvali? She was right behind me.
Skyrim.esm|0BBC93: This is it, you know. Through this door. Can you feel it?
Skyrim.esm|0BBC94: Come on, we can't stop now. We have to keep moving!
Skyrim.esm|0BBC95: What else could we do? Stay there and die with her? She refused to go on, we didn't have a choice!
Skyrim.esm|0BBC97: I promise you, I'll never let this happen again! I'll seal this whole place away...
Skyrim.esm|0BBC98: We shouldn't have left her there to die!
Skyrim.esm|0BBC99: Come on, you can make it. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|0BBC9A: I had no choice! It was the only way to make sure that monster never escaped!
Skyrim.esm|0BBC9B: Going back would be the end of all of us. We keep pushing forward, and we'll make it. We will!
Skyrim.esm|0BBCAA: Very well. Perhaps when my research is complete, I'll feel more comfortable discussing my findings with you.
Skyrim.esm|0BBCAB: What kind of a mentor would I be if I denied a potential student a glimpse at his master's ingenuity! Here, this key will provide access to my museum. Feel free to browse for as long as you wish. However, I must insist that my laboratory remains strictly off limits.
Skyrim.esm|0BBCAD: I hope you enjoy touring the museum. Just don't touch anything.
Skyrim.esm|0BBCAE: Now, stand aside. There's work to be done.
Skyrim.esm|0BBCAF: Try not to disturb me in the future. I'm in the midst of groundbreaking research!
Skyrim.esm|0BBCB3: Thanks for the help. The Reach has been crawling with Forsworn lately. It's a wonder people can get anywhere around here. Maybe I'll avoid this entire hold for a while. That said... I do have a few things to sell.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD91: Well... all right. Just because we are friends. Give me, say... 750 gold and we can call it even.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD92: Nice try, but those bandits almost killed me. Now pay up.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD93: See you, friend. And take it easy on the wine.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD94: Hey! There you are, you drunken bastard. I did it, so pay up.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD95: Whoa, not so fast there, friend. You owe me money.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD96: You and Sam dared me to go into that bandit camp. Well I did and brought back his stupid hat like you said, so give me the 10,000 gold you promised.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD97: We agreed to 750 gold. I want my money.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD98: So you're telling me you and Sam just sent me in there to die, then? I want my money, you bastard.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD99: Thanks. You and Sam drink too hard for me. Think I'll take my gold and go buy a nice bit of swamp in Black Marsh.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD9A: Thanks. You and Sam drink too hard for me. Think I'll take my gold and go buy something for this headache.
Skyrim.esm|0BBD9B: We caroused together with Sam Guevenne, remember? We broke into the Temple of Dibella? How much did you end up drinking?
Skyrim.esm|0BBD9C: Hey, where do you think you're going? Come back here.
Skyrim.esm|0BBE04: Thank Her Benevolence it wasn't burned like the other tomes.
Skyrim.esm|0BBE05: Well done. I wasn't certain you'd even know what to look for.
Skyrim.esm|0BBE13: Back from whence you came, heathen!
Skyrim.esm|0BBE19: Rest well and sleep deeply, my brother.
Skyrim.esm|0BBE1F: Lady Mara... I await your embrace...
Skyrim.esm|0BBE20: No... not when we're so close...
Skyrim.esm|0BBE2E: Can't fail now... must hang on...
Skyrim.esm|0BBE30: How dare you defile this temple, Orc!
Skyrim.esm|0BBE5C: By Her Benevolence, you shall not stop us!
Skyrim.esm|0BBE69: Fine, I'll show you, you big bully.
Skyrim.esm|0BBEA7: Trust your instincts, Dovahkiin. Your blood will show you the way.
Skyrim.esm|0BBEA8: Not intentionally. Some hoped he would be gone forever, forever lost. Meyye. I knew better. Tiid bo amativ. Time flows ever onward. One day he would surface. Which is why I have lived here. For thousands of mortal years I have waited. I knew where he would emerge but not when.
Skyrim.esm|0BBEAA: Hmm. How to explain in your tongue? The dov have words for such things that joorre do not. It is an... artifact from outside time. It does not exist, but it has always existed. Rah wahlaan. They are... hmm... fragments of creation. The Kelle... Elder Scrolls, as you name them, they have often been used for prophecy. Yes, your prophecy comes from an Elder Scroll. But this is only a small part of their power. Zofaas suleyk.
Skyrim.esm|0BBEAB: Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as elf, but as dovah!
Skyrim.esm|0BBEAC: Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Orc, but as dovah!
Skyrim.esm|0BBEAD: Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Khajiit, but as dovah!
Skyrim.esm|0BBEAE: Now, show me what you can do. Greet me not as Argonian, but as dovah!
Skyrim.esm|0BC045: Just a pilgrim. I'd prefer to leave it at that, if you don't mind.
Skyrim.esm|0BC046: Walking the Steps. Meditating on the emblems. I make this trip every few years.
Skyrim.esm|0BC047: I'm walking the 7,000 Steps to honor the Greybeards and reflect on my own life. It's a tradition among the Nords of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BC049: I like to spend time up here. Walk the Steps, meditate on the emblems. Doesn't hurt when I bag some game along the way.
Skyrim.esm|0BC04A: Enjoying the hospitalities of the inn. Suggest you do the same.
Skyrim.esm|0BC04B: They're not the sort to take visitors, but I never go that high up the path anyway. Some folk who make the trip leave them food or other essentials, but not to make conversation.
Skyrim.esm|0BC04C: I was just outside Ivarstead when it happened. It's an exciting moment. Nothing like this has happened in centuries.
Skyrim.esm|0BC04D: I did. Strange days when the monks will do that. I wonder what it means.
Skyrim.esm|0BC04E: Keep an eye out for wolves if you're headed up the path to High Hrothgar.
Skyrim.esm|0BC092: All right. I wrote down the last place I'd heard about. Don't get killed.
Skyrim.esm|0BC093: There are more books to be recovered and secured in the Arcanaeum.
Skyrim.esm|0BC094: Looks like you're still in one piece. And more importantly, so is the book. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0AF: I just try to keep out of the way when they start roughhousing.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B0: Don't mind them. Just working out some issues, in the old way.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B1: Little blood to clear the air. Hope you have the stomach for it, outsider.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B4: Not now, I'm trying to watch this!
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B5: Pay attention, you could learn something.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B6: Are you going to let me watch, or not?
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B7: Look at the young ones scrap!
Skyrim.esm|0BC0B8: Twelve septims on the girl! Look at that speed.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0BA: Could you move? You're in the way.
Skyrim.esm|0BC0BB: Hope they don't get carried away here.
Skyrim.esm|0BC175: Is it that obvious? I should take pains to seem more happy. I wouldn't want to upset our host. But the truth of it is, I have a mountain of paperwork back in Windhelm and it's not getting any smaller. I just hope this party doesn't drag on all night.
Skyrim.esm|0BC178: Well enough, I suppose. Elenwen puts a great deal of effort into these parties, and it shows. I just wonder what her real motivations are. That woman is inscrutable.
Skyrim.esm|0BC179: I beg your pardon? You must have me mistaken for someone else. I am Elisif, widow of High King Torygg and Jarl of Solitude. Lucky for you, I possess a forgiving nature and I'm not easily insulted.
Skyrim.esm|0BC17C: Ill luck. I'm a busy man and I don't have time for this sort of frivolity. But at the same time, I don't dare refuse an invitation from Elenwen.
Skyrim.esm|0BC17D: We have a relationship of mutual respect. I care little about the politics and rivalries between the Thalmor and the Empire. But I do respect power, and the Thalmor have that. They leave me alone, and I leave them alone.
Skyrim.esm|0BC17E: By the gods, let this mine be small so we can be done with it quickly.
Skyrim.esm|0BC17F: Very much so, yes. Elenwen's parties are always so elegant. She really has impeccable taste. My only regret is that she doesn't host them more often.
Skyrim.esm|0BC180: Elenwen is a formidable woman. She lacks for neither cunning nor ambition. Now that I think of it, she would have made a fine Imperial general.
Skyrim.esm|0BC181: A delicate question, to be sure. I'm the Emperor's cousin, and the Empire has little love for the Thalmor. On the other hand, the Thalmor have considerable funds and are looking to expand their shipping. I haven't signed any deals yet, but I'm sure my cousin would want a cut if I do. What better way to soothe his wounded pride?
Skyrim.esm|0BC182: It is not eyes that reveal another's true nature. It is the heart.
Skyrim.esm|0BC183: If your eyes and your ears are open and your mind is free of judgement and expectation, you might learn much in this chamber.
Skyrim.esm|0BC184: There are words spoken, and words unspoken. Beware these Thalmor, for they are adept in both languages.
Skyrim.esm|0BC185: Now here is a face I did not expect to see. Be mindful, for you walk among adders.
Skyrim.esm|0BC186: That's putting it mildly. The Thalmor invite me here to remind everyone that they can tell the Empire what to do. I would refuse, but I don't want to jeopardize the peace between us.
Skyrim.esm|0BC187: I am not. I have no more taste for Thalmor wine than I do for Thalmor company. What is more, I'm away from the hold at a time when there are dragons and Stormcloaks on the loose. There's every chance I'll return to find a pile of smoking ash where Whiterun once stood.
Skyrim.esm|0BC188: I trust them completely, because I understand them. When the Thalmor want something, they tell you straight. In my experience, they have no interest in subtleties and innuendo. So if one of them says they're going to do something, I believe them. When you can just take what you want, there's no need to lie.
Skyrim.esm|0BC189: Yes, I know what you mean. Most Nords struggle to accept change. Those who fought the Thalmor in the Great War still see an enemy. But that war is over, and a new day has dawned. Besides, the Thalmor reward their friends richly. Anyone who doesn't take advantage of that is a stubborn fool.
Skyrim.esm|0BC18A: Not well at all, and I'm happy to keep it that way. That woman and her Thalmor Justiciars have caused nothing but problems for the people of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BC18B: A jarl has many duties. Attending parties isn't the most important one, but neither is it unpleasant.
Skyrim.esm|0BC18C: Take advantage of the fine food and drink. Such things have been scarce since the war began.
Skyrim.esm|0BC18D: I came here hoping to learn that more Imperial reinforcements were on their way to Skyrim, but so far I've been disappointed.
Skyrim.esm|0BC18F: I admit, I've never felt entirely at ease in the Thalmor Embassy.
Skyrim.esm|0BC190: There is peace now, and that peace will continue for as long as it suits our needs. But make no mistake, this is not a peace forged out of necessity between rival nations of equal strength. It is more like the calm between storms. And the next storm, I think, will be far deadlier than the last.
Skyrim.esm|0BC191: As much as can be expected, yes.
Skyrim.esm|0BC195: Very much so, and thank you for asking.
Skyrim.esm|0BC196: Very much so, and thank you for asking.
Skyrim.esm|0BC197: I've spent most of my life as a trader. I've got a stake in several shipping ventures across Tamriel, mostly imports and exports. I made a lot of friends in a lot of ports, and the Thalmor approach me with a generous offer. I give them access to my business associates, and they keep my coffers full.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1AD: With any luck, a worthy hero will step forward and solve our problems, just like in the tales of old.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1B1: We must keep faith in ourselves and the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1B7: There are words spoken, and words unspoken. Beware these Thalmor, for they are adept in both languages. For this reason, I avoid them when I can.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1B8: When I married Torygg, he promised we'd go to lavish parties just like this. I wish he were here now.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1B9: Isn't this something? Elenwen really knows how to host a party!
Skyrim.esm|0BC1BA: I could learn a lot from the people in this room.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1BB: Have you tried the wine? It's better than anything in the Blue Palace.
Skyrim.esm|0BC1BC: We've got our hands full dealing with the Stormcloaks. What about you?
Skyrim.esm|0BC1E6: Are the dragons frightening your people as much as they're frightening mine?
Skyrim.esm|0BC204: Have you found it difficult to get provisions lately? I can scarcely feed the staff in my palace.
Skyrim.esm|0BC20D: Between the dragons and the Stormcloaks, my coffers are running dry.
Skyrim.esm|0BC212: I've known her long enough to appreciate her excellent taste in food and wine. And also long enough to respect her importance to Skyrim. She plays a vital role in rooting out the forbidden worship of Talos. As far as I'm concerned, those Talos worshippers get what they deserve. All they do is cause problems for me and the other jarls.
Skyrim.esm|0BC21A: It's true. For as long as I can remember our crops have always done well. I'm not sure whether it's the soil, the climate or the favor of the Divines, but we've never had a poor harvest.
Skyrim.esm|0BC21D: Quite often, yes. The only thing I enjoy more than luxury is sharing that luxury with those who can appreciate it. But I also enjoy learning the latest gossip and hearing the most intriguing rumors.
Skyrim.esm|0BC224: Oh, hello there. I didn't expect to see you here. Yes, I am indeed. Elenwen always serves the best wine at her parties. The food is excellent as well. Though I confess, being surrounded by so many wealthy and important people makes me feel more than a little nervous.
Skyrim.esm|0BC225: I lead the Justiciars. We are charged with enforcing the ban on Talos worship. Nords can be quite stubborn. They're slow to change their ways, and we find more heretics every day. But mark me well -- I will purge the heresy from this land as surely as the flame burns the flesh from the bone.
Skyrim.esm|0BC22C: Of course not. One does not gather the most important men and women of Skyrim and then serve them cheap ale and stale bread. This is only a taste of the benefits that accrue to those that align themselves with Thalmor interests.
Skyrim.esm|0BC233: I've spent many hours in council meetings lately grappling with that problem.
Skyrim.esm|0BC23E: I've spent many hours in council meetings lately grappling with that problem.
Skyrim.esm|0BC247: I've spent many hours in council meetings lately grappling with that problem.
Skyrim.esm|0BC24C: Fair faces and fine food are well and good, but honesty is rarely so pleasing.
Skyrim.esm|0BC24D: I am the Steward of Whiterun. I serve Jarl Balgruuf in Dragonsreach. Truth be told, I'd be more comfortable if I were back there. I get nervous in the company of so many worthies.
Skyrim.esm|0BC24F: These Thalmor know how to treat a guest.
Skyrim.esm|0BC252: A ruler is entitled to luxury, don't you think? The Thalmor understand that.
Skyrim.esm|0BC253: Why would anyone join the Stormcloaks? They have a fraction of the Empire's wealth and power.
Skyrim.esm|0BC258: If I had my way, I'd spend all my time at parties like this, and none of it dealing with the common rabble.
Skyrim.esm|0BC259: Have you learned nothing about me? I have dealings with everyone. Now, leave me. I don't want to arouse suspicion by talking to someone that clearly doesn't belong here.
Skyrim.esm|0BC25D: Is the food and drink to your liking?
Skyrim.esm|0BC25E: Is my staff seeing to your needs?
Skyrim.esm|0BC260: This gathering is nothing more than a boast. The Thalmor are reminding us that we're at their beck and call.
Skyrim.esm|0BC278: We've got troubles enough without the Thalmor and their witch-hunts.
Skyrim.esm|0BC27A: I'd rather be back at Whiterun, but while I'm here I might as well get some real news from the other parts of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BC27F: These Thalmor might not like to admit it, but as long as the Empire has Skyrim, the Empire has strength.
Skyrim.esm|0BC280: Born and raised right here in Rorikstead. My father was a soldier. He fought in the great war and when it was over, he retired here to raise a family. My mother passed away when I was just a babe, so he did his best to raise me on his own. It's not the most exciting place in the world, but the people here work hard and don't cause trouble for anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2A6: Not every Nord is obsessed with strength, honor and skill at arms. A rare few are born with a gift for making money. My investments are my strength and my wealth is my weapon. As for honor, well, there are some luxuries even I can't afford.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2AA: Oh, yes! Quite! So many important people from all across Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2AD: Splendid. Call if you have need of me.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2BE: It pleases me to hear you say so.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2C1: Excellent. I'll leave you to it, then.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2C2: Don't mention the Guild. In fact, it's better if you don't speak to me at all. This is not the place.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2C3: For now, perhaps. But I've heard disturbing rumors that they're marshaling their strength. I suspect the treaty that ended the war was just a tactic to buy time so the Thalmor can rebuild their armies. I hope I'm wrong. I'm not sure the Empire can afford another war.
Skyrim.esm|0BC2CE: Why, thank you. You are most courteous. I admit, I'm surprised to see you here. Normally these parties are reserved for the jarls and business leaders of Skyrim. Elenwen must be very fond of you.
Skyrim.esm|0BC6E7: Wait, where did you get this? This piece was thought to be lost forever, please take this with my utmost thanks!
Skyrim.esm|0BC8B3: Careful, huh? Thanks, Skulvar. I'll remember that. Ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0BC8B4: Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0BC8B5: Like to see him laugh his way out that.
Skyrim.esm|0BC913: I see no harm in it, if you have the skill. The grindstone will improve your weapons. Use the table to improve your armor. If you've got the raw materials, you can use the forge to make something new.
Skyrim.esm|0BCAF3: I guess you don't have potatoes in your ears after all. Just make sure we get a fresh batch in soon.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC86: That's a fair price. My blade is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC87: Looks like you've got some help already. I'll join you, but you'll have to let your comrade go. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|0BCC89: I'm what you'd call of soldier of fortune. Make me an offer, and I just might fight at your side.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC8A: If you've got the gold to pay my fee, then my sword arm is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC8B: Can't be too careful these days, with the war and other dangers. For a fair price, I'll watch your back.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC8D: Some call me a mercenary, but I like to think of myself as a freelance adventurer for hire.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC8E: My skill in battle is unmatched. Fortunately for you, that skill can be bought.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC8F: By blade or by spell, if you want it dead, I'll make it dead. All you need to do is pay my fee.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC90: Blade and shadow, silence and death - these are my arts. For a modest fee, I'll make great art for you.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC91: If it's a mercenary you're looking for, you've found him. There's no stronger sword arm for hire in all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BCC9B: Truly? So maybe my mother isn't crazy after all. You had best tell the Jarl in Whiterun. He needs to know about this.
Skyrim.esm|0BCCB1: The ale is going bad. We need to get a new batch.
Skyrim.esm|0BCCC6: Lucan's got a little bit of everything at the Riverwood Trader. If you need weapons or armor, see Alvor, the blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|0BCCCA: I've never seen anything quite like that.
Skyrim.esm|0BCCCE: By the Hist, what a sight...
Skyrim.esm|0BCCD2: Wow. Would you look at that?
Skyrim.esm|0BCCF8: He would dote on her so much. Let her work the forge and everything. I miss him so.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD00: Riverwood's agreeable enough, I suppose. For a Nord village.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD16: I understand. Speaking to a goddess is overwhelming for many. Return when you're prepared.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD17: Oh, a customer. Sorry you had to hear that.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD19: I'm with the Vigil of Stendarr. We believe this house might have been used for daedra worship. Evil rites and so forth.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD1D: I can't talk about it. Rest assured, the jarl hears everyone's concerns. You will be able to visit the dead again soon.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD22: I don't need to know the details. Blessings of Arkay to you for your help. Here. Take my amulet as a reward.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD23: This shrine was built by the Dunmer. As our land was scorched by fire and brimstone, Azura's prophecies led us to safety. She is a daedra, a powerful being who watches from beyond our mortal plane. She has chosen you to be her champion.
Skyrim.esm|0BCD24: Fine. I can't force you to help me, but the offer is open.
Skyrim.esm|0BCF18: That's it! We'll bring that bastard down!
Skyrim.esm|0BCF1B: Come down here and fight, you coward!
Skyrim.esm|0BCF1C: I can't get a bead on him!
Skyrim.esm|0BCF23: Hold it there! Keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|0BCF29: Take him down! It's our best chance!
Skyrim.esm|0BCF2A: I can't believe we actually killed a dragon!
Skyrim.esm|0BCF2C: You saved Whiterun today. We won't forget.
Skyrim.esm|0BCF2E: Come on, I want a better look at that thing.
Skyrim.esm|0BCF2F: We taught that dragon not to mess with Whiterun, didn't we?
Skyrim.esm|0BCF30: I'm glad you're on our side.
Skyrim.esm|0BCF55: Good luck. I hope you get there in time.
Skyrim.esm|0BD14E: And what's wrong with him, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0BD156: I don't know where we're going, but Sovngarde awaits.
Skyrim.esm|0BD157: Ulfric? The Jarl of Windhelm? You're the leader of the rebellion. But if they've captured you... Oh gods, where are they taking us?
Skyrim.esm|0BD159: Watch your tongue. You're speaking to Ulfric Stormcloak, the true high king.
Skyrim.esm|0BD168: Fascinating! So the ancients were more daring... or foolhardy... similar to the Dragon Break itself, although less far-reaching... This Elder Scroll might be able to show you the past in some way, then... yes, yes, it could work.
Skyrim.esm|0BD169: The first mortals that I taught the Thu'um - the first Tongues. The leaders of the rebellion against Alduin.
Skyrim.esm|0BD16A: Return it here, to the Tiid-Ahraan. Then... Kelle vomindok. Nothing is certain with such things... But I believe the Scroll's bond with the Tiid-Ahraan will allow you a... a seeing, a vision of the moment of its creation. Then you will feel - know - Dragonrend, in the power of its first expression. You will see them... wuth fadonne... my friends - Hakon, Gormlaith, Felldir.
Skyrim.esm|0BD16B: Do you know the College of Winterhold? They have a deep interest in such arcana.
Skyrim.esm|0BD16C: Hmm. Well, if that's what the Greybeards told you, I confess I don't have a better plan. So little is known about the Elder Scrolls, almost anything is possible where they're concerned. Artifacts of truly inconceivable power.
Skyrim.esm|0BD16E: Paarthurnax? That name is familiar... the legendary leader of the Greybeards? I would not have expected a scholar of such an esoteric turn of mind among their ranks.
Skyrim.esm|0BD631: Vilkas, take him out to the yard and see what he can do.
Skyrim.esm|0BD635: Vilkas, take her out to the yard and see what she can do.
Skyrim.esm|0BD639: Come on now, watch the footwork. Keep your balance.
Skyrim.esm|0BD63A: Strike when the shoulder turns. He's giving you openings!
Skyrim.esm|0BD63C: Just keep moving, you'll wear him out!
Skyrim.esm|0BD682: Are those two at it again?
Skyrim.esm|0BD686: I've been working on a shield for Aela. My wife is in mourning and I need to get back to her soon. I'd be much obliged if you could take this to Aela for me.
Skyrim.esm|0BD68B: Keep a sharp eye about you and maybe I'll teach you to fight some day.
Skyrim.esm|0BD68D: Vilkas will see if you're worth our trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0BD68E: I hear you have spirit. This is good. You may yet join our numbers.
Skyrim.esm|0BD691: Keep up the effort, boy. Eyes on the horizon, always.
Skyrim.esm|0BD692: Keep up the effort, young one. Eyes on the horizon, always.
Skyrim.esm|0BD6FA: I won't be the one to go back there. I won't return to Avanchnzel. You can't make me do it.
Skyrim.esm|0BD6FB: Then you didn't know Roggvir, and you have no idea what it means to be a Nord of Skyrim! He did the honorable thing, even when he knew it would cost him everything. My brother was the best man in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BD6FC: Depends on what you mean by "busy." I'm also the head jailor in the dungeon here. Quiet job, usually.
Skyrim.esm|0BD6FD: Roggvir. He opened a gate and they executed him for it. He opened a gate and the wrong man rode out of it. If that man hadn't been Ulfric Stormcloak. If Ulfric hadn't killed High King Torygg... But it was Ulfric. And he did kill the high king in honorable combat. My brother refused to allow the Imperials to take revenge for the deed. So now he's dead.
Skyrim.esm|0BD6FE: You mean Roggvir. Yes. I made an appeal to Captain Aldis. He allowed Roggvir to have a proper Nord burial, as is the custom in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BD6FF: Work in it? I own it.
Skyrim.esm|0BD700: You could say that. I own the place.
Skyrim.esm|0BD701: You must bring it to Avanchnzel, in the west. Return it to them. It must go back.
Skyrim.esm|0BD702: Then like Roggvir, you're a Nord at heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand. I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had... I... I think I need to stop talking now...
Skyrim.esm|0BD703: This city has a long history of madness and murder. The Wolf Queen... Pelagius... The death of High King Torygg... And now, public executions. My books tell the stories. Have no doubt - as pretty as its streets are, as jovial as the bards may be... darkness is drawn to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BD704: Listen, Roggvir and I grew up together. It was us, a few others and Vivienne. Vivienne Onis. I have no idea why, but one day, I told Roggvir I liked her. It was a stupid thing to do... He mocked me incessantly after that. From that point forward, every time Vivienne and I were anywhere close to each other, he made sure to humiliate me. He'd beat me up, dump mead on my head, anything he could get away with. Once he even slipped a beehive into my bed. We were children, sure. But Roggvir never outgrew that immaturity, or his cruelty. So any talk about him being an "honorable" Nord? Pure folly.
Skyrim.esm|0BD706: Sometimes you just do what needs to be done. You don't have to hate a man to kill him. Though, admittedly... it helps.
Skyrim.esm|0BD707: Oh, if you're looking for a good attitude go see the bards. They'll fake it for you if nothing else.
Skyrim.esm|0BD708: It is done. I won't go back to Avanchnzel. I won't.
Skyrim.esm|0BD709: I could have left it behind. I could have put it back where it came from.
Skyrim.esm|0BD70A: Oh, wow. I knew it. I knew they were real! I have to remember to tell Papa.
Skyrim.esm|0BD70B: No. I think he was entirely wrong. Roggvir's execution was justified and necessary. Being honorable might make you a good man, but it doesn't make you right. Be a better world if it did.
Skyrim.esm|0BD70C: Let's just say that the headsman's axe may not be the worst way for a Solitude jail prisoner to die. Look at that. I've told you to watch your tongue and mine is waggling. I've said enough.
Skyrim.esm|0BD70D: I showed up to watch, yeah. I don't know if they were right to execute him for what he did, but I'll tell you something... It couldn't have happened to a nicer guy.
Skyrim.esm|0BD70E: Really? Was it big? Did it breathe fire?
Skyrim.esm|0BD70F: Not my finest hour. Roggvir was a fine and honorable man.
Skyrim.esm|0BD710: No. That honor is Sybille Stentor's. A smart man steers clear of Stentor. And keeps himself out of the dungeon when she's having a bad day...
Skyrim.esm|0BD712: You really don't know? He lets Ulfric Stormcloak escape Solitude. Ulfric rides in, murders the high king, and Roggvir lets him ride right back out.
Skyrim.esm|0BD713: Then we should soon be selling arrows by the bushel. A sword is no good against a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0BD714: Where the dragon attacked? We've heard rumors of death and... and fire from the skies. But no real news.
Skyrim.esm|0BD715: Can any man hope to be ready for a dragon? I can but hone their skills and harden their hearts. The rest is up to the gods.
Skyrim.esm|0BD716: You're the Dragonborn, aren't you? I'm sort of hoping you are going to fight the dragons for us. A normal man is no match for a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0BD717: Don't forget me next time you need herbs and potions.
Skyrim.esm|0BD718: Thanks again for finding out about my daughter.
Skyrim.esm|0BD719: Then like Roggvir, you have a Nord's heart. It's hard for many of the people here to understand. I think Aldis understands. I don't think I could have stayed here if anyone else had... I... I think I need to be done talking for the moment.
Skyrim.esm|0BD71B: I'm in mourning. Can't you see that?
Skyrim.esm|0BD71C: New in town, right? If you need a place to sleep, head over to the Winking Skeever. We keep a fine inn.
Skyrim.esm|0BD71D: You know, the Winking Skeever's not just the best inn in Solitude. It's the also the only inn in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BD71E: Ain't got one. But I'd fancy a chance at that Jaree-Ra's scrawny neck. Filthy Argonian never would have set foot in this city, if High King Torygg was still alive. I can tell you that.
Skyrim.esm|0BD72F: Oh, I am well. I will be well. Well to be within the will inside the walls.
Skyrim.esm|0BD733: Excellent. You've done us a great service. Here is your reward.
Skyrim.esm|0BD734: The jarl has put out a bounty to slay a giant. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Skyrim.esm|0BD736: If you change your mind, seek me out here.
Skyrim.esm|0BD743: Oh, thank the gods. Here, take this with my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0BD744: Here, I'll show you on your map so you can stay away. Please, if you can help me, let me know or get out of my way so I can try to find a priest.
Skyrim.esm|0BD745: You there, please you have to help me! I was attacked by a vampire! Do you have a potion or something that can cure this before I turn?
Skyrim.esm|0BD746: I'm on my way to Windhelm to join up with the Stormcloaks. Ulfric has the right of it.
Skyrim.esm|0BD747: The Empire is in shambles. They've banned the worship of Talos.
Skyrim.esm|0BD748: I may have roots in Cyrodiil, but Skyrim's as much my home as any Nord's.
Skyrim.esm|0BD749: I'm on my way to Solitude to join the Legion. A united Empire is better for everyone.
Skyrim.esm|0BD74A: I was born and raised in Skyrim, but the Nords treat us like dirt.
Skyrim.esm|0BD74B: I want to be on the right side.
Skyrim.esm|0BD74C: Then get out of my way. I have to get to a town and find a priest.
Skyrim.esm|0BD752: The jarl has put out a bounty on Forsworn. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Skyrim.esm|0BD765: Really? Power? Haven't you already killed enough of your foes using the more mundane means at your disposal? Swords, magic, etc.? How... uninspired.
Skyrim.esm|0BD76F: Defense? No one would dare attack Riften. I'm not sure you realize just how well connected Jarl Maven is with the Empire. With a single snap of her fingers, we could have a legion here in a fortnight.
Skyrim.esm|0BD770: As steward, I serve as an intermediary between the jarl and her subjects. I also handle the less important and day-to-day policies that affect daily life in Riften. Lately I've had my hands full suppressing these ridiculous accusations of corruption in the keep.
Skyrim.esm|0BD771: I'm not certain what business it is of yours, but I'm Moth- I mean Jarl Maven's steward. I run the prisons in Riften, keep the riff-raff from robbing the good folk of this city and have the drunks tossed into the lake.
Skyrim.esm|0BD772: As long as she continues to keep me at her side, she can count on my blade to protect her. To be honest, I doubt someone would be stupid enough to make an attempt on Maven... sorry, Jarl Maven's life. But just in case, I'll be ready for them.
Skyrim.esm|0BD773: Ulfric's a bloody hero. I don't think there's a worthy Nord alive who'd dare argue the point. He's taking the Empire to task and making them answer for their crimes. I don't think I'll be content until Ulfric sits upon the throne of Skyrim with the ashes of the Concordat at his feet.
Skyrim.esm|0BD774: Hey, if the Empire and the Stormcloaks want to kill each other over some foolish cause, I say let 'em. All I care about is two things: watchin' Maven's back and countin' the gold she pays me to do it.
Skyrim.esm|0BD775: I don't care what you think. The Empire put Maven on the throne for a reason. That's all you need to know.
Skyrim.esm|0BD777: Indeed. You don't get to the position my mother's achieved by blind luck... her business sense and diplomatic skills are legendary.
Skyrim.esm|0BD778: The throne suits me, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|0BD779: My title is just a formality. I've always been in charge around here.
Skyrim.esm|0BD77A: Jarl Maven Black-Briar. Has a pleasant sound to it, wouldn't you say?
