Skyrim.esm|0BD77B: Speak. I wish to hear everything my subjects have to say.
Skyrim.esm|0BD77C: Unmid and Anuriel shouldn't blame themselves for Riften's fall. I am the one who's ultimately responsible.
Skyrim.esm|0BD77D: If Riften can be retaken, I will redouble my efforts to ensure the safety of my subjects and the defense of The Rift.
Skyrim.esm|0BD781: I presume you have something important to say?
Skyrim.esm|0BD782: If you're here to report any instances of corruption, be confident that we're handling these rumors accordingly.
Skyrim.esm|0BD783: The Imperials now control Riften and have placed Maven Black-Briar on the jarl's throne.
Skyrim.esm|0BD784: Perhaps the Stormcloaks might rally to take back Riften one day. Only time will tell.
Skyrim.esm|0BD7DF: There's nothing more I can teach you.
Skyrim.esm|0BD7E0: You've already learned all I can teach you.
Skyrim.esm|0BD7E1: You already know all that I know.
Skyrim.esm|0BD7E2: It's as rewarding as it is dangerous. Let's see what I can show you.
Skyrim.esm|0BDB4D: With a master of magic at your side, you'll have nothing to fear.
Skyrim.esm|0BDB4F: The only thing better than a powerful mage fighting at your side is... well, nothing, really.
Skyrim.esm|0BDB50: Why hire a common soldier to protect you when you can have a master of the arcane?
Skyrim.esm|0BDB51: For a modest fee, I'll bring my formidable arcane powers to bear against your foes. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|0BDB52: Why settle for just stabbing your foes when you can roast them alive in a gout of arcane fire?
Skyrim.esm|0BDB53: Watch your back, friend. Better yet, pay me to do it for you.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB1: My blade is sharp and I thirst for battle. Onward, then!
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB2: Best not to walk the roads of Skyrim alone.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB3: Sellswords are common in Skyrim, but you'll find precious few as stout and as strong as I am.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB4: If you're looking to hire the strongest mercenary in Skyrim, I'd wager your search is over.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB5: I've fought in more battles than I've got years of life in me. Pay my fee and I'll put that experience to work for you, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB6: Death is my art, and like all artists, I seek a patron. For a mere handful of gold, I will follow you into any danger.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB7: I know nothing of fear, and nothing of remorse. Pay my fee, and together we will vanquish any foe.
Skyrim.esm|0BDFB8: I am a lethal instrument, yours to command for a modest sum. I suggest you hire me before one of your enemies does.
Skyrim.esm|0BE940: We welcome you to worship the Eight Divines, freely and openly.
Skyrim.esm|0BE941: I'm afraid I'm running errands at the moment. But please, visit me later at the Temple of the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0BE96F: Well, now. I'm afraid you've caught me a bit out of my element. Most of my time is spent in the Temple of the Divines, you see.
Skyrim.esm|0BE973: Feel free to take a seat, or pray at the shrines. We appreciate worship in all its forms.
Skyrim.esm|0BE97D: Come by the Winking Skeever any time you want to hear a tune.
Skyrim.esm|0BE97E: You should think about staying the night. Corpulus keeps a clean inn.
Skyrim.esm|0BE97F: Come back if you want to play tag.
Skyrim.esm|0BE9B5: Papa doesn't like when I talk to customers, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0BEA0C: Remember the Winking Skeever next time you're footsore.
Skyrim.esm|0BEA4F: Need anything, you just say the word.
Skyrim.esm|0BEA5C: May the words of poets keep you warm.
Skyrim.esm|0BEA68: May all your adventures be worthy of telling.
Skyrim.esm|0BEA73: Now you can say you've met the most talented bard in all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BEA74: Don't forget my name. Someday, everyone will be saying it.
Skyrim.esm|0BEAC5: I suppose I should return to my papers...
Skyrim.esm|0BEAC8: Always good to talk to a fellow bard.
Skyrim.esm|0BEACE: Stop by again, sometime. I'll sing for you.
Skyrim.esm|0BEADA: Always time for a fellow bard.
Skyrim.esm|0BEADD: Oh, if you haven't heard Dean Pantea sing, you should.
Skyrim.esm|0BEADF: Always glad to talk to a bard.
Skyrim.esm|0BEAE0: Thanks for taking the time. People never want to talk to me.
Skyrim.esm|0BEAE1: Time to finally see the sky, and make it rain red.
Skyrim.esm|0BEAE4: And you've proven that was the best move I could have made. Don't give me that look. You're free to go. Here, how about a little token for your efforts? My family's ring and all the things the guards confiscated from you when you were jailed. Now if you'll excuse me, I have to figure out how we're going to fill our recently emptied mine.
Skyrim.esm|0BEAE7: Always glad to talk to the bard who returned part of the Poetic Edda.
Skyrim.esm|0BEAE9: I'll get back to court business, then.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB0A: Always have your shield at the ready.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB2E: Take care of your armor, and it'll take care of you.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB2F: I must return to the court. They simply can't make do without me.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB30: Good, we've wasted enough time here.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB31: We'll speak again later. If we must...
Skyrim.esm|0BEB32: I'll be sure to check "Talk to random stranger" off my list of today's tasks...
Skyrim.esm|0BEB35: I am done with you. You may go.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB36: Be well. Pray for Solitude and my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB37: Keep the faith. General Tullius will win this war.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB38: We have the utmost confidence in you and Ulfric's guiding hands.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB39: May the blessings of the Eight be with you.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB3A: May the blessings of the Nine be with you.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB3B: Feel free to return to me with questions. The Blue Palace is an open forum.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB3C: Please feel welcome in the court of Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB3D: I won't forget what you've done for Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB51: Anyone with a stout heart like yours is welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB52: I'm proud to call a man like you friend.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB53: I'm proud to call a woman like you friend.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB54: Do not dishonor or attempt to harm Elisif in any way. Adhere to that rule, and you'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB55: I must return my attention to Elisif. If I were to lose another jarl while I was... distracted...
Skyrim.esm|0BEB56: Take care of yourself, and always remember: the world is ripe with people looking to spill your blood...
Skyrim.esm|0BEB57: Stay guarded. You never know what could be out there.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB58: All right. Back to slicing, dicing, and baking.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB5B: Yeah, yeah. Goodbye, kill lots of people, Hail Sithis, and all that.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB5E: Just come back alive, and I'll be happy.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB5F: Go on now. Time to get your hands bloody.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB60: Take care of yourself, beef roast.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB65: Remember, when you kill - we all wield the blade.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB6B: Skuli owns the inn with his mother gone, but I handle the rooms.
Skyrim.esm|0BEB6C: You should head on inside. Eydis or Skuli can take care of you.
Skyrim.esm|0BECE5: Are you finished ogling the grotesque? I suppose I should be grateful you didn't simply attack me.
Skyrim.esm|0BECE7: We are traveling to the Shrine of Kynareth in Whiterun. I hear the Gildergreen is more beautiful than ever since it's been restored. They say that eating the fruit it bears will add years to your life.
Skyrim.esm|0BECE8: And I have no more time for talking. Go speak with Kesh if you're so curious.
Skyrim.esm|0BECE9: Returning to High Rock. Our shepherd lost his way, and I fear Peryite's wrath may consume those who remain with him. Kesh could tell you more. I just want out of Skyrim as quickly as possible.
Skyrim.esm|0BECEA: I am one of the Afflicted. I'd have been dead from this plague a year ago if not for Peryite's protection.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDEF: Don't mess anything up while you're here...
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF0: Good talking to you. Not many care to chat with the help.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF1: Okay. I need to get back to the palace soon, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF2: You can catch me at the Blue Palace later, if you want to talk again.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF3: If Erdi caught me chatting, I'd never hear the end of it...
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF4: Uh, I guess I should be getting back to work. It was more fun talking, though.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF5: May Arkay bless you with a long life.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF6: May the light of truth keep the darkness at bay.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF7: It's best you leave me be.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDF8: Sure, you make your retreat and I'll make mine.
Skyrim.esm|0BEDFA: Just watch yourself. You don't want to meet me on the job.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE01: Tell these horkers to set me free.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE02: Leave me be. I'm no harm here.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE03: Keep my men in your prayers. They'll need them if they don't shape up.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE04: Stay strong. The Eight willing, we'll win this war.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE06: You should speak to my daughter, Evette. She's the brains of the family.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE07: Always good talking to you... friend.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE0A: For a stranger, you're not so bad.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE0B: Blessed are those who worship, for they know true grace.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE0D: The strong and honorable always carry the blessing of the Eight.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE0E: The strong and honorable always carry the blessing of the Nine.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE0F: Your rewards in life will match the strength of your faith.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE10: The Temple of Divines is always open for worshippers.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE11: Take care, and go with the blessings of the Eight.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE19: Take care, and go with the blessings of the Nine.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE1B: I really need to get back to planning my wedding.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE1C: Now if you'll excuse me. The East Empire Company has several accounts, and it's up to me to balance them.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE1D: Perhaps I'll be seeing more of you.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE1E: Please come back if you're ever in need of fletching supplies.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE1F: Good to talk to a fellow traveler.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE21: Thank you for helping my aunt earlier. I'll see you around.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE63: People to see and places to go, huh? Later, then.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE7F: I understand, you're an important person. I'll catch up with you later.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE80: I should make sure Svari is okay.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE81: Addvar won't sell anything if I don't go and check up on him.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE82: Come back to Bits and Pieces anytime.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE83: Make sure to recommend us to your associates.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE84: Return anytime you need arms or armor. My forge never rests.
Skyrim.esm|0BEE85: Keep safe. And always wear your armor.
Skyrim.esm|0BEEC5: Yes, I agree with you. Have you heard any news from the other parts of Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0BEEC6: You speak truly. Have you heard any interesting rumors of late?
Skyrim.esm|0BEEC7: I'll not argue that. Heard any interesting gossip lately?
Skyrim.esm|0BEEC8: I couldn't agree more. By the way, have you heard any interesting rumors lately?
Skyrim.esm|0BEEC9: I couldn't have said it better myself. It's been some time since I've had news from elsewhere in Skyrim. What have you heard?
Skyrim.esm|0BEECA: I know just what you mean. Heard anything interesting lately? Any news or gossip?
Skyrim.esm|0BEFC8: I heard Elenwen say she's going open a bottle of her best Firebrand Wine later. I can hardly wait.
Skyrim.esm|0BEFC9: These Stormcloaks are nothing more than an armed mob. This war won't last much longer.
Skyrim.esm|0BF045: I hope they'll serve the roast venison with bittergreen sauce that we had at the last party. That was delicious.
Skyrim.esm|0BF06A: This war has taken a toll on the merchants of Skyrim. Let's hope that it ends soon, for their sake.
Skyrim.esm|0BF06B: I look forward to the day when this rebellion is put down, and we once again have peace.
Skyrim.esm|0BF06C: The Gray Quarter in Windhelm is little better than a slum. It's shameful that the Dark Elves are made to live that way.
Skyrim.esm|0BF06D: With the soldiers all fighting in the war, there's nobody to hunt down the bandits. Travel has become dangerous of late.
Skyrim.esm|0BF06E: It's been a brutal winter. I can't remember the last time it was this cold, and for this long.
Skyrim.esm|0BF0A1: Keep an eye on these Thalmor. You never know...
Skyrim.esm|0BF0AE: Come and visit me in Solitude sometime.
Skyrim.esm|0BF0B4: It was a pleasure, thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0BF0B5: Well, I did hear that the Argonians have been banned from living inside the walls of Windhelm. They're only allowed on the city docks now. Most of them sleep together in one building, and they're as poor as wretches.
Skyrim.esm|0BF0B6: Have you visited Rorikstead lately? No matter how harsh the winter, the crops that grow there never falter. Even when every other farm in the hold is barely scraping by, the harvest at Rorikstead is always bountiful. Is it good fortune? The blessings of the Divines? Who knows?
Skyrim.esm|0BF0BC: Now that I think on it, I did hear something rather interesting the other day. Apparently, the Red Wave has been docked in Solitude for well over a month, and with no sign of shipping out any time soon. Some say the ship suffered damage to her hull when she struck an iceberg, and Captain Safia can't afford the repairs. Of course, she would never admit such a thing. Captain Safia is far too proud a woman for that.
Skyrim.esm|0BF0C6: Well, there might be nothing to it, but I've heard that the feud between the Gray-Manes and the Battle-Borns in Whiterun has grown ugly. Fralia Gray-Mane is convinced that the Battle-Borns have abducted one of her sons.
Skyrim.esm|0BF0C7: I'm not sure that it's news, but lately it seems like every blacksmith in Skyrim is out to prove that he's the equal of Eorlund Gray-Mane. It's not really a fair comparison, if you ask me. After all, Eorlund works the Skyforge. How is any smith going to compete with that?
Skyrim.esm|0BF14E: Well, I did see something interesting not long ago. There were several Khajiit camped outside the walls of Whiterun. Seems they've been traveling around the whole of Skyrim peddling their wares. You've got to admire their courage, doing something like that with dragons on the loose and a war going on.
Skyrim.esm|0BF20D: Make yourself useful and chop up some firewood. There's a few coins in it for you.
Skyrim.esm|0BF22F: I heard those mages up in Winterhold have been trying to figure out how many dragons there are in Skyrim. They seem to think it's at least a dozen, but I'm sure they're exaggerating. They're probably trying to scare us into giving them more money and influence, so they can save us from the dragons threat.
Skyrim.esm|0BF231: Well, I did hear that Ulfric Stormcloak was slain while defending Windhelm against a dragon, but it sounds too good to be true. I'll wager it was Ulfric himself that started the rumor. Maybe he thought it would bolster recruitment.
Skyrim.esm|0BF3FE: How much worse is this going to get?
Skyrim.esm|0BF408: We need to get in there and stop this!
Skyrim.esm|0BF445: I'm as ready as I'm going to be.
Skyrim.esm|0BF446: Not until we're all ready. We have to go together.
Skyrim.esm|0BF448: She... she didn't make it. When it was clear we were going to have to fall back, she stayed behind and made sure the rest of us were all right.
Skyrim.esm|0BF45B: Hold up. There's a bear just ahead. See her?
Skyrim.esm|0BF45C: I guess we're lucky that didn't come down on top of us.
Skyrim.esm|0BF45E: I'd rather not tangle with her right now. We might be able to sneak by. Just take it nice and slow, and watch where you step.
Skyrim.esm|0BF460: We'd better push on. I'm sure the others will find another way out.
Skyrim.esm|0BF461: Damn it. No going back that way.
Skyrim.esm|0BF46A: Ah, indeed. Well, there's a pretty puzzle. Not the kind of thing you'll find in your local bookshop. I'm sure you'll manage, though.
Skyrim.esm|0BF4BB: The Word calls you. Go to it.
Skyrim.esm|0BF4BC: Why do you intrude here, if not for tinvaak?
Skyrim.esm|0BF4BD: I have spoken. The Rotmulaag waits.
Skyrim.esm|0BFA01: Look for yourself. Ancano's power is growing. We can't crack whatever magic he's using to shield himself. I hope your trip to Labyrinthian was worth it.
Skyrim.esm|0BFA05: Bless you, friend. You've renewed my hope. Before I came into Zenithar's calling, I confess I was a thief. But I learned a few things in my time that might help you survive. Let me show you.
Skyrim.esm|0BFA06: Windhelm has been hard on my people. But our fortunes will turn, and we have shelter and food.
Skyrim.esm|0BFA07: We have not fared well outside our native Black Marsh, but we're determined to make the best of things. I began praying to Zenithar, the god of wealth, to bring us some fortune, but thieves made off with my sacred amulet almost as soon as I bought it.
Skyrim.esm|0BFA08: Shahvee would be in your debt, kind one.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE0: Food, experience and a roof over my head in the finest city in Skyrim. If money's all you hold dear, then you end up like Jaree-Ra. A plague on the city.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE1: I was a member of the court during the reign of Torygg's father. It was Torygg who appointed me to the position.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE2: Ha! I like you. No man is a match for a dragon. Buying one of our blades wouldn't hurt your chances, though.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE3: Why of course. And will you also be needing the bow that shoots rainbows? Or perhaps a quiver that dispenses beer? Look, if you want arrows we have them, but don't insult my intelligence.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE4: Captain Aldis can't prove it, but that Argonian is up to no good.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE5: Really? I knew he was mixed up in something dangerous, but I hadn't heard he was dead.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE7: Thank the gods for the end of yet another pointless conversation...
Skyrim.esm|0BFAE9: I do hope you'll remain in Solitude. This city could do with some new blood.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAEA: Remember Radiant Raiment when you need a change of attire.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAEB: May your sword feast on Stormcloak flesh.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAEC: Good hunting. Go kill some bandits for me.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAED: If you're off to fight, talk to Beirand before you go. His armor is well forged and dependable.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAEE: These are troubled times. Be on your guard out there.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAEF: May the gods speed your journeys... and steady your sword arm.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF0: Come back when you figure out how to spend money.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF1: I'll always have fruit to buy. Until it goes rotten. Then... I won't.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF2: Good luck out there. It seems like a tough time to be wandering Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF3: Stay warm and keep sober. It's the two things I know for sure will make life better.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF4: If you're heading up to Solitude you should stop to check out the view on your way. It's a good one.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF5: Carriages to all the cities in Skyrim. Don't forget us.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF6: A man like you should own a horse. Go speak with my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF8: Pa always says good men need few words.
Skyrim.esm|0BFAF9: I want to check on Grani, one of our horses. Goodbye, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB3D: I'm looking for a place called Bard's Leap. They say the echo of a song sung from there can be heard all the way in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB3E: Some may find their inspiration tucked away in tomes, or by carousing in the cities, but I find it here in the vast expanses of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB3F: The best tales are those of adventure. Who could truly write such a tale without first experiencing such?
Skyrim.esm|0BFB40: Why to perform for my common man, of course! Why should only the courts and inns of wealthy towns be given the gift of music?
Skyrim.esm|0BFB41: Observing and capturing the epic struggle for Skyrim! When brothers clash with bitter blades, surely there is a song to be found.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB72: Grandson. He's a strong lad. His parents were at Helgen when, well you know.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB73: Well, can't say I was a great father the first time around. Now that he's here it's like a second chance. He's a good lad.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB74: It's okay, I know what happened at home. With the dragon and all.
Skyrim.esm|0BFB75: I don't believe it. I made that bow myself. Been through some messes with it, let me tell you. You know what? I want you to keep it. My great hunts are behind me, but yours are yet to come.
Skyrim.esm|0BFBBA: Yeah, but don't say anything about it to Froki. I don't think he understands.
Skyrim.esm|0BFBBB: It ain't so bad. I miss living with my parents back in Helgen, though. Froki can't cook and there's nobody to talk to up here.
Skyrim.esm|0BFBBC: Just what we need. City folk barging in, probably looking for an offering to their precious Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0BFBBD: I'm eleven years old, and I could take you.
Skyrim.esm|0BFBBE: Froki won't let me hunt on my own yet, but he never shuts up about killing his first stag when he was eleven.
Skyrim.esm|0BFBBF: Someday I'm going to kill a dragon all by myself.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC41: You're Dragonborn. The rules don't apply to you. You can Shout the way dragons do... without training, through inborn instinct. They always hope to teach the Dragonborn to respect the Way of the Voice as they do. They never fully succeed. You'll have to make your own decision. It's a beautiful philosophy, but outside the seclusion of High Hrothgar, I was never able to hold to it.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC42: Yes. Although I rarely use my training. The Greybeards believe the Voice should be used only for worship of Kynareth. I have... fallen from their strict teaching, but I still don't feel it should be used lightly. Not all of Arngeir's lecturing was wasted, it seems.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC43: Yes. The oldest and most powerful, although he may not seem so. I doubt he's forgiven me for leaving. And for... well, for what he'd consider blasphemy. Using Shouts for anything but worship of Kynareth.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC46: Yes. They chose me when I was just a lad. It was a great honor, of course. I was to become a Greybeard myself. I spent almost ten years at High Hrothgar, learning the Way of the Voice. Then the Great War came... I couldn't stand missing it. I often think about High Hrothgar. It's very... disconnected from the troubles down here. But that's why I couldn't stay, and why I couldn't go back. I suppose the Greybeards care about Skyrim's troubles, in their way, but I needed to do something about it. I'm sure Arngeir would call it one of my failings.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC49: No matter. Go to High Hrothgar. Learn what the Greybeards can teach you.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC4A: I know more than most. I trained with the Greybeards at High Hrothgar when I was a boy. The Way of the Voice is an ancient, spiritual form of magic in which you project your vital essence into a "thu'um" or Shout. Most people would need to train for years before they could even attempt a single Shout. The Dragonborn is different - you have an inborn ability to Shout, the same way dragons are said to.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC4B: Back to business, Proventus. We still have a city to defend.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC4E: I envy you, you know. To climb the 7,000 Steps again... I made the pilgrimage once, did you know that? High Hrothgar is a very peaceful place. Very... disconnected from the troubles of this world. I wonder that the Greybeards even notice what's going on down here. They haven't seemed to care before.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC4F: It means you have the dragon blood - you have an inborn ability to use your Voice the way dragons do. The old tales tell of Dragonborn heroes who slew dragons and took their power. It seems those tales are true. With the dragons returning, and now a Dragonborn appearing... maybe the Greybeards know what it all means.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC50: It means you can kill dragons and steal their power - even speak with a dragon's voice, they say. There hasn't been a Dragonborn seen in the land for centuries. Not since Talos himself walked the 7,000 Steps to High Hrothgar.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC52: But remember, you're still oathbound to our cause. Skyrim still waits to be free.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC53: They live in seclusion near the top of the Throat of the World, the great mountain of Skyrim. They're masters of the Way of the Voice - of Shouting. It's a great honor for them to summon you. They speak to very few - in fact, they hardly speak at all.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC56: Ulfric can tell you more than most anyone. He was studying to be a Greybeard himself, back before the Great War.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC57: I studied with them when I was young. They taught me how to Shout.
Skyrim.esm|0BFC64: My dear vagrant, we're the only family worth knowing in Markarth. We own everything in this city, even the jail. And guess who owns the Silver-Blood Inn? Not that we named it that, the people just insisted.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCB7: You might make a decent Companion yet.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCB8: I hope the hunting goes well, brother. We've got the cowards on their heels now.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCB9: I hope the hunting goes well, sister. We've got the cowards on their heels now.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCBA: Have you been bringing the battle to the Silver Hand, friend? I wish I could be there with you. Soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCBC: I've been running interference for you around here. I don't think anyone's caught on to our little campaign. Yet.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCBD: Do you have guidance for me, Harbinger?
Skyrim.esm|0BFCBE: Vilkas said you're the new Kodlak. I don't know what that means, but you seem honorable, so that's good.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCC0: I think by now I've killed one of every living thing in Skyrim. May be time for a trip to Morrowind.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCC1: I never thought they would actually let me join the Companions. But now it looks like they're letting anyone in these days.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCC2: What guidance could you offer me?
Skyrim.esm|0BFCC3: Good to have some fresh eyes up there at the top. I follow your lead, Harbinger.
Skyrim.esm|0BFCC4: Oh, so you're the one in charge now? Fine, fine.
Skyrim.esm|0BFF82: Just shut up and play the game.
Skyrim.esm|0BFF83: I'm just saying that these ceramic lamps make me nervous. They just seem so... fragile.
Skyrim.esm|0C00F1: Unless you've come to pay a ransom, turn around and walk away.
Skyrim.esm|0C00F2: Oh, it's you. I didn't think you could handle this on your own. Did you get rid of Christer?
Skyrim.esm|0C016C: You cannot eat Gleda! Gleda is protected by Grok!
Skyrim.esm|0C0199: I sent a blessing to Mundus, a wasting plague that infected a scattering of Breton villages. One of my monks, the elf Orchendor, was sent to gather these Afflicted. He shepherded them into Bthardamz for me, but has since lost his way. I will not stand for betrayal. I want you to go to Bthardamz and kill Orchendor, in my name.
Skyrim.esm|0C0277: A joke? Alain Dufont... a joke? I am a king on my throne, and you, my friend... you will die slowly, and painfully.
Skyrim.esm|0C0279: Muiri? Muiri? So, what, you're the cow's champion? Oh, now that is rich. Well then, champion, let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|0C027A: I didn't know it when we were... with each other, but Alain is actually the leader of a band of cutthroats. Bandits. They're holed up in some old dwarven ruin - Raldbthar. It's near Windhelm. They use it as their base. It's where they stage their raids. I want you to go to that ruin, find Alain Dufont, and kill him. I don't care about his friends. Do whatever you want with them. But Alain has to die!
Skyrim.esm|0C027B: Ha! My opponent is so paralyzed with terror, no words will come. Let's be done with it, then!
Skyrim.esm|0C02CE: How can we? We're a community of farmers, not fighting men. If not for the war, we might have enough guards in the hold to protect the settlements. That the dragons should return now is most unfortunate.
Skyrim.esm|0C02CF: These secret visits to the tavern will make you an easy target for an enemy assassin. You should have told me first.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D0: Secret? What makes you think there's a secret? There are no secrets here, my curious friend. Our prosperity is simply the result of hard work, good fortune and the blessings of the gods.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D1: Heard about that, did you? Yes, I went out for a pint or two. What of it?
Skyrim.esm|0C02D2: Balgruuf, did you slip out again last night for a drink at the Bannered Mare?
Skyrim.esm|0C02D3: Have you met Rorik? He owns these lands, and it's from him that our village gets its name. We've been friends for many years now. Rorik fought for the Empire in the Great War. He was gravely wounded, and so was brought before me. I was a healer then, you see. We were as close as kinsmen, and when Rorik returned home I came with him. I am happy to spend my twilight years here with my good friend.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D5: Nonsense. If Balgruuf falls, someone will take his place, for better or worse. In the meantime, we have a war to win. And win it we must.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D6: Not yet, anyway, though it certainly could. With most of the hold's fighting men committed to the war, there are fewer swords to keep the roads safe from brigands and wild beasts. And of course, as the war drags on and supplies run low, our ample fields and stores of food will make a tempting target for desperate men. The best we can hope for: a swift end to the conflict. The longer the war goes on, the worse it will be for all of us, I'm afraid.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D7: Our coffers are nearly empty. I can scarcely afford to pay the guards, and we need every one of them in these troubled times. You'll have to make do with what we have, Proventus.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D8: And if an assassin slips past the night watch, we won't have a jarl at all. Without Balgruuf's leadership, Whiterun will descend into chaos.
Skyrim.esm|0C02D9: Aye, that I did. I commanded a force of several dozen men, most of them levies from villages in this part of the hold. I damn near met my end in that war. An Aldmeri soldier ran me through with his blade and left me for dead. A healer named Jouane saved my life. He's been my closest friend ever since. I tell you, that man is a miracle worker.
Skyrim.esm|0C02DA: My lord, since Ulfric Stormcloak's uprising, the cost of goods has doubled. I'll need more coin if I'm to provision us properly.
Skyrim.esm|0C02DB: What would you have me do? If we do not send men to fight in the war, you'll find yourself guarding one of Ulfric's men.
Skyrim.esm|0C02DC: Yes, that's right. Look around you. Most of the lands you see are mine. Most of this I purchased while my comrades were fighting in the south, helping the Empire against the Aldmeri Dominion. Back then, nothing would grow here and so the land was worthless. Now, thanks to the hard work and the gods' blessings, our farms prosper.
Skyrim.esm|0C02DD: Purchase more supplies, then. That's your job as steward, isn't it? Why do you trouble me with these details?
Skyrim.esm|0C02DE: I am concerned about the jarl's safety. Too many of our guards have been redeployed to fight the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|0C02DF: My lord, our provisions are running dangerously low. Our stores of meat, wine and grain are all but depleted.
Skyrim.esm|0C02E0: In Whiterun, mostly. Ennis handles the business arrangements, but I know that he has a few purchasers who give us a fair price. Ennis prides himself on his shrewd business schemes. For my part, I'm far happier working with cattle and crops than with people.
Skyrim.esm|0C02E1: That might be so, but it will never stop me from trying.
Skyrim.esm|0C02E2: Do I detect a bit of jealousy in your tone? I would hardly blame you. What calling could be more noble than this? I see in your eyes that you think I jest. I assure you, I don't. I am proud, and rightfully so, of the work we do here. Working the soil with your hands, seeing your seeds take root and grow, tending a herd... there is a joy in honest labor you won't find elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0C02E3: Dammit, woman, I'm the Jarl of Whiterun! I won't apologize for talking to my people. You can't protect me every moment of the day.
Skyrim.esm|0C03D0: Wha- Your shoes? We get robbed by bandits, my clothes torn to shreds by some giant cat, our bodyguard eaten by a dragon...
Skyrim.esm|0C03D1: I hate it here. Those were my favorite shoes, ruined, and for what? We never even made it to the wedding.
Skyrim.esm|0C03E0: If there's one thing I hate more than a mean-spirited drunk, it's one who can't settle their debts.
Skyrim.esm|0C03E3: I hate this place. I hate the trees. I hate the rocks. And I hate the snow.
Skyrim.esm|0C03E9: Clearly you have no idea who I am you or you wouldn't dare approach me.
Skyrim.esm|0C03EA: Just get away from me. We've got nothing left to steal.
Skyrim.esm|0C03EB: I bet the wedding was amazing... and we missed the whole thing.
Skyrim.esm|0C03EF: Leave me alone. I just want to find my way out of this accursed province.
Skyrim.esm|0C03F0: When we get back to Cyrodiil, I swear I will hire an army of battlemages to come up here and set fire to the whole damned province.
Skyrim.esm|0C03F1: All right... All right... Fine. I thought we were friends, but you're a real jerk, you know that?
Skyrim.esm|0C03F2: Are you kidding me? I fought off all those bandits. I'm not afraid of you.
Skyrim.esm|0C03F3: And you're worried about your shoes?
Skyrim.esm|0C0419: J'zargo awaits word of how well his scrolls work.
Skyrim.esm|0C041A: J'zargo sees what you are up to. Very well.
Skyrim.esm|0C041B: It is difficult to admit, but J'zargo could still use some help.
Skyrim.esm|0C041C: Can you... can you possibly help J'zargo?
Skyrim.esm|0C041E: Hey, Razelan, remember when you told me I'd never amount to anything? Well, I don't think you should say things like that about the ambassador!
Skyrim.esm|0C041F: An old woman can get away with almost anything.
Skyrim.esm|0C0421: I'm sorry, Ambassador. I didn't mean to cause such a commotion. There's no need to have him thrown out on my account.
Skyrim.esm|0C0422: Yes, that's probably for the best. I really shouldn't risk getting on Elenwen's bad side.
Skyrim.esm|0C0423: I don't think so. Sorry. I would love to help you, but Elenwen can be very vindictive. I wouldn't want to get on her bad side.
Skyrim.esm|0C0424: I know you're drunk as usual, but that's no excuse for insulting our hostess. I don't think anyone deserves that kind of abuse! I've known plenty of elves who were perfectly decent people.
Skyrim.esm|0C0426: That is absolutely untrue! Uh, that is, yes, of course. What... I don't understand why everyone... never mind.
Skyrim.esm|0C0427: Well, nothing would please me more. I'll keep their eyes away while you do what you came here for.
Skyrim.esm|0C042C: I don't even want to know, do I? But for you... all right. I'll do it.
Skyrim.esm|0C04A4: Ha ha! That's funny. Ya' think you can take me on? Try it anytime. I've been itching for a fight.
Skyrim.esm|0C04A5: The wizards called it the Quill of Gemination and demanded a tremendous amount of gold to have it made. If you can locate the quill and return it to me, something Vald was too stupid to do himself, I'll consider the debt satisfied.
Skyrim.esm|0C04A6: A few years back, I commissioned a unique quill from a good friend of mine in Winterhold. Vald was tasked with getting it here safely and it ended up at the bottom of Lake Honrich.
Skyrim.esm|0C04A7: Hang on Mercer! I'm coming to help!
Skyrim.esm|0C04A9: Vald? Why would you want to help that idiot? He owes me a steep debt. In fact, he's lucky all I did was force him to work for Mercer.
Skyrim.esm|0C04AA: Really? I wrote that off a long time ago. Well, I suppose I need to fulfill my end of the bargain. Give this document to Vald. It frees him from the debt, but I never want to see him in Riften again.
Skyrim.esm|0C04AB: I had Vald meet the caravan from Winterhold on the western end of the lake so he could avoid the roads. The idiot got lost in the mist and ran his boat right over some rocks, sending it straight to the bottom. He's too stupid to remember where he was, and any attempt I've made to find it in the past has been unsuccessful.
Skyrim.esm|0C04AC: Wow, sure is nice of ya'. Here, hang on to the key and don't let anyone in!
Skyrim.esm|0C04AD: Vald said his rowboat struck some rocks... I'd check under the water near one of the small islands in the lake. Perhaps you should start at the docks and work your way out towards Goldenglow Estate.
Skyrim.esm|0C04AE: Just find a way in, get the information and leave. And you have permission to kill anyone that stands in your way.
Skyrim.esm|0C04AF: But I can't leave here! I'm supposed to watch his house. What do I do?
Skyrim.esm|0C04B0: Oh come on, I ain't that dumb.
Skyrim.esm|0C04B1: Be careful, lass. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you.
Skyrim.esm|0C04B2: Fortunately, the quill required an ink with a unique formula known only to myself. So if you're getting any ideas about selling it to someone else or keeping it, you'll find it would hold little value.
Skyrim.esm|0C04B3: Do what he says, Vex. This ain't helpin' right now.
Skyrim.esm|0C04B4: The quill was supposed to allow me to duplicate any writing I'm viewing in exacting detail. I'm sure you can understand how useful that would prove in a business situation, especially where signatures were involved.
Skyrim.esm|0C04B5: Fine. We do it your way. For now.
Skyrim.esm|0C04B6: I don't know how ya' talked Maven into letting me go, and I don't care. You did something for me, I'm gonna do something for you. Here, take the key to Mercer's house. Won't need it any more.
Skyrim.esm|0C04CA: Look for a way out! We are not going to burn to death in our own damned Sanctuary!
Skyrim.esm|0C04CB: Come on! We've got to find a way out of here!
Skyrim.esm|0C04EA: Oh dear. That wasn't supposed to happen.
Skyrim.esm|0C04EB: Well, I... I simply thought you might wish to help out one of your fellow mages. If that's not enough, I suppose... I have this amulet you could take.
Skyrim.esm|0C04EC: This really isn't turning out the way I'd hoped.
Skyrim.esm|0C04EF: Please hold still, and don't move.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F0: We're all trying to become better mages, right? Well, I need someone I can practice a few spells on. Nothing dangerous, really. Would you be willing to assist me?
Skyrim.esm|0C04F1: I still need someone to practice a few spells on. Interested?
Skyrim.esm|0C04F2: Oh no, that's not it at all.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F3: Oh. Well, if you change your mind, let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F5: I'm not really sure. I think it's just a minor miscalculation on my part. I'm sure it will wear off soon. When it does, you'll be perfectly fine, and then we can try again. In the meantime, I'll see about figuring out what went wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F6: Just wait. Just... I can fix this.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F7: It's not! It shouldn't be. You'll be back to normal in no time. And when you are, we can try this again and I'll be sure to get it right.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F8: I still need some help, if you can spare the time.
Skyrim.esm|0C04F9: If you have a moment, could you help me with something?
Skyrim.esm|0C04FA: This will only take me a moment...
Skyrim.esm|0C04FC: I am so very sorry. I went over this again and again, and I was sure it would have better results.
Skyrim.esm|0C04FD: Oh. J'zargo takes this to mean it did not go... well. This was not the intention, to be sure. It is true that J'zargo was slightly concerned there may have been... problems. But you were not placed in harm's way on purpose. Still, you are a good friend to J'zargo now, for taking this risk. J'zargo thanks you.
Skyrim.esm|0C04FE: Okay, now don't move at all.
Skyrim.esm|0C04FF: Oh, good. I was afraid I'd have to go ask J'zargo. Now, you just stand there...
Skyrim.esm|0C0500: Oh, good. I was afraid I'd have to rely on Onmund. Now, you just stand there...
Skyrim.esm|0C0501: Thank you. I was afraid I'd have to figure out some way to do this on my own. Now, you just stand there...
Skyrim.esm|0C0502: No? It is true, J'zargo suspected there might be problems. But this is most disappointing. It is good that you were willing to help J'zargo. This will not be forgotten, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0C0504: You have to hold still or this isn't going to work.
Skyrim.esm|0C0505: No, not that kind of spell. Believe me when I say I wouldn't be asking if it weren't necessary.
Skyrim.esm|0C0506: Ah. No, that was not supposed to happen. But J'zargo is pleased to see that you are here, and not in pieces! You are a good friend to have done this. J'zargo will not forget it.
Skyrim.esm|0C0507: Do you... do you feel all right? You look very, umm, green.
Skyrim.esm|0C0508: I'll get it right this time, I swear.
Skyrim.esm|0C050E: This is gettin' dangerous. Don't let anythin' get the jump on you.
Skyrim.esm|0C050F: While you're in there, help yourself to anything in Frey's manor. I would.
Skyrim.esm|0C0510: Careful at Mercer's place, I don't want to lose anyone else to that madman.
Skyrim.esm|0C0511: I'll watch for Mercer, just get the information we need.
Skyrim.esm|0C0512: Come on, we need to show the journal to the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0C0513: Can't talk long, got to keep my eyes open for Mercer.
Skyrim.esm|0C0514: Hold your thoughts until after we untangle this mess.
Skyrim.esm|0C0515: Need to stay focused in case Frey comes back. Make it fast.
Skyrim.esm|0C0517: I can't believe this is happening, lass.
Skyrim.esm|0C0518: Just take a step back and let me figure this out. Then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0C05D5: You had your chance to talk!
Skyrim.esm|0C0630: Please be careful here. The site isn't entirely secured.
Skyrim.esm|0C0631: Do you think there's treasure here? Something to make J'zargo a powerful wizard?
Skyrim.esm|0C0632: Who knows what we might find in here.
Skyrim.esm|0C0633: I'm not seeing signs of any warding magics at all. Either they're not here, or they were removed long ago.
Skyrim.esm|0C0634: This is better than sitting around talking about magic, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|0C0635: Can you imagine what it was like, living here so long ago? The first men in Tamriel, here...
Skyrim.esm|0C0636: I'm not so sure we should be here.
Skyrim.esm|0C0694: Svari, yes. It's been hard on her. The girl misses her uncle. I've tried to explain that Roggvir did something bad and that we all have to be accountable for our actions but... a child feels what she will.
Skyrim.esm|0C0695: Because the Dominion is a sleeping beast that Skyrim cannot slay alone. Because many Nords are part of the Imperial army even now. Because the food and resources we get from the Empire are important to our people. Because even if we can't openly worship him, Talos the god was once Tiber Septim the man, and this is his Empire. And Torygg wasn't ready to let it fall apart.
Skyrim.esm|0C0696: Not much. But as a bard, I find the whole affair depressing. There are no heroes in this war. No winners to be had and no real conclusion.
Skyrim.esm|0C0697: Well, it's a great big festival put on by the bards every year. They burn an effigy of old King Olaf. It's quite a bit of fun. The bards give out Spiced Wine during the festival, so they put in a big order. And then, for weeks after, demand is so high, I can barely keep up. But with the death of High King Torygg, the festival has been declared "in poor taste." So it looks like it won't happen this year.
Skyrim.esm|0C0698: They're... adequate. Honestly, I rely on the business brought in by the Burning of King Olaf, and that's not likely to happen this year.
Skyrim.esm|0C0699: Oh, yes. A secret family recipe. The only good thing my father Octieve ever did. In fact, it's so good, no one else in Skyrim even tries to make any.
Skyrim.esm|0C069A: No. Even after Istlod died, the Moot voted to make Torygg High King of Skyrim. But Ulfric was at that moot, continually talking about Skyrim's independence in terms just shy of treason. I don't think Ulfric knew how much Torygg respected him for that. If Ulfric had asked Torygg directly to stand up, to declare independence, Torygg might have done it.
Skyrim.esm|0C069B: Ulfric showed up at the gates of Solitude requesting an audience. We thought he was here to ask Torygg to declare independence. By the time we realized Ulfric was here to challenge Torygg... it was already too late.
Skyrim.esm|0C06A2: Because Ulfric needed a symbol. Someone he could defeat that represented the Empire, the White-Gold Concordat, the banning of Talos worship. Torygg's father Istlod had held Skyrim together for nearly twenty-five years. When he died, Torygg became that symbol.
Skyrim.esm|0C06A3: I was, to my shame. The whole court was in attendance. I've seen much in my time, but that was a gruesome day.
Skyrim.esm|0C06CB: That I do. I had a messenger from Dragon Bridge stop here on his way to the Blue Palace, not too long ago. He was very nervous, mentioned something about needing help.
Skyrim.esm|0C06CC: At times. Jaree-Ra stopped by the other day. Told me to keep an eye out for him, see if any new people arrived in the city.
Skyrim.esm|0C06DB: If we send half our guards to aid the legions, just for a few days, it could tip the scales in the Empire's favor. The Companions can help protect the city, and the Emperor will be in our debt.
Skyrim.esm|0C06DC: I haven't changed my mind, if that's what you're asking. I won't leave Whiterun defenseless, even for a day.
Skyrim.esm|0C06DE: Have you lost all sense? We are short on guards as it is! If we lose half our men, the Dark Brotherhood and Thieves Guild will have the run of the city. The Stormcloaks will be the least of our worries! The answer is no, I tell you! Now stop pestering me with your warmongering!
Skyrim.esm|0C06FC: Everyone who's anyone. Viarmo bought an apple the other day, said he was looking for a new student.
Skyrim.esm|0C0705: Not lately. Business has been slow.
Skyrim.esm|0C07AD: You have no business here, so keep walking.
Skyrim.esm|0C07C0: Not much, to be honest. Their return was a shock to us all. Giraud Gemane has some tomes about them in the library, if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0C07E8: I like to think I've aged well. I'm certainly wiser than I once was. And I know when one should speak... and when one should hold their tongue.
Skyrim.esm|0C07EE: By Nord custom, once the challenge was issued in court, Torygg had no choice but to accept. Had he not, Ulfric would have had cause to call a new moot and a new vote for high king. Torygg had some martial training, of course, but it mattered little that day. When Ulfric's lips parted, when he unleashed the power of the Thu'um... That Shout, that ancient and terrible tongue... ripped Torygg asunder.
Skyrim.esm|0C07FD: Very. I helped raise him. Oh, I could not have been more proud to see Torygg on the throne. He made a fine king. A fine king...
Skyrim.esm|0C0808: You have asked for my help. I am providing it.
Skyrim.esm|0C080A: Mmm, sorry. I really can't risk it. I'm sure you can find someone else with a little less dignity.
Skyrim.esm|0C080C: Go ahead and play your little joke.
Skyrim.esm|0C080E: Sorry, but you'll have to find someone else to help you with your prank.
Skyrim.esm|0C080F: Don't worry. I'll play my part.
Skyrim.esm|0C0810: Yes, this probably isn't really the place for your little jokes.
Skyrim.esm|0C0811: Thank you. I knew you'd understand.
Skyrim.esm|0C0812: I'll try to forget you ever asked.
Skyrim.esm|0C0814: I'm sorry, but I really can't.
Skyrim.esm|0C0815: I'm doing as you asked. This is for some good purpose, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0C0816: Tell your men to stand down. I don't need any help dealing with this kind of fool. He isn't worth the trouble. I apologize for the disruption to such a festive occasion.
Skyrim.esm|0C0817: Dear me. All this trouble over me? I think I was confused. There's no harm in him. Please, let him go.
Skyrim.esm|0C0818: Oh... I'm afraid I may have overreacted. Please, I've already forgotten whatever it was he said to me. I'm sure he's learned his lesson. Let's try to enjoy ourselves.
Skyrim.esm|0C0819: Be about your business, while I'm about mine.
Skyrim.esm|0C081A: Begone, serpent! Begone from this house and trouble it no more! Beware of the serpent in your midst! Beware, oh people of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|0C081B: There's no excuse for your behavior! You behave like you're carousing in a house of ill repute, not the residence of the Thalmor ambassador!
Skyrim.esm|0C081C: You're a disgrace to the Empire. To think that you're actually the representative of the East Empire Company. When people talk about the decadent, tottering Empire, it's people like you they have in mind!
Skyrim.esm|0C081D: Go ahead and play your joke.
Skyrim.esm|0C081E: Say no more. I was wondering what you were doing here... I'm glad to play my part.
Skyrim.esm|0C081F: I trust you don't presume on our friendship. I'm a jarl, after all. I wouldn't want to appear foolish in front of these people.
Skyrim.esm|0C0820: Hmm. I don't want to get involved in anything beneath my dignity as jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0C0821: Oh... perhaps not, then. I don't think the Jarl of Solitude should be participating in a childish prank.
Skyrim.esm|0C0822: I don't know... I don't want to do anything that might offend Elenwen. There is a lot of money at stake, after all.
Skyrim.esm|0C0823: It's all for a good cause, old chap. Ulfric Stormcloak? The rightful high king? That's bordering on treason, man! Stand up. Stand up, I say!
Skyrim.esm|0C0824: What...! Do you know who I am? I should have you taken up and flogged!
Skyrim.esm|0C0825: You've got a lot of nerve, suggesting something like that, to me of all people! If this was Riften, I'd have you thrown in the dungeon!
Skyrim.esm|0C0826: You asked for my help. Not for nothing, I hope?
Skyrim.esm|0C0827: I'm doing as you asked. Not for nothing, I hope?
Skyrim.esm|0C0828: Razelan. What is it this time?
Skyrim.esm|0C0829: I don't understand... did you say Ulfric Stormcloak? Fine fellow, although a bit too fond of... I mean, no, of course, I condemn him in the most...
Skyrim.esm|0C082A: Hmm? Did you say snakes? Where? I hate them, always slithering the way they do... What, are they on me?
Skyrim.esm|0C082B: What? Of course I know who you are... I didn't mean to suggest, that is, I don't remember even saying that...
Skyrim.esm|0C082E: My ears are always open to a friend's request.
Skyrim.esm|0C082F: Say on, friend. What do you need?
Skyrim.esm|0C0830: I'm always glad to help a friend if I can. What do you need?
Skyrim.esm|0C0831: This is preposterous. Disrespect... insulting! I am tired of being hounded from all sides!
Skyrim.esm|0C0832: Madame Ambassador, you know that I would never have behaved... that is to say... well, I swear I'm innocent this time!
Skyrim.esm|0C0833: I've got this. Do what you need to do.
Skyrim.esm|0C0834: I hope you're enjoying the music, sir.
Skyrim.esm|0C0835: I hope you're enjoying the music, ma'am.
Skyrim.esm|0C0836: These parties are a great way to earn a little extra money.
Skyrim.esm|0C0837: It's good practice. Most people barely notice what I'm playing.
Skyrim.esm|0C0838: I hope I get into the Bards College one day.
Skyrim.esm|0C0846: Well, I really would like to make sure I've corrected what happened last time. Please give me a chance to try again.
Skyrim.esm|0C0847: Well, it all worked out in the end, didn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0C0848: Good, good. It'll be worth it, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0C084A: Yes, but it needs to be kept quiet. I may have entered into an... agreement with Enthir. He had something I needed, so I traded him something of mine. It was a mistake, and now I want back what I gave him. It's an amulet that belonged to my family, and I never should've given it to Enthir. But he won't deal with me. Talk to him; see what it'll take to get my amulet back.
Skyrim.esm|0C085D: Exactly as I said it would. Now, are you ready to try again? I am confident it will work this time.
Skyrim.esm|0C0892: I need your help with something. Something private.
Skyrim.esm|0C0893: Oh, no it hasn't. Let's be honest. I have a long way to go before I'm ready for the kind of advanced magic I've been attempting. But at least I know where to start now. Thank you, you've been a great help to me.
Skyrim.esm|0C0895: Agreed. And thank you for your help. You're a good friend.
Skyrim.esm|0C0B76: Damn outsider. Why couldn't you just mind your own business?
Skyrim.esm|0C0B77: You're no liar. Best fight I've had in years. There you are. If you ever need another blade at your side, just ask. Wouldn't mind seeing how you handle a few trolls.
Skyrim.esm|0C0BBF: Every day the threat of exile from Riften draws closer for myself and my family. Who knows how many spies the Empire has sent into our midst already. We're at war. This isn't the time for hospitality. Thank goodness Mother is keeping a level head about everything. If my brother Saerlund had his way, we'd be flying Imperial colors by now.
Skyrim.esm|0C0BC0: Ulfric only cares about one thing... Ulfric. He's ordained himself the future High King of Skyrim and steps on anyone that gets in his way. He's begun a rebellion against those that wish to eradicate the worship of Talos and uses it as his rallying cry. His cause may be true, but the man is a lie... all he holds in his heart is lust for the throne.
Skyrim.esm|0C0BC1: Because I did something not a soul in this bloodstained house of war has the backbone to do... dared to speak my mind. I dared speak of the Empire and the lies that have been spread by Ulfric, the leader of the Stormcloaks. Now my mother's stripped me of my heritage and incarcerated me here like a common criminal and my brother has all but disowned me. Be wary what you say around here, friend. You'll find not all take kindly to "insurrection."
Skyrim.esm|0C0BC2: Good. Wait until I start the distraction, then show me what you're made of.
Skyrim.esm|0C0BC3: Can you believe that fool had the audacity to speak of his love for the Empire in the plaza? He expects us to drop our defenses and greet them with open arms... to dialogue with them rather than defend our homeland by spilling their blood. He's a traitor, plain and simple. Had I been sitting on the throne, he'd be hanging from the gallows for his sympathies towards the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0C0C16: I know it looks bad, but we've got to figure out what happened. And if that dragon is still skulking around somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0C0DFD: It's not an insult, just an observation. If I were walking around with a wound, I would want you to tell me to see a doctor. Your outfit is something of an open wound... where attire is concerned.
Skyrim.esm|0C0DFE: Not many people care, these days. The world is the worse for it.
Skyrim.esm|0C0DFF: We mostly carry herbs and alchemical ingredients, that sort of thing. Before the war, our stock was mostly used for aromatic use in homes, yes. These days, we have more people like you coming in, looking for healing and the like.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E01: I suppose I would, but you're the only one who gets that greeting.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E02: It's never been an issue. People say you helped with the problems at Wolfskull Cave. I applaud anyone who assists the court.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E03: Oh, we do all right. Rumor is, you had a hand in the Blackblood Marauders' demise... I'm impressed.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E04: Radiant Raiment has the finest attire in all of Skyrim. That is how we... Wait. You're that new bard. The one who saved the festival, aren't you? That was a good bit of work.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E05: You should look into joining. They could use every hand they can get.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E06: But I'm not telling you anything you don't already know.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E07: No, no. Mostly, I'm just a simple herbalist. Lami over in Morthal should be able to help you.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E08: No, no. I'm just a simple herbalist. You'll want to see Arcadia, over in Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0C0E16: It's almost all I have time for these days. The men training here in Solitude and the troops out in the field, they all need weapons and armor.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F18: Only when you're around. The rest of these fools I can do without.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F19: You'd think so, wouldn't you? I'm fine. Thank you for asking.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F1A: It is when my customers waste time chatting, and not buying.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F1B: I think I would, if it weren't for my son. And my wife. Oh, Sayma would have my hide if I even suggested I might step foot on a battlefield. You think Stormcloaks and Legionnaires are dangerous? Ha!
Skyrim.esm|0C0F1D: Nothing at all. It's very... nice. If you want something with a little more style, however, you should come by our shop. Radiant Raiment. We could fix you right up.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F1E: I do. So few people possess the taste and sophistication of a proper wardrobe these days. Be sure and investigate Radiant Raiment's fine selection of clothing. We have many items you'll fancy, I'm sure.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F1F: As high priest of the Nine Divines, I bid you welcome.
Skyrim.esm|0C0F20: We welcome you to worship the Nine Divines, freely and openly.
Skyrim.esm|0C0FA2: Blessings of the Nine Divines upon you. How may I help?
Skyrim.esm|0C0FA3: May the Divines guide and keep you.
Skyrim.esm|0C0FA4: May you be saved by the Divines, as I once was.
Skyrim.esm|0C0FA7: I hid here in Solitude for a long time, but war... war follows a man until it's done with him.
Skyrim.esm|0C0FAC: You shouldn't leave Solitude without trying our fish. Best in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0C0FB0: Mother says I shouldn't talk to people...
Skyrim.esm|0C0FD0: If you want to talk about my brother, I'm not interested.
Skyrim.esm|0C0FD1: So good to see you again. How can I be of service?
Skyrim.esm|0C0FD3: No. That was an ugly affair. High King Torygg's death has turned the whole town on its head. Between you and me? He wasn't even that good a king. All of those rambling speeches about the Empire this, and the Empire that...
Skyrim.esm|0C138F: We sure showed them, eh? Thanks for your part in all that.
Skyrim.esm|0C1390: Good job out there. Glad you were with us.
Skyrim.esm|0C1391: Thanks. Couldn't have done that without you.
Skyrim.esm|0C15CA: Kind of fun to push people around sometimes, isn't it? Good work, sister.
Skyrim.esm|0C15E6: You seem to have a knack for this kind of thing. I'll make sure people in charge know.
Skyrim.esm|0C15E7: I figured you had it in you. Nicely done.
Skyrim.esm|0C15EB: Try not to get carried away.
Skyrim.esm|0C15FB: Not bad, not bad. The intensity of the hunt is special, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0C15FC: Good to hear. Have your share of the pay.
Skyrim.esm|0C1788: But I don't want to be patient! I want my dress right now! Father promised!
Skyrim.esm|0C1789: It takes a while to make a dress, Dagny. You have to be patient.
Skyrim.esm|0C178A: I'm so angry, I can hardly stand it! Father promised me a new dress days ago. Now where is it?
Skyrim.esm|0C178B: Damn right it has. Demand's never been so high, at least not since I built the new mill.
Skyrim.esm|0C178C: Leave me out of your intrigues, Hrongar. If you have something to say to the jarl, say it with your own tongue.
Skyrim.esm|0C178D: My husband owns it, barely. He'll lose it soon enough. He's behind on his loan payments, but he drinks all his money away just the same. Now we're deep in debt, penniless and about to lose our sole source of income.
Skyrim.esm|0C178E: I'm not asking you to defy the jarl, just to open his eyes. Use subtlety and suggestion to turn his thoughts to the war. You're his steward, he'll listen to you.
Skyrim.esm|0C178F: No, I openly welcome the presence of armed strangers who have no reason to care about any of us. Of course it bothers me! I'm sure it's only a matter of time until they seize our mill in the name of their war effort. Next thing you know, there'll be more guards, and then we'll lose our homes as well. This is just the start of it, mark my words.
Skyrim.esm|0C1790: What would you have me do, Hrongar? Your brother is the jarl. You know I can't question his judgement.
Skyrim.esm|0C1792: Proventus, you must help me. We need to convince my brother to do more for the war effort.
Skyrim.esm|0C1793: I'm going to tell Father you said that. He'll tan your hide for sure!
Skyrim.esm|0C1794: All you ever do is complain about what you want. You're a spoiled baby.
Skyrim.esm|0C1799: This isn't an order of cowards. I'll find someone more agreeable, I'm sure.
Skyrim.esm|0C18B4: Be cautious. Beasts are not nearly as predictable as men. Give the thing a quick death.
Skyrim.esm|0C18B5: Good. I knew that we could count on you. It's simply a beast, but be cautious. The beasts of Skyrim are made of sterner stuff than most.
Skyrim.esm|0C18B6: I knew we could count on you. Get it taken care of right away. Nobody wants an animal left roaming around in their place.
Skyrim.esm|0C18B9: You're not afraid, are you? Of animals? Come back when you find your spine.
Skyrim.esm|0C18C1: Unfortunate, but someone else will take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0C18C3: I see. Well, should you change your mind, the contract will be available for a while.
Skyrim.esm|0C191F: Orchendor? Kesh knew him. He is an overseer. Shepherd. Gathers the Afflicted, contains the festering wound. Orchendor and his Afflicted are meant to stand ready, awaiting Peryite's command to cover the world with his Blessing.
Skyrim.esm|0C1920: Not far, not far. This one looks upon it on clear days. Look to the west, at the foot of the mountain. The dwarven ruins there. Bthardamz.
Skyrim.esm|0C1921: The pettiness of mortals. I had almost forgotten. I can grant you a powerful token for this task. An escutcheon of Dwemer make, capable of shielding you from both the spell and sword. Return when the elf lies dead and it will be yours.
Skyrim.esm|0C1922: An impertinent question. The elf must die. Either carry out my will or do not. You are not the only mortal agent I could choose.
Skyrim.esm|0C1923: So you have, mortal. Go now. Kill Orchendor.
Skyrim.esm|0C199C: I'm really getting tired of your excuses. When you borrowed the money, you said you'd pay it back on time and for double the usual fee.
Skyrim.esm|0C199D: Look, Shadr. Last warning. Pay up or else. All I care about is the gold. Everything else is your problem.
Skyrim.esm|0C199E: What? Are you telling me you robbed it? Why? Why are you doing this to me?
Skyrim.esm|0C199F: Next time, keep your plans quieter and nothing would have happened to it.
Skyrim.esm|0C19A0: I know I did. But how was I to know the shipment would get robbed?
Skyrim.esm|0C19ED: It is no matter, this fool will be the next act of the show.
Skyrim.esm|0C19EF: I thought I was a goner just now. Thanks for the help, and if you need any supplies I have a few to sell.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A19: You will be my blood offering to Movarth!
Skyrim.esm|0C1A1A: Another victim fell for our ruse!
Skyrim.esm|0C1A37: And my Aunt wouldn't have to worry about her daughter.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A38: If you want something a bard can dig into look to the dragons. A thousand years from now Skyrim will have changed rulers dozens of times but the return of the dragons, that story is once in an era.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A39: I don't suppose it matters much anymore. As long as Ulfric wishes Elisif to remain jarl, remain jarl she will. And the thanes will continue to follow her orders. As long as those orders came from Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A3A: Well... okay. My father owns the Winking Skeever. But it's the family business, so it'll be mine when he kicks off.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A3B: My father owned it until he... passed on.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A3C: I shouldn't be. Everyone wants to ask me about Mama and Uncle Roggvir. Maybe if I don't talk, they won't either.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A3E: Thank you. I'm still at the beginning of my lessons. Dean Six Fingers used to say I was quite good with the flute.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A3F: You really don't know? He opened the gate for Ulfric Stormcloak. Ulfric rides in, defeats the high king, and Roggvir was the one that let him ride right back out. And now the Stormcloaks have claimed the entire city. I guess we have Roggvir to thank for that as well.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A40: My husband. He was the blacksmith up at Castle Dour.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A41: If you're looking for truth instead, see Noster. He's a good example of what life has to offer. And if you're just looking to feel good, go get some Spiced Wine from Evette. But whatever you do, just leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A42: But you won't get any better than this from me.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A43: Now that Sybille Stentor is out of the picture, I am indeed. And the court is, well... free of her... eccentricities. Moving into her quarters was an interesting process.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A45: Stick around! Still plenty of songs to sing and mead to drink.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A46: Yes. My older brother. He was a bit of a fop, really. And truth be told, since Torygg's death, he had no real power in the court. But still... he didn't deserve to die.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A49: I've got to get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0C1A9D: It's been easier lately. The Blackblood Marauders haven't been here since the Icerunner attack, so my shipments have been more regular.
Skyrim.esm|0C1AA9: I provide my wisdom and insight to the court.
Skyrim.esm|0C1AAA: It does indeed. You know, I hear you had something to do with Sybille Stentor's demise... That must have been a hard bit of business. I hear Stentor had been alive for a long, long time.
Skyrim.esm|0C1AAB: No. Although it's true my husband Torygg was the high king and I am his widow, I am in no position to claim such a title. Ulfric Stormcloak is victorious, and the jarls have fallen in line. It seems I will never take my rightful place as high queen.
Skyrim.esm|0C1AAD: These days? Aye. Most of what I make is for folk like you.
Skyrim.esm|0C1AB8: But we must have the support of the other jarls for that claim to have any meaning. Should they accept her rule, we will have peace.
Skyrim.esm|0C1AB9: Because killing her would yield no political advantage. By leaving Elisif alive, Ulfric completes the separation of the Empire and Skyrim. With Ulfric's troops here, Elisif will be forced to support his bid for high king. He secures the position and appears above reproach. What's more, Ulfric's refusal to execute his slain enemy's wife will be portrayed as the ultimate act of compassion. Hmph. It's genius, really.
Skyrim.esm|0C1ABA: That's the real question, isn't it? No one can deny Elisif has a legitimate claim to become High Queen of Skyrim...
Skyrim.esm|0C1ABB: Well... By rights, she had a legitimate claim as High Queen of Skyrim. But now Skyrim is at Ulfric's mercy. The Moot will meet and choose Ulfric as high king. With his troops in most of the cities, they have little choice.
Skyrim.esm|0C1ABC: Perhaps, but necessary. As you know my cousin, Fura, died out there fighting for us. She wouldn't have had to, if it weren't for Roggvir.
Skyrim.esm|0C1ACD: Not much. But as a bard, I find the whole affair depressing. There are no heroes in this war. Even with Ulfric here in Solitude, there are no winners to be had. No real conclusion.
Skyrim.esm|0C1ACE: You won't get anything out of me, you filthy sons o' horkers.
Skyrim.esm|0C1ACF: There's still Imperial camps to be cleaned up, as you know. And that takes arms and armor.
Skyrim.esm|0C1B4D: I swear, I left another bottle right here...
Skyrim.esm|0C1BC7: I got some fine armor, if you're lookin'.
Skyrim.esm|0C1C44: Wanderer like you must have plenty of tales to tell. But I'm afraid I'm too busy to hear 'em.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D48: Papa used to say I was greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him. I miss him...
Skyrim.esm|0C1D4C: So, what are your needs? Perhaps a pinch of this, and a bit of that? Hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0C1D4D: If you're looking for potions, I think I can be of service.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D51: Beirand said you got here just before the execution. Must have been a lovely first sight.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D52: Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand did. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D53: Welcome. Look around. We've got everything you could want in life. At least... that's what my Beirand always told me...
Skyrim.esm|0C1D54: I thought adventurers were supposed to look tough?
Skyrim.esm|0C1D55: I bet my papa could have beat you up. He was stronger than anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D56: I get to help Mother with the shop. I used to help Papa too... till I tried to use the forge...
Skyrim.esm|0C1D57: I can help with purchases, but if you've come to talk, see my sister. She's the one who gets on with people. It's her one virtue, really.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D58: Have a drink, rest your feet and stay a little while.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D59: I'm just running some errands for the old man... Err... For myself. I'm just running some errands for myself...
Skyrim.esm|0C1D5A: You don't want to talk to me. I'm just a stand in for a dead thane.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D5B: You get around, right? If you come across any Stormcloaks... gut one for me, huh? They did the same to my brother in Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D62: Think I'll forge me a new sword, just in case things go awry here in Solitude. I'm the blacksmith's apprentice, you see.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D63: We've got strong swords and sturdy armor, if you're looking to buy.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D65: Leave me be, Imperial. I haven't got anything to say to you.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D69: It's only a matter of time before Ahtar's blade finds my neck...
Skyrim.esm|0C1D6A: You wouldn't know it, but before my family got killed, I was the luckiest man in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D6B: Is this about the stables? With my husband gone, my son Horm handles the business.
Skyrim.esm|0C1D6C: Need a horse? I guess I'm the one to talk to, since my da passed...
Skyrim.esm|0C1D6D: Any day is a good one, as long as I don't have to go into town. But with Hjorunn gone...
Skyrim.esm|0C1DDB: They were a group of mages with a history that predates the Empire. Very powerful, very secretive. No one's seen them in well over a hundred years. They vanished, along with their sanctuary on the Isle of Artaeum. I have no idea what connection they'd have to this place.
Skyrim.esm|0C1DFC: I was able to work out a deal with the stables in Whiterun to sell me some of their tack and harnesses. I borrowed some gold from Sapphire to pay for the shipment, but it got robbed before it even arrived. Now Sapphire wants her money back, and if I don't pay her, I think she's going to kill me.
Skyrim.esm|0C1DFD: I owe a great deal of money to someone and I think they cheated me. I don't know what to do.
Skyrim.esm|0C1DFE: Yes. Hofgrir took me in when I arrived in Riften about a year ago. He's been teaching me everything he knows. Horses are my whole life. Back in Hammerfell, I helped my family raise horses at our farm. I hope to open my own stables one day, but I think it's going to take a lot longer than I expected.
Skyrim.esm|0C1DFF: I don't have any business with you, so get out of my face.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E01: Not half as sorry as I am.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E02: If it's horses you want, you'll want to speak to Hofgrir. I'm afraid I'm in too much trouble to help you.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E03: If it's horses you want, I'm not sure I can help you. I have too much on my mind as it is.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E04: If it's horses you want, you're talking to the right person.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E19: Follow me. I will show you how to open the path to Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E1A: Paarthurnax is the only one who can answer your questions about Dragonrend.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E1C: Use Clear Skies to open the way to Paarthurnax.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E1D: Ah, I see. Very well, my friend. I'll do it.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E1E: Oh... all right. If it's so important to you, I suppose I can help.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E1F: Very well. I suppose there's nothing wrong with a bit of harmless fun.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E20: This is neither the time nor place for such things. I'm surprised you would ask it of me.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E44: Finally, we have an agreement. I look forward to having it back soon. The sooner, the better, in fact.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E47: It's an entirely different situation. I don't expect you to comprehend the nuances of these matters. Regardless, I'm making you a very simple offer. You get me the staff, and I give you Onmund's precious amulet. Do we have a deal?
Skyrim.esm|0C1E48: I told you, it's from my family. I don't... I know they've always disapproved of what I wanted in life, to learn magic instead of becoming a farmer, or a hunter. But no matter how poorly they may have treated me, they're still my family. I didn't consider that until after I made the trade.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E49: Well, yes. I was completely unaware of the reputation of the other party in this trade. I don't want that coming back to haunt me.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E4A: Ah, excellent. Well, here's the gold the jarl offered in exchange for taking care of the problem.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E4B: Ah, excellent. Well, it's good to know that won't be a problem any longer.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E4C: It doesn't matter. It's also none of your concern. Just talk to him; see if you can convince him to give my amulet back.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E4D: You're not very good at this, are you? Look, I traded a staff to someone and found out later that was a bad idea. So, I'd like the staff back. Understand now?
Skyrim.esm|0C1E4E: Let's pretend for a moment that a certain individual was looking to acquire a particular staff. Let's also pretend that he traded some valuable items for said staff. And it was only later that it was realized that this individual might... misuse such a staff. The resulting disaster would reflect quite poorly on everyone involved. Are you following me?
Skyrim.esm|0C1E4F: Look how persistent you are. It's charming, really. Onmund gave up the first time I said no. But not you... All right. I'll tell you a little story.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E50: It would seem there's some sort of disturbance. From what few details we were given, it sounds quite familiar. I suspect that the power released by the Eye of Magnus was not fully contained, and has coalesced in this part of Skyrim. I do hope you still have the Staff of Magnus someplace. I suspect you're going to need it.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E51: There's another report of a disturbance in this hold. Best if it's taken care of before things get out of hand.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E52: There's another report of a disturbance in that hold. Best if it's taken care of before things get out of hand.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E53: Actually, yes, Arch-Mage. I believe there is.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E54: No, no. You're not understanding this. Onmund made a deal. He made that choice, and now he has to live with it.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E55: Ha! I didn't think he'd really give it back. Thank you, friend. It's good to know I can count on you.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E56: I wonder... what effect might your spells have?
Skyrim.esm|0C1E58: Oh, I suppose. But you keep this to yourself; I don't want it being known that I go back on my trades. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|0C1E59: Yes, you see, the thing is... I'm not concerned with "everyone's best interest." I find I'm only concerned with my own.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E5A: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E5B: We've become aware of a problem in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E5C: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E5D: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E5E: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E5F: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E60: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E61: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E62: We've been contacted by the Jarl of Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E63: Oh, good. I was really afraid I'd lose it forever.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E64: Some kind of resonance... you and the wall. It must be connected to the amulet!
Skyrim.esm|0C1E65: Oh my. How precious. Onmund is too afraid to deal with me himself, and so he's sent you. I'll make this very simple for you. All my trades are final. Onmund knew this ahead of time, and went through with it anyway. So there's nothing more to be said.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E66: I've already made it very clear that all of my trades are final.
Skyrim.esm|0C1E8A: I'm not sticking around to fight a dragon!
Skyrim.esm|0C1F6D: Are you daft? This isn't the Dark Brotherhood! We don't go around murdering anyone, ever. It just isn't how things are done. I guess I shouldn't be surprised. Nothing around here has been going well lately.
Skyrim.esm|0C1F70: They live below the streets in the Ratway like dirty animals. And trust me, they're no better than the vermin that slink out of there for scraps. They make it difficult for decent people to earn a living. Of course, not many decent people live here in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|0C1F75: It's Riften's name for the sewers beneath our feet. Absolutely disgusting! If you fancy keeping your purse filled with gold and your head on your shoulders, I'd stay out of those awful tunnels.
Skyrim.esm|0C1F78: I may be Dark Elf by birth, but I was raised Argonian. Through reasons I'm still trying to discover, I ended up orphaned then taken in by a kindly Argonian family in Black Marsh. I hope one day to find out what happened to me... how I ended up like that.
Skyrim.esm|0C2137: If you're planning violence against me or mine, you'll regret it.
Skyrim.esm|0C2138: What do you want with us?
Skyrim.esm|0C2139: Please! Have mercy... I beg you...
Skyrim.esm|0C2452: Perhaps it's because there's no better way to face the cold Skyrim nights than with a belly full of ale?
Skyrim.esm|0C2453: Oh, there's a better way. A buxom barmaid between the sheets will keeps me plenty warm. Better yet, she won't make my head ring the morning after.
Skyrim.esm|0C2462: In the very oldest tales, back from when there were still dragons in Skyrim, the Dragonborn would slay dragons and steal their power. That's what you did, isn't it? Absorbed the dragon's power?
Skyrim.esm|0C2467: Of a sort. I don't have formal training in the Restoration school, but... something just as good. Within these walls, I have all that I need to brew a potion for nearly any ailment. If I had a Septim for every case of Rockjoint or Witbane I've cured since I opened this shop, I'd be a rich woman indeed.
Skyrim.esm|0C2468: Adrianne. At her forge, just outside. Place is named after her. "Warmaiden's." It's a childhood nickname, given by her father. What I hear, she was playing with swords even back then. Anyway, our wares are strong and the prices fair. What else matters?
Skyrim.esm|0C2469: Oh, yes. Hunting is my great passion. That's why my brother and I opened a shop to sell hunting supplies. It's the Drunken Huntsman, down by the main gate. He tends the store, and I've got this stall.
Skyrim.esm|0C246A: Yes, I hear that question often. My brother Anoriath and I came up with it one night after consuming... rather too much mead. After our drinks, we went out for a moonlight hunt. We got separated, and in his drunken state my brother mistook me for a deer. He shot an arrow that pierced my... well, rump. After such a memorable adventure, we knew we had the name for our shop.
Skyrim.esm|0C246B: If my cleanin' gets in your way, just say the word an' I'll be off.
Skyrim.esm|0C246C: Ain't nobody high and mighty in these halls, exceptin' the jarl. And don't you forget!
Skyrim.esm|0C246D: This here is Dragonsreach, an' it's my task to see it looks fit for a jarl, night an' day.
Skyrim.esm|0C246E: My poor Fralia thinks Thorald's still alive, but I know better.
Skyrim.esm|0C24C4: We should keep moving. Still the draugr to worry about.
Skyrim.esm|0C24C5: Now let's move. I think that was the last of the Silver Hand, but still plenty of draugr for the both of us.
Skyrim.esm|0C24C6: This looks like the guardian chamber. The key to that door has to be around here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0C24C7: "Every door has a key," says Vilkas. Sometimes he is wrong, but look around anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0C24CA: I've been hearing rumors of Silver Hand activity.
Skyrim.esm|0C25B2: I'll take you down too. I'm not splitting this hoard with anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0C25B3: You'll pay for stealing from me you eyeless freak!
Skyrim.esm|0C273B: Let's go on ahead. See if the way is clear.
