Skyrim.esm|0C273E: I better stay back and see to the old man. Good luck you two.
Skyrim.esm|0C273F: All right, let's see if we can find a way out.
Skyrim.esm|0C2740: I'll keep watch in case Ulfric comes through here. Talos guide the both of you.
Skyrim.esm|0C277C: The staff! Use it on the Eye!
Skyrim.esm|0C277E: I am beyond your pathetic attempts at magic. You cannot touch me.
Skyrim.esm|0C278A: I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.
Skyrim.esm|0C27ED: Seems your purse is lighter than you thought.
Skyrim.esm|0C27F0: Now, now! That won't be necessary.
Skyrim.esm|0C28C6: I can't believe it! You're... Dragonborn...
Skyrim.esm|0C28C7: Yes. I think I am. You can Shout now. That can only mean one thing. You must be Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0C28C8: There's only one way to find out. Try to Shout... According to the old legends, only the Dragonborn can Shout without training, the way the dragons do.
Skyrim.esm|0C28CA: Well, you can Shout now. You couldn't before, right? That can only mean one thing. You must be Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0C28CB: There's only one way to find out. Try to Shout... that would prove it. According to the old legends, only the Dragonborn can Shout without training, the way the dragons do.
Skyrim.esm|0C28E3: Now let us see how quickly you can master your new Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|0C2A55: I do hope the court likes the verse. I think we've done an excellent job of recreating it.
Skyrim.esm|0C2C1F: Gods. A woman attacked right on the streets. Are you all right? Did you see what happened?
Skyrim.esm|0C2D01: Well done, mortal. All things are in their order, and Orchendor roams the Pits. His betrayal will be punished, and your obedience is rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|0C2D02: This one would try a shop. You might prefer the direct route, hm? Deathbell grow in the frozen tundra. You might nip into a mine for the ruby and silver. Vampire Dust is tricky. Vampires, you know.
Skyrim.esm|0C2D03: And yet you have done my bidding. Why, I wonder?
Skyrim.esm|0C2D04: The vapors are waning, but not yet gone. Take a breath. Peryite will speak if he sees fit.
Skyrim.esm|0C2D05: Did you leave any alive? The Afflicted are mere vessels for my Blessing. It will spread to others through their touch and my own. Another overseer will replace Orchendor, when the time comes. For now all is cleansed and ordered.
Skyrim.esm|0C2D06: Go, seek your fate. I will be watching, and perhaps we will meet again - afterwards.
Skyrim.esm|0C2D07: Embrace order and hard truth, mortal. Goodbye.
Skyrim.esm|0C2D08: Ahh, no. Don't think you've got the stuff. Come back when you've seen a bit more in life, perhaps.
Skyrim.esm|0C33B8: I think Giraud here would give you the best explanation of both it and the history of King Olaf's Verse. You should speak to him about it.
Skyrim.esm|0C33B9: Get the coat. Then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0C33BA: Nice to meet the person who went to King Olaf's tomb.
Skyrim.esm|0C33BB: He's sending you after King Olaf's Verse, then? That's good, we shouldn't leave it lying around now that I've figured out where it is. The verse was Svaknir's contribution to the Poetic Edda, the living history of Skyrim. Each bard adds to the Edda in his or her time.
Skyrim.esm|0C33BC: And a very ancient one. The verse criticized the reigning King Olaf. He was so incensed the bard was put to death and all the copies burned. At least, that's what we thought until I translated some ancient texts a year or so ago. We now believe King Olaf buried the truth with the bard. If I'm right, Svaknir and King Olaf's Verse lie in Dead Men's Respite, along with the burial chamber of King Olaf himself.
Skyrim.esm|0C3471: Not at all. That was as much a test of your abilities as it was your resolve. I wasn't sure your heart was in it. But you've proven me wrong. And I respect that. Now, if we're done being sentimental, it's time for you to take the Oath.
Skyrim.esm|0C3472: True, he's a dangerous and bloodthirsty man, but he's also a Nord that honors our traditions. Keep your wits about you and you won't be harmed. And then get back here. Because if Ulfric isn't bluffing, I'll need every able body to defend Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0C3473: Only the ones I'm not sure about. This will prove your abilities, but more importantly, it will prove your commitment.
Skyrim.esm|0C3474: "...and unwavering obedience to the officers of his great Empire."
Skyrim.esm|0C3475: My father, the great Bear of Eastmarch, died during my imprisonment after the Markarth Incident. I, his only son, forced to deliver his eulogy via a letter I had smuggled out of prison. Such is the love of Titus Mede for his subjects. When finally set free, I returned to Windhelm, and was greeted by a city in mourning, at one with my own grief and anger. Clamoring in angry voices, calling out for justice, for war, they sat me on the throne. The Throne of Ysgramor! The throne of my father... I only hope I can prove worthy of that honor.
Skyrim.esm|0C3477: Indeed, Elisif has become Jarl of Solitude, historically and conveniently home of the high king, backed by Imperial interests. But the Moot has not yet met to name her high queen. And they won't. Not as long as I have any say in it.
Skyrim.esm|0C3478: I'm originally from Cyrodiil, sent here at the request of the Emperor himself. I'm often ordered to places with problems that need fixing. Now, while I'm certain this is all terribly interesting to you, I have important matters to attend.
Skyrim.esm|0C3479: I'm sorry to hear that. If you change your mind, speak with Galmar. What does bring you to me?
Skyrim.esm|0C347A: There hasn't been a true high king in Skyrim for generations. For too long he's been hand-picked by the Emperor, and given emphatic nods by milk-drinking jarls addicted to Imperial coin. It's time we had a real high king. One of our own making.
Skyrim.esm|0C347B: Now, that's it. One free outfit per soldier. You lose that, or get it banged up, you'll need to pay like everyone else.
Skyrim.esm|0C347C: The Legion's always been here. Without us to keep order, the provinces would fall into barbarism and lawlessness. Especially Skyrim. Take for example, Ulfric Stormcloak and his little "rebellion." But rest assured, his days are numbered.
Skyrim.esm|0C347E: I challenged him in the traditional way, and he accepted. There were many witnesses. No "murder" was committed. True, he didn't stand a chance against me. But that was precisely the point! He was a puppet-king of the Empire, not a high king of Skyrim. His father before him perhaps, but not Torygg. He was too privileged and too foolish, more interested in entertaining his queen than ruling his country.
Skyrim.esm|0C347F: They don't make finer steel anywhere else. If this doesn't keep you from dying, nothing will.
Skyrim.esm|0C3480: I see. Then there's nothing further to discuss. If you change your mind, speak with the legate.
Skyrim.esm|0C3481: I killed Torygg to prove our wretched condition. How is the high king supposed to be the defender of Skyrim, if he can't even defend himself?
Skyrim.esm|0C3482: Alright. This here's enough to keep your insides from spilling out, except from the fiercest of blows, and shouldn't weigh you down too much.
Skyrim.esm|0C3484: Don't wait for me. Get to Korvanjund as soon as you're ready. I'll meet you there.
Skyrim.esm|0C3485: Alright... here you go. Should keep you light on your feet, and hold up decent enough if you wind up trading blows with the enemy.
Skyrim.esm|0C3486: One moment. But don't go far. I expect I'll need you.
Skyrim.esm|0C3487: I've made up my mind, even if my advisors are still squabbling. I'm sending you to Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0C3488: Well, then. Repeat after me. "Upon my honor I do swear undying loyalty to the Emperor..."
Skyrim.esm|0C3489: I remember you... You were at Helgen with us! Come to join the war? Speak with Galmar. He handles the new recruits.
Skyrim.esm|0C348A: Light armor for scouting detail, heavy armor for full on combat, or something in between?
Skyrim.esm|0C348B: I remember you, you were at Helgen. Speak to Legate Rikke. I suspect we could use someone like you.
Skyrim.esm|0C348C: Ulfric Stormcloak's head rolling away from his lifeless body.
Skyrim.esm|0C348D: I hope Ulfric will come to his senses. But I fear he's in too far to stop now. Likely only Ulfric's death, or the overthrow of the jarls supporting him will end this. Either way, much blood will be spilt.
Skyrim.esm|0C348E: First we'll kick the Thalmor and their bloody Imperial puppets out of the country. Then we will rebuild Skyrim into the land she once was. When we are done with that, we will take our army to the Dominion, and show those pointy-eared bastards not every man is fit to be their slave.
Skyrim.esm|0C348F: We're fighting because we're done bleeding for an Empire that won't bleed for us. Untold numbers of Nords died defending the Empire against the Dominion. And for what? Skyrim being sold to the Thalmor so the Emperor could keep his throne! We're fighting because our own jarls, once strong, wise men, have become fearful and blind to their people's suffering. We're fighting because Skyrim needs heroes, and there's no one else but us.
Skyrim.esm|0C3490: Yep. I just have one question for you. Light, medium, or heavy?
Skyrim.esm|0C34A6: Properly controlled, it has knowledge enough to turn paupers into kings.
Skyrim.esm|0C34A7: Besides, you're not scared of a few sleeping metal men. Are you, Drennen?
Skyrim.esm|0C3524: What are you waiting for? Get in there and find that mask.
Skyrim.esm|0C352B: Don't bother me if you haven't found anything about the mask.
Skyrim.esm|0C3534: It seems that after the strength of the cult was broken in the Dragon War, a last contingent came here to create a stronghold and regroup. King Harald's forces crushed stragglers here during the First Era and it has been haunted ever since, but the specifics of the siege are unclear.
Skyrim.esm|0C355B: They were among the first men to arrive from Atmora and they worshipped certain animals as avatars of the gods; dragons being foremost. And for their bloody sacrifices, the dragons gifted their priests with terrible power. But their cruelty was too great for men to bear, and they rose up in what would be called the Dragon War and the cult swept from the land.
Skyrim.esm|0C355D: You there, hold! If you are a true friend to Skyrim, you will help me in my mission.
Skyrim.esm|0C3562: Are you going to help me with my mission? I need someone very capable.
Skyrim.esm|0C368F: Isn't it obvious? Why, the wonderful weather and hospitable people, of course! Not to mention my great fondness for dragons and petty political power struggles. Ah, but without a doubt, the most compelling feature of this frozen wasteland is the volley of inane questions leveled at me on a regular basis.
Skyrim.esm|0C3690: Interesting, to say the least. What with all that's been going on. On the one hand, supplies are harder to come by, since fewer traders are traveling the roads these days. But on the other, the scarcity of supplies has led to some, well, "elevated" prices. All that matters is that I have what you need. And it's worth every septim.
Skyrim.esm|0C3691: That'll be the jarl, Balgruuf the Greater. He's an honorable enough man. Never done wrong by me or my kin. Things are tough for him now, I'd wager. What with the fighting, and the dragons. But Balgruuf's a tough old Nord. He can handle it.
Skyrim.esm|0C3692: Of course. Every night I pray that we don't get a visit from a dragon, or that two armies don't show up to fight over the bridge. You ask if I'm worried? I'm terrified, if you want the truth of it. But I have to stay strong for my wife and children.
Skyrim.esm|0C3693: Why, because I'm a proud citizen of the Empire? Because the Stormcloaks only want Nords in Skyrim? The Stormcloaks can rot in Oblivion for all I care. I've lived in this city for twenty years. Twenty!
Skyrim.esm|0C3694: Both the Imperials and Stormcloaks have sent riders this way, scouting the bridge and the lands nearby. They stayed at the inn, but didn't pay. They said soldiers don't need to pay because they're risking their lives to protect us. And that's not all. They took a heap of our lumber, and one tried to have his way with my daughter. They think they can do whatever they want.
Skyrim.esm|0C3695: Try Hulda, the innkeeper at the Bannered Mare. It's just up the road, near the market. You should check in at Dragonsreach too. There's so much going on these days, the jarl or his steward might have need of help.
Skyrim.esm|0C3696: I grew up an orphan on the streets of Markarth. I'd have died there, too, but for the kindness of an old warrior named Logrolf the Bent. Logrolf rescued me and gave me enough money to get out of the city. I followed the Karth river north, and came here. When I met Michel, I knew this was where I belonged. I suppose that's it, really.
Skyrim.esm|0C3697: I can tell you're new around here, askin' a question like that. My husband Eorlund tends the Skyforge up at Jorrvaskr, the Companions mead hall. There's no finer smith in the nine holds. You should ask him about his work. It's the only subject that'll get him talking. Well, that and maybe those stuck-up, dung-sniffing Battle-Borns.
Skyrim.esm|0C3698: You've got no idea. Half the men in Whiterun have proposed to me. Some were even single. They'll never understand. No amount of flowers or honeyed words are going to change my mind. Right now, all that matters is my daughter Mila. No man's going to get between me and my little girl.
Skyrim.esm|0C3699: Hard to say. It's ancient, that much we know for sure. It was here long before the town itself. I just hope the bridge survives the war. It's the only place you can cross the Karth river for a long way in either direction. Could be if one side wants to keep the other from coming across, they might try to knock it down.
Skyrim.esm|0C369A: Her name's Olda. If you've met her, then you probably already know what I'm talking about. I swear that woman is the most spiteful shrew in all of Skyrim. I think there's a chunk of ice where her heart ought to be.
Skyrim.esm|0C39FB: Master Wulfgar will demonstrate Whirlwind Sprint. Then it will be your turn.
Skyrim.esm|0C39FC: You must hear the Word within yourself before you can project it into a Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A04: Use both words, if you can.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A05: Use both Fus and Ro. Together, they will make the Shout more powerful.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A09: Master Borri will teach you the first Word of a new Shout. Attend to him.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A27: Must have come from Mercer's place. He'd admired the Gray Fox for some time. Sure, I'll buy it from you... here you are.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A29: Are you serious? The war is tearing the land apart, the dragons have returned seeking who knows what... Skyrim is going nowhere fast. If I were you, I'd pack up and get out of here before you end up in one of those burial cairns.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A2A: It's worth quite a bit actually. Quite a catch! Here you go, this should make you smile.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A2C: Look, I've moved all over Skyrim selling these bits of junk and I'm barely scraping by. I'm not out to win friends and I could care less if you're happy about your purchase or not. I just need the coin. The sooner I get out of Skyrim the better.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A2E: By the Eight... you actually got your hands on it! This alone is worth more than some thieves earn in a lifetime.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A2F: What a delightful find! This should cover it.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A30: This should compensate you for your find.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A31: There are those that believe the jarl does nothing for this city... that it's corrupt and run by the Black-Briar family. I can assure you that this is entirely false. We are fully in control of everything.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A32: You gonna buy something or just here for training? Otherwise, move on.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A33: You gonna stare all day or buy some armor?
Skyrim.esm|0C3A34: Can't you bother me at my armor stand in the market? Or are you that desperate for training?
Skyrim.esm|0C3A35: Come on, come on. The sooner you buy, the sooner I can get drunk.
Skyrim.esm|0C3A38: Maven Black-Briar is the owner of the largest and most profitable business in all Skyrim, the Black-Briar Meadery. We're pleased to have her in Riften. She brings stability, opportunities for employment, and strategic value to our city.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CB1: I can't do this. We shouldn't be here. I'm sorry.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CB2: A few hours sleep and we should be ready to continue through.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CB4: Best get rid of the ones we see. Just in case.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF1: Ah, Karliah. I was wondering when I'd hear from you again. Lose something did we?
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF2: Before we depart, Brynjolf has some business to discuss. I suggest you listen to him.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF3: If the Key isn't returned to its lock in the Twilight Sepulcher, things will never be the same for the Guild. As time passed, our luck would diminish to the point of non-existence. And whether you know it or not, our uncanny luck defines our trade.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF4: If you wish to talk, I'll be here for a bit.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF5: Proceed down the hall to the gate when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF7: Please hurry, we need to catch Mercer before he leaves Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF8: Revenge? How interesting... Very well, the conditions are acceptable. You may proceed.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CF9: My appetite for Mercer's demise exceeds my craving for wealth, Your Grace.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CFA: Very well. I name your initiates Nightingale and I restore your status to the same, Karliah. And in the future, I'd suggest you refrain from disappointing me again.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CFB: You surprise me, Karliah. This offer is definitely weighted in my favor.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CFC: You're already mine, Karliah. Your terms were struck long ago. What could you possibly offer me now?
Skyrim.esm|0C3CFD: You appear ready for the Oath.
Skyrim.esm|0C3CFE: You appear hesitant to don your Nightingale Armor. What's troubling you?
Skyrim.esm|0C3CFF: It's just ahead. Please, keep following.
Skyrim.esm|0C3D00: I'm preparing to leave for the standing stone. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|0C3D14: You have returned with the gauntlets! Enter. Speak to our chief right away.
Skyrim.esm|0C3D15: Return with the gauntlets, or not at all.
Skyrim.esm|0C3D16: Most of my kin are in the longhouse. Come back during the day if you wish to trade.
Skyrim.esm|0C3EBC: No! You will not deny me this!
Skyrim.esm|0C3EBD: You cannot take this away from me!
Skyrim.esm|0C41D4: Well, I know the Companions make their home here. Their mead hall, Jorrvaskr, is the oldest building in the city. Oh, and there's a nasty feud between two families called the Gray-Manes and the Battle-Borns. You'll want to be careful there. The rest you can find out by asking the townsfolk. I'd start with the barkeep at the Bannered Mare, or the castle steward.
Skyrim.esm|0C41D5: Father says I should pick a name that describes what I really am, but nobody's going to hire Erik Greenthumb or Erik Hoe-Pusher. No, I need a name that will put some fear in the hearts of my enemies. When I have some enemies, that is.
Skyrim.esm|0C41D6: Another time, or not at all. It's really none of your business, after all.
Skyrim.esm|0C41D7: None of 'em's older than nine, and the youngest is six. Now, the horse I got for sale is seven years old, but in fine health. She's got a lot of spirit, that one. We've taken to calling her Queen Alfsigr, or just Allie for short. I expect you can name her anything you like once she's yours.
Skyrim.esm|0C41D8: Go ahead, hit me as often as you want. It's not going to change anything, other than to make me and the town guard angry.
Skyrim.esm|0C41D9: Exploiting? What's that supposed to mean? Erik works the farm because he's a good lad! I'm not forcing him to stay here. I know that he wants a life of adventure, but I've told him before that I can't afford to buy him armor.
Skyrim.esm|0C41DA: Perhaps you should let a father decide what is best for his own son.
Skyrim.esm|0C41DB: You can get a room at the Bee and Barb, but mind your coin purse -- it's no secret the Thieves Guild makes its home here. Oh, and if you meet Maven Black-Briar, stay on her good side. This is her city, and nobody else's.
Skyrim.esm|0C41DC: My carriage is the safest way to travel.
Skyrim.esm|0C41DD: Solitude's been the capital of Skyrim as long as I can remember. Most of the high kings of Skyrim were jarls of Solitude first. Ah, let's see, what else... The Bards College is here, and the main office of the East Empire Company. If you need it, you can get a room for a fair price at the Winking Skeever, just inside the main gate.
Skyrim.esm|0C41DE: I appreciate the gesture, but I'd rather not be in debt to a stranger.
Skyrim.esm|0C41DF: It's an old city, I can tell you that. The Palace of the Kings is ancient. It's not the friendliest place for outsiders. The Dark Elves were all forced to live in a slum called the Gray Quarter. The Argonians can't even live inside the walls. They're all stuck out on the docks. Still, Candlehearth Hall's got decent food and cheap bunks. You could do worse.
Skyrim.esm|0C41FB: Well, you were deep in your cups when you got here. You were ranting but most of it was slurred. You said something about Rorikstead.
Skyrim.esm|0C41FC: All right, all right. You're mean when you're sober. You said the ceremony was going to be in Morvunskar. You said your friend Sam was going to be your best man.
Skyrim.esm|0C41FD: You think you can bully me. I grew up with five brothers. Go... get... my... ring.
Skyrim.esm|0C41FE: Okay, okay. Most of what you said didn't make sense, but you left a note. The only bit I could read said "after repaying Ysolda in Whiterun." That's all I know.
Skyrim.esm|0C41FF: Do what you have to do. I'm not giving up on Gleda.
Skyrim.esm|0C4200: Well, we can always use contributions. You were ranting when you got here, but most of it was slurred. You did say something about Rorikstead. Maybe you should take a look there.
Skyrim.esm|0C4201: Oh you must be nobility of some sort. Running around partying and paying others to clean it up. I don't think so. Clean up your own mess.
Skyrim.esm|0C4202: You must have had a wild night if you can't remember that. But I suppose everyone gets the jitters before their wedding. And I did say you could take a bit of time paying me back. You said the ceremony was going to be at Morvunskar. Don't forget you still owe me!
Skyrim.esm|0C4203: I'm sure your bride-to-be will tell you when and where the ceremony will be. If she still wants to marry you when you tell her you can't afford the ring.
Skyrim.esm|0C4204: Fine, but I'll still be missing my Gleda. Doubt I'll ever have a prize winning goat again. Most of what you said was gibberish, but you left a note. The only bit I could read was "after repaying Ysolda in Whiterun."
Skyrim.esm|0C4205: There's not enough money in the world to replace Gleda.
Skyrim.esm|0C4206: I suppose that makes sense. You didn't mention a Sam and nothing you said about the staff made sense. You left a note, but it was mostly gibberish. The only bit I could make out was "after repaying Ysolda in Whiterun."
Skyrim.esm|0C4207: You didn't seem to need either to sell Gleda to the giant in the first place. I think you'll figure it out.
Skyrim.esm|0C4208: Decided to go through with it, then? I knew you couldn't have forgotten about your fiancee. You spoke of her so glowingly. I don't know much about Morvunskar, but it sounded like a lovely place for the ceremony. Congratulations.
Skyrim.esm|0C4209: I think I would prefer my ring back. I put a lot of time into making it and you just gave it away as a joke.
Skyrim.esm|0C435F: I'll do anything if it means saving my brother. You'll join us, won't you? Thorald can't be left to those... monsters.
Skyrim.esm|0C4368: Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Skyrim.esm|0C4369: Stand between us, and prepare yourself. Few can withstand the unbridled Voice of the Greybeards. But you are ready.
Skyrim.esm|0C436A: You have completed your training, Dragonborn. We would Speak to you.
Skyrim.esm|0C43A5: Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Skyrim.esm|0C43A6: Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Skyrim.esm|0C43A7: Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Skyrim.esm|0C43A8: Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Skyrim.esm|0C43A9: Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Skyrim.esm|0C43AA: Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Skyrim.esm|0C43AB: Lingrah krosis saraan Strundu'ul, voth nid balaan klov praan nau.
Skyrim.esm|0C43AC: Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Skyrim.esm|0C43AD: Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Skyrim.esm|0C43AE: Meyz nu Ysmir, Dovahsebrom. Dahmaan daar rok.
Skyrim.esm|0C43AF: Naal Thu'umu, mu ofan nii nu, Dovahkiin, naal suleyk do Kaan, naal suleyk do Shor, ahrk naal suleyk do Atmorasewuth.
Skyrim.esm|0C43EC: Let me know. We shouldn't stay here all day. My men want their mead.
Skyrim.esm|0C4401: This thing from Saarthal. What does it do? J'zargo cannot say.
Skyrim.esm|0C4402: We shouldn't have been disturbing Saarthal, no matter what you found down there.
Skyrim.esm|0C4403: At least something good came out of that trip to Saarthal. I didn't find anything but dust.
Skyrim.esm|0C4404: I owe Thorald my life. Helping to save him is the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|0C4405: No one should ever be left to the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|0C4406: The Thalmor need to be taught a lesson.
Skyrim.esm|0C4407: I pledged to aid Avulstein and Thorald a long time ago. There's nothing that could keep me from helping them now.
Skyrim.esm|0C4421: Not well I'm afraid. That Brynjolf... he keeps draining the people's pockets with his ridiculous miracle cures. A few months ago it was troll fat salve and now he's got something new. Not that there's anything that can be done about it. He's in good with the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0C4422: Maven Black-Briar wants me to keep the mead flowing and that's what I aim to do despite all these lazy good-for-nothings that work for us. Ever since I took over as the foreman the production of our mead has never been more profitable. And if the meadery is profitable, I get rich. Get the picture?
Skyrim.esm|0C4423: Indeed. There have been multiple attempts on the jarl's life. We're not certain if it's the Dark Brotherhood or simply Imperial sympathizers. We've also had run-ins with spies attempting to probe our security for weaknesses. I work with the city guard to make certain they fail. At the end of the day, I'm the last line of defense for the jarl. I will not allow her to fall.
Skyrim.esm|0C4424: While I feel Ulfric's cause is just, my concern is for the people of The Rift. How can they continue to lead their already meager lives with dark clouds looming overhead? My heart goes out to them. If only our coffers were deeper, I could protect them as they were my own family.
Skyrim.esm|0C4425: I've been adventuring across Tamriel since I was a fresh-faced young woman barely able to swing a blade. My travels have taken me from High Rock to Valenwood, Elsweyr to Morrowind and all points in between.
Skyrim.esm|0C4426: Well, the moisture in the air is better for the ingredients. It isn't every day that you get your hands on fresh nirnroot or painted troll fat. It also makes it easier to gather water when the canal is right out the front door. Besides, I also prefer the quiet. I didn't open this business for browsing. Come in, buy what you need and get out.
Skyrim.esm|0C4428: Talos is part of our culture. He's part of our heritage and our tradition. He's Skyrim personified. To suddenly have all that wrenched away is like losing someone you hold dearly in your heart. The Empire already took my daughter from us, but by Talos they will not take our homeland!
Skyrim.esm|0C4429: People say I'm making gold off of other people's misery, but what else would I do? Smithing is all I know. Next time one of my shields deflects a killing blow, they'll change their tune.
Skyrim.esm|0C442A: Lady Maven could buy or sell anyone in Skyrim. I wouldn't be surprised if she's the one pulling the strings around this city. She's always welcome in Mistveil Keep and between you and me, I'm pretty sure she even has some pretty influential friends within the Empire. I'd also warn you not to cross her. She employs quite a few... troubleshooters to take care of messy problems.
Skyrim.esm|0C442B: Been stuck with the name for years. I've been ridin' horses since I was a lad... nothin' I liked more. But as I got bigger, so did my mounts. Then one day, I was drunker than I've ever been and mounted a poor colt. Broke his back in two. Haven't ridden since.
Skyrim.esm|0C442C: I'd hardly call them a Guild. More like unorganized rabble. In fact, I wouldn't say they were a threat at all. No, our resources should be spent on preparations for invasion.
Skyrim.esm|0C442D: We're all eager to see them brought to justice. Maven Black-Briar has assured me that they're being dealt with appropriately. As one of our city's most influential citizens, she's taken it upon herself to oversee their incarceration. Sadly, they're proving elusive but I have confidence that Maven won't give up until this city has been rid of them all.
Skyrim.esm|0C442E: Liars and bastards. Every one of them. I'd have their heads on a pike if it wasn't for the war effort. All I need is a dozen men and we could march into the Ratway and burn them out like rodents. Stormcloaks won't have it though. Too busy keeping the Imperial forces at bay.
Skyrim.esm|0C442F: They're vermin... garbage. They're exactly what makes this city such a horrible place to live. How appropriate they should live in the Ratway with the rest of the trash.
Skyrim.esm|0C4433: Of course! I'd be more than happy to add a donation to our charity box. Right now, the best you could provide is coin. We need all the help we can get.
Skyrim.esm|0C4434: Yes, I suppose someone has to be around here. Have you seen the condition of this sorry excuse for a city? Beggars roam the streets, crime runs rampant and the threat of war is driving away business. The paltry amount of coin that I place in the charity box at the temple is the least I can do to help.
Skyrim.esm|0C4435: He's the protector of Man, the Dragon of the North, the patron of all Nords. And despite the Imperials and their treaty, he will always be one of the Nine Divines. A devout Nord would rather fall in battle and have his blood spill upon the earth than allow the Empire to strike his name from the divinity.
Skyrim.esm|0C4436: Surely you're aware of the Great War between the Imperials and the elves who called themselves the Aldmeri Dominion? When the smoke cleared, the White-Gold Concordat was signed... it was supposedly a treaty aimed at establishing peace within the Empire. One of the terms of the treaty was the outlawing of worship to Talos. To the Stormcloaks, this was viewed as the moment when the Empire became unworthy of the allegiance of any true Nord.
Skyrim.esm|0C4437: As you wish. Whatever you can afford will certainly be put to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0C4439: The Black-Briars have been instrumental in keeping Riften on the map. Before the meadery was opened almost fifty years ago, this town was nothing... a simple waypoint for those journeying on to Morrowind. Now, thanks to my mother's generosity, this city is making its mark.
Skyrim.esm|0C443A: When speaking of the Black-Briars, only one thought should spring to mind. We are not to be trifled with. Help us, you end up rich. Cross us, and you'll end up a memory. Is that enough information for you?
Skyrim.esm|0C443B: Hmph. My family. All that wealth and they squander it on foolish ventures and political schemes. I was meant for so much more than all of this... I wish to pledge my life and ply my talents in darker circles. If only my mother would let me, I would make her proud.
Skyrim.esm|0C443C: I haven't seen so much celebrating in years. Mercer's dead, we have a new Guild Master and things are finally looking up. Business is booming, coin is flowing and everyone seems content as things keep going our way. A new chapter in the Thieves Guild is being written this day and we all owe it to you. Many thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0C443D: I haven't seen so much celebrating in years. Mercer is dead, we have a new Guild Master and things are finally looking up. I only hope we can restore the Guild to its former glory after all the damage that's been done.
Skyrim.esm|0C443E: So, word has it you and Brynjolf know where Mercer's hiding. Good. Make sure you make him pay for what he did to Gallus... heck, for what he did to all of us. We're all behind you, friend. Do your best.
Skyrim.esm|0C443F: The word is that Mercer Frey is a dead man. The Guild wants his blood. I can't say I blame them. Murder is not our way. Gallus was before my time, but I still can't believe it. You need to teach him a lesson... show him the penalty for betrayal around here.
Skyrim.esm|0C4440: Look, I like you, so I'll tell it to you straight. Brynjolf and the rest are in the Guild, and they're ready to cut you down. I know you have a damn good reason for siding with Karliah, so I hope you can give it to them.
Skyrim.esm|0C4441: Everyone's been worried since you and Mercer haven't shown up in a while. Brynjolf even went out to find you. Delvin and Vex have been running things for now. I hope this all gets straightened out soon or I'm headed on the first caravan out of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0C4442: Glad to hear Gulum-Ei got what he deserved. That lizard owes me big for a bad shipment of Shadowbanish Wine he tried to pass off on me. Mercer's been storming around waiting for you to come back. I think he's finally figured out who's been messing with the Guild. Never seen the guy so angry. Better help him out any way you can.
Skyrim.esm|0C4443: Maven was down here right before you got back and she was all smiles. Haven't seen her like that in a long time. Whatever you did at Honningbrew, you're now on her good side. Best of all, if she's happy, Mercer's happy. Keep it up and you'll go places.
Skyrim.esm|0C4444: Well, you did something Vex couldn't do at Goldenglow, so you're looking pretty damn good. Word is that you found something while you were out there though... something that could be a problem for the Guild. Just watch your back and do what Mercer says... he'll sort it out.
Skyrim.esm|0C4445: I don't know about the others, but I sure am glad to see a fresh face down here. The Flagon used to be packed every night with the boys from the Guild but now look at it. Last few years have been pretty bad. I've almost closed this place up.
Skyrim.esm|0C4446: I don't know you so we've got nothing to talk about. How about you finish the job Brynjolf gave you, then we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|0C4447: Sure. How about you mind your own business about it, and I won't have to get Dirge to toss you into the cistern?
Skyrim.esm|0C4448: Disgusting. Ruined sewers filled with goodness knows what. There's an entrance down by the canal, but I'd highly advise you to stay out of there... it's the Thieves Guild's territory.
Skyrim.esm|0C444A: Everyone knows the Thieves Guild uses the old sewer system beneath Riften as their hideout. I'd go down there myself, but that would leave Jarl Laila unprotected.
Skyrim.esm|0C444B: Thank you! I can promise you that this will be put to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0C444C: With the Stormcloaks at his back, Ulfric's poised to rid Skyrim of the Empire's forces and invalidate our involvement with the White-Gold Concordat. Between you and I, I think his motivations are a bit more self-serving. He uses this holy war as leverage in order to pursue the throne. If he were to be crowned high king, I'm not so certain it would be the golden age his followers expect.
Skyrim.esm|0C444D: With the Stormcloaks at his back, Ulfric's poised to rid Skyrim of the Empire's forces and invalidate our involvement with the White-Gold Concordat. Many have died taking up arms for this cause. I fear that the land will be stained with blood for years to come.
Skyrim.esm|0C44A2: Wealth is my business. Help me out and I can add to yours. Would you like a taste?
Skyrim.esm|0C44A4: There are ruined sewer tunnels beneath the city. The only way I know how to get in there is through a door off of the canal. If I were you, I'd keep my feet dry and stay up here.
Skyrim.esm|0C483C: I'm going to make you sorry for this!
Skyrim.esm|0C483E: Come on, give it your best shot!
Skyrim.esm|0C483F: How could you possibly know about... Please. My family means too much to me. Don't hurt them.
Skyrim.esm|0C4A7C: Imperial business. Be on your way.
Skyrim.esm|0C4A7D: Citizen. I'm warning you. Your presence is interfering with Imperial business. Be gone!
Skyrim.esm|0C4A7E: Citizen. You have been warned. You leave me no choice but to resort to violence.
Skyrim.esm|0C4A80: Go on, get out of here!
Skyrim.esm|0C4A81: I warned you. You leave me no choice but violence.
Skyrim.esm|0C4B15: I can't thank you enough for what you did.
Skyrim.esm|0C4B16: The East Empire Company is in your debt.
Skyrim.esm|0C4B17: Glad Orthus was able to find someone competent to help out.
Skyrim.esm|0C4B18: You handled yourself pretty well back at Japhet's Folly. We'll remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0C4DFB: We have guests as often as we can, but there aren't a lot of travelers these days. The war keeps people off the roads.
Skyrim.esm|0C4DFC: We supply Falkreath to the south. Until recently we supplied Helgen. Falkreath trades with the surrounding holds and without our wood they would have trouble keeping their prices low.
Skyrim.esm|0C4DFD: We would but there's a big wedding taking place at Castle Dour. We'll have to wait until after the wedding. Come back in a few nights when the wedding reception is over and we'll start the ceremony when you do.
Skyrim.esm|0C4DFE: Other people just make things complex. Out here, Hern and I can get everything we need from the occasional traveler.
Skyrim.esm|0C503C: I am Burguk's mother and wise woman to our tribe, outlander.
Skyrim.esm|0C5046: I'm Burguk's forge-wife. I shape all the steel for the stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|0C513E: I'm sorry, were you about to say something?
Skyrim.esm|0C513F: Ayleid, Dwemer, Daedric... not even Falmer. None of them are a match. Quite curious indeed.
Skyrim.esm|0C5173: I hear you found some... thing in Saarthal. You think it could help make me a powerful mage?
Skyrim.esm|0C5174: Is it true? One of the Psijics was here in the College?
Skyrim.esm|0C5175: No one's heard from the Psijic Order in so many years. Hard to believe one would just suddenly show up here.
Skyrim.esm|0C5176: J'zargo regrets there was no chance to speak with the Psijic Monk.
Skyrim.esm|0C5219: There is indeed much that we know that you do not. That does not mean that you are ready to understand it. Do not let your easy mastery of the Voice tempt you into the arrogance of power; that has been the downfall of many Dragonborn before you.
Skyrim.esm|0C521C: Good. Then you will be ready for whatever lies ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0C521D: That is commendable. But remember, the dragon blood is itself a gift of Akatosh. Do not try to deny that gift. Your destiny requires you to use your Voice - why else would Akatosh have bestowed this power upon you? If you remember to use your Voice in service to the purpose of Akatosh, you will remain true to the Way.
Skyrim.esm|0C521F: The Dragonborn is an exception to all the rules - the dragon blood itself is a gift of the gods. If we accept one gift, how can we deny the other? As Dragonborn, you have received the ability to Shout directly from Akatosh. We therefore seek to guide you on the proper use of your gift, which transcends the restrictions which bind other mortals.
Skyrim.esm|0C5220: He was a great war leader of the ancient Nords, a master of the Voice, or Tongue. After the disaster at Red Mountain, where the Nord army was annihilated, he spent many years pondering the meaning of that terrible defeat. He finally came to realize that the gods had punished the Nords for their arrogant and blasphemous misuse of the Voice. He was the first to understand that the Voice should be used solely for the glory and worship of the gods, not the glory of men. Jurgen Windcaller's mastery of the Voice eventually overcame all opposition, and the Way of the Voice was born.
Skyrim.esm|0C5221: Ah. I sometimes forget you are not versed in the dragon tongue as we are. This is a rough translation: "Long has the Stormcrown languished, with no worthy brow to sit upon." "By our breath we bestow it now to you in the name of Kyne, in the name of Shor, and in the name of Atmora of Old." "You are Ysmir now, the Dragon of the North, hearken to it."
Skyrim.esm|0C5222: We spoke the traditional words of greeting to a Dragonborn who has accepted our guidance. The same words were used to greet the young Talos, when he came to High Hrothgar, before he became the Emperor Tiber Septim.
Skyrim.esm|0C5225: No doubt. The appearance of a Dragonborn at this time is not an accident. Your destiny is surely bound up with the return of the dragons. You should focus on honing your Voice, and soon your path will be made clear.
Skyrim.esm|0C5226: The Voice was a gift of the goddess Kynareth, at the dawn of time. She gave mortals the ability to speak as dragons do. Although this gift has often been misused, the only true use of the Voice is for the worship and glory of the gods. True mastery of the Voice can only be achieved when your inner spirit is in harmony with your outward actions. In the contemplation of the sky, Kynareth's domain, and the practice of the Voice, we strive to achieve this balance.
Skyrim.esm|0C5227: We study the Way of the Voice, according to the teachings of our founder, Jurgen Windcaller. Very few are permitted to study with us here at High Hrothgar. But in your case, Dragonborn, it is a privilege to guide you towards mastery of your Voice.
Skyrim.esm|0C5228: Stand in the center of the room and prepare yourself to taste the Voice of the Greybeards.
Skyrim.esm|0C5229: Come inside. The other masters will meet us there.
Skyrim.esm|0C522A: First, Master Wulfgar will teach you the final word of Unrelenting Force. You are ready.
Skyrim.esm|0C57CE: Please, stop! I'll pay! Take it!
Skyrim.esm|0C57CF: Please, no more. I'll pay, I'll pay. Here!
Skyrim.esm|0C57D0: Here... take your damn coin and get out of here!
Skyrim.esm|0C57F7: I'll get everything ready and meet you there.
Skyrim.esm|0C57F8: Our new home. I'll see you there!
Skyrim.esm|0C5844: Some layabout named Sabjorn. Been a thorn in my side for the last few years now.
Skyrim.esm|0C5845: Well, whoever this mysterious marking represents, they'll regret starting a war with me. You should bring this information to the Thieves Guild immediately.
Skyrim.esm|0C5875: You might want to rethink that. You're missing out on some wonderful opportunities, working in that bar.
Skyrim.esm|0C5876: I'm not interested, Jaree. It doesn't matter how many times you ask.
Skyrim.esm|0C593E: Degaine the beggar got himself kicked out of the Temple of Dibella. Caused quite the ruckus.
Skyrim.esm|0C5C15: Yes, I have something special for you. But it's not for everyone to hear. Meet me in the Underforge tonight. We will speak more.
Skyrim.esm|0C5C16: Still eager to please. This is good. Skjor might be right about you. He has something special planned for you. Better talk to him.
Skyrim.esm|0C5C1A: Are you? Well. I'm afraid I can't help you with that. I need the crystal to do anything useful, and I don't have it.
Skyrim.esm|0C5D0B: Hello, my love. Back from some adventure I bet.
Skyrim.esm|0C5FF5: *Sigh*... and that's the last one. All right, enough of this.
Skyrim.esm|0C5FF6: C'mon, let's get something to eat.
Skyrim.esm|0C6447: Here, this is fresh. I'll have another meal ready tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0C644A: Yes, my love? What do you need?
Skyrim.esm|0C64EC: You have it. The Kel - the Elder Scroll. Tiid kreh... qalos. Time shudders at its touch. There is no question. You are doom-driven. Kogaan Akatosh. The very bones of the earth are at your disposal. Go then. Fulfill your destiny. Take the Scroll to the Time-Wound. Do not delay. Alduin will be coming. He cannot miss the signs.
Skyrim.esm|0C64ED: Use Dragonrend! It's the only chance to defeat him!
Skyrim.esm|0C64EE: He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F0: Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F2: Aav uv dir. Join me or perish with your mortal friends.
Skyrim.esm|0C64F3: Use Dragonrend! It is the only chance to defeat him!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F4: He is too strong on the wing! Bring him to gol with Dragonrend!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F6: Dovahkiin, vosaraan! Use Dragonrend before Alduin consumes you!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F7: Now, Dovahkiin! Now he is vulnerable!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F8: This is your chance, Dovahkiin! Strike with all your force!
Skyrim.esm|0C64F9: Hurt him while he is grounded!
Skyrim.esm|0C659F: Kynesgrove is this way. We can travel together or split up and meet there, your choice.
Skyrim.esm|0C65A0: That's better. Let's get on the road to Kynesgrove.
Skyrim.esm|0C65A1: The Greybeards seem to think you're the Dragonborn. I hope they're right.
Skyrim.esm|0C65A2: I think you're looking for this.
Skyrim.esm|0C65A7: When he shows up here and vouches for you, then I'll see what I can do for you.
Skyrim.esm|0C65AB: If you really are Ralof's friend, I'll be glad to help you. But you didn't answer my question - how do you know him?
Skyrim.esm|0C6C38: Here it is. Magnificent, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0C6C39: Place the crystal in the central apparatus, and we can start the process for focusing it.
Skyrim.esm|0C6C3A: Took an incredible amount of work to get it running again. Now I'm hoping it'll all be worth it.
Skyrim.esm|0C6CE7: No one seems to know. Perhaps the return of the Dwemer. Perhaps the end of the world.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E18: We're at war, old fool, or have you forgotten? Whiterun is the crown jewel of Skyrim, and you would leave us defenseless?
Skyrim.esm|0C6E1C: As you command, my lord. But, the issue could be solved another way. If we spend less on new arms and armor for the guards, for example...
Skyrim.esm|0C6E1D: Raise the taxes, then! Put anyone that refuses to pay in the stocks and make an example of them.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E20: My lord Jarl, if we do not take steps soon, our coffers will be empty.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E24: I'll be glad to help you. There are many ways to improve your home, provided you have the coin. And of course, don't forget to consult your home decorating guide for descriptions of the decorations you can buy. Now, what would you like to purchase?
Skyrim.esm|0C6E25: You've already purchased every available improvement. I'm afraid I have nothing else to offer you.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E26: Splendid! There's a house available right now.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E27: The house I mentioned earlier is still available, if you want to buy it.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E28: Wonderful! Here is the key to your new home.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E29: Very well, just let me know when you're ready to purchase the house.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E2A: Very well, just let me know when you have the necessary funds.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E2B: Is there anything else you'd like to purchase?
Skyrim.esm|0C6E2C: As you wish. Speak to me again if you decide to purchase some decorations for your home.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E2D: I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough gold to pay for that.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E2E: Very well, I'll make the arrangements. The next time you visit your house, your new furnishings will be in place.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E2F: Hrongar became the Jarl when his brother Balgruuf, who had the job before him, was murdered. Balgruuf was an even-tempered man, but his brother's a hot-blooded war-monger who wants to settle everything with a blade. If you've got some business with Hrongar, mind you stay on his good side. The palace dungeon's filled with folk who roused his temper.
Skyrim.esm|0C6E30: Vignar the Revered is the jarl in Whiterun. I guess he was a great hero once, because the Companions all call him "Vignar the Revered." Is Vignar too old to be jarl? It's a fair question. If he's grown infirm and is losing his wits, where does that leave the people of Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|0C7032: Come on. Hurry. We might be too late.
Skyrim.esm|0C7034: No, you don't want to go up there! A dragon... it's attacking!
Skyrim.esm|0C7232: The memories. I cannot stand them. You must take them away, return them to Avanchnzel. You must take the Lexicon from me. Please... take it now.
Skyrim.esm|0C72D5: Ha ha! I'd rather have two bottles of mead than two wenches, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0C72D6: You've never had a taste of sweet Honningbrew? Why, it's only the finest mead outside of Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|0C72D7: Drink Honningbrew Mead with a wench on each arm? Ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0C72D8: Black-Briar? How dare you shove that swill in my face. I'd rather drink mammoth drool!
Skyrim.esm|0C72D9: Ah, but you look tired. Come, share a bottle of Honningbrew Mead with me!
Skyrim.esm|0C72DA: Ah, is there any way you'd rather spend the day than drinking Honningbrew Mead?
Skyrim.esm|0C72DB: Cheers, my friend! May your adventures find you fame and fortune!
Skyrim.esm|0C72DC: Hail, friend! It's good to see another merry soul enjoying this fine day.
Skyrim.esm|0C72DD: I don't talk to people who drink that Black-Briar drench.
Skyrim.esm|0C72DE: This good deed should not go unrewarded. Here, take this!
Skyrim.esm|0C72DF: Ha! Too true, my friend. Too true...
Skyrim.esm|0C72E0: Ysmir's beard! You're one after my own heart. I would love another bottle.
Skyrim.esm|0C72E1: Ah, nothing like fine spirits to help raise your... well, your spirits!
Skyrim.esm|0C72E2: The meads won't argue your ear off about which one should go first!
Skyrim.esm|0C72E3: Black-Briar Mead? What are you trying to do? Poison me?
Skyrim.esm|0C72E4: But enough talk. Come! Won't you share a drink with me and my companions?
Skyrim.esm|0C72E6: Its sweetness is like a fair maiden's kiss on a starry night. And it's got enough kick to put a frost troll on his hind quarters! Ha!
Skyrim.esm|0C78B3: Poisons can kill your enemies faster than any blade.
Skyrim.esm|0C78B4: I am wise woman to this stronghold.
Skyrim.esm|0C78B5: We can trade in the morning, outlander.
Skyrim.esm|0C78B6: One day, I must fight my brother Nagrub for chiefdom. It will not be easy.
Skyrim.esm|0C78B7: It's late outlander. Come back in the morning if you need to spend coin.
Skyrim.esm|0C78B8: We only trade during the day, outlander. At night, most rest in the longhouse.
Skyrim.esm|0C78B9: I'm here to mine, outlander. So unless you want to dig, get out.
Skyrim.esm|0C78BB: Malacath has given me the task to mine. Good ore brings strength to our people.
Skyrim.esm|0C78BC: Every Orc learns the heavy labor of mining. All spend years in the caves.
Skyrim.esm|0C78BD: The mines are hard, but Malacath rewards those who rise to the challenge.
Skyrim.esm|0C7916: Come on in. Just stoked the fire. Take a seat and get the cold out.
Skyrim.esm|0C7918: Oh, do come in! I hope I have what you need.
Skyrim.esm|0C7ABF: You don't need my fruit, I've heard when you sing people throw tomatoes at you anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0C7AC0: I think I may wait until the Burning of King Olaf to have a Spiced Wine.
Skyrim.esm|0C7AC5: You bards don't cut each other any slack do you?
Skyrim.esm|0C7AC6: How about I trade you a song for some fruit?
Skyrim.esm|0C7AF8: Perhaps if I were a little more blessed I could afford to give away my fruit. As is you'll have to pay, Rorlund.
Skyrim.esm|0C7AF9: Perhaps you could spare a tomato for a priest of the Divines?
Skyrim.esm|0C7B00: It's too bad the Burning of King Olaf was called off this year. I think some of the younger bards would have really gotten a kick out of it.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B01: I think our flutist here has gotten better since the Burning of King Olaf.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B03: That's wonderful. You've done us a great service. As promised the outfit is yours and here's something for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B04: He's okay. A little breathy and a little harsh on the notes.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B05: Take a good look around. I'm sure you'll find what you're looking for. If not, let me know, I might have it stored away.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B06: Got something for just about everybody in here. Give a holler if you have any questions.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B08: Aren't you a sight for sore eyes.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B09: Hello friend. Make yourself at home.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B0B: I trust you're not planning any trouble. What can I do for you, friend?
Skyrim.esm|0C7B0C: If you've got the coin, you've come to the right place. Pull up a seat.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B0D: Welcome. Let me know if you want anything... think I got a clean mug around here somewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B0E: Come on in. We got warm food, warm drinks, and warm beds.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B19: She's okay. A little breathy and a little harsh on the notes.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B3E: My mother always used to say that too.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B3F: It is. We train the bards you hear throughout the land. Viarmo is the head of the college and in charge of admissions.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B5C: Only in that I'm a graduate. I work here at the Winking Skeever. I would highly recommend it, though. Talk to Viarmo, if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B5D: Only in that I'm a graduate. I work over at the Winking Skeever. I would highly recommend it, though. Talk to Viarmo, if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B5E: Oh really? Well you can tell them that I will be putting in a request for a few dresses quite soon.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B64: Lurbuk? Fancies himself a bard. He pays, so I let him stay. If I had any customers, I'd be worried about him annoying them. But, well... Look around.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B65: No, no. Well, sometimes, but that's not the problem. I just often don't have much to do, so I tend to... try things out. They don't usually work, but I try. Next thing I know, I've used up most of my stock.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B66: The marsh is treacherous. You would do well to not wander at night.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B67: As anyone will tell you, Morthal can be a dangerous place. My... talents... are useful here. I help maintain order, even if it goes unnoticed.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B68: Slow? No. It just ain't there at all. Few enough reasons to pass through Morthal before the war started. Now... Well, let's just say the front door doesn't get much use.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B75: Fine, Ataf. You should eat some more tomatoes, though.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B76: It's quite fetching actually. The craftsmanship is excellent.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B78: I could listen to him play all day.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B79: I could listen to her play all day.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B7B: Um, excuse me. Could I buy two leeks?
Skyrim.esm|0C7B7D: Well, you can understand Elisif not wanting it.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B89: I'm so glad we got to have it. I thought for sure Elisif would never consent.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B99: Look silly! As if you could look any sillier than you do now.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B9A: I'm afraid that kind of thing doesn't work on captains in the Imperial Army.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B9B: Generally, if you want rumors, you should talk to Corpulus. That said... I did see a visitor pass through on his way to the Blue Palace.
Skyrim.esm|0C7B9C: Absolutely not. I prefer to be near enough to come visit, but free to come and go as I please.
Skyrim.esm|0C7BA4: Good. Here's the outfit. Try to get Elisif's opinion on it and if she likes it mention it's from Radiant Raiment.
Skyrim.esm|0C7BAD: It's those damned Blackblood Marauders. They're scaring off shipping.
Skyrim.esm|0C7BAF: One apple. Since the Blackbloods were dispatched I've had good luck getting shipments.
Skyrim.esm|0C7BB2: Oh, no. But being surrounded by good music, good drink and good friends is all I've ever asked for.
Skyrim.esm|0C7BBB: Do you miss your days training at the College?
Skyrim.esm|0C7C1F: If you're heading to the Blue Palace, you might want to rethink that outfit.
Skyrim.esm|0C7C21: Hmm... If you ever want to replace those old rags, stop by Radiant Raiment.
Skyrim.esm|0C7C25: Youngest bard at the Bards College, at your service.
Skyrim.esm|0C7C46: Anything I can do for you, I will.
Skyrim.esm|0C7C58: So exciting when new folk come into town. I bet you've had plenty of adventures.
Skyrim.esm|0C7C6A: Someday, I'll be a gallant heroine. You make it look so... appealing.
Skyrim.esm|0C7C84: Maybe you need a partner? Hmm? Just picture it. Two women, the dusty road, strapping men at every tavern, a fortune in gold to be claimed...
Skyrim.esm|0C7CB9: You're really going to the Blue Palace? That presents an opportunity. If you are willing to wear one of Radiant Raiment's outfits and speak to the jarl, I would not only pay you but let you keep the outfit.
Skyrim.esm|0C7CBF: Your fruit are looking a little less ripe today.
Skyrim.esm|0C7CC8: Your selection looks very delicious. I'll take an apple.
Skyrim.esm|0C7CC9: Ah, you and everyone else. Word is it may not happen this year though.
Skyrim.esm|0C7CCC: Damn shame, Roggvir. You were a good man.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D33: She did, did she? She's going to get a sit down later.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D34: Yeah, that's what Papa says too.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D35: You would do that? That's great. You should go do it right now.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D36: Thanks. Papa talks about it some. Mum doesn't say much since Uncle Roggvir died. She doesn't even go to temple anymore. I wish she would. At least that way she'd have people to talk to.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D37: Thanks so much. I'll return to temple. Here's something for your time and effort.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D3F: Why did you... nevermind, it's a sign. I'll return to temple. Here's something for your help.
Skyrim.esm|0C7D41: Well, I wish it were that simple. Maybe it is. Look, I want to go back but I won't feel right in there without a tie to Talos. My brother Roggvir wore a symbol of Talos. He kept it hidden on his person. If I had that... I can't go near his body. Would you get it back for me?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F37: Hello, Silana. Would you like an apple?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F38: I'm pleased when the palace is clean, I'm pleased when the beds are made on time, I'm pleased when Erdi isn't around.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F39: You're a hard woman to please, Una.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F3A: You don't put up with a man like that. He puts up with you.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F3B: Vegetables are the food of the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F3C: There's nothing quite like a good vegetable.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F3D: I once saw Fihada put an arrow in between the eyes of a Blackblood Marauder at five hundred paces.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F3E: I keep coming out here thinking I'll see what others see in this... frivolity.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F3F: How do you put up with that fop Fihada?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F40: I've heard stranger things are eaten over at the Temple of the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F41: Most of those don't happen very often.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F42: I've tasted your food, Odar. It's better than most songs I've heard.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F44: Sometimes I like to think my cooking is like playing the flute or singing a song.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F45: The guy in from Dragon Bridge? He didn't seem the type.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F46: I don't think that was a compliment.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F47: I'll need a whole crate of fruit. That Varnius Junius went through quite a few of our supplies.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F48: You like everyone, Jorn. You'll make a good court bard some day.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F49: That's generous, Jala, but I am happy to pay my way.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F4A: I like you, Una. You've got presence.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F5E: By the gods! What manner of power is that?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F60: Your shield... Dwarf-make, is it not? But yet it seems so much... more.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F61: What a foul and unnatural weapon you wield. Never have I seen its like.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F62: What manner of staff is that? Such a horror...
Skyrim.esm|0C7F63: You wield... Azura's Star? How... how can such a thing be possible?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F64: Fine armor you've got there. Dwarven make, am I right?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F65: What do you aim to do with that hammer, friend? Knock down a house?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F66: No... get that accursed mace away from me...
Skyrim.esm|0C7F6A: Still can't wake him up? Perhaps you're barking up the wrong tree?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F6B: You seem to be having a small problem... or perhaps it's a big problem? Maybe if you shrunk the whole thing down a little first?
Skyrim.esm|0C7F6C: Hmm... Your creature doesn't appear to be fairing any better than before. Methinks your aim is off.
Skyrim.esm|0C7F8C: I think this might be the end...
Skyrim.esm|0C7FC3: Been about eight years now. I used to be a mercenary, but one trip to Ravenscar Hollow cured me of that. There's hagravens in that cave with talons like daggers. They'll open your bowels quick as you wink if you let 'em get close. Creature like that'll make a man think long and hard about what he wants out of life.
Skyrim.esm|0C7FC4: My pa used to say there ain't no use worryin' about the things you can't change. If it's my fate to be ended by a dragon, so be it. Meantime, I'll live my life the way I want to.
Skyrim.esm|0C7FED: Enchanting services are one of the few things that keep us in touch with the rest of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0C8189: Perhaps we can go another few decades without a disaster. That would be nice.
Skyrim.esm|0C818A: I'm working on some very important research, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0C818B: The Eye of Magnus. Here, but lost. Slipped through our grasp. Oh, what we might have learned.
Skyrim.esm|0C818C: I wonder what we could've done with the Eye, had it been put to better use?
Skyrim.esm|0C818D: Have you seen Mirabelle around? I know she's dead, but I thought... Well, I thought she might stop in and say hello anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0C818E: You know, they say knowledge is its own reward.
Skyrim.esm|0C8194: Arch-Mage or not, my rules about books still stand.
Skyrim.esm|0C8195: All this knowledge might have been lost, were it not for you.
Skyrim.esm|0C8196: I don't want to see you treating any of these books poorly. Are we clear?
Skyrim.esm|0C8197: Tolfdir is master wizard. Certainly didn't anticipate that.
Skyrim.esm|0C8198: And to think, Mirabelle questioned my allowing you into the College. I can just imagine the look on her face now.
Skyrim.esm|0C8199: Mirabelle does a fine job as master wizard, I suppose. Still, there's always room for improvement.
Skyrim.esm|0C819B: I care little for idle conversation, but perhaps you need help with Conjuration magic?
Skyrim.esm|0C819C: Ancano is exactly why we're so restrictive about access to the College. We'd all have perished if it weren't for you.
Skyrim.esm|0C819D: I'm sure you've made note of who's been the most helpful during your time here, Arch-Mage.
Skyrim.esm|0C819E: May I just say, it's a pleasure to have you leading the College. Really.
Skyrim.esm|0C81A7: If there's ever any way I can assist you, please let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0C81A9: I'm quite pleased that we're back to business as usual.
Skyrim.esm|0C81AA: Certainly nothing illicit going on here, Arch-Mage. You can be quite sure of that.
Skyrim.esm|0C81AB: Even with the College's laid-back attitude, there are still some things that are off-limits.
Skyrim.esm|0C81AC: Like it or not, you are a shining example to future generations of College mages.
Skyrim.esm|0C81AF: After all that it's been through, the College still stands. In no small part thanks to you!
Skyrim.esm|0C81D8: I don't know what it's doing up there, but I'm not waiting around to find out!
Skyrim.esm|0C81D9: It flew over the town and landed on the old dragon burial mound.
Skyrim.esm|0C81DA: Well, I don't know. Not yet... it flew over the town and landed on the old dragon mound!
Skyrim.esm|0C81DD: It's always encouraging to see the younger generations embracing education.
Skyrim.esm|0C81DF: I still can't believe it. You, the Arch-Mage? Never would have guessed it.
Skyrim.esm|0C81E6: I never would have suspected that the Eye could be so dangerous. Tolfdir is right, isn't he? Magic really can overwhelm you.
Skyrim.esm|0C81ED: I've certainly learned more here than I ever expected to.
Skyrim.esm|0C81F6: I wonder if the College's reputation will ever recover from our adventures.
Skyrim.esm|0C81FA: The College is a bit of an oddity in Skyrim. Magic isn't something that's looked upon fondly here.
Skyrim.esm|0C8201: Back in 42, I was stationed in Hammerfell, on leave in Sentinel, trying to track down some refugee relatives who had fled persecution in Alinor. Suddenly an explosion of magic in the refugee quarter. Thalmor mages were attacking the Altmer dissidents who were resisting with magic of their own. I ran to the scene with other Legionnaires who were stationed there, but the entire quarter was a smoking ruin by the time we arrived. Everyone was dead. Wholesale slaughter. The Dominion, not content with killing dissidents at home, came to Hammerfell to finish the job. We're supposedly at peace now. But I put in to be stationed here to keep an eye on the Thalmor. I've a feeling they're behind this unrest in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0C8202: My parents were traveling merchants eventually settling in Cyrodiil. I must have inherited their wanderlust... I joined the Legion to see the world. I know, that's one of those things people say, but it's completely true in my case. Wasn't long before I saw more of the world than I bargained for.
Skyrim.esm|0C8203: For the glory of the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0C8204: The life of a soldier is full of hardship. That's nothing. But they send the Legion to places that've gotten too bad to be settled without violence. What's hard is seeing good people warped by evil. And I've seen the face of evil. It was in the air above Sentinel on the Night of Green Fire.
Skyrim.esm|0C8205: Home? Home to me is a hot cup of ale at the end of the day and five minutes without someone needing something from me... Cyrodiil is a beautiful place, full of diverse peoples and histories. But so too, Hammerfell and Skyrim, and every other place I've been. Don't get me wrong, every country has its dark corners. But that's why we're here. To bring order and civilization, and to protect the people.
Skyrim.esm|0C8206: Ulfric and his thugs are stirring up trouble for their own agenda. The Empire is the only thing keeping the Dominion from walking all over Skyrim. It matters little if some of the people here are ignorant to that truth. They are still citizens of the Empire. It's our solemn duty to protect them.
Skyrim.esm|0C8229: Aye. I've worked on a few moorings here and there for it. Most of the ship captains are superstitious about who works on their vessels, though. And Dawnstar is known for its bad luck. We're the last port of call before Windhelm, and the ore we trade makes rich targets for pirates.
Skyrim.esm|0C822A: Pfft. I'm the oldest woman in Dawnstar. Was here when Skald the Elder was Skald the Younger. He's a fool, if you haven't met him already. Thinks Ulfric Stormcloak is invincible and spits dragon fire. The people here look to Brina Merilis when they need things settled. Real firebrand going up. Wasn't surprised when she joined the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|0C822B: That's right. In the Second Era, Tiber Septim himself led the army that conquered Old Hroldan from the barbarians of The Reach. Septim would later found the Empire that united Tamriel, but his first known battle and victory was right here. And this inn has the very bed the great general slept in on his first night as Old Hroldan's liberator. As good as it was hundreds of years ago.
Skyrim.esm|0C826C: Every Nord should admire Ulfric. He fights for all of us. In fact, he's the reason I chose to learn the smithing trade. I want to make weapons and armor for the great Stormcloak army.
Skyrim.esm|0C826D: Grew up here. Cold as Atmora, but that just grows the beards thicker! Lot of history in these walls. We're trying to make some more. It's a lucky time to be alive.
Skyrim.esm|0C826E: Wherever I can find them. If you're the type who worries over where something came from, you may want to move on.
Skyrim.esm|0C826F: Are you kidding? I came all the way from Cyrodiil just to work with him. He's... he's not the best teacher. But just watching him, I've learned so much.
Skyrim.esm|0C8270: I don't see these troubles as business. This is a labor of honor. To bring Ulfric his rightful glory, I would hammer a thousand shields. Shor's bones, I might just have to!
Skyrim.esm|0C8271: No, but then I don't have much need for Ulfric. We both leave well enough alone.
Skyrim.esm|0C8272: Smithing isn't the most exciting thing for me, but we all have to lend a hand as best we can. If a Stormcloak uses one of my swords to strike down an Imperial soldier, then I've done my part to free our land.
Skyrim.esm|0C8273: It doesn't matter what I believe. Nurelion does, and it might be the only thing keeping him alive right now.
Skyrim.esm|0C8274: The Silver Hand has been scouring the world for more pieces of Wuuthrad.
Skyrim.esm|0C8275: The Silver Hand has been scouring the world for more pieces of Wuuthrad.
Skyrim.esm|0C8276: Another piece of glory. Good work, Shield-Brother.
Skyrim.esm|0C8277: Another piece of glory. Good work, Shield-Sister.
Skyrim.esm|0C8278: Whatever I don't already know. Power is the crux of this world. There's always more of it to be had, if you look in the right places.
Skyrim.esm|0C8279: We work where we can get work. The Nords don't like unloading their ships, so we do it instead. Sometimes cargo goes missing.
Skyrim.esm|0C827A: Every bit as sharp as what you'll find in Whiterun. I respect Eorlund, but he has the good fortune to work the Skyforge. Something about the fires... their steel just holds tighter.
Skyrim.esm|0C827B: Get it back, for the honor of us all.
Skyrim.esm|0C827C: There's an old saying that only a Nord can work the soil in Skyrim. I don't think anyone else would have the patience for it. It takes half a day just to dig a planting row in the cold, stony earth here. But what else can we do, eat rocks and ice?
Skyrim.esm|0C827D: I'll carry on his tradition as best I can. I could spend a lifetime just going over his notes.
Skyrim.esm|0C827E: I came here as a hatchling. The only thing colder than Skyrim is the Nords themselves, but we stick together.
Skyrim.esm|0C827F: You know what I mean. Forges have personalities, right?
Skyrim.esm|0C8281: Just got here from the Summerset Isles. Lots of opportunity in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0C8282: For Ulfric? Oh, nothing official. Known him for years. He seems to value my thoughts, and I'm grateful for that. I don't really have a mind for war, and I think he likes having an untrained opinion from time to time.
Skyrim.esm|0C8283: Did you see the candle above the fire upstairs? It was lit one hundred and sixty-three years ago, back when this building was the home of a grand warrior named Vundheim. When word came that he'd fallen, his son Deroct lit the candle in his honor. Nobody knows why it still burns.
Skyrim.esm|0C8284: One group has hidden out in The Reach.
Skyrim.esm|0C8285: One group has hidden out in Haafingar.
Skyrim.esm|0C8286: One group has hidden out in Hjaalmarch.
Skyrim.esm|0C8287: One group has hidden out in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0C8288: One group has hidden out in Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0C8289: One group has hidden out in The Pale.
Skyrim.esm|0C828A: One group has hidden out in The Rift.
Skyrim.esm|0C828B: One group has hidden out in Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|0C828C: One group has hidden out in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0C82AB: J'zargo had expected to be named Arch-Mage. But there is still time.
Skyrim.esm|0C82AD: Are the Arch-Mage's robes as comfortable as J'zargo believes them to be?
Skyrim.esm|0C82AF: Skyrim could not be more different from Elsweyr. But magic... Magic is the same no matter where you go.
Skyrim.esm|0C82E2: Have you found proof of Alva's treachery?
Skyrim.esm|0C82E3: You better get proof of your accusations against Alva.
Skyrim.esm|0C8577: That's right. Home of the Redguards. The land of sand and seas. I hope one day I'll convince Rustleif to take us back there. Until then, I'm content running the forge together here in Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0C8613: Not much. I came here a few years ago from Hammerfell when I married. The land is beautiful, but I can't stand this cold. I don't know how Rustleif manages it.
Skyrim.esm|0C8614: Come back if you need steel.
Skyrim.esm|0C8615: We forge every day if you need anything.
Skyrim.esm|0C8616: The only thing I dislike about my husband's homeland is the cold. Why is it always so cold?
Skyrim.esm|0C8954: Come on. We need to find out what's happening up there!
Skyrim.esm|0C8956: I'm not going out there. Not with that dragon on the loose.
Skyrim.esm|0C8957: I wouldn't go out there for all the money in the world.
Skyrim.esm|0C8958: Do you think it's gone yet?
Skyrim.esm|0C8959: We've got to find someplace to hide!
Skyrim.esm|0C8EA0: Ful, losei Dovahkiin? Zu'u koraav nid nol dov do hi.
Skyrim.esm|0C8EA2: Don't you know there's a dragon attacking?
Skyrim.esm|0C8EA3: You do not even know our tongue, do you? Such arrogance, to dare take for yourself the name of Dovah.
Skyrim.esm|0C96A9: Pretty good arm you have there.
Skyrim.esm|0C96BB: Come at me. You couldn't hurt me if you wanted to.
Skyrim.esm|0C97D0: I want to thank you for your help. I know my master can be a bit short at times.
Skyrim.esm|0C9829: Thank you, Legate. In the future, I'll let you know when your opinion is needed.
Skyrim.esm|0C982E: I thought I gave you something to do?
Skyrim.esm|0C9831: You don't think this is a war already? Sir, I...
Skyrim.esm|0C9836: Good luck with that ice wraith.
Skyrim.esm|0C9838: We have it on good authority that Ulfric has raised enough men to attack the city of Whiterun. The jarl, however, refuses the Legion's support. This missive should convince him. Be aware, soldier, these documents contain sensitive intelligence for the jarl's eyes only.
Skyrim.esm|0C983E: Yes. An axe. How long have you been in Skyrim? Give the man my axe. If he keeps it, I will bide my time. If he returns it to you, it means war.
Skyrim.esm|0C983F: Men who understand each other often have no need for words. There are but a few simple truths behind one warrior giving another his axe. Balgruuf will know my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|0C9842: Men who understand each other often have no need for words. There are but a few simple truths behind one warrior giving another his axe. Hrongar will know my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|0C9843: Keep your wits about you. The Jarl of Whiterun is known for his temper.
Skyrim.esm|0C9844: What? Get over with the rest of the men at Korvanjund.
Skyrim.esm|0C99E8: I can take a hint, lad. You want to make some coin, come find me.
Skyrim.esm|0C99E9: I can take a hint, lass. You want to make some coin, come find me.
Skyrim.esm|0C99EA: It's our name for the old sewers that run under the city. It used to be a huge system hundreds of years ago, but it fell into decay, just like the rest of Riften. The Guild stocks the place full of thugs that aren't good enough to join, so watch yourself if you venture down there.
Skyrim.esm|0C99EC: They'd stop at nothing to take your last bit of food if that's all you had. The Ratway's too good a place for that scum.
Skyrim.esm|0C99ED: They used to be pretty feared around here. I mean, you'd whisper the name and it'd send chills down your spine. Now, they're nothing more than ruffians and thugs trying to pry a few extra coin from honest people. All it would take is a small force of guard to go into the Ratway and flush them out.
Skyrim.esm|0C99FE: Get moving, now. I'll be fine.
Skyrim.esm|0C99FF: Come on, get up... I need you on your feet.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A00: Do we have to? They'd never lift a finger to help us.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A01: We'll stay here. Get back to the College and let them know what's going on.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A02: I don't see the Arch-Mage. He must've been blown clear. I need you to find him immediately, all right? Make sure he's safe.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A03: Then, as I've said, 1000 gold will be required to set things right.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A04: Ah, yes. There was an incident, wasn't there? Well, as this is the first time this has happened, I'll make it very clear that the College does not condone that sort of behavior. As an act of good faith on your part, I'd advise that you consider donating an amount of gold to the College. Strictly to cover damages, you understand. I believe 250 gold should be sufficient.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A05: Then I'm afraid your suspension will stand for now. I'm sorry, but that's the way it has to be.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A07: That looks like it's the last of them.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A08: Yes. I hope this will be enough to maintain order. Once again, your suspension is lifted. Please, try and control yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A09: You don't seem to have the gold on you. Perhaps there's a misunderstanding?
Skyrim.esm|0C9A0A: I have no idea what happened. Ancano did it, I think.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A0B: It's none of your concern. All you need to know is that we're paying for information. If that doesn't interest you, feel free to walk away.
Skyrim.esm|0C9A0C: What have we gotten ourselves into now?
Skyrim.esm|0C9A0D: Well, this certainly won't help our image.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AD3: Watch out some gray-skin doesn't pick your pocket.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AD7: Until our next meeting, honored friend.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AD8: May Talos shield you from all harm.
Skyrim.esm|0C9ADA: Blessings of Talos be upon you.
Skyrim.esm|0C9ADB: Too few remember to honor Talos. Do not be one of those.
Skyrim.esm|0C9ADC: The Divines smile on a charitable soul.
Skyrim.esm|0C9ADD: Don't forget them who's less fortunate than yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0C9ADF: I'll see you again soon, I hope.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE0: Enjoy your stay, and don't break nothin'!
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE1: Mind you don't put out that candle on the hearth upstairs!
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE2: Be careful! That murderer could be anywhere, and anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE3: The streets are safer now, thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE6: A word of advice -- don't buy discounted armor.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE7: If you've need of weapons or armor, come back and see Oengul.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE8: May the earth beneath your feet be always soft.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AE9: I hope we will speak again soon, marsh-friend.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AEA: Trust me, marsh-friend, you do not want to live in this city.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AEB: I would not complain if you returned with some skooma.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AEC: Fair winds and calm seas. That's an old sailor's parting.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AED: It was a pleasure to talk to someone so interesting.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AEE: Travel safely. It's dangerous out there.
Skyrim.esm|0C9AEF: If you'll excuse me, I must return to my duties.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B77: Do you know I look forward to the Burning of King Olaf each year just because of the fire?
Skyrim.esm|0C9B78: I'll take a bottle of Spiced Wine.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B79: Brave man, with the Blackbloods out there. Did you catch anything?
Skyrim.esm|0C9B7A: A bit of warming wine, Illdi. You know you can't resist.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B7B: I went out ice fishing a few days ago.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B7C: Not fair. You're well aware I can't seem to get warm here.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B7E: Plenty of ways to worship the Divines as you know.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B7F: The last one was a warm up.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B80: It should be quite the affair. Nice to have something happy in the midst of the war.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B81: Of course. And I'm sure it's mostly for ceremonial purposes.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B82: Sure, Sorex. Just like you did the last one.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B83: Ah, a few bottles of Spiced Wine should hit the spot.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B84: Actually just a bottle for me today. I'm thinking of giving it to Vivienne.
