Skyrim.esm|0C9B85: Spiced Wine for the Winking Skeever?
Skyrim.esm|0C9B86: Not too bad. A few of the guests from the upcoming wedding have placed orders.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B87: They seem to when you're around Silana.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B88: Viarmo is trying to get Elisif to allow the Burning of King Olaf. I don't want to look like I don't know how to drink in front of the other bards.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B89: Oh, no thank you. I'm still feeling sick from the festival.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B8B: I hope the Divines have been keeping you well.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B8C: I haven't known you to drink before, Ataf.
Skyrim.esm|0C9B8D: A bottle of Spiced Wine for ya, Ataf?
Skyrim.esm|0C9B8E: Well, keep at it. The fish have to get to know you before they're willing to bite.
Skyrim.esm|0C9F06: It's true, the scrolls are a subject of interest to us, and work has been done to learn of them. But outsiders are not allowed within the College.
Skyrim.esm|0C9F07: A lofty goal, to be sure. And what brings your search to our doorstep?
Skyrim.esm|0C9F15: Oh, well, it's just... I never imagined the Gourmet was a Khajiit. You know, because of all the fur, and the potential for getting hair in the...
Skyrim.esm|0CA138: You there! Fool! Hold fast. You've been accused of a crime against the laws of this land. What say you in your defense?
Skyrim.esm|0CA139: You just keep quiet and stay where you are. Soon as my relief arrives, we'll all take a nice little trip.
Skyrim.esm|0CA13A: You just keep quiet and stay where you are. Soon as my relief arrives, we'll all take a nice little trip.
Skyrim.esm|0CA13B: You look like a crazy man to me. And what's in that coffin, hmm? Maybe some time in a cell will help loosen that lying tongue.
Skyrim.esm|0CA1F4: Really? You convinced that lute player to stop chasing me? I'd thank the gods, but I'll settle for thanking you. Here's some coin for your help.
Skyrim.esm|0CA1F5: Whoa. Hey there. I didn't mean to make you upset. On my honor, Carlotta won't have to worry about me again.
Skyrim.esm|0CA1F7: That bard Mikael is begging for a dagger up against his throat, the way he goes on about me. I heard him boasting at the Bannered Mare, saying he'll "conquer me as a true Nord conquers any harsh beast." Hmph.
Skyrim.esm|0CA1F8: How about a test? Your muscle against mine. The victor gains 100 gold coins from the loser.
Skyrim.esm|0CA1F9: This will be good training. Let's go!
Skyrim.esm|0CA1FA: He's a stubborn oaf. I don't know what good it'll do.
Skyrim.esm|0CA200: Your fists speak louder than your words.
Skyrim.esm|0CA203: All right. Tell the old man his debts are settled.
Skyrim.esm|0CA204: Bribing me? Well, a bard does need to eat. On my ancestor's honor, I won't trouble sweet Carlotta again.
Skyrim.esm|0CA317: Ah, a test subject! Go, my pets! Attack!
Skyrim.esm|0CA434: My wife Seren and I run it together. She still smiths even though we found out she is with child. Our son or daughter isn't going to get much Redguard culture here in Skyrim. I know my wife worries how she will pass along her traditions. Hey, if you find a book called "Night Falls on Sentinel," could you bring it to me? It's a story from my wife's people. Be good to have.
Skyrim.esm|0CA438: Good. I'll see if I can get some copies made for the men. Here, something for your trouble. The Legion thanks you.
Skyrim.esm|0CA626: No, please! There's no need for that! I'll tell you everything. It's Karliah... her name is Karliah.
Skyrim.esm|0CA627: Aye, he does indeed... and with his fingers in the East Empire Company's pie, we'll make good use of that debt. If I'm not being clear enough, that means we don't want him killed. For now, just keep on his tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
Skyrim.esm|0CA630: I hope so. But you'll forgive me if I don't assume that something's true just because the Greybeards say so. I just handed you the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller. Does that make me Dragonborn too?
Skyrim.esm|0CA633: Why didn't you say so earlier? At least now we don't have to waste time searching the area. We should get moving. There's no time to waste.
Skyrim.esm|0CA635: I can't wait around for you to make up your mind. I'll meet you at Kynesgrove. Don't waste time getting there.
Skyrim.esm|0CA636: I shouldn't let you walk out of here, knowing what you know. But I guess even my paranoia has its limits. You know where to find me when you change your mind. Because you will. You have to.
Skyrim.esm|0CA637: I need to get into my traveling gear. Give me a minute and I'll be ready.
Skyrim.esm|0CA638: We've got to bring it down!
Skyrim.esm|0CA639: Don't let it get too close!
Skyrim.esm|0CA63A: We've got to ground that bastard!
Skyrim.esm|0CA63B: Come on! This is our best chance!
Skyrim.esm|0CA63C: Now it's my turn, you son of a bitch.
Skyrim.esm|0CA63E: I am Sahloknir! Hear my Voice and despair!
Skyrim.esm|0CA63F: It's to be a real fight, then. Good!
Skyrim.esm|0CA640: My lord Alduin requires your death. I am glad to oblige him.
Skyrim.esm|0CA641: I see that mortals have become arrogant while I slept.
Skyrim.esm|0CA642: My Voice has been silent for too long!
Skyrim.esm|0CA644: Dovahkiin, your Voice is no match for mine!
Skyrim.esm|0CA645: Your Voice is strong... for a mortal.
Skyrim.esm|0CA648: I do not fear you, Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|0CA649: Good. Good. Now we won't have to spend time searching for it. We should get moving. There's no time to waste.
Skyrim.esm|0CA6DA: Word is Ulfric Stormcloak escaped from the Imperials. War's only going to get worse, now.
Skyrim.esm|0CA6DB: They say a dragon attacked Helgen. Sounds like hogwash to me.
Skyrim.esm|0CA749: We surrender. The capital is yours. If you pledge no harm will come of my people, I turn over the seat of power to you.
Skyrim.esm|0CA74A: Go. Tell your commanders you have won. I, my family, and my men will be gone before they arrive. There is no point in further talk. Go now!
Skyrim.esm|0CA74B: Enough! Stop! Cease fighting. It's over.
Skyrim.esm|0CA74C: We're taking the capital. Meet the soldiers gathering for the attack. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|0CA74D: We're taking the capital. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB0D: Let's see if I can teach you a few things about using a bow.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB19: Well, let me know if you ever want to practice.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB1A: You chose the wrong girl to mess with.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB7A: It wasn't my fault! I told them over and over that it was an accident! They wanted me to prove my worth, so they threw me up against a young whelp of a lad, hardly old enough to grow his first chin-hairs. I guess they thought a woman wasn't strong enough to hurt him. I didn't mean for him to die! Why would I want that? I just... I just lost control.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB7B: You been talking to those Companions? "Too hot-headed," they cried. Weak, pathetic cowards, the lot of them!
Skyrim.esm|0CAB7C: The heat of battle is the fire that forges the strongest blades. It's an old Nord proverb. That, and a true Nord never misses a chance to test her worth.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB7E: About the same as everyone else. They're the cat-folk of Elsweyr. Great warriors, good traders. The way I hear it, Elsweyr ain't nothing like Skyrim. It's got tropical forests and dusty badlands. It sounds awful!
Skyrim.esm|0CAB7F: Yeah, these Khajiit make a living traveling the roads and selling their wares. It's got to be tough. Skyrim's a hard enough land when you've got a roof over your head. Worst thing is, nobody wants them in the cities. Nobody trusts them.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB80: Hmph. Plenty. I'd say we're prosperous enough. Most folk don't go hungry, if they're willing to work hard. The city's ruled by the jarl up in Dragonsreach. That's the fortress, there, up on the tall hill. But you're probably more interested in Jorrvaskr, mead hall of the Companions. Their deeds of war and valor are... legendary.
Skyrim.esm|0CAB81: I am an artisan, painting in strokes of blood red upon the canvas of life. But yes, I will take coin to fight at your side. After all, where is the value in creating art if there is nobody left alive to see it?
Skyrim.esm|0CADE0: Damn Imperials. They won't hold Dawnstar forever.
Skyrim.esm|0CADE3: This isn't over. I catch you sending one letter to General Tullius, I'll have you both executed.
Skyrim.esm|0CADE4: About 30 years ago, the Aldmeri Dominion invaded the Empire. It was a surprise attack, caught everyone off guard.
Skyrim.esm|0CADE5: Jarl gets booted from Dawnstar, and I'm still stuck cleaning up the dirt.
Skyrim.esm|0CADE6: Jarl Skald is upset over the loss of Dawnstar. I'll admit, it's less I have to worry about.
Skyrim.esm|0CADE7: You should see the grin on Horik's face now that we're in charge. I know he hides it, but I can tell.
Skyrim.esm|0CADEF: The jarl and I have our disagreements. Sorry if you had to overhear one of them.
Skyrim.esm|0CADF0: I wasn't born in Skyrim, but when I served in the Legion this land became my home.
Skyrim.esm|0CADF1: Let me know if you come down with the Rattles.
Skyrim.esm|0CADF2: Back to another fine day in Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0CADF4: Don't bother me again unless it's important.
Skyrim.esm|0CADFC: If only we'd stayed out of Saarthal.
Skyrim.esm|0CAF65: You cannot prevail against me. I will outlast you... mortal!
Skyrim.esm|0CAF66: Do not delay. Alduin must feel the tremors in Time by the Scroll's presence here. He will know what it means.
Skyrim.esm|0CB1A7: I knew it... Mercer sent you to kill me! Guards, there's an intruder!
Skyrim.esm|0CB1B7: In honor of your service in battle, I am hereby granting you permission to purchase property in Whiterun. Talk to my steward if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0CB1B8: In honor of your service in battle, I am hereby granting you permission to purchase property in Markarth. Talk to my steward if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0CB1B9: In honor of your service in battle, I am hereby granting you permission to purchase property in Riften. Talk to my steward if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0CB1BA: In honor of your service in battle, I am hereby granting you permission to purchase property in Windhelm. Talk to my steward if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0CB264: Good to see you. Come on in.
Skyrim.esm|0CB266: Ah, a visitor. Old Hroldan Inn has hundreds of years of history, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0CB267: The Mortar and Pestle makes potions, if you can't tell from the name. Just step up to the counter.
Skyrim.esm|0CB269: Just... leave here! Leave us alone!
Skyrim.esm|0CB26A: Begone from here! Get out of this orphanage! Help! Somebody help me!
Skyrim.esm|0CB3A7: A bit of jewelry for your journey home?
Skyrim.esm|0CBA95: Here. Take a look at this. Some of the jarl's men came by and left this bounty letter.
Skyrim.esm|0CBA97: The jarl has put out a bounty on the dragon that's been terrorizing the countryside. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Skyrim.esm|0CBAAE: Don't even get me started on that lot. They put on airs like they're somethin' special. Oh sure, they've got money, but they ain't got a shred of honor between 'em. Gray-Mane. Now that's a name fills you with pride. We've got roots in Whiterun. We've got history.
Skyrim.esm|0CBAAF: Head up the steps from the market an' look to your right. Can't miss it.
Skyrim.esm|0CBAB0: Matter of fact, I do. They've been fair enough with me, far as I can tell. And a Nord knows a liar when she sees one.
Skyrim.esm|0CBAB1: Reputation, mostly. A lot of Khajiit turn to smuggling and thievery to get by. A few bad apples spoil the bunch. You know how it is.
Skyrim.esm|0CC339: If you can remain calm, even in the midst of chaos, your arrow will most likely find its target.
Skyrim.esm|0CC33B: When I tell you to, try and hit each of the three closest targets in eight seconds.
Skyrim.esm|0CC33D: Thanks for sticking around and keeping me company. It was nice to finally meet someone out here who doesn't want to rob you or take off your head. I know it isn't much, but here, take this bow. It was given to me by my family. It's special to me, but brings back too many painful memories.
Skyrim.esm|0CC33E: I think I've taught you everything I know. You might even be as good as me now.
Skyrim.esm|0CC340: If you can learn to keep your composure and trust that each shot is true, then you should be able to quickly handle multiple targets.
Skyrim.esm|0CC341: Now hit the target to the left.
Skyrim.esm|0CC342: It's easy - stay inside the boundaries and only use the practice arrows.
Skyrim.esm|0CC347: I'll count to three and then say "go." You have eight seconds to hit each of the three targets. Draw string and get ready.
Skyrim.esm|0CC348: My older brother took me out hunting a lot when I was little.
Skyrim.esm|0CC349: She always said composure was the key to a successful archer.
Skyrim.esm|0CC34B: Here, take these. Looks like you're running low.
Skyrim.esm|0CC34D: It was my pleasure. It's not often I get visitors out here. Let alone friendly ones.
Skyrim.esm|0CC355: So whenever we came across more than one deer in a single location, it was imperative we kill as many as we could.
Skyrim.esm|0CC356: Great shot. Now hit the target to the right.
Skyrim.esm|0CC357: You did it! All three targets in eight seconds. Nice job.
Skyrim.esm|0CC35D: My father was rarely home, so it was my mother who actually taught me how to use a bow.
Skyrim.esm|0CC366: Let me know if you want to practice some more. Next time we'll make it a little more challenging.
Skyrim.esm|0CC367: You need to stay inside the boundaries.
Skyrim.esm|0CC369: Stay within the boundaries and only use the practice arrows.
Skyrim.esm|0CC371: Remember, composure and speed. You don't need to see your arrow hit before moving onto your next target. Trust your shot is true.
Skyrim.esm|0CC372: Take care, stranger. Be careful out there.
Skyrim.esm|0CC374: Okay, let's see what you can do. Remember to stay within the boundaries and only use the practice arrows. I have some if you don't have any. Aim for the middle target.
Skyrim.esm|0CC375: At the time, it was difficult to keep food on the table, let alone have enough left over to sell at the market.
Skyrim.esm|0CC378: Try and hit the middle target.
Skyrim.esm|0CC37A: Let me know if you want to try again.
Skyrim.esm|0CC37B: Try and hit the left target.
Skyrim.esm|0CC37C: Try and hit the right target.
Skyrim.esm|0CC37D: Great job hitting those targets. You definitely surprised me with your speed and composure.
Skyrim.esm|0CC772: I don't want to choose! They're both so amazing.
Skyrim.esm|0CC774: Are you prepared, then, to help bring the light across this land?
Skyrim.esm|0CC775: Always, child. Mara's mercy is ever-flowing.
Skyrim.esm|0CC776: Well, aren't you a strange one.
Skyrim.esm|0CC777: Of course you were. Now stop wasting my time.
Skyrim.esm|0CC778: Do not forsake your heart, child, for it may one day come to surprise you. Mara will always accept you into her arms, should you seek to return to her fold.
Skyrim.esm|0CC779: Please, stop wasting my time. You're embarrassing yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0CC77A: Think of him? He's an odd little man. Always poking around the ruins and wasting time in his museum. Seems friendly enough. Obviously intelligent. Why do you ask?
Skyrim.esm|0CC77B: What? You're out of your mind. That old coot's more interested in dead dwarves than anybody around here. Don't talk such rot.
Skyrim.esm|0CC77D: That may be the case. But she is as volatile as a sabre cat. One wrong word, and I've seen her turn on a man so quickly...
Skyrim.esm|0CC79C: You again? We have nothing else to discuss. Now move.
Skyrim.esm|0CC7AC: Have it your way. I'll take the job, but don't expect a miracle.
Skyrim.esm|0CC7AD: Ha! I'd sooner bend my knee to Ulfric Stormcloak. Besides, Gray-Mane would never make steel for the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|0CC7AE: We'll pay whatever it takes, but we must have more swords for the Imperial soldiers.
Skyrim.esm|0CC7AF: I just can't fill an order that size on my own. Why don't you swallow that stubborn pride of yours and ask Eorlund Gray-Mane for help?
Skyrim.esm|0CC83C: They are profane creatures with no hope of redemption. They lure innocents to their deaths and corrupt the souls of all they touch. And their heretical artifacts grant powers to mortals that wreak havoc across Tamriel. They must be destroyed or guarded to prevent temptation.
Skyrim.esm|0CC83D: And any other abominations that prey on mortals. Vampires. Werewolves. Witches. But the daedra are the worst. Their callous disregard for our lives is abhorrent in the eyes of the God of Mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0CC83E: Do you think you could handle Vilkas in a real fight?
Skyrim.esm|0CC83F: Of course. How are you in battle, boy?
Skyrim.esm|0CC851: He is the God of Mercy. The patron of order and justice for all of Tamriel. We bring his compassion where none can be found, by cleansing all those who would offend his children.
Skyrim.esm|0CC855: Yes. Our order was founded after the Oblivion Crisis. We dedicate our lives to facing the threat of daedra wherever they appear.
Skyrim.esm|0CC95B: The Vigil will be watching you.
Skyrim.esm|0CC95C: None escape the Vigil. All come into the light.
Skyrim.esm|0CC96F: You're bloated with disease. The Vigilant of Stendarr can help you, as long as you promise to never assist the daedra.
Skyrim.esm|0CC970: I am the Keeper of the Vigil here in Skyrim. We bring Stendarr's Mercy to the innocent and His Justice to the daedra.
Skyrim.esm|0CC971: You speak to a Vigilant of Stendarr. Cavort with any daedra, and we will hunt you down.
Skyrim.esm|0CC972: Wherever the daedra hide, the Vigil of Stendarr will cast them into the light.
Skyrim.esm|0CC9A4: Stendarr's Mercy be upon you, for the Vigil has none to spare.
Skyrim.esm|0CC9BD: Walk always in the light, or we will drag you to it.
Skyrim.esm|0CC9BE: The suffering the daedra cause will not go unpunished.
Skyrim.esm|0CC9BF: The Mercy of Stendarr does not extend to daedra worshippers.
Skyrim.esm|0CC9C0: Stendarr's Light purify you of your ills.
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB1: This is a fine wedding. And to think, it's for us.
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB2: This ceremony is so different from an Orc marriage. Less blood.
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB3: I never would have dreamed I would be getting married in the Temple of Mara.
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB4: Weddings always make me cry. What am I supposed to do now that I'm the one getting married?
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB5: It's a simple ceremony, but it'll do. You're here. That's enough.
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB6: Who would have thought I would be getting married?
Skyrim.esm|0CCBB7: I don't want to see you ever again.
Skyrim.esm|0CCBD0: You're abandoning me? Me? I never want to see you ever again.
Skyrim.esm|0CCCD8: Very well. I'll meet you there.
Skyrim.esm|0CCCDF: All right. I'll see you there.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD3E: If that's what you want. I'll see you there, soon.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD40: Then I'll see you at home!
Skyrim.esm|0CCD41: Wise decision. The vaults in the Hall of the Vigilant will guard this against future temptation.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD50: I'll be here if you need me, love.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD51: You're going? I'll miss you, dear.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD52: Leaving? Take the world by storm, dearest.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD5A: You have quite a house. I'm thinking of setting up a small store here. Sell odds and ends.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD5B: Strange house, but strong. Solid. I think I'll open a store. A task to tend to whenever you're away.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD5C: This house of yours is wondrous, at least to these eyes. I've thought about starting a store. Something to do here when you're out.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD5D: This house is huge. I'll be tripping my feet all over this place. Hey, I think I'll start a little business here. Something to do, you know?
Skyrim.esm|0CCD5E: I've seen bigger houses, but it's yours, so I like it. Now, I think I need something to do if you're ever away, so I'll be opening a store.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD5F: A good house. You chose well. I don't like sitting still, so I've decided to open a store. Buy and sell whatever is needed.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD60: It's good to see you again, dear.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD61: It's pleasing to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD62: Ah, it's my love, come to visit.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD63: Hello, dear. I haven't been drinking too much, if that's what you're thinking.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD64: You and I, we're the only people in Skyrim worth loving. That's what I think.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD66: Do you need anything, my love?
Skyrim.esm|0CCD67: Speak. You can ask anything of me, love.
Skyrim.esm|0CCD68: Is there anything you need, dearest?
Skyrim.esm|0CCD69: I love you. You know that, right?
Skyrim.esm|0CCD6A: What do you need, love? Ask anything.
Skyrim.esm|0CD072: Ain't sure if they're takin' on new members, but you should talk to the Companions. They're over in their hall, Jorrvaskr. If by "work" you mean "testing your steel in bloody battle."
Skyrim.esm|0CD073: Aye, that I do. Have all my life. We Battle-Borns have been here since the beginning. Along with the Gray-Manes. Our families been close as kin for generations. But Ulfric Stormcloak's uprising has divided us. Now seems we can scarce look at each other without comin' to blows. Petty and foolish, that. They're sayin' dragons have returned to Skyrim. Now that there, that's a real problem.
Skyrim.esm|0CD074: Truly? Ha! By Shor, this city could use more like you. Best head up to Dragonsreach, then, and see the jarl. He's lord of Whiterun, and can set you on the path.
Skyrim.esm|0CD075: Ha! The most important question of all, that one. You'll want the Bannered Mare. See old Hulda, she'll keep you swimmin' in mead. But mind the bard, Mikael. Poncy little milk drinker, that one.
Skyrim.esm|0CD076: A pity. And words I hear too often. But there's no shame in it. One of them dragons comes callin' in Whiterun, the place'll go up like tinder.
Skyrim.esm|0CD077: Ah, so it's gold you're after, then? You need only ask about it. Plenty o' folk in Whiterun with too much fat and gold to be bothered doing anything for themselves.
Skyrim.esm|0CD078: Well met, kinsman. What brings you to Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|0CD079: Well met, traveler. What brings you to Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|0CD07A: Ah, hello my Argonian friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Skyrim.esm|0CD07B: Ah, hello my Khajiit friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Skyrim.esm|0CD07C: Ah, kinsman. How my heart soars to look upon a brother Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs?
Skyrim.esm|0CD07D: Ah... How my heart soars to look upon a sister Bosmer. Please, how can I serve your hunting needs?
Skyrim.esm|0CD07E: Hello, friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Skyrim.esm|0CD0A0: The citizens of Whiterun are forever in your debt!
Skyrim.esm|0CD0A2: In defeating these Stormcloak traitors, you have proven the hollowness of their cause and the fullness of your hearts.
Skyrim.esm|0CD0A9: They already sing of your valor and skill! The halls of Sovngarde are no doubt ringing with your praises!
Skyrim.esm|0CD0AA: Revel in your victory here today, even as the gods revel in your honor!
Skyrim.esm|0CD0B1: Thank you for your role in all of this. It'd be my honor should you decide to make Whiterun your home. Speak with Proventus. He'll make the necessary arrangements.
Skyrim.esm|0CD0FE: Everyone, on me! The jarl wishes to speak!
Skyrim.esm|0CD10A: You won't. The jarl has ordered a life sentence for you. Get comfortable in the dark, 'cause you'll never see the sun again.
Skyrim.esm|0CD10B: We're not. The Silver-Bloods pay our wage. Every man here is a trained mercenary.
Skyrim.esm|0CD10E: All right, prisoner. Eyes front. You're in Cidhna Mine, now. And we expect you to earn your keep. There's no resting your hide in a cell in this prison. Here, you work. You'll mine ore until you start throwing up silver bars. You got it?
Skyrim.esm|0CD10F: I said get down there. Now.
Skyrim.esm|0CD110: This is a jail, owned by the Silver-Blood family. We use the prisoners to mine for ore. It's the most secure prison in Skyrim. Throw scum in, close the gates. No one gets out. So unless you're here to dig, you don't belong here.
Skyrim.esm|0CD111: It's off to Cidhna Mine for you.
Skyrim.esm|0CD113: Carry on men, my gratitude and blessings go with you!
Skyrim.esm|0CD115: Lest our honor be lessened should we allow these bloodthirsty beasts to prowl our lands!
Skyrim.esm|0CD116: Oh? Were you after her yourself? I won't stand in your way, my friend. You have my word.
Skyrim.esm|0CD117: Carlotta put you up to this, didn't she? I'm sorry, but that fiery widow is mine. She just doesn't know it yet.
Skyrim.esm|0CD118: And so, we must each one of us, continue to fight this insurrection, lest our fallen brothers have died for naught!
Skyrim.esm|0CD119: Maybe you're right. I guess I just didn't want anyone to think I couldn't handle one lass. On my honor, I won't bother Carlotta ever again.
Skyrim.esm|0CD11A: But Ulfric will not stop here. No, he will continue to strike out against any true Nord who remains faithful to the Empire. He will continue to sow discord and chaos wherever he can.
Skyrim.esm|0CD11B: What did you just say? All I heard was the sound of jealousy.
Skyrim.esm|0CD11C: Cidhna Mine's for prisoner scum only. Keep out.
Skyrim.esm|0CD11D: Don't get smart with me. I'm in charge. You keep it up, I'll have your toes cut off.
Skyrim.esm|0CD124: They'll think twice before coming back here, that's for sure!
Skyrim.esm|0CD130: We showed those Imperials what real Nords are made of!
Skyrim.esm|0CD157: We've driven the Imperials out of Whiterun. This is good. Very good. We now control the center. It's a powerful position. One I aim to keep.
Skyrim.esm|0CD57D: Here. Wouldn't want you going into a fight on an empty stomach. I'll have another meal ready tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0CD57E: A warrior marches on the gut. Here. This should give you strength. I'll cook another tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0CD581: Here. This is from a recipe my people have passed on to me. I can cook another tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0CD587: Here you are. I added a little mead into the recipe. I think it tastes better. Let me know what you think tomorrow, and I'll make another one.
Skyrim.esm|0CD58B: I should be asking you the same question, but all right. I'll cook. Here, try this, and don't you dare ask for another one before tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0CD58C: I can. Here. This is fresh. I'll have another one made tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0CD58D: A home for the two of us. I'll meet you there.
Skyrim.esm|0CD590: It will be our little nest. I'll be waiting for you there.
Skyrim.esm|0CD591: The two of us, living together. I'll see you at home!
Skyrim.esm|0CD593: I'll see you at home, then. Don't keep me waiting!
Skyrim.esm|0CD594: I'll be waiting for you at home, then.
Skyrim.esm|0CD5A8: I was just getting settled in. Where are we going?
Skyrim.esm|0CD5A9: Oh really? And where were you thinking?
Skyrim.esm|0CD60C: I guess we have our entire lives together, now. Where should we live? You could stay with me...
Skyrim.esm|0CD60D: We face the world together now. Where should we live? My home is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0CD60E: We're united, but where should we live? My home is yours, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0CD60F: I'm a married man! Hey, where should we live? I guess you could always stay with me.
Skyrim.esm|0CD610: This is the happiest day of my life. Where should we live? My home is your home now, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0CD611: Married. It doesn't feel like I thought it would, but I'm happy. Where should we live? You could stay with me...
Skyrim.esm|0CD612: We're quartered in the Hall of the Vigilant. Keeper Carcette heads Skyrim's branch of our order there, providing healing and justice as needed.
Skyrim.esm|0CD668: To the block, prisoner. Nice and easy.
Skyrim.esm|0CD66B: Next, the Nord in the rags!
Skyrim.esm|0CD66C: Forget the list. He goes to the block.
Skyrim.esm|0CD672: As we commend your souls to Aetherius, blessings of the Eight Divines upon you, for you are the salt and earth of Nirn, our beloved...
Skyrim.esm|0CD678: Captain. What should we do? He's not on the list.
Skyrim.esm|0CD67E: Ulfric Stormcloak. Some here in Helgen call you a hero. But a hero doesn't use a power like the Voice to murder his king and usurp his throne.
Skyrim.esm|0CD7B1: Wait a moment... do I detect a hint of genuine avarice from this noble thief? Perhaps Karliah and Brynjolf misjudged you and your true nature is no different than my own.
Skyrim.esm|0CD81B: He's no longer abusing the power of the Eye. I would've expected all this to stop.
Skyrim.esm|0CD81D: You've done it! You've beaten him!
Skyrim.esm|0CD830: Caves sounded more exciting in the bard's songs.
Skyrim.esm|0CD831: It's kind of dark in here.
Skyrim.esm|0CD833: It feels like a whole mountain of rock is above  us.
Skyrim.esm|0CD834: Hard to believe people actually have to work in places like this.
Skyrim.esm|0CD835: Wonder what they mine from these walls.
Skyrim.esm|0CD836: I'll take a sword or mace over a pick axe any day.
Skyrim.esm|0CD837: Do you think we'll find gold nuggets in here?
Skyrim.esm|0CD838: Never knew a place could be so ancient. I bet it's over a thousand years old.
Skyrim.esm|0CD839: Wonder if any of my ancestors ever walked these halls?
Skyrim.esm|0CD83A: Everything in here seems to be about death. Burial urns, crypts, funeral shrouds. It's a bit depressing.
Skyrim.esm|0CD83B: I wonder what the ancient nords used this place for?
Skyrim.esm|0CD83C: This place is amazing! It's hard to believe that mortal hands built them.
Skyrim.esm|0CD83D: What do you think these devices were for?
Skyrim.esm|0CD83E: The stories don't say why the dwarves disappeared. I wonder if their machines turned on them.
Skyrim.esm|0CD83F: It's amazing this place is so well preserved. There isn't even a hint of rust.
Skyrim.esm|0CD840: The Jarls never want to pay to keep the forts. They just keep slipping further into ruin.
Skyrim.esm|0CD841: I wonder what great battles were fought here?
Skyrim.esm|0CD842: Do you think Tiber Septim ever fought here?
Skyrim.esm|0CD843: I can see why they built the fort here. A small force could control most of the nearby land.
Skyrim.esm|0CD844: Wizards. Why is it always wizards.
Skyrim.esm|0CD845: Most bandits are cowards. These seem to be braver than most.
Skyrim.esm|0CD846: I hate bandits and thieves. Men like these raid our town every year.
Skyrim.esm|0CD847: I hope that when I die, I don't end up as a shambling corpse.
Skyrim.esm|0CD848: The Forsworn are strange men. They fight to reclaim Markarth, but they live like savages.
Skyrim.esm|0CD849: I don't like wizards and their spells. Give me an axe any day.
Skyrim.esm|0CD84A: These walking devices are strange yet amazing. What magic powers them?
Skyrim.esm|0CD84B: Taken alone, the draugr aren't so fearsome. They're far more deadly in greater numbers.
Skyrim.esm|0CD84C: These Forsworn are just savages. They live in caves and ruins when civilized folk build towns and cities.
Skyrim.esm|0CD84D: I thought about becoming a bandit. I decided I preferred clean clothes and fresh mead.
Skyrim.esm|0CD84E: These Forsworn don't even have the decency to dress right.
Skyrim.esm|0CD84F: Draugr are slow and clumsy. Hardly a challenge for a man like me.
Skyrim.esm|0CD859: Ah yes, the extract, good. Poisonous, of course, but it has its uses. I suppose you want something for your trouble? Here.
Skyrim.esm|0CD85B: We have nothing to talk about. I can't believe what you did to me.
Skyrim.esm|0CD85C: Your words are cheap. Why should I speak to you after what you've done?
Skyrim.esm|0CD85D: And why should I speak with you? You broke my heart.
Skyrim.esm|0CD85E: You. You should just go. I don't have enough ale to stomach talking to you.
Skyrim.esm|0CD85F: Look at you. Come crawling back, after everything you've done to me?
Skyrim.esm|0CD860: What do you want? There's no words left to say to each other.
Skyrim.esm|0CD862: Get out of here. Get out of here and never come back!
Skyrim.esm|0CD863: What did you say? Get out of my sight!
Skyrim.esm|0CD864: You're a monster. Leave and never come back!
Skyrim.esm|0CD865: What'd you say to me? Get out! I never want to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|0CD866: You come all this way just to hurt me a second time? Get out of my sight!
Skyrim.esm|0CD868: Everything you see is grown at my family's own Hollyfrost Farm. Fresh and filling. I also do a little herb growing. The court wizard, Wuunferth, pays us to plant a little Nightshade. Who knows what he uses it for? Hey, could you deliver this bottle of Nightshade Extract to Wuunferth?
Skyrim.esm|0CD869: I am. I'd be glad to stand by your side until the Divines take us, if you'll have me.
Skyrim.esm|0CD86A: I'll give you another chance, but watch yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0CD86B: You want to try again? Fine, but I'll be watching you carefully.
Skyrim.esm|0CD86C: Try again? You and me? Fine, but I got my eyes on you.
Skyrim.esm|0CD86D: You're testing my patience, but very well. I'll give you another chance.
Skyrim.esm|0CD86E: All right, we'll start again, but watch yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0CD86F: An Amulet of Mara? You're not married? Surprising.
Skyrim.esm|0CD870: That amulet. I've seen the Nords wear these when they're looking to marry. With your strength, you should have no trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0CD871: Is that Lady Mara's amulet? I'm shocked a fine person like you isn't married already.
Skyrim.esm|0CD872: You're wearing Mara's amulet? You? I'd marry you. I'd marry you twice.
Skyrim.esm|0CD873: Look at you. Wearing an Amulet of Mara. Are you saying you're available?
Skyrim.esm|0CD874: An Amulet of Mara, you're looking for marriage, then?
Skyrim.esm|0CD875: You go ahead and arrange the wedding ceremony when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|0CD876: You should speak to the priest in the Temple of Mara to arrange the ceremony. No sense waiting.
Skyrim.esm|0CD877: Have you spoken to the Temple of Mara, yet? I'm so excited.
Skyrim.esm|0CD878: Hey, shouldn't you tell the Temple of Mara that we're getting married? Be hard to have a wedding without a priest.
Skyrim.esm|0CD879: Now shouldn't you be off arranging our wedding day? Don't keep me waiting.
Skyrim.esm|0CD87A: You should tell the priest we're getting married. They'll take things from there.
Skyrim.esm|0CD87B: I'll see you at our wedding!
Skyrim.esm|0CD87C: Our wedding is soon. I'll see you then!
Skyrim.esm|0CD87D: Never thought I'd get married under a Nord god. But with you, it feels right.
Skyrim.esm|0CD87E: Our wedding is soon, under Mara's loving eyes. It'll be perfect.
Skyrim.esm|0CD87F: We have our wedding soon, don't we? That's right, we do!
Skyrim.esm|0CD880: I'm looking forward to our wedding. You and me...
Skyrim.esm|0CD881: The wedding is soon. It'll be a good day.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8EA: But we haven't taken a side because it's not our fight.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8EC: Maybe we'll pay you a visit tonight, little spy. We got ways of finding out what you really are.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8EE: Imperial spies? You can't be serious!
Skyrim.esm|0CD8EF: Hey, maybe the reason these gray-skins don't help in the war is because they're Imperial spies!
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F0: You come here where you're not wanted, you eat our food, you pollute our city with your stink, and you refuse to help the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F1: Ever since that Imperial craven ran me through from behind, I've had trouble breathing. Don't have any trouble drinking, though.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F4: May you lose yourself in the pages of a good book!
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F5: As you travel Skyrim, make sure you stop and take in the view from time to time!
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F6: If you need another drink, I'll be right here.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F7: Have a seat, there should be plenty of space.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F8: Please forgive my mood. I'm still coming to terms with my daughter's death.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8F9: Don't take anything for granted in life. Nothing lasts forever.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8FA: Be sure to stop by Sadri's Used Wares in the Gray Quarter.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8FC: Sometimes the difference between life an' death is a swift steed.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8FD: Sorry about the smell. It's a stable, after all.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8FE: Whether it's armor you need or a new blade, come see me.
Skyrim.esm|0CD8FF: If you meet anyone in your travels who needs good steel, send them my way.
Skyrim.esm|0CD900: Strength and steel are well and good, but magic is the true power in this world.
Skyrim.esm|0CD901: A strong sword arm wins battles, but a keen mind wins wars.
Skyrim.esm|0CD904: A most pleasant chat. Until the next!
Skyrim.esm|0CD909: If you're not doing something you enjoy, then you're wasting your life.
Skyrim.esm|0CD90A: I hope to see you again soon.
Skyrim.esm|0CD90B: If you have need of tonics or potions, come see me at my shop.
Skyrim.esm|0CD90D: Battle reveals who a man really is. Remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0CD90E: Keep on your guard. This city can be a little rough for those such as us.
Skyrim.esm|0CD910: I should have been an adventurer like you, instead of a farmer.
Skyrim.esm|0CD911: Never trade a sword for a hoe, my friend.
Skyrim.esm|0CD914: Spare a coin for a poor old woman?
Skyrim.esm|0CD915: A few septims for my supper is all I ask.
Skyrim.esm|0CD916: I ask only for a little charity.
Skyrim.esm|0CD917: Will you spare a few coins to help someone who's got nothing?
Skyrim.esm|0CD918: I ain't askin' for much, just a few septims.
Skyrim.esm|0CD919: The Divines smile on those who show mercy an' charity.
Skyrim.esm|0CD91A: A few septims ain't nothing. You can spare that, can't you?
Skyrim.esm|0CD981: The Augur was a brilliant mage. Truly inspired. Mastered spells others could barely comprehend. He was especially gifted when it came to Restoration. He's... Well, he's very particular about who shares the knowledge. So you'll need his approval first. Go on, go talk to him. I'm sure he's been paying attention, and will be expecting you.
Skyrim.esm|0CD982: Oh, goodness no. There are those far more skilled than I.
Skyrim.esm|0CD984: It looks like you're ready to speak with the Augur.
Skyrim.esm|0CD985: Look at you. Such a devoted student of Restoration. It's comforting to see that not everyone has dismissed it as entirely as most members of the College. Truly comforting.
Skyrim.esm|0CD986: You are worthy. The knowledge is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0CD9DF: Meyye! Tahrodiis aanne! Him hinde pah liiv! Zu'u hin daan!
Skyrim.esm|0CD9E4: Which is why I brought the Elder Scroll.
Skyrim.esm|0CD9E5: You feel fear for the first time, worm. I see it in your eyes.
Skyrim.esm|0CD9E6: You are banished! Alduin, we shout you out from all our endings unto the last!
Skyrim.esm|0CD9EB: Begone, World-Eater! By words with older bones than your own we break your perch on this age and send you out!
Skyrim.esm|0CD9EC: If I die today, it will not be in terror.
Skyrim.esm|0CD9ED: Gormlaith! We're running out of time! The battle...
Skyrim.esm|0CDA0C: As short as life can be in this land, we will have each other.
Skyrim.esm|0CDA0D: Well, look at us, getting married. Life in Skyrim ain't all bad, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0CDA0E: Good. The two of us then. All of Skyrim should get ready to stand aside.
Skyrim.esm|0CDA0F: Then it's settled. You and me.
Skyrim.esm|0CDA12: Very well. I am poorer for it.
Skyrim.esm|0CDA13: What are you, some kind of tease? Stendarr's Mercy...
Skyrim.esm|0CDA16: You're strong. Clever. I'd be proud to face the challenges of life with you, if you feel the same way.
Skyrim.esm|0CDA17: You have a kind heart. I'd be honored to walk by your side until the trees themselves fade away, if you would have me.
Skyrim.esm|0CDA18: I'd be a fool to turn down a chance of marrying you. I'm game if you're game!
Skyrim.esm|0CDA19: You are pleasant company, more than pleasant. And what about you? Would you... be interested in a life together?
Skyrim.esm|0CDA1A: Won't lie, I am. And you?
Skyrim.esm|0CDAEC: I doubt it can be repaired. You'll have to buy a new one.
Skyrim.esm|0CDAED: My tunic is fading. Too much sun and salt spray.
Skyrim.esm|0CDAEE: If you give me a list, I'll have them delivered to the dock by tomorrow morning.
Skyrim.esm|0CDAEF: I need some things for the ship.
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF0: You should be more careful about the women you spend time with in port. Come back later and it will be ready.
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF1: Can you make some new poultices for my...you know?
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF4: We've got a murderer on the loose...
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF5: What...? Whoever did this, you're dead. You hear me? Dead!
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF6: Kill one of us, and three more will take their place. The Forsworn are unstoppable.
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF7: Your body lies dead, but your soul has joined our ranks...
Skyrim.esm|0CDAF8: Freshly killed. Whoever did this is still close... and will suffer for their insolence.
Skyrim.esm|0CDB48: I'll claw the eyes out of whoever did this!
Skyrim.esm|0CDB4A: Dead! Someone's going to pay for this.
Skyrim.esm|0CDB4E: What...? No... No, this can't be happening...
Skyrim.esm|0CDB50: Murdered? By Azura, who would do such a thing?
Skyrim.esm|0CDB51: What? Someone will suffer for this injustice.
Skyrim.esm|0CDB52: I'm going to find whoever did this, and when I do...
Skyrim.esm|0CDB53: By Ysmir! There's a killer about.
Skyrim.esm|0CDB54: Hmph. Looks like we got a cutthroat skulkin' around...
Skyrim.esm|0CDD66: Man gives bards a bad name. We Nords have a long history as warrior poets. I am proud to count myself among them. But Mikael... He sings only for gold and the affections of wenches. He dishonors our traditions.
Skyrim.esm|0CDD69: Shop at Belethor's for the best prices and selection. He pays me to say that.
Skyrim.esm|0CDD6A: I work for Belethor, at the general goods store.
Skyrim.esm|0CDD6B: Come by Belethor's shop, at the market. I'm sure we've got something you need.
Skyrim.esm|0CDD6C: Don't mean to be rude, but I'm too busy to talk right now.
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF0: Dovahkiin, you call yourself? Arrogant mortal.
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF2: I will feast on your soul in Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF3: You are no match for me, joor!
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF4: Paarthurnax is weak. I am strong.
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF7: You are no match for me, Dovahkiin.
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF8: I have feasted on many greater than you.
Skyrim.esm|0CDDF9: You will pay for your defiance!
Skyrim.esm|0CDDFA: My teeth to your neck, Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|0CDE46: And they call me a savage. Least I don't leave my trash lying around...
Skyrim.esm|0CDE61: Not a chance. Not for this. You're going to the Cidhna Mine for the rest of your days.
Skyrim.esm|0CDE63: Men and women join the war, they travel far on these roads to spill each other's blood.
Skyrim.esm|0CDE64: Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
Skyrim.esm|0CDE8B: Wouldn't surprise me. They've done nothing to help in the fight for Skyrim's freedom. Those Thalmor are elves too. I bet they're working together. Maybe I should round up some men and take us a few prisoners to interrogate.
Skyrim.esm|0CDEC3: They're parasites. They're living in our city, under our protection, but what do they do for us? Nothing! I know the high king invited them here, but he didn't ask me or anyone else first. Maybe he should have.
Skyrim.esm|0CDEC6: It was difficult at first. The Nords of this city are, at best, suspicious of outsiders. But in time, I made the right friends and proved myself useful enough that they don't give me trouble anymore. The Dark Elves are too proud and naive to understand the way things truly are, and so they continue to dwell in that slum.
Skyrim.esm|0CDEC7: I'm a Dark Elf and I live in Windhelm, so yes, I live in the Gray Quarter. You must be new around here, or you'd know they don't let my kind live anywhere outside that slum.
Skyrim.esm|0CDEC8: Nothing new there. Most of the Nords living in Windhelm don't care much for us, but Rolff is the worst by far. He likes to get drunk and walk around the Gray Quarter yelling insults at us in the small hours of the morning. A real charmer, that one.
Skyrim.esm|0CDEE0: Did you hear all that noise outside?
Skyrim.esm|0CDEE1: You there. If you're here to trade, step up to the counter.
Skyrim.esm|0CDEE2: It's just the market rabble. We should get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|0CDF9F: Sit back and enjoy the ride.
Skyrim.esm|0CDFA0: Don't be long. I can't wait around forever.
Skyrim.esm|0CDFA2: Where do you want to go?
Skyrim.esm|0CE08C: You'd be wasted as a regular soldier. I have special plans for you. You'll be of greater use to me with greater flexibility. Make your way to our hidden military camp in The Pale. Rikke will have important tasks for you, and will need you when we reclaim the capital.
Skyrim.esm|0CE08D: I suspect you'll be of greater use to us with greater freedom, so you're free to engage the Imperials as you see fit. But I also want you to find our hidden camp in Falkreath. Galmar will have special tasks for you, and will need you when we liberate the capital.
Skyrim.esm|0CE08E: You're needed in Winterhold. Report to our hidden camp there.
Skyrim.esm|0CE08F: You're needed in Hjaalmarch. There's much to be done.
Skyrim.esm|0CE090: I'm promoting you to Quaestor. Take this blade, a symbol of your new station and responsibility. I'll keep an eye on your progress. The Empire rewards excellence, and so do I.
Skyrim.esm|0CE091: Battles are won by trained and disciplined men. Wars are won by talented and exceptional individuals. I'm raising you to Praefect. Take this blade, as a gift and symbol of your new rank. You've earned it.
Skyrim.esm|0CE092: You've come a long way. I don't think we could do without you. I'm hereby raising you to Tribune. Congratulations. Here, take this gift as a sign of the Empire's appreciation, and my own.
Skyrim.esm|0CE096: I have come to rely heavily upon you. It is only fitting that you join the upper echelon. I am hereby elevating you to the rank of Legate. On behalf of the Empire, and myself, please accept this gift along with your new rank. Congratulations.
Skyrim.esm|0CE097: However, I'm disappointed to hear that you fled from the field of battle. We do not reward cowardice in the Legion. I expect better of you.
Skyrim.esm|0CE098: We'll call you Ice-Veins now, for the thick blood of our land has seeped into your heart. Here, take this. It's an Imperial officer's sword. A fitting weapon to use against our enemy.
Skyrim.esm|0CE099: Some brothers have taken to calling you Bone-Breaker, evidence of your ferocity and determination. A fitting name. So we shall all call you thus. The war demands so much from us, and we give all we have to it and to the people... but I've kept a little something to offer you in appreciation. Please take this on behalf of all the brothers and sisters. And I need capable and loyal warriors close at hand. So I'd like to offer you a home in Windhelm. Speak with my steward who'll make the arrangements.
Skyrim.esm|0CE09A: Multitudes of our enemies are buried in an avalanche of pain and retribution delivered by your hands. I shall call you Snow-Hammer now. You show great passion for battle, and compassion for those we fight for. You are becoming indispensable to our cause. We do what we do out of love for our brothers and sisters. Here, take this as a token of such love reciprocated.
Skyrim.esm|0CE09E: You've become a true hero of Skyrim. I number you among my kin. You shall now be known as Stormblade. The love of the land and her people flows from your heart, even as death to her enemies flows from your hands. On behalf of the sons and daughters of Skyrim, on behalf of all that is righteous and true, take this token of our appreciation for your service.
Skyrim.esm|0CE09F: But I'm disappointed to hear you ran away from the battle. The gods do not reward cowardice. Nor do I. I expect better from you.
Skyrim.esm|0CE253: Sorry, a girl can't be too careful out here. Like I said, name's Angi and I've been living out here for a couple of years now.
Skyrim.esm|0CE258: I moved here from Helgen after my family was killed. At the time I couldn't stand to be around anyone. They all felt sorry for me and I didn't want them to. It was my problem, not theirs.
Skyrim.esm|0CE25D: Name's Angi and if you try anything stupid, I won't hesitate to put an arrow in your head.
Skyrim.esm|0CE25E: It's not often I get visitors out here. You lost, stranger?
Skyrim.esm|0CE25F: Two Imperial drunks who thought they were above the law.
Skyrim.esm|0CE260: Don't be like them. Don't feel sorry for me.
Skyrim.esm|0CE29B: My money's on the big one.
Skyrim.esm|0CE2B8: Why are they hurting each other?
Skyrim.esm|0CE2C7: I could also do some good for a change. Sure, I'd go with you.
Skyrim.esm|0CE2C8: I suppose it would be better than sitting around thinking about all this.
Skyrim.esm|0CE328: New in town, huh? Whiterun's got two clans, both old and both respected. Difference is, the Gray-Manes turned their backs on the Empire and we Battle-Borns stayed loyal. So I'll ask again, Gray-Mane or Battle-Born?
Skyrim.esm|0CE329: Then I say well met, friend. I could tell you were a sharp one the moment I laid eyes on you.
Skyrim.esm|0CE32A: Then you're either a Stormcloak sympathizer or a fool. Either way, you're no friend of mine.
Skyrim.esm|0CE32B: Got stones in your ears? I asked what side you're on, Gray-Mane or Battle-Born!
Skyrim.esm|0CE32C: Sooner or later, we all have to choose a side.
Skyrim.esm|0CE6EF: There's a whole college up in Solitude where they teach the arts. Song. Lute-playing. Poetry. I studied there myself. Had to leave over a misunderstanding with a headmistress. Well, more a misunderstanding with her husband.
Skyrim.esm|0CE6FE: I set up those targets a long time ago. I shoot at them from time to time. Don't want to get rusty with my bow.
Skyrim.esm|0CE6FF: That I am. You know how to use one?
Skyrim.esm|0CED8C: They say that these ancient Nordic burial mounds are sometimes impenetrable.
Skyrim.esm|0CED8D: Fortunately, these doors have a weakness if you know how to exploit it. Quite simple, really.
Skyrim.esm|0CED8F: Quite simple really, I don't know what the fuss is about these locks. All it takes is a bit of know-how and a lot of skill.
Skyrim.esm|0CED90: Without the matching claw, they're normally impossible to open. And since I'm certain Karliah already did away with it, we're on our own.
Skyrim.esm|0CED91: That should do it. After you.
Skyrim.esm|0CED93: Karliah's close, I'm certain of it. Now let's get moving.
Skyrim.esm|0CED94: Ah, it's one of the infamous Nordic puzzle doors. How quaint.
Skyrim.esm|0CED95: Hmm. This one doesn't look too difficult.
Skyrim.esm|0CEDF7: Gal like you needs a warhorse, I reckon.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFB9: I'll cut right to it. You murdered the old woman in the orphanage. You owe the Dark Brotherhood a kill. I've come to collect. One of these poor sods has a contract out on their life. Which one is it? Any idea? Make your choice. Make your kill. And you get to walk away.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFBE: Not so tough now, are you?
Skyrim.esm|0CEFBF: Suit yourself, but you better decide what you want to do about this arrest. No one walks away for free.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFC0: Done. Now move along, before you get me in trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFC3: Well... maybe just this once. But there's still a price on your head. Better make yourself scarce, or we'll have to do this little dance again.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD3: Only inside the gates. Much of Falkreath Hold is wilderness and there's plenty of trouble to be had.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD4: Kust is responsible for the cemetery here in Falkreath. I take care of the shrine.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD5: The number of graves in this cemetery never decreases.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD6: War is not new to Falkreath. You have only to stand in our cemetery to see that.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD7: Dengeir deserves more respect than his nephew, Jarl Siddgeir, affords him.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD8: Without birth there is no life, without death there is only stasis. Such is the truth of Arkay.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFD9: Strangers like you bring war. I've seen enough war.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFDA: These graves hold the sons and daughters of Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFDB: Show respect while you're here and you and I will be fine.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFDC: You have the look of a killer.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFDD: Arkay helps us find peace with death.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFDE: Indara and I like to keep to ourselves and run our farm.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFDF: My wife, Indara, has a gift with animals. It makes her a better farmer than anyone I've ever seen.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE0: Runil has been a true friend to my wife and I in our time of need.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE1: This war isn't needed. Imperials like my wife can be hard to handle, but we Nords could work with them. I should know.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE2: I don't know if the war is Arkay's will or man's, but it's none of my doings.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE3: My husband Mathies and I tend Corpselight Farm.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE4: Falkreath is a hard place and tending a farm here is thankless work.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE5: Mathies is a kind and wise man. The Nords could use more like him. So could the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE6: How could people see all the graves in Falkreath and still want to make war?
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE7: Only a hearty soul travels the road these days.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE8: The war bleeds Skyrim. It bleeds her dry and I think none of us are the better for it.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFE9: Men and women join the war, they travel far on these roads to spill each other's blood.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFEA: Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFEB: Someone new, perhaps someone looking for me to mix something up for them?
Skyrim.esm|0CEFEC: If either these Nords or Imperials had some red in their blood this war would be over.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFED: Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFEE: A hearty welcome to Falkreath! All of Skyrim's true sons are welcome here!
Skyrim.esm|0CEFEF: Stay out of my way, boot.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFF0: I can't believe we let provincials like you wander Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFF1: That Valga Vinicia is a gossip and a troublemaker.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFF2: You'll find my brother Solaf and I are the only true Nords in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFF4: Don't mind Bolund. He's young and gets riled easily.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFF7: Shor's bones! A handsome man in Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFF8: You're going to have the men around here wrapped around your finger in no time.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFFA: Can I get you a drink?
Skyrim.esm|0CEFFC: In Falkreath if I don't know it, it's not worth knowing.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFFD: I escaped fighting in Cyrodiil only to have it track me down again in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFFE: From maid to steward. I can hardly believe it. I hope I'm up to the task.
Skyrim.esm|0CEFFF: I'm sorry, I should really go now.
Skyrim.esm|0CF000: I can't really help you, I'm just Dengeir's maid.
Skyrim.esm|0CF001: Be mindful. My maid Tekla has been known to pass information to Siddgeir.
Skyrim.esm|0CF002: My nephew has always been handed things, even the title of jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0CF003: Stendarr protect Falkreath until Skyrim's sons rise.
Skyrim.esm|0CF004: Enemies and ears. Both are everywhere.
Skyrim.esm|0CF005: The war has changed Falkreath, even our neighbors can be our enemies.
Skyrim.esm|0CF006: Nenya's history of service is impeccable. She truly is the Steward of Falkreath.
Skyrim.esm|0CF007: Falkreath's cemetery reminds each of us that war is not new here.
Skyrim.esm|0CF008: Being Housecarl of Falkreath is an honor.
Skyrim.esm|0CF009: Dengeir was a good jarl in his day. Even if his day has passed.
Skyrim.esm|0CF00A: I'm the steward here. I serve under Jarl Siddgeir and Jarl Dengeir before him.
Skyrim.esm|0CF00B: Siddgeir can sometimes act too quickly, but he does listen to Helvard and I.
Skyrim.esm|0CF00C: For Falkreath and I this is just one more war in a long line.
Skyrim.esm|0CF00D: Stand aside. I don't need to speak to you.
Skyrim.esm|0CF00E: Bolund speaks much about the war and the Stormcloaks, but he knows who provides.
Skyrim.esm|0CF00F: The Stormcloaks want freedom but don't understand its price.
Skyrim.esm|0CF010: There are opportunities everywhere. Why don't you go find them?
Skyrim.esm|0CF011: No time, I have business to conduct.
Skyrim.esm|0CF012: Unless you intend to bury someone this isn't the place you're looking for, traveler.
Skyrim.esm|0CF013: Falkreath was once full of wanderers and warriors like yourself. Those were better days.
Skyrim.esm|0CF014: Skyrim's history of war is well documented here in Falkreath's graveyard.
Skyrim.esm|0CF015: My brother Dengeir can be... difficult. Take his words with a grain of salt.
Skyrim.esm|0CF030: While I appreciate the sentiment, I still have to decline. Being an admirer, I'm sure you can appreciate the need to keep my research a secret.
Skyrim.esm|0CF031: Do you realize that at a snap of my fingers I can bring the entire Markarth city guard to my defense? You best rethink this course of action or you may find yourself on the executioner's block.
Skyrim.esm|0CF212: If you need a bow, there's one on the rack to the left of the door.
Skyrim.esm|0CF213: Already did. Part of the reason I'm living out here now. Thanks for offering, though.
Skyrim.esm|0CF215: I'd give you a bow, but it looks like you've already taken it from the rack.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D3: An outsider, eh? Never heard of the Companions? An order of warriors. We are brothers and sisters in honor. And we show up to solve problems if the coin is good enough.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D4: Certainly not. But a true warrior would have relished the opportunity to take on a giant. That's why I'm here with my shield-brothers.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D5: Well nobody asked you. If you think you're better than we are, go talk to Kodlak Whitemane. See what a warrior of true mettle is like.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D6: Ha. Another milk drinker crying about their effort. Glory only awaits those who triumph. And as Companions, our glory must always be hard-fought.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D7: Not for me to say. You'll have to talk to Kodlak Whitemane up in Jorrvaskr. The old man's got a good sense for people. He can look in your eyes and tell your worth. If you go to him, good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D8: You handle yourself well. You could make for a decent shield-brother.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2D9: You handle yourself well. You could make for a decent shield-sister.
Skyrim.esm|0CF2DA: Well, that's taken care of. No thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|0CF756: I am! I am! But not just my mother. Our mother, hmm? The Night Mother! Oh yes! But you disappeared! You were there... then you weren't! Did you mean to help poor Cicero or not? I'm left to wonder... Ah, it doesn't matter. What's done is done, right? Cicero is here! As is the Night Mother! Joy of joys!
Skyrim.esm|0CF7AE: Here to shame an old woman? Leave me be.
Skyrim.esm|0CF909: But... stealing the boss' sword? Did he have a death wish?
Skyrim.esm|0CF90C: Ra'jirr was always dragging her into things.
Skyrim.esm|0CF90D: But... Eisa? She's smarter than that.
Skyrim.esm|0CF90F: Who knows? The cat was crazy. She was a fool to trust him.
Skyrim.esm|0D0312: We've talked about composure, speed, and precision. Now it's time to use all three at once.
Skyrim.esm|0D0316: Just remember everything I've taught you and you should be able to complete it.
Skyrim.esm|0D0317: However, with such a long shot, it's more than likely that's the only shot you'll be able to take before your prey runs off.
Skyrim.esm|0D0318: I'll count to three and then say "go." You have ten seconds to hit each of the four targets. Draw string and get ready.
Skyrim.esm|0D031B: Okay, your turn. Try and hit the target way in the back, far behind the other three. Remember to take your time and make your shot count.
Skyrim.esm|0D031D: You did it! All four targets in ten seconds. Nice job.
Skyrim.esm|0D031F: Often times you won't be able to get as close to your prey as you'd like and you'll have to settle with a long shot.
Skyrim.esm|0D0320: When I tell you to, try and hit all four targets in ten seconds.
Skyrim.esm|0D0321: I'm warning you now, this challenge will be quite difficult.
Skyrim.esm|0D0323: Try and hit the far target.
Skyrim.esm|0D0326: Excellent. Follow me to the targets and we'll begin.
Skyrim.esm|0D0329: Time's up! Better luck next time.
Skyrim.esm|0D032A: Here, take these. Looks like you're running low.
Skyrim.esm|0D032B: Listen to me and you might learn something.
Skyrim.esm|0D032C: We've talked about speed and composure. Now we'll go over precision.
Skyrim.esm|0D032E: If you find yourself in such a situation, then you need to make your shot count. Don't rush your shot. Crouch down, get comfortable, and take aim.
Skyrim.esm|0D0503: I seem to have more chores for the Winking Skeever each day. I still have a delivery to do too.
Skyrim.esm|0D0505: As long as they're playing instruments they're passable. You don't want to hear them sing.
Skyrim.esm|0D0506: Greta is taking it hardest. She won't go to the Temple of Divines anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0D0507: A little late in the day for your rounds Sorex.
Skyrim.esm|0D0509: Speaking of sounding good, I've heard a couple of your classmates on the street recently.
Skyrim.esm|0D050A: I'm sorry about Roggvir. It's a shame, I liked him.
Skyrim.esm|0D050B: Thank you, Addvar, I am picky about what I eat these days.
Skyrim.esm|0D050F: I've been pushing the Salmon but I'll tell you the Slaughterfish is a bit better today.
Skyrim.esm|0D0510: Sorry, I need to pay attention so I can remember this.
Skyrim.esm|0D0511: A war veteran should be respected.
Skyrim.esm|0D0512: Thanks, Aia. That means a lot.
Skyrim.esm|0D0514: Stand back. Interrupting an execution is a serious crime.
Skyrim.esm|0D0515: Fresh fish sounds like just the thing today, Addvar.
Skyrim.esm|0D0518: Roggvir always talked about his honor. Seems to me honorable men don't get executed.
Skyrim.esm|0D0519: A sale's a sale but I have to know... why are you buying something for Noster?
Skyrim.esm|0D051A: None of what he collected tasted as good as your Spiced Wine, Evette.
Skyrim.esm|0D051B: I'm still finding your Spiced Wine to be the best drink I've ever tasted.
Skyrim.esm|0D051D: Excuse me, I'm trying to watch a traitor to the Emperor lose his head.
Skyrim.esm|0D051E: I am buying a fish for Noster. He likes fish.
Skyrim.esm|0D051F: Rum from Hammerfell, mulled wine from Daggerfall and the like.
Skyrim.esm|0D0521: I don't think he would have wanted your absolution, Rorlund. He worshipped Talos.
Skyrim.esm|0D0522: A traitor right up to the end.
Skyrim.esm|0D0524: Okay. Well. That's all I've got.
Skyrim.esm|0D0528: You know, my father collected exotic alcohols.
Skyrim.esm|0D052A: I wish we could have absolved your brother-in-law of his sins before his execution.
Skyrim.esm|0D052B: Jawanan, I don't see you here often.
Skyrim.esm|0D054F: Stop moping about, girl. Tears won't change what you've done.
Skyrim.esm|0D0550: Real men prove their love. Words are as empty as the air they run through.
Skyrim.esm|0D0552: You should be more careful with the men you pick, dear.
Skyrim.esm|0D0553: Oh right, the rum. Thank you. I developed a taste for it after a fair weather trip to Hammerfell. Here, this is for you.
Skyrim.esm|0D0555: Save me a lot of trouble. Here. Take it to the Blue Palace.
Skyrim.esm|0D0556: Jarl Elisif the Fair. Poor woman. Losing her husband and king like that. It was a dark day for us all. Damn, that reminds me, I have a delivery to make to her steward, Falk Firebeard. He ordered a special bottle of Stros M'Kai Rum.
Skyrim.esm|0D0557: We all get used, dear. It's living with it that makes you a woman.
Skyrim.esm|0D0558: I was such a fool. How could I let him use me like that?
Skyrim.esm|0D0591: Come on! Before the dragon brings down the whole tower!
Skyrim.esm|0D0592: What are you waiting for? Up the stairs! Now!
Skyrim.esm|0D0598: I do hope mummy and father come to get me soon. Ever since that Aventus boy left, Grelod has been so angry.
Skyrim.esm|0D0599: Things are much better now that Grelod is dead, but still. I'm sick of this place.
Skyrim.esm|0D059A: I can't believe Grelod is dead. I had no love for the woman, but to be murdered, in cold blood...
Skyrim.esm|0D05AB: The boss went down there? Today?
Skyrim.esm|0D05AC: Now you're sounding as crazy as the cat. Be going on about the Pale Lady next.
Skyrim.esm|0D05AD: What? There's a whole shift down there!
Skyrim.esm|0D05AF: Kyr's orders. Nobody goes in or out until he gets back. Or the boss will skin you soon as he's done with Ra'jirr.
Skyrim.esm|0D05B0: I've got a bad feeling about this... something's just felt wrong down there lately. Eerie.
Skyrim.esm|0D05B2: Yeah, he's after Ra'jirr- never seen him so angry.
Skyrim.esm|0D0652: We have a cozy little profit. Here. This is your share, love.
Skyrim.esm|0D0653: We've made a bit of gold. Here, love, this is your share.
Skyrim.esm|0D0654: Fortune smiles on us. Here's your share, love.
Skyrim.esm|0D0655: The gold is flowing nicely. Here's your half of the profits, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|0D0656: You want your share, don't you? Here you are, love.
Skyrim.esm|0D0657: Of course it has. Would you expect any less of me? Here, this is your half, my love.
Skyrim.esm|0D0658: It has. Here, this is your half, love.
Skyrim.esm|0D0695: You should probably just take this directly to Faleen. Wouldn't want the old icebrain stumbling over the words.
Skyrim.esm|0D0696: You should probably just let Faleen read it herself. Don't want you stumbling over the words and ruining my flow.
Skyrim.esm|0D0697: As for what she might like... I didn't tell you this. Faleen likes to act tough, but she really has a soft spot for, of all things, poetry. You know, I took some classes at the Bards College as a youth. Poems come in handy when wooing. There's a poem I once used on an older lady of Rorikstead. I can change it to be about Faleen, if you've got some gold.
Skyrim.esm|0D0698: Calcelmo? Is he interested in Faleen? That sly old codger. I should have guessed. Good for him. For the both of them. Between you and me, she could use a bit of warmth.
Skyrim.esm|0D0699: Ah, a secret admirer, eh? Don't worry, I won't tell a soul.
Skyrim.esm|0D069B: I don't have time for your foolishness. Either help me or be gone.
Skyrim.esm|0D06E4: If it's a fight you want, it's a fight you'll get.
Skyrim.esm|0D06E6: You'll have to take it from me.
Skyrim.esm|0D06E8: You'll have to fight me for it.
Skyrim.esm|0D06E9: I'll hurt you if I have to.
Skyrim.esm|0D06EA: You're too slow and stupid to take it from me.
Skyrim.esm|0D06EC: Well! Finders, keepers as they say.
Skyrim.esm|0D06ED: If nobody wants that, I'll take it.
Skyrim.esm|0D06EE: Damn shame to leave that lying around.
Skyrim.esm|0D06EF: It's a bit worn, but I'll take it.
Skyrim.esm|0D06F0: Looks like it's my lucky day.
Skyrim.esm|0D06F2: I saw it first! I'm taking it.
Skyrim.esm|0D06F3: Not if I get it first.
Skyrim.esm|0D0708: Although... it might be difficult to persuade the Jarl to allow you to use his palace as a dragon trap... I'm sure you'll manage, though. If you can defeat the World-Eater, surely sweet-talking the Jarl of Whiterun isn't beyond you.
Skyrim.esm|0D0709: Esbern should be able to help. He's spent his whole life poring over every old book he could find about dragons and that prophecy. He's been digging around the records the Blades left here, too. But we can talk about that later.
Skyrim.esm|0D070C: Did you find the Elder Scroll? What happened when you took it back to the Throat of the World?
Skyrim.esm|0D070D: What's happened? Did you find a way to learn the Dragonrend Shout?
Skyrim.esm|0D070F: The Jarl's palace in Whiterun? Ah, yes... the old story about King Olaf's pet dragon. Ingenious!
Skyrim.esm|0D073D: Arrogance will serve you poorly. Your worth must be determined before knowledge can be bestowed upon you. And so you shall be tested.
Skyrim.esm|0D073E: I need you to collect some dragon scales. And not just any dragon scales.
Skyrim.esm|0D0742: Colette has already told you. You must be tested before knowledge can be imparted to you.
Skyrim.esm|0D0743: I need dragon scales. And not just any dragon scales.
Skyrim.esm|0D0744: Once again you have come seeking something. The circumstances differ. This time I have what you seek. Are you prepared?
Skyrim.esm|0D0745: The trial awaits. Are you now prepared?
Skyrim.esm|0D0746: At this very moment? No. However... Well, I've been working on something. An improvement on the Ebonyflesh spell. I've made progress, but then hit a snag. I think perhaps you could help.
Skyrim.esm|0D0748: Knowledge is power. Knowing spells can be powerful, but applying that knowledge is key. You will be called upon to rely solely on your Restoration spells.
Skyrim.esm|0D0749: Step into the light, and your test begins.
Skyrim.esm|0D074A: You will rely on your skill as a mage. Not on your belongings, not on your scrolls and potions. Only what lies within. Survive, and you are worthy. This is the test put before you. Will you accept?
Skyrim.esm|0D074B: As I've said, I made some progress using the essence of dragon scales. But they're just not quite potent enough. My knowledge of Skyrim's history is far from complete, but I did some research and discovered two things. First, there are ancient references to dragon "heartscales". What little I could learn suggests they may be what I need. Second, there are stories of a dagger called Kahvozein's Fang, said to be sharp enough to carve individual scales off a dragon. First you'll need to retrieve the dagger, then use it to carve a few scales off a dragon. Presumably a deceased one.
Skyrim.esm|0D07D6: No, no. You just stay here and rest.
Skyrim.esm|0D07D7: You want to stay here, fine. But the rest of us aren't giving up now.
Skyrim.esm|0D07D8: We'll come back for you as soon as we've reached our goal. You have my word.
Skyrim.esm|0D07D9: I don't care... We're going all the way in. I want to know what Shalidor hid in here.
Skyrim.esm|0D07EB: Don't just stand there! Kill that thing! Guards, get the townspeople to safety! Someone get the battlemages out here. Now!
Skyrim.esm|0D07FD: So. You were at Helgen? You saw this dragon with your own eyes?
Skyrim.esm|0D081D: Ahhh, a fine but bloody tale. I can do that one.
Skyrim.esm|0D082C: You must be a Stormcloak through and through to request that one.
Skyrim.esm|0D0836: We drink to our youth, and to days come and gone. For the age of oppression is now nearly done.
Skyrim.esm|0D0837: We'll drive out the Empire from this land that we own. With our blood and our steel we will take back our home.
Skyrim.esm|0D0838: All hail to Ulfric! You are the High King! In your great honor we drink and we sing.
Skyrim.esm|0D0839: We're the children of Skyrim, and we fight all our lives. And when Sovngarde beckons, every one of us dies!
Skyrim.esm|0D083A: But this land is ours and we'll see it wiped clean. Of the scourge that has sullied our hopes and our dreams.
Skyrim.esm|0D083C: There once was a hero named Ragnar the Red, who came riding to Whiterun from ole Rorikstead!
Skyrim.esm|0D083D: And the braggart did swagger and brandish his blade, as he told of bold battles and gold he had made!
Skyrim.esm|0D083E: But then he went quiet, did Ragnar the Red, when he met the shieldmaiden Matilda who said...
Skyrim.esm|0D083F: "Oh, you talk and you lie and you drink all our mead! Now I think it's high time that you lie down and bleed!"
Skyrim.esm|0D0840: And so then came the clashing and slashing of steel, as the brave lass Matilda charged in full of zeal!
Skyrim.esm|0D0841: And the braggart named Ragnar was boastful no moooooree... when his ugly red head rolled around on the floor!
Skyrim.esm|0D1200: Forgive me, my lord. It was foolish of me to make such a suggestion.
Skyrim.esm|0D1201: The finest cuts, fresh from the wilds!
Skyrim.esm|0D1202: Chops and steaks fit for a Jarl! Come and see my selection of finest-quality meats!
Skyrim.esm|0D1203: Shiny trinkets for your good lady! All crafted by the great Eorlund Gray-Mane himself!
Skyrim.esm|0D1204: Bits and baubles for sale, all crafted by the best blacksmith in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D1205: Ripe fruit and fresh vegetables for sale!
Skyrim.esm|0D1206: Fresh-baked loaves, still warm from the oven!
Skyrim.esm|0D13DA: He is set to leave the Penitus Oculatus outpost at Dragon Bridge, and inspect the security of each city in Skyrim. Go there now. Observe Gaius Maro's departure, and follow him. Waylay him in one of the cities, and send his soul to Sithis. Once he's dead, plant the incriminating letter on his body, and let fate take care of the rest. Oh, and one final thing...
Skyrim.esm|0D13DC: Killing Commander Maro would do no good. Someone would merely replace him. No, we need him to remain in charge. But... distracted. Weakened. Implicate and kill his son Gaius, and the commander will be grief-stricken and humiliated, his family name ruined. The security plan will then fail. Best yet, the Emperor will be lulled into a false sense of security, thinking an assassination plot had already been discovered and foiled.
Skyrim.esm|0D13DD: Walk in Gaius Maro's shadow, and deliver to him the judgment of Sithis.
Skyrim.esm|0D13E0: You must be lost. The tavern is down the road a piece.
Skyrim.esm|0D13E1: I'm fairly certain you wandered into the wrong building, friend...
Skyrim.esm|0D1428: That's not the Skyrim I want to live in.
Skyrim.esm|0D142A: Stop what? Taking Skyrim back from those who'd leave her to rot?
Skyrim.esm|0D142B: Stand aside woman. We've come for the General.
Skyrim.esm|0D142C: Rikke. Go. You're free to leave.
Skyrim.esm|0D142D: You were there with us. You saw it. The day the Empire signed that damn treaty was the day the Empire died.
Skyrim.esm|0D142E: Rikke. You don't have to do this.
Skyrim.esm|0D142F: The Empire is weak, obsolete. Look at how far we've come and with so little. When we're done rooting out Imperial influence here at home, then we will take our war to the Aldmeri Dominion.
Skyrim.esm|0D1431: This is what you wanted? Shield brothers and sisters killing each other? Families torn apart? This is the Skyrim you want?!
Skyrim.esm|0D1432: I'm also free to stay and fight for what I believe in.
Skyrim.esm|0D1435: He has given up. But I have not.
Skyrim.esm|0D1437: Always was a stubborn woman. She's not going to stand down.
Skyrim.esm|0D1438: War always comes down to this, doesn't it? A single truth-laden moment.
Skyrim.esm|0D1439: Dragonborn... Perhaps this is the fate of Skyrim after all. To be destroyed from the inside.
Skyrim.esm|0D143A: Aye, I'll hate it, but I'll do it if I have to.
Skyrim.esm|0D143B: It's up to her, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0D143C: If that must be, then it will. And why should I be any different? How many the lives already lost. What's one more?
Skyrim.esm|0D143E: You're a cold hearted bastard, aren't you?
Skyrim.esm|0D143F: I fear that will be the way of it.
Skyrim.esm|0D1440: A true Nord never fears death. It's the how and why of it that one needs consider.
Skyrim.esm|0D1441: You're wrong. Ulfric. We need the Empire. Without it Skyrim will assuredly fall to the Dominion.
Skyrim.esm|0D1442: You've left me no choice... Talos preserve us.
Skyrim.esm|0D1443: You're also free to die for it.
Skyrim.esm|0D15AE: We Gray-Manes got just as much to be proud of as them Battle-Borns, but you won't see us with our noses in the air.
Skyrim.esm|0D16CB: I think my voice is ready. I hope we've done this well.
Skyrim.esm|0D180B: Does the journal say where this wealth came from?
Skyrim.esm|0D180C: Hmm. This is intriguing, but highly disturbing.
Skyrim.esm|0D180D: I'm not familiar with the Twilight Sepulcher. What is it? What's Mercer Frey done?
Skyrim.esm|0D180E: Anything else, Enthir? Anything about... the Nightingales?
Skyrim.esm|0D180F: It's alright Karliah. You don't have to say a word.
Skyrim.esm|0D1811: The last few pages seem to describe "the failure of the Nightingales" although it doesn't go into great detail. Gallus also repeatedly mentions his strong belief that Mercer desecrated something known as the Twilight Sepulcher.
Skyrim.esm|0D1813: Yes. Gallus seems certain that Mercer had been removing funds from the Guild's treasury without anyone's knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|0D1814: It appears that Gallus had suspicions about Mercer Frey's allegiance to the Guild for months. Gallus had begun to uncover what he calls an "...unduly lavish lifestyle replete with spending vast amounts of gold on personal pleasures."
Skyrim.esm|0D1815: Shadows preserve us. So it's true...
Skyrim.esm|0D1817: As a Nightingale, I've been sworn to secrecy regarding the Sepulcher. I know the Guild doesn't do much to foster faith, but I'm going to have to ask that you continue to trust me.
Skyrim.esm|0D1818: I'm sorry Enthir, I can't say. All that matters is we deliver your translation to the Guild immediately. Farewell, Enthir... words can't express...
Skyrim.esm|0D1819: Shhh. Listen to Enthir... we can't afford to miss a single detail.
Skyrim.esm|0D181A: We haven't much time for discussion, but what did you need?
Skyrim.esm|0D1821: Don't you go trackin' your dirty boots on my clean floor!
Skyrim.esm|0D1822: Soldiers! They're everywhere these days. And not a one of them knows how to pick up after themselves.
Skyrim.esm|0D194E: No. I was just getting some fresh air. Had one too many pints of mead at the Silver-Blood Inn.
Skyrim.esm|0D194F: Oh, I think you dropped this. Some kind of note. Looks important.
Skyrim.esm|0D1950: You don't have to say sorry to me. I just hope the Eight bring us more peace in the future.
Skyrim.esm|0D1951: Huh. Not sure. I don't worship Talos, myself. I think I heard someone mention it was underneath the Temple of Dibella, in the big crag in the center of the city.
Skyrim.esm|0D1952: My note? No, that's yours. Must have fallen out of your pocket.
Skyrim.esm|0D195D: Sure, I understand. It's not easy to go from being executed by the Legion one day to joining up the next. But I think you'll see that the Legion is Skyrim's only hope for real peace right now. I know you'll make the right choice in the end.
Skyrim.esm|0D1961: I hope so. The Legion is Skyrim's only hope right now. Come on, we'd better get moving.
Skyrim.esm|0D197B: Welcome to Whiterun, friend. Go right on in.
Skyrim.esm|0D197C: Halt! City's closed with the dragons about. Official business only.
Skyrim.esm|0D197D: Oh yes, many times. But mighty Talos was my keeper, my shield against the heresy of the south! If not for Talos, and a few modest bribes paid to the right jailors, why, I would not be standing before you today, resplendent in my faith!
Skyrim.esm|0D197F: Or else what? You think you can stand against the entire Whiterun city guard? The gate's closed.
Skyrim.esm|0D1980: Riverwood's in danger, too? You'd better go on in. You'll find the Jarl in Dragonsreach, at the top of the hill.
Skyrim.esm|0D1981: Fine, but we'll be keeping an eye on you.
Skyrim.esm|0D19A3: Of course! I know, today wasn't the best introduction to the Legion, but I hope you'll give us another chance. The Legion could really use someone like you, especially now. And if the rebels have themselves a dragon, General Tullius is the only one who can stop them.
Skyrim.esm|0D19A4: General Tullius has his headquarters in Castle Dour, in the city of Solitude, northwest of here. You'll want to talk to Legate Rikke. She deals with the new recruits. I'll be sure to put in a good word for you if I get back to Solitude first.
Skyrim.esm|0D19A5: Oh, of course you want me to leave. Just waiting for me to turn my back. So you can have all the glory for yourself!
Skyrim.esm|0D19A6: Sulla look, the passage is open, wait... there's someone down there!
Skyrim.esm|0D19A7: Sulla, let's just get out of here. Hasn't there been enough death?
Skyrim.esm|0D19A9: No! Blackreach was to be my discovery!
Skyrim.esm|0D1D55: No, I'm a Redguard. My family hails from the Imperial Province. Ah, but I left home when I was just a lad. Been wandering ever since.
Skyrim.esm|0D1D57: No, I'm not. Looks like we've got something in common, then.
Skyrim.esm|0D1D58: Hmph. No, friend, that I'm not. But I get along with your kind well enough.
