Skyrim.esm|0D1D59: Ha! And neither are you, eh? Good to see another Redguard. I like to think we bring a little heat to this frozen wasteland.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E06: That Empire is dead. And so are you.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E07: It's a little more than a rebellion, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|0D1E08: We aren't the bad guys you know.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E09: Well, Dragonborn. What do you say? Do you want the honor?
Skyrim.esm|0D1E0B: Perhaps you're right. But then what does that make you?
Skyrim.esm|0D1E0C: Just kill him and let's be done with it already.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E0D: This is it for you. Any last words before I send you to Oblivion?
Skyrim.esm|0D1E0E: By the gods! If it's a good ending to some damn story you're after - perhaps the Dragonborn should be the one to do it.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E11: The Empire I remember never surrendered.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E15: Maybe not, but you certainly aren't the good guys.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E16: You realize this is exactly what they wanted.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E18: The Thalmor. They stirred up trouble here. Forced us to divert needed resources and throw away good soldiers quelling this rebellion.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E19: Dragonborn. Here, use my sword to do it. This moment will be immortalized in song. Make it a good kill.
Skyrim.esm|0D1E1A: Come, Galmar. Where's your sense of the dramatic moment?
Skyrim.esm|0D1E1B: As you wish. This moment, we three, will be immortalized in song.
Skyrim.esm|0D231A: You want answers? Well so do I. So does everyone in this city. A man goes crazy in the market. Everyone knows he's a Forsworn agent. Guards do nothing. Nothing but clean up the mess.
Skyrim.esm|0D2321: You're right, I saw it. Didn't want to believe my own eyes, is all.
Skyrim.esm|0D2322: A dragon... here in Skyrim. What's this world coming to? First the war, now dragons... trouble loves company, they say...
Skyrim.esm|0D2323: Suit yourself. I hate to turn away any friend of Hadvar, but in times like these... you understand if I can't just take your word for it.
Skyrim.esm|0D2324: What? A dragon? In Helgen? That explains what I saw earlier... flying down the valley from the south... I was hoping I was wrong about what I thought it was...
Skyrim.esm|0D2325: Of course. Take what you need.
Skyrim.esm|0D2326: There's something you could do for me. For all of us here.
Skyrim.esm|0D2327: The Jarl needs to know if there's a dragon on the loose. Riverwood is defenseless... We need to get word to Jarl Balgruuf in Whiterun to send whatever soldiers he can. If you'll do that for me, I'll be in your debt.
Skyrim.esm|0D2377: Trouble is in the air here.
Skyrim.esm|0D2378: I've got a bad feeling about this.
Skyrim.esm|0D237A: I don't like where this is going.
Skyrim.esm|0D237B: I have a feeling there's going to be trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0D237E: Don't like the looks of this.
Skyrim.esm|0D2380: Got a feeling there's trouble ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0D2382: I don't think I like where this is going.
Skyrim.esm|0D2383: Another filthy cave. Always so dark.
Skyrim.esm|0D2385: Hey look, a cave. Wonder what's inside?
Skyrim.esm|0D2386: Look, a cave. An exciting place to venture, if you know what you're doing.
Skyrim.esm|0D2387: Look at that, a cave. Who knows what could be down there?
Skyrim.esm|0D2388: Look, a cave. A fine place to find trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0D238B: There's a cave here. Think we should check it out?
Skyrim.esm|0D238C: We've found a cave. I don't think there are warm fires and friendly faces inside.
Skyrim.esm|0D238D: There's a cave. Animals or bandits inside, no doubt.
Skyrim.esm|0D238E: A cave. Up for some hunting?
Skyrim.esm|0D238F: Cave up ahead. Trouble or maybe treasure?
Skyrim.esm|0D2390: Look. Nordic ruins. I hope there's gold inside.
Skyrim.esm|0D2392: Is that one of the old ruins?
Skyrim.esm|0D2394: My word, look at those ruins. Remarkable.
Skyrim.esm|0D2395: Look at those ruins. How long have they stood here, I wonder?
Skyrim.esm|0D2396: Old stonework. Gold, traps, and bones.
Skyrim.esm|0D2397: Nord stonework. Thousands of years old.
Skyrim.esm|0D2398: Look at those ruins. Think there's gold inside?
Skyrim.esm|0D2399: The old Nords sure did love their stonework.
Skyrim.esm|0D239A: Look at these stones. The ancestors knew how to build, no doubt.
Skyrim.esm|0D239B: Ruins ahead. Means old traps and bonewalkers.
Skyrim.esm|0D239C: Look. One of the ancient Nord ruins. Who knows what's down there?
Skyrim.esm|0D239D: The entrance to a dwarven city. All manner of strange traps and machines will be inside.
Skyrim.esm|0D239E: Is this that dwarven city? I hear the treasure from their ruins is worth a fortune.
Skyrim.esm|0D239F: Dwemer ruins. The people under the mountains. All gone now.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A0: Look over there. That's a dwarven ruin.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A1: Dwarven metalwork. So much wasted effort.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A2: Oh, look. It's the entrance to a dwarven city. I've heard stories....
Skyrim.esm|0D23A3: I dislike lurking near dwarven cities. It's like their ghosts are watching us.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A5: A Dwemer city. The lost people. The heretics.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A6: Is that a dwarven city? I've never seen a dwarf, ever. Don't think anyone has.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A7: Seems like a ruin. Dwarven, I think?
Skyrim.esm|0D23A8: By the Mane, look. A dwarven city. Still sitting here after thousands of years.
Skyrim.esm|0D23A9: Get nervous around dwarven stonework. Legends say these cities are haunted.
Skyrim.esm|0D23AA: Old dwarven ruin. Look at that metalwork. Such a waste.
Skyrim.esm|0D23AB: That's a dwarven city. I hear they built armor that walks like people.
Skyrim.esm|0D23AF: Hey, there's a cave. Bad place to stumble drunk.
Skyrim.esm|0D23B0: Look at those old stones. Ancient Nords sure could build.
Skyrim.esm|0D23C8: I want to help you, Dragonborn. And I will. But I need your help first.
Skyrim.esm|0D23C9: Now, what's this nonsense about trapping a dragon in my palace?
Skyrim.esm|0D23CA: Ulfric and General Tullius are both just waiting for me to make a wrong move. Do you think they will sit idle while a dragon is slaughtering my men and burning my city?
Skyrim.esm|0D23CB: Spoken like a true Nord. I'll stand beside you, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0D2446: No Nord could have said it better! I'll stand beside you, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0D2447: I don't know about such things, but I heard the Greybeards summon you. That's good enough for me.
Skyrim.esm|0D2448: I didn't say anything about giving up.
Skyrim.esm|0D246B: We'll be ready, don't worry. You do have a plan for actually luring a dragon into this trap, don't you?
Skyrim.esm|0D251D: My men will be ready when you are, Dragonborn. You have a truce to arrange first, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0D2540: Then... Whiterun will stand with you, Dragonborn. We will take the risk, and gain the glory, whether it be in victory or defeat! So what's the plan, then? How do you intend to lure a dragon into the trap?
Skyrim.esm|0D2580: Alduin? The World-Eater himself? But... how can we fight him? Doesn't his return mean it's the end times?
Skyrim.esm|0D2581: How can we fight Alduin? Doesn't his return mean it's the end times?
Skyrim.esm|0D2595: What you're asking for is insane. Impossible! Why would I agree to let a dragon into the heart of my city, after working so hard to keep them out?
Skyrim.esm|0D2597: Aye, Dragonborn. Maybe you can stop the dragons - and this war into the bargain.
Skyrim.esm|0D265A: Need you to deliver some false orders to the Imperial Legate in Morthal. But first we need to get our hands on some Imperial orders to make the forgeries.
Skyrim.esm|0D267A: Need you to deliver some false orders to the Stormcloak commander in Dawnstar. Once you find the courier's route, kill him and take his place.
Skyrim.esm|0D26E2: Need you to deliver some false orders to the Imperial Legate in Morthal. Once you find the courier's route, kill him and take his place.
Skyrim.esm|0D26E4: We understand the Nightgate and Roughwater inns are frequent stops for Stormcloak runners. Set an ambush along the road between, or camp by one of the inns. Bring whatever he's carrying to me, and I'll send you with convincing misinformation back to Dawnstar.
Skyrim.esm|0D2798: My scouts tell me the inns at Dragonbridge and Rorikstead are frequent stops for Imperial runners. Set an ambush on the road between, or camp one of the inns. Bring me whatever he's carrying, and I'll send you to Morthal with something convincing but full of lies.
Skyrim.esm|0D27D5: Two things I have for you. Two shapes. One edged, one round. The round one, for tuning. Dwemer music is soft and subtle, and needed to open their cleverest gates. The edged lexicon, for inscribing. To us, a hunk of metal. To the Dwemer, a full library of knowings. But... empty. Find Mzark and its sky-dome. The machinations there will read the Scroll and lay the lore upon the cube. Trust Septimus. He knows you can know.
Skyrim.esm|0D27D9: Now you just stay here, with all your friends, and I'm going to go get my shovel.
Skyrim.esm|0D27DA: Let me just take this coin purse off your hands for you. And those nice boots you've got there.
Skyrim.esm|0D27DB: Well, seems like you have a bit of gold left in your pockets, don't you, my friend?  Mmm, yes...
Skyrim.esm|0D27DE: The city's changed, friend. The Thieves Guild is on the rise... and they've got a firm foothold here in Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0D27DF: Brigands I can handle, but this talk of dragons? World's gone mad, I say.
Skyrim.esm|0D27E1: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Watch the magic!
Skyrim.esm|0D27E2: Go cast your fancy magic someplace else.
Skyrim.esm|0D27E3: Guard might get nervous, a man approaches with his weapon drawn...
Skyrim.esm|0D27E4: Whatever you need, Dragonborn. Just say the word.
Skyrim.esm|0D27EE: I just want to go home and read.
Skyrim.esm|0D27EF: Excuse me, but I'm trying to read.
Skyrim.esm|0D27F0: I import books  whenever I can. Most Nord works are too bawdy and violent for my tastes.
Skyrim.esm|0D27F1: Stormcloaks, Imperials, dragons. Ain't no matter to me what I kill. Let them come.
Skyrim.esm|0D28C4: He's in the inn. Look around. I'm sure you'll find him.
Skyrim.esm|0D28CB: He was here, but he left. If you leave now, you can probably catch him. Or, you can just wait here. He'll be back soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D28CC: I tend to keep my patrons' privacy.
Skyrim.esm|0D28D3: Very good... Let's see what you have here. Interesting... They know more of our plans than I expected... It would seem Fort Dunstad is in need of reinforcements... We'll make sure they won't be getting those...
Skyrim.esm|0D28D4: One moment while I "correct" some of these documents... That should do it.
Skyrim.esm|0D28EB: Make sure those forged documents get to the Stormcloak commander in Dawnstar. It'll throw him off our trail, allowing us to maneuver more freely.
Skyrim.esm|0D28EC: Good work! Let's see what you got. Hmm... They know more of our plans than I thought... Not good... Oh, what's this? Fort Snowhawk is in need of reinforcements... We'll make sure they won't be getting those...
Skyrim.esm|0D28F5: Give me a moment while I make a few, um, "corrections" to these reports... There we go.
Skyrim.esm|0D28F9: Make sure those forged documents get to the Legate in Morthal. They should give them the wrong idea... and us the advantage.
Skyrim.esm|0D298E: All right. Let's take a look... Ah, good. Looks like the reinforcements for the fort are on the way. And we have some information about enemy troop movements as well. Excellent. It's not easy running messages, what with Imperials scouts crawling all over the place. Well done. Why don't you grab yourself a drink at the Windpeak before heading back?
Skyrim.esm|0D298F: Thank you, soldier. Let's take a look at these reports... I'm waiting on word about reinforcements for the fort... Good, they're on the way. And we have some information on enemy troop movements. Excellent. Stormcloak scouts are making it difficult to move around. It's good you made it here in one piece. Why don't you have yourself a drink at the Moorside before you head back?
Skyrim.esm|0D2A46: Look out, I think we have company.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A48: I don't know if we can outrun 'em.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A4C: First time to Solitude? Beautiful old city. Capital of Skyrim, but I'm sure you already knew that.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A4D: Ever been to Markarth? Some say it was built by the dwarves. I don't believe a word of it though.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A4E: Solitude's famous for it's Bards College. Worth a visit if you've never been. Some of them can sing just as pretty as a lark.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A4F: You'll want to watch out for the Forsworn if you do any travelling around the Reach. Bloodthirsty bastards, them.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A50: There once was a hero named Ragnar the Red, who came riding to Whiterun from ole Rorikstead!
Skyrim.esm|0D2A55: Nice weather for a ride. Hope it lasts.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A56: This'll clear up soon, I'm sure of it.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A58: Better bundle up. This may keep up for a while.
Skyrim.esm|0D2A59: A little snow never stopped me, don't worry.
Skyrim.esm|0D2ABB: These fruits and vegetables are disappointing, Carlotta.
Skyrim.esm|0D2ABC: I get them fresh from the farms daily, Nazeem.
Skyrim.esm|0D2ABD: If you were getting them from my farm, they'd be twice as fresh.
Skyrim.esm|0D2AE6: If you can... I want you to kill someone else, as well. You don't have to - not as part of our deal. But if you do... I'll pay you even more. It's Nilsine Shatter-Shield, in Windhelm. If Nilsine dies, too... I'll make it worth your while.
Skyrim.esm|0D2AEA: Don't you see? I was like a daughter to Tova. A sister to Nilsine and Friga. But the family refuses to believe my innocence. No matter what I say.
Skyrim.esm|0D2AED: That hammer... Why that's Aegisbane, heirloom of Clan Shatter-Shield! Last I heard, it was stolen by a couple of swindlers. Alain and... Muiri. Yeah.
Skyrim.esm|0D2AEF: Ah, Aegisbane. Sigil of Clan Shatter-Shield. But didn't that get stolen awhile back? Hmph. Must be rememberin' wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0D2AF3: We got a nickname for anyone who trifles with us guards here in Windhelm - "Suicide."
Skyrim.esm|0D2AFC: My heart goes out to Clan Shatter-Shield. Kin murdered, family warhammer stolen... There's no fairness in all that.
Skyrim.esm|0D2B1C: Please forgive my mood. I'm still coming to terms with the death of my wife and daughters...
Skyrim.esm|0D2B20: Karliah... she's still alive? I feared she'd befallen the same fate, ending up a victim of Mercer's betrayal.
Skyrim.esm|0D2B3B: Yes, very good. Here, then, is the first of the Illusion spells you sought.
Skyrim.esm|0D2B3D: Wonderful, just wonderful! And it's just as I suspected. The energies need to be channeled back through the nexus a second and third time... And that's it. I certainly think you deserve the first copy of the tome. Use it well!
Skyrim.esm|0D2B42: Not just any dragon scales. Heartscales. Very special. You'll need Kahvozein's Fang, of course. Then, if you should happen upon the corpse of a dragon, you should be able to use it to slice off a heartscale or two. Even just one should be enough for me to complete my research.
Skyrim.esm|0D2B43: Not much, I'm afraid. It was named Kahvozein's Fang after the dragon worshipped by this particular sect of the cult. They were said to be quite fond of ritual sacrifices. So fond, in fact, that the high priest spent a great deal of time sharpening the dagger. That way it was always ready for the next inevitable victim. Perhaps not the brightest spot in Skyrim's history, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0D2B44: Ah, well now you can help me directly! Far more useful, I think. Please do let me know if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0D2B48: That would be wonderful. I'll be sure and have everything ready. This is exciting, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0D2BB1: Dorthe won't eat that. Are you trying to starve the child?
Skyrim.esm|0D2BB9: I'll talk to Alvor about getting us a new one.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BC9: Until then, I've no ale for you.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BCA: Gettin' dull. We'll need a new one soon.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BD4: I've got some fresh cabbage. I'm thinking of making some apple cabbage stew.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BD6: I'll find some coin for you. I always do.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BEE: Ay, it can. Wouldn't want them any bigger though.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BF6: You've had more than enough. Besides, you still haven't paid for the last one.
Skyrim.esm|0D2BFB: Can the sawmill handle those logs? They look a might big.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C02: I need some more ale, Orgnar.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C04: Just never you mind about that.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C06: You come do an honest day's work at my mill, and I'll pay you an honest day's wages. Then we'll drink.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C0B: And speaking of spoiling Dorthe, who made her a new doll last week?
Skyrim.esm|0D2C10: It's only right seein' as your the important lumber man.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C12: Ay, it is.  I suppose it won't hurt the girl to eat it this once.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C1C: You'll be wantin' me to pay for them?
Skyrim.esm|0D2C1E: She needs to learn to eat what I serve her. I swear, you spoil that child rotten. Besides, it's one of your favorites.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C7E: You're under the arrest, for the Murder of Susanna of Candlehearth Hall. And Friga Shatter-Shield. And all the rest. We're wise to your scheming, you coward.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C88: What is the meaning of this?
Skyrim.esm|0D2C91: You. You're coming with me. We're finally going to arrest the Butcher.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C95: I'm sorry, sir. But you'll have to come with me.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C98: Get him out of my sight.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C99: Make your excuses from the Bloodworks, wizard.
Skyrim.esm|0D2C9C: Has the whole city lost their brains? I've been scrying and auguring to find the murderer myself.
Skyrim.esm|0D2CEF: Now - hear his name. Odahviing. Taste it on the wind. Od-ah-viing. Know it in your su'um. Od-ah-viing!
Skyrim.esm|0D2CF0: He is not compelled to come, but the dov are prideful by nature. Few could resist such a challenge. Especially from you, Dovahkiin. But Odahviing, he is... headstrong? Boziik. Rash. Even among the dov, he was known for this. He will not resist the challenge of your Voice, Dovahkiin. He will come.
Skyrim.esm|0D2CF3: Ah, I forget how little you know of the dov. Our names are always made up of three Rotmulaag - Words of Power. You see - Paar-thur-nax - a Thu'um - a Shout, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0D2CF4: Odahviing will come to your challenge. Boziik. Krilot. He will not be able to resist your call, from the Hill of the Dovah itself... he will come.
Skyrim.esm|0D2CF5: Hmm, yes. I have been pondering on exactly that question. Lingrah morah. I have tasted the voices of Alduin's allies on the wind. Pogaan nahlaas, vokrii wah jun. There is one who I remember well. Odahviing. He is the one to tell us where Alduin has gone.
Skyrim.esm|0D2CF8: An unguessed trove of lost lore... but the important thing is that the Blades recorded many of the names of dragons they slew. Cross-referencing this with Delphine's map of dragon burial sites, and I believe I've identified one of the dragons that Alduin has raised up.
Skyrim.esm|0D2CFE: Od - Ah - Viing. "Winged Snow Hunter," as I read it.
Skyrim.esm|0D2CFF: Oh... well, I'll leave that to you. I trust you know your business. Probably for the best. It will give me time to see if that old trap still works - and break the news to the men.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D00: Ah, indeed. I'm no master of the Voice, but it is written here in this scroll.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D01: I'm afraid there's a problem. A serious one. I've discovered who the Greybeards' leader really is.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D04: Interesting problem. Hmm. Yes. I've been poring over the records here in Sky Haven Temple.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D15: Why are we doing this again?
Skyrim.esm|0D2D2C: I've got a bad feeling about this.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D4F: Dragonborn, do whatever you're going to do!
Skyrim.esm|0D2D50: We may not be trying to kill him, but he sure seems to be trying to kill us!
Skyrim.esm|0D2D57: We've got to bring it down!
Skyrim.esm|0D2D73: So this is how you defeated Alduin. Ruth... zu'u ni Alduin.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D7E: Your Thu'um is no match for mine.
Skyrim.esm|0D2D9A: Do not try to hide from me.
Skyrim.esm|0D2DBC: I will conquer where Alduin failed.
Skyrim.esm|0D2DBD: I claim the glory of your defeat, Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|0D2DBE: Your Thu'um is strong, Dovahkiin. But not strong enough!
Skyrim.esm|0D2DC8: Hold your fire until he's close!
Skyrim.esm|0D2E08: We need to bring it down!
Skyrim.esm|0D2E14: Keep under cover until it's down!
Skyrim.esm|0D2E1D: You're getting pretty good at this dragon-fighting business.
Skyrim.esm|0D2E1E: I don't know how you talked the Jarl into this plan of yours.
Skyrim.esm|0D2E1F: My job is to keep the Jarl safe, despite his best efforts.
Skyrim.esm|0D33A8: Huh? You know who I am, fool. So why you askin'?
Skyrim.esm|0D33A9: You blind, boy? I'm a blacksmith. Any half-wit could see that.
Skyrim.esm|0D33AA: You blind, girl? I'm a blacksmith. Any half-wit could see that.
Skyrim.esm|0D33B1: Well that all depends on you, now. Doesn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0D33B2: Not anymore. Thanks to you. Friend.
Skyrim.esm|0D33B3: Closest town from here is Riverwood. My uncle's the blacksmith there. I'm sure he'd help you out.
Skyrim.esm|0D33B4: Listen, you should go to Solitude and join up with the Imperial Legion. We could really use someone like you. And if the rebels have themselves a dragon, General Tullius is the only one who can stop them.
Skyrim.esm|0D33BD: Good. Then there isn't much more to say. Here's your armor, just make sure you put it to good use.
Skyrim.esm|0D33BE: Sure, how about I get Dirge to knock you over your head and dump you into the cistern? Look, I've been in this business a long time and I've seen all types. You can play it tough, you can play it smart... whatever. At the end of the day you'll find all we care about down here is how much gold you can make us.
Skyrim.esm|0D33BF: I'm the fence down here. You come by anything you don't exactly own and I'll pay you some coin for it. Minus a little slice for the Guild, of course. I can also provide a few supplies useful to our trade now and again, for a small fee.
Skyrim.esm|0D33C0: So, you're the new recruit, eh? Well, looks like you and I are going to have to get very well acquainted.
Skyrim.esm|0D3609: Well... that was an unpleasant bit of business.
Skyrim.esm|0D360A: Remind me not to make you angry.
Skyrim.esm|0D360B: This is the last time I'll say it. Get out.
Skyrim.esm|0D360D: You're not really allowed in here, you know.
Skyrim.esm|0D360E: You can stay, but you're not supposed to be here.
Skyrim.esm|0D3612: Fool! The Imperial Legion will have your blood!
Skyrim.esm|0D3615: Think you can steal from me?
Skyrim.esm|0D3616: You dare try and steal from me?
Skyrim.esm|0D361A: You're not getting anything from me!
Skyrim.esm|0D361D: You won't get away with that!
Skyrim.esm|0D36CA: Well, you could always speak to Delvin or Vex if you're looking for extra work. Or if you're looking for training, we've got plenty of it down here. Delvin, Vex, Niruin and Vipir can give you a leg up on that.
Skyrim.esm|0D36CB: If you're lookin' to get some coin for the fruits of your labor, you should talk to Tonilia. She sounds tough, but she'll cut you a fair deal. I also suggest you spend some time in the trainin' room. Just talk to Vipir. He'll show you around.
Skyrim.esm|0D36CC: If you're ever in need of some quick coin for items you find on the job, Tonilia is the best fence around. And besides myself, Delvin, Niruin and Vipir can help you with any sort of training you might need to sharpen your skills.
Skyrim.esm|0D36CD: I'll point you to the first and last person you're ever going to need to talk to. Tonilia. She's the Guild's fence. Treat her right and she'll make you rich. Try and rip her off and you'll have the whole Guild to answer to.
Skyrim.esm|0D36CE: If talking your way out of trouble isn't enough, you could always pick up some training from Delvin, Vex or Vipir. Also, I recommend hitting the training room if you need lockpick practice. Helped me out of a jam more than once.
Skyrim.esm|0D399F: It's all right, Irileth. I want to hear what he has to say.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A0: It's all right, Irileth. I want to hear what she has to say.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A1: You'll see! It was a dragon! It'll kill us all and then you'll believe me!
Skyrim.esm|0D39A3: My lord. Please. You have to listen.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A4: I only counsel caution. We cannot afford to act rashly in times like these.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A5: If the news from Helgen is true... well, there's no telling what it means.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A6: Whatever you have to say to the Jarl, you can say to me.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A7: My lord. Please. This is no time for rash action. I just think we need more information before we act.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A8: You know about Helgen? The Jarl will want to speak to you personally. Approach.
Skyrim.esm|0D39A9: What would you have me do, then? Nothing?
Skyrim.esm|0D39AB: As housecarl, my job is to deal with all dangers that threaten the Jarl or his people. So you have my attention. Now, explain yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0D39AC: Ah, I forget you're new to Skyrim. Jarl Elisif, I should say, although only because she was married to Jarl Torryg when he was murdered. Ulfric murdered Torygg, you know. Walked right into his palace in Solitude and killed him. Shouted him to death, if you believe the stories. That's what started this whole war. The Empire couldn't ignore that. Once the jarls start killing each other, we're back to the bad old days.
Skyrim.esm|0D39AE: Well, that explains why the guards let you in. Come on then, the Jarl will want to speak to you personally.
Skyrim.esm|0D39AF: Of course. Skyrim has always been part of the Empire. That doesn't mean I support everything the Empire's been doing lately, but Nords have never been fair-weather friends.
Skyrim.esm|0D39B1: It's probably best if we split up. Good luck. I wouldn't have made it without your help today.
Skyrim.esm|0D39B2: What's the meaning of this interruption? Jarl Balgruuf is not receiving visitors.
Skyrim.esm|0D39B3: I don't think he likes either Ulfric or Elisif much. Who can blame him? But I've no doubt he'll prove loyal to the Empire in the end. He's no traitor.
Skyrim.esm|0D39B8: Jarl Balgruuf? He rules Whiterun Hold. A good man, perhaps a bit over-cautious, but these are dangerous times. So far he's managed to stay out of the war. I'm afraid it can't last, though.
Skyrim.esm|0D39B9: Just follow the road north. When you get to Honningbrew Meadery, turn left and the road will take you to the city gates. It's not far. We used to go to the market there every month or so, until the roads got so dangerous with the war and all.
Skyrim.esm|0D3AA7: Of course... otherwise it wouldn't be much of a reward, now would it? Your new piece will enhance your skill beyond that of the original. Trust me.
Skyrim.esm|0D3AA9: Well, looks like you're fitting in well down here. I'm supposed to allow you to trade in one of your pieces of Guild armor, so what will it be?
Skyrim.esm|0D3AAC: Take this with you. I'm not certain if it will help within the walls of the Sepulcher, but I certainly don't need it as much as you. I've had this bow almost my entire life, and it's never let me down. I hope it brings you the same luck.
Skyrim.esm|0D3AAE: Tough luck for you then. Come back when you have at least one of the pieces.
Skyrim.esm|0D3AB0: Are you sure? Once I exchange it for you, I'm not taking it back... unless you want to sell it at a discount of course.
Skyrim.esm|0D3AE7: I wish I could say that it was, but it's not that simple. Many Stormcloaks will fight until they die, because they still believe they're fighting for Skyrim's freedom. But without Ulfric, the movement will wither and die. It's only a matter of time.
Skyrim.esm|0D3B9D: Well, a few reasons come to mind. In my days as a riverboat captain, I was known to run a tight ship. That, and I made my fortune by knowing when to spend my septims, and when not to. I can't stand waste.
Skyrim.esm|0D3B9E: Windhelm is one of the oldest human cities in all of Tamriel. In fact, it was the capital of the First Empire, which was founded by Ysgramor The Palace of the Kings in which you now stand is one of the last buildings that remain from that era. Long ago, the Empire used the city to garrison troops who guarded the passes to Morrowind. And now, thanks to you, the city is back under the Empire's banner.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA1: I don't appreciate loiterers, if that's what you're here to do.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA2: I hope you have coin to spend, and you're not just here to gawk at my goods.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA3: Have a look around. I buy and sell just about anything.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA4: I'm always happy to see a customer, but I won't deal in stolen goods. That's trouble I don't need.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA5: Make yourself at home, please. Let me know if I can be of any assistance.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA6: Hear my prayer, son of Akatosh, and grant peace to these departed souls.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA7: You are a welcome guest, but you must still respect the rules of the Jarl's household.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA8: We can never rest easy, for Balgruuf's enemies will not.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BA9: Please keep your weapons sheathed in the Jarl's presence.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BAA: Think very carefully about your next move...
Skyrim.esm|0D3BAB: Take pity, sir, and spare me a few septims.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BAC: Take pity, lady, and spare me a few septims.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BAD: Spare a coin for a poor old beggar?
Skyrim.esm|0D3BAE: A few septims for my supper is all I ask.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BAF: I ain't askin' for much, just a few septims.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB0: If you have need of hunting supplies then you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB1: We sell bows, arrows and ale. A unique combination, to be sure.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB2: I'll be happy to assist you if you'd like to purchase something.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB3: I can tell you're no stranger to the wilds. I'm sure we can meet your hunting needs.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB4: We've got arms and armor of all kinds.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB5: You look like someone who knows how to wield a weapon. Well, you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BB6: If it's armor you need, we've got all kinds, both light and heavy.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BC6: Don't cause any trouble in the city.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BDA: Everything you see here was made with skill and care by my wife Adrianne.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BE1: Please, do look around. I'm sure I have something you'll want.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BE2: I can tell you're a discriminating customer. Perhaps you're a wealthy one, too, hm?
Skyrim.esm|0D3BF6: Everything's for sale, my friend! You need visit no other shops today!
Skyrim.esm|0D3BF7: You! You're the one who wiped out the Dark Brotherhood! All the guards are talking about it... You're a hero to us all!
Skyrim.esm|0D3BFA: I assure you, I'm prepared to make any deal.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BFB: You'll find tonics, salves, poultices and potions on my shelves. Browse to your heart's content.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BFC: I offer remedies for ailments both common and rare. Do let me know if I can be of service.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BFD: If there's anything I can help you with, you have but to ask.
Skyrim.esm|0D3BFE: I sell cures for all ills, and I'll be happy to serve you.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C55: Not sure I understand the question. The Legion's always been in Skyrim. We have garrisons throughout all the provinces of the Empire. Our mission is to serve the Emperor, uphold the law, and protect the citizenry. Skyrim's days are darkening, and the Legion will soon be called into service like never before. But we stand ready, as always, to meet that call.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C56: At first they called us that to belittle our cause. But we gladly accept being named for Ulfric Stormcloak, the only true High King of Skyrim. We are the true sons and daughters of Skyrim, and Ulfric... he's the truest of us all. We fight to make our land great once more, to raise up strong, noble thanes, and lay low false Jarls and the cowards who hide behind them. The sound of our fury is a storm about to break on the heads of our enemies. Our friendship, a cloak of true brotherhood for all who would wear it.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C57: If you are, then you make your way to Windhelm and talk to the man himself. If you're not, well friend, you'd best walk away before this gets ugly.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C58: The Legion's always looking for strong, capable warriors. If you think you've got what it takes, our headquarters is in Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C59: If you know any true sons and daughters of Skyrim, tell them to head to Windhelm. Ulfric Stormcloak wants to see them.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C5A: Think you've got the mettle, eh? Honestly, the Legion can use all the able bodies it can muster. We have our hands full. A dead king, a rebellion, and now dragons? Skyrim's a mess. If you're serious, make your way to Solitude. Our headquarters is Castle Dour. You find General Tulius there, commanding our forces in Skyrim. But talk to Legate Rikke. She handles the local recruiting.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C5B: Ulfric's not running a social club. You don't join anything. Either you're a true son of Skyrim, or you're not.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C5C: Ulfric's not running a social club. You don't join anything. Either you're a true daughter of Skyrim, or you're not.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C5D: You mean Ulfric's so-called "Stormcloak rebellion"? The Nord kings - oh, excuse me, "Jarls" - they can't seem to control their own people. So the Legion has stepped in to keep order. Sadly, the Empire's stretched a little thin these days, and we've gotten very few reinforcements. So we've been forced to recruit locally. But rest assured, citizen. We'll put an end to this uprising... and things will get back to normal soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D3C5E: Skyrim is the birthplace of humanity. The birthplace of honor. And those snowbacked Imperials renounced both when they laid down before the Thalmor. Worse still, to pay for the privilege to keep his throne, the Emperor sells Skyrim and our very gods - Talos, no less! - to the damn elves. And what did Torygg do? He and the Jarls that supported him? They took the Emperor's gold. Sold out their people for a slave master's "peace treaty." Ulfric did right killing him. Soon we'll rid Skyrim of elves, their bloody Justiciars, and the Jarls in their pockets. The whole damn lot of them. And we'll cleave through the Legion to get to them.
Skyrim.esm|0D3D5E: Well, as I said, Arkay is the god of life and death. You'll find his temples and Halls of the Dead all across Tamriel. Priests of Arkay such as myself perform rites for the dead and sometimes console the bereaved. We also keep a constant vigil against those who practice the vile arts of necromancy. I asked the question because I was hoping you might be able to help me recover something I've lost.
Skyrim.esm|0D3D5F: So be it. I'm sure Arkay will find it in his heart to forgive you... eventually.
Skyrim.esm|0D3D60: I'll just wait here and make sure nothing foul escapes the catacombs.
Skyrim.esm|0D3D65: Tired of bread and stew? Had enough of fish and fowl? Treat yourself and your kin to a choice cut of steak or a tender rack of ribs!
Skyrim.esm|0D3D66: I heard you were at the wedding of the Emperor's cousin. In Solitude? Gods, that must have been horrible...
Skyrim.esm|0D3D67: I heard the Gourmet is hiding out somewhere in Skyrim. You know, fella who wrote that cookbook.
Skyrim.esm|0D3DE0: Oh, good. She'll listen to you, I just know it.
Skyrim.esm|0D3DE1: Oh, okay. I didn't think you'd help me. Nobody ever does.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E50: She's not from Markarth. The air about her screamed "outsider." Visitors to the city usually stay at the Silver-Blood Inn.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E51: He was one of the smelter workers. I used to have a job down there myself, casting silver ingots. I never knew much about Weylin, except he lives in the Warrens, like all the other workers.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E52: You want to walk away? Fine. But there's going to be no justice for that poor woman. No one to care what happened to her.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E53: Please. I don't want to talk about it. All that blood. If you want answers, ask at the inn. She told me she was staying there.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E55: Of course. But I can't just hand that over. Privacy, after all.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E56: They're remnants of the old rulers of Markarth. Natives of the Reach. Followers of the old ways. The Nords drove them out of the city. Ulfric Stormcloak and his men. That was about 20 years ago. But somehow they're still here, and they're killing people.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E57: Well, you seem like the honest sort. Here you are.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E58: Right. You adventuring types would never go into a room just to steal everything.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E59: Well, I guess Margret isn't around to complain. It's yours.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E5A: Sorry. Margret might be dead, but the room's rented.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E5B: Yes. It all started when I was a boy. My father owned one of the mines. Rare for anyone who isn't a Nord. He was killed. Guards said it was just a madman, but everyone knew the murderer was a member of the Forsworn. I've been trying to find out why ever since. Gotten nowhere so far, and then I got married. Have a child of my own on the way. I swore I was going to just give up, for my child's sake, but it's like my father's ghost is haunting me. Asking me "Why?"
Skyrim.esm|0D3E5C: Changed your mind? Ready to find out the truth about Markarth?
Skyrim.esm|0D3E5D: This has been going on for years. And all I've been able to find is murder and blood. I need help. Please. You find out why that woman was attacked, who's behind Weylin and the Forsworn, and I'll pay you for any information you bring me.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E5E: All right. I don't want any trouble. It's yours.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E5F: Are you serious? I hear more threatening words from my wife.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E60: Ah, yes. Rented the nicest room we had for a whole month. I think she's sitting by the fire. Poor girl.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E61: Ah, yes. Rented the nicest room we had for a whole month. Best we all forget about her. Bad luck to talk about the dead in Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E62: Oh yeah. Pretty little thing. Been sitting by the fire all day.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E63: I remember her. Her stance. The way she walked. A trained warrior, no doubt. She had a room here. Might talk to the innkeeper about it.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E64: Who? You mean that woman who was attacked in the market? Didn't know her. Guards dragged her body away. Last we'll hear of that.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E65: Who? You mean that woman who was attacked in the market the other day? Don't know her. Guards dragged that Forsworn's body away, though. Last we'll hear of that.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E76: Well, I used to sneak out at night and try to tip over the big ox in the Gray-Manes' yard. And me and Mila used to climb up on the roof of Dragonsreach and look for birds' eggs. But I don't do that stuff anymore. Father keeps catching me and then I get in trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E7D: Now now, fella. Let's not get too close to my new bride, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0D3E7E: This wedding is boring. I wish they had jugglers.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E7F: Vittoria did pretty well for herself, marrying that Asgeir. Not a bad looking fellow at all.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E80: Long as I stay out of the way, the guards don't seem to care about me being here.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E81: If you want my opinion, I think they should have paid for an entire quartet. Plenty of bards at the College would have loved the opportunity.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E82: My nephew, the Emperor. Too busy to attend the wedding of his beloved cousin. We won't stand for this slight, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E83: I still think my boy should have had a traditional Nord wedding. Outside, in the cool mountain air.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E84: Vittoria may be pretty, but does she have the right hips for the job? I want grandchildren, and lots of them.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E93: I'll look the other way this time.
Skyrim.esm|0D3E98: Get your thieving hands off that!
Skyrim.esm|0D3E99: Put that down, you mangy cat!
Skyrim.esm|0D3E9B: I don't think there's a need for that!
Skyrim.esm|0D42C4: But now what? I can't just walk away. Not with my friends' bodies in there, being torn apart by those beasts.
Skyrim.esm|0D4F91: Lord of Seasons, I pray to you now that you might watch over the dead with mercy and kindness.
Skyrim.esm|0D4F92: Mighty Arkay, God of Mortals, hear now the prayers of your humble servant. Bless these departed souls and welcome them into the realms beyond.
Skyrim.esm|0D4F93: Mighty Arkay, I pray that you will shepherd these souls as they pass into the realm of death.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FB5: You can go back and forth all you want, he just needs to pay.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FB6: I got the message. Consider it forgotten, boss.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FB7: Yeah, I figured it would be like that. Here you go.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FB8: All right, all right! Take it easy. No idea why you'd care about a stranger so much, but this isn't worth a fight. Tell Shadr I'm forgetting about his debt.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FB9: Ha ha! Are you kidding me? I've got the entire Thieves Guild at my back. What've you got?
Skyrim.esm|0D4FBA: Remember, this is between us now.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FBB: That'll teach me to try and make extra coin around this city.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FBC: Be careful with Sapphire. She's mixes with all sorts of nasty people.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FBD: Oh, you heard about that, huh? Uh, sorry boss. I was going to speak to Delvin about cutting the Guild in, but I forgot. You aren't angry about it, right?
Skyrim.esm|0D4FBE: Woah, woah. Calm down. Let's just think about this for a moment. Sigh. You win. Tell Shadr he can forget the debt.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FBF: Don't know why you'd help a stranger like that, but a septim's a septim... don't care where it comes from. You can tell Shadr his debt's been paid.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC0: You don't have enough gold to pay it off, and I'm not cutting any deals.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC1: Now you're talking. Oh, if you see Shadr, remind him he still owes me that debt.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC2: All right, all right. I guess I made enough from his shipment. No need to waste any more time threatening a stable hand. Tell Shadr he doesn't owe me anything.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC3: I don't care, a deal's a deal.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC4: Why should I bother? I've already made a bunch of coin off of his shipment, so anything else I squeeze out of him would be a bonus. Oh... wait a second. I see what this is. You want me to cut you in or you'll rat me out to Brynjolf. Tell you what. I'll give you a share right now if you forget the whole thing.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC5: I knew that stupid kid would try and find a way to weasel out of his debt. Look, this is really simple. I lent him some gold, he promised to pay me back and now he says he's broke. End of story.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC6: Hey, I cut you into the deal. There's nothing else to discuss.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FC7: Look, I already said Shadr doesn't owe me a thing, so get off my back.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FCB: Back off, this action's all mine. No room for anyone else in the Guild on this one.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FCC: Oh. Well, thanks for trying I suppose. I'll just have to come up with the coin somehow.
Skyrim.esm|0D4FCD: By the Eight! You actually talked her into it? I don't know what to say. I didn't think anyone in Riften even cared what happened to me. Look, I was saving this, but I wanted you to have it. I thought I might need it if Sapphire came for me, but I don't need it anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0D5079: No, but that won't account for the size issue. Blast, if only so many of these creatures hadn't escaped.
Skyrim.esm|0D507A: Perhaps some more Glow Dust in the next batch of potions. That should increase their resistance.
Skyrim.esm|0D50C6: Ah, no mere brute mercenary, but a thinker - perhaps even a scholar? You see, when the stories of dragons began to circulate, many dismissed them as mere fantasies, rumors. Impossiblilities. One sure mark of a fool is to dismiss anything that falls outside his experience as being impossible. But I began to search for information about dragons - where had they gone all those years ago? And where were they coming from?
Skyrim.esm|0D50C8: Irileth, send a detachment to Riverwood at once.
Skyrim.esm|0D50C9: Oh, no. Many were killed, of course. But many survived into historical times. Why, this very palace was built by one of Balgruuf's ancestors to hold a captive dragon. Hence its name - Dragonsreach.
Skyrim.esm|0D50CB: Straight to the point, eh? No need for tedious hows and whys. I like that. Leave those details to your betters, am I right?
Skyrim.esm|0D50CC: Succeed at this, and you'll be rewarded. Whiterun will be in your debt.
Skyrim.esm|0D50CE: I'm not surprised you've never heard of it. Even I used to think it was just a myth. But not anymore. The Dragon War was a real event, although only the barest glimmer of the actual events has come down to us. Far back in the Mythic Era, the dragons were worshipped as gods in Skyrim. Many of the monumental ruins that still dot the landscape were, in fact, built as temples to the dragons. The details are lost, but at some point the Nords rebelled. After a long and terrible war, the Nords overthrew their dragon overlords.
Skyrim.esm|0D50CF: The Jarl can be found in the Great Hall, probably sitting on his Jarl's throne. Not over here in a wizard's laboratory.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D0: The Jarl of Falkreath will view that as a provocation! He'll assume we're preparing to join Ulfric's side and attack him.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D1: Well. Must preserve some professional secrets, mustn't we? I have my sources... reliable sources.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D2: Alvor? The smith, isn't he? Reliable, solid fellow. Not prone to flights of fancy... And you're sure Helgen was destroyed by a dragon? This wasn't some Stormcloak raid gone wrong?
Skyrim.esm|0D50D3: Please forgive my earlier rudeness. I am so often interrupted by visitors wandering in, I can occasionally become quite savage. Now... what did you say you wanted?
Skyrim.esm|0D50D4: I'm sure he will prove most useful.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D5: I'm sure she will prove most useful.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D6: It's in the most immediate danger, if that dragon is lurking in the mountains...
Skyrim.esm|0D50D7: Where? I would never... ah, I see. You are a fellow enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D8: Of course, Jarl Balgruuf. You seem to have found me an able assistant.
Skyrim.esm|0D50D9: My lord, we should send troops to Riverwood at once.
Skyrim.esm|0D50DA: An old tomb, built by the ancient Nords, perhaps dating back to the Dragon War itself. Ah. Maybe you just want to know how to get there. It's near Riverwood, a miserable little village a few miles south of here. I'm sure some of the locals can point you in the right direction once you get there.
Skyrim.esm|0D50DB: I should have guessed Ulfric would be mixed up in this.
Skyrim.esm|0D50DC: What? I never even cast... ah, I see. You have some knowledge of the Higher Art.
Skyrim.esm|0D50DD: This is a priority now. Anything we can use to fight this dragon, or dragons. We need it, quickly. Before it's too late.
Skyrim.esm|0D50DE: What do you say now, Proventus? Shall we continue to trust in the strength of our walls? Against a dragon?
Skyrim.esm|0D50DF: What? I'm not even... ah, I see. You have some knowledge of the alchemical art.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E3: Go ahead and fill him in with all the details.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E4: Go ahead and fill her in with all the details.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E5: Really? You're certainly... forthright about your criminal past. But it's none of my concern who the Imperials want to execute. Especially now. What I want to know is what exactly happened at Helgen.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E6: If you'll excuse me, I'll return to my duties.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E7: Ah, indeed? The Jarl... Oh yes, he must be referring to my research into the dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E8: Hmm? What? Project? You think you could help me? I really don't think so.
Skyrim.esm|0D50E9: What's this about Riverwood being in danger?
Skyrim.esm|0D50EA: Well. I trust you have something vitally important to tell me. Important enough to interrupt me in the middle of council?
Skyrim.esm|0D50EB: Well done. You sought me out, on your own initiative. You've done Whiterun a service, and I won't forget it.
Skyrim.esm|0D50EC: You were at Helgen when it was attacked? You saw this with your own eyes?
Skyrim.esm|0D50ED: You are eager to begin your adventure. Excellent. The sooner begun, the sooner done, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0D50EE: I'll not stand idly by while a dragon burns my hold and slaughters my people!
Skyrim.esm|0D50F0: Farengar, I see you've already met my new friend here. I'm sure you two will get along famously.
Skyrim.esm|0D50F1: Farengar, I think I've found someone who can help you with your dragon project.
Skyrim.esm|0D512B: Is that confidence, or bravado? Confidence I can use. Bravado gets soldiers killed. We'll soon find out.
Skyrim.esm|0D5130: It's not a cause. It's a rebellion.
Skyrim.esm|0D5133: Is that so? I was right about you.
Skyrim.esm|0D5135: One of the prisoners, if I recall correctly.
Skyrim.esm|0D5136: I suspect we might have use for someone resourceful like you. Not many survived Helgen. Besides, I'm sure your being imprisoned was all a terrible misunderstanding.
Skyrim.esm|0D5137: Balgruuf refuses the Legion's right to garrison troops in his city. On the other hand, he also refuses to acknowledge Ulfric's claim.
Skyrim.esm|0D5138: The ancients built many of the fortresses that dot the landscape of Skyrim. Sadly, most have fallen into disrepair. And nearly all have been overrun with bandits or other vagabonds. Fort Hraagstad is one of the few that remains mostly intact. We're going to install a garrison there, but first, your going to clean out the bandits that have moved in.
Skyrim.esm|0D5139: I'm telling you, Ulfric's planning an attack on Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0D513A: Sir? You can't force a Nord to accept help he hasn't asked for.
Skyrim.esm|0D513C: I'm sending you to clear out Fort Hraagstad. If you survive, you'll pass. If you die, then I'll have no further use for your corpse.
Skyrim.esm|0D513D: Well, if you change your mind, you know what to do.
Skyrim.esm|0D513E: That's not what my scouts report, sir. Every day more join his cause. Riften, Dawnstar, and Winterhold support him.
Skyrim.esm|0D513F: Oh, not so confident now, are we?
Skyrim.esm|0D5140: Yes. This is a test. I don't think you're regular militia material. I want to see what you're capable of.
Skyrim.esm|0D5144: Call it whatever you like, General. The man's going to try to take Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0D5145: Are my men now giving free reign to anyone who wanders into the castle? Do you have some reason to be here, citizen?
Skyrim.esm|0D5146: There something I can do for you? Perhaps direct you to the nearest prison...
Skyrim.esm|0D5147: Speak with Legate Rikke. She will determine if you are Legion material.
Skyrim.esm|0D5149: You people and your damn Jarls.
Skyrim.esm|0D514B: Hadvar's alive? I hope that's true. He's a damn good soldier. But he hasn't reported in yet, so he can't exactly confirm your story. In the meantime, why don't you have a chat with Legate Rikke.
Skyrim.esm|0D514C: Well, if he wants to stand outside the protection of the Empire, fine. Let Ulfric pillage his city.
Skyrim.esm|0D514E: You survived Helgen? General Tullius told me what happened. Not many made it out alive... I've got a good feeling about you. And I don't often get good feelings about anything. A warrior knows to trust her gut... I'm not going to go through the normal process with you. I've got a little test lined up. Pass that, and we'll talk about you joining the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|0D514F: Are you ready to test yourself at Fort Hraagstad?
Skyrim.esm|0D5150: I suppose that's true. Fine. Why don't you have a chat with Legate Rikke?
Skyrim.esm|0D5151: The kind that evaluates your usefulness during... duress.
Skyrim.esm|0D5152: He'd be insane to try. He doesn't have the men.
Skyrim.esm|0D5153: Good. That's what I want to hear. Now go make it happen, soldier.
Skyrim.esm|0D5154: Have we? Oh. Of course. You were at Helgen!
Skyrim.esm|0D5209: Oh yeah. Pretty little thing. Had a room here I think. Ask the innkeeper.
Skyrim.esm|0D5316: It's just us against a dragon, is that it?
Skyrim.esm|0D5317: Your main job will be to keep an eye out and get the people to safety if the town is attacked.
Skyrim.esm|0D5319: I can't afford to send anyone else. And we don't know where the dragon is.
Skyrim.esm|0D531A: The Jarl has finally agreed to send you back to Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|0D531B: Let's move out. Time's a wasting.
Skyrim.esm|0D531C: Of course. We'll keep Riverwood safe. You can count on it.
Skyrim.esm|0D531D: I don't expect the three of you to fight off a dragon by yourselves. But I do expect you to do your duty.
Skyrim.esm|0D536C: I'm afraid I don't have a primary contract for you yet.
Skyrim.esm|0D536D: So, what of the old beggar, Narfi? Is it done? Has he groveled for his last septim?
Skyrim.esm|0D54E8: You little wretch, I saw that!
Skyrim.esm|0D5531: Hadvar, thank Talos! Your friend told me about Helgen...
Skyrim.esm|0D5532: Mara's mercy! A dragon... in Helgen? Why, it could be here at any moment!
Skyrim.esm|0D5533: Bring back this tablet that Farengar wants and I'll be in your debt.
Skyrim.esm|0D5534: That tablet is your priority now. Bring that back from Bleak Falls Barrow and I'm sure we'll have a lot to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|0D5535: Farengar can explain the situation better than I can.
Skyrim.esm|0D5536: Farengar is probably puttering around in his lab. Day and night. I'm not sure he ever sleeps.
Skyrim.esm|0D5537: I'll introduce you to Farengar. He can be a bit... difficult. Mages. You know.
Skyrim.esm|0D5538: Inn's closed. Bar's still open, though. Feel free to sit and put your head down on the table for as long as you like. I won't bother you.
Skyrim.esm|0D5539: Go on, Hadvar. Let's hear the rest of it.
Skyrim.esm|0D553A: Out. She owns the place, she does what she wants.
Skyrim.esm|0D553B: Come on inside. Sigrid will get you something to eat and you can tell us what happened.
Skyrim.esm|0D553C: I'm sure Riverwood will be all right, dear. When the Jarl learns what's happened, he's sure to send soldiers.
Skyrim.esm|0D553D: Now, then, boy. Helgen was destroyed? How did you get away?
Skyrim.esm|0D553E: Here, take this as a small token of my esteem. There is another thing you could do for me. Suitable for someone of your particular talents, perhaps. Come, let's go find Farengar, my court wizard. He's been looking into a matter related to these dragons and... rumors of dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0D553F: When your friend told me, I didn't believe it at first either.
Skyrim.esm|0D5584: These fights are invite only. And you're not invited. Slag off.
Skyrim.esm|0D55E7: The only unusual thing is the shape of the cuts. They look like they were made with... well, the ancient Nords used these kinds of curved blades when they embalmed their dead. I don't know who in Windhelm would even have something like that. Other than me, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0D55EA: Well, she's dead. But I guess that's not unusual, at least not for somebody in here. I mean, someone who's not me, that is. Sorry, was only joking with you.
Skyrim.esm|0D55EB: You've done this city a mighty service, friend. I believe you'll find the guards to be a bit more cordial with you in the future.
Skyrim.esm|0D55EC: We're stretched thin as it is with the war. Nobody has the time to spend on this. Not pleasant, but it's the truth.
Skyrim.esm|0D55ED: I wouldn't hold out too much hope.
Skyrim.esm|0D55EE: Ysmir's beard! The man was always a bit odd, but I wouldn't have expected...
Skyrim.esm|0D55EF: Now, I really got to get back to the body. Lot of work to prepare her for the grave.
Skyrim.esm|0D55F0: I'm too busy tending to the dead to spend my time making more of them. And I wouldn't very well tell you about the cuts if I had made them, now would I?
Skyrim.esm|0D562C: I'm just here to provide protection for his eminence's cousin. Please, enjoy yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0D562D: May you all be as happy in your lives as I know I will be in mine. Thank you again for everything.
Skyrim.esm|0D562E: I never should have taken this job!
Skyrim.esm|0D562F: Sorry, no time to talk. I need to keep playing.
Skyrim.esm|0D5630: Ba ba ba, bum ba, ba ba ba, bum ba, ba da da da, dum da da...
Skyrim.esm|0D5631: Dum, di di dum, dum di dum, dum di di di di dum da da dum.
Skyrim.esm|0D5632: In this courtyard, there are no Stormcloaks. There are no Imperials. There are only people, come to celebrate a union of two souls. So make merry!
Skyrim.esm|0D5634: My husband Aesgir and I thank you for coming to our reception. We thank you for sharing the love we have for each other.
Skyrim.esm|0D5C3A: Oh, but Cicero can't talk to you. You've been naughty, naughty. I think you need to clear this whole mess up with Nazir.
Skyrim.esm|0D5C3B: Looks like Cicero's not the only only one to go a little crazy from time to time. Eh, Listener? You should talk to Nazir, work this whole mess out.
Skyrim.esm|0D5C3C: With all due respect, Listener, you broke what few rules we seem to have. Nazir insists you talk to him and work this issue out.
Skyrim.esm|0D5C3D: I do apologize, Listener, but Nazir made it clear the initiates are not to speak with you until you first deal with him, and atone for your actions.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E8D: You're that one from the College. Heard about you.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E8E: Try to hide it all you want. I know you're in the Thieves Guild... and so do the other guards.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E91: Psst. Hey, I know who you are. Hail Sithis.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E92: Ugh. That armor stinks of death. You been grave robbing?
Skyrim.esm|0D5E93: Ah, now that's a fine shield. And not mere Dwarven... is it?
Skyrim.esm|0D5E94: Ebony armor. By Ysmir, 'tis a wondrous sight.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E95: Is your armor made of... dragon bones? By the gods, what I wouldn't do for a set of that.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E96: Is your armor made of... dragon scales? By the gods, what I wouldn't do for a set of that.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E97: Some advice, friend. That armor won't offer much protection in a real fight.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E98: You come talking to me wearing Imperial armor? You got stew for brains?
Skyrim.esm|0D5E99: That's some fine Imperial armor you've got there.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E9A: Judging by your armor, I'd say you're an Imperial scout. If so, well met.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E9B: For your sake, I hope your took that armor from a dead Imperial scout...
Skyrim.esm|0D5E9C: You wear the armor of a mercenary. Now that's the life.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E9D: That's good armor you've got there, friend. Iron, solid and true.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E9E: Fancy the leather armor, huh? Something to be said for going light.
Skyrim.esm|0D5E9F: That armor... it's like nothing I've ever seen. Like forged midnight...
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA0: Orcish armor, hmm? Had me a set of that once. Ugly and strong, like those that forged it.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA1: That armor... Are you one of the Emperor's protectors? Can't say I envy you that job.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA2: That armor... Are you one of the Emperor's protectors? Not very good at your job, are you?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA3: That's fine scale armor you've got on there. Shiny.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA4: Aye, now that's some fine armor. Good old-fashioned steel.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA5: Ah, steel plate. Now that's a solid choice of armor.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA6: You wear the armor of the bear, my friend. A fine choice.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA7: You dare strut around here, wearing that filthy Stormcloak hide?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA8: That Stormcloak armor's getting on my nerves...
Skyrim.esm|0D5EA9: You wear that Stormcloak armor well, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EAA: You wear the armor of a brigand. Best not cause any trouble on my watch.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EAB: I know Thieves Guild armor when I see it. You're not fooling anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EAC: You wear the garb of a true Nord. I salute you.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EAD: Pigsticker like that's not going to get you far. Best visit the blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EAE: Aye, now that there's a beautiful sword. Like a sliver of midnight.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EAF: Why the Elven blade, hmm? Nord steel not good enough for you?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB0: Favor a steel sword, do ya? Good choice for slashing or stabbing.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB1: Takes a fair bit of strength to carry a greatsword like that. But can you use it?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB2: I fear the night. Because the werewolves and vampires don't.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB6: So you can cast a few spells. Am I supposed to be impressed?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB7: I've a lot of respect for the Restoration School. Skyrim could use more healers.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB8: Lightly armored means light on your feet. Smart.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EB9: Hey, you mix potions, right? Can you brew me an ale?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EBB: I find your hand in my pocket, I'm going to cut it off.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EBC: I know your kind, always sneaking about.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EBD: Go fiddling with any locks around here, we're going to have a real problem.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EBE: Fancy yourself an alchemist, hmm? Never could get the hang of that.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EBF: You're the one casts those illusions. Impressive.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC0: Don't think you can barter with me like I'm one of those damned shopkeepers.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC1: Best offense is a good defense, am I right?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC2: Don't suppose you'd enchant my sword? Dull old blade can barely cut butter.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC3: Heard about you and your honeyed words...
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC4: Hail, summoner. Conjure me up a warm bed, would you?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC5: Destruction magic's fine, just don't go burning down any buildings.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC6: Keep your arrows in their quiver, archer.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC7: Favor the bow, eh? I'm a sword man, myself.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC8: The gods gave you two hands, and you use them both for your weapon. I can respect that.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EC9: You're like me, eh? Don't fancy those clunky two-handed weapons.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EDD: Make sure you take the Auxiliary here. You can send him back when you get there and find nothing but old bones and cobwebs.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EDF: So what - Ulfric gets this crown and then suddenly he's High King?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EE0: The Stonefist's no fool. He's found the Crown. But we'll get to it first.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EE1: And we gave it to Elisif?
Skyrim.esm|0D5EE2: In the absence of the Moot, it would further legitimize her claim.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EE5: Don't you Nords put any stock in your own traditions? I thought the Moot chose the king.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EE8: What's the matter, soldier? I gave you an order. I want that fort, and I want it now. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|0D5EE9: Speak with General Tullius. He'll administer the oath.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EEA: Tell me again, why I'm wasting men chasing after a fairy tale.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EEC: I believe the Legate gave you an order. I expect you to follow it. Dismissed.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EED: If Ulfric gets his hand on that crown, it won't be a fairy tale. It'll be a problem.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EEF: Not everyone's agreed to the Moot. You've been here long enough to know that Nords aren't always sensible. We follow our hearts.
Skyrim.esm|0D5EF0: Perhaps... I'm entrusting you with what resources I can spare. But I'm warning you, if this turns out to be a waste of time and men...
Skyrim.esm|0D5EF1: No, it's not as simple as that, but the Jagged Crown would be a potent symbol for his cause to rally around. But, if we found it first...
Skyrim.esm|0D5F13: That iron sword's pretty undependable. You should try steel.
Skyrim.esm|0D61D9: Come, Karliah. It's time for you and Gallus to become reunited!
Skyrim.esm|0D61DA: Woah there, lass. I appreciate the armor, but becoming a Nightingale? That was never discussed.
Skyrim.esm|0D61DB: Indirectly. The Trinity is usually selected from the ranks of the Guild although its existence is a closely-guarded secret.
Skyrim.esm|0D61DC: Yes. That's why we need to prepare ourselves and meet Mercer on equal footing.
Skyrim.esm|0D61DD: Gallus, Mercer Frey and I were once members of what's known as the Nightingale Trinity. The Trinity disbanded twenty-five years ago when Mercer Frey betrayed us by slaying Gallus and dumping his body in the ruins of Snow Veil Sanctum.
Skyrim.esm|0D61DE: To hold any hope of defeating Mercer, we must have Nocturnal at our backs. If she's to accept you as one of her own, an arrangement must be struck.
Skyrim.esm|0D61DF: The Nightingales protect the temple of Nocturnal, a place known as the Twilight Sepulcher.
Skyrim.esm|0D660D: We have it on good authority that Ulfric has raised enough men to attack the city of Whiterun. The Jarl, however, refuses the Legion's support. This missive should convince him. Be aware soldier, these documents contain sensitive intelligence for the Jarl's eyes only.
Skyrim.esm|0D660F: A legendary crown, dating back to King Harald's time, or before. A powerful relic of a golden age, long since passed. Legend has it that the crown is made from the bones and teeth of ancient dragons, and is said to increase the power of the wearer. Whatever the truth, if Ulfric get's his hands on it, it would be a powerful symbol around which to rally support for his cause.
Skyrim.esm|0D6610: Well, to be frank, we don't. Its location was lost with King Borgas, when the Great Hunt killed him while off on his Alessian campaigns. Supposedly, his body was brought back to Skyrim and secretly buried with the crown. Knowledge of that location was lost, in the Wars of Succession. But my agents report that Galmar is pretty damned convinced Korvanjund is the tomb of King Borgas. I know Galmar. We fought in many wars together. He's not a sentimental man taken to fancy. If he believes the crown is there... he's likely found it.
Skyrim.esm|0D6611: We're backing Elisif. When the Moot meets, they'll do the sensible thing.
Skyrim.esm|0D6612: We're backing Erikur. When the Moot meets, they'll do the sensible thing.
Skyrim.esm|0D6613: I trust you'll be finished with Fort Hraggstad soon?
Skyrim.esm|0D6615: I need someone I can trust to deliver a message of great import to Jarl Balgruuf of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0D6616: And we gave it to Erikur?
Skyrim.esm|0D6619: Just shut up and keep out of sight.
Skyrim.esm|0D6670: When their uprising was crushed, I had Madanach brought to me. He was a wild animal, but a useful one. I offered him a stay from execution if he used his influence to deal with any annoyances that came up. Competitors, agents, idiots. So I've let him run his little Forsworn rebellion from inside Cidhna Mine. Now he's out of control.
Skyrim.esm|0D6671: Or else what? You'll kill me? I already had to face that today.
Skyrim.esm|0D6672: Well then, look. Look all you want. And when you're done looking, maybe we can get some work done.
Skyrim.esm|0D6675: If you must know, I'm the maid. And the master of the house is old and needs his rest. So if you don't have any business. Leave.
Skyrim.esm|0D6676: You're finding it. That's bad enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D6677: Sorry, but you don't exactly belong here.
Skyrim.esm|0D6678: Madanach. The King in Rags. While we were off fighting the Elves in the Great War, Madanach was busy ruling over the Reach. Until Ulfric came and put them down.
Skyrim.esm|0D6679: Rather not talk. I have to head back to Cyrodiil soon.
Skyrim.esm|0D667A: What did you expect? That no one was going to notice you butting into business that wasn't yours? The guards know who holds the purse strings in this city, and I don't like snoops. Markarth is my city. You have no right looking into my business. Now get out.
Skyrim.esm|0D667C: Shut up. Only the gods can judge me. You want to know what the Forsworn really are?
Skyrim.esm|0D667D: This is a private home, and the master of the house is a very old man. He's not to be disturbed.
Skyrim.esm|0D667E: Just visiting. I had some business here. That's all.
Skyrim.esm|0D6680: My prison. The source of half the silver in Skyrim. The most secure prison in Tamriel. No one escapes. I thought keeping Madanach down there would keep him under control.
Skyrim.esm|0D6681: They're my puppets. I have their "king" rotting in Cidhna Mine. He was supposed to keep them under control.
Skyrim.esm|0D6682: It's an old family. Very well-respected throughout the Reach. The Silver-Bloods run nearly all the lands in this Hold, as well as the local inn. And of course, they own Cidhna Mine, the finest prison and source of silver in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D6683: For one, the landowners of the Reach. It goes without saying that they rely heavily on us for our silver and support. All the miners, farmers, and laborers in the Reach get their daily wages through us. Nepos the Nose handles that part of our business. He has a way with the workers.
Skyrim.esm|0D6684: Every other day, seems one of the laborers runs off in the hills saying he'll join the Forsworn. You don't like the Nords? Fine. But pledging your whole life to killing them? That's just dumb.
Skyrim.esm|0D6685: I'm sorry about my housekeeper. She's a little protective of me. Now, what is it you want?
Skyrim.esm|0D6686: I'm one of General Tullius's agents. I was sent to investigate the Treasury House and the Silver-Blood family. They own Cidhna Mine, one of the toughest jails in Skyrim. I was hoping I could buy or steal the deed, but I don't think that's how things work here. Mark my words. Thonar Silver-Blood was behind that attack in the market. Somehow.
Skyrim.esm|0D6687: Someone who doesn't like you asking questions.
Skyrim.esm|0D6688: Well fought, ya swinging bit of skeever bait.
Skyrim.esm|0D6689: Oh, yes. Weylin. Bastard could have at least gone crazy after we made our silver quota.
Skyrim.esm|0D668A: Go... go right ahead then. Don't let me keep you.
Skyrim.esm|0D668B: I must have misheard you. Did you say you were leaving?
Skyrim.esm|0D668C: It's where you go if you can't afford a room anywhere else. About the time they opened the mines, someone got the idea to throw beds in here. Laborers. The sick. The lame. We're all here.
Skyrim.esm|0D668D: I'm afraid he's asked to not be disturbed. He has important business.
Skyrim.esm|0D668F: What are you doing here? I told them no visitors.
Skyrim.esm|0D6690: You were seen coming in. The girl at the door is a Forsworn agent masquerading as a maid. You aren't the first one to have gotten this far. You won't be the last.
Skyrim.esm|0D6691: No. I'm telling you. There was no other reason.
Skyrim.esm|0D6692: Thank you. The Eight keep you.
Skyrim.esm|0D6693: My wife. They killed her. Damn Madanach. Damn his Forsworn backside.
Skyrim.esm|0D6694: Now don't get all upset. Here. Take it.
Skyrim.esm|0D6695: And I wasn't stuttering. No key.
Skyrim.esm|0D6697: Ah, yes. You've proven to be a real bloodhound. Well, you've sniffed me out. I've been playing this game for almost 20 years. Sending the young to their deaths. All in the name of the Forsworn. And I'm tired. So tired.
Skyrim.esm|0D6698: Because my king told me to. Madanach. When the uprising fell at the hands of the Nords, they threw him in the mines. I don't know how, but he lives. I get his messages, and I hand out his orders without question.
Skyrim.esm|0D6699: What? No. Thonar Silver-Blood owns it. He owns everything in Markarth. But he hands all the paperwork over to Nepos the Nose to finish. That old man's back is heavy with burden.
Skyrim.esm|0D669A: A monster? No. I'm just a servant. To my king, Madanach. When the uprising fell at the hands of the Nords, they threw him in the mines. I don't know how, but he lives. I get his messages, and I hand out his orders without question.
Skyrim.esm|0D669C: For that amount, I can. Fine. I did notice something odd last time we handed out the pay. Little extra slip of paper for Weylin. Took it right to his room after he got it.
Skyrim.esm|0D669E: You've been digging around where you don't belong. It's time you learned a lesson.
Skyrim.esm|0D669F: Nepos is in charge. That's all I know.
Skyrim.esm|0D66A0: Oh yes. I know everyone who sleeps in the Warrens. Kind of the one who passes the keys around. I guess someone else will be taking his room, now.
Skyrim.esm|0D66A1: Nepos the Nose? He's been in Markarth forever. And he's well-respected among the natives of the Reach. Good work. Here's your gold. I have a feeling Nepos gets his own orders, though. If you find out, I'll have more for you.
Skyrim.esm|0D66A4: We haven't been expecting you, and the old man needs his rest. Come back some other time.
Skyrim.esm|0D66A5: Fine. You want to know what the Forsworn really are?
Skyrim.esm|0D66A6: They're crazy. Think they rule the Reach. Well guess who really runs things around here? Me. I own the mines. I make the coin pass to the right hands. That answer your damn questions? Now get out.
Skyrim.esm|0D66A7: You already got what you wanted, you damn hound. This is your fault. You and Madanach are animals, and I'll see you both rot to death in Cidhna Mine for this. Now get out of my house!
Skyrim.esm|0D66A9: Not much. One moment I was buying a necklace, and the rest is just a blur.
Skyrim.esm|0D66AA: No you're not. You want to know what the Forsworn really are?
Skyrim.esm|0D66AB: You heard him. Go on in.
Skyrim.esm|0D66AC: Markarth and the Reach are our lands. That is why we are the Forsworn. We cannot claim the home that is rightly ours. But then during their war with the elves, we had our moment. We drove the Nords out of the Reach in a great uprising. Then Ulfric and his men came. Those of us who didn't run were executed, except for myself, my king, and a handful of others.
Skyrim.esm|0D66AD: He is the King in Rags. A man who once held all the Reach within his grip. He stokes the passions of the downtrodden in this city. Directs them to kill the enemies of the Forsworn in our name. All from inside Cidhna Mine. A Nord prison. The irony is quite thick.
Skyrim.esm|0D66AE: You want Garvey. He handles the keys for the Warrens. About the only thing that disease-riddled native is good for.
Skyrim.esm|0D66AF: Trust is hard to come by here, but all right.
Skyrim.esm|0D66B1: Was is that obvious? Damn. I must be losing my touch.
Skyrim.esm|0D66B3: The Warrens isn't a place for your type. What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|0D66B4: Warrens ain't for you. Trust me.
Skyrim.esm|0D66B5: You don't want to be here. No one wants to.
Skyrim.esm|0D66B6: Don't know anyone here doesn't have Rattles or Ataxia.
Skyrim.esm|0D66B7: My dear boy, what makes you think you're getting out of here alive?
Skyrim.esm|0D66B8: My dear girl, what makes you think you're getting out of here alive?
Skyrim.esm|0D66B9: No. I was just buying jewelry for my sister in Cyrodiil. I've never even seen that man before.
Skyrim.esm|0D66BA: Oh, I'm sorry to keep you. Head right in.
Skyrim.esm|0D66BC: Whatever you want to know, I don't care. I run a smelter. That's it.
Skyrim.esm|0D66BE: Wait. It's okay, my dear. Send him in.
Skyrim.esm|0D66BF: Wait. It's okay, my dear. Send her in.
Skyrim.esm|0D66C0: I doubt he'll be free, but all right.
Skyrim.esm|0D66C1: You're not one of my workers. What are you doing here?
Skyrim.esm|0D66C2: You. I've seen you snooping around. Asking questions. Back off. You don't want to know what happens to troublemakers here.
Skyrim.esm|0D66C4: My king. Madanach. When the uprising fell at the hands of the Nords, they threw him in the mines. I don't know how, but he lives. I get his messages, and I hand out his orders without question.
Skyrim.esm|0D66C5: You hard of hearing? I run this smelter. And I'm late meeting our silver quota. What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|0D66C9: Hold, on there. I think I just remembered something. The last time we handed out pay. Little extra slip of paper for Weylin. Took it right to his room.
Skyrim.esm|0D66CB: I was sent by Nepos the Nose. The old man hands out the orders. He told me to make sure you didn't get in the way. That's all I know, I swear!
Skyrim.esm|0D66CE: Damn. Another body to get rid of.
Skyrim.esm|0D66CF: Betrid. No! We had a deal you Forsworn bastards!
Skyrim.esm|0D66D0: Why? We had a deal Madanach. A deal!
Skyrim.esm|0D66D2: Riches are a gate-opener around here. Head right in.
Skyrim.esm|0D66D3: Private business, I mean. No visitors.
Skyrim.esm|0D66D5: The Imperial agent? That's right. I knew. How many dogs is the Empire going to send after me? This is my business. My city. You Empire lovers should learn to stay out of it. Now get out.
Skyrim.esm|0D66D6: So the clues point to the Treasury House, then. That makes sense. Thonar is involved in every facet of the city. Here's the gold I promised. Keep following the trail and there will be more in it for you.
Skyrim.esm|0D66D8: I'm sorry, I'm still a bit dizzy from everything.
Skyrim.esm|0D66D9: The Treasury House is really just for patrons of the Silver-Blood family. You don't belong here.
Skyrim.esm|0D66DA: I'm sorry. We're only interested in helping our patrons.
Skyrim.esm|0D66DB: I work for the Silver-Bloods. Help clean.
Skyrim.esm|0D66DD: Excuse me. What's your business here?
Skyrim.esm|0D66DE: You again. What are you doing here?
Skyrim.esm|0D66E0: Fine. I did notice something odd last time we handed out the pay. Little extra slip of paper for Weylin. Took it right to his room after he got it.
Skyrim.esm|0D66E2: You want Thonar Silver-Blood. He handles all the family business. If you're here to talk politics, though, you'd want his brother Thongvor. He spends most of his time in Understone Keep.
Skyrim.esm|0D66E3: Really should talk to Mulush if you have a problem with one of the workers.
Skyrim.esm|0D66E4: The Forsworn? That's right. I'll say it. We all knew. Even that fat-headed, slave-driving Mulush knew. Just ask him about it. Bah. Another honest man lost in all this madness. And for what?
Skyrim.esm|0D66E5: I'm sorry, but you're not leaving alive.
Skyrim.esm|0D6757: Oh, Cicero takes care of our Lady's body. Oils it, preserves it, keeps it safe. Makes sure nobody disrespects our Matron's coffin.
Skyrim.esm|0D6758: What? Who is the Night Mother? Oh! Oh, you jest! You jest with gullible Cicero! You of course know that the Night Mother is our Unholy Matron. The undying spirit of a great woman who birthed the children of Sithis. And killed them. In his honor.
Skyrim.esm|0D6759: The Night Mother's crypt in Bravil was... desecrated. The Imperial Province is ravaged by strife. Nowhere there is safe, at present. So Cicero brought our Lady to her new home. Here! This is the only Sanctuary left in all of Skyrim, you see. Such was my... honor. As Keeper.
Skyrim.esm|0D675A: Ha ha! Behold the final trick of the Fool of Hearts! You think me near death? Think again!
Skyrim.esm|0D6760: Hmm... That's like telling you about the cold of space, or terror of midnight. Sithis is all those things. He is... the Void.
Skyrim.esm|0D6762: Come on! Do it again! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0D6763: I'm bleeding! Ha ha ha ha ha!
Skyrim.esm|0D6765: Is that the best the Listener can do?
Skyrim.esm|0D676B: By Sithis, I'll see you dead!
Skyrim.esm|0D676C: You won't get that lucky again!
Skyrim.esm|0D6774: Stab you, stab you, stab you!
Skyrim.esm|0D67AE: Of course, leave an old man with a ruined dream. Just like everyone else. Fool.
Skyrim.esm|0D67CD: We don't know that. We don't even know what it is. Where is Aren, anyway?
Skyrim.esm|0D67CE: You may be used to the Empire bowing to your every whim, but I'm afraid you'll find the Thalmor receive no such treatment here.
Skyrim.esm|0D67CF: Yes of course. I'm simply trying to understand the reasoning behind the decision.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D0: I still don't see why we had to bring it back here.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D1: Very good. Then we're done here.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D2: Well, perhaps that is why you're merely an Apprentice here.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D3: Yes, of course. The Arch-Mage has my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D4: The Arch-Mage believed it important. It must be powerful.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D5: I believe I've made myself rather clear.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D6: In his quarters, with that Thalmor, Ancano. Something urgent, it sounded like.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D7: Nothing you need to concern yourself with. I shall be quite capable of observing everything that goes on here.
