Skyrim.esm|0D67D8: You are a guest of the College, here at the pleasure of the Arch-Mage. I hope you appreciate the opportunity.
Skyrim.esm|0D67D9: Your superior and I were simply having a... discussion about my level of access to the College.
Skyrim.esm|0D67DA: All right, that's enough. Let's return to our business please.
Skyrim.esm|0D67DB: All right, that's enough. Back to bed, please.
Skyrim.esm|0D69B7: Thank you so much. I swear it's a fight with them every shipment. Here's something for your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|0D69B8: Great. Get back to me when you're done and I'll give you something for your time.
Skyrim.esm|0D69BA: You wouldn't have a few minutes to run down to the docks would you?
Skyrim.esm|0D69C4: Be sure to watch your finger positions. Especially during the transitions.
Skyrim.esm|0D69D2: That would be acceptable. You can tell Evette San her shipment will be brought up to Solitude shortly.
Skyrim.esm|0D69D4: I'm afraid your bargaining without the metal to back it up.
Skyrim.esm|0D69D5: I've got a shipment of Spices the East Empire Company is holding up. I need someone to convince them to release it.
Skyrim.esm|0D69D7: Ummm, Morrowind suffered a massive explosion...
Skyrim.esm|0D69D8: Remember, it's your job to "deliver" these songs. To excite the crowd. Draw them in and they will love you for it.
Skyrim.esm|0D69D9: Most people head over to the Winking Skeever. If you're not interested in music or drink, you can at least talk to Corpulus. He can fill you in on any of the latest gossip.
Skyrim.esm|0D69DA: Vvardenfell's Red Mountain erupts. Try to be precise, Jorn.
Skyrim.esm|0D69DB: I suppose I could make an exception. I do like her spiced wine. Fine, tell Evette San her shipment will be brought up to Solitude shortly.
Skyrim.esm|0D69DD: That was better Ataf. Add a bit of emphasis on "Ragnar" and "Red."
Skyrim.esm|0D69DF: You will? I mean of course you will. You can tell Evette San her shipment will be brought up to Solitude shortly.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E1: It's the peace treaty that ended the war with the Elves of the Aldmeri Dominion. The Dominion recognized the heresy of proclaiming Talos a god. Talos was a great man, and a great Emperor... but that does not make him a god. No matter how much the Stormcloaks may wish it were so.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E2: Illdi. What year did the Aldmeri Dominion invade the Empire?
Skyrim.esm|0D69E3: All right, let's try something a bit more... fun.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E4: Hmm? Ah, yes. That once held the shrine to Talos. But the worship of Talos was banned by the White-Gold Concordat, some time ago.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E5: Remember, being a bard isn't just about the music.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E6: Ah yes. If she wants to pay the two thousand gold tariff than we'll be all set.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E7: Very good. Let's continue with our practice.
Skyrim.esm|0D69E8: Good, Illdi. Soak in the lore. Jorn, what happened during the Red Year?
Skyrim.esm|0D69E9: Ah well. I'll keep looking for someone. Thanks anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0D69EA: A coin or two for a veteran? It's not much to ask...
Skyrim.esm|0D69EB: You know you can afford a septim or two.
Skyrim.esm|0D69EC: Let us walk in the steps of the Divines.
Skyrim.esm|0D69ED: Remind us of our place, when we forget.
Skyrim.esm|0D69EE: May knowledge of your Divinity carry us through our daily trials.
Skyrim.esm|0D69EF: Mind yourself in my city, friend. This here axe in full swing is a right terrible sight...
Skyrim.esm|0D69F0: Always good to see a fellow traveler. I hope you've found the roads relatively safe. Free from dragons, at the very least...
Skyrim.esm|0D69F1: Ataf, please play back that piece for me.
Skyrim.esm|0D69F2: Those who worship at the shrines will sometimes receive boons from the gods themselves. The nature of the favors varies per deity. Please, feel free to worship at any or all of the shrines.
Skyrim.esm|0D6B46: The Nightgate and Candlehearth inns are frequent stops for Stormcloak runners. See if you can't "convince" one of those innkeepers to help you. One way or another, get me those documents. But don't do anything rash if you go to Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0D6B47: The inns at Dragonbridge and Rorikstead are frequent stops for Imperial runners. See if you can't "convince" one of those innkeepers to help you. One way or another, get me those documents.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C28: I should think so! I'm well used to taking care of a house and the folk living in it. Taking care of the entire hold is another matter entirely. It would be a big enough job without the war on and the dragons about. I just hope I can do right by the good folk who are looking to us to see to their needs.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C42: So Sibbi finally got the nerve to bare his teeth, eh? Look, this sounds like a family matter and I don't want the Guild in the middle of it. You do what you want. Just don't expect us to at your back when Maven has your head on the executioner's block.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C43: Whoa, lad. I'd do just as well drawing a blade across my own neck. Where did this foolish request come from?
Skyrim.esm|0D6C44: Whoa, lass. I'd do just as well drawing a blade across my own neck. Where did this foolish request come from?
Skyrim.esm|0D6C51: I will have someone take care of the cave as well Varnius, you can rest easy. You're dismissed.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C52: Thank you, Jarl Elisif. But about the cave...
Skyrim.esm|0D6C54: Oh. Yes, of course you are right. Falk, tell Captain Aldis I said to assign a few extra soldiers to Dragon Bridge.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C55: If you're looking for opportunities to make some coin, well... I'm your man.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C56: Everyone needs work, right? I have good work for you.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C57: Looks like you had no trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C58: Perhaps a more... tempered reaction... might be called for?
Skyrim.esm|0D6C59: Th...thank you, my Jarl thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C5A: Deeja and I are more like their representatives here in Solitude. None of the Blackbloods can show themselves without being arrested.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C5B: The Blackblood Marauders are... very identifiable. I didn't want anyone thinking the Blackbloods were getting too bold.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C5C: You've done a larger service to the realm than you could possibly know. A resurrected Potema... I shudder at the thought.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C5D: I've got a little work if you're interested, traveler.
Skyrim.esm|0D6C5E: You should see me when you get bored, stranger.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CBE: Get to the nearest way out!
Skyrim.esm|0D6CBF: We've got to find Thorald quickly!
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC0: Here's the letter. Now leave me out of this.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC1: Haven't you heard it by now? He made the mistake of supporting the Stormcloaks, and it cost him. Bitter news for his family, but a fate they should've expected.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC2: No, nor do I expect to. Would be too dangerous for now. It's enough for me to know that he's out there somewhere, and not locked up like an animal in a cage.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC3: No, nor do I expect to. Would be too dangerous for now. It's enough for me to know that they're out there somewhere, and together again.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC4: The Battle-Borns. The Emperor's biggest boot-lickers here in Whiterun. Their connections to the Empire and the Legion are well-known. When Thorald didn't return home, there was no question in my mind. They knew Thorald supported the Stormcloaks, knew he was going to aid them in battle. Made sure he didn't come back. Locked him up someplace to get back at my family. I'm sure of it.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC6: He must have been very convincing.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC7: I've never known Avulstein to be that convincing! Where is the little scamp?
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC8: Just one more thing the Gray-Manes want to blame on others. It's not my fault they turned their backs on the Empire, or their mule of a son raised arms against it. And yet they want to heap the fault on my family's good name? Bah.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CC9: More bad blood between the houses. Fralia is convinced our family is somehow at fault for the loss of her son. There's no reasoning with these people anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CCA: I can't believe you came for me!
Skyrim.esm|0D6CCB: If you are here to purchase, I have many spells available. Do come in.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CCC: Welcome. There are many things here that cannot be found elsewhere.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CCD: Good to have a customer! You need food or drink, you say the word.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CCE: Always good to see folk come back for more. You need a drink, you just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0D6CCF: It's down the stairs, dear. Just come right in.
Skyrim.esm|0D7246: Whatever I can to put my family here back to rest. After that? Probably just stick around here. Saw some good game on my way here, and I'd like to stay close for a while.
Skyrim.esm|0D724B: Ah! Oh, by Kyne you startled me. There's a necromancer around here so watch yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0D74BD: By Ysmir, are you thick, or just too new to know better? The Companions are the oldest, most honored band of warriors in Skyrim. Whiterun was built around their hall, Jorrvaskr. Don't reckon they can use you. But stranger things have happened.
Skyrim.esm|0D74BE: By Ysmir's beard, I ain't one for humor. You know who the Companions are. So quit your asking.
Skyrim.esm|0D74CF: I'm glad to know that, because I need your help. You see, I've lost something precious.
Skyrim.esm|0D74D6: I can't bear it... make it stop...
Skyrim.esm|0D74D7: So sick... can hardly stand it...
Skyrim.esm|0D74DA: I see. Well, perhaps you'll take pity on a humble priest who has lost something precious.
Skyrim.esm|0D74DC: The fever's down a bit, an' I can almost see straight.
Skyrim.esm|0D74DD: I still can't hold nothin' down, but it's better than yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|0D74DE: My joints ache, I can't eat nothin' and my head feels like a furnace. I've seen better days.
Skyrim.esm|0D74DF: The pain's a little less than before, but it still hurts bad enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D74E0: It's not as bad as before, but I still can't get a good night's sleep.
Skyrim.esm|0D74E1: I ain't going to lie, it's still pretty bad.
Skyrim.esm|0D74E3: How is the pain? Any better?
Skyrim.esm|0D74E7: More rest will do you good.
Skyrim.esm|0D74E8: Patience. The body needs time to mend.
Skyrim.esm|0D74E9: Try to relax. You'll be well soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D74EC: Thank you. The pain is already easing.
Skyrim.esm|0D761E: You there! What are you doing?
Skyrim.esm|0D761F: Well, everything worked out nicely, didn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0D7620: Gods, I've lost count of the days. It feels like an age has passed. I wish I could believe that I'd have held out forever against their torture, but I can't be so sure. Now thanks to you I needn't worry anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0D7621: I'm not sure. At first they accused me of being a Stormcloak, a secret Talos worshipper... They wanted a confession. To what didn't matter, they simply wanted me to admit to something. I think they wanted to break me. Use me as a way to get to the rest of the Gray-Mane family. I gave them no such pleasure.
Skyrim.esm|0D7622: Please, get word to my mother.
Skyrim.esm|0D7624: I can't believe any of this is happening!
Skyrim.esm|0D7625: J'zargo is going to stay right here until this is all over.
Skyrim.esm|0D7626: If only Savos were alive to see this...
Skyrim.esm|0D7627: To Oblivion with the rest of them - save yourself!
Skyrim.esm|0D7629: I'm not ready to die. I'm just not...
Skyrim.esm|0D762A: So much energy... So much power. Never in my life have I seen something like this...
Skyrim.esm|0D766F: You may have a point. It's getting difficult to even move troops around without attracting a dragon attack. By all accounts the Stormcloaks are suffering just as badly. Even Ulfric might see the sense of a truce under these conditions.
Skyrim.esm|0D7670: I'll be the judge of that. Besides, by all reports the Stormcloaks are suffering just as much as we are from these dragon attacks.
Skyrim.esm|0D7671: Has he? I suppose he doesn't want to miss a chance to bluster about the Empire's many crimes. He's probably hoping I'll refuse so he can blame the Empire for being unreasonable. I think I'll have to disappoint him yet again.
Skyrim.esm|0D7672: Hmm. You may have a point. I'm always surprised by how seriously the Nords take these things.
Skyrim.esm|0D7673: Yes. But understand - during the days of Alduin's rule, all dragons were his allies. There was nothing else they could be. If not for Paarthurnax, Alduin could not have been overthrown. It was he that first taught men to use the Thu'um.
Skyrim.esm|0D7674: There must be another way. The risk is too great.
Skyrim.esm|0D7676: They are getting to be a problem. But I wasn't sent to Skyrim to fight dragons. My job is to quell this rebellion, and I intend to do just that, dragons or no dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0D767A: Deliver the message to the warring parties. If they will listen, I will do what I can to bring them to terms.
Skyrim.esm|0D767B: That's what comes of trying to win a war with a bare handful of legions. If the Emperor would just give me the reinforcements I've requested!
Skyrim.esm|0D767D: I have the greatest respect for the Greybeards, of course. And the dragon attacks are a growing plague. But the political situation is still delicate. Not all the Jarls are fully committed to supporting me as High King. I can't afford to appear weak. I can't agree to this unless Tullius himself will be there.
Skyrim.esm|0D767F: Of course. You already saved Whiterun from that dragon. I owe you a great deal. But I don't understand. Why let a dragon into the heart of my city when we've been working so hard to keep them out?
Skyrim.esm|0D7680: I'm sorry, but I can't do it. We'll just have to keep fighting the dragons as best we can.
Skyrim.esm|0D7681: I don't need you to teach me how to play high politics in Skyrim. But... you're right. There's nothing to be gained by refusing the Greybeards' request.
Skyrim.esm|0D7682: You speak truly, Dragonborn. Sometimes the grubby business of politics clouds my eyes. Besides, it will be good to see High Hrothgar again.
Skyrim.esm|0D7683: True. A pause to consolidate our gains would be useful. And the Empire can afford to look magnanimous in victory.
Skyrim.esm|0D7684: Ulfric's insurrection has reached its high tide. Soon enough the rebel Jarls will realize what it means to oppose the Empire. But, as you say, these negotiations may offer a chance for his supporters to gracefully return to the Imperial fold.
Skyrim.esm|0D7685: I think you overestimate Ulfric's chances. He's stretched himself too thin. But... we could use the breathing space to regroup.
Skyrim.esm|0D7689: Alduin? The World-Eater of song and legend? If that's true... well, it changes the situation doesn't it? Even Tullius may be forced to talk sense in the face of such a threat.
Skyrim.esm|0D768A: Alduin? The World-Eater of song and legend? If that's true, we're all doomed anyway. And if it isn't true... well, then the fight to free Skyrim goes on. Either way, I don't see the use in talking to the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0D769F: I believe I'll stay here and bask in Eldergleam's warmth a bit longer.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A0: I've thought about that... Trees like this never really die. They only slumber. I think if we had some of the sap from the parent tree, we could wake up its child. But even if you could get to the Eldergleam, you couldn't tap it. Not with any normal metal.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A1: You should take it to Whiterun. Danica will want to see that the true blessings of nature lie in renewal, not a slavish maintenance.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A2: A big dead tree isn't very inspiring if you're coming to worship the divine of wind and rains. Kynareth gives life, and we need a living tree to be her symbol.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A3: You're right. It's mine. I may not live to see this little one grow into a new Gildergreen, but I can be remembered as the woman who planted it. I thank you. And may Kynareth's soft rains fall before you.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A5: It's an old tree. Very old. They say it was a seedling when the first men were arriving from Atmora, thousands of years ago. The sap is precious. It can restore barren fields or bring life to rocks. I can use it to repair the Gildergreen, so we can worship properly again.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A7: Your spirit is strong. Kynareth's winds will guide your path. It's held in a Hagraven nest called Orphan Rock.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A8: Eldergleam is older than metal, from a time before men or elves. To even affect it, you have to tap into the old magic.
Skyrim.esm|0D76A9: You'll have to deal with the Hagravens. I've heard about a weapon they've made for sacrificing Spriggans. It's called "Nettlebane." The hags terrify me, or I would have gone after it myself.
Skyrim.esm|0D76AA: The Gildergreen, yes. It's a bit of an eyesore at the moment. More of a problem for the pilgrims than for me, but not many of them around anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0D76AB: This is the Temple of Kynareth. The Gildergreen, outside, was planted as a seedling in the early years of Whiterun. Disciples of Kynareth could sense something holy in it, and traveled far to hear the winds of the goddess in its branches. Of course, not as many pilgrims these days.
Skyrim.esm|0D76AC: This is the Gildergreen. It was planted as a seedling in the early years of Whiterun. Disciples of Kynareth could sense something holy in it, and traveled far to hear the winds of the goddess in its branches. They built the temple. Of course, not as many pilgrims these days.
Skyrim.esm|0D76AD: If you want to help, you'll need something to cut into it first.
Skyrim.esm|0D76AE: Large diagonal cut from left shoulder...
Skyrim.esm|0D76AF: Good luck on your travels. May Kynareth's wind carry only the sweetest scents.
Skyrim.esm|0D76B0: It was my pleasure, friend. In a way, I envy you getting to carry such a direct sign of Kynareth's graces.
Skyrim.esm|0D76E4: Hmmmm... "Fixed" is such a subjective term. I think "treated" is far more appropriate, don't you? Like one does to a rash, or an arrow in the face. Ah, but no matter. Heartless mortal that you are, you've actually succeeded and survived. I am forced to honor my end of the bargain. So congratulations! You're free to go! I... have been known to change my mind. So... go. Really.
Skyrim.esm|0D76E5: Oh... lovely. Now all my dear Pelagius has to worry about are the several hundred legitimate threats... But you're not out of the woods yet! Get it? Out of the woods?
Skyrim.esm|0D76E6: Ah, wonderful, wonderful! Why waste all that hatred on yourself when it can so easily be directed at others! But someone still has quite a bit to do. Hmmm?
Skyrim.esm|0D76E7: Ah! Good, good! No more barking at all hours, and chewing up my slippers. You used the hemlock, then? Damned good idea! I... Um... We're not talking about Barbas, are we? Clavicus Vile's... dog? Oohh... awkward. Oh! That's right! Pelagius! Yes, yes, now I recall. Well done, well done. Now keep going.
Skyrim.esm|0D76E8: Mortal? Insufferable. Yes, yes, you're entirely brilliant. Conquering madness and all that. Blah blah blah. But you're not done yet. And you could still die...
Skyrim.esm|0D76E9: Ah, so now my dear Pelagius can hate himself for being legitimately afraid of things that actually threaten his existence... ...but only during... the day? Honestly, I can't keep up. But by all means, don't stop now.
Skyrim.esm|0D76EA: Oh, I am so happy for you! My, what a burden to have carried. But you've done it! You've conquered your own inner demons! Bravo! Um... You... didn't mean... yourself. You meant... Pelagius... Well! Same congratulations apply! Just, you know, for what you did for the insane emperor. Conquering paranoia should be a snap after that ordeal, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|0D76EB: Is it just me or is it getting saner and saner these days?
Skyrim.esm|0D76EC: Pelagius stopped by and mentioned he hated you. Or I mentioned he hated you and he agreed.
Skyrim.esm|0D76ED: I hope you're having a good time. Actually, I hope I'm having a good time. I could care less about you.
Skyrim.esm|0D76EE: I see you've been minding your own business. So how is the mind business?
Skyrim.esm|0D76EF: You're just in time to say hello to Roggvir.
Skyrim.esm|0D76F0: Wish I could head inside Solitude myself. Wouldn't mind seeing Roggvir get what's coming to him.
Skyrim.esm|0D7708: Look, I had no choice. They're crazy... I could be killed!
Skyrim.esm|0D7709: Fjotli...  Fjotli... Where have I heard that name? Oh, of course, the poor girl who was murdered. Such a beautiful young thing... a tragedy to be certain.
Skyrim.esm|0D770A: Yes, can I help you with something?
Skyrim.esm|0D770B: I'm sorry I mislead you. Return when this is over, and we'll talk about my involvement in the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0D770C: Next time you wish to accuse someone of a crime, I suggest you obtain proof!
Skyrim.esm|0D770D: Now, just a moment. Let's think about this... you know, discuss it like two rational people.
Skyrim.esm|0D770E: Think I'm defenseless? Well you're wrong!
Skyrim.esm|0D770F: What do you want now? You still hurling baseless accusations?
Skyrim.esm|0D7713: What do you want now? You still hurling baseless accusations?
Skyrim.esm|0D771D: Linwe is holed up at a place called Uttering Hills Cave. He's got his entire Guild there, so be careful. After you're done with all this, come back anytime, and I'll make good on my end of the bargain.
Skyrim.esm|0D771E: Now, just a moment. Let's think about this... you know, discuss it like two rational people.
Skyrim.esm|0D771F: Now you're threatening me? What in the name of the Divines is wrong with you?
Skyrim.esm|0D7723: If I tell you, you need to promise not to kill me. I'd prove to be quite an asset to the Thieves Guild... I'm one of the best fences in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D7727: I used to fence for the Thieves Guild in Skyrim a long time ago. When Linwe moved into the area, he contacted me and said if I didn't fence for them, he'd kill me.
Skyrim.esm|0D7728: Now, just a moment. Let's think about this... you know, discuss it like two rational people.
Skyrim.esm|0D7729: I'm afraid I have absolutely no idea what you're speaking of.
Skyrim.esm|0D772D: It's a guild of Altmer thieves, they call themselves the Summerset Shadows. Their leader, Linwe, he's the worst of the lot. He steals valuables from the dead.
Skyrim.esm|0D773A: How dare you! You're accusing me of taking part such a... such a... heinous act? I should have you arrested for even suggesting such a thing!
Skyrim.esm|0D775A: I had to use your pay to smooth things over with the client. They didn't want to be associated with a criminal. Next time, don't get arrested.
Skyrim.esm|0D775B: Sure, no problem. We'll find someone else to do it.
Skyrim.esm|0D775C: Don't even give it a second thought. Maybe next time.
Skyrim.esm|0D775D: All-righty. Good luck out there, boss.
Skyrim.esm|0D775E: Here you go, boss. All the details.
Skyrim.esm|0D775F: Great. Watch yourself out there, boss.
Skyrim.esm|0D7760: A pleasure as always, boss. Here's your payment.
Skyrim.esm|0D7761: Got all your gold ready, boss. You can count every coin, it's all there.
Skyrim.esm|0D7762: I hardly noticed you were gone. Got your share of the spoils right here, boss.
Skyrim.esm|0D7763: Sorry, boss. Had to use your share to cover expenses after you done and got messy.
Skyrim.esm|0D7764: I'm afraid I had to pay off a few officials to keep you out of jail, boss. Expensive to get murder off the books, know what I mean?
Skyrim.esm|0D7765: Now don't get cross with me, but the officials are demanding compensation for the mark you killed. Had to do it... had no choice, boss.
Skyrim.esm|0D7768: No problem, I'll find someone else. Don't worry about it.
Skyrim.esm|0D7769: I want to pay you, but I had to cover up the murder. Didn't expect you to go on a killing spree.
Skyrim.esm|0D776A: Sorry, I don't have a single septim left after paying the town guard to keep quiet about the mark you killed.
Skyrim.esm|0D776B: Look, I told the client you probably had a good reason to kill the mark, but well, they demanded reparations.
Skyrim.esm|0D7781: Sovngarde take your soul. I'll avenge your body.
Skyrim.esm|0D7782: Ain't nothing pretty about a corpse. No matter how fresh.
Skyrim.esm|0D77E1: I don't know. Rumor has it that she was murdered... butchered by a madman. I really try and stay out of such things. Torsten might think Linwe or myself are responsible for Fjotli's death, but I can assure you nothing could be further from the truth.
Skyrim.esm|0D77E2: No! Linwe prefers stealing from the deceased. Digs up the corpses, breaks into the Hall of the Dead... He even stole that locket right off that poor murdered girl's body... or what was left of it.
Skyrim.esm|0D77E3: I've already told you everything I know, I swear.
Skyrim.esm|0D77E6: Sure, what can I do for you?
Skyrim.esm|0D77ED: There it is again. Did you hear that?
Skyrim.esm|0D77F7: Hello again, friend. I'll never forget what you did for me.
Skyrim.esm|0D7845: Every now and again, we like to remind the shopkeepers that we're in charge. If that means breaking into their strongboxes and stealing something valuable from them, then so be it. Feel like doing a bit of shopping... the Guild way?
Skyrim.esm|0D7855: Sometimes we like to remind a city that we mean business, so we hit it hard. Just steal whatever we can until we've made an impression. Simple right? Almost. The catch is it doesn't count if anyone sees you take it. The message needs to be clear; we work in the shadows. Steal from anywhere in the hold, and you even get to keep everythin' you stole. So, you in?
Skyrim.esm|0D7857: Are you bloody serious? You're not going to get ahead by quitting around here, that I can promise you.
Skyrim.esm|0D7858: You can't keep quittin' jobs when it strikes your fancy. You're going to make us all look like a bunch of amateurs. Think next time.
Skyrim.esm|0D7859: Keep droppin' these jobs and I might quit assignin' them to you.
Skyrim.esm|0D785A: No problem, I'll get someone else to take it.
Skyrim.esm|0D785B: Fine. Next time, save me the trouble and pass on it from the beginning.
Skyrim.esm|0D785C: Make up your mind, will you? You're starting to make us look bad.
Skyrim.esm|0D785D: First you take the job, now you don't even want it. I wish you'd make up your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0D7880: Done. Convince him to stay out of the city while you're at it.
Skyrim.esm|0D7881: Takes coin to pay a fine. And you don't have enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D7882: Ah, yes! There it is! Thank you so much.
Skyrim.esm|0D7883: Shor's blood, what have they done?
Skyrim.esm|0D788C: I think the Bards College might be looking for recruits. You should speak to the head of the college, Viarmo, if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0D788D: Varnius Junius stopped by here on his way to court. He was worried about something. I'm sure Falk Firebeard could tell you what.
Skyrim.esm|0D788E: People say there's a man wandering the streets near the Blue Palace. He seems distressed but he's not making any sense.
Skyrim.esm|0D788F: When the Stormcloaks took Solitude, I thought for sure things would slow down. But they keep me just as busy as the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0D7890: Need a weapon? Hmmm... By the looks of ya, yeah... Think I've got somethin' you can use.
Skyrim.esm|0D7891: It's good to be away from the forge for a bit.
Skyrim.esm|0D7892: I think Bryling's the one you want to talk to. I'm mostly just here to scare away the riff raff.
Skyrim.esm|0D7893: It shouldn't have been like this.
Skyrim.esm|0D7895: Good riddance to bad rubbish I say.
Skyrim.esm|0D7933: It's a smart man that demands something up front for his work. Some gold now and some when I have my dog. Here's the meat.
Skyrim.esm|0D7934: Ha! You've done it! Got to admit, I wasn't sure you could pull it off. But you've definitely earned your pay.
Skyrim.esm|0D7935: Did you see a hound on the road? A fine, strong creature that's been wandering near town.
Skyrim.esm|0D7936: There's one out on the road. I can't afford to chase him down but I could use a fierce, loyal beast to keep me company.
Skyrim.esm|0D7937: Yes, yes. Here it is. I think I'd best be off now.
Skyrim.esm|0D79A5: Tell me, do you believe in mighty Arkay, god of life and death?
Skyrim.esm|0D7B0A: This is your last warning, outsider. We keep the peace, here. Stay out of our business.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B0B: Looks like you weren't followed. Good.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B0C: Once you've found out anything, come straight to me.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B0D: City's got eyes. Be careful when you're out there.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B1D: "Night of Tears", by Dranor Seleth. Old book, one I'd forgotten about. This thing here... it might be what the book was about.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B1F: He proposed that the real reason Saarthal was fought over by the Elves and the Nords was because something was unearthed there.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B20: Let's not jump to conclusions. We'll do all the research we need to figure out what we're dealing with.
Skyrim.esm|0D7B22: Something arcane. Very old, very powerful. Do you think...?
Skyrim.esm|0D7D5A: Thank you. The pain is already easing.
Skyrim.esm|0D7D65: Good, then you understand why this is about more than just Mercer's lust for power.
Skyrim.esm|0D7D66: I've been pouring over the plans you brought us, and I'm convinced the Eyes of the Falmer are in the dwarven ruins at Irkngthand. Karliah and I will meet you there. Prepare yourself, lass. This will be a fight to remember!
Skyrim.esm|0D7D67: Although it would benefit the Guild more than you can imagine, in the end, it would do more harm than good.
Skyrim.esm|0D7D6F: Gunnar, take care of the boy. I have to find General Tullius and join the defense.
Skyrim.esm|0D82C6: I wish Elgrim had spent more time tutoring me then that Ingun girl.
Skyrim.esm|0D82C7: If there's anything you need, I'd be glad to help you.
Skyrim.esm|0D82C8: My husband Bersi will be the death of me. He spends too much time worrying about Riften and not enough about himself.
Skyrim.esm|0D82C9: Can you believe my former husband used to give most of our gold to the poor?
Skyrim.esm|0D82CA: If you're looking for a handout, you'll want to speak with my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0D82CB: Horses are all I know. I've been riding since I could walk.
Skyrim.esm|0D82CC: If the forest wasn't so dangerous, I'd spend every spare moment riding.
Skyrim.esm|0D8306: Your skin's as pale as the snow. You scared of sunlight, or something?
Skyrim.esm|0D8307: Don't like those eyes you got. There's a bad hunger to them.
Skyrim.esm|0D830C: Why are you speaking to me, Imperial?
Skyrim.esm|0D830D: You should leave. Skald gets upset around Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0D830E: What do you want, Imperial? Ain't my life hard enough?
Skyrim.esm|0D830F: What do you want? Haven't you done enough damage to Markarth?
Skyrim.esm|0D8310: I don't think we have anything to discuss, Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|0D8311: You lay one finger on Igmund, and I'll end you Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E1: Not long, in truth. I came to Skyrim after I found myself unwelcome in both Elsweyr and Cyrodiil. I seem to have an unfortunate talent for getting myself involved in misunderstandings with the law. Ri'saad was able to look past that, and it was he who helped to set me up with a trade caravan. Now I work for him.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E2: Mostly it is the Nords. They do not like outsiders in their land, and so we are forbidden to enter the cities. When they look upon us, they see only pickpockets and skooma dealers. It is most unfair, but we do our best to ignore them.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E3: An astute question, for we are far from home and this is a cold, hard land. The wise trader finds the best opportunities, even if he must travel far to find them. Skyrim is a ripe opportunity indeed. The dragons and the war have scared many other traders away, but for those with courage, there is much profit to be made.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E4: The Khajiit hail from a distant land called Elsweyr, bordered on the north by Cyrodiil and the south by the glistening blue waters of the sea. Elsweyr is an arid land of deserts and rocky canyons, where the sun shines warmly, always. There are cities so ancient, the sands have swallowed them whole. But now I will say no more, for I miss my home greatly.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E5: Quite the opposite, in fact. The soldiers are some of our best customers, for we offer supplies and wares they cannot obtain by other means. It was Ri'saad's idea to come here now, while the land is in turmoil. Where others saw only danger, he saw opportunity.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E6: It is Ri'saad who owns the three trading caravans that travel the roads of Skyrim. Long ago, I was an orphan on the streets of Corinthe. I stowed away aboard a trade ship that brought me here. It was Ri'saad who found me. He provided the funds to start this caravan, and put me in charge of it. I owe him more than I could ever hope to repay.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E7: Tools, wares and weapons, all for sale at fair prices.
Skyrim.esm|0D83E8: Goods and wares of all kinds, and all can be yours!
Skyrim.esm|0D83E9: Come! Come and see what goods I offer.
Skyrim.esm|0D83EA: Far have I traveled to bring you these fine goods. Come and see what I offer.
Skyrim.esm|0D83EB: He hired the others and paid for my goods. I am allowed to run the caravan, but I pay Ri'saad a percentage of my profits. I say this without bitterness, for it was Ri'saad who saved me from a life of servitude aboard a trading ship. I am grateful to have the life that I do now. It is a far cry from my days as an orphan on the streets of Corinthe.
Skyrim.esm|0D83EC: Don't let the shop's name frighten you away. I have plenty of reliable tonics and healing potions to sell.
Skyrim.esm|0D83ED: A strong drink will chase off that chill in the air.
Skyrim.esm|0D83EE: I've half a mind to ask if you'll take me on one of your adventures, but I can't just leave Valga on her own.
Skyrim.esm|0D83EF: Not much exciting happens around here. You're as like as not to be bored to death if you stay too long.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F0: May your road lead you to warm sands.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F1: I hope that you will come to do business with us again.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F2: May the sun keep you warm even in this land of bitter cold.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F3: Until our next meeting, if such is fated.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F4: I hope you will find a warmer welcome in your travels than we have found in ours.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F5: The roads of Skyrim can be treacherous. If you must walk them, be wary.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F6: So many refuse to talk to us. They call us thieves and smugglers. I am glad to see that you are not such a one.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F7: Welcome. If I cannot serve you, I am sure that one of my other traders can do so.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F8: I have traveled far across Tamriel to serve you.
Skyrim.esm|0D83F9: Perhaps you have encountered Ma'dran or Akhari? Both are sworn to me, and both have worthy goods to offer.
Skyrim.esm|0D83FA: The road makes this one weary.
Skyrim.esm|0D840C: When I was young, our leaders became obsessed with the Thalmor threat. They believed that if and when we found a Dragonborn, we would need to protect him against the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|0D8412: You were given this gift by the gods for a reason. It is up to you to figure out how best to use it.
Skyrim.esm|0D8415: Turns out, we fatally underestimated the Thalmor. They smashed us with ease during the Great War. I was one of the few who escaped. For a long time, all I cared about was staying alive, and taking revenge on the Thalmor when I could. But then the dragons returned. And I remembered that the Blades used to be dragonslayers. And that we were sworn to protect the Dragonborn, the greatest dragonslayer of all.
Skyrim.esm|0D8418: I expected better from you Dragonborn. I came here in good faith, and now it seems you help the Empire at every turn.
Skyrim.esm|0D8419: You disappoint me, Dragonborn. I accepted your invitation on trust in your good name. But it seems you intend to favor Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|0D841A: You did the best you could, Dragonborn. But I've learned the hard way that these Imperials are best dealt with at sword point.
Skyrim.esm|0D8700: Are you one of them? Please don't hurt me.
Skyrim.esm|0D8701: Skjor was looking for you, earlier.
Skyrim.esm|0D871D: I'm sorry, just... Just don't hurt me. I know you're not one of them, but you just can't help them. You can't let them know I'm here. Please, will you help me? There's no one here I can trust.
Skyrim.esm|0D871E: I mean it! I'll... I'll cut you in half!
Skyrim.esm|0D871F: So, are you working with them? You think you can take me? You so much as touch me, and you're going to lose fingers.
Skyrim.esm|0D8788: Let's see. Ah, so that's what happened to Staubin. Tragic, but I warned him he was leading those people on a fool's errand. Unfortunately, sometimes the stone of scholarship is built on the foundation of death. I'll inform the kin. Here, I usually pay researchers for their work when they emerge from the ruins, but they won't be needing it anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0D87D3: You know, I was there for that whole sordid affair. Marvelous time! Butterflies, blood, a Fox, a severed head... Oh, and the cheese! To die for.
Skyrim.esm|0D87D6: Some of these Nords will come up with any excuse to despise us. And it isn't just the dark elves they hate -- they make a target of the Argonians as well. In fact, just about anyone who isn't a Nord is fair game for their bullying.
Skyrim.esm|0D87DF: You just sit tight. I'll get around to it eventually.
Skyrim.esm|0D87E0: Right, right... Oh, look at that. I seem to have lost my keys. Might take me a while to find them.
Skyrim.esm|0D87E1: I said my fine has been paid! Now let me out!
Skyrim.esm|0D87E2: Sorry, my ears aren't so good. You say somethin'?
Skyrim.esm|0D87E3: Guard! My fine's been paid, now set me free!
Skyrim.esm|0D87E4: Please, we need to speak in private.
Skyrim.esm|0D87E6: Find me the moment the Alik'r are taken care of.
Skyrim.esm|0D87F6: I have faith in you. But, if you find more trouble than you can handle, run out to the courtyard. We'll come as soon as we hear fighting.
Skyrim.esm|0D87F8: We found a cave under the lake. With luck, it'll lead into the prison. I need you to sneak in there, and free our men, killing anyone inside as you go. Once you've rescued the prisoners, head out to the courtyard. We'll stand guard out here and rush in as soon as we hear fighting. Should be fun.
Skyrim.esm|0D87F9: There's a grate outside the wall. Looks like it used to be buried in snow. I don't think they know it's there. From the look of it, I'd bet it leads straight into the prison. I need you to sneak in there, and free our men, killing anyone inside as you go. Once you've rescued the prisoners, head out to the courtyard. We'll stand guard out here and rush in as soon as we hear fighting.
Skyrim.esm|0D87FA: Break our men out and head to the courtyard. We'll meet you there.
Skyrim.esm|0D87FB: You go report our success. I'll set up a garrison here. A shame none of the prisoners made it, but their names will be remembered. Glad you were here. Couldn't have done it without you!
Skyrim.esm|0D87FC: Great work! Get back and report our success. I'll stay here and clean up the mess. Glad you were here. Couldn't have done it without you!
Skyrim.esm|0D8806: Calcelmo is stubborn as a mule. Be careful when you speak to him.
Skyrim.esm|0D8807: Working with the Guild again is the best decision I've ever made. Protection, respect and a constant flow of coin... it couldn't get any better. Best of all, there's no need to smuggle things quietly anymore. With the influence we have in Solitude, I can march it right out the front door.
Skyrim.esm|0D8808: How could I complain when I went from Sabjorn's lackey to one of the wealthiest merchants in Whiterun in no time at all? Plus, the meadery's doing so well, I'm almost having trouble keeping up with the demand. I'd say things couldn't be any better.
Skyrim.esm|0D8809: Business? Business is actually inconsequential to me. I share little of your lust for coin. However, as promised, I'll still be more than happy to take any stolen merchandise off your hands.
Skyrim.esm|0D880A: I've been setting up some open trading with Tonilia back at the Guild in Riften, and it's proven to be quite profitable. There's been no word from the remnants of the Summerset Shadows, so I'm assuming you either eliminated all of them or they've fled Skyrim. Everything seems to be working out nicely, wouldn't you agree?
Skyrim.esm|0D880B: Business is absolutely booming, my friend. I've taken to hiring a few extra men of my own just to keep up with all the shipments. Old Delvin certainly takes care of his own, doesn't he.
Skyrim.esm|0D880C: If trying to rid yourself of stolen goods becomes a burden, and you find yourself in Winterhold, visit me at the College. I've been known to handle items of questionable interest from time to time and I'll see what I can do.
Skyrim.esm|0D880D: If you ever manage to gain entry to the College, and you find trying to rid yourself of stolen goods becoming a burden, come visit me. I've been known to handle items of questionable interest from time to time and I'll see what I can do.
Skyrim.esm|0D880E: Just remember me if you get your hands on anything else.
Skyrim.esm|0D880F: Do me a favor. If you speak to Maven, tell her everything's well at hand down here, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0D8810: Give my regards to Karliah if you see her.
Skyrim.esm|0D8811: Hurry back... and bring more merchandise.
Skyrim.esm|0D8812: When you get back to Riften, tell Delvin this new arrangement's working out quite well.
Skyrim.esm|0D8813: Listen, all I want is the truth to be revealed to the Guild. They respected Karliah, and she deserves better. Do whatever you can and I'd consider it a personal favor.
Skyrim.esm|0D8814: Crossing the Guild was a bad idea. I'll buy goods from you after you settle your accounts.
Skyrim.esm|0D8815: Come to sell me something special, or did you just want to look through my merchandise?
Skyrim.esm|0D8816: Ah, you're back. I'm hearing good things about the Guild through the grapevine.
Skyrim.esm|0D8817: Brought me more than some Firebrand Wine I trust?
Skyrim.esm|0D8818: I heard about what you did at the Guild all the way out here. Sorry, can't buy or sell anything from you.
Skyrim.esm|0D8819: What brings you to Whiterun? Got some juicy goods to sell?
Skyrim.esm|0D881A: What have you got for me today? Nothing illegal I hope!
Skyrim.esm|0D881B: I've got plenty of good merchandise for you. Take a look.
Skyrim.esm|0D881C: Rumor has it you couldn't follow the Guild's rules, so we can't do any business until it's been worked out.
Skyrim.esm|0D881D: I'm assuming you're here for more than a social call. Down to business, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0D881E: I'm always on the lookout for all sorts of goods. Bring me whatever you... well, "find" and I'll see what I can do.
Skyrim.esm|0D881F: So, did Delvin send you again or you here on your own this time?
Skyrim.esm|0D8820: In trouble with the Guild, eh? Well, looks like we won't be trading merchandise until you smooth it over.
Skyrim.esm|0D8821: I've got all sorts of interesting things for you to buy, and I assure you they're of the finest quality.
Skyrim.esm|0D8822: If you've got the time to sell, I've got the time to buy.
Skyrim.esm|0D8823: Let's see what you have for me today. Something simple to turn around for a profit I trust.
Skyrim.esm|0D8824: I've been forbidden to deal with you until amends are made with the Guild. Sorry.
Skyrim.esm|0D8825: Now I know you've come by for more than just a visit. What did you bring?
Skyrim.esm|0D8826: I promise you, I buy much more than simple silver trinkets.
Skyrim.esm|0D8827: I've made a lot of new contacts thanks to you, and more contacts means more coin for everyone.
Skyrim.esm|0D884C: Let's teach these bastards a lesson.
Skyrim.esm|0D884E: Last but not least. Let's go!
Skyrim.esm|0D8B67: Perhaps dating to just after the Dragon War. If so, I could use this to cross-reference the names with other later texts.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B68: I should come along. I would very much like to see this dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B6A: You see? The terminology is clearly First Era or even earlier. I'm convinced this is a copy of a much older text.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B6B: Don't worry, my lord. I'm the very soul of caution.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B6D: Tell him what you told me. About the dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B6F: So, Irileth tells me you came from the western watchtower?
Skyrim.esm|0D8B71: I'd take this a bit more seriously if I were you. If a dragon decides to attack Whiterun I don't know if we can stop it.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B72: Time is running, Farengar, don't forget. This isn't some theoretical question. Dragons have come back.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B73: A dragon! How exciting! Where was it seen? What was it doing?
Skyrim.esm|0D8B74: Yes, yes. Don't worry. Although the chance to see a living dragon up close would be tremendously valuable...
Skyrim.esm|0D8B76: Oh, have no fear. The Jarl himself has finally taken an interest, so I'm now able to devote most of my time to this research.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B77: Farengar, you need to come at once. A dragon's been sighted nearby.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B7A: Good. I'm glad you're making progress. My employers are anxious to have some tangible answers.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B7B: No. I can't afford to risk both of you. I need you here working on ways to defend the city against these dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B7C: Ah! The Dragonstone of Bleak Falls Barrow! You already found it! You are cut from a different cloth than the usual brutes the Jarl foists on me.
Skyrim.esm|0D8B8C: What did it do? Is it attacking the watchtower?
Skyrim.esm|0D8B8D: Uh... that's right. We saw it coming from the south. It was fast... faster than anything I've ever seen.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BCB: Thank you. The pain is already easing.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BCC: The pain's a little less than before, but it still hurts bad enough.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BCD: It's not as bad as before, but I still can't get a good night's sleep.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BCE: I ain't going to lie, it's still pretty bad.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BCF: The fever's down a bit, an' I can almost see straight.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BD0: I still can't hold nothin' down, but it's better than yesterday.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BD1: My joints ache, I can't eat nothin' and my head feels like a furnace. I've seen better days.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BEB: I have things I should see to, but go ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BEC: I'll tell you more about it when we are nearby.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BED: It's somewhere around here, Agna said to look to the Bear to find the way.
Skyrim.esm|0D8BEF: We should get after Vals Veran before he does more damage here.
Skyrim.esm|0D8C3D: The glory of killing it is ours, if you're with me!
Skyrim.esm|0D8C3E: Could you call yourselves Nords if you ran from this monster? Are you going to let me face this thing alone?
Skyrim.esm|0D8C3F: But it's more than our honor at stake here. Think of it - the first dragon seen in Skyrim since the last age.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DB6: As you say, it will happen.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DBE: I'll shoulder some of your burden.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DC1: What do you need to take?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DC7: Not for you. Not for anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DC8: Even Khajiit have their limits. No.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DC9: Maybe you want to rethink what you're asking?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DCA: I will not drag my honor through the mud for you, or anyone.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DCB: Forget it. I don't do that kind of work.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DD1: Then back home is where you'll find me.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DD2: I'll be heading home, then, if you need me.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DD3: You need my strength again, I'll be home.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DD4: I'm heading home if you need me.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DDA: Did you require something of J'zargo?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DDB: Do you still need me to do something?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DDC: You wanted me to do something, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DDD: You still wish something of me?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DE0: We are in this together, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DE1: Lead, then. You have my steel.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DE3: You have someone with you, already.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DE5: You already have someone to do your fighting for you. Don't need me.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DE6: Looks like you got someone already.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DE9: To Oblivion and back, as they say.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DEC: If anyone sneaks up on us, I'll smell them coming. Or I might not. We'll see.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DED: You're taking us somewhere warm, I trust?
Skyrim.esm|0D8DEF: An Orc follows to the death.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DF0: My blood's calm. I prefer it boiling.
Skyrim.esm|0D8DFA: You are ready to move, yes?
Skyrim.esm|0D8E00: Back to the great task of waiting.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E0A: Lead us to our next task.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E0B: Go where the challenge is greatest.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E0F: Oh Listener. Can't you see that's impossible?
Skyrim.esm|0D8E19: Head on, then. I'll keep an eye out.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E1B: You sure? Fine. I'll stand watch.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E1C: Go on ahead. I'll stand guard.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E31: Actually, I want you to bring him something. You see, the caravans are notorious for transporting illegal substances. Present Ri'saad with this satchel of Moon Sugar, and I bet my last septim he'll make a deal.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E32: Good, I'll contact some of my people and have them prepare some shipments right away. Good job. Here, let me give you something for all the leg work.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E33: I'm not sure if you've noticed, but there are several Khajiit Caravans that travel across the realm. They're shrewd traders and don't mind getting their hands dirty. I've bartered with their leader, Ri'saad, on more than one occasion.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E34: Ah, Moon Sugar. I could smell it on you. I'm pleased with this offer, and accept. Take my word to the leaders of your guild and tell them we look forward to a prosperous and profitable future. If you happen upon any of our caravans in your travels, we'd also be more than willing to pay you a fair sum for any of your stolen goods.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E35: Well, as you know, the Guild's growing and things are looking up around here. The only thing we're lacking is a reliable way to transport our merchandise across Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E36: Before we get down to business, I've got something I need you to do.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E37: Yes, I've heard the Guild is rising back to power. An alliance would be most beneficial to both parties. I'll consider the offer, but I'll have to discuss it with the other caravans.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E38: You approach as if you know us, stranger. Who are you and what do you want?
Skyrim.esm|0D8E39: Ri'saad is the head of a small syndicate of independent merchant families. Each caravan is obliged to pay dues, but in return a small portion of the profits are shared between them and the leader parcels out the routes. If we could make contact with them, they could provide an excellent way to inconspicuously transport goods for us.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E3A: Give my regards to Tonilia. Tell her I'll contact her soon.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E3B: You'll find Ri'saad with his caravan group. Just don't do anything stupid when you get there.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E3C: Was there anything else, or is our transaction complete?
Skyrim.esm|0D8E3E: It's an illegal substance highly favored by the Khajiit. Very difficult to come by. If Moon Sugar is refined properly, it can be used to make skooma, another substance that will knock you over after a single draught.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E55: Look at that. Am I drunk? I must be drunk.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E5C: The cemetery is ancient. It's been here longer than the town. I'm no scholar, but I know that a lot of battles have been fought here over the course of history. After each battle, more dead were buried, and the graveyard got bigger. I've heard it's now the biggest graveyard in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E5D: Yes, and I couldn't tell you why. My wife and I think there's some dark magic at work. Or perhaps Arkay's influence is strong here, and he likes to keep it this way.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E5E: Oh, merciful Arkay, no! I am too old and frail, and this graveyard is too large. Kust is my assistant. He helps me tend the headstones and keep our cemetery clean, as befits a place of rest.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E5F: Kust is responsible for the cemetery here in Falkreath, I take care of the Shrine.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E60: Call upon me if I can be of any service.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E61: Browse as much as you like, please.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E62: Look around all you want, but don't try anything funny.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E63: Hey! You planning to steal something? Don't even think about it.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E64: Steal anything from my shop, and you'll regret it.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E66: You hear any juicy gossip in town, be sure to share it with me!
Skyrim.esm|0D8E67: I'll be right here if you need anything else.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E68: See me at my forge if you need arms or armor.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E69: Good steel's worth every septim. Remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E6A: Life is far too short, my friend. Don't waste it!
Skyrim.esm|0D8E6B: We'll all meet Arkay sooner or later. Best to enjoy the time we've got.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E6D: You'll meet a lot of tongue-wagging gossips in this town. Try not to take them too seriously.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E6E: Mind what you say! The Empire has ears all over this town.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E6F: Mark my words, I'll be Jarl again someday!
Skyrim.esm|0D8E70: May wisdom forever light your path.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E72: Don't judge Dengeir too harshly. He is old, but he means well.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E73: May your time in Falkreath be pleasant.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E74: Come and see me again sometime. I might have some use for you.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E76: Keep in song in your heart to ward off the gloom.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E77: Perhaps one day, I'll write a song about you!
Skyrim.esm|0D8E96: I'm trying to speak to the Jarl.
Skyrim.esm|0D8E97: Oh, I thought that matter was handled. I think Falk is taking care of that.
Skyrim.esm|0D8F04: I'm still looking for someone to deliver my message to Sibbi Black-Briar...
Skyrim.esm|0D8F08: He's Solitude's priest of Arkay. He's the one who figured out Potema was still around. He'll help as much as he can.
Skyrim.esm|0D906B: I'm not really sure. Styrr thinks you have some sort of link to Potema. I trust his judgment on this. As a priest of Arkay he's had to deal with necromancy before. Nothing as dangerous as Potema though.
Skyrim.esm|0D906C: It's from... Falk Firebeard at the Blue Palace? You've got friends in high places.
Skyrim.esm|0D906E: You know what needs to be done. Is there something more you need?
Skyrim.esm|0D906F: The courier must have found you.
Skyrim.esm|0D9070: Speak to Styrr. He can help.
Skyrim.esm|0D907F: I won't be able to relax until I'm sure Potema is dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|0D909D: Did you find anything at Wolfskull Cave?
Skyrim.esm|0D90A0: I've done my part, Dragon Bridge will have extra guards. Falk Firebeard will find a way to take care of the cave.
Skyrim.esm|0D90CB: Not really. Varnius Junius is a fool. I'm sure nothing will come of it. I suppose if you're truly interested you could talk to Falk. He's the better-safe-than-sorry type.
Skyrim.esm|0D90CC: Been up to the Blue Palace? It's a sight to see. Even if it is filled with fops...
Skyrim.esm|0D90ED: We couldn't have taken this city without you, sir.
Skyrim.esm|0D9169: We couldn't have taken this city without you, ma'am.
Skyrim.esm|0D91CF: Solitude is as glorious as they said it was. Nothing like Falkreath where I grew up.
Skyrim.esm|0D922F: You'll pay off your debt to the Reach. Just work and you'll earn your freedom.
Skyrim.esm|0D923D: There's yet hope that our relationship with the College can be repaired.
Skyrim.esm|0D9277: Keep going! We need to get farther away from here!
Skyrim.esm|0D927E: Let's get out of here, fast as we can!
Skyrim.esm|0D931F: You should never have come here!
Skyrim.esm|0D9320: It's so nice to have a visitor.
Skyrim.esm|0D9321: I get so lonely out here.
Skyrim.esm|0D9322: It's tough, just scraping by out here. But I make do.
Skyrim.esm|0D9388: We are used to the cycle of gain and loss. It is no trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0D9389: You are kind to offer. We believe the bandits are a part of a group headquartered here. Be careful. I miss my amulet, but a life cannot be replaced as easily.
Skyrim.esm|0D938A: A few days ago, we were ambushed. A nuisance, really, but many of the marauders had quick fingers. One of them stole my Moon Amulet, given to me by my mother when I was just a cub. It is my only memory of home in this cold land.
Skyrim.esm|0D938B: Bandits harass the caravans at every step, but Khajiit usually scare them off.
Skyrim.esm|0D938D: What's this? Some kind of code? "Need as much iron ore as you can dig up...." He's not spying, but it's clear the Empire is making Lod smith up more swords and armor for them. Good work. Take this.
Skyrim.esm|0D938E: Damn those Forsworn. Think they can kill people in my city? I'll have Madanach's head!
Skyrim.esm|0D938F: My brother tells me you've done us a service. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0D9390: Not against me. Against all of Falkreath. Those Imperial bastards are spying on every one of us. Last night I saw Lod the blacksmith writing a letter. Probably sending word to Solitude and General Tullius. Are you loyal to Skyrim? Break into Lod's house and get that letter.
Skyrim.esm|0D9391: There it is. Ah.... Home.... If you ever need the skills of a Khajiit warrior by your side, I would be honored to travel with you. Until then, here. This is for you, in thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0D93A0: Oh really? Hmm... this looks like someone from Staubin's little group. A brave scholar, but not very wise.... If you find Staubin, it would be good to see any notes he may have collected. I doubt you'll find him alive, though.
Skyrim.esm|0D93D9: That's it! Wonderful! I knew I could count on you. I don't have any money to give you. but I can show you a few tricks I learned playing for the Winterhold wizards.
Skyrim.esm|0D93DA: Larina. That fool student of mine stole my flute and sold it to some necromancer. I just got a ransom note for it. That flute has been handed down for seventeen generations! Well don't just stand there. Go get my flute!
Skyrim.esm|0D93DB: That damn fool! I should ring her neck!
Skyrim.esm|0D93DC: At last! I have searched for this drum for 20 years. The college treasury is a bit thin right now. But I can show you some tricks I learned from my days with the army.
Skyrim.esm|0D93DD: What? You did? Oh sweet Divines! You did! There is no way I can pay you what this Lute is worth. But I can teach you a few tricks I've learned over the years.
Skyrim.esm|0D93DE: It makes me sad whenever I think about it.
Skyrim.esm|0D93DF: You're joking, right? Rjorn? Only the most famous battle drummer of the second age. Well, famous among bards at least. That drum has been in half a dozen famous battles. It's a priceless artifact. Rjorn was always looking for new stories to tell. It seems he was writing a lay about Halldir when he vanished. That was the missing clue.
Skyrim.esm|0D93E0: Rjorn's drum! Nobody knows where he died, and therefore where his drum might be. Halldir was the missing link. Rjorn entered Halldir's Cairn in secret, and presumably died there. Now all I need to do is find someone to get it. Wait. You. You could do it. I need you to get me that drum.
Skyrim.esm|0D93E1: Finn was the bard who invented the eight course lute that is commonplace today. Until he found a way to string the extra courses, bards only had six course lutes. The lute they stole is Finn's original eight course lute. I doubt they even realize its true value.
Skyrim.esm|0D93E2: They found it! They actually found it!
Skyrim.esm|0D93E3: Apparently Larina told him some story about the flute being able to make the dead dance. It's just a flute. A really old and valuable flute, but just a flute.
Skyrim.esm|0D93E4: Hmm? Oh, sorry. My mind wanders sometimes. I was just thinking about Finn's Lute. Over a year ago, thieves broke into the college and made off with a lot of valuable things. Mostly gold and silver. But they also stole Finn's Lute. We just learned where the bandits fled to, and I very much want that lute back.
Skyrim.esm|0D94B3: Please. If we have to negotiate the terms of the negotiation, we will never get anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|0D94B4: This is how the Empire repays us for our loyalty?
Skyrim.esm|0D94B5: Now that everyone is here, please take your seats so we can begin.
Skyrim.esm|0D94B7: She's part of the Imperial delegation. You can't dictate who I bring to this council.
Skyrim.esm|0D94BA: I have every right to be at this negotiation. I need to ensure that nothing is agreed to here that violates the terms of the White-Gold Concordat.
Skyrim.esm|0D94D7: Maybe so, but bringing her here is a deliberate provocation. Tullius needs to know I won't be pushed around.
Skyrim.esm|0D94DB: Besides, Jarl Laila will never agree to...
Skyrim.esm|0D94DE: You are weak. Sooner you realize it, the sooner we can see the back of you.
Skyrim.esm|0D94DF: Ulfric, you can't be taking this demand seriously? We can hold Riften against anything the Empire can throw at it.
Skyrim.esm|0D94E0: No. You insult us by bringing her to this negotiation?
Skyrim.esm|0D94E1: Vignar. We will do whatever I decide is in the best interests of Skyrim. Are we clear?
Skyrim.esm|0D94E3: What do you think? I can't let Ulfric dictate terms to me before the negotiations even start.
Skyrim.esm|0D94E4: By Ysmir's beard, the nerve of those Imperial bastards, eh? To think that I would sit down at the same table with that... Thalmor bitch. Either she walks or I do.
Skyrim.esm|0D94E5: This council is unprecedented. We are gathered here at the Dragonborn's request.
Skyrim.esm|0D94E6: I ask that you all respect the spirit of High Hrothgar, and do your best to begin the process of achieving a lasting peace in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D94EA: Take your seat and we can begin.
Skyrim.esm|0D94EB: We all wait upon you, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0D94EC: Why delay? Please, take your seat so we can begin.
Skyrim.esm|0D94F7: Perhaps this would be a good time to get the Dragonborn's input on this matter.
Skyrim.esm|0D94FD: By Talos, the stones on this one! You're in no position to dictate terms to us, Tullius!
Skyrim.esm|0D9509: Maybe so, but I can't be making concessions before negotiations even start. It makes the Empire look weak.
Skyrim.esm|0D950A: I hope that we have all come here in the spirit of...
Skyrim.esm|0D950B: And so we meet again. But this time I know who and what you really are.
Skyrim.esm|0D950C: I'll give you credit for boldness. But associating yourself with the Blades is a choice you will come to regret.
Skyrim.esm|0D950D: I'm here to ensure that General Tullius does not exceed his authority.
Skyrim.esm|0D950E: You gave Ulfric more than he deserved. This will certainly prolong the war.
Skyrim.esm|0D950F: These terms are satisfactory to my government. The truce is unlikely to last long in any case.
Skyrim.esm|0D9510: I'm here because it's required of me. But there's nothing to be gained by talking to that murderer.
Skyrim.esm|0D9511: I don't know why you gave Ulfric so much. That murderer will never rule Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0D9512: I'm glad you were able to negotiate a truce, but I don't trust Ulfric. He'll break his word as soon as it suits him.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B4F: There ain't much more to tell... they're haunted and you should stay away. Look, I've seen one of the spirits with my very own eyes. When it glared at me, I swear it burned right through my soul.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B51: I just said that to make the poor guy feel better. I'm pretty sure she's dead. Reyda would gather ingredients from the small island in the river east of here. Then one day, she just vanished. I tried to look for her, but she never turned up.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B52: Are you kidding? Those damn things will drive me right out of business! Tell you what. Bring me ten of their pelts from anywhere in Skyrim I'll gladly pay you for thinning out the herd.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B53: Well, least I can do is give you something for taking care of him. If you won't accept it as a payment, consider it a gift.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B54: Reyda! You saw Reyda? Did you tell her Narfi cries? Did you tell her Narfi never said goodbye like mother and father?
Skyrim.esm|0D9B55: Reyda! You saw Reyda? Did you tell her Narfi cries? Did you tell her Narfi never said goodbye like mother and father?
Skyrim.esm|0D9B56: If you think there's anything you can do, be my guest.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B57: He's harmless. He's been in a state ever since his sister Reyda disappeared over a year ago. He just keeps to himself in what's left of his folk's farmhouse across the river.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B58: With father I said goodbye... with mother I said goodbye. Reyda leaves and Narfi can't say goodbye. Makes Narfi very, very sad. Narfi needs Reyda to say goodbye.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B59: Let me see that! ...I can't believe this... It was all just a fabrication of this Wyndelius character? I can't believe we were so stupid.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B5A: Fortunately, they seem to be sticking to the barrow. I think they're guarding it. Certainly isn't helping my business any; who'd want to rent a room anywhere near a haunted barrow?
Skyrim.esm|0D9B5B: Have you ever seen what a bear does to the trees? They jump up on their hind legs and scratch them to bits... marking their territory or something. It's getting to the point where I have to scour Skyrim for untouched trees at the right size. Costs me too much time and money.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B5C: You've made Narfi so happy! Narfi now waits for his sister until she comes home. At least Narfi has Reyda's Necklace... reminds Narfi of his sister. Thank you for giving this to Narfi.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B5D: I've seen some sort of a cave entrance over there. Folks call it "Geirmund's Hall," but I don't know why. Probably best if you avoid it for now... it didn't seem to do Reyda any good.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B5E: Let me see that! ...I can't believe this... It was all just a fabrication of this Wyndelius character? I can't believe we were so stupid.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B5F: Nice to finally meet someone who can follow simple instructions. Here's your reward, as promised. Now if you'll excuse me, I have to get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B60: Reyda was here, then gone. Went to gather plants and never came home... nope, nope. Everyone looked and no one could find her. Wilhelm said she'll be back... told Narfi not to worry... Reyda will come back.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B61: Oh no! No, no, no. Narfi never got to say goodbye! Now Narfi's all alone. At least Narfi has Reyda's Necklace... reminds Narfi of his sister. Thank you for giving this to Narfi.
Skyrim.esm|0D9B62: About a year or two ago, some fella named Wyndelius came through; said he was some kind of a treasure hunter. I warned him not to go in there, just like I warned you. The very next night we heard screams from the barrow, and that was it. We never saw him again.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DB1: You again. Guess you need another lesson, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0D9DB7: He is quick to anger, and slow to forgive. One who wrongs Malacath is one who will endure great torment in return. He demands loyalty and strength from his orcs. We have tried to please him, but he is now angry with us, and so we suffer.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DB8: You do not know of Malacath? He is the keeper of oaths, the master of curses.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DB9: You are an Orc, but do not know of Malacath, our master and yours?
Skyrim.esm|0D9DBA: We have been on our own for some time now. I fear we will not survive.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DBB: Please hurry. We are counting on you.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DBC: No, don't go! You may not be one of our tribe, but you are an Orc nonetheless!
Skyrim.esm|0D9DBD: Malacath has heard my pleas! He speaks to us!
Skyrim.esm|0D9DBE: You have returned. Will you now aid me in contacting Malacath?
Skyrim.esm|0D9DBF: Weeks? Longer? It feels like an age has gone by. I haven't slept in so long.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DC0: Oh, of course! Because Malacath would take pity on us if only a useless outsider would lend a hand.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DC1: We have sworn loyalty to Yamarz. He will see us through these dark times.
Skyrim.esm|0D9DC2: By the Ashpit, we can't go on like this.
Skyrim.esm|0DA18A: I would be honored. Beyond honored to have such a thing here at the College. This opens up entire new realms of research for us. Oh, if only Septimus were here to see this! Thank you. Please, take this. It can't quite compare to an Elder Scroll, but still. You will be remembered for generations for this.
Skyrim.esm|0DA18B: Wait. Are you... Are you serious? You have an Elder Scroll? You?
Skyrim.esm|0DA18D: Very funny. Did Enthir put you up to this?
Skyrim.esm|0DA1D8: Fair enough. You claim to be neutral, but it seems that you actually favor the Empire. Still...
Skyrim.esm|0DA229: Oh? True, I may meet my end in a dark alley with a dagger in my back. But the people have seen the truth, and they will not back down.
Skyrim.esm|0DA22C: Oh, he's serious. But so am I.
Skyrim.esm|0DA22D: I have shown the people that when our Jarls drink the Empire's milk, it makes us weak. I must now show them the path that will lead us back to our strength. There is no progress without sacrifice. No wheat without threshing the chaff. The Empire and the Jarls who back them must be swept away. The people demand it. I demand it.
Skyrim.esm|0DA22E: You plan to march on Windhelm?
Skyrim.esm|0DA22F: As in all things, Lord, caution... I urge us to wait and see.
Skyrim.esm|0DA230: Then might I urge you to consider General Tullius's request? I mean, if you are bent on offending Jarl Ulfric...
Skyrim.esm|0DA231: Torygg? He simply walked up to the boy and murdered him!
Skyrim.esm|0DA232: You there. I have a message for you to deliver to our friend, the esteemed Jarl of Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0DA233: Proventus. Bring me my pen. And the good parchment.
Skyrim.esm|0DA234: The chests of gold didn't hurt.
Skyrim.esm|0DA236: You've returned with my axe. I knew that would be his response. As soon as you left I sent word to General Tullius, who's been kind enough to lend us some of his troops and Legate Cipius here. Let Ulfric try to make it past our combined forces. I'll turn you back over to your Legion. Legate Cipius will have use for you. Gods be with us all.
Skyrim.esm|0DA237: Blocking Pale Pass would cut one of the main Imperial supply routes... and would leave Whiterun dangerously exposed...
Skyrim.esm|0DA238: Is that so? I've been wondering when he'd come around.
Skyrim.esm|0DA239: Hrongar carries on the policies of his brother and refuses to acknowledge the Legions right to garrison troops in his city. On the plus side, he also refuses to acknowledge Ulfric's claim.
Skyrim.esm|0DA23A: I'm not the High King, but neither am I a boy. If Ulfric wants to challenge my rule in the old way, let him. Though I suspect he'll prefer to send his "Stormcloaks" to do it for him.
Skyrim.esm|0DA23B: How long have you been in Skyrim? Yes. Give the man my axe. If he returns it to you it means we have business to settle. If he keeps it, then we are at peace.
Skyrim.esm|0DA23C: And what harm is there in letting a few legionnaires die in place of your own men?
Skyrim.esm|0DA23D: Yes, to General Tullius. I need to make a few things clear before I accept these Legionnaires of his.
Skyrim.esm|0DA23E: Lord, wait. Let us see if Ulfric is serious.
Skyrim.esm|0DA23F: I'm of a mind with Irileth.
Skyrim.esm|0DA241: Proventus, what do you make of all this? If Ulfric were to attack Whiterun...
Skyrim.esm|0DA242: Deliver this axe to Ulfric Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|0DA243: Don't be daft. Proventus is my eyes. Just give me the letter. I presume once I have it, I can do as I please with it? Good.
Skyrim.esm|0DA244: I can see now that this is not a negotiation at all.
Skyrim.esm|0DA245: He was rather straight forward with Torygg.
Skyrim.esm|0DA246: Was it cowardly then to accept the White-Gold Concordat?
Skyrim.esm|0DA247: True. He's already proven his personal strength. Now he seeks to prove his army's.
Skyrim.esm|0DA249: Here we are. Solitude. Safe and sound, as always.
Skyrim.esm|0DA24A: Men who understand one another need not waste words. There are but a few simple truths behind one warrior giving another his axe. Ulfric will know my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|0DA24C: Oh. What's this? Ahhh... You're quite brave to carry such a message. It's a pity you've chosen the wrong side... You can return this axe to the man who sent it. And tell him he should prepare to entertain... visitors. I expect a great deal of excitement in the city of Whiterun in the near future...
Skyrim.esm|0DA24D: I'm not a fool, Proventus. I mean it's time to challenge Ulfric to face me as a man, or to declare his intentions.
Skyrim.esm|0DA24E: This again?! That was different. Was I given a chance to object to the terms of the treaty? No. The Jarls weren't asked. We were told. And we had to like it.
Skyrim.esm|0DA24F: This again?! That was different. Was Balgruuf given a chance to object? No. The Jarls weren't asked. He was told. And he had to like it.
Skyrim.esm|0DA251: That "boy" was High King of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0DA252: Are we writing a letter, Lord?
Skyrim.esm|0DA255: Now, I believe you have some business to conclude with my court wizard?
Skyrim.esm|0DA256: Now get out of here and to the outpost! I need to know what's happening out there.
Skyrim.esm|0DA258: Ulfric is the one who has offended.
Skyrim.esm|0DA25A: No doubt requesting to garrison his men in my castle. How many times must I deny him? Well? Out with it.
Skyrim.esm|0DA25B: We can talk about that once this dragon is dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|0DA25C: Ah yes. I've studied those documents... Very interesting...
Skyrim.esm|0DA25E: Until that dragon's dealt with, I don't care what Tullius wants. For now, give the papers to my Steward.
Skyrim.esm|0DA25F: I see... Give the papers to my Steward.
Skyrim.esm|0DA260: But, Proventus has a point. Ulfric has made it clear. In his mind, to refuse his claim is to side with the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0DA2C3: End of the road. Markarth, right on schedule.
Skyrim.esm|0DA339: Here we are. Whiterun. Everybody out.
Skyrim.esm|0DA590: This is it. Riften, jewel of Skyrim. End of the road.
Skyrim.esm|0DA5D9: We made it. Windhelm. Hope you enjoyed the ride.
Skyrim.esm|0DA638: Not enough to outweigh the loss of Riften. The whole south of Eastmarch would be open to Imperial attack.
Skyrim.esm|0DA639: Not enough to outweigh the loss of Markarth. With the Reach in enemy hands, our whole position in Solitude would be threatened.
Skyrim.esm|0DA63A: The circle of stones to the southwest? That's Secunda's Kiss. The giants hold it sacred, so tread carefully.
Skyrim.esm|0DA63B: You know, I saw something near Shimmermist Cave, to the northeast. Some kind of creature... small and wicked. Never seen its like before.
Skyrim.esm|0DA63C: If you're heading east, steer clear of Hillgrund's Tomb. Thing about those old crypts - the dead having trouble staying that way.
Skyrim.esm|0DA63D: Looking to do some hunting? Avoid Brittleshin Pass. It's southeast of here.
Skyrim.esm|0DA63E: Yesterday, I saw a dragon fly right over the city. Headed northeast. Toward Shearpoint, by the looks of it.
Skyrim.esm|0DA640: By Shor, you're hauling around a lot of junk. Best stop in at the Arnleif and Sons Trading Company, sell it off.
Skyrim.esm|0DA641: Let me guess - you need a drink. It's the Silver-Blood Inn you're looking for.
Skyrim.esm|0DA642: Break the law in Markarth, and it's off to Cidhna Mine with you.
Skyrim.esm|0DA643: Caught a case of Rockjoint, once. Bothela at the Hag's Cure fixed me right up.
Skyrim.esm|0DA644: Lots of nasty holes in the Reach, and Blind Cliff Cave is one of them. A filthy Hagraven nest, northeast of here.
Skyrim.esm|0DA645: There's an evil to the north, friend, and its name is Ragnvald. A tomb like so many in Skyrim, where the dead have found no peace.
Skyrim.esm|0DA646: Travel southeast and you'll find an Orc stronghold, up around the old Dushnikh orichalcum mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DA647: Beware the region northeast, across the river. Forsworn have taken refuge in Broken Tower Redoubt and Red Eagle Redoubt.
Skyrim.esm|0DA648: There's a small skeever den just east of the city, at Reachwater Rock.
Skyrim.esm|0DA649: The Forsworn have been spotted to the north, near Bruca's Leap Redoubt.
Skyrim.esm|0DA64A: If it's arms or armor you need, see Beirand in Castle Dour. Bloody good blacksmith, that one.
Skyrim.esm|0DA64B: If you need potions, head over to Angeline's Aromatics.
Skyrim.esm|0DA64C: You can sell off that junk at Bits and Pieces.
Skyrim.esm|0DA64D: The Winking Skeever's got warm beds and cold mead. You look like you could use both.
Skyrim.esm|0DA64E: I don't abide fancy clothes, but you look like you might. I hear Radiant Raiments has a large selection.
Skyrim.esm|0DA650: Off the coast a ways to the east, you'll find the wreck of the Brinehammer. Never know what treasures may still be aboard.
Skyrim.esm|0DA651: You heard of Ravenscar Hollow? Cave on the island to the west. Place is a roost for those foul Hagravens.
Skyrim.esm|0DA652: If you must venture southwest, stay clear of Volskygge. It is a cursed place, a tomb where the dead still live...
Skyrim.esm|0DA653: The road to Whiterun's not safe these days. Lots of travelers just... disappear, south of Dragon Bridge. Can't find a trace of them.
Skyrim.esm|0DA654: Pinemoon Cave is down to the southwest, but it ain't fit for exploring. There are stories... Vampires, some say...
Skyrim.esm|0DA655: You could say the Ratway is the city under the city. Dark, dangerous, and no place for decent folk.
Skyrim.esm|0DA656: There's an apothecary in town, yeah. But it's down on the lower walkway, so stay sharp if you go down there.
Skyrim.esm|0DA657: There's fine steel at the Scorched Hammer, if you fancy a new blade.
Skyrim.esm|0DA658: Need a drink, you go to the Bee and Barb. Stay out of the Ragged Flagon, stay out of the Ratway.
Skyrim.esm|0DA65A: If it's a bed you need, talk to Keerava in the Bee and Barb. She'll set you right.
Skyrim.esm|0DA65B: City got attacked once by those damn bandits, come east across the lake, from Faldar's Tooth. They won't be trying that again.
Skyrim.esm|0DA65C: Best not go wandering east of the city. Bandits been seen around Broken Helm Hollow.
Skyrim.esm|0DA65D: Talk of strange happenings at Autumnshade Clearing, just north of the lake. My guess? Spriggans.
Skyrim.esm|0DA65E: There's a foul presence coming from Darklight Tower, southwest of the city. Some say the place is home to necromancers.
Skyrim.esm|0DA660: If you journey north, keep well away from Snapleg Cave. Damned Hagravens have infested the place.
Skyrim.esm|0DA661: By the gods, a dragon's been sighted just south of the city, flying around Lost Tongue Overlook.
Skyrim.esm|0DA662: You should unload some of that unwanted gear. Head over to Sadri's Used Wares, talk to the Elf.
Skyrim.esm|0DA663: Stay away from the Aretino Residence. The place is cursed.
Skyrim.esm|0DA664: If you need an apothecary, the White Phial has respectable stock.
Skyrim.esm|0DA665: By the gods, you're diseased. Get yourself to the White Phial and purchase a remedy, before you infect us all.
Skyrim.esm|0DA666: The Dunmer get their drinks at the New Gnisis Cornerclub. Why they can't just call it a tavern is beyond me...
Skyrim.esm|0DA668: If it's bed or board you're after, Candlehearth Hall's got both.
Skyrim.esm|0DA669: Across the river to the northeast lies Yngol Barrow. Steer clear of that foul place, lest the walking dead claim you as their own.
Skyrim.esm|0DA66B: The Eye of Talos was a fine ship, once. Now it lies wrecked, off an island just north of here.
Skyrim.esm|0DA66C: Maybe you've seen masts sticking out of the water to the northeast? That would be the wreck of the Winter War. Fine vessel, back in its day.
Skyrim.esm|0DA66D: South of here, and west of the road, lies Cronvangr Cave. Stay away from that cursed place, and the vampires that call it home.
Skyrim.esm|0DA66E: Tell me you've seen them. Those... things. Small and evil, like something out of a nightmare. They come from Stillborn Cave, to the north. I know it.
