Skyrim.esm|0DA66F: I've no problem with your kind, Orc. But that stronghold you've got in the southeast? Narzulbur? It's an affront to my people.
Skyrim.esm|0DA670: I've no problem with Orcs. But that stronghold they've got in the southeast? Narzulbur? It's an affront to my people.
Skyrim.esm|0DA671: You hear the news? There's a dragon roosting up on Mount Anthor, to the northwest.
Skyrim.esm|0DA678: Him? Oh... Ah, name's Balablob or Malaclob, one of them funny Orc names. Talks real good, though. Not a savage at all. Said he's a writer. Don't know what kind of job that is, but it must earn him some pretty coin. He's paid up for the next few months. He mostly just hangs about. Goes down to the lake, sometimes samples the stores of wine in the cellar. Man can do whatever he pleases, far as I care.
Skyrim.esm|0DA679: You see any other inns around here? Where else would I go to drink?
Skyrim.esm|0DA67A: Nah, not so much. The odd traveler on the road. But mostly just old Fultheim, come to drink away a lifetime of bad memories, I'd wager. Course there's the Orc. Long-term tenant, that one. For what he pays, I could afford to shut this place down.
Skyrim.esm|0DA67B: This old place? Been here forever. Built by my great grandda. Run by him, then all the way up the line to me.
Skyrim.esm|0DA67C: Ah, hello there, traveler. Come to the Nightgate for food or lodging?
Skyrim.esm|0DA67D: Need a room? Or maybe just some mead?
Skyrim.esm|0DA67E: Something I can get for ya?
Skyrim.esm|0DA67F: I can't help you, friend. Talk to Hadring, the Innkeeper.
Skyrim.esm|0DA680: I value my privacy. So if you don't mind?
Skyrim.esm|0DA681: I really don't mean to be rude, friend, but I'm not feeling particularly sociable...
Skyrim.esm|0DA682: Hadring got himself another customer, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0DA683: The beds and beer are both lousy, you ask me.
Skyrim.esm|0DA685: Well he don't like company, I can tell you that much. Just wants to be left alone. But no... that's not really it. It's like... he wants to talk. Likes people and all. But he stays separate, because he's supposed to. Kind of sad, really.
Skyrim.esm|0DA687: Sure, walk away. I wasn't talking or anything...
Skyrim.esm|0DA705: Really? That would be kind of you. Here, take this bag of supplies. At the top of the steps you'll see the offering chest. Just leave the bag inside and you're done.
Skyrim.esm|0DA707: Well, it's kind of an understanding between us. I mean, it just wouldn't feel right to charge them for a bit of preserved food. Trouble is, my legs aren't what they used to be and climbing the 7,000 Steps takes its toll.
Skyrim.esm|0DA70B: Mostly food supplies like dried fish and salted meats; you know, things that keep fresh for a long time. The Greybeards tend not to get out much, if you catch my meaning.
Skyrim.esm|0DA70C: Well, there's the occasional wolf pack or stray, but that's all I've ever had to deal with. Shouldn't be a problem for the likes of you. Other than that, watch your footing. In these wintry conditions, the stairs can be treacherous.
Skyrim.esm|0DA70D: Quite a climb, wasn't it? Anyway, much appreciated. Here, take this for your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|0DA710: Maybe I'll hold off and go another day. Get some rest first.
Skyrim.esm|0DA744: Pardon me, milady. Would you care to hear me play my lute?
Skyrim.esm|0DA74B: You a pilgrim or just passing through?
Skyrim.esm|0DA766: Watch yourself on High Hrothgar, I hear wolves prowl the steps.
Skyrim.esm|0DA767: You better not be here to stir up any trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0DA768: Narfi's sad now. Still wait for Reyda. No more sleep. No, no... no.
Skyrim.esm|0DA770: Ah, good to see you again! How did that delivery go?
Skyrim.esm|0DA771: Passing through on your way to High Hrothgar? About to make a delivery up there myself.
Skyrim.esm|0DA7FE: This has to be the worst place in Skyrim to run a mill. How am I supposed to do anything with all those bears running around?
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF0: Keep your eyes open in the barrow... that's how they get you!
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF1: If you're headed up to the monastery, watch your step... it's a long way down.
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF2: If you pass through again, stop by for a drink.
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF5: Just get out of town as fast as you can. Nothing for anyone here.
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF7: I wish I could go with you...
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF8: Narfi so happy! So happy! So happy!
Skyrim.esm|0DAAF9: The mountain will eat you! Watch the mountain!
Skyrim.esm|0DAAFA: If you see Reyda, tell her that Narfi misses her and to come home soon... very soon. Soon... soon, like the moon!
Skyrim.esm|0DAAFF: Welp, time to gather more supplies I guess.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB00: If you get a chance, kill a few more bears for me, will you?
Skyrim.esm|0DAB01: Now go give those stupid bears what's coming to them!
Skyrim.esm|0DAB02: Just get out of my way, I've got work to do.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB16: This is life, brother. The struggle is what reminds us to draw breath.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB17: This is life, sister. The struggle is what reminds us to draw breath.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB18: Family and honor. That's what it means to be one of us, boy.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB19: Family and honor. That's what it means to be one of us, girl.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB1A: How did you... you reach beyond your station, new blood. Let's wait and see how we judge your Trial before speaking further.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB1B: You shouldn't even know about that. Remember you're not a full Companion yet, whelp. Know your place.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB1C: What did I tell you? Keep quiet about that.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB1D: I think there are some things that... oh, wait. Are you trying to trick me? You are not a full-blood yet. We can't talk about that.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB1E: We should not be talking about that.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB1F: I don't know what you're talking about. But if I were you, I'd hold my tongue.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB20: I don't know who you've been talking to, but they were clearly mistaken. Especially for a new whelp, you should choose your words more carefully.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB21: For me, a heavy burden. The beastblood has given me prowess, but my soul is now forfeit to Hircine upon my death. I keep searching for some hope of Sovngarde, but to no avail.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB22: A sensitive question. You may want to be careful broaching that subject with some. The old man and I have our differences when it comes to this. It's a blessing, given by Hircine. To worry about the hereafter means losing sight of the now. What do I care about Sovngarde? Give me the power to tear my foes as a beast of the wild, and I will savor it.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB23: I was raised in the woods. Hunting every animal I could see, with my father. I know them all as well as I know my own mind. And when I feel myself filling with the blood of the wolf... it's like I'm bringing all the strength of all the beasts inside me. But my mind and soul remain my own. I become more than either human or beast. It's like being born. Every time.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB24: I've enjoyed the boons that come with beastblood. Just like every member of the Circle. Kodlak is right, though. We've given a piece of our souls for this power. I know my mythic histories. Bargains like that lead to ruin. This is a curse that was laid upon us. That much is clear.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB25: I like being stronger when I become the wolf. But Kodlak says it is bad for the soul, and I trust him.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB26: Politics are something best avoided. I prefer more personal confrontations, myself.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB27: None of our business. There's no honor to be had in the squabbling of jarls.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB28: Kodlak says not to worry about it. I worry that a bunch of snowberries are out there earning glory while we stay out of it. But I'll follow the old man's lead. As ever.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB29: Too confusing for me. Empire, Nords, Talos. Who cares? Just tell me who needs bludgeoning.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB2A: There are always good reasons to fight. I just wish this war had them. Who cares who worships what dead god? Give me something to make me draw my sword.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB2B: I learned the ways of the blade in the Great War. Nearly lost my life outside the Imperial City. I came home to Skyrim when it was all over. But I wasn't much good at anything other than fighting. Wandered around as a blade for hire. Was a damned good one, too. The money was good, and the women were good, and the drinks were good. But I was losing myself. My heart. I was lucky the Companions found me. Now there's a reason to be fighting. The honor of my brothers and sisters is worth more than coin. Of course, the money is still good. And the drinks!
Skyrim.esm|0DAB2C: My mother was a Companion. And her mother. And all the women in my family, back to Hrotti Blackblade. I stayed with my father in the woods until I was old enough for my Trial. We hunted everything there was to hunt. Good training. Ma didn't live long enough to see me join, but I fight to honor her and all my Shield-Sisters through time.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB2D: Vilkas and I have been here since we were little whelps. Our father, Jergen, raised us here. Even Vignar couldn't remember Companions younger than us!
Skyrim.esm|0DAB2E: To hear Farkas tell it, our father raised us here as happy pups, running around biting knees. I love my brother, but his brains are not his strong suit. We were brought here by Jergen. Whether he was our father or not, I don't care. He left to fight in the Great War and never came back. So he's not my problem anymore. We've been here as long as either of us can remember, though. So try to show some respect.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB2F: Like most of our band, I found this family after losing my own. I traveled the length and breadth of this land, learning all I could of the sword and the axe. I was just a boy, but I had the fire of a man in my heart. Eventually, my body caught up to my spirit. My predecessor, Askar, found me in Hammerfell. I was serving as bodyguard for some weak-necked lord out there. He brought me back here, and I realized... that I was actually coming home. I work to bring honor to this family, and to the family that I lost. For my mother, my father, and my grandfather. For all my Shield-Siblings.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB30: Fortune and glory, friend. Fortune and glory.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB31: So I wouldn't have to answer stupid questions from idiots.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB32: Are you kidding? I've wanted to be with them since I was a little girl. Haven't you heard the stories of Kodlak and Skjor fighting off the hundred-and-one Orc berserkers? Skjor says it was more like forty, but he's just being modest. Where else would I want to be but here? Learning from them, fighting at their sides.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB33: A man of my station has many debts. I'm not much use at labor or learning, but I can throw a punch with the best of 'em. The Companions let me fight for gold, and so I fight.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB34: When you get to be my age, you don't remember why you did much of anything. Only that it happened.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB35: Oh, I'm not a Companion, myself. I just help Vignar. He took me in when I was having a tough time, and... well, I owe him.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB36: Ask any fool around here, and get a different answer. Mercenaries. Warriors of honor. Brothers and sisters of the blade. Drunken rabble. Take your pick. I've been here longer than most of them. Even I don't know sometimes. I just hope they don't kill each other.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB38: The Companions are my family. We fight so that other people don't have to. We bring honor and glory to ourselves and each other.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB39: You'll hear some of the brighter faces around here talk about honor and glory. I've got nothing against it, but for me, the promise of coin is what feeds my blade. Wherever someone in Skyrim doesn't feel up to defending their own honor, we'll take up their burden.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB3A: Your question carries more weight than you may know, newcomer. The sort of thing some of us spend our lives pondering. The difference between a noble band of warriors and a ragged bunch of assassins is as thin as a blade's edge. I try to hold us to the right path.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB3B: Why would you even come to Jorrvaskr without knowing what we stand for? This is not play-acting, here. We are the true spirit of Skyrim. Honor is in our blood, death in our hearts. If you seek either, you've found the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB3C: Are you looking to join? I wouldn't get your hopes up. The short answer is: we fight. The long answer is... eh, forget it. You're not worth my time.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB3D: Only the most famous warrior band in all of Skyrim. Have you been living with the horkers? When we arrive, blood is spilled and our blades sing to the glories of Ysgramor.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB3E: We're the best damned fighters in Skyrim. At least, some of us are.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB3F: They used to be something. Nowadays they just squabble amongst themselves. Take my advice and keep away. You look too smart to get caught up with this bunch.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB40: To hear Vignar tell it, they're a bunch of layabouts and useless whiners who used to know how to fight. But he thinks everything used to be better. I, for one, would trust any Companion with my life.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB41: A good bunch of men and women, who make too much a mess, if you ask me.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB42: It means living such that your Shield-Siblings would proudly say they fought at your side. Glory in battle, honor in life. Deal with problems head on. Leave whispers and sneaking to the gutter rats who can't fight for themselves.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB43: I wish more of our ranks asked such questions. I've started seeing the Companions as a kind of family. I've battled next to every man and woman here, and the bonds of sweat are stronger than those of blood. I would trust anyone here with my life, which is more than I'd say for the milk drinkers who raised me.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB44: It means resting your haunches in Whiterun more than I'd care to, for one. But when it's time to draw blood, there's no one in Skyrim I'd rather have at my back. It means waking up every day knowing that you could die, and having to earn your life by clawing for every breath. I don't know how those cozy lords manage to drag themselves out of bed every day. Why bother if you're not living?
Skyrim.esm|0DAB45: I've never been a smart one. But the Companions welcome anyone with the heart of a warrior. When we step into battle, we fight for our own name, and the name of the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB46: There's been a group called the Companions for over 4000 years. It's been many different things in that time. A conquering army. Ruthless mercenaries. A band of drunken louts. And the esteemed company you see before you. But there's always been a kind of honor to it. We don't deal in politics or underhanded sneaking. We try to uphold the legacy of Ysgramor. To bear his good name such that it never be forgotten, and always be spoken with reverence.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB47: It means signing on for the Nord ideas of how to live your life. Honor, battle, glory, all that. "Even an elf can be born with the heart of a Nord," Skjor said when I joined. I think he meant it as a compliment.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB48: Just because you've been accepted here so quickly, don't think I'm going to help you rise any faster. Fight well, don't be a lout, and you'll be fine. You don't need my help.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB49: So much. This group... this family... this band... this is the best thing I've ever been a part of. The oldest fighting group in Skyrim, and nothing but glory from Ysgramor's day to our own. To be counted in that line is a bit of immortality. Even if I never see Sovngarde, I'll have that much.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB4A: It means when I have troubles, I always have someone on my side. We have Shield-Siblings to keep our backs as dangerous as our fronts. I think.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB4B: My advice? Don't overthink it. Fight well and the rest takes care of itself.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB4E: Soon enough, it'll be me. In the meanwhile, the Circle handles our daily routine, and Kodlak advises them as Harbinger.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB4F: You don't just waddle in here and get to speak to the boss. Who do you think you are? If you want to embarrass yourself, I won't stop you. Kodlak is the Harbinger. He's the closest thing you'll find to a leader around here.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB50: The Circle are the ones who parcel out the jobs. That's Aela, Skjor, and the brothers, Farkas and Vilkas. Kodlak advises them, but he doesn't really give orders.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB51: In charge of what? I'm in charge of me, and you're in charge of you. If you're looking to join up, Kodlak's the one to be talking to.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB52: I can't wait to learn from the priestesses.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB53: Please, let's hurry to the Temple before the Forsworn find me again.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB54: I hope Fjotra is happy in the temple. We're so honored to have given her.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB55: Thank you for getting her back from the Forsworn. Dibella was smart to have sent you.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB56: I swear, if those things have laid a hand on her...
Skyrim.esm|0DAB57: How could they live like this? It's disgusting.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB66: Please step back, there's an execution in process.
Skyrim.esm|0DAB67: Last warning. Stand back from the execution.
Skyrim.esm|0DB287: You have no choice. You must complete your contract. You must kill me.
Skyrim.esm|0DB288: Please, Listener. Release my soul, and let Sithis be the judge.
Skyrim.esm|0DB35C: My family were in the Mythic Dawn. My museum is our legacy.
Skyrim.esm|0DB35D: Wrong or right, the Mythic Dawn changed Tamriel's history. They deserve study.
Skyrim.esm|0DB35E: Scholars from around the world will come here one day.
Skyrim.esm|0DB367: These spells are difficult, even for one as skilled as I. To even begin to inscribe them, I need something very special. A sigil stone from an Oblivion gate. I do not have one in my possession. Nor do you, I'm sure. So we require a Daedra to retrieve one for us. You will summon a Dremora, and order it to bring a sigil stone to you. I will then inscribe the spells for you.
Skyrim.esm|0DB36A: Summoning an Unbound Dremora is not like other Conjuration spells. It will not simply do your bidding. You must first prove you are in control. Here is the spell you need. Go to the top of the Hall of Attainment; I have prepared a place for you there.
Skyrim.esm|0DB36B: Because I have no wish to be harmed.
Skyrim.esm|0DB36D: I will teach you the spell to summon the Dremora, but know that it will only work in a place properly prepared for the event. Once you have summoned the Dremora, you will order it to deliver to you a sigil stone from an Oblivion gate. Once I have the sigil stone, I shall inscribe the spells that you seek.
Skyrim.esm|0DB36E: Every blade I make honors Malacath. Every rivet, every plate of armor is for him.
Skyrim.esm|0DB36F: Only a true Orc knows how to temper Orichalcum properly.
Skyrim.esm|0DB370: The old traditions are passed down from one generation to the next. The secrets of smithing are passed down as well.
Skyrim.esm|0DB371: Lob will one day take my place, and bring honor to our tribe as I have.
Skyrim.esm|0DB372: I learn much from Garakh every day. I must learn if I am to take her place.
Skyrim.esm|0DB373: I've worked with metal long enough that I almost never burn myself anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0DB374: My weapons will someday be as strong as my mother's, and Malacath will be pleased with me.
Skyrim.esm|0DB375: The days of staying within Largashbur's walls are over.
Skyrim.esm|0DB376: Malacath demands much of us now. We will not fail him again.
Skyrim.esm|0DB377: Gularzob has been chosen as chief. He will one day be great, but he has much to learn.
Skyrim.esm|0DB378: Largashbur will not soon forget what you have done. We have a second chance thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|0DB379: The ways of the mystic are known to me. I have remedies and cures, should you need them.
Skyrim.esm|0DB37A: It is good to finally know that Malacath has not forsaken us.
Skyrim.esm|0DB37B: We have learned from our mistakes. Malacath's needs come first, and we will serve him faithfully.
Skyrim.esm|0DB37C: I will do my best to lead the tribe, but I don't know why Malacath chose me.
Skyrim.esm|0DB37D: I am honored to have been chosen to lead. But I know that being picked by Malacath does not mean that I am ready.
Skyrim.esm|0DB37E: Yamarz betrayed us all. I will never allow that to happen again, so long as I rule.
Skyrim.esm|0DB37F: Do not raise arms against my people, and you shall always have a place here, Champion of Malacath.
Skyrim.esm|0DB380: Atub is wise beyond her years. I take her advice seriously, and you should as well.
Skyrim.esm|0DB381: I am... surprised that Gularzob was named chief.
Skyrim.esm|0DB382: We are all blood here. One family, one tribe.
Skyrim.esm|0DB383: I will serve Gularzob as best I can, even though I am his elder.
Skyrim.esm|0DB835: A Nord nobleman I served was stabbed in the night. Wasn't me, but I knew I'd be blamed. So I ran. Joined the Forsworn. Started killing. Got caught. Now I'm here.
Skyrim.esm|0DB836: Innocent? So was I, for the first one. The other murders were all me, though.
Skyrim.esm|0DB837: Getting the shakes, huh? All right. Take it. Old gods keep you.
Skyrim.esm|0DB83A: Ah, you're a clever one aren't you? Now, here's your shiv. Promise you won't ever use it on me, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0DB83B: Because life was better under the old ways. No Nords and their laws.
Skyrim.esm|0DB83C: Small blade. Easy to hide. I mean, sure, you could just swing a pickaxe into someone's face, but people tend to see that coming. Got a problem with a prisoner? Get a shiv. Hear Grisvar has a spare, if you could get him to part with it.
Skyrim.esm|0DB83D: Ah, you want protection? I can get you what you need. Maybe you can do something for me, first? Duach has a bottle of Skooma. Finest distilled Moon Sugar. I'm shaking just thinking about it.
Skyrim.esm|0DB83E: What are you in for, new blood?
Skyrim.esm|0DB83F: All right, but if you change your mind. Not like we're going anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|0DB840: They come in here once a week to clean out the bodies, grab any ore we've mined, and beat down the troublemakers. That's the only time when we get food, too. And if there's not enough ore mined up, we don't get any.
Skyrim.esm|0DB843: Petty thieving, huh? Just like poor Grisvar. Hopefully you'll learn your lesson faster than he has.
Skyrim.esm|0DB844: One day, the Forsworn will paint the walls of Markarth in your kinsmen's blood. Best you not be there on that day, Nord.
Skyrim.esm|0DB845: One day, the Forsworn will paint the walls of Markarth in their blood.
Skyrim.esm|0DB846: Easy there. I didn't mean nothing. Take it.
Skyrim.esm|0DB848: You give me one more look, and I cut you open. That Skooma's mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DB849: Violent one, huh? Best keep that to yourself, new blood. Others find out, they'll consider that a challenge.
Skyrim.esm|0DB84A: Here's your shiv. Promise you won't ever use it on me, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0DB84B: I hear you're in for life. Sad. I'd get a shiv if I was you, or maybe some Skooma to drown your troubles.
Skyrim.esm|0DB84C: My advice? Serve your time at the pickaxe and get out. You don't want to end up getting a shiv in the guts over a bottle of Skooma.
Skyrim.esm|0DB874: You wouldn't understand, Housecarl. You ain't a Nord.
Skyrim.esm|0DB875: Now, let me show you something else I found... very intriguing... I think your employers may be interested as well...
Skyrim.esm|0DB878: My... associate here will be pleased to see your handiwork. She discovered its location, by means she has so far declined to share with me.
Skyrim.esm|0DB879: Hmm? Ah, yes, the Jarl's protege! Back from Bleak Falls Barrow? You didn't die, it seems.
Skyrim.esm|0DB87C: That is where your job ends and mine begins. The work of the mind, sadly undervalued in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0DB87D: Just send me a copy when you've deciphered it.
Skyrim.esm|0DB87E: You'll have to see the Jarl about that. Maybe his steward, Avenicci. I'm sure one of them will pay you appropriately.
Skyrim.esm|0DB87F: You went into Bleak Falls Barrow and got that? Nice work.
Skyrim.esm|0DB880: Here he comes! Find cover and make every arrow count!
Skyrim.esm|0DB881: So your information was correct after all. And we have our friend here to thank for recovering it for us.
Skyrim.esm|0DB882: I'd advise you all to trust in the strength of your sword arm over tales and legends.
Skyrim.esm|0DB883: But I don't need some mythical Dragonborn. Someone who can put down a dragon is more than enough for me.
Skyrim.esm|0DB884: Oh, I thought you were already on your way to Bleak Falls Barrow...
Skyrim.esm|0DB885: What are you still doing here? The Jarl said retrieving that stone tablet is a priority. Remember?
Skyrim.esm|0DB886: I've been all across Tamriel. I've seen plenty of things just as outlandish as this.
Skyrim.esm|0DB892: Now that's what I call a punch. You got me.
Skyrim.esm|0DB8D3: Still alive, prisoner? Keep close to me if you want to stay that way.
Skyrim.esm|0DB8D7: That a boy. You're doing great.
Skyrim.esm|0DB8D8: Haming, you need to get over here. Now!
Skyrim.esm|0DB8D9: That's it, son. Make me proud.
Skyrim.esm|0DB9E8: Then we got nothin' more to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|0DB9E9: Argonian Ale! I can almost taste it!
Skyrim.esm|0DB9EA: Well, let's see... travel-stained clothes, worn soles, blank and unintelligent expression... Yes, in fact you do.
Skyrim.esm|0DB9EB: Good. You're clearly better suited than I am to carry out such a menial task.
Skyrim.esm|0DB9F4: Oh, all right then, Listener. Cicero will just carry on with what he was doing. Until you say otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA0E: Would you like to buy a lady a drink?
Skyrim.esm|0DBA10: You tempt me mightily, Alva. But I won't cuckhold another man, even on murderer like Hroggar.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA11: Hroggar doesn't have to know if we sit together. Or drink together. I doubt he would even care.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA1D: If you leave that kinslayer Hroggar, I would be happy to keep your company.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA1E: I'm bored, Benor. Nobody wants to have any fun with me.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA1F: After all you've put that man through. You should be more loyal to him.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA20: Forget about Hroggar. He's always so tired lately. I need someone with a little fire in their blood.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA2A: Thank you. Here, this is for you.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA2B: Just bring it back to me.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA2D: Well, aren't you the handsome one. You and I should spend some time together.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA2E: Well, aren't you the pretty one. I bet all the men chase after you.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA2F: I... I'm not one for talk. Try to keep to myself.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA30: In a fight, if you see a big wind up, get out of the way or bash him with your shield. Attacks like that go right through a regular block.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA31: When the other guy has his shield up, regular blows are useless. Wind up and hit him with a really powerful attack to get past that shield.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA32: Even if your opponent doesn't have a shield, those big, powerful blows can stagger him. Leaves him open for a quick follow up blow.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA33: Using two weapons means you've got no defense. You can't block anything. But if you hit him with both at the same time, he may not get up again.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA34: Only a fool lets himself get surrounded. Back up and make them come at you one at a time.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA35: Those big, powerful blows are tiring. Pace yourself until you've got the stamina to do one.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA36: Any fool can just flail about, swinging his weapon as fast as he can. Any fool can die. Keep your guard up and wait for an opening.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA37: Don't just stand still to do your big wind-up blows. Which way you're moving changes what happens and can be a big advantage.
Skyrim.esm|0DBA3B: What would Hroggar say if he heard I was drinking with you?
Skyrim.esm|0DBA4B: I am not your plaything, worm!
Skyrim.esm|0DBA4C: I'll tear out your heart first!
Skyrim.esm|0DBAF0: Well it stops now, and that's final!
Skyrim.esm|0DBAF4: Welcome to the Vilemyr Inn. If there's anything I can get you, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0DBAF5: Finally, someone comes in. Kick off your boots, stay awhile. Let me know if there's anything I can help you with. I got nothing but time these days.
Skyrim.esm|0DBAF8: Hey, Talen... get off your lazy tail and take care of the customer!
Skyrim.esm|0DBAF9: Frabbi, a customer needs a drink!
Skyrim.esm|0DBAFB: Too lazy to give him a drink yourself? Oaf.
Skyrim.esm|0DBAFC: That's so sweet of you. I'll tell you what... I'll play a special song, just for you, for no charge at all.
Skyrim.esm|0DBAFD: Why thank, you... you're very kind. Still interested?
Skyrim.esm|0DBAFE: My father taught me an important lesson many years ago.
Skyrim.esm|0DBAFF: I play the lute. If you'd like to hear something, it only costs five gold.
Skyrim.esm|0DBB00: My business is falling apart, my apprentice never listens to me and now there's talk of dragons. If I hadn't sunk every bit of gold I had into my mill, I would have picked up and left Ivarstead long ago.
Skyrim.esm|0DBB01: Pardon me, milord. Would you care to hear me play my lute?
Skyrim.esm|0DBB02: My daughter's driving me crazy, so forgive me if I'm a bit on edge.
Skyrim.esm|0DBB03: Pilgrim or not, if I were you, I'd move right through our backwards little town.
Skyrim.esm|0DBB04: Can you believe it? Another pile of logs ruined.
Skyrim.esm|0DBB05: I hope life's treating you well, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0DBE48: No. This coven has existed for thousands of years. Namira is a goddess. She has been worshipped since time began. I'm honored to be leading the latest generation of Namira's faithful. Those who have embraced what they are.
Skyrim.esm|0DBE49: There's a cave here. Bears if we're lucky. Trolls if we're not.
Skyrim.esm|0DBE4B: A dwarven city. A place of mystery and death.
Skyrim.esm|0DBE4C: Then my answer is the Hall of the Dead is closed. By order of the Jarl. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|0DC166: Well then, you're talking to the right person. I'm the only one left in Tamriel that can get my hands on it. It's damn near impossible to find anymore. You want to buy it off of me?
Skyrim.esm|0DC167: I do. Still want to buy it?
Skyrim.esm|0DC168: I'm sorry you feel my illegal contraband is overpriced, perhaps you should bring it up at the next merchant guild meeting. Look, you want it, I got it. You know the price, so talk to me when you want to cough up the gold.
Skyrim.esm|0DC169: Good. Here, take this key. The Balmora Blue is locked in a chest under the docks near the Red Wave. Hope you like getting wet.
Skyrim.esm|0DC16A: Sorry, guess it's too rich for your blood. Come back when you find the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0DC16B: Not sure. I know that it starts with Moon Sugar, but all sorts of other ingredients are added to increase its potency. Used to be a lucrative underworld commodity when Balmora was still standing. Now the stuff is beyond valuable. It's also very illegal. Anyone caught with Balmora Blue looks forward to rotting in jail for a very long time.
Skyrim.esm|0DC16C: There's pirates and then there's the crew of the Red Wave. They're in a class by themselves. They usually make runs along the coast shipping all sorts of contraband to and from Morrowind. Rumor has it they can get you anything for the right price.
Skyrim.esm|0DC16D: I'm afraid not. How else can a poor, overworked sailor like myself expect to earn a living?
Skyrim.esm|0DC239: Oblivion take them. Every miserable last one of them.
Skyrim.esm|0DC23A: Why didn't you say so immediately?!
Skyrim.esm|0DC23B: My men are fearless. It's the Imperial milk drinkers I'm worried about.
Skyrim.esm|0DC23C: No. Of course not. Just - don't let me down Cipius. We'll need to set up water brigades to combat the flames.
Skyrim.esm|0DC23D: You Imperials are efficient, I'll give you that. How long until they arrive?
Skyrim.esm|0DC240: Not long. They're hiding in the country side.
Skyrim.esm|0DC242: This is it! Time to see what these Stormcloaks are made of.
Skyrim.esm|0DC244: My scouts tell me they're loading them with fire.
Skyrim.esm|0DC245: Move it soldier. Spread the word. Go. Go. Go.
Skyrim.esm|0DC246: Sir, they're on the move. They'll be at the gates at any moment!
Skyrim.esm|0DC247: The men will be fighting in flames.
Skyrim.esm|0DC249: The Stormcloaks are here. In force. Get down to the front line. This is it. We must hold the city. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|0DC24B: Take a moment to breathe, soldier.
Skyrim.esm|0DC24C: If you prefer I took my men and left...
Skyrim.esm|0DC24E: The outer walls are strong. If we can hold them there...
Skyrim.esm|0DC24F: You'll be doing whatever Rikke tells you to do. And, I expect you to find creative ways to disrupt the Stormcloaks along the way.
Skyrim.esm|0DC250: You'll be doing whatever Galmar tells you to do, and causing as much mayhem as possible for the Empire and any Jarl who supports them.
Skyrim.esm|0DC255: The men are already gathering at the gates.
Skyrim.esm|0DC258: Damnit. Where'd they get catapults? The city walls are already falling apart as it is.
Skyrim.esm|0DC259: So, he wants to take my city, walls intact.
Skyrim.esm|0DC28A: I don't want to die. I can't die here!
Skyrim.esm|0DC28B: Weak. He's weak. You're strong. Crush him.
Skyrim.esm|0DC28C: No. Kill him. Crush his bones. Tear at his flesh.
Skyrim.esm|0DC28D: Madanach's guard. Big, even for an Orc. Heard he ripped a man's arm off and beat him to death with it. He's old-fashioned like that.
Skyrim.esm|0DC28E: Come out! We know you're here!
Skyrim.esm|0DC28F: Fresh food. No wood rot on the furniture. Someone's been here. Recently. But the people I asked say no one enters or leaves....
Skyrim.esm|0DC290: If you're asking, that means you're the new lifer. Tough luck, friend. Those guards sold you out but good. No one talks to Madanach, I'm afraid. Not without getting past Borkul the Beast. And you don't want to talk to Borkul the Beast.
Skyrim.esm|0DC291: You will kill. You will kill, or you will die!
Skyrim.esm|0DC292: That's it. Something's inside the house. Come on, we're getting to the bottom of this.
Skyrim.esm|0DC293: Stendarr's Mercy! This isn't an ordinary Daedra. We have to get help.
Skyrim.esm|0DC294: Get out of my head, Daedra!
Skyrim.esm|0DC295: You first. Come on. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|0DC296: Wait. Did you hear that? I think it came this way.
Skyrim.esm|0DC297: There's another door. See if you can get it open.
Skyrim.esm|0DC532: The finest jewelry in all of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0DC534: The bloodiest beef in the Reach.
Skyrim.esm|0DC535: You want a cut of venison or mutton? Of course you do.
Skyrim.esm|0DCAE1: Now, if you'll both proceed to the armory to don your Nightingale Armor, we can begin the Oath.
Skyrim.esm|0DD037: Nord nonsense?! Why you puffed-up ignorant... these are our sacred traditions that go back to the founding of the First Empire!
Skyrim.esm|0DD038: Yes, I could use someone to fetch something for me. Well, when I say fetch, I really mean delve into a dangerous ruin in search of an ancient stone tablet that may or may not actually be there.
Skyrim.esm|0DD039: So the Jarl thinks you can be of use to me? Oh yes, he must be referring to my research into the dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0DD03A: Capable as he may be, I don't see any signs of him being this, what, "Dragonborn."
Skyrim.esm|0DD03B: Capable as she may be, I don't see any signs of her being this, what, "Dragonborn."
Skyrim.esm|0DD03C: Hrongar. Don't be so hard on Avenicci.
Skyrim.esm|0DD03D: Whatever happened when you killed that dragon, it revealed something in you, and the Greybeards heard it. If they think you're Dragonborn, who are we to argue? You'd better get up to High Hrothgar immediately. There's no refusing the summons of the Greybeards. It's a tremendous honor.
Skyrim.esm|0DD03E: Hrongar, calm yourself. What does any of this Nord nonsense have to do with our friend here?
Skyrim.esm|0DD03F: Good. You're finally here. The Jarl's been waiting for you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD040: I envy you the chance to see this dragon up close!
Skyrim.esm|0DD041: That's the Greybeards' business, not ours.
Skyrim.esm|0DD042: The Dragonborn is said to be uniquely gifted in the Voice - the ability to focus your vital essence into a Thu'um, or Shout. If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your gift.
Skyrim.esm|0DD043: I want to hear about this dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0DD044: It's just that... what do these Greybeards want with him?
Skyrim.esm|0DD045: It's just that... what do these Greybeards want with her?
Skyrim.esm|0DD046: I meant no disrespect, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0DD047: Off to Bleak Falls Barrow with you. The Jarl is not a patient man. Neither am I, come to think of it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD048: I don't know if you've heard, but Jarl Balgruuf said my work is a priority. Which means you should be on your way.
Skyrim.esm|0DD062: It's too bad they executed Roggvir before we got here.
Skyrim.esm|0DD063: The Greybeards have summoned the Dragonborn to High Hrothgar. Such a thing has not happened for... hundreds of years.
Skyrim.esm|0DD064: There was a fight down in the Ratway, but reports are... sketchy. Some say it was Thalmor Agents, others say Thieves Guild. Some say it was both.
Skyrim.esm|0DD065: Word is Elenwen's furious. Big party at the Thalmor Embassy, and one of the guests went someplace they shouldn't. Caused quite a commotion.
Skyrim.esm|0DD066: Have you heard? There's been another dragon attack. Kynesgrove, this time.
Skyrim.esm|0DD067: What could it mean? The return of the Dragonborn? And who among us could possibly hold that honor? And such power?
Skyrim.esm|0DD068: By the gods, it's true, isn't it? A dragon has attacked Whiterun. How could mere men bring down such a beast?
Skyrim.esm|0DD069: By Ysmir, you did it. You killed a dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0DD06A: There's been talk amongst the guards. That you are... Dragonborn. But such a thing... Surely that's not possible...
Skyrim.esm|0DD06B: The guards in Dragonsreach say you've aided the Jarl. On behalf of Whiterun, I thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD06C: I've seen you helping the Jarl. If only all visitors to Whiterun were as noble.
Skyrim.esm|0DD06D: It was you convinced the Jarl to trap that dragon in the keep. And by Ysmir, it actually worked.
Skyrim.esm|0DD06E: There's a dragon. Here. In Dragonsreach. And I thought it was just a name.
Skyrim.esm|0DD06F: We had that beast contained. And you released it! Why? Why would you do such a thing?
Skyrim.esm|0DD070: Is it true what they say? There was a dragon held captive in Whiterun, and you... you released it? By the gods, man, why?
Skyrim.esm|0DD071: Is it true what they say? There was a dragon held captive in Whiterun, and you... you released it? By the gods, woman, why?
Skyrim.esm|0DD072: The Greybeards have arranged a truce between the Stormcloaks and Imperials. How long can such a thing last?
Skyrim.esm|0DD073: The Greybeards have called a peace council, at High Hrothgar. And can you believe it - both Ulfric Stormcloak and General Tullius have agreed to go.
Skyrim.esm|0DD074: Word is spreading like magefire! The great evil has been vanquished! You have truly saved us all!
Skyrim.esm|0DD076: In the ancient tongue, you are Dovahkiin - Dragonborn!
Skyrim.esm|0DD077: By the gods. The Emperor's been murdered. On his own ship... moored in the inlet. It was... the Dark Brotherhood. There was nothing we could do...
Skyrim.esm|0DD078: That old woman who runs the orphanage has been murdered. Say, didn't I see you coming out of there...?
Skyrim.esm|0DD079: Been a murder, over in Riften. Some old lady who runs an orphanage. Those poor children must be heartbroken.
Skyrim.esm|0DD07A: Hey! You're the one who killed Alain Dufont, up in Raldbthar. Oh yeah, word about that got around. Man was a piece of bandit trash, is what I hear.
Skyrim.esm|0DD07B: The Emperor's cousin, killed. Right here in Solitude. I feel nothing but shame...
Skyrim.esm|0DD07C: The cousin of the Emperor was murdered! In Solitude, at her own damn wedding. Not a chance we would have let that happen here.
Skyrim.esm|0DD07D: You didn't hear this from me, but we've uncovered a plot to kill the Emperor! It gets better. The ringleader? One of the Emperor's own elite guard...
Skyrim.esm|0DD07E: There was some ruckus over by that weird black door. You weren't involved in that craziness, were you?
Skyrim.esm|0DD07F: Been lots of people coming and going through that creepy black door. And noises...  I'd say it sounds like construction, but that's crazy. Right?
Skyrim.esm|0DD080: There are some that say the Dark Brotherhood is back, and stronger than ever. That they've made Dawnstar their home. Tell me - am I wrong?
Skyrim.esm|0DD081: The guards have been saying things about you... That you're the one that... Well... They say the Dark Brotherhood... Well. Never mind. Sir.
Skyrim.esm|0DD082: The guards have been saying things about you... That you're the one that... Well... They say the Dark Brotherhood... Well. Never mind. Ma'am.
Skyrim.esm|0DD083: Wait, I know you. You went into the Emperor's Tower, just before... before his decoy was murdered...
Skyrim.esm|0DD084: We just received word that somebody tried to kill the Emperor! Turned out to just be his double, but can you imagine if he had succeeded?
Skyrim.esm|0DD085: They're saying the Dark Brotherhood has been destroyed. Wiped off the face off Tamriel. But that's not entirely true... is it?
Skyrim.esm|0DD086: You're the one all the guards are talking about. You went into that Sanctuary... wiped out the Dark Brotherhood! Friend, I owe you a drink.
Skyrim.esm|0DD087: Maybe you haven't heard, but the Thieves Guild is back. Windhelm is their city now, if you catch my meaning...
Skyrim.esm|0DD088: Markarth's a different place, now. Do business here, and you do business with the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0DD089: A change has swept into Solitude, like a breeze off the ocean - the Thieves Guild has come...
Skyrim.esm|0DD08A: Ain't no doubt about it. The Thieves Guild is back, and they've got Riften in their grip.
Skyrim.esm|0DD08B: Welcome to Riften, home of the Thieves Guild. Or so they'd have you believe. It's all lies. They're just thugs... vermin, creeping around the Ratway.
Skyrim.esm|0DD08C: The say the College has been snooping around Saarthal. Mages in a burial crypt. No good can come of that...
Skyrim.esm|0DD08D: Some are saying the Psijic Monks have been seen in Skyrim. They're an ancient order. I had 'em figured for dead.
Skyrim.esm|0DD08E: You hear the news? Those damn mages blew up Winterhold! Guess it was just a matter of time...
Skyrim.esm|0DD08F: Word is the College of Mages is back to normal. If there's such a thing for that lot.
Skyrim.esm|0DD090: Your name's been cleared... by Thonar Silver-Blood. Consider yourself lucky.
Skyrim.esm|0DD091: So Madanach was behind those murders. Gods help us.
Skyrim.esm|0DD092: By Shor, is that... is that Azura's Star? How did you come to possess such a rare treasure?
Skyrim.esm|0DD093: Who did you have to kill to get that blade?
Skyrim.esm|0DD094: That mace... get it away from me. Get it away...
Skyrim.esm|0DD095: Ugh. Your breath is foul. What've you been eating?
Skyrim.esm|0DD096: As if Skyrim didn't have enough outsiders. Now we've got mercenaries from Hammerfell struttin' about...
Skyrim.esm|0DD097: From what I hear, you're the one gave the Thalmor a black eye. Sure know how to pick your enemies, don't you?
Skyrim.esm|0DD098: You've come to Dawnstar at a strange time, friend. Seems everyone in the town is having nightmares...
Skyrim.esm|0DD099: The damned nightmares have finally stopped. Don't suppose you'd know anything about that?
Skyrim.esm|0DD09A: You've been seen in the company of the Companions. That's an honorable path you're on, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0DD09B: I know of your deeds, and am honored to address a member of the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0DD09C: Uch. Been tending your hounds? You smell like wet dog.
Skyrim.esm|0DD09D: Heard about what you did. Killing those foul witches. Well done, Companion.
Skyrim.esm|0DD09E: Kodlak killed, Jorrvaskr attacked. How could this happen, within the walls of Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|0DD09F: I heard about the attack on Jorrvaskr, and the killing of your honored leader. You have my sympathies, Companion.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A0: Kodlak White-Mane has been laid to rest, I know. May his soul live forever in the halls of Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A1: You... you're the Harbinger. Of the Companions. It is my honor to stand before you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A5: I hear the East Empire Company has reopened, thanks in no small part to you. Well done. All of Skyrim benefits from their trade.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A6: You did this city a great service by catching that killer. Well done.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A7: Good thing you did for Nurelion, retrieving that phial for him before he died. Man was obsessed.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A8: By Ysmir, you've helped save the Gildergreen. Words can't express what that means to this city.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0A9: That sapling you recovered means the Gildergreen will live again. Your deed honors the people of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0AA: You've actually done it. You've found the Sybil of Dibella. You have done Markarth a tremendous service.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0AB: You... you were talking to that murderous savage. And then he escaped. What are you hiding?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0AC: First Torygg and now Elisif. Damn those Stormcloaks. Let's hope that Erikur fares better than they did.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0AD: Filthy she-hound Elisif got what she deserved. Let her serve as "High Queen" of Oblivion, for all I care.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0AE: Releasing a captured dragon. I don't think I'll ever understand that one...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0AF: Yes, Dovahkiin? How can I help you?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B0: You have vanquished a great evil from Skyrim. You have saved this land... and our very souls.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B1: No matter what else happens, the guards will always be grateful for everything you've done.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B2: Looks like it's really happening. A peace council, attended by both Ulfric and Tullius, up on High Hrothgar. Maybe there's hope for Skyrim yet.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B3: This truce with the Stormcloaks won't last a fortnight. Bloody savages will kill us while we sleep.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B4: A truce? With the Imperials? They'll murder us all, first chance they get.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B5: I caught a glimpse of that captured dragon. It's.. beautiful. In its own way.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B6: Trapping a dragon in Dragonsreach. Damn clever idea you had, I have to admit.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B7: You have the Jarl's confidence, friend. And so you have mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B8: You couldn't possibly be the Dragonborn of legend. Could you?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0B9: If we killed a dragon once, we can do it again. With your help, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0BA: If those Whiterun guards can take down a dragon, so can we.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0BB: Got to thinking, maybe I'm the Dragonborn, and I just don't know it yet.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0BC: For a dragon to attack Kynesgrove... Why? Why there? Is no place safe?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0BD: If I'd been on guard at the Thalmor Embassy, wouldn't nobody be disturbing that party...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0BE: Thalmor in the Ratway? What's next, Spriggans in the Bee and Barb?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0BF: I have to wonder... what does the Dragonborn do once he's summoned by the Greybeards? Can the Thu'um be taught, like any skill?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C0: Helgen... destroyed by a dragon. Hard to believe, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C1: You know, I could have gone into that Sanctuary. Killed all those Dark Brotherhood types. But I was... sick that day.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C2: I know who you are, assassin. I guess the rumors about your kind's "extinction" have been... exaggerated.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C3: So the Emperor had a decoy. Poor bastard got murdered, too. But then, I guess that was his job...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C4: Now, I know who you are. And... and I know your business... Just, please... These are good folk we got here. Mostly...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C5: It ain't true what they say about you and... and the Dark Brotherhood? Is it?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C6: There are stories about you, and dark tidings in Dawnstar...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C7: Seems the Emperor's coming to Skyrim... and one of his own protectors was going to have him assassinated. Can't trust anyone these days.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C8: I heard you were at the wedding of the Emperor's cousin. In Solitude? Gods, that must have been horrible...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0C9: Look, that Vici woman had a bodyguard. I know it's terrible she got murdered at her own wedding, but the city guard is hardly to blame...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0CB: You ask me, those brats in the orphanage are better off, now that old Grelod is dead.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0CC: It's no secret the Aretino boy is doing some ritual, trying to call the Dark Brotherhood. But who's going to stop him? Me? I'll have no part of that.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0CD: The Emperor's cousin Vittoria Vici is getting married soon. The reception will be public - anyone in Solitude can attend.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0CE: The Emperor of Skyrim. Dead. These are dark times, my friend. Dark times, indeed...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0CF: We may guard Whiterun, but the Thieves Guild guards us. If you know what I mean.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D0: Shirking the law in Windhelm ain't what it used to be. Not with the Thieves Guild running things...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D1: Break the law here in Markarth, and maybe we can work something out. Courtesy of the Thieves Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D2: You know, the law here in Solitude can be a bit... "flexible." So long as you've got the gold. That's the way the Thieves Guild likes it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D3: Seeing as how Riften is home to the Thieves Guild, maybe we can work something out if you have trouble with the law. For the right price.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D4: The Thieves Guild used to run this city. Now they're nothing but braggarts and bullies, rotting to death down in their Ratway...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D5: What business do College mages have in a place like Saarthal, anyway? It's a place for Nord dead... not your weird experiments.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D6: One of the guards said he saw a mage appear out of thin air. Claimed it was one of those Psijic Monks. Man's been hitting the skooma, I say.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D7: Imagine, blowing up an entire town. Damn College... Winterhold will never be the same.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D8: You're that Mage from the College. Right? Hear things have finally calmed down over there.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0D9: You put those Forsworn down. Good job.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0DA: Gods damn those Forsworn. So many dead...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0DB: You see those warriors from Hammerfell? They've got curved swords. Curved. Swords.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0DC: Wonder what it's like for those Thalmor, now that they've been taken down a notch...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0DD: The people here are still plagued with nightmares. It's... unnatural.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0DE: No more nightmares. Thank the gods for that.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0DF: Now I remember - you're that new member of the Companions. So you, what - fetch the mead?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E0: Honored Companion. How can I assist you?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E1: Is that... fur? Coming out of your ears?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E2: I'm telling you, I heard it. Howling. Those werewolf tales are true.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E3: I find your wolfish grin... unsettling.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E4: With respect, Companion, I'd ask that you and your friends muzzle that dog of yours. The howling coming from Jorrvaskr has gotten out of hand.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E5: If it isn't the slayer of the Glenmoril Witches. How can I assist you, Companion?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E6: Who would dare attack the leader of the Companions? In his own hall? There'll be blood for this, I know it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0E7: Kodlak White-Mane is in a better place now. For is there any place better than Sovngarde?
Skyrim.esm|0DD0ED: Thanks again for helping reopen the East Empire Company. Gonna have them import me some of that aged Breton cheese.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0EE: I was this close to catching that killer. I swear it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0EF: With Nurelion gone, I suppose Quintus has taken over business in the White Phial.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0F0: The Gildergreen blooms again. All thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0F1: The Gildergreen will bloom again. All thanks to you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0F2: Wonder what kinds of things they've got the Dibella doing, over in the temple. Mostly praying, I reckon...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0F3: I know you had some part in Sinding's escape. Don't deny it...
Skyrim.esm|0DD0F4: We'll protect Erikur as best we're able, but I fear the throne of Solitude is cursed.
Skyrim.esm|0DD0F5: It is whispered that one of the Daedric Princes himself is behind the murder of Elisif. It would seem even the nobility of Oblivion favors our cause.
Skyrim.esm|0DD5ED: The Blackblood girl? Now there was a fighter. If I didn't know better, I'd say she was a Forsworn woman.
Skyrim.esm|0DD5EE: Hey, remember that girl, Eisa? The one who sliced open that pickpocket they threw in here?
Skyrim.esm|0DD5F5: Not today. I'm just not ready to make the climb to High Hrothgar. The path isn't safe.
Skyrim.esm|0DD5F6: On your way up the 7,000 Steps again, Klimmek?
Skyrim.esm|0DD5F7: Honestly, I'm not certain. I've yet to be allowed into the monastery. Perhaps one day.
Skyrim.esm|0DD5F8: Aren't the Greybeards expecting some supplies?
Skyrim.esm|0DD609: Ulfric coveted the High King's throne. He thought he deserved it more than Torygg. Now it would seem Ulfric has his wish. And there truly is no justice in the world.
Skyrim.esm|0DD60A: Most people head over to the Winking Skeever. If you're not interested in music or drink, you can at least talk to Corpulus. He can fill you in on any of the latest gossip.
Skyrim.esm|0DD60B: No. That honor is Sybille Stentor's. A smart woman steers clear of Stentor. And keeps herself out of the dungeon when she's having a bad day...
Skyrim.esm|0DD60C: A woman like you should own a horse. Go speak with my husband.
Skyrim.esm|0DD60E: Arms and armor! Forged by a master craftsman!
Skyrim.esm|0DD60F: Good business for the East Empire Company means all the more gold for the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0DD610: We bards record history - we do not make it. You'd do well to remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0DD611: Viarmo is too concerned with precision. What of instinct? Hmm? What of passion?
Skyrim.esm|0DD612: I understand what's best for Solitude... and Jarl Elisif knows it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD613: Mind yourself in my city, lass. This here axe in full swing is a right terrible sight...
Skyrim.esm|0DD614: Somethin' just ain't right about that Sybille. You know, I've never seen her eat a thing? That can't be natural.
Skyrim.esm|0DD615: Don't misunderstand me, friend. I like my quiet, and I like to read. But you trifle with my ship, my crew, or my captain? I will kill you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD61F: After that, Ulfric... there will be a reckoning. Count on it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD620: The Thalmor will treat with whatever government rules Skyrim. We would not think of interfering in your civil war.
Skyrim.esm|0DD622: Giving up Markarth is a heavy price for this truce, Dragonborn. I hope it was worth it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD623: Giving up Riften is a heavy price to pay for this truce, Dragonborn. I hope it was worth it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD624: Thank you, Jarl Elisif. I appreciate your loyalty.
Skyrim.esm|0DD625: General, you've proven yourself a good friend to Skyrim. I continue to trust that you will do your utmost to safeguard our interests.
Skyrim.esm|0DD626: I have nothing to say to that murderer.
Skyrim.esm|0DD627: You did well here today. I don't think the truce will last long, but that will not be on your account.
Skyrim.esm|0DD628: I fear this truce will not last. You gave the Empire too much. Ulfric will not let that stand for long.
Skyrim.esm|0DD629: I fear this truce will not last. General Tullius will be looking for any opportunity to regain the advantage.
Skyrim.esm|0DD62A: You had best take advantage of this truce while it lasts.
Skyrim.esm|0DD62B: You should be pleased, Elisif. You've done well for yourself as the Empire's pet Jarl. But beware - the Empire's loyalty is fickle. They will tire of this war, and then I will be the one dictating terms to you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD62C: What about you, Elisif? Are these terms to your liking? Speak up. I'm sure General Tullius is waiting to do your bidding.
Skyrim.esm|0DD638: Make sure to pull your bow all the way back to get the most power out of your shot.
Skyrim.esm|0DD639: If you want to sharpen your blade, use the grindstone. Although you'll need the right raw materials, such as iron or steel.
Skyrim.esm|0DD63A: Want to improve your armor? Use the workbench. Make sure you have the right raw materials, such as iron or steel.
Skyrim.esm|0DD63B: If you want to make new armor or weapons, use the forge. It takes plenty of raw materials though, such as iron or steel.
Skyrim.esm|0DD63C: Before you can enchant your weapon or armor, you'll need to learn an enchantment by sacrificing an enchanted item. Just use the arcane enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|0DD63D: If you know an enchantment and have a filled soul gem, you can enchant armor and weapons. Just use the arcane enchanter.
Skyrim.esm|0DD63E: You can eat an ingredient to learn one of its properties. However, they taste disgusting.
Skyrim.esm|0DD63F: You can make your own potions by mixing up to three ingredients together. Just use the Alchemy Lab.
Skyrim.esm|0DD64E: Yes, my lord. Summon me again, and I shall have your stone.
Skyrim.esm|0DD64F: Yes, my mistress. Summon me again, and I shall have your stone.
Skyrim.esm|0DD650: No, I... I submit. No more. What is your bidding?
Skyrim.esm|0DD651: I... I submit. What is your bidding, master.
Skyrim.esm|0DD652: I am not yours to toy with!
Skyrim.esm|0DD653: Your sigil stone. Lord Dagon is... less than pleased at its loss.
Skyrim.esm|0DD656: Never been to Whiterun before? The Jarl's palace is something to see. Dragonsreach they call it. Big old dragon skull hanging on the wall.
Skyrim.esm|0DD657: If you've never been to Riften, be sure to visit the Black-Briar meadery. A few mugs of that and you'll forget all about the long trip.
Skyrim.esm|0DD658: Ever been to Windhelm? Oldest city in Skyrim, by some accounts. They say the big old palace there was built by Ysgramor himself.
Skyrim.esm|0DD659: When I'm in Whiterun, I always visit the Gildergreen. Never hurts to pay your respects to Lady Kynareth, particularly in my line of work.
Skyrim.esm|0DD65A: You'll want to watch yourself in Riften. A lot of ways to get yourself in trouble in that town.
Skyrim.esm|0DD65B: Windhelm's a bit cold for my tastes. And I ain't talking about the weather. You're not from Windhelm, are you?
Skyrim.esm|0DD68E: His name is Torsten Cruel-Sea. He's really rich, 'cause he owns a farm called Hollyfrost. He told me once that he wanted me to grow up and be a sailor, 'cause that's where our family name comes from. But I don't want to be a sailor or a farmer. I want to be a warrior! The strongest one ever!
Skyrim.esm|0DD68F: His name was Torsten Cruel-Sea. He was really rich, 'cause he owned a whole farm called Hollyfrost. He told me once that he wanted me to grow up and be a sailor, 'cause that's where our family name comes from. But I don't want to be a sailor or a farmer. I want to be a warrior! The strongest one ever!
Skyrim.esm|0DD690: Oh yes. After all, what's the point in being miserable all the time? I know life on the docks isn't easy. Sometimes life puts you in difficult circumstances you didn't choose. But being happy or unhappy is a choice you make, and I've chosen to make the best of things that I can.
Skyrim.esm|0DD691: I've scraped this collection together by whatever means I could. I do some business with the trade caravans, and I've picked up a number of items from adventurers like yourself. A few intrepid souls even brought some items back from Morvunskar, the fort southwest of the city.
Skyrim.esm|0DD692: I make no secret of it. I don't care if the Empire controls the city or not, I won't hide my loyalty to the dream of a free Skyrim. Ulfric fought for that dream with more courage and honor than any Nord who ever lived. But now he's gone, and the dream is gone with him. But I'll be ready when a new leader takes up the cause. As sure as ice is cold and stone is hard, I'll be ready.
Skyrim.esm|0DD693: You've come to the wrong city, then. Windhelm's a haven of prejudice and narrow thinking, unworthy of one such as you.
Skyrim.esm|0DD694: Then we have nothing more to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|0DD695: Do you hate the dark elves? Are you here to bully us and tell us to leave?
Skyrim.esm|0DD696: Ulfric Stormcloak was a true hero of Skyrim. He stood up to the Thalmor and refused to turn his back on Talos.
Skyrim.esm|0DD697: Ulfric was a true hero of Skyrim. He will be missed.
Skyrim.esm|0DD698: I just can't believe that Ulfric is gone. Skyrim will never be free now.
Skyrim.esm|0DD699: Did you know my father? He was really important. He even talked to the Jarl sometimes.
Skyrim.esm|0DD69A: The best way for us to win the Nords' respect is through hard work.
Skyrim.esm|0DD69B: Too many dark elves in Windhelm complain about the way we're treated. What good does complaining do?
Skyrim.esm|0DD69C: I'm proud of my farm. It takes no small effort to grow crops in this part of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0DD69D: I own Hollyfrost Farm outside the city. It's honest work, if a bit dull.
Skyrim.esm|0DD69E: The Cruel-Seas have been sailors for generations. In truth, I regret not following in the footsteps of my ancestors.
Skyrim.esm|0DD69F: If I make enough money from the farm, I'll buy myself a longship to sail the rivers of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0DD751: Now, I don't want to alarm you, but there are bandits in these parts. We're safe up here, of course. Just be careful if you're ever here on foot.
Skyrim.esm|0DD752: Lots of lawless folk around here. Foreigners, mostly. No offense.
Skyrim.esm|0DD753: Old Embershard Mine's up there. Used to produce a lot of iron, but collapsed and taken over by criminals, I hear.
Skyrim.esm|0DD754: Ever taken a swim in the Illinalta? Take care you don't get nipped if you do. Folk say it's teeming with slaughterfish. Who knows what else.
Skyrim.esm|0DD755: See those stones peeking through the surface? Used to be an old Imperial fort on the lake. Really! Stories say it up and sunk one day, but who knows?
Skyrim.esm|0DD756: Thieves used to hole up in Fort Snowhawk there, but they were run out. Wizards, they say. Suits me fine, so long as they mind their own by.
Skyrim.esm|0DD757: Hold your breath if you're superstitious. We're passing Shroud Hearth Barrow. Everyone knows spirits guard the old treasure there.
Skyrim.esm|0DD758: Got keen eyes? Might see nasty old Hajvarr up on White River Watch. Piece of work. Jarl should just run him out and have done with it.
Skyrim.esm|0DD759: Orc town nearby. Between you and me, we should have had them out years ago.
Skyrim.esm|0DD75A: You ever hear the one about the Breton and the bear-tamer?
Skyrim.esm|0DD75C: I used to play in the Yorgrim River when I was just a boy. It's true! I was never afraid of the stories about Forsaken Cave, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0DD75D: I wouldn't wander here alone at night. Folk go missing around Chillwind Depths, the cave just over those falls.
Skyrim.esm|0DDCD9: In my time, the Blades protected the Emperor. It would seem these Penitus Oculatus will prove equally incompetent.
Skyrim.esm|0DDCDA: All hail the savior of the Dark Brotherhood! All Hail the Listener!
Skyrim.esm|0DDCDB: Have you heard the tale of Mathieu Bellamont, and the great treachery of Cheydinhal? Kill a boy's mother, and vengeance festers in the son...
Skyrim.esm|0DDCDC: Shadowmere, my old and dear friend...
Skyrim.esm|0DDCEE: Sharp, aren't they? This was the last thing many saw before they were sacrificed in my name. But a Daedric Lord has his enemies, and my rival Boethiah had her priest desecrate it. So long since it's tasted blood. Until you came.
Skyrim.esm|0DDCEF: The Daedra has us. It's you or me!
Skyrim.esm|0DDCF0: Damn. It's like everyone in this city has amnesia.
Skyrim.esm|0DDCF1: So close. Your prize is waiting.
Skyrim.esm|0DDCF3: Don't know what that man expects. House is empty.
Skyrim.esm|0DDD46: All right. You infiltrate their camp, we'll cover you from the ridge. Good luck!
Skyrim.esm|0DDD4A: All right. You infiltrate their camp, we'll cover you. Good luck!
Skyrim.esm|0DDDA7: There's a sentry patrolling the hill. We'll go in quiet and drop him with a combined arrow barrage. Keep low, fire on my word.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDCD: Good idea. Let me know when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDCE: Stalking your prey, planting false evidence, destroying an innocent man's reputation. Truly a contract worthy of the Listener.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDCF: The Keeper is a sacred position within the Dark Brotherhood. Ask yourself - do you trust the wisdom of our Lady?
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD0: I will kill this jester if you so desire, but there is a disturbance in the Void. Our Dread Father does not wish this.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD1: Killing the Gourmet will leave an emptiness in the collective soul of Skyrim. This pleases me.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD2: Yes. Kill the chef, and then steal his very identity. For that is the true death.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD3: A poison stew, eh? I was always partial to apples, myself.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD4: My time saw the assassination of an Emperor as well. Alas, the Dark Brotherhood did not have the honor of that kill.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD5: We must return to the Sanctuary! Your Family is in dire peril!
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD6: We are the messengers of death, you and I. Together, we will unleash the unholy wrath of our Dread Father, Sithis.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD7: The Sanctuary must be purged of its invading filth!
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD8: The time has come to fulfill your destiny, Listener. Kill the Emperor, and restore the Dark Brotherhood to greatness.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDD9: You stand now at the precipice of the Void. I am reminded of another Listener, a protege I knew long ago. So long ago...
Skyrim.esm|0DDDDA: One day, you will serve our Dread Father as I do now.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDDB: I remember Skyrim from my youth, and the glistening crimson on fields of white.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDDF: We are bonded now, you and I. Joined, through the powers of the Void.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDE0: You know, a good Purification might be just what this Sanctuary needs...
Skyrim.esm|0DDDE1: Dreary, dank, and dimly lit. Truly a Sanctuary to call home.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDE2: In life, I was but a Speaker for the Black Hand. But you have been named Listener. There is no higher honor.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDE3: There is murder in the air. I can taste it.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDE4: The Dread Father works through me. And his work has just begun.
Skyrim.esm|0DDDF4: All right, if you insist. But we'll come running if it sounds like things have gotten out of hand.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE06: It just so happens, I've got some scouts along the road. Meet up with them, and together see if you can't overpower the caravan.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE07: It just so happens, I've got some scouts along the road. Meet up with them, and together see if you can't overpower the caravan.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE3D: We're going to take out their sentry, then sneak into their camp and ambush them.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE3E: We make quite a team, eh? I'll stay here and guard the shipment. You get back to camp with news. Have them send some men - with a new wagon. This one isn't going anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE3F: Hey there! Can you believe this place? So cold my breath turns to icicles! What are we even doing up here? Guess Tullius wants to make sure everything's locked down before launching a serious campaign for Eastmarch. Anyway, I'm glad you're here. We're outnumbered again, more so than when we took that wagon. But if we work together, we can pull this off.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE41: First, we're going to take out their sentry, then we'll situate ourselves overlooking the camp. Next, you'll infiltrate their position and get their attention while we hit them with a barrage of arrows. With a bit of luck, we'll catch them completely off guard and even the odds a little.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE86: Excellent work. I knew you'd bring back something useful.
Skyrim.esm|0DDE87: Good job. I knew you'd come back with something for me.
Skyrim.esm|0DDEDE: See you on the other side, mortal.
Skyrim.esm|0DDF82: Fool! None may know my secret!
Skyrim.esm|0DDF85: I'm just a poor old woman, dear. No need to trouble yourself with me.
Skyrim.esm|0DE14A: There's Treva's Watch. I hear bandits moved in while the steward was off fighting the war. I wonder if he ever got that sorted out?
Skyrim.esm|0DE14B: Between the cliff and Snapleg Cave there, I hate this stretch of road. There's an evil air up here. Getalong, horse.
Skyrim.esm|0DE14C: What's your sign? I was born under the Steed, as luck would have it. Really ought to visit my doomstone somtime. It's fairly near here.
Skyrim.esm|0DE14D: You can see Azura's Shrine for miles on a clear day. You people did a good job with that, I'll say it to anybody.
Skyrim.esm|0DE14E: Say what you will about the dark elves, but they did a proper job with Azura's Shrine. Can see it for miles on a clear day.
Skyrim.esm|0DE14F: Thalmor got their embassy up that way. If I had my way, the lot of those yellowback elves would wake up in a box back to Summerset tomorrow.
Skyrim.esm|0DE150: I'm not asking, but if you happen to be the type, I hear that Kilkreath Ruin is a shrine to Merdia. You know. Daedra worship.
Skyrim.esm|0DE151: Did you spot the Dwarven ruins up there? Mzinfazzle, Finchafft, Mzinchaleft? Good thing the Dwarves disappeared and stopped naming things.
Skyrim.esm|0DE152: We're passing Fort Sungard. Unbelievable view from up there. No wonder the Empire built a fort on that hill way back when.
Skyrim.esm|0DE19C: Yeah, sure, I understand. No need to decide now. But I know that after you think about what you saw today, you'll realize that Skyrim deserves to be free.
Skyrim.esm|0DE19D: I hope so. Skyrim needs people like you to fight for her freedom. Come on, we'd better get moving.
Skyrim.esm|0DE19E: You should come to Windhelm with me and join the fight to free Skyrim. You've seen the true face of the Empire here today. If anyone will know what the coming of the dragon means, it's Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A0: Damn right. Just like all true sons of Skyrim should.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A1: Damn right. You don't have to be a Nord to fight for Skyrim's freedom.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A2: Ulfric's capital is Windhelm, northeast of here. You'll want to talk to Galmar Stone-Fist, Ulfric's right-hand man. He handles the new recruits. I'll be sure to put in a good word if I get to Windhelm ahead of you.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A3: No way to know if anyone else made it out alive. But this place is going to be swarming with Imperials soon enough. We'd better clear out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A5: My sister Gerdur runs the mill in Riverwood, just up the road. I'm sure she'd help you out.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A6: It's probably best if we split up. Good luck. I wouldn't have made it without your help today.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1A7: You know, you should go to Windhelm and join the fight to free Skyrim. You've seen the true face of the Empire here today. If anyone will know what the coming of the dragon means, it's Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1AD: Fine. I offered you a reward. You'll get the freedom your kind enjoys so much. But Boethiah's priest is imprisoned as well. But not by me. He is hurt. Suffering. Save him. Let him perform his rites one more time. And when he does, I will be waiting for him.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1AF: Revenge? No. I want submission. I want the priest who did this to bend his knee and give me his soul. He comes by to perform Boethiah's insulting rites at my altar, but he's been missing. Captured and bound. Left to rot. Save him. Let him perform his rite one more time. And when he does, we will be waiting for him.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1B0: Out the way we came. Hurry!
Skyrim.esm|0DE1B1: We're getting out of here. Now.
Skyrim.esm|0DE1B2: See if you can get the door open.
Skyrim.esm|0DE40A: Good, my clients are gettin' impatient.
Skyrim.esm|0DE40B: Bloody good timin'. Just got some more clients.
Skyrim.esm|0DE40C: All right, anythin' in particular strike your fancy?
Skyrim.esm|0DE40D: 'Bout time. Brynjolf's been gettin' on me about money.
Skyrim.esm|0DE40E: Great. What do you want to do?
Skyrim.esm|0DE411: Got more than you can handle.
Skyrim.esm|0DE412: I bet you are. Which one do you want?
Skyrim.esm|0DE413: Good, we could use the coin.
Skyrim.esm|0DE430: You didn't touch it did you? Well no, of course you must have! Did you attempt to wield it? And you're not dead? Gods, it's a wonder it's in one piece!
Skyrim.esm|0DE431: I think it only fitting that the Arch-Mage personally receive a large portion of the reward. Well done.
Skyrim.esm|0DE432: The dagger...? By Akatosh, they didn't even wrap it correctly?
Skyrim.esm|0DE433: I have the knowledge I need. And so you may have your stone back, and also please take this. You have done well.
Skyrim.esm|0DE434: How in the name of Akatosh... How do you have that?
Skyrim.esm|0DE436: It worked, then? Let me see! Goodness, that's it, isn't it?! Yes! It's not perfect, but it's a close enough approximation that it should certianly suffice for initial tests! You've done excellent work here. Truly excellent. There's only one thing missing now, and that should be arriving shortly. I'll begin preparations. Thank you again so much for this.
Skyrim.esm|0DE437: Ahh, yes. I see it now.
Skyrim.esm|0DE438: I need only look for a few moments...
Skyrim.esm|0DE439: Very good. I'm sure the Jarl will be quite pleased. Pleased enough to justify the significant amount of gold offered to the College to rectify the problem.
Skyrim.esm|0DE43A: Excellent news. Were it to have gotten out of hand, it could have become a major problem for the College.
Skyrim.esm|0DE43C: Mine? Oh no, the stone is yours. I simply need to borrow it a moment... Now, let's see what there is to see.
Skyrim.esm|0DE43D: I do... I do indeed. Keening, one of the great tools of Kagrenac himself, is finally in my possession. I don't think I really believed this day would come.
Skyrim.esm|0DE43E: This is not just a dagger! This is a dwarven artifact beyond nearly all value. A singular instrument of immense power, a tool of impossible import. Keening, and its counterpart, Sunder. Used by the tonal architects of the Dwemer to tap into the Heart of Lorkhan.
Skyrim.esm|0DE43F: Good. One less worry for my people.
Skyrim.esm|0DE452: I'm done for, little cub. Go. Run for it!
Skyrim.esm|0DE45B: Hey, kinsman. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE45C: Hey, dark elf. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE45D: Hey, Breton. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE45E: Hey, Khajiit. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE45F: Hey, Argonian. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE460: Hey, high elf. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE461: Hey, wood elf. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE462: Hey, Redguard. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE463: Hey, Orc. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE464: Hey, you. Get up! Come on, the gods won't give us another chance!
Skyrim.esm|0DE4D7: It seems we may have an agreement.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4D8: What do you suppose this is all about?
Skyrim.esm|0DE4D9: Hold on. This can get a bit tricky.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4DA: We're almost there. Windhelm's just ahead.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4DB: That's Solitude just ahead. Only a few more minutes now.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4DC: Ah, good. Markarth is just ahead. I'm glad to make it through the Reach one more time.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4DD: That's Whiterun up on the hill there. We'll be at the main gate before you know it.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4DE: Just a few more minutes and we'll be in Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4E0: This here's Rorikstead. Quiet place. Good people, though. You can get a room and a mug at the Frostfruit Inn if you care to stay.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4E1: This is Karthwasten. Mining town. I hear they've had a lot of trouble with the Forsworn.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4E2: This here's the Darkwater. Comes down from Lake Honrich into the White River, a few miles downstream. Good fishing, I hear.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4E3: The Braidwood Inn's up that way. I know the innkeeper - Iddra. Nice lady.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4E4: Shor's Stone - pissant little town, not even an inn. Just a bunch of miners scratching in the dirt.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4EB: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Half-moon Mill.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4EC: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Heljarchen.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4ED: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Anga's Mill.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4EE: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Loreius Farm.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4EF: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Whistling Mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F0: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Ivarstead.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F1: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Shor's Stone.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F2: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Heartwood Mill.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F3: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Sarethi Farm.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F4: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Barleydark Farm.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F5: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Riverwood.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F6: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Rorikstead.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F7: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Karthwasten.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F8: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Old Hroldan.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4F9: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Kolskeggr Mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4FA: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Stonehills.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4FB: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Frost River Farm.
Skyrim.esm|0DE4FC: The Empire will pay appropriate compensation for the massacre at Granite Hill.
Skyrim.esm|0DE50D: The Jarl has recognized you as a person of great importance in the hold. A hero. The title of Thane is an honor, a gift for your service. Guards will know to look the other way, if you tell them who you are.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAF0: Ah, you're just in time. I got in a shipment of fine wares courtesy of the East Empire Company.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAF1: Been a while since I've seen you at the meadery. Bring me anything interesting?
Skyrim.esm|0DEAF2: Taking me up on my offer, eh? Good.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAF3: So, you've come to see me to sell or to buy? Hm?
Skyrim.esm|0DEAF9: Glad your here, I've been looking for some more merchandise to buy. Have lots of orders to fill and my clients don't like to wait.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAFA: I'll serve you after you've paid Vex.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAFB: You better straighten things with the Guild or you won't be welcome here anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAFC: Settle your accounts with Vex, and then we can talk.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAFD: Hmph. I don't talk with Guild traitors.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAFE: Pay your dues and we'll speak.
Skyrim.esm|0DEAFF: It's a shame you got booted from the Guild... things were going so well.
Skyrim.esm|0DEB00: Come back when you're in the Guild's good graces.
Skyrim.esm|0DEB01: You want my advice? Pay your debt to Vex quickly and she'll forget the whole thing.
Skyrim.esm|0DEB02: You're in it deep... better start shoveling your way out.
Skyrim.esm|0DEB03: I'm surprised you're even showing your face around here after what you did.
