Skyrim.esm|0DEC69: You drove them off? By yourself? Thank you! We'll head back to work, right away. Take this, it's the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC6A: I never thought I'd see Kolskeggr again.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC89: Go. Your very presence sickens me.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC8A: Be warned. You have been marked by the Thalmor.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC8B: Now remove yourself from my presence, while you still draw breath.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC91: Go now. Return to your pathetic pursuits.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC92: Filthy Nord. In time, your entire race will be eradicated.
Skyrim.esm|0DEC93: The Justiciars know your face. And we elves have long memories...
Skyrim.esm|0DECA0: Back to the swamp with you, Argonian. Lest I carve you into boots.
Skyrim.esm|0DECA1: Crawl back into whatever pit spawned you, Orc.
Skyrim.esm|0DECA5: Slink away now, little Khajiit, with your tail between your legs.
Skyrim.esm|0DECB1: Flee from my sight, Dunmer, or I'll have you shipped back to Vvardenfell in a crate.
Skyrim.esm|0DECC3: You are a disgrace to the Altmer. I should kill you on general principle.
Skyrim.esm|0DECE2: Reason, reason, reason. Your kind and their reasons.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE67: You pledge your soul to me?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE68: Ah ha ha! You mortals and your frail, limp, pathetic bodies. Try it again.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE6A: You forsake the weak and pitiful Boethiah?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE6B: The Dark Mistress? She sent you? Wait. Molag Bal's altar. Of course. I have to get to Markarth at once. Cut me loose.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE6C: The Dark Mistress? Sent you? Ha. Now tell me the one about the Troll who gives gifts to good little boys and girls.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE6E: Ah, freedom. Now get out of my way. I have a task to attend to.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE71: That's all I can do, you idiot. What are you waiting for?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE75: Fine. I won't ask questions. Cut me loose.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE76: You think it's money I need? I'm a prisoner, not a beggar.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE78: So. The King of Corruption sends his messenger to challenge me. Very well. I will meet with Molag Bal, and I will venerate his altar in Boethiah's name, as I did before. Cut me loose, minion of the Hated One. I need to get to Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE79: You? A servant of the King of Corruption? Has the beast's standards fallen so far? Tell me another one.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE85: Depends. Are you thirsty, hungry, both?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE87: Lynly, would you mind taking care of the customer please?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE89: Come on in. Let me know if you need anything, or take a seat by the fire and I'll send someone over.
Skyrim.esm|0DEE8F: How are we doing over here?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE90: You want another one of those?
Skyrim.esm|0DEE91: There's plenty more where that came from. Just give a holler.
Skyrim.esm|0DEED4: If the Guild isn't willing to listen to reason, you might have to.
Skyrim.esm|0DEFE3: Don't kill me.... I don't want to die!
Skyrim.esm|0DF01D: The Jarl needs to know if there's a dragon on the loose. Riverwood is defenseless... We need to get word to Jarl Balgruuf in Whiterun to send whatever troops he can. If you'll do that for me, I'll be in your debt.
Skyrim.esm|0DF01E: You're right, I saw it. But... how's that possible? Dragons don't exist... they're just stories from long ago...
Skyrim.esm|0DF01F: I don't know why... but I actually believe you. You've got the look of someone who's just seen a dragon. Things just go from bad to worse. First the war, now dragons... what's this world coming to?
Skyrim.esm|0DF020: I'm sorry to turn you away. I hope you understand. But look... if you need work, Hod can always use help at the mill.
Skyrim.esm|0DF021: A dragon? In Helgen? It can't be... although... It would explain what I saw earlier... flying down the valley from the south... I thought I must have just been seeing things...
Skyrim.esm|0DF022: Of course. Take what you need.
Skyrim.esm|0DF023: There's something you could do for me. For all of us here.
Skyrim.esm|0DF02F: Purposely killing your fellow College members is a bad idea. I'd advise against it. Theft and assault can get you into trouble as well. As for research... Well, a sufficient explanation will cover almost anything. If you're going to need live test subjects for your experiments, it's easier to do that off of College grounds.
Skyrim.esm|0DF031: Purpose? I should think it's self-evident. Magic is a true power, not something to be shunned by commoners or treated as an amusing diversion by politicians. It shapes worlds, creates and destroys life... It deserves proper respect and study. The College is a place where we can focus on that, without the pressures of the world weighing down on us.
Skyrim.esm|0DF032: The Sea of Ghosts practically came alive. No one was expecting it. Monstrous waves battered the shore for weeks on end. Winterhold was ancient and weathered, but it couldn't withstand the sea's fury. Entire districts of the city were lost overnight. The waves receded in time, but the damage was irreversible. Most residents of Winterhold abandoned what was left of the city. The College survived, and so here we remain.
Skyrim.esm|0DF034: I'm here to assist anyone interested in joining the College. Also, my presence tends to frighten the locals and keep them away.
Skyrim.esm|0DF035: The Great Collapse, as it has come to be known. An unfortunate natural disaster that ravaged the area. The College fared far better than the city of Winterhold, but it was not left untouched.
Skyrim.esm|0DF036: No one is sure of the cause. Some believed the eruption of Red Mountain had far-reaching consequences that were only felt years later. I know there are some who have blamed the College, said that we were responsible. I assure you this is not the case.
Skyrim.esm|0DF045: Years ago, the Guild used to have a foothold in every major city in Skyrim. You wouldn't dare even lift an apple without checkin' with us. When things started goin' downhill around here, it became difficult to keep it all together. We lost fences, influential contacts and coin. It wasn't long before we lost what we depend on to survive... respect. By doin' these extra jobs and puttin' some fear into the people, we can take back the cities and start bein' taken seriously once again.
Skyrim.esm|0DF046: Are you kidding me? Ever since the Guild's luck turned sour, we haven't had a coin to our name. And when the coin dried up, that's when people started to leave. We had the best of everything down here... the Ratway was a damn palace. The only way this place will ever return to its glory days is if we can finish these extra jobs and start the gold flowing again.
Skyrim.esm|0DF047: Yes. Now that you're a Nightingale, you may consider this your home. You'll find that this place offers many things that will help you in your endeavors as well as a wealth of information for you to learn. Once the Skeleton Key has been restored to the Twilight Sepulcher, I'll make this place my home as well.
Skyrim.esm|0DF048: With the Skeleton Key missing from the Twilight Sepulcher, I'm afraid Mercer's seen to it that none of us can benefit from Nocturnal's gifts.
Skyrim.esm|0DF049: If you would have asked me that yesterday, I'd have said no. But now I think our chances have improved. Look, call me crazy if you like, but I trust Karliah. I don't think she'd lead us down a suicidal path. Besides, I'd rather die with some of Mercer's blood on my blade than spend the rest of my life regretting that I ran the other way.
Skyrim.esm|0DF04A: Aye, and some of what Karliah said is starting to make sense. Mercer may have damaged our reputation and raided our coffers, but this goes well beyond even his twisted form of larceny. Old Delvin kept calling it a curse and we all laughed at him. Looks like the joke's on us.
Skyrim.esm|0DF04B: The Flagon was once a city beneath a city. We had our own smith, our own alchemist... you name it. If we can make a name for ourselves in Skyrim once again, I can almost promise you those merchants would return. Best of all, we'd have enough gold to throw around so we can start living in the lap of luxury again.
Skyrim.esm|0DF04C: Thanks to Maven Black-Briar, we still have some pull in Riften. But get arrested in Whiterun and you'll be tossed right into the prisons. If we can gain the confidence of someone very influential who lives there by doing a unique job for them, we'd be able to have some leverage there too. The only way to get that special job is by doin' smaller ones in those cities until we catch their ear... then they'll contact me and we're off.
Skyrim.esm|0DF04D: If Nocturnal was truly displeased with me... with any of us, she wouldn't have answered my call. I have no doubt that we still hold her favor and I believe it gives us enough of an edge to defeat Mercer Frey.
Skyrim.esm|0DF04E: You merely transacted the Oath; signed the unwritten contract with Nocturnal. In order for us to receive our abilities... our end of the bargain, I'm afraid the Key must be returned.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1C9: A true killer, like me. The gods put us here to fill their halls with souls. You'll fit in fine down here.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1CA: Bottles of Moon Sugar. Khajiit use it as a... pick me up. Good way to pass the time. Prisoners smuggle the stuff in. Only currency we have down here.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1CB: You think you're the only one who knows that? We had a nice little deal going between Thonar and Madanach until you showed up. You'll have plenty of time to take it up with the King in Rags while you're serving a life sentence in Cidhna Mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1CC: All right, head on in. But don't try anything in there. Madanach is smarter than you think.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1CD: That's right. Turn around and leave.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1CE: You want to talk to the King in Rags? Fine. But first you got to pay the toll. How about you get me a shiv? Not that I need one, but it's nice to have in case I need to do some "shaving." Ha ha.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1CF: Same thing we do with all the other natives who want to change things around here. We had a nice little deal going between Thonar and Madanach until you and Eltrys started snooping around. Well, you wanted to find the man responsible for those killings? You'll have plenty of time with the King in Rags when you're in Cidhna mine.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D1: The new meat. So soft. Tender. What was it like killing your first one, huh?
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D2: Hmph. Fine. Go on in. But don't try anything in there. Madanach knows more than you think.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D3: The only thing Madanach expects is a bottle of Skooma in tribute every so often. You're not getting through.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D4: We have a nice arrangement in this city, and we're not letting you get in the way. You have a problem wth that? Take it up with Madanach. I'm sure the King in Rags and his Forsworn would love to meet you. Now you're coming with us. It's a life sentence in Cidhna Mine for you.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D5: Pah. The gods have a place for killers. You can't carry the burden? You're weak.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D6: Here. I hope you choke on it.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D7: You win. On my honor, Carlotta won't have to worry about me ever again.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D8: Those eyes. All right, killer, go on through. But don't try anything. Madanach knows more than you think.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1D9: That's what I was hoping you'd say.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F2: I suppose she's Jarl Elisif now. She married High King Torygg just before Ulfric killed him. The Empire supports her claim to be High Queen. I don't really have anything against her - not her fault that her husband Torygg was bought and paid for by the Empire. But she's nothing but a puppet for the Empire now, with her husband Torygg dead. Ulfric will make sure she never takes the throne as High Queen.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F3: Of course. Ulfric's cause is just. It's time for Skyrim to rid itself of the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F4: I wouldn't say that. But he and Ulfric have been at odds for years, and I'm afraid Balgruuf will end up siding with the Empire because of it. But it's hard to believe that even Balgruuf would choose Elisif over Ulfric.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F5: There's no time to stand on ceremony, my friend. I need your help again. I want you to go with Irileth and help her fight this dragon. You survived Helgen, so you have more experience with dragons than anyone else here.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F6: Jarl Balgruuf? I don't mean to be disrespectful, as he's ruled Whiterun Hold well for years, but he seems in over his head now. He's been trying to stay out of the war, but it can't last. He's going to have to pick a side. I'm afraid he's going to make the wrong choice.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F7: Gerdur? Owns the lumber mill, if I'm not mistaken. Pillar of the community. Not prone to flights of fancy... And you're sure Helgen was destroyed by a dragon? This wasn't some Stormcloak raid gone wrong?
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F8: But I haven't forgotten the service you did for me in retrieving the Dragonstone for Farengar. As a token of my esteem, I have instructed Avenicci that you are now permitted to purchase property in the city. And please accept this gift from my personal armory.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1F9: The Jarl will definitely want to talk to you. You'll find him in Dragonsreach, at the top of the hill.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1FA: No, not until the Moot meets to choose another. And it won't meet until one side or the other wins the war. Don't worry, though. Ulfric is our rightful High King. He'll drive out the Empire and Skyrim will have peace at last.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1FB: Brother! Mara's mercy, you're safe! Your friend told me about Helgen...
Skyrim.esm|0DF1FC: When your friend told me, I didn't believe it at first either.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1FD: Some say murdered, but it was a lawful challenge in the old way. Ulfric called him out as a traitor to Skyrim, and killed him in single combat. If Torygg couldn't defend his throne, he had no business being High King.
Skyrim.esm|0DF1FE: But is it safe for you to be here? Won't the Imperials be looking for you?
Skyrim.esm|0DF791: Come on. Give me your hand, I'm getting you out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0DF794: Tell my family I fought bravely.
Skyrim.esm|0DF797: It's too late for that. Leave me. Save yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0DF7A4: The wounds are deep, Vilod! I can't stop the blood.
Skyrim.esm|0DF7A7: See the inn on the other side? Jump through the roof and keep going!
Skyrim.esm|0DF8B5: Not just ordinary gems. They're said to be flawlessly cut and as big as a man's head. Can you imagine how much they're worth? Gallus and Mercer spent the better part of a month infiltrating Irkngthand, but the dwarves had protected the place far too well. There were just too many obstacles blocking the way. The plans were shelved and the rest is history.
Skyrim.esm|0DF8B6: Mercer's been careful so far. I don't think he'd just leave those plans behind unless he had his reasons. For someone in possession of the Skeleton Key, stealing the Eyes of the Falmer would be child's play. No, he means to ambush us down here, I'm almost certain of it.
Skyrim.esm|0DF8B7: The lass seems to think old Mercer is pulling a fast one on us... leading us here and letting the dwarven constructs wear us down. I've learned to trust her lead at this point. After all, we Nightingales need to stick together, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0DF8B8: Aye, that's what the Falmer were known as long ago... before they became the blind monstrosities they are today. When Gallus took one look at this statue, he knew it was something special. He took it right up to Enthir at the College of Winterhold. Didn't take long for Enthir to find a book in the college's library that told of Irkngthand and a great statue with gemmed eyes within.
Skyrim.esm|0DF8B9: A few years before Mercer murdered Gallus, the Guild took in a thief who specialized in dwarven antiquities. The thief had broken into a nobleman's home somewhere in Windhelm and made off with a small figurine of a snow elf with crystalline eyes.
Skyrim.esm|0DF8C9: *Gasp* Finally, someone came!... I... *coughs* The cat... *gasp* Ra'jiir... ambushed me. He's trying to take the sword back! I... can't... *gasp*
Skyrim.esm|0DFB08: Morthal, capital of Hjaalmarch hold. Not quite in the same league as Solitude or Whiterun, but a nice enough place if you like swamps.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB09: Here we are. Dawnstar. Capital of the Pale. Don't get up this way much. Beautiful country though.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB0A: I can't say I've been to Winterhold more than a handful of times. Can't see much reason most honest folk would want to come up here.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB0B: Well, this is Falkreath. Not much to look at if you're used to the bigger cities, but the folk here are friendly enough if you give them a chance.

Skyrim.esm|0DFB1F: Solitude's a big port town, you know? Full of sailors and their tales of far away lands. Most of 'em's foreigners and liars, of course.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB20: If you don't mind a piece of advice, I'd stay out of trouble in Markarth. They'll toss you in the Cidhna Mine, and you'll never get out.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB21: I might have to stop and get myself a pint of Black-Briar mead while I'm in Riften. Almost worth the trip all by itself.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB22: You might run into some of them lizard-folk in Riften. Quite a few of them work the docks there, not sure why. Like swimming in the lake, maybe.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB23: You'll find Windhelm's full of dark elves. The kind from Morrowind, you know. Not that I have anything against them, long as they keep to themselves.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB24: You ever met one of them cats? - kah-jeet, I think they call themselves. I hear there's whole countries full of them down south.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB25: It's almost getting downright hot out now.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB27: This rain will stop eventually. Always does.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB28: Hope you're not getting too wet back there.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB29: I bet you'll be glad to get to Whiterun and get out of this weather.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB2A: You picked quite a day for a trip to Solitude.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB2B: You picked quite a day for a trip to Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB2C: I swear it rains like this every time I go to Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0DFB2D: Better get used to it. Snows most all the time in Windhelm.
Skyrim.esm|0DFE89: A trick of the dark perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|0DFEA2: You can't hide from the hidden.
Skyrim.esm|0DFEC7: The games have ended, time to face your death!
Skyrim.esm|0DFEC8: Come mortal, let's end this here and now!
Skyrim.esm|0DFEE6: I've failed... the mortal has escaped.
Skyrim.esm|0DFEE9: I feel a change in the room...
Skyrim.esm|0DFEEA: The presence of the living is near...
Skyrim.esm|0DFF24: This place will be your tomb!
Skyrim.esm|0DFF26: Nocturnal will feed upon your soul!
Skyrim.esm|0DFF66: Defend the Sepulcher at all costs!
Skyrim.esm|0E0233: Let the darkness rip you asunder!
Skyrim.esm|0E0250: You've breathed your last breath, mortal!
Skyrim.esm|0E0251: Defend the Ebonmere! Kill the mortal!
Skyrim.esm|0E0252: I cast shadows across your grave!
Skyrim.esm|0E05D0: I have eternity to find you.
Skyrim.esm|0E07B1: Another soul claimed for Lady Nocturnal!
Skyrim.esm|0E07B2: Glory to the Empress of Murk!
Skyrim.esm|0E0B87: Oh, no. I'm no fool. You'll learn what you need to know once we've come to an agreement.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B88: Oh, no. I won't tell you anything more until we have an agreement.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B89: Let me know when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B8A: I'd like to offer you a place in my court, as a Thane. It's mainly an honorary title, but there are a few perks. Now, by law and tradition, I can only grant the title to one who is known throughout Eastmarch, and who owns property in Windhelm. If you do good works for the people and purchase a home from my steward, I'll name you my Thane.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B8B: Well I... I suppose I'm not left much choice in the matter, am I? Very well. I trust a hefty purse of septims will suffice?
Skyrim.esm|0E0B8C: Well I... I suppose I'm not left much choice in the matter, am I? Very well. I trust a hefty purse of septims will suffice?
Skyrim.esm|0E0B8E: What if I told you about a large shipment of silver and weapons?
Skyrim.esm|0E0B91: They're taking it by wagon to Windhelm. If you hurry, you'll catch them before they get far. It'll be a fairly slow moving caravan. The shipment is quite heavy, and guarded by many men. Now, let's pretend we never had this discussion.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B92: They're taking it by wagon to Solitude. If you hurry, you'll catch them before they get far. It'll be a fairly slow moving caravan. The shipment is quite heavy, and guarded by many men. Now, let's pretend we never had this discussion.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B93: Not here. Come with me. Stay close and the guards will leave you alone.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B94: Enough to make a significant difference in the war.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B95: Enough to make a significant difference in the war.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B96: It would ruin me. I have a good thing going here. The Jarl benefits from my more creative arrangements as well, though it'd be impossible for her to admit that. Laila is a simple and traditional woman. Which makes it easy to manipulate her, but impossible to regain her graces once offended. Look. Perhaps we could come to some kind of agreement?
Skyrim.esm|0E0B99: The Thalmor would need to make an example of me... I'd be thrown into prison, but our family's honor would be stained for generations. I'm the Jarl's uncle. He, his father, and I, swore oaths to the Empire to abandon Talos, as conditions of our return to the city.
Skyrim.esm|0E0B9A: There's a sentry patrolling the area. We'll go in quiet and drop him with a combined arrow barrage. Keep low, fire on my word.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C37: I really do need you to stay in place, please. I don't want to risk hurting any of the other Apprentices.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C38: Over there, please. Just opposite me. Wouldn't want anyone else in the way.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C39: Yes, I thought we'd gotten past that bit already. Onmund's amulet in exchange for my staff.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C3A: The existence of so-called "Doomstones" throughout Skyrim has been repeatedly verified. The meaning of these stones has not.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C3B: The prevailing opinion of Skyrim natives is that the stones are indeed magical in nature.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C3C: While there is no direct evidence of this, it does seem likely.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C3D: References to similar stones appear in lore throughout the various Tamrielic cultures.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C3E: None, however, exactly match the markings or distribution of Skyrim's stones.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C3F: At present, there is no confirmation of any of the various theories surrounding the nature of these stones.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C40: Their relative positions do not indicate that any individual stone is part of a larger, unobserved pattern.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C41: Also, their placement throughout Skyrim does not correspond to any known magical phenomenon.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C42: The age of the stones themselves has yet to be officially determined. It has been widely assumed that they were placed during the Merethic Era.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C43: Writings from that period, including those of Ysgramor himself, do not mention the stones and thus this idea cannot be verified.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C44: Nonetheless, many are drawn to these stones based on the local stories describing them as a source of significant power.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C45: The College will continue to research these intriguing objects, and of course any findings will be relayed with all possible haste.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C46: It is no secret that both the Synod and the College of Whispers have recently made inquiries as to the status of our College here in Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C47: At this time, there is no indication that either group is aware of the other's correspondence.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C48: The College of Winterhold has thus far declined requests for direct meetings. This has been at the specific request of Arch-Mage Aren.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C49: Aren believed that although the initial communications were innocent enough, they were sent with a particular motive in mind.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C4B: The Synod's harsh rules and draconian structure are maintained only by suppressing any opposition to their Council's policies.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C4C: It is entirely possible that they look to our College here in Winterhold in order to find supporters for their organization.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C4D: Likewise, the College of Whispers has long been driven by its desire to directly oppose the Synod.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C4E: They focus on research banned by the Synod, such as Conjuration and Necromancy.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C4F: The College of Whispers hopes to learn that our College also supports these avenues of research.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C50: Thus they may claim that the Synod is indeed a political minority in the Empire and should be treated as such.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C51: Our actual position and policies are irrelevant. No matter the facts of the response, it will certainly be twisted to suit the whims of either group.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C52: Indeed, it has been jokingly suggested that we send the exact same response to both, which each will warp into support for their side.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C53: At present, these two groups do little beyond attempting to gain the attention and favor of the Emperor.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C54: They appear to have little interest in real study and research for the sake of gaining knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C55: Arch-Mage Aren believes that their conflict poses a significant threat to the autonomy of our College, and I concur.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C56: Arch-Mage Aren believed that their conflict poses a significant threat to the autonomy of our College, and I concur.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C57: Falling in with either would threaten to draw much unwanted attention to our College.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C58: If either group goes through less official channels and attempt to contact you directly, please refer them to the College's Master Wizard.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C59: Say as little as possible so as to avoid compromising our neutral position.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C5A: I would just like to remind everyone, once again, that Restoration is indeed a valid school of magic.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C5B: It is absolutely worthy of research, despite many of the notes I've had left in my bed. And my desk. And on occasion, my meals.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C5C: Anyone suggesting that Restoration is better left to the priests of the Temples, I think, is forgetting a few things.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C5D: Firstly, the ability to repel the undead cannot be ignored.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C5E: Skyrim is well known to be full of these... Draugr, ancient Nord warriors who cannot find peace.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C5F: I submit that everyone in this College has, at one time or another, relied on one of the Restoration spells that can keep them at bay.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C60: Secondly, how can anyone forget wards? They have become essential to any mage working in dangerous situations.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C61: They are counted upon every bit as much as Candlelight, or Invisibility.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C62: But more importantly, wards have saved lives. This is a simple fact.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C63: Every mage in this College regularly uses wards for practice, so as to avoid physical harm.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C64: I truly hope that these points actually sink in, and that more care and thought is given to this subject in the future. Thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C6E: Dragonborn, Dragonborn, by his honor is sworn, To keep evil forever at bay!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C6F: And the fiercest foes rout when they hear triumph's shout, Dragonborn, for your blessing we pray!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C70: Hearken now, sons of snow, to an age, long ago, and the tale, boldly told, of the one!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C71: Who was kin to both wyrm, and the races of man, with a power to rival the sun!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C72: And the Voice, he did wield, on that glorious field, when great Tamriel shuddered with war!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C73: Mighty Thua??um, like a blade, cut through enemies all, as the Dragonborn issued his roar!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C74: And the Scrolls have foretold, of black wings in the cold, that when brothers wage war come unfurled!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C75: Alduin, Bane of Kings, ancient shadow unbound, with a hunger to swallow the world!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C76: But a day, shall arise, when the dark dragon's lies, will be silenced forever and then!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C77: Fair Skyrim will be free from foul Alduin's maw, Dragonborn be the savior of men!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C78: Dovahkiin, Dovahkiin, naal ok zin los vahriin, wah dein vokul mahfaeraak ahst vaal!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C79: Ahrk fin norok paal graan fod nust hon zindro zaan, Dovahkiin, fah hin kogaan mu draal!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C7A: Huzrah nu, kul do od, wah aan bok lingrah vod, Aahrk fin tey, boziik fun, do fin gein!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C7B: Wo lost fron wah ney dov, ahrk fin reyliik do jul, voth aan suleyk wah ronit faal krein!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C7C: Ahrk fin zul, rok drey kod, nau tol morokei frod, rul lot Taazokaan motaad voth kein!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C7D: Sahrot Thu'um, med aan tuz, vey zeim hokoron pah, ol fin Dovahkiin komeyt ok rein!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C7E: Ahrk fin Kel lost prodah, do ved viing ko fin krah, tol fod zeymah win kein meyz fundein!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C7F: Alduin, feyn do jun, kruziik vokun staadnau, voth aan bahlok wah diivon fin lein!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C80: Nuz aan sul, fent alok, fod fin vul dovah nok, fen kos nahlot mahfaeraak ahrk ruz!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C81: Paaz Keizaal fen kos stin nol bein Alduin jot, Dovahkiin kos fin saviik do muz!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C85: Ah, yes. Solitude. Seat of the High King of Skyrim. Messy business, kingslaying. Ah, but so very satisfying...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C86: Windhelm. White as bone, and cold as the Void.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C87: I do so love Riften. I journeyed here in my youth. A thief took my purse... so I took his eyes. It was a fair exchange.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C88: Whiterun, home of the Skyforge. It is said a blade forged in its fires can cut through sinew as if it were parchment.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C89: An ancient city, Markarth. Build by the Dwemer, the dwarves of old. To think, an entire race... eradicated. Not even I can take credit for that feat.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C8A: Do you feel it? Magic. Deep and ancient. It resonates from the College, like the beating of a heart.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C8B: I grow restless. As does my blade.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C8C: Your enemies will soon know the wrath of Sithis.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C8D: Lead, and I will follow, child of darkness.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C8E: Perhaps we should find a random stranger to murder. Practice does make perfect.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C8F: He he he he he he he he he he.
Skyrim.esm|0E0C90: ...and if I spy a singing bird, I'll snap its neck before it's heard...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C91: ...oh if I chance to see a cat, I'll feed its corpse to my pet rat...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C92: ...when I next meet, that fair maid Nelly, I'll plunge my knife into her belly...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C93: ...tra la la, tra la lee, da da dum dum, dee dee...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C94: ...What? Mother? Is that your voice I hear? Hmm... No, no... Just my head playing tricks... Foolish Cicero...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C95: Our sweet Lady is maiden, mother... and crone...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C96: ...need to sharpen my blade... make it shiny, gleamy, and oh so deadly...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C97: ...dear Cicero will keep you from harm, sweet Mother. Forever and always...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C98: ...is there singing in the Void? Dancing...? Surely the Dread Lord will at least allow poor Cicero to caper...
Skyrim.esm|0E0C9E: I won't let you hurt Alva!
Skyrim.esm|0E0C9F: Now I'm going to have to kill you!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA0: I shall sing of thee in Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA1: Feel the bite of my axe!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA2: It is glorious to battle once again in Tamriel!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA3: Feel the thunder of my voice!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA4: You will die for the glory of the Dragonborn!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA5: You are not worthy of Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA8: My sword will taste of your blood!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CA9: Suddenly I don't feel like fighting.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CAB: I really don't want to fight.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CAC: I see no reason to keep fighting.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CAE: I'm not interested in fighting anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CAF: I see no reason to fight.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CBB: How can I argue with that?
Skyrim.esm|0E0CBC: Okay, okay. You've talked me into it.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CC0: A little coin always greases the wheels.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CC1: Gold solves most problems, doesn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0E0CC3: You expect me to believe that?
Skyrim.esm|0E0CC6: I'll teach you to talk to me that way!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CC7: Hope you're willing to back those words up with your fists!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CC8: I don't have to take that from you!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CCB: I answer the call of the Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF1: Now... Now we see whether my theories are correct.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF3: But please, don't leave. You've been instrumental in this process. I'd like you to see the results first-hand.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF4: Well, I suppose it's time, isn't it? Let's see what happens. You, uhh... You may want to stand back a step or two.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF5: I certainly don't expect it to have quite the same results. I'm no tonal architect, of course!
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF6: I also lack Sunder, the counterpart to the dagger Keening. I am reasonably, confident, however, that this will still work.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF7: Lacking the heart of a dead god, I am substituting the crystal you helped craft in its place.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CF9: This little experiment is a first step in recreating the events of their disappearance in an effort to unravel that mystery.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CFA: I don't understand. This should produce some notable effect.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CFB: Everyone knows the dwarves disappeared. No one knows why.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CFD: I apologize if I have not been clear about my work. I didn't want to discuss it for fear this moment might never come.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CFE: The mages don't let us go in the College. They said it's not safe in there.
Skyrim.esm|0E0CFF: How come you're in Winterhold? My pa says people like you are the reason no one lives here anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D00: Some people can't be trusted to accomplish anything that's asked of them. They don't take pride in their work.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D01: I won't complain about Korir being gone, but I don't expect things to really be any different.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D02: If there's anything you need, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D03: I'd rather see the Sea of Ghosts swallow Winterhold entirely than have it run by the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D04: Ours is the only family left that truly cares what happens to Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D05: It wasn't enough that the College still stands, now the damnable Empire has Winterhold. I hope they all freeze to death.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D06: I don't care how many Colleges they build, or how much the sea swallows up. I'll outlast them all.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D07: Unless you're bringin' me another round, you can just keep walking.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D08: Hmm. That... That didn't really do anything, did it?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D0D: He he he... Crazy? Cicero? He he he he... That's madness...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D0E: ...ho ho ho, and hee hee hee, break that lute across my knee... and if the bard, should choose to fight, why then I'll set his clothes alight...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D0F: ...and I said to the baker, "You're not dead! You're a faker!" But if that's your wish, I'll oblige...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D10: ...and he says to the man, "That's not a horker! That's my wife!" Ha ha ha ha ha... Ah... I love that one...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D11: ...madness is merry, and merriment's might, when the jester comes calling with his knife in the night...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D13: ...Cicero is hungry... ...need a sweetroll... or a carrot...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D14: ...need to get Mother some flowers... pretty, pretty flowers...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D15: Riften. Cicero likes Riften. Cheats and ruffians and cutthroats abound. Finally, some fun.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D16: ...Cicero never understood thieves, really... take someone's things before you kill them? And they call me crazy...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D18: Oohhh... Cicero's heard about the Butcher. Interesting knifework. Just... Stab, stab, stab, stab, stab! And then... Stab, stab, stab, stab, stab!
Skyrim.esm|0E0D19: Hmph. "Solitude." Lonely Cicero could tell you a thing or two about solitude...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D1A: ...the High King's court needs a jester... But not me. No, not Cicero. The Fool of Hearts is busy enough, thank you very much...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D1B: ...I saw a dwarf! I did! I did! Cicero saw a dwarf! There... Oh. No, sorry. No dwarf...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D1C: ...Forsworn think they're so scary... Cicero will show you who's scary... Stupid Forsworn...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D1D: ...Oooh... Cicero wants some Skyforge Steel! Sharp sharp, for easy stabbing...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D1E: Oh! Oh! Maybe Cicero will go to Jorrvaskr and dance for the Companions! They'll... Um... On second thought... maybe not.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D1F: ...must oil Mother soon... ...get all the hard to reach places...
Skyrim.esm|0E0D2C: I only just got my hands on it. I can only work so quickly.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D2D: It's coming along slowly. I expect there will be some results soon.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D2E: We were able to figure out the last text you brought in. I thought the least I could do is pass the knowledge along.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D2F: Hjaalmarch isn't much, but it's a good start. I'll make a name for myself here, then maybe work my way up to someplace like Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D30: Finally, my leadership is being recognized.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D31: It's good that Sorli has finally been given some real authority.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D32: I'll see to it that Sorli is safe now that she's running Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D33: We left Sirgar in charge back at Stonehills. I hope that wasn't a mistake.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D34: I figured that I would run the mine someday. I just didn't figure that would mean now.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D35: Guess the Empire's going to need plenty of iron what with all the fighting. That'll be good for us, I suppose.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D36: Talk to my parents if you need something.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D37: Dark times, indeed. I fear the worst for Morthal and my people.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D38: I try and take comfort in knowing that when all is lost, there is much to be gained.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D39: It is my hope that I will one day return to Hjaalmarch. But the Divines have not shown this to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D3A: The Empire brought stability, assurance to the people of Morthal, even if they didn't appreciate it. Now what do they have?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D45: I asked the gate guards to look for him. I can't afford to chase him down but could use a fierce, loyal beast to keep me company.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D46: Did you see a Dog out there?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D47: Good! There's some gold in it for you if you succeed. Here's the meat - check the road just outside of town.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D48: You see, my name is Barbas. And I have a problem I think you can help sort out.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D49: I don't know really.  The blacksmith is offering a reward for a dog he saw on the road. I was hoping you'd seen it.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D4A: If you were willing to retrieve him for me I'd give you some fresh meat to attract him out on the road.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D4B: Ah, well. The blacksmith is offering a reward for a dog he saw on the road. I was hoping you'd seen it.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D4C: No, this was a specific one. The blacksmith is offering a reward for a dog he saw on the road. I was hoping you'd seen it.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D4D: No, no, this one's alone on the road outside of town. I can't afford to chase him down but I could use a fierce, loyal beast to keep me company.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D4E: I guess I'll stay on the lookout. Keep your nose clean while you're here outsider.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D59: Solitude wouldn't be the same without the Fire Festival. City owes you a debt of gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D5A: You're the one saved the Fire Festival. I remember.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D5C: May the shroud of death embrace you.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D5E: Remember, fireballs. You can never go wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D5F: Go, incinerate some poor bastard for me.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D60: Go. Do what has to be done.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D62: Can't wait to hear all about the next person you murder.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D63: Nobody ever expects a fireball from the darkened corner.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D64: My targets make the mistake of not perceiving me as a threat. Last mistake they ever make.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D65: What do you want, beef roast?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D74: I fear for what this change will do to my family.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D75: If you've business with the Jarl, I'd ask that you speak to me first.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D76: Leave Idgrod and her family alone. They've been through enough.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D7E: This wasn't what I wanted. Not at all.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D8B: All that ever mattered was what was best for Morthal. Leaving it in Ulfric's hands... It's not right.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D95: To be driven from our homes, to be forced here... this is the last thing that Joric needs.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D96: So, my fellow beast, what do you want? Answers about the Forsworn? Revenge for trying to have you killed?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D97: Fralia, do you ever deal with the Khajiit at all? I'm sure they would love to trade in your husband's steel.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D98: Owning a farm does have its advantages.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D99: Are you still working at the Bannered Mare in the evenings, Olfina?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D9A: And why shouldn't I? Just because I'm a Gray-Mane doesn't mean I can't earn my own keep.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D9B: Your freedom? Yes. But even if you were to escape Cidhna Mine, your name would still be stained with all that blood.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D9C: Ah, Ysolda was it? What brings you to the market?
Skyrim.esm|0E0D9D: I'm here to buy food, Nazeem. I suppose you don't need to worry about that.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D9E: Plenty of people here that need Eorlund's craftsmanship, my dear. We've never had any reason to deal with those caravans of theirs.
Skyrim.esm|0E0D9F: I didn't mean... I'm sorry I asked.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DA0: Well, well. Look at you. Your kinsmen have turned you into an animal, Nord. A wild beast caged up and left to go mad.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DA1: Well, well. Look at you. The Nords have turned you into an animal. A wild beast caged up and left to go mad.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DB0: I keep hoping this is another vision, that it's not real. But it is, isn't it? We really have lost our home.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DCA: I can't sleep here. The dreams are even worse than before.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DCB: I get lost sometimes. I'm not sure where I go, but I'm not... I'm not here.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DD6: My talents are much needed here, in order to keep Morthal... safe.
Skyrim.esm|0E0DE6: The mill is my responsibility, and that's what I care about. That's all. Understand?
Skyrim.esm|0E0E02: Just that, I'm afraid. You lack the ability, in any school of magic, to contribute meaningfully to the College's research. I suggest you learn more on your own. Come back once you've attained some measure of success.
Skyrim.esm|0E0E03: It would seem that you have yet to reach a level of skill that the College would find valuable, so I'm afraid I can't allow you to join at this time.
Skyrim.esm|0E0E54: Your target's name is Vittoria Vici. She oversees the East Empire Company's business holdings in Solitude. The wedding is being held in that city, at the Temple of the Divines. Her death will cause an uproar, which is exactly what we want. Vici is likely to address her guests frequently, as is the wedding custom. Kill her when she does that, and I promise you a significant bonus. Now go. And give the city of Solitude a celebration they're not likely to forget.
Skyrim.esm|0E0E55: Very well. The target's name is Vittoria Vici. She oversees the East Empire Company's business holdings in Solitude. The wedding is being held in that city, at the Temple of the Divines. Her death will cause an uproar, which is exactly what we want. Remember, we want people to notice. Kill Vici while she's addressing her guests, as is the custom, and I can promise a bonus. Now go. And give the bride a special kiss, from me.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EB7: I, ah, learned of a certain stone tablet said to be housed in Bleak Falls Barrow - a "Dragonstone," said to contain a map of dragon burial sites. Go to Bleak Falls Barrow, find this tablet - no doubt interred in the main chamber - and bring it to me. Simplicity itself.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EB8: By my right as Jarl, I name you Thane of Whiterun. It's the greatest honor that's within my power to grant. I assign you Lydia as a personal Housecarl, and this weapon from my armory to serve as your badge of office. I'll also notify my guards of your new title. Wouldn't want them to think you're part of the common rabble, now would we? We are honored to have you as Thane of our city, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EB9: Ah! The Dragonstone of Bleak Falls Barrow! Seems you are a cut above the usual brutes the Jarl sends my way.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EBB: You've done a great service for me and my city, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0E0ED8: Malacath blesses this land for the Orcs. And the Orcs alone.
Skyrim.esm|0E0ED9: We won't kill you, outlander, but we don't want you here.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EE4: Trust you saw the house that burned down. Peculiar bit of business, that was. And that's on top of noises from the marsh in the night, tales of monsters, and now this wizard in our midst. What's a man to do if he can't look to his Jarl for help?
Skyrim.esm|0E0EE5: You mean the inn, or Winterhold? Suppose it's the same answer, either way. Winterhold's fallen on hard times, to say the least. Most folk packed up and left years ago. A few of us are either too stubborn or too crazy to go, so we do our best to make a living.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EE6: The world's going mad, and our Jarl doesn't care. She's only in it for herself. If she's the best Ulfric has to offer, we might've been better off with the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EE7: It's not much, but we get by. Very little money passes through Winterhold anymore, but if there's one thing you can count on, it's folks needing a drink now and then.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EE8: I figure the guards could always use some help, so I keep my eyes open.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EE9: Been hoping they'd make me a guard, but not yet. Still, I'll help them out if I can.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EEA: Bah, it's no use. Let's get back to it, then.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EEB: Wasn't by choice, I can tell you that. Options were in short supply. Ranmir and I grew up here, and nearly any money I make, he drinks away. Without the coin to pack up and leave, I decided to take to trading.
Skyrim.esm|0E0EEC: Wasn't by choice, I can tell you that. Options were in short supply. Ranmir and I grew up here, and his drinking soaked up whatever coin we had. So I opened a shop to try and get by. Now, though... Have to admit, I'm attached to the place. Suppose that makes me crazy, doesn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0E0EED: Oh, well. You know. A bit of this, a bit of that. They haven't made me a guard, even though I've been asking. So I'll take odd jobs, anything that comes along for now.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FA7: How can I help a brother Nord?
Skyrim.esm|0E0FAE: Bit late to be wandering around, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0E0FAF: Only burglars and vampires creep around after dark. So which are you?
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB0: You hear that? I swear, there's something out there. In the dark.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB1: Thinking of settling in? There's a house for sale - Breezehome. You should talk to the steward up in the keep if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB2: You're that one lives in Breezehome, right? Fine place. Almost bought it myself.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB3: Hjerim is a fine house, and it's up for sale. You should talk to the steward in the keep if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB4: Don't think you'll get special treatment just because you have a house in the city...
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB5: Proudspire Manor, now there's a fine home. Up for sale, too. If you're interested, talk to the steward, in the keep.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB6: You own Proudspire Manor, hmm? Beautiful house. At least from the outside.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB7: If you're looking to settle in Riften, there's a house for sale - Honeyside. See the steward in the keep if you want it.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB8: Smart thing you did, buying Honeyside. Nice place, and why pay for an inn?
Skyrim.esm|0E0FB9: Ah, Vlindrel Hall's a fine home, if you can afford it. The steward in the keep can tell you more.
Skyrim.esm|0E0FBA: So you bought Vlindrel Hall, eh? Wish I could afford a house that nice.
Skyrim.esm|0E107B: Even though Nocturnal doesn't desire worship in the traditional sense, the Twilight Sepulcher propagated a small group of priests. Of course, they'd never come into direct contact with Nocturnal but they insisted they had her favor. As part of their "duties," the priests created all sorts of baseless rituals and ceremonies all on Nocturnal's behalf.
Skyrim.esm|0E107C: I can't think of anyone better suited to return the Key. Just remember to keep your eyes open and walk true with the shadows.
Skyrim.esm|0E107D: I've been a Nightingale for a very long time. I sold my allegiance to Nocturnal in exchange for many profitable years of thieving. Falling in love with Gallus was wrong. It was a distraction that allowed the Sepulcher to be desecrated and it likely cost him his life. Until the Key is returned, I will never set foot inside that place again.
Skyrim.esm|0E107E: Right now, the Guild needs someone to maintain order. Everyone is awaiting the news of Mercer's demise; it couldn't be a more precarious time. I also need to reassure Maven that it's business as usual without Mercer... and that a new Guild Master will soon be taking over.
Skyrim.esm|0E107F: The conduit to Nocturnal's realm, the realm of Evergloam, has been in Skyrim... well, longer than recorded history. The Twilight Sepulcher was constructed around it by man and mer in order to shield it from those who would exploit its power. It's through this conduit that we're given Nocturnal's greatest gift, our luck. What she gains in return is a complete mystery.
Skyrim.esm|0E1080: Listen, lad. I have faith in you. I know that sounds strange coming from a thief, but these recent events have changed my perspective.
Skyrim.esm|0E1081: Listen, lass. I have faith in you. I know that sounds strange coming from a thief, but these recent events have changed my perspective.
Skyrim.esm|0E1082: These priests weren't threat to the Skeleton Key or the conduit to Nocturnal's realm, so they were tolerated. One of their ceremonies involved the Pilgrim's Path, a so called "test of worthiness." If a pilgrim was able to complete the path, it was said that they would become one with the shadows. What that means is anyone's guess.
Skyrim.esm|0E1373: Haven't you been paying attention to anything I've said? Only you can finally defeat Alduin and avert the end of the world! If we can just find out what Shout the ancient Nords used, you may have a chance. Or not. There's no guarantee with prophecy. Merely hope.
Skyrim.esm|0E1374: Haven't you been listening to me? All the old tales agree that he has some means to travel to Sovngarde itself. There he devours the souls of the heroic dead to feed his power. You must find his portal to Sovngarde before he returns, stronger than ever.
Skyrim.esm|0E1375: The old tales say that he can travel into Sovngarde to devour the souls of the dead. You must find out how he does this, before he regains his strength and returns.
Skyrim.esm|0E1377: That's the Honningbrew Meadery. Supposed to be a big rivalry between them and the Black-Briars of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0E1379: Remember when that used to be the Honningbrew Meadery? Didn't stand a chance against those Black-Briars of Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0E14B7: It's you and me, prisoner. Stay close!
Skyrim.esm|0E14BA: Come on! We need to get inside!
Skyrim.esm|0E14BB: Where are you going? The barracks is through here!
Skyrim.esm|0E14BC: We've got to get inside. Now!
Skyrim.esm|0E14CC: My sister and I inherited a modest sum of money. We decided to travel and seek out whatever adventures we could find. As we journeyed across Tamriel, we encountered tales of exotic and wondrous artifacts. We decided to collect as many as we could. My sister passed away some years ago, so I settled down here and opened the House of Curiosities. I think she would be happy to know that our collection has brought smiles to faces both young and old.
Skyrim.esm|0E14CD: No more than I was already getting. Four of the guards have already decided they're in love with me. Two of the guards got into a fight over me at the tavern. At least Balgruuf's men bathed regularly. These Stormcloaks always smell of ale and sweat, and their breath reeks.
Skyrim.esm|0E14CE: Don't let this innocent-looking flute fool you, for this is the Dancer's Pipe. Legend holds that the Dancer's Pipe has won wars, toppled empires and changed the very course of history. None know its origins, but the stories say that men who hear its music are compelled to dance uncontrollably, no matter the peril. To activate this strange power, one must only speak the magic words, which are... Oh my, I very nearly got us both into a nasty predicament, didn't I?
Skyrim.esm|0E14CF: Well, the Stormcloak guards drink a lot more than the Imperials did, so I've been making more money lately. On the other hand, they're a violent bunch. Every night a fight breaks out and I end up with a smashed chair or a broken table. So, not much has changed, really.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D0: Ah, now here is an item out of legend. This is Ysgramor's Soup Spoon. Now, I know what you're thinking - this is no spoon, it's a fork! Nobody can eat soup with a fork! Well, my friend, you did not know Ysgramor.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D1: Oh no, not at all. I simply locked the doors, hid beneath my counter and prayed to the Divines that my life would be spared and my shop wouldn't burn to the ground. But other than that, everything was business as usual. Now, do you have any other painfully obvious things to say?
Skyrim.esm|0E14D2: Well, not a great deal, no. But I do less business than I used to. The Stormcloaks don't like buying from anyone that isn't a Nord. If I wasn't married to Ulfberth, I'd be out of business.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D3: Feast your eyes on my strange and wondrous collection of curiosities.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D4: For a few septims, I'll give you a brief tour that includes some of my most unusual items.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D5: The objects you see here might look mundane at first glance, but I assure you, behind each is an unusual tale.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D6: Here is the Book of Fate, discovered in a secret room in the Arcane University. The writing in the book describes the destiny of its reader, so the words change from one person to the next. Some see only blank pages, and nobody knows why. Perhaps some of us are born with no destiny, or maybe the blank pages signify an imminent death.
Skyrim.esm|0E14D9: These tools were found in a crypt outside Windhelm. They belonged to the ancient Nords who dwelt in Skyrim before the days of the First Empire. Most scholars believe that the Nords of old used these implements to prepare their dead for burial. What macabre mysteries would these tools reveal if they could but speak?
Skyrim.esm|0E14DA: Yes, I was hired to protect the others as we walk the roads of Skyrim. It is a thankless task and I would rather be back home in Elsweyr, but I have little choice. Ahkari freed me from a prison in Cyrodiil, and now I must repay my debt to him. A word of advice, my friend --  do not mix gambling and drink. Taken together, they will empty your pockets of every septim.
Skyrim.esm|0E14DB: Very well. If you change your mind, you can usually find me here. In the meantime, feel free to look around.
Skyrim.esm|0E14DC: Skyrim is filled with all manner of dangerous beasts. Wolves, trolls, mammoths and giants would all like to make a meal of us. But the dragons are the most fearsome. Lucky for us, they don't seem to hunt along the roads.
Skyrim.esm|0E14DD: Splendid! If you'll just follow me, I'll tell you tales and show you wonders such as you've never seen.
Skyrim.esm|0E14DE: If you've got some time, stop by my House of Curiosities. I promise you'll see wonders!
Skyrim.esm|0E14DF: And with that, the tour is over. I thank you for your patronage, and I hope to see you again soon!
Skyrim.esm|0E14E0: Pardon me, but do I detect a case of the Rattles? I've got something for that.
Skyrim.esm|0E14E1: Everything's for sale, my friend. Everything. If I had a sister, I'd sell her in a second.
Skyrim.esm|0E14E2: Whiterun belongs to the true sons of Skyrim now, just as it should.
Skyrim.esm|0E14E4: Bits and baubles for sale, all crafted by the best blacksmith in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E14E7: It's not easy being a woman in Skyrim, I know. But stay strong, and men will come to respect you, and maybe even fear you.
Skyrim.esm|0E14E8: If it's a lady you're looking for, you'd best look elsewhere. Once Mikael gets them, they're got.
Skyrim.esm|0E14E9: The people saw fit to name me Jarl, and I don't intend to let them down.
Skyrim.esm|0E14EA: You're lookin' at a real warrior! At least I used to be.
Skyrim.esm|0E14EB: When I became Steward, I had to give up the bottle. The people of the hold need me at my best.
Skyrim.esm|0E14EC: I'd be dead if not for Vignar. He took me in and helped me turn my life around.
Skyrim.esm|0E14ED: I hope the Empire is sending reinforcements to drive these heathens out of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0E14EE: There's a rift here in Skyrim, and can't neither magic nor the passin' of time make it right.
Skyrim.esm|0E14EF: Damn those Gray-Manes to Oblivion. They want to back the Stormcloaks, they'll get what's comin' to 'em.
Skyrim.esm|0E14F0: Indeed I did! For a few septims, I'll tell you about some of the most interesting curiosities in my collection.
Skyrim.esm|0E1524: Troll's blood! It's a torture room.
Skyrim.esm|0E1525: The torture room. Gods, I wish we didn't need these....
Skyrim.esm|0E1537: We'll get him when he comes back around. Hold your fire.
Skyrim.esm|0E1538: I don't think so. How do I know you won't continue to extort me? A wagon full of gold isn't good enough for you?
Skyrim.esm|0E1539: I don't think so. How do I know you won't continue to extort me? A wagon loaded with silver isn't good enough for you?
Skyrim.esm|0E154D: It feels good to finally have a chance to help these people. Helplessly watching them suffer's been difficult.
Skyrim.esm|0E154E: Release the Miasma! Release it now! Hurry, Casimir, hurry!
Skyrim.esm|0E1550: We can't hold them! Go, Casimir! Run!
Skyrim.esm|0E1551: Perhaps my prayers were answered and your reason for stumbling across Dawnstar is more than a mere coincidence.
Skyrim.esm|0E1552: We can't hold them back! Go! Get to the Barrier! Release the Miasma!
Skyrim.esm|0E1553: There's a small shrine to Mara I established inside the tower's entry hall. I was hoping to seek spiritual guidance from Her.
Skyrim.esm|0E1554: Keep going, Casimir! Release the Miasma!
Skyrim.esm|0E1555: The temple's been abandoned for decades now. Ironic isn't it... a ruin within a ruin?
Skyrim.esm|0E1557: When the temple was active the priests would rarely be seen in Dawnstar. They preferred to live a solitary existence.
Skyrim.esm|0E1558: Beyond these doors is the inner sanctum and the Skull of Corruption.
Skyrim.esm|0E1559: I'm not familiar with the tower's history, but it was deserted for quite a long time before Nightcaller Temple was established inside.
Skyrim.esm|0E155A: I understand. We must all follow our own paths. If you wish to find me again, I'll be in Nightcaller Temple.
Skyrim.esm|0E155B: Farewell, then. If you need my services again, just come and look for me in the usual place.
Skyrim.esm|0E155D: Quickly! Kill him now. Kill him and claim the Skull for your own! Vaermina commands you!
Skyrim.esm|0E155E: The tower on that hill is our destination. People around here call it the Tower of the Dawn.
Skyrim.esm|0E155F: When the ritual's complete, the Skull will be free and then Erandur will turn on you.
Skyrim.esm|0E1564: So, did you still need my assistance?
Skyrim.esm|0E1565: Still need me to do something?
Skyrim.esm|0E1568: Running out of room, eh? I'll be glad to help.
Skyrim.esm|0E1569: Do I get to keep it? Just kidding.
Skyrim.esm|0E156A: So is it Riften or Rifton? This place has changed its name so many times, I can't even remember.
Skyrim.esm|0E156B: Seems like capturing Riften is turning out to be more of a hassle than a benefit. But what do I know?
Skyrim.esm|0E156C: Why we bothered to capture Riften I'll never understand. Just look at this place.
Skyrim.esm|0E156D: This city still reeks with the stench of the Stormcloaks. Makes me sick to my stomach.
Skyrim.esm|0E156E: I hope you're finding the city in proper order, thane.
Skyrim.esm|0E1574: ...w...what happened? Wait! We're still under attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E1575: ...the Miasma... They're still here! Stop them!
Skyrim.esm|0E1577: ..wha?... where?... D-destroy this place! Tear it apart!
Skyrim.esm|0E1578: ...s-sleeping? No! Burn the temple! Kill the priests!
Skyrim.esm|0E1579: ...not dead? Kill them, kill them all!
Skyrim.esm|0E158B: I'll stay here. You try not to get into too much trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0E158D: All right. Don't take too long.
Skyrim.esm|0E1590: The Forsworn haven't been kind to Karthwasten. I've heard rumors that they've attacked this place without mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0E1593: I grew up in The Pale. This land was my home for most of my life... and for a Dunmer, that's quite a statement, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0E159E: We should bow our heads in silent reverence for those who are interred in Falkreath's cemetery.
Skyrim.esm|0E15A5: I've always wanted to make a pilgrimage here to the Temple of Mara. Thank you for bringing me here... you've made me quite happy.
Skyrim.esm|0E15A6: Watch your step, Markarth has quite a reputation thanks to the Silver-Blood family. I wouldn't advise crossing them.
Skyrim.esm|0E15A8: What an amazing city. I've actually never managed to visit Whiterun, but I always wanted to.
Skyrim.esm|0E15A9: Windhelm is absolutely freezing. How can these people tolerate it?
Skyrim.esm|0E15AB: I was a student at the Bards College for a short time... until my affiliation with Vaermina was discovered. I haven't been back to Solitude since.
Skyrim.esm|0E15AC: I've heard Riften's home to the worst Skyrim has to offer. I hope we don't run into any trouble while we're here.
Skyrim.esm|0E15C5: Her Benevolence has taught me patience and mercy, but these vampires deserve none of it.
Skyrim.esm|0E15D0: A good friend of mine was infected by a vampire long ago. He killed himself rather than change into one of those... things.
Skyrim.esm|0E15D4: Vampires are utter abominations. We need to do everything in our power to destroy them.
Skyrim.esm|0E15D9: Being a priest of Vaermina put me very close to dark sorcery at its worst.
Skyrim.esm|0E1621: Do you have any family? Anyone waiting for you on the outside?
Skyrim.esm|0E1622: Then you know the hard looks as judgment gets set upon you. The sneers of people who never had to face sentence.
Skyrim.esm|0E1623: I had a daughter, once. She'd be 23 this year. Married to some hot-headed silver worker or maybe on her own learning the herb trade.
Skyrim.esm|0E1624: So many say that, at first, until they realize the truth. If you change your mind, speak to Braig. Tell him I sent you. I want you to know how widespread the injustice of Markarth is.
Skyrim.esm|0E1625: My story, huh? Everyone in Cidhna Mine has a tale. Let's hear yours first. When was the first time you felt chains around your wrists?
Skyrim.esm|0E1627: Do I? And what about you? What right did you have to meddle in my affairs? Kill my people? Was it worth it? Your truth?
Skyrim.esm|0E1628: This was our land. We were here first. Then the Nords came and put chains on us. Forbid us from worshipping our gods. Some of us refused to bow. We knew the old ways would lead us back to having a kingdom of our own. That is who we are. The Forsworn. Criminals in our own lands. And we will cut a bloody hole into the Reach until we are free.
Skyrim.esm|0E162A: Fine. Don't tell me. But I know that look in your eyes. Someone who's faced death at the hands of the law.
Skyrim.esm|0E162B: I had Markarth. My men and I drove the Nords out. We had won, or so we thought. Retribution was swift. I was captured, quickly tried, and sentenced to death. But my execution never came. Thonar Silver-Blood stopped it. He wanted the Forsworn at his call, that I would point their rage at his enemies and spare his allies. And I have. Humiliating at first, but I knew he would let his guard down eventually. That he would come to trust I was under control.
Skyrim.esm|0E162D: So you know what it's like to have your life in someone else's hands. Why should they get to decide? Isn't judgment for the gods?
Skyrim.esm|0E162E: There's a man named Braig inside these mines. Besides me, he's been here the longest. Tell him I sent you. Ask him why he's here. I want you to know how widespread the injustice of Markarth is.
Skyrim.esm|0E162F: Then come for me, "son of Skyrim." Come for me!
Skyrim.esm|0E1630: Then come for me, "daughter of Skyrim." Come for me!
Skyrim.esm|0E1631: Very well. Come for me, then. Come and let's end this!
Skyrim.esm|0E1632: You're one of us now, you see? A slave. The boot of your kinsmen stepping on your throat. Maybe if you understood that, I could help you.
Skyrim.esm|0E1633: You're one of us now, you see? A slave. The boot of the Nord stepping on your throat. Maybe if you understood that, I could help you.
Skyrim.esm|0E163E: Of course. Take what you need. But what's this all about? What are you two doing here?
Skyrim.esm|0E1644: Let's try and stay together, the sorcerers in these places tend to... well, harvest bodies for parts.
Skyrim.esm|0E1648: I've seen my share of black magic, but that's all in the past now.
Skyrim.esm|0E165C: This place must be cleansed. These draugr were once living beings and they deserve better.
Skyrim.esm|0E165D: These poor souls... suffering in eternal servitude.
Skyrim.esm|0E1668: The thought of the dead rising... makes me sick to my stomach.
Skyrim.esm|0E1670: Bandits know nothing of pity or mercy. No need to show them any either.
Skyrim.esm|0E1673: A group of bandits descended on Dawnstar once... if it wasn't for the guard, who knows what they could have done to the place.
Skyrim.esm|0E1674: Bandits are the worst lot. They'd steal from their own mothers given the chance.
Skyrim.esm|0E1675: I thought I heard footsteps. Be careful.
Skyrim.esm|0E167C: When I was a young acolyte of Vaermina, I paid my dues in a shrine located within the catacombs of a fort in Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|0E167D: I served as a healer in a fort not unlike this one long ago.
Skyrim.esm|0E167E: I can't imagine what happened to the dwarves. How could a race with the capability to build a place like this simply vanish?
Skyrim.esm|0E167F: I've never been inside a dwarven ruin. They are simply astounding.
Skyrim.esm|0E1680: Did you hear that? A metal on stone sound... hmmm, perhaps it was nothing.
Skyrim.esm|0E1681: This place could be massive... very easy to get lost. We have to be careful not to lose our way.
Skyrim.esm|0E1682: What a marvel of engineering. The ancient Nords were ingenious builders.
Skyrim.esm|0E1684: Watch yourself, these Nordic ruins are notorious for their traps.
Skyrim.esm|0E1685: These ruins are old, maybe a thousand years or more.
Skyrim.esm|0E1686: Why anything would chose to reside down here is beyond me.
Skyrim.esm|0E1687: These caves... they harbor ill omens, I can sense it.
Skyrim.esm|0E1688: Trust in Mara... she'll protect us in this dark place.
Skyrim.esm|0E1689: I don't like the looks of this place... not at all.
Skyrim.esm|0E168A: I don't know what persuaded you to assist me when we met in Dawnstar, but I'm glad you did.
Skyrim.esm|0E168B: I've done a lot of... questionable things in my life. Serving Vaermina was a horrible mistake, and I hope Mara will forgive me one day.
Skyrim.esm|0E168C: I was recruited as an acolyte of Vaermina as a young elf. I had no real childhood to speak of... we weren't permitted to socialize.
Skyrim.esm|0E168D: I've always felt a great deal of guilt running from Nightcaller Temple and leaving my friends to die. I'll never forgive myself for that.
Skyrim.esm|0E168E: After running from Nightcaller Temple, I wandered Skyrim for years until I was taken in by a priest of Mara who lived in Morthal.
Skyrim.esm|0E168F: Finding Mara was the greatest moment of my life. I'll never forget the warmth that spread through my heart when we embraced.
Skyrim.esm|0E1690: It was a bit difficult to come to the ways of Mara at first, but my fellow priests were kind and quite patient with me.
Skyrim.esm|0E1691: I've never admitted this before, but I've killed more than I care to admit. I'll just leave it at that.
Skyrim.esm|0E1692: My parents are long dead, but they lived wonderful lives... I have no sad stories to tell about them. Perhaps one day I'll visit their resting place.
Skyrim.esm|0E1693: I've been enjoying this journey with you. Not for the wealth we've accumulated, but just being carry Mara's will to every corner of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E1694: It's wonderful to be able to sleep soundly, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|0E1695: The Greybeards are an amazing lot. Imagine, living your entire life without speaking to a friend or a loved one. I couldn't stand it.
Skyrim.esm|0E1696: I've been to Ivarstead a few times, but never climbed High Hrothgar. I wouldn't mind visiting the monastery at the summit.
Skyrim.esm|0E1697: When the weather permitted, Aerin and I would often walk the Rift's forests. The trees here are so beautiful.
Skyrim.esm|0E1698: Ah, Winterhold. I've always wanted to visit this place. The secrets locked inside must be astounding.
Skyrim.esm|0E1699: Incredible... imagine the manpower and ingenuity needed to carve the buildings of Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0E169A: Whiterun is a such beautiful city. Have you ever seen the likes of this place?
Skyrim.esm|0E169B: This is the first time I've set foot in Windhelm. What a dreary place.
Skyrim.esm|0E169C: My mother always loved the sea. When I was young, we'd visit Solitude and watch the ships from the bluff. Ah... better times.
Skyrim.esm|0E169D: It feels good to walk the streets of Riften again.
Skyrim.esm|0E169E: Vampires... an unclean lot. It will be a pleasure to clear this place.
Skyrim.esm|0E169F: Don't let the vampires strike you or else you may get infected with their blood poisoning.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A0: Be aware of rune traps on the ground or else this could be a very short expedition.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A1: Kill these casters as fast as you can, otherwise they may summon creatures we don't want to deal with.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A2: Ugh... dark sorcery. Always makes my skin crawl.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A3: If there's one thing I'm certain of, it's that draugr burn like tinder. We could use that to our advantage.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A4: Many have fallen in this crypt. The Draugr show no mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A5: Ugh, Draugr. They reek of decay.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A6: When I was a child, my village was burned to the ground by bandits. Do you know I actually saw one laugh as he ran my brother through...
Skyrim.esm|0E16A7: I hope there's more bandits around... their numbers could use a little more thinning.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A8: These bandits would stab each other in the back if it meant an extra coin in their pockets.
Skyrim.esm|0E16A9: Bandits... the worst type of scum.
Skyrim.esm|0E16AA: Perhaps we can find some treasure if we search the grounds of the fort thoroughly.
Skyrim.esm|0E16AB: If fought more than my share of bandits in places like this.
Skyrim.esm|0E16AC: Keep your eyes open for ambushes. These forts have too many blind corners.
Skyrim.esm|0E16AD: These are the most dangerous, but the most profitable types of ruins. The dwarves must have been a prosperous race.
Skyrim.esm|0E16AE: Watch the walls for the sphere constructs... they can appear in the blink of an eye.
Skyrim.esm|0E16AF: The dwarves were masters at subterranean construction... these ruins show  only a fraction of their capabilities.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B0: Mzinchaleft. I swore I'd never return here.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B1: I almost died in a ruin just like this.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B2: Danger's about, I can tell. Be cautious.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B3: Can you just imagine how ancient this place must be? Hundreds of years? Thousands?
Skyrim.esm|0E16B4: There could be Draugr about, I can smell the decay in the air.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B5: I've ventured into quite a few Nord ruins in my day.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B6: Killed my first creature in a cave just like this... I'll never forget it.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B7: I'm certain we'll find some treasure down here.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B8: Keep your eyes open, I don't like the looks of this place.
Skyrim.esm|0E16B9: Exploring these caves feels like coming home. I missed this.
Skyrim.esm|0E16BA: I've really missed traveling like this. Walking the breadth of Skyrim has always filled my heart with excitement.
Skyrim.esm|0E16BB: I've never been a sellsword... never traded my skills for pay. I've always adventured on my own terms.
Skyrim.esm|0E16BC: You should have seen me at the height of my adventuring career... I was fearless and took too many risks. But that's what it was all about.
Skyrim.esm|0E16BD: I remember a cutpurse trying to rob me on the road once. When I drew Grimsever, I think the poor man soiled his armor!
Skyrim.esm|0E16BE: I've seen enough death to last three lifetimes, yet I never tire of a good fight. What do you make of that?
Skyrim.esm|0E16BF: When I was young, I'd journey with my father on hunting expeditions into Morrowind. The cliff racers made for excellent sport.
Skyrim.esm|0E16C0: I love the cold air... it's exhilarating. Perhaps it's because of my Nord blood, but there's something about it that makes me feel alive.
Skyrim.esm|0E16C1: My father wanted me to follow in his footsteps as a hunter, but I set my aspirations a bit higher. He never complained... I miss him.
Skyrim.esm|0E16C2: My mother was a strong woman... she's the one who instructed me on my swordfighting techniques. Can you imagine?
Skyrim.esm|0E16C3: People say Skyrim is one of the most dangerous places in Tamriel. I think they're wrong... it's magnificent and I'm proud to call it home.
Skyrim.esm|0E16C4: As we walk these stone passages, we delve into the very bones of the earth.
Skyrim.esm|0E16E5: The Thalmor Justiciars are so often misunderstood. Our primary goal is to preserve the peace between the Empire and the Aldmeri Dominion. The worship of the false god Talos contributed to the unfortunate friction that led to the Great War. Following the Great War, the White-Gold Concordat set out the terms for peaceful coexistence between the two powers. The Empire and the Dominion agreed that the worship of Talos was a relic of the past, and must be eliminated in the interests of peace. But I'm sure you didn't come here for a lecture on high politics.
Skyrim.esm|0E16E6: One of my main duties is to foster a better understanding between the Aldmeri Dominion and the people of Skyrim. I also oversee the activities of the Thalmor Justiciars throughout Skyrim, although this has been unfortunately disrupted by the war.
Skyrim.esm|0E16E8: Father won't let me go see the dragon.
Skyrim.esm|0E16E9: Is it true? Did you really capture a dragon on the Great Porch?
Skyrim.esm|0E16EA: A dragon in Dragonsreach. That's something I never saw coming.
Skyrim.esm|0E16EB: So my brother really did it. Whiterun will be the toast of Skyrim for this!
Skyrim.esm|0E16EC: I'll be glad when that beast is gone from Dragonsreach. I never understood what the point of all this was.
Skyrim.esm|0E16ED: A captive dragon, just think of the possibilities!
Skyrim.esm|0E16EE: You will release me - ro laan - if in return I promise to take you to Skuldafn and stop helping Alduin?
Skyrim.esm|0E16EF: I trust that this is just the begining of a long and mutually beneficial relationship.
Skyrim.esm|0E16F0: I'm so glad you could attend today.
Skyrim.esm|0E16F2: Please, help yourself to more wine.
Skyrim.esm|0E16F3: The hospitality of the Thalmor Embassy is at your service.
Skyrim.esm|0E16F4: As long as the Empire continues to uphold its treaty obligations, my government does not concern itself with the internal politics of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E16F6: This is far as I can take you. Krif voth ahkrin. I will look for your return, or Alduin's.
Skyrim.esm|0E16FA: Zu'u uth nall thurri dein daar miiraak.
Skyrim.esm|0E16FF: Dovahkiin zol mul. Zu'u gahvon miri Alduin.
Skyrim.esm|0E1705: Alduin saraan hin sil ko Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|0E1734: Oh, fine. Keep your secrets. I see how it is. That wagon recently had a little accident. They're stranded now, just up the road. We're outnumbered, but I have a plan. You got here just in time.
Skyrim.esm|0E1737: That was crafty. I'm sure having a steward in the pocket will come in handy. Lucky for us, that the wagon recently had a little accident. They're stranded now. Just up the road! We're outnumbered, but I have a plan. You got here just in time.
Skyrim.esm|0E173E: There's more of them somewhere around here.
Skyrim.esm|0E173F: Really? It just so happens we've been tracking a wagon! For about a day now. So that's what's in there? Coins and weapons? How do you know that?
Skyrim.esm|0E1740: I can't believe we're the only ones who survived. Glad you were here. I guess... I'll hold down the fort by my lonesome. Hurry back and tell them to send a garrison. Quickly!
Skyrim.esm|0E1745: Hey there! I was wondering if I'd run into you out here. The Reach is a beautiful but dangerous place, eh? One false step and you'll fall to your death, that is if those Forsworn don't get you first. Have you seen those Briarheart men? That's some evil magic right there. What brings you? You have the look of purpose in your eyes.
Skyrim.esm|0E1749: Well, well. Look who it is! How have you been?
Skyrim.esm|0E174A: Good to hear. I need this to go well. This is my first real command since they promoted me after Whiterun. You know, some nights when I close my eyes, I see the battle stretched out before me, like I'm still there... Do the men you've killed haunt you? Mine do...
Skyrim.esm|0E174B: These are dark days. But at least we know we're doing our part to make things better, eh? I guess I'm doing all right. I need this to go well. This is my first real command since they promoted me after Whiterun. You know, some nights when I close my eyes, I see the battle stretched out before me, like I'm still there... Do the men you killed haunt you? Mine do...
Skyrim.esm|0E174C: Straight to business with you, huh? Well, it just so happens I have the perfect mission for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E174D: I suppose you're right... they say it gets easier... I'm not sure if that's a good thing... But never mind all that. We've got a job to do.
Skyrim.esm|0E174E: That's what I keep telling myself. But, never mind all that. We've got a job to do. Don't we?
Skyrim.esm|0E174F: I remember you... you were with us at Helgen, and Whiterun. Glad to have you with us. How are you?
Skyrim.esm|0E1750: I suppose you're right... they say it gets easier... I'm not sure if that's a good thing... But never mind all that. What brings you out this way?
Skyrim.esm|0E1751: That's what I keep telling myself. But, never mind all that. What brings you out this way?
Skyrim.esm|0E1755: Huh. I had heard you died.
Skyrim.esm|0E1756: I have to admit. This is more fun that I thought it'd be.
Skyrim.esm|0E1757: I can't remember the last time I visited home. Think I've forgotten what a proper bed looks like, let alone feels like.
Skyrim.esm|0E1758: Who do you think's killed more Imperials? You? Or me?
Skyrim.esm|0E1759: Have you always been that ugly?
Skyrim.esm|0E175A: I've heard some things about Ulfric I don't like, but he's right about this war and about Skyrim's future.
Skyrim.esm|0E175B: I don't think Galmar even knows my name. Probably because I'm not "The Dragonborn."
