Skyrim.esm|0E175C: Ever wonder if maybe we should a call a truce with the Imperials and join forces for just a little while take care of some of these dragons?
Skyrim.esm|0E175D: Who do you think killed more rebels? You? Or me?
Skyrim.esm|0E175E: "Dragonborn" huh? Was it your ma or your pa that was the dragon?
Skyrim.esm|0E175F: Ever get the impression Rikke's not always sure what side she's on? I'm not doubting her loyalty. Just, she seems rather sympathetic to the rebels.
Skyrim.esm|0E1760: I don't think Rikke even knows my name. Probably because I'm not "The Dragonborn."
Skyrim.esm|0E1761: Ever wonder if maybe we should a call a truce with the Stormcloaks, join forces for just a little while, and take care of some of these dragons?
Skyrim.esm|0E1763: What? Now's not the time to chat.
Skyrim.esm|0E1765: I wonder how many of us think we'll be the lucky ones and survive this?
Skyrim.esm|0E1766: So, do you get that weird taste in your mouth before battle, too? What is that?
Skyrim.esm|0E1767: Pleasant weather for a war, no?
Skyrim.esm|0E1768: Gah! Let's get on with it already!
Skyrim.esm|0E1769: You made it? I was sure I'd find you face down in the dirt.
Skyrim.esm|0E176A: I'm pretty sure I killed more than you. I was counting.
Skyrim.esm|0E176C: That went pretty well, all things considered.
Skyrim.esm|0E176D: Do colors seem brighter to you? Everything seems bigger, too. Strange...
Skyrim.esm|0E176E: Are your ears still ringing? I hope that goes away.
Skyrim.esm|0E177D: So, you're the one Brynjolf's been jabberin' about. Been waitin' to have a word with you.
Skyrim.esm|0E177E: I think you better listen to Mercer and Brynjolf first. We can talk later.
Skyrim.esm|0E177F: So, you're the new recruit everyone's been talking about, eh? I guess it's about time we talked.
Skyrim.esm|0E1780: I have no business with you.
Skyrim.esm|0E1791: Imagine hearing a story like that, over and over. Each time a different family Each time a different injustice. Your meddling above ground reminded me of how removed I've been from the struggle. My men and I should be in the hills, fighting.
Skyrim.esm|0E1792: Yes, but I need a show of loyalty from you. I don't need a shiv in the back while we break out.
Skyrim.esm|0E1793: Let me out. They're going to kill me!
Skyrim.esm|0E1794: You know, I'm almost beginning to like you, but you haven't earned your place out of here yet.
Skyrim.esm|0E1795: My daughter is the one who needs your pity. I'm just a poor Forsworn whose only regret is not killing more Nords before I was locked up.
Skyrim.esm|0E1796: What? No. No don't kill me. Please!
Skyrim.esm|0E1798: You've finally become one of us. Come with me. I think it's time I announced my plans to you and your new brothers.
Skyrim.esm|0E1799: Have you met Grisvar the Unlucky? He's rightly named, and he's also a thief and a snitch. He's outlived his minor usefulness. Take care of him, and then we can leave Cidhna Mine for good.
Skyrim.esm|0E179A: We found this in the Jarl's treasury after we captured the city. I thought you should be the one to have it.
Skyrim.esm|0E179C: I'm not Madanach. I was never a leader of the Forsworn. The only anger I can justify is my own. But every family in the Reach has a story like mine. There are no innocent onlookers in this struggle. Just the guilty, and the dead.
Skyrim.esm|0E17B1: Did you? So Ulag didn't make it passed those giants then? More's the pity. If you found any of the sap, I'd be willing to pay you the same cut I offered him. Better price than you are likely to find otherwise.
Skyrim.esm|0E17B2: Ah, of course! How much have you got?
Skyrim.esm|0E18E6: We found this in the Jarl's treasury after we captured the capital. I thought you should be the one to have it.
Skyrim.esm|0E18E7: Found this among the dead. Something tells me I should give it to you.
Skyrim.esm|0E18E8: Here. I want you to have this. Don't ask. Just take it.
Skyrim.esm|0E1937: I've come to rely on you. There's something I want you to have. It was my father's. It would be my honor if you carried it.
Skyrim.esm|0E1ADF: Well put, friend. Tell me, Galmar, why do you fight for me?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE1: Yes, but why do you fight? If not for me, what then?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE2: I still say you should take them all out like you did Deadking Torygg.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE3: You think I need to send Balgruuf a stronger message.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE4: I fight for the men I've held in my arms, dying on foreign soil. I fight for their wives and children, who's names I heard whispered in their last breaths.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE7: That's what I like to hear. So long as you can back up those words with steel.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE8: All right. But before I can put you to use, I need to know how much you can take. I have a little test for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AE9: I would gladly retire from the world were such a day to dawn.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AEA: If he's not with us, he's against us.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AEB: Taking his city and leaving him in disgrace would make a more powerful statement, don't you think?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AEC: I fight for we few who did come home, only to find our country full of strangers wearing familiar faces. I fight for my people impoverished to pay the debts of an Empire too weak to rule them, yet brands them criminals for wanting to rule themselves!
Skyrim.esm|0E1AED: What's left of Skyrim to wager?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AEE: Then let them die with their false kings.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AEF: Destined for the chopping block if I'm not mistaken.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF0: Don't be so sure of that. We've intercepted couriers from Solitude. The Empire's putting a great deal of pressure on Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF1: When you kill that Ice Wraith, then we'll talk about you becoming a Stormcloak.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF2: Fair enough. But are you willing to die for your home?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF3: How long are you going to wait?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF4: How many of their sons and daughters follow your banner? We are their families.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF6: Not so sure of yourself after all?
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF7: At the end of the day, every warrior is alone. Before you can stand next to your shield brother, you need to be able to stand on your own.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AF8: He's a true Nord. He'll come around.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AFA: Balgruuf won't give us a straight answer.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AFB: I'd follow you into the depths of Oblivion, you know that.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AFC: I fight so that all the fighting I've already done hasn't been for nothing. I fight... because I must.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AFD: Is that so...? Ah yes, you were with us at Helgen.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AFE: It's where men have tested their mettle for ages. There's a strange rock formation, built by the ancients. Something about that place attracts the Ice Wraiths. You kill an Ice Wraith out there, and I'll have all the proof I need about you.
Skyrim.esm|0E1AFF: And what would you have me do?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B00: Things hinge on Whiterun. If we can take the city without bloodshed all the better. But if not...
Skyrim.esm|0E1B01: I'll die before elves dictate the fates of men. Are we not one in this?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B02: Torygg was merely a message to the other Jarls. Whoever we replace them with will need the support of our armies.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B03: I'm sending you to Serpentstone Island. If you survive, you pass. If you die, well, you weren't going to be much use to me anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B04: We'll see about that, won't we?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B05: Ralof's alive? I hope that's true. He's a damn good man. But he hasn't returned yet, so I'll need to wait for his account. For now, speak with Galmar.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B06: I'm not sure you belong here, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B07: Hmm. Helgen, eh? Ulfric told us quite the story. If you made it through all that, you're likely worth something to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B08: Are you ready to for Serpentstone Island?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B09: Many I fear still need convincing.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B0A: If by message you mean shoving a sword through his gullet.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B0B: Only the foolish or the courageous approach a Jarl without summons... Do I know you?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B0C: As you can see, I have much that requires my attention. If you're inclined to gab, there are some nice prison guards you may like to meet...
Skyrim.esm|0E1B0D: Speak with Galmar. He'll size you up and see where we can best use your talents.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B21: A fair point... Well, you've come to the right place, then. Speak with Galmar.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B22: You mistake me. I'm not saying no - just wondering about your intentions. We're not looking for sellswords. The Stormcloaks need dedicated men and women who're devoted to the cause and willing to die for it.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B23: Can't fault anyone for that! As long as you hate the Empire as much as I do, that I can work with.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B24: Aye. But in the meantime, we have a war to plan.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B25: I'm always looking for able fighters. Not everyone can say they made it out of Helgen. Seems we're all branded villains these days... So long as your criminal past stays in the past, and you fight for me with honor and integrity, we'll welcome you into our ranks.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B26: We've been soldiers a long time. We know the price of freedom. The people are still weighing things in their hearts.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B27: So we're ready to start this war in earnest then?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B28: He knows that. They all know that.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B29: They have families to think of.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B2A: Your words give voice to what we all feel, Ulfric. And that's why you will be High King. But the day words are enough, will be the day when soldiers like us are no longer needed.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B2D: But first, tell me. Why's an Elf want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B2E: But first, tell me. Why's a cat want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B2F: But first, tell me. Why's a lizard want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B30: But first, tell me. Why's an Orc want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B31: But first, tell me. Why's a Redguard want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B32: But first, tell me. Why's a Breton want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B33: But first, tell me. Why's a foreigner want to fight for Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0E1B34: The kind men use to measure themselves.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B9D: While technically in charge of the College, the Arch-Mage's responsibilities often keep him occupied. Thus, I run the day-to-day operations.
Skyrim.esm|0E1B9E: There are no expectations. This College is a place to study and practice magic freely. Hopefully any discoveries made in your pursuits will be shared with the members of the College first. That way we all benefit.
Skyrim.esm|0E1BA0: Now, if you'll please follow me, I'll show you the living quarters.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE2: Absolutely. When we say "walk with the shadows," we are asking those Nightingales who have passed on to protect us. It's believed that they are literally what guides our uncanny luck... by placing their hands in ours. That's why the Ebonmere needed to be reopened. Without it, there's no way Nocturnal was able to allow them through.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE3: If this place is in danger ever again, the shadows will call. Should the need arise, a portal connects the Sepulcher and Nightingale Hall. Use it whenever you wish.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE4: What will you do now, my love?
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE5: Farewell, Gallus. Eyes open... walk with the shadows.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE7: Farewell. Eyes open and walk with the shadows.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE8: I feared I would never see you again. I was afraid you'd become like the others.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EE9: Gallus's Oath has been paid. His actions have satisfied the terms. Now his spirit becomes one with the Evergloam... the realm of perpetual twilight and the cradle of shadow.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EEA: When your debt to Nocturnal has been paid, we'll embrace once again.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EEC: No, not gone... he's become one with the shadows. This is the greatest honor a Nightingale can possibly achieve. In death, he's become a part of that which we use to live.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EED: Nocturnal calls me to the Evergloam. My contract has been fulfilled.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EF1: I wish to speak to Karliah for the moment.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EF2: If it were not for the actions of this Nightingale, your fears would have come true. He honors us all.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EF3: If it were not for the actions of this Nightingale, your fears would have come true. She honors us all.
Skyrim.esm|0E1EF4: Will I ever see you again?
Skyrim.esm|0E1EF5: I've decided to make my home at Nightingale Hall. Since it's your home as well, I hope to see you and Brynjolf there. Of course, I may visit some of Skyrim's cities to "acquire" things from time to time. Can't afford to get rusty, now can we?
Skyrim.esm|0E1FB9: Very good. Be sure to bring any more Sleeping Tree Sap you find to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E1FD6: My Saffir doesn't like that I've been spending so much time looking for my father's old sword. He fed his entire family with the gold he made using that weapon. I'm not about to let it gather dust in some thief's trophy room.
Skyrim.esm|0E1FD7: I tracked it to a group of bandits nearby. But I'm no fool. I'd need the Whiterun guards or maybe hire the Companions to get it. I don't know why I'm saying this, but if you find it out in your travels, I'd be grateful to you.
Skyrim.esm|0E1FD9: We will endure. We always have.
Skyrim.esm|0E240F: My... associate will be pleased at your resourcefulness. She discovered its location, by means she has so far declined to share with me.
Skyrim.esm|0E24E6: My wife's a good woman. She can be a little hard-headed sometimes, but I love her just the same.
Skyrim.esm|0E24E7: What is it that you need?
Skyrim.esm|0E24EA: It pleases me to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|0E24F7: Madanach! Think you can escape my prison do you? You'll pay for what you've done to my family!
Skyrim.esm|0E24FA: Your family? You've poisoned the Reach with your tainted silver for long enough, Thonar!
Skyrim.esm|0E24FC: You're a slave. Just like us.
Skyrim.esm|0E24FD: Do you remember the sun? Because you'll forget. Soon.
Skyrim.esm|0E24FE: You're back. Have you done what I asked?
Skyrim.esm|0E2500: Remember. It was the Nords who locked you up.
Skyrim.esm|0E2501: Our struggle is our people's struggle.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F2: J'zargo is ready for more adventure.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F4: As you wish. Just don't forget about me.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F5: Here? Right here? Very well, J'zargo shall wait.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F6: If it will help you, then certainly.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F7: J'zargo only has so much room to carry things.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F8: You'll be all right on your own? Then I'll see you back at the College.
Skyrim.esm|0E26F9: Really? J'zargo was having such fun. Ah, well. Back to the College then.
Skyrim.esm|0E26FD: Do you need alchemy components? Or training?
Skyrim.esm|0E2701: Yes, Listener? How can Cicero be of service?
Skyrim.esm|0E2702: Such a shame. I thought we were getting along so well. I'll head back to Riften if you should have second thoughts.
Skyrim.esm|0E2CFA: Don't take too long figuring it out. Might mistake your hesitation for Imperial sympathies. Come back when you're committed to the cause, or go join the Legion. They're taking anyone with a pulse these days.
Skyrim.esm|0E2CFB: A crown doesn't make a king.
Skyrim.esm|0E2CFC: Fine. I'll send the Unblooded here with you. Fancy a crawl through a moldering dungeon to see if you can't stir up Galmar's Jagged Crown?
Skyrim.esm|0E2CFD: Aye. The Great Hunt killed old King Borgas. Gods only know with what profane arts those elves cursed his remains. Curses notwithstanding, the tombs of kings are always full of traps to ward off grave robbers, are they not? But nothing a band of Stormcloaks can't handle, I assure you.
Skyrim.esm|0E2CFE: Tell me again why we're wasting time and dwindling resources chasing a legend. We don't even know it exists!
Skyrim.esm|0E2CFF: When you kill that Ice Wraith, then we'll talk about you becoming a Stormcloak. Here. Take this. You'll need it. Use it on the island. Don't lose it. That's all the help you're getting. Try not to die.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D04: All the more reason then. The crown would legitimize your claim.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D05: Hrongar won't give us a straight answer.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D06: That's the spirit. By swearing this oath you become one of us. A hero of the people. A true son of Skyrim. A Stormcloak. Repeat after me. "I do swear my blood and honor to the service of Ulfric Stormcloak..."
Skyrim.esm|0E2D07: That's the spirit. By swearing this oath you become one of us. A heroine of the people. A true daughter of Skyrim. A Stormcloak. Repeat after me.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D09: "All hail the Stormcloaks, the true sons and daughters of Skyrim!"
Skyrim.esm|0E2D0A: Just shut up and keep out of sight.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D0B: When have I ever been false with you?
Skyrim.esm|0E2D0D: You're alive! I'm impressed! Galmar had his doubts about you, but I knew you'd pull through. You should speak with him. He's going to need your help.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D0E: Speak with Galmar. You'll be going on a little errand with him.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D10: It's true, the location of the crown was lost with King Borgas. After the Great Hunt killed him, while he was off on his damned Alessian campaigns. But legend holds, his body was secretly returned to Skyrim and buried with the crown. Its exact whereabouts lost in the following Wars of Succession. Through sources that shall remain nameless, I've tracked down what I believe to be the final resting place of King Borgas - Korvanjund. If the crown exists, it will be there.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D11: I need someone I can trust to deliver a message of great import to Jarl Hrongar of Whiterun.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D12: Before you're one of us, you must swear fealty to Jarl Ulfric Stormcloak, future High King of Skyrim. You must also pledge unswerving loyalty to your fellow Stormcloaks, to Skyrim and to her people.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D14: I'm glad you're here. I have a message I need delivered to the Jarl of Whiterun. Deliver this axe to Balgruuf the Greater.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D15: I'm glad you're here. I have a message I need delivered to the Jarl of Whiterun. Deliver this axe to Jarl Hrongar.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D18: You're alive. I owe Ulfric a drink. I have to admit, I didn't think we'd be seeing you again. I misjudged you. You're definitely Stormcloak material. It's time we made this official. You ready to take the Oath?
Skyrim.esm|0E2D19: You ready to take the Oath?
Skyrim.esm|0E2D1A: You can stick a sword through an Imperial any day you want. But that doesn't make you a Stormcloak. We're not just fighting Imperials. We're fighting to restore Skyrim to her glory and give her the king she deserves.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D1D: You think I need to send Hrongar a stronger message.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D21: The Jarls are upset. They don't all support you.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D23: And damn the Moot! We should risk letting those milkdrinkers put Thorryg's woman on the throne? She'll hand Skyrim over to the elves on a silver plate.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D24: It exists. And it'll be the symbol of the righteousness of our cause. Think about it. The Jagged Crown! It heralds back to a time before jarls and moots. Back to the time when a king was a king because his enemies fell before him, and his people rose because they loved him. Skyrim needs that king. You will be that king, Ulfric. You must be.
Skyrim.esm|0E2D25: Have you not heard that ancient verse? "Maw unleashing razor  snow, Of dragons from the blue brought down, Births the walking winter's woe, The High King in his Jagged Crown." Going way back to King Harald's time or even before, the High King always wore the Jagged Crown. It was the symbol of his might and power. The crown is made from the bones and teeth of ancient dragons, and it is said to contain a portion of the power of  every king who has worn it. True or not, who would dare deny Ulfric's claim, when the legendary Jagged Crown sits upon his brow?
Skyrim.esm|0E2D28: We'll see about that, won't we? Here. Take this. You'll need it. Use it on the island. Don't lose it. That's all the help you're getting. Try not to die.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FE1: I don't know what we do with it now. Maybe you can figure it out.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FE2: And what do you plan to do with it? Do you even know what you're asking about, or are you just someone's errand boy?
Skyrim.esm|0E2FE3: And what do you plan to do with it? Do you even know what you're asking about, or are you just someone's errand girl?
Skyrim.esm|0E2FE7: Lead on, to Ysgramor and glory.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FE8: This is good. Could you show the way to the tomb again?
Skyrim.esm|0E2FE9: Give it here. They've asked for the utmost discretion on this matter, so I'll make sure it gets to them. Here's your share of their reward.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FEA: And it looks undamaged. They'll be pleased, I'm sure. And we're pleased with their money.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FEB: Nice work with this one. You make us all proud.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FEE: You look to your left, you see one way. You look to your right, you see another. But neither is any harder than the opposite. But the Elder Scrolls... they look left and right in the stream of time. The future and past are as one. Sometimes they even look up. What do they see then? What if they dive in? Then the madness begins.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FEF: Kodlak was right. I let vengeance rule my heart. I regret nothing of what we did at Driftshade. But I can't go any further with my mind fogged or my heart grieved.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FF0: For the glory of the Companions! For Ysgramor!
Skyrim.esm|0E2FF1: We bring honor to the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FF2: And here I figured you as one who enjoyed our special gifts. I guess I was wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FF3: We choose our battles, and you've chosen yours. Someone else can take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FFB: A sharp mind on you. We should find out for ourselves.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FFC: That is the kind of thing Kodlak used to say. And he was right. But still, I'd like to see for myself.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FFD: It would take a month to explain to you how that very question doesn't even make sense. The Scrolls exist here, with us, but also beyond and beneath. Before and after. They are bits of Divine made substance so we could know them. Sorry. Talking about the Scrolls, you usually end up in irritating and vague metaphors like that. Some people who study them devoutly go mad.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FFE: Kodlak's final teaching. I think he was right about beastblood and Sovngarde. I wish to cleanse myself that I might know glory in the afterlife.
Skyrim.esm|0E2FFF: I think that maybe Kodlak was right. As a werewolf, I can't be a good Nord. I want to be clean, like he was, and go to Sovngarde when I finally die.
Skyrim.esm|0E3003: His spirit is departed. Members of the Circle, let us withdraw to the Underforge, to grieve our last together.
Skyrim.esm|0E3004: The simplest way to put it is "knowledge," but there's nothing simple about an Elder Scroll. It's a reflection of all possible futures and all possible pasts. Each reader sees different reflections through different lenses, and may come away with a very different reading. But at the same time, all of it is true. Even the falsehoods. Especially the falsehoods.
Skyrim.esm|0E3009: Be careful. This man is known to be dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|0E300A: Be careful. This woman is known to be dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|0E300B: Can't say I blame you. I'll find someone to take care of it.
Skyrim.esm|0E300C: Not the response I was hoping for, but suit yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E3025: I don't know who told you that, but I'll do what I can. What we do have are plenty of books.
Skyrim.esm|0E3035: What about... wait. Are you? Were you the one the Greybeards were calling?
Skyrim.esm|0E3036: There is always work for the Companions. But there is more personal task before me.
Skyrim.esm|0E3038: Of course, there is always work. My mind is someplace else, though.
Skyrim.esm|0E3039: We have a bit of a sensitive matter this time. A wealthy family has... misplaced one of their heirlooms.
Skyrim.esm|0E3048: Just in time. I was worried I'd have to do this myself. Some rich folk had a trinket stolen and they want us to get it back.
Skyrim.esm|0E3049: I... it's like waking up out of a dream. I can breathe more deeply now. I can't smell your heart beating the way I used to. But my mind is... clear. This is a great service you've done for me, Harbinger. I will not soon forget. Now... I'd like to spend some time communing with Ysgramor. My soul is clean now. Perhaps he'll still welcome me when my time comes.
Skyrim.esm|0E304A: It's like relaxing into a warm mug of spiced mead. I'm losing aches I didn't know I had. This is how a warrior should feel. Alive and aware. Not clouded with thoughts of the hunt. Thank you for your help. I'm going to stay in the tomb for a while. My shame kept me out before, but now I want to see it.
Skyrim.esm|0E3051: At this point they don't care what happens to him and neither do I. Best to just kill him and be done with it.
Skyrim.esm|0E3054: At this point they don't care what happens to her and neither do I. Best to just kill her and be done with it.
Skyrim.esm|0E3055: I envy you. Facing such a beast in combat. Smelling its blood on the air. That is what songs are sung about, Shield-Brother.
Skyrim.esm|0E3056: I envy you. Facing such a beast in combat. Smelling its blood on the air. That is what songs are sung about, Shield-Sister.
Skyrim.esm|0E3059: You are a very brave man. But I already knew that.
Skyrim.esm|0E305A: You are a very brave woman. But I already knew that.
Skyrim.esm|0E305F: Hircine will bless us for finding his Totem.
Skyrim.esm|0E3061: The spirit of Skjor is with us on this errand. I feel him.
Skyrim.esm|0E3062: This is not the time for talk. Let's get to the Underforge and see what this can do.
Skyrim.esm|0E3064: Careful with those. Don't want even more fragments, do we?
Skyrim.esm|0E3065: We don't all lead your life of grand adventure, Harbinger.
Skyrim.esm|0E3068: Not at this rate. I'll just go back to clubbing horkers and scaring the debtors.
Skyrim.esm|0E306A: I had once heard Skjor and Kodlak talking about the Totems of Hircine. Simple-looking objects that could channel the powers of the werewolf in new directions. Once Kodlak began looking for his cure, I'd wager Skjor didn't bring it up again. But I was going through his things and found a book I had never seen before. All about the Totems. I've been talking to my sources around the holds, and think I may have heard where one of them lives.
Skyrim.esm|0E306B: I think I've located another one of the Totems. Come with me, and we'll write ourselves into their history.
Skyrim.esm|0E306C: I've finally tracked down the last of the Totems, I think. The information wasn't easy to come by, but everyone has a price. And a pain threshold.
Skyrim.esm|0E306D: One of them's escaped, and you're going to find that coward.
Skyrim.esm|0E306E: You still bear a head of the Glenmoril betrayers, yes? Then lead on, back to the Tomb of Ysgramor. My soul is now prepared.
Skyrim.esm|0E306F: You still have one of the witch's heads. This is good. Let's go to the Tomb. We can do the cleansing there.
Skyrim.esm|0E3075: Aela said that the cleansing used a head from one of the witches of Glenmoril, yes? A strange kind of ritual, but I won't mind killing those wretched things.
Skyrim.esm|0E3076: Aela said we would need a Hagraven's head. I already have one of them, but she said it was a special kind. And that you knew where to get more. Let's go find one!
Skyrim.esm|0E3077: Good, good. These aren't the most glorious jobs, but they pay well.
Skyrim.esm|0E3078: Move quickly so we can have this over and done with.
Skyrim.esm|0E3079: We're trusting you on this one. Bring honor to the Companions.
Skyrim.esm|0E307A: You think that even if I did have one here, I would let you see it? It would be kept under the highest security. The greatest thief in the world wouldn't be able to lay a finger on it.
Skyrim.esm|0E307B: I'm going to stay a while. Rest among these bones. This creature honored us with its life, and I will honor it with my respect.
Skyrim.esm|0E307D: You go if you want to. I've never seen bones this large, and want to make sure I remember them so I can tell the story right. Otherwise Vilkas will just say I was making it up.
Skyrim.esm|0E3080: That definitely matches the description of the totem from Skjor's old book. Let's get it to the Underforge.
Skyrim.esm|0E3082: That's it! That's the second Totem. Quickly, let's put it back with the other one.
Skyrim.esm|0E3083: At last... the final Totem is ours. Let's go complete our set.
Skyrim.esm|0E3084: Bit by bit, we bring honor to this land. And you've brought honor to the Companions. More importantly, some gold.
Skyrim.esm|0E3087: Another job well done. Your payment is secure, and you have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0E308A: Of course, I have a small favor to ask of you. There's another piece, that Kodlak always kept close to himself. Would you go to his chambers and bring it back for me? I'm not sure I'm the best one to go through his things.
Skyrim.esm|0E308B: Yes! I like the way you think. There are whispers of where we might find one, too. They say he killed twelve of the Imperial Legion and shattered their bones with his breath. I think you and I are worth about a dozen soldiers each. We'll send that beast back where it came from.
Skyrim.esm|0E308D: Well, that's one way to find out if they're real. I even know where we might find one. Lead on!
Skyrim.esm|0E308E: Harbinger, I'd be honored if you joined me in my search.
Skyrim.esm|0E3095: I would be honored for you to accompany me, Harbinger.
Skyrim.esm|0E3096: I've been hearing stories about dragons. That they're back. That they can eat the sun out of the sky and freeze a man where he stands. Have you seen one in your travels?
Skyrim.esm|0E3098: Travelers have been coming to Whiterun. They tell stories about dragons. I've heard of them, but didn't think they were real. Have you ever seen one?
Skyrim.esm|0E309F: I tell you this province is losing its grip. The bandits become more brazen every day.
Skyrim.esm|0E30A0: Some don't even believe they exist, but the Falmer can be devious. Tunneling into homes, stealing off with valuables.
Skyrim.esm|0E30A1: Those hill people who call themselves "the Forsworn" show no mercy.
Skyrim.esm|0E30A2: Giants don't usually cause problems like this, but these ones are stirred up for some reason.
Skyrim.esm|0E30A3: The rumors say the culprits are vampires. I try not to listen to swaggle like that, but figured you should know.
Skyrim.esm|0E30A4: Of course, it's a group of wizards they're pointing their fingers at. Who knows what they want it for, but we want it back.
Skyrim.esm|0E30AF: I knew it. Everyone comes in here, expecting my help, but they don't even have the proper questions. An Elder Scroll is an instrument of immense knowledge and power. To read an Elder Scroll, a person must have the most rigorously trained mind, or else risk madness. Even so, the Divines usually take the reader's sight as a price.
Skyrim.esm|0E30B0: It sounds like Eastmarch is too busy with their war to keep their own prisoners in line.
Skyrim.esm|0E30B1: The braggarts in the Reach always claim that no prisoner escapes their mine. What you don't hear about are the ones who break off from escorts on the way to Markarth.
Skyrim.esm|0E30D5: Haafingar seems to have trouble keeping their prisoners in line.
Skyrim.esm|0E30DB: I'm sorry to have to send you to Hjaalmarch. I can't stand the smell of it myself. But it seems their criminals can't either and just want to get out.
Skyrim.esm|0E30DC: It seems that law-breakers in the Pale have no desire to stay there. I don't blame them.
Skyrim.esm|0E30DD: The idiots in Falkreath still think the best place for prisoners is that hole in the ground. No surprise, but one of them's gotten out. Again.
Skyrim.esm|0E30DF: The so-called prison in Riften leaks like one of their fishnets. This is what happens when you let thieves run your town. The prisoners just walk right out.
Skyrim.esm|0E30E0: One of the guards from Dragonsreach came to me, worried about a prisoner who's escaped. The Jarl has too much on his mind right now, we won't be troubling him with this.
Skyrim.esm|0E30E1: The snowberries up in Winterhold have lowered themselves to ask for our help. For some reason they just throw their lawbreakers out into the sea and think that ends the problem. But every now and then, one swims back.
Skyrim.esm|0E30E3: I am sorry if I disappointed, Harbinger. Still, the battle was won.
Skyrim.esm|0E30E5: I will train better for the future.
Skyrim.esm|0E3181: As many know, there is an ongoing effort to research the work of the Archmagus Shalidor.
Skyrim.esm|0E3183: He is most remembered for his great maze of Labyrinthian, said to hold "Glamoril", or the secret of life.
Skyrim.esm|0E3186: While stories have persisted since the First Era, none have ever confirmed the existence of this "Glamoril" or its purpose or function.
Skyrim.esm|0E3187: The College has developed some theories, however.
Skyrim.esm|0E3189: We know that Shalidor had an understanding of magic that surpassed almost any of either his age or ours.
Skyrim.esm|0E318A: The few works of his that have been recovered suggest that he had an understanding of magic and the world that few have ever achieved.
Skyrim.esm|0E318B: What is interesting is that it appears he was also incredibly prolific, writing on a diverse array of subjects.
Skyrim.esm|0E3190: An array so great, in fact, that it remains a source of curiosity.
Skyrim.esm|0E3192: What, then, of this Glamoril? It means "secret of life" in elvish. Could this be an explanation for Shalidor's works?
Skyrim.esm|0E319F: Is it possible that it somehow contributed to his work? Perhaps allowed him to life multiple lifetimes in a short span of time?
Skyrim.esm|0E31A0: Increased his intelligence and knowledge in ways unfathomable to us? We may never know for certain.
Skyrim.esm|0E31A1: The College is always searching for more of Shalidor's writings in an attempt to understand both our knowledge of the man, and of magic in general.
Skyrim.esm|0E31B0: At this time, I would like to make a few statements regarding policy here at the College.
Skyrim.esm|0E31B1: Please refrain from practicing Conjuration spells in view of the town of Winterhold.
Skyrim.esm|0E31B2: Atronachs have a tendency to frighten the locals. Undead.... well, I don't even think it needs to be said.
Skyrim.esm|0E31B6: Urag has asked me to remind everyone to please return materials borrowed from the Arcanaeum in the same condition as you received them.
Skyrim.esm|0E31BA: If this is impossible due to misuse or accident, Urag recommends finding a replacement copy to deliver to the Arcanaeum.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C2: Failure to do so will result in paying, in Urag's words, "a blood price." I did not ask him to elaborate on that point.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C3: Once again, I must ask that everyone please clean up any materials used in the common areas.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C4: We've had yet another sprained ankle due to soul gems being left on the floor. Let's please try and keep injuries to a minimum.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C5: The Midden remains off-limits at this time; while the initial outbreak has been cleaned up, the area is still considered hazardous.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C6: No more experiments are to be carried out there, and mages are advised that you enter the Midden at your own risk.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C7: While Drevis appreciates the spellcasting skill that went into somehow cramming several hundred apples into his pillow...
Skyrim.esm|0E31C8: He would ask that it please not happen again.
Skyrim.esm|0E31C9: He has suggested that, should he find out who is responsible, he is well versed in making things disappear permanently.
Skyrim.esm|0E31CA: There have been unconfirmed reports that someone has been sneaking into the town of Winterhold while invisible, and causing... issues.
Skyrim.esm|0E31D1: This goes quite against College policy, and the party responsible is advised to cease these actions at once.
Skyrim.esm|0E31D2: Any information as to the whereabouts of the previous group of Apprentices would be greatly appreciated.
Skyrim.esm|0E31D3: As of yet, there has been no sign of them.
Skyrim.esm|0E31D4: Projects are underway to discern the origin and nature of the College's recent find in Saarthal.
Skyrim.esm|0E31D5: Work continues on the Eye of Magnus, as it has been commonly referred to.
Skyrim.esm|0E31E2: Any and all theories are currently being considered. Those with ideas should please speak with Mirabelle.
Skyrim.esm|0E31E4: At this time, there is no indication that, as has been rumored, the object is in fact a physical part of Magnus, the god of magic.
Skyrim.esm|0E31F4: It has been suggested that the object is a gateway to the realm of Aetherius, but nothing has proven that idea one way or the other.
Skyrim.esm|0E31FC: It has been proposed that the object is in fact the entirety of Aurbis in one physical space.
Skyrim.esm|0E31FF: This would of course mean that Tamriel, indeed all of Mundus, is actually contained within the sphere.
Skyrim.esm|0E3200: It further suggests that we are somehow then outside our own existence while looking in at it.
Skyrim.esm|0E3209: While the idea seems dubious at best, it has not, at present, been entirely ruled out.
Skyrim.esm|0E32F0: Aduri is what some would call a free spirit. She likes to wander the fall forest for hours on end. If we were still living at my alchemy shop in Vivec City, it wouldn't be so bad, she could wander the canals all day. But around here, there's a tremendous amount of work to get done, not to mention the dangers she's not ready to face.
Skyrim.esm|0E32F7: I had quite the mentor. He taught me everything he know about the nirnroot and its strange properties.
Skyrim.esm|0E32F8: Actually, I'm growing it if you hadn't noticed. I'm the only person alive that can cultivate nirnroot from a seed to a fully grown plant.
Skyrim.esm|0E32F9: Why don't you take a look at some of my merchandise and we'll see what kind of a deal we can make.
Skyrim.esm|0E32FD: I've got coin and I've got merchandise. Let's see what we can do.
Skyrim.esm|0E3300: I give special rates to members of the Thieves Guild. Show me what you've got.
Skyrim.esm|0E3302: I'm a bit busy at the moment, what did you want?
Skyrim.esm|0E3530: I have no idea, but it's fascinating. Assuming it's true, of course. The Isle of Artaeum disappeared over a hundred years ago, and no one has seen them since. And yet now, suddenly, they have chosen to contact you? Why, it's intriguing! If nothing else, I'd take it as a compliment. The Psijics have only ever dealt with those they feel worthy.
Skyrim.esm|0E3531: The Arcanaeum is located above the hall, and the Arch-Mage's quarters above that.
Skyrim.esm|0E3532: None. It's been long enough that honestly, I don't expect to hear from them.
Skyrim.esm|0E3533: No, and after what you've discovered I fear the worst for them.
Skyrim.esm|0E3534: No more than anyone else, I don't think. They're a very old order. Pre-dated the Imperial Mages Guild by quite a bit. In fact, the Mages Guild was founded in opposition to the idea of the Psijic Order, that only an elite few should have control over magic. No one has seen or heard from them in over a hundred years now, though.
Skyrim.esm|0E3535: My problem with her? She's the one who has a problem with me! She's threatened by me! By my skill as a wizard, by my elegance and posture, by my superior good looks! But she's not going to get the better of me, oh no. I won't let it get to me one bit. That's what she's after. She's trying to undermine my confidence, make me doubt myself. Well it won't work, I tell you!
Skyrim.esm|0E3536: I don't know. If the Augur suggested it, there must be a good reason for locating it. I'm afraid I don't see the connection, though.
Skyrim.esm|0E3537: As I recall, Yisra was trying to improve flame cloak spells to better work in Skyrim's harsh environment. Ilas-tei was practicing Illusions spells. She was having trouble with the calm spells, I believe, but only ever worked with skeevers. And then there's Borvir and Rundi. Twins, though they rarely agreed on anything. Those two boys were obsessed with mead. Convinced they could concoct something to compete with Honningbrew. Something about using frost magic to chill the mead for a certain period of time. I felt it was a waste, but the Arch-Mage let them proceed.
Skyrim.esm|0E3538: Well, all of the previous Apprentices have been accounted for, thanks to you. Here, why don't you take this. It was intended to be given to the best in their group as a reward for their hard work. It's the least I can do for your help in putting this matter to rest. Oh dear, I didn't mean to say it like that. That wasn't a joke, really.
Skyrim.esm|0E3539: I hadn't really considered it in terms of time. I suppose it's been at least a month. Let me think... what were they working on?
Skyrim.esm|0E353A: I'm not certain what use it would be. The Staff is said to be able to absorb great amounts of energy. Perhaps it can interact with the Eye in a way that nothing else can.
Skyrim.esm|0E353B: I don't know, not for sure. But perhaps the staff can absorb some of the energy coming out of the Eye, weaken it. Allow us to get close to Ancano.
Skyrim.esm|0E353E: Gods, this is hers, isn't it? She will certainly be missed.
Skyrim.esm|0E353F: I'm not sure. None of them were working on anything terribly dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|0E3540: Oh dear. I had particularly high hopes for her. Once she'd overcome her difficulties, she could have been quite successful here.
Skyrim.esm|0E3541: Ah, that. Yes, the group prior to yours has disappeared entirely. It's possible that there was less than the necessary amount of oversight. That's still being looked into. But we've had no contact with them for quite some time now. Arniel wondered if perhaps they'd somehow managed to get themselves lost in the Outer Realms, but I think that's giving them too much credit.
Skyrim.esm|0E3542: It certainly is. Was, I suppose. He would never have willingly parted with it. What a shame.
Skyrim.esm|0E3543: Personally? No, not I. One of their number used to advise the Arch-Mage when I was but an Apprentice here. But that was a great many years ago, before all the members of the order were called back to the Isle of Artaeum, and it disappeared entirely.
Skyrim.esm|0E3555: Yes, I recognize it. Well, I suppose I needn't wonder where he is anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0E3556: So you're off to find some staff now? I don't see the point of that.
Skyrim.esm|0E355B: J'zargo has heard of the Staff of Magnus. Once you are done with it, perhaps you will allow J'zargo to... Borrow it?
Skyrim.esm|0E355E: Have you managed to track down the Synod, or the Staff of Magnus?
Skyrim.esm|0E35F5: The Reach will be ours again.
Skyrim.esm|0E35F6: Ah, my friend from Cidhna Mine. Hello.
Skyrim.esm|0E35F7: This is where it will begin. A new uprising.
Skyrim.esm|0E35F8: We'll have our freedom. One day.
Skyrim.esm|0E35F9: The Silver-Bloods pay their debts. You have my thanks.
Skyrim.esm|0E35FA: Madanach has been found to be the true murderer of Markarth. On behalf the city, I apologize for your wrongful imprisonment.
Skyrim.esm|0E35FB: By killing Madanach you've freed Markarth from his hidden tyranny. On behalf of the city, I thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0E35FC: You. You aren't with the Forsworn, are you? People say they saw you when the prisoners escaped. So many dead.
Skyrim.esm|0E35FD: I heard about you being pardoned. What were the guards thinking, taking you in?
Skyrim.esm|0E35FE: Huh. Heard you broke out of Cidhna Mine with those Forsworn. Do you have any idea how many died?
Skyrim.esm|0E35FF: Can't believe anyone was dumb enough to mistake you for a Forsworn agent.
Skyrim.esm|0E3600: Can't believe Madanach is on the loose again. I hope those rumors I'm hearing about your part in that are wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0E3601: Everyone's been talking about you. The guards will have to answer for throwing an innocent person in Cidhna Mine.
Skyrim.esm|0E3602: Forsworn rampaging through the streets. As if business wasn't slow enough.
Skyrim.esm|0E3603: Oh, it's you! Sorry to hear about your imprisonment. I'm sure the guards didn't mean to throw you in jail
Skyrim.esm|0E3604: I saw you. With the Forsworn. By the old gods, that was something.
Skyrim.esm|0E3605: I'll be damned. Someone who actually escaped Cidhna Mine. You're the talk of the Warrens.
Skyrim.esm|0E3606: You escaped Cidhna Mine? With Madanach? Everyone in the Warrens has been praising your name.
Skyrim.esm|0E3607: What was it like? Cidhna Mine? We've all been talking about your escape. And being pardoned after. Amazing.
Skyrim.esm|0E363F: I'll be down here, trying to coordinate everything with Delvin and Vex; to make sure the coin keeps flowing... and no one skims! If you still feel like doing some jobs, I'm sure Delvin and Vex have more than their fair share to give out. Either way, it's been a pleasure, my friend. Here's to the future of the Guild. May it last another thousand years!
Skyrim.esm|0E3640: I just wanted to give you a proper thank you for everything you've done. The Guild is back on its feet again and on its way to a prosperous future. What's become of the Skeleton Key?
Skyrim.esm|0E3641: That's it then. After all of those years of helplessly watching the Guild decline. But enough of that... I'm confident that with you in charge, we'll soon have more gold than we could possibly spend.
Skyrim.esm|0E3642: It's good to see you in one piece, lad!
Skyrim.esm|0E3643: It's good to see you in one piece, lass!
Skyrim.esm|0E3653: Arise, Olaf! My vengeance is at hand!
Skyrim.esm|0E36B9: I'm sorry, I've come too far to lose it now.
Skyrim.esm|0E36BA: You've already taken care of it?  Thank you friend.
Skyrim.esm|0E36BB: Thank you so much, this scroll means a lot to my family. Take this for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0E36BC: Can I have it?  It's worthless to you, but I can pay.
Skyrim.esm|0E36BD: Could it really be?  Wow this is what I've been looking for.
Skyrim.esm|0E36DC: Anything, as long as I get to fight.
Skyrim.esm|0E36DE: No problem, I can carry a good bit of gear.
Skyrim.esm|0E36E1: Did you still need me for something?
Skyrim.esm|0E38C0: Are you sure? I'll need to ask them to take an oath to leave their old life behind and stay here from now on.
Skyrim.esm|0E38C1: The Thalmor hunted us down, remember? It wasn't exactly great for recruitment. But we have a headquarters of a sort now. We'll rebuild the Blades. Someday.
Skyrim.esm|0E38C2: If you find any more candidates bring them here.
Skyrim.esm|0E38C4: Very well. I'll do the oath later. Let's just get 'em geared up and ready to fight.
Skyrim.esm|0E38C5: If you find anyone you think would make a good recruit, I can certainly take a look at them. Remember, though, being a Blade is a lifelong commitment. Their loyalty has to be with us once they're in.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A37: I can certainly share a bit of what I know.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A38: You wish to learn from the best, eh? So be it.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A39: You deal with powerful forces. Take care they do not overwhelm you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A3A: I can teach you, but I won't be held responsible for what you do with the knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A3B: You've come to the right place. I'd be happy to teach you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A3C: I'll show you what I can about protecting yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A3D: I shall explain to you the mysteries of the outer realms.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A3E: I'll teach you how to use it effectively, and keep you quick on your feet.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A3F: All right, but if you get arrested don't blame it on me.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A40: I'll show you what I know.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A41: You'll never compare to me, but I can try and teach you anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A42: Up to no good, are we? I'll teach you, but it'll cost you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A43: I'll gladly share my knowledge with you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A44: It's an honorable craft. I'll show you what I can.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A45: Stick with me, and they'll never even know you're there.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A46: Well, you've come to the right place.
Skyrim.esm|0E3A47: It's all in the swing. Let me show you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3C53: We've got an old saying -- the Gray-Manes have lived in Whiterun since the first snows fell in Skyrim. Well, maybe that's not entirely true, but our clan's got a long an' proud history in this hold. We respect the old ways, the Nord ways, an' a Gray-Mane has worked the Skyforge since before the time of my grandfather's father.
Skyrim.esm|0E3C54: How many Nords died to save the Empire? And what was our reward? The worship of Talos outlawed, and now the Thalmor come to conduct their witch-hunts while the Empire stands idly by.
Skyrim.esm|0E3CD5: I warn you, Gray-Mane, you are treading on dangerous ground.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC2: What? Speak your meaning plainly, old man.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC3: Do your loyalties lie with the Thalmor, then?
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC4: Bold words, Battle-Born. While you're meting out justice to traitors, are you going to deal with the Emperor, as well?
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC5: I'm no happier about this than you are, but I don't want the see the Thalmor rounding up people in the streets and throwing them in prison.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC6: I've heard you Gray-Manes are collaborating with the Stormcloaks. If I see any such treachery, I'll deal with you myself.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC7: A law made at the tip of an Aldmeri sword, aye. A sword stained red with Nord blood. Is this what our people fought and died for? To forsake our most beloved ancestor and divine?
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC8: I'll remember that when the Stormcloaks liberate the city. No amount of money will protect you then, Battle-Born.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DC9: I won't say it again, Vignar - Talos worship is forbidden. It's the Empire's law, and we're still a part of the Empire.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DCA: And we've done as much as we can to prepare for that. But remember, Whiterun is home to the Companions. Any dragon that comes here will get more than he's bargained for, I think.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DCB: Aye, with a certainty. It was Talos that helped us win back the city. I'm as sure of that as the sun rising in the morning. Might even build a new temple and put Heimskr in charge of it. Hah! I bet he'd like that.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DCC: You won't be laughing when the Thalmor come to root out Stormcloak sympathizers.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DCD: Well, first an' foremost there's repairs to make an' wounds to heal. Folks are depending on us getting the city back in working order. Once things settle down, I'll be lookin' to recruit more guards for the city, then shore up our stores of food an' water. The Empire might try an take the city back, or worse, we'll get a visit from a dragon. We've got to be ready for both.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DCE: Well, it could be worse. The former steward, Proventus, kept very detailed records. But yes, it's been a lot of work. Vignar doesn't want the change to disrupt the lives of his people. They've got enough to deal with already.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DCF: I'm surprised you had the wits to notice, Olfrid, what with your nose buried so far up the Emperor's backside.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD0: Aye, we used to be fast friends, though it was long ago. We were two of the city's oldest an' most-respected clans, an' we could trace our histories all the way back to Ysgramor's companions. But then Olfrid got rich an' decided he an his kin were too good for us simple, backwards Nord folk. When the troubles began, he took the Empire's side. We haven't spoken much since then.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD1: Someone's been pilfering crops from my farm. If I find out it's one of your kin, I'll see the culprit rot in prison.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD2: Aye, they're an old clan like ours, but they cast their lot with the Empire when the uprising began. Olfrid Battle-Born made his money from farming, an' got himself some new friends among the Empire's nobles. Now he's in the Emperor's pocket like the rest of 'em, with no regard for honor an' tradition.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD3: Don't lecture me about treachery, whelp. You don't know the meaning of the word.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD4: Don't threaten me, boy. You're either a Nord who respects our traditions, or you're not. If not, it isn't me you'll have to answer to, but the people of this city and this hold.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD5: I take my duties as Housecarl very seriously. I won't hesitate to shed blood if my uncle Vignar is threatened.
Skyrim.esm|0E3DD6: When the Empire surrendered to the Aldmeri Dominion, they shamed us all!
Skyrim.esm|0E3E11: Are you kidding me? We're all alone out here except for a few guards who couldn't give a damn about us. We make easy pickings for thieves looking to steal everything we worked so hard for. Not only did I they take my bow, but they took all of our coin as well. Nothing's sacred to these people.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E12: My goodness, how did you ever come by all of these? No matter, I appreciate your generosity.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E1C: I bumped into them when I was coming back to my farm after buying supplies. I know those lowlifes took it with them back to the Ratway. They left an unmistakable stench behind. It was a family heirloom. I can't even put a price on how much that thing means to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E61: Let me at least give you something for your efforts... finding all of these couldn't have been easy... or cheap.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E62: Dragons, thieves and war. I should have stayed in Morrowind.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E63: That's enough recruits for now. Ask Esbern about the dragon lairs he's been studying. We'll have the recruits do some hunting with you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E64: Very well. She seems like a fighter to me. I'll give her the oath.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E6A: The Ratway is somewhere under Riften, shouldn't be too tough to find.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E6B: Watch yourself out there or they'll take everything away from you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E6C: Fantastic. I was concerned I had asked for too many, but I'm glad to see you proved me wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E6D: He was a wonderful man, and he loved us very much. I'm sorry I yelled at you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E6E: I don't know how you got it back, and I don't want to know. Frankly, I don't even care... I'm just so glad to see it again. Here, this is all can afford to give you as compensation. Hope it's enough.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E6F: I don't need luck, I need rest.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E70: Good. If you see him, tell him Grosta said he can go straight to Oblivion... he's not welcome here any more.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E73: Is it that obvious? Sigh. I've got my hands full here. Between the farm chores, cultivating my supply of nirnroot and keeping a tight rein on my sister Aduri, there's little time for rest.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E74: Fine. I'll wait until someone else with a little more courage comes along.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E75: Really? That would be a pleasant change. One of the ingredients I need to cultivate nirnroot are jazbay grapes... the acidic content of the juice is perfect for the soil. I need twenty of them for my current crop.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E76: Well, he said that a few men have been going by to some of the local farms promising a good price from all the local mills and farms. Leifnarr said they'd pay double what you can usually get at the markets. The catch was, he had to head out there with our wood. So not only is Leifnarr gone, but a good bit of our supplies as well.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E77: I've been a fool, wasting my time sitting here helpless when I could have sent help sooner. Perhaps this is all my doing... What's done is done. Here, I want you to have this. Bringing me closure was kind of you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E78: Thanks. It would be nice to have it back... means a lot to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E7A: Doesn't matter. I'm sure he's long gone by now anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E7B: I'm sorry mother, now that he's dead, I just want to know everything I can about him.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E7C: With just myself and one child, it's damn near impossible. With Leifnarr gone, it's really put me in a terrible bind. I'm having trouble making ends meet. At this rate, we won't last more than a few more seasons at best. Then I'll just have to sell the old place.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E7D: So, has my bow turned up yet?
Skyrim.esm|0E3E7E: Who in the name of Azura are you?
Skyrim.esm|0E3E7F: You look suspicious. You better not be one of those damn thieves.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E80: It's not safe around here. There's thieves out there looking to steal everything you own.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E81: You even think of taking something from my house... and I'll kill you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E82: I'm afraid we have little to offer you in the way of hospitality.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E83: No, it can't be... oh my... and all this time, I was thinking he'd been unfaithful to me. I feel so stupid.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E84: I'm still busy, but I can always make time to speak with you.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E85: Always a pleasure. What brings you back?
Skyrim.esm|0E3E86: Any luck locating those jazbay grapes?
Skyrim.esm|0E3E87: I hope this is important... I get little sleep as it is.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E89: Good luck finding those jazbay grapes, they're very rare.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E8B: Hmph. If you see my good-for-nothing husband Leifnarr on your journeys tell him he's no longer welcome here.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E8C: You men are utter pigs... you only care about one thing.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E8D: Men are complete pigs, am I right? All they care about is one thing... and you know exactly what I mean.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E8E: I knew it! I knew my father was a hero and he didn't just run away.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E8F: Someday I'll take over the mill... when I'm old enough.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E92: Drop by and see us again if you're ever in the area.
Skyrim.esm|0E3E93: Remember to tell that bastard I never want to see his ugly face again!
Skyrim.esm|0E3E94: Quit taking advantage of us and this world might be a better place, you got that?
Skyrim.esm|0E3E95: Don't let the men take advantage of you, always be on your guard.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F4B: Well, my friend... the time's come to make it official. It's time to become our Guild Master. Don't worry, I promise this will be short and sweet. If you'll just meet us in the center of the cistern room, we can begin.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F4D: Everyone is in agreement, so all I can do now is name you Guild Master and wish you good fortune and long life.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F50: Look, I've never been good at these things, so I'm just going to keep it short. Being Guild Master means more than just getting a cut of all the loot, it's about being a leader and keeping this rabble in order. With that in mind, I propose that the position of Guild Master should be yours.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F51: I think it's time we make this Guild Master thing official. Go and talk to Brynjolf, he's makin' preparations.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F52: I heard Brynjolf is looking for you. I think it's time for the Guild to have a new leader.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F53: So, they're making you leader, huh? Better go find Brynjolf so he can seal the deal.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F54: I heard Brynjolf wants to see you about being Guild Master. I suppose my turn will come one day, but for now you'll do nicely.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F55: So, they're making you Guild Master, eh? Good. Just find Brynjolf and he can make it official.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F56: Brynjolf's been looking for you about the Guild Master ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F57: Brynjolf tells me you'll make an excellent Guild Master. Oh, he's looking for you, by the way.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F58: What are you doing talking to me? Didn't you know Brynjolf's been trying to find you? It's time to install you as Guild Master!
Skyrim.esm|0E3F5A: Now everyone get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F5C: I suppose you deserve it. Couldn't do any worse than Mercer, anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F5D: I'm not getting any younger. Let's get this ceremony done with.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F5E: So now the pupil is the master, eh? Good show!
Skyrim.esm|0E3F5F: Not now, it's time for the ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F60: You need anything, anything at all... don't hesitate to ask.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F61: We can talk after the Guild Master ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F63: Don't talk to me, you're supposed to be at the ceremony!
Skyrim.esm|0E3F64: We're all rich thanks to you. Cheers!
Skyrim.esm|0E3F66: I promise to live up to your expectations.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F67: Shouldn't the ceremony be completed first?
Skyrim.esm|0E3F68: Glad to see they picked the right person for the job.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F69: We can get better acquainted once the ceremony's ended.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F6B: We can speak after the ceremony's done.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F6C: I can't think of a better person for the job, Nightingale.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F6D: After Brynjolf has installed you as Guild Master, we can speak at length.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F6E: I suppose congratulations are in order. Now don't screw it up.
Skyrim.esm|0E3F6F: Not now, you idiot. We'll talk after this ridiculous ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0E4099: Send the word. "A new day is dawning and the sun rises over Whiterun."
Skyrim.esm|0E409A: And I'm sure you could do it, too. Alright. This is it.
Skyrim.esm|0E409C: I've toured our camps. We're ready, Ulfric... Whenever you are.
Skyrim.esm|0E409D: I'm in no mood to joke.
Skyrim.esm|0E409E: You're certain we're ready? Whiterun's army will no doubt be bolstered with Legionnares. And those walls around Whiterun are old, but they still stand.
Skyrim.esm|0E409F: I'm not a fool, Proventus. I mean it's time to challenge Ulfric to face me as a man, or march his Stormcloaks up to the gates.
Skyrim.esm|0E40A1: No. But neither is every man able to give that order when he must. But you are that man, Ulfric.  You've been that man before, and you'll be him again. And these men and women - they call themselves Stormcloaks because they believe in you... They're the meanest, toughest sons of bitches Skyrim has to offer. And they want this. They want this as much as you do. Perhaps they want it more.
Skyrim.esm|0E40A2: He'll do no such thing! A dagger in the back is all you could expect!
Skyrim.esm|0E40A5: Whiterun is only a means to an end.
Skyrim.esm|0E40A6: Then I was wrong about him.
Skyrim.esm|0E40A7: Aye, and the sons of Skyrim will greet that dawn teeth and swords flashing.
Skyrim.esm|0E40A8: You can return this axe to our friend. The esteemed Jarl of Windhelm has my answer. Make sure he gets it.
Skyrim.esm|0E40AA: Then I commit them to whatever gods they still believe in. Talos be with you!
Skyrim.esm|0E40AB: We're ready. And I might be old myself, but I'll kick those damn walls down with my bare feet! - if you would only ask me to do it!
Skyrim.esm|0E40AC: Make haste to our camp in Whiterun. I want you on the front lines. I have a feeling about you. Your place is on that battlefield. I need you there.
Skyrim.esm|0E40AD: Did he now? The man is persistent, I'll give him that.
Skyrim.esm|0E40AE: We can talk about that once this dragon is dealt with.
Skyrim.esm|0E40AF: Ah yes. I suppose it's time I gave him an answer...
Skyrim.esm|0E40B0: Is any man ever ready to give the order that will mean the deaths of many.
Skyrim.esm|0E40B1: Fight well, or die well. Talos be with you!
Skyrim.esm|0E40B2: But until that dragon's dealt with, Ulfric's just going to have to wait. For now, I'll hold onto this axe. But it's a tenuous peace at best.
Skyrim.esm|0E40B3: I suppose it's time I give him an answer...
Skyrim.esm|0E40B4: Give the word, my lord, and Whiterun is yours.
Skyrim.esm|0E41B6: Try casting a spell at the wall. See what happens!
Skyrim.esm|0E482E: I'll already told that buffoon I'm not paying you people a single coin!
Skyrim.esm|0E48FA: Go! We'll follow when we can!
Skyrim.esm|0E48FB: I had a bad fall in the mine when I was bringing some food to the men. I was pretty bad off for a while, but a priest of Mara was passing through on his way to Dawnstar and healed me to the best of his abilities. I'm still pretty sore and can't really walk too far without having to rest for a while.
Skyrim.esm|0E48FC: It was nice to spend time with someone who enjoys archery as much as I do.
Skyrim.esm|0E4905: Finally, we can reopen the mine and put Shor's Stone back on the map! Here, please accept this gift as a token of our gratitude.
Skyrim.esm|0E49A5: I can handle training one more. After that, I think we'll be ready for a little dragon hunting of our own.
Skyrim.esm|0E49A8: You would? Well then, I would insist on rewarding you for your time. Here, take my satchel and give it to my father, Verner. He'll likely have something for you to bring back.
Skyrim.esm|0E49A9: It's full of spiders, that's what's wrong with it! Almost killed Grogmar and me when they showed up. I'm worried that they'll come out of the mine looking for food soon... then we're in even bigger trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0E49AA: It's a shame our daughter couldn't make the journey herself, but at least she still thinks of us.
Skyrim.esm|0E49AB: My parents live there. I usually make the journey to bring them gifts and to just say hello, but I haven't been well lately. I've written some letters and placed them in a satchel. Perhaps you wouldn't mind delivering it to them on my behalf?
Skyrim.esm|0E49E2: They're as useless as a fifth wheel on a wagon. Gave me some sort of a line about "keeping an eye out for enemy soldiers." What's the point in protecting this place if the mine's completely useless? Idiots.
Skyrim.esm|0E49E3: Wait... you mean you killed every last one of them? I can't believe it!
Skyrim.esm|0E49E4: I never asked you to help in the first place. Why would I? Everyone in Skyrim only helps themselves.
Skyrim.esm|0E49E5: Ah, yes... seems he sent back my satchel stuffed with letters. I look forward  to reading them. I appreciate the delivery, here... I want you to have this. I insist.
Skyrim.esm|0E49E6: Tell you what. Keep to your word, and I'll line your pockets with as much as I can scrape up.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A03: Redbelly Mine gets its name from the red mist filling the place. You get used to it after a while... kind of smells like the hot springs out in the tundra. No idea what's causing it though.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A04: Mining iron takes a lot of strength and special reinforced tools. I must have broken five or six pickaxes in the last few months alone. But now that I've got Rocksplinter here, I can cut through stone like a hot knife through butter.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A05: I might be a woman, but I can crack rock with the best of them. Be careful in there, you can take a nasty spill.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A06: Damn place is filled with this reddish mist. Can't see more than ten feet in front of your face. But when you can sniff out a vein of iron like me, it isn't too much trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A08: I don't want to be responsible for sending you to your death. Be careful in the mine.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A09: Don't kid yourself... stone runs in our blood.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A0A: May the road favor the foolish...
Skyrim.esm|0E4A0B: Darkwater Crossing is a difficult journey. Mind yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A0D: Fortune to you and your children.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A0E: The spiders put you through the ringer?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A0F: I hope you haven't arrived here looking for work in the mine.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A10: What brings you out to Shor's Stone?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A11: Mining's my life now, I wouldn't want to do anything else.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A12: Would you happen to be headed towards Darkwater Crossing?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A14: I suppose you're used to traveling, being a stranger around here.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A15: Takes a lot of strength to swing a pick axe, know what I mean?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A2C: Thank you. Your Shield-Siblings have withdrawn to the Underforge. I think they're waiting for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A2E: Ah, I think that'll do just the trick.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A2F: You'll need a little more coin than what you're floatin' around with.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A35: Oh, but the Dwemer had more than even Septimus expected. This will take time to decryptify. Be on your way, and Septimus will find  you if he has further need.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A36: To glimpse the world inside an Elder Scroll can damage the eyes. Or the mind, as it has to Septimus. The Dwemer found a loophole, as they always do. To focus the knowledge away and inside without harm. Place the lexicon into their contraption and focus the knowings into it. When it brims with glow, bring it back and Septimus can read once more.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A37: The deepest doors of Dwemer listen for singing. It plays the attitude of notes proper for opening. Can you not hear it? Too low for hearings?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A38: You see this masterwork of the Dwemer. Deep inside their greatest knowings. Septimus is clever among men, but he is but an idiot child compared to the dullest of the Dwemer. Lucky then they left behind their own way of reading the Elder Scrolls. In the depths of Blackreach one yet lies. Have you heard of Blackreach? "Cast upon where Dwemer cities slept, the yearning spire hidden learnings kept."
Skyrim.esm|0E4A39: Oh, a brutish one. Septimus has no fear of you. But as one block raises another, perhaps ourselves could help us each.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A3A: I think her mother, Tova, has the key.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A3B: Her mother would have had the key, but she... that family is cursed, I tell you.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A3C: One block lifts the other. Septimus will give what you want, but you must bring him something in return.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A3D: Now, how would you like me to align the Phial's properties?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A3E: I'm not as skilled as Curalmil, so I'm afraid it will only ever refill with one type of liquid.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A3F: It's just one of those things that happens. The lungs just... give up. Even elves get old.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A40: What's it matter to you? Not like you could do anything about it.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A43: The old man had one wish before he died. And he didn't get it. It's as simple as that.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A44: That's fine for you. But he wanted to be clean. He wanted to meet Ysgramor and know the glories of Sovngarde. But all that was taken from him.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A45: No, Farkas, he didn't. And that's not what this is about. We should be honoring Kodlak, no matter our own thoughts on the blood.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A46: Kodlak used to speak of a way to cleanse his soul, even in death. You know the legends of the Tomb of Ysgramor.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A47: Is that? Did you repair the blade?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A48: Being moon-born is not so much of a curse as you might think, Vilkas.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A4A: You're right. It's what he wanted, and he deserved to have it.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A4B: There the souls of the Harbingers will heed the call of northern steel.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A4C: We can't even enter the tomb without Wuuthrad, and it's in pieces, like it has been for a thousand years.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A4D: Kodlak did not care for vengeance.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A4E: And dragons were just stories. And the elves once ruled Skyrim. Just because something is, doesn't mean it must be. The blade is a weapon. A tool. Tools are meant to be broken. And repaired.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A4F: This is the first time I've had all the pieces, thanks to our Shield-Brother here.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A50: This is the first time I've had all the pieces, thanks to our Shield-Sister here.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A51: "The flames of a hero can reforge the shattered." The flames of Kodlak shall fuel the rebirth of Wuuthrad. And now it will take you to meet him once more.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A52: The rest of you, prepare to journey to the Tomb of Ysgramor. For Kodlak.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A53: As the one who bore the fragments, I think you should be the one to carry Wuuthrad into battle.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A54: I can't go any further, Shield-Brother.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A55: I can't go any further, Shield-Sister.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A60: Ever since Dustman's Cairn, the big crawly ones have been too much for me. Everyone has his weakness, and this one is mine. I'm not proud, but I will stay back with Vilkas. Give my regards to Ysgramor.
Skyrim.esm|0E4A81: Did I hear right? Did he say you were to lead the Companions?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A87: Little fool's going to experience the hard reality of Skyrim justice, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0E4A88: Just need to gather my tools and finish up a couple of things. No worries - I'll see to Cicero, make sure that wagon gets fixed.
Skyrim.esm|0E4C8D: I'm just surprised. But your strength and honor are apparent to all.
Skyrim.esm|0E4C8F: You've earned the right. Your strength and honor are apparent to all.
Skyrim.esm|0E4C91: And it's my honor to be first to address you as Harbinger.
Skyrim.esm|0E4C93: More of those things. Bah. Viola Giordano's been putting them all around the city. Frankly, whoever keeps taking them down should get a medal.
Skyrim.esm|0E4C95: Oh, Friga Shatter-Shield's old place? Well, since she's not using it anymore, Ulfric might just let you have it, if you have the money. And if he likes you. Or you could head over to the family and see if they'll let you in. But I wouldn't want to do that myself. Bad luck over there.
Skyrim.esm|0E4C99: Doesn't mean anything to me. Got an unusual shine to it, though. I'd take it to Calixto. He's got a good eye for pieces like that.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CAF: I don't know if I've ever seen any of those before. I've heard about them, though. Sorry, can't help you.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC0: Sorry, I don't know anything about those kinds of things.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC1: Not very good quality, is it? Probably came from the Gray Quarter. Have a talk with Revyn Sadri. He usually knows what's going in and out of that place. Thankfully that means I don't have to.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC2: Looks like something one of the dark elves would wear. Ask Revyn Sadri, he probably sold whatever it used to be.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC4: Nothing anybody I know would wear. Maybe ask one of the Dark Elves, they usually have some kind of rag falling off of them. The one who sells things, what's his name. Oh, yes! Revyn Sadri. Maybe he'd know something.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC5: Of course, of course. But such things are valuable, and usually falling apart anyway. I don't see why someone would actually use them. Although... that court wizard does all manner of experiments. And I know he owns some truly strange things. Nobody else would even know which end to hold. If I were you, I'd go straight to the steward with this information.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC6: Oh, good, more of those. Did you know Viola Giordano wanted to put one of them on my front door? "Everyone will see it!" she said. Of course! That was my problem with it. If you want to ask her about them, good luck. But I'm not going near her.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CC7: Are you joking? Even I wouldn't sell something like that. They definitely have some magic in them, I can tell you that much. But it's nothing I know about.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CCD: Please, tell me you have good news.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CCF: Now that's interesting. Tell me more.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CD1: Well, come back when you have some more evidence. I think you're on the trail, though.
Skyrim.esm|0E4CD3: So what do you think about it all, then?
Skyrim.esm|0E4CD4: I hate to admit it, but you're probably right.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D5F: Blackreach... why does that sound so familiar? Of course! It's where my mentor, Sinderion said he was going to do his field research. It's been... years I think, but perhaps you could still find him.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D60: Yes. He was an absolute genius when it came to the nirnroot. Sinderion made it his life's work, developing all sorts of interesting concoctions.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D61: Crimson Nirnroot? I'd heard such a strain existed, but never imagined I'd see any in my lifetime. Where did you find this?
Skyrim.esm|0E4D62: I used to be. There isn't much time for that in my life anymore I'm afraid. I actually owned a shop in Vivec City long ago, but I had to leave all that behind when the Red Mountain erupted. Perhaps someday I'll reopen a shop here in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D63: I knew this day would eventually come. At least poor Sinderion died doing what he loved, his field research. I told the old fool he should take it easy, but he was too stubborn. Perhaps someday I'll continue where he left off, it's the least I can do considering how much he did for me.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D64: One day, these beautiful plants will make us famous and we can finally move off of this farm... thanks to Sinderion.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D65: They're beautiful. I can see why he wanted to find them.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D66: Remember Uncle Sinderion? These are the plants he was searching for... the Crimson Nirnroot.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D67: One day he simply ran out to me in the field, excited that he'd made some sort of a breakthrough and he was going to head out on another trip. He never returned. From the journal you discovered, I'm sure you know the rest.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D68: Where did all these pretty red plants come from?
Skyrim.esm|0E4D69: Well, after I fled Morrowind during the eruption, I made my way here... my family had purchased this farm and its all we had left. Years later, Sinderion simply showed up at our doorstep asking to stay for a while... said he was going to do field research. He stayed with us for decades... became an uncle of sorts to Aduri.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D6A: Thank you. You've done alchemy a great service by helping to complete Sinderion's work. I'd like to give you this tome...it's one of Sinderion's greatest works. I believe you've more than earned it.
Skyrim.esm|0E4D6B: When I was very young, I was sent to Skingrad in Cyrodiil to study with him. He was a strange Altmer... paid more attention to his research than his own well-being. He lived in the basement of an inn for goodness sakes!
Skyrim.esm|0E4D6C: You've gathered quite a few of them I see. I'll search through Sinderion's notes and find some more information about the Crimson Nirnroot and its properties.
Skyrim.esm|0E4EBB: Ah. Of course. Not everyone has an appreciation for the artistic wonders of the world.
Skyrim.esm|0E5021: Well, you're certainly making a name for yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E502C: Well, there were eight of them and she'd already killed three by the time I joined the fight. Together, we made short work of them and the last one limped away licking its wounds.
Skyrim.esm|0E502F: Well, after a while my father caught on to what I was doing. He confronted me one night and gave me a choice.
Skyrim.esm|0E5035: I didn't have a choice. When the Red Mountain erupted in Morrowind, the Tong fled... spread to the nine winds. We made a pact that one day, we might reunite... but I believe that day is long in coming.
Skyrim.esm|0E5036: I didn't mean to imply I was earning an honest living either. With my lock picking aptitude, I was a natural at jailbreaking... made a great deal of gold doing it too.
Skyrim.esm|0E5037: It's kind of an amusing tale. I was making my way down the road from Ivarstead towards Riften, and I saw Vex fighting off a couple of wolves.
Skyrim.esm|0E503B: I hooked up with a guild in Valenwood... I think they called themselves the Silver Crescents. Spent quite a few years doing jobs for them. Made a lot of coin, but I didn't care. I didn't really need it... I was running with them because it fit, made me feel alive.
Skyrim.esm|0E503C: No, we usually let them go. I refused to do it, and Garthek ordered the clan to kill me as well. Luckily, I had made some friends with the clan who immediately sided with me. We tore each other to pieces. After it was over, those of us that remained simply went our separate ways.
Skyrim.esm|0E503F: Pull up a chair, my friend... this is quite a tale. When I started out in this business, I wasn't really interested in the Guild or being a thief.
Skyrim.esm|0E5046: Because it was dull. Every day was the same boring routine; working at the winery, social visits with friends, parties with no one I cared about. I just wanted a little excitement. Something dangerous.
