Skyrim.esm|0E5049: We raided a caravan one spring... I think it was a few wagons with some farmers moving to a new village. They didn't put up much of a fight. All that was left were the women and the children. Then the leader of our clan... Garthek... he ordered us to kill the rest.
Skyrim.esm|0E504F: No. In fact, the Tong were quite the bitter rivals with the Dark Brotherhood. Where they operate within the shadows, we operated on the side of justice.
Skyrim.esm|0E5050: Maybe one day, just not today.
Skyrim.esm|0E5053: I used to live in Valenwood working at my father's winery... we made the finest wine to ever cross your lips I promise you. We were doing well, plenty of coin, a huge mansion and I was even betrothed to a lovely young woman.
Skyrim.esm|0E5059: We were an assassins guild that was sanctioned by the Empire to provide public as well as private executions. We did this in the name of Mephala, whom I used to quite fervently worship.
Skyrim.esm|0E505A: Over time, I managed to gain their confidence. Then one night I grabbed a knife, waited until they fell asleep and cut their throats. I never returned to that pig farm you know. There's nothing for me there. So that's my sad story. What do you think?
Skyrim.esm|0E505D: Actually, the fisherman who found me, the man I call my father gave it to me. Thought it was fitting I suppose. I never changed it, because it never felt right to do so.
Skyrim.esm|0E5062: Well, I had forgotten we had our horses tied up just outside of Windhelm... Vex rode hers back and arrived hours before I did. So, that's how I earned the name. Now keep it to yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E5063: Don't get me wrong. The first few years with that clan were some of the best years of my life. All the food I could eat, all the wine I could drink and all the women I could bed.
Skyrim.esm|0E5064: It's fine... I had to tell someone I suppose. Carrying around a weight like that... it hurts after a while. Cuts you inside like a dagger to the heart.
Skyrim.esm|0E506C: Vex was waiting for me at the Flagon when I came in... drenched in sweat. Everyone just took a look at me and laughed.
Skyrim.esm|0E506F: I joined up with them maybe ten years ago. Small clan... maybe twenty men. We'd raid villages, rob caravans... but it was always about the killing for them.
Skyrim.esm|0E5070: Morrowind of course. Any Dunmer worth their weight in ash would tell you the same. I used to be a member of the Morang Tong.
Skyrim.esm|0E5076: Oh, wait... it gets much better. How about the fact that our farm was attacked by bandits, and that they killed my entire family who didn't even brandish a weapon against them. Here's the best part. They took me as a prize, and violated me for a fortnight. Tossed me from bandit to bandit like... like...
Skyrim.esm|0E5079: Perhaps. They could be nonsense... inane scribbles done by someone in idle boredom. But if not... if they actually mean something, they might tell me where I'm from... what ship was I on. Everything.
Skyrim.esm|0E507A: Are you kidding me? Vex lost them in seconds... once she steps into the shadows, she vanishes. Me? I ran... and I ran... straight through the gates of Windhelm and all the way back to Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0E5080: I don't know. I think I realized that out in the world, my skills would bring me more wealth as a thief rather than an assassin. Killing someone in a jail is much easier than what the Dark Brotherhood deals with. I guess I wanted to play it safe. I already knew Delvin, I asked if I could join up, and that was that.
Skyrim.esm|0E5083: Something told me I wouldn't regret it. I had heard the Guild was back on its feet again and most of Skyrim under its belt. I'll tell you, I wouldn't want to cross blades with Vex again, but I certainly wouldn't mind a bit of a scuffle...
Skyrim.esm|0E5084: Look, you want to know about me? All right, I'll tell you. I'll tell you about the time I was a young girl, barely out of her teens... living on a pig farm in the middle of nowhere. Didn't have a coin to spend between our entire family. Ate the same slop we fed our livestock.
Skyrim.esm|0E508A: No one does. I've even taken the damn thing to the College of Winterhold. I must have spent every last coin I've made with the Guild trying to find out what it means.
Skyrim.esm|0E508D: Well it was, up until the point where we set foot outside. The house was surrounded by town guard... the client had ratted us out. Vex just tells me to run, so I did. Half of them went after me, the other half went after her.
Skyrim.esm|0E508E: The last jailbreak I attempted failed. I was imprisoned in High Rock for three years before they let me go. After that I promised myself I'd never do it again.
Skyrim.esm|0E5097: Indeed. After a few minutes, she looked me dead in the eye and asked me if I wanted to join the Guild. Just like that, no apologies.
Skyrim.esm|0E5098: Look, I don't really know you. I don't even really know anyone here. Why do you care anyway? It's not like we're family. This is a business.
Skyrim.esm|0E5099: My father told me he found me as a young boy in the wreckage of a ship that sank off of the coast near Solitude. All he found in my pocket was a tiny smooth stone inscribed with some sort of strange runes.
Skyrim.esm|0E509A: It was on a job a few years back... it was supposed to be a simple burglary on a house in Windhelm. I was working with Vex and we got inside with no trouble. We found the loot, and made our way out.
Skyrim.esm|0E509B: I should hope so. The Dark Brotherhood is also known to place contracts on those who associate with the Morang Tong as well. So, unless you wish a visit from one of their own, I'd suggest you follow your own advice and keep your mouth shut.
Skyrim.esm|0E509C: Well, as a jailbreaker, you work alone. No Guild to back you up. I'd do jobs for the Thieves Guild and the Dark Brotherhood, but if things didn't go as planned, I was on my own.
Skyrim.esm|0E509D: Actually, that's when she politely asked me to hand over my coin purse. Can you imagine? We scuffled for a while, broke each other's weapons, and finally fell to the ground exhausted.
Skyrim.esm|0E509F: I ended up in Skyrim thanks to a contact I'd made when I was with the Crescents... good old Delvin. He introduced me to Gallus, and that was it. I've been here ever since. And you know what? Despite what I left behind, I don't regret it one bit.
Skyrim.esm|0E50A0: I left his head on a pike at the wreckage of the caravan. Never knew what became of the rest of the clan that survived. I suppose they moved on just like I did.
Skyrim.esm|0E50A3: Have you taken leave of your senses? If the Dark Brotherhood discovered I had been a Morang Tong, they would come after me with a vengeance. Better to stay out of sight and ply my trade with the Thieves Guild than risk a contract on my head.
Skyrim.esm|0E50A4: It's where a client pays you to get arrested, and you get thrown into a prison for the express purpose of breaking out. Usually it's to free someone the client cares about... and sometimes to... well, to kill someone on their behalf. Either way, the trick was in the escape, and that's where my strengths came in handy.
Skyrim.esm|0E50A5: Pull up a seat and have a drink.
Skyrim.esm|0E50A7: You're making waves around here. I like that. If you ever want to talk about anything, you let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0E50A9: Hey, how's it going. Need pickpocket training?
Skyrim.esm|0E50AA: If you need pickpocket training, just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0E50AD: If you want to be a master pickpocket like me, I can get you there.
Skyrim.esm|0E50AE: If you wondering why they call me Vipir the Fleet, watch me outrun the town guard sometime.
Skyrim.esm|0E50AF: A lot of people are beginning to get impressed with you here. Me included. Just wanted to let you know.
Skyrim.esm|0E50B0: Interest you in some marksman training?
Skyrim.esm|0E50B1: If you need marksman training, and you've got the coin... I'm ready to teach you.
Skyrim.esm|0E50B4: Of course you can buy me a drink... you didn't need to say a word, but I heard you loud and clear.
Skyrim.esm|0E50B8: Look, if I was a little harsh before, I just wanted you to know it's because you were new. Know what I mean?
Skyrim.esm|0E50B9: Well, hey there. What can Sapphire do for you?
Skyrim.esm|0E50BA: Back from a job, huh? Hope it went well.
Skyrim.esm|0E50BD: They call me Sapphire because I love to steal them.
Skyrim.esm|0E50BE: Don't get me wrong, I'll steal any gem that isn't nailed down. But there's something about those blue stones that gets my blood boiling.
Skyrim.esm|0E50C1: When people ask my real name, know what I tell them? Don't ask.
Skyrim.esm|0E50C2: Sure I might have some dirt in my past, but that's just ancient history.
Skyrim.esm|0E50C3: I'm a deadeye marksman, lethal with a blade and dangerous in bed. And it only gets better from there.
Skyrim.esm|0E50C4: Last time someone cheated me, I cut off their hand. Still have it actually.
Skyrim.esm|0E50C5: Wanted to say something to you.
Skyrim.esm|0E50C6: I think you're all right. In fact, I'm kind of impressed how well you're doing around here. Just keep it between us, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0E50C9: Sure, we can talk a minute.
Skyrim.esm|0E50CA: I've seen a lot of thieves come and go, but I've never seen anyone as good as me.
Skyrim.esm|0E50CE: I could crack the lock on any house blindfolded before you even got your picks out of your pocket.
Skyrim.esm|0E50CF: Been in the Guild a long time... joined right after Brynjolf. Not interested in leadership though, that's more his kind of thing.
Skyrim.esm|0E50D0: I don't care if Vex was chosen as a Guild Third over me, she deserved it.
Skyrim.esm|0E50D3: I kind of like watching things from where I am. Making money is all I care about.
Skyrim.esm|0E50D4: Hey, I want to talk to you... now.
Skyrim.esm|0E50D6: You're pretty tough, I respect that. Bringing in a lot of coin for the Guild, making us look good too. Let me know if you need anything, okay?
Skyrim.esm|0E50D8: You want to talk to me? Okay.
Skyrim.esm|0E50D9: Yeah, I guess I have a minute. What do you want?
Skyrim.esm|0E50DA: I'm the heavy hitter around here. When someone needs pushing around, I'm the one they send in.
Skyrim.esm|0E50DD: Used to run with a bandit clan in The Pale. Turns out I didn't like them and they didn't like me, so we parted ways.
Skyrim.esm|0E50DE: Brynjolf pulled me in a few years ago. Said the Guild was lacking some muscle, so I joined up.
Skyrim.esm|0E50E0: I've busted heads for Maven Black-Briar when she wants to keep her name out of it. Pays good, so why not?
Skyrim.esm|0E50E1: I leave all that sneaking around nonsense to everyone else; believe me, when I'm coming for you... you'll know it.
Skyrim.esm|0E50E2: Got a minute? Wanted to say something.
Skyrim.esm|0E50E3: I've never seen anyone with skills like yours. I just wanted to let you know that if you need anything, you can talk to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E50E7: Something I can help with or do you just want to talk?
Skyrim.esm|0E50E8: My brother in crime... what'd you need?
Skyrim.esm|0E50EA: My sister in crime. What'd you need?
Skyrim.esm|0E50EB: Name's Rune. Yeah, rune... just like you're thinking.
Skyrim.esm|0E50EC: Have no idea what my birth name really is, and I frankly don't care.
Skyrim.esm|0E50ED: Being brought up by a poor family, I had to learn how to steal if I wanted to make ends meet.
Skyrim.esm|0E50EF: Brynjolf actually caught me trying to pick his pocket in the market. If he was anyone else I would have gotten away with it.
Skyrim.esm|0E50F0: I'm glad Brynjolf decided to let me join. Been trying to make some coin for him ever since.
Skyrim.esm|0E50F4: Hmph, so you're the one everyone's falling over.
Skyrim.esm|0E50F5: I don't know, you don't look so impressive to me. I mean, you're no Gray Fox, but you must be doing something right.
Skyrim.esm|0E50FA: Are you bothering me for a reason?
Skyrim.esm|0E5103: Name's Garthar. Remember it, because you're going to hear a lot about me.
Skyrim.esm|0E5104: Still learning the ropes, but I'll get the hang of it.
Skyrim.esm|0E5181: Been doing a few jobs for Vex... how about you?
Skyrim.esm|0E56B0: If you want to get paid, keep your weapon sheathed.
Skyrim.esm|0E56EB: No one dies. Remember that. We're not assassins.
Skyrim.esm|0E56FC: If you get arrested, it's on your head.
Skyrim.esm|0E5743: Keep to the shadows and you'll do fine.
Skyrim.esm|0E5758: I don't want a trail of dead bodies leading here. Keep it clean.
Skyrim.esm|0E5759: Get the job done and get back alive. That's all there is to it.
Skyrim.esm|0E5761: Don't give up now... there's plenty of loot out there just ripe for the taking.
Skyrim.esm|0E5762: Hey, I still have plenty of work to do here.
Skyrim.esm|0E5763: Walking away without taking a job is like passing a bloated coinpurse on a table.
Skyrim.esm|0E5767: You think you're finished? I've got plenty more where that came from.
Skyrim.esm|0E576D: Come on, take another job so we can both make some money.
Skyrim.esm|0E576E: These jobs aren't going to finish themselves you know.
Skyrim.esm|0E57A6: Hey, where do you think you're going? We have work to do.
Skyrim.esm|0E57A8: Don't forget your represntin' the Guild. Don't do anythin' stupid.
Skyrim.esm|0E57AE: Be careful out there... wouldn't want to lose such a promisin' cutpurse now, would we.
Skyrim.esm|0E57AF: Use your skills, not your blade and you'll do fine.
Skyrim.esm|0E57B0: No killin' or no pay. Remember that.
Skyrim.esm|0E57C9: Remember, keep it clean. We don't want blood on our hands.
Skyrim.esm|0E57D2: What does he want now? I already explained to him that you can't get blood from a stone.
Skyrim.esm|0E57D3: Wha... what? Oh, it's one of you people. So, Brynjolf doesn't even bother to show up himself anymore, eh? What's this message?
Skyrim.esm|0E57D4: I know you... everyone's been talking about it. What are you here for?
Skyrim.esm|0E57D6: Oh no! You're the one from Brynjolf's outfit, aren't you?
Skyrim.esm|0E57D7: If you're looking for a room, try the Bee and Barb. This place is for the working man.
Skyrim.esm|0E57DB: Yeah... Let's wait a little longer. Then...
Skyrim.esm|0E57DC: Something's wrong... Boss' been down there too long.
Skyrim.esm|0E57DF: Can I give you a little tip? Take every job you can get or else you'll end up a beggar.
Skyrim.esm|0E57EF: Hey, this guild needs the coin... walkin' away without takin' work is not going to make you popular around here.
Skyrim.esm|0E57F2: There's plenty more work for the likes of you.
Skyrim.esm|0E57F3: What's the matter, afraid of getting your hands dirty?
Skyrim.esm|0E57F5: Where you off to? There's work to be done.
Skyrim.esm|0E57FC: Only way this guilds going to continue to grow is by takin' extra work.
Skyrim.esm|0E5817: Find me if you're ever lookin' for some extra coin.
Skyrim.esm|0E584B: Finally. Now I can get back to work.
Skyrim.esm|0E58CD: Don't hurry back, I won't be waiting.
Skyrim.esm|0E58EC: Good luck on your next job.
Skyrim.esm|0E58F4: Off for some wine, eh? Drink one for me.
Skyrim.esm|0E590E: I hope we bump into each other again.
Skyrim.esm|0E5915: Maybe I'll see you around the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0E5919: If you ever need more practice with the bow, just grab me.
Skyrim.esm|0E594B: Come find me if you need more marksman training.
Skyrim.esm|0E594C: Need any more pickpocket training, you just let me know.
Skyrim.esm|0E5959: Come back and see me if you ever need pickpocket training.
Skyrim.esm|0E6529: I'm a Blade, now. I can hardly believe it.
Skyrim.esm|0E652A: By my honor, I pledge to rid Tamriel of dragons.
Skyrim.esm|0E66C9: Aye, that he was. And you sniffed him out because he broke one of the cardinal rules of our business... he got greedy. He was told to run the scam on merchants, but he smelled the gold on you and his judgment went out the window.
Skyrim.esm|0E66CB: It's been a pleasure doing business with you. Let me open the gate and you can head into the city.
Skyrim.esm|0E66CC: The gate's unlocked. You can head inside when you're ready.
Skyrim.esm|0E66CF: Don't want in, huh? Fine. Have a good walk to the next city.
Skyrim.esm|0E66D0: For the privilege of entering the city. What does it matter?
Skyrim.esm|0E66D2: All right, keep your voice down... you want everyone to hear you? I'll let you in, just let me unlock the gate.
Skyrim.esm|0E66D3: Okay, got another one for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E66D5: Killing him was a bit... impulsive. A true professional would have kept his cool and dealt with it a different way, but I'll let it go.
Skyrim.esm|0E66D6: Actually, I admire how you handled the situation. You kept your cool and you didn't kill him. That's the mark of a professional thief.
Skyrim.esm|0E66D7: Three guards run into a bar, but the fourth one just ducks. Why?
Skyrim.esm|0E66D8: Look how you sniffed out my little scheme at the North Gate. You knew it was a shakedown and you called him on it. That's what I'm talking about.
Skyrim.esm|0E66DB: Hold there. Before I let you into Riften, you need to pay the visitor's tax.
Skyrim.esm|0E66DC: Have you decided to pay the visitor's tax?
Skyrim.esm|0E66DF: Hold and stand down. If you want to get into Riften, use the North Gate. This one's closed.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E0: My orders are to tell the riff-raff to use the North Gate, that's it.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E1: No. You can use the North Gate, or you can head to the next city.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E2: Keep it. Not worth going to prison over.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E3: That's a quick way to the executioner's block.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E4: That's the North Gate in case you forget.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E6: If you want to enter Riften, use the North Gate. Otherwise, we have nothing to talk about.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E7: There's nothing else to discuss. Use the North Gate if you want to enter Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E8: You can use the North Gate to enter the city, or you can hit the road. Your choice.
Skyrim.esm|0E66E9: It's a long way to the next city if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0E6757: I'm sorry lass, I... I can't...
Skyrim.esm|0E67AA: Stay clear of that College, if you know what's good for you. Nothing but foul deeds behind those walls.
Skyrim.esm|0E67AB: It does. The College has caused enough problems; don't need you or anyone else adding to them. If you're not here for the College, then you've little other reason to be in Winterhold at all.
Skyrim.esm|0E67AC: What's your business here in Winterhold?
Skyrim.esm|0E67AD: Should've known. Not that it matters anymore. No one bothers coming to Winterhold for any other reason.
Skyrim.esm|0E67AE: Look around you. Winterhold is in this state because of those damned mages. They sit up there in their tower, doing gods-know-what, and who watches over them? The guards don't even dare go up there unless they've no choice.
Skyrim.esm|0E67AF: I do, and if you count yourself among their numbers then you've blood on your hands as well. There's nothing left of Winterhold. Nothing! Everyone knows it's the College's fault that the sea swallowed our city. Still they deny it, but we all know the truth.
Skyrim.esm|0E67D0: What's... what's happening... I can't stop myself.
Skyrim.esm|0E67D5: Yeah, sure it is. You don't fool me.
Skyrim.esm|0E67E7: I have. One of the old Akaviri records names a dragon living nearby. Shall we send the Blades to help you?
Skyrim.esm|0E67E9: Let me know when you are, then.
Skyrim.esm|0E67EB: It's good to see the Blades fighting alongside the Dragonborn once again. To think we're treading in the footsteps of thousands of years past. While you're out hunting, keep a sharp eye open for any dragon scales and bones you might find. I'd be interested in looking at them.
Skyrim.esm|0E67EE: Very well. He seems like a fighter to me. I'll give him the oath.
Skyrim.esm|0E67EF: You there. The Dragonborn wishes to give you the chance to join the lost guardians of Tamriel. The dragon slayers. The Blades. Do you wish to become a Blade?
Skyrim.esm|0E67F0: Are you willing to trade away all claims and titles of your former life? To live here and devote yourself to protecting Tamriel from danger?
Skyrim.esm|0E67F1: Then by my right as acting grandmaster, I name you a Blade, with all the privileges, rights, and burdens that brings. Godspeed.
Skyrim.esm|0E67F4: I am. The Blades of the 2nd Era hunted down many dragons in their day, and they have records of where the beasts lived. Only you can truly defeat these monsters, Dragonborn, but you do not have to do so alone. The Blades can help you. Just ask whenever you're ready to take on a dragon. We will send the Blades to help you fulfill your destiny.
Skyrim.esm|0E67F5: It's quiet for now, Dragonborn. Perhaps later we'll find where one of those serpents is hiding.
Skyrim.esm|0E67F6: You want this one to become a Blade? You're sure?
Skyrim.esm|0E686E: Fight it, Brynjolf... he's taken control of you!
Skyrim.esm|0E68A0: Sorry, lass... I've got important things to do. We'll speak another time.
Skyrim.esm|0E68B8: Sorry, lad... I've got important things to do. We'll speak another time.
Skyrim.esm|0E68B9: I'm busy right now, lass. We can talk later.
Skyrim.esm|0E68BC: I'm busy right now, lad. We can talk later.
Skyrim.esm|0E68C9: Take your time. I understand your hesitation.
Skyrim.esm|0E69F4: The Torpor should be in a tall bottle with dark liquid. If you find it, bring it to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E69F5: If you'll check the shelves around the balcony, I'll check around the lower level.
Skyrim.esm|0E69F7: Let's continue, we still have much to do.
Skyrim.esm|0E69FA: Come, we must get to the Skull and destroy it.
Skyrim.esm|0E69FD: I promise to answer all of your questions. Follow me.
Skyrim.esm|0E69FE: This way... the library isn't far.
Skyrim.esm|0E6BC6: The laboratory adjoins the library. I'm hoping we'll find a sample of the Torpor left undamaged.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C24: If you change your mind, I'll be here.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C25: Once we get inside, all will become clear.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C26: We mustn't tarry... the Skull needs to be destroyed as soon as possible.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C30: Everyone on me. Let's show these Imperials what we're made of! Rrrrraaaaaarrrrr!!!!!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C31: All right men. On me! For the Emperor! For his Legionnaires! Rrrrrraaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhh!!!!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C32: Ready now! Everyone, with me! For the sons and daughters of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C33: I remember you... you were with us at Helgen. I heard you joined us. Welcome to the Stormcloaks! Ooh... This place really chills the blood, eh?
Skyrim.esm|0E6C36: But no! We are farmers! We are craftsmen! We are sons and daughters of shopkeepers, maid servants and soldiers!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C37: This is the day we send a message to Ulfric Stormcloak and the rebel Jarls who support him.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C38: On this day, our enemy will know the fullness of our determination, the true depth of our anger, and the exalted righteousness of our cause.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C39: Ulfric thinks he can hide behind the walls of his castle. But we will fight our way in and drag him out through the rubble to face justice.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C3E: Aaaahhhhhrrrr.... the pain, somebody, stop... the pain...
Skyrim.esm|0E6C40: What we do here today, we do for our country! For all the true Nords of Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C41: But make no mistake. What we do here today, we do for Skyrim and her people. By cutting out the disease of this rebellion, we will make this country whole again!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C42: Fear neither pain, nor darkness. For Sovngarde awaits those who die with weapons in their hands, and courage in their hearts.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C43: But they are no match for Legionnaires. You are the best and the brightest warriors in Tamriel. Professional soldiers, fearless and devastating. The Emperor will be paying close attention to what happens here today. Men who distinguish themselves will be well rewarded.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C44: They say that our cause is false and that we are nothing more than thieves, thugs and murderers!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C45: This is an important day for the Empire and for the Legion. And for all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C46: We come to this moment carried by the sacrifices and courage of our fellows. Those who have fallen. And those still bearing the shields to our right.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C47: All right. It's time to deliver the final blow to the Stormcloak rebellion. You have all fought bravely, and sacrificed much to bring us to this point.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C52: And we have come this far because our cause is true. Because we fight as one. And because our hearts are bursting with anger!
Skyrim.esm|0E6C53: That message is this. Any king who denies the Emperor will wake one morning to face the Legion staring down his gates. And on that day, we will confiscate his city, and restore the rule of law to his lands.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C54: The gods are watching. The spirits of our ancestors are stirring. And men under suns yet to dawn will be transformed by what we do here today.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C55: Because this is the end for them, the Stormcloaks will fight like cornered rats. They will be fierce and crafty.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C5A: I remember you... You were with us at Helgen... I heard you came around and picked the right side. Welcome to the Legion! I'm glad you're with us. I don't like the looks of this place... and I'm not talking about the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C7D: It was important we reclaimed the Reach. Letting Ulfric control the flow of silver was a mistake. One I'm glad we've finally corrected.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C7E: Reclaiming Hjaalmarch lets me worry less about the rear guard, and push more men to the front. It was a mistake to let Ulfric control it for so long.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C82: By taking back Falkreath, we control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. We should never have given it up.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C83: I'm glad we've finally restored the Pale. It was extremely foolish giving the Empire control of another port.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C84: I'm glad we've taken back Winterhold. While the Imperials were up there, important resources were tied up stiffening our northern border.
Skyrim.esm|0E6C85: I'm glad we liberated the Rift. It was dangerous having Imperials skulking about on the southern border of Eastmarch.
Skyrim.esm|0E6CA7: I don't know how you convinced Ulfric to talk to the Empire. Worse than a waste of time if you ask me.
Skyrim.esm|0E6CA8: Talking peace with Ulfric? You must be an optimist.
Skyrim.esm|0E6CB3: They've barricades to block us, but we'll tear through them and the rebels behind them!
Skyrim.esm|0E6CB4: They'll have barricades set, destroy them quickly. Keep pressing forward.
Skyrim.esm|0E6CB5: The inns at Dragonbridge and Solitude are frequent stops for Imperial runners. See if you can't "convince" one of those innkeepers to help you. One way or another, get me those documents.
Skyrim.esm|0E6CC5: Get over to the capital! We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6CC6: Get over to Markarth. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6CEE: Get over to Morthal. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D25: Get over to Falkreath. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D54: Who are they, daddy? Where are they going?
Skyrim.esm|0E6D55: You need to go inside, little cub.
Skyrim.esm|0E6D68: Get over to Whiterun. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D6A: Get over to Dawnstar. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D6B: Get over to Winterhold. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D6C: Get over to Riften. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D6D: Get over to Windhelm. We're mounting our final attack!
Skyrim.esm|0E6D6E: Get down to the front line.
Skyrim.esm|0E6D72: Get down there and join Rikke on the front line.
Skyrim.esm|0E6D9B: Get down there and join Galmar on the front line.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DAC: We're not quite ready to muster the final attack.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DAD: I need you to create some chaos in the hold so I can maneuver men into position without drawing too much attention.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DCF: Come now. You did more than just survive all those years. Your evasion of the Thalmor would be useful for future Blades to study.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD0: You're in luck. There is a blessing the Blades used on the eve of battle. It's supposed to prepare the mind for slaying dragons. I doubt the blessing's effects last for very long, so ask whenever you're heading out, and I'll perform the proper rites.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD2: Have you ever heard of the expression, "the scale of the Argonian that bit you?" No, I guess not. Well, the principle applies here. It's a potion, made from those dragon parts. Give it a try.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD3: The other Blades? No. Best not to think what the Thalmor do to their victims, my dear.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD4: Nonsense. Is a child who learns to read "not a scholar" of literature? Is a smith's apprentice "not a scholar" of blacksmithing?
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD6: I've been writing some notes about all the Akaviri designs we've discovered. If only the Imperial Library could see my research.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD7: The architecture of this temple is marvelous. Have you ever seen stonework like this, Delphine?
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD8: Do you ever think about them, Esbern? The others?
Skyrim.esm|0E6DD9: Now, now my dear. I'm talking about academia, not politics.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DDA: What? Your heroic tale of locking yourself in the Ratway for years isn't enough?
Skyrim.esm|0E6DDB: Good. The dragon's soul should be making you stronger. Come back another time, and we'll track down another one for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DDC: You're right. Sorry to bring it up.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DDD: I'm not a scholar, Esbern. How many times do I have to tell you?
Skyrim.esm|0E6DDE: I've been chronicling the recent history of the Blades. What would you like your entry to say?
Skyrim.esm|0E6DDF: "May the scales of the dragonkind splinter at the touch of your arrows and crack under the weight of your sword." That's all there is to it, Dragonborn. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DE0: Thank you, friend. Don't worry. I'll have that shiv ready for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DE1: Well, I'm "not a scholar" of architecture. And that's that.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DE2: Esbern, if the Empire could even acknowledge the Blades existence, we'd all have reason to celebrate.
Skyrim.esm|0E6DFD: I need you to create some chaos in the hold.
Skyrim.esm|0E6E18: Search out enemy soldiers in the wilderness, or guarding towns and settlements, and kill them.
Skyrim.esm|0E6E19: Find enemy controlled resources like sawmills, flour-mills, and smelters, and sabotage them.
Skyrim.esm|0E6E1A: And take out resources like sawmills, flour-mills, and smelters, with a little sabotage.
Skyrim.esm|0E6E1B: A god? Assuredly not! Gods are weak and ineffectual. When was the last time you heard or saw evidence of the Aedra in the world? No, we Daedra are far more powerful than any god. And among my brethren, I am the most feared.
Skyrim.esm|0E6E31: If you run across any enemy couriers, take them down, and bring me whatever they're carrying.
Skyrim.esm|0E706B: Excellent. These should come in handy.
Skyrim.esm|0E7183: I would hate to think what would happen if this fell into enemy hands.
Skyrim.esm|0E7194: Here. Take this for your trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0E7199: So why'd you join up with the Legion?
Skyrim.esm|0E7203: What are your thoughts on all this?
Skyrim.esm|0E7221: My father was a Legionnaire, and his before him. I guess I never considered doing anything else.
Skyrim.esm|0E7231: I signed on 'cause I couldn't watch the Stormcloaks tearing everything apart and not do something. But, most of my family disagrees with my choice.
Skyrim.esm|0E724D: I know it's what everyone says, "To go to new places and see the world." But it's true in my case... I never bargained on seeing more than I liked.
Skyrim.esm|0E724E: So, why'd you join the Stormcloaks?
Skyrim.esm|0E724F: What are your thoughts on all this?
Skyrim.esm|0E7251: My cousin disappeared one night. Some say the Thalmor grabbed him. It wasn't long before I found myself under Ulfric's banner.
Skyrim.esm|0E7252: I don't want some snotty elf telling me what gods I can and can't worship.
Skyrim.esm|0E7253: I'm a true Nord. Simple as that.
Skyrim.esm|0E7254: Get over to our camp in Hjaalmarch. We need to strengthen our defensive position. I trust you've come to see the error handing the hold over to the rebels?
Skyrim.esm|0E7255: Make your way to our camp in Falkreath. We're going to take it back. And don't think I've forgotten that you were the one that gave it to Ulfric in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|0E7256: Hurry over to our military camp tucked away in the Reach. Ulfric's been enjoying those silver mines for far too long. And, soldier. You better help win me back Markarth, or die trying. It was your brilliant strategy to give it over to the rebels in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|0E7257: Get over to our camp in the Pale. It's time we bring them back into the fold. And don't think for a moment I've forgotten it was you that lost us the hold in the first place.
Skyrim.esm|0E7258: Get up to Winterhold. We're going to take it back. I know you thought we weren't losing much when you gave it to the enemy. But I'm wasting troops guarding the border that belong on the front lines.
Skyrim.esm|0E7259: Make your way to our hidden camp in the Rift. I can't continue to push forward when I'm worried about Eastmarch's southern border. I hold you responsible for this situation. So, you better win me back Riften, or die trying.
Skyrim.esm|0E725A: This is it men! It's time to make this city ours!
Skyrim.esm|0E728D: You see, all you need is a bit of spirit and any song can be made into a classic.
Skyrim.esm|0E728E: No but my husband Torygg would want a proper burial and this is the way Nords are buried in Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E728F: Let's try a lute piece. Follow along...
Skyrim.esm|0E7290: Come back to me if you change your mind.
Skyrim.esm|0E7291: Aia. You're so mean. I don't think he means anything by it.
Skyrim.esm|0E7292: That's all for today. You're dismissed.
Skyrim.esm|0E7293: That's all right. Return to me if you change your mind though.
Skyrim.esm|0E7363: Wonderful. Just speak to various bards outside of the college, get there parts of the Poetic Edda and return them here to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E736D: We need coin, Ranmir, and you're not bringing home any.
Skyrim.esm|0E736E: And you'd have me do what, join the College and prance about casting spells all day?
Skyrim.esm|0E737F: You should tell him to mind his own business.
Skyrim.esm|0E7398: I have responsibilities to attend to. We'll continue this discussion some other time.
Skyrim.esm|0E739C: Thank you. It would mean a lot to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E739D: The Poetic Edda is never truly complete. As a living history of Skyrim we Bards continually add parts to it. In fact, If you collected parts of the Edda from any Bard you encountered in your travels I would be willing to pay you for them.
Skyrim.esm|0E73A0: Ataf keeps making eyes at me.
Skyrim.esm|0E73AF: Read carefully. You will see there are ways to stay true to the essence, Illdi.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B1: I think someone has a crush on Illdi.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B3: Come back to me if you find anything.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B4: That's all I ask. With your help, we have a chance to make a better life for ourselves here. For that, I thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B5: Falk told me what you did for us at Wolfskull cave. He says you're someone we can trust. As you may know Talos worship is outlawed in the Empire. When we buried my husband I made offerings to all the gods... except Talos. I would like you to take an item of his, a warhorn handed down from his father, and place it at a Shrine of Talos.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B6: You've seen how we live -- cramped alleys, run-down buildings, few guard patrols. Even the name "Gray Quarter" is an insult.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B7: Yes, when we buried my husband I wasn't able to make an offering to Talos. I was hoping you would to take something of his, a warhorn handed down from his father, and place it at a Shrine of Talos.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B8: You're a war hero, Brunwulf. Ulfric will listen to you.
Skyrim.esm|0E73B9: Wonderful. Soon we'll have the latest section of the Edda complete. Keep up the good work.
Skyrim.esm|0E73BA: That's it! You've collected every last verse. I can't thank you enough. This deserves a special bonus.
Skyrim.esm|0E73BB: Can we really just make things up? It doesn't seem right.
Skyrim.esm|0E73BC: I think Illdi's better than Dean Pantea actually knows. She just gets intimidated when she's in class and can't sing.
Skyrim.esm|0E73F9: Just one bottle per customer. Except for the man who saved the festival. Here's a couple bottles for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E73FA: This time let's try "The Age of Aggression". I will demonstrate for the class.
Skyrim.esm|0E74EA: This time let's try "The Age of Oppression". I will demonstrate for the class.
Skyrim.esm|0E74EB: Falk said I could count on you. You have more than earned the right to purchase property and take the next step to becoming a Thane. Speak to Falk when you have the funds. And thank you.
Skyrim.esm|0E74EC: Seems like most of these are nine parts fiction.
Skyrim.esm|0E75ED: I see you but I don't see Frost. Come back when you have the horse with you.
Skyrim.esm|0E768D: I believe you know what needs to be done. Something about putting out a fire?
Skyrim.esm|0E768F: I said "a free meat pie". As in one. Here's yours.
Skyrim.esm|0E7690: There's something... personal... I was hoping you would do for me.
Skyrim.esm|0E7691: A good story is nine parts truth and one part fiction.
Skyrim.esm|0E7693: One of Clavicus's little jests. A wizard named Sebastian Lort had a daughter who worshiped Hircine.
Skyrim.esm|0E7696: The festival would not have been possible without the dedication and hard work of our most recent inductee.
Skyrim.esm|0E7698: Please don't leave. You must help me.
Skyrim.esm|0E7699: You've found the Bards College kitchen. Congratulations.
Skyrim.esm|0E769C: Meals are served to Bards at designated times. Find an inn if you want to make requests.
Skyrim.esm|0E769D: You look a little sturdier than most of the Popinjays around here.
Skyrim.esm|0E769E: Very good, Aia. Class, observe her finger movements. Let's try a few more pieces.
Skyrim.esm|0E769F: Ah wonderful, I knew it would be. This is a significant find. I'll let Viarmo take possession but I will be looking forward to adding this to our library. Good work.
Skyrim.esm|0E76A0: I knew it was at the Dead Man's Respite.
Skyrim.esm|0E76A3: The axe isn't the only item dear old Clavicus has. Give him the Rueful Axe and once we're reunited the Masque of Clavicus Vile will be yours.
Skyrim.esm|0E76A4: Come in and shut the door.
Skyrim.esm|0E76A5: Welcome to Bits and Pieces. I'm sure you'll find what you need here.
Skyrim.esm|0E76C8: Your path has brought you to the finest fletcher in all the land.
Skyrim.esm|0E76D9: We have felt the whisper of a Word. Give me your map, and I will show you where its echo can be found.
Skyrim.esm|0E76DA: You have learned so much already, Dragonborn. Growing your gift too quickly would be dangerous. But there are many Words of Power in Skyrim, carved in the Dragon tongue. Even from here, we can feel the Thu'um resonate from them. Finding these lost Words would be a sufficient test, to temper your abilities with experience. Ask when you are ready to search.
Skyrim.esm|0E76DB: There is only silence right now. Perhaps later we will hear one of the lost Words.
Skyrim.esm|0E76EC: Feel free to look around. We have a fine selection of herbs here at Angela's Aromatics.
Skyrim.esm|0E76ED: Welcome to the Winking Skeever. Take a load off and enjoy the atmosphere.
Skyrim.esm|0E770E: No, that's pushing it too far. Very exciting but highly unlikely, I can't possibly write that in.
Skyrim.esm|0E772D: Only true Imperials request that one.
Skyrim.esm|0E773D: You can't have the Axe! It's all I have left now!
Skyrim.esm|0E773E: We should go get the axe.
Skyrim.esm|0E773F: Let's get the axe and be done with this.
Skyrim.esm|0E7741: Don't bother, Clavicus won't talk to me.
Skyrim.esm|0E7742: Wait a second, there's another option here.
Skyrim.esm|0E7743: I have a feeling I'm not going to like what happens when we get back to Clavicus.
Skyrim.esm|0E7744: All this just to kill me Clavicus?
Skyrim.esm|0E7745: You're worth putting in the extra effort for, pooch.
Skyrim.esm|0E7747: And I was so close to being rid of you.
Skyrim.esm|0E7748: I still don't know if rewriting the verse is going to work.
Skyrim.esm|0E7749: We should have the festival soon.
Skyrim.esm|0E774A: Good to meet a prospective Bard.
Skyrim.esm|0E774B: Good luck. I'm counting on you.
Skyrim.esm|0E774C: Taking on Potema won't be easy. But you are the one to do it.
Skyrim.esm|0E774D: I do. I've been working on mine for a while now.
Skyrim.esm|0E774E: I suppose Giraud sent you for it? I was still trying to get the wording right but here it is.
Skyrim.esm|0E774F: Here you are. Always good to see a fellow bard. Tell Giraud I said hello.
Skyrim.esm|0E7750: This is part of me. Guard it with your life.
Skyrim.esm|0E7751: I've got it right here. Just don't read it, it's not very good.
Skyrim.esm|0E7752: I find that highly unlikely... I think it would be irresponsible to write that in to the verse.
Skyrim.esm|0E7760: Your performance has been excellent lately Aia. You still need training but one day you will make an adequate singer.
Skyrim.esm|0E7769: Look along the east coast. The boat will be there.
Skyrim.esm|0E776A: It would be best if we spoke little until you've completed your task.
Skyrim.esm|0E776B: It's yours now. I won't take it back. I won't.
Skyrim.esm|0E776C: Avanchnzel. Take it to Avanchnzel. You must take it back.
Skyrim.esm|0E776D: We should really get to court.
Skyrim.esm|0E776E: We should present the verse soon.
Skyrim.esm|0E776F: Don't forget to speak to Jorn when you're ready for the festival.
Skyrim.esm|0E7770: Be careful, you might find more than just King Olaf's Verse in Dead Man's Respite.
Skyrim.esm|0E7779: I still don't have my Gleda back.
Skyrim.esm|0E777A: Don't come back until you have the ring.
Skyrim.esm|0E777E: The temple is still not clean. Get to it.
Skyrim.esm|0E7785: You can't have the ring back! You made a commitment!
Skyrim.esm|0E7786: Once you leave the party you can't just come back.
Skyrim.esm|0E7787: Lets see how tough you are without Sam keeping an eye on you.
Skyrim.esm|0E7788: No Sam, no protection. Time to die.
Skyrim.esm|0E7789: We remember all those Solitude soldiers out fighting the good fight in this thankless war.
Skyrim.esm|0E778A: I'll meet you outside of town.
Skyrim.esm|0E778B: Hey, don't you want to make this exchange?
Skyrim.esm|0E778C: Candy! Get your King Olaf's Festival candy!
Skyrim.esm|0E778E: Spiced Wine here. First cup is paid for by the college.
Skyrim.esm|0E778F: Get some Spiced Wine. A Festival Favorite.
Skyrim.esm|0E7790: Meat Pies. Try one for free, come back for more. Get your meat pies.
Skyrim.esm|0E7791: Can't have a meal without meat. Come and get some here.
Skyrim.esm|0E7792: I believe Sanguine would be pleased with this festival.
Skyrim.esm|0E7793: You've done well by restoring our Festival.
Skyrim.esm|0E7794: Thank you so much. The festival has really increased my sales.
Skyrim.esm|0E7798: Congratulations on getting the festival restored.
Skyrim.esm|0E7799: Get your festival meat pie. Specially made.
Skyrim.esm|0E779A: Try our meats made here at the Festival. They're the best.
Skyrim.esm|0E779B: So nice to get this break from my cleaning duties.
Skyrim.esm|0E779C: You might as well have some sweets. I made them for the festival after all.
Skyrim.esm|0E77C9: I'll speak to Ulfric soon, but I make no promises that I can change his mind.
Skyrim.esm|0E77CA: It's not that simple. Ulfric wants a Skyrim for the Nords. He doesn't trust what he calls outsiders.
Skyrim.esm|0E7830: Genuine Falmerblood Elixir for sale! Live for thousands of years... see into other people's thoughts!
Skyrim.esm|0E7831: Learn a library's worth of knowledge in moments or grow back that missing limb with my genuine Falmerblood Elixir!
Skyrim.esm|0E7832: Don't miss out... purchase a bottle of my genuine Falmerblood Elixir! Only 20 septims each!
Skyrim.esm|0E7833: Make love like a sabercat or crush your enemies to dust like a giant! Only a mere 20 gold coins and all this could be yours!
Skyrim.esm|0E7834: Rare trinkets and the finest oddities from Morrowind!
Skyrim.esm|0E7835: Gems, potions, exotic ingredients... I have them all!
Skyrim.esm|0E7836: The fairest prices and the highest quality in Skyrim!
Skyrim.esm|0E7837: Beautiful baubles and gleaming gemstones over here!
Skyrim.esm|0E7839: Buying and selling fine jewelry here!
Skyrim.esm|0E783A: Fresh meats and produce! Get them before they're gone!
Skyrim.esm|0E783B: All meats guaranteed clean and free from Rockjoint!
Skyrim.esm|0E783C: Vegetables as crisp as a winter's morning!
Skyrim.esm|0E783D: Looking to stay alive? Why take a chance?
Skyrim.esm|0E783E: Buy some armor and live to tell about it!
Skyrim.esm|0E783F: Protect yourself right... buy armor from Grelka.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A23: I believe the Legate gave you an order. Why are you standing around here flapping your jaw? Move out!
Skyrim.esm|0E7A24: Make sure you take the Auxiliary here. You can send her back when you get there and find nothing but old bones and cobwebs.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A25: Why are you still here? You're supposed to be at Korvanjund, proving Galmar right, or proving him wrong.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A26: Deliver your report to Legate Rikke.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A27: I believe Galmar gave you something to do. So go do it.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A28: What? We've got nothing to talk about until you come back from Serpentstone Isle.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A29: Welcome back, soldier. I'm glad you made it in one piece. I'll send men to garrison the fort right away. You did well. I'm impressed. But before we go any further, it's time for you to officially join the Legion.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A95: The mine is rich, and so are we. But I am cursed, it would seem, to live alone.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A96: I've had several wives. But each dies sooner than the last. I was lucky to get children out of the ones I did.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A98: Everyone said it had run out, but I smashed down one wall and found the widest veins of ebony I'd ever seen. So now we're rich and now I'm chief.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A9A: I think of Uglarz. An old friend. When we were little she ran to the woods to hunt, and never came back. If someone found her, she would make an excellent mate.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A9E: Ha. Good luck to you, friend. She's not one who likes to be found.
Skyrim.esm|0E7A9F: I am Uglarz, huntress of all.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AA1: It was my home. But I left. The woods felt more alive, at least for a time.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AA3: Mauhulakh. I remember him. Little, scrawny, fellow.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AA5: Ha! Now that I would like to see. I may go back there some day.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AA7: Always thought that old mine had more to it. Just like Mauhulakh to smash his way to good fortune.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AAB: I'm going back to Narzulbur. Want to see how it's come.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AAC: You were right about this place. And about Mauhulakh.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AAE: This was worth coming back for.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AB4: Urog is a good learner. I'm glad I have the chance to teach her. Makes me not miss the woods as much.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AC1: Urog and I still hunt a lot, so it's not like I'm cooped up in the longhouse.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AC3: I am proud to have a mate to share in my blessings.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AC5: I'm hoping this wife can outlast the winter.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AC7: All my auguring never predicted this. A woman for Mauhulakh, eh? We'll see how this goes.
Skyrim.esm|0E7ACD: Well at least now Mauhulakh has nothing to whine about. Until this one dies, too.
Skyrim.esm|0E7ACE: Uglarz is a great huntress. Perhaps she can teach me something new.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AD1: I never would have thought, but Uglarz is an even better hunter than Yatul was. The things I'm learning now...
Skyrim.esm|0E7AE8: Glad my father seems to have found someone. I just hope she can hold her own against my aunts.
Skyrim.esm|0E7AF5: I think I like this new mate of my father's. I mean... nothing like that. I just think she's nice.
Skyrim.esm|0E7B20: You're losing a lot of blood. Maybe you should sit down for a moment.
Skyrim.esm|0E7B3D: Come on, at least try to hurt me.
Skyrim.esm|0E7B97: Oh, we have unfinished business you and I.
Skyrim.esm|0E7B99: By all I hold sacred... that's a Shout! Like the Graybeards on their mountain!
Skyrim.esm|0E7B9A: The Thu'um! He summons the Thu'um!
Skyrim.esm|0E7B9B: The Thu'um! She summons the Thu'um!
Skyrim.esm|0E7B9C: It is the power of old! The Voice of the Dragonborn!
Skyrim.esm|0E7BC6: Wake me up when you're ready to fight me for real.
Skyrim.esm|0E7BC8: Try not to? But I do realize it's sometimes not that easy. You may want to make a statement, or a contract may require a public killing. Murder is, obviously, a very serious crime, and a very high bounty will be put on your head in the hold where the act was committed. You can run, obviously, but you'll still have a price on your head. If a guard attacks, you can attempt to yield by sheathing your weapon. If the guard accepts, you can serve your time in a cell, or pay your bounty. We've all gone through it. We do what we must to survive. But, there is another option. Some members of the Thieves Guild in Riften can get their bounties... erased. For a price. Something to think about.
Skyrim.esm|0E7BCB: No. Gods no, not for years. I left Winterhold for some time, and returned to stay here at the inn.
Skyrim.esm|0E7BCC: I still have research that keeps me busy, and being here in Winterhold ensures I have access to former colleagues.
Skyrim.esm|0E7BEB: You want me to close my eyes? Would that even things up a little?
Skyrim.esm|0E7C01: I've seen fresh recruits with more skill than you!
Skyrim.esm|0E7C03: I have no problem killing you. How do you feel about dying?
Skyrim.esm|0E7C07: Now you're just making me mad.
Skyrim.esm|0E7C08: Your blood is such a pretty shade of red!
Skyrim.esm|0E7C09: The Emperor can kiss my ass!
Skyrim.esm|0E7C0B: I eat Legionnaires like you for breakfast!
Skyrim.esm|0E7C0C: I got something for you. But you'll have to come and get it.
Skyrim.esm|0E7C10: Come on, you're not even trying!
Skyrim.esm|0E7C43: Keep your brother, and he'll keep you.
Skyrim.esm|0E7D6D: Go on, get out of here.
Skyrim.esm|0E7EA5: Maybe you're not so soft, after all. Now you're ready for a real challenge. I'll anoint you with the symbol of the Bear, the Sabrecat and the Mammoth. Tread carefully, these are mighty beasts.
Skyrim.esm|0E7EA6: The guardian spirits of Bear, Mammoth and Sabre Cat await you. Return when they are defeated.
Skyrim.esm|0E7EA7: You do Kyne proud. Only one challenge remains. Go and defeat the troll champion.
Skyrim.esm|0E8273: I have taught the Way of the Voice for centuries and the Thu'um since long before that.
Skyrim.esm|0E8274: "Fade" in your tongue. Mortals have greater affinity for this Word than the dov. Everything mortal fades away in time, but the spirit remains. Ponder the meaning of spirit. Unslaad zii. Where mortal flesh may wither and die, the spirit endures. That is "Feim." Let that meaning fill you. Su'um ahrk morah. You will find that your spirit will give you more strength.
Skyrim.esm|0E8275: In your tongue, the Word simply means "Fire." It is change given form. Power at its most primal. That is the true meaning of "Yol." Suleyk. Power. You have it, as do all dov. But power is inert without action and choice. Think of this as the fire builds in your su'um, in your breath. Su'um ahrk morah. What will you burn? What will you spare?
Skyrim.esm|0E8276: It is called "Force" in your tongue. But as you push the world, so does the world push back. Think of the way force may be applied effortlessly. Imagine but a whisper pushing aside all in its path. That is "Fus." Let its meaning fill you. Su'um ahrk morah. You will push the world harder than it pushes back.
Skyrim.esm|0E8277: Knowing a Word of Power is to take its meaning into yourself. Contemplate the meaning of a Rotmulaag. You will become closer to that Word, as it fills your inner self. Will I teach you, Dovahkiin? What Word calls you to deeper understanding? There are three to master: Fus, Feim, and Yol.
Skyrim.esm|0E8278: Which calls to you, Dovahkiin? Fus, Feim, or Yol?
Skyrim.esm|0E82A2: I learned that a matron who had served for House Telvanni had escaped Morrowind during the Accession War. Records showed her buying passage aboard a sailing vessel named "The Pride of Tel Vos," but that's where the trail ran cold. I spent years looking for what became of the ship, but I ended up empty-handed.
Skyrim.esm|0E82A5: Well, I'd be careful if I were you. I hear the Nords hunt them for some kind of initiation rite and not all of them end up victorious. The last traveler that traded me some said he found them at a place called Ancient's Ascent. Careful though, rumor has it a dragon makes its home there.
Skyrim.esm|0E82A8: Oh! Well if you change your mind, come back and see me.
Skyrim.esm|0E82A9: I picked up a trick from a butcher in Elinhir, a small town over the border in Hammerfell. He showed me a way to grind up ice wraith teeth and use it as a preservative. Keeps everything cold and fresh, but only for a limited time. I'm always looking to replenish my supply, so if you ever come across five of them, I'd be more than happy to take them off your hands.
Skyrim.esm|0E82AA: Anything you can find would be of great help. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0E82AB: I really appreciate what you've done for me. I'll never forget it.
Skyrim.esm|0E82AC: Keep your eyes to the road.
Skyrim.esm|0E82AD: Thanks again for the ice wraith teeth.
Skyrim.esm|0E82AE: Be careful looking for those teeth... those little bastards are dangerous.
Skyrim.esm|0E82AF: Keep your belly full and your shoulders strong.
Skyrim.esm|0E82B0: Come back when you're ready to spend more gold... goodness knows I could use it.
Skyrim.esm|0E82B3: Perfect, just perfect! These should help keep my stock fresh for quite a while. And, as promised, here's my trade. Hope you like it.
Skyrim.esm|0E82B4: Just one. I know when I was found by my Argonian father, I was wrapped in a blanket bearing the symbol of House Telvanni. It was one of the great houses in Morrowind long ago. Whether that means I was one of them or not, I'm uncertain. If you come across anything in your travels that might provide me with the answers I'm looking for, I'd be grateful.
Skyrim.esm|0E82B5: Thanks, I was actually worried I'd run out.
Skyrim.esm|0E82B6: Let me see that! Yes... yes. This is it! I don't know how you found this stranger, but you've obviously succeeded where I had failed. Please, let me offer you this key. It unlocks my strongbox under my stand in the marketplace. Help yourself to anything inside.
Skyrim.esm|0E82B9: My food is the best in all of Skyrim; always as fresh as the day it was collected.
Skyrim.esm|0E82BA: The only way to find fresher meats is to hunt it yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E82BB: Some fresh meat to fill your belly perhaps?
Skyrim.esm|0E82BC: I run the meat and produce stand at the market. Come by and see me if you want some of the freshest food you've ever eaten.
Skyrim.esm|0E8451: To me, it means not having to answer foolish questions a second time.
Skyrim.esm|0E863E: You freed Svaknir, a fool of a poet, but an honest enemy who has earned my respect. Sadly, he's now lost in the soul-snaring mist - defeat Alduin, and his doom avert - I hope to greet him in this Hall as a friend.
Skyrim.esm|0E88F8: By the gods! It... it can't be!
Skyrim.esm|0E88F9: It's a werewolf! To arms! To arms!
Skyrim.esm|0E898B: I'll be glad when this beast is gone from here. Do whatever it is you need to do with it, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0E898C: Faas nu, zini dein ruthi ahst vaal.
Skyrim.esm|0E898D: Farengar, very bad idea. Even for you.
Skyrim.esm|0E8994: May Kynareth guard you while you pass through her realm!
Skyrim.esm|0E8995: Joor mey! What are you doing back there?
Skyrim.esm|0E8996: Carry on, soldier. This is all part of the Dragonborn's plan.
Skyrim.esm|0E8997: Surely you wouldn't miss a few scales... or a small amount of blood...
Skyrim.esm|0E8998: Capturing that dragon was a mighty deed. In my younger days, I'd have been the first to join in the attempt.
Skyrim.esm|0E8999: I assure you, you will not even notice me. Most of them are hardly painful at all to a large dragon such as yourself.
Skyrim.esm|0E899A: I would be most appreciative if you would permit me to perform some, ah, tests on you. Purely in the interests of the advancement of knowledge.
Skyrim.esm|0E899B: Get it into the trap before it kills us all!
Skyrim.esm|0E899D: Incredible! Uh... sir, you have no idea how long I have waited for such an opportunity!
Skyrim.esm|0E899E: Begone, mage. Do not test my promise to the Dovahkiin.
Skyrim.esm|0E899F: You're either the bravest person I've ever met, or the biggest fool.
Skyrim.esm|0E89D3: This next song goes out to the Dragonborn. The savior of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0E89D4: This is a local favorite and one of the first songs I ever learned. Ragnar the Red.
Skyrim.esm|0E89D5: This one's a favorite of mine. A legend we all know and love...
Skyrim.esm|0E89D6: This is an ode to Skyrim's truest sons and daughters... the Stormcloaks.
Skyrim.esm|0E89D7: This is an ode to Skyrim's staunch protectors... the Imperials.
Skyrim.esm|0E89DE: It's as if the merchandise and coin are simply falling from the sky. Vex and Delvin are having to turn clients away at this point, it's almost impossible to keep up. Don't forget to check your tribute chest once in a while. As Guild Master you're getting a significant cut of the spoils.
Skyrim.esm|0E89E1: Take a good look around you! Have you ever see the Guild in such a prosperous state? With Mercer Frey gone and our influence present in all of Skyrim's cities, the Guild's accumulating wealth faster than we can spend it. I couldn't be more proud to be a part of the Thieves Guild, or its new Guild Master.
Skyrim.esm|0E89E2: Take a good look around you! Have you ever see the Guild in such a prosperous state? With Mercer Frey gone and our influence spreading across Skyrim, the Guild's earned a new level of respect it hasn't seen in decades. I couldn't be more proud to be a part of the Thieves Guild, or its new Guild Master.
Skyrim.esm|0E89E3: I think by now, most within the Guild are well aware of what transpired between Mercer Frey and ourselves. The notion that the Nightingales are a fictive group has long since passed. If you wish to wear your Nightingale Armor within the Guild, I'm certain that no one would give it a second thought.
Skyrim.esm|0E89E4: I'm feeling a sense of inner peace... something I haven't felt in a very long time. Instead of being clouded with thoughts of revenge and retribution, I feel only serenity and fulfillment. Of course, I've been also dabbling in my old pastimes as well. Take a look around, and feel free to share the fruits of my labor.
Skyrim.esm|0E89E5: Yes, lad? Did you need something?
Skyrim.esm|0E89EB: Yes, lass? Did you need something?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F0: Good to see you, lad! How are things?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F1: Good to see you, lass! How are things?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F2: Things are looking up around here, lass.
Skyrim.esm|0E89F3: Things are looking up around here, lad.
Skyrim.esm|0E89F4: Planning some exciting heists I hope?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F5: Enjoying your newfound abilities I trust?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F6: What brings you back to Nightingale Hall?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F7: I trust the night finds you well?
Skyrim.esm|0E89F9: Things seem to be going well at the Guild.
Skyrim.esm|0E89FA: I'm hearing good things about you from Brynjolf.
Skyrim.esm|0E89FB: It was good speaking with you again.
Skyrim.esm|0E89FC: May the shadows guide your journey.
Skyrim.esm|0E8A03: Eyes open and walk with the shadows.
Skyrim.esm|0E8A05: Eyes open and walk with the shadows.
Skyrim.esm|0E8A06: Now go and make us some coin, lad!
Skyrim.esm|0E8A07: Now go and make us some coin, lass!
Skyrim.esm|0E8AAB: Get ready to open the trap!
Skyrim.esm|0E8BC1: Oh that's very sweet of you. Anything you can find out from Captain Aldis would be welcome news.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BC2: Ah well, it was a long shot.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BC3: Now wait a minute... No need to do anything drastic. Look, Keep the horse, all right? Then we all walk away happy...
Skyrim.esm|0E8BC4: Well ain't that the truth of it. All right, then. Let's see what you've got!
Skyrim.esm|0E8BC5: Wonderful. I hadn't really hoped to run in to someone who had been there.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BC6: Ah, well I guess it was unlikely. I'm sorry to waste your time.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BCA: You're playing a dangerous game, my friend. Fine, you win. Take the horse and leave.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BCB: And telling her what? That you stole her horse? I don't think so.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BCC: My daughter, Fura, was assigned to Whiterun after she joined the Imperial Army.  I was hoping you might have met her.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BCD: You're a traveller, correct? Have you been to Whiterun?
Skyrim.esm|0E8BCE: Sure - take advantage of the guy in jail. Okay, fine. I can make the deal sweeter. I obviously don't have anything here, but... there's a hidden stash in the Lodge. And I did manage to hold on to the key... Take it.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BCF: Nice try, but my mother's already got me in jail. Maybe you should try that one on Letrush.
Skyrim.esm|0E8BD1: Ah, welcome to Angela's Aromatics. Can I help with some herbal needs?
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA1: Hey, I found a crown over here on this corpse. Is this the one we're looking for?
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA2: Have it your way. Let's get that crown men!
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA3: Well, King... If you don't mind, we'll just take that crown of yours and be on our way.
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA5: Alright, get the crown off that draugr!
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA6: Alright, get the crown off that draugr!
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA8: Ralof... get away from there, fool!
Skyrim.esm|0E8CA9: Have it your way. Let's get that crown men!
Skyrim.esm|0E8CAA: Hey, I found a crown over here on this corpse. Is this the one we're looking for?
Skyrim.esm|0E8CAB: If you don't mind king, we'll just be taking that crown of yours and be on our way.
Skyrim.esm|0E8F28: Markarth is... different from the rest of Skyrim, there's no doubt about that. The Reach has been part of Skyrim for a thousand years, but the Reachmen still haven't accepted it. It was only 20 years back that the Reachmen rebelled. Even took control of Markarth itself for a time. They were crushed, of course, but the survivors - the Forsworn - still lurk in the wilds and make the Reach dangerous for honest folk.
Skyrim.esm|0E952E: Ysgramor would be ashamed of you!
Skyrim.esm|0E9531: We knew you'd be coming here.
Skyrim.esm|0E9532: Well, look what we have here.
Skyrim.esm|0E9535: It doesn't matter. She wears that armor, she dies.
Skyrim.esm|0E9536: It doesn't matter. He wears that armor, he dies.
Skyrim.esm|0E9537: Killing you will make for an excellent story.
Skyrim.esm|0E9538: None of you will be alive to tell it.
Skyrim.esm|0EA25A: No? Well, if you think you're capable of it, then I'd be happy to provide it to you for a mere 30 gold. Or you can try your luck with one of the court wizards around Skyrim. They also sell spells.
Skyrim.esm|0EA25B: Here you are. Now I'm anxious to see you cast it.
Skyrim.esm|0EA25C: Yes, you were going to demonstrate your ability, were you not?
Skyrim.esm|0EA261: They say Helgen got hit by a dragon. One of those horrors comes here, we'll be ready.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2B2: Take a look around. Have you ever laid eyes on such wealth?
Skyrim.esm|0EA2B3: Never. This is the best the Guild's done since I've been a part of it.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2B5: Better than ever. With the Guild back on its feet, there's more coin to throw around. How about you?
Skyrim.esm|0EA2B7: Aren't you supposed to be in Markarth? Finish that job and we'll talk about more.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2B8: Let's just hope it stays this way for a long time.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2B9: Yeah, it's really something else, isn't it?
Skyrim.esm|0EA2BA: So I'm finally seeing you with a smile on your face.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2BB: You know, things are really starting to move around here. I mean, look at the place. I've never seen so much merchandise.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2BC: At this rate, the Guild won't be such a laughingstock anymore.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2BD: It's this place. It feels like home. It's just nice to finally have something going right in my life.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2BE: Well, looks like you're running the show now. Good luck.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2BF: Wait until the ceremony is over, then we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C0: As long as you continue to make us all rich, I'm behind you.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C1: Hang on. When the ceremony's done, we'll talk.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C2: So, now that you're Guild Master, how about drinks for all of us!
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C3: Not yet... wait until Brynjolf's finished the ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C4: I think everyone's waiting for the ceremony to get going.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C5: I think everyone's waiting for the ceremony to get going.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C6: We'll talk later... after this whole ceremony thing's over.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C7: We'll talk later... after this whole ceremony thing's over.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C8: Here's to a long and prosperous future... may the Guild last another hundred years.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2C9: What are you doing? You're supposed to be attending the ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2CA: Brynjolf's looking for you. Something about becoming the Guild Master.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2CB: So, you're finally going to make Guild Master, eh? Well, you better track down Brynjolf to seal the deal.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2CC: Guild Master, huh? Well, guess we could do worse. Better go see Brynjolf about it.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2CD: Brynjolf told me he was looking for you about the Guild Master ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2CE: Did you know Brynjolf was looking for you? I think it's about the Guild Master ceremony.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2CF: Brynjolf's been looking for you. He wants to discuss the Guild Master ceremony. Congratulations... boss.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2D0: I agree. It'll be nice to get back the respect we lost in the last few years.
Skyrim.esm|0EA2FF: Living or dead, by decree of Shor, none may pass this perilous bridge 'til I judge them worthy by the warrior's test.
Skyrim.esm|0EA477: You were ranting when you got here  but most of it was slurred. You did say something about Rorikstead . Maybe you should take a look there.
Skyrim.esm|0EA574: When Ulfric Stormcloak, with savage Shout, sent me here, my sole regret was fair Elisif, left forlorn and weeping. I faced him fearlessly - my fate inescapable, yet my honor is unstained - can Ulfric say the same?
Skyrim.esm|0EA575: The bitter war of the world beyond was all for naught.
Skyrim.esm|0EA576: Skyrim was betrayed, the blood of her sons spilled in doomed struggle against fate. And so in death, too late, I learn the truth - fed by war, so waxed the power of Alduin, World-Eater - wisdom now useless. By gods' jest in this grim mist together snared, Stormcloak and Imperial, we wander hopeless, waiting for succor.
Skyrim.esm|0EA577: Dragonborn, even in death you dog my steps? How come you here? The king of this realm will cast you out - cursed be your name by all sons of Skyrim, with scorn unceasing.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5B4: You watch yourself on that island. Those mercenaries don't take prisoners.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5B5: If you find Mercer, give no quarter... kill him and be done with it.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5B6: One more time in case I wasn't clear. You butcher this job and you'll be sorry.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5B7: I need you to put all your efforts into this job. We can't afford any mistakes.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5B8: Good luck in Solitude. Keep Gulum-Ei alive, but remind him who we are.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5BA: I hope Brynjolf chokes on his gold.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5BB: I can't stand this city. It's impossible to make a decent living here.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5BC: I dare Mercer to come back here! He sets one foot in the cistern and I'll cut it off!
Skyrim.esm|0EA5BD: I never saw this coming, I thought we were just having a run of bad luck.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5BE: I hope he turns up soon... he needs to answer for his betrayal.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5BF: I won't rest until Mercer's dead. How could he murder Gallus and betray all of us like that?
Skyrim.esm|0EA5C0: How can Karliah remain so calm when Mercer had her on the run for that long? Amazing.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5C1: If I find Mercer first, I'm going to crush his skull with my bare hands!
Skyrim.esm|0EA5C2: Punishment's too good for Mercer Frey. The only penalty for his betrayal is death.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5C3: There's no doubt Mercer will make for the borders of Skyrim. I hope you find him before he escapes our grasp.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5C4: I heard what happened and the Guild owes you thanks for bringing this to light.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5E6: Keep it busy. I'll try to drain some of its power!
Skyrim.esm|0EA5F8: Gettin' pinched and endin' up in jail doesn't cut it with our clients. If you want to get paid next time, stay clean.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5F9: Yeah, your right. Probably a waste of your time anyway.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5FA: Being connected to a criminal didn't make the client happy... next time, act like a real thief and don't get caught.
Skyrim.esm|0EA5FB: Good going... you got arrested. Now how do you think that looks for the Guild? Better luck next time.
Skyrim.esm|0EA634: Yes, well. After the unfortunate incident that occurred, I think it's best if we just move on.
Skyrim.esm|0EA64E: We've got an assassin! Check the shadows!
Skyrim.esm|0EB7C7: When you are ready to rejoin the living, just bid me so, and I will send you back.
Skyrim.esm|0EB7C8: Tarry not too long - the land of the dead is not meant for mortals to linger.
Skyrim.esm|0EB7C9: Return now to Nirn, with this rich boon from Shor, my lord: a Shout to bring a hero from Sovngarde in your hour of need.
Skyrim.esm|0EC396: Now ends this tale, my eternity be damned!
Skyrim.esm|0EC398: Through fire and time, the terror has lived. No more, I say! I've set my heart on terrible deeds, death's grim art.
Skyrim.esm|0EC39A: To battle, my friends! The fields will echo with the clamor of war, our wills undaunted.
Skyrim.esm|0EC39B: We must drive away his mist, Shouting together, and then unsheathe our blades in desperate battle with our black-winged foe.
Skyrim.esm|0EC39C: Felldir speaks wisdom - the World-Eater, coward, fears you, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0EC39D: But with four Voices joined, our valor combined, we can blast the mist and bring him to battle.
Skyrim.esm|0EC39E: Welcome, Dragonborn! Our door has stood empty since Alduin first set his soul-snare here.
Skyrim.esm|0EC39F: The three await your word to loose their fury upon the perilous foe.
Skyrim.esm|0EC3A0: Far you've travelled, your trials long, to taste of Sovngarde's sweet delights.
Skyrim.esm|0EC3A1: Seek ye food or song unending? Fighting, drinking, fellowship and boasting? Find welcome here, and warm your soul.
Skyrim.esm|0EC3A2: Alduin's mist is more than a snare - its shadowy gloom is his shield and cloak.
Skyrim.esm|0EC3A3: If it is our lot to end, we'll live in song if not in soul. At Sovngarde's gates I'll meet my fate with mirthful heart, and know I fell in noblest cause.
Skyrim.esm|0EC3A4: By Shor's command we sheathed our blades and ventured not the vale's dark mist. But three await your word to loose their fury upon the perilous foe. Gormlaith the fearless, glad-hearted in battle; Hakon the valiant, heavy-handed warrior; Felldir the Old, far-seeing and grim.
Skyrim.esm|0ECC77: The endless wait gives way to battle! Alduin's doom, his death or ours!
Skyrim.esm|0ECC7A: The mist is gone, my mind is clear.
Skyrim.esm|0ECC7B: For Skyrim! For Shor! For Sovngarde's freedom!
Skyrim.esm|0ECC7D: I'll fight and die, if such fate is mine.
Skyrim.esm|0ECC7E: Better to die with blade in hand than feed his hunger, hopeless in the mist.
Skyrim.esm|0ECC7F: No higher fate than fall in battle at Sovngarde's gates, a glorious end!
Skyrim.esm|0ECC80: Before I fall, you'll feel the bite of my sharp blade, as Shor's my witness.
Skyrim.esm|0ECC82: Stand fast! The fell worm's death is ours at last, the light returns!
Skyrim.esm|0ECC83: Today you die, your doom has come.
Skyrim.esm|0ECC84: Use Dragonrend, brothers! Bring him to swords-reach!
Skyrim.esm|0ECC85: Shor's Hall awaits, if worthy you prove.
Skyrim.esm|0ED093: Her heart beats with fury and courage that have united the Companions since the days of the distant green summers.
Skyrim.esm|0ED35A: In life we were enemies, but eternity's too long to hold a grudge - my heart's now free of such old hatreds. Hero of the Nords I name you, Dragonborn, let none deny it.
Skyrim.esm|0ED35B: Fen du hin sille ko Sovngarde!
Skyrim.esm|0ED361: Pahlok joorre! Hin kah fen kos bonaar.
Skyrim.esm|0ED362: Zu'u Alduin, zok sahrot do naan ko Lein.
Skyrim.esm|0ED363: Nust wo ni qiilaan fen kos duaan.
Skyrim.esm|0ED364: Dovahkiin, hin kah fen kos bonaar.
Skyrim.esm|0ED365: Zu'u lost kriaan hi ont, nu hin sille fen nahkip suleyki.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF08: Stop, outlander! You must pay bloodprice for your crimes.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF09: By the Code of Malacath, you must pay bloodprice for your crimes. Which will it be, blood or gold?
Skyrim.esm|0EDF0A: Your crimes offend the Code of Malacath and we demand payment. Choose. Gold or blood.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF0B: There are no jails in an Orc Stronghold, criminal. You must pay in gold or blood.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF18: There must be something about that amulet. Have you tried putting it on?
Skyrim.esm|0EDF47: Dovahkiin, you should not have come here. Nu hin sil dii.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF48: I have already defeated your friends once. Beyn. I do not fear them.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF49: You are persistent, Dovahkiin. Pruzah ol aar. A fine slave you would have made.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF4A: My teeth to your neck, Dovahkiin!
Skyrim.esm|0EDF4C: Ease your heart, our honor is unstained - it's by Shor's command we curb our onslaught.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF4D: It is by Shor's command that we curb our wrath - so let's feast and sing 'til our fate calls us. Let us not spurn our lord's bounty - this hall is his gift, for glory well-earned.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF55: I long to fight that foul worm.
Skyrim.esm|0EDF56: Shall Alduin remain so long unchallenged?
Skyrim.esm|0EF97A: Hear that? Stormcloaks. Maybe we can reason with them.
Skyrim.esm|0EF97B: Just give me a minute... I'm out of breath...
Skyrim.esm|0EF97C: We need to get moving! That dragon is tearing up the whole keep!
Skyrim.esm|0EF97D: Not many wood elves would choose to come alone to Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|0EF97E: What're you doing here, Redguard? You a sellsword? A sailor from Stros M'kai?
Skyrim.esm|0EF97F: You from one of the strongholds, Orc? How did you end up here?
Skyrim.esm|0EF980: You're a long way from the Imperial City. What're you doing in Skyrim?
Skyrim.esm|0EF981: You're not with the Thalmor Embassy, are you high elf? No, that can't be right....
Skyrim.esm|0EF982: Another refugee? Gods really have abandoned your people, dark elf.
Skyrim.esm|0EF983: You from Daggerfall, Breton? Fleeing from some court intrigue?
Skyrim.esm|0EF984: You with one of the trade caravans, Khajiit? Your kind always seems to find trouble.
Skyrim.esm|0EF985: Are you a relative of one of the Riften dock workers, Argonian?
Skyrim.esm|0EF986: You picked a bad time to come home to Skyrim, kinsman.
Skyrim.esm|0F07B9: Ugh. You mangy piece of pit-bait.
Skyrim.esm|0F0A07: Hail, comrade! Shall we by clash of steel ease the gloom of the encompassing mist?
Skyrim.esm|0F0A08: With mead and fellowship let's mend our wounds.
Skyrim.esm|0F0A09: Shor's favor found me today. Let's lift a mug of mead together, our wounds to salve and weariness cure.
Skyrim.esm|0F0A0A: By a bold stroke, you bested me, friend.
Skyrim.esm|0F0A0C: Well fought, friend. Your fury is unmatched.
Skyrim.esm|0F0A0D: Aye. Let us forget our sorrow in the fury of battle.
Skyrim.esm|0F11B0: I'll be right here. Be careful now!
Skyrim.esm|0F11B2: Well, it's been fun. I guess I'll head back to Rorikstead.
Skyrim.esm|0F11B3: No problem! How can I help?
Skyrim.esm|0F11BB: You know this one? I saw her training in the yard with Vilkas.
Skyrim.esm|0F11BC: I told you, this is the whelp that Vilkas mentioned.
Skyrim.esm|0F11BD: New blood? Oh, hello. I'm Farkas. Come, follow me.
Skyrim.esm|0F11BE: Ah, good. I've been waiting for this. Wait... I remember you. So the old man thinks you've got some heart, I guess.
Skyrim.esm|0F11BF: Ah, good. I've been waiting for this. Are you new here?
Skyrim.esm|0F1A24: I crave a tale of tall ships and fearless heroes, for heart's ease.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A2A: A cup I'll gladly raise in company with you. None have yet bested me in boasting or drinking.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A2C: Come, let us share a cup of mead, and trade tales of our time on Mundus, reliving the glory of our remembered deeds.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A35: Listen well, friend, and lighten your mood while words I'll spin of warriors bold, whose deeds of valor brought undying fame.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A37: No gloom can hold my heart for long in Shor's bless'd hall, where no shadows lie.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A38: A cup, Dragonborn, to death and glory!
Skyrim.esm|0F1A39: Take your ease, let troubles fade.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A3A: By Shor's command we curb our wrath, so we feast and sing, til the final doom.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A3B: Shor's power protects this hall; but beyond is fear, brought by Alduin.
Skyrim.esm|0F1A3C: Shor's high seat stands empty; his mien is too bright for mortal eyes.
Skyrim.esm|0F1B29: You what? How could you run out? J'zargo gave you more than enough. Bah, just forget J'zargo ever asked for your help.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C71: Does this mean... is it done? Is Alduin truly defeated?
Skyrim.esm|0F1C72: I'm afraid she's right. I'm deeply sorry that this has to come between us. But just as Paarthurnax's later deeds do not expiate his crimes, your deeds do not allow us to ignore our duty. I hope you will return to us soon with the news that justice has finally been done. With both Alduin and Paarthurnax dead, a dark chapter in history will finally be closed.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C73: We heard the news from Whiterun - that you left the city on the back of a dragon! That's a little... showy, even for you.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C74: Please... Delphine has been very worried about you.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C75: I can see it in your eyes - you've seen the land of the gods and returned.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C76: There's still the matter of Paarthurnax. I'm not ungrateful for what you've done. Esbern speaks for both of us. But our oath as Blades binds us. Paarthurnax must die. There's no excuse now that Alduin is dead.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C77: Thank you. Thank you, Dragonborn. You've done a great service for us all.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C78: Perhaps, perhaps not. Dragons are not like normal mortal creatures, and Alduin is unique even among dragonkind. He may be permitted to return at the end of time to fulfill his destiny as the World-Eater. But that is for the gods to decide. You have done your part.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C79: I knew you could do it... I believed in you, and yet... I still didn't think I'd live to see this day.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C7B: Meat and mead must ever make way for the clash of steel - the song of battle.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C7C: You've done it... just as the prophecy said...
Skyrim.esm|0F1C7D: You've shown yourself mighty, both in Voice and deed. In order to defeat Alduin, you've gained mastery of dreadful weapons. Now it is up to you to decide what to do with your power and skill. Will you be a hero whose name is remembered in song throughout the ages? Or will your name be a curse to future generations? Or will you merely fade from history, unremembered? Let the Way of the Voice be your guide, and the path of wisdom will be clear to you. Breath and focus, Dragonborn. Your future lies before you.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C7E: My disciples still follow the difficult path - the Way of the Voice is neither wide, nor easy. But if you stray from wisdom then to Sovngarde you will not return.
