Skyrim.esm|0F1C7F: Fate drives you, but you follow your own path. Choose wisely, lest you wander into evil.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C80: Gods above... that's better news than I had hoped! When you walked into my inn that day, I never imagined where it would all lead... dragons out of legend... Sovngarde...
Skyrim.esm|0F1C82: My blade is restless, unblooded too long.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C83: We are all in your debt, Dragonborn.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C84: Then it is done at last. Perhaps it was all worth it, in the end.
Skyrim.esm|0F1C85: Since you're still alive, I assume you have good news to report?
Skyrim.esm|0F1C86: At last. It is over. Perhaps it was all worth it, in the end.
Skyrim.esm|0F20AB: Since you put it that way, I think we can overlook your crime for now.
Skyrim.esm|0F20AC: All right, all right. Just keep your voice down and don't let me catch you again.
Skyrim.esm|0F20AD: Everything here appears to be in order. You can move along now.
Skyrim.esm|0F20AE: I can make all your problems with the guards go away. But it'll cost you. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|0F23B1: You just going to stand there, gawking? Go away. You're starting to... frighten me.
Skyrim.esm|0F23B2: What kind of cruel, horrible person are you? My sister was murdered. Do you have any idea what that's like? What I'm going through?
Skyrim.esm|0F23B3: Muiri? I can't believe my family trusted that backstabbing little strumpet. You tell her she's dead to us. You hear me? Dead!
Skyrim.esm|0F23B5: I have nothing to say to you. Just... leave me alone.
Skyrim.esm|0F25D8: I hear your heart beat like the Harbingers of old. Your glories in Skyrim are seen and honored.
Skyrim.esm|0F2BA0: Just how long did you expect me to keep those people distracted? I had to let the whole plan fall through. Well, at least you didn't do anything stupid. Next time, when you're given a job, don't wander off or take your sweet time. I guess I shouldn't be surprised. Nothing around here has been going well lately.
Skyrim.esm|0F42FB: No, I'm not a rebel. You can't do this!
Skyrim.esm|0F42FC: Face your death with some courage, thief.
Skyrim.esm|0F46DB: Step towards the block when we call your name. One at a time.
Skyrim.esm|0F46DC: My ancestors are smiling at me, Imperials. Can you say the same?
Skyrim.esm|0F46DD: Come on, I haven't got all morning.
Skyrim.esm|0F46DE: For the love of Talos, shut up and let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|0F500B: Please, enjoy the festivities! This is a day of peace and happiness, when Imperials and Stormcloaks join together, as equals!
Skyrim.esm|0F500C: The Emperor's cousin is dead! This is a disaster!
Skyrim.esm|0F500E: It was the Empire killed her! I know it! They killed her so they can blame it on the Stormcloaks!
Skyrim.esm|0F5370: What in the Eight Divines is this thing?
Skyrim.esm|0F5374: Die! For the love of the gods, die!
Skyrim.esm|0F5B4D: These Imperials have potions in here. We're going to need them.
Skyrim.esm|0F5B4E: What are you doing? We need to get out of Helgen. Now!
Skyrim.esm|0F5B4F: I just need to gather some more potions.
Skyrim.esm|0F5B50: Grab everything important and let's move! Dragon's burning everything to the ground!
Skyrim.esm|0F5BAE: I'm not waiting to be killed by a dragon! We need to fall back!
Skyrim.esm|0F5BAF: Orders are to wait until General Tullius arrives.
Skyrim.esm|0F5BB0: Just give me a minute. Let me think.
Skyrim.esm|0F5BB1: Where in Oblivion are we supposed to go? Where's the way out?
Skyrim.esm|0F5BB2: Just give the General some time!
Skyrim.esm|0F5E78: Look at him, General Tullius the Military Governor.
Skyrim.esm|0F626F: Excellent! I'll start translating it. Shouldn't take me more than a day or two.
Skyrim.esm|0F6270: I've heard whispers of more of his work having turned up. If you can track it down, it may prove useful to all of us.
Skyrim.esm|0F6271: As a matter of fact, there are. Have you ever heard of Shalidor? Immensely powerful mage, back in the First Era. Had an understanding of magic few have ever matched. He holed himself up in Labyrinthian, and devoted all of his time to research and study. Wrote more than you would imagine possible. Since then, his writings have been scattered all over Skyrim, but are little use to anyone who can't translate them. I'm one of the few who can.
Skyrim.esm|0F6272: I'm always trying to find more of Shalidor's writings.
Skyrim.esm|0F7788: No, this can't be happening. This isn't happening.
Skyrim.esm|0F778A: General Tullius, sir! The headsman is waiting!
Skyrim.esm|0F778C: And it looks like the Thalmor are with him. Damn elves. I bet they had something to do with this.
Skyrim.esm|0F7941: By the way, if you're looking for something to do...
Skyrim.esm|0F7B30: Forget the list. She goes to the block.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B32: You're not going to kill me!
Skyrim.esm|0F7B34: Get these prisoners out of the carts. Move it!
Skyrim.esm|0F7B35: Captain. What should we do? She's not on the list.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B3F: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Black Marsh.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B40: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to High Rock.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B41: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Morrowind.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B42: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to the Summerset Isle.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B43: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Cyrodiil.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B44: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Elsweyr.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B45: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Orsinium.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B46: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Hammerfell.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B47: I'm sorry. We'll make sure your remains are returned to Valenwood.
Skyrim.esm|0F7B48: I'm sorry. At least you'll die here, in your homeland.
Skyrim.esm|0F82B0: Come on now, fight your own battles.
Skyrim.esm|0F8390: See if one of these chests has some armor for you.
Skyrim.esm|0F8391: Got to be a sword or two in one of these chests. Look around.
Skyrim.esm|0F8392: Grab Gunjar's gear. No shame in borrowing a friend's axe.
Skyrim.esm|0F8393: Gunjar isn't coming back from the dead. Take his armor and anything else on him.
Skyrim.esm|0F8394: That armor on Gunjar isn't doing him any good. Go ahead. Take it.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A53: There's a way out through here!
Skyrim.esm|0F8A56: There's a way out through here!
Skyrim.esm|0F8A73: You have quite the bounty on your head in The Reach. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A74: You have quite the bounty on your head in The Pale. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A75: You have quite the bounty on your head in Hjaalmarch. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A76: You have quite the bounty on your head in Haafingar. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A77: Then you are worth more to me dead!
Skyrim.esm|0F8A78: You have quite the bounty on your head in Falkreath. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A79: I will make sure this gets back to the Jarl. Minus my cut, of course. Consider your name cleared, for now.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A7A: You have quite the bounty on your head in Eastmarch. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A7B: You have quite the bounty on your head in Winterhold. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A7D: You have quite the bounty on your head in Whiterun. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F8A7E: You have quite the bounty on your head in The Rift. You pay me, and I see that your name is cleared.
Skyrim.esm|0F9078: I have no more business with you.
Skyrim.esm|0F9079: People think they can run away from their crimes, but I will always catch up with them and make them pay.
Skyrim.esm|0F935D: It's very unfortunate that you've been suspended.
Skyrim.esm|0F94BE: Okay, great! Now I'm finally going to have some fun around here. Bye!
Skyrim.esm|0FBA67: Think you've got the mettle, eh? Honestly, the Legion can use all the able bodies it can muster. We have our hands full. A dead king, a rebellion, and now dragons? Skyrim's a mess. If you're serious, you're in the right place. Our headquarters is Castle Dour. Here you'll find General Tullius, commanding our forces in Skyrim. But talk to Legate Rikke. She handles the local recruiting.
Skyrim.esm|0FBA68: If you are, then you talk to the man himself. If you're not, well friend, you'd best walk away before this gets ugly.
Skyrim.esm|0FC235: Okay lad, ready to make some coin?
Skyrim.esm|0FC236: Okay lass, ready to make some coin?
Skyrim.esm|0FD94A: Looks like the way out's up ahead. Come on.
Skyrim.esm|0FD968: That the best the Legion has? Heh.
Skyrim.esm|0FD96A: That's how a true Nord fights.
Skyrim.esm|0FDA29: I'll keep watch in case Ulfric comes through here. Talos guide the both of you.
Skyrim.esm|0FDA30: I'll stay here and wait for General Tullius. Good luck you two.
Skyrim.esm|0FDA31: I'll keep watch in case Ulfric comes through here. Talos guide the both of you.
Skyrim.esm|0FDA32: I'll stay here and wait for General Tullius. Good luck you two.
Skyrim.esm|0FDBCE: You! You're responsible for the siege of Solitude! By Sithis, this undermines all our plans! Fool! Finish that mess, and only then will we speak!
Skyrim.esm|0FDBE6: You! You're responsible for the siege of that... city! By Sithis, this undermines all our plans! Fool! Finish that mess, and only then will we speak!
Skyrim.esm|0FE2A0: I do the cookin' and cleanin' round the palace.
Skyrim.esm|0FE2A1: Don't need to pay me no mind. I'm the least important person in here.
Skyrim.esm|0FE2A2: Keepin' the palace clean an' the lords fed ain't easy work, but it's honest work.
Skyrim.esm|0FE2D3: It's my mill. Some folks think it's my town too, but it ain't. The Jarl in Whiterun owns the land and the town. I just pay the taxes.
Skyrim.esm|0FE2D4: And by "supplies" I suppose you mean weapons and armor. Alvor can help you out. He's our blacksmith.
Skyrim.esm|0FE513: The anticipation is killing me. Can you figure out who has the contract? Which one will you choose?
Skyrim.esm|0FE6F5: I'm sorry, but I'm not going to do something like that. Even for you.
Skyrim.esm|0FE6F7: You already appear to have someone with you.
Skyrim.esm|0FE6F8: I'm sorry, that can't be done.
Skyrim.esm|0FF22D: Hold your head up. I'm sure it won't happen again.
Skyrim.esm|0FF22E: Now that's just sad. Come back when you actually have something to show for your thieving.
Skyrim.esm|0FF230: We may be rough, but we don't tolerate thieving around here. If that's your game, go join the lowlives in Riften.
Skyrim.esm|0FF231: Be careful around here. You don't want to anger the wrong person.
Skyrim.esm|0FF274: Oh, well, the Listener is the only person the Night Mother speaks to. It is the highest honor attainable by a member of the Dark Brotherhood. Of course our Lady is dead, so she doesn't talk with words, since her lips are... rotted. But inside the Listener's head. I hear it's... intimate. Ah, but there has not been a Listener in years and years. Our Lady has not chosen Cicero, and certainly not Astrid. Or... anyone. But some day...
Skyrim.esm|0FF275: Ha! Why you are, silly! You are the Listener! You are the Listener! Such a kidder... You must realize what a great honor this is. For the Night Mother to have chosen you... I am just in awe. You have Cicero's utmost confidence!
Skyrim.esm|0FF98A: Get the damn Key and let's get out of here before we end up like Mercer!
Skyrim.esm|0FF98C: Don't you have better things to do than disturb me?
Skyrim.esm|0FF98D: This better be important, I'm quite busy.
Skyrim.esm|0FF98E: A word of advice. Don't disappoint me.
Skyrim.esm|0FF990: You can't kill what you can't see!
Skyrim.esm|0FF991: I've mastered the shadows... and now your life is mine!
Skyrim.esm|0FF992: Come, "Nightingale!" Find me if you can!
Skyrim.esm|0FF993: Karliah's taught you nothing! Your lessons begin anew!
Skyrim.esm|0FF994: I should've finished you when I had the chance!
Skyrim.esm|0FF995: Hmm. The gate below still looks closed. I've seen a puzzle like this before... there's got to be another lever to pull, but you'd better hurry. A septim says this mechanism's on a timer.
Skyrim.esm|0FF996: Karliah, I'll deal with you after I rid myself of your irksome companions. In the meantime, perhaps you and Brynjolf should get better acquainted.
Skyrim.esm|100565: There you are. Look, I... I just wanted to say thanks for coming to find me. Back in Dawnstar. Crazy jester finally met his match, am I right?
Skyrim.esm|100736: Hiding will not save you now.
Skyrim.esm|10073D: There could be no other end.
Skyrim.esm|100741: Are you prepared for your death?
Skyrim.esm|100743: I honor my lord by destroying you!
Skyrim.esm|100744: I will feast on your heart.
Skyrim.esm|1007ED: This is the East Empire Company's central warehouse. All of their shipments go through here. Unfortunately, thanks to some problems they've been having, it hasn't seen much use lately. Either way, it's off limits to you.
Skyrim.esm|1007EE: This is the East Empire Company's central warehouse. All of their shipments go through here. And now that they've solved their little pirate problem, they're busier than ever. Either way, it's off limits to you.
Skyrim.esm|1007EF: How should I know? I don't work for them. If you want to help, I suppose you could head to Windhelm and talk to Orthus Endario. He's in charge over there.
Skyrim.esm|1007F0: The warehouse is off-limits. I suggest you head back the way you came.
Skyrim.esm|1007F1: There's nothing for you in the warehouse, so why don't you take a walk?
Skyrim.esm|1007F2: The warehouse is for East Empire Company workers only. Entering without permission is a crime.
Skyrim.esm|101734: All that effort, and you just kill her. You've ruined everything.
Skyrim.esm|101ACD: You hear the news? The Emperor has been murdered! Right here, in Skyrim. By the gods, if the Dark Brotherhood can do that, nobody's safe...
Skyrim.esm|101ACE: Well, well. Looks like one of the Dark Brotherhood rats escaped its burning nest. I can fix that.
Skyrim.esm|101ACF: To arms, brothers! It's the assassin!
Skyrim.esm|101AD0: The Dark Brotherhood is dead... and so are you!
Skyrim.esm|101AD1: You want the Emperor? You'll have to get through us!
Skyrim.esm|101AD2: You'll never reach the Emperor! Not while I'm standing!
Skyrim.esm|101AD4: You'll never leave this ship alive, cutthroat!
Skyrim.esm|101AE3: We're all brothers and sisters in binds now, thief.
Skyrim.esm|101AE4: You there. You and me - we shouldn't be here. It's these Stormcloaks the Empire wants.
Skyrim.esm|101AE5: Damn you Stormcloaks. Skyrim was fine until you came along. Empire was nice and lazy. If they hadn't been looking for you, I could've stolen that horse and be halfway to Hammerfell.
Skyrim.esm|101AE7: Shor, Mara, Dibella, Kynareth, Akatosh. Divines, please help me.
Skyrim.esm|101AE8: You were trying to cross the border, right? Walked right into that Imperial ambush, same as us, and that thief over there.
Skyrim.esm|102014: Farewell, Nightingale. See to it the Key stays this time, won't you?
Skyrim.esm|10272C: Has Tolfdir been telling stories again? I thought I made it quite clear that this was a subject inappropriate for conversation. Please don't allow him to continue to discuss the subject.
Skyrim.esm|1027E5: You started this war, plunged Skyrim into chaos, and now the Empire is going to put you down, and restore the peace.
Skyrim.esm|103224: This is notorious bandit hideout. But it's also the shortest way to Kynesgrove, so... we may have to kill a few bandits.
Skyrim.esm|103225: We'll cross to the White River and follow it to Windhelm. Then we can swing south to Kynesgrove.
Skyrim.esm|103226: There's an inn at Kynesgrove - the Braidwood. I hear they serve a nice dark ale. Nothing on the Sleeping Giant, of course.
Skyrim.esm|103227: This is Kynesgrove. Not much to look at. The innkeeper ought to be able to tell us if there's any dragon mound nearby.
Skyrim.esm|103228: This is Kynesgrove. Not much to look at. Let's see if we can find that dragon mound.
Skyrim.esm|103229: No need to bother with giants right now. Just keep your distance.
Skyrim.esm|10322A: I'm glad you were willing to trust me. I know it probably wasn't the best way to introduce myself. But old habits... you know.
Skyrim.esm|10322B: Not too much farther now. Kynesgrove is just down the road to the southeast of here.
Skyrim.esm|10322C: I hope you're Dragonborn, I really do. But we'll find out soon enough.
Skyrim.esm|10322D: We may both end up dead, but at least it gets me out of Riverwood. I don't really think I'm cut out for the quiet life.
Skyrim.esm|10322E: I doubt the Thalmor are aware of you yet. So we should be safe from them, at least.
Skyrim.esm|10322F: Remember, we're not looking for trouble. We need to get to Kynesgrove as fast as we can.
Skyrim.esm|103230: Keep a sharp lookout. We can't afford any delays.
Skyrim.esm|103232: Better to stick together. I don't want you to get yourself killed before we even get there.
Skyrim.esm|103233: Be careful. The Forsworn are just as dangerous as they look.
Skyrim.esm|103234: The roads just aren't safe these days.
Skyrim.esm|103235: I don't have time for this.
Skyrim.esm|103236: Where do they keep coming from?
Skyrim.esm|103238: Should have picked somebody else for your dinner.
Skyrim.esm|103239: If that's the way you want it.
Skyrim.esm|10323A: This looks promising. Akaviri stonework, I think. Reminds me a bit of Cloud Ruler Temple.
Skyrim.esm|10323B: I think we're getting close. Let's keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|10323C: Lead on to Sky Haven Temple.
Skyrim.esm|10323F: Let's get this door open and see what we've got.
Skyrim.esm|103240: Alduin isn't getting any weaker while we stand around here gawping at the architecture.
Skyrim.esm|10326B: What? Do you want to be that thing's next meal? Into the keep, already!
Skyrim.esm|10327D: Look at that dark elf whore... strutting around like she's innocent. I'll cut her open from gullet to groin!
Skyrim.esm|10327E: So it's true. You've turned traitor. I can't believe it.
Skyrim.esm|10327F: If you don't have a good reason to be partnered with Karliah, you're dead.
Skyrim.esm|103280: They say that Karliah murdered Gallus, but I'm not so sure.
Skyrim.esm|103281: They're waiting for you in the cistern. No tricks.
Skyrim.esm|103282: Vex is ready to tear you apart. I hope you have a good excuse for bringing Karliah here.
Skyrim.esm|103283: I can't believe he emptied the vault. Right from under our noses.
Skyrim.esm|103284: How dare you bring that Dunmer bitch here.
Skyrim.esm|103285: Vex better leave a piece of Mercer for us. It's payback time.
Skyrim.esm|103286: What in blazes is going on? Are you working with Karliah?
Skyrim.esm|10339A: There's no time to waste. Let's go.
Skyrim.esm|10339C: I can always use the practice.
Skyrim.esm|10339E: I was just getting warmed up.
Skyrim.esm|1033A0: That's the end of that, hmm?
Skyrim.esm|1033A1: That seems to be all of them.
Skyrim.esm|1033A2: A shame it had to come to that.
Skyrim.esm|1033A3: Seems I still remember my old training.
Skyrim.esm|1033A4: I'm getting too old for this.
Skyrim.esm|1033A6: Come on. We've got to get to Kynesgrove.
Skyrim.esm|1033AA: I don't know. It's like a ward, but who's casting it? Ancano? How?
Skyrim.esm|1033FF: You will die in terror, knowing your final fate...
Skyrim.esm|103402: Know that Gormlaith sent you down to death!
Skyrim.esm|1034D4: There are dragons about, traveler. Be warned.
Skyrim.esm|1034DD: Thank you, traveler. Be safe and don't forget to keep an eye on the sky.
Skyrim.esm|1034DE: You're too kind. Thank you. Be careful and stay safe.
Skyrim.esm|1035B8: Well, I suppose he wouldn't mind... It was all before my time, you understand. I've heard the stories, the same as anyone else. He was a brilliant student, an accomplished wizard. Delved into magic in a way none had seen before. But, I think, he became too focused on just how much power he could acquire. That's what led to the accident.
Skyrim.esm|1035B9: Do you remember what I first told you, about how not being able to control magic could destroy you? I didn't simply mean it could kill you. The Augur's accident is another very real type of a life destroyed. Well, it's been described as an accident. I can't imagine it was intentional. Something must've gone wrong, and he ended up in the state he's in now, fused to the energies that flow through the College. I've never felt it appropriate to ask him about it, about how that must feel. Or, I suppose, if he can feel at all.
Skyrim.esm|1038A3: By the gods, did that really just happen?!
Skyrim.esm|1039B3: We're ready to launch our attack on Solitude. But the Emperor's cousin is getting married! We can't risk attacking now. So we'll bide our time.
Skyrim.esm|1039B4: We're ready to launch our attack on Solitude. But the Emperor's visiting! We can't risk attacking now. So we'll bide our time.
Skyrim.esm|1039B5: We're ready to march on Solitude, but the Emperor's cousin is getting married! If royal blood was spilt, all of Cyrodiil would be up in arms. We can't afford an all out war with the Empire. So we'll bide our time for now.
Skyrim.esm|1039B6: We're ready to march on Solitude, but the Emperor's visiting! The goddamned Emperor! And, as much as I'd like to kill the man myself, we can't risk an all out war with the Empire. We'll bide our time for now...
Skyrim.esm|1041A6: So that's why you were in the cart. Thieving.
Skyrim.esm|1041ED: All right, get that armor on and give that axe a few swings.
Skyrim.esm|1046AB: It's locked. See if you can get it open with some picks. We might need that gold once we get out.
Skyrim.esm|1046AC: Sure, take all my things. Please.
Skyrim.esm|1046AD: You better get that armor on. Give that sword a few swings, too.
Skyrim.esm|1046AE: Grab anything useful and let's go.
Skyrim.esm|10480A: I'm... I'm not sure what happened. A monk from the Psijic Order, here, after all these years, and then he just leaves. I hope we didn't offend him somehow.
Skyrim.esm|10480B: Beyond asking for you, he never said. Very strange indeed.
Skyrim.esm|104F1F: My blade is yours once more.
Skyrim.esm|104F20: Good, I can use the exercise. Haha!
Skyrim.esm|104F22: Looks like you've got some help already. I'll join you, but you'll have to let your comrade go. What do you say?
Skyrim.esm|104F23: You've been a worthy patron. Shall I join you once more, free of charge?
Skyrim.esm|104F24: I could really use some more experience in the field. I'd be happy to join you again, and it won't cost a thing.
Skyrim.esm|104F25: Back for more? Since we had so much fun the last time, I'll join you free of charge.
Skyrim.esm|104F26: If you still need my blade, I'll join you. I want to make sure you get your money's worth.
Skyrim.esm|104F27: Since you paid my fee recently, I suppose it's only fair that I join you once more.
Skyrim.esm|104F28: I'm bored. What do you say we go smash a few heads in? This one's on me.
Skyrim.esm|104F29: My patron appears once more. Have you need of my blade?
Skyrim.esm|104F2A: It pleases me to see you again, my patron.
Skyrim.esm|104F2B: Didn't think I'd be seeing you again so soon.
Skyrim.esm|104F2C: You're looking hale and healthy. It's good to see you again.
Skyrim.esm|104F2D: You're back! Great! Are we going on another adventure?
Skyrim.esm|104F2E: It's great to see you again! It's been too quiet without you around.
Skyrim.esm|104F2F: Well, look what the horker dragged in. Still wandering all over Skyrim and causing trouble?
Skyrim.esm|104F30: I knew you couldn't stay away. Admit it, you were lost without me.
Skyrim.esm|104F31: Well, look who it is. Been keeping out of trouble much? I hope not!
Skyrim.esm|104F32: If you need some skulls crushed, I'm still available for hire.
Skyrim.esm|104F33: It's good to see you. If you need my help again, it's the same fee as always.
Skyrim.esm|104F34: Well met, friend. It's good to see you're still in one piece, with all the trouble you get into.
Skyrim.esm|104F7A: Or if you're feeling lucky, you can take this bow. Might take her by surprise.
Skyrim.esm|104F84: Or if you're feeling lucky, you can take this bow. Might take her by surprise.
Skyrim.esm|104FC6: A storeroom. Grab anything you can, but let's keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|104FC7: An old storeroom. Grab anything you can, but let's keep moving.
Skyrim.esm|10537F: You have talent. Keep working at your craft, and you'll be a fine smith one day. Why don't you keep that dagger and helmet? Maybe you'll remember me when you're making Skyforge Steel, huh?
Skyrim.esm|105380: I should hire you to be my assistant at this rate. Let's improve the fit. Take this leather to the workbench over there.
Skyrim.esm|105381: Ah, good. A lot of weapons and armor need leather for straps, fittings, that kind of thing. Let's see if you can make a hide helmet. Here's the rest of what you need.
Skyrim.esm|105382: This looks good. You put time into your blades, they'll serve you well when you need them. You want to keep helping? How about you make some armor? Let's start by tanning some leather on the rack.
Skyrim.esm|105383: Not bad, but it's a little dull. How about you sharpen it up? Just need a bit of metal and the grindstone over there.
Skyrim.esm|105384: Yes, actually. How about you smith me an iron dagger? Here's everything you need to make one. Go ahead.
Skyrim.esm|10556C: Long as you clean up. You know how to use it right? Mix a couple of ingredients together, see what happens. A little Wheat and Blisterwort makes a healing potion, if you didn't know. We have plenty of ingredients for sale, too.
Skyrim.esm|105A5F: Ah, a student of the craft. You'll need to start by learning an enchantment. Take an enchanted weapon or piece of armor, then use the Arcane Enchanter to learn its secrets. The item is destroyed in the process, so be wary. Once you know an enchantment, you'll need a filled soul gem, and something to enchant. You'll use the Arcane Enchanter for that, too. Of course, I have everything you need for sale, if you have the coin.
Skyrim.esm|105CBA: You got what you came for, didn't you? Go then, back to your college.
Skyrim.esm|105D0B: Who said anything about trouble? Now get out of my face.
Skyrim.esm|105D0F: What's a milk drinker like you doing out here? Go home to your mother.
Skyrim.esm|105D11: Why? What are you gonna do, cry?
Skyrim.esm|10600E: I probably shouldn't tell everyone who asks. This is important business, you know.
Skyrim.esm|10600F: There's been word of some trouble nearby. I'm on my way to investigate.
Skyrim.esm|106010: Now I have some time to kill...
Skyrim.esm|106011: Just make sure to take care of whatever's going on there. It's my name on the line.
Skyrim.esm|106012: You want to pay me to do my job? Fine. I'll mark the location on your map.
Skyrim.esm|106013: All right, all right! No need to get violent. We're on the same side. I'll mark it on your map. This one's on you.
Skyrim.esm|106014: Someone's going to get hurt, all right.
Skyrim.esm|106015: If you really want to do my job for me... Here, I'll mark the location on your map.
Skyrim.esm|106016: No, I think I've got this one. Thanks, though.
Skyrim.esm|1065EA: Important deliveries to make! No time for chatting!
Skyrim.esm|1065EC: Greetings, brother. You look weary. I have something to help you relax, if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|1065ED: Greetings, sister. You look weary. I have something to help you relax, if you're interested.
Skyrim.esm|10660F: Instead of projecting the sky, we project all of Tamriel, and then harness the latent energies to overlay the positions of...
Skyrim.esm|106610: Have you ever seen the Orrery in the Imperial City? It was the inspiration for this idea.
Skyrim.esm|106611: This projection should be lit up like the night sky...
Skyrim.esm|106639: I have nothing to say to you. Now move along, or I'll order my guards to attack.
Skyrim.esm|10663A: No need to get so... hostile. Just take it. Sounds like you need it more than I do.
Skyrim.esm|10663B: Yeah... That's not going to happen.
Skyrim.esm|10663C: Ah, a snitch, eh? Can't have you running to any guards now, can I?
Skyrim.esm|10663D: Nice doing business with you, friend.
Skyrim.esm|106A44: I'm on my way to a giant camp and I can't guarantee those beasts won't think you're part of the offering. Keep your distance and you shouldn't have anything to worry about.
Skyrim.esm|106A45: Thanks. You take care of yourself, now.
Skyrim.esm|106A46: I'd stay away if I were you.
Skyrim.esm|106A47: The cow, of course. Others do it differently, but I've found that an annual offering usually keeps the giants away from our livestock. Some think it's superstition, but I believe it works. I've yet to have a giant kill any of my livestock.
Skyrim.esm|106A48: No. I was too afraid to look. I don't know what we're going to do now. We have nothing left.
Skyrim.esm|106A49: It happened so fast. One minute the dragon was there, the next it was gone. Everything was destroyed.
Skyrim.esm|106A4A: I have everything under control. Thanks for offering.
Skyrim.esm|106A4B: Then you've seen them, too. We lost our home, our livestock... We barely have enough gold to get by.
Skyrim.esm|106A4D: It's tradition. We mark the animal as a way to let the giants know we're giving it to them willingly. Sort of a peace offering.
Skyrim.esm|106A52: My wife's going to kill me when I tell her we'll need to bring another cow.
Skyrim.esm|106A54: Either way this ends up, I'm headed to the inn.
Skyrim.esm|106A55: It's true about the dragons returning. Saw it with my own eyes.
Skyrim.esm|106A57: Thank you for the gold, traveler.
Skyrim.esm|106A58: That's okay. We'll find a way to get by. Safe travels.
Skyrim.esm|106BF6: What does it take to kill this monster?
Skyrim.esm|106BF7: It won't die, it just keeps coming!
Skyrim.esm|106BF8: How does it move so damn fast?
Skyrim.esm|106BFA: How in Oblivion do we kill this thing?
Skyrim.esm|106BFC: It's the End Times! The End Times have come!
Skyrim.esm|106BFD: By the gods, a dragon! How?
Skyrim.esm|106BFE: A dragon? It's a dragon! Gods help us!
Skyrim.esm|106CF0: Remember to put in a good word with Maven for me.
Skyrim.esm|106CF1: I love it when a plan comes together.
Skyrim.esm|106E2E: Ready now! Everyone, with me! For the Empire! For the Legion! Rrrrrraaaaaaaaaaaaahhhhhhh!!!!
Skyrim.esm|106E2F: This is the day we send a message to Ulfric Stormcloak and the rebel Jarls who support him.
Skyrim.esm|106E30: This is an important day for the Empire and for the Legion. And for all of Skyrim.
Skyrim.esm|106E31: But make no mistake. What we do here today, we do for Skyrim and her people. By cutting out the disease of this rebellion, we will make this country whole again!
Skyrim.esm|1074C4: Does it involve sneaking and stabbing? Ooh, please say yes!
Skyrim.esm|1074C6: Of course, Listener. What is it?
Skyrim.esm|1074C7: Now wait a minute, Listener. Even Cicero has his limits! Not many, mind you... but I do have them...
Skyrim.esm|1074C8: I won't do it. You ask too much.
Skyrim.esm|1074C9: But Listener... Are you sure? I just can't bring myself to do it...
Skyrim.esm|1074CA: Sorry, Listener. It can't be done.
Skyrim.esm|1074CD: You want me to do something or not?
Skyrim.esm|1074CF: Still something you'd like me to do?
Skyrim.esm|107560: Funny, when I was a boy, Imperial walls and towers used to make me feel so safe.
Skyrim.esm|107562: A Nord's last thoughts should be of home.
Skyrim.esm|107564: Hey, what village are you from horse thief?
Skyrim.esm|107600: I suppose it would be. Good thinking.
Skyrim.esm|107601: Is that so? Lost your uniform along the way did you, soldier?
Skyrim.esm|107602: Is that so? I don't recognize you. Why aren't you wearing your Stormcloak colors?
Skyrim.esm|107703: Climb in back and we'll be off.
Skyrim.esm|107A73: I'm not sure. Just... Well, mind what you tell him, all right?
Skyrim.esm|107A74: Of course. That's your choice. I just wanted to let you know.
Skyrim.esm|107A76: No, no. Well, I don't think so.
Skyrim.esm|107A77: Between the two of us, there are rumors about him. That this "advisor" position he has is a sham, an excuse. That what he's really doing is spying for the Thalmor, trying to feed them information. Whether it's true, I can't say. But it never hurts to be a little suspicious, does it?
Skyrim.esm|107A78: There you are. I've been trying to find you. I just wanted to let you know that Ancano's been asking about you. I think he's looking for you.
Skyrim.esm|107A82: Has everyone here forgotten their training? Kill that thing!
Skyrim.esm|107A83: I want every arrow in the sky!
Skyrim.esm|107A86: Up through the tower, let's go!
Skyrim.esm|107A87: Jarl Ulfric! What is that thing? Could the legends be true?
Skyrim.esm|107AAC: I am well aware. Tolfdir is still in Saarthal, is he? I shall be expecting a full report from him when he returns.
Skyrim.esm|107AAD: I know I don't like the way he looks at me. I can't tell if he expects me to blow myself up, or to try and murder him. But he clearly doesn't trust any of us.
Skyrim.esm|107AAE: I know he's from the Thalmor, and that he claims to be here simply as an advisor. I also know that no one really believes that. I've been trying to avoid him, honestly.
Skyrim.esm|107AAF: I know full well that you have. Please do not insult my intelligence. Tolfdir is still there now, is he? I shall expect a full report when he returns.
Skyrim.esm|107AB0: What? Preposterous, and just the sort of thing I would expect from mages who have nothing better to do with their time. I have made it quite clear that my only role here is as an advisor to the Arch-Mage. I would suggest that you not further spread this rumor.
Skyrim.esm|107AB1: You there. I have questions for you. You were in Saarthal, yes? It has come to my attention that something was found there.
Skyrim.esm|107AB2: Thank you for your help. You may go now.
Skyrim.esm|107AB3: Don't trust that one. He's up to something. Most here are up to something, of course, but in his case, it's not good. I haven't figured it out yet, but I will. He is rather handsome though, isn't he?
Skyrim.esm|107AB4: Something was discovered in Saarthal that was significant enough that Tolfdir sent a new member of the College, alone, to deliver word. That sounds precisely like the sort of thing that should matter to everyone. Especially me.
Skyrim.esm|107AB5: He's asked far, far too many questions about my research. I think the Thalmor are trying to steal my work! I haven't told him anything, of course. What I'm doing is far too important. Can't breathe a word of it.
Skyrim.esm|107AB7: It is my job to know these things. My role as advisor to the Arch-Mage is aided by knowing everything that transpires here.
Skyrim.esm|107D8A: Come on, you need your hands free.
Skyrim.esm|107D8B: Those binds aren't going to cut themselves.
Skyrim.esm|107D8D: Come on, you need your hands free.
Skyrim.esm|107D8E: Those binds aren't going to cut themselves.
Skyrim.esm|107E3C: Members of the College -- your new Arch-Mage!
Skyrim.esm|107E3D: You've done it! The College is safe again, thanks to your work. I knew you had it in you. I daresay the Psijics are right. There's no one more deserving to be Arch-Mage, in my opinion. Here, consider these yours. And the Arch-Mage's quarters as well. I shall be here for advice, should you need me.
Skyrim.esm|107EF3: And speak to Eorlund if you want a better weapon than... whatever that is.
Skyrim.esm|107F97: Not entirely true, though not entirely false either. Any Nord can learn the Way of the Voice by studying with the Greybeards, given enough ambition and dedication. My shouting Torygg to the ground proved he had neither. However, it was my sword piercing his heart that killed him.
Skyrim.esm|108030: You should wait until night fall before trying to sneak in. Talos preserve you.
Skyrim.esm|108152: Certainly! Glad to see you have the task well at hand.
Skyrim.esm|108227: What are you waiting for? Go!
Skyrim.esm|108CF4: I see. It probably doesn't even matter now, what with the mess that's been made in here. I'll add this to the rest of the items. I don't think there's anything further I need from you.
Skyrim.esm|1092B0: Let's get out of here before we end up like Mercer!
Skyrim.esm|1092B1: This place is coming down. Let's get out of here!
Skyrim.esm|1093B3: Keep away. If I am harmed, people will come looking.
Skyrim.esm|1093EC: You've more than proven that the Thieves Guild is back on its feet in our city and earned every bit of your reward. Tell Delvin that he can count on me to provide the influence around here with the right people when he needs it. If you're ever looking to sell any illegally obtained merchandise, I'd also provide my services as a fence. It's the least I can do.
Skyrim.esm|1093EF: Oh, Beem-Ja. I never meant for this. What have I done?
Skyrim.esm|1093F1: Eyes open. Danger could be anywhere.
Skyrim.esm|1093F2: This place is sure to make us rich!
Skyrim.esm|1093F3: I don't care what Beem says. It's high time we get in there and find our fortune.
Skyrim.esm|1093FA: You claim this recruit possesses an aptitude for our line of work. If so, let her prove it.
Skyrim.esm|1095CD: Just walk towards me, come on!
Skyrim.esm|1095CE: Don't look up. Just focus on me. You can do it!
Skyrim.esm|1095D1: We'll have to carry this one on our backs....
Skyrim.esm|1095D2: Another second out there with the dragon, and they'd both be dead....
Skyrim.esm|1095D5: What are you doing? Get off the road!
Skyrim.esm|1097B1: By bringing down my other Hunters, you turned the chase inside out. And they were no base prey. You continue to amuse and impress. Go forth, with my blessing.
Skyrim.esm|1097B8: Kraldar's family was once very powerful in Winterhold. It's my honor to assist him even now.
Skyrim.esm|109B5F: I can cut you loose inside. Come on!
Skyrim.esm|109B60: Ralof! You damned traitor. Out of my way!
Skyrim.esm|109B61: You! Come on, into the keep!
Skyrim.esm|109B62: Fine. I hope that dragon takes you all to Sovngarde.
Skyrim.esm|109B63: We're escaping, Hadvar. You're not stopping us this time.
Skyrim.esm|109BAA: I thought it would be better to leave some of the details out of our previous discussion. Didn't want to risk you walking away from the job. Besides, you've done Maven a favor getting rid of him and saved me from wasting coin hiring someone else to do it later.
Skyrim.esm|109BAB: You're not sweet-talking your way out of this. Not after what you did. You're headed to Cidhna Mine, and that's that.
Skyrim.esm|109BAC: All right, but you just watch yourself. Next time, I might not be so lenient.
Skyrim.esm|109BAD: I don't care who you're with. After what you did, you're lucky I'm not cutting you down where you stand!
Skyrim.esm|109C16: Don't bother with that. Lost the key ages ago. Poor fellow screamed for weeks.
Skyrim.esm|109C17: Wait a second. Looks like there's something in this cage.
Skyrim.esm|109C18: See if you can get it open with some picks. We'll need everything we can get.
Skyrim.esm|109C24: Mage, eh? Well, to each his own. It's not for me to judge.
Skyrim.esm|109C25: Thief, eh? It's never too late to take charge of your own fate, you know.
Skyrim.esm|109C26: Warrior, good! I knew you shouldn't have been on that cart the minute I laid eyes on you.
Skyrim.esm|109C28: These are the Guardian Stones, three of the thirteen ancient standing stones that dot Skyrim's landscape.
Skyrim.esm|109C29: Mage, eh? Well, to each his own. It's not for me to judge.
Skyrim.esm|109C2A: Thief, eh? It's never too late to take charge of your own fate, you know.
Skyrim.esm|109C2B: Warrior, good! Those stars will guide you to honor and glory.
Skyrim.esm|109C2D: When I woke from cold death, my doom was lifted - there was Shor's Hall, my heart's desire. But now I wander, weary and lost. Alduin hunts me as we once hunted our prey - a bitter payment for many bloody deeds.
Skyrim.esm|109C2E: These are the Guardian Stones, three of the thirteen ancient standing stones that dot Skyrim's landscape.
Skyrim.esm|109C45: Father never trusted Beem. I always thought he was just being overprotective.
Skyrim.esm|109C47: Beem! What are you doing? Stop it!
Skyrim.esm|109C48: This is no time for idle talk.
Skyrim.esm|109C4A: I grow tired of debating this. If Miss Salma insists on this folly, so be it.
Skyrim.esm|109C4B: I admit, there is strength in numbers. Perhaps between us...
Skyrim.esm|109C4C: Hey now, don't take all the good stuff!
Skyrim.esm|109C77: This one has something sweet for the fellow traveler, if it entices.
Skyrim.esm|109C79: The finest skooma, the sweetest moon sugar. At a fair price, of course.
Skyrim.esm|109D07: As fearless in death as he was in life.
Skyrim.esm|109D08: It has been an honor, Jarl Ulfric!
Skyrim.esm|109E39: Are you ready to see the world as only a dovah can?
Skyrim.esm|109E44: Good. Let's get this over with.
Skyrim.esm|109E69: Assorted books, scrolls... I'm sure you'll find something useful.
Skyrim.esm|109E6A: I have a few things laying around that weren't worth adding to the collection. You may look through them.
Skyrim.esm|109E6B: Please make sure your hands are clean before touching anything, all right?
Skyrim.esm|10A183: We just need to move some of these rocks to clear the way!
Skyrim.esm|10A187: Hadvar! Into the keep, soldier, we're leaving!
Skyrim.esm|10A188: Thought I told you to run for it! Go!
Skyrim.esm|10A189: Soldiers, townspeople, and dregs into the keep!
Skyrim.esm|10A18B: Damn you mages! Aren't you good for anything?
Skyrim.esm|10A2BD: Maybe I'll make the Potage le Magnifique, hmm? You may have killed the Gourmet, but true artistry never dies.
Skyrim.esm|10A2BE: Maybe I'll make the Potage le Magnifique, hmm? But not the way you make it! Ha ha ha. No, certainly not...
Skyrim.esm|10A2BF: What do you think? Some more Nirnroot, maybe?
Skyrim.esm|10A2C0: I learned to cook from my father. Ah, but that was a long time ago. Before, you know... I killed him. But let's not talk about that now.
Skyrim.esm|10A2C2: Listener, please... if Cicero is going to be a part of this Sanctuary I'll accept that. But can't you do something about the... dancing?
Skyrim.esm|10A2C3: Got to admit, I find the Night Mother's presence... comforting.
Skyrim.esm|10A765: The new initiates are working out well. Eager, dedicated, and not afraid to get their hands wet.
Skyrim.esm|10AA18: You know, since Kodlak's funeral the Skyforge feels more... awake. It's always been said that the souls of the heroes of old are what give Skyforge steel its strength. But I think the forge knows the greatness of Kodlak's soul. I can't really explain, but it feels like it's... young. I'll wager it could now forge metal the likes of which hasn't been seen since eras long forgotten. I'm eager to try.
Skyrim.esm|10EBBB: To return to its past glory, it must reacquaint itself with the hatred of this world.
Skyrim.esm|10EBBC: To return to its past glory, it must first drink the blood of deceit.
Skyrim.esm|10EBBE: I can feel their heartbreak swelling in my blade.
Skyrim.esm|10EBBF: Their tears shine the ebony to a sharpest gleam.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC0: Ah, the blood of deceit is a nourishing flow.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC1: At last, my blade is returned to its full glory. Now go forth, child. Continue your tiny subversions against the orders of trust and intimacy.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC2: Suit yourself. But those who listen at doors always wonder what is beyond them. Eventually your curiosity will drive you back to me.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC3: The blade will feed on the ire of Tamriel. Bathe in your infamy. Feel the weight of their loathing, and my power will course through it once more.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC4: You may think so. But in the end, you mortals will always flit like fire with the winds of your desires and conveniences. And those winds issue from my whispers.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC5: Seek out those closest to you. The final pluck of their misguided heartstrings will accompany my blade in the song of your grandeur.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC6: I forgive your not knowing my name. Few can hear my whispers anymore. I am Mephala, the Lady of Whispers. I tug at the web of connections between mortals. Love, hatred, loyalty, betrayal.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC7: Your world is admirably seeped in lies and inclinations. My blade is a darling leech that feeds on deceptions, and nourishes its master.
Skyrim.esm|10EBC8: The boy is good at sussing out secrets, but the corruptibility of children is ultimately too limited for my purposes. You, though, I expect will prove far more malleable.
Skyrim.esm|10FD90: I think you've been taking things that don't belong to you. Pay your debts, and we'll forget this happened.
